G3981.mounce: also spelled πιθός, persuasive, skillful, 1 Cor. 2:4*

G1216.mounce: Demetrius, pr. name (1) The Ephesian silversmith, Acts 19:24, 38 (2) A certain Christian, 3 Jn. 12*
G1216.strongs: from (Ceres); Demetrius, the name of an Ephesian and of a Christian

G4891.mounce: to raise up with any one; to raise up with Christ by spiritual resemblance of His resurrection, Eph. 2:6; Col. 2:12; 3:1*
G4891.abbottSmith: to raise together : metaph., of the Christian's mystical resur­rection with Christ, Eph 2:6 ; pass., Col 2:12 3:1 .†
G4891.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1453 (ἐγείρω); to rouse (from death) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to revivify (spirtually) in resemblance to

G4146.abbottSmith: richly , abundantly : Col 3:16 , I Ti 6:17 , Tit 3:6 , II Pe 1:11 .†

G4418.abbottSmith: the heel : fig., ἐπαίρειν τὴν π. ἐπί , Jo 13:18 (LXX) .†

G5432.thayer: I guard, keep, as by a military guard.
G5432.mounce: to keep watch; trans. to guard, watch, with a military guard, 2 Cor. 11:32; to keep in a condition of restraint, Gal. 3:23; to keep in a state of settlement or security, Phil. 4:7; 1 Pet. 1:5*
G5432.abbottSmith: to guard, keep under guard, protect or keep by guarding: II Co 11:32 ; metaph., Ga 3:23 , Phl 4:7 , I Pe 1:5 .†
G5432.strongs: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G3708 (ὁράω); to be a watcher in advance, i.e. to mount guard as a sentinel (post spies at gates); figuratively, to hem in, protect

G3943.mounce: pr. pertaining to wine, drunken; hence, quarrelsome, insolent, overbearing, 1 Tim. 3:3; Tit. 1:7*
G3943.abbottSmith: given to wine , drunken : I Ti 3:3 , Tit 1:7 .†
G3943.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3631 (οἶνος); staying near wine, i.e. tippling (a toper)

G2450.thayer: I live as a Jew (in religion, ceremonially).
G2450.abbottSmith: to conform to Jewish practice , to Judaize : Ga 2:14 .†
G2450.strongs: from G2453 (Ἰουδαῖος); to become a Judæan, i.e. "Judaize"

G4212.mounce: How many times? How often? Mt. 18:21; 23:37; Lk. 13:34*
G4212.strongs: multiplicative from G4214 (πόσος); how many times

G5461.thayer: (a) I light up, illumine, (b) I bring to light, make evident, reveal.
G5461.mounce: to light, give light to, illuminate, shine upon, Lk. 11:36; Rev. 18:1; 21:23; met. to enlighten spiritually, Jn. 1:9; Eph. 1:18; 3:9; Heb. 6:4; 10:32; to reveal, to bring to light, make known, 1 Cor. 4:5; 2 Tim. 1:10; intrans. shine, Rev. 22:5*
G5461.abbottSmith: intrans., to shine, give light (Arist., Plut., a1.): seq. ἐπί , Re 22:5 .
G5461.strongs: from G5457 (φῶς); to shed rays, i.e. to shine or (transitively) to brighten up (literally or figuratively)
G5461.kjv: enlighten, illuminate, (bring to, give) light, make to see

G2491.thayer: John: the Baptist, the apostle, a member of the Sanhedrin, or John Mark.
G2491.mounce: also spelled Ἰωάνης, Joannes, John, pr. name (1) John the Baptist, Mt. 3:1, et al. (2) John, son of Zebedee, the apostle, Mt. 4:21, et al. (3) John, surnamed Mark, Acts 12:12, et al. (4) John, the high-priest, Acts 4:6
G2491.abbottSmith: John ( I Es 8:38(41) *), viz., John the Baptist: Mt 3:1 , al. John the Apostle, son of Zebedee: Mt 4:21 , Mk 1:19 , Lk 5:10 , Ac 1:13 , al. The father of St. Peter: Jo 1:43 21:15-17 . John surnamed Mark: Ac 12:12 , 25 13:5 , 13 15:37 . The writer of the Apocalypse, traditionally identified with 2: Re 1:1 , 4 , 9 .
G2491.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03110); Joannes (i.e. Jochanan), the name of four Israelites

G3590.thayer: the eighth, one of eight, with seven others.
G3590.mounce: the eighth, Lk. 1:59; Acts 7:8; 2 Pet. 2:5; Rev. 17:11; 21:20*
G3590.abbottSmith: the eighth : Lk 1:59 , Ac 7:3 (LXX) , Re 17:11 21:20 ; one of eight , with seven others (usually, in this sense, with αὐτός added, but cf. Plat., Legg. , iii, 695c; Plut., Pelop. , 13; II Mac 5:27 ): II Pe 2:5 .†

G2703.thayer: I flee for refuge (implying that the refuge is reached); aor. indicates moment of arrival.
G2703.mounce: to flee to for refuge, Acts 14:6; Heb. 6:18*
G2703.abbottSmith: to flee for refuge : seq. εἰς , Ac 14:6 ; metaph., c. inf., He 6:18 .†
G2703.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5343 (φεύγω); to flee down (away)

G933.mounce: royal, regal; met. possessed of high prerogatives and distinction, 1 Pet. 2:9; τὰ βασίλεια, i.e. δώματα, regal mansion, palaces, Lk. 7:25*
G933.abbottSmith: a capital city. Freq. in pl., τὰ β. , a palace : Lk 7:25 .†

G1224.thayer: I cross, pass through, step across.
G1224.mounce: to pass through or over, Lk. 16:26; Acts 16:9; Heb. 11:29*
G1224.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to cross

G644.thayer: either a shadow cast by an object, or a faint image or copy of an object.
G644.mounce: a shadow cast; met. a shade, the slightest trace, Jas. 1:17*
G644.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G4639 (σκιά); a shading off, i.e. obscuration

G128.strongs: from (to scorch) and (the face, from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)); an Æthiopian (as a blackamoor)

G4240.mounce: also spelled πραότης, ητος, ἡ, meekness, mildness, forbearance, 1 Pet. 3:15; gentleness, kindness, Jas. 1:21; 3:13; Gal. 5:23
G4240.abbottSmith: gentleness , meekness : I Co 4:21 , II Co 10:1 , Ga 5:23 6:1 , Eph 4:2 , Col 3:12 (v. Lft., in l.), II Ti 2:25 , Tit 3:2 , Ja 1:21 3:13 , I Pe 3:15 .†
G4240.strongs: from G4239 (πραΰς); mildness, i.e. (by implication) humility

G760.thayer: Asa, son of Abijah and father of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah about 900 B.C. for 41 years.
G760.abbottSmith: Asaph , an obvious error for Ἀσά , found in the best texts, and adopted by LTTr. and WH, R, mg.: Mt 1:7 , 8 .†
G760.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0609); Asa, an Israelite

G2701.abbottSmith: to run down : seq. ἐπί , c. acc. pers., Ac 21:32 .†
G2701.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5143 (τρέχω); to run down, i.e. hasten from a tower

G809.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (in the sense of its congener G4976 (σχῆμα)); properly, shapeless, i.e. (figuratively) inelegant

G5378.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G3056 (λόγος); fond of words, i.e. talkative (argumentative, learned, "philological"); Philologus, a Christian

G639.thayer: I am at a loss, am perplexed; mid: I am in doubt.
G639.mounce: also spelled ἀπορρίπτω, pr. to be without means; met. to hesitate, be at a stand, be in doubt and perplexity, Jn. 13:22; Acts 25:20; 2 Cor. 4:8; Gal. 4:20
G639.abbottSmith: to be at a loss , be perplexed : absol., Mk 6:20 ( ἐποίει , R, mg.); mid., be in doubt : absol., II Co 4:8 ; c. acc., Ac 25:20 ; seq. περί , Lk 24:4 ; ἐν , Ga 4:20 ; περὶ τίνος λέγει , Jo 13:22 .†
G639.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G4198 (πορεύομαι); to have no way out, i.e. be at a loss (mentally)

G801.thayer: unintelligent, without wisdom, unwise, undiscerning (implying probably moral defect).
G801.mounce: unintelligent, dull, Mt. 15:16; Mk. 7:18; reckless, perverse, Rom. 1:21, 31; unenlightened, heathenish, Rom. 10:19
G801.abbottSmith: without understanding or discernment : Mt 15:16 , Mk 7:18 (Swete, in l.), Ro 1:21 , 31 10:19 (for an ex. of its use in the moral sense, v. MM, s.v.).†
G801.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4908 (συνετός); unintelligent; by implication, wicked

G3119.mounce: softness; listlessness, indisposition, weakness, infirmity of body, Mt. 4:23; 9:35; 10:1*

G2606.mounce: to deride, laugh at, jeer, Mt. 9:24; Mk. 5:40; Lk. 8:53*
G2606.abbottSmith: to deride , laugh scornfully at : c. gen. pers., Mt 9:24 , Mk 5:40 , Lk 8:53 .†

G2978.thayer: a sudden storm, squall, whirlwind, hurricane.
G2978.mounce: a squall of wind, a hurricane, Mk. 4:37; Lk. 8:23; 2 Pet. 2:17*
G2978.abbottSmith: a hurricane , whirlwind : Mk 4:37 , Lk 8:23 , II Pe 2:17 .†

G1521.thayer: I lead in, bring in, introduce.
G1521.mounce: to lead or bring in, introduce, conduct or usher in or to a place or person, Lk. 2:27; 14:21; 22:54; Jn. 18:16; Acts 9:8; 21:28f., 37; Heb. 1:6
G1521.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G71 (ἄγω); to introduce (literally or figuratively)
G1521.kjv: bring in(-to), (+ was to) lead into

G4189.mounce: pr. badness, bad condition; in NT evil disposition of mind, wickedness, mischief, malignity, Mt. 22:18; pl. πονηρίαι, wicked deeds, villanies, Mk. 7:23; Acts 3:26
G4189.abbottSmith: iniquity , wickedness : Mt 22:18 , Lk 11:39 , Ro 1:29 , Eph 6:12 ; pl. (v. Bl., § 32, 6; WM, 220; Swete, Mk. , 153), Mk 7:22 , Ac 3:26 ; κακία καὶ π. , I Co 5:8 .†
G4189.strongs: from G4190 (πονηρός); depravity, i.e. (specially), malice; plural (concretely) plots, sins

G4715.thayer: an Attic silver coin equal in value to the Jewish shekel.
G4715.mounce: pr. a weight; a stater, an Attic silver coin, equal in value to the Jewish shekel, or to four Attic or two Alexandrian drachmas, Mt. 17:27*
G4715.strongs: from the base of G2746 (καύχησις); a stander (standard of value), i.e. (specially), a stater or certain coin

G630.thayer: I release, let go, send away, divorce, am rid; mid: I depart.
G630.mounce: pr. to loose; to release from a tie or burden, Mt. 18:27; to divorce, Mt. 1:19; to remit, forgive, Lk. 6:37; to liberate, discharge, Mt. 27:15; to dismiss, Mt. 15:23; Acts 19:40; to allow to depart, to send away, Mt. 14:15; to permit, or, signal departure from life, Lk. 2:29; mid. to depart, Acts 28:25; pass. to be rid, Lk. 13:12
G630.abbottSmith: to set free , release : Lk 13:12 , Jo 19:10 , al.; a debtor, Mt 18:27 ; metaph., of forgiveness, Lk 6:37 .
G630.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3089 (λύω); to free fully, i.e. (literally) relieve, release, dismiss (reflexively, depart), or (figuratively) let die, pardon or (specially) divorce
G630.kjv: (let) depart, dismiss, divorce, forgive, let go, loose, put (send) away, release, set at liberty

G4746.thayer: a bough, branch of a tree.
G4746.strongs: from a primary (to "step" or "stamp"); a spread (as if tramped flat) of loose materials for a couch, i.e. (by implication) a bough of a tree so employed

G1385.thayer: a beam or spar of timber.
G1385.mounce: a beam or spar of timber, Mt. 7:3, 4, 5; Lk. 6:41, 42*
G1385.abbottSmith: a beam of timber: Mt 7:3-5 , Lk 6:41 , 42 ( DCG , i, 176).†
G1385.strongs: from G1209 (δέχομαι) (through the idea of holding up); a stick of timber

G4832.mounce: of like form, assimilated, conformed, similar in form, Rom. 8:29; Phil. 3:21*
G4832.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3444 (μορφή); jointly formed, i.e. (figuratively) similar

G1569.mounce: to wonder at, wonder greatly, Mk. 12:17*
G1569.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2285 (θάμβος); utterly astounded

G5274.thayer: (a) I take up, (b) I welcome, entertain, (c) I answer, (d) I suppose, imagine.
G5274.abbottSmith: to take or bear up (by supporting from beneath): c. acc pers., Ac 1:9 .
G5274.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take from below, i.e. carry upward; figuratively, to take up, i.e. continue a discourse or topic; mentally, to assume (presume)

G4961.thayer: a fellow soldier (in the Christian faith).
G4961.mounce: a fellow soldier, co-militant, in the service of Christ, Phil. 2:25; Phlm. 2*
G4961.abbottSmith: a fellow-soldier (Plat., Xen., al.). Metaph., of fellowship in Christian service: Phl 2:25 , Phm 2 .†
G4961.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4757 (στρατιώτης); a co-campaigner, i.e. (figuratively) an associate in Christian toil

G1347.thayer: acquittal, justifying, justification, a process of absolution.
G1347.mounce: pr. a making right or just; a declaration of right or justice; a judicial sentence; in NT, acquittal, acceptance, justification, Rom. 4:25; 5:18*
G1347.abbottSmith: the act of pronouncing righteous, justification, acquittal : Ro 4:25 5:18 (Cremer, 199).†

G3340.thayer: I repent, change my mind, change the inner man (particularly with reference to acceptance of the will of God), repent.
G3340.mounce: to undergo a change in frame of mind and feeling, to repent, Lk. 17:3, 4; to make a change of principle and practice, to reform, Mt. 3:2
G3340.abbottSmith: to change one's mind or purpose , hence, to repent ; in NT (exc. Lk 17:3 , 4 ), of repentance from sin, involving amendment: seq. ἀπό , Ac 8:22 ; ἔκ , Re 2:21 , 22 9:20 , 21 16:11 ( מִן שׁוּב ); ἐπί , II Co 12:21 ; absol., Mt 3:2 4:17 11:20 12:41 , Mk 1:15 6:12 , Lk 11:32 13:3 , 5 15:7 , 10 16:30 17:3 , 4 , Ac 2:38 3:19 17:30 26:20 Re 2:5 , 16 , 21 3:3 , 19 ; c. inf., Re 16:9 ; ἐν σάκκῳ κ. σποδῷ ,
G3340.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G3539 (νοιέω); to think differently or afterwards, i.e. reconsider (morally, feel compunction)

G3098.thayer: (Hebrew), Magog, sometimes as name of a people, sometimes as name of a country in the Old Testament, probably the Scythians; hence: used in apocalyptic literature.
G3098.abbottSmith: Magog , associated with Gog: Re 20:8 (v. Swete, in l.).†
G3098.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04031); Magog, a foreign nation, i.e. (figuratively) an Antichristian party

G2375.mounce: a stone or other material employed to close a doorway; later, a large oblong shield, Eph. 6:16*
G2375.strongs: from G2374 (θύρα); a large shield (as door-shaped)

G1124.thayer: (a) a writing, (b) a passage of scripture; plur: the scriptures.
G1124.mounce: a writing; in NT the Holy Scriptures, the Jewish Scriptures, or Books of the Old Testament, Mt. 21:42; Jn. 5:39, et al.; by synec. doctrines, declarations, oracles, or promises contained in the sacred books, Mt. 22:29; Mk. 12:24, et al.; spc. a prophecy, Mt. 26:54; Mk. 14:49; Lk. 4:21; 24:27, 32; with the addition of προφητική, Rom. 16:26; of τῶν προφητῶν, Mt. 26:56
G1124.abbottSmith: a drawing, painting . writing ; that which is written, a writing : πᾶσα γ. , II Ti 3:16 ; γ. ἅγιαι , Ro 1:2 ; προφητικαί , Ro 16:26 ; al αἱ γ. τ. προφητῶν , Mt 26:56 ; ἡ γ., αἱ γ. , the sacred writings, the Scriptures (i.e. the OT; v. Milligan, NTD , 205); in pl., when the sacred writings as a whole are meant, e.g. Mt 21:42 26:54 , Jo 5:39 , Ro 15:4 ; in sing., when a particular passage is referre
G1124.strongs: from G1125 (γράφω); a document, i.e. holy Writ (or its contents or a statement in it)

G508.strongs: from G507 (ἄνω) and G1093 (γῆ); above the ground, i.e. (properly) the second floor of a building; used for a dome or a balcony on the upper story

G3332.thayer: I change my position, depart, remove.
G3332.mounce: without accompaniment, alone, Mt. 14:23; 18:15; Lk. 10:40; singly existent, sole, only, Jn. 17:3; lone solitary, Jn. 8:29; 16:32; alone in respect of restriction, only, Mt. 4:4; 12:4; alone in respect of circumstances, only, Lk. 24:18; not multiplied by reproduction, lone, barren, Jn. 12:24
G3332.abbottSmith: trans., to remove (LXX, IV Ki 16:17 25:11 , Ps 79 (80):8 , Pr 22:28 ).
G3332.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G142 (αἴρω); to betake oneself, i.e. remove (locally)

G1805.thayer: I buy out, buy away from, ransom; mid: I purchase out, buy, redeem, choose.
G1805.mounce: to buy out of the hands of a person; to redeem, set free, Gal. 3:13; 4:5; mid. to redeem, buy off, to secure for one’s self or one’s own use; to rescue from loss or misapplication, Eph. 5:16; Col. 4:5*
G1805.abbottSmith: to redeem, ransom (esp. of slaves): metaph., Ga 3:13 4:5 .
G1805.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G59 (ἀγοράζω); to buy up, i.e. ransom; figuratively, to rescue from loss (improve opportunity)

G2859.thayer: (a) sing. and plur: bosom; (sinus) the overhanging fold of the garment used as a pocket, (b) a bay, gulf.
G2859.mounce: the bosom, Lk. 16:22, 23; Jn. 1:18; 13:23; the bosom of a garment, Lk. 6:38; a bay, creek, inlet, Acts 27:39*
G2859.abbottSmith: prop., the bosom : Jo 13:23 ; fig., of close association, ἐν ( εἰς τὸν ) τοῖς κ. Ἀβραάμ , Lk 16:22 , 23 (cf. IV Mac 13:16 ); ὁ ὢν εἰς τὸν κ. τ. πατρός , Jo 1:18 .
G2859.strongs: apparently a primary word; the bosom; by analogy, a bay

G2509.strongs: from G2505 (καθά) and G4007 (περ); exactly as

G3538.thayer: I wash; mid. I wash my own (hands, etc.).
G3538.mounce: to wash; spc. to wash some part of the person, as distinguished from λούω, Mt. 6:17; Jn. 13:8
G3538.abbottSmith: to wash , usually of a part of the body: c. acc. pers., Jo 13:8 ; τ. πόδας , Jo 13:5 , 6 , 8 , 12 , 14 , I Ti 5:10 ; mid., reflexive, to wash oneself : Jo 9:7 , 11 , 15 ; τ. χεῖρας , Mt 15:2 , Mk 7:3 ; τ. πόδας , Jo 13:10 ; τ. πρόσωπον , Mt 6:17 (in cl. Att. prose, used only in compounds; cf. ἀπο-νίπτω ).†
G3538.strongs: to cleanse (especially the hands or the feet or the face); ceremonially, to perform ablution

G5433.thayer: I am wanton, insolent; I roar, rage.
G5433.mounce: pr. to snort, neigh, stamp, etc.; as a high-spirited horse; hence, to be noisy, fierce, insolent, and tumultuous, to rage, tumultuate, Acts 4:25*
G5433.abbottSmith: prop., of horses, to neigh, whinny and prance (Plut., al.). Metaph., to be wanton, insolent : Ac 4:25 (LXX) .†
G5433.strongs: akin to G1032 (βρύω), G1031 (βρύχω); to snort (as a spirited horse), i.e. (figuratively) to make a tumult

G2923.mounce: a judge, Mt. 5:25; from the Hebrew, a magistrate, ruler, Acts 13:20; 24:10
G2923.abbottSmith: a judge : Mt 5:25 , Lk 12:14 , 58 18:2 ; τ. ἀδικίας , Lk 18:6 ; c. gen. rei (obj.), Ac 18:15 , Ja 4:11 ; (qual.), διαλογισμῶν πονηρῶν , Ja 2:4 ; of a Roman procurator, Ac 24:10 ; of God, He 12:23 , Ja 4:12 ; Christ, Ac 10:42 , II Ti 4:8 , Ja 5:9 ; of those whose conduct is made a standard for judging, Mt 12:27 , Lk 11:19 ; in the OT sense ( Jg 2:16 , Ru 1:1 , al), of a ruler in Israel, Ac 13:20 .†
G2923.strongs: from G2919 (κρίνω); a judge (genitive case or specially)

G3619.thayer: (a) the act of building, (b) a building, (c) met: spiritual advancement, edification.
G3619.mounce: pr. the act of building; a building, structure, Mt. 24:1; in NT a spiritual structure, as instanced in the Christian body, 1 Cor. 3:9; Eph. 2:21; religious advancement, edification, Rom. 14:19; 1 Cor. 14:3
G3619.abbottSmith: the act of building ; in NT always metaph., building up , edifying : Ro 14:19 15:2 , I Co 14:26 , II Co 10:8 13:10 , Eph 4:29 ; c. gen. obj., I Co 14:12 , II Co 12:19 , Eph 4:12 , 16 ; λαλεῖν , λαβεῖν , οἰ. , I Co 14:3 .
G3619.strongs: feminine (abstract) of a compound of G3624 (οἶκος) and the base of G1430 (δῶμα); architecture, i.e. (concretely) a structure; figuratively, confirmation

G2066.mounce: also spelled ἔσθησις, a robe, vestment, raiment, garment, Lk. 23:11; 24:4; Acts 1:10; 10:30; 12:21; Jas. 2:2, 3*

G1804.mounce: to tell forth, divulge, publish; to declare abroad, celebrate, 1 Pet. 2:9, shorter ending of Mark*
G1804.abbottSmith: to tell out, proclaim : I Pe 2:9 [ Mk 16:1-20 , "shorter conclusion"] (Cremer, 29).†
G1804.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to publish, i.e. celebrate

G4974.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative probably of the same as (a ball, "sphere"; compare the feminine , a hammer); the ankle (as globular)

G2158.thayer: (a) comely, seemly, decorous, (b) of honorable position (in society), wealthy, influential.
G2158.mounce: of good appearance, pleasing to look upon, comely, 1 Cor. 12:24; met. becoming, decent, τὸ εὔσχημον, decorum, propriety, 1 Cor. 7:35; honorable, reputable, of high standing and influence, Mk. 15:43; Acts 13:50; 17:12*
G2158.abbottSmith: elegant, graceful, comely (Eur., Plat., al.): τὰ εὐ. ἡμῶν (opp. to τὰ ἀσχ- ἡμ- ), I Co 12:24 ; in moral sense, seemly, becoming , I Co 7:35 .
G2158.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G4976 (σχῆμα); well-formed, i.e. (figuratively) decorous, noble (in rank)

G2480.thayer: I have strength, am strong, am in full health and vigor, am able; meton: I prevail.
G2480.mounce: to be strong, be well, be in good health, Mt. 9:12; to have power, be able, Mt. 8:28; 26:40; to have power or efficiency, avail, be valid, Gal. 5:6; Heb. 9:17; to be of service, be serviceable, Mt. 5:13; meton. to prevail, Acts 19:16; Rev. 12:8
G2480.abbottSmith: to be strong in body: Mt 9:12 , Mk 2:17 .
G2480.strongs: from G2479 (ἰσχύς); to have (or exercise) force (literally or figuratively)
G2480.kjv: be able, avail, can do(-not), could, be good, might, prevail, be of strength, be whole, + much work

G2652.mounce: an execration, curse; by meton. what is worthy of cursing or condemnation, Rev. 22:3*
G2652.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) (intensive) and G331 (ἀνάθεμα); an imprecation

G4377.thayer: I call to, summon; I call (out) to, address, give a speech to, harangue.
G4377.mounce: to speak to, address, Mt. 11:16; Lk. 7:32; 13:12; to address, harangue, Acts 22:2; to call to one’s self, Lk. 6:13
G4377.abbottSmith: to address , call to ; in cl., c. acc. pers., c. dupl. acc. (cf. II Mac 15:15 ); absol. (Hom., Od. , v, 159, al.): Ac 21:40 . In late writers, c. dat. pers.: Mt 11:16 , Lk 7:32 13:12 23:20 , Ac 22:2 .
G4377.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G5455 (φωνέω); to sound towards, i.e. address, exclaim, summon

G3174.abbottSmith: greatness : Eph 1:19 (cf. MM, xvi, s.v. μέγας ).†

G5575.abbottSmith: a false witness : Mt 26:60 ; c. gen. obj. (v. WM, § 30, 1 a ), I Co 15:15 .†
G5575.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and a kindred form of G3144 (μάρτυς); a spurious witness, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony

G3884.thayer: I deceive, beguile, reason falsely, mislead.
G3884.mounce: to misreckon, make a false reckoning; to impose upon, deceive, delude, circumvent, Col. 2:4; Jas. 1:22*
G3884.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3049 (λογίζομαι); to misreckon, i.e. delude

G1453.thayer: (a) I wake, arouse, (b) I raise up.
G1453.mounce: to excite, arouse, awaken, Mt. 8:25; mid. to awake, Mt. 2:13, 20, 21; met. mid. to rouse one’s self to a better course of conduct, Rom. 13:11; Eph. 5:14; to raise from the dead, Jn. 12:1; and mid. to rise from the dead, Mt. 27:52; Jn. 5:21; met. to raise as it were from the dead, 2 Cor. 4:14; to raise up, cause to rise up from a prone posture, Acts 3:7; and mid. to rise up, Mt. 17:7; to restore to health, Jas. 5:15; met. et seq. ἐπή, to excite to war; mid. to rise up against, Mt. 24:7; to raise up again, rebuild, Jn. 2:19, 20; to raise up from a lower place, to draw up or out of a ditch, Mt. 12:10; from Hebrew, to raise up, to cause to arise or exist, Acts 13:22, 23; mid. to arise, exist, appear, Mt. 3:9; 11:11
G1453.abbottSmith: to awaken, arouse from sleep : Mk 4:38 , Ac 12:7 ; metaph., of spiritual awakening, Ro 13:11 (pass.), Eph 5:14 ; pass., to be aroused, wake up : Mt 25:7 , Mk 4:27 ; ἀπὸ τ. ὕπνου , Mt 1:24 . Freq. in NT, to raise from the dead: νεκρούς , Jo 5:21 , Ac 26:8 , II Co 1:9 ; ἐκ νεκρῶν , Jo 12:1 , Ac 3:15 , Ro 8:11 , al.; pass., rise from death: Mt 11:5 , Lk 7:22 , Jo 2:22 , Ro 6:9 , al.; ἀπὸ τ. νεκρῶν ,
G1453.strongs: probably akin to the base of G58 (ἀγορά) (through the idea of collecting one's faculties); to waken (transitively or intransitively), i.e. rouse (literally, from sleep, from sitting or lying, from disease, from death; or figuratively, from obscurity, inactivity, ruins, nonexistence)
G1453.kjv: awake, lift (up), raise (again, up), rear up, (a-)rise (again, up), stand, take up

G4623.thayer: I keep silence, am silent, either voluntarily or involuntarily.
G4623.mounce: to be silent, keep silence, hold one’s peace, Mt. 20:31; 26:63; Mk. 3:4; 9:34; 10:48; 14:61; Lk. 19:40; Acts 18:9; σιωπῶν, silent, dumb, Lk. 1:20; met. to be silent, still, hushed, calm, as the sea, Mk. 4:39*
G4623.abbottSmith: to be silent or still, keep silence : Mt 20:31 26:63 , Mk 3:4 9:34 10:48 14:61 , Lk 19:4 , Ac 18:9 ; of one dumb, Lk 1:20 (cf. IV Mac 10:18 ); addressed rhetorically to the sea, σιώπα , Mk 4:39 .†
G4623.strongs: from (silence, i.e. a hush; properly, muteness, i.e. involuntary stillness, or inability to speak; and thus differing from G4602 (σιγή), which is rather a voluntary refusal or indisposition to speak, although the terms are often used synonymously); to be dumb (but not deaf also, like 2974 properly); figuratively, to be calm (as quiet water)

G1810.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G160 (αἰφνίδιος); of a sudden (unexpectedly)

G4544.thayer: Samos, an island in the Aegean sea off the coast of Asia Minor, near Ephesus and Miletus.
G4544.mounce: Samos, a celebrated island, in the Aegean sea, Acts 20:15*
G4544.abbottSmith: Samos , an island in the Ægean: Ac 20:15 .†
G4544.strongs: of uncertain affinity; Samus, an island of the Mediterranean

G4734.strongs: probably contraction for (crowned; from G4737 (στεφανόω)); Stephanas, a Christian

G1267.mounce: division; met. in NT disunion, dissension, Lk. 12:51*
G1267.abbottSmith: a division : opp. to εἰρήνη , Lk 12:51 .†
G1267.strongs: from G1266 (διαμερίζω); disunion (of opinion and conduct)

G959.thayer: Bernice, daughter of Agrippa I and Kypros, and sister of M. Julius Agrippa II.
G959.abbottSmith: Bernice , Berenice , dau. of Herod Agrippa I: Ac 25:13 , 23 26:30 .†
G959.strongs: from a provincial form of G5342 (φέρω) and G3529 (νίκη); victorious; Bernice, a member of the Herodian family

G5393.strongs: active participle of the base of G5395 (φλόξ); blazing; Phlegon, a Christian

G2981.thayer: (in classical Greek: babble, chattering) speech, talk; manner of speech, dialect.
G2981.mounce: talk, speech; in NT matter of discourse, Jn. 4:42; 8:43; language, dialect, Mt. 26:73*

G2546.thayer: and there, and yonder, there also.
G2546.mounce: crasis, and there, Mt. 5:23; 10:11; there also, Acts 17:13
G2546.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1563 (ἐκεῖ); likewise in that place

G4033.abbottSmith: to encircle , encompass : of a besieged city, Lk 19:43 .†
G4033.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2944 (κυκλόω); to encircle all around, i.e. blockade completely

G5574.abbottSmith: absol., to lie : He 6:18 , I Jo 1:6 , Re 3:9 ; οὐ ψεύδομαι , Ro 9:1 , II Co 11:31 , Ga 1:20 , I Ti 2:7 c. dat. pers. ( Ps 17 (18):45 , Je 5:12 , al.), Ac 5:4 ; seq. εἰς , Col 3:9 ; κατά , Ja 3:14 (Hort, in l.).
G5574.strongs: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to utter an untruth or attempt to deceive by falsehood

G1590.thayer: I loose, release, unloose (as a bow-string), relax, enfeeble; pass: I am faint, grow weary.
G1590.mounce: to be weary, exhausted, faint, Mt. 15:32; Mk. 8:3; Gal. 6:9; to lose courage, to faint, Heb. 12:3, 5*
G1590.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3089 (λύω); to relax (literally or figuratively)

G2507.thayer: (a) I take down, pull down, depose, destroy.
G2507.mounce: take or bring down, Mk. 15:36, 46; Lk. 1:52; 23:53; Acts 13:29; tear down, destroy, Lk. 12:18; Acts 13:19; 19:27; 2 Cor. 10:4*
G2507.abbottSmith: to take down : c. acc. pers. (the technical term for removal after crucifixion, Field, Notes , 44), Mk 15:36 , 46 , Lk 23:53 , Ac 13:29 .
G2507.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G138 (αἱρέομαι) (including its alternate); to lower (or with violence) demolish (literally or figuratively)

G5526.mounce: pr. to feed or fill with grass, herbage, etc., to fatten; used of animals of prey, to satiate, gorge, Rev. 19:21; of persons, to satisfy with food, Mt. 14:20; 15:33, 37; met. to satisfy the desire of any one, Mt. 5:6
G5526.abbottSmith: prop., of animals (v. Lft. on Phl 4:12 ), to feed, fatten : Re 19:21 ;
G5526.strongs: from G5528 (χόρτος); to fodder, i.e. (generally) to gorge (supply food in abundance)

G2945.mounce: from κύκλος, functions in the NT only as an improper prep., a circle; in NT κύκλῳ functions adverbially, round, round about, around, Mk. 3:34; 6:6, 36
G2945.abbottSmith: a ring , circle . Dat., κύκλῳ , as adverb., round about , around : Mk 3:34 6:6 , 36 , Lk 9:12 , Ro 15:19 ; c. gen., Re 4:6 5:11 7:11 .†
G2945.strongs: as if dative case of (a ring, "cycle"; akin to G2947 (κυλιόω)); i.e. in a circle (by implication, of G1722 (ἐν)), i.e. (adverbially) all around

G1883.thayer: (a) adv: on the top, above, (b) prep: on the top of, above, over, on, above, more than, superior to.
G1883.mounce: to come in upon, invade, surprise, Lk. 21:35*
G1883.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G507 (ἄνω); up above, i.e. over or on (of place, amount, rank, etc.)

G617.mounce: to roll away, Mt. 28:2; Mk. 16:3, 4; Lk. 24:2*
G617.abbottSmith: to roll away : Mt 28:2 , Mk 16:3 , Lk 24:2 .†
G617.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2947 (κυλιόω); to roll away

G2663.thayer: (in the Old Testament of the rest attained by the settlement in Canaan), resting, rest, dwelling, habitation.
G2663.mounce: pr. the act of giving rest; a state of settled or final rest, Heb. 3:11, 18; 4:1, 3, 4, 5, 11; a place of rest, place of abode, dwelling, habitation, Acts 7:49*
G2663.abbottSmith: in cl., a putting to rest , causing to cease .
G2663.strongs: from G2664 (καταπαύω); reposing down, i.e. (by Hebraism) abode

G372.mounce: rest, intermission, Mt. 11:29; Rev. 4:8; 14:11; meton. place of rest, fixed habitation, Mt. 12:43; Lk. 11:24*
G372.abbottSmith: cessation , rest , refreshment : Mt 11:26 12:43 , Lk 11:24 , Re 4:8 14:11 .†
G372.strongs: from G373 (ἀναπαύω); intermission; by implication, recreation

G599.thayer: I am dying, am about to die, wither, decay.
G599.mounce: to die, Mt. 8:32; to decay, rot, as seeds, Jn. 12:24; 1 Cor. 15:36; to wither, become dry, as a tree, Jude 12; met. to die the death of final condemnation and misery, Jn. 6:50; 8:21, 24; to die to a thing by renunciation or utter separation, Rom. 6:2; 1 Cor. 15:31; Gal. 2;19; Col. 3:3
G599.abbottSmith: to die : of natural death, Mk 5:35 , al.; of violent death (pass. of ἀποκτείνω ), esp. of Christ, Mt 26:35 , Jo 12:33 , He 10:28 , al.; of spiritual death, Jo 6:50 , Ro 8:13 , al.; c. dat. ref., Ro 6:2 , 10 14:7 , 8 , Ga 2:19 ; acc., ὅ , Ro 6:10 ; seq. ἐν , Mt 8:32 , Jo 8:21 , 24 , I Co 15:22 , He 11:37 , Re 14:13 ; seq. ὑπέρ , περί , Jo 11:50 , 51 18:14 , Ro 5:6-8 14:15 , I Co 15:3 , II Co 5:15 ,
G599.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2348 (θνήσκω); to die off (literally or figuratively)
G599.kjv: be dead, death, die, lie a-dying, be slain (X with)

G2064.mounce: to come, to go, to pass. By the combination of this verb with other terms, a variety of meaning results, which, however, is due, not to a change of meaning in the verb, but to the adjuncts. Ὁ ἐρχόμενος, He who is coming, the expected Messiah, Mt. 11:3
G2064.abbottSmith: to come ; of persons, either as arriving or returning from elsewhere: Mt 8:9 , Mk 6:31 , Lk 7:8 , Jo 4:27 , Ro 9:9 , al.; seq. ἀπό , Mk 5:35 7:1 , Jo 3:2 , al.; ἐκ , Lk 5:17 , Jo 3:31 , al.; εἰς , Mk 1:29 , al.; διά seq. εἰς , Mk 7:31 ; ἐν (Cremer, 263 f., but v.s. ἐν ), Ro 15:29 , I Co 4:21 ; ἐπί , c. acc., Mk 6:53 11:13 , Jo 19:33 , al.; κατά , c. acc., Lk 10:33 Ac 16:7 ; παρά , c. gen., Lk 8:49
G2064.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb (used only in the present and imperfect tenses, the others being supplied by a kindred (middle voice) , or (active) , which do not otherwise occur); to come or go (in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively)
G2064.kjv: accompany, appear, bring, come, enter, fall out, go, grow, X light, X next, pass, resort, be set

G1841.thayer: (a) an exit, going out, departure from a place; the exodus, (b) death.
G1841.mounce: a way out, a going out; a going out, departure, the exodus, Heb. 11:22; met. a departure from life, decease, death, Lk. 9:31; 2 Pet. 1:15*
G1841.abbottSmith: a going out, departure : He 11:22 ; of death, Lk 9:31 , II Pe 1:15 .†
G1841.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3598 (ὁδός); an exit, i.e. (figuratively) death

G1415.thayer: (a) of persons: powerful, able, (b) of things: possible.
G1415.mounce: able, having power, powerful, mighty; δυνατὸς εἶναι, to be able, i.q. δύνασθαι, Lk. 14:31; Acts 11:17; ὁ δυνατός, the Mighty One, God, Lk. 1:49; τὸ δυνατόν, power, i.q. δύναμις, Rom. 9:22; valid, powerful, efficacious, 2 Cor. 10:4; distinguished for rank, authority, or influence, Acts 25:5; 1 Cor. 1:26; distinguished for skill or excellence, Lk. 24:19; Acts 7:22; Rom. 15:1; δυνατόν and δυνατά, possible, capable of being done, Mt. 19:26; 24:24 See ability; mighty; possible.
G1415.abbottSmith: strong, mighty, powerful : absol., Lk 1:49 , 1 Col 1:26 ; οἱ δ. , the chief men, Ac 25:5 ; of spiritual strength, Ro 15:1 , II Co 12:10 13:9 ; seq. ἐν , Lk 24:19 , Ac 7:22 18:24 ; πρός , II Co 10:4 .
G1415.strongs: from G1410 (δύναμαι); powerful or capable (literally or figuratively); neuter possible
G1415.kjv: able, could, (that is) mighty (man), possible, power, strong

G2758.thayer: (a) I empty, (b) I deprive of content, make unreal.
G2758.mounce: to empty, evacuate; ἑαυτόν, to divest one’s self of one’s prerogatives, abase one’s self, Phil. 2:7; to deprive a thing of its proper functions, Rom. 4:14; 1 Cor. 1:17; to show to be without foundation, falsify, 1 Cor. 9:15; 2 Cor. 9:3*
G2758.abbottSmith: to empty . Metaph., to empty , make empty , vain or of no effect : καύχημα , I Co 9:15 ; ἑαυτόν , of Christ, Phl 2:7 (v Lft.; ICC , in l, and esp. Gifford, Incarn. , 54 ff.); pass., πίστις , Ro 4:14 ; ὁ σταυρός , I Co 1:17 ; καύχημα , II Co 9:3 .†
G2758.strongs: from G2756 (κενός); to make empty, i.e. (figuratively) to abase, neutralize, falsify
G2758.kjv: make (of none effect, of no reputation, void), be in vain

G1932.mounce: also spelled ἐπιεικία, reasonableness, equity; in NT gentleness, mildness, 2 Cor. 10:1; clemency, Acts 24:4*
G1932.abbottSmith: fairness, moderation, gentleness (" sweet reasonableness ," Matthew Arnold): Ac 24:4 ; c. πραΰτης , II Co 10:1 .†
G1932.strongs: from G1933 (ἐπιεικής); suitableness, i.e. (by implication) equity, mildness

G567.thayer: I abstain from, keep myself away from.
G567.mounce: trans. to have in full what is due or is sought, Mt. 6:2, 5, 16; Lk. 6:24; Phil. 4:18; to have altogether, Phlm. 15; hence, it is enough, Mk. 14:41; intrans. to be distant, Lk. 7:6; to be estranged, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6, mid. to abstain from, Acts 14:20
G567.strongs: middle voice (reflexively) of G568 (ἀπέχω); to hold oneself off, i.e. refrain

G3936.thayer: I bring, present, prove, come up to and stand by, am present.
G3936.mounce: pluperfect, παρειστήκειν, also formed as παριστάνω, trans. to place beside; to have in readiness, provide, Acts 23:24; to range beside, to place at the disposal of, Mt. 26:53; Acts 9:41; to present to God, dedicate, consecrate, devote, Lk. 2:22; Rom. 6:13, 19; to prove, demonstrate, show, Acts 1:3; 24:13; to commend, recommend, 1 Cor. 8:8; intrans. perf. παρέστηκα, part. παρεστώς, pluperf. παρειστήκειν, 2 aor. παρέστην, and mid., to stand by or before, Acts 27:24; Rom. 14:10; to stand by, to be present, Mk. 14:47, 69, 70; to stand in attendance, attend, Lk. 1:19; 1:24; of time, to be present, have come, Mk. 4:29; to stand by in aid, assist, support, Rom. 16:2
G3936.abbottSmith: Trans. verb. in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor.; to place beside , present , provide : c. acc., Ac 9:41 23:24 , II Co 4:14 , Col 1:28 ; c. acc. et dat., Mt 26:53 , Lk 2:22 , Ac 1:3 23:33 , I Co 8:8 ; seq. acc. pred., Ro 6:13 , 16 (late pres., παριστάνετε ), Ro 6:19 12:1 , II Co 11:2 , Eph 5:27 , Col 1:22 , II Ti 2:15 . to present to the mind (cl.): by argument, to prove (Xen., FlJ, al.), c. acc., Ac 24
G3936.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand beside, i.e. (transitively) to exhibit, proffer, (specially), recommend, (figuratively) substantiate; or (intransitively) to be at hand (or ready), aid
G3936.kjv: assist, bring before, command, commend, give presently, present, prove, provide, shew, stand (before, by, here, up, with), yield

G1413.thayer: (lit: a man who rules by force), a ruler, potentate; also: courtier, member of the court.
G1413.mounce: a potentate, sovereign; prince, Lk. 1:52; 1 Tim. 6:15; a person of rank and authority, Acts 8:27*
G1413.abbottSmith: a prince, ruler, potentate : Lk 1:52 ; of God ( Si 46:5 , 16 , II Mac 15:3 ), I Ti 6:15 ; of a high official (cf. δυνάσται Φαραώ , Ge 50:4 ), Ac 8:27 (Cremer, 221).†
G1413.strongs: from G1410 (δύναμαι); a ruler or officer

G4510.thayer: I am filthy; hence (morally): I am stained with sin.
G4510.abbottSmith: to make filthy : Re 22:11 Rec. (AV, tr. as = ῥυπάω , to be filthy ).†
G4510.strongs: from G4509 (ῥύπος); to soil, i.e. (intransitively) to become dirty (morally)

G592.thayer: I make a logical distinction, make an invidious distinction.
G592.mounce: pr. to separate by intervening boundaries; to separate or divide, Jude 19*
G592.abbottSmith: to mark off , hence metaph. to make separations : Ju 19 (Cremer, 806).†
G592.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a compound of G223 (Ἀλέξανδρος) and G3724 (ὁρίζω); to disjoin (by a boundary, figuratively, a party)

G2382.mounce: a breast-plate, armor for the body, consisting of two parts, one covering the breast and the other the back, Eph. 6:14; 1 Thess. 5:8; Rev. 9:9, 17*
G2382.abbottSmith: a breastplate : Re 9:9 , 17 ; θ. τ. δικαιοσύνης , Eph 6:14 (cf. Is 59:17 , Wi 5:18 ( 19 )); θ. πίστεως , I Th 5:8 .†
G2382.strongs: of uncertain affinity; the chest ("thorax"), i.e. (by implication) a corslet

G2274.thayer: I am defeated, am overcome, am made inferior.
G2274.mounce: to be less, inferior to; to fare worse; by impl. to be overcome, vanquished, 2 Pet. 2:19, 20*
G2274.abbottSmith: to be inferior : seq. ὑπέρ , II Co 12:13 .
G2274.strongs: from the same as G2276 (ἥττον); to make worse, i.e. vanquish (literally or figuratively); by implication, to rate lower

G971.thayer: (a) mid: I use force, force my way, come forward violently, (b) pass: I am forcibly treated, suffer violence.
G971.mounce: also written as a middle deponent, βιάζομαι, to urge, constrain, overpower by force; to press earnestly forward, to rush, Lk. 16:16; pass. to be an object of a forceful movement, Mt. 11:12*
G971.abbottSmith: to force , constrain , rare in act. (poet, and late prose), but found in cl. in pass., and so perh. βιάζεται , suffereth violence , Mt 11:12 , EV (but v. infr.), whether in good sense, of disciples (Thayer, al.), or in bad sense, of the enemies of the kingdom (Meyer, in l.; Dalman, Words , 139 ff.; Cremer, 141 ff.). Mid., advanceth violently , Mt 11:12 (Deiss., BS ., 258; Banks, v. ref. in DCG , i
G971.strongs: from G979 (βίος); to force, i.e. (reflexively) to crowd oneself (into), or (passively) to be seized

G2421.thayer: (Hebrew), Jesse, son of Obed, and father of King David.
G2421.mounce: Jesse, father of David (1 Sam. 16), pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:5f.; Lk. 3:32; Acts 13:22; Rom. 15:12*
G2421.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03448); Jessæ (i.e. Jishai), an Israelite

G126.abbottSmith: everlasting (freq. in Inscr.; MM, VGT , s.v.): Ro 1:20 , Ju 6 .†
G126.strongs: from G104 (ἀεί); everduring (forward and backward, or forward only)

G739.mounce: entirely suited; complete in accomplishment, ready, 2 Tim. 3:17*
G739.strongs: from G737 (ἄρτι); fresh, i.e. (by implication) complete

G1757.mounce: to bless in respect of, or by means of, Acts 3:25; Gal. 3:8*
G1757.abbottSmith: to bless : pass., seq. ἐν , Ac 3:25 (LXX) , Ga 3:8 (LXX) (Cremer, 770).†
G1757.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2127 (εὐλογέω); to confer a benefit on

G3437.mounce: a complaint, cause or ground of complaint, Col. 3:13*
G3437.strongs: from G3201 (μέμφομαι); blame, i.e. (by implication), a fault

G3615.thayer: one of a family, whether child or servant.
G3615.mounce: belonging to a house; pl. the members of a household or family, kindred, Mt. 10:25, 36*
G3615.abbottSmith: belonging to the household , one's own : Mt 10:36 ; opp. to οἰκοδεσπότης , Mt 10:25 .†
G3615.strongs: from G3614 (οἰκία); familiar, i.e. (as noun) relatives

G3530.thayer: Nicodemus, a member of the Sanhedrin.
G3530.strongs: from G3534 (νῖκος) and G1218 (δῆμος); victorious among his people; Nicodemus, an Israelite

G3367.mounce: not one, none, no one, Mt. 8:4
G3367.abbottSmith: no , none , no one ; neut., nothing : Mt 16:20 , Mk 5:43 6:8 , Lk 3:14 , Ac 8:24 , Ro 13:8 , al.; c. gen., Ac 4:17 24:23 ; neut. acc., μηδέν , adverbially, in no respect , Ac 10:20 11:12 ; as acc. obj. after verb, βλάπτειν , Lk 4:35 ; ὠφελεῖσθαι , Mk 5:26 ; ὑστερεῖν , II Co 11:5 ; μεριμνᾶν , Phl 4:6 ; in double negation, strengthening the denial, μηκέτι μ. , Mk 11:14 , Ac 4:17 ; μὴ . . . μηδέν ( μ
G3367.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G1520 (εἷς); not even one (man, woman, thing)
G3367.kjv: any (man, thing), no (man), none, not (at all, any man, a whit), nothing, + without delay

G5238.mounce: in over abundance; beyond all measure, superabundantly, Eph. 3:20; 1 Thess. 3:10; 5:13*
G5238.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the neuter plural of G1565 (ἐκεῖνος); above those parts, i.e. still farther

G1181.mounce: tenth, Jn. 1:39; Rev. 11:13; 21:20; δεκάτη, i.e. μερίς, a tenth part, tithe, Heb. 7:2, 4, 8, 9*
G1181.abbottSmith: a tenth part, a tithe : He 7:2 , 4 , 8 , 9 (for a curious inversion of the Biblical use, v. MM, s.v.).†
G1181.strongs: feminine of G1182 (δέκατος); a tenth, i.e. as a percentage or (technically) tithe

G3043.mounce: flax; by meton. a flaxen wick, Mt. 12:20; linen, Rev. 15:6*
G3043.strongs: probably a primary word; flax, i.e. (by implication) "linen"

G53.thayer: (originally, in a condition prepared for worship), pure (either ethically, or ritually, ceremonially), chaste.
G53.abbottSmith: free from ceremonial defilement , in a condition prepared for worship (for exx. of pagan usage, V. MM, VGT , s.v.). holy , sacred , venerable ( II Mac 13:8 ). As in OT (cl.), pure , chaste , undefiled , guiltless ; of persons: II Co 7:11 11:12 , I Ti 5:22 , Tit 2:5 , I Jo 3:3 ; of things: Phl 4:8 , Ja 3:17 , I Pe 3:2 .†
G53.strongs: from the same as G40 (ἅγιος); properly, clean, i.e. (figuratively) innocent, modest, perfect

G5435.thayer: Phrygia, an ethnic district in Asia Minor, the north-western part of which was in the Roman province Asia and the south-eastern part in the Roman province Galatia.
G5435.mounce: Phrygia, an inland province of Asia Minor, Acts 2:10; 16:6; 18:23; 1 Tim. subscr.*
G5435.abbottSmith: Phrygia , a region of Asia Minor: Ac 2:10 ; Φ. καὶ Γαλατικὴ χώρα ( Γ. χ. κ. Φ. ), Ac 16:6 18:23 (on these phrases v. DB , i, 89 f.; CGT , Gal. , xxii f.).†
G5435.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Phrygia, a region of Asia Minor

G4179.abbottSmith: = πολλαπλάσιος , many times more : Mt 19:29 , Lk 18:30 .†
G4179.strongs: from G4183 (πολύς) and probably a derivative of G4120 (πλέκω); manifold, i.e. (neuter as noun) very much more

G2982.abbottSmith: why : Mt 27:46 (WH, mg., λεμά TTr., WH, txt., λεμά L), Mk 15:34 ( λεμά LT, λαμμᾶ , Rec.).†
G2982.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04100 with prepositional prefix); lama (i.e. why)

G3344.abbottSmith: to turn about , turn , change : pass., Ac 2:20 (LXX) , Ja 4:9 (WH, txt., μετατρέπω , q.v.); in evil sense, to pervert , corrupt (cf. primary sense reverse ): Ga 1:7 .†
G3344.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G4762 (στρέφω); to turn across, i.e. transmute or (figuratively) corrupt

G975.mounce: a written volume or roll, book, Lk. 4:17, 20; a scroll, bill, billet, Mt. 19:7; Mk. 10:4
G975.abbottSmith: a paper , letter , written document : β. ἀποστασίου , bill of divorce , Mt 19:7 , Mk 10:4 .
G975.strongs: a diminutive of G976 (βίβλος); a roll

G3814.thayer: a female slave, maidservant, maid, young girl.
G3814.mounce: a girl, damsel, maiden; a female slave or servant, Mt. 26:69; Mk. 14:66, 69
G3814.abbottSmith: a young girl , a maiden ( נַעֲרָה , Ru 4:12 ).
G3814.strongs: feminine diminutive of G3816 (παῖς); a girl, i.e. (specially), a female slave or servant

G3819.thayer: of old, long ago, in times past, former.
G3819.mounce: of old, long ago, Mt. 11:21; Lk. 10:13; Heb. 1:1; Jude 4; οἱ πάλαι, old, former, 2 Pet. 1:9; some time since, already, Mk. 15:44
G3819.abbottSmith: long ago , of old , in time past (denoting past time absolutely, as πρότερον relatively): Mt 11:21 , Lk 10:13 , He 1:1 , Ju 4 ; as adj., c. art., II Pe 1:9 ; of time just past, Mk 6:47 (WH, mg.), 15:44 (WH, mg., R, txt.); c. durat. praes. (RV, all this time ), II Co 12:19 .†
G3819.strongs: probably another form for G3825 (πάλιν) (through the idea of retrocession); (adverbially) formerly, or (by relatively) sometime since; (elliptically as adjective) ancient
G3819.kjv: any while, a great while ago, (of) old, in time past

G5121.thayer: on the contrary, on the other hand.
G5121.abbottSmith: by crasis for τὸ ἐνανίον , neut. acc, used adverbially, on the contrary, contrariwise : II Co 2:7 , Ga 2:7 , I Pe 3:9 .†
G5121.strongs: contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G1726 (ἐναντίον); on the contrary

G2908.mounce: can also be spelled κρείσσων, better, more useful or profitable, more conducive to good, 1 Cor. 7:8, 38; superior, more excellent, of a higher nature, more valuable, Heb. 1:4; 6:9; 7:7, 19, 22
G2908.strongs: neuter of an alternate form of G2909 (κρείττων); (as noun) better, i.e. greater advantage

G3953.thayer: prop: a dainty side-dish; meton: a plate, dish, platter.
G3953.mounce: pr. a dainty side dish; meton. a plate, platter, Mt. 23:25*
G3953.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the base of G3795 (ὀψάριον); a side-dish (the receptacle)

G4174.thayer: (a) commonwealth, polity; citizen body, (b) (the Roman) citizenship, citizen-rights, franchise.
G4174.mounce: the state of being a citizen; citizenship, the right or privilege of being a citizen, freedom of a city or state, Acts 22:28; a commonwealth, community, Eph. 2:12*
G4174.abbottSmith: citizenship : Ac 22:28 ( III Mac 3:21 , 23 , Hdt., Xen., a1.).
G4174.strongs: from G4177 (πολίτης) ("polity"); citizenship; concretely, a community

G4680.mounce: wise generally, 1 Cor. 1:25; shrewd, clever, Rom. 16:19; 1 Cor. 3:10; 6:5; learned, intelligent, Mt. 11:25; Rom. 1:14, 22; 1 Cor. 1:19, 20, 26, 27; 3:18; in NT divinely instructed, Mt. 23:34; furnished with Christian wisdom, spiritually enlightened, Jas. 3:13; all wise, Rom. 16:27; 1 Tim. 1:17; Jude 25
G4680.abbottSmith: skilled, clever, wise , whether in handicraft, the affairs of life, the sciences or learning: Ro 16:19 , I Co 3:10 ; of the learned, Ro 1:14 , 22 , I Co 1:19, 20 , 26, 27 3:18-20 ; of Jewish teachers, Mt 11:25 , Lk 10:21 ; Christian, Mt 23:34 ; of those en­dowed with practical wisdom, I Co 6:5 , Eph 5:15 , Ja 3:13 ; of God, Ro 16:27 ; compar., τ. μωρὸν τ. θεοῦ σοφώτερον , I Co 1:25 .†
G4680.strongs: akin to (clear); wise (in a most general application)

G3162.mounce: a large knife, dagger; a sword, Mt. 26:47, 51; the sword of the executioner, Acts 12:2; Rom. 8:35; Heb. 11:37; hence, φορεῖν μάχαιραν, to bear the sword, to have the power of life and death, Rom. 13:4; meton. war, Mt. 10:34
G3162.abbottSmith: (in Hom., al.) a large knife or dirk , for sacrificial purposes ( Ge 22:6 , 10 , Jg 19:29 A).
G3162.strongs: probably feminine of a presumed derivative of G3163 (μάχη); a knife, i.e. dirk; figuratively, war, judicial punishment

G4091.mounce: to fill, Mt. 27:48; pass. to be filled mentally, be under full influence, Lk. 1:15; 4:28; to be fulfilled, Lk. 21:22; of stated time, to be brought to a close, arrive at its close, Lk. 1:23, 57; 2:6, 21, 22
G4091.abbottSmith: Pontius Pilate : Mt 27:2 , Mk 15:1 , Lk 3:1 , Jo 18:29 , Ac 3:13 , I Ti 6:13 , al.
G4091.strongs: of Latin origin; close-pressed, i.e. firm; Pilatus, a Roman

G5193.mounce: made of glass; glassy, translucent, Rev. 4:6; 15:2*

G3083.thayer: the purchasing money for manumitting slaves, a ransom, the price of ransoming; especially the sacrifice by which expiation is effected, an offering of expiation.
G3083.mounce: pr. price paid; a ransom, Mt. 20:28; Mk. 10:45*
G3083.abbottSmith: a ransom (as for a life, Ex 21:30 ; for slaves, Le 19:20 ; for captives, Is 45:13 ): ἀντὶ πολλῶν , Mt 20:28 , Mk 10:45 (v. Swete, in l., and for discussion of λ. and its cognates, Westc., He. , 295 f.; Deiss., LAE , 331 f.; cf. also ἀντί-λύτρον ).†
G3083.strongs: from G3089 (λύω); something to loosen with, i.e. a redemption price (figuratively, atonement)

G996.thayer: (a) abstr: assistance, (b) concr: (a technical term of nautical language), a help.
G996.mounce: help, succor, Heb. 4:16; meton. pl. helps, contrivances for relief and safety, Acts 27:17*
G996.abbottSmith: help : He 4:16 ; pl., helps, "frapping," a technical nautical term (MM, Exp. , x; DB , ext. , 367): Ac 27:17 .†
G996.strongs: from G998 (βοηθός); aid; specially, a rope or chain for frapping a vessel

G844.mounce: self-excited, acting spontaneously, spontaneous, of his own accord, Mk. 4:8; Acts 12:10*
G844.abbottSmith: of persons, acting of one's own will .
G844.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and the same as G3155 (μάτην); self-moved ("automatic"), i.e. spontaneous

G2041.thayer: work, task, employment; a deed, action; that which is wrought or made, a work.
G2041.mounce: anything done or to be done; a deed, work, action, Jn. 3:21; Eph. 2:10; 2 Cor. 9:8, et al. freq.; duty enjoined, office, charge, business, Mk. 13:34; Jn. 4:34, et al. freq.; a process, course of action, Jas. 1:4; a work, product of an action or process, Acts 7:41; Heb. 1:10; substance in effect, Rom. 2:15
G2041.abbottSmith: work, task, employment : Mk 13:34 , Jo 4:34 17:4 , Ac 13:2 , Phl 1:22 2:30 , I Th 5:13 , al.; of an enterprise or undertaking ( De 15:10 , Wi 2:12 ), Ac 5:38 . a deed, action : Tit 1:16 , Ja 1:25 . disting. from λόγος , Lk 24:19 , Ro 15:18 , II Th 2:17 , I Jo 3:18 ; ἐν λόγοις κ. ἔ. , Ac 7:22 ; of acts of God, Jo 9:3 , Ac 13:41 (LXX) , He 4:10 , Re 15:3 ; of Christ, Mt 11:2 ; esp. in Jo, e.g. 5:20
G2041.strongs: from a primary (but obsolete) (to work); toil (as an effort or occupation); by implication, an act

G4724.thayer: I set, arrange; mid: I provide for, take care, withdraw from, hold aloof, avoid.
G4724.mounce: pr. to place in set order, to arrange; to equip; to despatch; to stow; mid. to contract one’s self, to shrink; to withdraw from, avoid, shun, 2 Cor. 8:20; 2 Thess. 3:6*
G4724.abbottSmith: prop., to set, place, arrange, fit out ; hence , mid., to set oneself for, prepare ( Wi , II Mac, ll. c. ).
G4724.strongs: probably strengthened from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); properly, to set fast ("stall"), i.e. (figuratively) to repress (reflexively, abstain from associating with)

G3621.thayer: I am a steward, manage a household.
G3621.mounce: to manage a household; to manage the affairs of any one, be steward, Lk. 16:2*
G3621.abbottSmith: to manage as house-steward , be a steward : absol., Lk 16:2 ; hence, generally (v.s. οἰκονομία ), to manage , regulate , arrange (cl., II Mac 3:14 , III Mac 3:2 R).†
G3621.strongs: from G3623 (οἰκονόμος); to manage (a house, i.e. an estate)

G4266.mounce: to be or happen before, be previously done or committed; προγεγονώς, bygone, previous, Rom. 3:25
G4266.abbottSmith: to happen before : pf. pass. ptcp., Ro 3:25 .†
G4266.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1096 (γίνομαι); to be already, i.e. have previousy transpired

G4425.thayer: a simple wooden pitchfork; a winnowing-shovel or fan.
G4425.mounce: a fan, winnowing fork, Mt. 3:12; Lk. 3:17*
G4425.abbottSmith: a winnowing shovel or fan : Mt 3:12 , Lk 3:17 .†
G4425.strongs: from G4429 (πτύω); a winnowing-fork (as scattering like spittle)

G1153.thayer: a Damascene, an inhabitant of Damascus.
G1153.mounce: A Damascene, a native of Damascus, 2 Cor. 11:32*
G1153.strongs: from G1154 (Δαμασκός); a Damascene or inhabitant of Damascus

G2999.thayer: service rendered to God, perhaps simply: worship.
G2999.mounce: service, servitude; religious service, worship, Jn. 16:2; Rom. 9:4; 12:1; Heb. 9:1, 6*
G2999.strongs: from G3000 (λατρεύω); ministration of God, i.e. worship

G1014.mounce: to be willing, disposed, Mk. 15:15; Acts 25:20; 28:18; to intend, Mt. 1:19; Acts 5:28; 12:4; 2 Cor. 1:15; to desire, 1 Tim. 6:9; to choose, be pleased, Jn. 18:39; Acts 18:15; Jas. 3:4; to will, decree, appoint, Lk. 22:42; Jas. 1:18; 1 Cor. 12:11; 1 Tim. 2:8; 5:14; ἐβουλόμην, I could wish, Acts 25:22
G1014.abbottSmith: to will, wish, desire, purpose, be minded , implying more strongly than θέλω (q.v.), the deliberate exercise of volition (v. Hort on Ja 1:18 ): c. inf. (M, Pr. , 205; Bl., §69, 4), Mk 15:15 , Ac 5:28 , 33 12:4 15:37 17:20 18:15 , 27 19:30 22:30 23:28 27:43 28:18 , II Co 1:15 , I Ti 6:9 , He 6:17 , II Jo 12 , III Jo 10 , Ju 5 ; c. acc., II Co 1:17 ; c. acc. et inf., Phl 1:12 , I Ti 2:8 5:14 , Tit 3
G1014.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to "will," i.e. (reflexively) be willing
G1014.kjv: be disposed, minded, intend, list, (be, of own) will (-ing)

G3551.thayer: usage, custom, law; in NT: of law in general, plur: of divine laws; of a force or influence impelling to action; of the Mosaic law; meton: of the books which contain the law, the Pentateuch, the Old Testament scriptures in general.
G3551.mounce: a law, Rom. 4:15; 1 Tim. 1:9; the Mosaic law, Mt. 5:17, et al. freq.; the Old Testament Scripture, Jn. 10:34; a legal tie, Rom. 7:2, 3; a law, a rule, standard, Rom. 3:27; a rule of life and conduct, Gal. 6:2, Jas. 1:25
G3551.abbottSmith: that which is assigned, hence, usage , custom , then law ; in NT (only in Mt, Jo, Ja, and the Lucan and Pauline bks.); of law in general: Ro 3:27 5:13b ; pl., of divine laws, He 8:10 10:16 ; ὁ ν. τ. Χριστοῦ , Ga 6:2 ; (τ.) ἐλευθερίας , Ja 1:25 2:12 ; βασιλικιός (Hort., in l.; Deiss., LAE , 367 3 ), Ja 2:8 . Of a force or influence impelling to action: Ro 7:21 , 23a , 25 8:2 . Of the Mosaic law: Mt
G3551.strongs: from a primary (to parcel out, especially food or grazing to animals); law (through the idea of prescriptive usage), genitive case (regulation), specially, (of Moses (including the volume); also of the Gospel), or figuratively (a principle)

G5431.thayer: I am thoughtful, careful, give heed.
G5431.mounce: to be considerate, be careful, Tit. 3:8*
G5431.abbottSmith: to give heed, take thought (in cl. usually absol., c acc., c. gen.), c. inf., Tit 3:8 (v. Bl., § 69, 4; M, Pr. , 206 f.).†
G5431.strongs: from a derivative of G5424 (φρήν); to exercise thought, i.e. be anxious

G5494.thayer: a storm, tempest; winter, the rainy season.
G5494.mounce: stormy weather, Mt. 16:3; a storm, tempest, Acts 27:20; winter, Mt. 24:20; Mk. 13:18; Jn. 10:22; 2 Tim. 4:21*
G5494.abbottSmith: winter : Jo 10:22 , II Ti 4:21 ; gen., χειμῶνος , in winter , Mt 24:20 , Mk 13:18 .
G5494.strongs: from a derivative of (to pour; akin to the base of 5490 through the idea of a channel), meaning a storm (as pouring rain); by implication, the rainy season, i.e. winter

G5298.thayer: I withdraw, retire, go back, retreat.
G5298.abbottSmith: to go back, retire : seq. ἐν , Lk 5:16 ; seq. εἰς , Lk 9:10 .†
G5298.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5562 (χωρέω); to vacate down, i.e. retire quietly

G3033.mounce: Libya, a part of Africa, bordering on the west of Egypt, Acts 2:10*
G3033.strongs: probably from G3047 (λίψ); Libye, a region of Africa

G2632.thayer: I condemn, judge worthy of punishment.
G2632.mounce: to give judgment against, condemn, Mt. 27:3; Jn. 8:10, 11; to condemn, to place in a guilty light by contrast, Mt. 12:41, 42; Lk. 11:31, 32; Heb. 11:7
G2632.abbottSmith: to give judgment against , condemn : Ro 8:34 ; c. acc. pers., Mk 14:64 , Jo 8:[10 , 11] ; disting. fr. κρίνειν , Ro 2:1 , I Co 11:32 ; seq. θανάτῳ (cl. -ου or -ον ), Mt 20:18 , Mk 10:33 ; pass., Mt 27:3 , Mk 16:[16] , Ro 14:23 , I Co 11:32 , II Pe 2:6 . Metaph., of condemning through a good example: Mt 12:41 , 42 , Lk 11:31 , 32 , Ro 8:3 , He 11:7 .†
G2632.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2919 (κρίνω); to judge against, i.e. sentence

G2697.strongs: from G2722 (κατέχω); a holding down, i.e. occupancy

G985.thayer: intrans: I sprout; trans: I cause to sprout, make to grow up.
G985.mounce: also spelled βλαστάω, intrans. to germinate, bud, sprout, spring up, Mt. 13:26; Mk. 4:27; Heb. 9:4; trans. and causative, to cause to shoot, to produce, yield, Jas. 5:18*
G985.abbottSmith: to sprout : Mt 13:26 , Mk 4:27 , He 9:4 . In late Gk., causal, to make to grow, produce : c. acc., Ja 5:18 .†
G985.strongs: from (a sprout); to germinate; by implication, to yield fruit

G5034.mounce: swiftness, speed, quickness; ἐν τάχει, with speed, quickly, speedily; soon, shortly, Lk. 18:8; Acts 25:4; hastily, immediately, Acts 12:7; 22:18; Rom. 16:20; 1 Tim. 3:14; Rev. 1:1; 22:6*
G5034.abbottSmith: swiftness, speed . Adverbially, ἐν τ. (= ταχέως ), quickly, speedily, soon : Lk 18:8 , Ac 12:7 22:18 25:4 , Ro 16:20 , I Ti 3:14 , Re 1:1 22:6 .†
G5034.strongs: from the same as G5036 (ταχύς); a brief space (of time), i.e. (with G1722 (ἐν) prefixed) in haste

G4055.thayer: greater, more, even more, more abundantly.
G4055.mounce: comparative adj. from περισσός, greater, more, Mk. 12:40; Lk. 20:47; even more, Lk. 12:48; 1 Cor. 15:10; even more, so much more, Mk. 7:36
G4055.strongs: comparative of G4053 (περισσός); more superabundant (in number, degree or character)

G1907.thayer: (a) trans: I hold forth, (b) intrans: I mark, pay attention (heed), note; I delay, stay, wait.
G1907.mounce: trans. to hold out, present, exhibit, display, Phil. 2:16; intrans. to observe, take heed to, attend to, Lk. 14:7; Acts 3:5; 1 Tim. 4:16; to stay, delay, Acts 19:22*
G1907.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold upon, i.e. (by implication) to retain; (by extension) to detain; (with implication, of G3563 (νοῦς)) to pay attention to
G1907.kjv: give (take) heed unto, hold forth, mark, stay

G3331.mounce: the accusative singular form of monos (G3668) used as an adverb meaning only, alone
G3331.strongs: from G3346 (μετατίθημι); transposition, i.e. transferral (to heaven), disestablishment (of a law)

G500.thayer: antichrist, either one who puts himself in the place of, or the enemy (opponent) of the Messiah.
G500.mounce: antichrist, an opposer of Christ, 1 Jn. 2:18, 22; 4:3; 2 Jn. 7; plural in 1 Jn. 2:18*
G500.abbottSmith: Antichrist, "one who assuming the guise of Christ opposes Christ" (Westc., Epp. Jo. , 70): I Jo 2:18 , 22 4:3 , II Jo 7 ; pl. I Jo 2:18 (cf. ψευδόχριστος , and v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G500.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G5547 (Χριστός); an opponent of the Messiah

G1638.mounce: an olive garden; in NT the mount Olivet, Lk. 19:29; 21:37; Acts 1:12*
G1638.abbottSmith: olive-grove, olive-garden (so in FlJ and in π. ; Deiss., BS , 209 ff.; MM, Exp. , iii; M, Pr. , 49, 69, 235): Lk 19:29 21:37 (WH, -ῶν ; v. their App. , 158; Field, Notes , 73; Bl., § 10, 5; 33, 1; Thayer, s.v.), Ac 1:12 (where Bl, ll. c., proposes the conjectural emendation ἐλαιῶν for -ῶνος ).†
G1638.strongs: from G1636 (ἐλαία); an olive-orchard, i.e. (specially) the Mount of Olives

G1276.mounce: to pass through or over, Mt. 9:1; 14:34; Mk. 5:21; 6:53; Lk. 16:26; Acts 21:2*
G1276.abbottSmith: to pass over, cross over : Mt 9:1 ; seq. ἐπὶ τ. γῆν , Mt 14:34 , Mk 6:53 ; εἰς , Mk 5:21 , Ac 21:2 ; πρὸς ἡμᾶς , Lk 16:26 .†
G1276.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of the base of G4008 (πέραν); to cross entirely

G3542.mounce: pasture, pasturage, Jn. 10:9; ἔχειν νομήν, to eat its way, spread corrosion, 2 Tim. 2:17*
G3542.strongs: feminine from the same as G3551 (νόμος); pasture, i.e. (the act) feeding (figuratively, spreading of a gangrene), or (the food) pasturage

G3500.thayer: (a) putting to death, (b) dead or lifeless condition.
G3500.mounce: new moon, first of the month, Col. 2:16*

G942.abbottSmith: a bramble-bush : Lk 6:44 , Ac 7:30 , 35 ; ἐπὶ τοῦ (τῆς) β. , in the place concerning the bush : Mk 12:26 , Lk 20:37 .†
G942.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a brier shrub

G4278.mounce: to begin before a particular time, 2 Cor. 8:6, 10*
G4278.abbottSmith: to begin before : II Co 8:6 ; c. acc., τὸ θέλειν , II Co 8:10 (not elsewhere).†
G4278.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1728 (ἐνάρχομαι); to commence already

G1555.abbottSmith: to tell in detail, relate, declare : Ac 13:41 (LXX) 15:3 .†
G1555.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a compound of G1223 (διά) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); to narrate through wholly

G3461.thayer: a myriad, group of ten thousand, a ten thousand.
G3461.mounce: a myriad, ten thousand, Acts 19:19; indefinitely, a vast multitude, Lk. 12:1; Acts 21:20; Heb. 12:22; Jude 14; Rev. 5:11; 9:16*
G3461.abbottSmith: ten thousand , a myriad : pl., Ac 19:19 , Re 5:11 9:16 ; hyperb., of vast numbers, Lk 12:1 , Ac 21:20 , He 12:22 , Ju 14 .†
G3461.strongs: from G3463 (μύριοι); a ten-thousand; by extension, a "myriad" or indefinite number

G4507.mounce: filth; met. moral filthiness, uncleanness, pollution, Jas. 1:21*
G4507.abbottSmith: filthiness : metaph., of moral defile­ment, Ja 1:21 .†

G5561.thayer: (a) a country or region, (b) the land, as opposed to the sea, (c) the country, distinct from town, (d) plur: fields.
G5561.mounce: space, room; a country, region, tract, province, Mk. 5:10; Lk. 2:8; a district, territory, suburbs, Mt. 8:28; meton. the inhabitants of a country, region, etc., Mk. 1:5; Acts 12:20; the country, as opposed to the city or town, Lk. 21:21; a field, farm, Lk. 12:16; Jn. 4:35
G5561.abbottSmith: most freq. in cl., a space, place .
G5561.strongs: feminine of a derivative of the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of empty expanse; room, i.e. a space of territory (more or less extensive; often including its inhabitants)
G5561.kjv: coast, county, fields, ground, land, region

G2009.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G5092 (τιμή); properly, esteem, i.e. citizenship; used (in the sense of G2008 (ἐπιτιμάω)) of a penalty

G3201.mounce: to find fault with, blame, censure; to intimate dissatisfaction with, Heb. 8:8; absol. to find fault, Rom. 9:19*
G3201.abbottSmith: to blame , find fault : absol., Ro 9:19 ; c. acc., αὐτούς (WH, txt.; αὐτοῖς , Rec., WH, mg.; on rendering with αὐτοῖς v. Westc., in l.), He 8:8 .†
G3201.strongs: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to blame

G673.thayer: I separate from; mid: I part; pass: I am swept aside.
G673.mounce: to separate; pass. to be swept aside, Rev. 6:14; mid. to part, Acts 15:39*
G673.abbottSmith: to separate , part asunder : pass., Re 6:14 ; reflexively, to separate oneself : Ac 15:39 .†
G673.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5563 (χωρίζω); to rend apart; reflexively, to separate

G613.thayer: I hide away, conceal, keep secret.
G613.mounce: to hide away; to conceal, withhold from sight or knowledge, Lk. 10:21; 1 Cor. 2:7; Eph. 3:9; Col. 1:26*
G613.abbottSmith: to hide , conceal , keep secret : c. acc., Lk 10:21 ; pass., I Co 2:7 , Eph 3:9 , Col 1:26 (MM, s.v.).†
G613.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2928 (κρύπτω); to conceal away (i.e. fully); figuratively, to keep secret

G2528.strongs: from G2596 (κατά); and G3695 (ὁπλίζω); to equip fully with armor

G3169.mounce: pr. becoming a great man; magnificent, glorious, most splendid, 2 Pet. 1:17*
G3169.abbottSmith: befitting a great man , magnificent , majestic : II Pe 1:17 (cf. MM, xvi).†
G3169.strongs: from G3173 (μέγας) and G4241 (πρέπω); befitting greatness or magnificence (majestic)

G2303.strongs: probably neuter of G2304 (θεῖος) (in its original sense of flashing); sulphur

G2331.mounce: Thessalonian, of Thessalonica, Acts 20:4; 27:2; inscription to 1 and 2 Thess.*
G2331.abbottSmith: a Thessalonian : Ac 20:4 27:2 , I Th 1:1 , II Th 1:1 .†
G2331.strongs: from G2332 (Θεσσαλονίκη); a Thessalonican, i.e. inhabitant of Thessalonice

G3863.thayer: I make jealous, provoke to jealously, provoke to anger.
G3863.mounce: to provoke to jealousy, Rom. 10:19; to excite to emulation, Rom. 11:11, 14; to provoke to indignation, 1 Cor. 10:22*
G3863.abbottSmith: to provoke to jealousy : Ro 10:19 (LXX) 11:11 , 14 , I Co 10:22 .†
G3863.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2206 (ζηλόω); to stimulate alongside, i.e. excite to rivalry

G1615.thayer: I complete, bring to completion, carry out, perform.
G1615.mounce: to bring to an end, to finish, complete, Lk. 14:29, 30*
G1615.abbottSmith: to bring to an end, finish, complete : Lk 14:29 , 30 .†
G1615.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5055 (τελέω); to complete fully

G5096.thayer: Timon, one of the seven original deacons at Jerusalem.
G5096.strongs: from G5092 (τιμή); valuable; Timon, a Christian

G2808.mounce: to close, shut, Mt. 6:6; 25:10; to shut up a person, Rev. 20:3; met. of the heavens, Lk. 4:25; Rev. 11:6; κλεῖσαι τὰ σπλάγχνα, to shut one’s bowels, to be hard-hearted, void of compassion, 1 Jn. 3:17; κλείειν τὴν βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν, to endeavor to prevent entrance into the kingdom of heaven, Mt. 23:13
G2808.abbottSmith: to shut : θύραν , Mt 6:6 ; τ. ἄβυσσον , Re 20:3 ; pass., θύρα , Mt 25:10 , Lk 11:7 (pl.), Jo 20:19 , 26 , Ac 21:30 ; δεσμωτήριον , Ac 5:23 ; πυλῶνες , Re 21:25 . Metaph., τ. οὐρανόν , Lk 4:25 , Ro 11:6 ; τ. σπλάγχνα , I Jo 3:17 ; τ. βασιλείαν , Mt 23:14 ; id. absol., Re 3:7 ; τ. θύραν , Re 3:8 . (Cf. ἀπο- , ἐκ- , κατα- , συν-κλείω .)†
G2808.strongs: a primary verb; to close (literally or figuratively)

G164.strongs: from (a spear) and a derivative of the same as G259 (ἅλωσις); properly, a prisoner of war, i.e. (genitive case) a captive

G1814.mounce: to leap or spring up or forth, Acts 3:8*
G1814.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G242 (ἅλλομαι); to spring forth

G8.thayer: Abiathar, a priest in King David's time.
G8.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H054); Abiathar, an Israelite

G4185.thayer: very costly, very precious, of great value.
G4185.mounce: expensive, costly, Mk. 14:3; 1 Tim. 2:9; of great value, very precious, 1 Pet. 3:4*
G4185.abbottSmith: very costly , very precious , of great value : Mk 14:3 , I Ti 2:9 ; metaph., I Pe 3:4 .†
G4185.strongs: from G4183 (πολύς) and G5056 (τέλος); extremely expensive
G4185.kjv: costly, very precious, of great price

G2830.thayer: rough water, a wave, billow, surge.
G2830.mounce: a wave, billow, surge, Lk. 8:24; Jas. 1:6*
G2830.strongs: from (to billow or dash over); a surge of the sea (literally or figuratively)

G4173.thayer: a ruler of a city, city magistrate.
G4173.mounce: a ruler or prefect of a city, city magistrate, Acts 17:6, 8*
G4173.abbottSmith: the ruler of a city , a politarch : Ac 17:6 , 8 (v. MM, xx).†
G4173.strongs: from G4172 (πόλις) and G757 (ἄρχω); a town-officer, i.e. magistrate

G3357.mounce: moderately; slightly; οὐ μετρίως no little, not a little, much, greatly, Acts 20:12*
G3357.abbottSmith: moderately : litotes, οὐ μ. , exceedingly , Ac 20:12 .†
G3357.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G3358 (μέτρον); moderately, i.e. slightly

G732.mounce: ill, sick, an invalid, Mt. 14:14; Mk. 6:5, 13; 16:18; 1 Cor. 11:30
G732.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4517 (ῥώννυμι); infirm

G4107.mounce: a rover, roving, a wanderer, wandering; ἀστὴρ πλανήτης, a wandering star, Jude 13*
G4107.abbottSmith: = πλάνης , a wanderer : ἀστέρες π. (cl. planets ), wandering stars , Ju 13 (WH, mg., -τες ).†
G4107.strongs: from G4108 (πλάνος); a rover ("planet"), i.e. (figuratively) an erratic teacher

G808.thayer: unseemly behavior, unseemliness, indecency, shame, nakedness, an indecent (lewd) act.
G808.mounce: pr. external indecorum; nakedness, shame, pudenda, Rev. 16:15; indecency, infamous lust or lewdness, Rom. 1:27*
G808.abbottSmith: unseemliness : Ro 1:27 (MM, s.v.); euphemism for ἡ αἰσχύνη , as freq. in LXX, shame , nakedness : Re 16:15 .†
G808.strongs: from G809 (ἀσχήμων); an indecency; by implication, the pudenda

G2452.thayer: in the manner of Jews (religiously, ceremonially).
G2452.mounce: in the manner of Jews, according to Jewish custom, Gal. 2:14*
G2452.strongs: adverb from G2451 (Ἰουδαϊκός); Judaïcally or in a manner resembling a Judæan

G4469.mounce: raca, an Aramaic term of bitter contempt, worthless fellow, fool, Mt. 5:22*
G4469.abbottSmith: usually taken to represent the Aram. רֵיקָא , a shortened form of רֵיקָן , "empty," as vocalized in the Galilæan dialect; an expression of contempt, raca : Mt 5:22 (cf. DB , iv, 191 f.; and for other explanations, v. Zorell, s.v.).†
G4469.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H07386); O empty one, i.e. thou worthless (as a term of utter vilification)

G181.thayer: disturbance, upheaval, revolution, almost anarchy, first in the political, and thence in the moral sphere.
G181.mounce: pr. instability; hence, an unsettled state; disorder, commotion, tumult, sedition, Lk. 21:9; 1 Cor. 14:33; 2 Cor. 6:5; 12:20; Jas. 3:16*
G181.abbottSmith: instability (MM, VGT , s.v.); hence, confusion , tumult : I Co 14:33 , Ja 3:16 ; pl. (Bl., §32, 6), Lk 21:9 , II Co 6:5 , 12:20 (Polyb., al.; v. Cremer, 739).†

G709.mounce: to take the first meal, breakfast, Jn. 21:12, 15; also, to take a mid-day meal, Lk. 11:37*
G709.strongs: from G712 (ἄριστον); to take the principle meal

G3848.mounce: transgressor, violator of law, Rom. 2:25, 27; Gal. 2:18; Jas. 2:9, 11*
G3848.abbottSmith: one who stands beside , the warrior who stands by the charioteer (cf. παραβαίνω , 1).

G4626.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to dig

G5569.mounce: a false brother, a pretend Christian, 2 Cor. 11:26; Gal. 2:4*
G5569.abbottSmith: a false brother : of professing Christians, II Co 11:26 , Ga 2:4 .†
G5569.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G80 (ἀδελφός); a spurious brother, i.e. pretended associate

G1837.thayer: I sound out (forth) (referring either to the clearness or to the loudness of the sound).
G1837.mounce: act., to make to sound forth; pass., to sound forth, 1 Thess. 1:8*
G1837.abbottSmith: to sound forth (as a trumpet, or thunder; v. M, Th. , l.c.): pass., I Th 1:8 .†
G1837.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2278 (ἠχέω); to "echo" forth, i.e. resound (be generally reported)

G465.mounce: a price paid in exchange for a thing; compensation, equivalent ransom, Mt. 16:26; Mk. 8:37*
G465.abbottSmith: an exchange , the price received as an equivalent for an article of commerce: Mt 16:26 , Mk 8:37 (cf. Si 26:14 ; and v. Swete, Mk. 8:37 ; Cremer, 90).†
G465.strongs: from a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); an equivalent or ransom

G3353.mounce: a partaker, Heb. 3:1, 14; 6:4; 12:8; an associate, partner, fellow, Lk. 5:7; Heb. 1:9*
G3353.abbottSmith: sharing in , partaking of : c. gen. rei, He 3:1 6:4 12:8 ; τ. Χριστοῦ , He 3:14 .
G3353.strongs: from G3348 (μετέχω); participant, i.e. (as noun) a sharer; by implication, an associate

G2081.thayer: (a) from within, from inside, (b) within, inside; with the article: the inner part, the inner element, (c) the mind, soul.
G2081.mounce: from within, from the interior, Mk. 7:21, 23; within, in the internal parts, Mt. 7:15; ὁ, ἡ, τὸ ἔσωθεν, interior, internal, Lk. 11:39, 40; ὁ ἔσωθεν ἄνθρωπος, the mind, soul, 2 Cor. 4:16
G2081.strongs: from G2080 (ἔσω); from inside; also used as equivalent to G2080 (ἔσω) (inside)

G3475.thayer: Moses; met: the books of Moses, the Pentateuch.
G3475.mounce: also spelled Μωσῆς, Moses, pr. name, Mt. 8:4; Jn. 1:17; Rom. 5:14
G3475.abbottSmith: Moses : Mt 8:4 17:3 , 4 , al.; νόμος Μωυσέως , Lk 2:22 24:44 , Jo 7:23 , Ac 13:39 15:5 28:23 , I Co 9:9 , He 10:28 ; by meton., of the books of Moses, Lk 16:29 24:27 , Ac 15:21 , II Co 3:15 .
G3475.strongs: of Hebrew origin; (H04872); Moseus, Moses, or Mouses (i.e. Mosheh), the Hebrew lawgiver

G1511.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G1511.strongs: present infinitive from G1510 (εἰμί); to exist

G2464.mounce: Isaac, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:2; 8:11; 23:32; Acts 3:13;. Rom. 9:7f.; Gal. 4:28; Heb. 11:9ff.; Jas. 2:21
G2464.abbottSmith: Isaac ( Ge 17:19 , al.): Mt 1:2 , Ro 9:10 , al.
G2464.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03327); Isaac (i.e. Jitschak), the son of Abraham

G904.thayer: Balak, son of Zippor, King of Moab.
G904.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01111); Balak, a Moabite

G5275.thayer: I leave behind; pass: I am left surviving.
G5275.mounce: to leave remaining, leave behind; pass. to be left surviving, Rom. 11:3*
G5275.abbottSmith: to leave remaining (Hom., Thuc., al.): pass., of survivors, Ro 11:3 .†
G5275.strongs: from G5295 (ὑποτρέχω) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave under (behind), i.e. (passively) to remain (survive)

G418.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of the same as G1735 (ἐνδέχεται); unadmitted, i.e. (by implication) not supposable

G3140.thayer: I witness, bear witness, give evidence, testify, give a good report.
G3140.mounce: trans. to testify, depose, Jn. 3:11, 32; 1 Jn. 1:2; Rev. 1:2; 22:20; absol. to give evidence, Jn. 18:23; to bear testimony, testify, Lk. 4:22; Jn. 1:7, 8; to bear testimony in confirmation, Acts 14:3; to declare distinctly and formally, Jn. 4:44; pass. to be the subject of testimony, to obtain attestation to character, Acts 6:3; 10:22; 1 Tim. 5:10; Heb. 11:2, 4; mid. equivalent to μαρτύρομαι, to make a solemn appeal, Acts 26:22; 1 Thess. 2:12
G3140.abbottSmith: prop., to be a witness , bear witness , testify : absol. (Pind., al.), Jo 15:27 , Ac 26:5 ; parenthetical (Bl., § 79, 7; MM, xvi), II Co 8:3 ; c. dat. pers. (comm. et incomm.; Bl., § 37, 2), Ac 22:5 , He 10:15 ; id. seq. ὅτι , Mt 23:31 , Ro 10:2 , al.; acc. et inf., Ac 10:43 ; c. acc. rei (cl.), Jo 3:11 , Re 22:16 , 20 ; c. acc. cogn., seq. περί , Jo 5:32 , I Jo 5:10 ; c. dat. rei, Jo 5:33 , Ac 14
G3140.strongs: from G3144 (μάρτυς); to be a witness, i.e. testify (literally or figuratively)
G3140.kjv: charge, give (evidence), bear record, have (obtain, of) good (honest) report, be well reported of, testify, give (have) testimony, (be, bear, give, obtain) witness

G845.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); self-seeing, i.e. an eye-witness

G4265.mounce: to foresee; mid. to provide beforehand, Heb. 11:40*
G4265.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G991 (βλέπω); to look out beforehand, i.e. furnish in advance

G1275.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the genitive case of G3956 (πᾶς); through all time, i.e. (adverbially) constantly

G4357.thayer: I remain; I abide in, remain in, persist in, adhere to.
G4357.mounce: to continue, remain, stay in a place, 1 Tim. 1:3; to remain or continue with any one, Mt. 15:32; Mk. 8:2; Acts 18:18; to adhere to, Acts 11:23; met. to remain constant in, persevere in, Acts 13:43; 1 Tim. 5:5*
G4357.abbottSmith: to wait longer , continue , remain still : Ac 18:18 ; seq. ἐν , I Ti 1:3 .
G4357.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G3306 (μένω); to stay further, i.e. remain in a place, with a person; figuratively, to adhere to, persevere in
G4357.kjv: abide still, be with, cleave unto, continue in (with)

G4661.mounce: spoils stripped off an enemy; σκῦλα, spoil, plunder, booty, Lk. 11:22*
G4661.abbottSmith: mostly in pl., σκῦλα , arms stripped from a foe, spoils : Lk 11:22 .†
G4661.strongs: neuter from G4660 (σκύλλω); something stripped (as a hide), i.e. booty

G3350.mounce: change of abode or country, migration, Mt. 1:11, 12, 17*
G3350.abbottSmith: change of abode , migration : of the Babylonian exile, μ. Βαβυλῶνος , Mt 1:11 , 12 , 17 .†
G3350.strongs: from a derivative of a compound of G3326 (μετά) and G3624 (οἶκος); a change of abode, i.e. (specially), expatriation

G2451.mounce: Jewish, current among the Jews, Tit. 1:14*
G2451.strongs: from G2453 (Ἰουδαῖος); Judaïc, i.e. resembling a Judæan

G977.strongs: a reduplicated and prolonged form of an obsolete primary verb (perhaps causative of G1006 (βόσκω)); to eat

G2674.mounce: to enumerate, number with, count with, Acts 1:17*
G2674.abbottSmith: to number or count among : seq. ἐν , Ac 1:17 .†
G2674.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G705 (ἀριθμέω); to reckon among

G5375.thayer: Philippi, a great city of the Roman province Macedonia.
G5375.mounce: Philippi, a considerable city of Macedonia, east of Amphipolis, Acts 16:12; 20:6; Phil. 1:1; 1 Thess. 2:2; 1 & 2 Cor. subscr.*. See PhotoGuide.
G5375.strongs: plural of G5376 (Φίλιππος); Philippi, a place in Macedonia

G3944.mounce: to have gone by; perf. part. παρῳχημένος, by-gone, Acts 14:16*
G3944.abbottSmith: to have passed by ; of time, to be gone by : ἐν τ. παρῳχημέναις γενεαῖς , Ac 14:16 .†
G3944.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and (to depart); to escape along, i.e. be gone

G504.thayer: without water, dry; subst: dry places, desert.
G504.mounce: without water, dry, 2 Pet. 2:17; Jude 12; τόποι ἄνυδροι, dry places, and therefore, in the East, barren, desert, Mt. 12:43; Lk. 11:24*
G504.abbottSmith: waterless : τόποι , Mt 12:43 , Lk 11:24 ; πηγαί , II Pe 2:17 ; νεφέλαι , Ju 12 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G504.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5204 (ὕδωρ); waterless, i.e. dry

G3782.thayer: a debt, a duty, what is due.
G3782.mounce: a debt, Mt. 18:32; met. a duty, due, Rom. 13:7; 1 Cor. 7:3*
G3782.abbottSmith: a debt : Mt 18:32 ; metaph., one's due : Ro 13:7 , I Co 7:3 (found also in π. ; v. Deiss., BS , 221; MM, xviii).†
G3782.strongs: from G3784 (ὀφείλω); indebtedness, i.e. (concretely) a sum owed; figuratively, obligation, i.e. (conjugal) duty

G3435.thayer: I soil, stain, pollute, defile, lit. and met.
G3435.mounce: pr. to stain, sully; to defile, contaminate morally, 1 Cor. 8:7; Rev. 14:4; to soil, Rev. 3:4*
G3435.abbottSmith: to stain , soil , defile ; in NT always symb. and fig.: I Co 8:7 , Re 3:4 14:4 .†

G5045.mounce: an artisan; and spc. one who works with wood, a carpenter, Mt. 13:55; Mk. 6:3*
G5045.abbottSmith: an artificer in wood, stone or metal, but esp. a carpenter (v. MM, xxiv): Mt 13:55 , Mk 6:3 .†
G5045.strongs: from the base of G5098 (τιμωρία); an artificer (as producer of fabrics), i.e. (specially), a craftsman in wood

G3488.strongs: a flower of the same name, from (stupefaction, as a "narcotic"); Narcissus, a Roman

G3637.thayer: of or belonging to the eighth day, eight days old.
G3637.abbottSmith: of the eighth day , eight days old : c. dat. ref., περιτομῇ ὀ. , Phl 3:5 (words of this class denote duration , cf. τεταρταῖος ).†
G3637.strongs: from G3638 (ὀκτώ) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); an eight-day old person or act

G1419.mounce: difficult or grievous to be borne, oppressive, Lk. 11:46
G1419.abbottSmith: hard to be borne : Mt 23:4 (om. WH, txt., R, mg.), Lk 11:46 .†
G1419.strongs: from G1418 (δυσ-) and a derivative of G941 (βαστάζω); oppressive

G103.abbottSmith: to sing , intrans., c. dat. (MM, VGT , s.v.), of praise to God: Eph 5:19 , Col 3:16 ; trans., c. cogn. acc.: ᾠδὴν , Re 5:9 14:3 15:3 .†

G4318.mounce: approach; access, admission, to the presence of any one, Rom. 5:2; Eph. 2:18; 3:12*

G1131.thayer: rarely: stark-naked; generally: wearing only the under-garment; bare, open, manifest; mere.
G1131.mounce: naked, without clothing, Mk. 14:51, 52; without the upper garment, and clad only with an inner garment or tunic, Jn. 21:7; poorly or meanly clad, destitute of proper and sufficient clothing, Mt. 25:36, 38, 43, 44; Acts 19:16; Jas. 2:15; met. unclothed with a body, 2 Cor. 5:3; not covered, uncovered, open, manifest, Heb. 4:13; bare, mere, 1 Cor. 15:37; naked of spiritual clothing, Rev. 3:17; 16:15; 17:16
G1131.abbottSmith: naked, without clothing , and sometimes (as freq. in cl.) scantily or poorly clad ( Is 20:2 ff., To 1:16 , II Mac 11:12 ): Mt 25:36 , 38 43 , 44 , Mk 14:52 , Jo 21:7 , Ac 19:16 , Ja 2:15 , Re 3:17 16:15 , 17:16 ; as subst., to γ. , the naked body , Mk 14:51 . Metaph., of things exposed, He 4:13 ; of the soul without a body (Plat., Crat. , c., 20, and cf. Deiss., LAE , 293), II Co 5:3 ; of seed, ba
G1131.strongs: of uncertain affinity; nude (absolute or relative, literal or figurative)

G424.thayer: I come up, go up, ascend.
G424.mounce: to ascend, go up, Jn. 6:3; Gal. 1:17, 18*
G424.abbottSmith: to go up : Jo 6:3 , Ga 1:17 , 18 (cf. ἐπανέρχ- ; and on its use of "going up" to the capital, MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G424.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to ascend

G1477.abbottSmith: a support, bulwark, stay (Vg. fermamentum ): I Ti 3:15 (eccl.).†
G1477.strongs: from a derivative of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a support, i.e. (figuratively) basis

G4051.mounce: more than enough, residue over and above, Mk. 8:8; abundance, exuberance, Mt. 12:34; Lk. 6:45; superabundance, affluence, 2 Cor. 8:14*
G4051.abbottSmith: that which is over and above , superfluity , abundance : opp. to ὑστέρημα (q.v.), II Co 8:13 , 14 ; pl., Mk 8:8 ; metaph., π. τ. καρδίας , Mt 12:34 , Lk 6:45 (cf. Ec 2:15 ; Plut., al.).†
G4051.strongs: from G4052 (περισσεύω); a surplus, or superabundance
G4051.kjv: abundance, that was left, over and above

G3947.mounce: to sharpen; met. to incite, stir up, Acts 17:16; to irritate, provoke, 1 Cor. 13:5*
G3947.abbottSmith: primarily, but never so in cl., to sharpen ( μάχαιραν , De 32:41 , שׁנן ).
