G3947.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and a derivative of G3691 (ὀξύς); to sharpen alongside, i.e. (figuratively) to exasperate

G3810.thayer: an instructor, trainer; almost: a chastiser.
G3810.mounce: a preceptor, instructor, teacher, pr. of boys; genr. Rom. 2:20; in NT a chastiser, Heb. 12:9*
G3810.strongs: from G3811 (παιδεύω); a trainer, i.e. teacher or (by implication) discipliner

G2332.thayer: Thessalonica (modern Saloniki), an important city of the Roman province Macedonia.
G2332.mounce: Thessalonica, a city of Macedonia, Acts 17:1, 11, 13; Phil. 4:16; 2 Tim. 4:10*. See PhotoGuide.
G2332.abbottSmith: Thessalonica , a city of Macedonia: Ac 17:1 , 11 , 13 , Phl 4:16 , II Ti 4:10 .†
G2332.strongs: from (a Thessalian) and G3529 (νίκη); Thessalonice, a place in Asia Minor

G2499.thayer: (Hebrew), gen. case of Joses (a) son of Eliezer, (b) son of Mary, brother of Jesus, (c) surnamed Barnabas (also called Joseph).
G2499.strongs: genitive case of G2500 (Ἰωσῆς); Jose, an Israelite

G4211.thayer: a female seller of purple cloth.
G4211.mounce: a female seller of purple cloths, Acts 16:4*
G4211.abbottSmith: a seller of purple fabrics : Ac 16:14 .†
G4211.strongs: feminine of a compound of G4209 (πορφύρα) and G4453 (πωλέω); a female trader in purple cloth

G3429.thayer: I commit adultery, not only of a married woman but of a married man.
G3429.mounce: act., to cause to commit adultery, pass., to commit or be guilty of adultery, Mt. 5:32; 19:9; Mk. 10:11f.*
G3429.abbottSmith: to commit adultery with : c. acc., fem. In NT always mid. in same sense; of the man: absol., Mt 5:32 19:9 (WH, txt., R, mg., om.); seq. ἐπ’ αὐτήν , Mk 10:11 ; of the woman: Mk 10:12 .†
G3429.strongs: from G3432 (μοιχός); (middle voice) to commit adultery

G1968.thayer: I fall upon, press upon, light upon, come over.
G1968.mounce: to fall upon; to throw one’s self upon, Lk. 15:20; Jn. 13:25; Acts 20:10, 37; to press, urge upon, Mk. 3:10; to light upon, Rom. 15:3; to come over, Acts 13:11; to come upon, fall upon mentally or spiritually, Lk. 1:12; Acts 8:16; 10:10, 44; 11:15; 19:17
G1968.abbottSmith: to fall upon (Field, Notes , 25): c. dat. pers., Mk 3:10 (v. Swete, in l), Ac 20:10 ; seq. ἐπὶ τ. τράχηλον (as Ge 46:29 , To 11:8 , III Mac 5:49 , al.), Lk 15:20 , Ac 20:37 . Metaph., φόβος , Lk 1:12 , Ac 19:17 , Re 11:11 ; ὀνειδισμοί , Ro 15:3 (LXX) ; of the Holy Spirit: seq. ἐπί , c. dat., Ac 8:16 ; ἐπί , c. acc, Ac 10:44 11:15 ; absol., Ac 23:7 .†
G1968.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4098 (πίπτω); to embrace (with affection) or seize (with more or less violence; literally or figuratively)
G1968.kjv: fall into (on, upon) lie on, press upon

G1149.thayer: Dalmatia, a province of the Roman Empire, east of the Adriatic, a later name for part of what was earlier called Illyricum.
G1149.abbottSmith: Dalmatia , a part of Illyria on E. coast of the Adriatic: II Ti 4:10 .†
G1149.strongs: probably of foreign derivation; Dalmatia, a region of Europe

G4028.thayer: I cover up, cover round about, veil round, blindfold.
G4028.mounce: to cover round about, cover over; to cover the face, Mk. 14:65; to blindfold, Lk. 22:64; pass. to be overlaid, Heb. 9:4*
G4028.abbottSmith: to cover around , cover up or over : c. acc., Mk 14:65 , Lk 22:64 ; pass., seq. χρυσίῳ , He 9:4 .†
G4028.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to cover all around, i.e. entirely (the face, a surface)

G4237.thayer: a company formed into divisions (like garden-beds).
G4237.mounce: a small area or bed in a garden; trop. a company of persons disposed in squares; from the Hebrew, πρασιαὶ πρασιαί, by areas, by squares, like beds in a garden, Mk. 6:40*
G4237.abbottSmith: a garden-bed ; metaph., of ranks or orderly groups of persons: Mk 6:40 .†
G4237.strongs: perhaps from (a leek, and so an onion-patch); a garden plot, i.e. (by implication, of regular beds) a row (repeated in plural by Hebraism, to indicate an arrangement)

G1566.abbottSmith: thither : Ac 21:3 ; constr. pregn. (MM, Exp. , xii; Field, Notes , 134), τοὺς ἐ. ὄντας , Ac 22:5 .†

G5562.thayer: (lit: I make room, hence) (a) I have room for, receive, contain, (b) I make room for by departing, go, make progress, turn myself.
G5562.mounce: to make room, either by motion or capacity; to move, pass, Mt. 15:17; to proceed, go on, 2 Pet. 3:9; to progress, make way, Jn. 8:37; trans. to hold as contents, contain, afford room for, Mk. 2:2; Jn. 2:6; 21:25; met. to give mental admittance to, to yield accordance, Mt. 19:11, 12; to admit to approbation and esteem, to regard cordially, 2 Cor. 7:2*
G5562.strongs: from G5561 (χώρα); to be in (give) space, i.e. (intransitively) to pass, enter, or (transitively) to hold, admit (literally or figuratively)
G5562.kjv: come, contain, go, have place, (can, be room to) receive

G2029.thayer: I am an eyewitness of, behold, look upon.
G2029.mounce: to look upon, observe, watch; to witness, be an eye-witness of, 1 Pet. 2:12; 3:2*
G2029.abbottSmith: to watch (in Hom., as an overseer; cf Ps, ll. c.), look upon : I Pe 2:12 3:2 .†
G2029.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); to inspect, i.e. watch

G282.thayer: (lit: motherless), whose mother's name is not recorded (or known).
G282.mounce: pr. without mother; independent of maternal descent, Heb. 7:3*
G282.abbottSmith: without a mother (freq. in Gk. writers of the gods): ἀπάτωρ ἀ. of one without recorded genealogy, He 7:3 (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.).
G282.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3384 (μήτηρ); motherless, i.e. of unknown maternity

G4689.thayer: I pour out as a libation.
G4689.mounce: to pour out a libation or drink-offering; in NT mid. to make a libation of one’s self by expending energy and life in the service of the Gospel, Phil. 2:17; pass. to be in the act of being sacrificed in the cause of the Gospel, 2 Tim. 4:6*
G4689.abbottSmith: to pour out as a drink-offering, make a libation ; pass., fig., σπένδομαι , I am poured out or offered as a libation (in the shedding of my life-blood): Phl 2:17 (v. Lft., in l.), II Ti 4:6 (for exx. from π. , v. Milligan, Selections, 114 f.; MM, xxiii).†
G4689.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to pour out as a libation, i.e. (figuratively) to devote (one's life or blood, as a sacrifice) ("spend")

G113.abbottSmith: lawless , esp. of those who violate the law of nature and conscience (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.): II Pe 2:7 3:17 .†
G113.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι) (in the sense of enacting); lawless, i.e. (by implication) criminal

G1948.abbottSmith: to decree, give sentence : c. acc et inf., Lk 23:24 .†
G1948.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2919 (κρίνω); to adjudge

G5108.mounce: such, such like, of this kind or sort, Mt. 18:5; 19:14; such, so great, Mt. 9:8; Mk. 6:2; ὁ τοιοῦτος, such a fellow, Acts 22:22; also, the one alluded to, 1 Cor. 1:5; 2 Cor. 2:6, 7; 12:2, 3, 5
G5108.abbottSmith: correlat. of οἷος, ὁποῖος, ὥς , etc.,
G5108.strongs: (including the other inflections); from G5104 (τοί) and G3778 (οὗτος); truly this, i.e. of this sort (to denote character or individuality)

G3193.thayer: belonging to bees, coming from bees.
G3193.abbottSmith: made by bees : Lk 24:42 (Rec., WH, R, mg.).†
G3193.strongs: from G3192 (μέλι); relating to honey, i.e. bee (comb)

G5492.mounce: to excite a tempest, toss with a tempest; pass. to be storm-tossed, Acts 27:18*
G5492.abbottSmith: to expose to winter cold, go into winter quarters .
G5492.strongs: from the same as G5494 (χειμών); to storm, i.e. (passively) to labor under a gale

G481.thayer: right opposite, off (nautical sense), over against.
G481.mounce: can function as an improper preposition with the gen., also spelled ἀντικρύ, opposite to, over against, Acts 20:15*
G481.abbottSmith: in cl., outright ; in κοινή (= cl. καταντικρύ ), over against : Ac 20:15 (v. Bl., § 5, 4; 40, 7; Rutherford, NPhr. , 500 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G3958.thayer: I am acted upon in a certain way, either good or bad; I experience ill treatment, suffer.
G3958.mounce: to be affected by a thing, whether good or bad, to suffer, endure evil, Mt. 16:21; 17:12, 15; 27:19; absol. to suffer death, Lk. 22:15; 24:26
G3958.abbottSmith: to suffer , be acted on , as opp. to acting, often limited by a word expressive of good or evil; of misfortunes (most freq. without any limiting word): absol., Lk 22:15 24:46 , Ac 1:3 3:18 17:3 , I Co 12:26 , He 2:18 9:26 13:12 , I Pe 2:19 , 20 , 23 3:17 4:15 , 19 ; seq. ὑπό , c. gen., Mt 17:12 ; ὑπέρ , Ac 9:16 , Phl 1:20 , II Th 1:5 , I Pe 2:21 ; c. dat. ref., I Pe 4:1 ; περί , c. gen. (seq. ὑπέρ
G3958.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to experience a sensation or impression (usually painful)

G400.abbottSmith: to cry out , exclaim : Lk 1:42 (Arist., al.).†
G400.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5455 (φωνέω); to exclaim

G493.thayer: Antipas, a Christian martyr of Pergamum.
G493.strongs: contracted for a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and a derivative of G3962 (πατήρ); Antipas, a Christian

G3003.thayer: properly: a division of the Roman army, numbering about 6,000 infantry with additional cavalry; hence: a very large number; a legion.
G3003.mounce: also spelled λεγεών, a Roman legion; in NT used indefinitely for a great number, Mt. 26:53; Mk. 5:9, 15, Lk. 8:30*
G3003.strongs: of Latin origin; a "legion", i.e. Roman regiment (figuratively)

G3410.mounce: hire, rent; in NT a hired dwelling, Acts 28:30*

G420.thayer: enduring evil, patient of evil, patiently forbearing.
G420.mounce: enduring or patient under evils and injuries, 2 Tim. 2:24*
G420.abbottSmith: patiently forbearing (cf. ἀνεξικακία , Wi 2:19 ; and v. MM, VGT , s.v.): II Ti 2:24 .†
G420.strongs: from G430 (ἀνέχομαι) and G2556 (κακός); enduring of ill, i.e. forbearing

G620.thayer: I leave, leave behind; pass: I am reserved, remain; I desert, abandon.
G620.mounce: to leave, leave behind; pass. to be left, remain, 2 Tim. 4:13, 20; Heb. 4:6, 9; 10:26; to relinquish, forsake, desert, Tit. 1:5; Jude 6*
G620.abbottSmith: to leave , leave behind (in π. a term. techn. in wills; v. MM, s.v.): II Ti 4:13 , 20 , Tit 1:5 ; pass., to be reserved , remain : He 4:6 , 9 10:26 .
G620.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave behind (passively, remain); by implication, to forsake

G2089.thayer: (a) of time: still, yet, even now, (b) of degree: even, further, more, in addition.
G2089.mounce: yet, still, Mt. 12:46; still, further, longer, Lk. 16:2; further, besides, in addition, Mt. 18:16; with a compar. yet, still, Phil. 1:9
G2089.strongs: perhaps akin to G2094 (ἔτος); "yet," still (of time or degree)
G2089.kjv: after that, also, ever, (any) further, (t-)henceforth (more), hereafter, (any) longer, (any) more(-one), now, still, yet

G2684.thayer: I view closely, inspect, spy out.
G2684.mounce: to view closely and accurately; to spy out, Gal. 2:4*
G2684.abbottSmith: to view closely , inspect , spy out : c. acc., Ga 2:4 .†
G2684.strongs: from G2685 (κατάσκοπος); to be a sentinel, i.e. to inspect insidiously

G2950.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G2949 (κῦμα); a "cymbal" (as hollow)

G3965.mounce: descent, lineage; a family, tribe, race, Lk. 2:4; Acts 3:25; Eph. 3:15*
G3965.strongs: as if feminine of a derivative of G3962 (πατήρ); paternal descent, i.e. (concretely) a group of families or a whole race (nation)

G5438.thayer: a watching, keeping guard; a guard, prison; imprisonment.
G5438.mounce: a keeping watch, ward, guard, Lk. 2:8; a place of watch, Rev. 18:2; a watch, guard, body of guards, Acts 12:10; ward, custody, imprisonment, 2 Cor. 6:5; 11:23; Heb. 11:36; prison, 1 Pet. 3:19; a place of custody, prison, Mt. 14:10; 25:39, 44; a watch or division, of the night, which in the time of our Savior was divided into watches of three hours each, called ὀψέ, μεσονύκτιον, ἀλεκτοροψωνία and πρωία, or πρωί, Mt. 14:25; 24:43; Mk. 6:48; Lk. 12:38 (2x)
G5438.abbottSmith: actively, a guarding, guard, watch (Hom., Plat., Xen., al.): cogn. acc., φυλάσσειν φυλακάς , to keep watch , Lk 2:8 ;
G5438.strongs: from G5442 (φυλάσσω); a guarding or (concretely, guard), the act, the person; figuratively, the place, the condition, or (specially), the time (as a division of day or night), literally or figuratively

G4726.mounce: a sighing, groaning, groan, Acts 7:34; an inward sighing, Rom. 8:26*

G2072.thayer: a mirror, looking-glass (made of highly polished metal).
G2072.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and a presumed derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); a mirror (for looking into)

G1753.thayer: working, action, productive work, activity; in the NT, confined to superhuman activity.
G1753.mounce: energy, efficacy, power, Phil. 3:21; Col. 2:12; active energy, operation, Eph. 1:19; 3:7; 4:16; Col. 1:29; 2 Thess. 2:9, 11*
G1753.abbottSmith: operative power (as distinct from δύναμις , potential power ), working : of God, Eph 1:19 3:7 4:16 , Phl 3:21 , Col 1:29 2:12 ; of Satan, II Th 2:9 , 11 (cf. M, Th. , l.c.; AR, Eph. , 241 ff.; Cremer, 261).†

G1006.mounce: to feed, pasture, tend while grazing; βόσκομαι, to feed, be feeding, Mt. 8:30, 33; Lk. 8:32, 34
G1006.abbottSmith: prop., of a herdsman, to feed : Mt 8:33 , Mk 5:14 , Lk 8:34 15:15 ; metaph., of Christian pastoral care, Jo 21:15 , 17 . Pass., of cattle, to feed, graze : Mt 8:30 , Mk 5:11 , Lk 8:32 .†
G1006.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb (compare G977 (βιβρώσκω), G1016 (βοῦς)); to pasture; by extension to, fodder; reflexively, to graze

G2457.thayer: Julius, a Roman centurion on special service.
G2457.strongs: of Latin origin; Julius, a centurion

G4820.thayer: properly: I throw together, hence: I ponder, come up with, encounter, with or without hostile intent; I dispute with; mid: I confer, consult with, contribute.
G4820.mounce: pr. to throw together; absol. to meet and join, Acts 20:14; to meet in war, to encounter, engage with, Lk. 14:31; to encounter in discourse or dispute, Acts 17:18; to consult together, Acts 4:15; mid. to contribute, be of service to, to aid, Acts 18:27; συμβάλλειν ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ, to revolve in mind, ponder upon, Lk. 2:19*
G4820.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G906 (βάλλω); to combine, i.e. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute, (mentally) to consider, (by implication) to aid, (personally) to join, attack
G4820.kjv: confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder

G851.mounce: to take away, remove, Lk. 1:25; 10:42; to take off, cut off, remove by cutting off, Mt. 26:15; Mk. 14:47; Lk. 22:50
G851.abbottSmith: to take from , take away , take off : c. acc., τὸ ὠτίον , Mt 26:51 , Mk 14:47 ( ὠτάριον , WH), Lk 22:50 ( οὖς ); ὄνειδος , Lk 1:25 ; seq. ἀπό , Re 22:19 , Lk 16:3 (mid.); pass., c. gen., Lk 10:42 ; ἀ. ἁμαρτίος , He 10:4 , Ro 11:27 (mid.) (MM, s.v.).†
G851.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G138 (αἱρέομαι); to remove (literally or figuratively)

G3926.thayer: I trouble, cause disturbance to, annoy, harass.
G3926.abbottSmith: to annoy concerning a matter ( παρά ): c. dat. pers., Ac 15:19 .†
G3926.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1776 (ἐνοχλέω); to harass further, i.e. annoy

G512.abbottSmith: unprofitable : Tit 3:9 ; neut. as subst., unprofitableness : He 7:18 .†
G512.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G5624 (ὠφέλιμος); useless or (neuter) inutility

G1960.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G1959 (ἐπιμελέομαι); carefully

G3007.thayer: (earlier: I leave behind, abandon), (a) I am wanting, (b) mid: e.g. with gen: I come behind (in a race), am left behind in, fall short of (some standard), am wanting in.
G3007.mounce: trans. to leave, forsake; pass. to be left, deserted; by impl. to be destitute of, deficient in, Jas. 1:4, 5; 2:15; intrans. to fail, be wanting, be deficient, Lk. 18:22; Tit. 1:5; 3:13*
G3007.abbottSmith: trans., to leave , leave behind ; pass., to be left behind , to lack seq. prep. (as more usual in cl.), ἐν , Ja 1:4 ; c. gen. rei, Ja 1:5 2:15 .
G3007.strongs: a primary verb; to leave, i.e. (intransitively or passively) to fail or be absent

G4499.thayer: Rhodes, an island in the Aegean sea, south-west of Asia Minor.
G4499.mounce: Rhodes, an island in the Mediterranean, south of Caria, Acts 21:1*
G4499.strongs: probably from (a rose); Rhodus, an island of the Mediterranean

G2067.strongs: from a derivative of G2066 (ἐσθής); clothing (concretely)

G4643.thayer: hardness, hardness of heart, obstinacy, perverseness.
G4643.mounce: hardness; met. σκληρότης τῆς καρδίας, hardness of heart, obstinacy, perverseness, Rom. 2:5*

G834.mounce: a player on a pipe or flute, Mt. 9:23; Rev. 18:22*

G1550.abbottSmith: strengthened form of δαπανάω , to spend wholly ; pass., with reflexive force, to spend oneself wholly : seq. ὑπέρ , II Co 12:15 .†
G1550.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1159 (δαπανάω); to expend (wholly), i.e. (figuratively) exhaust

G5196.mounce: insolence; shame, insult, outrage, 2 Cor. 12:10; damage by sea, Acts 27:10, 21*
G5196.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ); insolence (as over-bearing), i.e. insult, injury

G4683.thayer: I swathe, wrap in swaddling clothes.
G4683.mounce: to swathe, wrap in swaddling cloths, Lk. 2:7, 12*
G4683.abbottSmith: to swathe, wrap in swaddling-clothes : Lk 2:7 , 12 (Hipp., Arist., Plut.).†
G4683.strongs: from (a strip; from a derivative of the base of G4682 (σπαράσσω) meaning to strap or wrap with strips); to swathe (an infant after the Oriental custom)

G2942.thayer: a steersman, pilot; met: a guide, governor.
G2942.strongs: from the same as G2941 (κυβέρνησις); helmsman, i.e. (by implication) captain

G2004.mounce: with dat., to set over or upon; to enjoin, charge, Mk. 1:27; 6:39; Lk. 4:36
G2004.abbottSmith: to command, charge : c. acc rei, Lk 14:22 ; c. dat. pers., Mk 1:27 9:25 , Lk 4:36 8:25 ; id. c. acc rei, Phm 8 ; id. c. inf., Mk 6:39 , Lk 8:31 , Ac 23:2 ; id. c. imperat., Mk 9:25 ; c. acc et inf., Mk 6:27 .†
G2004.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange upon, i.e. order

G32.thayer: a messenger, generally a (supernatural) messenger from God, an angel, conveying news or behests from God to men.
G32.abbottSmith: a messenger , one sent: Mt 11:10 , Ja 22:25 .
G32.strongs: from (probably derived from G71 (ἄγω); compare G34 (ἀγέλη)) (to bring tidings); a messenger; especially an "angel"; by implication, a pastor

G4471.thayer: Rama, a place in Ephraim, two hours north of Jerusalem.
G4471.mounce: Rama, a city of Judea. See PhotoGuide.
G4471.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07414); Rama (i.e. Ramah), a place in Palestine

G5520.mounce: pr. to be melancholy; used later as an equivalent to χολοῦμαι, to be angry, incensed, Jn. 7:23*
G5520.strongs: from G5521 (χολή); to be bilious, i.e. (by implication) irritable (enraged, "choleric")

G5273.thayer: (lit: a stage-player), a hypocrite, dissembler, pretender.
G5273.mounce: the giver of an answer or response; a stageplayer, actor; in NT a moral or religious counterfeit, a hypocrite, Mt. 6:2, 5, 16; 7:5
G5273.strongs: from G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); an actor under an assumed character (stage-player), i.e. (figuratively) a dissembler ("hypocrite"

G4732.mounce: to make firm; to strengthen, Acts 3:7, 16; to settle, Acts 16:5*
G4732.strongs: from G4731 (στερεός); to solidify, i.e. confirm (literally or figuratively)

G1198.mounce: one bound, a prisoner, Mt. 27:15, 16; Mk. 15:6

G5548.mounce: to anoint; in NT to anoint, by way of instituting to a dignity, function, or privilege, Lk. 4:18; Acts 4:27; 10:38; 2 Cor. 1:21; Heb. 1:9*
G5548.strongs: probably akin to G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of contact; to smear or rub with oil, i.e. (by implication) to consecrate to an office or religious service

G5256.mounce: to subserve, Acts 13:36; to relieve, supply, Acts 20:34; to render kind offices, Acts 24:23*
G5256.abbottSmith: prop., to serve as rower on a ship (Diod., al.). In cl. always metaph., to minister to, serve : c. dat. pers., Ac 13:36 20:34 24:23 .†
G5256.strongs: from G5257 (ὑπηρέτης); to be a subordinate, i.e. (by implication) subserve

G5503.abbottSmith: a widow : Mt 23:13 (Rec., R, mg.), Mk 12:40-43 , Lk 2:37 4:25 7:12 18:3 , 5 20:47 21:2, 3 , Ac 6:1 9:39 , 41 , I Co 7:8 , I Ti 5:3-5 , 11 , 16 , Ja 1:27 ; γυνὴ χ. , Lk 4:26 ; of one of an ordo viduarum (v. CGT , in l.), I Ti 5:9 ; metaph., of a city forsaken, Re 18:7 .†
G5503.strongs: feminine of a presumed derivative apparently from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of deficiency; a widow (as lacking a husband), literally or figuratively

G3946.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3664 (ὅμοιος); alike nearly, i.e. similar

G2572.mounce: to cover, Mt. 8:24; Lk. 8:16; 23:30; to hide, conceal, Mt. 10:26; 2 Cor. 4:3; met. to cover, throw a veil of oblivion over, Jas. 5:20; 1 Pet. 4:8
G2572.abbottSmith: in cl., rare in prose, to cover : c. acc. pers., Lk 23:30 ; c. acc. rei et dat., Lk 8:16 ; pass., Mt 8:24 ; metaph., to veil , conceal : pf. ptcp. pass., Mt 10:26 , II Co 4:3 ; of the forgiveness of sins (cf. Ps 31 (32):5 84 (85):2 ; DB , ii, 56 b ): I Pe 4:8 , Ja 5:20 ; (v.l. for καιομένη , WH, mg., Lk 24:32 ).†
G2572.strongs: akin to G2813 (κλέπτω) and G2928 (κρύπτω); to cover up (literally or figuratively)

G3972.mounce: Paulus, Paul, pr. name. (1) Paul, the Apostle, Acts 13:9, et al. freq. (2) Sergius Paulus, the deputy or proconsul of Cyprus, Acts 13:7
G3972.strongs: of Latin origin; (little; but remotely from a derivative of G3973 (παύω), meaning the same); Paulus, the name of a Roman and of an apostle

G3110.thayer: a Macedonian, an inhabitant of the Roman province Macedonia.
G3110.mounce: a native of Macedonia, Acts 16:9; 19:29; 27:2; 2 Cor. 9:2, 4*
G3110.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a Macedon (Macedonian), i.e. inhabitant of Macedonia

G2394.thayer: Jason, a Christian of Thessalonica, perhaps the same as the "relative" of Paul.
G2394.abbottSmith: Jason : Ac 17:5-7 , 9 ; perhaps the same, Ro 16:21 .†
G2394.strongs: future active participle masculine of G2390 (ἰάομαι); about to cure; Jason, a Christian

G3399.thayer: Miletus, a city on the coast of the Roman province Asia.
G3399.mounce: Miletus, a seaport city of Caria, on the west coast of Asia Minor, Acts 20:15, 17; 2 Tim. 4:20*
G3399.abbottSmith: Miletus , a maritime city of Caria: Ac 20:15 , 17 , II Ti 4:20 .†
G3399.strongs: of uncertain origin; Miletus, a city of Asia Minor

G605.mounce: pr. a restitution or restoration of a thing to its former state; hence, the renovation of a new and better era, Acts 3:21*
G605.abbottSmith: restoration : Ac 3:21 (in π. of repairs and restorations of temples, v. MM, s.v.).†

G1609.thayer: I spit out, disdain, reject, loathe.
G1609.mounce: lit., to spit out; met. to reject, Gal. 4:14*
G1609.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4429 (πτύω); to spit out, i.e. (figuratively) spurn

G1987.mounce: pr. care of, attention to, 2 Cor. 11:28
G1987.abbottSmith: to know, know of, understand : c. acc pers., Ac 19:15 ; c. ptcp., ib. 24:10 ; c. acc rei, Mk 14:68 , Ac 18:25 , I Ti 6:4 , Ja 4:14 , Ju 10 ; seq. περί , Ac 26:26 ; ὅτι , ib. 15:7 19:25 22:19 ; ὡς , ib. 10:28 ; πῶς , ib. 20:18 ; ποῦ , He 11:8 .†
G1987.strongs: apparently a middle voice of G2186 (ἐφίστημι) (with G3563 (νοῦς) implied); to put the mind upon, i.e. comprehend, or be acquainted with

G3661.thayer: with one mind, unanimously, with one accord, at the same time.
G3661.mounce: with one mind, with one accord, unanimously, Acts 1:14; Rom. 15:6; together, at once, at the same time, Acts 2:1, 46; 4:24
G3661.abbottSmith: with one mind , with one accord : Ac 1:14 2:46 4:24 5:12 7:57 8:6 12:20 15:25 18:12 19:29 , Ro 15:6 (Hatch, Essays , 63 f., argues that the un-cl. sense together , which is found in Jb 3:18 38:33 , Nu 24:24 , Wi 18:5 , 12 (but not 10:20 ), al., should be attached to the NT instances, but v. Abbott, Essays , 96; MM, xviii).†
G3661.strongs: adverb from a compound of the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ) and G2372 (θυμός); unanimously

G4564.mounce: Sara, Sarah, pr. name, the wife of Abraham, Rom. 4:19; 9:9; Heb. 11:11; 1 Pet. 3:6*
G4564.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08283); Sarra (i.e. Sarah), the wife of Abraham

G4651.mounce: a scorpion, a large insect, sometimes several inches in length, shaped somewhat like a crab and furnished with a tail terminating in a stinger from which it emits a dangerous poison, Lk. 10:19; 11:12; Rev. 9:3, 5, 10*
G4651.strongs: probably from an obsolete (perhaps strengthened from the base of G4649 (σκοπός) and meaning to pierce); a "scorpion" (from its sting)

G5500.thayer: I elect by show of hands, choose by vote, appoint.
G5500.mounce: to stretch out the hand; to constitute by voting; to appoint, constitute, Acts 14:23; 2 Cor. 8:19*
G5500.abbottSmith: to vote by stretching out the hand in the Athenian ἐκκλησία (Luc., Plut.).
G5500.strongs: from a comparative of G5495 (χείρ) and (to stretch); to be a hand-reacher or voter (by raising the hand), i.e. (generally) to select or appoint

G3356.thayer: I bear gently with, have compassion.
G3356.mounce: to moderate one’s passions; to be gentle, compassionate, Heb. 5:2*
G3356.abbottSmith: to hold one's passions or emotions in restraint ; hence, to bear gently with , feel gently towards : He 5:2 .†
G3356.strongs: from a compound of the base of G3357 (μετρίως) and G3806 (πάθος); to be moderate in passion, i.e. gentle (to treat indulgently)

G2564.thayer: (a) I call, summon, invite, (b) I call, name.
G2564.mounce: to call, call to, Jn. 10:3; to call into one’s presence, send for a person, Mt. 2:7; to summon, Mt. 2:15; 25:14; to invite, Mt. 22:9; to call to the performance of a certain thing, Mt. 9:13; Heb. 11:8; to call to a participation in the privileges of the Gospel, Rom. 8:30; 9:24; 1 Cor. 1:9; 7:18; to call to an office or dignity, Heb. 5:4; to name, style, Mt. 1:21; pass. to be styled, regarded, Mt. 5:9, 19
G2564.abbottSmith: to call , summon : c. acc. pers., Mt 20:8 25:14 , Mk 3:31 , Lk 19:13 , Ac 4:18 ; seq. ἐκ , Mt 2:15 (LXX) ; metaph., I Pe 2:9 . to call to one's house , invite : Lk 14:16 , I Co 10:27 , Re 19:9 ; εἰς τ. γάμους , Mt 22:3 , 9 , Lk 14:8 , 9 , Jo 2:2 ; ὁ καλέσας , Lk 7:39 ; οἱ κεκλημένοι , Mt 22:8 ; metaph., of inviting to partake of the blessings of the kingdom of God (Dalman, Words , 118 f.): Ro 8:30
G2564.strongs: akin to the base of G2753 (κελεύω); to "call" (properly, aloud, but used in a variety of applications, directly or otherwise)

G1380.thayer: I think, seem, appear, it seems.
G1380.mounce: to think, imagine, suppose, presume, Mt. 3:9; 6:7; to seem, appear, Lk. 10:36; Acts 17:18; it seems; it seems good, best, or right, it pleases, Lk. 1:3; Acts 15:22, 25
G1380.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb, (used only in an alternate in certain tenses; compare the base of G1166 (δεικνύω)) of the same meaning; to think; by implication, to seem (truthfully or uncertainly)
G1380.kjv: be accounted, (of own) please(-ure), be of reputation, seem (good), suppose, think, trow

G1997.mounce: the act of being gathered together or assembled, 2 Thess. 2:1; an assembling together, Heb. 10:25*
G1997.abbottSmith: a gathering together, assembly : He 10:25 ; seq. ἐπί , II Th 2:1 (cf. II Mac, l.c. ).†
G1997.strongs: from G1996 (ἐπισυνάγω); a complete collection; especially a Christian meeting (for worship)

G3099.thayer: (Hebrew), Midian, generally taken to mean or to include the peninsula of Sinai.
G3099.mounce: Madian, a district of Arabia Petra, Acts 7:29*
G3099.abbottSmith: Midian ; son of Abraham ( Ge 25:2 ). An Arabian tribe ( Ge 36:35 , Ps 82 (83):9 , al). γῆ Μ. (Heb. אֶרֶץ מִדְיָן ), the land of Midian ( Ex 2:15 , al.): Ac 7:29 .†
G3099.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04080); Madian (i.e. Midian), a region of Arabia

G133.abbottSmith: praise (Eccl.): θυσία αἰνέσεως ( Le 7:12 , זֶבַח תּוֹדָה ), He 13:15 .†
G133.strongs: from G134 (αἰνέω); a praising (the act), i.e. (specially) a thank(-offering)

G4275.mounce: to foresee, Acts 2:31; Gal. 3:8; to see before, Acts 21:29; in NT to have vividly present to the mind, to be mindful of, Acts 2:25*
G4275.abbottSmith: to foresee : Ac 2:31 ( προϊδών ; WH, προιδών ), Ga 3:8 .†

G666.strongs: from the participle of G548 (ἄπειμι); a being away

G5084.mounce: a keeping, custody; meton. a place of custody, prison, ward, Acts 4:3; 5:18; met. practical observance, strict performance, 1 Cor. 7:19*
G5084.abbottSmith: a watching , hence, imprisonment, ward (v. Deiss., BS , 267): Ac 4:3 5:18 .
G5084.strongs: from G5083 (τηρέω); a watching, i.e. (figuratively) observance, or (concretely) a prison

G2983.thayer: (a) I receive, get, (b) I take, lay hold of.
G2983.mounce: to take, take up, take in the hand, Mt. 10:38; 13:31, 33; to take on one’s self, sustain, Mt. 8:17; to take, seize, seize upon, Mt. 5:40; 21:34; Lk. 5:26; 1 Cor. 10:13; to catch, Lk. 5:5; 2 Cor. 12:16; to assume, put on, Phil. 2:7; to make a rightful or successful assumption of, Jn. 3:27; to conceive, Acts 28:15; to take by way of provision, Mt. 16:5; to get, get together, Mt. 16:9; to receive as payment, Mt. 17:24; Heb. 7:8; to take to wife, Mk. 12:19; to admit, give reception to, Jn. 6:21; 2 Jn. 10; met. to give mental reception to, Jn. 3:11; to be simply recipient of, to receive, Mt. 7:8; Jn. 7:23, 39; 19:30; Acts 10:43; in NT λαμβάνειν πεῖραν, to make encounter of a matter of difficulty or trial, Heb. 11:29, 36; λαμβάνειν ἀρχήν, to begin, Heb. 2:3; λαμβάνειν συμβούλιον, to take counsel, consult, Mt. 12:14; λαμβάνειν λήθην, to forget, 2 Pet. 1:9; λαμβάνειν ὑπόμνησιν, to recollect, call to mind, 2 Tim. 1:5; λαμβάνειν περιτομήν, to receive circumcision, be circumcised, Jn. 7:23; λαμβάνειν καταλλαγήν, to be reconciled, Rom. 5:11; λαμβάνειν κρίμα, to receive condemnation or punishment, be punished, Mk. 12:40; from the Hebrew, πρόσωπον λαμβάνειν, to accept the person of any one, show partiality towards, Lk. 20:21
G2983.abbottSmith: to take , lay hold of : absol., Mt 26:26 , Mk 14:22 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 5:40 26:52 , al. mult.; c. acc. pers., Mt 21:35 , Mk 12:3 , al.; pleonastic λαβών (M, Pr. , 230; Bl., §74, 2), Mt 13:31 14:19 , al.; so also indic., Mk 7:27 , Jo 19:1 , 40 , Re 8:5 , al.; metaph., c. acc. rei, ἀφορμήν , Ro 7:8 , 11 ; ὑδόδειγμα , Ja 5:10 ; id. c. acc. pers., φόβος , Lk 7:16 ; πνεῦμα , Lk 9:39 ; πειρασμός , I Co 1
G2983.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb, which is use only as an alternate in certain tenses; to take (in very many applications, literally and figuratively (properly objective or active, to get hold of; whereas G1209 (δέχομαι) is rather subjective or passive, to have offered to one; while G138 (αἱρέομαι) is more violent, to seize or remove))
G2983.kjv: accept, + be amazed, assay, attain, bring, X when I call, catch, come on (X unto), + forget, have, hold, obtain, receive (X after), take (away, up)

G4765.mounce: any small bird, spc. a sparrow, Mt. 10:29, 31; Lk. 12:6, 7*
G4765.strongs: diminutive of (a sparrow); a little sparrow

G855.mounce: not appearing, not seen, invisible; hence, ἄφαντος γενέσθαι, to disappear, vanish, Lk. 24:31*
G855.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω); non-manifested, i.e. invisible

G2086.thayer: I am yoked with one different from myself, unequally yoked.
G2086.mounce: to be unequally yoked or matched, 2 Cor. 6:14*
G2086.abbottSmith: to be unequally yoked : metaph., c. dat. pers., II Co 6:14 .†
G2086.strongs: from a compound of G2087 (ἕτερος) and G2218 (ζυγός); to yoke up differently, i.e. (figuratively) to associate discordantly

G5207.mounce: a son, Mt. 1:21, 25; 7:9; 13:55 freq.; a legitimate son, Heb. 12:8; a son artificially constituted, Acts 7:21; Heb. 11:24; a descendant, Mt. 1:1, 20; Mk. 12:35; in NT the young of an animal, Mt. 21:5; a spiritual son in respect of conversion or discipleship, 1 Pet. 5:13; from the Hebrew, a disciple, perhaps, Mt. 12:27; a son as implying connection in respect of membership, service, resemblance, manifestation, destiny, etc., Mt. 8:12; 9:15; 13:38; 23:15; Mk. 2:29; 3:17; Lk. 5:34; 10:6; 16:8; 20:34, 36; Jn. 17:12; Acts 2:25; 4:36; 13:10; Eph. 2:2; 5:6; Col. 3:6; 1 Thess. 5:5; 2 Thess. 2:3; υἱὸς θεοῦ, κ.τ.λ., son of God in respect of divinity, Mt. 4:3, 6; 14:33; Rom. 1:4; also, in respect of privilege and character, Mt. 5:9, 45; Lk. 6:35; Rom. 8:14, 19; 9:26; Gal. 3:26; ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ, κ.τ.λ., a title of the Messiah, Mt. 26:63; Mk. 3:11; 14:61; Jn. 1:34, 50; 20:31; υἱὸς ἀνθρώπου, a son of man, a man, Mk. 3:28; Eph. 3:5; Heb. 2:6; ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου, a title of the Messiah, Mt. 8:20 freq.; as also, ὁ υἱὸς Δαβίδ, (Δαύιδ) Mt. 12:23
G5207.abbottSmith: a son ; in the ordinary sense: Mt 10:37 , Mk 9:17 , Lk 1:13 , al. mult.; omitted with the art. of origin (WM, § 30, 3; Bl., §35, 2), τὸν τοῦ Ἰεσσαί , Ac 13:22 (LXX) ; also c. gen. anarth. (cl.), Σώπατρος Πύρρου Βεροιαῖος , Ac 20:4 ; c. adj., προτότοκος , Lk 2:7 ; μονογένης , Lk 7:12 ; opp. to νόθος , He 12:8 ; in a wider sense, of posterity: ὁ υἱ. Δαυΐδ , of the Messiah (cf. Dalman, Words , 316 ff
G5207.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "son" (sometimes of animals), used very widely of immediate, remote or figuratively, kinship

G3555.mounce: a brood of young birds, Lk. 13:34*

G385.thayer: I drag up, pull up, draw up, draw out.
G385.mounce: to draw up, to draw out, Lk. 14:5; Acts 11:10*
G385.abbottSmith: to draw up : Lk 14:5 , Ac 11:10 (in π. of pulling up barley; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G385.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4685 (σπάω); to take up or extricate

G1090.thayer: I work the soil, cultivate the earth.
G1090.mounce: to cultivate, till the earth, Heb. 6:7*
G1090.abbottSmith: to till the ground: pass., He 6:7 .

G2397.strongs: from G1492 (εἴδω); a sight (comparative figuratively "idea"), i.e. aspect

G5321.mounce: a disclosure, clear display, 2 Cor. 4:2; an outward evidencing of a latent principle, active exhibition, 1 Cor. 12:7*
G5321.strongs: from G5319 (φανερόω); exhibition, i.e. (figuratively) expression, (by extension) a bestowment

G331.thayer: a votive offering, a thing devoted to God; a curse, the thing cursed.
G331.mounce: a devoted thing, ordinarily in a bad sense, a person or thing accursed, Rom. 9:3; 1 Cor. 12:3; 16:22; Gal. 1:8, 9; a curse, execration, anathema, Acts 23:14*
G331.abbottSmith: prop. = τὸ ἀνατιθέμένον , that which is laid by to be kept, a votive offering (as ἀνάθημα in II Mac 2:13 , Lk 21:5 —where LT read -θεμα , v. M, Pr. , 46).
G331.strongs: from G394 (ἀνατίθεμαι); a (religious) ban or (concretely) excommunicated (thing or person)

G3840.thayer: from all sides, on all sides.
G3840.mounce: from every place, from all parts, on all sides, on every side, round about, Mk. 1:45; Lk. 19:43; Heb. 9:4*
G3840.abbottSmith: from all sides : Mk 1:45 , Lk 19:43 , He 9:4 .†
G3840.strongs: adverb (of source) from G3956 (πᾶς); from (i.e. on) all sides

G1659.abbottSmith: to make free : from sin, Jo 8:32 , 36 ; seq. ἀπό , Ro 6:18 , 22 8:2 , 21 ; τ. ἐλευθερία (dat. commodi), Ga 5:1 (on the "punctiliar" force of this verb, v. M, Pr. 149; cf. also Cremer, 251).†
G1659.strongs: from G1658 (ἐλεύθερος); to liberate, i.e. (figuratively) to exempt (from moral, ceremonial or mortal liability)

G5173.thayer: Tryphosa, a Christian woman in Rome, perhaps a sister of Tryphaena.
G5173.strongs: from G5172 (τρυφή); luxuriating; Tryphosa, a Christian female

G2702.thayer: I bear down, overpower; I give a vote or verdict, bring charges.
G2702.mounce: to bear down; to overpower, as sleep, Acts 20:9; καταφέρειν ψῆφον, to give a vote or verdict, Acts 26:10; to bring charges, Acts 25:7*
G2702.abbottSmith: to bring down : αἰτιώματα , Ac 25:7 ; ψῆφον , to cast a ballot , Ac 26:10 ; pass., to be borne down : ὕπνῳ , ἀπὸ τ. ὕ. , Ac 20:9 .†
G2702.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate); to bear down, i.e. (figuratively) overcome (with drowsiness); specially, to cast a vote

G5065.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G2532 (καί) and G1182 (δέκατος); fourteenth

G997.thayer: I come to the rescue of, come to help, help.
G997.mounce: to run to the aid of those who cry for help; to advance to the assistance of any one, help, aid, succor, Mt. 15:25; Mk. 9:22, 24
G997.abbottSmith: to come to aid, to help, succour : absol., Ac 21:28 ; c dat., Mt 15:25 , Mk 9:22 , 24 Ac 16:9 , II Co 6:2 (LXX) , He 2:18 , Re 12:16 .†
G997.strongs: from G998 (βοηθός); to aid or relieve

G3508.thayer: Naphtali, son of Jacob, founder of a tribe which occupied territory.
G3508.mounce: Nephthalim, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 4:13, 15; Rev. 5:6*
G3508.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05321); Nephthaleim (i.e. Naphthali), a tribe in Palestine

G5221.abbottSmith: to go to meet, meet : c. dat. pers. (v. M, Pr. , 64), Mt 8:28 28:9 , Mk 5:2 , Lk 8:27 17:12 ( ἀπ- , WH, txt.), Jo 4:51 11:20 , 30 12:18 , Ac 16:16 ; of meeting in battle, Lk 14:31 .†
G5221.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to go opposite (meet) under (quietly), i.e. to encounter, fall in with

G2578.mounce: trans. to bend, inflect the knee, Rom. 11:4; Eph. 3:14 intrans. to bend, bow, Rom. 14:11; Phil. 2:10*
G2578.abbottSmith: to bend , bow : c. acc. rei, γόνυ, τὰ γ. ; c. dat. pers., τῇ Βάαλ , Ro 11:4 (LXX) ; πρὸς τ. πατέρα , Eph 3:14 ; by meton., πᾶν γόνυ for πᾶς , Ro 14:11 (LXX) , Phl 2:10 (cf. ἀνα- , συν-κάμπτω ).†
G2578.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to bend

G2071.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2071.kjv: shall (should) be (have), (shall) come (to pass), X may have, X fall, what would follow, X live long, X sojourn

G352.thayer: I raise myself, look up, am elated.
G352.mounce: pr. to raise up one’s self, look up, Lk. 13:11; Jn. 8:7, 10; met. to look up cheerily, to be cheered, Lk. 21:28*
G352.abbottSmith: to lift oneself up ; bodily; Lk 13:11 , Jo 8:7 , 10 ; mentally, to be elated : Lk 21:28 (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G352.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) (in the sense of reversal) and G2955 (κύπτω); to unbend, i.e. rise; figuratively, be elated

G1651.thayer: (a) I reprove, rebuke, discipline, (b) I expose, show to be guilty.
G1651.mounce: to put to proof, to test; to convict, Jn. 8:46; Jas. 2:9; to refute, confute, 1 Cor. 14:24; Tit. 1:9; to detect, lay bare, expose, Jn. 3:20; Eph. 5:11, 13; to reprove, rebuke, Mt. 18:15; Lk. 3:19; 1 Tim. 5:20; to discipline, chastise, Heb. 12:5; Rev. 3:19; pass. to experience conviction, Jn. 3:20; 1 Cor. 14:24
G1651.abbottSmith: in Hom., to treat with contempt .
G1651.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to confute, admonish
G1651.kjv: convict, convince, tell a fault, rebuke, reprove

G97.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle); and G1388 (δόλος); undeceitful, i.e. (figuratively) unadulterated

G4158.thayer: reaching to the feet (of a garment).
G4158.mounce: reaching to the feet; as subst. i.e. ἐσθής, a long, flowing robe reaching down to the feet, Rev. 1:13*
G4158.abbottSmith: reaching to the feet : of a garment (sc. χιτών , cf. Ex 25:6 ( 7 ) 28:4 , 27 ( 31 ) 29:5 35:9 , Ez 9:2 , 3 , 11 ; Xen., al.), Re 1:13 (for -η , LT 7 read -ην ; v. M, Pr. , 49).†
G4158.strongs: from G4228 (πούς) and another element of uncertain affinity; a dress (G2066 (ἐσθής) implied) reaching the ankles

G1922.thayer: knowledge of a particular point (directed towards a particular object); perception, discernment, recognition, intuition.
G1922.mounce: the coming at the knowledge of a thing, ascertainment, Rom. 3:20; a distance perception or impression, acknowledgment, insight, Col. 2:2
G1922.abbottSmith: acquaintance, discernment, recognition (Plut., al.): Phl 1:9 , Col 3:10 ; c. gen. rei, Col 1:9 2:2 , Phm 6 ; τ. ἀληθείας , I Ti 2:4 , II Ti 2:25 3:7 , Tit 1:1 , He 10:26 ; τ. ἁμαρτίας , Ro 3:20 ; c. gen. pers., of God: Eph 1:17 , Col 1:10 , II Pe 1:2 , 3 ; of Christ: Eph 4:13 , II Pe 1:8 2:20 ; of God and Christ: II Pe 1:2 ; κατ ̓ ἐ. , Ro 10:2 ; ἔχειν ἐν ἐ. , Ro 1:28 (v. AR, Eph. , 248 ff.; and fo
G1922.strongs: from G1921 (ἐπιγινώσκω); recognition, i.e. (by implication) full discernment, acknowledgement

G1527.strongs: from G1520 (εἷς) repeated with G2596 (κατά) inserted; severally

G2724.mounce: an accusation, crimination, Jn. 18:29; 1 Tim. 5:19; Tit. 1:6*
G2724.abbottSmith: an accusation , charge : c. gen. pers., Jo 18:29 ; seq. κατά , c. id., I Ti 5:19 ; c. gen. rei, Tit 1:6 .†
G2724.strongs: from G2725 (κατήγορος); a complaint ("category"), i.e. criminal charge

G1403.mounce: pr. reception of guests; in NT a banquet, feast, Lk. 5:29; 14:13
G1403.strongs: from G1209 (δέχομαι); a reception, i.e. convivial entertainment

G5311.mounce: height, Eph. 3:18; Rev. 21:16; met. exaltation, dignity, eminence, Jas. 1:9; from the Hebrew, the height of heaven, Lk. 1:78; 24:49; Eph. 4:8*
G5311.abbottSmith: height : Eph 3:18 Ja 1:9 , Re 21:16 ; of heaven (EV, on high ), ἐξ ὕ. , Lk 1:78 24:49 ; εἰς ὕ. , Eph 4:8 (LXX) †
G5311.strongs: from a derivative of G5228 (ὑπέρ); elevation, i.e. (abstractly) altitude, (specially), the sky, or (figuratively) dignity

G405.thayer: an enslaver, one who forcibly enslaves, a kidnapper.
G405.abbottSmith: a slave-dealer , kidnapper : I Ti 1:10 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G405.strongs: from a derivative of a compound of G435 (ἀνήρ) and G4228 (πούς); an enslaver (as bringing men to his feet)

G1448.thayer: trans: I bring near; intrans: I come near, approach.
G1448.mounce: pr. to cause to approach; in NT intrans. to approach, draw near, Mt. 21:1; Lk. 18:35; met. to be at hand, Mt. 3:2; 4:17; μέχρι θανάτου ἐγγίζειν, to be at the point of death, Phil. 2:30; from Hebrew to draw near to God, to offer Him reverence and worship, Mt. 15:8; Heb. 7:19; Jas. 4:8; used of God, to draw near to men, assist them, bestow favors on them, Jas. 4:8
G1448.abbottSmith: trans., to bring near : Ge 48:10 , Is 5:8 . Intrans., to come near : absol., Mt 26:46 , Mk 14:42 , Lk 18:40 19:41 21:20 , 28 24:15 , Ac 21:33 23:15 ; c. adv., ὅπου , Lk 12:33 ; c. dat., Lk 7:12 15:1 , 25 22:47 , Ac 9:3 10:9 22:6 ; τ. θεῷ , He 7:19 , Ja 4:8 ; seq. εἰς , Mt 21:1 , Mk 11:1 , Lk 18:35 19:29 24:28 ; πρός , c. dat., Lk 19:37 ; μέχρι θανάτου , Phl 2:30 ; of time, ὥρα , Mt 26:45 ; ἡμέρα ,
G1448.strongs: from G1451 (ἐγγύς); to make near, i.e. (reflexively) approach
G1448.kjv: approach, be at hand, come (draw) near, be (come, draw) nigh

G3088.mounce: a light, lamp, candle, etc., Mt. 5:15; Mk. 4:21; met. a lamp, as a figure of a distinguished teacher, Jn. 5:35
G3088.abbottSmith: a lamp (portable, and usually set on a stand, λυχνία ): Mt 5:15 , Lk 11:36 ; ἔρχεται ὁ λ. , Mk 4:21 ; λ. ἅπτειν , Lk 8:16 11:33 15:8 ; φῶς λύχνου , Re 18:23 ; id. opp. to φ. ἡλίου , Re 22:5 ; metaph., of the eye, Mt 6:22 , Lk 11:34 ; of John the Baptist, Jo 5:35 ; of the Lamb, Re 21:23 ; of prophecy, II Pe 1:19 ; of spiritual readiness, pl., λύχνοι (as always in LXX; freq. in Att. λύχνα ), Lk 12:3
G3088.strongs: from the base of G3022 (λευκός); a portable lamp or other illuminator (literally or figuratively)

G1491.thayer: visible form, shape, appearance, outward show, kind, species, class.
G1491.mounce: form, external appearance, Lk. 3:22; 9:29; Jn. 5:37; kind, species, 1 Thess. 5:22; sight, perception, 2 Cor. 5:7*
G1491.abbottSmith: that which is seen, appearance, external form : Lk 3:22 9:29 , Jo 5:37 , II Co 5:7 ( ICC in l.). form, sort, kind : I Th 5:22 .†
G1491.strongs: from G1492 (εἴδω); a view, i.e. form (literally or figuratively)

G2753.mounce: to order, command, direct, bid, Mt. 8:18; 14:19, 28
G2753.abbottSmith: to urge on , bid by word of mouth, order , command (mostly of one in authority): c. acc. et. inf. aor., Mt 14:19 , 28 18:25 27:64 , Lk 18:40 , Ac 4:15 5:34 8:38 22:30 23:10 25:6 , 17 ; with ellipse of acc., Mt 8:18 14:9 27:58 , Ac 12:19 21:33 ; c. acc. et. inf. Pres., Ac 21:34 22:24 23:3 , 35 25:21 27:43 ; with ellipse of acc., Ac 16:22 ; ptcp. aor., Ac 25:23 .†
G2753.strongs: from a primary (to urge on); "hail"; to incite by word, i.e. order

G1988.mounce: pr. one who stands by; one who is set over; in NT in voc., equivalent to διδάσκαλε, or ῥαββί, master, doctor, Lk. 5:5; 8:24, 45; 9:33, 49; 17:13*
G1988.abbottSmith: a chief, commander, master : Lk 5:5 8:24 , 45 9:33 , 49 17:13 (cf. Dalman, Words , 336 ff.).†
G1988.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a presumed derivative of G2476 (ἵστημι); an appointee over, i.e. commander (teacher)

G4942.thayer: I dissemble with, play a part with.
G4942.mounce: to dissemble, feign with, or in the same manner as another, join in the playing of the hypocrite, Gal. 2:13*
G4942.abbottSmith: to play a part with, dissemble with : c. dat., Ga 2:13 (Polyb.).†
G4942.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); to act hypocritically in concert with

G5399.thayer: I fear, dread, reverence, am afraid, terrified.
G5399.mounce: has an active form, φοβέω (G5828), but only occurs as a passive (deponent) in our literature, to fear, dread, Mt. 10:26; 14:5; to fear reverentially, to reverence, Mk. 6:20; Lk. 1:50; Acts 10:2; Eph. 5:33; Rev. 11:18; to be afraid to do a thing, Mt. 2:22; Mk. 9:32; to be reluctant, to scruple, Mt. 1:20; to fear, be apprehensive, Acts 27:17; 2 Cor. 11:3; 12:20; to be fearfully anxious, Heb. 4:1; absol. to be fearful, afraid, alarmed, Mt. 14:27; 17:6, 7; Mk. 16:8; to be fearfully impressed, Rom. 11:20
G5399.abbottSmith: in Hom., to put to flight . Pass., to be put to flight, to flee affrighted
G5399.strongs: from G5401 (φόβος); to frighten, i.e. (passively) to be alarmed; by analogy, to be in awe of, i.e. revere

G261.abbottSmith: unlearned , ignorant : II Pe 3:16 (on the rareness of this word, v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G261.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3129 (μανθάνω); ignorant

G4462.thayer: Rabbi, my master, teacher; a title of respect often applied to Christ.
G4462.mounce: a form of ῥαββί, my teacher, Mk. 10:51; Jn. 20:16
G4462.abbottSmith: Rabboni : Mk 10:51 (WH, mg., κύριε ῥαββεί ), Jo 20:16 .†
G4462.strongs: of Chaldee origin; corresponding to G4461 (ῥαββί)

G291.thayer: Ampliatus (Amplias), a male member of the church at Rome, probably of the imperial household.
G291.strongs: contracted for Latin ampliatus (enlarged); Amplias, a Roman Christian

G896.thayer: Baal, chief deity of the Phoenicians and other Semitic nations.
G896.mounce: Baal, (Hebrew for Master) pr. name, indecl., Rom. 11:4*
G896.abbottSmith: Baal : Ro 11:4 (LXX) . The fem. art. here agrees with the usage of LXX, where, following a similar Hebrew practice ( בֹּשֶׁת for בַּעַל ), αἰσχύνη appears to have been substituted in reading for the written Βάαλ (cf. III Ki 18:19 ), and to account for the freq. use of the fem. art. bef. Β . The usage, however, is not general, and in the passage cited in Ro ( III Ki 19:18 ), LXX reads τῷ Β .†
G896.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01168); Baal, a Phœnician deity (used as a symbol of idolatry)

G1943.thayer: I put a cover on, cover up.
G1943.mounce: to cover over; met. to cover or veil by a pardon, Rom. 4:7*
G1943.abbottSmith: to cover over, cover up : metaph., Ro 4:7 (LXX) .†
G1943.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to conceal, i.e. (figuratively) forgive

G5534.mounce: impersonal verb, there is need or occasion, it is necessary, it is requisite; it becomes, it is proper, Jas. 3:10*
G5534.strongs: third person singular of the same as G5530 (χράομαι) or G5531 (χράω) used impersonally; it needs (must or should) be

G5440.thayer: a phylactery, an amulet, a parchment capsule containing little parchment rolls with the Hebrew texts, affixed to the left upper arm or the forehead of men at morning prayer, and regarded as a protection (hence the name) against evil spirits.
G5440.mounce: the station of a guard or watch; a preservative, safeguard; hence, a phylactery or amulet, worn about the person; from which circumstance the word is used in the NT as a term for the Jewish Tephillin or prayer-case, which took their rise from the injunction in Deut. 6:8; 11:18; Mt. 23:5*
G5440.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G5442 (φυλάσσω); a guard-case, i.e. "phylactery" for wearing slips of Scripture texts

G17.thayer: a doer of that which is good.
G17.mounce: doing good or right; subst., a well-doer, 1 Pet. 2:14*
G17.abbottSmith: doing well , acting rightly (Plut.): I Pe 2:14 (Cremer, 8; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G17.strongs: from G18 (ἀγαθός) and G4160 (ποιέω); a well-doer, i.e. virtuous

G3707.strongs: from G3708 (ὁράω); gazed at, i.e. (by implication) capable of being seen

G2385.thayer: James, (a) the Small, son of Alphaeus, and one of the Twelve, (b) half-brother of Jesus, (c) father (?) of Jude, (d) son of Zebedee, and brother of John, one of the Twelve, killed A.D. 44.
G2385.mounce: James, pr. name (1) Son of Zebedee, Mt. 4:21. (2) Son of Alphaeus and Mary, brother of Jude, Mt. 10:3. (3) James the less, brother of Jesus, Gal. 1:19
G2385.abbottSmith: James ; Son of Zebedee: Mt 4:21 , Mk 1:19 , 29 , Ac 1:13 12:2 , al. Son of Alphæus: Mt 10:3 , Mk 2:14 , al.; commonly identified with Ἰ. ὁ μικρός , James the little , son of Mary (v.s. Μαρία , 3; Κλωπᾶς ), Mt 27:56 , Mk 15:40 16:1 (cf. Jo 19:25 ). The Lord's brother (v.s. ἀδελφός ): Mt 13:55 , Mk 6:3 , Ac 12:17 , al., I Co 15:7 (probably), Ga 1:19 2:9 , 12 , Ja 1:1 , Ju 1 . The father of the apost
G2385.strongs: the same as G2384 (Ἰακώβ) Græcized; Jacobus, the name of three Israelites

G1189.thayer: I want for myself; I want, need; I beg, request, beseech, pray.
G1189.mounce: to be in want, to need; to ask, request, Mt. 9:38; Lk. 5:12; 8:28, 38; in NT absol. to pray, offer prayer, beseech, supplicate, Lk. 21:36; 22:32; Acts 4:31; 8:22, 24
G1189.strongs: middle voice of G1210 (δέω); to beg (as binding oneself), i.e. petition

G2932.thayer: (a) I acquire, win, get, purchase, buy, (b) I possess, win mastery over.
G2932.mounce: to get, procure, provide, Mt. 10:9; to make gain, gain, Lk. 18:12; to purchase, Acts 8:20; 22:28; to be the cause or occasion of purchasing, Acts 1:18; to preserve, save, Lk. 21:19; to get under control, to be winning the mastery over, 1 Thess. 4:4; perf. κέκτημαι, to possess*
G2932.abbottSmith: in pres., impf., fut. and aor., to procure for oneself , get , gain , acquire (the pf. and plpf., to have acquired , hence to possess , do not occur in NT): c. acc. rei, Mt 10:9 , Lk 18:12 , Ac 8:20 ; c. gen. pret., Ac 22:28 ; ἐκ c. gen. pret., Ac 1:18 ; τ. ψυχὰς ὑμῶν (MM, xvi), Lk 21:19 ; τ. ἑαυτοῦ σκεῦος κτᾶσθαι , I Th 4:4 (where if σ. = body , κ. must = pf., κέκτημαι ; v. MM, xvi; M, Th. , in l
G2932.strongs: a primary verb; to get, i.e. acquire (by any means; own)

G5259.mounce: (1) gen., under; hence, used to express influence, causation, agency; by, Mt. 1:22 freq.; by the agency of, at the hands of, 2 Cor. 11:24; Heb. 12:3. (2) acc., under, with the idea of motion associated, Mt. 5:15; under, Jn. 1:49; 1 Cor. 10:1; under subjection to, Rom. 6:14; 1 Tim. 6:1; of time, at, about, Acts 5:21
G5259.abbottSmith: C. gen., primarily of place, under , hence, metaph., of the efficient cause, by : after passive verbs, c. gen. pers., Mt 1:22 , Mk 1:5 , Lk 2:18 , Jo 14:21 , Ac 4:11 , I Co 1:11 , He 3:4 , al.; c. gen. rei, Mt 8:24 , Lk 7:24 , Ro 3:21 , al.; with neut. verbs and verbs with pass. meaning, Mt 17:12 , Mk 5:26 , I Co 10:9 , 10 I Th 2:14 , al. C. acc., under ; of motion: Mt 5:15 8:8 , Mk 4:21 , Lk 13:3
G5259.strongs: a primary preposition; under, i.e. (with the genitive case) of place (beneath), or with verbs (the agency or means, through); (with the accusative case) of place (whither (underneath) or where (below) or time (when (at))
G5259.kjv: among, by, from, in, of, under, with

G4733.mounce: pr. what is solid and firm; met. firmness, steadfastness, constancy, Col. 2:5*
G4733.strongs: from G4732 (στερεόω); something established, i.e. (abstractly) confirmation (stability)

G5571.mounce: false, lying, Acts 6:13; Rev. 2:2; in NT pl. maintainers of religious falsehood, corrupters of the truth of God, Rev. 21:8*
G5571.abbottSmith: lying, false, untrue (of persons and things): Re 2:2 ; μάρτυρες , Ac 6:13 ; as subst., ὁ ψ. , a liar : Re 21:8 .†
G5571.strongs: from G5574 (ψεύδομαι); untrue, i.e. erroneous, deceitful, wicked

G5213.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G5213.abbottSmith: Emphatic (so always in nom. exc. when preceded by the art., v. infr., iii); self ( ipse ), expressing opposition, distinction, exclusion, etc., αὐ. ἐκχυθήσεται , Lk 5:37 ; αὐ. ἐγινώσκεν , Jo 2:25 ; αὐ. ὑμεῖς , Jo 3:28 ; καὶ αὐ. ἐγώ , Ro 15:14 ; αὐ. Ἰησοῦς , Jo 2:24 ; αὐ. καὶ οἱ μετ’ αὐτοῦ , Mk 2:25 ; ὑμεῖς αὐ. , Mk 6:31 ; esp. (as freq in cl.) αὐ. ὁ , Mt 3:4 , Mk 6:17 , Jo 16:27 , I Th 3:11 , al.;
G5213.strongs: irregular dative case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); to (with or by) you

G1325.thayer: I offer, give; I put, place.
G1325.mounce: pluperfect, ἐδεδώκειν, to give, bestow, present, Mt. 4:9; 6:11; Jn. 3:16; 17:2, et al. freq.; to give, cast, throw, Mt. 7:6; to supply, suggest, Mt. 10:19; Mk. 13:11; to distribute alms, Mt. 19:21; Lk. 11:41; to pay tribute, etc., Mt. 22:17; Mk. 12:14; Lk. 20:22; to be the author or source of a thing, Lk. 12:51; Rom. 11:8; to grant, permit, allow, Acts 2:27; 13:35; Mt. 13:11; 19:11; to deliver to, entrust, commit to the charge of anyone, Mt. 25:15; Mk. 12:9; to give or deliver up, Lk. 22:19; Jn. 6:51; to reveal, teach, Acts 7:38; to appoint, constitute, Eph. 1:22; 4:11; to consecrate, devote, offer in sacrifice, 2 Cor. 8:5; Gal. 1:4; Rev. 8:3; to present, expose one’s self in a place, Acts 19:31; to recompense, Rev. 2:23; to attribute, ascribe, Jn. 9:24; Rev. 11:13; from the Hebrew, to place, put, fix, inscribe, Heb. 8:10; 10:16; to infix, impress, 2 Cor. 12:7; Rev. 13:16; to inflict, Jn. 18:22; 19:3; 2 Thess. 1:8; to give in charge, assign, Jn. 5:36; 17:4; Rev. 9:5; to exhibit, put forth, Mt. 24:24; Acts 2:19; to yield, bear fruit, Mt. 13:8; διδόναι ἐργασίαν, to endeavor, strive, Lk. 12:58; διδόναι ἀπόκρισιν, to answer, reply, Jn. 1:22; διδόναι τόπον, to give place, yield, Lk. 14:9; Rom. 12:19
G1325.abbottSmith: to give —in various senses, acc. to context— bestow, grant, supply, deliver, commit, yield : absol., Ac 20:35 ; c. acc. rei et dat. pers., Mt 4:9 5:31 , Jo 1:12 , al. mult.; c. dat. pers., seq. ἐκ , Mt 25:8 ; id. c. gen. part.. Re 2:17 ; c. acc. pers., Jo 3:16 , Re 20:13 , al.; δεξιάς , Ga 2:9 ; φίλημα , Lk 7:45 ; γνῶσιν , Lk 1:77 ; κρίσιν , Jo 5:22 ; of seed yielding fruit, Mk 4:7 , 8; ἐργασίαν (
G1325.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb (which is used as an alternative in most of the tenses); to give (used in a very wide application, properly, or by implication, literally or figuratively; greatly modified by the connection)
G1325.kjv: adventure, bestow, bring forth, commit, deliver (up), give, grant, hinder, make, minister, number, offer, have power, put, receive, set, shew, smite (+ with the hand), strike (+ with the palm of the hand), suffer, take, utter, yield

G2106.thayer: I am well-pleased, think it good, am resolved.
G2106.mounce: to think well, approve, consent, take delight or pleasure, Mt. 3:17; 17:5; Mk. 1:11; Lk. 3:22; 12:32
G2106.abbottSmith: c. inf. (Polyb., al.), to be well pleased, to think it good, to give consent (so freq. in π. in legal docu­ments; Milligan, Th. , 22 f.): Lk 12:32 , Ro 15:26-27 , I Co 1:21 , Ga 1:15 , Col 1:19 , R, mg. ( ICC , in l., but v. infr.), I Th 2:8 3:1 ; μᾶλλον εὐ. , II Co 5:8 ; c. acc et inf. (Polyb., i, 8, 4), Col, l.c. , R, txt. (Lft., in l).
G2106.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G1380 (δοκέω); to think well of, i.e. approve (an act); specially, to approbate (a person or thing)
G2106.kjv: think good, (be well) please(-d), be the good (have, take) pleasure, be willing

G832.mounce: to play on a pipe or flute, pipe, Mt. 11:17; Lk. 7:32; 1 Cor. 14:7*
G832.abbottSmith: to play on a flute , to pipe : mid., Mt 11:17 , Lk 7:32 , I Co 14:7 .†
G832.strongs: from G836 (αὐλός); to play the flute

G609.thayer: I smite, cut off, cut loose; mid: I emasculate, castrate, mutilate myself.
G609.mounce: to cut off, Mk. 9:43, 45; Jn. 18:10, 26; Acts 27:32; to castrate, make a eunich, Gal. 5:12*
G609.abbottSmith: to cut off : Mk 9:43 , 45 , Jo 18:10 , 26 , Ac 27:32 . Mid., to mutilate oneself , have oneself mutilated : Ga 5:12 (cf. De 23:1 LXX; and v. Cremer, 751; MM, s.v.).†
G609.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2875 (κόπτω); to amputate; reflexively (by irony) to mutilate (the privy parts)

G1499.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G2532 (καί); if also (or even)

G5361.thayer: loving like a brother, loving one's brethren.
G5361.mounce: brother-loving; in NT loving the members of the Christian brotherhood, 1 Pet. 3:8*
G5361.abbottSmith: loving one's brother, loving like a brother (Soph., Xen., al.): of Christians' love for one another (EV, loving as brethren ), I Pe 3:8 .†
G5361.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G80 (ἀδελφός); fond of brethren, i.e. fraternal

G1208.thayer: second; with the article: in the second place, for the second time.
G1208.mounce: second, Mt. 22:26; τὸ δεύτερον, again, the second time, another time, Jude 5; so ἐκ δευτέρου, Mt. 26:42; and ἐν τῷ δευτέρῳ, Acts 7:13
G1208.abbottSmith: second in order, with or without idea of time: Mt 22:26 , 39 , al.; θάνατος , Re 2:11 20:14 21:8 ; χάρις , II Co 1:15 . In neut. as adv., secondly, a second time ; opp. to πρῶτον : Jo 3:4 21:16 , I Co 12:28 , Re 19:3 ; τὸ δ. , II Co 13:2 , Ju 5 ; ἐν τ. δ. , at the second time : Ac 7:13 ; in later usage (as I Mac 9:1 ), ἐκ δ. , Mk 14:72 , Jo 9:24 , Ac 11:9 , He 9:28 .
G1208.strongs: as the comparative of G1417 (δύο); (ordinal) second (in time, place, or rank; also adverb)

G498.thayer: I set myself against, resist (the attack of).
G498.mounce: to post in adverse array, as an army; mid. to set oneself in opposition, resist, Acts 18:6; Rom. 13:2; Jas. 5:6; to be averse, Jas. 4:6; 1 Pet. 5:5*
G498.abbottSmith: to range on battle against ; mid., to set oneself against , resist : absol., Ac 18:6 ; c. dat., Ro 13:2 , Ja 4:6 5:6 , I Pe 5:5 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G498.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and the middle voice of G5021 (τάσσω); to range oneself against, i.e. oppose

G3820.thayer: old, ancient, not new or recent.
G3820.mounce: old, not new or recent, Mt. 9:16, 17; 13:52; Lk. 5:36
G3820.abbottSmith: old , ancient ; opp. to νέος , καινός : οἶνος , Lk 5:39 ; διαθήκη , II Co 3:14 ; ἐντολή , I Jo 2:7 ; ζύμη , I Co 5:7 , 8 ; ὁ π. ἄςθρωπος (for similar phrases, v. Westc., Eph. , 68), Ro 6:6 , Eph 4:22 , Col 3:9 ; neut. pl., καινὰ καὶ π. , Mt 13:52 ; of things not merely old, but worn by use (as Jos 9:4 , 5 , LXX), Mt 9:16 , 17 , Mk 2:21 , 22 , Lk 5:36 , 37 .†
G3820.strongs: from G3819 (πάλαι); antique, i.e. not recent, worn out

G2959.abbottSmith: a lady : II Jo 1 , 5 (on the interpretation, v. ICC , 167 ff.).†
G2959.strongs: feminine of G2962 (κύριος); Cyria, a Christian woman

G1964.thayer: I take an oath, swear falsely.
G1964.mounce: to forswear one’s self, to fail of observing one’s oath, Mt. 5:33*
G1964.abbottSmith: to swear falsely, forswear oneself : Mt 5:33 .†

G1036.thayer: I cause to sink; mid: I sink, submerge, drown.
G1036.mounce: to immerse, submerge, cause to sink, Lk. 5:7; to plunge deep, drown, 1 Tim. 6:9*
G1036.abbottSmith: trans., to cause to sink, to sink : metaph., εἰς ὄλεθρον , I Ti 6:9 ; pass., to sink , intrans.: Lk 5:7 .†
G1036.strongs: from G1037 (βυθός); to sink; by implication, to drown

G66.mounce: belonging to the field, wild; fierce, raging, Mt. 3:4; Mk. 1:6; Jude 13*
G66.abbottSmith: living in fields, wild : μέλι . Mt 3:4 , Mk 1:6 . savage, fierce : Ju 13 . (Cf. usage in π. of a malignant wound; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G66.strongs: from G68 (ἀγρός); wild (as pertaining to the country), literally (natural) or figuratively (fierce)

G715.mounce: also spelled ἄρκτος, a bear, Rev. 13:2*
G715.abbottSmith: a bear : Re 13:2 . (This form is also found in late Inscr.; MM, s.v.)†
G715.strongs: probably from G714 (ἀρκέω); a bear (as obstructing by ferocity)

G2621.thayer: I recline (at table); more often: I keep my bed, am lying ill (in bed).
G2621.mounce: to lie, be in a recumbent position, be laid down, Mk. 1:30; 2:4; Lk. 5:25; Jn. 5:3, 6; Acts 9:33; 28:8; to recline at table, Mk. 2:15; 14:3; Lk. 5:29; 7:37; 1 Cor. 8:10*
G2621.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to lie down, i.e. (by implication) be sick; specially, to recline at a meal

G594.thayer: (properly: reception, welcome, of guests), acceptance, appreciation, approbation.
G594.mounce: pr. reception, welcome; met. reception of hearty assent, 1 Tim. 1:15; 4:9*
G594.abbottSmith: acceptance , approbation (Field, Notes , 203): I Ti 1:15 4:9 (Cremer, 686; MM, s.v.).†

G5424.mounce: pr. the diaphragm, midriff; the mind, intellect, understanding, 1 Cor. 14:20 (2x)*
G5424.abbottSmith: chiefly in Hom. and Trag., but also in Plat., al., both sing. and pl.;
G5424.strongs: probably from an obsolete (to rein in or curb; compare G5420 (φράσσω)); the midrif (as a partition of the body), i.e. (figuratively and by implication, of sympathy) the feelings (or sensitive nature; by extension (also in the plural) the mind or cognitive faculties)

G5604.thayer: the pain of childbirth, acute pain, severe agony, a snare.
G5604.mounce: the spasms or pains, of a woman in travail, a birth pang, 1 Thess. 5:3; pl. met. birth throes, preliminary troubles to the development of a catastrophe, Mt. 24:8; Mk. 13:8; from the Hebrew, a stringent band, a snare, noose, Acts 2:24*
G5604.abbottSmith: a birth pang, travail pain : I Th 5:3 ; metaph., of extreme suffering, Mt 24:8 , Mk 13:8 ; ὠδῖνες θανάτου ( Ps 17 (18):4 הֶבְלֵי מָוֶת ), Ac 2:24 .†
G5604.strongs: akin to G3601 (ὀδύνη); a pang or throe, especially of childbirth

G3469.thayer: I blame, find fault with, slander.
G3469.mounce: to find fault with, censure, blame, 2 Cor. 8:20; passively, 2 Cor. 6:3*
G3469.abbottSmith: to find fault with , blame : II Co 8:20 ; pass., II Co 6:3 .†
G3469.strongs: from G3470 (μῶμος); to carp at, i.e. censure (discredit)

G2309.thayer: I will, wish, desire, am willing, intend, design.
G2309.mounce: to exercise the will, properly by an unimpassioned operation; to be willing, Mt. 17:4; to be inclined, disposed, Rom. 13:3; to choose, Lk. 1:62; to intend, design, Lk. 14:28; to will, Jn. 5:21; 21:22; ἤθελον, I could wish, Gal. 4:20
G2309.abbottSmith: to will , be willing , wish , desire (more freq. than βούλομαι , q.v., in vernac. and late Gk., also in MGr.; for various views as to its relation to β. , v. Thayer, 286; but v. also Bl., §24, s.v.): absol., Ro 9:16 , I Co 4:19 12:18 , Ja 4:15 ; τ. θεοῦ θέλοντος , Ac 18:21 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 20:21 , Mk 14:36 , Jo 15:7 , Ro 7:15 , 16 , I Co 4:21 7:36 , Ga 5:17 ; c. inf., Mt 5:40 , Mk 10:43 , Jo 6:21
G2309.strongs: apparently strengthened from the alternate form of G138 (αἱρέομαι); to determine (as an active option from subjective impulse; whereas G1014 (βούλομαι) properly denotes rather a passive acquiescence in objective considerations), i.e. choose or prefer (literally or figuratively); by implication, to wish, i.e. be inclined to (sometimes adverbially, gladly); impersonally for the future tense, to be about to; by Hebraism, to delight in
G2309.kjv: desire, be disposed (forward), intend, list, love, mean, please, have rather, (be) will (have, -ling, - ling(-ly))

G3446.mounce: pr. a shaping, moulding; in NT external form, appearance, 2 Tim. 3:5; a settled form. prescribed system, Rom. 2:20*
G3446.strongs: from G3445 (μορφόω); formation, i.e. (by implication), appearance (semblance or (concretely) formula)

G3751.mounce: the loins, Mt. 3:4; Mk. 1:6. On the accent see BDF, 13.
G3751.abbottSmith: the loin : Mt 3:4 , Mk 1:6 ; metaph., περιζώννυσθαι ( ἀναζ- ) τ. ὀσφύας , Lk 12:35 , Eph 6:14 , I Pe 1:13 ; regarded, as by the Hebrews, as the seat of generative power, He 7:5 , 10 ; metaph., καρπὸς τ. ὀσφύος , Ac 2:30 .
G3751.strongs: of uncertain affinity; the loin (externally), i.e. the hip; internally (by extension) procreative power

G2516.thayer: I am sitting, sit down, am seated.
G2516.mounce: to seat one’s self, sit down, Mt. 26:55; Lk. 2:46; Jn. 4:6; 1:20; 20:12; Acts 6:15; 20:9*
G2516.abbottSmith: to sit down , sit : Jo 20:12 ; seq. ἐν , Mt 26:55 , Lk 2:46 , Jo 11:20 , Ac 6:15 ; seq. ἐπί , c. gen., Ac 20:9 ; id. c. dat., Jo 4:6 (cf. παρα-καθέζ- , κάθημαι ).†
G2516.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); to sit down

G4423.abbottSmith: a fluttering , excitement , caused by any emotion, but esp. by fear, hence, terror : φοβεῖσθαι πτόησιν , to be afraid with (cogn. acc.) or of any terror (v. ICC , in l.): I Pe 3:6 .†

G3364.strongs: i.e. G3756 (οὐ) and G3361 (μή); a double negative strengthening the denial; not at all
G3364.kjv: any more, at all, by any (no) means, neither, never, no (at all), in no case (wise), nor ever, not (at all, in any wise)

G986.thayer: Blastus, chamberlain of King Herod Agrippa I.
G986.strongs: perhaps the same as the base of G985 (βλαστάνω); Blastus, an officer of Herod Agrippa

G4717.thayer: I fix to the cross, crucify; fig: I destroy, mortify.
G4717.mounce: to fix stakes; later, to crucify, affix to the cross, Mt. 20:19; 23:34; met. to crucify, to mortify, to deaden, to make a sacrifice of, Gal. 5:24; pass. to be cut off from a thing, as by a violent death, to be come dead to, Gal. 6:14
G4717.strongs: from G4716 (σταυρός); to impale on the cross; figuratively, to extinguish (subdue) passion or selfishness

G5413.thayer: a burden; the freight of a ship.
G5413.mounce: a load, burden; of a ship, freight, cargo, Acts 27:10; met. a burden of imposed precepts, etc., Mt. 11:30; 23:4; Lk. 11:46 (2x); of faults, sins, etc., Gal. 6:5*
G5413.abbottSmith: a burden, load : of the cargo of a ship (Hdt., al.), Ac 27:10 ; metaph., Mt 11:30 23:4 , Lk 11:46 , Ga 6:5 .†
G5413.strongs: diminutive of G5414 (φόρτος); an invoice (as part of freight), i.e. (figuratively) a task or service

G3671.mounce: assent, consent; profession, 2 Cor. 9:13; 1 Tim. 6:12, 13; Heb. 3:1; 4:14; 10:23*
G3671.abbottSmith: in cl., an agreement , assent , compact (in π. , of a contract; Deiss., BS , 249).
G3671.strongs: from the same as G3670 (ὁμολογέω); acknowledgment

G533.thayer: I deny, disown, repudiate (either another person or myself), disregard.
G533.mounce: to deny, disown, Mt. 26:34; to renounce, disregard, Mt. 16:24
G533.abbottSmith: to deny , i.e. to refuse to recognize, to ignore: c. acc., of oneself ( DCG , ii, 598 f.), Mt 16:24 , Mk 8:34 (MM, VGT , s.v.), Lk 9:23 (WH, mg., txt., ἀρν- ) of Peter's denials of Christ, Mt 26:34 , 35 , 75 , Mk 14:30 , 31 , 72 , Lk 22:34 , 61 ; pass., Lk 12:9 (Cremer, 111).†
G533.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G720 (ἀρνέομαι); to deny utterly, i.e. disown, abstain

G4541.mounce: a Samaritan, an inhabitant of the city or region of Samaria, applied by the Jews as a term of reproach and contempt, Mt. 10:5; Jn. 4:9, 39f.; 8:48; Lk. 9:52; 10:33; 17:16; Acts 8:25*
G4541.abbottSmith: a Samaritan , i.e. an inhabitant of the city or the region of Samaria, in NT always the latter (v.s. Σαμαρία ): Mt 10:5 , Lk 9:52 10:33 17:16 , Jo 4:9 , 39, 40 , Ac 8:25 ; as an opprobrious epithet, Jo 8:48 .†
G4541.strongs: from G4540 (Σαμάρεια); a Samarite, i.e. inhabitant of Samaria

G3368.mounce: not at any time, never, 2 Tim. 3:7*
G3368.strongs: from G3366 (μηδέ) and G4218 (ποτέ); not even ever

G3684.mounce: pertaining to a donkey; μύλος ὀνικός, a millstone turned by a donkey, a large or an upper millstone, Mt. 18:6; Mk. 9:42*
G3684.abbottSmith: of or for an ass : μύλος ὀ. , Mt 18:6 , Mk 9:42 (elsewhere only in π. ; v. MM, xviii).†
G3684.strongs: from G3688 (ὄνος); belonging to a ass, i.e. large (so as to be turned by a ass)

G2297.mounce: wonderful, admirable, marvellous; τὸ θαυμάσιον, a wonder, wonderful work, Mt. 21:15*
G2297.strongs: from G2295 (θαῦμα); wondrous, i.e. (neuter as noun) a miracle

G1371.thayer: I cut in two, perhaps: scourge severely.
G1371.mounce: pr. to cut into two parts, cut asunder; in NT to inflict a punishment of extreme severity, Mt. 24:51; Lk. 12:46*
G1371.abbottSmith: to cut in two, cut asunder : perh. metaph. of severe scourging (but v. Meyer on Mt, l.c., and cf. I Ki 15:33 , II Ki 12:31 , He 11:37 ), Mt 24:51 , Lk 12:46 .†
G1371.strongs: from a compound of a derivative of G1364 (δίς) and a derivative of (to cut); to bisect, i.e. (by extension) to flog severely

G1603.thayer: I fill completely, fulfill in every particular (to the utmost), make good.
G1603.mounce: to fill out, complete, fill up; met. to fulfil, perform, accomplish, Acts 13:33*
G1603.abbottSmith: to fill full, make up a number .
G1603.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4137 (πληρόω); to accomplish entirely

G270.mounce: to collect; to reap, mow or cut down, Jas. 5:4*
G270.strongs: from G260 (ἅμα); properly, to collect, i.e. (by implication) reap

G523.thayer: I ask back, ask what is my due, demand back.
G523.mounce: to demand, require, Lk. 12:20; to demand back, Lk. 6:30*
G523.abbottSmith: to ask back , demand back : Lk 6:30 12:20 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G523.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G154 (αἰτέω); to demand back

G3975.thayer: I fatten, thicken; pass. fig: I become stupid, dull, unfeeling.
G3975.mounce: to fatten, make gross; met. pass. to be rendered gross, dull, unfeeling, Mt. 13:15; Acts 28:27*
G3975.abbottSmith: to thicken , fatten ; pass., to grow fat . Metaph., to make dull or stupid ( τ. ψυχάς , Plut.); pass., to wax gross : ἡ καρδία , Mt 13:15 , Ac 28:27 (LXX).†
G3975.strongs: from a derivative of G4078 (πήγνυμι) (meaning thick); to thicken, i.e. (by implication) to fatten (figuratively, stupefy or render callous)

G2255.abbottSmith: as adj., agreeing in gender and number with the substantive following, τὰ ἡμίσια τ. ὑταρχόντων , Lk 19:8 .
G2255.strongs: neuter of a derivative from an inseparable prefix akin to G260 (ἅμα) (through the idea of partition involved in connection) and meaning semi-; (as noun) half

G462.abbottSmith: unholy , profane (Cremer, 464): I Ti 1:9 , II Ti 3:2 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G462.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3741 (ὅσιος); wicked

G3440.strongs: neuter of G3441 (μόνος) as adverb; merely

G3202.thayer: blaming one's lot or destiny, discontented, complaining.
G3202.mounce: finding fault or being discontented with one’s lot, querulous; a discontented, querulous person, a complainer, Jude 16*
G3202.abbottSmith: complaining of one's fate , querulous : Ju 16 .†
G3202.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3201 (μέμφομαι) and (fate; akin to the base of G3313 (μέρος)); blaming fate, i.e. querulous (discontented)

G3573.thayer: I nod in sleep, am drowsy, slumber.
G3573.mounce: to nod; to nod in sleep; to sink into a sleep, Mt. 25:5; to slumber in inactivity, 2 Pet. 2:3*
G3573.abbottSmith: to nod in sleep , fall asleep : Mt 25:5 . Metaph., of negligence or delay, ἡ ἀπώλεια αὐτῶν οὐ νυστάζει (cf. Ps 120 (121):4 , Is 5:27 ): II Pe 2:3 .†
G3573.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3506 (νεύω); to nod, i.e. (by implication) to fall asleep; figuratively, to delay

G4638.thayer: a tent pitched, a dwelling, tabernacle.
G4638.mounce: a habitation, abode, dwelling, Acts 7:46; the corporeal tabernacle of the soul, 2 Pet. 1:13, 14*
G4638.abbottSmith: a tent, tabernacle (cf. σκηνή , σκῆνος ): of the temple as God's dwelling ( Ps 131 (132):5 , al.), Ac 7:46 . Metaph., of the body: II Pe 1:13, 14 .†
G4638.strongs: from G4637 (σκηνόω); an encampment, i.e. (figuratively) the Temple (as God's residence), the body (as a tenement for the soul)

G2709.mounce: under the earth, subterranean, infernal, Phil. 2:10*
G2709.abbottSmith: subterranean , under the earth (in cl., of the infernal gods): of the departed in Hades, opp. to ἐπουράνιος , ἐπίγειος , Phl 2:10 .†
G2709.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and (the ground); subterranean, i.e. infernal (belonging to the world of departed spirits)

G2206.thayer: (a) intrans: I am jealous, (b) trans: I am jealous of, with acc. of a person; I am eager for, am eager to possess, with acc. of a thing.
G2206.mounce: to have strong affection towards, be ardently devoted to, 2 Cor. 11:2; to make a show of affection and devotion towards, Gal. 4:17; to desire earnestly, aspire eagerly after, 1 Cor. 12:31; 14:1, 39; absol. to be fervent, to be zealous, Rev. 3:19; to be jealous, envious, spiteful, Acts 7:9; 17:5; 1 Cor. 13:4; Jas. 4:2; pass. to be an object of warm regard and devotion, Gal. 4:18
G2206.abbottSmith: to burn with envy or jealousy , to be jealous : absol., Ac 7:9 17:5 , I Co 13:4 , Ja 4:2 (R, mg., cf. Mayor, in l.).
G2206.strongs: from G2205 (ζῆλος); to have warmth of feeling for or against
G2206.kjv: affect, covet (earnestly), (have) desire, (move with) envy, be jealous over, (be) zealous(-ly affect)

G2278.thayer: I make a sound, give forth a sound, sound (when struck); I roar (as the sea).
G2278.abbottSmith: to sound : I Co 13:1 (cf. ἐξ- , κατ-ηχέω ).†
G2278.strongs: from G2279 (ἦχος); to make a loud noise, i.e. reverberate

G5555.thayer: a topaz, a gem with a bright yellow color.
G5555.mounce: chrysolite, a name applied by the ancients to all gems of a gold color; spc. the modern topaz, Rev. 21:10*
G5555.abbottSmith: a chrysolite (on its identification, v. Swete, Ap. , 288 f.; DB , iv, 620): Re 21:20 .†
G5555.strongs: from G5557 (χρυσός) and G3037 (λίθος); gold-stone, i.e. a yellow gem ("chrysolite")

G5484.thayer: for the sake of, by reason of, on account of.
G5484.mounce: the acc. sg form of the noun χάριν which can be used as an improper prep., on account of, Lk. 7:47; Eph. 3:1, 14; 1 Jn. 3:12; for the sake of, in order to, Gal. 3:19; Tit. 1:5, 11; Jude 16; on the score of, 1 Tim. 5:14*
G5484.strongs: accusative case of G5485 (χάρις) as preposition; through favor of, i.e. on account of
G5484.kjv: be-(for) cause of, for sake of, +…fore, X reproachfully

G4958.thayer: I contract, shorten, wrap around, swathe.
G4958.mounce: to draw together, contract, straiten; to enwrap; hence, i.q. περιστέλλω, to lay out, prepare for burial, Acts 5:6; pass. to be shortened, 1 Cor. 7:29*
G4958.abbottSmith: to draw together, contract, shorten : of time, I Co 7:29 .
G4958.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4724 (στέλλω); to send (draw) together, i.e. enwrap (enshroud a corpse for burial), contract (an interval)

G1105.abbottSmith: darkness, gloom (including "an element of tempest," Tr., Syn. , §c): He 12:18 .†
G1105.strongs: akin to G3509 (νέφος); gloom (as of a storm)

G821.abbottSmith: = ἀτιμάζω , to dishonour , treat with indignity : Mk 12:4 , Rec.†
G821.strongs: from G820 (ἄτιμος); used like G818 (ἀτιμάζω), to maltreat

G0956a.abbottSmith: a sharp point , as of a spear. a needle : Lk 18:25 (Rec. ῥαφίς , q.v.).†

G1966.mounce: to come upon; to come after; to succeed immediately, Acts 7:26; 16:11; 20:15; 21:18; 23:11*
G1966.strongs: feminine singular participle of a comparative of G1909 (ἐπί) and (to go); supervening, i.e. (G2250 (ἡμέρα) or G3571 (νύξ) being expressed or implied) the ensuing day or night

G4081.mounce: moist earth, mud, slime, Jn. 9:6, 11, 14, 15; clay, potter’s clay, Rom. 9:21*
G4081.abbottSmith: clay , as used by a potter: Ro 9:21 (cf. Is 29:16 , al.).

G1033.mounce: food, Mt. 14:15; Mk. 7:19; solid food, 1 Cor. 3:2
G1033.abbottSmith: food : Ro 14:15 , 20 I Co 8:8 , 13 10:3 ; pl., Mt 14:15 , Mk 7:19 , Lk 3:11 9:13 , I Co 6:13 , I Ti 4:3 , He 9:10 13:9 ; trop., of spiritual food, Jo 4:34 , I Co 3:2 (cf. βρῶσις ).†
G1033.strongs: from the base of G977 (βιβρώσκω); food (literally or figuratively), especially (ceremonially) articles allowed or forbidden by the Jewish law

G341.abbottSmith: to make new : II Co 4:16 , Col 3:10 (v. Cremer, 323).†
G341.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a derivative of G2537 (καινός); to renovate

G2996.thayer: Lasea, a city in Crete, about the middle of the south coast.
G2996.mounce: Lasaea, also spelled Λασέα, a maritime town in Crete, Acts 27:8. See PhotoGuide.
G2996.abbottSmith: Lasea , a city of Crete, otherwise unknown: Ac 27:8 .†
G2996.strongs: of uncertain origin; Lasæa, a place in Crete

G3842.mounce: always, at all times, ever, Mt. 26:11; Mk. 14:7; Lk. 15:31; 18:1
G3842.abbottSmith: at all times , always : Mt 26:11 , Mk 14:7 , Lk 15:31 , Jo 6:34 , Ro 1:10 , and freq. in Paul. Epp.
G3842.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and G3753 (ὅτε); every when, i.e. at all times

G558.thayer: a freedman, one who has been a slave but has been manumitted by his master.
G558.abbottSmith: a freedman : τ. κυρίου (MM, s.v.), I Co 7:22 .†
G558.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1658 (ἐλεύθερος); one freed away, i.e. a freedman

G2431.strongs: from the same as G2436 (ἵλεως); propitious or merry ("hilarious"), i.e. prompt or willing

G3395.abbottSmith: a mixture : Jo 19:39 ( ἕλιγμα , WH, R, mg.).†

G529.thayer: the act of meeting, to meet (a phrase seemingly almost technical for the reception of a newly arrived official).
G529.mounce: a meeting, encounter; εἰς ἀπάντησιν, to meet, Mt. 25:6; Acts 28:15; 1 Thess. 4:17*
G529.abbottSmith: usually with v.l., ὑπ- ; a meeting ; εἰς ἀ. , c. gen. or dat., to meet : Mt 25:6 27:32 (WH, txt., omits), Ac 28:15 , I Th 4:17 (v. M, Th. , in l.; M, Pr. , 14, 242; MM, VGT , s.v.; Lft., Notes , 69).†

G4829.thayer: I partake with, divide together with, have a share in.
G4829.mounce: to divide with another so as to receive a part to one’s self, share with, partake with, 1 Cor. 9:13*
G4829.abbottSmith: to distri­bute in shares . Mid., to have a share in : I Co 9:13 (Diod., al.).†
G4829.strongs: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and G3307 (μερίζω); to share jointly, i.e. participate in

G3868.thayer: I beg off, make excuse, deprecate, refuse, reject, decline, shun, avoid.
G3868.abbottSmith: to beg of or from another: Mk 15:6 (Rec. ὅνπερ ᾐτοῦντο , v. Field, Notes , 43).
G3868.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the middle voice of G154 (αἰτέω); to beg off, i.e. deprecate, decline, shun

G4316.thayer: I address by name, designate, accost.
G4316.mounce: to speak to, accost, to name, declare, Heb. 5:10*
G4316.abbottSmith: to address , greet , salute ; hence, to call by name , address , style : c. dupl. acc., pass., He 5:10 .†
G4316.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and a derivative of G58 (ἀγορά) (mean to harangue); to address, i.e. salute by name

G4718.mounce: a cluster or bunch of grapes, Mt. 7:16; Lk. 6:44, Rev. 14:18*
G4718.abbottSmith: a bunch of grapes : Mt 7:16 , Lk 6:44 , Re 14:18 . " σ. is properly the ripe grape-cluster as opp. to ὄμφαξ , cf. Ge 40:10 , Jb 15:33 ; as contrasted with βότρυς , it describes the grapes rather than the cluster on which they grow" (Swete, Apoc. , 187 f.).†
G4718.strongs: probably from the base of G4735 (στέφανος); a cluster of grapes (as if intertwined)

G2851.thayer: chastisement, punishment, torment, perhaps with the idea of deprivation.
G2851.mounce: chastisement, punishment, Mt. 25:46; painful disquietude, torment, 1 Jn. 4:18*
G2851.abbottSmith: correction , penalty , punishment : Mt 25:46 , I Jo 4:18 .†

G1869.mounce: to lift up, raise, elevate; to hoist, Acts 27:40; τὴν φωνήν, to lift up the voice, to speak in a loud voice, Lk. 11:27; τὰς χεῖρας, to lift up the hands in prayer, Lk. 24:50; 1 Tim. 2:8; τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς, to lift up the eyes, to look, Mt. 17:8; τὴν κεφαλήν, to lift up the head, to be encouraged, animated, Lk. 21:28; τὴν πτέρναν, to lift up the heel, to attack, assault; or, to seek one’s overthrow or destruction, Jn. 13:18; pass. to be borne upwards, Acts 1:9; met. mid. to exalt one’s self, assume consequence, be elated, 2 Cor. 10:5
G1869.abbottSmith: to lift up, raise : τ. ἀρτέμονα , Ac 27:40 ; χεῖρας , Lk 24:50 , I Ti 2:8 ; κεφαλάς , Lk 21:28 ; ὀφθαλμούς , Mt 17:8 , Lk 6:20 16:23 18:13 , Jo 4:35 6:5 17:1 ; φωνήν , Lk 11:27 , Ac 2:14 14:11 22:22 ; τ. πτέρναν (fig.), Jo 13:18 . Pass., Ac 1:9 ; metaph., to be lifted up with pride : II Co 10:5 11:20 .†
G1869.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G142 (αἴρω); to raise up (literally or figuratively)

G1793.thayer: (a) I meet, encounter, hence: (b) I call (upon), make a petition, make suit, supplication.
