G1793.mounce: to fill in with, meet; to have conversation with, address; to address or apply to any one, Acts 25:24; ὑπέρ τινος, to intercede for any one, plead the cause of, Rom. 8:27, 34; Heb. 7:25; κατά τινος, to address a representation or suit against any one, to accuse, complain of, Rom. 11:2*
G1793.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5177 (τυγχάνω); to chance upon, i.e. (by implication) confer with; by extension to entreat (in favor or against)

G3620.abbottSmith: the act of building : metaph., I Ti 1:4 (BE for οἰκοδομία , q.v.).†
G3620.strongs: from the same as G3619 (οἰκοδομή); confirmation

G1233.thayer: judicial examination, decision; an act of discernment.
G1233.mounce: pr. an act of distinguishing or discernment; a determination; examination judicially, hearing, trial, Acts 25:21*
G1233.abbottSmith: a distinguishing , also as medical term.

G4009.thayer: (a) a boundary, limit, extremity, (b) an end, conclusion.
G4009.mounce: an extremity, end, Mt. 12:42; Lk. 11:31; Rom. 10:18; an end, conclusion, termination, Heb. 6:16*
G4009.abbottSmith: an end , limit , boundary ; of space: chiefly in pl., τὰ π. τ. γῆς , Mt 12:42 , Lk 11:31 ; τ. οἰκουμένης , Ro 10:18 (LXX) ; (opp. to ἀρχή ) the end : c. gen., ἀντιλογίας , He 6:10 .†
G4009.strongs: from the same as G4008 (πέραν); an extremity

G162.mounce: to lead captive; met. to captivate, Eph. 4:8*

G4581.thayer: Seleucia, on the Syrian coast, the harbor of Syrian Antioch.
G4581.mounce: Seleucia, a city of Syria, west of Antioch, on the Orontes, Acts 13:4*. See PhotoGuide.
G4581.abbottSmith: Seleucia , a city of Syria: Ac 13:4 .†
G4581.strongs: from (Seleucus, a Syrian king); Seleuceia, a place in Syria

G907.thayer: lit: I dip, submerge, but specifically of ceremonial dipping; I baptize.
G907.mounce: pr. to dip, immerse; to cleanse or purify by washing; to administer the rite of baptism, to baptize; met. with various reference to the ideas associated with Christian baptism as an act of dedication, e.g. marked designation, devotion, trial, etc.; mid. to procure baptism for one’s self, to undergo baptism, Acts 22:16
G907.abbottSmith: to dip , immerse , sink ; generally (in Polyb., iii, 72, of soldiers wading breast-deep; in i, 51, of the sinking of ships); metaph., to overwhelm ( Is 21:4 ; cf. MM, Exp. , x); c. cogn. acc., βάπτισμα β. , Mk 10:38 , 39 , Lk 12:50 . Mid., to perform ablutions , wash oneself , bathe ( IV Ki 5:14 , Jth 12:7 , Si 31 (34):30 ): Mk 7:4 ; aor. pass, in same sense, Lk 11:38 . Of ablution, immersion, as
G907.strongs: from a derivative of G911 (βάπτω); to immerse, submerge; to make whelmed (i.e. fully wet); used only (in the New Testament) of ceremonial ablution, especially (technically) of the ordinance of Christian baptism

G2133.mounce: good will, kindliness; heartiness, enthusiasm, Eph. 6:7*
G2133.abbottSmith: goodwill : Eph 6:7 (of slaves; cf. MM, Exp. , xiv).†
G2133.strongs: from the same as G2132 (εὐνοέω); kindness; euphemistically, conjugal duty

G5388.mounce: loving one’s children, duly parental, Tit. 2:4*
G5388.abbottSmith: loving one's children (Hdt., Arist., Plut., al.): of women, joined with φίλανδρος , q.v., Tit 2:4 .†
G5388.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G5043 (τέκνον); fond of one's children, i.e. maternal

G4660.mounce: to flay, lacerate; met. to vex, trouble, annoy, Mk. 5:35; Lk. 7:6; 8:49; pass. met. ἐσκυλμένοι, in sorry plight, Mt. 9:36*
G4660.abbottSmith: in cl., to skin, flay, rend (Æsch., Anthol.).
G4660.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to flay, i.e. (figuratively) to harass

G1971.thayer: I long for, strain after, desire greatly, have affection for.
G1971.mounce: to desire besides; also, to desire earnestly, long for, 2 Cor. 5:2; to have a strong bent, Jas. 4:5; by impl. to love, have affection for, 2 Cor. 9:14
G1971.abbottSmith: to long for, desire : c. inf., Ro 1:11 , II Co 5:2 , I Th 3:6 , II Ti 1:4 , Phl 2:26 (WH, [txt.]); c. acc rei, I Pe 2:2 ; c. acc pers., II Co 9:14 , Phl 1:8 2:26 (WH, mg); absol., Ja 4:5 (v. Mayor, in l.).†
G1971.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and potheo (to yearn); to dote upon, i.e. intensely crave possession (lawfully or wrongfully)

G3096.mounce: to be a magician; to use magical arts, practise magic, sorcery, Acts 8:9*
G3096.abbottSmith: to be a Magus , or skilled in Magian lore .

G628.thayer: I wash off, mid: I wash away (my sins, in baptism).
G628.mounce: to cleanse by bathing; mid. to cleanse one’s self; to procure one’s self to be cleansed; met., of sin, Acts 22:16; 1 Cor. 6:11*
G628.abbottSmith: to wash off , wash away ; mid., metaph., c. acc. rei, to wash off oneself : ἁμαρτίας , Ac 22:16 ; absol., ἀπελούσασθε , ye washed yourselves clean (cf. Cremer, 406), I Co 6:11 .†
G628.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3068 (λούω); to wash fully, i.e. (figuratively) have remitted (reflexively)

G4335.thayer: (a) prayer (to God), (b) a place for prayer (used by Jews, perhaps where there was no synagogue).
G4335.mounce: prayer, Mt. 17:21; 21:13, 22; Lk. 6:12; Acts 1:14; meton. a place where prayer is offered, an oratory, perhaps, Acts 16:13, 16
G4335.abbottSmith: prayer to God: Mt 17:21 (WH, R, txt., om.) 21:22 , Mk 9:29 , Lk 22:45 , Ac 3:1 6:4 10:31 , Ro 12:12 , I Co 7:5 , Col 4:2 ; pl., Ac 2:42 10:4 , Ro 1:10 , Eph 1:16 , Col 4:12 , I Th 1:2 , Phm 4 , 22 , I Pe 3:7 4:7 , Re 5:8 8:3 , 4 ; οἶκος προσευχῆς , Mt 21:13 , Mk 11:17 , Lk 19:46 (LXX); π. καὶ δέησις , Eph 6:18 , Phl 4:6 ; pl., I Ti 2:1 5:5 ; ἡ π. τοῦ θεοῦ , prayer to God (cf. Wi 16:28 ), Lk 6:12 ;
G4335.strongs: from G4336 (προσεύχομαι); prayer (worship); by implication, an oratory (chapel)

G3189.mounce: black, Mt. 5:36; Rev. 6:5, 12, the form μέλαν means ink, 2 Cor. 3:3; 2 Jn. 12; 3 Jn. 13
G3189.abbottSmith: black : Re 6:5 , 12 ; opp. to λευκός , Mt 5:36 ; neut., τὸ μ. , ink : II Co 3:3 , II Jo 12 , III Jo 13 .†

G2393.mounce: jasper, a precious stone of various colors, as purple, cerulian green, etc. Rev. 4:3; 21:11, 18, 19*
G2393.abbottSmith: jasper , apparently not the modern stone of that name, but a translucent stone ( DB , s.v.): Re 4:3 21:11 , 18 , 19 .†
G2393.strongs: probably of foreign origin (see H03471); "jasper", a gem

G807.thayer: I act improperly, am unseemly, behave unbecomingly (or even dishonorably); perhaps: I consider (something) unseemly.
G807.mounce: to behave in an unbecoming manner or indecorously, 1 Cor. 13:5; to behave in a manner open to censure, 1 Cor. 7:36*
G807.abbottSmith: to act unbecomingly , behave dishonourably : I Co 13:5 ; seq. ἐπί , I Co 7:36 (MM, s.v.).†
G807.strongs: from G809 (ἀσχήμων); to be (i.e. act) unbecoming

G4497.thayer: Rehoboam, son of Solomon, and King of Israel.
G4497.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07346); Roboam (i.e. Rechobam), an Israelite

G4345.thayer: I assign by lot, allot, associate with, follow as a disciple.
G4345.mounce: pr. to make to lean upon or against a thing; met., to join one’s self to, follow as an adherent, Acts 5:36*
G4345.abbottSmith: to allot to , assign to by lot ; pass. (but perh. as mid., EV, consorted with , so Syr.): Ac 17:4 (for exx., v, Cremer, 749).†
G4345.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2820 (κληρόω); to give a common lot to, i.e. (figuratively) to associate with

G3428.thayer: (a) an adulteress (that is, a married woman who commits adultery), (b) Hebraistically: extended to those who worship any other than the true God.
G3428.mounce: an adulteress, Rom. 7:3; Jas. 4:4; by meton., an adulterous appearance, lustful significance, 2 Pet. 2:14; from the Hebrew, spiritually adulterous, faithless, ungodly, Mt. 12:39; 16:4; Mk. 8:38*
G3428.strongs: a prolonged form of the feminine of G3432 (μοιχός); an adulteress (literally or figuratively)

G2135.thayer: (a) a chamberlain, keeper of the bed-chamber of an eastern potentate, eunuch, (b) a eunuch, castrated person, or one who voluntarily abstains from marriage.
G2135.mounce: pr. one who has charge of the bedchamber; hence, a eunuch, one emasculated, Mt. 19:12; as eunuchs in the East often rose to places of power and trust, hence, a minister of a court, Acts 8:27, 34, 36, 38f.*
G2135.abbottSmith: an emasculated man, a eunuch : Mt 19:12 ; one such holding, as was common, high office, as of chamberlain, at court, Ac 8:27 , 34 , 36 , 38 , 39 ; metaph., of one naturally incapacitated for or volun­tarily abstaining from wedlock, Mt 19:12 .†
G2135.strongs: from (a bed) and G2192 (ἔχω); a castrated person (such being employed in Oriental bed-chambers); by extension an impotent or unmarried man; by implication, a chamberlain (state-officer)

G3843.thayer: wholly, entirely, in every way, by all means, certainly.
G3843.mounce: wholly, altogether; at any rate, by all means, 1 Cor. 9:22; by impl. surely, assuredly, certainly, Lk. 4:23; Acts 21:22; 28:4; 1 Cor. 9:10; οὐ πάντως, in nowise, not in the least, Rom. 3:9; 1 Cor. 5:10; 16:12*
G3843.abbottSmith: altogether , by all means ; without neg. (from Hdt. on): I Co 16:12 ; esp. in strong affirmations, surely , at all events : Lk 4:23 , Ac 21:22 28:4 , I Co 9:10 ; c. neg. (so always in Hom.), in a complete negation: Ro 3:9 ; in a partial negation (Bl., § 75, 7), I Co 5:10 .†
G3843.strongs: adverb from G3956 (πᾶς); entirely; specially, at all events, (with negative, following) in no event
G3843.kjv: by all means, altogether, at all, needs, no doubt, in (no) wise, surely

G2931.mounce: in secret, secretly, not openly, Eph. 5:12*

G98.thayer: belonging to Adramyttium, a port in Mysia, in Asia Minor.
G98.mounce: of Adramyttium, a Greek city on the coast of Aeolia, in Asia Minor, Acts 27:2*
G98.abbottSmith: of Adramyttium , a seaport of Mysia: Ac 27:2 .†
G98.strongs: from (a place in Asia Minor); Adramyttene or belonging to Adramyttium

G4209.thayer: a purple garment, indicating power or wealth.
G4209.mounce: purpura, murex, a species of shellfish that yielded the purple dye, highly esteemed by the ancients, its tint being a bright crimson; in NT a purple garment, robe of purple, Lk. 16:19; Rev. 18:12
G4209.strongs: of Latin origin; the "purple" mussel, i.e. (by implication) the red-blue color itself, and finally a garment dyed with it

G4080.mounce: how large, Gal. 6:11; how great in dignity, Heb. 7:4*
G4080.abbottSmith: interrog., how large , how great? (prop., of magnitude, as πόσος of quantity): in exclamations, = ἡλίκος (v. Bl., § 51, 4), Ga 6:11 ; of personal greatness, He 7:4 .†
G4080.strongs: a quantitative form (the feminine) of the base of G4225 (πού); how much (as an indefinite), i.e. in size or (figuratively) dignity

G2986.mounce: bright, resplendent, shining, Rev. 22:16; clear, transparent, Rev. 22:1; white, glistening, Acts 10:30; Rev. 15:6; Rev. 19:8; of a bright color, gaudy, Lk. 23:11; by impl. splendid, magnificent, sumptuous, Jas. 2:2, 3; Rev. 18:14*
G2986.abbottSmith: bright , brilliant : ποταμός , Re 22:1 ( ΕV , clear ); ἀστήρ , Re 22:16 ; of clothing, brilliant , splendid : Lk 23:11 , Ac 10:30 , Ja 2:2 , 3 , Re 15:6 18:14 19:8 .†
G2986.strongs: from the same as G2985 (λαμπάς); radiant; by analogy, limpid; figuratively, magnificent or sumptuous (in appearance)
G2986.kjv: bright, clear, gay, goodly, gorgeous, white

G1401.mounce: a male slave, or servant, of various degrees, Mt. 8:9, et al. freq.; a servitor, person of mean condition, Phil. 2:7; fem. δούλη, a female slave; a handmaiden, Lk. 1:38, 48; Acts 2:18; δοῦλος, used figuratively, in a bad sense, one involved in moral or spiritual thraldom, Jn. 8:34; Rom. 6:17, 20; 1 Cor. 7:23; 2 Pet. 2:19; in a good sense, a devoted servant or minister, Acts 16:17; Rom. 1:1; one pledged or bound to serve, 1 Cor. 7:22; 2 Cor. 4:5
G1401.abbottSmith: in bondage to, subject to : Ro 6:19 . As subst., ὁ, ἡ δ. , a slave ; fem., ἡ δ. , a female slave, bondmaid (Cremer, 702; DB , iii, 215): Lk 1:38 , 48 , Ac 2:18 (LXX) ; masc., ὁ δ. , a slave, bondman : Mt 8:9 18:23 , al.; opp. to ἐλεύθερος , I Co 7:22 12:13 , Ga 3:28 , Eph 6:8 , Col 3:11 , Re 6:15 13:16 19:18 ; opp. to κύριος, δεσπότης, οἰκοδεσπότης , Mt 10:24 13:27 , 28 , Lk 12:46 , Jo 15:15 , Eph
G1401.strongs: from G1210 (δέω); a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

G873.thayer: I rail off, separate, place apart.
G873.mounce: to limit off; to separate, sever from the rest, Mt. 13:49; to separate from society, cut off from all intercourse, excommunicate, Lk. 6:22; to set apart, select, Acts 13:2; Rom. 1:1; Gal. 1:15
G873.abbottSmith: to mark off by boundaries from, separate from: c. acc., Ac 19:9 , Ga 2:12 ; id. seq. ἐκ ( ἀπό ), Mt 13:49 25:32 (MM, s.v.); of excommunication, Lk 6:22 . Pass., absol., II Co 6:17 ;
G873.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3724 (ὁρίζω); to set off by boundary, i.e. (figuratively) limit, exclude, appoint, etc.

G2360.thayer: I disturb, agitate; pass: I am troubled, alarmed.
G2360.mounce: to cry aloud; in NT pass., to be disturbed, disquieted, alarmed, terrified, Mt. 24:6; Mk. 13:7; 2 Thess. 2:2*
G2360.abbottSmith: to cry aloud , make an outcry .
G2360.strongs: from to wail; to clamor, i.e. (by implication) to frighten

G4249.mounce: also spelled πρίω, to saw, saw in two, Heb. 11:37*
G4249.abbottSmith: to saw , saw asunder (= π. δίχα , Thuc., iv, 100): pass, He 11:37 (cf. δια-πρίω ).†
G4249.strongs: a strengthened form of a primary prio (to saw); to saw in two

G4236.strongs: from G4235 (πρᾷος); gentleness, by implication, humility

G2170.mounce: grateful, pleasing; mindful of benefits, thankful, Col. 3:15*
G2170.abbottSmith: = εὔχαρις , winning, gracious, agreeable ( Pr, l.c. ).
G2170.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5483 (χαρίζομαι); well favored, i.e. (by implication) grateful

G392.thayer: I arrange, draw up, compose, but perhaps: I set down from memory, restore from memory.
G392.mounce: pr. to arrange; hence, to compose, Lk. 1:1*
G392.abbottSmith: to arrange in order , bring together from memory (Blass., Phil. Gosp. , 14 ff.; MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 1:1 .†
G392.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the middle voice of G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange

G2113.mounce: to run on a straight course; to sail on a direct course, Acts 16:11; 21:1*
G2113.abbottSmith: of ships, to run a straight course (Philo): Ac 16:11 21:1 .†
G2113.strongs: from G2117 (εὐθύς) and G1408 (δρόμος); to lay a straight course, i.e. sail direct

G563.mounce: without distraction, without care or solicitude, 1 Cor. 7:35*
G563.strongs: adverb from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative participle) and a presumed derivative of G4049 (περισπάω); undistractedly, i.e. free from (domestic) solicitude

G3676.mounce: yet, nevertheless; with μέντοι, but nevertheless, but for all that, Jn. 12:42; even, though it be but, 1 Cor. 14:7; Gal. 3:15*
G3676.abbottSmith: yet : ὅ. μέντοι , but yet , nevertheless , Jo 12:42 ; by hyperbaton, out of its proper position, I Co 14:7 , Ga 3:15 (but v. Bl., § 77, 14).†
G3676.strongs: adverb from the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ); at the same time, i.e. (conjunctionally) notwithstanding, yet still

G4617.thayer: I sift, prove by trials, winnow.
G4617.mounce: to sift; met. to sift by trials and temptations, Lk. 22:31*

G4996.mounce: in the manner of a person in his right mind; soberly, temperately, Tit. 2:12*
G4996.abbottSmith: with sound mind, prudently, soberly : Tit 2:12 .†
G4996.strongs: adverb from G4998 (σώφρων); with sound mind, i.e. moderately

G1574.mounce: to stab, pierce deeply, Jn. 19:37; Rev. 1:7*
G1574.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G2759 (κέντρον); to transfix

G4126.thayer: I sail, travel by sea, voyage.
G4126.abbottSmith: to sail : Lk 8:23 , Ac 27:24 ; seq. εἰς , Ac 21:3 27:2 , 6 ; ἐπί , Re 18:17 (cf. ἀπό- , δια- , ἐκ- , κατα- , παρα- , ὑπο-πλέω ).†
G4126.strongs: probably a form of G4150 (πλύνω) (through the idea of plunging through the water); to pass in a vessel

G1273.mounce: to pass the night, spend the whole night, Lk. 6:12*
G1273.abbottSmith: to pass the night : seq. ἐν τ. προσευχῆ , Lk 6:12 .†
G1273.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G3571 (νύξ); to sit up the whole night

G3546.mounce: pr. a thing sanctioned by law or custom; lawful money, coin, Mt. 22:19*
G3546.abbottSmith: that which is established by usage , a custom .
G3546.strongs: from G3543 (νομίζω); what is reckoned as of value (after the Latin numisma), i.e. current coin

G4036.mounce: greatly grieved, exceedingly sorrowful, Mt. 26:38; Mk. 14:34
G4036.abbottSmith: very sad , deeply grieved : Mt 26:38 , Mk 6:26 14:34 , Lk 18:23 .†
G4036.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3077 (λύπη); grieved all around, i.e. intensely sad

G1475.mounce: pr. a bottom, base; hence, the ground, Acts 22:7*
G1475.strongs: from the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a basis (bottom), i.e. the soil

G5214.thayer: I sing, sing hymns to, praise.
G5214.mounce: to hymn, praise, celebrate or worship with hymns, Acts 16:25; Heb. 2:12; absol. to sing a hymn, Mt. 26:30; Mk. 14:26*
G5214.abbottSmith: trans., c. acc pers. (in cl. also c. acc. rei), to sing to, laud, sing to the praise of : Ac 16:25 , He 2:12 .
G5214.strongs: from G5215 (ὕμνος); to hymn, i.e. sing a religious ode; by implication, to celebrate (God) in song

G4482.strongs: a primary verb; for some tenses of which a prolonged form is used to flow ("run"; as water)

G77.thayer: without expense, for which nothing has to be paid.
G77.abbottSmith: without expense , free of charge : I Co 9:18 .†
G77.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle); and G1160 (δαπάνη); costless, i.e. gratuitous

G1979.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G4621 (σῖτος); a provisioning, i.e. (concretely) food

G1080.thayer: I beget (of the male), (of the female) I bring forth, give birth to.
G1080.mounce: to beget, generate, Mt. 1:2-16, et al.; of women, to bring forth, bear, give birth to, Lk. 1:13, 57, et al.; pass. to be born, produced, Mt. 2:1, 4, et al.; met. to produce, excite, give occasion to, effect, 2 Tim. 2:23; from the Hebrew, to constitute as son, to constitute as king, or as the representative or viceregent of God, Acts 13:33; Heb. 1:5; 5:5; by impl. to be a parent to any one; pass. to be a son or child to any one, Jn. 1:13; 1 Cor. 4:15, et al.
G1080.abbottSmith: of the father, to beget : c. acc., Mt 1:1-16 , Ac 7:8 , 29 ; seq. ἐκ , Mt 1:3 , 5 , 6 . Of the mother, to bring forth, bear : Lk 1:13 , 57 23:29 , Jo 16:21 ; εἰς δουλείαν , Ga 4:24 . Pass. to be begotten : Mt 1:20 ; to be born : Mt 2:1 , 4 19:12 26:24 , Mk 14:21 , Lk 1:35 , Jo 3:4 , Ac 7:20 , Ro 9:11 , He 11:23 ; seq. εἰς , Jo 16:21 18:37 , II Pe 2:12 ; ἐν , Ac 2:8 22:3 , ( ἁμαρτίαις ), Jo 9:34 ;
G1080.strongs: from a variation of G1085 (γένος); to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
G1080.kjv: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

G2501.abbottSmith: Joseph ; the Son of Jacob: Jo 4:5 , Ac 7:9 , 13 , 14 , 18 , He 11:21 , 22 , Re 7:8 . In the genealogy of our Lord, the son of Matthias: Lk 3:24 ; the son Joram: Lk 3:30 . The husband of Mary, the Lord's mother: Mt 1:16 ff., Lk 1:27 , Jo 1:46 , al. One of the brethren of our Lord (v.s. ἀδελφός ): Mt 13:55 . Son of Mary: Mt 27:56 ( -σῆς , WH, mg., RV). Joseph of Arimatbæa: Mt 27:57 , 59 , Mk 15:43 ,
G2501.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03130); Joseph, the name of seven Israelites

G1827.abbottSmith: to convict: Ju 15 , Rec. (for ἐλέγχω , WH, q.v.).†
G1827.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1651 (ἐλέγχω); to convict fully, i.e. (by implication) to punish

G156.thayer: a cause, reason, excuse; a charge, accusation; guilt; circumstances, case.
G156.mounce: cause, motive, incitement; accusation, crime, case
G156.abbottSmith: cause , reason , occasion , case : Mt 19:3 , Lk 8:47 , Ac 10:21 22:24 28:20 , II Ti 1:6 , 12 , Tit 1:13 , He 2:11 ; εἰ οὕτως ἐστιν ἡ αἰ. (cf. Lat. si ita res se habet , and v. MM. VGT, s.v.), Mt 19:10 .
G156.strongs: from the same as G154 (αἰτέω); a cause (as if asked for), i.e. (logical) reason (motive, matter), (legal) crime (alleged or proved)

G960.thayer: Berea, a town of the province Macedonia.
G960.mounce: Berea, a town of Macedonia, Acts 17:10, 13*. See PhotoGuide.
G960.abbottSmith: Berœa , a city of Macedonia: Ac 17:10 , 13 .†
G960.strongs: perhaps a provincial from a derivative of G4008 (πέραν) (Perœa, i.e. the region beyond the coast-line); Berœa, a place in Macedonia

G2226.mounce: a living creature, animal, Heb. 13:11; 2 Pet. 2:12
G2226.abbottSmith: a living creature , an animal : Re 4:6-9 5:6 ff. Re 6:1 ff. Re 7:11 14:3 15:7 19:4 ; ζῴων τ. αἷμα , He 13:11 ; ἄλογα ζ. , II Pe 2:12 , Ju 10 .†
G2226.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G2198 (ζάω); a live thing, i.e. an animal

G1983.thayer: I exercise oversight, care for, visit.
G1983.mounce: to look at, inspect; met. to be circumspect, heedful, Heb. 12:15; to oversee, to exercise the office of ἐπίσκοπος, 1 Pet. 5:2*
G1983.abbottSmith: to look upon, observe, examine : seq. μή , He 12:15 .
G1983.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4648 (σκοπέω); to oversee; by implication, to beware

G5447.strongs: adverb from G5446 (φυσικός); "physically", i.e. (by implication) instinctively

G1383.thayer: a test, trial, what is genuine.
G1383.mounce: that by means of which anything is tried, proof, criterion, test; trial, the act of trying or putting to proof, Jas. 1:3; approved character, 1 Pet. 1:7*
G1383.abbottSmith: usually regarded as a variant form of δοκιμεῖον , a test , and so perh. Ja 1:3 (v. Mayor, in l.), but see next word (cf. Cremer, 212, 702).†
G1383.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G1382 (δοκιμή); a testing; by implication, trustworthiness

G4610.thayer: Silvanus, a Roman citizen and a helper of Paul.
G4610.mounce: Silvanus, pr. name, 2 Cor. 1:19; 1 Thess. 1:1; 2 Thess. 1:1; 1 Pet. 5:12, see Σίλας*
G4610.abbottSmith: Silvanus , latinized name of Silas: II Co 1:19 , I Th 1:1 , II Th 1:1 , I Pe 5:12 .†
G4610.strongs: of Latin origin; "silvan"; Silvanus, a Christian

G2781.abbottSmith: honeycomb : κ. μελίσσιον , Lk 24:42 (Rec.; WH, txt., R, txt., omit).†
G2781.strongs: diminutive from (wax); a cell for honey, i.e. (collectively) the comb

G999.mounce: a pit, well or cistern, Mt. 12:11; 15:14; Lk. 6:39*
G999.strongs: akin to G900 (βαθύνω); a hole (in the ground); specially, a cistern

G271.thayer: amethyst (a kind of rock crystal: the best specimens are the color of unmixed wine, whence perhaps the name), believed to stave off drunkenness.
G271.mounce: an amethyst, a gem of a deep purple or violet color, so called from its supposed efficacy in keeping off drunkenness, Rev. 21:20*
G271.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3184 (μεθύω); the "amethyst" (supposed to prevent intoxication)

G1201.strongs: from a derivative of G1199 (δεσμόν) (equivalent to G1196 (δεσμέω)); a place of bondage, i.e. a dungeon

G793.mounce: not made firm; unsettled, unstable, unsteady, 2 Pet. 2:14; 3:16*
G793.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4741 (στηρίζω); unfixed, i.e. (figuratively) vacillating

G2813.mounce: to steal, Mt. 6:19, 20; 19:18; to take away stealthily, remove secretly, Mt. 27:64; 28:13

G2276.abbottSmith: inferior , less ; neut., τὸ ἧ. , adverbially, less : II Co 12:15 ; εἰς τὸ ἧ. , for the worse (opp. to κρεῖττον ): I Co 11:17 .†
G2276.strongs: neuter of comparative of (slightly) used for that of G2556 (κακός); worse (as noun); by implication, less (as adverb)

G433.mounce: to come up to, to pertain to; ἀνήκει, impers. it is fit, proper, becoming, Col. 3:18; Eph. 5:4; Phlm. 8*
G433.abbottSmith: prop., to have come up to ; in later writers, impers. it is due , it is befitting : in ethical sense (MM, VGT , s.v.), Eph 5:4 , Col 3:18 ; τὸ ἀνῆκον , Phm 8 .†
G433.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2240 (ἥκω); to attain to, i.e. (figuratively) be proper

G1637.abbottSmith: olive-oil : Lk 16:6 , Re 6:6 18:13 ; for lamps, Mt 25:3 , 4 , 8 ; for healing, Mk 6:13 , Lk 10:34 , Jo 5:14 ; for anointing at feasts, Lk 7:46 , He 1:9 (LXX) .†
G1637.strongs: neuter of the same as G1636 (ἐλαία); olive oil

G3146.thayer: I flog, scourge, the victim being strapped to a pole or frame; met: I chastise.
G3146.mounce: to scourge, whip, Mt. 10:17; 20:19; 23:34; Mk. 10:34; Lk. 18:33; Jn. 19:1; met. to chastise, Heb. 12:6*
G3146.strongs: from G3148 (μάστιξ); to flog (literally or figuratively)

G4157.mounce: breath, respiration, Acts 17:25; a wind, a blast of wind, breeze, Acts 2:2*

G384.thayer: I pervert, subvert, dismantle, unsettle, overthrow, destroy.
G384.mounce: pr. to collect one’s effects or baggage (σκεύη) in order to remove; to lay waste by carrying off or destroying every thing; met. to unsettle, pervert, subvert, Acts 15:24*
G384.abbottSmith: prop. to pack up baggage , hence, to dismantle , ravage , destroy ; metaph., to unsettle , subvert (MM, VGT , s.v.): ψυχάς , Ac 15:24 .†
G384.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) (in the sense of reversal) and a derivative of G4632 (σκεῦος); properly, to pack up (baggage), i.e. (by implication, and figuratively) to upset

G5371.thayer: Philemon, a Christian man of Colossae.
G5371.mounce: Philemon, pr. name, Phlm. 1; subscr. and title*
G5371.strongs: from G5368 (φιλέω); friendly; Philemon, a Christian

G4436.thayer: a divining spirit, Python, called after the Pythian serpent said to have guarded the oracle at Delphi and been slain by Apollo.
G4436.abbottSmith: in cl., Python , a serpent slain by Apollo, who is hence surnamed the Pythian.
G4436.strongs: from (the name of the region where Delphi, the seat of the famous oracle, was located); a Python, i.e. (by analogy, with the supposed diviner there) inspiration (soothsaying)

G415.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1655 (ἐλεήμων); merciless

G3876.mounce: an erroneous or imperfect hearing; disobedience, Rom. 5:19; a deviation from obedience, 2 Cor. 10:6; Heb. 2:2*
G3876.strongs: from G3878 (παρακούω); inattention, i.e. (by implication) disobedience

G2484.thayer: Ituraean, an adjective applied to a district also called Trachonitis, about 60 miles east of the Sea of Galilee, and partly inhabited by the nomad tribe called Ituraeans.
G2484.mounce: Ituraea, a district of Palestine beyond Jordan, Lk. 3:1*
G2484.abbottSmith: Ituræan (in cl. always ὁι Ἰ. , the Ituræans ) χώρα ( ἡ Ἰτουραίων ὀρεινή, τὰ τῶν Ἰ. μέρη , Strabo, XVI, ii, 16, 20), Lk 3:1 (cf. Exp. (1894), ix, 51 ff., 143 ff., 288 ff.).†
G2484.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03195); Ituræa (i.e. Jetur), a region of Palestine

G3916.abbottSmith: on the spot , forthwith , instantly : Mt 21:19 , 20 , Lk 1:64 4:39 5:25 8:44 , 47 , 55 13:13 18:43 19:11 22:60 , Ac 3:7 5:10 12:23 13:11 16:26 , 33 (on the usage in Mt, Lk, v. Dalman, Words , 28 f.).†
G3916.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5536 (χρῆμα) (in its original sense); at the thing itself, i.e. instantly

G5338.abbottSmith: light, brightness , usually c. gen. of something that shines or reflects: of the moon, Mt 24:29 , Mk 13:24 ; of a lamp, Lk 11:33 (WH, RV, φῶς ).†
G5338.strongs: probably akin to the base of G5457 (φῶς) (compare G5350 (φθέγγομαι)); brilliancy

G735.thayer: Artemis, a goddess, worshipped principally at Ephesus, typifying fertility (she had no relation with the other Artemis, the maiden huntress, to whom corresponded the Latin Diana).
G735.abbottSmith: Artemis , an Asiatic goddess, to be disting. from the Gk. goddess of the same name: Ac 19:24 , 27 , 28 , 34 , 35 .†
G735.strongs: probably from the same as G736 (ἀρτέμων); prompt; Artemis, the name of a Grecian goddess borrowed by the Asiatics for one of their deities

G4190.mounce: bad, unsound, Mt. 6:23; 7:17, 18; evil, afflictive, Eph. 5:16; 6:13; Rev. 16:2; evil, wrongful, malignant, malevolent, Mt. 5:11, 39; Acts 28:21; evil, wicked, impious, and τὸ πονηρόν, evil, wrong, wickedness, Mt. 5:37, 45; 9:4; slothful, inactive, Mt. 25:26; Lk. 19:22; ὁ πονηρός, the evil one, the devil, Mt. 13:19, 38; Jn. 17:15; evil eye, i.q. φθονερός envious, Mt. 20:15; Mk. 7:22; impl. covetous, Mt. 7:11
G4190.abbottSmith: of persons, oppressed by toils (Hes.); of things, toilsome , painful ( καιρός , Si 51:12 ): Eph 5:16 6:13 , Re 16:2 .
G4190.strongs: from a derivative of G4192 (πόνος); hurtful, i.e. evil (properly, in effect or influence, and thus differing from G2556 (κακός), which refers rather to essential character, as well as from G4550 (σαπρός), which indicates degeneracy from original virtue); figuratively, calamitous; also (passively) ill, i.e. diseased; but especially (morally) culpable, i.e. derelict, vicious, facinorous; neuter (singular) mischief, malice, or (plural) guilt; masculine (singular) the devil, or (plural) sinners
G4190.kjv: bad, evil, grievous, harm, lewd, malicious, wicked(-ness)

G1204.thayer: (originally: hither, hence) (a) exclamatory: come, (b) temporal: now, the present.
G1204.mounce: here; used also as a sort of imperative, come, Come here! Mt. 19:21; Mk. 10:21; used of time, ἄχρι τοῦ δεῦρο, i.e. χρόνου, to the present time, Rom. 1:13
G1204.strongs: of uncertain affinity; here; used also imperative hither!; and of time, hitherto

G5540.mounce: use, employment; manner of using, Rom. 1:26, 27*
G5540.abbottSmith: use : in a sexual sense, Ro 1:26 ; c. gen. obj., θηλείας , ib. 27 (for exx., v. Thayer, s.v.).†
G5540.strongs: from G5530 (χράομαι); employment, i.e. (specially), sexual intercourse (as an occupation of the body)

G5483.thayer: (a) I show favor to, (b) I pardon, forgive, (c) I show kindness.
G5483.mounce: to gratify; to bestow in kindness, grant as a free favor, Lk. 7:21; Rom. 8:32; to grant the deliverance of a person in favor to the desire of others, Acts 3:14; 27:24; Phlm. 22; to sacrifice a person to the demand of enemies, Acts 25:11; to remit, forgive, Lk. 7:42; 2 Cor. 2:7, 10
G5483.abbottSmith: to show favour or kindness : c. dat. pers., Ga 3:18 .
G5483.strongs: middle voice from G5485 (χάρις); to grant as a favor, i.e. gratuitously, in kindness, pardon or rescue

G2098.thayer: the good news of the coming of the Messiah, the gospel; the gen. after it expresses sometimes the giver (God), sometimes the subject (the Messiah, etc.), sometimes the human transmitter (an apostle).
G2098.mounce: glad tidings, good or joyful news, Mt. 4:23; 9:35; the Gospel; doctrines of the Gospel, Mt. 26:13; Mk. 8:35; meton. the preaching of, or instruction in, the Gospel, 1 Cor. 4:15; 9:14
G2098.strongs: from the same as G2097 (εὐαγγελίζω); a good message, i.e. the gospel

G3321.thayer: mid-heaven, the middle of heaven, the zenith.
G3321.strongs: from a presumed compound of G3319 (μέσος) and G3772 (οὐρανός); mid-sky

G4487.thayer: a thing spoken, (a) a word or saying of any kind, as command, report, promise, (b) a thing, matter, business.
G4487.mounce: that which is spoken; declaration, saying, speech, word, Mt. 12:36; 26:75; Mk. 9:32; 14:72; a command, mandate, direction, Lk. 3:2; 5:5; a promise, Lk. 1:38; 2:29; a prediction, prophecy, 2 Pet. 3:2; a doctrine of God or Christ, Jn. 3:34; 5:47; 6:63, 68; Acts 5:20; an accusation, charge, crimination, Mt. 5:11; 27:14; from the Hebrew, a thing, Mt. 4:4; Lk. 4:4; a matter, affair, transaction, business, Mt. 18:16; Lk. 1:65; 2 Cor. 13:1
G4487.abbottSmith: prop., of that which is said or spoken,
G4487.strongs: from G4483 (ῥέω); an utterance (individually, collectively or specially),; by implication, a matter or topic (especially of narration, command or dispute); with a negative naught whatever

G431.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and an obsolete (a brood); properly, akin, i.e. (specially) a cousin

G3024.abbottSmith: forgetfulness : λ. λαβεῖν (on the phrase, v. Mayor, in l.): II Pe 1:9 .†

G603.mounce: earnest expectation, eager hope, Rom. 8:19; Phil. 1:20*
G603.abbottSmith: to watch with outstretched head , watch anxiously (Polyb., π. ; v. Deiss., LAE , 374 5 , 377 f.), Ro 8:19 , Phl 1:20 (Lft., in l.; Cremer, 177).†
G603.strongs: from a comparative of G575 (ἀπό) and a compound of (the head) and G1380 (δοκέω) (in the sense of watching); intense anticipation

G653.mounce: pr. to speak or repeat offhand; also, to require or lead others to speak without premeditation, as by questions calculated to elicit unpremeditated answers, to endeavor to entrap into unguarded language, Lk. 11:53*
G653.abbottSmith: In cl., to speak from memory , to dictate to a pupil (Plat.).
G653.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a (presumed) derivative of G4750 (στόμα); to speak off-hand (properly, dictate), i.e. to catechize (in an invidious manner)

G4300.mounce: to sanction and establish previously, ratify and confirm before, Gal. 3:17*
G4300.abbottSmith: to establish or confirm beforehand : Ga 3:17 .†
G4300.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2964 (κυρόω); to ratify previously

G447.thayer: I send up, produce, send back; I let go; I relax, loosen, hence met: I give up, desist from.
G447.mounce: (1) to loose, slacken, Acts 27:40; to unbind, unfasten, Acts 16:26; (2) to omit, dispense with, Eph. 6:9; (3) to leave or neglect, Heb. 13:5*
G447.abbottSmith: to send up , produce , to send back .
G447.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and (to send); to let up, i.e. (literally) slacken or (figuratively) desert, desist from

G2287.mounce: bringing or causing death, deadly, fatal, Jas. 3:8*
G2287.strongs: from (the feminine form of) G2288 (θάνατος) and G5342 (φέρω); death-bearing, i.e. fatal

G1323.thayer: a double-drachma, two drachmae, a Greek silver coin.
G1323.mounce: a didrachmon or double drachma, a silver coin equal to the drachma of Alexandria, to two Attic drachmas, to two Roman denarii, and to the half-shekel of the Jews, Mt. 17:24 (2x)*
G1323.abbottSmith: worth two drachmae ; τὸ δ. (sc. νόμισμα , coin ),
G1323.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G1406 (δραχμή); a double drachma (didrachm)

G4439.mounce: a gate, Mt. 7:13, 14; Lk. 7:12; Acts 12:10; πύλαι ᾅδου, the gates of hades, the nether world and its powers, the powers of destruction, dissolution, Mt. 16:18
G4439.abbottSmith: a gate : Lk 7:12 , Ac 9:24 12:10 16:13 , He 13:12 ; ἡ Ὡραία Π. τ. ἱεροῦ , Ac 3:10 . Metaph., Mt 7:13 , 14 ; πύλαι ᾅδου ( Wi 16:13 , III Mac 5:51 , and cf. κλεῖς ᾅδου , Re 1:18 ): Mt 16:18 .†
G4439.strongs: apparently a primary word; a gate, i.e. the leaf or wing of a folding entrance (literally or figuratively)

G5107.thayer: of this kind, such, such as follows.
G5107.mounce: such as this; such as follows, 2 Pet. 1:17*
G5107.strongs: (including the other inflections); from a derivative of 5104 and G1161 (δέ); such-like then, i.e. so great

G726.thayer: I seize, snatch, obtain by robbery.
G726.mounce: to seize, as a wild beast, Jn. 10:12; take away by force, snatch away, Mt. 13:19; Jn. 10:28, 29; Acts 23:10; Jude 23; met. to seize on with avidity, eagerly, appropriate, Mt. 11:12; to convey away suddenly, transport hastily, Jn. 6:15
G726.abbottSmith: to seize , catch up , snatch away , carry off by force : c. acc. rei, Mt 12:29 13:19 , Jo 10:12 , 28 , 29 ; τ. βασιλείαν τ. θεοῦ , Mt 11:12 ; c. acc. pers., Jo 6:15 , Ac 8:39 23:10 , Ju 23 ; pass., seq. ἕως , II Co 12:2 ; εἰς , II Co 12:4 , I Th 4:17 ; πρός , Re 12:5 (cf. δι- , συν-αρπάζω , and v. MM, s.v.).†
G726.strongs: from a derivative of G138 (αἱρέομαι); to seize (in various applications)
G726.kjv: catch (away, up), pluck, pull, take (by force)

G2195.abbottSmith: Zaccai , Zacchaeus , a publican: Lk 19:2 , 5 , 8 (cf. II Mac 10:19 ).†
G2195.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H02140); Zacchæus, an Israelite

G1461.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G2759 (κέντρον); to prick in, i.e. ingraft

G3041.mounce: a tract of standing water; a lake, Lk. 5:1; Rev. 20:14
G3041.abbottSmith: a lake : of the Sea of Galilee (Mt, Mk, v.s. θάλασσα ), Lk 5:2 8:22 , 23 , 33 ; λ. Γεννησαρέτ (q.v.), Lk 5:1 ; λ. τ. πυρός , Re 19:20 20:10 , 14 , 15 ; καιομένη πυρί , Re 21:8 .†
G3041.strongs: probably from G3040 (λιμήν) (through the idea of nearness of shore); a pond (large or small)

G3064.mounce: remaining; the rest, remainder, Mt. 22:6; as an adv., οῦ λοιποῦ, henceforth, Gal. 6:17; τὸ λοιπόν, or λοιπόν, henceforward, Mt. 26:45; 2 Tim. 4:8; Acts 27:20; as to the rest, besides, 1 Cor. 1:16; finally, Eph. 6:10; ὁ δὲ λοιπόν, but, now, furthermore, 1 Cor. 4:2
G3064.abbottSmith: the remaining , the rest , pl., οἱ λ. : c. subst., Mt 25:11 , Ac 2:37 , Ro 1:13 , al.; absol., Mt 22:6 , Mk 16:[13] , Lk 24:10 , al.; οἱ λ. οἱ (Bl., § 47, 8), Ac 28:9 , I Th 4:13 , Re 2:24 ; οἱ λ. τ. ἀνθρώπων (LS, s.v., ad. init.), Re 9:20 ; τὰ λ. , Mk 4:19 , Re 3:2 , al. Neut. sing. (acc. ref.), adverbially, τὸ λ. ; for the future , henceforth : Mk 14:41 , I Co 7:29 (Lft., Notes , 232 f.), He 10:
G3064.strongs: genitive case singular of the same as G3062 (λοιποί); remaining time

G246.thayer: foreign, of another tribe or race.
G246.mounce: of another race or nation, i.e., not a Jew, a foreigner, Acts 10:28*
G246.abbottSmith: foreign , of another race (MM, VGT , s.v.); as opp. to a Jew, a Gentile : Ac 10:28 .†
G246.strongs: from G243 (ἄλλος) and G5443 (φυλή); foreign, i.e. (specially) Gentile

G5111.thayer: I dare, endure, am bold, have courage, make up the mind.
G5111.mounce: to assume resolution to do a thing, Mk. 15:43; Rom. 5:7; Phil. 1:14; to make up the mind, 2 Cor. 10:12; to dare, Acts 5:13; 7:32; to presume, Mt. 22:46; Mk. 12:34; Lk. 20:40; Jn. 21:12; Rom. 15:18; Jude 9; to have the face, 1 Cor. 6:1; absol. to assume a bold bearing, courageous, 2 Cor. 10:2; 11:21*
G5111.abbottSmith: to have courage, to venture, dare, be bold : absol, II Co 11:21 ; seq. ἐπί , II Co 10:2 ; c. inf. (Bl., § 69, 4), Mt 22:46 , Mk 12:34 , Lk 20:40 , Jo 21:12 , Ac 5:13 7:32 , Ro 15:18 , I Co 6:1 , II Co 10:12 , Phl 1:14 , Ju 9 ; to submit to (in cl. usually absol. in this sense), Ro 5:7 (v. Field, Notes , 155); τολμήσας εἰσῆλθεν , took courage and went in (v. Field, op. cit. , 44), Mk 15:43 (cf. ἀπο
G5111.strongs: from (boldness; probably itself from the base of G5056 (τέλος) through the idea of extreme conduct); to venture (objectively or in act; while G2292 (θαῤῥέω) is rather subjective or in feeling); by implication, to be courageous

G4535.thayer: the tossing of the sea in a tempest; agitation, rolling.
G4535.mounce: agitation, tossing, rolling, spc. of the sea, Lk. 21:25*
G4535.abbottSmith: in poets and late prose, a tossing , as of an earthquake; esp. the tossing , the rolling swell of the sea: Lk 21:25 .†
G4535.strongs: probably from the base of G4525 (σαίνω); a vibration, i.e. (specially), billow

G2462.abbottSmith: a horse : Ja 3:3 , Re 6:2 ff. Re 9:7 ff. Re 14:20 18:13 19:11 ff.†

G2751.thayer: I shear, cut the hair of; mid: I cut my own hair, have my hair cut.
G2751.mounce: to cut off the hair, shear, shave, Acts 8:32; 18:18; 1 Cor. 11:6 (2x)*
G2751.abbottSmith: to cut short the hair, shear : a sheep, Ac 8:32 ( Is 53:7 ). Mid., to have one's hair cut off , be shorn : absol., I Co 11:6 ; τ. κεφαλήν , Ac 18:18 .†

G5241.thayer: I intercede for, make petition for.
G5241.abbottSmith: to intercede or make petition for (v. Deiss., BS , 121 f.): seq. ὑπέρ , Ro 8:26 .†
G5241.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1793 (ἐντυγχάνω); to intercede in behalf of

G2524.mounce: to let down, lower, Lk. 5:19; Acts 9:25; 10:11; 11:5*
G2524.abbottSmith: to send or let down : seq. εἰς , Lk 5:19 ; διά , c. gen., Lk 5:19 , Ac 9:25 ; ptcp., seq. ἐπί , c. gen., Ac 10:11 ; ἐκ , Ac 11:5 .†
G2524.strongs: from G2596 (κατά); and (to send); to lower

G1530.mounce: to leap or spring in, rush in eagerly, Acts 16:29*
G1530.abbottSmith: to spring in, rush in : Ac 14:14 (Rec.), Ac 16:29 (for exx. from π. , v. MM, Exp. , xii).†
G1530.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and (to leap); to rush in

G598.thayer: (lit: I rub), jostle, press hard, crowd.
G598.mounce: pr. to press out; to press close, press upon, crowd, Lk. 8:45*
G598.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2346 (θλίβω); to crowd (from every side)

G1545.thayer: (a) a way out, escape, (b) result, issue.
G1545.mounce: a way out, egress; hence, result, issue, Heb. 13:7; means of clearance or successful endurance, 1 Cor. 10:13*
G1545.abbottSmith: a way out (Hom., Xen.): I Co 10:13 .
G1545.strongs: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G939 (βάσις) (meaning to go out); an exit (literally or figuratively)

G4433.thayer: I am in poverty, am a beggar.
G4433.mounce: to be a beggar; to be or become poor, be in poverty, 2 Cor. 8:9*
G4433.abbottSmith: to be a beggar , to beg (so chiefly in cl.).
G4433.strongs: from G4434 (πτωχός); to be a beggar, i.e. (by implication) to become indigent (figuratively)

G70.thayer: the state of being awake (at night), sleeplessness, watching.
G70.abbottSmith: sleeplessness , watching : II Co 6:5 11:27 . (Plat., Hdt.; for exx. in π. , v. MM, VGT, s.v.).†
G70.strongs: from G69 (ἀγρυπνέω); sleeplessness, i.e. a keeping awake

G3940.thayer: a sojourning, a dwelling in a strange land.
G3940.mounce: a sojourning, temporary residence in a foreign land, Acts 13:17; 1 Pet. 1:17*

G1998.mounce: to run together to a place, Mk. 9:25*
G1998.abbottSmith: to run together again : Mk 9:25 (v. Swete, in l.).†
G1998.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4936 (συντρέχω); to hasten together upon one place (or a particular occasion)

G5048.thayer: (a) as a course, a race, or the like: I complete, finish (b) as of time or prediction: I accomplish, (c) I make perfect; pass: I am perfected.
G5048.mounce: to execute fully, discharge, Jn. 4:34; 5:36; 17:4; to reach the end of, run through, finish, Lk. 2:43; Acts 20:24; to consummate, place in a condition of finality, Heb. 7:19; to perfect a person, advance a person to final completeness of character, Heb. 2:10; 5:9; 7:28; to perfect a person, advance a person to a completeness of its kind, which needs no further provision, Heb. 9:9; 10:1, 14; pass. to receive fulfillment, Jn. 19:28; to be brought to the goal, to reach the end of one’s course, Lk. 13:32; Phil. 3:12; Heb. 11:40; 12:23; to be fully developed, 2 Cor. 12:9; Jas. 2:22; 1 Jn. 2:5; 4:12, 17; to be completely organized, to be closely embodied, Jn. 17:23
G5048.abbottSmith: bring to an end, finish, accomplish, fulfil : Jo 4:34 , Ac 20:24 ; of time, Lk 2:43 , Jo 5:36 17:4 ; pass., ἡ γραφή , Jo 19:28 .
G5048.strongs: from G5046 (τέλειος); to complete, i.e. (literally) accomplish, or (figuratively) consummate (in character)

G1798.mounce: a dream; in NT a supernatural suggestion or impression received during sleep, a sleep-vision, Acts 2:17*
G1798.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5258 (ὕπνος); something seen in sleep, i.e. a dream (vision in a dream)

G2008.thayer: (a) I rebuke, chide, admonish, (b) I warn.
G2008.mounce: pr. to set a value upon; to assess a penalty; to allege as a crimination; hence, to reprove, chide, censure, rebuke, reprimand, Mt. 19:13; Lk. 23:40; in NT to admonish strongly, enjoin strictly, Mt. 12:16; Lk. 17:3
G2008.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5091 (τιμάω); to tax upon, i.e. censure or admonish; by implication, forbid

G4129.mounce: optative, πληθύναι (3 sg), trans. to multiply, cause to increase, augment, 2 Cor. 9:10; Heb. 6:14; pass. to be multiplied, increase, be accumulated, Mt. 24:12; Acts 6:7; 7:17; intrans. to multiply, increase, be augmented, Acts 6:1
G4129.abbottSmith: trans., to increase , multiply : II Co 9:10 , He 6:14 (LXX) ; pass., to be increased , to multiply : Mt 24:12 , Ac 6:7 7:17 9:31 12:24 ; c. dat. pers. ( Da LXX TH 3:31 ( 98 ) al.), I Pe 1:2 , II Pe 1:2 , Ju 2 .
G4129.strongs: from another form of G4128 (πλῆθος); to increase (transitively or intransitively)

G2656.thayer: I nod, make a sign, beckon.
G2656.mounce: pr. to nod, signify assent by a nod; genr. to make signs, beckon, Lk. 5:7*
G2656.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3506 (νεύω); to nod down (towards), i.e. (by analogy) to make signs to

G783.abbottSmith: a salutation (so always in RV), greeting : oral, Mt 23:7 , Mk 12:38 , Lk 1:29 , 41 , 44 11:43 20:46 ; written, I Co 16:21 , Col 4:18 , II Th 3:17 .†
G783.strongs: from G782 (ἀσπάζομαι); a greeting (in person or by letter)

G5613.thayer: as, like as, about, as it were, according as, how, when, while, as soon as, so that.
G5613.mounce: conjunction formed from the relative pronoun ὅς, used as a comparative part. and conj., as, correlatively, Mk. 4:26; Jn. 7:46; Rom. 5:15; as, like as, Mt. 10:16; Eph. 5:8; according as, Gal. 6:10; as, as it were, Rev. 8:8; as, Lk. 16:1; Acts 3:12; before numerals, about, Mk. 5:13; conj. that, Acts 10:28; how, Rom. 11:2; when, Mt. 28:9; Phil. 2:23; as an exclamatory particle, how, Rom. 10:15; equivalent to ὥστε, accordingly, Heb. 3:11; also, on condition that, provided that, Acts 20:24; ὡς εἰπεῖν, so to speak, Heb. 7:9
G5613.abbottSmith: As relat. adv. of manner, as, like as, just as, even as ; with a demonstrative, like οὕτως , expressed or understood: οὕτως . . . ὡς , Mk 4:26 , I Co 3:15 , Eph 5:28 , Ja 2:12 , al.; ὡς . . . οὕτως , Ac 8:32 , I Co 7:17 , al.; elliptically (sc. οὕτως, οὕτω ), c. nom., Mt 6:29 , al.; c. acc., Mt 19:19 , Mk 12:31 , al.; c. prep., Mt 26:55 , Mk 14:48 , Lk 22:52 , Jo 7:10 , al.; c. verb., Jo 15:6 , II
G5613.strongs: probably adverb of comparative from G3739 (ὅς); which how, i.e. in that manner (very variously used, as follows)
G5613.kjv: about, after (that), (according) as (it had been, it were), as soon (as), even as (like), for, how (greatly), like (as, unto), since, so (that), that, to wit, unto, when(-soever), while, X with all speed

G25.thayer: I love, wish well to, take pleasure in, long for; denotes the love of reason, esteem.
G25.mounce: to love, value, esteem, feel or manifest generous concern for, be faithful towards; to delight in, to set store upon, Rev. 12:11
G25.abbottSmith: to love , to feel and exhibit esteem and goodwill to a person, to prize and delight in a thing. Of human affection, to men: τ. πλησίον , Mt 5:43 ; τ. ἐχθρούς , ib. 44 ; to Christ, Jo 8:42 ; to God, Mt 22:37 ; c. acc. rei, Lk 11:43 , Jo 12:43 , Eph 5:25 , II Tim 4:8 , 10 , He 1:9 , I Pe 2:17 , 3:10 , II Pe 2:15 , I Jo 2:15 , Re 12:11 . Of divine love; God's love: to men, Ro 8:37 ; to Christ, Jo 3:3
G25.strongs: perhaps from (much) (or compare G5689); to love (in a social or moral sense)

G574.mounce: in simplicity; sincerely, really, or, liberally, bountifully, Jas. 1:5*
G574.abbottSmith: simply , sincerely , graciously : Ja 1:5 . ("Later writers comprehend under the one word the whole magnanimous and honourable type of character in which ... singleness of mind is the central feature"—Hort, Ja. , 1:5 )†
G574.strongs: adverb from G573 (ἁπλοῦς) (in the objective sense of G572 (ἁπλότης)); bountifully;

G5044.thayer: I bring up children, rear young.
G5044.strongs: from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and G5142 (τρέφω); to be a childrearer, i.e. fulfil the duties of a female parent

G3799.thayer: (a) the face, countenance, (b) the features, outward appearance.
G3799.mounce: a sight; the face, countenance, Jn. 11:44; Rev. 1:16; external appearance, Jn. 7:24*
G3799.abbottSmith: the act of seeing , the sense of sight .
G3799.strongs: from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); properly, sight (the act), i.e. (by implication) the visage, an external show

G2107.thayer: (a) good-will (good-pleasure), favor, feeling of complacency of God to man, (b) good-pleasure, satisfaction, happiness, delight of men.
G2107.mounce: good will, favor, Lk. 2:14; good pleasure, purpose, intention, Mt. 11:26; Lk. 10:21; Eph. 1:5, 9; Phil. 2:13; by impl. desire, Rom. 10:1; Phil. 1:15; 2 Thess. 1:11*
G2107.abbottSmith: good pleasure, good-will, satisfaction, approval : Mt 11:26 , Lk 10:21 , Ro 10:1 , Eph 1:5 , 9 , Phl 1:15 2:13 ; c. gen. obj., II Th 1:11 (v. Milligan, in l); ἐν ἀνθρώποις εὐδοκία , Rec., R, mg., WH, mg. (v. Field, Notes , 48 f.), Lk 2:14 ; -ας , Lk, l.c. , R, txt., WH, txt. (v. ICC , in l; WH, App. , in l).†
G2107.strongs: from a presumed compound of G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G1380 (δοκέω); satisfaction, i.e. (subjectively) delight, or (objectively) kindness, wish, purpose
G2107.kjv: desire, good pleasure (will), X seem good

G5132.thayer: a table, (a) for food or banqueting, (b) for money-changing or business.
G5132.mounce: a table, an eating table, Mt. 15:27; Mk. 7:28; Heb. 9:2; by impl. a meal, feast, Rom. 11:9; 1 Cor. 10:21; a table or counter of a money changer, Mt. 21:12; a bank, Lk. 19:23; by impl. pl. money matters, Acts 6:2
G5132.abbottSmith: a table, dining-table : Mt 15:27 , Mk 7:28 , Lk 16:21 19:23 22:21 , 30 ; of the table of shewbread, He 9:2 . By meton., of food provided (v. DB , iv, 670 a): Ac 16:34 , Ro 11:9 (LXX) I Co 10:21 ; διακονεῖν ταῖς τ. , Ac 6:2 (cf. Page, in l., but v. infr.).
G5132.strongs: probably contracted from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3979 (πεζῇ); a table or stool (as being four-legged), usually for food (figuratively, a meal); also a counter for money (figuratively, a broker's office for loans at interest)

G4569.mounce: Saul, the Hebrew name of the Apostle Paul, Σαούλ with a Greek termination, Acts 7:58; 8:1, 3; 9:1
G4569.abbottSmith: Saul , the Jewish name of the Apostle Paul: Ac 7:58 8:1 , 3 9:1 , 8 , 11 , 22 , 24 11:25 , 30 12:25 13:1, 2 , 7 , 9 .†
G4569.strongs: of Hebrew origin, the same as G4549 (Σαούλ); Saulus (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul

G2316.mounce: a deity, Acts 7:43; 1 Cor. 8:5; an idol, Acts 7:40; God, the true God, Mt. 3:9, et al. freq.; God, possessed of true godhead, Jn. 1:1; Rom. 9:5; from the Hebrew, applied to potentates, Jn. 10:34, 35; τῷ θεῷ, an intensive term, from the Hebrew, exceedingly, Acts 7:20, and, perhaps, 2 Cor. 10:4
G2316.abbottSmith: a god or deity , God . In polytheistic sense, a god or deity : Ac 28:6 , I Co 8:4 , II Th 2:4 , al.; pl., Ac. 14:11 19:26 , Ga 4:8 , al. Of the one true God ; anarthrous: Mt 6:24, Lk 20:38, al.; esp. c. prep. (Kühner 3 , iii, 605), ἀπὸ θ. , Jo 3:2 ; ἐκ , Ac 5:39 , II Co 5:1 , Phl 3:9 ; ὑπό , Ro 13:1 ; παρὰ θεοῦ , Jo 1:6 ; παρὰ θεῷ , II Th 1:6 , I Pe 2:4 ; κατὰ θεόν , Ro 8:27 , II Co 7:9 , 10 ; als
G2316.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very

G1452.strongs: neuter of the comparative of G1451 (ἐγγύς); nearer

G3817.mounce: to strike, smite, with the fist, Mt. 26:68; Lk. 22:64; with a sword, Mk. 14:47; Jn. 18:10; to strike as a scorpion, to sting, Rev. 9:5*
G3817.abbottSmith: to strike , smite : with the hand or fist, Mt 26:68 , Lk 22:64 ; with a sword, Mk 14:47 , Jo 18:10 ; of a reptile, to sting : Re 9:5 .†
G3817.strongs: a primary verb; to hit (as if by a single blow and less violently than G5180 (τύπτω)); specially, to sting (as a scorpion)

G2187.mounce: Ephraim, pr. name, indecl. Jn. 11:54*. See PhotoGuide.
G2187.abbottSmith: Ephraim , a town near Jerusalem: Jo 11:54 .†
G2187.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0669 or better H06085); Ephraim, a place in Palestine

G416.thayer: I am blown with the wind (referring to the gentler motions of the air).
G416.mounce: to agitate with the wind; pass. to be agitated or driven by the wind, Jas. 1:6*
G416.abbottSmith: pass., to be driven by the wind : Ja 1:6 .†
G416.strongs: from G417 (ἄνεμος); to toss with the wind

G2323.thayer: I care for, attend, serve, treat, especially of a physician; hence: I heal.
G2323.mounce: to heal, cure, Mt. 4:23, 24; 8:16; pass. to receive service, Acts 17:25; to serve, minister to, render service and attendance; to render divine service, worship, Acts 17:25
G2323.abbottSmith: to do service , serve : c. acc. pers., pass., Ac 17:25 .
G2323.strongs: from the same as G2324 (θεράπων); to wait upon menially, i.e. (figuratively) to adore (God), or (specially) to relieve (of disease)

G5498.mounce: handwriting; a written form, literal instrument, as distinguished from a spiritual dispensation, Col. 2:14*
G5498.abbottSmith: written with the hand . As subst., τὸ χ. , a handwriting (Inscr., Polyb., al.): metaph., Col 2:14 .†
G5498.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5495 (χείρ) and G1125 (γράφω); something hand-written ("chirograph"), i.e. a manuscript (specially, a legal document or bond (figuratively))

G3786.strongs: from (to heap up, i.e. accumulate or benefit); gain

G2030.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a presumed derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); a looker-on

G127.abbottSmith: a sense of shame , modesty : I Ti 2:9 (for exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G127.strongs: perhaps from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1492 (εἴδω) (through the idea of downcast eyes); bashfulness, i.e. (towards men), modesty or (towards God) awe

G1432.thayer: as a free gift, without payment, freely.
G1432.mounce: gratis, gratuitously, freely, Mt. 10:8; Rom. 3:24; in NT undeservedly, without cause, Jn. 15:25; in vain, Gal. 2:21
G1432.strongs: accusative case of G1431 (δωρεά) as adverb; gratuitously (literally or figuratively)
G1432.kjv: without a cause, freely, for naught, in vain

G1879.thayer: I rest upon, rely on, trust in.
G1879.mounce: pr. to make to rest upon; mid. to rest upon; to abide with, Lk. 10:6; to rely on, confide in, abide by confidingly, Rom. 2:17*
G1879.abbottSmith: to refresh, cause to rest ( upon ); mid., to rest upon : metaph., c. dat., Ro 2:17 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc, Lk 10:6 (Cremer, 827).†
G1879.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G373 (ἀναπαύω); to settle on; literally (remain) or figuratively (rely)

G1913.mounce: to cause to ascend or mount, to set upon, Lk. 10:34; 19:35; Acts 23:24*
G1913.abbottSmith: to place upon : c. acc pers., Lk 10:34 19:35 , Ac 23:24 .†
G1913.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a reduplicated derivative of the base of G939 (βάσις) (compare G307 (ἀναβιβάζω)); to cause to mount (an animal)

G5391.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G5424 (φρήν); friendly of mind, i.e. kind

G5525.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a ring, i.e. round dance ("choir"

G4915.mounce: one of the same age, an equal in age, Gal. 1:14*
G4915.abbottSmith: one of the same age, an equal in age : Ga 1:14 .†
G4915.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G2244 (ἡλικία); a co-aged person, i.e. alike in years

G2629.thayer: I cut up, cut in pieces, mangle, wound.
G2629.mounce: to cut or dash in pieces; to mangle, wound, Mk. 5:5*
G2629.abbottSmith: to cut up , cut in pieces (cf. Is 27:9 , Je 21:7 , II Ch 34:7 ): ἑαυτὸν λίθοις , Mk 5:5 .†
G2629.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2875 (κόπτω); to chop down, i.e. mangle

G4168.mounce: a flock; met. a flock of Christian disciples, Lk. 12:32; Acts 20:28, 29; 1 Pet. 5:2, 3*
G4168.abbottSmith: a flock , prop., of sheep. Metaph., of Christians: Lk 12:32 , Ac 20:28 , 29 , I Pe 5:3 ; τ. θεοῦ , I Pe 5:2 .†
G4168.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4167 (ποίμνη); a flock, i.e. (figuratively) group (of believers)

G5264.thayer: I receive as a guest, entertain hospitably, welcome.
G5264.mounce: to give reception to; to receive as a guest, welcome, entertain, Lk. 10:38; 19:6; Acts 17:7; Jas. 2:25*
G5264.abbottSmith: to receive under one's roof, receive as a guest, entertain hospitably : c. acc pers., Lk 19:6 , Ac 17:7 , Ja 2:25 ; εἰς τ. οἶκον , Lk 10:38 (v. MM, xxv).†
G5264.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to admit under one's roof, i.e. entertain hospitably

G797.thayer: I flash (with, then like, lightning), am lustrous.
G797.mounce: to lighten, flash as lightning, Lk. 17:24; to be bright, shining, Lk. 24:4*
G797.abbottSmith: to lighten , flash forth : Lk 17:24 24:4 (MM, s.v.).†
G797.strongs: probably from G792 (ἀστήρ); to flash as lightning

G5365.abbottSmith: love of money, avarice : I Ti 6:1 .†

G2554.thayer: I do harm, do wrong, do evil, commit sin.
G2554.mounce: to cause evil, injure, do harm, Mk. 3:4; Lk. 6:9; to do evil, commit sin, 1 Pet. 3:17; 3 Jn. 11*
G2554.abbottSmith: to do harm , to do evil : Mk 3:4 , Lk 6:9 , I Pe 3:17 , III Jo 11 (Cremer, 329).†
G2554.strongs: from G2555 (κακοποιός); to be a bad-doer, i.e. (objectively) to injure, or (genitive) to sin

G4629.abbottSmith: a covering , esp. clothing : I Ti 6:8 .†
G4629.strongs: from a derivative of (a covering; perhaps akin to the base of G4649 (σκοπός) through the idea of noticeableness); clothing

G2802.thayer: Cauda, Clauda, an island twenty-three miles south of the western end of Crete.
G2802.mounce: also spelled Κλαῦδα (G3084) and Κλαῦδη (G3085), Cauda, indecl. pr. name of an island, Acts 27:16*
G2802.abbottSmith: Cauda , Clauda , an island near Crete: Ac 27:16 .†
G2802.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Claude, an island near Crete

G3860.thayer: I hand over, pledge, hand down, deliver, commit, commend, betray, abandon.
G3860.mounce: pluperfect, παραδεδώκεισαν (3 pl), to give over, hand over, deliver up, Mt. 4:12; 5:25; 10:4, 17; to commit, intrust, Mt. 11:27; 25:14; to commit, commend, Acts 14:26; 15:40; to yield up, Jn. 19:30; 1 Cor. 15:24; to abandon, Acts 7:42; Eph. 4:19; to stake, hazard, Acts 15:26; to deliver as a matter of injunction, instruction, etc., Mk. 7:13; Lk. 1:2; Acts 6:14; absol. to render a yield, to be matured, Mk. 4:29
G3860.abbottSmith: to give or hand over to another: c. acc. et dat., Mt 11:27 25:14 , Lk 4:6 , al.; of being delivered up to a course of teaching, pass. seq. εἰς , Ro 6:17 . to commit , commend : Ac 14:26 15:40 , I Pe 2:23 . to give or deliver up to prison or judgment: c. acc. pers., Mt 4:12 , Mk 1:14 , Ro 4:25 , II Pe 2:4 ; id. seq. ὑπέρ , Ro 8:32 ; c. dat., Mt 5:25 , Mk 15:1 , Lk 12:58 , Jo 19:11 , al.; id. seq. ἵ
G3860.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to surrender, i.e yield up, intrust, transmit
G3860.kjv: betray, bring forth, cast, commit, deliver (up), give (over, up), hazard, put in prison, recommend

G2852.thayer: I strike with the fist, buffet; hence: I mistreat violently.
G2852.mounce: to beat with the fist, buffet, Mt. 26:67; Mk. 14:65; met. to maltreat, treat with excessive force, 1 Cor. 4:11; to punish, 1 Pet. 2:20; to buffet, fret, afflict, 2 Cor. 12:7*
G2852.abbottSmith: to strike with the fist , to buffet : Mt 26:67 , Mk 14:65 , II Co 12:7 ; pass., I Co 4:11 , I Pe 2:20 .†
G2852.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G2849 (κολάζω); to rap with the fist

G3982.mounce: pluperfect, ἐπεποίθειν, to persuade, seek to persuade, endeavor to convince, Acts 18:4; 19:8, 26; 28:23; to persuade, influence by persuasion, Mt. 27:20; Acts 13:43; 26:28; to incite, instigate, Acts 14:19; to appease, render tranquil, to quiet, 1 Jn. 3:19; to strive to conciliate, aspire to the favor of, Gal. 1:10; to pacify, conciliate, win over, Mt. 28:14; Acts 12:20; pass. and mid. to be persuaded of, be confident of, Lk. 20:6; Rom. 8:38; Heb. 6:9; to suffer one’s self to be persuaded, yield to persuasion, to be induced, Acts 21:14; to be convinced, to believe, yield belief, Lk. 16:31; Acts 17:4; to assent, listen to, obey, follow, Acts 5:36, 37, 40; 2 perf. πέποιθα, to be assured, be confident, 2 Cor. 2:3; Phil. 1:6; Heb. 13:18; to confide in, trust, rely on, place hope and confidence in, Mt. 27:43; Mk. 10:24; Rom. 2:19
G3982.abbottSmith: Active; trans., to apply persuasion ("conative" in pres.; v. M, Pr. , 147), to prevail upon or win over , persuade : absol., Mt 28:14 , Ac 19:26 ; seq. περί , c. gen. rei, Ac 19:8 ; c. acc. pers., Ac 12:20 14:19 18:4 , II Co 5:11 , Ga 1:10 ; τ. καρδίας ἡμῶν , I Jo 3:19 ; c. acc. seq. περί , Ac 28:23 ; c. acc. et inf., Ac 13:43 26:28 (v. Field, Notes , 141 ff.); c. acc. seq. ἵνα (Plut.), Mt 27:20 .
G3982.strongs: a primary verb; to convince (by argument, true or false); by analogy, to pacify or conciliate (by other fair means); reflexively or passively, to assent (to evidence or authority), to rely (by inward certainty)
G3982.kjv: agree, assure, believe, have confidence, be (wax) conflent, make friend, obey, persuade, trust, yield

G4992.mounce: imparting salvation, saving, Lk. 2:30; 3:6; Acts 28:28; Eph. 6:17; Tit. 2:11*
G4992.abbottSmith: saving, bringing salvation , in NT always in spiritual sense (v.s. σώζω, σωτηρία ): ἡ χάρις ἡ σ. , Tit 2:11 . Neut., -τὸ σ. , as subst. (cl.), salvation : Lk 2:30 ; τ. σ. τ. θεοῦ , Lk 3:6 , Ac 28:28 ; περικεφαλαίαν τοῦ σ. , Eph 6:17 .†
G4992.strongs: neuter of the same as G4991 (σωτηρία) as (properly, concretely) noun; defender or (by implication) defence

G549.mounce: to go away, depart, come Acts 17:10*
G549.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to go); to go away

G3847.mounce: a stepping by the side, deviation; a transgression, violation of law, Rom. 2:23; 4:15

G1762.strongs: contraction for the third person singular present indicative of G1751 (ἔνειμι); impersonally, there is in or among

G4981.thayer: leisure, a school, place where there is leisure.
G4981.mounce: freedom from occupation; later, ease, leisure; a school, Acts 19:9*
G4981.strongs: probably feminine of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω); properly, loitering (as a withholding of oneself from work) or leisure, i.e. (by implication) a "school" (as vacation from physical employment)

G2269.thayer: Esau, elder son of Isaac the patriarch, brother of Jacob.
G2269.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06215); Esau, an Edomite

G3128.thayer: (Hebrew), Manasseh, (a) son of Joseph, founder of a tribe of Israel, (b) son of Hezekiah and father of Amon (Amos).
G3128.mounce: Manasses, pr. name (1) the tribe of Manasseh, Rev. 7:6 (2) Manasseh, king of Judah, Mt. 1:10*
G3128.abbottSmith: Manasseh ; the first-born son of Joseph; the tribe which bore his name: Re 7:6 . King of Judah: Mt 1:10 .†
G3128.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04519); Mannasses (i.e. Menashsheh), an Israelite

G4478.thayer: Rachel, younger wife of the patriarch Jacob.
G4478.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07354); Rachel, the wife of Jacob

G1411.thayer: (a) physical power, force, might, ability, efficacy, energy, meaning (b) plur: powerful deeds, deeds showing (physical) power, marvelous works.
G1411.mounce: power; strength, ability, Mt. 25:15; Heb. 11:11; efficacy, 1 Cor. 4:19, 20; Phil. 3:10; 1 Thess. 1:5; 2 Tim. 3:5; energy, Col. 1:29; 2 Tim. 1:7; meaning, purport of language, 1 Cor. 14:11; authority, Lk. 4:36; 9:1; might, power, majesty, Mt. 22:29; 24:30; Acts 3:12; Rom. 9:17; 2 Thess. 1:7; 2 Pet. 1:16; in NT a manifestation or instance of power, mighty means, Acts 8:10; Rom. 1:16; 1 Cor. 1:18, 24; ἡ δύναμις, omnipotence, Mt. 26:64; Lk. 22:69; Mt. 14:62; pl. authorities, Rom. 8:38; Eph. 1:21; 1 Pet. 3:22; miraculous power, Mk. 5:30; Lk. 1:35; 5:17; 6:19; 8:46; 24:49; 1 Cor. 2:4; a miracle, Mt. 11:20, 21, et al. freq.; a worker of miracles, 1 Cor. 12:28, 29; from the Hebrew αἱ δυνάμεις τῶν οὐρανῶν, the heavenly luminaries, Mt. 24:29; Mk. 13:25; Lk. 21:26; αἱ δυνάμεις, the spiritual powers, Mt. 14:2; Mk. 6:14
G1411.abbottSmith: power, might, strength ; relatively, ability, power to perform: Mt 25:15 , Ac 3:2 , He 11:11 ; κατὰ δ. , II Co 8:3 ; παρὰ δ. , ib. ; ὑπὲρ δ. , II Co 1:8 ; of pecuniary ability, II Co 8:3 , Re 18:3 ; absol., power, might : Lk 24:49 , Ac 1:8 ; opp. to ἀσθένεια , I Co 15:43 ; ἡ δ. τ. ἁμαρτίας , I Co 15:56 ; of power in action, Ro 1:16 , 20 , I Co l:18 , Phl 3:10 , al. ἡ δ. τ. Θεοῦ , Mt 22:29 , Mk 12:
G1411.strongs: from G1410 (δύναμαι); force (literally or figuratively); specially, miraculous power (usually by implication, a miracle itself)
G1411.kjv: ability, abundance, meaning, might(-ily, -y, -y deed), (worker of) miracle(-s), power, strength, violence, mighty (wonderful) work

G5458.thayer: a light, an illuminator, perhaps the sun; a star; brilliancy.
G5458.mounce: a cause of light, illuminator; a light, luminary, star, Phil. 2:15; radiance, or, luminary, Rev. 21:11*
G5458.strongs: from G5457 (φῶς); an illuminator, i.e. (concretely) a luminary, or (abstractly) brilliancy

G4467.thayer: a careless action, an act of villainy.
G4467.mounce: pr. anything done lightly, levity; reckless conduct, crime, Acts 18:14*
G4467.strongs: from a comparative of (easy, i.e. reckless) and G2041 (ἔργον); easy-going behavior, i.e. (by extension) a crime

G1490.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ), G1161 (δέ), and G3361 (μή) (sometimes with G1065 (γέ) added); but if not

G3327.thayer: I change my place (abode), leave, depart, remove, pass over.
G3327.mounce: to go or pass from one place to another, Jn. 5:24; to pass away, be removed, Mt. 17:20; to go away, depart, Mt. 8:34
G3327.abbottSmith: to pass over from one place to another: Mt 17:20 , Lk 10:7 ; with reference to the point of departure only, to withdraw , depart : Mt 8:34 11:1 12:9 15:29 , Jo 7:3 , Ac 18:7 ; of removal from this life, ἐκ τ. κότμου πρὸς τ. Πατέρα , Jo 13:1 ; metaph., ἐκ τ. θανάτου εἰς τ. ζωήν , Jo 5:24 , I Jo 3:14 .†
G3327.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to change place

G117.mounce: Athenian, inhabiting or belonging to Athens, Acts 17:21, 22*
G117.strongs: from G116 (Ἀθῆναι); an Athenæan or inhabitant of Athenæ

G5601.mounce: Obed, David’s grandfather, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:5; Lk. 3:32*
G5601.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05744); Obed, an Israelite

G69.thayer: I am not asleep, am awake; especially: I am watchful, careful.
G69.mounce: to be awake, watch; to be watchful, vigilant
G69.abbottSmith: to be sleepless, wakeful (Theogn., Xen., al.); metaph. (LXX) = cl. ἐγρήγορα , to be watchful, vigilant : Mk 13:33 , Lk 21:36 , Eph 6:18 , He 13:17 .†
G69.strongs: ultimately from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G5258 (ὕπνος); to be sleepless, i.e. keep awake

G2346.thayer: (a) I make narrow (strictly: by pressure); I press upon, (b) I persecute, press hard.
G2346.mounce: to squeeze, press; to press upon, encumber, throng, crowd, Mk. 3:9; met. to distress, afflict, 2 Cor. 1:6; 4:8; pass. to be compressed, narrow, Mt. 7:14
G2346.abbottSmith: to press : c. acc. pers., Mk 3:9 ; ὁδὸς τεθλιμμένη , a narrow (compressed) way , Mt 7:14 . Metaph. (as freq. in LXX), to oppress , afflict , distress : c. acc. pers., II Th 1:6 ; pass. (Vg., tribulor , tribulationem patior ), II Co 1:6 4:8 7:5 , I Th 3:4 , II Th 1:7 , I Ti 5:10 , He 11:37 (cf. ἀπο- , συν-θλίβω ).†
G2346.strongs: akin to the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to crowd (literally or figuratively)
G2346.kjv: afflict, narrow, throng, suffer tribulation, trouble

G543.mounce: an uncompliant disposition; obstinacy, disobedience, unbelief, Rom. 11:30, 32; Eph. 2:2; 5:6; Heb. 4:6, 11; Col. 3:6*
G543.abbottSmith: disobedience (MM, VGT , s.v.): Ro 11:30 , 32 , He 4:6 , 11 ; υἱοὶ τῆς ἀ. (gen. of definition, v. M, Pr. , 73 f.), Eph 2:2 5:6 , Col 3:6 (T, WH, R, mg., omit).†

G1151.strongs: probably from the base of G1150 (δαμάζω); a heifer (as tame)

G505.abbottSmith: unfeigned : Ro 12:9 , II Co 6:6 , I Ti 1:5 , II Ti 1:5 , Ja 3:17 , I Pe 1:22 (Cremer, 380; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G505.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); undissembled, i.e. sincere

G180.thayer: not ceasing from, not abandoning (giving up).
G180.mounce: also spelled ἀκατάπαστος, which cannot be restrained from a thing, unceasing, 2 Pet. 2:14*
G180.abbottSmith: that cannot cease , not to be restrained : c. gen. rei, II Pe 2:14 , T, Tr. txt.†
G180.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2664 (καταπαύω); unrefraining

G794.mounce: devoid of natural or instinctive affection, without affection to kindred, Rom. 1:31; 2 Tim. 3:3*
G794.abbottSmith: without natural affection : Ro 1:31 , II Ti 3:3 (MM, s.v.).†
G794.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of (to cherish affectionately); hard-hearted towards kindred

G2277.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2277.strongs: third person singular imperative of G1510 (εἰμί); let him (or it) be

G3862.mounce: delivery, handing over, transmission; in NT what is transmitted in the way of teaching, precept, doctrine, 1 Cor. 11:2; 2 Thess. 2:15; 3:6; tradition, traditionary law, handed down from age to age, Mt. 15:2, 3, 6
G3862.abbottSmith: a handing down or over , transfer , transmission (Arist., Polyb., al., LXX).
G3862.strongs: from G3860 (παραδίδωμι); transmission, i.e. (concretely) a precept; specially, the Jewish traditionary law

G196.abbottSmith: exact , precise , careful , of things and persons: superl., Ac 26:5 .†
G196.strongs: superlative of (a derivative of the same as G206 (ἄκρον)); most exact

G3525.thayer: (lit: I am sober), I am calm (vigilant), circumspect.
G3525.mounce: to be sober, not intoxicated; in NT met., to be vigilant, circumspect, 1 Thess. 5:6, 8
G3525.abbottSmith: to be sober , abstain from wine ; metaph., of moral alertness, to be sober , calm , circumspect : I Th 5:6 8 , II Ti 4:5 (v. Ellic., in l.), I Pe 1:13 4:7 5:8 (cf. ἀνα- , ἐκ-νήφω , and v. MM, xvii).†
G3525.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to abstain from wine (keep sober), i.e. (figuratively) be discreet

G3800.mounce: provisions; a stipend or pay of soldiers, Lk. 3:14; 1 Cor. 9:7; wages of any kind, 2 Cor. 11:8; due wages, a stated recompense, Rom. 6:23*
G3800.abbottSmith: provisions , provision-money , soldiers' pay : Lk 3:14 , I Co 9:7 .
G3800.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the same as G3795 (ὀψάριον); rations for a soldier, i.e. (by extension) his stipend or pay

G5533.strongs: from a derivative of G5531 (χράω) and G3781 (ὀφειλέτης); a loan-ower, i.e. indebted person

G5570.mounce: a false apostle, pretend minister of Christ, 2 Cor. 11:13*
G5570.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G652 (ἀπόστολος); a spurious apostle, i.e. pretended pracher

G5370.abbottSmith: a kiss : Lk 7:45 22:48 ; as a token of Christian brotherhood, φ. ἅγιον , Ro 16:16 , I Co 16:20 , II Co 13:12 , I Th 5:26 ; φ. ἀγάπης , I Pe 5:14 (v. Lft., Notes , 90; DB , DCA , s.v. "Kiss").†

G3328.thayer: I change; mid: I change my mind.
G3328.mounce: to change; mid. to change one’s mind, Acts 28:6*
G3328.abbottSmith: to turn about , change . Pass. and mid., to turn oneself about ; metaph., to change one's mind : Ac 28:6 .†
G3328.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw over, i.e. (middle voice figuratively) to turn about in opinion

G3964.thayer: a patricide, a murderer of his father.
G3964.mounce: also spelled πατραλῴας, one who kills one’s father, a patricide, 1 Tim. 1:9*
G3964.strongs: from G3962 (πατήρ) and the same as the latter part of G3389 (μητραλῴας); a parricide

G1258.thayer: language, speech, conversation, manner of speaking.
G1258.mounce: speech; manner of speaking; peculiar language of a nation, dialect, vernacular idiom, Acts 1:19; 2:6, 8; 21:40; 22:2; 26:14
G1258.strongs: from G1256 (διαλέγομαι); a (mode of) discourse, i.e. "dialect"

G5205.strongs: from a primary (to rain); rain, especially a shower

G2847.thayer: crimson, scarlet, dyed with Kermes (coccum), the female coccus of the Kermes oak.
G2847.mounce: dyed with coccus, crimson, scarlet, Mt. 27:28; Heb. 9:19; Rev. 17:3, 4; 18:12, 16*
G2847.abbottSmith: scarlet : Mt 27:28 , He 9:19 , Re 17:3 ; neut., without substantive, of clothing, Re 17:4 18:12 , 16 ,†
G2847.strongs: from G2848 (κόκκος) (from the kernel-shape of the insect); crimson-colored

G5290.mounce: to turn back, return, Mk. 14:40; Lk. 1:56; 2:39, 43, 45
G5290.abbottSmith: trans., to turn back or about (Hom.).
G5290.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4762 (στρέφω); to turn under (behind), i.e. to return (literally or figuratively)
G5290.kjv: come again, return (again, back again), turn back (again)

G5345.mounce: pr. a celestial or oracular utterance; an utterance; fame, rumor, report, Mt. 9:26; Lk. 4:14*
G5345.abbottSmith: a saying or report : Mt 9:26 , Lk 4:14 .†
G5345.strongs: from G5346 (φημί); a saying, i.e. rumor ("fame")

G3961.mounce: intrans. to tread, Lk. 10:19; trans. to tread the winepress, Rev. 14:20; 19:15; to trample, Lk. 21:24; Rev. 11:2*
G3961.abbottSmith: intrans., to tread , walk : seq. ἐπάνω ὄφεων κ.τ.λ. (fig.), Lk 10:19 (cf. Ps 90 (91):13 ).
G3961.strongs: from a derivative probably of G3817 (παίω) (meaning a "path"); to trample (literally or figuratively)

G4744.thayer: I shine, glisten, flash, am radiant.
G4744.mounce: to shine, glisten, be radiant, Mk. 9:3*
G4744.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to gleam, i.e. flash intensely

G4650.thayer: I disperse, scatter abroad (as of sheep); I dissipate, waste; I distribute alms.
G4650.abbottSmith: in vernac. and in Ion, and late writers for σκεδάννυμι (v. MM, xxiii; Rutherford, NPhr. , 295), to scatter : ὁ λύκος , Jo 10:12 ; ὁ μὴ συνάγων μετ’ ἐμοῦ σκορπίζει , Mt 12:30 , Lk 11:23 ; pass., seq. εἰς , c. acc. loc., Jo 16:32 ; of one who dispenses blessings, II Co 9:9 (LXX) (of. δια-σκορπίζομαι ).†
G4650.strongs: apparently from the same as G4651 (σκορπίος) (through the idea of penetrating); to dissipate, i.e. (figuratively) put to flight, waste, be liberal

G4940.thayer: I meet with, come to, fall in with.
G4940.mounce: to meet or fall in with; join, in NT to get to, approach, Lk. 8:19*
G4940.abbottSmith: to meet with, fall in with : c. dat. pers., Lk 8:19 .†
G4940.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5177 (τυγχάνω); to chance together, i.e. meet with (reach)

G1730.thayer: (a thing proved, hence) a plain token (sign, proof).
G1730.abbottSmith: a plain token, proof : II Th 1:5 (cf. ἔνδειξις , which refers rather to the "act of proving"; ἔ. , with the passive formation, to the thing proved, v. Lft., Notes , 100; M, Th. , l.c.).†

G1935.thayer: at the point of death, condemned to death.
G1935.mounce: condemned to death, under sentence of death, 1 Cor. 4:9*
G1935.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2288 (θάνατος); doomed to death

G5294.thayer: I put under, lay down, suggest to, put in mind.
G5294.mounce: to place under; to lay down the neck beneath the sword of the executioner, to set on imminent risk, Rom. 16:4; mid. to suggest, recommend to attention, 1 Tim. 4:6*
G5294.abbottSmith: to place under, lay down . Metaph., τ. τράχηλον ὑποθεῖναι , to risk one's life (v. Deiss., LAE , 119 f.): Ro 16:4 ; mid., to suggest : I Ti 4:6 .†
G5294.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place underneath, i.e. (figuratively) to hazard, (reflexively) to suggest

G1538.thayer: each (of more than two), every one.
G1538.abbottSmith: each, every (Lat. quisque ) ; with a noun: Lk 6:44 , Jo 19:23 , seq. κατά , He 3:13 , Re 22:2 ; εἷς ἕ. , Eph 4:16 ; without a noun: Ac 4:35 , Ro 2:6 , al.; partit. gen., Ro 14:12 , I Co 1:12 , al.; in sing, with pl. verb, Lk 2:3 , Ac 11:29 , al.; in apposition with pl. noun or pron., Lk 2:3 , Jo 16:32 , Ac 2:8 3:26 , al.; εἷς ἕ. (Lat. unusquisque ), Ac 2:6 21:26 , Col 4:6 , al; ἕ. τι ἀδελφῷ (= Heb
G1538.strongs: as if a superlative of (afar); each or every
G1538.kjv: any, both, each (one), every (man, one, woman), particularly

G1162.abbottSmith: a wanting, need (so Ps 21(22):25 ). an asking, entreaty, supplica­tion ; in NT always addressed to God: Lk 1:13 , II Co 1:11 , Phl 1:19 , II Ti 1:3 , Ja 5:16 , I Pe 3:12 (LXX) ; with νηστεῖαι , Lk 2:37 ; προσευχή, -αί , Eph 6:18 , Phl 4:6 , I Ti 2:1 5:5 ; ἱκετηρίαι , He 5:7 ; προσκαρτέρησις , Eph 6:18 ; ἐντεύξεις , I Ti 2:1 ; δ. ποιεῖσθαι (Deiss., BS , 250), Lk 5:33 , Phl 1:4 (pl.), I Ti 2:1 ; seq

G1763.mounce: a year, more particularly as being a cycle of seasons, and in respect of its revolution, Jn. 11:49, 51; 18:13; in NT an era, Lk. 4:19
G1763.strongs: prolongation from a primary (a year); a year

G685.thayer: a prayer; more commonly: a prayer for evil, a curse, imprecation.
G685.strongs: probably from G142 (αἴρω); properly, prayer (as lifted to Heaven), i.e. (by implication) imprecation

G1596.thayer: willingly, of one's own accord, spontaneously.
G1596.strongs: adverb from the same as G1595 (ἑκούσιον); voluntarily

G5075.thayer: I rule over as a tetrarch.

G5075.abbottSmith: to be tetrarch : c. gen., Lk 3:1 (FlJ, BJ , iii, 10, 7).†
G5075.strongs: from G5076 (τετράρχης); to be a tetrarch

G2560.mounce: ill, badly; physically ill, sick, Mt. 4:24; 8:16; grievously, vehemently, Mt. 15:22; wretchedly, miserably, Mt. 21:41; wickedly, reproachfully, Acts 23:5; wrongly, criminally, Jn. 18:23; amiss, Jas. 4:3
G2560.abbottSmith: badly , ill ; in physical sense: κ. ἔχειν , to be ill , Mt 4:24 8:16 9:12 14:35 17:15 , Mk 1:32 , 34 2:17 6:55 , Lk 5:31 7:2 ; πάσχειν , Mt 17:15 (WH, mg.); δαιμονίζεσθαι , Mt 15:22 ; κακοὺς κ. ἀπολέσει (as freq. in cl., v. LS, s.v.; MM, Exp. , xv), evil that they are , he will evilly , etc., Mt 21:41 ; in moral sense, wrongly ( I Mac 7:42 , IV Mac 6:17 , al.): κ. λαλεῖν , Jo 18:23 ; εἰπεῖν , c. a
G2560.kjv: amiss, diseased, evil, grievously, miserably, sick, sore

G5280.thayer: remembrance, recollection, putting in mind; a reminder.

G5280.strongs: from G5279 (ὑπομιμνήσκω); a reminding or (reflexively) recollection

G2927.thayer: hidden, secret; as subst: the hidden (secret) things (parts), the inward nature (character).
G2927.abbottSmith: hidden , secret : Mt 10:26 , Mk 4:22 , Lk 8:17 12:2 ; ὁ κ. τῆς καρδίας ἄνθρωπος , I Pe 3:4 ; neut., ἐν τῷ κ. , Mt 6:4 , 6 ; ἐν κ. , Jo 7:4 , 10 18:20 ; ὁ ἐν κ. Ἰουδαῖος , Ro 2:29 ; pl., τὰ κ. τ. σκότους , I Co 4:5 ; τ. ἀνθρώπων , Ro 2:16 ; τ. καρδίας , I Co 14:25 ; τ. αἰσχύνης , II Co 4:2 .†

G1859.mounce: a solemn feast, public festival, Lk. 2:41; 22:1; Jn. 13:1; spc. used of the passover, Mt. 26:5; 27:15
G1859.abbottSmith: a feast, festival : Lk 2:42 , Jo 5:1 6:4 7:37 , Col 2:16 ; ἡ ἑ. τοῦ πάσχα , Lk 2:41 , Jo 13:1 ; τ. ἀζύμων , Lk 22:1 ; ἡ σκηνοπηγία , Jo 7:2 (Deiss., LAE , 116); ἐν τ. ἑ. , Mt 26:5 , Mk 14:2 , Jo 4:45 7:11 12:20 ( εἶναι ἐν ἑ. ), Jo 2:23 ; εἰς τ. ἑ. ( for the feast ), Jo 13:29 ; ἀναβαίνειν, ἔρχεσθαι εἰς τ. ἑ. , Jo 4:45 7:8 , 10 11:56 12:12 ; τῆς ἐ μεσούσης , Jo 7:14 ; κατὰ ἑ. ( at each feast ), Mt 2

G458.mounce: lawlessness; violation of law, 1 Jn. 3:4; iniquity, sin, Mt. 7:23
G458.abbottSmith: lawlessness , iniquity : Mt 7:23 13:41 23:28 24:12 , Ro 6:19 , II Co 6:14 , II Th 2:3 , 7 , Tit 2:14 , He 1:9 , I Jo 3:4 ; in pl. (as LXX, Ps 31:1 , al.; v. Bl., § 32, 6; Swete, Mk. , 153), of acts or manifestations of lawlessness: Ro 4:7 (LXX) , He 10:17 .†
G458.strongs: from G459 (ἄνομος); illegality, i.e. violation of law or (genitive case) wickedness

G4922.mounce: to journey or travel with, accompany on a journey, Acts 9:7*
G4922.abbottSmith: to journey with : c. dat. pers., Ac 9:7 (Plut., al.).†

G1421.thayer: difficult to interpret, hard to be understood.
G1421.mounce: difficult to be explained, hard to be understood, Heb. 5:11*
G1421.strongs: from G1418 (δυσ-) and a presumed derivative of G2059 (ἑρμηνεύω); difficult of explanation

G3675.mounce: of like mind, of the same mind, like-minded, 1 Pet. 3:8*
G3675.abbottSmith: agreeing , of one mind : I Pe 3:8 .†
G3675.strongs: from the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ) and G5424 (φρήν); like-minded, i.e. harmonious

G389.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4727 (στενάζω); to sigh deeply

G5619.thayer: just as if, as it were.
G5619.mounce: just as if; as it were, 1 Cor. 15:8*
G5619.abbottSmith: as, as it were: I Co 15:8 .†
G5619.strongs: from G5618 (ὥσπερ) and G1487 (εἰ); just as if, i.e. as it were

G1225.mounce: to throw or convey through or over; to thrust through; to defame, inform against, Lk. 16:1*
G1225.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G906 (βάλλω); (figuratively) to traduce

G3334.thayer: trans: I move away, dislodge, remove.

G3334.abbottSmith: trans., to move away , remove ( ὅρια , De 19:14 ). Mid., to remove oneself , remove , shift : metaph., ἀπὸ τ. ἐλπίδος , Col 1:23 .†

G3089.thayer: (a) I loose, untie, release, (b) met: I break, destroy, set at naught, contravene; I break up a meeting, annul.
G3089.mounce: to loosen, unbind, unfasten, Mk. 1:7; to loose, untie, Mt. 21:2; Jn. 11:44; to disengage, 1 Cor. 7:27; to set free, set at liberty, deliver, Lk. 13:16; to break, Acts 27:41; Rev. 5:2, 5; to break up, dismiss, Acts 13:43; to destroy, demolish, Jn. 2:19; Eph. 2:14; met to infringe, Mt. 5:19; Jn. 5:18; 7:23; to make void, nullify, Jn. 10:35; in NT to declare free, of privileges, or, in respect of lawfulness, Mt. 16:19

(v.s. λούω ).
G3089.strongs: a primary verb; to "loosen" (literally or figuratively)
G3089.kjv: break (up), destroy, dissolve, (un-)loose, melt, put off

G3856.thayer: I put to open shame, make a public example of, put to disgrace.
