G3856.abbottSmith: to set forth as an example ; in bad sense, to put to open shame : He 6:6 (Polyb., Plut., al.).†
G3856.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1165 (δειγματίζω); to show alongside (the public), i.e. expose to infamy
G3856.kjv: make a public example, put to an open shame

G1305.strongs: from a compound of G1223 (διά) and G5142 (τρέφω); nourishment

G3141.mounce: judicial evidence, Mk. 14:55, 56, 59; Lk. 22:71; testimony in general, Tit. 1:13; 1 Jn. 5:9 testimony, declaration in a matter of fact or doctrine, Jn. 1:19; 3:11; Acts 22:18; attestation to character, Jn. 5:34, 36; reputation, 1 Tim. 3:7
G3141.abbottSmith: witness , testimony , evidence : Mk 14:56 , Lk 22:71 , Jo 5:34 19:35 21:24 , I Ti 3:7 , Tit 1:13 , III Jo 12 , Re 11:7 ; c. gen. subj., Mk 14:9 , Jo 8:17 (LXX aliter) , I Jo 5:9 ; seq. κατά , c. gen. pers., Mk 14:55 ; esp. of witness concerning Christ and divine things, Jo 1:7 3:11 , 32 , 33 5:32 , 36 , Re 6:9 ; c. gen. subj., Jo 1:19 5:31 8:13 , 14 , Ac 22:18 , I Jn 5:9-11 , Re 12:11 ; c. gen. ob
G3141.strongs: from G3144 (μάρτυς); evidence given (judicially or genitive case)

G1167.abbottSmith: cowardice, timidity (never in good sense): II Ti 1:7 .†

G4903.mounce: to work together with, to cooperate, etc., 1 Cor. 16:16; 2 Cor. 6:1; to assist, afford aid to, Mk. 16:20; Jas. 2:22; absol. to conspire actively to a result, Rom. 8:28*

G2328.thayer: I warm; mid: I warm myself.
G2328.mounce: to warm; mid. to warm one’s self, Mt. 14:54, 67; Jn. 18:18, 25; Jas. 2:16*
G2328.abbottSmith: to warm , heat ; mid., to warm oneself : Mk 14:54 , 67 , Jo 18:18 , 25 , Ja 2:16 .†

G2032.thayer: heavenly, celestial, in the heavenly sphere, the sphere of spiritual activities; met: divine, spiritual.
G2032.mounce: heavenly, in respect of locality, Eph. 1:20; Phil. 2:10; τὰ ἐπουράνια, the upper regions of the air, Eph. 6:12; heavenly, in respect of essence and character, unearthly, 1 Cor. 15:48, 49; met. divine, spiritual, Jn. 3:12
G2032.abbottSmith: in or of heaven, heavenly (in Hom., of the Gods): οἱ ἐ. , opp. to ἐπίγειοι and καταχθόνιοι , Phl 2:10 ; to χοϊκός , I Co 15:48 , 49 ; σώματα (v. Lft., Col. , 376), I Co 15:40 ; βασιλεία , II Ti 4:18 ; παπρίς , He 11:16 ; Ἱερουσαλήμ , He 12:22 ; κλῆσις (cf. Lft. on Phl 3:14 ), He 3:1 ; τὰ ἐ. , He 8:5 9:23 ; opp. to ἐπίγειος , Jo 3:12 ; id., of the heavenly regions, Eph 1:3 , 20 2:6 3:10 6:12 ; ἡ δω

G4076.thayer: rue, a plant used for flavoring or garnishing food.

G1456.thayer: a renewal, dedication; the feast of rededication.

G4577.mounce: a cord, rope, band; in NT a chain, 2 Pet. 2:4*
G4577.abbottSmith: a cord ; a chain (cf. Pr. , l.c.): σειραὶ ζόφου , II Pe 2:4 , Rec., R, mg. (v.s. σειρός ).
G4577.strongs: probably from G4951 (σύρω) through its congener (to fasten; akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι)); a chain (as binding or drawing)

G2078.thayer: last, at the last, finally, till the end.
G2078.mounce: farthest; last, latest, Mt. 12:45; Mk. 12:6; lowest, Mt. 19:30; 20:16; in the lowest plight, 1 Cor. 4:9
G2078.strongs: a superlative probably from G2192 (ἔχω) (in the sense of contiguity); farthest, final (of place or time)

G4760.mounce: pr. the site of an encampment; an encampment; meton. an army, Lk. 21:20*
G4760.strongs: from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and the same as G3977 (πεδινός); a camping-ground, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops

G1528.thayer: I call in (to my house), invite.

G1528.abbottSmith: to call in : mid., c. acc pers., Ac 10:23 .†
G1528.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G2564 (καλέω); to invite in

G2967.thayer: I prevent, debar, hinder; with infin: from doing so and so.
G2967.mounce: to hinder, restrain, prevent, Mt. 19:14; Acts 8:36; Rom. 1:13

G2967.strongs: from the base of G2849 (κολάζω); to estop, i.e. prevent (by word or act)

G1384.mounce: proved, tried; approved after examination and trial, Rom. 16:10; Jas. 1:12; by impl. acceptable, Rom. 14:18

G1358.mounce: to dig or break through, Mt. 6:19, 20; 24:43; Lk. 12:39*

G2475.thayer: an Israelite, one of the chosen people of Israel, a Jew.
G2475.mounce: an Israelite, a descendant of Ἰσραήλ, Israel or Jacob, Jn. 1:47; Acts 2:22
G2475.abbottSmith: an Israelite , the name expressive of theocratic privilege (v.s. Ἑβραῖος ): Ro 9:4 11:1 , II Co 11:22 ; ἀληθῶς Ἰ. , Jo 1:48 ; ἄνδρες Ἰ. , Ac 2:22 3:12 5:35 13:16 21:28 .†
G2475.strongs: from G2474 (Ἰσραήλ); an "Israelite", i.e. descendant of Israel (literally or figuratively)

G3108.thayer: regarding as happy, blessed, or enviable.
G3108.mounce: a happy calling, the act of pronouncing happy, Rom. 4:6, 9; self-congratulation, Gal. 4:15*
G3108.abbottSmith: a declaration of blessedness , felicitation : Ro 4:6 , 9 , Ga 4:15 (Plat., Arist.).†
G3108.strongs: from G3106 (μακαρίζω); beatification, i.e. attribution of good fortune

G217.mounce: variant spellings of ἅλα and ἁλός, salt, Mt. 5:13; Mk. 9:50; met. the salt of wisdom and prudence, Col. 4:6
G217.abbottSmith: salt , lit, and fig.: Mt 5:13 , Mk 9:50 Lk 14:34 ; like cl. ἅλες , wit , of wisdom and grace in speech: Col 4:6 .†

G1053.thayer: Galatia, a large Roman province in central Asia Minor, comprising the districts of Paphlagonia, Pontus Galaticus, Galatia (in the narrower sense, which some still think is intended in the NT), Phrygia Galatica, Lycaonia Galatica, Pisidia and Isaurica.
G1053.mounce: Galatia or Gallo-Graecia, a province of Asia Minor, 1 Cor. 6:1; Gal. 1:2; 2 Tim. 4:10; 1 Pet. 1:1*
G1053.abbottSmith: Galatia ; a gentilic region in Asia Minor, settled by Gauls (iii/B.C). A Roman Province which included this region ( DB , ii, 85ff.): I Co 16:1 , Ga 1:2 , II Ti 4:10 (T, Tr., mg., Γαλλίαν ), I Pe 1:1 .†
G1053.strongs: of foreign origin; Galatia, a region of Asia

G2523.thayer: (a) trans: I make to sit; I set, appoint, (b) intrans: I sit down, am seated, stay.
G2523.mounce: (1) trans. to cause to sit, place; καθίζομαι, to be seated, sit, Mt. 19:28; Lk. 22:30; to cause to sit as judges, place, appoint, 1 Cor. 6:4; (2) intrans. to sit, sit down, Mt. 13:48; 26:36; to remain, settle, stay, continue, live, Lk. 24:49
G2523.abbottSmith: causal, to make to sit down , set , appoint : Ac 2:30 , I Co 6:4 , Eph 1:20 .
G2523.strongs: another (active) form for G2516 (καθέζομαι); to seat down, i.e. set (figuratively, appoint); intransitively, to sit (down); figuratively, to settle (hover, dwell)

G3930.thayer: act. and mid: I offer, provide, confer, afford, give, bring, show, cause.
G3930.mounce: to hold beside; to hold out to, offer, present, Lk. 6:29; to confer, render, Lk. 7:4; Acts 22:2; 28:2; Col. 4:1; to afford, furnish, Acts 16:16; 17:31; 19:24; 1 Tim. 6:17; to exhibit, Tit. 2:7; to be the cause of, occasion, Mt. 26:10; Mk. 14:6; Lk. 11:7
G3930.abbottSmith: Act., to furnish , provide , supply ; with ref. to incorporeal things, to afford , show , give , cause : c. acc., Ac 22:2 , I Ti 1:4 ; c. acc. et dat., Mt 26:10 , Mk 14:6 , Lk 11:7 18:5 , Ac 16:16 17:31 28:2 , Ga 6:17 , I Ti 6:17 . to present , offer : c. acc. et dat., Lk 6:29 ; c. prop. reflex. et acc. pred., to show or present oneself (v. infr., and cf. Bl., § 55, 1).
G3930.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold near, i.e. present, afford, exhibit, furnish occasion
G3930.kjv: bring, do, give, keep, minister, offer, shew, + trouble

G4805.mounce: an associate, comrade, fellow laborer, or it could be the person’s name, Phil. 4:3*
G4805.abbottSmith: a yoke fellow . Prob., as proper name, Σ. γνήσιε , genuinely Synzygus, S. properly so-called , Phl 4:3 (v. ICC , Lft., in l.; MM, xxiii).†
G4805.strongs: from G4801 (συζεύγνυμι); co-yoked, i.e. (figuratively) as noun, a colleague; probably rather as a proper name; Syzygus, a Christian

G4777.mounce: to suffer evils along with someone; to be enduringly adherent, 2 Tim. 1:8; 2:3*
G4777.abbottSmith: to bear evil treatment along with, take one's share of ill-treatment : II Ti 2:3 ; c. dat. commod., τ. εὐαγγελίῳ , ib. 1:18 .†
G4777.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2553 (κακοπαθέω); to suffer hardship in company with

G680.thayer: prop: I fasten to; I lay hold of, touch, know carnally.
G680.mounce: pr. to bring in contact, fit, fasten; to light, kindle, Mk. 4:21; Lk. 8:16; to touch, Mt. 8:3; to meddle, venture to partake, Col. 2:21; to have intercourse with, to know carnally, 1 Cor. 7:1; by impl. to harm, 1 Jn. 5:18
G680.strongs: reflexive of G681 (ἅπτω); properly, to attach oneself to, i.e. to touch (in many implied relations)

G130.thayer: a shedding or pouring forth of blood (in sacrifice).
G130.mounce: an effusion or shedding of blood, Heb. 9:22*
G130.strongs: from G129 (αἷμα) and a derivative of G1632 (ἐκχέω); an effusion of blood

G3326.thayer: (a) gen: with, in company with, (b) acc: (1) behind, beyond, after, of place, (2) after, of time, with nouns, neut. of adjectives.
G3326.mounce: (1) gen., with, together with, Mt. 16:27; 12:41; 26:55; with, on the same side or party with, in aid of, Mt. 12:30; 20:20; with, by means of, Acts 13:17; with, of conflict, Rev. 11:7; with, among, Lk. 24:5; with, to, towards, Lk. 1:58, 72; (2) acc., after, of place, behind, Heb. 9:3; of time, after, Mt. 17:1; 24:29; followed by an infin. with the neut. article, after, after that, Mt. 26:32; Lk. 22:20
G3326.abbottSmith: C. gen., among , amid : Mk 1:13 , Lk 22:37 (LXX, ἐν ) 24:5 , Jo 18:5 , al.; διωγμῶν , Mk 10:30 . Of association and companionship, with (in which sense it gradually superseded σύν , than which it is much more freq. in NT; cf. Bl., § 42, 3): c. gen. pers., Mt 8:11 20:20 , Mk 1:29 3:7 , Lk 5:30 , Jo 3:22 , Ga 2:1 , al. mult.; εἶναι μετά , Mt 5:25 , Mk 3:14 , al.; metaph., of divine help and guidance
G3326.strongs: a primary preposition (often used adverbially); properly, denoting accompaniment; "amid" (local or causal); modified variously according to the case (genitive association, or accusative succession) with which it is joined; occupying an intermediate position between G575 (ἀπό) or G1537 (ἐκ) and G1519 (εἰς) or G4314 (πρός); less intimate than G1722 (ἐν) and less close than G4862 (σύν))
G3326.kjv: after(-ward), X that he again, against, among, X and, + follow, hence, hereafter, in, of, (up-)on, + our, X and setting, since, (un-)to, + together, when, with (+ -out)

G3986.thayer: (a) trial, probation, testing, being tried, (b) temptation, (c) calamity, affliction.
G3986.mounce: a putting to the proof, proof, trial, 1 Pet. 4:12; Heb. 3:8; direct temptation to sin, Lk. 4:13; trial, temptation, Mt. 6:13; 26:41; 1 Cor. 10:13; trial, calamity, affliction, Lk. 22:28
G3986.strongs: from G3985 (πειράζω); a putting to proof (by experiment (of good), experience (of evil), solicitation, discipline or provocation); by implication, adversity

G1621.thayer: I shake off; mid: I shake off from myself.
G1621.mounce: to shake out, shake off, Mt. 10:14; Mk. 6:11; Acts 13:51; 18:6*
G1621.abbottSmith: to shake off : κονιορτόν , Mt 10:14 ; χοῦν , Mk 6:11 . Mid.: κονιορτόν , Ac 13:51 ; ἱμάτια , Ac 18:6 (cf. MM, Exp. , iii).†
G1621.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and tinasso (to swing); to shake violently

G193.thayer: lacking self-control, powerless, inclined to excess.
G193.abbottSmith: powerless , impotent ; in moral sense, lacking self-control , incontinent : II Ti 3:3 .†
G193.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2904 (κράτος); powerless, i.e. without self-control

G1217.thayer: an artisan, builder, maker; one who labors for the public.
G1217.mounce: pr. one who labors for the public, or, exercises some public calling; an architect, especially, the Divine Architect of the universe, Heb. 11:10
G1217.abbottSmith: one who works for the people .
G1217.strongs: from G1218 (δῆμος) and G2041 (ἔργον); a worker for the people, i.e. mechanic (spoken of the Creator)

G444.thayer: a man, one of the human race.
G444.mounce: a human being, Jn. 16:21; Phil. 2:7; an individual, Rom. 3:28, et al. freq.; used also pleonastically with other words, Mt. 11:19; et al.; met. the spiritual frame of the inner man, Rom. 7:22; Eph. 3:16; 1 Pet. 3:4
G444.abbottSmith: man : generically, a human being, male or female (Lat. homo ): Jo 16:21 ; c. art., Mt 4:4 12:35 , Mk 2:27 , Jo 2:25 , Ro 7:1 , al; disting. from God, Mt 19:6 , Jo 10:33 , Col 3:23 , al.; from animals, etc., Mt 4:19 , Lk 5:10 , Re 9:4 , al.; implying human frailty and imperfection, I Co 3:4 ; σοφία ἀνθρώπων , I Co 2:5 ; ἀνθρώπων ἐπιθυμίαι , I Pe 4:2 ; κατὰ ἄνθρωπον περιπατεῖν , I Co 3:3 ; κατὰ ἄ. λ
G444.strongs: from G435 (ἀνήρ) and (the countenance; from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)); man-faced, i.e. a human being

G3442.mounce: one-eyed; deprived of an eye, Mt. 18:9; Mk. 9:47*
G3442.abbottSmith: one-eyed , having one eye : Mt 18:9 , Mk 9:47 .†

G3482.thayer: Nathanael, of Cana in Galilee, an early disciple, probably to be identified with Bartholomew.
G3482.abbottSmith: Nathanael , prob. to be identified with Bartholomew (q.v.): Jo 1:45-49 21:2 .†
G3482.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05417); Nathanaël (i.e. Nathanel), an Israelite and Christian

G3666.thayer: I make like, liken; I compare.
G3666.mounce: to make like, cause to be like or resemble, assimilate; pass. to be made like, become like, resemble, Mt. 6:8; 13:24; 18:23; to liken, compare, Mt. 7:24, 26; 11:16
G3666.abbottSmith: to make like , c. gen. et dat.; pass., to be made or become like : Mt 6:8 13:24 18:23 22:2 25:1 , Ac 14:11 , He 2:17 ; seq. ὡς (cf. Ez 32:2 , Heb.), Ro 9:29 .
G3666.strongs: from G3664 (ὅμοιος); to assimilate, i.e. compare; passively, to become similar

G4576.mounce: mid., to stand in awe; to venerate, reverence, worship, adore, Mt. 15:9; Mk. 7:7; Acts 18:13; 19:27; part. σεβόμενος, η, ον, worshiping, devout, pious, a term applied to proselytes to Judaism, Acts 13:43; 16:14; 18:7; 13:50; 17:4, 17*
G4576.abbottSmith: to worship : c. acc. pers., Mt 15:9 , Mk 7:7 , Ac 18:13 19:27 ; σεβόμενος τ. θεόν , Ac 16:14 18:7 ; σεβόμενοι ( σ. προσήλυτοι, σ. Ἕλληνες ), devout , Ac 13:43 , 50 17:4 , 17 (cf. προσήλυτος ).†
G4576.strongs: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to revere, i.e. adore

G1855.thayer: (a) from outside, from without, (b) outside, both as adj. and prep; with article: the outside.
G1855.mounce: can function as an improper prep., outwardly, externally, Mt. 23:27, 28; Mk. 7:15; ὁ, ἡ, τὸ ἔξωθεν, outer, external, Mt. 23:25; Lk. 11:39; τὸ ἔξωθεν, the exterior, Lk. 11:40; οἱ ἔξωθεν, those who are without the Christian community, 1 Tim. 3:7
G1855.abbottSmith: prop. (in answer to the question, Whence ? ), from without : Mk 7:18

G2631.thayer: punishment following condemnation, penal servitude, penalty.
G2631.abbottSmith: penalty (RV, condemnation ; but cf. Deiss., BS , 264 f.; MM, Exp. , xv): Ro 5:16 , 18 8:1 .†
G2631.strongs: from G2632 (κατακρίνω); an adverse sentence (the verdict)

G4463.mounce: to beat with rods, Acts 16:22; 2 Cor. 11:25*
G4463.abbottSmith: to beat with a rod : Ac 16:22 , II Co 11:25 .†
G4463.strongs: from G4464 (ῥάβδος); to strike with a stick, i.e. bastinado

G2351.thayer: (a) din, hubbub, confused noise, outcry, (b) riot, disturbance.
G2351.mounce: an uproar, din; an outward expression of mental agitation, outcry, Mk. 5:38; a tumult, commotion, Mt. 26:5
G2351.abbottSmith: a noise , uproar , tumult , as of an excited mob: Mt 26:5 27:24 , Mk 14:2 , Ac 20:1 21:34 24:18 ; of mourners, = ὄχλος θορυβούμενος , Mk 5:38 .†
G2351.strongs: from the base of G2360 (θροέω); a disturbance

G2103.thayer: Eubulus, a Christian with Paul in Rome.
G2103.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G1014 (βούλομαι); good-willer; Eubulus, a Christian

G4247.strongs: feminine of G4246 (πρεσβύτης); an old woman

G5596.abbottSmith: dimin. of ψωμός , a fragment, morsel : Jo 13:26, 27 , 30 .†
G5596.strongs: diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5597 (ψώχω); a crumb or morsel (as if rubbed off), i.e. a mouthful

G3365.thayer: by no means, not at all.
G3365.abbottSmith: by no means , not at all . μ., Κύριε (sc. τοῦτο γένοιτο ), Ac 10:14 11:8 .†
G3365.strongs: adverb from a compound of G3361 (μή) and (somebody); by no means

G3199.thayer: it is a care, it is an object of anxiety, it concerns.
G3199.mounce: there is a care, it concerns, Mt. 22:16; Acts 18:17; 1 Cor. 7:21; 9:9
G3199.abbottSmith: intrans., to be an object of care , be a care ; commonly in third pers.: c. dat. pers., Ac 18:17 ; very freq. impers., I Co 7:21 ; seq. ὅτι , Mk 4:38 , Lk 10:40 ; c. gen. rei (as freq. in Att.), I Co 9:9 ; seq. περί , Mt 22:16 , Mk 12:14 , Jo 10:13 12:6 , I Pe 5:7 .
G3199.strongs: a primary verb; to be of interest to, i.e. to concern (only third person singular present indicative used impersonally, it matters)

G1439.thayer: I allow, permit, let alone, leave.
G1439.mounce: to let, allow, permit, suffer to be done, Mt. 24:43; Lk. 4:41; to let be, let alone, desist from, stop, Lk. 22:51; to commit a ship to the sea, let her drive, Acts 27:40
G1439.abbottSmith: to let, permit : c. acc., c. inf., c. acc. et inf. (M, Pr. , 205): Mt 24:43 , Lk 4:41 22:51 , Ac 14:16 16:7 19:30 23:32 27:32 28:4 , I Co 10:13 . to let alone, leave : ἀγκύρας , Ac 27:40 (cf. προσ-εάω ).†
G1439.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to let be, i.e. permit or leave alone

G4447.mounce: pr. of fire, fiery, burning; shining, glittering, Rev. 9:17*
G4447.strongs: from G4443 (πυρά); fiery, i.e. (by implication) flaming

G4440.thayer: a large gate; a gateway, porch, vestibule.
G4440.mounce: a gateway, vestibule, Mt. 26:71; Lk. 16:20; a gate, Acts 14:13; Rev. 21:12, 13, 15, 21, 25
G4440.abbottSmith: the porch or vestibule of a house or palace: Mt 26:71 , Lk 16:20 , Ac 10:17 12:13 , 14 .
G4440.strongs: from G4439 (πύλη); a gate-way, door-way of a building or city; by implication, a portal or vestibule

G3585.mounce: wooden, of wood, made of wood, 2 Tim. 2:20; Rev. 9:20*
G3585.abbottSmith: wooden : II Ti 2:20 , Re 9:20 (cf. Ep. Je 4 ff.).†

G3068.thayer: (lit. or merely ceremonially), I wash, bathe (the body); mid: of washing, bathing one's self; met: I cleanse from sin.
G3068.mounce: pr. to bathe the body, as distinguished from washing only the extremities, Jn. 13:10; to bathe, wash, Acts 9:37; 16:33; Heb. 10:22; 2 Pet. 2:22*
G3068.abbottSmith: to bathe , wash the body: c. acc. pers., Ac 9:37 (of a dead body); id. seq. ἀπό (Deiss., BS , 226 f.), Ac 16:33 ; pass. ptcp. pf., Jo 13:10 , He 10:22 ; mid., to wash oneself (Mayor, in l.; M. Pr. , 155 f., 238 f.), II Pe 2:22 ; metaph., Re 1:5 , Rec., R, mg. (cf. ἀπο-λούω ).†
G3068.strongs: a primary verb; to bathe (the whole person; whereas G3538 (νίπτω) means to wet a part only, and G4150 (πλύνω) to wash, cleanse garments exclusively)

G1944.thayer: on whom a curse has been invoked, accursed, doomed to destruction.
G1944.mounce: cursed, accursed; subject to the curse of condemnation, Gal. 3:10; infamous, Gal. 3:13*
G1944.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G2672 (καταράομαι); imprecated, i.e. execrable

G2409.thayer: a priest, one who offers sacrifice to a god (in Jewish and pagan religions; of Christians only met.).
G2409.mounce: a priest, one who performs sacrificial rites, Mt. 8:4; Lk. 1:5; Jn. 1:19
G2409.abbottSmith: a priest : ἱ. τ. Διός , Ac 14:13 ; of Jewish priests, Mt 8:4 12:4 , 5 , Mk 1:44 2:26 , Lk 1:5 5:14 , Jo 1:19 , He 8:4 , al.; of Christ, He 5:6 (LXX) He 10:21 ; of Christians, Re 1:6 5:10 20:6 (Cremer, 293; on the acc. pl., ἱερεῖς , v. Thack., Gr. , 147 f.; Bl., § 8, 2).
G2409.strongs: from G2413 (ἱερός); a priest (literally or figuratively)

G2668.thayer: I sail down (from the high seas to the shore).
G2668.mounce: to sail towards land, to come to land, Lk. 8:26*
G2668.abbottSmith: to sail down , sail to land , put in : εἰς τ. χώραν , Lk 8:26 .†
G2668.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4126 (πλέω); to sail down upon a place, i.e. to land at

G2657.thayer: I take note of, perceive, consider carefully, discern, detect, make account of.
G2657.mounce: to perceive, understand, apprehend, Lk. 20:23; to observe, consider, contemplate, Lk. 12:24, 27; to discern, detect, Mt. 7:3; to have regard to, make account of, Rom. 4:19
G2657.abbottSmith: to take note of , perceive , consider carefully : Ac 7:31 , 32 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 7:3 , Lk 6:41 12:24 , 27 20:23 , Ac 11:6 27:39 , Ro 4:19 ; c. acc. pers., He 3:1 10:24 , Ja 1:23 , 24 (on the distinction bet. κ. and νοέω simplex , v. M, Pr. , 117).†
G2657.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3539 (νοιέω); to observe fully

G2976.thayer: Lazarus, Eliezer, (a) the beggar, (b) the brother of Martha and Mary, of Bethany.
G2976.abbottSmith: colloquial abbreviation of Ἐλεαζάρ ( άζαρος ), q.v.), Lazarus ; of Bethany: Jo 11:1 ff. Jo 12:1 , 2 , 9 , 10 , 17 . The beggar in the parable: Lk 16:20 , 23-25 .†
G2976.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (H0499); Lazarus (i.e. Elazar), the name of two Israelites (one imaginary)

G4071.abbottSmith: in trag., Ion. and late writers, winged , flying ; as subst., τὸ π. , a winged fowl , a bird : pl., Mt 6:26 8:20 13:4 , 32 , Mk 4:4 , 32 , Lk 8:5 9:58 12:24 13:19 , Ac 10:12 11:6 , Ro 1:23 , Ja 3:7 .†
G4071.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G4072 (πέτομαι); a flying animal, i.e. bird

G1709.mounce: gen., to breathe into or upon; to respire, breathe; met. to breathe of, be animated with the spirit of, Acts 9:1*
G1709.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4154 (πνέω); to inhale, i.e. (figuratively) to be animated by (bent upon)

G4833.thayer: I bring to the same form with, conform.
G4833.strongs: from G4832 (σύμμορφος); to render like, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate

G4119.mounce: great in magnitude or quantity, much, large, Mt. 13:5; Jn. 3:23; 15:8; pl. many, Mt. 3:7; in time, long, Mt. 25:19; Mk. 6:35; Jn. 5:6; οἱ πολλοί, the many, the mass, Rom. 5:15; 12:5; 1 Cor. 10:33; τὸ πολύ, much, 2 Cor. 8:15; πολύ, as an adv., much, greatly, Mk. 12:27; Lk. 7:47; of time, ἐπὶ πολύ, a long time, Acts 28:6; μετ’ οὐ πολὺ, not long after, Acts 27:14; followed by a compar., much, 2 Cor. 8:22; πολλῷ, much, by much, Mt. 6:30; Mk. 10:48; τὰ πολλά, as an adv., most frequently, generally, Rom. 15:22; πολλά, as an adv., much, greatly, vehemently, Mk. 1:45; 3:12; of time, many times, frequently, often, Mt. 9:14
G4119.strongs: comparative of G4183 (πολύς); more in quantity, number, or quality; also (in plural) the major portion
G4119.kjv: X above, + exceed, more excellent, further, (very) great(-er), long(-er), (very) many, greater (more) part, + yet but

G2282.thayer: (properly: I warm, then) I cherish, nourish, foster, comfort.
G2282.mounce: to impart warmth; met. to cherish, nurse, foster, comfort, Eph. 5:29; 1 Thess. 2:7*
G2282.abbottSmith: to heat , warm ; metaph., to inflame (Trag.); to foster , cherish : Eph 5:29 , I Th 2:7 .†
G2282.strongs: probably akin to (to warm); to brood, i.e. (figuratively) to foster

G3304.mounce: also spelled as two words, μενοῦν γε (Lk. 11:28), a combination of particles serving to take up what has just preceded, with either emphasize or to correct; indeed, really, truly, rather, Rom. 9:20; 10:18; Phil. 3:8*
G3304.strongs: from G3203 and G3767 (οὖν) and G1065 (γέ); so then at least

G705.abbottSmith: to number (esp. for payment, MM, s.v.): Mt 10:30 , Lk 12:7 , Re 7:9 .†
G705.strongs: from G706 (ἀριθμός); to enumerate or count

G4445.thayer: I am sick with a fever.
G4445.mounce: to be feverish, be sick of a fever, Mt. 8:14; Mk. 1:30*
G4445.abbottSmith: to be ill of a fever : Mt 8:14 , Mk 1:3 .†
G4445.strongs: from G4443 (πυρά); to be on fire, i.e. (specially), to have a fever

G395.thayer: (a) rising of the sun, hence (b) (sing. and plur.) the quarter whence the sun rises, the East.
G395.mounce: pr. a rising of the sun, etc.; the place of rising, the east, as also pl. ἀνατολαί, Mt. 2:1, 2; met. the dawn or day-spring, Lk. 1:78
G395.strongs: from G393 (ἀνατέλλω); a rising of light, i.e. dawn (figuratively); by implication, the east (also in plural)

G4620.thayer: a measured portion of grain or food.
G4620.mounce: a certain measure of grain distributed for food at set times to the slaves of a family, a ration, Lk. 12:42*
G4620.abbottSmith: a measured portion of food : Lk 12:42 (for exx. in π. , v. Deiss., BS , 158; LAE , 103 1 ).†
G4620.strongs: from G4621 (σῖτος) and G3358 (μέτρον); a grain-measure, i.e. (by implication) ration (allowance of food)

G4960.thayer: I am in the same rank with; I answer to, correspond to.
G4960.mounce: pr. to be in the same row with; met. to correspond to, Gal. 4:25*
G4960.abbottSmith: to stand in the same rank or line (Polyb). Metaph., to correspond to : Ga 4:25 (v. Lft., in l.).†
G4960.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4748 (στοιχέω); to file together (as soldiers in ranks), i.e. (figuratively) to correspond to

G2689.mounce: pr. an arranging in order; adjustment of dress; in NT apparel, dress, 1 Tim. 2:9*
G2689.strongs: from G2687 (καταστέλλω); a deposit, i.e. (specially) costume

G2366.strongs: from G2380 (θύω) (in the sense of blowing) a storm

G3874.thayer: a calling for, summons, hence: (a) exhortation, (b) entreaty, (c) encouragement, joy, gladness, (d) consolation, comfort.
G3874.mounce: a calling upon, exhortation, incitement, persuasion, Rom. 12:8; 1 Cor. 14:3; hortatory instruction, Acts 13:15; 15:31; entreaty, importunity, earnest supplication, 2 Cor. 8:4; solace, consolation, Lk. 2:25; Rom. 15:4, 5; 2 Cor. 1:3, 4, 5, 6, 7; cheering and supporting influence, Acts 9:31; joy, gladness, rejoicing, 2 Cor. 7:13; cheer, joy, enjoyment, Lk. 6:24
G3874.abbottSmith: a calling to one's aid , summons (Thus.), hence, appeal , entreaty (Strab., Plut., al., I Mac 10:24 ; cf. Lft., Notes , 20): II Co 8:4 .

G4709.mounce: earnestly, eagerly, diligently, Lk. 7:4; 2 Tim. 1:17; Tit. 3:13; compar. σπουδαιοτέρως, more earnestly, with special urgency, Phil. 2:28
G4709.abbottSmith: with haste or zeal , i.e. earnestly, zealously, diligently : Lk 7:4 , II Ti 1:17 , Tit 3:13 ; compar., -οτέρως , Phl 2:28 (RV, Lft., Weymouth, al., but v. infr.)

G5550.mounce: time, whether in respect of duration or a definite point of its lapse, Mt. 2:7; 25:19 freq.; an epoch, era, marked duration, Acts 1:7; 1 Thess. 5:1
G5550.abbottSmith: time (a space of time, whether long or short; cf. Lft., Notes , 70): Mt 2:7 , Mk 9:21 , Lk 1:57 , Ac 3:21 7:17 , 23 13:18 17:30 27:9 , He 11:32 , I Pe 1:17 4:3 , Re 10:6 ; στιγμὴ χρόνου , Lk 4:5 ; πλήρωμα τοῦ χ. , Ga 4:4 ; ποιεῖν χ. , Ac 15:33 18:23 ; βιῶσαι , I Pe 4:2 ; διδόναι , Re 2:21 ; pl., χ. καὶ ( ἢ ) καιροί (Lft., l.c.), Ac 1:7 , I Th 5:1 ; ἐπ’ ἐσχάτου τῶν χ. ( χρόνου ), I Pe 1:20 , Ju 18
G5550.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a space of time (in general, and thus properly distinguished from G2540 (καιρός), which designates a fixed or special occasion; and from G165 (αἰών), which denotes a particular period) or interval; by extension, an individual opportunity; by implication, delay
G5550.kjv: + years old, season, space, (X often-)time(-s), (a) while

G553.thayer: I expect eagerly, wait for eagerly, look for.
G553.mounce: to expect, wait or look for, Rom. 8:19, 23, 25; 1 Cor. 1:7; Gal. 5:5; Phil. 3:20; Heb. 9:28
G553.abbottSmith: to await or expect eagerly (Lft., Notes , 149; MM, VGT , s.v.): absol., I Pe 3:20 ; c. acc. rei, Ro 8:19 , 23 , 25 , I Co 1:7 , Ga 5:5 ; c. acc. pers., Phl 3:20 , He 9:28 .†
G553.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1551 (ἐκδέχομαι); to expect fully

G5167.strongs: from (to murmur; akin to G5149 (τρίζω), but denoting a duller sound); a turtle-dove (as cooing)

G1668.mounce: pr. a wound; hence, an ulcer, sore, Lk. 16:21; Rev. 16:2, 11*
G1668.strongs: probably from G1670 (ἑλκύω); an ulcer (as if drawn together)

G2039.thayer: working, activity, work, service, trade, business, gains of business, performance, practice.
G2039.mounce: work, labor; in NT ἐργασίαν διδόναι, to endeavor, strive, Lk. 12:58; performance, practice, Eph. 4:19; a trade, business, craft, Acts 19:25, gain acquired by labor or trade, profit, Acts 16:16, 19; 19:24*
G2039.abbottSmith: work, business : Ac 16:16 , 19 19:24 , 25 ; δὸς ἐ. (Lat. da operam ), Lk 12:58 .
G2039.strongs: from G2040 (ἐργάτης); occupation; by implication, profit, pains

G5363.mounce: philanthropy, love of mankind, Tit. 3:4; benevolence, humanity, Acts 28:2*
G5363.abbottSmith: humanity, kindness (v. Field, Notes , 147 f.): Ac 28:2 , Tit 3:4 (cf. also DCG , ii, 356 ff.).†
G5363.strongs: from the same as G5364 (φιλανθρώπως); fondness of mankind, i.e. benevolence ("philanthropy")

G5339.mounce: to spare; to be tender of, Rom. 8:32; to spare, in respect of hard dealing, Acts 20:29; Rom. 11:21; 1 Cor. 7:28; 2 Cor. 1:23; 13:2; 2 Pet. 2:4, 5; absol. to forbear, abstain, 2 Cor. 12:6*
G5339.abbottSmith: to spare : II Co 13:2 ; c. gen. pers., Ac 20:29 , Ro 8:32 11:21 , I Co 7:28 , II Co 1:23 , II Pe 2:4 , 5 ; c. inf., to forbear , II Co 12:6 .†
G5339.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to be chary of, i.e. (subjectively) to abstain or (objectively) to treat leniently

G5013.thayer: I make or bring low, humble, humiliate; pass: I am humbled.
G5013.mounce: to bring low, depress, level, Lk. 3:5; met. to humble, abase, Phil. 2:8; mid. to descend to, or live in, a humble condition, 2 Cor. 11:7; Phil. 4:12; to humble, depress the pride of, any one, Mt. 18:4; mid. to humble one’s self, exhibit humility and contrition, Jas. 4:10; 1 Pet. 5:6; to humble with respect to hopes and expectations, to depress with disappointment, Mt. 23:12; Lk. 14:11; 18:14; 2 Cor. 12:21*
G5013.abbottSmith: to make low : ὄρος ( βουνόν ), Lk 3:5 (LXX) . Metaph., to humble, abase : Mt 18:4 23:12 , Lk 14:11 18:14 , II Co 11:7 12:21 , Phl 2:8 ; pass., Mt 23:12 , Lk 14:11 18:14 , Phl 4:12 ; id. with mid. sense, Ja 4:10 , I Pe 5:6 .†
G5013.strongs: from G5011 (ταπεινός); to depress; figuratively, to humiliate (in condition or heart)

G4114.abbottSmith: breadth : Eph 3:18 , Re 21:16 ; τ. π. τῆς γῆς ( Hb 1:6 , מֶרְחָב ), Re 20:9 .†

G2200.mounce: pr. boiled; boiling, boiling hot; met. glowing with zeal, fervent, Rev. 3:15, 16*
G2200.strongs: from G2204 (ζέω); boiled, i.e. (by implication) calid (figuratively, fervent)

G2487.mounce: a footstep, track; in NT pl. footsteps, line of conduct, Rom. 4:12; 2 Cor. 12:18; 1 Pet. 2:21*
G2487.abbottSmith: a track , footstep : metaph. (as freq. in cl.), στοιχεῖν τοῖς ἴ. , Ro 4:12 ; περιπατεῖν τοῖς ἴ. , II Co 12:18 ; ἐπακολουθεῖν τοῖς ἴ. , I Pe 2:21 (cf. MM, Exp. , xv).†
G2487.strongs: from (to arrive; compare G2240 (ἥκω)); a track (figuratively)

G57.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1110 (γνωστός); unknown

G5384.thayer: friendly; subst: a friend, an associate.
G5384.mounce: loved, dear; devoted; Acts 19:31; as a subst., a friend, Lk. 7:6; 11:5, 6, 8; a congenial associate, Mt. 11:19; Lk. 7:34; Jas. 4:4; used as a word of courteous appellation, Lk. 14:10
G5384.strongs: properly, dear, i.e. a friend; actively, fond, i.e. friendly (still as a noun, an associate, neighbor, etc.)

G2183.thayer: a class of priests who served for a stated number of days.
G2183.mounce: pr. daily course; the daily service of the temple; a class of priests to which the daily service for a week was allotted in rotation, Lk. 1:5, 8*
G2183.abbottSmith: a course of daily services ( Ne 13:30 , I Ch 25:8 , al.).
G2183.strongs: from G2184 (ἐφήμερος); diurnality, i.e. (specially) the quotidian rotation or class of the Jewish priests' service at the Temple, as distributed by families

G5469.strongs: from G5465 (χαλάω); a curb or head-stall (as curbing the spirit)

G2069.thayer: Esli, son of Naggai and father of Nahum.
G2069.abbottSmith: Esli , an ancestor of Jesus: Lk 3:25 .†
G2069.strongs: of Hebrew origin (probably for H0454); Esli, an Israelite

G4305.thayer: I am anxious beforehand, ponder beforehand.
G4305.mounce: to be anxious or solicitous beforehand, to ponder beforehand, Mk. 13:11*
G4305.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3309 (μεριμνάω); to care (anxiously) in advance

G2601.thayer: I bring down, cast down, cause to go down.
G2601.abbottSmith: to cause to go down , cast down : pass., ἕως ᾅδου , Mt 11:23 (WH, R, mg.), Lk 10:15 (WH, mg.).†
G2601.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of the base of G939 (βάσις); to cause to go down, i.e. precipitate

G738.strongs: from G737 (ἄρτι) and G1084 (γεννητός); just born, i.e. (figuratively) a young convert

G167.mounce: uncleanness; lewdness; impurity of motive, 1 Thess. 2:3
G167.abbottSmith: uncleanness , impurity , physical (MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 23:27 ; moral: Ro 1:24 6:19 , II Co 12:21 , Ga 5:19 , Eph 4:19 5:3 , Col 3:5 , I Th 2:3 4:7 .†
G167.strongs: from G169 (ἀκάθαρτος); impurity (the quality), physically or morally

G3027.mounce: a plunderer, robber, highwayman, Mt. 21:13; 26:55; Mk. 11:17; Lk. 10:30; 2 Cor. 11:26; a bandit, brigand, Mt. 27:38, 44; Mk. 15:27; Jn. 18:40; trop. a robber, rapacious imposter, Jn. 10:1, 8

G4112.thayer: formed, molded; fig: feigned, made up, counterfeit.
G4112.mounce: formed, fashioned, moulded; met. fabricated, counterfeit, delusive, 2 Pet. 2:3*
G4112.strongs: from G4111 (πλάσσω); moulded, i.e. (by implication) artificial or (figuratively) fictitious (false)

G4143.mounce: a vessel, ship, bark, whether large or small, Mt. 4:21, 22; Acts 21:2, 3
G4143.abbottSmith: a boat , also (= obsol. ναῦς ) a ship : Mt 4:21 , 22 , and freq. in Gosp. and Ac, Ja 3:4 , Re 8:9 18:19 .
G4143.strongs: from G4126 (πλέω); a sailer, i.e. vessel

G3859.abbottSmith: useless wrangling : v.l. in Rec. for διαπαρατριβή (q.v.), I Ti 6:5 .†
G3859.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G1304 (διατρίβω); misemployment, i.e. meddlesomeness

G1705.mounce: also spelled ἐμπίμπλημι (G1855) and ἐμπιμπλάω (G1857), to fill, Acts 14:17; pass. to be satisfied, satiated, full, Lk. 1:53; 6:25; Jn. 6:12; met. to have the full enjoyment of, Rom. 15:24*
G1705.abbottSmith: to fill full, fill up, satisfy : c. acc pers. et gen. rei, Lk 1:53 , Ac 14:17 ; pass., Lk 6:25 , Jo 6:12 ; metaph., c. gen. pers., to take one's fill of : Ro 15:24 (cf. Da LXX Su 32 ).†
G1705.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G4118 (πλεῖστος); to fill in (up), i.e. (by implication) to satisfy (literally or figuratively)

G5138.mounce: rough, rugged, uneven, Lk. 3:5; εἰς τραχεῖς τόπους, on a rocky shore, Acts 27:29*
G5138.abbottSmith: rough : ὁδοί ( Is, l.c. ), Lk 3:5 (LXX) ; τοποί (i.e. rocky), Ac 27:29 .†
G5138.strongs: perhaps strengthened from the base of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι) (as if jagged by rents); uneven, rocky (reefy)

G1815.thayer: a rising up and out, resurrection.
G1815.mounce: a raising up; a dislodgment; a rising up; a resurrection from the dead, Phil. 3:11*
G1815.abbottSmith: a rising again : ἐκ τ. νεκρῶν , Phl 3:11 (Cremer, 308).†
G1815.strongs: from G1817 (ἐξανίστημι); a rising from death

G1774.thayer: I dwell in, am settled (stationary) in; met: I am indwelling.
G1774.abbottSmith: to dwell in ; metaph., seq. ἐν , c. dat. pers: ὁ θεός , II Co 6:16 ; τ. πνεῦμα , Ro 8:11 , II Ti 1:14 ; ὁ λόγος , Col 3:16 ; πίστις , II Ti 1:5 ; ἁμαρτία , Ro 7:17 .†
G1774.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to inhabit (figuratively)

G2440.thayer: a long flowing outer garment, tunic.
G2440.mounce: a garment; the upper garment, mantle, Mt. 5:40; 9:16, 20, 21; pl. the mantle and tunic together, Mt. 26:65; pl. genr. garments, raiment, Mt. 11:8; 24:18
G2440.abbottSmith: a garment , but in usage always (exc. in p1., v. infr.) of an outer garment, a mantle , cloak (thrown over the χιτών ; v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 22; DCG , i, 499 a ): Mt 9:16 , 20 , 21 , Mk 2:21 5:27 , 28 , 30 , Jo 19:2 , Ac 12:8 , al.; opp. to χιτών , Mt 5:40 , Lk 6:29 , Ac 9:39 ; pl., garments , clothes (i.e. the cloak and the tunic), Mt 17:2 26:65 27:31 , 35 , Mk 5:30 15:20 , 24 , Jo 19:23 , 24 ,
G2440.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of ennumi (to put on); a dress (inner or outer)
G2440.kjv: apparel, cloke, clothes, garment, raiment, robe, vesture

G5019.thayer: Tarsus, the capital of the Roman province Cilicia.
G5019.mounce: Tarsus, the chief city of Cilicia, and birth-place of the Apostle Paul, Acts 9:30; 11:25; 22:3*. See PhotoGuide.
G5019.abbottSmith: Tarsus , a city of Cilicia: Ac 9:30 11:25 22:3 .†
G5019.strongs: perhaps the same as (a flat basket); Tarsus, a place in Asia Minor

G4934.thayer: mid. and pass: I make a compact (agreement) with (together), covenant with, agree.
G4934.mounce: to agree together, come to a mutual understanding, Jn. 9:22; Acts 23:20; to bargain, to pledge one’s self, Lk. 22:5*
G4934.abbottSmith: to place or put together , in various senses. Mid.,
G4934.strongs: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to consent (bargain, stipulate), concur

G5468.mounce: pr. to guide with a bridle; met. to bridle, control, sway, Jas. 1:26; 3:2*
G5468.abbottSmith: to lead with a bridle ; metaph., to bridle, restrain : c. acc., γλῶσσαν , Ja 1:26 ; σῶμα , 3:2 .†
G5468.strongs: from a compound of G5469 (χαλινός) and the reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω); to be a bit-leader, i.e. to curb (figuratively)

G234.thayer: pollution, perhaps a polluted thing (especially of food).

G2909.mounce: can also be spelled κρείσσων, better, more useful or profitable, more conducive to good, 1 Cor. 7:8, 38; superior, more excellent, of a higher nature, more valuable, Heb. 1:4; 6:9; 7:7, 19, 22
G2909.abbottSmith: better ; as to advantage or usefulness: I Co 11:17 , He 11:40 12:24 ; πολλῷ μᾶλλον κ. , Phl 1:23 ; κρεῖσσον ποιεῖν , I Co 7:38 ; ἐστιν , seq. inf., I Co 7:9 , II Pe 2:21 ; as to excellence: He 1:4 6:9 7:7 , 19 , 22 8:6 9:23 10:34 11:16 , 35 ; κ. ἐστι , seq. inf., I Pe 3:17 .†
G2909.strongs: comparative of a derivative of G2904 (κράτος); stronger, i.e. (figuratively) better, i.e. nobler

G1360.mounce: on the account that, because, Lk. 2:7; 21:28; in as much as, Lk. 1:13; Acts 18:10
G1360.abbottSmith: because : Lk 1:13 2:7 21:28 , Ac 13:35 18:10 20:26 22:18 , Ro 1:19 , 21 3:20 8:7 , 21 , I Co 15:9 , Phl 2:26 , I Th 2:8 , 18 (Lft., Notes , 37) 4:6 , He 11:5 , 23 , Ja 4:3 , I Pe 1:16 , 24 2:6 . It is usually stronger than ὅτι , but sometimes, as in Lk 1:13 , Ro 1:19 , I Th 2:18 , approximates to MGr. sense, for ; cf. Milligan, Th. , l.c. †
G1360.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3754 (ὅτι); on the very account that, or inasmuch as

G4798.mounce: use in common; associate with, have dealings with, Jn. 4:9*
G4798.abbottSmith: to use together with . Metaph., c. dat. pers., to associate with : Jo 4:9 (Polyb., al.).†
G4798.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5530 (χράομαι); to use jointly, i.e. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common

G4711.mounce: also spelled σφυρίς, a basket, handbasket for provision, Mt. 15:37; 16:10; Mk. 8:8, 20; Acts 9:25*
G4711.abbottSmith: a flexible mat- basket for carrying provisions: Mt 15:37 16:10 , Mk 8:8 , 20 Ac 9:25 (v.s. κόφινος ).†
G4711.strongs: from G4687 (σπείρω) (as woven); a hamper or lunch-receptacle

G2821.thayer: a calling, invitation; in the NT, always of a divine call.
G2821.mounce: a call, calling, invitation; in NT the call or invitation to the privileges of the Gospel, Rom. 11:29; Eph. 1:18; the favor and privilege of the invitation, 2 Thess. 1:11; 2 Pet. 1:10; the temporal condition in which the call found a person, 1 Cor. 1:26; 7:20
G2821.abbottSmith: a calling , call ; in NT, always of the Divine call to salvation: Ro 11:29 , I Co 1:26 7:20 , Eph 1:18 4:1 , 4 , Phl 3:14 , II Th 1:11 , II Ti 1:9 , He 3:1 , II Pe 1:10 (Cremer, 332).†
G2821.strongs: from a shorter form of G2564 (καλέω); an invitation (figuratively)

G2948.thayer: crippled, lame, especially in the hands.
G2948.mounce: pr. crooked, bent, maimed, lame, crippled, Mt. 18:8; Mk. 9:43, used as a noun meaning cripple, Mt. 15:30ff.*
G2948.strongs: from the same as G2947 (κυλιόω); rocking about, i.e. crippled (maimed, in feet or hands)

G4720.thayer: Stachys, a Christian man at Rome.
G4720.abbottSmith: Stachys : Ro 16:9 (v. Lft., Phl. , 174; MM, xxiii).†
G4720.strongs: the same as G4719 (στάχυς); Stachys, a Christian

G3853.thayer: I notify, command, charge, entreat solemnly.
G3853.mounce: to announce, notify; to command, direct, charge, Mt. 10:5; Mk. 6:8, 8:6; Lk. 9:21; to charge, entreat solemnly, 1 Tim. 6:13
G3853.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to transmit a message, i.e. (by implication) to enjoin

G242.thayer: I leap, leap up; of water: I spring up, bubble up.
G242.mounce: to leap, jump, leap up, Acts 3:8; 14:10; to spring, as water, Jn. 4:14*
G242.abbottSmith: to leap : Ac 3:8 14:10 ; of water, to spring up , Jo 4:14 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G242.strongs: middle voice of apparently a primary verb; to jump; figuratively, to gush

G4095.mounce: to drink, Mt. 6:25, 31; 26:27, 29, et al. freq.; trop. of the earth, to drink in, imbibe, Heb. 6:7
G4095.abbottSmith: to drink : absol., Lk 2:19 , Jo 4:7 , al.; c. acc. rei, Mt 6:25 , 31 , Mk 14:25 , al.; of habitual use, Lk 1:15 , Ro 14:21 , al.; by meton., τὸ ποτήριον , I Co 10:21 , al.; of the earth absorbing rain (Hdt., al.), He 6:7 ; spiritually, of the blood of Christ, Jo 6:53 , 54 , 56 ; seq. ἐκ (of the vessel), Mt 26:27 , al.; id. (of the drink; Bl., § 36, 1), Mt 26:29 , Jo 4:13 , 14 , Re 14:10 , al.; ἀπό
G4095.strongs: a prolonged form of ; which (together with another form) ; occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses; to imbibe (literally or figuratively)

G4551.thayer: Sapphira, wife of Ananias, an early Christian.
G4551.mounce: Sapphira, wife of Ananias and a member of the Jerusalem church, Acts 5:1*
G4551.strongs: feminine of G4552 (σάπφειρος); Sapphire, an Israelitess

G4696.mounce: a spot, stain, blot; a moral blemish, Eph. 5:27; 2 Pet. 2:13*
G4696.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a stain or blemish, i.e. (figuratively) defect, disgrace

G4132.abbottSmith: a flood (of sea or river): Lk 6:48 .†
G4132.strongs: prolonged from G4130 (πλήθω); flood-tide, i.e. (by analogy) a freshet

G860.thayer: a band, fastening (hence, possibly: a ligament), joint.
G860.mounce: a fastening; a ligament, by which the different members are connected, commissure, joint, Eph. 4:16; Col. 2:19*
G860.abbottSmith: a joint (MM, s.v.): Eph 4:16 , Col 2:19 (Lft., in l.).†
G860.strongs: from G680 (ἅπτομαι); probably a ligament (as fastening)

G2436.mounce: propitious, favorable, merciful, gracious, Heb. 8:12; from the Hebrew, ἵλεως σοι (ὁ θεός) God have mercy on thee, God forbid, far be it from thee, Mt. 16:22*
G2436.abbottSmith: propitious , merciful : τ. ἀδικίαις , He 8:12 ( Je 38(31):34 ; cf. III Ki 8:30 ff.); ἵ. σοι (sc. ἔστω ὁ θεός ), Mt 16:22 (cf. I Ki 14:45 ).†
G2436.strongs: perhaps from the alternate form of G138 (αἱρέομαι); cheerful (as attractive), i.e. propitious; adverbially (by Hebraism) God be gracious!, i.e. (in averting some calamity) far be it

G227.thayer: unconcealed, true, true in fact, worthy of credit, truthful.
G227.mounce: true, Jn. 4:18; worthy of credit, Jn. 5:31; truthful, Jn. 7:18
G227.abbottSmith: of things, true , conforming to reality: Jo 4:18 5:31,32 6:55 (= ἀληθινός , q.v.) Jo 8:13,14 , 17 10:41 19:35 21:24 , Ac 12:9 , Phl 4:8 , Tit 1:13 , I Pe 5:12 , II Pe 2:22 , I Jo 2:8 , III Jo 12 ;
G227.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2990 (λανθάνω); true (as not concealing)

G1724.mounce: marine, living in the sea, Jas. 3:7*
G1724.abbottSmith: of the sea : τὰ ἐ. , marine creatures , Ja 3:7 .†
G1724.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G251 (ἅλς); in the sea, i.e. marine

G5276.thayer: a wine-vat, pit under the wine-press dug in the ground.
G5276.mounce: a vat, placed under the press, ληνός, to receive the juice, Mk. 12:1*
G5276.abbottSmith: a vessel or trough beneath a winepress to receive the juice (RV, a pit for the winepress) Mk 12:1 (v. Swete, in l, and cf. ληνός ).†
G5276.strongs: neuter of a presumed compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G3025 (ληνός); vessel or receptacle under the press, i.e. lower wine-vat

G4191.mounce: bad, unsound, Mt. 6:23; 7:17, 18; evil, afflictive, Eph. 5:16; 6:13; Rev. 16:2; evil, wrongful, malignant, malevolent, Mt. 5:11, 39; Acts 28:21; evil, wicked, impious, and τὸ πονηρόν, evil, wrong, wickedness, Mt. 5:37, 45; 9:4; slothful, inactive, Mt. 25:26; Lk. 19:22; ὁ πονηρός, the evil one, the devil, Mt. 13:19, 38; Jn. 17:15; evil eye, i.q. φθονερός envious, Mt. 20:15; Mk. 7:22; impl. covetous, Mt. 7:11

G2036.mounce: to lay, to arrange, to gather; to say, Mt. 1:20; to speak, make an address or speech, Acts 26:1; to say mentally, in thought, Mt. 3:9; Lk. 3:8; to say in written language, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 1:63; Jn. 19:37; to say, as distinguished from acting, Mt. 23:3; to mention, speak of, Mk. 14:71; Lk. 9:31; Jn. 8:27; to tell, declare, narrate, Mt. 21:27; Mk. 10:32; to express, Heb. 5:11; to put forth, propound, Lk. 5:36; 13:6; Jn. 16:29; to mean, to intend to signify, 1 Cor. 1:12; 10:29; to say, declare, affirm, maintain, Mt. 3:9; 5:18; Mk. 12:18; Acts 17:7; 26:22; 1 Cor. 1:10; to enjoin, Acts 15:24; 21:21; Rom. 2:22; to term, designate, cull, Mt. 19:17; Mk. 12:37; Lk. 20:37; 23:2; 1 Cor. 8:5; to call by a name, Mt. 2:23; pass. to be further named, to be surnamed, Mt. 1:16; to be explained, interpreted, Jn. 4:25; 20:16, 24; in NT σὺ λέγεις, you say, a form of affirmative answer to a question Mt. 27:11; Mk. 15:2; Jn. 18:37
G2036.strongs: a primary verb (used only in the definite past tense, the others being borrowed from G2046 (ἐρέω), G4483 (ῥέω), and G5346 (φημί)); to speak or say (by word or writing)
G2036.kjv: answer, bid, bring word, call, command, grant, say (on), speak, tell

G3677.abbottSmith: indecl., used only in nom, and acc. sing. (the other cases are supplied by ὄνειρος ), a dream : κατ’ ὄ. (in later writers only), in a dream , Mt 1:20 2:12 , 13 , 19 , 22 27:19 .†

G2116.thayer: (a) I make straight (of the direction, not the surface, of a road), (b) I guide, steer.
G2116.mounce: to guide straight; to direct, guide, steer a ship, Jas. 3:4; to make straight, Jn. 1:23*
G2116.strongs: from G2117 (εὐθύς); to straighten (level); technically, to steer

G2699.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and (to cut); a cutting down (off), i.e. mutilation (ironically)

G1993.thayer: I muzzle, silence, stop the mouth.
G1993.mounce: to apply a curb or muzzle; met. to put to silence, Tit. 1:11*
G1993.abbottSmith: to bridle ; metaph., to stop the mouth, to silence : Tit 1:11 .†
G1993.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4750 (στόμα); to put something over the mouth, i.e. (figuratively) to silence

G4102.thayer: faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.
G4102.mounce: faith, belief, firm persuasion, 2 Cor. 5:7; Heb. 11:1; assurance, firm conviction, Rom. 14:23; ground of belief, guarantee, assurance, Acts 17:31; good faith, honesty, integrity, Mt. 23:23; Gal. 5:22; Tit. 2:10; faithfulness, truthfulness, Rom. 3:3; in NT faith in God and Christ, Mt. 8:10; Acts 3:16, et al. freq.; ἡ πίστις, the matter of Gospel faith, Acts 6:7; Jude 3
G4102.abbottSmith: in active sense, faith , belief , trust , confidence , in NT always of religious faith in God or Christ or spiritual things: Mt 8:10 , Lk 5:20 , Ac 14:9 , Ro 1:8 , I Co 2:5 , II Co 1:24 , I Ti 1:5 , al.; c. gen. obj., Mk 11:22 , Ac 3:16 , Ro 3:22 , Ga 2:16 , Eph 3:12 , Ja 2:1 , al.; c. prep., ἐν , Ro 3:25 , Ga 3:26 , Eph 1:15 , Col 1:24 , I Ti 1:14 3:13 , II Ti 1:13 3:15 , II Pe 1:1 ; εἰς , Ac 20:
G4102.strongs: from G3982 (πείθω); persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension, the system of religious (Gospel) truth itself

G2104.thayer: (a) of noble birth, of high birth, (b) noble in nature.
G2104.mounce: well-born, of high rank, honorable, Lk. 19:12; 1 Cor. 1:26; generous, candid, Acts 17:11*
G2104.abbottSmith: well born, of noble race : Lk 19:12 , I Co 1:26 .
G2104.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G1096 (γίνομαι); well born, i.e. (literally) high in rank, or (figuratively) generous

G2324.mounce: an attendant, a servant; a minister, Heb. 3:5*
G2324.strongs: apparently a participle from an otherwise obsolete derivative of the base of G2330 (θέρος); a menial attendant (as if cherishing)

G969.thayer: a beryl, a precious stone of various colors, the best known being sea-green.
G969.mounce: a beryl, a precious stone of a sea-green color, found chiefly in India, Rev. 21:20*
G969.abbottSmith: beryl , a jewel of sea-green colour: Re 21:20 .†

G5141.mounce: to tremble, be agitated from fear, Mk. 5:33; Lk. 8:47; by impl. to fear, be afraid, 2 Pet. 2:10*
G5141.abbottSmith: to tremble , esp. with fear: Mk 5:33 , Lk 8:47 ; c. ptcp. (in cl more freq. c. inf.), II Pe 2:10 .†
G5141.strongs: strengthened from a primary (to "dread", "terrify"); to "tremble" or fear

G3544.thayer: (a) adj: connected with law, about law, (b) noun: a lawyer, one learned in the Law, one learned in the Old Testament.
G3544.mounce: pertaining to law; relating to the Mosaic law, Tit. 3:9; as a subst., one skilled in law, a jurist, lawyer, Tit. 3:13; spc. an interpreter and teacher of the Mosaic law, Mt. 22:35
G3544.strongs: from G3551 (νόμος); according (or pertaining) to law, i.e. legal (ceremonially); as noun, an expert in the (Mosaic) law

G937.thayer: connected with a king, royal, regal, (a) an officer in the service of the king, (b) the king's country.
G937.mounce: royal, regal, Acts 12:20, 21; βασιλικός, used as a subst. a person attached to the king, courtier, Jn. 4:46, 49; met. royal, of the highest excellence, Jas. 2:8*
G937.abbottSmith: royal , belonging to a king : χώρα , Ac 12:20 ; ἐσθής , Ac 12:21 ; νόμος β. , a supreme law , "a law which governs other laws and so has a specially regal character" (Hort), or because made by a king ( LAE , p. 367 3 ), Ja 2:8 ; τις , one in the service of a king , a courtier , Jo 4:46 , 49 (WH, mg., βασιλίσκος ).†
G937.strongs: from G935 (βασιλεύς); regal (in relation), i.e. (literally) belonging to (or befitting) the sovereign (as land, dress, or a courtier), or (figuratively) preeminent

G1118.mounce: a father; pl. in NT parents, Mt. 10:21; Lk. 2:27, 41; 2 Cor. 12:14
G1118.abbottSmith: a begetter, a father ; mostly in pl., οἱ γ. , parents : Lk 2:41 , 43 8:56 21:16 , Jo 9:2 , 3 , 20 , 22 , 23 Ro 1:30 , II Co 12:14 , Eph 6:1 , Col 3:20 , II Ti 3:2 ; acc. pl. (Hellenistic, v. Thackeray, Gr. , i, 148), γονεῖς , Mt 10:21 , Mk 13:22 , Lk 2:27 18:29 , Jo 9:18 .†
G1118.strongs: from the base of G1096 (γίνομαι); a parent

G471.thayer: I speak or say in opposition, contradict (oppose, resist).
G471.mounce: to speak against, contradict; to gainsay, deny, Lk. 20:27; to oppose, Jn. 19:12; Acts 13:45; 28:19; Rom. 10:21; Tit. 1:9; 2:9; pass. to be spoken against, decried, Lk. 2:34; Acts 28:22*
G471.abbottSmith: 2 aor., without present in use, to speak against , gainsay : Lk 21:15 , Ac 4:14 .†
G471.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G2036 (ἔπω); to refute or deny

G843.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and a derivative or G2632 (κατακρίνω); self-condemned

G3341.thayer: repentance, a change of mind, change in the inner man.
G3341.mounce: a change of mode of thought and feeling, repentance, Mt. 3:8; Acts 20:21; 2 Tim. 2:25; practical reformation, Lk. 15:7; reversal of the past, Heb. 12:17
G3341.abbottSmith: after-thought , change of mind , repentance : He 12:17 ; of repentance from sin, Mt 3:8 , 11 , Lk 3:8 15:7 24:47 , Ac 26:20 , II Co 7:9 , 10 ; βάπτισμα (q.v.) μετανοίας , Mk 1:4 , Lk 3:3 , Ac 13:24 19:4 ; ἡ εἰς θεὸν μ. , Ac 20:21 ; μ. ἀπὸ νεκρῶν ἔργων , He 6:1 ; εἰς μ. καλεῖν , Lk 5:32 ; id. ἄγειν , Ro 2:4 ; ἀνακαινίζειν , He 6:6 ; εἰς μ. χωρῆσαι , II Pe 3:9 ; μ. δοῦναι , Ac 5:31 11:18 , II Ti 2:2
G3341.strongs: from G3340 (μετανοέω); (subjectively) compunction (for guilt, including reformation); by implication, reversal (of (another's) decision)

G383.thayer: I shake up, stir up, excite.
G383.mounce: pr. to shake up; met. to stir up, instigate, Mk. 15:11; Lk. 23:5*
G383.abbottSmith: to shake out , shake back , move to and fro (Thuc., al.).
G383.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4579 (σείω); figuratively, to excite

G374.mounce: also spelled ἀνάπηρος, maimed, deprived of some member of the body, or at least of its use, Lk. 14:13, 21
G374.abbottSmith: to persuade , incite : Ac 18:13 (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G374.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3982 (πείθω); to incite

G5332.mounce: a drug; an enchantment; magic potion, charm, Rev. 9:21*
G5332.strongs: from (a drug, i.e. spell-giving potion); a druggist ("pharmacist") or poisoner, i.e. (by extension) a magician

G5406.mounce: a homicide, murderer, Mt. 22:7; Acts 3:14; 7:52; 28:4; 1 Pet. 4:15; Rev. 21: 8; 22:15*
G5406.abbottSmith: a murderer : Mt 22:7 Ac 7:52 28:4 , I Pe 4:15 , Re 21:8 22:15 ; ἀνὴρ φ. , Ac 3:14 .†
G5406.strongs: from G5408 (φόνος); a murderer (always of criminal (or at least intentional) homicide; which G443 (ἀνθρωποκτόνος) does not necessarily imply; while G4607 (σικάριος) is a special term for a public bandit)

G197.mounce: accurately, diligently, Mt. 2:8; Lk. 1:3; Acts 18:25; circumspectly, strictly, Eph. 5:15; precisely, distinctly, 1 Thess. 5:2
G197.abbottSmith: with exactness , carefully : Mt 2:8 , Lk 1:3 , Ac 18:25 , Eph 5:15 , I Th 5:2 (M, Th. , in l.). Compar., ἀκριβέστερον (Milligan, NTD , 111; MM, VGT , s.v.), Ac 18:26 23:15 , 20 24:22 .†
G197.strongs: neuter of the comparative of the same as G196 (ἀκριβέστατος); (adverbially) more exactly

G1302.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5101 (τίς); through what cause ?, i.e. why?

G3480.thayer: a Nazarene, an inhabitant of Nazareth.
G3480.mounce: also spelled Ναζαρηνός, a Nazarite; an inhabitant of Nazareth, Mt. 2:23; 26:71; Lk. 18:37; Jn. 18:5, 7; 19:19; Acts 2:22; 3:6; 4:10; 6:14; 22:8; 24:5; 26:9*
G3480.strongs: from G3478 (Ναζαρέθ); a Nazoræan, i.e. inhabitant of Nazareth; by extension, a Christian

G3686.mounce: a name; the proper name of a person, etc., Mt. 1:23, 25; 10:2; 27:32; a mere name or reputation, Rev. 3:1; in NT a name as the representative of a person, Mt. 6:9; Lk. 6:22; 11:2; the name of the author of a commission, delegated authority, or religious profession, Mt. 7:22; 10:22; 12:21; 18:5, 20; 19:29; 21:9; 28:19; Acts 3:16; 4:7, 12; εἰς ὄνομα, ἐν ὀνόματι, on the score of being possessor of a certain character, Mt. 10:41, 42; Mk. 9:41
G3686.abbottSmith: in general, the name by which a person or thing is called: Mt 10:2 , Mk 3:16 , Lk 1:63 , Jo 18:10 , al.; ἄνθρωπος (etc.), ᾧ ( οὗ ) ὄ. ( τ. ὄ ), sc. ἦν or ἐστίν (Bl., § 30, 3), Mk 14:32 , Lk 1:26 , 27 ; with same ellipsis, καὶ τ. ὄ. αὐτοῦ ( ὄ. αὐτῷ ), Lk 1:5 , Jo 1:6 , al.; ὀνόματι , seq. nom. prop., Mt 27:32 , Mk 5:22 , Lk 1:5 , Ac 5:1 , al.; acc. absol. (Bl., § 34, 7), τοὔνομα (= τ. ὄνομα ), Mt 2
G3686.strongs: from a presumed derivative of the base of G1097 (γινώσκω) (compare G3685 (ὀνίνημι)); a "name" (literally or figuratively) (authority, character)

G1357.strongs: from a compound of G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G3717 (ὀρθός), meaning to straighten thoroughly; rectification, i.e. (specially) the Messianic restauration

G4862.mounce: with, together with, Mt. 25:27; 26:35; 27:38; attendant on, 1 Cor. 15:10; besides, Lk. 24:21; with, with the assistance of, 1 Cor. 5:4; with, in the same manner as, Gal. 3:9; εἶναι σύν τινι, to be with any one, to be in company with, accompany, Lk. 2:13; 8:38; to be on the side of, be a partisan of any one, Acts 4:13; 14:4; οἱ σύν τινι, those with any one, the companions of any one, Mk. 2:26; Acts 22:9; the colleagues, associates of any one, Acts 5:17, 21
G4862.abbottSmith: expressing association, fellowship and inclusion. It gradually gave way to μετά , c. gen. (cf. LS, s.v.; Bl., § 41, 3), and is therefore comparatively infrequent in NT, being rare in Mt 4 , Mk 6 , Jo 3 , and elsewhere (exx. Ja 1:11 , II Pe 1:18 ) only in Lk (Gosp. and Ac) and Paul. With, together with : of companionship and association, Lk 2:13 , Jo 21:3 , Ac 10:23 , al.; εἶναι σύν τινι , Lk 7:12
G4862.strongs: a primary preposition denoting union; with or together (but much closer than G3326 (μετά) or G3844 (παρά)), i.e. by association, companionship, process, resemblance, possession, instrumentality, addition, etc.

G2692.mounce: an overthrow, destruction, 2 Pet. 2:6; met. overthrow of right principle or faith, utter detriment, perversion, 2 Tim. 2:14*
G2692.strongs: from G2690 (καταστρέφω); an overturn ("catastrophe"), i.e. demolition; figuratively, apostasy

G1016.mounce: an ox, a bull or cow, an animal of the ox kind, Lk. 13:15
G1016.strongs: probably from the base of G1006 (βόσκω); an ox (as grazing), i.e. an animal of that species ("beef")

G3409.thayer: I hire out, mid: I hire, engage.
G3409.mounce: to hire out, let out to hire; mid. to hire, Mt. 20:1, 7*
G3409.abbottSmith: to let out for hire . Mid., to hire : c. acc., Mt 20:1 , 7 .†
G3409.strongs: from G3408 (μισθός); to let out for wages, i.e. (middle voice) to hire

G2542.thayer: Two cities of Palestine: one in Galilee (Caesarea Philippi), the other on the coast of the Mediterranean.
G2542.mounce: Caesarea (1) Caesarea Philippi, Mt. 16:13; Mk. 8:27; (2) Caesarea Augusta, Acts 8:40
G2542.abbottSmith: Cæsarea ; Cæsarea Philippi , at the foot of Lebanon: Mt 16:13 , Mk 8:27 . Cæsarea of Palestine, on the sea-coast: Ac 8:40 9:30 10:1 , 24 11:11 12:19 18:22 21:8 , 16 23:23 , 33 25:1 , 4 , 6 , 13 .†
G2542.strongs: from G2541 (Καῖσαρ); Cæsaria, the name of two places in Palestine

G3780.thayer: by no means, not at all.
G3780.mounce: a strengthened form of οὐ, not, Jn. 13:10, 11; when followed by ἀλλά, nay, not so, by no means, Lk. 1:60; 12:51; used also in negative interrogations, Mt. 5:46, 47; 6:25
G3780.abbottSmith: not ; in neg. sentences, not , not at all : Lk 1:60 12:51 , Jo 13:10 , al.; more freq. in questions where an affirm. ans. is expected (Lat. nonne ): Mt 5:46 , 47 , Lk 6:39 , Jo 11:9 , al.

G3163.thayer: (earlier: a battle, conflict, hence) in the sphere of words, etc: strife, contention, quarrel.
G3163.mounce: a fight, battle, conflict; in NT contention, dispute, strife, controversy, 2 Cor. 7:5; 2 Tim. 2:23; Tit. 3:9; Jas. 4:1*
G3163.strongs: from G3164 (μάχομαι); a battle, i.e. (figuratively) controversy

G1549.mounce: born of, descended from; as a noun ἔκγονα, descendants, grandchildren, 1 Tim. 5:4*
G1549.strongs: neuter of a derivative of a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and G1096 (γίνομαι); a descendant, i.e. (specially) grandchild

G4963.thayer: a gathering together, riotous concourse, conspiracy.
G4963.mounce: a gathering, tumultuous assembly, Acts 19:40; a combination, conspiracy, Acts 23:12*
G4963.strongs: from G4962 (συστρέφω); a twisting together, i.e. (figuratively) a secret coalition, riotous crowd

G1418.abbottSmith: inseparable prefix, opp. to εὖ , like un-, mis- (in unrest, mischance ), giving the idea of difficulty, opposition, injuriousness, etc.
G1418.strongs: a primary inseparable particle of uncertain derivation; used only in composition as a prefix; hard, i.e. with difficulty

G76.thayer: Adam, the first man, the first parent of the human race.
G76.abbottSmith: Adam : Lk 3:38 , Ro 5:14 , I Co 15:22 , 45 , I Ti 2:13 , 14 Ju 14 . Christ ὁ ἔσχατος Ἀ. , I Co 15:45 .†
G76.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0121); Adam, the first man; typically (of Jesus) man (as his representative)

G1642.mounce: to make less or inferior, Heb. 2:7; pass. to be made less or inferior, Heb. 2:9; to decline in importance, Jn. 3:30*
G1642.abbottSmith: to make less : He 2:7 (LXX) ; pass., Jo 3:30 , He 2:9 .†
G1642.strongs: from G1640 (ἐλάσσων); to lessen (in rank or influence)

G2130.thayer: willingly sharing, ready to impart, generous.
G2130.abbottSmith: ready to impart : assoc. with κοινωνικός (for the distinction bet. the two, v. Field, Notes , 213; CGT , in l.), I Ti 6:18 .†
G2130.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a presumed derivative of G3330 (μεταδίδωμι); good at imparting, i.e. liberal

G3687.thayer: I give a name to, mention, call upon the name of.
G3687.mounce: to name, Lk. 6:14; to style, entitle, Lk. 6:13; 1 Cor. 5:11; to make mention of, Eph. 5:3; to make known, Rom. 15:20; to pronounce in exorcism, Acts 19:13; in NT to profess, 2 Tim. 2:19
G3687.abbottSmith: to name , mention , or address by name : Ac 19:13 ; pass., Ro 15:20 , Eph 1:21 5:3 ; of the use of the Divine name in praise and worship, II Ti 2:19 (LXX, Nu 16:26 ; cf. Is 52:11 , Am 6:10 ).
G3687.strongs: from G3686 (ὄνομα); to name, i.e. assign an appellation; by extension, to utter, mention, profess

G4970.mounce: much, greatly, exceedingly, Mt. 2:10; 17:6; Mk. 16:4; Lk. 18:23; Acts 6:7
G4970.abbottSmith: very, very much, exceedingly : with verbs, Mt 17:6 , 23 18:31 19:25 26:22 27:54 , Ac 6:7 ; with adjectives, Mt 2:10 , Mk 16:4 , Lk 18:23 , Re 16:21 .†
G4970.strongs: neuter plural of (violent; of uncertain derivation) as adverb; vehemently, i.e. in a high degree, much

G5142.thayer: I feed, nourish; I bring up, rear, provide for.
G5142.mounce: to nourish; to feed, support, cherish, provide for,
G5142.abbottSmith: to make to grow, bring up, rear (cl.; I Mac 3:33 11:39 ): Lk 4:16 (T, WH, mg., ἀνατ- )
G5142.strongs: a primary verb (properly, ; but perhaps strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή) through the idea of convolution); properly, to stiffen, i.e. fatten (by implication, to cherish (with food, etc.), pamper, rear)

G4207.thayer: from afar, far off, from a distance.
G4207.mounce: from a distance, from afar, Heb. 11:13; at a distance, far, far off, Lk. 17:12*
G4207.strongs: from G4206 (πόῤῥω) with adverbial enclitic of source; from far, or (by implication) at a distance, i.e. distantly

G3348.thayer: I have a share of, participate in, share, partake of, am a member of.
G3348.mounce: to share in, partake, 1 Cor. 9:10, 12; 10:17, 21; 1 Cor. 10:30; Heb. 2:14; 5:13; to be a member of, Heb. 7:13*
G3348.abbottSmith: to partake of , share in : ἐπ’ ἐλπίδι τοῦ μετέχειν , I Co 9:10 ; c. gen. rei, I Co 9:12 10:21 , 30 , He 2:14 ; in sacramental sense, ἑκ τ. ἐνὸς ἄρτου μ. , I Co 10:17 (cf. MM, xvi); metaph., γάλακτος , He 5:13 ; of belonging to a tribe, He 7:13 .†
G3348.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G2192 (ἔχω); to share or participate; by implication, belong to, eat (or drink)
G3348.kjv: be partaker, pertain, take part, use

G4864.thayer: an assembly, congregation, synagogue, either the place or the people gathered together in the place.
G4864.mounce: a collecting, gathering; a Christian assembly or congregation, Jas. 2:2; the congregation of a synagogue, Acts 9:2; hence, the place itself, a synagogue, Lk. 7:5
G4864.strongs: from (the reduplicated form of) G4863 (συνάγω); an assemblage of persons; specially, a Jewish "synagogue" (the meeting or the place); by analogy, a Christian church

G912.abbottSmith: Barabbas : Mt 27:16 , 17 , 20 , 21 , 26 , Mk 15:7 , 11 , 15 , Lk 23:18 , Jo 18:40 . (In Mt 27:16 , some MSS. read Ἰησοῦν Β. ; v. WH, App. , 19 f.).†
G912.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01347 and H05); son of Abba; Bar-abbas, an Israelite

G2221.thayer: I capture alive, capture for life, enthrall.
G2221.mounce: pr. to take alive, take prisoner in war instead of killing; to take captive, enthral, 2 Tim. 2:26; also, to catch animals, as fish; in which sense it is used figuratively, Lk. 5:10*
G2221.abbottSmith: to catch alive , take captive : metaph., Lk 5:10 ; pass., II Ti 2:26 (on the meaning and construction, v. Ellic., CGT , in l.).†
G2221.strongs: from the same as G2226 (ζῶον) and G64 (ἀγρεύω); to take alive (make a prisoner of war), i.e. (figuratively) to capture or ensnare

G5223.mounce: goods possessed, substance, property, Acts 2:45; Heb. 10:34*
G5223.strongs: from G5225 (ὑπάρχω); existency or proprietorship, i.e. (concretely) property, wealth

G3518.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05374); Neri (i.e. Nerijah), an Israelite

G3625.thayer: (properly: the land that is being inhabited, the land in a state of habitation), the inhabited world, that is, the Roman world, for all outside it was regarded as of no account.
G3625.mounce: one who is occupied in domestic affairs, Tit. 2:5*
G3625.abbottSmith: the inhabited earth ; in cl., the countries occupied by Greeks, as disting. from barbarian lands (Hdt., Dem., al.); in later writers, the Roman world: Lk 2:1 , Ac 11:28 24:5 ; by meton., of its inhabitants: Ac 17:6 19:27 ; in LXX ( Ps 22 (23):1 70 (71):8 , al.) and NT, also of the whole inhabited world: Mt 24:14 , Lk 4:5 21:26 , Ro 10:18 , He 1:6 , Re 3:10 16:14 ; by meton. (ut supr.), Ac 17:31 ,
G3625.strongs: feminine participle present passive of G3611 (οἰκέω) (as noun, by implication, of G1093 (γῆ)); land, i.e. the (terrene part of the) globe; specially, the Roman empire

G544.thayer: I disobey, rebel, am disloyal, refuse conformity.
G544.mounce: to be uncompliant; to refuse belief, disbelieve, Jn. 3:36; to refuse belief and obedience, Rom. 10:21; 1 Pet. 3:20; to refuse conformity, Rom. 2:8
G544.abbottSmith: as in cl. (MM, VGT , s.v.); to disobey , be disobedient : absol., Ac 14:2 19:9 , Ro 10:21 11:31 15:31 , He 3:18 11:31 , I Pe 3:20 ; c. dat., Jo 3:36 , Ro 2:8 11:30 , I Pe 2:8 3:1 4:17 (Cremer, 475).†
G544.strongs: from G545 (ἀπειθής); to disbelieve (wilfully and perversely)
G544.kjv: not believe, disobedient, obey not, unbelieving

G1320.mounce: a teacher, master, Rom. 2:20; in NT as an equivalent, to ῥαββί, Jn. 1:39
G1320.abbottSmith: given as rendering of Heb. רַבּוֺן , רַבִּי (NT, Ῥαββεί , Ῥαββουνεί q.v.);
G1320.strongs: from G1321 (διδάσκω); an instructor (genitive case or specially)

G4218.thayer: at one time or other, at some time, formerly.
G4218.mounce: enclitic particle, once, some time or other, either past or future; formerly, Jn. 9:13; at length, Lk. 22:32; at any time, ever, Eph. 5:29; Heb. 2:1; intensive after interrogatives, ever, 1 Cor. 9:7; Heb. 1:5
G4218.abbottSmith: once , formerly , sometime : of the past, Jo 9:13 , Ro 7:9 11:30 , Ga 1:13 , 23 2:6 (Lft., in l.), Eph 2:2 , 3 , 11 , 13 5:8 , Col 1:21 3:7 , I Th 2:5 , Tit 3:3 , Phm 11 , I Pe 2:10 3:5 , 20 ; ἤδη ποτέ , now at length , Phl 4:10 ; of the fut., Lk 22:32 ; εἴ πως ἤδη ποτέ , if sometime soon at length , Ro 1:10 .
G4218.strongs: from the base of G4225 (πού) and G5037 (τέ); indefinite adverb, at some time, ever
G4218.kjv: afore-(any, some-)time(-s), at length (the last), (+ n- )ever, in the old time, in time past, once, when

G3325.mounce: to fill; pass. to be filled, be full, Acts 2:13*
G3325.abbottSmith: to fill : pass., c. gen. rei, Ac 2:13 .†
G3325.strongs: from G3324 (μεστός); to replenish, i.e. (by implication) to intoxicate

G4920.mounce: also συνίω, see BDAG 972 for a discussion, pr. to send together; met. to understand, comprehend thoroughly, Mt. 13:51; Lk. 2:50; 18:34; 24:45; to perceive clearly, Mt. 16:12; 17:13; Acts 7:25; Rom. 15:21; Eph. 5:17; absol. to be well judging, sensible, 2 Cor. 10:12; to be spiritually intelligent, Mt. 13:13, 14, 15; Acts 28:26, 27; to be religiously wise, Rom. 3:11
G4920.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to send); to put together, i.e. (mentally) to comprehend; by implication, to act piously

G4301.thayer: I take before, anticipate; pass: I am caught or overtaken, taken by surprise.
G4301.mounce: to take before another, 1 Cor. 11:21; trop. to anticipate, do beforehand, Mk. 14:8; to take by surprise; pass. be taken unexpectedly, be overtaken, be taken by surprise, Gal. 6:1*
G4301.abbottSmith: to take beforehand : c. acc., I Co 11:21 (but v. infr.).
G4301.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take in advance, i.e. (literally) eat before others have an opportunity; (figuratively) to anticipate, surprise

G4754.thayer: I wage war, fight, serve as a soldier; fig: of the warring lusts against the soul.
G4754.abbottSmith: used of the general, to make war, do battle , and (chiefly) of the soldiers serving under him, to serve as a soldier : Lk 3:14 , I Co 9:7 , II Ti 2:4 . Metaph. (cf. MM, ii, xxiii), of spiritual conflict, to war, make war : II Co 10:3 , I Ti 1:18 , Ja 4:1 , I Pe 2:11 .†
G4754.strongs: middle voice from the base of G4756 (στρατιά); to serve in a military campaign; figuratively, to execute the apostolate (with its arduous duties and functions), to contend with carnal inclinations

G701.mounce: pleasing, acceptable, Acts 12:3; 1 Jn. 3:22; 8:29, deemed proper, Acts 6:2
G701.abbottSmith: pleasing , agreeable (Hdt., Xen., and later writers; v. Cremer, 641 f; MM, s.v.): c. dat. pers., Jo 8:29 , Ac 12:3 ; seq. ἐνώπιον , c. gen. (Bl., § 37, 1; 40, 7), I Jo 3:22 ; ἀρεστόν ἐστιν , c. acc. et inf (Bl., § 69, 5; 72, 5), Ac 6:2 .†
G701.strongs: from G700 (ἀρέσκω); agreeable; by implication, fit

G4969.thayer: I slay, kill by violence, slaughter, wound mortally.
G4969.mounce: also spelled σφάττω, to slaughter, kill, slay; pr. used of animals killed in sacrifice, etc., Rev. 5:6, 9, 12; 13:8; of persons, etc., 1 Jn. 3:12; Rev. 6:4, 9; 18:24; to wound mortally, Rev. 13:3*
G4969.abbottSmith: to slay, slaughter (esp. of victims for sacrifice): ἀρνίον , Re 5:6 , 12 13:8 ; of persons, I Jo 3:12 , Re 5:9 6:4 , 9 18:24 ; ἐσφαγμένη εἰς θάνατον (RV, smitten unto death ), Re 13:3 (cf. κατα-σφάζω ).†
G4969.strongs: a primary verb; to butcher (especially an animal for food or in sacrifice) or (generally) to slaughter, or (specially), to maim (violently)

G2548.thayer: and he, she, it, and that.
G2548.mounce: crasis, and he, she, it; and this, and that, Mt. 15:18; 23:23; he, she, it also; this also, that also, Mt. 20:4
G2548.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1565 (ἐκεῖνος); likewise that (or those)
G2548.kjv: and him (other, them), even he, him also, them (also), (and) they

G3053.mounce: pr. a computation, act of computing; a thought, cogitation, Rom. 2:15; a conception, device, 2 Cor. 10:4*
G3053.abbottSmith: a reasoning , thought : Ro 2:15 , II Co 10:5 .†
G3053.strongs: from G3049 (λογίζομαι); computation, i.e. (figuratively) reasoning (conscience, conceit)

G1429.abbottSmith: of twelve tribes : as subst. neut., τό δ. (cf. λαὸς ὁ δ. , Sibyll. Orac.), the twelve tribes , Ac 26:7 .†
G1429.strongs: from G1427 (δώδεκα) and G5443 (φυλή); the commonwealth of Israel

G3372.strongs: probably akin to G3173 (μέγας); length (literally or figuratively) length.

G3427.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G3427.strongs: the simpler form of G1698 (ἐμοί); to me

G2742.mounce: fervent scorching heat; the scorching of the sun, Mt. 20:12; hot weather, a hot time, Lk. 12:55; the scorching wind of the East, Eurus, Jas. 1:11*
G2742.abbottSmith: burning heat (Ge, Isa (?), Jth 8:3 , Si 18:16 ): Mt 20:12 , Lk 12:55 ( Ja 1:11 , AV).

G3695.mounce: to arm, equip; mid. to arm one’s self, equip one’s self, 1 Pet. 4:1*
G3695.abbottSmith: to make ready , equip ; of soldiers, to arm . Mid., to arm oneself ; fig., ἔννοιαν : I Pe 4:1 (cf. θράσος ὁ. , Soph., Elec. , 995), (cf. καθ-οπλίζω ).†
G3695.strongs: from G3696 (ὅπλον); to equip (with weapons (middle voice and figuratively))

G4359.mounce: to owe besides or in addition, Phlm. 19*
G4359.abbottSmith: to owe besides : Phm 19 (v. Field, Notes , 225).†
G4359.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G3784 (ὀφείλω); to be indebted additionally

G345.thayer: I recline, especially at a dinner-table.
G345.mounce: to be laid up, as offerings; later, to lie, recline at table, Mt. 9:10
G345.abbottSmith: in cl., as pass. of ἀνατίθημ , to be laid up , laid : Mk 5:40 Rec.
G345.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to recline (as a corpse or at a meal)
G345.kjv: guest, lean, lie, sit (down, at meat), at the table

G4883.mounce: to join together fitly, fit or frame together, compact, Eph. 2:21; 4:16*
G4883.abbottSmith: to fit or frame together : of the parts of a building, Eph 2:21 ; of the members of the body, Eph 4:16 (Eccl.).†
G4883.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G719 (ἁρμός) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense of laying); to render close-jointed together, i.e. organize compactly

G953.mounce: to profane, pollute, violate, Mt. 12:5; Acts 24:6*
G953.abbottSmith: to profane : τ. σάββατον , Mt 12:5 ; τ. ἱερόν , Ac 24:6 (Cremer, 141).†

G2567.thayer: a teacher of that which is noble (honorable) and good.
G2567.mounce: teaching what is good; a teacher of good, Tit. 2:3*
G2567.abbottSmith: a teacher of that which is good : Tit 2:3 .†
G2567.strongs: from G2570 (καλός) and G1320 (διδάσκαλος); a teacher of the right

G1725.mounce: also an improper prep, over against, in the presence of, Lk. 1:8; Acts 8:21*
G1725.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G473 (ἀντί); in front (i.e. figuratively, presence) of

G2504.thayer: I also, I too, but I.
G2504.mounce: and I, I also, but I, a crasis of καί and ἐγώ, dat., κἀμοί, acc., κἀμέ
G2504.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1473 (ἐγώ); so also the dative case , and accusative case and (or also, even, etc.) I, (to) me
G2504.kjv: (and, even, even so, so) I (also, in like wise), both me, me also

G3190.thayer: Melea, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3190.strongs: of uncertain origin; Meleas, an Israelite

G716.strongs: probably from G142 (αἴρω) (perhaps with G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) prefixed); a chariot (as raised or fitted together (compare G719 (ἁρμός)))

G1929.thayer: (a) trans: I hand in, give up, (b) intrans: I give way (to the wind).
G1929.mounce: to give in addition; also, to give to, deliver to, give into one’s hands, Mt. 7:9, 10; Lk. 4:17; 11:11f.; 24:30, 42; Acts 15:30; intrans. probably a nautical term, to commit a ship to the wind, let her drive, Acts 27:15*
G1929.abbottSmith: to give over, to hand : c. acc rei et dat. pers., Mt 7:9 , 10 , Lk 11:11 , 12 24:30 , 42 , Ac 15:30 ; pass. c. dat. pers., Lk 4:17 .
G1929.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give over (by hand or surrender)
G1929.kjv: deliver unto, give, let (+ (her drive)), offer

G4707.strongs: comparative of G4705 (σπουδαῖος); more prompt, more earnest

G122.abbottSmith: of a goat : He 11:37 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G122.strongs: from (a goat); belonging to a goat

G2322.thayer: care, attention, especially medical attention (treatment); hence almost: healing; meton: those who render service.
G2322.strongs: from G2323 (θεραπεύω); attendance (specially, medical, i.e. cure); figuratively and collectively, domestics

G4018.thayer: a wrapper, mantle, veil, cloak, covering.
G4018.mounce: that which is thrown around any one, clothing, covering; a cloak, Heb. 1:12; a covering, 1 Cor. 11:15*
G4018.abbottSmith: that which is thrown around , a covering , in NT, a mantle ( Ps 101 (102):27 , Is 59:17 , al.): He 1:12 (LXX) ; a veil : I Co 11:15 (but v. ICC , in l.).†
G4018.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4016 (περιβάλλω); something thrown around one, i.e. a mantle, veil

G314.thayer: I read, know again, know certainly, recognize, discern.
G314.mounce: to gather exact knowledge of, recognize, discern; especially, to read
G314.abbottSmith: to know certainly , know again , recognize .
G314.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G1097 (γινώσκω); to know again, i.e. (by extension) to read

G4997.thayer: soundness of mind, sanity; self-control, sobriety.
G4997.mounce: sanity, soundness of mind, a sane mind, Acts 26:25; female modesty, 1 Tim. 2:9, 15*
G4997.abbottSmith: soundness of mind, good sense, sanity (opp. to μανία , Xen.): Ac 26:25 ;
G4997.strongs: from G4998 (σώφρων); soundness of mind, i.e. (literally) sanity or (figuratively) self-control

G4230.thayer: a business, an occupation, affair, transaction.
G4230.mounce: an application to a matter of business; in NT business, affair, transaction, 2 Tim. 2:4*
G4230.strongs: from G4231 (πραγματεύομαι); a transaction, i.e. negotiation

G4235.strongs: a form of G4239 (πραΰς), used in certain parts; gentle, i.e. humble

G169.abbottSmith: unclean, impure ; physically (LS, MM, VGT , s.v.); ceremonially: Ac 10:14 , 28 11:8 , I Co 7:14 , II Co 6:17 , Re 18:2 ; morally: Eph 5:5 , Re 17:4 ; c. πνεῦμα , as always in Gosp., Mt 10:1 12:43 , Mk 1:23 , 26 , 27 3:11 , 30 5:2 , 8 , 13 6:7 7:25 9:25 , Lk 4:33 , 36 6:18 8:29 9:42 11:24 , Ac 5:16 8:7 , Re 16:13 (cf. Cremer, 320).†
G169.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G2508 (καθαίρω) (meaning cleansed); impure (ceremonially, morally (lewd) or specially, (demonic))

G1121.thayer: a letter of the alphabet; collectively: written (revelation); (a) a written document, a letter, an epistle, (b) writings, literature, learning.
G1121.mounce: pr. that which is written or drawn; a letter, character of the alphabet, a writing, book, Jn. 5:47; an acknowledgment of debt, an account, a bill, note, Lk. 16:6, 7; an epistle, letter, Acts 28:21; Gal. 6:11; ἱερὰ γράμματα, the sacred books of the Old Testament, the Jewish Scriptures, 2 Tim. 3:15; spc. the letter of the law of Moses, the bare literal sense, Rom. 2:27, 29; 7:6; 2 Cor. 3:6, 7; pl. letters, learning, Jn. 7:15; Acts 26:24*
G1121.abbottSmith: that which is traced or drawn, a picture . that which is written ; a character, letter : Ga 6:11 ; a writing, a written document ; a bill or account : Lk 16:6 , 7 ; a letter : Ac 28:21 ; τὰ ἱερὰ γ. , the sacred writings , i.e. the OT: II Ti 3:15 (so in Philo, Vit. Mos. , iii, 39) ; τὸ γ. , the letter , the written word as an external authority in contrast with the direct influence of the Spirit as
G1121.strongs: from G1125 (γράφω); a writing, i.e. a letter, note, epistle, book, etc.; plural learning
G1121.kjv: bill, learning, letter, scripture, writing, written

G1317.thayer: able to teach, apt to teach.
G1317.mounce: apt or qualified to teach, 1 Tim. 3:2; 2 Tim. 2:24*
G1317.abbottSmith: apt at teaching : I Ti 3:2 , II Ti 2:24 .†

G2014.thayer: I appear (as of a light in the heavens or from the heavens), shine upon.
G2014.mounce: to make to appear, to display; pass. to be manifested, revealed, Tit. 2:11; 3:4; intrans. to give light, shine, Lk. 1:79; Acts 27:20*
G2014.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5316 (φαίνω); to shine upon, i.e. become (literally) visible or (figuratively) known

G5591.mounce: pertaining to the life or soul; in NT animal, as distinguished from spiritual subsistence, 1 Cor. 15:44, 46; occupied with mere animal things, animal, sensual, 1 Cor. 2:14; Jas. 3:15; Jude 19*
G5591.abbottSmith: of the ψυχή (as the lower part of the immaterial in man), EV, natural : opp. to πνευματικός , I Co 2:14 15:44 , 46 ; πνεῦμα μὴ ἔχοντες (EV, sensual ; R, mg., natural or animal ; better perhaps, of the mind ; v. infr.), Ju 19 ; with ἐπίγειος, δαιμονιώδης , opp. to ἄνωθεν κατερχομένη ( σοψία ), of the mind (Hort, in l.), Ja 3:15 †
G5591.strongs: from G5590 (ψυχή); sensitive, i.e. animate (in distinction on the one hand from G4152 (πνευματικός), which is the higher or renovated nature; and on the other from G5446 (φυσικός), which is the lower or bestial nature)

G3013.thayer: a scale, a scaly substance thrown off from the body.
G3013.mounce: a scale, shell, rind, crust, incrustation, Acts 9:18*

G1195.thayer: I bind, put in chains, tie together.
G1195.mounce: to bind, bind up, as a bundle, Mt. 23:4; to bind, confine, Lk. 8:29; Acts 22:4*
G1195.abbottSmith: to put in chains : Lk 8:29 , Ac 22:4 .
G1195.strongs: from a (presumed) derivative of G1196 (δεσμέω); to be a binder (captor), i.e. to enchain (a prisoner), to tie on (a load)

G280.thayer: not measurable, immeasurable, to a limitless degree.
G280.mounce: without or beyond measure, regardless of measure, 2 Cor. 10:13, 15*
G280.abbottSmith: without measure : adverbially, εἰς τὰ ἄ. , excessively , II Co 10:13 10:15 †
G280.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3358 (μέτρον); immoderate

G4411.thayer: the chief place at a banquet or table.
G4411.mounce: the first place of reclining at table, the most honorable place at table, Mt. 23:6; Mk. 12:39; Lk. 14:7, 8; 20:46
G4411.abbottSmith: the chief place at table (v. Swete, Mk 12:39 ): Mt 23:6 , Mk 12:39 , Lk 14:7 , 8 20:46 .†
G4411.strongs: from G4413 (πρῶτος) and G2828 (κλισία); a reclining first (in the place of honor) at the dinner-bed, i.e. preeminence at meals

G3765.abbottSmith: no longer , no more : Mt 19:6 , Mk 10:8 , Lk 15:19 , Jo 4:42 , Ro 6:9 , Ga 3:25 , Eph 2:19 , He 10:18 , al.; c. neg. (to strengthen the negation), Mt 22:46 , Mk 5:3 , Ac 8:39 , al.
G3765.strongs: from G3756 (οὐ) and G2089 (ἔτι); not yet, no longer
G3765.kjv: after that (not), (not) any more, henceforth (hereafter) not, no longer (more), not as yet (now), now no more (not), yet (not)

G5442.thayer: (a) I guard, protect; mid: I am on my guard, (b) act. and mid. of customs and regulations: I keep, observe.
