G5442.mounce: to be on watch, keep watch, Lk. 2:8; to have in keeping, Acts 20:20; to have in custody, Acts 28:16; to keep under restraint, confine, Lk. 8:29; Acts 12:4; 23:35; to guard, defend, Lk. 11:21; to keep safe, preserve, Jn. 12:25; 17:12; 2 Thess. 3:3; 2 Pet. 2:5; Jude 24; to keep in abstinence, Acts 21:25; 1 Jn. 5:21; to observe a matter of injunction or duty, Mk. 10:20; Lk. 11:28; Acts 7:53; 16:4; 21:24; mid. to be on one’s guard, beware, Lk. 12:15; 2 Tim. 4:15; 2 Pet. 3:17
G5442.strongs: probably from G5443 (φυλή) through the idea of isolation; to watch, i.e. be on guard (literally of figuratively); by implication, to preserve, obey, avoid

G1794.thayer: I wrap up, roll round, envelop.
G1794.mounce: to wrap up in, inwrap, envelope, Mt. 27:59; Lk. 23:53; to wrap up, roll or fold together, Jn. 20:7*
G1794.abbottSmith: to wrap up (LS), roll or coil about ( DCG , ii, 227 a , 507 a ): c. acc et dat., Mt 27:59 ( ἐν , Tr. [WH], cf. similar sentence in π. ; MM, Exp. , xiii), Lk 23:53 ; pass., Jo 20:7 .†
G1794.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (to twist; probably akin to G1507 (εἱλίσσω)); to entwine, i.e. wind up in

G4140.mounce: a filling up; met. gratification, satisfaction, Col. 2:23*
G4140.abbottSmith: a filling up , satiety : πρὸς π. σαρκός (RV, against the indulgence of the flesh ; but ICC , in l., for the full satisfaction of the flesh , op. cit. , 276 ff.), Col 2:23 .†
G4140.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G4130 (πλήθω); a filling up, i.e. (figuratively) gratification

G442.thayer: belonging to human beings (especially as contrasted with God), human (as contrasted with divine).
G442.mounce: human, belonging to man, 1 Cor. 2:4, 13; 4:3; 10:13; Jas. 3:7; 1 Pet. 2:13; suited to man, Rom. 6:19*
G442.abbottSmith: human , belonging to man: χεῖρες , Ac 17:25 ; σοφία , I Co 2:13 ; φύσις , Ja 3:7 ; κτίσις , I Pe 2:13 (MM, VGT , s.v.); ἀ. ἡμέρα , opp. to ἡ ἡμ. ( 3:13 , God's Judgment-Day), human judgment , I Co 4:3 (v. Lft., Notes , 198); πειρασμὸς ἀ. , temptation such as man can bear (AV, such as is common to man , v. Field, Notes , 175), I Co 10:13 ; ἀνθρώπινον λέγω , I speak in human fashion , with words not
G442.kjv: human, common to man, man(-kind), (man-)kind, men's, after the manner of men

G4241.thayer: it becomes, is fitting to, is right.
G4241.mounce: it becomes, it is fitting, it is proper, it is right, etc., and part. πρέπον, becoming, suitable, decorous, etc., Mt. 3:15; 1 Cor. 11:13; Eph. 5:3; 1 Tim. 2:10
G4241.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to tower up (be conspicuous), i.e. (by implication) to be suitable or proper (third person singular present indicative, often used impersonally, it is fit or right)

G1298.mounce: to throw into a state of perturbation, to move or trouble greatly, Lk. 1:29*
G1298.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5015 (ταράσσω); to disturb wholly, i.e. agitate (with alarm)

G5341.abbottSmith: by metath. for παινόλης (also φενόλης, φαινόλιον ; Lat. pænula )
G5341.strongs: by transposition for a derivative probably of G5316 (φαίνω) (as showing outside the other garments); a mantle (surtout)

G5240.thayer: I pour out so that it overflows.
G5240.mounce: to pour out above measure or in excess; pass. to run over, overflow, Lk. 6:38*
G5240.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the alternate form of G1632 (ἐκχέω); to pour out over, i.e. (passively) to overflow

G1544.thayer: I throw (cast, put) out; I banish; I bring forth, produce.
G1544.mounce: pluperfect, ἐκβεβλήκειν, to cast out, eject by force, Mt. 15:17; Acts 27:38; to expel, force away, Lk. 4:29; Acts 7:58; to refuse, Jn. 6:37; to extract, Mt. 7:4; to reject with contempt, despise, contemn, Lk. 6:22; in NT to send forth, send out, Mt. 9:38; Lk. 10:2; to send away, dismiss, Mt. 9:25; Mk. 1:12; met. to spread abroad, Mt. 12:20; to bring out, produce, Mt. 12:35; 13:52
G1544.abbottSmith: to drive, cast or send out, to expel : c. acc rei, mid. ( σῖτον ), seq. εἰς , Ac 27:38 ; pass., Mt 15:17 ; c. acc pers., Mt 21:12 , Mk 11:15 , al.; δαιμόνια , Mt 7:22 , Mk 1:34 , al.; id. seq. ἐκ , Mk 7:26 ; παρά , Mk 16:9 ; ἐν , Mk 3:22 ; ( ἐν ) τ. ὀνόματι , Mt 7:22 , Mk 9:38 ; λόγῳ , Mt 8:16 ; seq. ἔξω , Jo 6:37 ; id. c. gen., Mk 12:8 , al.; of expulsion from home, Ga 4:30 ; from the Church, III
G1544.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G906 (βάλλω); to eject (literally or figuratively)
G1544.kjv: bring forth, cast (forth, out), drive (out), expel, leave, pluck (pull, take, thrust) out, put forth (out), send away (forth, out)

G435.thayer: a male human being; a man, husband.
G435.mounce: a male person of full age and stature, as opposed to a child or female, 1 Cor. 13:11; a husband, Mt. 1:16; a man, human being, individual, Lk. 11:31; used also pleonastically with other nouns and adjectives, Lk. 5:8; Acts 1:16
G435.abbottSmith: a man , Lat. vir. As opp. to a woman, Ac 8:12 , I Ti 2:12 ; as a husband , Mt 1:16 , Jo 4:16 , Ro 7:2 , Tit 1:6 . As opp. to a boy or infant, I Co 13:11 , Eph 4:13 , Ja 3:2 . In appos. with a noun or adj., as ἀ. ἁμαρτωλός , Lk 5:8 ; ἀ. προφήτης , 24:19 ; freq. in terms of address, as ἀ. ἀδελφοί , Ac 1:16 ; and esp. with gentilic names, as ἀ. Ἰουδαῖος , Ac 22:3 ; ἀ. Ἐφέσιοι , 19:35 . In general, a
G435.strongs: a primary word (compare G444 (ἄνθρωπος)); a man (properly as an individual male)

G4068.abbottSmith: to boast or vaunt oneself : I Co 13:4 (elsewh. only in Antonin.; v. Abbott, Essays , 87).†
G4068.strongs: middle voice from (braggart; perhaps by reduplication of the base of G4008 (πέραν)); to boast

G20.thayer: wild joy, ecstatic delight, exultation, exhilaration.
G20.abbottSmith: exultation, exuberant joy : Lk 1:44 , Ac 2:46 , He 1:9 , Ju 24 ; χαρὰ καὶ ἀ. , Lk 1:14 (Cremer, 592).†

G2633.abbottSmith: condemnation : II Co 3:9 7:3 (cf. Deiss., LAE , 91 f.).†

G3549.thayer: (a) I ordain, lay down, give the sanction of law to, enact, (b) I base legally, regulate, direct.
G3549.mounce: to impose a law, give laws; in NT pass., to have a law imposed on one’s self, receive a law, Heb. 7:11; to be enacted, constituted, Heb. 8:6*
G3549.abbottSmith: intrans., to make laws ; pass., to be furnished with laws : He 7:11 .
G3549.strongs: from G3550 (νομοθέτης); to legislate, i.e. (passively) to have (the Mosaic) enactments injoined, be sanctioned (by them)

G2961.mounce: to be lord over, to be possessed of, mastery over, Rom. 6:9, 14; 7:1; 14:9; 2 Cor. 1:24; 1 Tim. 6:15; to exercise control over, Lk. 22:25*
G2961.abbottSmith: to be lord or master of , to rule ( over ): c. gen. obj., Lk 22:25 , Ro 14:9 , II Co 1:24 ; absol., I Ti 6:15 ; metaph., ὁ θάνατος , Ro 6:9 ; ἡ ἁμαρτία , Ro 6:14 ; ὁ νόμος , Ro 7:1 (cf. κατα-κυριεύω ).†
G2961.kjv: have dominion over, lord, be lord of, exercise lordship over

G2589.thayer: a knower of the inner life (character, hearts).
G2589.strongs: from G2588 (καρδία) and G1097 (γινώσκω); a heart-knower

G731.thayer: not to be uttered (because too sacred), secret, unspeakable, unspoken.
G731.mounce: pr. not spoken; what ought not to be spoken, secret; which cannot be spoken or uttered, 2 Cor. 12:4*
G731.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the same as G4490 (ῥητῶς); unsaid, i.e. (by implication) inexpressible

G115.abbottSmith: a disannulling , setting aside : He 7:18 9:26 (for similar usage in π. , v. Deiss., BS , 228 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G1938.mounce: one who has an ardent desire for anything, 1 Cor. 10:6*
G1938.abbottSmith: one longing for, lustful after : κακῶν , I Co 10:6 .†

G3694.mounce: can function as an improper prep., behind, after, at one’s back, Mt. 4:10; Lk. 7:38; Rev. 1:10; τὰ ὀπίσω, the things which are behind, Phil. 3:13; ὀπίσω and εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω, back, backwards, Mt. 24:18; Mk. 13:16; Lk. 9:62, when an improper prep., takes the gen.
G3694.abbottSmith: prop., as in cl., adv., of time (not in NT); of place, back , behind , after : Mt 24:18 , Lk 7:38 ; τὰ ὀ. , Phl 3:13 ; εἰς τὰ ὀ. , Mk 13:16 , Lk 9:62 17:31 , Jo 6:66 18:6 20:14 .
G3694.strongs: from the same as G3693 (ὄπισθεν) with enclitic of direction; to the back, i.e. aback (as adverb or preposition of time or place; or as noun)

G4890.mounce: a running together, forming a mob, Acts 21:30*
G4890.abbottSmith: a concourse , esp. of a riotous gathering: Ac 21:30 (Arist., Polyb., al.).†
G4890.strongs: from (the alternate of) G4936 (συντρέχω); a running together, i.e. (riotous) concourse

G3457.abbottSmith: of a mill : λίθος μ. , Lk 17:2 .†
G3457.strongs: from G3458 (μύλος); belonging to a mill

G2752.thayer: a word of command, a call, an arousing outcry.
G2752.mounce: a word of command; a mutual cheer; hence, in NT a loud shout, an arousing outcry, 1 Thess. 4:16*
G2752.abbottSmith: a call , summons , shout of command : I Th 4:16 (v. M, Th. , in l.).†
G2752.strongs: from G2753 (κελεύω); a cry of incitement

G5407.mounce: to put to death, kill, stay, Mt. 23:31, 35; absol. to commit murder, Mt. 5:21
G5407.abbottSmith: to kill, murder . absol., Mt 5:21 , Ja 4:2 ; μὴ ( οὐ ) φονεύσῃς ( -εις ), Mt 5:21 19:18 , Mk 10:19 , Lk 18:20 , Ro 13:9 , Ja 2:11 (all from Ex 20:13 , 14 ); c. acc., Mt 23:31 , 35 , Ja 5:6 .†
G5407.strongs: from G5406 (φονεύς); to be a murderer (of)

G4976.mounce: fashion, form; fashion, external show, 1 Cor. 7:31; Phil. 2:7*
G4976.strongs: from the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω); a figure (as a mode or circumstance), i.e. (by implication) external condition

G4044.thayer: I put on a spit; met: I pierce, wound deeply.
G4044.mounce: to put on a spit, transfix; met. to pierce, wound deeply, 1 Tim. 6:10*
G4044.abbottSmith: to put on a spit , hence, to pierce : metaph., ἑαυτὸν . . . ὀδύναις , I Ti 6:10 .†
G4044.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and the base of G4008 (πέραν); to penetrate entirely, i.e. transfix (figuratively)

G2602.thayer: (a) foundation, (b) depositing, sowing, deposit, technically used of the act of conception.
G2602.mounce: pr. a casting down; laying the foundation, foundation; beginning, commencement, Mt. 13:35; 25:34; conception in the womb, Heb. 11:11
G2602.abbottSmith: a laying down : εἰς κ. σπέρματος , He 11:11 (EV, to conceive seed ).
G2602.strongs: from G2598 (καταβάλλω); a deposition, i.e. founding; figuratively, conception

G4815.thayer: I seize, apprehend, assist, conceive, become pregnant.
G4815.mounce: to catch; to seize, apprehend, Mt. 26:55; Acts 1:16; to catch, as prey, Lk. 5:9; to conceive, become pregnant, Lk. 1:24, 31, 36; 2:21; met. Jas. 1:15; mid. to help, aid, assist, Lk. 5:7; Phil. 4:3
G4815.abbottSmith: c. acc., to bring together, collect .
G4815.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to clasp, i.e. seize (arrest, capture); specially, to conceive (literally or figuratively); by implication, to aid

G3354.mounce: to allot, measure, Mt. 7:2; Mk. 4:24; Lk. 6:38; Rev. 11:1, 2; 21:15-17; met. to estimate, 2 Cor. 10:12*
G3354.abbottSmith: to measure , of space, number, value, etc.: c. acc. rei, Re 11:2 21:15 , 17 ; c. dat. instr., Re 11:1 21:16 . Metaph., ἑαυτὸν ἐν ἑαυτῷ , II Co 10:12 .
G3354.strongs: from G3358 (μέτρον); to measure (i.e. ascertain in size by a fixed standard); by implication, to admeasure (i.e. allot by rule)

G4019.mounce: pluperfect, περιεδέδετο (pass., 3 sg), to bind round about; pass. to be bound around, be bound up, Jn. 11:44*
G4019.abbottSmith: to tie round , bind round : c. acc. et dat., pass., Jo 11:44 .†
G4019.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G1210 (δέω); to bind around one, i.e. enwrap

G4202.mounce: fornication, whoredom, Mt. 15:19; Mk. 7:21; Acts 15:20, 29; concubinage, Jn. 8:41; adultery, Mt. 5:32; 19:9; incest, 1 Cor. 5:1; lewdness, uncleanness, genr., Rom. 1:29; from the Hebrew, put symbolically for idolatry, Rev. 2:21; 14:8
G4202.abbottSmith: fornication : Ac 15:20 , 29 21:25 , I Co 5:1 6:13 , 18 , II Co 12:21 , Ga 5:19 , Eph 5:3 , Col 3:5 , I Th 4:3 (Lft., Notes , 53), Re 9:21 ; pl. (v. WM, 220; Bl., § 32, 6), I Co 7:2 ; disting. from μοιχεία , Mt 15:19 , Mk 7:21 ; = μοιχεία ( Am 8:17 , Si 23:23 , al.), Mt 5:32 19:9 . Metaph. (of idolatry: De 23:2 , Ho 1:2 ): Jo 8:41 (Westc., in l.), and so perh. (Thayer-Grimm, s.v.; but v. Swete, in
G4202.strongs: from G4203 (πορνεύω); harlotry (including adultery and incest); figuratively, idolatry

G3564.abbottSmith: Nympha (v. M, Pr. , 48): Col 4:15 , WH, R, mg.†
G3564.strongs: probably contracted for a compound of G3565 (νύμφη) and G1435 (δῶρον); nymph-given (i.e. -born); Nymphas, a Christian

G5322.abbottSmith: a torch or lantern (v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 131 f.): Jo 18:3 .†
G5322.strongs: from G5316 (φαίνω); a lightener, i.e. light; lantern

G665.mounce: mid: to turn any one away from a thing; mid. to turn one’s self away from any one; to avoid, shun, 2 Tim. 3:5*
G665.abbottSmith: to turn away ; mid., to turn oneself away from : c. acc., II Ti 3:5 .†
G665.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to deflect, i.e. (reflexively) avoid

G4849.mounce: a drinking together; a feast, banquet; a festive company; in NT, pl. συμπόσια, eating party, Mk. 6:39 (2x)*
G4849.strongs: neuter of a derivative of the alternate of G4844 (συμπίνω); a drinking-party ("symposium"), i.e. (by extension) a room of guests

G5293.thayer: I place under, subject to; mid, pass: I submit, put myself into subjection.
G5293.mounce: to place or arrange under; to subordinate, 1 Cor. 15:27; to bring under influence, Rom. 8:20; pass. to be subordinated, 1 Cor. 14:32; to be brought under a state or influence, Rom. 8:20; mid. to submit one’s self, render obedience, be submissive, Lk. 2:51; 10:17
G5293.abbottSmith: as a military term, to place or rank under (Polyb.).
G5293.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5021 (τάσσω); to subordinate; reflexively, to obey
G5293.kjv: be under obedience (obedient), put under, subdue unto, (be, make) subject (to, unto), be (put) in subjection (to, under), submit self unto

G4706.strongs: neuter of G4707 (σπουδαιότερος) as adverb; more earnestly than others), i.e. very promptly

G2658.thayer: (a) I come down, either from high land to lower (or actually to the sea-coast), or from the high seas to the coast; hence met: I arrive at, reach (my destination), (b) of property: I come down (descend) by inheritance to an heir.
G2658.mounce: to come to, arrive at, Acts 16:1; 20:15; of an epoch, to come upon, 1 Cor. 10:11; met. to reach, attain to, Acts 26:7
G2658.abbottSmith: to come to , arrive at : seq. εἰς , c. acc. loc., Ac 16:1 18:19 , 24 21:7 25:13 (v. M, Pr. , 132) 27:12 28:13 ; seq. ἀντικρύ , c. gen., Ac 20:15 . Metaph., seq. εἰς , c. acc. pers., I Co 10:11 14:36 ; id. c. acc. rei, to attain to : Ac 26:7 , Eph 4:13 , Phl 3:11 .†
G2658.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to meet against, i.e. arrive at (literally or figuratively)

G1670.thayer: I drag, draw, pull, persuade, unsheathe.
G1670.abbottSmith: to draw : c. acc rei, Jo 18:10 21:6 ; c. acc pers., seq. ἔξω , Ac 21:30 ; εἰς , Ac 16:19 , Ja 2:6 . Metaph., to draw, lead, impel : Jo 6:44 12:32 . (For discussion of ἕ. in Oxyrh. Log. , v. Deiss., LAE , 437 ff.)†
G1670.strongs: probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); to drag (literally or figuratively)

G1046a.abbottSmith: Gerasene , of Gerasa , not the G. of Decapolis, 30 m. S.E. of the Lake, but a Gerasa or Gergesa (perh. the mod. Kersa ) on E. shore; as subst., pl. οἱ Γ. , Mk 5:1 , Lk 8:26 , 37 ( Γεργεσηνῶν , RV, mg. 1 ; Γαδαρηνῶν , RV, mg. 2 ).†

G2595.thayer: a dry stalk, chip of wood, twig, splinter, chaff.
G2595.mounce: any small dry thing, as chaff, stubble, splinter; Mt. 7:3, 4, 5; Lk. 6:41, 42*
G2595.abbottSmith: a small dry stalk , a twig ; metaph., of a minor fault: Mt 7:3-5 , Lk 6:41 , 42 .†
G2595.strongs: from (to wither); a dry twig or straw

G334.thayer: an offering dedicated (hung up in a temple) by a worshipper; a gift or offering consecrated to God.
G334.mounce: a gift or offering consecrated to God, Lk. 21:5*
G334.abbottSmith: a gift set up in a temple, a votive offering : Lk 21:5 (LT, -θεμα ).†
G334.strongs: from G394 (ἀνατίθεμαι) (like G331 (ἀνάθεμα), but in a good sense); a votive offering

G1572.mounce: pass., to blaze out, to be inflamed, Rom. 1:27*
G1572.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2545 (καίω); to inflame deeply

G4756.thayer: an army; met: a host of angels, the hosts of heaven (i.e. the stars).
G4756.mounce: an army, host; from the Hebrew, στρατιὰ οὐράνιος, or τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, the heavenly host, the host of heaven, the hosts of angels, Lk. 2:13; the stars, Acts 7:42*
G4756.abbottSmith: = στρατός , an army, a host : of angels ( III Ki 22:19 , al.), Lk 2:13 ; of the stars of heaven ( Je 8:2 , II Ch 33:3 ), Ac 7:42 .
G4756.strongs: feminine of a derivative of (an army; from the base of G4766 (στρώννυμι), as encamped); camp-likeness, i.e. an army, i.e. (figuratively) the angels, the celestial luminaries

G3460.thayer: Myra, a port in Lycia, south-west Asia Minor.
G3460.mounce: neuter plural, Myra, a city of Lycia, Acts 27:5*
G3460.abbottSmith: Myra , a city of Lycia: Ac 27:5 .†
G3460.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Myra, a place in Asia Minor

G1629.abbottSmith: to frighten away, terrif y: c. acc pers., II Co 10:9 .†
G1629.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5399 (φοβέω); to frighten utterly

G2305.mounce: divinity, deity, godhead, divine majesty, Rom. 1:20*
G2305.abbottSmith: divine nature , divinity : Ro 1:20 (for ex. from π. , v. MM, Exp. , xv).†

G3752.mounce: when, whenever, Mt. 5:11; 6:2; Mk. 3:11; Rev. 4:9, et al. freq.; in NT in case of, on occasion of, Jn. 9:5; 1 Cor. 15:27; Heb. 1:6
G3752.abbottSmith: prop., whenever ; c. subjc. praes.: Mt 6:2 , 5 , Mk 14:7 , Lk 11:36 , Jo 7:27 , Ac 23:35 , I Co 3:4 , al.; ἕως τ. ἡμέρας ἐκείνης, ὅ. , Mt 26:29 , Mk 14:25 ; seq. τότε , I Th 5:3 ; c. subjc. aor. (M, Pr. , 185): Mt 5:11 , Mk 4:15 , Lk 6:22 , Jo 2:10 8:28 (Field, Notes , 94), 10:4 , al.
G3752.strongs: from G3753 (ὅτε) and G302 (ἄν); whenever (implying hypothesis or more or less uncertainty); also causatively (conjunctionally) inasmuch as
G3752.kjv: as long (soon) as, that, + till, when(-soever), while

G223.thayer: Alexander, a name of Greek origin, (a) son of Simon of Cyrene, (b) the High Priest's kinsman, (c) an Ephesian Jew, (d) the coppersmith.
G223.mounce: Alexander, pr. name. (1) The High Priest’s kinsman, Acts 4:6. (2) A Jew of Ephesus, Acts 19:33. (3) The coppersmith, 1 Tim. 1:20; 2 Tim. 4:14. (4) Son of Simon of Cyrene, Mk. 15:21*
G223.abbottSmith: Alexander Son of Simon of Cyrene: Mk 15:21 A kinsman of the High Priest: Ac 4:6 . A certain Jew: Ac 19:33 . A coppersmith: I Ti 1:20 . Perh. = 4 (v. Ellic. on I Ti 1:20 ): II Ti 4:14 .†
G223.strongs: from the same as (the first part of) G220 (ἀλέκτωρ) and G435 (ἀνήρ); man-defender; Alexander, the name of three Israelites and one other man

G4186.thayer: of great value, very costly, very precious.
G4186.mounce: of great price, costly, precious, Mt. 13:46; Jn. 12:3; 1 Pet. 1:7*
G4186.strongs: from G4183 (πολύς) and G5092 (τιμή); extremely valuable

G795.thayer: I miss the mark, miss my aim, make a false aim, fail.
G795.mounce: pr. to miss the mark; met. to err, deviate, swerve from, 1 Tim. 1:6; 6:21; 2 Tim. 2:18*
G795.abbottSmith: to miss the mark , fail : c. gen., I Ti 1:6 (so in π. , MM, s.v.); seq. περί , I Ti 6:21 , II Ti 2:18 .†
G795.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and (an aim); to miss the mark, i.e. (figuratively) deviate from truth

G566.mounce: trans. to have in full what is due or is sought, Mt. 6:2, 5, 16; Lk. 6:24; Phil. 4:18; to have altogether, Phlm. 15; hence, it is enough, Mk. 14:41; intrans. to be distant, Lk. 7:6; to be estranged, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6, mid. to abstain from, Acts 14:20
G566.strongs: third person singular present indicative active of G568 (ἀπέχω) used impersonally; it is sufficient

G893.mounce: free from falsehood; incapable of falsehood, Tit. 1:2*
G893.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5579 (ψεῦδος); veracious

G1812.strongs: plural ordinal from G1803 (ἕξ) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); six hundred

G485.mounce: contradiction, question, Heb. 6:16; 7:7; opposition, rebellion, Jude 11; hostility, Heb. 12:3*
G485.abbottSmith: gain-saying , strife (the latter sense being found in π. ; v. MM, VGT , s.v.; cf. Field, Notes , 106): He 6:16 7:7 12:3 , Ju 11 .†
G485.strongs: from a derivative of G483 (ἀντιλέγω); dispute, disobedience

G4041.abbottSmith: one's own , of one's own possession : λαὸς π. (cf. LXX, Ex 19:5 23:22 , De 7:6 14:2 26:18 ), Tit 2:14 .†
G4041.strongs: from the present participle feminine of a compound of 4012 and G1510 (εἰμί); being beyond usual, i.e. special (one's own)

G3417.mounce: remembrance, recollection, Phil. 1:3; 1 Thess. 3:6; 2 Tim. 1:3; mention; μνείαν ποιεῖσθαι, to make mention, Rom. 1:9; Eph. 1:16; 1 Thess. 1:2; Phlm. 4*
G3417.abbottSmith: remembrance , mention (= μνήμη ): Phl 1:3 ; μ. ποιεῖσθαι , c. gen. pers., Ro 1:9 , Eph 1:16 , I Th 1:2 , Phm 4 (cf. Ps 110 (111):4 ); μ. ἔχειν , c. gen. pers., I Th 3:6 , II Ti 1:3 (on the v.l. in Ro 12:13 , v. ICC , in l.; Field, Notes , 163).†
G3417.strongs: from G3415 (μνάομαι) or G3403 (μιμνήσκω); recollection; by implication, recital

G4457.mounce: a hardening; met. hardness of heart, callousness, insensibility, Mk. 3:5; Rom. 11:25; Eph. 4:18*
G4457.abbottSmith: a covering with a callus , a hardening : metaph., Mk 3:6 , Ro 11:25 , Eph 4:18 .†

G1606.thayer: (lit: I breathe out), I breathe my last, expire.
G1606.mounce: to breathe out; to expire, die, Mk. 15:37, 39; Lk. 23:46*
G1606.abbottSmith: to breathe out ; sc. βίον , ψυχήν (expressed in cl., Æsch., al.; cf. LS, s.v.), to breathe one's last, expire : Mk 15:37 , 39 Lk 23:46 . For force of aorist, v. Swete, Mk 15:37 .†
G1606.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4154 (πνέω); to expire

G1624.thayer: (lit: I turn out from); mid. and pass: I turn aside (from the right road), wander, forsake, and with an object: I remove from myself, shun, avoid.
G1624.mounce: mid. and pass., to turn out or aside, Heb. 12:13; to turn aside or away, swerve, 1 Tim. 1:6; 5:15; 2 Tim. 4:4; to turn from, avoid, 1 Tim. 6:20*
G1624.abbottSmith: to turn out of the course, turn aside , c. acc Pass., with middle sense, intrans., to turn aside : He 12:13 (R, txt., for be put out of joint , R, mg., v. Thayer, s.v. Westc., in l); fig., seq. εἰς , I Ti 1:6 ; ἐπί , II Ti 4:4 ; ὀπίσω , I Ti 5:15 ; c. acc, to shun, avoid : I Ti 6:20 .†
G1624.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to deflect, i.e. turn away (literally or figuratively)
G1624.kjv: avoid, turn (aside, out of the way)

G1613.mounce: to disturb, disquiet, throw into confusion, Acts 16:20*
G1613.abbottSmith: to throw into great trouble, agitate : Ac 16:20 .†
G1613.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5015 (ταράσσω); to disturb wholly

G1682.strongs: of Chaldean origin (H0426 with pronominal suffix) my God

G139.thayer: a self-chosen opinion, a religious or philosophical sect, discord or contention.
G139.mounce: strictly, a choice or option; hence, a religious sect, faction; by implication, discord, contention
G139.strongs: from G138 (αἱρέομαι); properly, a choice, i.e. (specially) a party or (abstractly) disunion
G139.kjv: heresy (which is the Greek word itself), sect

G2444.mounce: Why is it that? For what reason? Why? Mt. 9:4; 27:46; Lk. 13:7; Acts 4:25; 7:26; 1 Cor. 10:29*
G2444.strongs: from G2443 (ἵνα) and G5101 (τίς); for what reason ?, i.e. why?

G349.mounce: to cry aloud, exclaim, shout, Mk. 1:23; 6:49; Lk. 4:33; 8:28; 23:18*
G349.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2896 (κράζω); to scream up (aloud)

G1558.thayer: avenging, subst: an avenger (the word occurs frequently in the sense of a special advocate [champion] of a city).
G1558.mounce: an avenger, one who inflicts punishment, Rom. 13:4; 1 Thess. 4:6*
G1558.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1349 (δίκη); carrying justice out, i.e. a punisher

G4856.thayer: I agree with, harmonize with, agree together.
G4856.mounce: to sound together, to be in unison, be in accord; trop. to agree with, accord with in purport, Acts 15:15; to harmonize with, suit with, Lk. 5:36; to agree with, make an agreement, Mt. 18:19; 20:2, 13; Acts 5:9*
G4856.abbottSmith: prop., to agree in sound, be in harmony (Plat., Arist.). Metaph.,
G4856.strongs: from G4859 (σύμφωνος); to be harmonious, i.e. (figuratively) to accord (be suitable, concur) or stipulate (by compact)

G4625.thayer: a snare, stumbling-block, cause for error.
G4625.mounce: pr. a trap-spring; also genr. a stumbling block, anything against which one stumbles, an impediment; met. a cause of ruin, destruction, misery, etc., Rom. 9:33; 11:9; 1 Pet. 2:8; a cause or occasion of sinning, Mt. 16:23; 18:7 (3x); Lk. 17:1; Rom. 14:13; 16:17; Rev. 2:14; scandal, offense, cause of indignation, Mt. 13:41; 1 Cor. 1:23; Gal. 5:11; 1 Jn. 2:10*
G4625.abbottSmith: prop., the bait-stick of a trap, a snare, stumbling-block ( Le 19:14 , Jth 5:1 ): fig., Ro 9:33 , I Pe 2:8 ( Is 8:14 ; aliter in LXX; στερεὸν σκανδάλου in Aq.). Metaph., of that which causes error or sin;
G4625.strongs: ("scandal"); probably from a derivative of G2578 (κάμπτω); a trap-stick (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)
G4625.kjv: occasion to fall (of stumbling), offence, thing that offends, stumblingblock

G3927.thayer: residing in a strange country; subst: a stranger, sojourner.
G3927.mounce: residing in a country not one’s own, a sojourner, stranger, Heb. 11:13; 1 Pet. 1:1; 2:11*
G3927.abbottSmith: sojourning in a strange place ; as subst., ὁ π. , a sojourner : of Christians, I Pe 1:1 ; ξένοι καὶ π. , He 11:13 ; πάροικοι καὶ π. , I Pe 2:11 (v. Deiss., BS , 149).†
G3927.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the base of G1927 (ἐπιδημέω); an alien alongside, i.e. a resident foreigner

G2108.thayer: good action, well-doing, benefiting, kind service.
G2108.mounce: well-doing, a good deed, benefit conferred, Acts 4:9; duty, good offices, 1 Tim. 6:2*
G2108.abbottSmith: a good deed, kindness, benefit : I Ti 6:2 ; c. gen. pers. ( εὐ. πόλεως , Plat., leg. , 805 b ), Ac 4:9 ,†
G2108.strongs: from G2110 (εὐεργέτης); beneficence (genitive case or specially)

G3867.abbottSmith: to exhort , advise : c. acc. pers. et inf. (v. Bl., § 72, 5; M, Pr. , 205), Ac 27:22 ; absol., seq. λέγων , Ac 27:9 .†
G3867.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G134 (αἰνέω); to mispraise, i.e. recommend or advise (a different course)

G1920.thayer: I arise, spring up, arrive, come on.
G1920.mounce: to come on, spring up, as the wind, Acts 28:13*
G1920.abbottSmith: to arrive, arise, come on : Ac 28:13 .†
G1920.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1096 (γίνομαι); to arrive upon, i.e. spring up (as a wind)

G1760.thayer: I meditate upon, reflect upon, ponder.
G1760.mounce: to ponder in one’s mind, think of, meditate on, Mt. 1:20; 9:4*
G1760.abbottSmith: to reflect on, ponder : c. acc rei, Mt 1:20 9:4 .†
G1760.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G2372 (θυμός); to be inspirited, i.e. ponder

G1205.thayer: come hither, come, hither, an exclamatory word.
G1205.mounce: come, Mt. 4:19; 11:28; as a particle of exhortation, incitement, etc., and followed by an imperative, come now, etc., Mt. 21:38; 28:6
G1205.abbottSmith: come on! come here! come! : c. imperat., Mt 25:34 28:6 , Jo 4:29 21:12 , Re 19:17 ; c. subjc., Mt 21:38 , Mk 12:7 ; seq. ὀπίσω , Mt 4:19 , Mk 1:17 ; πρός , Mt 11:28 ; εἰς , Mt 22:4 , Mk 6:31 .†
G1205.strongs: from G1204 (δεῦρο) and an imperative form of (to go); come hither!

G2886.thayer: earthly, worldly (belonging to the present earthly world as opposed to the heavenly and future).
G2886.mounce: pr. belonging to the universe, in NT accommodated to the present state of things, adapted to this world, worldly, Tit. 2:12; τὸ κοσμικόν, as a subst. the apparatus for the service of the tabernacle, Heb. 9:1*
G2886.abbottSmith: pertaining to the world or universe .
G2886.strongs: from G2889 (κόσμος) (in its secondary sense); terrene ("cosmic"), literally (mundane) or figuratively (corrupt)

G4853.thayer: one of the same tribe, a fellow country-man.
G4853.mounce: pr. one of the same tribe; a fellow citizen, fellow countryman, 1 Thess. 2:14*
G4853.abbottSmith: a fellow-tribesman, fellow-countryman : I Th 2:14 (v. M, Th. , in l., and Intr. liii). Not found elsewhere.†
G4853.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5443 (φυλή); a co-tribesman, i.e. native of the same country

G2525.thayer: I set down, bring down to a place; I set in order, appoint, make, constitute.
G2525.mounce: also formed as καθιστάνω, to place, set, Jas. 3:6; to set, constitute, appoint, Mt. 24:45, 47; Lk. 12:14; to set down in a place, conduct, Acts 17:15; to make, render, or cause to be, 2 Pet. 1:8; pass. to be rendered, Rom. 5:19
G2525.abbottSmith: to set down ; to bring down to a place (Hom., Xen., al.; II Ch 28:15 , I Ki 5:3 ): Ac 17:15 .
G2525.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to place down (permanently), i.e. (figuratively) to designate, constitute, convoy
G2525.kjv: appoint, be, conduct, make, ordain, set

G5154.mounce: third, Mt. 20:3; 27:64; ἐκ τρίτου, the third time, for the third time, Mt. 26:44; τὸ τρίτον, i.e. μέρος, the third part, Rev. 8:7, 12;
G5154.abbottSmith: the third : Mt 22:26 , Mk 12:21 15:25 , Lk 24:21 , Ac 2:15 , al.; τῇ τ. ἡμέρᾳ (i.e. the next day but one : Xen., al.; v. Field, Notes , 11 ff.), Mt 16:21 , Lk 24:46 , al.; substantively, acc. masc., τρίτον , a third (servant), Lk 20:12 ; neut., τὸ τ. , c. gen., the third part of : Re 8:7-12 9:15 , 18 12:4 . As adv., τὸ τ. , the third time : Mk 14:41 , Jo 21:17 ; anarth., τρίτον , a third time, Lk
G5154.strongs: ordinal from G5140 (τρεῖς); third; neuter (as noun) a third part, or (as adverb) a (or the) third time, thirdly

G1506.mounce: pr. that which being viewed in the sunshine is found clear and pure; met. spotless, sincere, ingenuous, Phil. 1:10; 2 Pet. 3:1*
G1506.abbottSmith: unalloyed, pure (Lat. sincerus ; v. DCG , ii, 635 a ); of unmixed substances; of abstract ideas; of ethical purity: Phl 1:10 , II Pe 3:1 .†
G1506.strongs: from (the sun's ray) and G2919 (κρίνω); judged by sunlight, i.e. tested as genuine (figuratively)

G1246.mounce: to maintain discussion strenuously and thoroughly, to totally refute, Acts 18:28*
G1246.strongs: middle voice from G1223 (διά) and a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G1651 (ἐλέγχω); to prove downright, i.e. confute

G4281.thayer: I go forward, go on, advance; I go before, precede.
G4281.mounce: to go forwards, advance, proceed, Mt. 26:39; Mk. 14:35; Acts 12:10; to precede, go before any one, Lk. 22:47; to precede in time, be a forerunner or precursor, Lk. 1:17; to outgo, outstrip in going, Mk. 6:33; to travel in advance of any one, precede, 20:5, 13; 2 Cor. 9:5*
G4281.abbottSmith: to go forward , go on , advance : seq. μικρόν , Mt 26:39 and Mk 14:35 (WH, mg., προσελθών ): ῥύμην μίαν (cf. Plat., Rep. , i, 328e), Ac 12:10 .
G4281.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι) (including its alternate); to go onward, precede (in place or time)
G4281.kjv: go before (farther, forward), outgo, pass on

G1265.mounce: to continue throughout; to continue, be permanent or unchanged, Lk. 1:22; Gal. 2:5; Heb. 1:11; 2 Pet. 3:4; to continue, remain constant, Lk. 22:28
G1265.abbottSmith: to remain, continue : Lk 1:22 22:28 , Ga 2:5 , He 1:11 (LXX) , II Pe 3:4 .†
G1265.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3306 (μένω); to stay constantly (in being or relation)

G5282.mounce: to suspect; to suppose, deem, Acts 13:25; 25:18; 27:27*
G5282.abbottSmith: to suspect, conjecture : Ac 25:18 ; c. acc et inf., Ac 13:25 27:27 .†
G5282.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3539 (νοιέω); to think under (privately), i.e. to surmise or conjecture

G2273.mounce: whether, with an elevated tone, Rom. 6:16*
G2273.strongs: from G2228 (ἤ) and G5104 (τοί); either indeed

G15.thayer: I do that which is good.
G15.mounce: to do good, do well; to do what is morally correct (1 Pet. 2:15, 20)
G15.abbottSmith: to do good ; univ.: I Pe 2:15 , 20 , 3:6 , 17 , III Jo 11 ; for another's benefit: Mk 3:4 (T, ἀγαθὸν ποιῆσαι ), Lk 6:9 ; acc. pers., Lk 6:33 , 35 (Cremer, 8).†
G15.strongs: from G17 (ἀγαθοποιός); to be a well-doer (as a favor or a duty)

G3379.thayer: lest at any time, lest; then weakened: whether perhaps, whether at all; in a principal clause: perhaps.
G3379.mounce: can function as an adverb, BDAG 648, lists it as a negative part., conj., and interrogative part., same signif. and usage as μή, Mt. 4:6; 13:15; Heb. 9:17; also, whether, Lk. 3:15
G3379.abbottSmith: As neg. particle, never : He 9:17 (R, mg., but v. infr.; WH, txt., μή τότε ).
G3379.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G4218 (ποτέ); not ever; also if (or lest) ever (or perhaps)
G3379.kjv: if peradventure, lest (at any time, haply), not at all, whether or not

G5066.thayer: of the fourth day, four days since.
G5066.abbottSmith: of or on the fourth day : τ. εἶναι (Hdt., τ. γενέσθαι ), to be four days dead , Jo 11:39 .†
G5066.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες); pertaining to the fourth day

G3425.abbottSmith: with toil or difficulty , hardly : Lk 9:39 ( μόγις , WH).†
G3425.strongs: adverb from a primary (toil); with difficulty

G2478.thayer: strong (originally and generally of physical strength); mighty, powerful, vehement, sure.
G2478.mounce: strong, mighty, robust, Mt. 12:29; Lk. 11:21; powerful, mighty, 1 Cor. 1:27; 4:10; 1 Jn. 2:14; strong, fortified, Rev. 18:10; vehement, Mt. 14:20; energetic, 2 Cor. 10:10; sure, firm, Heb. 6:18
G2478.abbottSmith: strong , mighty , powerful ; of persons, as to body or spirit: Mt 12:29 , Mk 3:27 , Lk 11:21 , 22 , I Co 4:10 (opp. to ἀσθενής ), He 11:34 ( ἐν πολέμῳ ), Re 5:2 10:1 18:21 19:18 ; οἱ πλούσιοι κ. οἱ ἰ. , Re 6:15 ; τὰ ἰ. = οἱ ἰ. (cf. IV Ki 24:15 ), I Co 1:27 ; of God (cf. De 10:17 ), Re 18:8 ; compar., Mt 3:11 , Mk 1:7 , Lk 11:22 , I Co 1:25 ( τὸ ἀσθενὲς τ. θεοῦ ) 10:22 ; of things: λιμός , Lk 15:14
G2478.kjv: boisterous, mighty(-ier), powerful, strong(-er, man), valiant

G2191.mounce: a viper, poisonous serpent, Acts 28:3; used also fig. of persons, Mt. 3:7; 12:34; 23:33; Lk. 3:7*
G2191.abbottSmith: a viper : Ac 28:3 ; metaph., γεννήματα ἐχιδνῶν , Mt 3:7 12:34 23:33 , Lk 3:7 .†
G2191.strongs: of uncertain origin; an adder or other poisonous snake (literally or figuratively)

G4953.abbottSmith: a fixed sign or signal : Mk 14:44 . (Strab., Plut., al.; condemned by Atticists, v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 492 f.) †
G4953.strongs: neuter of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); a sign in common, i.e. preconcerted signal

G1531.thayer: I journey in(to), I go in(to), enter, intervene.
G1531.mounce: to go or come in, enter, Mk. 1:21; 5:40; to come to, visit, Acts 28:30; to be put in, Mt. 15:17; Mk. 7:15, 18, 19; to intervene, Mk. 4:19
G1531.abbottSmith: to go into, enter : Lk 8:16 11:33 19:30 ; seq. εἰς , Mk 1:21 6:56 11:2 , Lk 22:10 , Ac 3:2 ; πρός , c. acc pers., Ac 28:30 ; ὅπου , Mk 5:40 ; κατά τ. οἴκους , house after house , Ac 8:3 ; of things (food), Mt 15:17 , Mk 7:15 , 18 , 19 . μετά (cf. εἰσέρχομαι ), Ac 9:28 .†
G1531.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to enter (literally or figuratively)

G4348.thayer: a stumbling-block, an occasion for falling, a moral embarrassment.
G4348.mounce: a stumbling, Rom. 9:32, 33; 1 Pet. 2:8; met. a stumbling block, an occasion of sinning, means of inducing to sin, Rom. 14:13; 1 Cor. 8:9; met. a moral stumbling, a shock to the moral or religious sense, a moral embarrassment, Rom. 14:20*
G4348.abbottSmith: a stumble , stumbling : λίθος προσκόμματος (= אֶבֶן נֶגֶף , Is 8:14 ), fig., Ro 9:32 , 33 , I Pe 2:8 (LXX) ;
G4348.strongs: from G4350 (προσκόπτω); a stub, i.e. (figuratively) occasion of apostasy

G2988.abbottSmith: splendidly : of sumptuous fare (as freq.; cf. ἐδέσματα λαμπρά , Si 29:22 ), Lk 16:19 .†
G2988.strongs: adverb from G2986 (λαμπρός); brilliantly, i.e. figuratively, luxuriously

G2410.thayer: Jericho, a city a little north of the Dead Sea.
G2410.mounce: Jericho, a city of Palestine, Mt. 20:29; Mk. 10:46; Lk. 10:30; 18:35; 19:1; Heb. 11:30*. See PhotoGuide.
G2410.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03405); Jericho, a place in Palestine

G2519.mounce: pr. a guide, leader; in NT a teacher, instructor, Mt. 23:10*
G2519.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); a guide, i.e. (figuratively) a teacher

G507.thayer: up, above, up to the top, up to the brim, things above, heaven, the heavenly region.
G507.mounce: above, Acts 2:19; Gal. 4:26; Col. 3:1; up, upwards, Jn. 11:41; ὁ, ἡ, τό, ἄνω, that which is above, Jn. 8:23; ἕως ἄνω, to the top
G507.strongs: adverb from G473 (ἀντί); upward or on the top

G2347.mounce: pr. pressure, compression; met. affliction, distress of mind, 2 Cor. 2:4; distressing circumstances, trial, affliction, Mt. 25:9
G2347.abbottSmith: pressure (Arist.). In LXX and NT metaph., tribulation , affliction , distress : Mt 24:9 , 21 , 29 , Mk 13:19 , 24 , Jo 16:21 , Ac 7:11 11:19 , Ro 12:12 , II Co 1:4 , 8 4:17 6:4 7:4 8:2 , 13 , Phl 4:14 , II Th 1:6 , Ja 1:27 , Re 1:9 2:9 , 22 7:14 ; c. ἀνάγκη (q.v.), I Th 3:7 ; στενοχωρία (which from the order of the words would appear to be the stronger term), Ro 2:9 8:35 ; διωγμός , Mt 13:21 , Mk
G2347.kjv: afflicted(-tion), anguish, burdened, persecution, tribulation, trouble

G4781.mounce: to bend or bow together; to bow down the back of any one afflictively, Rom. 11:10*
G4781.abbottSmith: to bend completely, bend together : τ. νῶτον , fig., Ro 11:10 (LXX) .†
G4781.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2578 (κάμπτω); to bend together, i.e. (figuratively) to afflict

G3789.thayer: a serpent, snake; used of the devil or Satan.
G3789.mounce: a serpent, Mt. 7:10; 10:16; an artificial serpent, Jn. 3:14; used of the devil or Satan, Rev. 12:9, 14, 15; 20:2; met. a man of serpentine character, Mt. 23:33
G3789.abbottSmith: a serpent , snake : Mt 7:10 , Mk 16:18 , Lk 10:19 11:11 , Jo 3:14 , I Co 10:9 , Re 9:19 ; as typical of wisdom and cunning, Mt 10:16 23:23 , II Co 11:3 (cf. Ge 3:1 ); of Satan (cf. Ge 3:1 , Wi 2:23 , 24 , IV Mac 18:8 ), Re 12:9 , 14 , 15 20:2 .†
G3789.strongs: probably from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (through the idea of sharpness of vision); a snake, figuratively, (as a type of sly cunning) an artful malicious person, especially Satan

G2831.thayer: I am tossed by waves, met: I am tossed to and fro.
G2831.mounce: to be tossed by waves; met. to fluctuate in opinion, be agitated, tossed to and fro, Eph. 4:14*
G2831.abbottSmith: to be tossed by waves ; metaph., to be tossed like waves (cf. FlJ, Ant. , ix, 11, 3): Eph 4:14 .†
G2831.strongs: middle voice from G2830 (κλύδων); to surge, i.e. (figuratively) to fluctuate

G3815.thayer: I play, sport (includes singing and dancing), play in the manner of children.
G3815.mounce: to play in the manner of children; to sport, to practise the festive gestures of idolatrous worship, 1 Cor. 10:7*
G3815.abbottSmith: prop., to play as a child , hence, generally, to play (as with singing and dancing): I Co 10:7 (LXX) (cf. ἐμ-παίζω ).†
G3815.strongs: from G3816 (παῖς); to sport (as a boy)

G878.mounce: unwise, inconsiderate, simple, foolish, Lk. 11:40; 12:20; 1 Cor. 15:36; ignorant, religiously unenlightened, Rom. 2:20; Eph. 5:17; 1 Pet. 2:15; boastfully foolish, vain, 2 Cor. 11:16, 19
G878.abbottSmith: without reason , senseless , foolish , expressing "want of mental sanity and sobriety, a reckless and inconsiderate habit of mind" (Hort; cf. MM, s.v.): Lk 11:40 12:20 , Ro 2:20 , I Co 15:36 , II Co 11:16 12:6 , 11 , I Pe 2:15 ; opp. to φρόνιμος , II Co 11:19 ; to συνιέντες , Eph 2:17 .†
G878.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5424 (φρήν); properly, mindless, i.e. stupid, (by implication) ignorant, (specially) egotistic, (practically) rash, or (morally) unbelieving

G3097.thayer: a sorcerer, a magician, a wizard.
G3097.mounce: (1) a magus, sage of the magician religion, magician, astrologer, wise man, Mt. 2:1, 7, 16; (2) a magician, sorcerer, Acts 13:6, 8*
G3097.abbottSmith: one of the Μάγοι , a Median tribe (Hdt).
G3097.strongs: of foreign origin (H07248); a Magian, i.e. Oriental scientist; by implication, a magician

G1486.thayer: I am accustomed, custom, what was customary.
G1486.mounce: perfect of an obsolete present ἔθω, pluperfect is εἰώθειν, to be accustomed, to be usual, Mt. 27:15; Mk. 10:1; Lk. 4:16; Acts 17:2*
G1486.abbottSmith: to be accustomed, wont : Mt 27:15 , Mk 10:1 ; ptcp., τὸ εἰωθός , custom : κατὰ τὸ εἰ. (Nu, l.c.), Lk 4:16 , Ac 17:2 .†
G1486.strongs: a primary verb; to be used (by habit or conventionality); neuter perfect participle usage

G2512.thayer: cleansing, purifying, purification, literal, ceremonial, or moral; met: expiation.
G2512.mounce: ceremonial cleansing, purification, Lk. 2:22; 5:14; mode of purification, Jn. 2:6; 3:25; cleansing of lepers, Mk. 1:44; met. expiation, Heb. 1:3; 2 Pet. 1:9*
G2512.abbottSmith: cleansing , purification : Jo 3:25 ; c. gen. subjc., τ Ἰουδαίων , Jo 2:6 ; c. gen. obj., of women after childbirth, Lk 2:22 ; of lepers, Mk 1:44 , Lk 5:14 ; c. gen. rei, τ. ἁμαρτιῶν , He 1:3 , II Pe 1:9 (Cremer, 319).†
G2512.strongs: from G2511 (καθαρίζω); a washing off, i.e. (ceremonially) ablution, (morally) expiation

G483.thayer: I speak or say in opposition, contradict (oppose, resist).
G483.mounce: to speak against, contradict; to gainsay, deny, Lk. 20:27; to oppose, Jn. 19:12; Acts 13:45; 28:19; Rom. 10:21; Tit. 1:9; 2:9; pass. to be spoken against, decried, Lk. 2:34; Acts 28:22*
G483.abbottSmith: contradict , oppose , resist (v. Field, Notes , 106; MM, VGT , s.v.); absol.: Ac 28:19 , Ro 10:21 , Tit 1:9 2:9 ; c. dat., Jo 19:12 , Ac 13:45 ; c. acc. et inf., Lk 20:27 T; pass., Lk 2:34 , Ac 28:22 .†
G483.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G3004 (λέγω); to dispute, refuse
G483.kjv: answer again, contradict, deny, gainsay(-er), speak against

G377.thayer: I lie down, recline (at a dinner-table), fall back upon (the breast of another person reclining at dinner).
G377.mounce: to fall or recline backwards; to recline at table, etc., Lk. 11:37; to throw one’s self back, Jn. 21:20
G377.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4098 (πίπτω); to fall back, i.e. lie down, lean back

G2971.thayer: a gnat, mosquito, referred to proverbially as something small.
G2971.mounce: a gnat, mosquito, which is found in wine when becoming sour, Mt. 23:24*
G2971.strongs: apparently a derivative of the base of G2759 (κέντρον) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); a mosquito (from its stinging proboscis)

G2839.thayer: (a) common, shared, (b) Hebraistic use: profane; dirty, unclean, unwashed.
G2839.mounce: common, belonging equally to several, Acts 2:44; 4:32; in NT common, profane, Heb. 10:29; Rev. 21:27; ceremonially unclean, Mk. 7:2; Acts 10:14
G2839.abbottSmith: common (general; = Lat. communis ): Ac 2:44 4:32 ; κ. πίστις , Tit 1:4 ; σωτηρία , Ju 3 .
G2839.strongs: probably from G4862 (σύν); common, i.e. (literally) shared by all or several, or (ceremonially) profane

G4496.thayer: I throw, cast, toss, set down; pass: I am dispersed.
G4496.mounce: also spelled ῥιπτέω, to hurl, throw, cast; to throw or cast down, Mt. 27:5; Lk. 4:35; 17:2; to throw or cast out, Acts 27:19, 29; to lay down, set down, Mt. 15:30; pass. to be dispersed, scattered, Mt. 9:36*
G4496.abbottSmith: to throw, cast, hurl : c. acc. rei, Mt 27:5 , Ac 27:19 , 29 ; c. acc. pers., Mt 15:30 , Lk 4:35 ; pass., Lk 17:2 ; ptcp., ἐριμμένοι , cast down, prostrate (Polyb., al.): Mt 9:36 ; of garments, to throw off (for flight, Eur., Xen., al.; ὅπλα , I Mac 5:43 ; so EV in Ac 22:23 , but v. infr.).
G4496.strongs: a primary verb (perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω), through the idea of sudden motion); to fling (properly, with a quick toss, thus differing from G906 (βάλλω), which denotes a deliberate hurl; and from (see in G1614 (ἐκτείνω)), which indicates an extended projection); by qualification, to deposit (as if a load); by extension, to disperse

G4757.mounce: a soldier, Mt. 8:9; 27:27; met. a soldier of Christ, 2 Tim. 2:3
G4757.abbottSmith: a soldier : Mt 8:9 , Mk 15:16 , Jo 19:2 , Ac 10:7 , al.; metaph., σ. Χριστοῦ Ἰησ. , II Ti 2:3 .†
G4757.strongs: from a presumed derivative of the same as G4756 (στρατιά); a camper-out, i.e. a (common) warrior (literally or figuratively)

G3448.mounce: pr. a tender branch, shoot; a young animal; a calf, young bull, Lk. 15:23, 27, 30; Heb. 12:19; Rev. 4:7*
G3448.strongs: probably strengthened for (a shoot); a young bullock

G5067.mounce: fourth, Mt. 14:25; the fourth part, quarter, Rev. 6:8

G1011.mounce: mid., to give counsel; to deliberate, Lk. 14:31; Jn. 12:10; 11:53; to purpose, determine, Acts 27:39; 2 Cor. 1:17 (2x)*
G1011.abbottSmith: to take counsel, deliberate, resolve . In mid., to take counsel with oneself, consider : seq. εἰ , Lk 14:31 . to determine with oneself, resolve : c. inf., Ac 5:33 27:39 ; c. acc., II Co 1:17 ; seq. ἵνα , Jo 11:53 12:10 (cf. παρα-, συμ- ).†
G1011.strongs: from G1012 (βουλή); to advise, i.e. (reflexively) deliberate, or (by implication) resolve
G1011.kjv: consult, take counsel, determine, be minded, purpose

G4979.mounce: pr. a cord made of rushes; genr. a rope, cord, Jn. 2:15; Acts 27:32*
G4979.abbottSmith: a rope (prop., one made of rushes): Jo 2:15 , Ac 27:32 .†
G4979.strongs: diminutive of (a rush or flag-plant; of uncertain derivation); a rushlet, i.e. grass-withe or tie (generally)

G2630.thayer: I cast down headlong, throw over a precipice.
G2630.abbottSmith: to throw over a precipice , cast down headlong : Lk 4:29 .†
G2630.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G2911 (κρημνός); to precipitate down

G2766.mounce: potter’s clay; earthenware; a roof, tile, tiling, Lk. 5:19*
G2766.strongs: probably from the base of G2767 (κεράννυμι) (through the idea of mixing clay and water); earthenware, i.e. a tile (by analogy, a thin roof or awning)

G256.thayer: Alphaeus; apparently two persons, (a) father of Levi, and (b) father of James.
G256.mounce: Alphaeus, pr. name (1) Father of Jas. the less, Mt. 10:3; Mk. 3:18; Lk. 6:15; Acts 1:13. (2) Father of Levi, (or Matthew) Mk. 2:14*
G256.abbottSmith: Alphæus . Father of Levi: Mk 2:14 . Father of James: Mt 10:3 , Mk 3:13 , Lk 6:15 , Ac 1:13 .†
G256.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H02501); Alphæus, an Israelite

G626.thayer: I give a defense, defend myself (especially in a law court): it can take an object of what is said in defense.
G626.mounce: to defend one’s self against a charge, to make a defence, Lk. 12:11; 21:14
G626.strongs: middle voice from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G3056 (λόγος); to give an account (legal plea) of oneself, i.e. exculpate (self)
G626.kjv: answer (for self), make defence, excuse (self), speak for self

G2754.abbottSmith: foolish fancy , vain opinion , error (LXX, Wi 14:14 , II Mac 2:15 8:19 ).
G2754.strongs: from G2755 (κενόδοξος); empty glorying, i.e. self-conceit

G2850.thayer: flattery, with a view to advantage or gain.
G2850.strongs: from a derivative of (a fawner); flattery

G1816.thayer: I rise (spring) up out (of the ground).
G1816.mounce: to raise up, make to spring up; intrans. to rise up, sprout, spring up or forth, Mt. 13:5; Mk. 4:5*
G1816.abbottSmith: trans., to cause to spring up (LXX) .
G1816.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G393 (ἀνατέλλω); to start up out of the ground, i.e. germinate

G3654.thayer: wholly, altogether, actually, really; with negative: not at all.
G3654.mounce: wholly, altogether; actually, really, 1 Cor. 5:1; 6:7; 15:29; with a negative, at all, Mt. 5:34*
G3654.abbottSmith: altogether , assuredly , actually (c. neg., at all ): Mt 5:34 , I Co 5:1 6:7 15:29 .†
G3654.strongs: adverb from G3650 (ὅλος); completely, i.e. altogether; (by analogy), everywhere; (negatively) not by any means

G1245.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2511 (καθαρίζω); to cleanse perfectly, i.e. (specially) winnow

G3421.thayer: I remember, hold in remembrance, make mention of.
G3421.mounce: to remember, recollect, call to mind, Mt. 16:9; Lk. 17:32; Acts 20:31; to be mindful of, to fix the thoughts upon, Heb. 11:15; to make mention, mention, speak of, Heb. 11:22
G3421.abbottSmith: to call to mind , remember : absol., Mk 8:18 ; c. gen. pers., Lk 17:32 , Col 4:18 , I Th 1:3 , He 11:15 (but v. infr.) 13:7 ; τ. πτωχῶν , Ga 2:20 ; c. gen. rei, Jo 15:20 16:4 , 21 , Ac 20:35 ; c. acc. obj. (as more freq. in cl.), of persons, II Ti 2:8 ; of things, Mt 16:9 , I Th 2:9 , Re 18:5 ; seq. ὅτι , Ac 20:31 , Eph 2:11 , II Th 2:5 ; πόθεν , Re 2:5 ; πῶς , Re 3:3 .
G3421.strongs: from a derivative of G3420 (μνήμη); to exercise memory, i.e. recollect; by implication, to punish; also to rehearse

G1735.mounce: to admit, approve; to be possible, impersonal, it is possible, Lk. 13:33*
G1735.strongs: third person singular present of a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G1209 (δέχομαι); (impersonally) it is accepted in, i.e. admitted (possible)

G5219.thayer: I listen, hearken to, obey, answer.
G5219.mounce: to give ear; to listen, Acts 12:13; to obey, Mt. 8:27; Mk. 1:27; in NT to render submissive acceptance, Acts 6:7; Rom. 6:17; 2 Thess. 1:8; Heb. 5:9; absol. to be submissive, Phil. 2:12
G5219.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G191 (ἀκούω); to hear under (as a subordinate), i.e. to listen attentively; by implication, to heed or conform to a command or authority

G4863.thayer: I gather together, collect, assemble, receive with hospitality, entertain.
G4863.mounce: to bring together, collect, gather, as grain, fruits, etc., Mt. 3:12 6:26; 13:30, 47; to collect an assembly; pass. to convene, come together, meet, Mt. 2:4; 13:2; 18:20; 22:10; in NT to receive with kindness and hospitality, to entertain, Mt. 25:35, 38, 43
G4863.abbottSmith: to gather or bring together : of things, Jo 6:12, 13 ; seq. εἰς , Mt 3:12 , al.; ἐκεῖ , Lk 12:18 ; ποῦ , Lk 12:17 ; συναγαγὼν πάντα (sc. εἰς ἀργύριον ; v. Field, Notes , 68, MM, xxiv), having sold off all : Lk 15:13 ; of persons, Jo 11:52 ; esp. of assemblies, Mt 2:4 , Jo 11:47 , Ac 14:27 , al. Pass., to be gathered or come together : Mt 22:41 , Mk 2:2 , Lk 22:66 , al.; seq. ἐπί Mk 5:21 , Ac 4:27
G4863.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead together, i.e. collect or convene; specially, to entertain (hospitably)
G4863.kjv: + accompany, assemble (selves, together), bestow, come together, gather (selves together, up, together), lead into, resort, take in

G656.thayer: away from the synagogue, expelled from the synagogue, excommunicated.
G656.mounce: expelled or excluded from the synagogue, excommunicated, cut off from the rights and privileges of a Jew, excluded from society, Jn. 9:22; 12:42; 16:2*
G656.abbottSmith: expelled from the congregation (Field, Notes , 96), excommunicated : Jo 9:22 12:42 16:2 (Cremer, 64, 607).†

G4518.mounce: (Aramaic) sabacthani, you have forsaken me; interrogatively, have you forsaken me? preceded with λαμᾶ, Why? Mt. 27:46; Mk. 15:34*
G4518.abbottSmith: sabachthani , i.e. thou host forsaken me : Mt 27:46 , Mk 15:34 ( Ps 21 (22):2 , Heb. עֲוַבְתָּנִי ).†
G4518.strongs: of Chaldee or (H07662 with pronominal suffix); thou hast left me; sabachthani (i.e. shebakthani), a cry of distress

G4649.thayer: a watcher; a goal, a mark aimed at.
G4649.mounce: a watcher; also, a distant object on which the eye is kept fixed; a mark, goal, Phil. 3:14*
G4649.strongs: from (to peer about ("skeptic"); perhaps akin to G4626 (σκάπτω) through the idea of concealment; compare G4629 (σκέπασμα)); a watch (sentry or scout), i.e. (by implication) a goal

G3351.thayer: I transport, cause to migrate, remove.
G3351.mounce: to cause to change abode, cause to emigrate, Acts 7:4, 43*
G3351.abbottSmith: to remove to a new abode, cause to migrate : Ac 7:4 , 43 (LXX) .†
G3351.strongs: from the same as G3350 (μετοικεσία); to transfer as a settler or captive, i.e colonize or exile

G2784.mounce: to publish, proclaim, as a herald, 1 Cor. 9:27; to announce openly and publicly, Mk. 1:4; Lk. 4:18; to noise abroad, Mk. 1:45; 7:36; to announce as a matter of doctrine, inculcate, preach, Mt. 24:14; Mk. 1:38; 13:10; Acts 15:21; Rom. 2:21
G2784.abbottSmith: to be a herald , to proclaim : Mk 1:45 7:36 , Lk 4:18 , 19 (LXX) (but v. Field, Notes , 174) 8:39 , Ro 2:21 , I Co 9:27 , Ga 5:11 , al.; in NT, chiefly of the proclamation of the Gospel, to proclaim , preach : τὸ εὐαγέλιον , Mt 4:23 9:35 24:14 26:13 , Mk 1:14 13:10 14:9 16:[15] , Ga 2:2 , Col 1:23 , I Th 2:9 ; seq. εἰς , Mk 1:39 13:10 , Lk 4:44 , I Th 2:9 ; ἵνα , Mk 6:12 ; c. inf., Ro 2:21 ; κ. Χρ
G2784.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to herald (as a public crier), especially divine truth (the gospel)

G3438.mounce: a stay in any place; an abode, dwelling, mansion, Jn. 14:2, 23*
G3438.abbottSmith: in cl., a staying , abiding ; continuance (LXX, I Mac 7:38 ).
G3438.strongs: from G3306 (μένω); a staying, i.e. residence (the act or the place)

G765.abbottSmith: ungodly , impious : Ro 4:5 5:6 , I Ti 1:9 , I Pe 4:18 , II Pe 2:5 , 6 3:7 , Ju 4 , 15 .†
G765.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4576 (σέβομαι); irreverent, i.e. (by extension) impious or wicked

G3839.mounce: everywhere; in every way, in every instance, Acts 24:3*

G4827.abbottSmith: a fellow-disciple : Jo 11:16 (on the use of συν- in such compounds as this, v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 255 f.).†
G4827.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3129 (μανθάνω); a co-learner (of Christianity)

G4858.mounce: symphony, harmony of sounds, concert of instruments, music, Lk. 15:25*
G4858.strongs: from G4859 (σύμφωνος); unison of sound ("symphony"), i.e. a concert of instruments (harmonious note)

G2201.thayer: a yoke, team; hence: a pair.
G2201.mounce: a yoke of animals; a pair, couple, Lk. 2:24; 14:19*
G2201.strongs: from the same as G2218 (ζυγός); a couple, i.e. a team (of oxen yoked together) or brace (of birds tied together)

G1871.thayer: I beg, am a beggar, ask alms.
G1871.mounce: to prefer a suit or request in respect of certain circumstances; to ask alms, beg, Lk. 16:3; 18:35*
G1871.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G154 (αἰτέω); to ask for

G2101.abbottSmith: well-pleasing, acceptable : Ro 12:2 ; c. dat. pers., Ro 12:1 14:18 , II Co 5:9 , Eph 5:10 , Phl 4:18 ; id. seq. ἐν , Tit 2:9 ( κυρίῳ ), Col 3:20 ; ἐνώπιον , He 13:21 (for ex. in Inser., v. Deiss., BS , 215).†
G2101.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G701 (ἀρεστός); fully agreeable

G596.mounce: a place where anything is laid up for preservation, repository, granary, storehouse, barn, Mt. 3:12; 6:26; 13:30; Lk. 3:17; 12:18, 24
G596.strongs: from G659 (ἀποτίθημι); a repository, i.e. granary

G5204.mounce: water, Mt. 3:11, 16; 14:28, 29; 17:15; Jn. 5:3, 4, 7; watery fluid, Jn. 19:34; ὕδωρ ζῶν, living water, fresh flowing water, Jn. 4:11; met. of spiritual refreshment, Jn. 4:10; 7:38
G5204.abbottSmith: water: Mt 3:16 , Mk 1:10 , Lk 7:44 , Jo 4:7 , Ja 3:12 , Re 8:10 , al.; pl., Mt 14:28 , 29 , Jo 3:23 , Re 1:15 , al.; βαπτίζειν ( ἐν ) ὕδατι , Mt 3:11 , Mk 1:8 , Jo 1:26 , al.; τ. λουτρὸν τοῦ ὕ. , Eph 5:26 ; opp. to οἴνος , Jo 2:9 4:46 ; αἷμα , Jo 19:34 , He 9:19 , I Jo 5:6 , 8 , Mt 17:15 , Mk 9:22 ; πνεῦμα , Jo 1:26 , 31 , 33 ; πνεῦμα καὶ πῦρ , Mt 3:11 , Lk 3:16 ; ἐξ ὕ. κ. πνεύματος γεννηθῆναι , J
G5204.strongs: from the base of G5205 (ὑετός); water (as if rainy) literally or figuratively

G2854.mounce: also spelled κολλύριον, collyrium, eye-salve, Rev. 3:18*
G2854.abbottSmith: a small bread roll (LXX, III Ki 12:24 hil ).
G2854.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a cake; prob akin to the base of G2853 (κολλάω)); properly, a poultice (as made of or in the form of crackers), i.e. (by analogy) a plaster

G3841.thayer: ruler of all, ruler of the universe, the almighty.
G3841.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and G2904 (κράτος); the all-ruling, i.e. God (as absolute and universal sovereign)

G5249.mounce: superabundantly, most vehemently, above all measure, Mk. 7:37*
G5249.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4057 (περισσῶς); superabundantly, i.e. exceedingly

G4794.thayer: I am bowed together, bent double.
G4794.mounce: to bend or bow together; to be bowed together, bent over, Lk. 13:11*
G4794.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2955 (κύπτω); to stoop altogether, i.e. be completely overcome by

G1092.thayer: a worker of the soil, husbandman, farmer, farm-laborer, vine-dresser.
G1092.mounce: a farmer, one who tills the earth, 2 Tim. 2:6; Jas. 5:7; in NT spc. a vine-dresser, keeper of a vineyard, i.q. ἀμπελουργός, Mt. 21:33, 34, et al.
G1092.strongs: from G1093 (γῆ) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); a land-worker, i.e. farmer

G4015.mounce: to lighten around, shine like lightning around, Acts 9:3; 22:6*
G4015.abbottSmith: to flash around : c. acc., Ac 9:3 ; seq. περί Ac 22:6 (Eccl. and Byzant.).†
G4015.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G797 (ἀστράπτω); to flash all around, i.e. envelop in light

G5053.mounce: to end, finish, complete; absol. to end
G5053.abbottSmith: trans., to complete, finish ; esp. τ. τ. αἰῶνα, τ. βίον , to complete life, to die (Æsch., Hdt., al.).
G5053.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G5055 (τελέω); to finish life (by implication, of G979 (βίος)), i.e. expire (demise)

G3026.mounce: idle talk; an empty tale, nonsense, Lk. 24:11*
G3026.strongs: apparently a primary word; twaddle, i.e. an incredible story

G3767.mounce: then, now then, Mt. 13:18; Jn. 19:29; then, thereupon, Lk. 15:28; Jn. 6:14; therefore, consequently, Mt. 5:48: Mk. 10:9; it also serves to mark the resumption of discourse after an interruption by a parenthesis, 1 Cor. 8:4. Sometimes it is not translated.
G3767.abbottSmith: wherefore , therefore , then : Mt 3:10 , Lk 3:9 , Jo 8:38 , Ac 1:21 , Ro 5:9 , al.; in exhortations, Mt 3:8 , Lk 11:35 , Ac 3:19 , Ro 6:12 , al.; in questions, Mt 13:28 , Mk 15:12 , Jo 8:[5] , Ro 6:1 , al.; continuing a narrative or resuming it after a digression, Mt 1:17 , Lk 3:18 , Jo 1:22 2:18 (and very freq. in this Gospel), Ac 26:22 , al.; ἄρα οὖν (v.s. ἄρα ); ἐπεὶ οὖν , He 2:14 ; οὖν c. ptcp
G3767.strongs: apparently a primary word; (adverbially) certainly, or (conjunctionally) accordingly
G3767.kjv: and (so, truly), but, now (then), so (likewise then), then, therefore, verily, wherefore

G107.thayer: Azor, son of Eliakim and father of Zadok, an ancestor of Jesus.
G107.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H05809); Azor, an Israelite

G4127.thayer: a blow, stripe, wound; an affliction, plague.
G4127.mounce: a blow, stroke, stripe, Lk. 10:30; 12:48; meton. a wound, Acts 16:33; Rev. 13:3, 12, 14; from the Hebrew, a plague, affliction, calamity, Rev. 9:20; 11:6
G4127.abbottSmith: a blow , stripe , wound : pl., Lk 10:30 12:48 , Ac 16:23 , 33 , II Co 6:5 11:23 ; ἡ π. τ. θανάτου (RV, death-stroke ), Re 13:3 , 12 ; τ. μαχαίρας , Re 13:14 .
G4127.strongs: from G4141 (πλήσσω); a stroke; by implication, a wound; figuratively, a calamity

G31.mounce: a messsage, doctrine, or precept, delivered in the name of any one; command, 1 Jn. 1:5; 3:11*
G31.abbottSmith: a message : I Jo 1:5 , 3:11 (Cremer, 18; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G31.strongs: from G32 (ἄγγελος); an announcement, i.e. (by implication) precept

G1058.thayer: Gallio, Lucius Iunius Gallio, who received this name by adoption into another family, but was born brother of the philosopher Seneca and originally named L. Annaeus Nouatus; proconsul of the Roman province Achaia from spring A.D. 52 to spring 53.
G1058.strongs: of Latin origin; Gallion (i.e. Gallio), a Roman officer

G721.thayer: (originally: a little lamb, but diminutive force was lost), a lamb.
G721.mounce: a young lamb, lamb, Jn. 21:15; Rev. 5:6, 8
G721.abbottSmith: a little lamb , a lamb : Jo 21:15 , Re 27 .†

G2853.thayer: (lit: I glue); hence: mid. and pass: I join myself closely, cleave, adhere (to), I keep company (with), of friendly intercourse.
G2853.mounce: to glue or weld together; mid. to adhere to, Lk. 10:11; met. to attach one’s self to, unite with, associate with, Lk. 15:15; Acts 5:13; Rev. 18:5
G2853.strongs: from ("glue"); to glue, i.e. (passively or reflexively) to stick (figuratively)

G4998.thayer: of sound mind, self-controlled, temperate, sober-minded, modest, chaste.
G4998.mounce: of a sound mind, sane; temperate, discreet, 1 Tim. 3:2; Tit. 1:8; 2:2; modest, chaste, Tit. 2:5*
G4998.strongs: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G5424 (φρήν); safe (sound) in mind, i.e. self-controlled (moderate as to opinion or passion)

G4486.thayer: I rend, break asunder; I break forth (into speech); I throw or dash down.
G4486.mounce: in our text (Mt. 9:17). to rend, shatter; to break or burst in pieces Mt. 9:17; Mk. 2:22; Lk. 5:37; to rend, lacerate, Mt. 7:6; to cast or dash upon the ground, convulse, Mk. 9:18; Lk. 9:42; absol. to break forth into exclamation, Gal. 4:27
G4486.abbottSmith: to rend, break asunder : Mt 7:6 , Mk 2:22 , Lk 5:37 ; pass., Mt 9:17 .
G4486.strongs: both prolonged forms of (which appears only in certain forms, and is itself probably a strengthened form of (see in G2608 (κατάγνυμι))) to "break," "wreck" or "crack", i.e. (especially) to sunder (by separation of the parts; G2608 (κατάγνυμι) being its intensive (with the preposition in composition), and G2352 (θραύω) a shattering to minute fragments; but not a reduction to the constituent particles, like G3089 (λύω)) or disrupt, lacerate; by implication, to convulse (with spasms); figuratively, to give vent to joyful emotions

G4402.thayer: Prochorus, one of the seven original deacons at Jerusalem.
G4402.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5525 (χορός); before the dance; Prochorus, a Christian

G1991.thayer: I prop up, uphold, support, confirm, make stronger.
G1991.mounce: pr. to cause to rest or lean on, to settle upon; met. to conform, strengthen, establish, 14:22; 15:32, 41; 18:23*
G1991.abbottSmith: to make stronger, confirm : c. acc, Ac 14:22 15:32 , 41 .†
G1991.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4741 (στηρίζω); to support further, i.e. reestablish

G1783.thayer: (lit: approaching the king, hence a technical term), a petition, prayer, intercession.
G1783.mounce: pr. a meeting with; hence address; prayer, supplication, intercession, 1 Tim. 2:1; 4:5*
G1783.strongs: from G1793 (ἐντυγχάνω); an interview, i.e. (specially) supplication

G1244.thayer: I divide into parts, cut asunder, distribute.
G1244.abbottSmith: to divide into parts, cut asunder .
G1244.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G138 (αἱρέομαι); to separate, i.e. distribute

G1778.mounce: a precept, commandment, ordinance, Mt. 15:9; Mk. 7:7; Col. 2:22*
G1778.abbottSmith: a precept : pl., Mt 15:9 (LXX) , Mk 7:7 , Col 2:22 .†
G1778.strongs: from G1781 (ἐντέλλομαι); an injunction, i.e. religious precept

G650.thayer: I defraud, deprive of, despoil; mid: I endure deprivation; pass: I am bereft of.
G650.mounce: to deprive, detach; to debar, 1 Cor. 7:5; to deprive in a bad sense, defraud, Mk. 10:19; 1 Cor. 6:7; mid. to suffer one’s self to be deprived or defrauded, 1 Cor. 6:8; pass. to be destitute or devoid of, 1 Tim. 6:5; to be unjustly withheld, Jas. 5:4*
G650.abbottSmith: to defraud , deprive of , despoil (in cl. chiefly of the misappropriation of trust funds, Field, Notes , 33; cf. MM, s.v.): absol., Mk 10:19 , I Co 6:8 ; c. acc. pers., I Co 7:5 . Mid., endure deprivation : I Co 6:7 (WM, § 38, 3; but v. Bl., § 54, 5; M, Pr. , 162); pass., ἀπεστερημένοι , bereft of : I Ti 6:5 .†
G650.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to deprive); to despoil
G650.kjv: defraud, destitute, kept back by fraud

G2529.mounce: pr. to look down upon, in the NT to mark, perceive, discern, Rom. 1:20*
G2529.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3708 (ὁράω); to behold fully, i.e. (figuratively) distinctly apprehend

G1227.mounce: to look through; to view steadily, Mk. 8:25; to see clearly or steadily, Mt. 7:5; Lk. 6:42*
G1227.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G991 (βλέπω); to look through, i.e. recover full vision

G2993.thayer: Laodicea, a city in the Lycos valley in the Roman province Asia, near Colossae and Hierapolis.
G2993.mounce: Laodicea, a city of Phrygia in Asia Minor, Rev. 3:14*. See PhotoGuide.
G2993.abbottSmith: Laodicea , a city on the Lycus in Phrygia: Col 2:1 4:13 , 15 , 16 , Re 1:11 3:14 .†
G2993.strongs: from a compound of G2992 (λαός) and G1349 (δίκη); Laodicia, a place in Asia Minor

G1408.mounce: a course, race, race-course; met. course of life or ministry, career, Acts 13:25; 20:24; 2 Tim. 4:7*
G1408.abbottSmith: a course : fig., of life or ministry, Ac 13:25 20:24 , II Ti 4:7 .†
G1408.strongs: from the alternate of G5143 (τρέχω); a race, i.e. (figuratively) career

G422.thayer: having no cause to be ashamed.
G422.mounce: without cause of shame, irreproachable, 2 Tim. 2:15*
G422.abbottSmith: not to be put to shame : II Ti 2:15 .†
G422.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G153 (αἰσχύνομαι); not ashamed, i.e. irreprehensible:

G2208.mounce: pr. a generous rival, an imitator; in NT an aspirant, 1 Cor. 14:12; Tit. 2:14; a devoted adherent, a zealot, Acts 21:20; 22:3; Gal. 1:14
G2208.strongs: the same as G2207 (ζηλωτής); a Zealot, i.e. (specially) partisan for Jewish political independence

G1874.thayer: I listen to, hear, hearken to.
G1874.mounce: gen., to hear, hearken, listen to, Acts 16:25*
G1874.abbottSmith: to listen attentively : Ac 16:25 (cf. Page, in l).†
G1874.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G202 (ἀκροατής); to listen (intently) to

G4796.mounce: to rejoice with any one, sympathize in joy, Lk. 1:58; 15:6, 9; Phil. 2:17, 18; met. 1 Cor. 12:26; to sympathize in the advancement of, congratulate, 1 Cor. 13:6*
G4796.abbottSmith: to rejoice with : c. dat pers., Lk 1:58 15:6 , 9 , Phl 2:17, 18 , EV; c. dat. rei, I Co 12:26 13:6 .
G4796.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5463 (χαίρω); to sympathize in gladness, congratulate

G4747.thayer: (a) plur: the heavenly bodies, (b) a rudiment, an element, a rudimentary principle, an elementary rule.
G4747.mounce: an element; an element of the natural universe, 2 Pet. 3:10, 12; an element or rudiment of any intellectual or religious system, Gal. 4:3, 9; Col. 2:8, 20; Heb. 5:12*
G4747.abbottSmith: prop., one of a row ( στοῖχος ) or series , hence,
G4747.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4748 (στοιχέω); something orderly in arrangement, i.e. (by implication) a serial (basal, fundamental, initial) constituent (literally), proposition (figuratively)

G1322.mounce: instruction, the giving of instruction, teaching, Mk. 4:2; 12:38; instruction, what is taught, doctrine, Mt. 16:12; Jn. 7:16, 17; meton. mode of teaching and kind of doctrine taught, Mt. 7:28; Mk. 1:27
G1322.abbottSmith: objectively, teaching, doctrine , that which is taught: Mk 1:27 , Jo 7:16 , Ac 17:19 , Ro 6:7 16:17 , II Jo 10 , Re 2:24 ; c. gen. poss., Mt 7:28 16:12 22:33 , Mk 1:22 11:18 , Lk 4:32 , Jo 18:19 , Ac 5:28 , Re 2:14 , 15 ; ἡ δ. , of Christ, Jo 7:17 , Ac 13:12 , II Jo 9 ; cf. τὴν δ. , Tit 1:9 ; c. gen. obj., βαπτισμῶν δ. , He 6:2 ; pl., He 13:9 .
G1322.strongs: from G1321 (διδάσκω); instruction (the act or the matter)

G1893.thayer: of time: when, after; of cause: since, because; otherwise: else.
G1893.mounce: when, after, since, because, in as much as, Mt. 18:32; 27:6; for, for then, for else, since in that case, Rom. 3:6; 11:6
G1893.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1487 (εἰ); thereupon, i.e. since (of time or cause)
G1893.kjv: because, else, for that (then, -asmuch as), otherwise, seeing that, since, when

G3985.mounce: to make proof or trial of, put to the proof, whether with good or mischievous intent, Mt. 16:1; 22:35; absol. to attempt, Acts 16:7; 24:6; in NT to tempt, Mt. 4:1; to try, subject to trial, 1 Cor. 10:13
G3985.strongs: from G3984 (πεῖρα); to test (objectively), i.e. endeavor, scrutinize, entice, discipline
G3985.kjv: assay, examine, go about, prove, tempt(-er), try

G3470.mounce: blame, ridicule; a disgrace to society, a stain, 2 Pet. 2:13*
G3470.abbottSmith: in cl. poets and late prose, blame , disgrace .
G3470.strongs: perhaps from G3201 (μέμφομαι); a flaw or blot, i.e. (figuratively) disgraceful person

G1868.mounce: praise, applause, honor paid, Rom. 2:29; 2 Cor. 8:18; meton. ground or reason of praise or commendation, Phil. 4:8; approval, Rom. 13:3; 1 Pet. 2:14; 1 Cor. 4:5
G1868.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G134 (αἰνέω); laudation; concretely, a commendable thing

G3713.thayer: I stretch forth, mid: I hanker after, long for, am eager for, aspire to.
G3713.mounce: to extend, stretch out; mid. to stretch one’s self out, to reach forward to, met. to desire earnestly, long after, 1 Tim. 3:1; Heb. 11:16; by impl. to indulge in, be devoted to, 1 Tim. 6:10*
G3713.abbottSmith: to reach , stretch out ; pass. and mid., to stretch oneself out , reach forth ; metaph., to reach after , grasp at , aspire to : c. gen. rei, I Ti 3:1 , He 11:16 ; φιλαργυρίας (v. Ellic. and CGT , in l.), I Ti 6:10 .†
G3713.strongs: middle voice of apparently a prolonged form of an obsolete primary (compare G3735 (ὄρος)); to stretch oneself, i.e. reach out after (long for)

G3075.mounce: some list as a deponent, λυμαίνομαι, to outrage, harm, violently maltreat; in NT to make havoc of, ruin, Acts 8:3*
G3075.abbottSmith: to outrage , maltreat : c. acc., Ac 8:3 .
G3075.strongs: middle voice from a probably derivative of G3089 (λύω) (meaning filth); properly, to soil, i.e. (figuratively) insult (maltreat)

G200.strongs: apparently from the same as G206 (ἄκρον); a locust (as pointed, or as lighting on the top of vegetation)

G4536.thayer: a trumpet, the sound of a trumpet.
G4536.mounce: trumpet, 1 Cor. 14:8; Heb. 12:19; Rev. 1:10; 4:1; 8:2, 6; 13:9; 1 Thess. 4:16; sound of the trumpet, Mt. 24:31; 1 Cor. 15:52; 1 Thess. 4:16*
G4536.abbottSmith: a trumpet , used in war and in religious ceremonies: I Co 14:8 , He 12:19 , Re 1:10 4:1 8:2 , 6 , 13 9:14 By meton., a trumpet blast : μετὰ σ. μεγάλης , Mt 24:31 ; ἐν σ. θεοῦ , I Th 4:16 ; ἐν τ. ἐσχάτῃ σ. , I Co 15:52 .†
G4536.strongs: perhaps from G4535 (σάλος) (through the idea of quavering or reverberation); a trumpet

G329.thayer: I stir up the fire, fan the flame of.
G329.mounce: pr. to kindle up a dormant fire; met. to revive, excite; to stir up, quicken one’s powers, 2 Tim. 1:6*
G329.abbottSmith: to kindle afresh : metaph., II Ti 1:6 (for vernac. exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G329.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a compound of the base of G2226 (ζῶον) and G4442 (πῦρ); to re-enkindle

G2087.thayer: (a) of two: another, a second, (b) other, different, (c) one's neighbor.
G2087.mounce: other, Mt. 12:45; another, some other, Mt. 8:21; besides, Lk. 23:32; ὁ ἕτερος, the other of two, Mt. 6:24; τῇ ἑτέρᾳ, on the next day, Acts 20:15; 27:3; ὁ ἕτερος, one’s neighbor, Rom. 13:8; different, Lk. 9:29; foreign, strange, Acts 2:4; 1 Cor. 14:21; illicit, Jude 7
G2087.abbottSmith: distributive pron. , prop. dual (Bl., §13, 5; 51, 6), denoting the second of a pair, but in late Gk. encroaching on ἄλλος (M, Pr. , 79 f.); of number, other ; c. art., the other ; of two, Lk 5:7 9:56 , al.; opp. to ὁ πρῶτος , Mt 21:30 ; ὁ εἷς , Mt 6:24 , Lk 7:41 , Ac 23:6 , al.; ἕ. μὲν . . . ἕ. δέ , the one . . . the other : I Co 15:40 ; the next : Lk 6:6 9:56 (sc. ἡμέρα , Xen.), Ac 20:15 27:3 ; =
G2087.strongs: of uncertain affinity; (an-, the) other or different
G2087.kjv: altered, else, next (day), one, (an-)other, some, strange

G1146.mounce: a ring for the finger, Lk. 15:22*

G4571.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G4571.strongs: accusative case singular of G4771 (σύ); thee

G1125.thayer: I write; pass: it is written, it stands written (in the scriptures).
G1125.mounce: to engrave, write, according to the ancient method of writing on plates of metal, waxes tables, etc., Jn. 8:6, 8; to write on parchment, paper, etc., generally, Mt. 27:37, et al.; to write letters to another, Acts 23:25; 2 Cor. 2:9; 13:10, et al.; to describe in writing, Jn. 1:46; Rom. 10:5; to inscribe in a catalogue, etc., Lk. 10:20; Rev. 13:8; 17:8, et al.; to write a law, command, or enact in writing, Mk. 10:5; 12:19; Lk. 2:23, et al.
G1125.abbottSmith: to scrape, graze (Hom.), and later (Hdt.) to sketch, draw . to write ; of forming or tracing letters on writing material: Jo 8:[6] , Ga 6:11 , II Th 3:17 ; to express in writing, commit to writing, record: Lk 1:63 , Jo 19:21 , 22 Re 1:11 , 19 al.; of scripture as a standing authority (Deiss., BS , 112ff.), γέγραπται , it stands written (Luther), Mt 4:4 , Mk 7:6 , Lk 4:8 , Ro 1:17 , I Co 1:31 , al.
G1125.strongs: a primary verb; to "grave", especially to write; figuratively, to describe

G4461.thayer: Rabbi, my master, teacher; a title of respect often applied to Christ.
G4461.mounce: also spelled ῥαββεί, rabbi, my master, teacher, Mt. 23:7, 8; 26:25, 49
G4461.abbottSmith: a title of respectful address to Jewish teachers, Rabbi : Mt 23:7 , 8 ; of John, Jo 3:26 ; of Christ, Mt 26:25 , 49 , Mk 9:5 11:21 14:45 , Jo 1:30 , 50 3:2 4:31 6:25 9:2 11:8 ; κύριε ῥ. , Mk 10:51 (WH, mg., v.s. ῥαββουνεί ).†
G4461.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07227 with pronominal suffix); my master, i.e Rabbi, as an official title of honor

G2598.thayer: (a) mid: I lay, of a foundation, (b) met: I cast down, prostrate.
G2598.mounce: to cast down, 2 Cor. 4:9; mid. to lay down, lay a foundation, Heb. 6:1*
G2598.abbottSmith: to cast down , prostrate : metaph., pass., II Co 4:9 .
G2598.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw down

G5486.thayer: a gift of grace, an undeserved favor.
G5486.mounce: a free favor, free gift, Rom. 5:15, 16; 6:23; 2 Cor. 1:11; benefit, Rom. 1:11; a divinely conferred endowment, 1 Cor. 12:4, 9, 28, 30, 31
G5486.abbottSmith: a gift of grace, a free gift , esp. of extra­ordinary operations of the Spirit in the Apostolic Church, but including all spiritual graces and endowments (Lft., Notes , 148 f.): Ro 1:11 5:15, 16 6:23 11:29 12:6 , I Co 1:7 7:7 12:4 , 9 , 28 , 30, 31 , II Co 1:11 , I Ti 4:14 , II Ti 1:6 , I Pe 4:10 .†
G5486.strongs: from G5483 (χαρίζομαι); a (divine) gratuity, i.e. deliverance (from danger or passion); (specially), a (spiritual) endowment, i.e. (subjectively) religious qualification, or (objectively) miraculous faculty

G683.thayer: I push (thrust) away, repulse, reject, refuse.
G683.mounce: to thrust away, repel from one’s self, repulse, Acts 7:27; to refuse, reject, cast off, Acts 7:39; 13:46; Rom. 11:1, 2; 1 Tim. 1:19*
G683.abbottSmith: to thrust away . Mid., to thrust away from oneself , refuse , reject: c. acc. pers. (MM, s.v.), Ac 7:27 , 39 13:46 , Ro 11:1 , 2 , I Ti 1:19 .†
G683.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the middle voice of or (to shove); to push off, figuratively, to reject
G683.kjv: cast away, put away (from), thrust away (from)

G4392.mounce: pr. that which appears in front, that which is put forward to hide the true state of things; a fair show or pretext, Acts 27:30; a specious cloak, Mt. 23:13; 1 Thess. 2:5; an excuse, Jn. 15:22
G4392.abbottSmith: a pretence , pretext : Mt 23:1 (WM, R, txt., om.), Mk 12:40 , Lk 20:47 , Jo 15:22 , Ac 27:30 , Phl 1:18 , I Th 2:5 .†
G4392.strongs: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5316 (φαίνω); an outward showing, i.e. pretext

G5344.thayer: Felix, third name of (Marcus) Antonius Felix, procurator of the Roman province Judea from an uncertain date (before A.D. 52 ?) till A.D. 59.
G5344.strongs: of Latin origin; happy; Phelix (i.e. Felix), a Roman

G3805.mounce: passible, capable of suffering, liable to suffer; in NT destined to suffer, Acts 26:23*
G3805.strongs: from the same as G3804 (πάθημα); liable (i.e. doomed) to experience pain

G4066.thayer: neighboring; subst: the neighboring country, neighboring inhabitants.
G4066.mounce: neighboring; ἡ περίχωρος, i.e. γῆ, an adjacent region, country round about, Mt. 14:35; Mk. 1:28; meton. inhabitants of the region round about, Mt. 3:5
G4066.abbottSmith: round about , neighbouring (Dem., Plut., al.). In LXX and NT, as subst., ἡ π. (sc. γῆ ), the region round about : Mt 14:35 , Mk 1:28 , Lk 4:14 , 37 7:17 8:37 , Ac 14:6 ; ἡ π. τοῦ Ἰορδάνου , Lk 3:3 ; by meton. for the people of the same region, Mt 3:5 .†
G4066.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G5561 (χώρα); around the region, i.e. circumjacent (as noun, with G1093 (γῆ) implied vicinity)
G4066.kjv: country (round) about, region (that lieth) round about

G2710.thayer: I use to the full, use up.
G2710.mounce: to use downright; to use up, consume; to make an unrestrained use of, use eagerly, 1 Cor. 7:31; to use to the full, stretch to the utmost, exploit, 1 Cor. 9:18*
G2710.abbottSmith: to make full use of , use to the uttermost , use up : I Co 7:31 ; c. dat., I Co 9:18 (for other senses, v. LS, s.v.).†
G2710.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5530 (χράομαι); to overuse, i.e. misuse

G212.thayer: boasting, show, arrogant display, ostentation; plur: occasions of ostentation.
G212.mounce: arrogance; presumptuous speech, Jas. 4:16; haughtiness, 1 Jn. 2:16*
G212.abbottSmith: the character of an ἀλαζών , boastfulness , vainglory , vaunting : Ja 4:16 (Mayor, in l.), I Jo 2:16 .†
G212.strongs: from G213 (ἀλαζών); braggadocio, i.e. (by implication) self-confidence

G1345.thayer: a thing pronounced (by God) to be righteous (just, the right); or the restoration of a criminal, a fresh chance given him; a righteous deed, an instance of perfect righteousness.
G1345.mounce: pr. a rightful act, act of justice, equity; a sentence, of condemnation, Rev. 15:4; in NT, of acquittal, justification, Rom. 5:16; a decree, law, ordinance, Lk. 1:6; Rom. 1:32; 2:26; 8:4; Heb. 9:1, 10; a meritorious act, an instance of perfect righteousness, Rom. 5:18; Rev. 19:8*
G1345.abbottSmith: a concrete expression of righteousness, the expression and result of the act of δικαίωσις , "a declaration that a thing is δίκαιον , or that a person is δίκαιος ," hence, an ordinance : δ. τ. θεοῦ , Ro 1:32 ; τ. κυρίου , Lk 1:6 ; τ. νόμου , Ro 2:26 8:4 ; λατρείας , He 9:1 ; σαρκός , He 9:10 ; a sentence : of acquittal , Ro 5:16 (also 5:18 ; ICC , in l.), or of condemnation ; a righteous act : Ro 5
G1345.strongs: from G1344 (δικαιόω); an equitable deed; by implication, a statute or decision

G4330.thayer: I permit one to approach, permit further.
G4330.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G1439 (ἐάω); to permit further progress

G4869.mounce: a fellow captive, Rom. 16:7; Col. 4:10; Phlm. 23*
G4869.abbottSmith: a fellow prisoner (prop., of a captive in war, v. Lft., Col. , 234): Ro 16:7 , Col 4:10 , Phm 23 .†
G4869.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G164 (αἰχμάλωτος); a co-captive

G1311.thayer: I destroy, waste; hence met: I corrupt.
G1311.mounce: to corrupt or destroy utterly; to waste, bring to decay, Lk. 12:33; 2 Cor. 4:16; to destroy, Rev. 8:9; 11:18 (2x); met. to corrupt, pervert utterly, 1 Tim. 6:5*
G1311.abbottSmith: to destroy utterly : Lk 12:33 , Re 11:18 ; pass., to be destroyed, disabled : Re 8:9 , II Co 4:16 .
G1311.strongs: from G1225 (διαβάλλω) and G5351 (φθείρω); to rot thoroughly, i.e. (by implication) to ruin (passively, decay utterly, figuratively, pervert)

G3061.mounce: a pestilence, plague, Lk. 21:11; met. a pest, pestilent fellow, Acts 24:5*
G3061.abbottSmith: pestilence : pl., Lk 21:11 ; metaph. (as in cl.; LXX: Ps 1:1 , Pr 21:24 , I Mac 15:21 , al.), of persons, a pest : Ac 24:5 (also as adj., ἄνδρες λοιμοί , I Mac 10:61 , al.).†
G3061.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a plague (literally, the disease, or figuratively, a pest)

G1483.thayer: in the manner of Gentiles, like the rest of the world.
G1483.strongs: adverb from G1482 (ἐθνικός); as a Gentile

G4333.mounce: pr. to work in addition; to gain in addition in trade, Lk. 19:16*
G4333.abbottSmith: to work or do service besides (Hdt., Plut.).
G4333.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2038 (ἐργάζομαι); to work additionally, i.e. (by implication) acquire besides

G702.thayer: Aretas IV, King of the Nabataeans.
G702.abbottSmith: Aretas , an Arabian king: II Co 11:32 (Deiss., BS , 183 f., thinks the proper spelling Ἀρέθας was changed, as Schürer suggests, "by desire to Hellenise a barbaric name by assimilation to ἀρετή ").†
G702.strongs: of foreign origin; Aretas, an Arabian

G2636.mounce: evil-speaking, detraction, backbiting, slandering, 2 Cor. 12:20; 1 Pet. 2:1*
G2636.abbottSmith: evil-speaking , railing : pl., II Co 12:20 , I Pe 2:1 .†

G21.thayer: I exult, am full of joy.
G21.mounce: to celebrate, praise; usually in the middle in the NT (ἀγαλλιάομαι) to exult, rejoice exceedingly; to desire ardently, Jn. 8:56
G21.abbottSmith: to exult, rejoice greatly : seq. ἐπί , c. dat., Lk 1:47 ; c. dat. mod., I Pe 1:8 , Re 19:7 .
