Mid., with same sense: Mt 5:12 , Lk 10:21 , Ac 2:26 , 16:34 , I Pe 4:13 ; seq. ἵνα , Jo 8:56 ; ἐν , Jo 5:35 (1 aor. pass. perh. as mid.; but v. Mozley, Psalter , 5), I Pe 1:6 (Cremer, 590).†
G21.strongs: from agan (much) and G242 (ἅλλομαι); properly, to jump for joy, i.e. exult
G21.kjv: be (exceeding) glad, with exceeding joy, rejoice (greatly)

G3832.thayer: with one's whole household or family.
G3832.mounce: also spelled πανοικί, with one’s whole household or family, Acts 16:34*
G3832.strongs: adverb from G3956 (πᾶς) and G3624 (οἶκος); with the whole family

G4913.mounce: to be pleased along with others; to congratulate; to delight in, approve cordially, Rom. 7:22*
G4913.abbottSmith: to rejoice together ; c. dat., to rejoice with or in : τ. νόμῳ τ. θεοῦ , Ro 7:22 (v. Lft., Notes , 304).†
G4913.strongs: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2237 (ἡδονή); to rejoice in with oneself, i.e. feel satisfaction concerning

G5367.abbottSmith: loving oneself (Arist.); in bad sense (ib.), selfish : II Ti 3:2 .†
G5367.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G846 (αὐτός); fond of self, i.e. selfish

G4046.thayer: I acquire, earn, purchase, make my own, preserve alive.
G4046.mounce: to cause to remain over and above, to reserve, save, Lk. 17:33; mid. to acquire, gain, earn, 1 Tim. 3:13; to purchase, Acts 20:28*
G4046.abbottSmith: to make to remain over , preserve . Mid., to keep or save for oneself: τ. ψυχήν (cf. Xen., Cyr. , iv, 4, 10; Arist., Pol. , v, 11, 30), Lk 17:33 ; to get or gain for oneself , get possession of (Thuc., Xen., al.): Ac 20:28 , I Ti 3:13 .†
G4046.strongs: middle voice from G4012 (περί) and G4160 (ποιέω); to make around oneself, i.e. acquire (buy)

G3308.mounce: care, Mt. 13:22; Mk. 4:19; Lk. 8:14; 21:34; anxiety, anxious interest, 2 Cor. 11:28; 1 Pet. 5:7*
G3308.abbottSmith: care , anxiety : I Pe 5:7 ; pl., Lk 8:14 21:34 ; c. gen. obj., Mt 13:22 , Mk 4:19 , II Co 11:28 .†
G3308.strongs: from G3307 (μερίζω) (through the idea of distraction); solicitude

G2236.mounce: with pleasure, gladly, willingly, Mk. 6:20; 12:37; 2 Cor. 11:19
G2236.strongs: neuter plural of the superlative of the same as G2234 (ἡδέως); with great pleasure

G2284.thayer: I amaze; pass: I am amazed (almost terrified).
G2284.mounce: to be astonished, amazed, awestruck Mt. 1:27; 10:24, 32*
G2284.strongs: from G2285 (θάμβος); to stupefy (with surprise), i.e. astound

G2020.mounce: to dawn, Mt. 28:1; hence, used of the reckoned commencement of the day, to be near commencing, to dawn on, Lk. 23:54*
G2020.strongs: a form of G2017 (ἐπιφαύω); to begin to grow light
G2020.kjv: begin to dawn, X draw on

G2898.strongs: diminutive of a derivative of the base of G2768 (κέρας); a skull ("cranium")

G1945.thayer: (a) dat: I am placed upon, am laid upon, lie upon, am imposed; I press upon, (b) absol: I press hard, am insistent, insist.
G1945.mounce: to lie upon, be placed upon, Jn. 11:38; 21:9; to press, urge upon, Lk. 5:1; Acts 27:20; be urgent, importunate upon, Lk. 23:23; to be imposed upon, be imposed by law, Heb. 9:10; by necessity, 1 Cor. 9:16
G1945.abbottSmith: to be placed, lie on : Jo 21:9 ; seq. ἐπί , c. dat., ib. 11:38 ; fig., He 9:10 ; ἀνάγκη , I Co 9:16 ; χειμῶν , to threaten, come on : Ac 27:20 ; of persons, to press upon : Lk 5:1 23:23 .†
G1945.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to rest upon (literally or figuratively)
G1945.kjv: impose, be instant, (be) laid (there-, up-)on, (when) lay (on), lie (on), press upon

G4378.thayer: a pouring upon, an effusion, a sprinkling.
G4378.abbottSmith: a pouring or sprinkling upon : He 11:28 .†
G4378.strongs: from a comparative of G4314 (πρός) and (to pour); a shedding forth, i.e. affusion

G1526.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G1526.strongs: 3rd person plural present indicative of G1510 (εἰμί); they are
G1526.kjv: agree, are, be, dure, X is, were

G3420.mounce: remembrance, recollection, memory; μνήμην ποιεῖσθαι, to make mention, 2 Pet. 1:15*
G3420.abbottSmith: memory , remembrance , mention : μ. ποιεῖσθαι , c. gen., to remember , II Pe 1:15 (but in cl., π. , μ. π. more freq. = to make mention , and so perh. here, cf. Mayor, in l.; and for ex. from π. , v. Zorell, s.v.).†

G27.thayer: loved, beloved, with two special applications: the Beloved, a title of the Messiah (Christ), as beloved beyond all others by the God who sent Him; of Christians, as beloved by God, Christ, and one another.
G27.abbottSmith: beloved (v. M, Pr. , 221); by God: of Christ, Mt 3:17 ; of men, Ro 1:7 ; by Christians, of one another: I Co 4:14 ; freq. as form of address, I Co 10:14 ; opp. to ἐχθρός , Ro 11:28 (v. AR, Eph. , 229; Cremer, 17; MM, VGT , s.v.).

G5296.thayer: a pattern, example; a form, sample.
G5296.mounce: a sketch, delineation; a form, formula, presentment, sample, 2 Tim. 1:13; a pattern, a model representation, 1 Tim. 1:16*
G5296.abbottSmith: an outline, sketch . Metaph., a pattern, example : I Ti 1:16 , II Ti 1:13 .†
G5296.strongs: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G5179 (τύπος); typification under (after), i.e. (concretely) a sketch (figuratively) for imitation

G661.thayer: I repay, pay what is due (by way of punishment or fine), make good.
G661.mounce: to pay off what is claimed or due; to repay, refund, make good, Phlm. 19*
G661.abbottSmith: to pay off , repay : Phm 19 (MM, s.v.).†
G661.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5099 (τίνω); to pay in full

G281.thayer: verily, truly, amen; at the end of sentences may be paraphrased by: So let it be.
G281.mounce: in truth, most certainly; so be it; ὁ ἀμήν, the faithful and true one, Rev. 3:14
G281.abbottSmith: As adj. (cf. Is 65:16 ), ὁ ἀ. , Re 3:14 .
G281.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0543); properly, firm, i.e. (figuratively) trustworthy; adverbially, surely (often as interjection, so be it)

G3993.thayer: one who works for his living; a laborer, poor man.
G3993.mounce: pr. one who labors for his bread; poor, needy, 2 Cor. 9:9*
G3993.abbottSmith: one who works for his living , a labourer , a poor man : II Co 9:9 .†
G3993.strongs: from a primary (to toil for daily subsistence); starving, i.e. indigent

G2214.thayer: a question, debate, controversy; a seeking, search.
G2214.mounce: a seeking; an inquiry, a question; a dispute, debate, discussion, Jn. 3:25; 1 Tim. 6:4; a subject of dispute or controversy, Acts 15:2, 7; 25:20; 2 Tim. 2:23; Tit. 3:9
G2214.strongs: from G2212 (ζητέω); a searching (properly, the act), i.e. a dispute or its theme

G5608.abbottSmith: to buy : ὠνήσατο (= cl. ἐπρίατο ; v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 210 ff.; Veitch, s.v.), c. gen. pret., Ac 7:16 .†
G5608.strongs: middle voice from an apparently primary (a sum or price); to purchase (synonymous with the earlier G4092 (πίμπρημι))

G3361.mounce: a negative particle, can function as a conj, not, for the particulars of its usage, especially as distinguished from that of οὐ, consult a grammar; as a conj., lest, that not, Mt. 5:29, 30; 18:10; 24:6; Mk. 13:36; μή, or μήτι, or μήποτε, when prefixed to an interrogative clause, it expresses an intimation either of the reality of the matters respecting which the question is asked, Mt. 12:23; or the contrary, Jn. 4:12
G3361.abbottSmith: used where the negation depends on a condition or hypothesis, expressed or understood, as distinct from οὐ , which denies absolutely. μή is used where one thinks a thing is not, as distinct from an absolute negation. As a general rule, οὐ negatives the indic., μή the other moods, incl, ptcp. [In LXX for אֵין , אַיִן , אַל .] As a neg. adv., not ; with ref. to thought or opinion: Jo 3:18 , Tit 1:11
G3361.strongs: a primary particle of qualified negation (whereas G3756 (οὐ) expresses an absolute denial); (adverb) not, (conjunction) lest; also (as an interrogative implying a negative answer (whereas G3756 (οὐ) expects an affirmative one)) whether
G3361.kjv: any but (that), X forbear, + God forbid, + lack, lest, neither, never, no (X wise in), none, nor, (can-)not, nothing, that not, un(-taken), without

G2985.mounce: a light, Acts 20:8; a lamp, Rev. 4:5; 8:10, a portable lamp, lantern, torch, Mt. 25:1, 3, 4, 7, 8; Jn. 18:3*
G2985.abbottSmith: a torch (freq. fed, like a lamp, with oil): Mt 25:1 ff., Jo 18:3 , Ac 20:8 , Re 4:5 8:10 .†

G2117.thayer: adj: (a) straight of direction, as opposed to crooked, (b) upright; adv: immediately.
G2117.mounce: straight forwards; directly, immediately, instantly, Mt. 3:16; 13:20, 21
G2117.abbottSmith: = εὐθέως , straightway, directly : Mt 3:16 13:20 , 21 14:27 21:2 , 3 26:74 , Lk 6:49 , Jo 13:30 , 32 19:34 , Ac 10:16 and 42 (41) times in Mk.†
G2117.strongs: perhaps from G2095 (εὖ) and G5087 (τίθημι); straight, i.e. (literally) level, or (figuratively) true; adverbially (of time) at once
G2117.kjv: anon, by and by, forthwith, immediately, straightway

G1532.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G5143 (τρέχω); to hasten inward

G2147.thayer: I find, learn, discover, especially after searching.
G2147.mounce: to find, to meet with; Mt. 18:28; 20:6; to find out, to detect, discover, Lk. 23:2, 4, 14; to acquire, obtain, win, gain, Lk. 1:30; 9:12; to find mentally, to comprehend, recognize, Acts 17:27; Rom. 7:21; to find by experience, observe, gather, Rom. 7:18; to devise as feasible, Lk. 5:19; 19:48
G2147.abbottSmith: to find , with or without previous search: absol., opp. to ζητέω , Mt 7:7 , 8 Lk 11:9 , 10 ; c. acc., Mt 2:8 , Mk 1:37 , Ac 13:22 , II Ti 1:17 , al.; pass., οὐχ εὑ. , of disappearance, He 11:5 , Re 16:20 , al.; γῆ κ. τὰ ἐν αὐτῇ ἔργα εὑρεθήσεται (for conjectures as to the meaning of this reading, v. Mayor, ICC , in l.), II Pe 3:10 , WH, R, mg. Metaph., to find, find out by inquiry, learn, discover
G2147.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary , which (together with another cognate form) is used for it in all the tenses except the present and imperfect; to find (literally or figuratively)

G4299.mounce: previous judgment, prejudice, or, preference, partiality, 1 Tim. 5:21*
G4299.strongs: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G2919 (κρίνω); a prejudgment (prejudice), i.e. prepossession

G1795.mounce: to impress a figure, instamp, engrave, 2 Cor. 3:7*
G1795.abbottSmith: to imprint, engrave : pass. ptcp., c. dat., II Co 3:7 .†
G1795.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G5179 (τύπος); to enstamp, i.e. engrave

G5578.thayer: a false prophet; one who in God's name teaches what is false.
G5578.mounce: a false prophet, one who falsely claims to speak by divine inspiration, whether as a foreteller of future events, or as a teacher of doctrines, Mt. 7:15; 24:24; Mk. 13:22; Acts 13:6; 1 Jn. 4:1; Rev. 16:13
G5578.abbottSmith: a false prophet (= cl. ψευδόμαντις ): Mt 7:15 24:11 , 24 , Mk 13:22 (v. Swete, in l.), Lk 6:26 , Ac 13:6 , II Pe 2:1 , I Jo 4:1 , Re 16:13 19:20 20:10 †
G5578.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G4396 (προφήτης); a spurious prophet, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor

G4908.mounce: intelligent, discerning, wise, prudent, Mt. 11:25; Lk. 10:21; Acts 13:7; 1 Cor. 1:19*
G4908.abbottSmith: intelligent, sagacious, understanding : Mt 11:25 , Lk 10:21 , Ac 13:7 , I Co 1:19 (LXX) .†
G4908.strongs: from G4920 (συνίημι); mentally put (or putting) together, i.e. sagacious

G2817.thayer: an inheritance, an heritage, regularly the gift of God to His chosen people, in the Old Testament: the Promised Land, in NT a possession viewed in one sense as present, in another as future; a share, participation.
G2817.mounce: an inheritance, patrimony, Mt. 21:38; Mk. 12:7; a possession, portion, property, Acts 7:5; 20:32; in NT a share, participation in privileges, Acts 20:32; Eph. 1:14; 5:5
G2817.abbottSmith: prop., an inherited property , an inheritance : Mt 21:38 , Mk 12:7 , Lk 12:13 20:14 ; in general, a possession , inheritance : Ac 7:5 , He 11:8 ; of the Messianic Kingdom and its blessings, Ac 20:32 , Ga 3:18 , Col 3:24 , Eph 1:14 , 18 5:5 , He 9:15 , I Pe 1:4 .†
G2817.strongs: from G2818 (κληρονόμος); heirship, i.e. (concretely) a patrimony or (genitive case) a possession

G2530.thayer: (a) in proportion as, according as, (b) because.
G2530.mounce: as, just as, according as, in proportion as, Acts 2:45; 4:35; inasmuch as, Lk. 1:7; 19:9; Acts 2:24; 17:31*
G2530.abbottSmith: in cl., according as , just as ( Ex 1:12 , 17 ): Ac 2:45 4:35 .
G2530.strongs: from G2596 (κατά); and G3739 (ὅς) and G5100 (τὶς); according to which certain thing, i.e. as far (or inasmuch) as

G767.mounce: pr. not marked; met. not noted, not remarkable, unknown to fame, ignoble, mean, inconsiderable, Acts 21:39*
G767.abbottSmith: without mark (in π. of an uncircumcised boy: Deiss., BS , 153; MM, s.v.). Metaph. (MM, s.v.), unknown , obscure : litotes, οὐκ ἄ (Eur., al.), πόλις , Ac 21:39 .†
G767.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); unmarked, i.e. (figuratively) ignoble

G5218.mounce: a hearkening to; obedience, Rom. 5:19; 6:16; 1 Pet. 1:14; submissiveness, Rom. 16:19; 2 Cor. 7:15; submission, Rom. 1:5; 15:18; 16:26; 2 Cor. 10:5; Heb. 5:8; 1 Pet. 1:2, 22; compliance, Phlm. 21
G5218.strongs: from G5219 (ὑπακούω); attentive hearkening, i.e. (by implication) compliance or submission

G2058.mounce: interpretation, explanation, 1 Cor. 14:26; meton. the power or faculty of interpreting, 1 Cor. 12:10*
G2058.strongs: from the same as G2059 (ἑρμηνεύω); translation

G4835.abbottSmith: affected by like feelings, sympathetic : I Pe 3:8 .
G4835.strongs: from G4841 (συμπάσχω); having a fellow-feeling ("sympathetic"), i.e. (by implication) mutually commiserative

G4836.thayer: I come together with, stand by one, help.
G4836.mounce: to be present together with; to come together, convene, Lk. 23:48*
G4836.abbottSmith: to come up or be present together : seq. ἐπί , Lk 23:48 .
G4836.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3854 (παραγίνομαι); to be present together, i.e. to convene; by implication, to appear in aid

G1257.thayer: I cease, give over, give up.
G1257.mounce: to leave an interval; to intermit, cease, Lk. 7:45*
G1257.abbottSmith: to intermit, leave off for a time: Lk 7:45 .†
G1257.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave off in the middle, i.e. intermit

G866.mounce: not fond of money, not covetous, generous, 1 Tim. 3:3; Heb. 13:5*
G866.abbottSmith: without love of money , not avaricious ; I Ti 3:3 , He 13:5 . (For other instances, v. MM, s.v.)†
G866.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5366 (φιλάργυρος); unavaricious
G866.kjv: without covetousness, not greedy of filthy lucre

G5233.thayer: I transgress; I go beyond, overreach, defraud.
G5233.mounce: to overstep; to wrong, transgress, 1 Thess. 4:6*
G5233.abbottSmith: trans., to step over, trans­gress (R, overreach : I Th 4:6 ).
G5233.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to transcend, i.e. (figuratively) to overreach

G4614.mounce: Mount Sinai, in Arabia, Acts 7:30, 38; Gal. 4:24, 25*
G4614.abbottSmith: indecl. (but τὸ Σιναῖον , FlJ, Ant. , iii, 5, 1; τὸ Σιναῖον ὄρος , ib. ii, 12, 1), τό
G4614.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05514); Sina (i.e. Sinai), a mountain in Arabia

G2587.thayer: Cappadocia, a large Roman province in the central eastern part of Asia Minor.
G2587.mounce: Cappadocia, a district of Asia Minor, Acts 2:9; 1 Pet. 1:1*
G2587.abbottSmith: Cappadocia , a province of Asia Minor: Ac 2:9 , I Pe 1:1 .†
G2587.strongs: of foreign origin; Cappadocia, a region of Asia Minor

G5247.mounce: prominence; met., excellence, rare quality, 1 Cor. 2:1; eminent station, authority, 1 Tim. 2:2*
G5247.abbottSmith: a projection, eminence , as the peak of a mountain. Metaph. (Arist.), excellence, pre-eminence : λόγου ἢ σοφίας , I Co 2:1 ; οἱ ἐν ὑ. (for a parallel to this phrase, v. Deiss., BS , 255, and cf. II Mac, l.c. ), I Ti 2:2 .†
G5247.strongs: from G5242 (ὑπερέχω); prominence, i.e. (figuratively) superiority (in rank or character)

G773.thayer: the Roman province of Asia, roughly the western third of Asia Minor.
G773.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Asia, i.e. Asia Minor, or (usually) only its western shore

G3942.thayer: a cryptic saying, an allegory; a proverb, figurative discourse.
G3942.mounce: a by-word, proverb, adage, 2 Pet. 2:22; in NT an obscure saying, enigma, Jn. 16:25, 29; a parable, similitude, figurative discourse, Jn. 10:6*
G3942.abbottSmith: a wayside saying (Hesych.; v. LS, s.v.), a byword , maxim , proverb : II Pe 2:22 .
G3942.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and perhaps a derivative of G3633 (οἴομαι); apparently a state alongside of supposition, i.e. (concretely) an adage; specially, an enigmatical or fictitious illustration

G3315.mounce: to perform offices between two parties; to intervene, interpose, Heb. 6:17*
G3315.abbottSmith: to interpose , mediate : ὅρκῳ , He 6:17 (MM, ii, iii).†
G3315.strongs: from G3316 (μεσίτης); to interpose (as arbiter), i.e (by implication) to ratify (as surety)

G4635.abbottSmith: making tents ; as subst., ὁ σ. , a tent-maker : Ac 18:3 .†
G4635.strongs: from G4633 (σκηνή) and G4160 (ποιέω); a manufacturer of tents

G4819.mounce: to step or come together; to happen, meet, fall out, Mk. 10:32; Lk. 24:14 ; Acts 3:10; 20:19; 21:35; 1 Cor. 10:11; 1 Pet. 4:12; 2 Pet. 2:22*
G4819.abbottSmith: to stand with the feet together .
G4819.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to walk (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. concur (take place)

G1699.abbottSmith: poss. prop. of first pers., representing the em­phasized gen. ἐμοῦ ,
G1699.strongs: from the oblique cases of G1473 (ἐγώ) (G1698 (ἐμοί), G1700 (ἐμοῦ), G1691 (ἐμέ)); my

G951.abbottSmith: confirmation : τ. εὐαγγελίου , Phl 1:7 ; εἰς β. , He 6:16 , a phrase freq. in π. of guarantee in a business transaction (Deiss., BS , 104 ff.; Cremer, 140).†

G2604.mounce: one who announces anything, a proclaimer, publisher, Acts 17:18*
G2604.abbottSmith: a proclaimer , herald (in Inscr., κ. ἀγώνων , Deiss., LAE , 97): c. gen. obj., Ac 17:18 .†

G3701.mounce: a vision, apparition, Lk. 1:22; 24:23; Acts 26:19; 2 Cor. 12:1*
G3701.abbottSmith: an appearing , coming into view ( Ma 3:2 , Es 4:7 , Si 43:2 , 16 ).
G3701.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); visuality, i.e. (concretely) an apparition

G4303.thayer: I predict, testify or protest beforehand.
G4303.mounce: pr. to witness or testify beforehand; to declare beforehand, predict, 1 Pet. 1:11*
G4303.abbottSmith: to protest beforehand (cf. μαρτύρομαι , and V. Hort, in l.): I Pe 1:11 (elsewhere only in Theod. Met., xiv/AD.).†
G4303.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3143 (μαρτύρομαι); to be a witness in advance i.e. predict

G4516.thayer: Rome, the famous city on the Tiber, the capital of the Roman Empire.
G4516.strongs: from the base of G4517 (ῥώννυμι); strength; Roma, the capital of Italy

G5217.thayer: I go away, depart, begone, die.
G5217.mounce: to lead or bring under; to lead or bring from under; draw on or away; in NT intrans. to go away, depart, Mt. 8:4, 13; 9:6; ὕπαγε ὀπίσω μου, Get behind me! Away! Begone! Mt. 4:10; 16:23; to go, Mt. 5:41; Lk. 12:58; to depart life, Mt. 26:24
G5217.abbottSmith: a word of the vulgar language, in Pres. and impf. only (Bl., § 53, 1).
G5217.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead (oneself) under, i.e. withdraw or retire (as if sinking out of sight), literally or figuratively

G2574.abbottSmith: camel : Mt 3:4 , Mk 1:6 ; in proverbs, Mt 19:24 23:24 , Mk 10:25 , Lk 18:25 (on the v.l. κάμιλος , v. WH, Notes , 151).†

G5422.thayer: I deceive the mind, impose upon.
G5422.mounce: to deceive the mind; to deceive, impose on, Gal. 6:3*
G5422.abbottSmith: to deceive one's mind (Lft., deceive by fancies , v. Ga. , l.c.): c. acc. pers., Ga 6:3 .†
G5422.strongs: from G5423 (φρεναπάτης); to be a mind-misleader, i.e. delude

G2150.mounce: reverential feeling; piety, devotion, godliness, Acts 3:12; 1 Tim. 2:2; 4:7, 8; religion, the Christian religion, 1 Tim. 3:16
G2150.strongs: from G2152 (εὐσεβής); piety; specially, the gospel scheme

G4170.thayer: I make war, contend, fight, battle.
G4170.mounce: to make or wage war, fight, Rev. 2:16; 12:7; to battle, quarrel, Jas. 4:2
G4170.abbottSmith: to make war , fight : Re 12:7b 19:11 ; seq. μετά ( II Ki 21:15 and v. M, Pr. , 106, 247), Re 2:16 12:7a 13:4 17:14 ; hyperb., of private quarrels, Ja 4:2 .†
G4170.strongs: from G4171 (πόλεμος); to be (engaged) in warfare, i.e. to battle (literally or figuratively)

G4559.mounce: fleshly; pertaining to the body, corporeal, physical, Rom. 15:27; 1 Cor. 9:11; carnal, pertaining to the flesh, 1 Pet. 2:11; carnal, low in spiritual knowledge and frame, 1 Cor. 3:3 (2x); carnal, human as opposed to divine, 2 Cor. 1:12; 10:4*
G4559.strongs: from G4561 (σάρξ); pertaining to flesh, i.e. (by extension) bodily, temporal, or (by implication) animal, unregenerate

G4228.abbottSmith: a foot , both of men and beasts: Mt 4:6 (LXX) , Mk 9:45 , Lk 1:79 , Jo 11:44 , Ac 7:5 , al.; ὑπο τοὺς π. , Ro 16:20 , I Co 15:25 , 27 , Eph 1:22 , He 2:8 ; ὑποκάτω τῶν π. , Mt 22:44 (LXX) ; πρὸς ( παρὰ ) τοὺς π. , Mk 5:22 , Lk 8:41 , al.; fig., Mt 15:30 , Lk 10:39 , Ac 5:2 , al.; ἔμπροσθεν τῶν π. , Re 3:9 19:10 , al.; ἐπὶ τοὺς π. , Ac 10:25 . By meton., of a person in motion ( Ps 118 (119):101 ):
G4228.strongs: a primary word; a "foot" (figuratively or literally)

G4403.mounce: the hinder part of a vessel, stern, Mk. 4:38; Acts 27:29, 41*
G4403.abbottSmith: prop. fem. of adj. πρύμνος , -η , -ον , (sc. ναῦς ), the hindmost part of a ship, the stern : Mk 4:38 , Ac 27:29 ; opp. to πρῷρα , Ac 27:41 .†
G4403.strongs: feminine of (hindmost); the stern of a ship

G5605.thayer: I am in travail, suffer birth-pangs.
G5605.mounce: to be in travail, Gal. 4:27; Rev. 12:2; met. to travail with, suffer birth pangs, to make effort to bring to spiritual birth, Gal. 4:19*
G5605.abbottSmith: to have birthpangs, to travail : Ga 4:27 (LXX) , Re 12:2 ; metaph., Ga 4:19 (cf. συν-ωδίνω ).†
G5605.strongs: from G5604 (ὠδίν); to experience the pains of parturition (literally or figuratively)

G3881.mounce: to gather a course along; to sail by, coast along, Acts 27:8, 13*
G3881.abbottSmith: to lay beside ; mid., to lie beside (Hom.); later, of sailors, to sail past : c. acc., Ac 27:8 , 13 .†
G3881.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the middle voice of G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense); (specially), to lay one's course near, i.e. sail past

G4522.strongs: from a derivative of (to equip) meaning furniture, especially a pack-saddle (which in the East is merely a bag of netted rope); a "seine" for fishing

G1559.thayer: I persecute, expel by persecuting, drive out, vex, harass.
G1559.mounce: pr. to chase away, drive out; in NT to persecute, vex, harass, 1 Thess. 2:15*
G1559.abbottSmith: to chase away, drive out : I Th 2:15 (cf. De 6:19 , Jl 2:20 ).†
G1559.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1377 (διώκω); to pursue out, i.e. expel or persecute implacably

G2070.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2070.strongs: first person plural indicative of G1510 (εἰμί); we are
G2070.kjv: are, be, have our being, X have hope, + (the gospel) was (preached unto) us

G3318.thayer: Mesopotamia, the Country between the (two) Rivers, i.e. the Euphrates and the Tigris.
G3318.mounce: Mesopotamia, the country lying between the rivers Tigris and Euphrates, Acts 2:9; 7:2*
G3318.strongs: from G3319 (μέσος) and G4215 (ποταμός); Mesopotamia (as lying between the Euphrates and the Tigris; compare 0763), a region of Asia

G4792.thayer: I bear away together with, as in carrying away a corpse.
G4792.mounce: to prepare for burial, take charge of the funeral of any one, bury, Acts 8:2*
G4792.abbottSmith: to bring together, collect (Hdt., Xen., al.).
G4792.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2865 (κομίζω); to convey together, i.e. collect or bear away in company with others

G3363.strongs: i.e. G2443 (ἵνα) and G3361 (μή); in order (or so) that not

G1170.thayer: so and so, such a one, where the name of the person is known but not used.
G1170.mounce: such a one, a certain one, Mt. 26:18*
G1170.abbottSmith: such an one, a certain one , whom one cannot or will not name: Mt 26:18 .†
G1170.strongs: probably from the same as G1171 (δεινῶς) (through the idea of forgetting the name as fearful, i.e. strange); so and so (when the person is not specified)

G3498.thayer: (a) adj: dead, lifeless, subject to death, mortal, (b) noun: a dead body, a corpse.
G3498.mounce: dead, without life, Mt. 11:5; 22:31; met. νεκρός τινι, dead to a thing, no longer devoted to, or under the influence of a thing, Rom. 6:11; dead in respect of fruitlessness, Jas. 2:17, 20, 26; morally or spiritually dead, Rom. 6:13; Eph. 5:14; dead in alienation from God, Eph. 2:1, 5; Col. 2:13; subject to death, mortal, Rom. 8:10; causing death and misery, fatal, having a destructive power, Heb. 6:1; 9:14
G3498.abbottSmith: dead , as adj. , prop.: Ac 5:10 20:9 , Ja 2:26 , Re 1:18 , al.; ὡσεὶ ν. , Mt 28:4 , Mk 9:26 , Re 1:17 ; of that which is subject to death, Ro 8:10 . Metaph., of persons: Lk 15:24 , 32 ; of those immersed in worldly cares, Mt 8:22 , Lk 9:60 ; of spiritual death, Jo 5:25 , Ro 6:13 , Eph 5:14 , Re 3:1 ; τ. παραπτώμασιν , Eph 2:1 , 5 , Col 2:13 ; of the opposite condition, ν. τῇ ἁμαρτίᾳ , Ro 6:11 ; of
G3498.strongs: from an apparently primary (a corpse); dead (literally or figuratively; also as noun)

G4034.abbottSmith: to shine around : c. acc., Lk 2:9 , Ac 26:13 .†
G4034.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2989 (λάμπω); to illuminate all around, i.e. invest with a halo

G1407.mounce: an instrument with a curved blade, as a sickle, Mk. 4:29; Rev. 14:14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19*
G1407.strongs: from (to pluck); a gathering hook (especially for harvesting)

G2551.thayer: I speak evil of, curse, revile, abuse.
G2551.mounce: to speak evil of, revile, abuse, insult, Mk. 9:39; Acts 19:9; to address with offensive language, to treat with disrespect, Matt, 15:4; Mk. 7:10*
G2551.abbottSmith: to speak ill of , revile , abuse : c. acc. pers., Mt 15:4 , Mk 7:10 (LXX) 9:39 ; c. acc. rei, Ac 19:9 .†
G2551.strongs: from a compound of G2556 (κακός) and G3056 (λόγος); to revile

G784.mounce: spotless, unblemished, pure, 1 Tim. 6:14; Jas. 1:27; 1 Pet. 1:19; 2 Pet. 3:14*
G784.abbottSmith: spotless , unstained : I Pe 1:19 ; metaph., I Ti 6:14 , Ja 1:27 , II Pe 3:14 (for exx., v. MM, s.v.).†
G784.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4695 (σπιλόω); unblemished (physically or morally)

G3156.strongs: a shorter form of G3164 (μάχομαι); Matthæus (i.e. Matthitjah), an Israelite and a Christian

G1392.thayer: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on.
G1392.mounce: to think, suppose, judge; to extol, magnify, Mt. 6:2; Lk. 4:15; in NT to adore, worship, Rom. 1:21; to invest with dignity or majesty, 2 Cor. 3:10; Heb. 5:5; to signalize with a manifestation of dignity, excellence, or majesty, Jn. 12:28; 13:32; to glorify by admission to a state of bliss, to beatify, Rom. 8:30
G1392.abbottSmith: to think, suppose, hold an opinion (Æsch., Plat., al.).
G1392.strongs: from G1391 (δόξα); to render (or esteem) glorious (in a wide application)
G1392.kjv: (make) glorify(-ious), full of (have) glory, honour, magnify

G796.thayer: a flash of lightning, brightness, luster.
G796.abbottSmith: lightning : Mt 24:27 28:3 , Lk 10:18 17:24 ; pl., Re 4:5 8:5 11:19 16:18 ; of a lamp, shining brightness , Lk 11:36 .†
G796.strongs: from G797 (ἀστράπτω); lightning; by analogy, glare

G1458.thayer: I bring a charge against, accuse.
G1458.mounce: can be followed by a dative, to bring a charge against, accuse; to institute judicial proceedings, Acts 19:38, 40; 23:28, 29; 26:2, 7; Rom. 8:33
G1458.abbottSmith: to call in, demand . to bring a charge against, accuse : c. dat. pers. (as in cl.), Ac 19:38 23:28 ; seq. κατά , c. gen. pers., Ro 8:33 . Pass., to be accused : c. gen. rei; στάσεως , Ac 19:40 ; ὦν (perh. by attraction = ἅ ), Ac 26:2 ; seq. περί , c. gen. rei, Ac 23:29 26:7 (Cremer, 743).†
G1458.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2564 (καλέω); to call in (as a debt or demand), i.e. bring to account (charge, criminate, etc.)
G1458.kjv: accuse, call in question, implead, lay to the charge

G1742.mounce: clothing, a garment, Mt. 6:25, 28; 22:11, 12; in particular, an outer garment, cloak, mantle, Mt. 3:4; 7:15; 28:3; Lk. 12:23*
G1742.abbottSmith: raiment, clothing, a garment : Mt 3:4 6:25 , 28 7:15 22:11-12 28:3 , Lk 12:23 .†
G1742.strongs: from G1746 (ἐνδύω); apparel (especially the outer robe)

G1808.mounce: pr. to lift up out of; in NT to remove, eject, 1 Cor. 5:13*
G1808.abbottSmith: to lift up, lift off the earth, remove : I Co 5:13 (LXX) †
G1808.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G142 (αἴρω); to remove

G2892.strongs: of Latin origin; "custody", i.e. a Roman sentry

G5101.abbottSmith: who, which, what? ; in neut., which, what? , used both in direct and in indirect questions. As subst., masc., fem.: τίς ; who, what? , Mt 3:7 26:68 ; Mk 11:28 , Lk 9:9 , al. mult.; c. gen. partit., Ac 7:52 , He 1:5 , al; seq. ἐκ (= gen. partit.), Mt 6:27 , Lk 14:28 , Jo 8:46 ; = ποῖος , Mk 4:41 6:2 , Lk 19:3 , Ac 17:19 , al.; = πότερος (M, Pr. , 77), Mt 21:31 27:17 , Lk 22:27 , al.; = ὅς or ὅστις
G5101.strongs: probably emphatic of G5100 (τὶς); an interrogative pronoun, who, which or what (in direct or indirect questions)
G5101.kjv: every man, how (much), + no(-ne, thing), what (manner, thing), where (-by, -fore, -of, -unto, - with, -withal), whether, which, who(-m, -se), why

G5268.thayer: a beast of burden, an ass or mule.
G5268.mounce: an animal subject to the yoke, a beast of burden; in NT spc. an ass, donkey, Mt. 21:5; 2 Pet. 2:16*
G5268.abbottSmith: under the yoke ; as subst., τὸ ζ. (Hdt., al.), a beast of burden ; colloq., an ass (v. Deiss., BS , 160 f.), and so always in LXX and NT: Mt 21:5 (LXX) , II Pe 2:16 .†
G5268.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G2218 (ζυγός); an animal under the yoke (draught-beast), i.e. (specially), a donkey

G4739.thayer: I stand fast, stand firm, persevere.
G4739.mounce: to stand, Mk. 3:31; 11:25; met. to stand when under judgment, to be approved, Rom. 14:4; to stand firm, be constant, persevere, 1 Cor. 16:13; Gal. 5:1; Phil. 1:27; 4:1; 1 Thess. 3:8; 2 Thess. 2:15*
G4739.abbottSmith: = ἵστημι , to stand : Mk 3:31 11:25 , Jo 1:26 , Re 12:4 ( ἕστηκεν T). Metaph., to stand firm, stand fast : absol., Ga 5:1 (but v. Field, Notes , 189 f.), II Th 2:15 ; seq. ἐν , Jo 8:44 ( ἕστηκεν , T, R, mg.), I Co 16:13 , Phl 1:27 4:1 ; c. dat., Ro 14:4 .†
G4739.strongs: from the perfect tense of G2476 (ἵστημι); to be stationary, i.e. (figuratively) to persevere

G1940.thayer: I sit upon, cause to sit upon.
G1940.mounce: to cause to sit upon, seat upon, Mt. 21:7 (where some mss read ἐπεκάθισεν, intrans. to sit upon)*
G1940.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2523 (καθίζω); to seat upon

G2759.thayer: a sting, goad; met: of death.
G2759.mounce: a sharp point; a sting of an animal, Rev. 9:10; a prick, stimulus, goad, Acts 26:14; met. of death, destructive power, deadly venom, 1 Cor. 15:55, 56*
G2759.abbottSmith: a sting ( IV Mac 14:19 ): Re 9:10 . Metaph. (as Ho 13:14 ): τ. θανάτου , I Co 15:55 (LXX) , 56 .
G2759.strongs: from (to prick); a point ("centre"), i.e. a sting (figuratively, poison) or goad (figuratively, divine impulse)

G4514.mounce: Roman; a Roman citizen, Jn. 11:48; Acts 2:10; 16:21
G4514.strongs: from G4516 (Ῥώμη); Romæan, i.e. Roman (as noun)

G4038.abbottSmith: round about : αἱ π. πολεῖς , Ac 5:16 .†
G4038.strongs: adverb from G4012 (περί); all around, i.e. (as an adjective) circumjacent

G4543.thayer: Samothrace, an island south of the province of Thrace.
G4543.mounce: Samothrace, an island in the northern part of the Aegean sea, Acts 16:11*
G4543.abbottSmith: Samothrace , an island in the Ægean: Ac 16:11 .†
G4543.strongs: from G4544 (Σάμος) and (Thrace); Samo-thrace (Samos of Thrace), an island in the Mediterranean

G3103.thayer: (Hebrew) Methuselah, son of Enoch and father of Lamech.
G3103.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04968); Mathusala (i.e. Methushelach), an antediluvian

G724.thayer: the act of plundering; plunder, spoil, robbery.
G724.mounce: plunder, pillage; the act of plundering, Heb. 10:34; prey, spoil, Mt. 23:25; Lk. 11:39*
G724.abbottSmith: pillage , plundering , robbery : Mt 23:25 , Lk 11:39 , He 10:34 .†

G4994.mounce: encourage, to restore to a right mind; to make sober-minded, to steady by exhortation and guidance, Tit. 2:4*
G4994.abbottSmith: to make σώφρων , recall one to his senses, control (RV, train ): c. acc. pers., Tit 2:4 .†
G4994.strongs: from G4998 (σώφρων); to make of sound mind, i.e. (figuratively) to discipline or correct

G1967.mounce: This word occurs nowhere else in Greek literature except in the context of the Lord’s prayer. Guesses include, necessary for today, necessary for tomorrow, daily, sufficient, Mt. 6:11; Lk. 11:3*
G1967.abbottSmith: found only in the phrase ἄρτος ἐ. , EV, daily ; R, mg., for the coming day : Mt 6:11 , Lk 11:3 . (Several derivations find support, each pointing to a different meaning.
G1967.strongs: perhaps from the same as G1966 (ἐπιοῦσα); tomorrow's; but more probably from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of the present participle feminine of G1510 (εἰμί); for subsistence, i.e. needful

G3660.thayer: I swear, take an oath, promise with an oath.
G3660.mounce: to swear, Mt. 5:34; to promise with an oath, Mk. 6:23; Acts 2:30; 7:17
G3660.abbottSmith: to swear , affirm by oath : Mt 26:74 , Mk 14:71 , He 7:21 ; seq. εἰ (q.v.), He 3:11 4:3 ; c. dat. pers., Mk 6:23 ; id. c. inf., He 3:18 ; seq. ὅρκῳ , Ac 2:30 ; ὀ. ὅρκον πρός , c. acc. pers. (cl.), Lk 1:73 ; c. acc. (of that by which one swears; cl., v. MM, xviii), Ja 5:12 ; seq. κατά , c. gen. (LXX; Bl., § 34, 1), He 6:13 , 16 ; seq. ἐν ( εἰς ), as in Heb. (Bl., § 39, 4), Mt 5:34 , 36 23:16 , 18 ,
G3660.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary, but obsolete , for which another prolonged form is used in certain tenses; to swear, i.e. take (or declare on) oath

G3723.strongs: adverb from G3717 (ὀρθός); in a straight manner, i.e. (figuratively) correctly (also morally)

G4549.thayer: Saul, (a) the first king of Israel, (b) the Hebrew name of the Apostle to the Gentiles.
G4549.mounce: Saul, pr. name, indecl. I. Saul, king of Israel, Acts 13:21; II. The Apostle Paul, Acts 9:4, 17; 22:7, 13; 26:14*
G4549.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07586); Saul (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul

G635.thayer: I cause to go astray; pass: I am led astray.
G635.mounce: to cause to wander; met. to deceive, pervert, seduce, Mk. 13:22; pass. to wander; met. to swerve from, apostatize, 1 Tim. 6:10*
G635.abbottSmith: to cause to go astray ; metaph., of leading into error: Mk 13:22 ; pass., to be led astray : I Ti 6:10 .†
G635.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4105 (πλανάω); to lead astray (figuratively); passively, to stray (from truth)

G1100.thayer: the tongue, a language, a nation (usually distinguished by their speech).
G1100.mounce: the tongue, Mk. 7:33, 35, et al.; meton. speech, talk, 1 Jn. 3:18; a tongue, language, Acts 2:11; 1 Cor. 13:1, et al.; meton. a language not proper to a speaker, a gift or faculty of such language, Mk. 16:17; 1 Cor. 14:13, 14, 26, et al.; from Hebrew, a nation, as defined by its language, Rev. 5:9, et al.; lit. a tongue-shaped flail, Acts 2:3
G1100.abbottSmith: the tongue , as the organ of speech: Mk 7:33 , 35 Lk 1:64 16:24 , Ac 2:26 (LXX) , Ro 3:13 (LXX) 14:11 (LXX) , I Co 14:9 , Phl 2:11 , Ja 1:26 3:5 , 6 , 8 , I Pe 3:10 (LXX) , I Jo 3:18 , Re 16:10 ; of a tongue-like object, Ac 2:3 . a tongue, language : Ac 2:11 ; joined with φυλή, λαός, ἔθνος , freq. in pl., Re 5:9 7:9 10:11 11:9 13:7 14:6 17:15 ; λαλεῖν ἑτέραις γ. , Ac 2:4 ; γ. λαλεῖν καιναῖς , WH,
G1100.strongs: of uncertain affinity; the tongue; by implication, a language (specially, one naturally unacquired)

G658.thayer: I complete, accomplish, form fully, perfect, bring to maturity.
G658.mounce: to complete; pass. to be perfected, to arrive at full stature or measure, Lk. 13:32; Jas. 1:15*
G658.abbottSmith: to bring to an end , complete , accomplish (cf. MM, s.v.): Lk 13:32 , Ja 1:15 .†
G658.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5055 (τελέω); to complete entirely, i.e. consummate

G3996.mounce: trans. to lament over, 2 Cor. 12:21; absol. to lament, be sad, mourn, Mt. 5:4; 9:15; Mk. 16:10; mid. to bewail one’s self, to feel guilt, 1 Cor. 5:2
G3996.abbottSmith: to mourn ( for ), lament ; intrans. : Mt 5:4 ( 5 ) 9:15 , I Co 5:2 ; π. καὶ κλαίειν , Mt 16:10 , Lk 6:25 , Ja 4:9 , Re 18:15 , 19 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc., Re 18:11 ; trans., c. acc., II Co 12:21 .†
G3996.strongs: from G3997 (πένθος); to grieve (the feeling or the act)

G1278.mounce: pr. to be thoroughly exercised with labor; to be wearied; to be vexed, Acts 4:2; 16:18*
G1278.abbottSmith: to work out with labour . Pass., to be worn out, sore troubled : Mk 14:4 (WH, mg.), Ac 4:2 16:18 .†
G1278.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G4192 (πόνος); to toil through, i.e. (passively) be worried

G4417.thayer: I stumble, fall, sin, err, transgress.
G4417.mounce: to cause to stumble; intrans. to stumble, stagger, fall; to make a false step; met. to err, transgress, Rom. 11:11; Jas. 2:10; 3:2 (2x); met. to fail of an object, 2 Pet. 1:10*
G4417.abbottSmith: trans., to cause to stumble ( I Ki 4:3 , cf. Deiss., BS , 68 1 ).
G4417.strongs: a form of G4098 (πίπτω); to trip, i.e. (figuratively) to err, sin, fail (of salvation)

G875.abbottSmith: to foam at the mouth : Mk 9:18 , 20 .†
G875.strongs: from G876 (ἀφρός); to froth at the mouth (in epilepsy)

G1899.mounce: thereupon, then, after that, in the next place, afterwards, Mk. 7:5; Lk. 16:7
G1899.abbottSmith: thereupon, thereafter, then : Lk 16:7 , Ga 1:21 , Ja 4:14 ; seq. μετὰ τοῦτο , Jo 11:7 ; μετὰ ἔτη τρία , Ga 1:18 ; διὰ δεκατεσσάρων ἐτῶν , Ga 2:1 ; πρῶτον . . . ἔ. , I Co 15:46 , I Th 4:17 , He 7:2 ; πρότεφον . . . ἔ , He 7:27 ; ἀπαρχὴ . . . ἔ. , I Co 15:23 ; εἶτα . . . ἔ. , I Co 15:5 , 6 (WH, txt.); . . . ἔ. , I Co 15:5-7 (WH, mg.); τρίτον . . . ἔ. (his), I Co 12:28 .†

G4391.thayer: I have been already, have been previously.
G4391.mounce: to be before, or formerly, Lk. 23:12; Acts 8:9*
G4391.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5225 (ὑπάρχω); to exist before, i.e. (adverbially) to be or do something previously

G646.mounce: a falling away, a rebellion, apostasy, Acts 21:21; 2 Thess. 2:3*
G646.abbottSmith: defection , apostasy , revolt ; in late Gk. (MM, Exp. , viii; Lft., Notes , 111; Cremer, 308) for cl. ἀπόστασις , freq. in sense of political revolt, in LXX (e.g. Jos 22:22 , II Ch 29:19 , Je 2:19 ) and NT always of religious apostasy: Ac 21:21 , II Th 2:3 .†
G646.strongs: feminine of the same as G647 (ἀποστάσιον); defection from truth (properly, the state) ("apostasy")

G5271.thayer: I act the part, pretend; I answer, respond.
G5271.mounce: to answer, respond; to act a part upon the stage; hence, to assume a counterfeit character; to pretend, feign, Lk. 20:20*
G5271.abbottSmith: = Att.. ἀποκρίνομαι (q.v.), to answer, reply (Hom., Hdt., al.).
G5271.strongs: middle voice from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2919 (κρίνω); to decide (speak or act) under a false part, i.e. (figuratively) dissemble (pretend)

G1661.strongs: from (an "elephant"); elephantine, i.e. (by implication) composed of ivory

G4797.thayer: I bewilder, stir up, throw into confusion.
G4797.mounce: to pour together, mingle by pouring together; hence, to confound, perplex, amaze, Acts 2:6; to confound in dispute, Acts 9:22; to throw into confusion, fill with uproar, Acts 19:32; 21:27, 31*
G4797.abbottSmith: to pour together, commingle, confuse ; metaph., confound, throw into confusion, stir up trouble : Ac 21:27 (cf. συν-χύννω ).†
G4797.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to pour) or its alternate; to commingle promiscuously, i.e. (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder, to perplex (the mind)
G4797.kjv: confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar

G1379.thayer: I subject to regulations, decree; mid: I subject myself to regulations, am decree-ridden.
G1379.mounce: to decree, prescribe an ordinance; mid. to suffer laws to be imposed on one’s self, to submit to, bind one’s self by, ordinances, Col. 2:20*
G1379.abbottSmith: to decree . Mid., to subject oneself to an ordinance : Col 2:20 .†
G1379.strongs: from G1378 (δόγμα); to prescribe by statute, i.e. (reflexively) to submit to, ceremonially rule

G408.thayer: Andronicus, a member of the Roman church, and a kinsman or fellow-tribesman of Paul.
G408.strongs: from G435 (ἀνήρ) and G3534 (νῖκος); man of victory; Andronicos, an Israelite

G3554.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a malady (rarely figuratively, of moral disability)

G5035.mounce: swift, fleet, quick; met. ready, prompt, Jas. 1:19; Mt. 28:7f.; Mk. 9:39; Lk. 15:22; Jn. 11:29
G5035.abbottSmith: quickly, speedily, forthwith : Mt 5:25 28:7 , 8 , Mk 9:39 , Lk 15:22 , Jo 11:29 , Re 2:16 3:11 11:14 22:7 , 12 , 20 . Compar., τάχειον (T, Rec. τάχιον ), = cl. θᾶσσον, θᾶττον (Att..), (v. Bl., § 44, 3): Jo 13:27 20:4 , I Ti 3:14 (T, Rec.), He 13:19 , 23 Superl., τάχιστα : ὡς τ. , as quickly as possible (BL, § 11, 3), Ac 17:15 .†
G5035.strongs: neuter singular of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); shortly, i.e. without delay, soon, or (by surprise) suddenly, or (by implication, of ease) readily

G4022.thayer: I go around, move about, visit; I make a circuit, tack (as a ship).
G4022.mounce: to go about, wander about, rove, Acts 19:13; Heb. 11:37; to go about, visit from house to house, 1 Tim. 5:13*
G4022.abbottSmith: to go about (as an itinerant): Ac 19:13 28:13 ( περιελόντες , WH, R, mg.), He 11:37 ; τ. οἰκίας , from house to house : I Ti 5:13 .†
G4022.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι) (including its alternate); to come all around, i.e. stroll, vacillate, veer
G4022.kjv: fetch a compass, vagabond, wandering about

G3434.thayer: Moloch, a god worshipped by several Semitic peoples.
G3434.abbottSmith: Moloch , the god of the Ammonites: Ac 7:43 (LXX) .†
G3434.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04432); Moloch (i.e. Molek), an idol

G4801.thayer: I yoke together, unite (as man and wife).
G4801.mounce: to join together; trop. join together, unite, Mt. 19:6; Mk. 10:9*
G4801.abbottSmith: to yoke to­gether : metaph., of union in wedlock, Mt 19:6 , Mk 10:9 .†
G4801.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2201 (ζεῦγος); to yoke together, i.e. (figuratively) conjoin (in marriage)

G3908.thayer: (a) I set (especially a meal) before, serve, (b) act. and mid: I deposit with, entrust to, (c) I bring forward, quote as evidence.
G3908.mounce: to place by the side of, or near; to set before, Mk. 6:41; 8:6, 7; Lk. 9:16; met. to set or lay before, propound, Mt. 13:24, 31; to inculcate, Acts 17:3; to deposit, commit to the charge of, entrust, Lk. 12:48; 23:46; to commend, Acts 14:23
G3908.abbottSmith: Act., to place beside , set before , c. acc. et dat.: of food, Mk 6:41 8:6 , 7 , Lk 9:16 11:6 ; τράπεζαν , Ac 16:34 ; pass. ptcp., Lk 10:8 , I Co 10:27 ; metaph., of teaching, παραβολήν , Mt 13:24 , 31 .
G3908.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place alongside, i.e. present (food, truth); by implication, to deposit (as a trust or for protection)
G3908.kjv: allege, commend, commit (the keeping of), put forth, set before

G3164.thayer: I engage in battle, fight; hence: I strive, contend, dispute.
G3164.mounce: to fight; to quarrel, Acts 7:26; 2 Tim. 2:24; to contend, dispute, Jn. 6:52; Jas. 4:2*
G3164.strongs: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to war, i.e. (figuratively) to quarrel, dispute

G3479.mounce: an inhabitant of Nazareth, Mk. 1:24; 10:47; 4:67; 16:6; Lk. 4:34; 24:19*
G3479.strongs: from G3478 (Ναζαρέθ); a Nazarene, i.e. inhabitant of Nazareth

G1132.mounce: nakedness; want of proper and sufficient clothing, Rom. 8:35; 2 Cor. 11:27; spiritual nakedness, being destitute of spiritual clothing, Rev. 3:18*

G3145.mounce: to chew, masticate, in NT to gnaw, Rev. 16:10*
G3145.strongs: from a primary (to handle or squeeze); to chew

G3697.thayer: of what kind or manner, of what sort.
G3697.mounce: what, of what sort or manner, 1 Cor. 3:13; Gal. 2:6; 1 Thess. 1:9; Jas. 1:24; after τοιοῦτος, as, Acts 26:29*
G3697.abbottSmith: of what sort : I Co 3:13 , Ga 2:6 , I Th 1:9 , Ja 1:24 ; τοιοῦτος ὁ. , such as , Ac 26:29 .†
G3697.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G4169 (ποῖος); of what kind that, i.e. how (as) great (excellent) (specially, as an indefinite correlative to the definite antecedent G5108 (τοιοῦτος) of quality)
G3697.kjv: what manner (sort) of, such as whatsoever

G935.thayer: a king, ruler, but in some passages clearly to be translated: emperor.
G935.mounce: a king, monarch, one possessing regal authority
G935.abbottSmith: a king : Mt 1:6 2:1 ; used by courtesy of Herod the Tetrarch, Mt 14:9 ; of the Roman Emperor, as freq. in κοινή (Deiss., LAE , p. 367), I Pe 2:13 , 17 ; of the Christ, in the phrase ὁ β. τ. Ἰουδαίων , Mt 2:2 , al.; τοῦ Ἰσραήλ , Mk 15:32 , Jo 1:50 12:13 ; of God, Mt 5:35 , I Ti 1:17 , Re 15:3 ; β. βασιλέων , Re 17:14 19:16 ; β. τ. βασιλευόντων , I Ti 6:15 (on the associations of the word to Jewish
G935.strongs: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power); a sovereign (abstractly, relatively, or figuratively)

G3987.mounce: to try, attempt, essay, endeavor, Acts 26:21*
G3987.strongs: from G3984 (πεῖρα); to test (subjectively), i.e. (reflexively) to attempt

G1923.mounce: an inscription; a legend of a coin, Mt. 22:20; Mk. 12:16; Lk. 20:24; a label of a criminal’s name and offence, Mk. 15:26; Lk. 23:38*

G1667.thayer: I roll, roll up, fold up.
G1667.mounce: to roll, fold up, as garments, Heb. 1:12; Rev. 6:14*
G1667.abbottSmith: to roll, roll up : He 1:12 (LXX) , Re 6:14 .†
G1667.strongs: a form of G1507 (εἱλίσσω); to coil or wrap

G1042.thayer: Gabbatha, a sort of paved square, on which the procurator had his judgment seat.
G1042.abbottSmith: Gabbatha , the Greek transliteration of an uncertain Aramaic word ( DB , s.v.; Dalman, Words , 7), used as the equivalent of λιθόστρωτον , stone pavement : Jo 19:13 .†
G1042.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01355); the knoll; gabbatha, a vernacular term for the Roman tribunal in Jerusalem

G813.thayer: (lit: out of order), disorderly, slack (in performance of duty).
G813.mounce: pr. used of soldiers, disorderly; met. irregular in conduct, disorderly, lazy, 1 Thess. 5:14*
G813.abbottSmith: out of order , out of place (Lat. inordinatus ), freq. of soldiers not keeping the ranks, or an army in disarray (cf. III Mac 1:19 ); hence, metaph., irregular , disorderly (v. previous word): I Th 5:14 .†
G813.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5021 (τάσσω); unarranged, i.e. (by implication) insubordinate (religiously)

G3400.thayer: a Roman mile, measuring 1478.5 meters or 5820.9 feet.
G3400.mounce: a Roman mile, which contained mille passuum, 1000 paces, or 8 stadia, 4,854 feet, Mt. 5:41*
G3400.strongs: of Latin origin; a thousand paces, i.e. a "mile"

G2823.abbottSmith: in cl., a clibanus , cribanus , an earthen vessel for baking bread.
G2823.strongs: of uncertain derivation; an earthen pot used for baking in

G347.thayer: I lay upon, lean against, lay down, make to recline; pass: I lie back, recline.
G347.mounce: to lay down, Lk. 2:7; to cause to recline at table, etc. Mk. 6:39; Lk. 9:15; 12:37; to recline at table, Mt. 8:11
G347.abbottSmith: to lay upon , lean against , hence, to lay down : Lk 2:7 ; to make to recline : Mk 6:39 , WH, mg., Lk 12:37 . Pass., to lie back , recline : Mt 8:11 14:19 , Lk 13:29 .†
G347.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2827 (κλίνω); to lean back

G1072.abbottSmith: to fill ; properly, of a ship (Thuc, al.): Mk 4:37 (pass.). In late writers, generally (MGr., v. Kennedy, Sources , 155), c. acc., seq. gen., Mk 15:36 , Jo 2:7 6:13 , Re 15:8 ; ἀπό , Lk 15:16 , Rec. WH, mg.; ἐκ , Lk 15:16 , WH, Re 8:5 . Pass., absol.: Lk 14:23 .†
G1072.strongs: transitive from G1073 (γέμω); to fill entirely

G3584.mounce: dry, withered, Lk. 23:31; ἡ χηρά, i.e. γῆ, the dry land, land, Mt. 23:15; Heb. 11:29; of parts of the body, withered, Mt. 12:10
G3584.abbottSmith: dry : metaph. (of a sinner), ξύλον ξ. , Lk 23:31 ; of members of the body shrunken by disease, withered : Jo 5:3 ; of the hand, Mt 12:10 , Mk 3:3 , Lk 6:6 , 8 ; of the dry land, ἡ ξηρά (sc. γῆ , cf. Ge 1:9 , 10 ; Jh 1:19 , al.): Mt 23:15 ; γῆ , He 11:29 .†
G3584.strongs: from the base of G3582 (ξέστης) (through the idea of scorching); arid; by implication, shrunken, earth (as opposed to water)

G4713.mounce: can be masculine, but not in the NT, a wine jar; a pot, jar, urn, vase, Heb. 9:4*
G4713.abbottSmith: prop., an earthen jar for racking off wine, hence, generally, a jar : He 9:4 .†
G4713.strongs: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι) (as stationary); a jar or earthen tank

G4244.thayer: an assembly of elders, the Sanhedrin, officers of the church assembly, presbytery.
G4244.mounce: a body of old men, an assembly of elders; the Jewish Sanhedrin, Lk. 22:66; Acts 22:5; a body of elders in the Christian church, a presbytery, 1 Tim. 4:14*
G4244.abbottSmith: a body of elders , presbytery : of the Sanhedrin (cf. συνέδριον ), Lk 22:66 , Ac 22:5 ; of Christian presbyters, I Ti 4:14 .†
G4244.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4245 (πρεσβύτερος); the order of elders, i.e. (specially), Israelite Sanhedrin or Christian "presbytery"

G1253.thayer: distinguishing; hence: deciding, passing sentence on; the act of judgment, discernment.
G1253.mounce: a separation; a distinction, or, doubt, Rom. 14:1; a discerning, the act of discerning or distinguishing, Heb. 5:14; the faculty of distinguishing and estimating, 1 Cor. 12:10*
G1253.abbottSmith: the act of judgment, discernment : Ro 14:1 , I Co 12:10 , He 5:14 .†

G1178.abbottSmith: late form of πεντεκαίδεκα , fifteen : Jo 11:18 , Ac 27:5 , WH, mg., ib. 28 , Ga 1:18 .†
G1178.strongs: from G1176 (δέκα) and G4002 (πέντε); ten and five, i.e. fifteen

G776.thayer: either: lack or wheat, lack of food (the literal meaning), or: abstinence from food, a fast, loss of appetite, sea-sickness (the extended meaning).

G2209.abbottSmith: damage , loss : Ac 27:10 , 21 , Phl 3:8 ; opp. to κέρδος , Phl 3:7 .†
G2209.strongs: probably akin to the base of G1150 (δαμάζω) (through the idea of violence); detriment

G3139.mounce: a white glistening stone; marble, Rev. 18:12*

G1562.thayer: I put off, take off, strip off, with acc. of person or garment or both.
G1562.mounce: pr. to go out from; to take off, strip, unclothe, Mt. 27:28, 31; mid. to lay aside, to put off, Mk. 15:20; Lk. 10:30; 2 Cor. 5:3f.*
G1562.abbottSmith: to take of, strip of, strip : c. acc pers. (sc. clothing), Mt 27:28 ; c. acc pers. et rei, Mt 27:31 , Mk 15:20 , Lk 10:30 ; mid., to put off : fig., of the body, II Co 5:4 .†
G1562.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G1416 (δύνω); to cause to sink out of, i.e. (specially as of clothing) to divest

G3794.mounce: a stronghold; met. an opposing bulwark of error or vice, 2 Cor. 10:4*
G3794.abbottSmith: a stronghold , fortress ( Ps 88 (89):40 , Na 3:12 , 14 al.); in LXX and NT, also metaph. of that in which confidence is placed ( Pr 10:29 21:22 , al.): II Co 10:4 .†
G3794.strongs: from a remote derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning to fortify, through the idea of holding safely); a castle (figuratively, argument)

G1283.mounce: to plunder, spoil, pillage, Mt. 12:29; Mk. 3:27 (2x)*
G1283.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G726 (ἁρπάζω); to seize asunder, i.e. plunder

G4542.abbottSmith: in FlJ ( B.J. , I, 21, 2, al.), the region of Samaria .
G4542.strongs: feminine of G4541 (Σαμαρείτης); a Samaritess, i.e. woman of Samaria

G1082.thayer: Gennesaret, a fertile district by the lake of Tiberias, which was in consequence sometimes called the Lake of Gennesaret.
G1082.mounce: Gennesaret, a lake of Palestine, called also the Sea of Tiberias, Mt. 14:34; Mk. 6:53; Lk. 5:1*
G1082.abbottSmith: Gennesaret , a fertile plain on W. shore of the Sea of Galilee; ἡ γῆ , Mt 14:34 , Mk 6:53 ; ἡ λίμνη ( יָם כִּנֶּרֶת , Nu 34:11 , elsewhere ἡ θάλασσα τ. Γαλιλαίας , Mk 1:16 ; ἡ θ. τ. Τιβεριάδος , Jo 6:1 ), Lk 5:1 .†
G1082.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H03672); Gennesaret (i.e. Kinnereth), a lake and plain in Palestine

G5047.mounce: completeness, perfectness, Col. 3:14; ripeness of knowledge or practice, maturity, Heb. 6:1*
G5047.strongs: from G5046 (τέλειος); (the state) completeness (mentally or morally)

G3592.thayer: this here, this, that, he, she, it.
G3592.mounce: this, that, he, she, it, Lk. 10:39; 16:25; Acts 15:23
G3592.abbottSmith: referring prop. to what is present, can be seen or pointed out: of a person just named, τῇδε (= ταύτῃ ), Lk 10:39 ; neut. pl., τάδε ( λέγει ), referring to words which follow (so in Att., and v. MM, xvii): Ac 21:11 , Re 2:1 , 8 , 12 , 18 3:1 , 7 , 14 ; εἰς τήνδε τ. πόλιν (= Att. τῇ καὶ τῇ , Plat., Legg. , iv, 721 B), such and such a city , Ja 4:13 .†
G3592.strongs: from G3588 (ὁ) and G1161 (δέ); the same, i.e. this or that one (plural these or those); often used as a personal pronoun

G815.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5043 (τέκνον); childless

G5439.mounce: to deliver into custody, put in prison, imprison, Acts 22:19*

G3824.mounce: a new birth; regeneration, renovation, Mt. 19:28; Tit. 3:5. See unpublished Ph.D. dissertation, William D. Mounce, The Origin of the New Testament Metaphor of Rebirth, University of Aberdeen, Scotland*
G3824.abbottSmith: new birth , renewal , restoration , regeneration ; of persons (Plut., Phil., al.), of spiritual regeneration, διὰ λουτροῦ παλινγενεστίας (gen. attr., v. Ellic., in l.), Tit 3:5 ; of the world, as in Stoics, Jewish Apocal., al. (v. Dalman, Words , 177 ff.), Mt 19:28 .†
G3824.strongs: from G3825 (πάλιν) and G1078 (γένεσις); (spiritual) rebirth (the state or the act), i.e. (figuratively) spiritual renovation; specially, Messianic restoration

G3829.mounce: a public inn, place where travelers may lodge, called in the East by the name of menzil, khan, caravanserai, Lk. 10:34*
G3829.strongs: neuter of a presumed compound of G3956 (πᾶς) and a derivative of G1209 (δέχομαι); all-receptive, i.e. a public lodging-place (caravanserai or khan)

G4523.thayer: a Sadducee, a member of the aristocratic party among the Jews, from whom the high-priests were almost invariably chosen.
G4523.mounce: a Sadducee, one belonging to the sect of the Sadducees, which, according to the Talmudists, was founded by one, Sadoc, about three centuries before the Christian era: they were directly opposed in sentiments to the Pharisees, Mt. 3:7; 16:1, 6, 11, 12; 22:23, 34; Mk. 12:18; Lk. 20:27; Acts 4:1; 5:17; 23:6-8*
G4523.strongs: probably from G4524 (Σαδώκ); a Sadducæan (i.e. Tsadokian), or follower of a certain heretical Israelite

G4987.mounce: to heap or pile up, Rom. 12:20; met. pass. to be filled with sins, 2 Tim. 3:6*
G4987.abbottSmith: to heap on : c. acc. seq. ἐπί c. acc., Ro 12:20 (LXX) (Arist., al.);
G4987.strongs: from another form of G4673 (σορός); to pile up (literally or figuratively)

G209.thayer: (characteristic of legal documents), without hindrance, freely.
G209.abbottSmith: without hindrance (so freq. in legal documents; MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 28:31 .†
G209.strongs: adverb from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2967 (κωλύω); in an unhindered manner, i.e. freely

G2403.thayer: Jezebel, name given to a false prophetess of Thyatira, possibly borrowed from the name of Ahab's wife, queen of Israel.
G2403.mounce: Jezebel, (1 Ki. 16:31), pr. name, indecl., Rev. 2:20*
G2403.abbottSmith: Jezebel ( III Ki 16:31 , al.): symbolically, Re 2:20 (v. Swete, in l.).†
G2403.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0348); Jezabel (i.e. Jezebel), a Tyrian woman (used as a synonym of a termagant or false teacher)

G315.mounce: to force, compel, Acts 28:19; to constrain, urge, Lk. 14:23
G315.abbottSmith: to necessitate , compel by force or persuasion, constrain : c. acc., II Co 12:11 ; id. c. inf., Mt 14:22 , Mk 6:45 , Lk 14:23 , Ac 26:11 (on the impf. here, v. Field, Notes , 141; M, Pr. , 128 f., 247), Ga 2:14 6:12 ; pass., C. inf., Ac 28:19 , Ga 2:3 (for exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G2302.thayer: (a) a theatre, a semi-circular stone building, generally open to the sky, (b) a spectacle, show.
G2302.mounce: a theater, a place where public games and spectacles are exhibited, Acts 19:29, 31; meton. a show, gazing-stock, 1 Cor. 4:9*
G2302.abbottSmith: a theatre (used also as a place of assembly): Ac 19:29 , 31 .
G2302.strongs: from G2300 (θεάομαι); a place for public show ("theatre"), i.e. general audience-room; by implication, a show itself (figuratively)

G1601.thayer: I fall out, fall off, fall away; hence in nautical language: I fall off from the straight course; of flowers: I fade away, wither away; I fall from, lose, forfeit; I am cast ashore; I am fruitless.
G1601.mounce: to fall off or from, Acts 12:7; 27:32; met. to fall from, forfeit, lose, Gal. 5:4; 2 Pet. 3:17; to be cast ashore, Acts 27:17, 26, 29; to fall to the ground, be fruitless, ineffectual, Rom. 9:6; to cease, come to an end, Jas. 1:11; 1 Pet. 1:24*
G1601.abbottSmith: to fall out of, fall from, fall off : seq. ἐκ τ. χειρῶν , Ac 12:7 ; absol., Ac 27:32 ; of the withering of flowers (as LXX, ll c.), Ja 1:11 , I Pe 1:24 (LXX) ; of navigators falling off from a straight course, Ac 27:17 , 26 , 29 . Metaph., c. gen. rei: Ga 5:4 , II Pe 3:17 ; absol., fall from its place, fail, perish : Ro 9:6 .†
G1601.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4098 (πίπτω); to drop away; specially, be driven out of one's course; figuratively, to lose, become inefficient
G1601.kjv: be cast, fail, fall (away, off), take none effect

G4472.thayer: I sprinkle, cleanse ceremonially by sprinkling.
G4472.mounce: to sprinkle, Heb. 9:13, 19, 21; met. and by impl. to cleanse by sprinkling, purify, free from pollution, Heb. 10:22*
G4472.strongs: from a derivative of (to sprinkle); to render besprinkled, i.e. asperse (ceremonially or figuratively)

G4730.thayer: a narrow space, great distress, anguish.
G4730.mounce: pr. narrowness of place, a narrow place; met. straits, distress, anguish, Rom. 2:9; 8:35; 2 Cor. 6:4; 12:10*
G4730.abbottSmith: narrowness of space, want of room (Thuc., al.). Metaph. (Xen., Polyb., De 28:53 , Wi 5:3 , al.), difficulty, distress : θλίψις καὶ ( ἢ ) σ. , Ro 2:9 8:35 (EV, anguish ); pl. (cf. Bl., § 32, 6; WM, 220), II Co 6:4 12:10 .†
G4730.strongs: from a compound of G4728 (στενός) and G5561 (χώρα); narrowness of room, i.e. (figuratively) calamity

G4491.thayer: a root, shoot, source; that which comes from the root, a descendent.
G4491.mounce: a root of a tree, Mt. 3:10; 13:6; met. ἔχειν ῥίζαν, or ἔχειν ῥίζαν ἐν ἑαυτῷ, to be rooted in faith, Mt. 13:21; Mk. 4:17; Lk. 8:13; met. cause, source, origin, 1 Tim. 6:10; Heb. 12:15; by synec. the trunk, stock of a tree, met. Rom. 11:16, 17, 18; met. offspring, progeny, a descendant, Rom. 15:12; Rev. 5:5; 22:16
G4491.abbottSmith: a root : Mt 3:10 , Lk 3:9 ; ἐκ ῥιζῶν , Mk 11:20 ; ῥ. ἔχειν , Mt 13:6 , Mk 4:6 ; id. seq. ἐν ἑαυτῷ , fig., Mt 13:21 , Mk 4:17 , Lk 8:13 . Metaph. (as in various senses in cl.), of cause, origin, source, etc.;
G4491.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "root" (literally or figuratively)

G1635.abbottSmith: willing, of one's own free will : Ro 8:20 , I Co 9:17 (Cremer, 246).†

G5467.mounce: hard, rugged; furious, ferocious, Mt. 8:28; difficult, trying, 2 Tim. 3:1*
G5467.strongs: perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of reducing the strength; difficult, i.e. dangerous, or (by implication) furious

G3311.thayer: (a) a distributing, a distribution, (b) a parting, dividing, severance, separation.
G3311.mounce: a dividing, act of dividing, Heb. 4:12; distribution, gifts distributed, Heb. 2:4*
G3311.abbottSmith: a dividing , division : ψυχῆς κ. πνεύματος (i.e. between them or of the things themselves, v. Westc., in l.), He 4:12 .
G3311.strongs: from G3307 (μερίζω); a separation or distribution

G5252.thayer: I have high notions, am over-proud.
G5252.mounce: to have lofty thoughts, be elated, haughty, Rom. 12:3*
G5252.abbottSmith: to be overproud, high-minded (Æsch.): μὴ ὑ. παρ’ ὃ δεῖ φρονεῖν (on the paranom., v. Vau., in l.), Ro 12:3 .
G5252.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5426 (φρονέω); to esteem oneself overmuch, i.e. be vain or arrogant

G2448.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03063 or perhaps H03194); Judah (i.e. Jehudah or Juttah), a part of (or place in) Palestine

G5547.mounce: pr. anointed; ὁ Χριστός, the Christ, the Anointed One, i.e. Μεσσίας, the Messiah, Mt. 1:16, 17; Jn. 1:20, 25, 42; meton. Christ, the word or doctrine of Christ, 2 Cor. 1:19; 21; Eph. 4:20; Christ, a truly Christian frame of doctrine and affection, Rom. 8:10; Gal. 4:19; Christ, the Church of Christ, 1 Cor. 12:12; Christ the distinctive privileges of the Gospel of Christ, Gal. 3:27; Phil. 3:8; Heb. 3:14
G5547.abbottSmith: as adj. , of things, anointing, to be used as ointment (Æsch., Eur., al.; τ. ἔλαιον τὸ χ. , Le 21:10 ); of persons, anointed ( ὁ ἰερεὺς ὁ χ. , Le 4:5 ; οἱ χ. ἰευρεῖς , II Mac 1:10 ): ὁ χ. τοῦ κυρίου or Θεοῦ ( I Ki 2:10 , Ps 2:2 , al.), of the Messiah (Aram., מְשִׁיחָא ; cf. Dalman, Words , 289 ff.), Lk 2:11 , 26 Jo 1:41 , Ac 2:36 4:26 , al. As subst., ὁ Χριστός , the Messiah, the Christ : Mt 2:4 ,
G5547.strongs: from G5548 (χρίω); anointed, i.e. the Messiah, an epithet of Jesus

G288.abbottSmith: vine : Mt 26:29 , Mk 14:25 , Lk 22:18 Ja 3:12 ; fig., of Christ, Jo 15:1 , 4-5 ; of his enemies (on the usage here, v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Re 14:18 , 19 .†
G288.strongs: probably from the base of G297 (ἀμφότερος) and that of G257 (ἅλων); a vine (as coiling about a support)

G254.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a fetter or manacle

G1809.thayer: I demand of, ask for, beg earnestly for.
G1809.mounce: to ask for; to demand; mid. to demand for one’s self, Lk. 22:31; also, to obtain by asking*
G1809.abbottSmith: to ask from ; mid., to ask for oneself, demand : aor., ἐξῃτήσατο , c. acc ( obtained you by asking , R, mg.), Lk 22:31 (v. Field, Notes , 76; Cremer, 73).†
G1809.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G154 (αἰτέω); to demand (for trial)

G2161.thayer: Eutychus, a young hearer of Paul at Troas.
G2161.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5177 (τυγχάνω); well- fated, i.e. fortunate; Eutychus, a young man

G2757.mounce: vain, empty babbling, vain disputation, fruitless discussion, 1 Tim. 6:20; 2 Tim. 2:16*
G2757.abbottSmith: empty talk , babbling : pl., I Ti 6:20 , II Ti 2:16 .†
G2757.strongs: from a presumed compound of G2756 (κενός) and G5456 (φωνή); empty sounding, i.e. fruitless discussion

G1669.thayer: I afflict with sores; pass: I am afflicted with sores.
G1669.mounce: pass. to be afflicted with ulcers, Lk. 16:20*
G1669.strongs: from G1668 (ἕλκος); to cause to ulcerate, i.e. (passively) be ulcerous

G2918.thayer: a lily growing wild, variously identified with the red anemone, the whole lily, the sword lily.
G2918.strongs: perhaps a prim word; a lily

G1799.thayer: before the face of, in the presence of, in the eyes of.
G1799.mounce: gen., before, in the presence of, Lk. 5:25; 8:47; in front of, Rev. 4:5, 6; immediately preceding as a forerunner, Lk. 1:17; Rev. 16:19; from the Hebrew, in the presence of, metaphysically, i.e. in the sphere of sensation or thought, Lk. 12:9; 15:10; Acts 10:31; in the eyes of, in the judgment of, Lk. 16:15; 24:11; Acts 4:19
G1799.abbottSmith: face to face, in sight (Theocr.; ἄρτοι ἐ. , Ex 25:29 ): neut., ἐνώπιον , in vernacular, with force of prep. c. gen. [in LXX for לְעֵינֵי , לִפְנֵי , etc., cf. Dalman, Words , 31 f., 209 f., and Deiss., BS ., 213], in NT, most freq. in Lk, Ac, Re, never in Mt, Mk, before, in the presence of : Lk 1:19 4:7 , Ac 4:10 6:5 , Re 1:4 2:14 , al.; esp. ἐ. Κυρίου ( θεοῦ ), in the sight of God, or with God as
G1799.strongs: neuter of a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); in the face of (literally or figuratively)
G1799.kjv: before, in the presence (sight) of, to

G3870.thayer: (a) I send for, summon, invite, (b) I beseech, entreat, beg, (c) I exhort, admonish, (d) I comfort, encourage, console.
G3870.mounce: to call for, invite to come, send for, Acts 28:20; to call upon, exhort, admonish, persuade, Lk. 3:18; Acts 2:40; 11:23; to beg, beseech, entreat, implore, Mt. 8:5, 31; 18:29; Mk. 1:40; to animate, encourage, comfort, console, Mt. 2:18; 5:4; 2 Cor. 1:4, 6; pass. to be cheered, comforted, Lk. 16:25; Acts 20:12; 2 Cor. 7:13
G3870.abbottSmith: to call to one, call for , summon : Ac 28:20 (R, mg.; R, txt., entreat ); hence (of the gods: Dem., Xen., al.), to invoke , call on , beseech , entreat : τ. πατέρα μου , Mt 26:53 ; τ. κύριον , II Co 12:8 ; in late writers (Polyb., Diod., al.; rarely in LXX; in π. , v. Deiss., LAE , 176 14 ), also of men: absol., Phm 9 ; c. acc., Mt 8:5 , Mk 1:40 , Ac 16:9 , al.; c. inf., Mk 5:17 , Lk 8:41 , Ac 8:3
G3870.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2564 (καλέω); to call near, i.e. invite, invoke (by imploration, hortation or consolation)
G3870.kjv: beseech, call for, (be of good) comfort, desire, (give) exhort(-ation), intreat, pray

G4229.thayer: a thing done, a deed, action; a matter, an affair.
G4229.mounce: a thing done, fact, deed, work, transaction, Lk. 1:1; Jas. 3:16; a matter, affair, Mt. 18:19; Rom. 16:2; a matter of dispute, 1 Cor. 6:1; a thing, genr., Heb. 10:1; 11:1; τὸ πρᾶγμα, a euphemism for unlawful sexual conduct, perhaps, 1 Thess. 4:6
G4229.abbottSmith: that which has been done, a deed , act : Lk 1:1 , Ac 5:4 , II Co 7:11 , He 6:18 .
G4229.strongs: from G4238 (πράσσω); a deed; by implication, an affair; by extension, an object (material)

G3074.thayer: a wolf, of perhaps a jackal; often applied to persons of wolfish proclivities.
G3074.mounce: a wolf, Mt. 10:16; Lk. 10:3; Jn. 10:12; met. a person of wolf-like character, Mt. 7:15; Acts 20:29*
G3074.strongs: perhaps akin to the base of G3022 (λευκός) (from the whitish hair); a wolf

G2251.abbottSmith: our : Ac 2:11 24:6 26:5 , Ro 15:4 , II Ti 4:15 , I Jo 1:3 2:2 ; τὸ ἡ. , Lk 16:12 , WH, txt., R, mg. ( τὸ ὑμ. , Rec., WH, mg., R, txt.); οἱ ἡ. , our (people), Tit 3:14 .†

G2338.mounce: female; τὸ θῆλυ, i.e. γένος, a female, Mt. 19:4; Mk. 10:6; Gal. 3:28; ἡ θήλεια, woman, Rom. 1:26, 27*
G2338.abbottSmith: female : as subst., ἡ θ. , a female , a woman : Ro 1:26 , 27 ; τὸ θ. , Mt 19:4 , Mk 10:6 , Ga 3:28 .†
G2338.strongs: from the same as G2337 (θηλάζω); female

G5026.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5026.strongs: dative case, accusative case and genitive case respectively of the feminine singular of G3778 (οὗτος); (towards or of) this
G5026.kjv: her, + hereof, it, that, + thereby, the (same), this (same)

G5052.mounce: to bring to maturity, as fruits, etc.; met. Lk. 8:14*
G5052.abbottSmith: of plants, to bring fruit to perfection (Theophr.): Lk 8:14
G5052.strongs: from a compound of G5056 (τέλος) and G5342 (φέρω); to be a bearer to completion (maturity), i.e. to ripen fruit (figuratively)

G3416.thayer: Mnason, an early Christian, native of Cyprus, resident at a place between Caesarea and Jerusalem.
G3416.strongs: of uncertain origin; Mnason, a Christian

G1634.thayer: I breathe my last, die, expire.
G1634.mounce: to expire, give up one’s spirit, Acts 5:5, 10; 12:23*
G1634.abbottSmith: to expire, breathe one's last : Ac 5:5 , 10 12:23 (cf. ἐκπνέω ; Cremer, 906).†
G1634.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5594 (ψύχω); to expire

G4657.abbottSmith: refuse , esp. dung (v. MM, ii, iii, xxiii): Phl 3:8 .†
G4657.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G1519 (εἰς) and G2965 (κύων) and G906 (βάλλω); what is thrown to the dogs, i.e. refuse (ordure)

G1355.mounce: inferential conj., on this very account, for this very reason, wherefore, 1 Cor. 8:13; 10:14*
G1355.abbottSmith: for which very reason : I Co 8:13 10:14 .†
G1355.strongs: from G1352 (διό) and G4007 (περ); on which very account

G5584.thayer: I feel, touch, handle; I feel after, grope for.
G5584.mounce: to feel, handle, Lk. 24:39; to feel or grope for or after, as persons in the dark, Acts 17:27; Heb. 12:18; 1 Jn. 1:1*
G5584.abbottSmith: to feel or grope about ; c. acc, to feel about for, search after : metaph., of seeking God, Ac 17:27 .
G5584.strongs: from the base of G5567 (ψάλλω) (compare G5586 (ψῆφος)); to manipulate, i.e. verify by contact; figuratively, to search for

G1441.abbottSmith: seventy times : ἑ. ἑπτά , seventy times seven , Mt 18:22 (R, txt., ICC , in l.), or seventy-seven times (R, mg.; cf. M, Pr. , 98; WM, 314; Meyer, in l.).†
G1441.strongs: multiple adverb from G1440 (ἑβδομήκοντα); seventy times

G407.thayer: I act like a man, am brave.
G407.mounce: to render brave or manly; mid. to show or behave one’s self like a man, 1 Cor. 16:13*
G407.abbottSmith: to make a man of . Mid., to play the man (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.): I Co 16:13 .†
G407.strongs: middle voice from G435 (ἀνήρ); to act manly

G3168.mounce: majesty, magnificence, glory, Lk. 9:43; Acts 19:27; 2 Pet. 1:16*
G3168.abbottSmith: splendour , magnificence : Lk 9:43 , Ac 19:27 , II Pe 1:16 (freq. in π. as a ceremonial title, MM, xvi).†
G3168.strongs: from G3167 (μεγαλεῖος); superbness, i.e. glory or splendor

G4932.thayer: I cut short, bring to swift accomplishment, determine, decide.
G4932.mounce: pr. to cut short, contract by cutting off; met. to execute speedily, or from the Hebrew, to determine, decide, decree, Rom. 9:28 (2x)*
G4932.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); to contract by cutting, i.e. (figuratively) do concisely (speedily)

G756.mounce: (1) pr. (act.) to be first; to rule, Mk. 10:42; Rom. 15:12 (2) mid. to begin, Mt. 4:17; to take commencement, Lk. 24:27; 1 Pet. 4:17
G756.strongs: middle voice of G757 (ἄρχω) (through the implication, of precedence); to commence (in order of time)

G1909.thayer: on, to, against, on the basis of, at.
G1909.mounce: (1) with the gen., upon, on, Mt. 4:6; 9:2; 27:19; in, of locality, Mk. 8:4; near upon, by, at, Mt. 21:19; Jn. 21:1; upon, over, of authority, Mt. 2:22; Acts 8:27; in the presence of, especially in a judicial sense, 2 Cor. 7:14; Acts 25:9; in the case of, in respect of, Jn. 6:2; Gal. 3:16; in the time of, at the time of, Acts 11:28; Rom. 1:10; ἐπ’ ἀληθείας, really, bona fide, Mk. 12:32; (2) with the dat., upon, on, Mt. 14:8; Mk. 2:21; Lk. 12:44; close upon, by, Mt. 24:33; Jn. 4:6; in the neighborhood or society of, Acts 28:14; over, of authority, Mt. 24:47; to, of addition, besides, Mt. 25:20; Eph. 6:16; Col. 3:14; supervening upon, after, 2 Cor. 1:4; 7:4; immediately upon, Jn. 4:27; upon, of the object of an act, towards, to, Mk. 5:33; Lk. 18:7; Acts 5:35; against, of hostile posture or disposition, Lk. 12:52; in dependence upon, Mt. 4:4; Lk. 5:5; Acts 14:3; upon the ground of, Mt. 19:9; Lk. 1:59; Phil. 1:3; Heb. 7:11; 8:6; 9:17; with a view to, Gal. 5:13; 1 Thess. 4:7; (3) with the acc., upon, with the idea of previous or present motion, Mt. 4:5; 14:19, 26; towards, of place, to, Mt. 3:13; 22:34; towards, of the object of an action, Lk. 6:35; 9:38; against, of hostile movement, Mt. 10:21; over, of authority, Lk. 1:33; to the extent of, both of place and time, Rev. 21:16; Rom. 7:1; near, by, Mt. 9:9; about, at, of time, Acts 3:1; in order to, with a view to, for the purpose of, Mt. 3:7; Lk. 7:44
G1909.abbottSmith: with primary sense of superposition, on, upon . C. gen., of place, answering the question, where? of the place on which, on, upon : ἐπὶ (τ.) γῆς , Mt 6:10 , 19 , al.; τ. κεφαλῆς , I Co 11:10 ; τ. νεφελῶν , Mt 24:30 , al.; like ἐν , in constr. praegn. after verbs of motion: βάλλειν , Mk 4:26 ; σπείρειν , ib. 31 ; ἔρχεσθαι , He 6:7 , al.; fig., ἐπ’ ἀληθείας (MM, s.v. ἀ. ); of the subject of thought
G1909.strongs: a primary preposition; properly, meaning superimposition (of time, place, order, etc.), as a relation of distribution (with the genitive case), i.e. over, upon, etc.; of rest (with the dative case) at, on, etc.; of direction (with the accusative case) towards, upon, etc.
G1909.kjv: about (the times), above, after, against, among, as long as (touching), at, beside, X have charge of, (be-, (where-))fore, in (a place, as much as, the time of, -to), (because) of, (up-)on (behalf of), over, (by, for) the space of, through(-out), (un-)to(-ward), with

G4485.mounce: a rent; a crash, ruin, Lk. 6:49*
G4485.abbottSmith: cl. (and so in LXX), a fracture ; then by meton., that which is torn .
G4485.strongs: from G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); something torn, i.e. a fragment (by implication and abstractly, a fall)

G2756.thayer: (a) empty, (b) met: empty (in moral content), vain, ineffective, foolish, worthless, (c) false, unreal, pretentious, hollow.
G2756.mounce: empty; having nothing, empty-handed, Mk. 12:3; met. vain, fruitless, void of effect, Acts 4:25; 1 Cor. 15:10; εἰς κενόν, in vain, to no purpose, 2 Cor. 6:1; hollow, fallacious, false, Eph. 5:6; Col. 2:8; inconsiderate, foolish, 1 Thess. 3:5; Jas. 2:20
G2756.abbottSmith: empty ( Ge 37:24 , Jg 7:16 ). Metaph., empty , vain : λόγοι , Eph 5:6 ; ἀπάτη , Col 2:8 ; κήρυγμα, πίστις , I Co 15:14 ; vain , fruitless : ἡ χάρις , I Co 15:10 ; κόπος , I Co 15:58 ; ἡ εἴσοδος , I Th 2:1 ; κενά , Ac 4:25 (LXX) ; εἰς κενόν , in vain , to no purpose (Diod., LXX; cl. διὰ κενῆς ), II Co 6:1 , Ga 2:2 , Phl 2:16 , I Th 3:5 ; of persons, empty-handed : Mk 12:3 , Lk 1:53 20:10 , 11 ; vai
G2756.strongs: apparently a primary word; empty (literally or figuratively)

G3968.mounce: one’s native place, country, or city, Mt. 13:54, 57; Mk. 6:1, 4; Lk. 4:23, 24; Jn. 4:44; a heavenly country, Heb. 11:14*
G3968.abbottSmith: of one's fathers ; as subst., ἡ π. = πάτρα , fatherland , country , home , native place : Ac 18:27 , WH, mg., He 11:14 ; of one's own town, Mt 13:54 , 57 , Mk 6:1 , 4 , Lk 4:23 , 24 , Jo 4:44 .†
G3968.strongs: from G3902 (παράσημος); a father-land, i.e. native town; (figuratively) heavenly home

G4681.thayer: Spain, roughly co-extensive with the modern country of the name.
G4681.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Spania, a region of Europe

G4367.thayer: (a) I instruct, command, (b) I appoint, assign.
G4367.mounce: pr. to place or station at or against; to enjoin, command, direct, Mt. 1:24; 8:4; Mk. 1:44; to assign, constitute, appoint, Acts 17:26
G4367.abbottSmith: c. acc. pers., to place at , to attach to .
G4367.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange towards, i.e. (figuratively) enjoin

G5011.thayer: humble, lowly, in position or spirit (in a good sense).
G5011.mounce: low in situation; of condition, humble, poor, mean, depressed, Lk. 1:52; 2 Cor. 7:6; Jas. 1:9; met. of the mind, humble, lowly, modest, Mt. 11:29; Rom. 12:16; 2 Cor. 10:1; Jas. 4:6; 1 Pet. 5:5*
G5011.strongs: of uncertain derivation; depressed, i.e. (figuratively) humiliated (in circumstances or disposition)
G5011.kjv: base, cast down, humble, of low degree (estate), lowly

G513.strongs: probably from (to break; compare G4486 (ῥήγνυμι)); an axe

G3845.mounce: pr. to step by the side of; to deviate; met. to transgress, violate, Mt. 15:2, 3; to incur forfeiture, Acts 1:25*
G3845.abbottSmith: in Hom. (twice), to go by the side of , stand beside .
G3845.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to go contrary to, i.e. violate a command

G3459.thayer: a mill-house, the place where grain was ground.
G3459.abbottSmith: a mill-house : Mt 24:41 (Rec.; μύλος , WH, R).†

G1857.mounce: comparative in form but used as a superlative, outer, exterior, external, Mt. 8:12; 22:13; 25:30*

G1820.thayer: I am utterly without resource, am in despair.
G1820.mounce: some list as a deponent ἐξαπορέομαι, pass., to be in the utmost perplexity or despair, 2 Cor. 1:8; 4:8*
G1820.abbottSmith: so in NT, depon. pass., to be utterly at a loss, be in despair : absol. (as Ps, l.c.), II Co 4:8 ; τοῦ ζῆν , II Co 1:8 .†
G1820.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G639 (ἀπορέω); to be utterly at a loss, i.e. despond

G1989.thayer: I write, send by letter to.
G1989.mounce: to send word to, to send injunctions, Acts 15:20; 21:25; to write to, write a letter, Heb. 13:22*
G1989.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4724 (στέλλω); to enjoin (by writing), i.e. (genitive case) to communicate by letter (for any purpose)

G3828.thayer: Pamphylia, a Roman province on the south coast of Asia Minor.
G3828.mounce: Pamphylia, a country of Asia Minor, Acts 2:10; 13:13; 14:24; 15:38; 27:5*
G3828.abbottSmith: Pamphylia , a province of Asia Minor: Ac 2:10 13:13 14:24 15:38 27:5 .†
G3828.strongs: from a compound of G3956 (πᾶς) and G4443 (πυρά); every-tribal, i.e. heterogeneous (G5561 (χώρα) being implied); Pamphylia, a region of Asia Minor

G2384.thayer: (Hebrew), Jacob, (a) the patriarch, son of Isaac, (b) father of Joseph, the husband of Mary.
G2384.mounce: Jacob, pr. name, indecl. (1) Son of Issac, Matt 1:2. (2) Father of Joseph, Mary’s husband, Matt 1:15, 16
G2384.abbottSmith: Jacob ; The patriarch: Mt 1:2 8:11 , Jo 4:5 , 6 , Ac 7:8 , al,; as in Heb. (cf. Nu 23:7 , Is 41:8 , Si 23:12 , al.), of his descendants, Ro 11:26 (LXX) . The father-in-law of Mary: Mt 1:15 , 16 (on the form as distinct from that of the next word, v. Deiss., BS , 316 1 )
G2384.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03290); Jacob (i.e. Ja`akob), the progenitor of the Israelites

G551.thayer: untried, inexperienced, untempted, incapable of being tempted.
G551.mounce: inexperienced, untempted, incapable of being tempted, Jas. 1:13*
G551.abbottSmith: untempted , untried , without experience : Ja 1:13 (v. Hort, in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G551.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G3987 (πειράω); untried, i.e. not temptable

G3077.mounce: pain, distress, Jn. 16:21; grief, sorrow, Jn. 16:6, 20, 22; meton. cause of grief, trouble, affliction, 1 Pet. 2:19
G3077.abbottSmith: pain of body or mind, grief , sorrow : Jo 16:6 , II Co 2:7 ; opp. to χαρά , Jo 16:20 , He 12:11 ; ἀπὸ τῆς λ. , Lk 22:45 ; ἐκ λ. , II Co 9:7 ; ἡ κατὰ θεὸν λ. , opp. to ἡ τ. κόσμου λ. , II Co 7:10 ; λ. μοί ἐστιν , Ro 9:2 ; λ. ἔχω , Jo 16:21 , 22 ; id. seq. ἀπό , II Co 2:3 ; λ. ἐπὶ λ. ἔχω , Phl 2:27 ; ἐν λ. ἐλθεῖν , II Co 2:1 (to come sad and cause sadness); pl. (cf. Ge 3:16 , Pr 15:13 , al.), I Pe 2

G1587.thayer: I fail, die out, come to an end, am defunct.
G1587.mounce: to fail, die out, Lk. 22:32; to come to an end, Heb. 1:12; to be defunct, Lk. 16:9; 23:45*
G1587.abbottSmith: trans., to leave out, pass over .
G1587.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3007 (λείπω); to omit, i.e. (by implication) cease (die)

G1689.thayer: I look into (upon); met: I consider; I see clearly.
G1689.mounce: to look attentively, gaze earnestly, at an object, followed by εἰς, Mk. 6:26; Acts 1:11; to direct a glance, to look searchingly or significantly, at a person, followed by the dat., Mk. 10:21; 14:67; Lk. 22:61; absol. to see clearly, Mk. 8:25; Acts 22:11
G1689.abbottSmith: to look at : c. acc rei, Mk 8:25 ; c. dat. pers. (part., seq. λέγει, εἶπεν , cf. Xen., Cyr ., i, 3, 2), Mt 19:26 , Mk 10:21 , 27 14:67 , Lk 20:17 22:61 ( ἐνέβλεψεν ), Jo 1:36 , 43 ; absol., to look , Ac 22:11 ; metaph., to consider : Mt 6:26 .†
