G1689.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G991 (βλέπω); to look on, i.e. (relatively) to observe fixedly, or (absolutely) to discern clearly
G1689.kjv: behold, gaze up, look upon, (could) see

G116.thayer: Athens, the intellectual capital of Greece.
G116.strongs: plural of (the goddess of wisdom, who was reputed to have founded the city); Athenæ, the capitol of Greece

G3808.mounce: a little boy, child; a boy, lad, Jn. 6:9*
G3808.abbottSmith: a little boy , a lad (in late and colloq. Gk. the word seems to be used with greater latitude and even to lose its dimin. force entirely; cf. To 6:2 , 3 , and v. MM, xviii): Jo 6:9 .†
G3808.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3816 (παῖς); a little boy

G519.thayer: I choke, strangle; mid: I strangle or hang myself.
G519.mounce: to strangle; mid. to choke or strangle one’s self, hang one’s self, Mt. 27:5*
G519.abbottSmith: to strangle ; mid., to hang oneself (or, to choke ; v. M, Pr. , 155): Mt 27:5 .†
G519.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to choke; akin to the base of G43 (ἀγκάλη)); to strangle oneself off (i.e. to death)

G375.thayer: I send up (to a higher tribunal), send back.
G375.mounce: to leap up, stand up, Mk. 10:50*
G375.abbottSmith: to send up , to a higher place (Æsch., Plat., al.); to a higher authority (Deiss., BS, 229; MM, VGT , s.v.; cf. also Field, Notes , 140): Lk 23:7 , 15 , Ac 25:21 .
G375.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3992 (πέμπω); to send up or back

G516.thayer: worthily, in a manner worthy of.
G516.mounce: worthily, Col. 1:10; suitably, in a manner becoming, Rom. 16:2
G516.abbottSmith: worthily : Ro 16:2 , Eph 4:1 , Phl 1:27 ; c. gen. (freq. in Inscr.; Deiss., BS , 248; MM, VGT , 51), ἀ. τ. Κυρίου , Col 1:10 ; τ. θεοῦ I Th 2:12 , III Jo 6 .†
G516.kjv: as becometh, after a godly sort, worthily(-thy)

G638.thayer: (a) I suffocate, choke, drown, (b) I stop the growth of.
G638.mounce: to choke, suffocate, Lk. 8:7; to drown, Lk. 8:33*
G638.abbottSmith: to choke : Mt 13:7 , Lk 8:7 ; pass., of drowning (= καταποντίζομαι ), Lk 8:33 .†
G638.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4155 (πνίγω); to stifle (by drowning or overgrowth)

G538.thayer: I deceive, cheat, lead into error.
G538.mounce: to deceive, seduce into error, Eph. 5:6; 1 Tim. 2:14; Jas. 1:26*
G538.abbottSmith: to deceive : c. acc., Ja 1:26 ; c. acc. pers., dat. rei, Eph 5:6 ; pass., I Ti 2:14 (on its infrequency in late writers, v. MM, VGT , s.v.; cf. ἐξαπατάω ).†
G538.strongs: of uncertain derivation; to cheat, i.e. delude

G4850.mounce: a fellow elder, fellow presbyter, 1 Pet. 5:1*
G4850.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4245 (πρεσβύτερος); a co-presbyter

G1894.thayer: of time: when, now, after that; of cause: seeing that, forasmuch as.
G1894.mounce: since, because, in as much as, Mt. 21:46; Lk. 11:6; Acts 13:46
G1894.strongs: from G1893 (ἐπεί) and G1211 (δή); since now, i.e. (of time) when, or (of cause) whereas
G1894.kjv: after that, because, for (that, -asmuch as), seeing, since

G3494.thayer: a young man, youth; a man in his prime (used even of a man of 40).
G3494.mounce: a young man, youth, Acts 20:9; 23:17; used of one who is in the prime of life, Acts 7:58*
G3494.strongs: from a derivative of G3501 (νέος); a youth (up to about forty years)

G3025.mounce: pr. a tub, trough; a wine-press, into which grapes were cast and trodden, Rev. 14:19, 20; 19:15; a wine-vat, i.q. ὑπολήνιον, the lower vat into which the juice of the trodden grapes flowed, Mt. 21:33*
G3025.abbottSmith: a trough or vat ; esp. for the treading of grapes: Mt 21:33 , Re 14:20 19:16 ; τὴν λ. . . . τὸν μέγαν (a solecism perhaps inadvertent), Re 14:19 (cf. ὑπολήνιον ).†
G3025.strongs: apparently a primary word; a trough, i.e. wine-vat

G5179.thayer: (originally: the mark of a blow, then a stamp struck by a die), (a) a figure; a copy, image, (b) a pattern, model, (c) a type, prefiguring something or somebody.
G5179.mounce: pr. an impress; a print, mark, of a wound inflicted, Jn. 20:25; a delineation; an image, statue, Acts 7:43; a formula, scheme, Rom. 6:17; form, Acts 23:25; a figure, counterpart, 1 Cor. 10:6; an anticipative figure, type, Rom. 5:14; a model pattern, Acts 7:44; Heb. 8:5; a moral pattern, Phil. 3:17; 1 Thess. 1:7; 2 Thess. 3:9; 1 Tim. 4:12; Tit. 2:7; 1 Pet. 5:3*
G5179.abbottSmith: the mark of a blow: τῶν ἥλων , Jo 20:25 .
G5179.strongs: from G5180 (τύπτω); a die (as struck), i.e. (by implication) a stamp or scar; by analogy, a shape, i.e. a statue, (figuratively) style or resemblance; specially, a sampler ("type"), i.e. a model (for imitation) or instance (for warning)
G5179.kjv: en-(ex-)ample, fashion, figure, form, manner, pattern, print

G3100.thayer: I make a disciple of, train in discipleship; pass: I am trained, discipled, instructed.
G3100.mounce: intrans. to be a disciple, follow as a disciple, Mt. 27:57; in NT trans. to make a disciple of, to train in discipleship, Mt. 28:19; Acts 14:21; pass. to be trained, disciplined, instructed, Mt. 13:52*
G3100.abbottSmith: intrans. (as prop. vb. in -εύω , and so Plut., mor. 837 c. and elsew.), to be a disciple : c. dat., Mt 27:57 (Rec., WH, mg.).
G3100.strongs: from G3101 (μαθητής); intransitively, to become a pupil; transitively, to disciple, i.e. enrol as scholar

G4736.thayer: Stephen, one of the seven original deacons at Jerusalem, and the first martyr.
G4736.strongs: the same as G4735 (στέφανος); Stephanus, a Christian

G3160.strongs: probably a shortened form of G3161 (Ματταθίας) (compare G4992 (σωτήριον)); Mattatha (i.e. Mattithjah), an Israelite

G2690.mounce: to invert; to overturn, upset, overthrow, throw down, Mt. 21:12; Mk. 11:15*
G2690.abbottSmith: to turn down , turn over ; as, the soil.
G2690.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4762 (στρέφω); to turn upside down, i.e. upset

G3978.thayer: I travel on foot, by land.
G3978.mounce: pr. to travel on foot; to travel by land, Acts 20:13*
G3978.abbottSmith: to travel on foot or by land : Ac 20:13 .†
G3978.strongs: from the same as G3979 (πεζῇ); to foot a journey, i.e. travel by land

G5257.thayer: a servant, an attendant, (a) an officer, lictor, (b) an attendant in a synagogue, (c) a minister of the gospel.
G5257.mounce: pr. an under-rower, a rower, one of a ship’s crew; a minister, attendant, servant; an attendant on a magistrate, officer, Mt. 5:25; an attendant or officer of the Sanhedrin, Mt. 26:58; an attendant, or servant of a synagogue, Lk. 4:20; a minister, attendant, assistant in any work, Lk. 1:2; Jn. 18:36
G5257.abbottSmith: prop., an under rower ; hence, generally, a servant, attendant, minister : of a magistrate's attendant, Mt 5:25 ; of officers of the Synagogue or Sanhedrin, Mt 26:58 , Mk 14:54 , 65 , Lk 4:20 , Jo 7:32 , 45 , 46 18:3 , 12 , 22 19:6 , Ac 5:22 , 26 ; of the attendants of kings, οἱ ὑ. οἱ ἑμοί Jo 18:36 ; of Christian ministers, Ac 13:5 26:16 ; ὑπηρέται λόγου , Lk 1:2 ; Χριστοῦ , I Co 4:1 ; δοῦλοι κ. ὑ
G5257.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of (to row); an under-oarsman, i.e. (generally) subordinate (assistant, sexton, constable)

G1037.mounce: the bottom, lowest part; the deep, sea, 2 Cor. 11:25*
G1037.abbottSmith: the bottom . the depth of the sea, the deep sea : II Co 11:25 .†
G1037.strongs: a variation of G899 (βάθος); depth, i.e. (by implication) the sea

G1694.thayer: Emmanuel, a Messianic title derived from Isaiah 7:14 = God with us.
G1694.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06005); God with us; Emmanuel, a name of Christ

G3536.thayer: a Ninevite, an inhabitant of Nineveh, a city on the Tigris in Assyria.
G3536.mounce: a Ninevite, an inhabitant of Nineveh, Mt. 12:41; Lk. 11:30, 32*
G3536.strongs: from G3535 (Νινευΐ); a Ninevite, i.e. inhabitant of Nineveh

G1900.mounce: BDAG 361, say it is an adverb with the gen.; others classify it as an improper prep., gen., on yonder side, beyond, Acts 7:43*
G1900.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and (the accusative case plural neuter of) G1565 (ἐκεῖνος); upon those parts of, i.e. on the further side of

G3014.strongs: from the same as G3013 (λεπίς); scaliness, i.e. "leprosy"

G2534.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1065 (γέ); and at least (or even, indeed)

G2179.abbottSmith: Ephesian : Re 2:1 , Rec. ( ἐν Ἐφέσῳ , WH, RV).†
G2179.strongs: from G2181 (Ἔφεσος); Ephesine, or situated at Ephesus

G1552.abbottSmith: strengthened form of δῆλος , q.v., quite clear, evident : II Ti 3:9 .†
G1552.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1212 (δῆλος); wholly evident

G2994.thayer: a Laodicean, an inhabitant of Laodicea.
G2994.mounce: a Laodicean, an inhabitant of Laodicea, Col. 4:16*
G2994.strongs: from G2993 (Λαοδίκεια); a Laodicean, i.e. inhabitant of Laodicia

G1933.thayer: gentle, mild, forbearing, fair, reasonable, moderate.
G1933.mounce: pr. suitable; fair, reasonable; gentle, mild, patient, 1 Tim. 3:3; Tit. 3:2; Jas. 3:17; 1 Pet. 2:18; τὸ ἐπιεικές, mildness, gentleness, Phil. 4:5*
G1933.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1503 (εἴκω); appropriate, i.e. (by implication) mild

G4429.mounce: to spit, spit out, Mk. 7:33; 8:23; Jn. 9:6
G4429.abbottSmith: to spit : Mk 7:33 8:23 , Jo 9:6 (cf. ἐκ- , ἐμ-πτύω ).†
G4429.strongs: a primary verb (compare G4428 (πτύσσω)); to spit

G4588.thayer: Sergius, the middle (gentile) name of the proconsul of Cyprus.
G4588.strongs: of Latin origin; Sergius, a Roman

G5012.abbottSmith: lowliness of mind, humility : Ac 20:19 , Eph 4:2 , Phl 2:3 , Col 3:12 , I Pe 5:5 ; of a false humility, Col 2:18 , 23 (rare outside of NT, but found in bad sense in FlJ, BJ, iv, 9, 2; also in Epictet., v. Tr., Syn. , § xlii).†
G5012.strongs: from a compound of G5011 (ταπεινός) and the base of G5424 (φρήν); humiliation of mind, i.e. modesty
G5012.kjv: humbleness of mind, humility (of mind, loneliness (of mind)

G3.thayer: Abaddon, Destroyer (i.e. Destroying Angel) or "place of destruction" (personified).
G3.mounce: Abaddon, pr. name, indecl., alternate spelling: Ἀββαδών, the angel who rules in hell, Rev. 9:11*
G3.abbottSmith: in NT, Abaddon , the angel of the Abyss: Re 9:11 .†
G3.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H011); a destroying angel

G1331.mounce: to sift by questioning, of persons; in NT, of things, to ascertain by inquiry, Acts 10:17*
G1331.abbottSmith: to find by inquiry : c. acc, Ac 10:17 .†
G1331.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2065 (ἐρωτάω); to question throughout, i.e. ascertain by interrogation

G428.thayer: unfitted, unsuitable, inconvenient, not well placed.
G428.abbottSmith: not well placed , not fit : Ac 27:12 .†
G428.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2111 (εὔθετος); not well set, i.e. inconvenient

G581.thayer: I am away, am removed from, depart life, die.
G581.mounce: to be away from, unconnected with; to die; met. to die to a thing by renouncing it, 1 Pet. 2:24*
G581.strongs: past participle of a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G1096 (γίνομαι); absent, i.e. deceased (figuratively, renounced)

G1884.thayer: I aid, relieve, do service, render help, am strong enough for.
G1884.mounce: dat., pr. to ward off; to assist, relieve, succor; 1 Tim. 5:10, 16 (2x)*
G1884.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G714 (ἀρκέω); to avail for, i.e. help

G4030.abbottSmith: a helmet : fig., I Th 5:8 ; c. gen. explic., π. τ. σωτηρίου , Eph 6:17 (cf. Is 59:17 ).†
G4030.strongs: feminine of a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2776 (κεφαλή); encirclement of the head, i.e. a helmet

G1122.thayer: (a) in Jerusalem, a scribe, one learned in the Jewish Law, a religious teacher, (b) at Ephesus, the town-clerk, the secretary of the city, (c) a man of learning generally.
G1122.mounce: a scribe; a clerk, town-clerk, registrar, recorder, Acts 19:35; one skilled in the Jewish law, a teacher or interpreter of the law, Mt. 2:4; 5:20, et al. freq.; genr. a religious teacher, Mt. 13:52; by synec. any one distinguished for learning or wisdom, 1 Cor. 1:20
G1122.abbottSmith: a secretary ; γ. τ. πόλεως , a state-clerk: Ac 19:35 . In π. , of a military officer (Deiss., BS , 110f.). So Jg 5:14 , IV Ki 25:19 ( ספר ), al. a scribe, a biblical scholar, teacher of the law (so first in I Es 8:3 , II Es 7:6 ; in Lk 5:17 , νομοδιδάσκαλος ; in Lk 10:25 , νομικός ): Mt 7:29 , Mk 1:22 , and freq. in Gosp. γ. καὶ ἀρχιερεῖς , Mt 2:4 , et al.; γ. κ. Φαρισαῖοι , Mt 5:20 , et al.; γ. μ
G1122.strongs: from G1121 (γράμμα). a writer, i.e. (professionally) scribe or secretary

G3114.thayer: I suffer long, have patience, am forbearing, perseverance.
G3114.mounce: to be slow towards, be long-enduring; to exercise patience, be long-suffering, clement, or indulgent, to forbear, Mt. 18:26, 29; 1 Cor. 13:4; 1 Thess. 5:14; 2 Pet. 3:9; to have patience, endure patiently, wait with patient expectation, Heb. 6:15; Jas. 5:7, 8; to bear long with entreaties for deliverance and avengement, Lk. 18:7*
G3114.strongs: from the same as G3116 (μακροθυμώς); to be long-spirited, i.e. (objectively) forbearing or (subjectively) patient
G3114.kjv: bear (suffer) long, be longsuffering, have (long) patience, be patient, patiently endure

G4197.mounce: a going, progress; a journey, travel, Lk. 13:22; from the Hebrew, way of life, business, occupation, Jas. 1:11*
G4197.strongs: from G4198 (πορεύομαι); travel (by land); figuratively (plural) proceedings, i.e. career

G5166.mounce: to harvest, gather, fruits, and spc. grapes, Lk. 6:44; Rev. 14:18, 19*
G5166.strongs: from a derivative of (to dry) meaning ripe fruit (as if dry); to collect the vintage

G4294.mounce: to make ready, prepare, or complete beforehand, 2 Cor. 9:5*
G4294.abbottSmith: to make ready beforehand : II Co 9:5 .†
G4294.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2675 (καταρτίζω); to prepare in advance

G2937.thayer: (often of the founding of a city), (a) abstr: creation, (b) concr: creation, creature, institution; always of Divine work, (c) an institution, ordinance.
G2937.mounce: (1) pr. a framing, founding; (2) in NT creation, the act of creating, Rom. 1:20; creation, the material universe, Mk. 10:6; 13:19; Heb. 9:11; 2 Pet. 3:4; a created thing, a creature, Rom. 1:25; 8:39; Col. 1:15; Heb. 4:13; the human creation, Mk. 16:15; Rom. 8:19, 20, 21, 22; Col. 1:23; a spiritual creation, 2 Cor. 5:17; Gal. 6:15; (3) an institution, ordinance, 1 Pet. 2:13
G2937.strongs: from G2936 (κτίζω); original formation (properly, the act; by implication, the thing, literally or figuratively)

G2518.mounce: to sleep, be fast asleep, Mt. 8:24; 9:24; met. to sleep in spiritual sloth, Eph. 5:14; 1 Thess. 5:6; to sleep the sleep of death, to die, 1 Thess. 5:10
G2518.abbottSmith: to sleep : Mt 8:24 9:24 13:25 25:5 26:40 , 43 , 45 , Mk 4:27 , 38 5:39 14:37 , 40 , 41 , Lk 8:52 22:46 , I Th 5:7 . Metaph., of death (as Ps 87 (88):5 , Da LXX TH 12:2 ): I Th 5:10 (cf. Mt 9:24 , Mk 5:39 , Lk 8:52 , and v. Swete, Mk 5:39 ); of moral and spiritual insensibility: Mk 13:36 , Eph 5:14 , I Th 5:6 .†
G2518.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and (to sleep); to lie down to rest, i.e. (by implication) to fall asleep (literally or figuratively)

G2084.thayer: speaking another language; subst: one who speaks another language.
G2084.mounce: one who speaks another or foreign language, 1 Cor. 14:21*
G2084.abbottSmith: of alien speech, of another tongue (v. Cremer, 681): I Co 14:21 (aliter in LXX).†
G2084.strongs: from G2087 (ἕτερος) and G1100 (γλῶσσα); other- tongued, i.e. a foreigner

G3486.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05151); Naum (i.e. Nachum), an Israelite

G4876.thayer: I meet, encounter, fall in with.
G4876.mounce: to meet with, fall in with, encounter, Lk. 9:37; 22:10; Acts 10:25; Heb. 7:1, 10; to occur, happen to, befall, Acts 20:22*
G4876.abbottSmith: to meet with : c. dat. pers., Lk 9:18 , 37 22:10 , Ac 10:25 , He 7:1 , 10 . Metaph., of events, to happen, befall (Diog. L., Plut., al.): Ac 20:22 .†
G4876.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to meet with; figuratively, to occur

G5351.mounce: to spoil, ruin, 1 Cor. 3:17; 2 Cor. 7:2; to corrupt, morally deprave, 1 Cor. 15:33; 2 Cor. 11:3
G5351.abbottSmith: to destroy, corrupt, spoil (on the varied usage and distinctive meaning of the word, v. Mayor on II Pe, App. , 175 ff.): c. acc., I Co 3:17 15:23 , II Co 7:2 ; seq. ἀπό , II Co 11:3 ; ἐν , II Pe 2:12 , Ju 10 , Re 19:2 ; κατά , Eph 4:22 (cf. δια- , κατα-φθείρω ).†
G5351.strongs: probably strengthened from (to pine or waste); properly, to shrivel or wither, i.e. to spoil (by any process) or (generally) to ruin (especially figuratively, by moral influences, to deprave)

G2694.mounce: to drag down, to drag away by force, Lk. 12:58*
G2694.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4951 (σύρω); to drag down, i.e. arrest judicially

G583.thayer: I enroll, inscribe in a register; mid: I give my name for registration (or census-taking).
G583.mounce: pr. to copy; hence, to register, enrol, Lk. 2:1; Heb. 12:23; mid. to procure the registration of one’s name, to give in one’s name for registration, Lk. 2:3, 5*
G583.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1125 (γράφω); to write off (a copy or list), i.e. enrol

G2467.strongs: assumed by some as the base of certain irregular forms of G1492 (εἴδω); to know

G1433.mounce: to give freely, grant, Mk. 15:45; 2 Pet. 1:3, 4*
G1433.abbottSmith: to present, bestow . As depon. (with same sense), -έομαι, -οῦμαι : Mk 15:45 , II Pe 1:3 , 4 .†
G1433.strongs: middle voice from G1435 (δῶρον); to bestow gratuitously

G5145.strongs: plural from G5140 (τρεῖς) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); three hundred

G3042.mounce: famine, scarcity of food, want of grain, Mt. 24:7; famine, hunger, famishment, Lk. 15:17; Rom. 8:35
G3042.strongs: probably from G3007 (λείπω) (through the idea of destitution); a scarcity of food

G629.thayer: release effected by payment of ransom; redemption, deliverance.
G629.mounce: redemption, a deliverance, procured by the payment of a ransom; meton. the author of redemption, 1 Cor. 1:30; deliverance, simply, the idea of a ransom being excluded, Lk. 21:28; Heb. 11:35
G629.abbottSmith: release effected by payment of ransom , redemption , deliverance ; metaph., He 11:35 ; of deliverance thr. Christ from evil and the consequences of sin: Lk 21:28 , Ro 3:24 (Vau., in l.) 8:23, I Co 1:30 , Eph 1:7 , 14 4:30 , Col 1:14 , He 9:15 . (On the extent to wh. the word retains the sense of ransom , v. ICC , Ro. , 3:24 , Westc., He. , 297 ff.; v. also ICC , Eph. , 11; DCG , ii, 605; Cremer, 4
G629.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G3083 (λύτρον); (the act) ransom in full, i.e. (figuratively) riddance, or (specially) Christian salvation

G421.mounce: to track out, ἴξνος, a track which cannot be explored, inscrutable, incomprehensible, Rom. 11:33; Eph. 3:8*
G421.abbottSmith: that cannot be traced out : Ro 11:33 , Eph 3:8 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G421.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2487 (ἴχνος); not tracked out, i.e. (by implication) untraceable

G1751.mounce: to be in or within; τὰ ἐνόντα, those things which are within, Lk. 11:41*
G1751.abbottSmith: to be in, within ( Jb 27:3 , al.): ptcp. pl., τὰ ἐνόντα , Lk 11:41 (R, txt., cf. MM, Exp. , xii).
G1751.strongs: from G1772 (ἔννομος) and G1510 (εἰμί); to be within (neuter participle plural)

G4530.abbottSmith: Salim : Jo 3:23 (v. Westc., in l.; DB , iii, 354).†
G4530.strongs: probably from the same as G4531 (σαλεύω); Salim, a place in Palestine

G879.mounce: to awake from sleep; in NT to go off into sleep, fall asleep, Lk. 8:23*
G879.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G5258 (ὕπνος); properly, to become awake, i.e. (by implication) to drop (off) in slumber

G1772.thayer: (a) legal, statutory, duly constituted, (b) under the law, obedient to the law.
G1772.mounce: within law; lawful, legal, Acts 19:39; in NT subject or under a law, obedient to a law, 1 Cor. 9:21*
G1772.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3551 (νόμος); (subjectively) legal, or (objectively) subject to

G2.thayer: Aaron, son of Amram and Jochebed, brother of Moses.
G2.mounce: Aaron, pr. name, indecl., the brother of Moses (Exod. 4:14), Lk. 1:15; Acts 7:40; Heb. 5:4; 7:11; 9:4*
G2.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0175); Aaron, the brother of Moses

G2888.thayer: ruler of this world, that is, of the world as asserting its independence of God; used of the angelic or demonic powers controlling the sublunary world.
G2888.mounce: pr. monarch of the world; in NT a worldly prince, a power paramount in the world of the unbelieving and ungodly, Eph. 6:12*
G2888.abbottSmith: in Orphic hymns, al. (v. AR, Eph 6:12 ), a ruler of the whole world (and so in Rabbinic writings: קוֺזמוֺקְרָטוֺר ).
G2888.strongs: from G2889 (κόσμος) and G2902 (κρατέω); a world-ruler, an epithet of Satan

G1522.mounce: to hear or hearken to, to heed, 1 Cor. 14:21; to listen to the prayers of any one, accept one’s petition, Mt. 6:7; Lk. 1:13; Acts 10:31; Heb. 5:7*
G1522.abbottSmith: to listen to , in two senses;
G1522.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G191 (ἀκούω); to listen to

G3135.abbottSmith: a pearl : Mt 13:45 , 46 , I Ti 2:9 , Re 17:4 18:12 , 16 21:21 ( -ῖται , WH); proverbially, Mt 7:6 .†

G3801.strongs: a phrase combining G3588 (ὁ) with the present participle and imperfect of G1510 (εἰμί) and the present participle of G2064 (ἔρχομαι) by means of G2532 (καί); the one being and the one that was and the one coming, i.e. the Eternal, as a divine epithet of Christ
G3801.lemma: ὁ ὢν καί ὁ ἦν καί ὁ ἐρχόμενος
G3801.kjv: which art (is, was), and (which) wast (is, was), and art (is) to come (shalt be)

G290.abbottSmith: a vineyard : Mt 20:1 ff. Mt 21:28 ff, Lk 13:6 20:9 ff., I Co 9:7 . Æschin., 49, 13; Diod., al.; v. MM, VGT , s.v.; LS, s.v. ἀμπελουργεῖον .)

G4401.abbottSmith: to choose or appoint beforehand : Ac 10:41 (Plat., al.).†
G4401.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5500 (χειροτονέω); to elect in advance

G3607.thayer: a linen cloth, a sheet, sail.
G3607.mounce: pr. fine linen; a linen cloth; a sheet, Acts 10:11; 11:5*
G3607.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a linen cloth, i.e. (especially) a sail

G333.thayer: I look at attentively, gaze at, consider.
G333.mounce: to view, behold attentively, contemplate, Acts 17:23; Heb. 13:7*
G333.abbottSmith: to observe carefully , consider well : Ac 17:23 , He 13:7 (Diod., al.).†
G333.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2334 (θεωρέω); to look again (i.e. attentively) at (literally or figuratively)

G790.thayer: I am unsettled, have no fixed abode, lead a vagabond life.
G790.mounce: to be unsettled, to be a wanderer, be homeless, 1 Cor. 4:11*
G790.abbottSmith: to be unsettled , be homeless , lead a vagabond life (Cremer, 738 MM, s.v.): I Co 4:11 .†
G790.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2476 (ἵστημι); to be non-stationary, i.e. (figuratively) homeless

G4545.mounce: (1 Sam. 1:1-25:1), Samuel, pr. name, indecl., Acts 3:24; 13:20; Heb. 11:32*
G4545.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08050); Samuel (i.e. Shemuel), an Israelite

G1044.abbottSmith: a gangrene, an eating sore , which leads to mortification: II Ti 2:17 .†

G5434.mounce: a dry twig, branch, etc., Acts 28:3*
G5434.abbottSmith: a dry stick : pl., brushwood : Ac 28:3 .†
G5434.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (to roast or parch; akin to the base of G5395 (φλόξ)); something desiccated, i.e. a dry twig

G4141.mounce: also spelled πλήττω, to strike, smite; from the Hebrew, to smite, to plague, blast, Rev. 8:12
G4141.abbottSmith: to strike , smite : pass., Re 8:12 (cf. ἐκ- , ἐπι-πλήσσω ).†
G4141.strongs: apparently another form of G4111 (πλάσσω) (through the idea of flattening out); to pound, i.e. (figuratively) to inflict with (calamity)

G5081.abbottSmith: at a distance clearly : Mk 8:25 (WH, mg., δηλαυγῶς ).†
G5081.strongs: adverb from a compound of a derivative of G5056 (τέλος) and G827 (αὐγή); in a far-shining manner, i.e. plainly

G153.thayer: I am ashamed, am put to shame.
G153.abbottSmith: to disfigure (Hom.). to dishonour ( Pr 29:15 ). to make ashamed ( Si 13:7 ). Pass., to be put to shame, be ashamed : II Co 10:8 ; Phl 1:20 , I Pe 4:16 , I Jo 2:28 ; c. inf. (M, Pr. , 205), Lk 16:3 (cf. ἐπ- ( -ομαι ), κατ-αἰσχύνω ).†
G153.strongs: from (disfigurement, i.e. disgrace); to feel shame (for oneself)

G711.strongs: from the same as G712 (ἄριστον) and G1012 (βουλή); best counselling; Aristoboulus, a Christian

G337.thayer: I take up, take away the life of, make an end of, murder.
G337.mounce: pr. to take up, lift, as from the ground; to take off, put to death, kill, murder, Mt. 2:16; to take away, abolish, abrogate, Heb. 10:9; mid. to take up infants in order to bring them up, Acts 7:21
G337.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and (the active of) G138 (αἱρέομαι); to take up, i.e. adopt; by implication, to take away (violently), i.e. abolish, murder
G337.kjv: put to death, kill, slay, take away, take up

G5452.mounce: to plant, set, Mt. 21:33; Lk. 13:6; 17:6, 28; 20:9; met. Mt. 15:13; Mk. 12:1; to plant the Gospel, 1 Cor. 3:6, 7, 8; 9:7*
G5452.abbottSmith: to plant : Lk 17:28 , I Co 3:6-8 ; c. acc., φυτείαν , Mt 15:13 ; ἀμπελῶνα , Mt 21:33 , Mk 12:1 , Lk 20:9 , I Co 9:7 ; pass., seq. ἐν , Lk 13:6 17:6 .†
G5452.strongs: from a derivative of G5453 (φύω); to set out in the earth, i.e. implant; figuratively, to instil doctrine

G4822.thayer: (a) I unite or knit together, (b) I put together in reasoning, and so: I conclude, prove, (c) I teach, instruct.
G4822.mounce: pr. to cause to come together; to unite, knit together, Eph. 4:16; Col. 2:2, 19; to infer, conclude, Acts 16:10; by impl. to prove, demonstrate, Acts 9:22; in NT to teach, instruct, Acts 19:33; 1 Cor. 2:16*
G4822.abbottSmith: to join or knit together, unite : Eph 4:16 , Col 2:2 (but v. infr.), ib. 19 .
G4822.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to force; causative (by reduplication) of the base of G939 (βάσις)); to drive together, i.e. unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach
G4822.kjv: compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove

G1026.mounce: to wet, moisten, Lk. 7:38; to rain, cause or send rain, Mt. 5:45; Lk. 17:29
G1026.abbottSmith: to wet : Lk 7:38 , 44 . In late Gk. writers and vernac. = ὕειν , to send rain, to rain (Kennedy, Sources , 39, 155): Mt 5:45 ; trop., β. πῦρ κ. θεῖον , Lk 17:29 ; impers., βρέχει , it rains : Ja 5:17 ; with ὐετός as subj., Re 11:6 .†
G1026.strongs: a primary verb; to moisten (especially by a shower)

G2511.thayer: I cleanse, make clean, literally, ceremonially, or spiritually, according to context.
G2511.mounce: to cleanse, render pure, purify, Mt. 23:25; Lk. 11:39; to cleanse from leprosy, Mt. 8:2, 3; 10:8; met. to cleanse from sin, purify by an expiatory offering, make expiation for, Heb. 9:22, 23; 1 Jn. 1:7; to cleanse from sin, free from the influence of error and sin, Acts 15:9; 2 Cor. 7:1; to pronounce ceremonially clean, Acts 10:15; 11:9
G2511.abbottSmith: to cleanse , make clean . In physical sense: c. acc. rei, fig., Mt 23:25 , 26 , Lk 11:39 ; of disease (leprosy), c. acc. pers., Mt 8:2 , 3 10:8 11:5 , Mk 1:40-42 , Lk 4:27 5:12 , 13 7:22 17:14 , 17 ; ἡ λέπρα ἐκαθερίσθη (on the spelling v. supr.), Mt 8:3 . In ethical sense: τ. καρδίας , Ac 15:9 (cf. Si 38:10 ); τ. χεῖρας , Ja 4:8 ; λαὸν ἑαυτῷ , Tit 2:14 ; τ. ἐκκλησίαν , Eph 5:26 ; c. acc. pers. (re
G2511.strongs: from G2513 (καθαρός); to cleanse (literally or figuratively)

G684.thayer: destruction, ruin, loss, perishing; eternal ruin.
G684.mounce: consumption, destruction; waste, profusion, Mt. 26:8; Mk. 14:4; destruction, state of being destroyed, Acts 25:6; eternal ruin, perdition, Mt. 7:13; Acts 8:20
G684.abbottSmith: destruction , waste , loss , perishing (in π. , of money, v. MM, s.v.): Mt 26:8 , Mk 14:4 , Ac 8:20 , Ro 9:22 , I Ti 6:9 , II Pe 2:1 ; in special sense of the loss of eternal life, perdition , the antithesis of σωτηρία : Mt 7:13 , Jo 17:12 , Phl 1:28 3:19 , II Th 2:3 , He 10:39 , II Pe 2:3 3:7 , 16 , Re 17:8 , 11 ( DB , iii, 744).†
G684.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G622 (ἀπόλλυμι); ruin or loss (physical, spiritual or eternal)
G684.kjv: damnable(-nation), destruction, die, perdition, X perish, pernicious ways, waste

G3071.thayer: Lycaonia, the country of the Lykaones, a district of Asia Minor, comprised within the Roman province Galatia and including the cities of Derbe and Lystra.
G3071.mounce: Lycaonia, a province of Asia Minor, Acts 14:6*
G3071.abbottSmith: Lycaonia , a region in Asia Minor: Ac 14:6 .†
G3071.strongs: perhaps remotely from G3074 (λύκος); Lycaonia, a region of Asia Minor

G3185.strongs: neuter of G3187 (μείζων); (adverbially) in greater degree

G419.thayer: that cannot be searched into, inscrutable, unfathomable.
G419.mounce: also spelled ἀνεξερεύνητος, unfathomable, incapable of human explanation, Rom. 11:33*
G419.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G1830 (ἐξερευνάω); not searched out, i.e. (by implication) inscrutable

G719.mounce: a joint or articulation of the bones, Heb. 4:12*
G719.strongs: from the same as G716 (ἅρμα); an articulation (of the body)

G4183.mounce: great in magnitude or quantity, much, large, Mt. 13:5; Jn. 3:23; 15:8; pl. many, Mt. 3:7; in time, long, Mt. 25:19; Mk. 6:35; Jn. 5:6; οἱ πολλοί, the many, the mass, Rom. 5:15; 12:5; 1 Cor. 10:33; τὸ πολύ, much, 2 Cor. 8:15; πολύ, as an adv., much, greatly, Mk. 12:27; Lk. 7:47; of time, ἐπὶ πολύ, a long time, Acts 28:6; μετ’ οὐ πολὺ, not long after, Acts 27:14; followed by a compar., much, 2 Cor. 8:22; πολλῷ, much, by much, Mt. 6:30; Mk. 10:48; τὰ πολλά, as an adv., most frequently, generally, Rom. 15:22; πολλά, as an adv., much, greatly, vehemently, Mk. 1:45; 3:12; of time, many times, frequently, often, Mt. 9:14
G4183.abbottSmith: as adj. , much , many , great , of number, space, degree, value, time, etc.: ἀριθμός , Ac 11:21 ; ὄχλος , Mk 5:24 ; θερισμός , Mt 9:37 ; χόρτος , Jo 6:10 ; χρόνος , Mt 25:19 ; γογγυσμός , Jo 7:12 ; πόνος , Col 4:13 ; δόξα , Mt 24:30 ; σιγή , Ac 21:40 ; pl., προφῆται , Mt 13:17 ; ὄχλοι , Mt 4:25 ; δαιμόνια , Mk 1:34 ; δυνάμεις , Mt 7:22 ,
G4183.strongs: including the forms from the alternate ; (singular) much (in any respect) or (plural) many; neuter (singular) as adverbial, largely; neuter (plural) as adverb or noun often, mostly, largely
G4183.kjv: abundant, + altogether, common, + far (passed, spent), (+ be of a) great (age, deal, -ly, while), long, many, much, oft(-en (-times)), plenteous, sore, straitly

G2537.mounce: new, recently made, Mt. 9:17; Mk. 2:22; new in species, character, or mode, Mt. 26:28, 29; Mk. 14:24, 25; Lk. 22:20; Jn. 13:34; 2 Cor. 5:17; Gal. 6:15; Eph. 2:15; 4:24; 1 Jn. 2:7; Rev. 3:12; novel, strange, Mk. 1:27; Acts 17:19; new to the possessor, Mk. 16:17; unheard of, unusual, Mk. 1:27; Acts 17:19; met. renovated, better, of higher excellence, 2 Cor. 5:17; Rev. 5:9
G2537.abbottSmith: of that which is unused or unaccustomed, new in respect to form or quality, fresh , unused , novel : opp. to παλαιός , ἀρχαῖος , Mt 9:17 13:52 , Mk 2:21 , 22 , Lk 5:36 , 38 , II Co 5:17 , Eph 4:24 ; πλήρωμα , Mk 2:21 ; μνημεῖον , Mt 27:60 , Jo 19:41 ; διαθήκη (T, WH, R, txt., omit), Lk 22:20 , I Co 11:25 , II Co 3:6 , He 8:8 (LXX) , 13 9:15 ; οὐρανοί, γῆ , II Pe 3:13 , Re 21:1 (LXX) Ἰερουσαλήμ , R
G2537.strongs: of uncertain affinity; new (especially in freshness; while G3501 (νέος) is properly so with respect to age

G2775.strongs: from the same as G2774 (κεφάλαιον); (specially) to strike on the head

G4023.thayer: (a) I contain (of a book containing subject matter); hence: it stands (has its content) thus, (b) I encompass, surround, get hold of, seize.
G4023.mounce: to encompass, enclose; met. to encompass, seize on the mind, Lk. 5:9; περιέχει, impers. it is contained, it is among the contents of a writing, 1 Pet. 2:6*
G4023.abbottSmith: to surround , encompass : Lk 5:9 (cf. MM, xix).
G4023.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold all around, i.e. include, clasp (figuratively)

G1262.abbottSmith: to part asunder, dissolve ; of an assembly, pass., to disperse : Ac 5:36 .†
G1262.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3089 (λύω); to dissolve utterly

G3670.thayer: (a) I promise, agree, (b) I confess, (c) I publicly declare, (d) a Hebraism, I praise, celebrate.
G3670.mounce: to speak in accordance, adopt the same terms of language; to engage, promise, Mt. 14:7; to admit, avow frankly, Jn. 1:20; Acts 24:14; to confess, 1 Jn. 1:9; to profess, confess, Jn. 9:22; 12:42; Acts 23:8; to avouch, declare openly and solemnly, Mt. 7:23; in NT ὁμολογεῖν ἐν, to accord belief, Mt. 10:32; Lk. 12:8; to accord approbation, Lk. 12:8; from the Hebrew, to accord praise, Heb. 13:15
G3670.strongs: from a compound of the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ) and G3056 (λόγος); to assent, i.e. covenant, acknowledge
G3670.kjv: con- (pro-)fess, confession is made, give thanks, promise

G560.thayer: I give up in despair, despair of; I hope to receive from or in return.
G560.mounce: to lay aside hope, despond, despair; also, to hope for something in return, Lk. 6:35*
G560.abbottSmith: to give up in despair , despair of (Polyb., Diod., LXX).
G560.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1679 (ἐλπίζω); to hope out, i.e. fully expect

G5137.mounce: the neck, Mt. 18:6; Mk. 9:42; Lk. 15:20; 17:2; ἐπιθεῖναι ζυγὸν ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον, to put a yoke upon the neck of someone, met. to bind to a burdensome observance, Acts 15:10; 20:37; ὑποτιθέναι τὸν τράχηλον, to lay down one’s neck under the axe of the executioner, to imperil one’s life, Rom. 16:4*
G5137.abbottSmith: the neck : Mt 18:6 , Mk 9:42 , Lk 17:2 ; ἐπιπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τὸν τ. , to embrace ( Ge 46:29 ), Lk 15:20 , Ac 20:37 . Metaph., ὑποθεῖναι τὸν ἑαυτοῦ τ. (for similar phrase in π. , v. Zorell, s.v.), Ro 16:4 ; ἐπιθεῖναι ζυγὸν ἐπὶ τὸν τ. , Ac 15:10 .†
G5137.strongs: probably from G5143 (τρέχω) (through the idea of mobility); the throat (neck), i.e. (figuratively) life

G990.thayer: a look, glance; sight and hearing.
G990.mounce: a look; the act of seeing, sight, 2 Pet. 2:8*
G990.abbottSmith: a look, a glance : βλέμματι καὶ ἀκοῇ , II Pe 2:8 , sight and hearing , a sense not found for β. in Gk. lit., but perh. recognized in the vernacular ( ICC , in l.)†
G990.strongs: from G991 (βλέπω); vision (properly concrete; by implication, abstract)

G2760.thayer: a centurion, an officer commanding about a hundred infantry in the Roman army.
G2760.mounce: in its original signification, a commander of a hundred foot-soldiers, a centurion, Mk. 15:39, 44, 45*
G2760.abbottSmith: a centurion : Mk 15:39 , 44 , 45 (Mt, Lk, use ἑκατόνταρχος ( -ης ), q.v.).†
G2760.strongs: of Latin origin; a centurion, i.e. captain of one hundred soldiers

G2223.thayer: a girdle, belt, waistband; because the purse was kept there, also: a purse.
G2223.mounce: a zone, belt, girdle, Mt. 3:4; 10:9; Mk. 1:6; 6:8; Acts 21:11; Rev. 1:13; 15:6*
G2223.abbottSmith: a belt , girdle : Mt 3:4 , Mk 1:6 , Ac 21:11 , Re 1:13 15:6 ; as a receptacle for money, Mt 10:9 , Mk 6:8 .†
G2223.strongs: probably akin to the base of G2218 (ζυγός); a belt; by implication, a pocket

G4799.mounce: pr. a pouring together; hence, confusion, commotion, tumult, uproar, Acts 19:29*
G4799.abbottSmith: confusion : of a popular uproar, Ac 19:29 .†
G4799.strongs: from G4797 (συγχέω); commixture, i.e. (figuratively) riotous disturbance

G2050.abbottSmith: a making desolate, laying waste : Lk 21:20 ; βδέλυγμα ἐρημώσεως (Da, ll. c., I Mac 1:54 ), Mt 24:15 (LXX) , Mk 13:14 (ib.) .†

G2250.thayer: a day, the period from sunrise to sunset.
G2250.mounce: day, a day, the interval from sunrise to sunset, opp. to νύξ, Mt. 4:2; 12:40; Lk. 2:44; the interval of twenty-four hours, comprehending day and night, Mt. 6:34; 15:32; from the Hebrew, ἡμέρᾳ καὶ ἡμέρᾳ, day by day, every day, 2 Cor. 4:16; ἡμέραν ἐξ ἡμέρας, from day to day, continually, 2 Pet. 2:8; καθ’ ἡμέραν, every day, daily, Acts 17:17; Heb. 3:13; a point or period of time, Lk. 19:42; Acts 15:7; Eph. 6:13; a judgement, trial, 1 Cor. 4:3
G2250.abbottSmith: day ; as distinct from night: gen. ἡμέρας , by day (WM, §30, 11), Re 21:25 ; ἡ. κ. νυκτός (ν. κ. ἡ.) , Ac 9:24 , I Th 2:9 , II Th 3:8 , Re 4:8 (Bl., §36, 13); ἡμέρας μέσης , at mid-day , Ac 26:13 ; acc. durat., τ. ἡμέρας , Lk 21:37 ; ὅλην τὴν ἡ. , Ro 8:36 ; ἐν ἡμέρᾳ , Jo 11:9 , Ro 13:13 ; ἡμέρας ὁδός , a day's journey , Lk 2:44 ; ἡ. γίνεται , Lk 4:42 22:66 ; κλίνει , Lk 9:12 , al.; metaph., Jo 9:4
G2250.strongs: feminine (with G5610 (ὥρα) implied) of a derivative of (to sit; akin to the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος)) meaning tame, i.e. gentle; day, i.e. (literally) the time space between dawn and dark, or the whole 24 hours (but several days were usually reckoned by the Jews as inclusive of the parts of both extremes); figuratively, a period (always defined more or less clearly by the context)
G2250.kjv: age, + alway, (mid-)day (by day, (-ly)), + for ever, judgment, (day) time, while, years

G3414.thayer: a mina, a Greek monetary unit equal to 100 drachma.
G3414.mounce: Latin mina; a weight, equivalent to 100 drachmas; also a sum, equivalent to 100 drachmas and the sixtieth part of a talent, Lk. 19:13, 16, 18, 20, 24f.*
G3414.abbottSmith: a mina (Lat.), mna , in Attic a weight and sum of money = 100 δραχμαί (q.v.): Lk 19:13 , 16 , 18 , 20 , 24 , 25 .†
G3414.strongs: of Latin origin; a mna (i.e. mina), a certain weight

G5014.mounce: depression; low estate, abject condition, Lk. 1:48; Acts 8:33; Phil. 3:21; Jas. 1:10*
G5014.abbottSmith: abasement, humiliation, low estate : Lk 1:48 , Ac 8:33 (LXX) , Phl 3:21 , Ja 1:10 (Plat., Arist., and later writers).†
G5014.strongs: from G5013 (ταπεινόω); depression (in rank or feeling)
G5014.kjv: humiliation, be made low, low estate, vile

G4320.thayer: I go up to, come up to, go up further.
G4320.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G305 (ἀναβαίνω); to ascend farther, i.e. be promoted (take an upper (more honorable) seat)

G2856.thayer: (lit: I maim, mutilate), I cut short, shorten, abbreviate.
G2856.mounce: in NT of time, to cut short, shorten, Mt. 24:22; Mk. 13:20*
G2856.abbottSmith: to cut off , amputate (LXX), hence, to curtail , shorten : Mt 24:22 , Mk 13:20 .†
G2856.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G2849 (κολάζω); to dock, i.e. (figuratively) abridge

G3586.thayer: anything made of wood, a piece of wood, a club, staff; the trunk of a tree, used to support the cross-bar of a cross in crucifixion.
G3586.mounce: wood, timber, 1 Cor. 3:12; Rev. 18:12; stocks, Acts 16:24; a club, Mt. 26:47, 55; a post, cross, gibbet, Acts 5:30; 10:29; 13:29; a tree, Lk. 23:31; Rev. 2:7
G3586.strongs: from another form of the base of G3582 (ξέστης); timber (as fuel or material); by implication, a stick, club or tree or other wooden article or substance

G4783.mounce: assent; in NT accord, alliance, agreement, 2 Cor. 6:16*
G4783.abbottSmith: concord, agreement : II Co 6:16 (Polyb., Plut., al.).†
G4783.strongs: from G4784 (συγκατατίθεμαι); a deposition (of sentiment) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) accord with

G5104.strongs: probably for the dative case of G3588 (ὁ); an enclitic particle of asseveration by way of contrast; in sooth
G5104.kjv: (used only with other particles in the comparative, as G2544 (καίτοιγε), G3305 (μέντοι), G5105 (τοιγαροῦν), G5106 (τοίνυν), etc

G4438.mounce: to box, fight as a boxer, 1 Cor. 9:26*
G4438.strongs: from a derivative of the same as G4435 (πυγμή); to box (with the fist), i.e. contend (as a boxer) at the games (figuratively)

G1069.strongs: from G1093 (γῆ); a neighbour (as adjoining one's ground); by implication, a friend

G2207.thayer: one who is eagerly devoted to a person or a thing, a zealot.
G2207.mounce: pr. a generous rival, an imitator; in NT an aspirant, 1 Cor. 14:12; Tit. 2:14; a devoted adherent, a zealot, Acts 21:20; 22:3; Gal. 1:14
G2207.abbottSmith: in cl. an emulator , zealous admirer (Plat., al.).

G1070.mounce: to laugh, smile; by impl. to be merry, happy, to rejoice, Lk. 6:21, 25*
G1070.abbottSmith: to laugh : Lk 6:25 ; fut., γελάσω ) (M, Pr. , 154), ib. 21 (cf. κατα-γελάω ).†
G1070.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to laugh (as a sign of joy or satisfaction)

G2585.thayer: I hawk, trade in, deal in for purposes of gain.
G2585.mounce: pr. to be κάπηλος, a retailer; to peddle with; to corrupt, adulterate, 2 Cor. 2:17*
G2585.abbottSmith: to make a trade of (RV, mg., make merchandise of ), or perhaps (cf. Is 1:22 (LXX) ) to corrupt (RV, txt.): II Co 2:17 .†
G2585.strongs: from (a huckster); to retail, i.e. (by implication) to adulterate (figuratively)

G3709.mounce: pr. mental bent, impulse; anger, indignation, wrath, Eph. 4:31; Col. 3:8; μετ’ ὀργῆς, indignantly, Mk. 3:5; vengeance, punishment, Mt. 3:7; Lk. 3:7; 21:23; Rom. 13:4, 5
G3709.strongs: from G3713 (ὀρέγομαι); properly, desire (as a reaching forth or excitement of the mind), i.e. (by analogy), violent passion (ire, or (justifiable) abhorrence); by implication punishment

G4552.mounce: a sapphire, a precious stone of a blue color in various shades, next in hardness and value to the diamond, Rev. 21:19
G4552.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05601); a "sapphire" or lapis-lazuli gem

G4203.thayer: I fornicate; met: I practice idolatry.
G4203.mounce: to commit fornication, 1 Cor. 6:18; 10:8; Rev. 2:14, 20; from the Hebrew, to commit spiritual fornication, practise idolatry, Rev. 17:2; 18:3, 9*
G4203.abbottSmith: to prostitute the body for hire.
G4203.strongs: from G4204 (πόρνη); to act the harlot, i.e. (literally) indulge unlawful lust (of either sex), or (figuratively) practise idolatry

G1173.thayer: a dinner, an afternoon or evening meal.
G1173.mounce: pr. a meal; supper, the principal meal taken in the evening, Lk. 14:12; Jn. 13:2, 4; meton. food, 1 Cor. 11:21; a feast, banquet, Mt. 23:6; Mk. 6:21; 12:39
G1173.abbottSmith: the chief meal of the day, dinner, supper : Mt 23:6 , Mk 12:39 , Lk 14:17 , 24 20:46 , Jo 13:2 , 4 21:20 , I Co 11:21 ; δ. ποιεῖν , Mk 6:21 , Lk 14:12 , 16 , Jo 12:2 ; κυριακὸν (q.v.) δ. , I Co 11:20 ; metaph. (Dalman, Words , 118), δ. τ. γάμου τ. ἀρνίου , Re 19:9 ; δ. τ. μέγα τ. Θεοῦ , Re 19:17 .†
G1173.strongs: from the same as G1160 (δαπάνη); dinner, i.e. the chief meal (usually in the evening)

G166.thayer: age-long, and therefore: practically eternal, unending; partaking of the character of that which lasts for an age, as contrasted with that which is brief and fleeting.
G166.mounce: indeterminate as to duration, eternal, everlasting
G166.abbottSmith: age-long , eternal , of that which is without either beginning or end: Ro 16:26 , He 9:14 ; of that which is without beginning: Ro 16:25 , II Ti 1:9 , Tit 1:2 ; of that which is without end (MM, VGT , s.v.): σκηναί , Lk 16:9 οἰκία , II Co 5:1 ; διαθήκη , He 13:20 ; εὐαγγέλιον , Re 14:6 ; παράκλησις , II Th 2:16 ; λύτρωσις , He 9:12 ; κληρονομία , ib. 15 ; κόλασις , Mt 25:46 ; κρίμα , He 6:2 ; κρίσ
G166.strongs: from G165 (αἰών); perpetual (also used of past time, or past and future as well)

G610.abbottSmith: prop., a judicial sentence : II Co 1:9 , R, mg.
G610.strongs: from G611 (ἀποκρίνομαι) (in its original sense of judging); a judicial decision

G2531.thayer: according to the manner in which, in the degree that, just as, as.
G2531.abbottSmith: according as , even as , just as , as : Lk 1:2 (v.s. καθά ), 24:39 , Jo 5:23 , Ac 7:48 , I Co 8:2 , Ga 3:6 , al.; seq. οὕτως , Lk 11:30 , Jo 3:14 , II Co 1:5 , Col 3:13 , I Jo 2:6 , al.; seq. καί , Jo 15:9 , I Co 15:49 , I Jo 2:18 , al.; οὕτως . . . κ. , Lk 24:24 ; id. with ellipsis of οὕτως , Mt 21:6 , Mk 16:7 , Ro 1:13 , al.; with other elliptical constructions, Jo 6:58 17:21 , 22 , Ac 15:8 , I
G2531.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5613 (ὡς); just (or inasmuch) as, that
G2531.kjv: according to, (according, even) as, how, when

G3170.thayer: (a) I enlarge, lengthen, (b) I increase, magnify, extol.
G3170.mounce: lit., to enlarge, amplify, Mt. 23:5; 2 Cor. 10:15; to manifest in an extraordinary degree, Lk. 1:58; fig., to magnify, exalt, extol, Lk. 1:46; Acts 5:13; Acts 10:46; 19:17; Phil. 1:20*
G3170.strongs: from G3173 (μέγας); to make (or declare) great, i.e. increase or (figuratively) extol

G4468.strongs: from the same as G4467 (ῥᾳδιούργημα); recklessness, i.e. (by extension) malignity

G4892.thayer: a council, tribunal; the Sanhedrin, the meeting place of the Sanhedrin.
G4892.mounce: pr. a sitting together, assembly, etc., in NT the Sanhedrin, the supreme council of the Jewish nation, Mt. 5:22; 26:59; meton. the Sanhedrin, as including the members and place of meeting, Lk. 22:66; Acts 4:15; genr. a judicial council, tribunal, Mt. 10:17; Mk. 13:9
G4892.abbottSmith: a council (Plat., Xen., al.; LXX; in π., σ. τ. πρεσβυτέρων , Deiss., BS , 156): of a local Jewish tribunal, Mt 10:17 , Mk 13:9 .
G4892.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a joint session, i.e. (specially), the Jewish Sanhedrin; by analogy, a subordinate tribunal

G2955.thayer: I bend, stoop down, bow the head.
G2955.mounce: to bend forwards, stoop down, Mk. 1:7; Jn. 8:6*
G2955.abbottSmith: to bow the head , stoop down : Mk 1:7 ; seq. κάτω , Jo 8:[6 , 8] (cf. ἀνα- , παρα- , συν-κύπτω ).†
G2955.strongs: probably from the base of G2949 (κῦμα); to bend forward

G486.thayer: I abuse in return, give abuse for abuse.
G486.mounce: to reproach or revile again or in return, 1 Pet. 2:23*
G486.abbottSmith: to revile in turn : I Pe 2:23 .†
G486.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G3058 (λοιδορέω); to rail in reply

G5389.thayer: I am zealous, strive eagerly, desire very strongly.
G5389.mounce: pr. to be ambitious of honor; by impl. to exert one’s self to accomplish a thing, use one’s utmost efforts, endeavor earnestly, Rom. 15:20; 2 Cor. 5:9; 1 Thess. 4:11*
G5389.abbottSmith: to love or seek after honour , hence, to be ambitious, emulous (Plat., Plut., al.): c. inf., Ro 15:20 , II Co 5:9 , I Th 4:11 .†
G5389.strongs: middle voice from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G5092 (τιμή); to be fond of honor, i.e. emulous (eager or earnest to do something)

G1529.mounce: a place of entrance; the act of entrance, Heb. 10:19; admission, reception, 1 Thess. 1:9; 2 Pet. 1:11; a coming, approach, access, 1 Thess. 2:1; entrance upon office, commencement or beginning of ministry, Acts 13:24*
G1529.abbottSmith: a means of entering, place of entrance : He 10:19 , II Pe 1:11 (cf. Westc., He., l.c.; MM, Exp. , xii; but v. infr.).
G1529.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G3598 (ὁδός); an entrance (literally or figuratively)

G2012.thayer: (a) (procurator) a steward, (b) (tutor) a guardian (appointed for an "infant" [under 14 perhaps] by the father or by a magistrate).
G2012.mounce: one to whose charge or control a thing is left; a steward, bailiff, agent, manager, Mt. 20:8; steward or overseer of the revenue, treasurer, Lk. 8:3; a guardian of children, Gal. 4:2*
G2012.abbottSmith: an administrator, a steward : Mt 20:8 , Lk 8:3 .
G2012.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5158 (τρόπος) (in the sense of G2011 (ἐπιτροπή)); a commissioner, i.e. domestic manager, guardian

G4311.thayer: I send forward, accompany, equip for a journey.
G4311.mounce: to send on before; to accompany or attend out of respect, escort, accompany for a certain distance on setting out on a journey, Acts 15:3; 20:38; 21:5; to furnish with things necessary for a journey, Tit. 3:13; 3 Jn. 6
G4311.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3992 (πέμπω); to send forward, i.e. escort or aid in travel
G4311.kjv: accompany, bring (forward) on journey (way), conduct forth

G2395.abbottSmith: a physician : Mt 9:12 , Mk 2:17 5:26 , Lk 4:23 5:31 8:43 (om. WH, R, mg.), Col 4:14 (on the status of physicians, v. MM, Exp. , xv.).†

G4950.thayer: Syrtis, a quicksand off the coast of North Africa.
G4950.mounce: a shoal, sand-bank, a place dangerous on account of shoals, two of which were particularly famous on the northern coast of Africa, one lying near Carthage, and the other, the syrtis major, lying between Cyrene and Leptis, which is probably referred to in Acts 27:17*
G4950.abbottSmith: Syrtis , the name of two large sand-banks on the Libyan coast: Ac 27:17 .†
G4950.strongs: from G4951 (σύρω); a shoal (from the sand drawn thither by the waves), i.e. the Syrtis Major or great bay on the north coast of Africa

G976.thayer: a written book, roll, or volume, sometimes with a sacred connotation.
G976.mounce: pr. the inner bark or rind of the papyrus, which was anciently used instead of paper; hence, a written volume or roll, book, catalogue, account, Mt. 1:1; Mk. 12:26
G976.abbottSmith: a book , a roll , used much less freq. than βιβλίον , and with a "connotation of sacredness and veneration" (MM, Exp. , x), Mt 1:1 Mk 12:26 , Lk 3:4 20:42 Ac 1:20 7:42 19:19 ; β. τ. ζωῆς , Phl 4:3 , Re 3:5 20:15 .†
G976.strongs: properly, the inner bark of the papyrus plant, i.e. (by implication) a sheet or scroll of writing

G308.thayer: I look up, recover my sight.
G308.mounce: to look upwards, Mt. 14:19; to see again, recover sight, Mt. 11:5
G308.abbottSmith: to look up : Mk 8:24 , al.; seq. εἰς , Mt 14:19 , al. (Xen., Plat.).
G308.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G991 (βλέπω); to look up; by implication, to recover sight

G3153.thayer: vanity, emptiness, unreality, purposelessness, ineffectiveness, instability, frailty; false religion.
G3153.mounce: vanity, folly, futility, from the Hebrew, religious error, Eph. 4:17; 2 Pet. 2:18; false religion, Rom. 8:20*
G3153.abbottSmith: vanity , emptiness , frailty , folly : Ro 8:20 , II Pe 2:18 ; τ. νοός , Eph 4:17 (elsewhere only in Pollux, 6, 134, and Eccl.).†
G3153.strongs: from G3152 (μάταιος); inutility; figuratively, transientness; morally, depravity

G4782.mounce: to go down with anyone, Acts 25:5*
G4782.abbottSmith: to go down with : Ac 25:5 . In late writers also metaph. ( Wis, l.c. ), to condescend , on wh. v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 485 f.†
G4782.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2597 (καταβαίνω); to descend in company with

G967.thayer: Bethphage, a village in the neighborhood of Jerusalem, on the Mt. of Olives.
G967.mounce: Bethphage, indecl., a part of the Mount of Olives, Mt. 21:1; Mk. 11:1; Lk. 19:29*. See PhotoGuide.
G967.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01004 and H06291); fig-house; Beth-phage, a place in Palestine

G3802.abbottSmith: to ensnare : metaph., c. acc., ἐν λόγῳ , Mt 22:15 (not elsewhere).†

G5524.mounce: to lead a chorus; at Athens, to defray the cost of a chorus; hence, to supply funds; to supply, furnish, 2 Cor. 9:10; 1 Pet. 4:11*
G5524.abbottSmith: to lead a χορός (v. LS, s.v.).
G5524.strongs: from a compound of G5525 (χορός) and G71 (ἄγω); to be a dance-leader, i.e. (generally) to furnish

G2244.thayer: age, term of life; full age, maturity; stature.
G2244.mounce: a particular period of life; the period fitted for a particular function, prime, Heb. 11:11; full age, years of discretion, Jn. 9:21, 23; perhaps, the whole duration of life, Mt. 6:27; Lk. 12:25; otherwise, stature, Lk. 2:52; 19:3; Eph. 4:13*
G2244.abbottSmith: always of age or maturity; "a stage of growth whether measured by age or stature";
G2244.strongs: from the same as G2245 (ἡλίκος); maturity (in years or size)

G4763.mounce: to be wanton, to revel, riot, Rev. 18:7, 9*
G4763.abbottSmith: a word which first appears in the middle comedy (Rutherford, NPhr. , 475 f.), to run riot, wax wanton : Re 18:7 , 9 (cf. κατα-στρηνιάω ).†
G4763.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G4764 (στρῆνος); to be luxurious

G3489.thayer: (a) I am shipwrecked; so (b) fig: I come to ruin.
G3489.mounce: to make shipwreck, be shipwrecked, 2 Cor. 11:25; 1 Tim. 1:19*
G3489.abbottSmith: to suffer shipwreck : II Co 11:25 ; metaph., seq. περὶ τ. πίστιν , I Ti 1:19 .†
G3489.strongs: from a compound of G3491 (ναῦς) and G71 (ἄγω); to be shipwrecked (stranded, "navigate"), literally or figuratively

G4646.mounce: crooked, tortuous, Lk. 3:5; met. perverse, wicked, Acts 2:40; Phil. 2:15; crooked, peevish, morose, 1 Pet. 2:18*
G4646.abbottSmith: curved, bent, winding (opp. to ὀρθός, εὐθύς ): Lk 3:5 (LXX) . Metaph., crooked, perverse, unjust : Ac 2:40 , Phl 2:15 , I Pe 2:18 .†
G4646.strongs: from the base of G4628 (σκέλος); warped, i.e. winding; figuratively, perverse

G662.mounce: to dare or risk outright; to speak outright, without reserve or restraint, Rom. 10:20
G662.abbottSmith: to be quite bold , make a bold venture : Ro 10:20 .†
G662.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5111 (τολμάω); to venture plainly

G3682.thayer: (originally adj: useful, hence the play upon words in Philemon 10, 11, and very common as slave name), Onesimus, a slave of Philemon, a Christian of Colossae.
G3682.abbottSmith: Onesimus : Col 4:9 , Phm 10 (a common name among slaves; v. MM, iii, xviii).†
G3682.strongs: from G3685 (ὀνίνημι); profitable; Onesimus, a Christian

G2260.mounce: strengthened form of ἤ, than, Jn. 12:43*
G2260.strongs: from G2228 (ἤ) and G4007 (περ); than at all (or than perhaps, than indeed)

G2001.thayer: I persist, insist, am more urgent.
G2001.mounce: to strengthen; intrans. to gather strength; met. to be urgent, to press on a point, insist, Lk. 23:5*
G2001.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2480 (ἰσχύω); to avail further, i.e. (figuratively) insist stoutly

G5152.thayer: having three stories; subst: the third story.
G5152.mounce: the third floor, third story, Acts 20:9*
G5152.abbottSmith: of three stories : τὸ τ. (sc. ὄκημα ),
G5152.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G4721 (στέγη) as noun; a third roof (story)

G1955.mounce: a loosing, liberation; met. interpretation of what is enigmatical and obscure, 2 Pet. 1:20*

G1681.thayer: Elymas, the name of the sorcerer at Paphos.
G1681.strongs: of foreign origin; Elymas, a wizard

G2470.mounce: equal, like, Mt. 20:12; Lk. 6:34; on an equality, Phil. 2:6; met. correspondent, consistent, Mk. 14:56, 59
G2470.abbottSmith: equal , the same in size, number, quality, etc.: δωρεά , Ac 11:17 ; μαρτυρίαι , Mk 14:56 , 59 ; ἴ. ποιεῖν , c. acc. et dat., Mt 20:12 ; ἑαυτὸν τ. θεῷ , Jo 5:18 ; τὰ ἴ. ἀπολαβεῖν , Lk 6:34 , n. pl., ἴσα , adverbially, Re 21:16 ; τ. θεῷ , Phl 2:6 .†
G2470.strongs: probably from G1492 (εἴδω) (through the idea of seeming); similar (in amount and kind)

G152.mounce: shame, disgrace; cause of shame, dishonorable conduct
G152.abbottSmith: shame (MM, VGT , s.v.): subjectively, Lk 14:19 , II Co 4:2 ; objectively, Phl 3:19 , He 12:2 ; as something to be ashamed of, Re 3:18 ; pl. (Bl., §32, 6), shameful deeds , Ju 13 .†
G152.strongs: from G153 (αἰσχύνομαι); shame or disgrace (abstractly or concretely)

G3320.thayer: a middle wall, partition wall, barrier.
G3320.mounce: a middle wall; a partition wall, a barrier, Eph. 2:14*
G3320.abbottSmith: a partition wall : Eph 2:14 (not elsewhere, but v. LS, s.v. μεσότοιχος ).†
G3320.strongs: from G3319 (μέσος) and G5109 (τοῖχος); a partition (figuratively)

G3182.thayer: I make drunk; pass: I become drunk.
G3182.mounce: to inebriate, make drunk; pass. to be intoxicated, to be drunk, Lk. 12:45; Eph. 5:18; 1 Thess. 5:7; Rev. 17:2; to drink freely, Jn. 2:10*
G3182.abbottSmith: to make drunk , intoxicate ; pass., to get drunk : Lk 12:45 , Eph 5:18 , I Th 5:7 .†
G3182.strongs: a prolonged (transitive) form of G3184 (μεθύω); to intoxicate

G3895.thayer: I fall away, fall back (into the unbelieving and godless ways of the old time).
G3895.mounce: pr. to fall by the side of; met. to fall off or away from, make defection from, Heb. 6:6*
G3895.abbottSmith: most freq. (Hdt., Thuc., al.), to fall in one's way , befall .
G3895.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G4098 (πίπτω); to fall aside, i.e. (figuratively) to apostatize

G5262.thayer: (a) a figure, copy, (b) an example, model.
G5262.mounce: a token, intimation; an example, model, proposed for imitation or admonition, Jn. 13:15; Heb. 4:11; Jas. 5:10; 2 Pet. 2:6; a copy, Heb. 8:5; 9:23*
G5262.abbottSmith: used by later writers (Xen. onwards) for παράδειγμα (v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 62),
G5262.strongs: from G5263 (ὑποδείκνυμι); an exhibit for imitation or warning (figuratively, specimen, adumbration)

G1101.thayer: (a vernacular word), bag, purse; some prefer to take as: box, chest.
G1101.mounce: pr. a box for keeping the tongues, mouth-pieces, or reeds of musical instruments; hence, genr. any box or receptacle; in NT a purse, money-bag, Jn. 12:6; 13:29*
G1101.abbottSmith: = cl. -εῖον (v. supr.), a case for holding the reeds or tongues of musical instruments. As in LXX, π. (MM, s.v.), a box, chest : Jo 12:6 13:29 .†
G1101.strongs: from G1100 (γλῶσσα) and the base of G2889 (κόσμος); properly, a case (to keep mouthpieces of wind-instruments in) i.e. (by extension) a casket or (specially) purse

G5285.mounce: to blow gently, as the wind, Acts 27:13*
G5285.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4154 (πνέω); to breathe gently, i.e. breeze

G1028.abbottSmith: = βροχετός , a wetting (in π. , of irrigation in Egypt; Deiss., LAE , 77). As in MGr. (Kennedy, Sources , 153), = ὑετός , rain : Mt 7:25 , 27 .†

G4546.thayer: Samson, one of the Judges of Israel.
G4546.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08123); Sampson (i.e. Shimshon), an Israelite

G496.thayer: I resist, oppose, fall against or upon.
G496.mounce: pr. to fall upon, rush upon any one; hence, to resist by force, oppose, strive against, Acts 7:51*
G496.abbottSmith: to fall against or upon (Arist., Polyb.).
G496.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G4098 (πίπτω) (including its alternate); to oppose

G1723.mounce: to take into or embrace in one’s arms, Mk. 9:36; 10:16*
G1723.abbottSmith: to take into one's arms : Mk 9:36 10:16 .†
G1723.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G43 (ἀγκάλη); to take in one's arms, i.e. embrace

G2062.thayer: a creeping creature, reptile, especially a serpent.
G2062.mounce: a creeping animal, a reptile, Acts 10:12; 11:6; Rom. 1:23; Jas. 3:7*
G2062.abbottSmith: a creeping thing, reptile : Ac 10:12 11:6 , Ro 1:23 , Ja 3:7 .†
G2062.strongs: neuter of a derivative of (to creep); a reptile, i.e. (by Hebraism (compare H07431)) a small animal

G5595.mounce: pr. to feed by morsels; hence, genr. to feed, supply with food, Rom. 12:20; to bestow in supplying food, 1 Cor. 13:3*
G5595.abbottSmith: to feed with morsels (as children or the sick; Hippocr.), hence, generally, in late writers, to feed, nourish : c. acc. pers., Ro 12:20 (LXX) ; c. acc rei, to give out for food , I Co 13:3 (cf. WM, § 32, 4a n ; for dupl. acc., cf. Nu 11:4 , Si 15:3 , al.).†
G5595.strongs: from the base of G5596 (ψωμίον); to supply with bits, i.e. (generally) to nourish

G922.thayer: a weight, burden, lit. or met.
G922.mounce: weight, heaviness; a burden, anything grievous and hard to be borne, Mt. 20:12; Acts 15:28; Gal. 6:2; Rev. 2:24; burden, charge or weight, influence, dignity, honor, 1 Thess. 2:7; with another noun in government, fulness, abundance, excellence, 2 Cor. 4:17*
G922.abbottSmith: weight ; a weight , burden , lit. and metaph.: Mt 20:12 , Ac 15:28 , II Co 4:17 , Re 2:24 ; ἀλλήλων τὰ β. , one another's faults , Ga 6:2 ; ἐν β. , burdensome : I Th 2:6 , R, txt., but v. infr.; in late Gk. (Soph., Lex. , s.v.), dignity , authority : ἐν βάρει , I Th 2:6 (R, mg.; v. Milligan, ICG , in l.).†
G922.strongs: probably from the same as G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of going down; compare G899 (βάθος)); weight; in the New Testament only, figuratively, a load, abundance, authority

G4909.thayer: I consent, agree, am of one mind with, am willing.
G4909.mounce: to approve with another; to agree with in principle, Rom. 1:32; to stamp approval, Lk. 11:48; Acts 8:1; 22:20; to be willing, agreeable, 1 Cor. 7:12, 13*
G4909.abbottSmith: to join in approving, consent, agree to or with : absol., Ac 22:20 ; c. dat. pers., Ro 1:32 ; c. dat. rei, Lk 11:48 , Ac 8:1 ; c. inf., I Co 7:12, 13 (chiefly in late writers).†
G4909.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2106 (εὐδοκέω); to think well of in common, i.e. assent to, feel gratified with
G4909.kjv: allow, assent, be pleased, have pleasure

G1232.strongs: from G1123 (γραπτός) and G1107 (γνωρίζω); to tell abroad

G2769.mounce: pr. a little horn; in NT a pod, the pod of the carob tree, or Ceratonia siliqua of Linnaeus, a common tree in the East and the south of Europe, growing to a considerable size, and producing long slender pods, with a pulp of a sweetish taste, and several brown shining seeds like beans, sometimes eaten by the poorer people in Syria and Palestine, and commonly used for fattening swine, Lk. 15:16*
G2769.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G2768 (κέρας); something horned, i.e. (specially) the pod of the carob-tree

G4741.thayer: (a) I fix firmly, direct myself towards, (b) generally met: I buttress, prop, support; I strengthen, establish.
G4741.mounce: a stuffing of leaves, boughs, etc., meton. a bough, branch, Mk. 11:8*
G4741.abbottSmith: to fix, set fast, make fast : Lk 16:26 ; τ. πρόσωτον ( Ez 6:2 13:17 , al.; v. Dalman, Words , 30 f.), Lk 9:51 . Metaph., to confirm, establish : c., acc., Lk 22:32 , Ac 18:23 , Ro 1:11 16:25 , I Th 3:2 , 13 , II Th 3:3 , Ja 5:8 , I Pe 5:10 , Re 3:2 ; id. seq. ἐν , II Th 2:17 , II Pe 1:12 .†
G4741.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G2476 (ἵστημι) (like G4731 (στερεός)); to set fast, i.e. (literally) to turn resolutely in a certain direction, or (figuratively) to confirm

G2345.mounce: to touch, Col. 2:21; Heb. 12:20; to harm, Heb. 11:28*
G2345.strongs: a prolonged form of an obsolete primary (to finger); to manipulate, i.e. have to do with; by implication, to injure

G4531.thayer: I shake, excite, disturb in mind, stir up, drive away.
G4531.mounce: to make to rock, to shake, Mt. 11:7; 24:29; Mk. 13:25; Lk. 6:38, 48; 7:24; 21:26; Acts 4:31; 16:26; Heb. 12:26; met. to stir up, excite the people, Acts 17:13; to agitate, disturb mentally, Acts 2:25; 2 Thess. 2:2; pass. impl. to totter, be ready to fall, be near to ruin, met. Heb. 12:36, 27*
G4531.abbottSmith: prop., of the action of wind, storm, etc., to agitate, shake : of a reed, Mt 11:7 , Lk 7:24 ; a house, Lk 6:48 , Ac 4:31 16:26 ; the earth, He 12:26 ; the heavenly bodies, Mt 24:29 , Mk 13:25 , Lk 21:26 ; of a vessel shaken in filling, Lk 6:38 . Metaph.,
G4531.strongs: from G4535 (σάλος); to waver, i.e. agitate, rock, topple or (by implication) destroy; figuratively, to disturb, incite
G4531.kjv: move, shake (together), which can(-not) be shaken, stir up

G450.thayer: I raise up, set up; I rise from among (the) dead; I arise, appear.
G450.mounce: trans. to cause to stand up or rise, Acts 9:41; to raise up, as the dead, Jn. 6:39; to raise up into existence, Mt. 22:24; intrans. and mid., to rise up, Mt. 9:9; to rise up into existence, Acts 7:18; 20:30
G450.abbottSmith: causal, in fut. and 1 aor. act., c. acc., to raise up : Ac 9:41 ; from death, Jo 6:39 , Ac 2:32 ; to raise up , cause to be born or appear : Mt 22:24 , Ac 3:22 , 26 .
G450.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand up (literal or figurative, transitive or intransitive)
G450.kjv: arise, lift up, raise up (again), rise (again), stand up(-right)

G1822.thayer: (a) I fit up, completely furnish, equip, furnish, supply, (b) I accomplish, finish.
G1822.mounce: to equip or furnish completely, 2 Tim. 3:17; to complete time, Acts 21:5*
G1822.abbottSmith: to com­plete, finish : τ. ἡμέρας , Ac 21:5 .
G1822.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G739 (ἄρτιος); to finish out (time); figuratively, to equip fully (a teacher)

G1877.thayer: (a) nautical: I put out (from the shore), (lit: I take up a ship on to the high seas), (b) I go up, possibly: I go up again, return.
G1877.mounce: to bring up or back; intrans. to return, Mt. 21:18; a nautical term, to put off from shore, Lk. 5:3, 4*
G1877.abbottSmith: to bring up or back (sc. ναῦς ); to put out to sea ( DB , iii, 63 b ): Lk 5:3 , 4 ; intrans., to return : Mt 21:18 .†
G1877.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G321 (ἀνάγω); to lead up on, i.e. (technical) to put out (to sea); (intransitively) to return

G5558.abbottSmith: to gild, cover with gold : pass., c. dat., χρυσίῳ (pleonast., cf. Ex 26:37 ), Re 17:4 18:16 .†
G5558.strongs: from G5557 (χρυσός); to gild, i.e. bespangle with golden ornaments

G3994.strongs: feminine of G3995 (πενθερός); a wife's mother

G43.thayer: an arm, especially as bent to receive a burden.

G5490.strongs: from a form of an obsolete primary (to "gape" or "yawn"); a "chasm" or vacancy (impassable interval)

G3949.mounce: to provoke to anger, irritate, exasperate, Rom. 10:19; Eph. 6:4*
G3949.abbottSmith: (in cl., passive only), to provoke to anger : Ro 10:19 (LXX) , Eph 6:4 .†
G3949.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3710 (ὀργίζω); to anger alongside, i.e. enrage

G1625.mounce: to nourish, promote health and strength, Eph. 5:29; to bring up, educate, Eph. 6:4*
G1625.abbottSmith: prop., of children, to nurture, bring up : Eph 6:4 .
G1625.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5142 (τρέφω); to rear up to maturity, i.e. (genitive case) to cherish or train

G3825.thayer: again, back, once more, further, on the other hand.
G3825.mounce: pr. back; again, back again, Jn. 10:17; Acts 10:16; 11:10; again by repetition, Mt. 26:43; again in continuation, further, Mt. 5:33; 13:44, 45, 47, 18:19; again, on the other hand, 1 Jn. 2:8
G3825.strongs: probably from the same as G3823 (πάλη) (through the idea of oscillatory repetition); (adverbially) anew, i.e. (of place) back, (of time) once more, or (conjunctionally) furthermore or on the other hand

G3627.mounce: also spelled οἰκτείρω, to have compassion on, exercise grace or favor towards, Rom. 9:15*
G3627.abbottSmith: to pity , have compassion on. c. acc. pers., Ro 9:15 (LXX) .†

G4165.thayer: I shepherd, tend, herd; hence: I rule, govern.
G4165.mounce: to feed, pasture, tend a flock, Lk. 17:7; 1 Cor. 9:7; trop. to feed with selfish indulgence, to pamper, Jude 12; met. to tend, direct, superintend, Mt. 2:6; Jn. 21:16; to rule, Rev. 2:27
G4165.abbottSmith: to act as shepherd , tend flocks: Lk 17:7 ; c. acc., ποίμνην , I Co 9:7 . Metaph., to tend , shepherd , govern : c. acc., Mt 2:6 (LXX) , Jo 21:16 , Ac 20:28 , I Pe 5:2 , Ju 12 , Re 2:27 7:17 12:5 19:15 .†
G4165.strongs: from G4166 (ποιμήν); to tend as a shepherd of (figuratively, superviser)

G2569.thayer: I do well, act honorably, do what is right.
G2569.mounce: to do well, do good, do what is right, 2 Thess. 3:13*
G2569.abbottSmith: to do well , act honourably : II Th 3:13 (cf. Ga 6:9 τὸ καλὸν π. ).†
G2569.strongs: from G2570 (καλός) and G4160 (ποιέω); to do well, i.e. live virtuously

G150.mounce: strictly, deformed, opposed to καλός; met. shameful, indecent, dishonorable, vile, 1 Cor. 11:6; 14:35; Eph. 5:12; Tit. 1:11*
G150.strongs: from the same as G153 (αἰσχύνομαι); shameful, i.e. base (specially, venal)

G3759.thayer: woe!, alas!, uttered in grief or denunciation.
G3759.mounce: Wo! Alas! Mt. 11:21; 18:7; 23:13, 14, 15, 16; ἡ οὐαί, subst., a woe, calamity, Rev. 9:12; 11:14
G3759.abbottSmith: alas! woe! most freq. c. dat. pers.. Mt 11:21 23:14 , Mk 13:17 14:21 , Lk 6:24-26 , Ju 11 , al.; c. vocat. (nom.), Re 18:10 , 16 , 19 (cf. Is 1:24 , al.); c. acc., Re 12:12 , seq. ἐκ , 8:13 ; c. dat. seq. ἀπό (v. M, Pr. , 246), Mt 18:7 . As subst., I Co 9:16 (cf. Je 6:4 ); ἡ οὐ. , Re 9:12 11:14 ; pl., Re 9:12 ; οὐ. , οὐ. , οὐ. , Re 8:13 .

G2870.thayer: beating of the breast or head in lamentation, lamentation.
G2870.mounce: pr. a beating of the breast, etc., in token of grief; a wailing, lamentation, Acts 8:2*
G2870.abbottSmith: = cl. κομμός , a beating of the head and breast , lamentation : seq. ἐπί c. dat. pers., Ac 8:2 .†
G2870.strongs: from G2875 (κόπτω); mourning (properly, by beating the breast)

G1817.thayer: (a) in trans. tenses: I raise up, cause to grow, (b) in intrans. tenses: I rise up from.
G1817.mounce: to cause to rise up, raise up; from the Hebrew, to raise up into existence, Mk. 12:19; Lk. 20:28; intrans. to rise up from, stand forth, Acts 15:5*
G1817.abbottSmith: trans., to raise up : σπέρμα (cf. Ge 38:8 ), Mk 12:19 , Lk 20:28 .
G1817.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G450 (ἀνίστημι); objectively, to produce, i.e. (figuratively) beget; subjectively, to arise, i.e. (figuratively) object

G2381.thayer: Thomas, also called Didymus, one of the Twelve.
G2381.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H08380); the twin; Thomas, a Christian

G1148.thayer: Dalmanutha, a town or village near Magdala.
G1148.mounce: Dalmanutha, indecl., a small town on the shore of the Sea of Tiberias, Mk. 8:10*
G1148.strongs: probably of Chaldee origin; Dalmanutha, a place in Palestine

G910.thayer: the baptizer, the Baptist, epithet used only of John, the son of Zechariah and Elizabeth, forerunner of Jesus.
G910.mounce: one who baptizes, a baptist, Mt. 3:1; 11:11, 12
G910.strongs: from G907 (βαπτίζω); a baptizer, as an epithet of Christ's forerunner

G45.abbottSmith: an anchor : Ac 27:29 , 30 40 ; fig. (MM, VGT , s.v.), He 6:19 .†
G45.strongs: from the same as G43 (ἀγκάλη); an "anchor" (as crooked)

G2686.mounce: to exercise cleverness to the detriment of any one, to outwit; to make a victim of subtlety, to practice on the insidious dealing, Acts 7:19*
G2686.abbottSmith: to deal craftily with , outwit : Ac 7:19 (LXX) .†
G2686.strongs: middle voice from G2596 (κατά) and G4679 (σοφίζω); to be crafty against, i.e. circumvent

G1519.thayer: into, in, unto, to, upon, towards, for, among.
G1519.abbottSmith: expressing entrance, direction, limit, into, unto, to, upon, towards, for, among (Lat. in , c. acc.). Of place. After verbs of motion; of entrance into: Mt 8:23 9:7 , Mk 1:45 , Lk 2:15 8:31 , al.; of approach, to or towards : Mk 11:1 , Lk 6:8 19:28 , Jo 11:31 21:6 , al.; before pl. and collective nouns, among : Mk 4:7 8:19 , 20 , Lk 11:49 , Jo 21:23 , al.; Of a limit reached, unto, on, upon : Mt 8
G1519.strongs: a primary preposition; to or into (indicating the point reached or entered), of place, time, or (figuratively) purpose (result, etc.); also in adverbial phrases
G1519.kjv: (abundant-)ly, against, among, as, at, (back-)ward, before, by, concerning, + continual, + far more exceeding, for (intent, purpose), fore, + forth, in (among, at, unto, -so much that, -to), to the intent that, + of one mind, + never, of, (up-)on, + perish, + set at one again, (so) that, therefore(-unto), throughout, til, to (be, the end, -ward), (here-)until(-to), …ward, (where-)fore, with

G233.thayer: I salt, sprinkle with salt (of sacrifices or of those who offer sacrifice), keep fresh and sound, and so acceptable to God.
G233.mounce: to salt, season with salt, preserve by salting, Mt. 5:13; Mk. 9:49*
G233.abbottSmith: to salt , season with salt : Mt 5:13 , Mk 9:49 .†

G2398.thayer: one's own, belonging to one, private, personal; one's own people, one's own family, home, property.
G2398.mounce: one’s own, Mk. 15:20; Jn. 7:18; due, proper, specially assigned, Gal. 6:9; 1 Tim. 2:6; 6:15; Tit. 1:3; also used in NT as a simple possessive, Eph. 5:22; τὰ ἴδια, one’s home, household, people, Jn. 1:11; 16:32; 19:17; οἱ ἴδιοι, members of one’s own household, friends, Jn. 1:11; Acts 24:23; ἰδίᾳ, adverbially, respectively, 1 Cor. 12:11; κατ’ ἰδίαν, adv., privately, aside, by one’s self, alone, Mt. 14:13, 23
G2398.abbottSmith: one's own ; of that which is private and personal (in cl. opp. to κοινός, δημόσιος ; cf. infr. 3); of property, friends, home, country, etc. (in cl. opp. to ἀλλότριος ; in late writers often, like ἑαυτοῦ , with weakened sense, v. M, Pr. , 87 ff.; Deiss., BS , 123 f.): Lk 6:41 , Jo 1:42 5:43 , Ac 2:6 20:28 , I Co 11:21 , Ga 6:5 , II Ti 1:9 , He 7:27 , Ju 6 , al.; πράσσειν τὰ ἴ. , I Th 4:11 ; κατὰ τ
G2398.strongs: of uncertain affinity; pertaining to self, i.e. one's own; by implication, private or separate
G2398.kjv: X his acquaintance, when they were alone, apart, aside, due, his (own, proper, several), home, (her, our, thine, your) own (business), private(-ly), proper, severally, their (own)

G536.thayer: the first-fruits, the earliest crop of the year, hence also met., for example, of the earliest converts in a district; there is evidence in favor of rendering in some passages merely by: sacrifice, gift.
G536.mounce: pr. the first act of a sacrifice; hence, the firstfruits, first portion, firstling, Rom. 8:23
G536.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G756 (ἄρχομαι); a beginning of sacrifice, i.e. the (Jewish) first-fruit (figuratively)

G4843.mounce: to embrace together; to embrace, Acts 20:10*
G4843.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take by enclosing altogether, i.e. earnestly throw the arms about one

G2306.strongs: from G2303 (θεῖον) and 1491; sulphur-like, i.e. sulphurous

G889.thayer: (lit: I become sour, I turn, of milk), I am good for nothing, render useless; met: I become corrupt.
G889.mounce: also ἀχρεόω, pass., to render useless; met., to become corrupt, depraved, Rom. 3:12*
G889.abbottSmith: to make useless , unprofitable : pass., Ro 3:12 (LXX) .†
G889.strongs: from G888 (ἀχρεῖος); to render useless, i.e. spoil

G3533.mounce: Nicopolis, a city of Macedonia, Tit. 3:12*. See PhotoGuide.
G3533.abbottSmith: Nicopolis , prob. the city of that name in Epirus ( CGT , in l.): Tit 3:12 .†
G3533.strongs: from G3534 (νῖκος) and G4172 (πόλις); victorious city; Nicopolis, a place in Macedonia

G3360.thayer: as far as, until, even to.
G3360.mounce: improper prep. and a conj (until), can also spelled μέχρις, unto, even to, Rom. 15:19; of time, until, till, Mt. 11:23; Mk. 13:30
G3360.abbottSmith: as prep., c. gen., as far as , even to , until ; of place: Ro 15:19 ; of time: Mt 11:23 13:30 28:15 , Lk 16:16 , Ac 10:30 20:7 , Ro 5:14 , I Ti 6:14 , He 3:6 , 14 9:10 ; of measure or degree: Phl 2:8 , 30 , II Ti 2:9 , He 12:4 ( μ. αἵματος , cf. II Mac 13:14 ).
G3360.strongs: from G3372 (μῆκος); as far as, i.e. up to a certain point (as a preposition, of extent (denoting the terminus, whereas G891 (ἄχρι) refers especially to the space of time or place intervening) or conjunction)

G3749.abbottSmith: made of clay , earthen : II Co 4:7 , II Ti 2:20 .†
G3749.strongs: from ("oyster") (a tile, i.e. terra cotta); earthen-ware, i.e. clayey; by implication, frail

G679.mounce: free from stumbling; met. free from moral stumbling, offence; irreprehensible, Jude 24*
G679.abbottSmith: without stumbling , sure-footed : metaph. (MM, s.v.), Ju 24 .†
G679.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4417 (πταίω); not stumbling, i.e. (figuratively) without sin

G2181.thayer: Ephesus, a coast city, capital of the Roman province Asia.
G2181.mounce: Ephesus, a celebrated city of Asia Minor, Acts 18:19, 21, 24; 1 Cor. 15:32*
G2181.abbottSmith: Ephesus , a city in Asia Minor Ac 18:19 , 21 , 24 , 27 19:1 , 17 , 26 (on the gen., v. M, Pr. , 73) Ac 20:16 , 17 , I Co 15:32 16:8 , Eph 1:1 , I Ti 1:3 , II Ti 1:18 4:12 , Re 1:11 2:1 .†
G2181.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Ephesus, a city of Asia Minor

G436.thayer: I set against; I withstand, resist, oppose.
G436.mounce: to oppose, resist, stand out against
G436.abbottSmith: in pres., impf., fut. and 1 aor. act., causal, to set against .
G436.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand against, i.e. oppose

G2891.mounce: an Aramaic imperative, also spelled κοῦμι, stand up, Mk. 5:41*

G4525.thayer: I fawn upon, flatter, beguile; pass: I am perturbed.
G4525.mounce: pr. to wag the tail; to fawn, flatter, cajole; pass. to be cajoled; to be wrought upon, to be perturbed, 1 Thess. 3:3*
G4525.abbottSmith: prop. (Hom., al.), of dogs, to wag the tail, fawn .
G4525.strongs: akin to G4579 (σείω); to wag (as a dog its tail fawningly), i.e. (generally) to shake (figuratively, disturb)

G3558.mounce: the south wind, Lk. 12:55; Acts 27:13; 28:13; meton. the south, the southern quarter of the heavens, Mt. 12:42; Lk. 11:31; 13:29; Rev. 21:13*
G3558.abbottSmith: prop., the south wind : Lk 12:55 , Ac 27:13 28:13 .
G3558.strongs: of uncertain affinity; the south(-west) wind; by extension, the southern quarter itself

G3997.strongs: strengthened from the alternate of G3958 (πάσχω); grief

G2635.thayer: I speak evil of, rail at, slander.
G2635.mounce: to blab out; to speak against, slander, Jas. 4:11; 1 Pet. 2:12; 3:16*
G2635.abbottSmith: to speak evil of , rail at : in cl., c. acc.; in LXX, c. gen., seq. κατά , c. gen.; in NT, c. gen. (M, Pr. , 65): Ja 4:11 , I Pe 2:12 ; pass., I Pe 3:16 .†
G2635.strongs: from G2637 (κατάλαλος); to be a traducer, i.e. to slander

G1915.mounce: that which is put over or upon; in NT a patch, Mt. 9:16; Mk. 2:21; Lk. 5:36 (2x)*
G1915.abbottSmith: that which is thrown over, a cover .

G1177.strongs: from G1176 (δέκα) and G1417 (δύο); two and ten, i.e. twelve

G5313.mounce: height, Rom. 8:39; a towering of self-conceit, presumption, 2 Cor. 10:5*
G5313.strongs: from G5312 (ὑψόω); an elevated place or thing, i.e. (abstractly) altitude, or (by implication) a barrier (figuratively)

G503.thayer: I face (lit: "I present my eye to"), resist, withstand; as nautical term: I bear up against the wind.
G503.mounce: pr. to look in the face, met. a nautical term, to bear up against the wind, Acts 27:15*
G503.abbottSmith: to look in the face , look straight at (Polyb.). Metaph., to face , withstand ( Wi 12:14 , Polyb.): c. dat., ἀ. τ. ἀνέμῳ , as nautical term, to beat up against the wind (v. DB , ext. , 366 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 27:15 .†
G503.strongs: from a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G3788 (ὀφθαλμός); to face

G1472.abbottSmith: to rub in, anoint : mid., c. dupl. acc., Re 3:18 .†
G1472.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5548 (χρίω); to rub in (oil), i.e. besmear

G5538.mounce: in NT a response from God, a divine communication, oracle, Rom. 11:4*
G5538.abbottSmith: a divine response, an oracle : Ro 11:4 (Xen., Plat., al.).†
G5538.strongs: from G5537 (χρηματίζω); a divine response or revelation

G1007a.abbottSmith: Beor , father of Balaam: II Pe 2:15 (Rec. Βοσόρ ).†

G2693.thayer: I strew or spread over; I lay low, overthrow.
G2693.mounce: to lay flat; pass. to be laid prostrate in death, 1 Cor. 10:5*
G2693.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4766 (στρώννυμι); to strew down, i.e. (by implication) to prostrate (slay)

G829.mounce: one who pleases himself, willful, obstinate; arrogant, stubborn, Tit. 1:7; 2 Pet. 2:10*
G829.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and the base of G2237 (ἡδονή); self-pleasing, i.e. arrogant

G2356.thayer: (underlying sense: reverence or worship of the gods), worship as expressed in ritual acts, religion.
G2356.mounce: religious worship, Col. 2:18; religion, a religious system, Acts 26:5; religion, piety, Jas. 1:26, 27*
G2356.abbottSmith: religion in its external aspect (MM, Exp. , xv), worship : Ac 26:5 , Ja 1:26 , 27 ; θ. τῶν ἀγγέλων , Col 2:18 .†
G2356.strongs: from a derivative of G2357 (θρησκός); ceremonial observance

G4145.thayer: rich, abounding in, wealthy; subst: a rich man.
G4145.mounce: rich, opulent, wealthy; and pl. οἱ πλούσιοι, the rich, Mt. 19:23, 24; 27:57; met. rich, abounding in, distinguished for, Eph. 2:4; Jas. 2:5; Rev. 2:9; 3:17; rich in glory, dignity, bliss, etc., 2 Cor. 8:9
G4145.abbottSmith: rich , wealthy : Mt 27:57 , Lk 12:16 14:12 16:1 , 19 18:23 19:2 . Substantively, ὁ π. , Lk 16:21 , 22 , Ja 1:10 , 11 ; οἱ π. , Lk 6:24 21:1 , I Ti 6:17 , Ja 2:6 5:1 , Re 6:15 13:16 ; anarth., a rich man , Mt 19:23 , 24 , Mk 10:25 12:41 , Lk 18:25 . Metaph., of God, ἐν ἐλέει (= cl., c. gen., dat.), Eph 2:4 ; of Christ, II Co 8:9 ; of Christians, Re 2:9 3:17 ; ἐν πίστει , Ja 2:5 .†
G4145.strongs: from G4149 (πλοῦτος); wealthy; figuratively, abounding with

G5266.mounce: anything bound under; a sandal, Mt. 3:11; 10:10
G5266.abbottSmith: a sole bound under the foot, a sandal: Mt 3:11 10:10 , Mk 1:7 , Lk 3:16 10:4 15:22 22:35 , Jo 1:27 ; τ. ποδῶν , Ac 7:33 (LXX) Ac 13:25 .†
G5266.strongs: from G5265 (ὑποδέω); something bound under the feet, i.e. a shoe or sandal

G250.thayer: aloes, the powdered fragrant aloe wood.
G250.mounce: aloe, lign-aloe, a tree which grows in India and Cochin-China, the wood of which is soft and bitter, though highly aromatic. It is used by the Orientals as a perfume; and employed for the purposes of embalming, Jn. 19:39*
G250.abbottSmith: the aloe , aloes (the powder of a fragrant wood): Jo 19:39 .†
G250.strongs: of foreign origin (compare G174 (ἀκάνθινος)); aloes (the gum)

G3121.thayer: Mahalaleel or Maleleel, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3121.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04111); Maleleel (i.e. Mahalalel), an antediluvian

G3404.thayer: I hate, detest, love less, esteem less.
