G3404.mounce: to hate, regard with ill-will, Mt. 5:43, 44; 10:22; to detest, abhor, Jn. 3:20; Rom. 7:15; in NT to regard with less affection, love less, esteem less, Mt. 6:24; Lk. 14:26
G3404.abbottSmith: to hate : c. acc. pers., Mt 5:43 24:10 , Lk 1:71 6:22 , 27 19:14 , Jo 7:7 15:18 , 19 , 23-25 17:14 , Tit 3:3 , I Jo 2:9 , 11 3:13 , 15 4:20 , Re 17:16 ; pass., Mt 10:22 24:9 , Mk 13:13 , Lk 21:17 ; c. acc. rei, Jo 3:20 , Ro 7:15 , Eph 5:29 , He 1:9 , Ju 23 , Re 2:6 ; pass., Re 18:2 . As the Heb. שָׂנֵא is sometimes found with the modified sense of indifference to or relative disregard for one thin
G3404.strongs: from a primary (hatred); to detest (especially to persecute); by extension, to love less

G326.thayer: I come to life again, revive, regain life.
G326.mounce: to live again, recover life, revive, Rom. 7:9; met. to live a new and reformed life, Lk. 15:24, 32*
G326.abbottSmith: to live again , regain life (cf. cl. ἀναβιόω ; Cremer, 722; and for other exx., v MM, VGT , s.v.): metaph. of moral revival, Lk 15:24 (WH, mg., ἔζησεν ); of sin, Ro 7:9 .†
G326.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2198 (ζάω); to recover life (literally or figuratively)

G2745.thayer: a boasting; a ground of boasting (glorying, exultation).
G2745.mounce: a glorying, boasting, 1 Cor. 5:6; a ground or matter of glorying or boasting, Rom. 4:2; joy, exultation, Phil. 1:26; complimentary testimony, 1 Cor. 9:15, 16; 2 Cor. 9:3
G2745.abbottSmith: a boast (Pind.): II Co 5:12 9:3 (Thayer, s.v.; e. contra, 2, Ellic., Lft., on Ga 6:4 ; Lft., Notes , 204, 277).
G2745.strongs: from G2744 (καυχάομαι); a boast (properly, the object; by implication, the act) in a good or a bad sense

G278.thayer: not to be repented of, about which no change of mind can take place, not affected by change of mind.
G278.mounce: not to be repented of; by impl. irrevocable, enduring, Rom. 11:29; 2 Cor. 7:10*
G278.abbottSmith: not repented of , unregretted : Ro 11:29 , II Co 7:10 .†
G278.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G3338 (μεταμέλλομαι); irrevocable
G278.kjv: without repentance, not to be repented of

G506.mounce: not subjected, not made subordinate, Heb. 2:8; insubordinate, refractory, disorderly, contumacious, lawless, 1 Tim. 1:9; Tit. 1:6, 10
G506.abbottSmith: of things, not subject to rule : He 2:8 ; of persons, unruly : I Ti 1:9 , Tit 1:6 , 10 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G506.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G5293 (ὑποτάσσω); unsubdued, i.e. insubordinate (in fact or temper)
G506.kjv: disobedient, that is not put under, unruly

G2921.thayer: Crispus, ruler of the synagogue at Corinth, converted and baptized by Paul.
G2921.mounce: Crispus, a Latin pr. name, Acts 18:8; 1 Cor. 1:14*
G2921.strongs: of Latin origin; "crisp"; Crispus, a Corinthian

G989.mounce: slanderous, railing, reproachful, 2 Tim. 3:2; 2 Pet. 2:11; blasphemous, Acts 6:11, 13; 1 Tim. 1:13
G989.abbottSmith: evil-speaking, slanderous, blasphemous : Ac 6:11 , II Ti 3:2 , II Pe 2:11 (cf. Ju 9 ); as subst. a blasphemer : I Ti 1:13 (Cremer, 570).†
G989.strongs: from a derivative of G984 (βλάπτω) and G5345 (φήμη); scurrilious, i.e. calumnious (against men), or (specially) impious (against God)

G3710.mounce: some list as deponent, ὀργίζομαι, to provoke to anger, irritate; pass. to be angry, indignant, enraged, Mt. 5:22; 18:34
G3710.abbottSmith: to make angry , provoke to anger ; in cl. most freq. in pass., and so always in NT, to be provoked to anger , be angry : absol., Mt 18:34 22:7 , Lk 14:21 15:28 , Eph 4:26 (LXX) , Re 11:18 ; c. dat., Mt 5:22 ; seq. ἐπί , c. dat. ( III Ki 11:9 , al.), Re 12:17 (cf. παρ-ὀργίζω ).†
G3710.strongs: from G3709 (ὀργή); to provoke or enrage, i.e. (passively) become exasperated

G849.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and G5495 (χείρ); self-handed, i.e. doing personally

G1807.thayer: I take out, remove; sometimes (mid): I choose, sometimes: I rescue.
G1807.mounce: to take out of; to pluck out, tear out, Mt. 5:29; 18:9; mid. to take out of, select, choose, Acts 26:17; to rescue, deliver, Acts 7:10, 34; 12:11; 23:27; Gal. 1:4*
G1807.abbottSmith: to take out : c. acc, ὀφθαλμόν , Mt 5:29 18:9 ; mid.
G1807.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G138 (αἱρέομαι); actively, to tear out; middle voice, to select; figuratively, to release

G3641.thayer: (a) especially in plur: few, (b) in sing: small; hence, of time: short, of degree: light, slight, little.
G3641.mounce: little, small, in number, etc.; pl. few, Mt. 7:14; 9:37; 20:16; Lk. 13:23; δι’ ὀλίγων, i.e. λόγων, in a few words, briefly, 1 Pet. 5:12; little in time, short, brief, Acts 14:28; Rev. 12:12; πρὸς ὀλίγον, i.e. χρόνον, for a short time, for a little while, Jas. 4:14; little, small, light, etc., in magnitude, amount, etc., Lk. 7:47; Acts 12:18; 15:2; ἐν ὀλίγῳ, concisely, briefly, Eph. 3:3; almost, Acts 26:28, 29
G3641.abbottSmith: of number, quantity, size, few , little , small , slight : Mt 9:37 15:34 , Mk 6:5 8:7 , Lk 10:2 12:48 (sc. πληγάς , opp. to πολλάς ), I Ti 5:23 , He 12:10 , Re 3:4 12:12 ; οὐκ ὀ. (in the best uncials written οὐκ ὀ. ; v. WH, App. , 143; M, Pr. , 44; Thackeray, Gr. , 126 f.), Ac 12:18 14:28 15:2 (c. gen. part.) Ac 17:4 , 12 19:23 , 24 27:20 ; pl., absol., Mt 7:14 20:16 (WH, txt., RV, om.) Mt 22:14 ,
G3641.strongs: of uncertain affinity; puny (in extent, degree, number, duration or value); especially neuter (adverbially) somewhat
G3641.kjv: + almost, brief(-ly), few, (a) little, + long, a season, short, small, a while

G2460.thayer: a horse-soldier, a mounted soldier, a cavalryman.
G2460.mounce: a horseman; pl. ἱππεῖς, horsemen, cavalry, Acts 23:23, 32*
G2460.strongs: from G2462 (ἵππος); an equestrian, i.e. member of a cavalry corps

G2865.thayer: (a) act: I convey, bring, carry, (b) mid: I receive back, receive what has belonged to myself but has been lost, or else promised but kept back, or: I get what has come to be my own by earning, recover.
G2865.mounce: pr. to take into kindly keeping, to provide for; to convey, bring, Lk. 7:37; mid. to bring for one’s self; to receive, obtain, 2 Cor. 5:10; Eph. 6:8; to receive again, recover, Mt. 25:27; Heb. 11:19
G2865.strongs: from a primary (to tend, i.e. take care of); properly, to provide for, i.e. (by implication) to carry off (as if from harm; genitive case obtain)

G1824.mounce: at the very time; presently, instantly, immediately, Mk. 6:25; Acts 10:33; 11:11
G1824.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the genitive case singular feminine of G846 (αὐτός) (G5610 (ὥρα) being understood); from that hour, i.e. instantly
G1824.kjv: by and by, immediately, presently, straightway

G5573.abbottSmith: speaking falsely, lying : I Ti 4:2 (Aristoph., Polyb., al.).†
G5573.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G3004 (λέγω); mendacious, i.e. promulgating erroneous Christian doctrine

G995.strongs: from G994 (βοάω); a halloo, i.e. call (for aid, etc.)

G3999.strongs: multiplicative adverb from G4002 (πέντε); five times

G1398.thayer: I am a slave, am subject to, obey, am devoted.
G1398.mounce: to be a slave or servant; to be in slavery or subjection, Jn. 8:33; Acts 7:7; Rom. 9:12; to discharge the duties of a slave or servant, Eph. 6:7; 1 Tim. 6:2; to serve, be occupied in the service of, be devoted, subservient, Mt. 6:24; Lk. 15:29; Acts 20:19; Rom. 14:18; 16:18; met. to be enthralled, involved in a slavish service, spiritually or morally, Gal. 4:9, 25; Tit. 3:3
G1398.abbottSmith: to be a slave, be subject to, serve : absol., Ro 7:6 , Ga 4:25 , I Ti 6:2 ; c. dat pers., Mt 6:24 , Lk 15:29 16:13 , Ro 9:12 (LXX) ; of nations, Jo 8:33 , Ac 7:7 (LXX) ; θεῷ, τ. κυρίῳ , Mt 6:24 , Lk 16:13 , Ac 20:19 , Ro 12:11 (R, mg., καιρῷ ) 16:18 , Eph 6:7 , Col 3:24 , I Th 1:9 ; τ. Χριστῷ , Ro 14:18 , Col 3:24 ; νόμῳ θεοῦ , Ro 7:25 ; τ. θεοῖς , Ga 4:8 ; τ. καιρῷ , Ro 12:11 (R, mg. for κυρίῳ );
G1398.strongs: from G1401 (δοῦλος); to be a slave to (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary)

G931.mounce: pr. lapis Lydius, a species of stone from Lydia, which being applied to metals was thought to indicate any alloy which might be mixed with them, and therefore used in the trial of metals; hence, examination of a person, especially by torture; in NT torture, torment, severe pain, Mt. 4:24; Lk. 16:23, 28*
G931.abbottSmith: prop., touchstone , a dark stone used in testing metals.
G931.strongs: perhaps remotely from the same as G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of going to the bottom); a touch-stone, i.e. (by analogy) torture

G771.mounce: pr. weakness, infirmity, met. doubt, hesitation, Rom. 15:1*
G771.strongs: from G770 (ἀσθενέω); a scruple of conscience

G5457.thayer: light, a source of light, radiance.
G5457.mounce: light, Mt. 17:2; 2 Cor. 4:6; daylight, broad day, Mt. 10:27; Lk. 12:3; radiance, blaze of light, Mt. 4:16; Acts 9:3; 12:7; an instrument or means of light, a light, Mt. 6:23; Acts 16:29; a fire, Mk. 14:54; Lk. 22:56; from the Hebrew, the light of God’s presence, 2 Cor. 11:14; 1 Tim. 6:16; met. the light of Divine truth, spiritual illumination, Lk. 16:8; Jn. 3:19; Rom. 13:12; Eph. 5:8; 1 Pet. 2:9; 1 Jn. 1:7; 2:8, 9, 10; a source or dispenser of spiritual light, Mt. 5:14; Jn. 1:4, 5, 7, 8, 9; 8:12; 9:5; pure radiance, perfect brightness, 1 Jn. 1:5
G5457.abbottSmith: light (opp. to τὸ σκότος, ἡ σκοτία ): Mt 17:2 , 5 , Jo 11:9 , 10 , II Co 4:6 ; ἡλίου , Re 22:5 ; of a lamp, Lk 8:16 11:33 , Jo 5:35 , Re 18:23 ; of a supernatural heavenly light, Ac 9:3 12:7 22:6 , 9 , 11 26:13 ; hence, ἄγγελος φωτός , II Co 11:14 ; ὁ κλήρος τ. ἁγίων ἐν τ. φ. , Col 1:12 ; of the divine glory, Re 21:24 ; by meton., of that which gives light: of fire, Mk 14:54 , Lk 22:56 (cl.; I Mac
G5457.strongs: from an obsolete (to shine or make manifest, especially by rays; compare G5316 (φαίνω), G5346 (φημί)); luminousness (in the widest application, natural or artificial, abstract or concrete, literal or figurative)

G4982.mounce: to save, rescue; to preserve safe and unharmed, Mark. 8:25; 10:22; 24:22; 27:40, 42, 49; 1 Tim. 2:15; σῴζειν εἰς, to bring safely to, 2 Tim. 4:18; to cure, heal, restore to health, Mt. 9:21, 22; Mk. 5:23, 28, 34; 6:56; to save, preserve from being lost, Mt. 16:25; Mk. 3:4; 8:35; σῴζειν ἀπό, to deliver from, set free from, Mt. 1:21; Jn. 12:27; Acts 2:40; in NT to rescue from unbelief, convert, Rom. 11:14; 1 Cor. 1:21; 7:16; to bring within the pale of saving privilege, Tit. 3:5; 1 Pet. 3:21; to save from final ruin, 1 Tim. 1:15; pass. to be brought within the pale of saving privilege, Acts 2:47; Eph. 2:5, 8; to be in the way of salvation, 1 Cor. 15:2; 2 Cor. 2:15
G4982.abbottSmith: to save from peril, injury or suffering: Mt 8:25 , Mk 13:20 , Lk 23:35 , al.; τ. ψυχήν , Mt 16:25 , al.; seq. ἐκ , Jo 12:27 , He 5:7 , Ju 5 ; of healing, restoring to health: Mt 9:22 , Mk 5:34 , al. In NT, esp. of salvation from spiritual disease and death, in which sense it is "spoken of in Scripture as either (1) past, (2) present, or (3) future, according as redemption, grace, or glory is the p
G4982.strongs: from a primary (contraction for obsolete , "safe"); to save, i.e. deliver or protect (literally or figuratively)
G4982.kjv: heal, preserve, save (self), do well, be (make) whole

G842.mounce: pr. sufficient or adequate in one’s self; contented with one’s lot, Phil. 4:11*
G842.abbottSmith: as in cl., in philosophical sense, self-sufficient , independent ; subjectively, contented : Phl 4:11 (in non-lit. π. , the word means simply enough , sufficient ; MM, s.v.).†
G842.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and G714 (ἀρκέω); self-complacent, i.e. contented

G85.thayer: I feel fear, lack courage, am distressed, troubled.
G85.mounce: to be depressed or dejected, full of anguish or sorrow, Mt. 26:37; Mk. 14:33; Phil. 2:26*
G85.abbottSmith: to be troubled, distressed (MM, l.c.): Mt 26:37 , Mk 14:33 , Phl 2:26 .†
G85.strongs: from a derivative of (to be sated to loathing); to be in distress (of mind)
G85.kjv: be full of heaviness, be very heavy

G4561.thayer: flesh, body, human nature, materiality; kindred.
G4561.mounce: flesh, Lk. 24:39; Jn. 3:6; the human body, 2 Cor. 7:5; flesh, human nature, human frame, Jn. 1:13, 14; 1 Pet. 4:1; 1 Jn. 4:2; kindred, Rom. 11:14; lineage, Rom. 1:3; 9:3; flesh, humanity, human beings, Mt. 24:22; Lk. 3:6; Jn. 17:2; the circumstances of the body, material condition, 1 Cor. 5:5; 7:28; Phlm. 16; flesh, mere humanity, human fashion, 1 Cor. 1:26; 2 Cor. 1:17; flesh as the seat of passion and frailty, Rom. 8:1, 3, 5; carnality, Gal. 5:24; materiality, material circumstance, as opposed to the spiritual, Phil. 3:3, 4; Col. 2:18; a material system or mode, Gal. 3:3; Heb. 9:10
G4561.abbottSmith: flesh ; as in cl. generally, prop., of the soft substance of the animal body: I Co 15:39 , II Co 12:7 , Ga 6:13 , al.; σ. καὶ αἷμα , I Co 5:50 ; σ. καὶ ὀστέα , Lk 24:39 ; pl., of the flesh of many or parts of the flesh of one (cl.), Re 17:16 19:18 ; φαγεῖν , Re, ll. c. (cf. κατεσθίειν , IV Ki 9:36 , al., and βιβρώσκειν , freq. in l.); metaph., Ja 5:3 ; mystically, φ. (τρώγειν) τὴν σ. τοῦ υἱοῦ τ. ἀ
G4561.strongs: probably from the base of G4563 (σαρόω); flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passions), or (specially), a human being (as such)

G5423.abbottSmith: a deceiver (B1., deceiver of his own mind , § 28, 5 2 , where v. ref. to π. ): Tit 1:10 (eccl.).†
G5423.strongs: from G5424 (φρήν) and G539 (ἀπάτη); a mind-misleader, i.e. seducer

G3698.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G4218 (ποτέ); what(-ever) then, i.e. (of time) as soon as

G2582.thayer: Candace, a proper name; the Candace, a dynastic name for queens of the Ethiopians in Abyssinia.
G2582.strongs: of foreign origin; Candacè, an Egyptian queen

G1187.thayer: one posted on the right hand, a spear-man.
G1187.mounce: one posted on the right hand; a flank guard; a light armed spearman, Acts 23:23*
G1187.abbottSmith: one who throws with the right hand : L for δεξιολάβος , Ac 23:23 .†
G1187.strongs: from G1188 (δεξιός) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); a guardsman (as if taking the right) or light-armed soldier

G1359.thayer: the Dioscuri, Castor and Pollux, sons of Zeus and Leda, and patrons of sailors.
G1359.mounce: the Dioscuri, Castor and Pollux, sons of Jupiter by Leda, and patrons of sailors, Acts 28:11*
G1359.abbottSmith: the Dioscuri (Castor and Pollux), twin sons of Zeus and Leda (RV, The Twin Brothers ): Ac 28:11 .†
G1359.strongs: from the alternate of G2203 (Ζεύς) and a form of the base of G2877 (κοράσιον); sons of Jupiter, i.e. the twins Dioscuri

G100.abbottSmith: thickness , vigour . abundance , bounty : II Co 8:20 .†

G1863.thayer: I bring upon; met: I cause to be imputed to.
G1863.mounce: to bring upon, cause to come upon, 2 Pet. 2:1, 5; met. to cause to be imputed or attributed to, to bring guilt upon, Acts 5:28*
G1863.abbottSmith: to bring upon : c. dat. et acc, II Pe 2:5 ; ἑαυτοῖς ἐ. (for cl. mid., v. Mayor, in l), II Pe 2:1 ; ἐ. τὸ αἷμα (cf. Ge 20:9 ), Ac 5:28 .†
G1863.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G71 (ἄγω); to superinduce, i.e. inflict (an evil), charge (a crime)

G317.thayer: by way of compulsion, unwillingly, by force, necessarily.
G317.mounce: by constraint or compulsion, unwillingly, opposite to ἑκουσίως, 1 Pet. 5:2*
G317.abbottSmith: necessarily or by constraint : opp. to ἑκουσίως , I Pe 5:2 (rare).†
G317.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G315 (ἀναγκάζω); compulsorily

G2234.mounce: with pleasure, gladly, willingly, Mk. 6:20; 12:37; 2 Cor. 11:19
G2234.abbottSmith: gladly , with pleasure : Mk 6:20 12:37 , II Co 11:19 ; superlat., ἥδιστα , very gladly (Bl., § 11, 3), II Co 12:9 , 15 .†
G2234.strongs: adverb from a derivative of the base of G2237 (ἡδονή); sweetly, i.e. (figuratively) with pleasure

G1619.mounce: intensely, fervently, earnestly, Lk. 22:44; Acts 12:5; 1 Pet. 1:22*
G1619.abbottSmith: fervently, earnestly : Ac 12:5 , I Pe 1:22 ; compar., Lk 22:44 (WH br., R, mg., omits).†

G5567.thayer: I sing, sing psalms; earlier: I play on a stringed instrument.
G5567.mounce: to move by a touch, to twitch; to touch, strike the strings or chords of an instrument; absol. to play on a stringed instrument; to sing to music; in NT to sing praises, Rom. 15:9; 1 Cor. 14:15; Eph. 5:19; Jas. 5:13*
G5567.abbottSmith: to pull, twitch, twang (as a bowstring, etc.; Æsch., Eur., al.), hence,
G5567.strongs: probably strengthened from (to rub or touch the surface; compare G5597 (ψώχω)); to twitch or twang, i.e. to play on a stringed instrument (celebrate the divine worship with music and accompanying odes)

G3632.strongs: from G3631 (οἶνος) and a form of the base of G5397 (φλύαρος); an overflow (or surplus) of wine, i.e. vinolency (drunkenness)

G5174.thayer: Troas, a harbor city of Mysia.
G5174.mounce: Troas, a city on the coast of Phrygia, near the site of ancient Troy, Acts 16:8, 11; 20:5f.; 2 Cor. 2:12; 2 Tim. 4:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G5174.abbottSmith: Troas , a city near the Hellespont: Ac 16:8 , 11 20:5 , 6 , II Co 2:12 , II Ti 4:13 .†
G5174.strongs: from (a Trojan); the Troad (or plain of Troy), i.e. Troas, a place in Asia Minor

G4365.mounce: to go or come to any one, Mk. 10:35*
G4365.abbottSmith: to come near , approach : c. dat. pers., Mk 10:35 .†
G4365.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to journey towards, i.e. approach (not the same as G4313 (προπορεύομαι))

G1492.abbottSmith: to have seen or perceived , hence, to know , have knowledge of : c. acc. rei, Mt 25:13 , Mk 10:19 , Jo 10:4 , Ro 7:7 , al.; c. acc. pers., Mt 26:72 , Jo 1:31 , Ac 3:16 , al.; τ. θεόν , I Th 4:5 , Tit 1:16 , al.; c. acc. et inf., Lk 4:41 , al.; seq. ὅτι , Mt 9:6 , Lk 20:21 , Jo 3:2 , Ro 2:2 11:2 , al.; seq. quaest. indir., Mt 26:70 , Jo 9:21 , Eph 1:18 , al.; c. inf., to know how (cl.), Mt 7:11 , L
G1492.strongs: a primary verb; used only in certain past tenses, the others being borrowed from the equivalent G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) and G3708 (ὁράω); properly, to see (literally or figuratively); by implication, (in the perfect tense only) to know
G1492.kjv: be aware, behold, X can (+ not tell), consider, (have) know(-ledge), look (on), perceive, see, be sure, tell, understand, wish, wot

G4973.thayer: a seal, signet ring, the impression of a seal, that which the seal attests, the proof.
G4973.strongs: probably strengthened from G5420 (φράσσω); a signet (as fencing in or protecting from misappropriation); by implication, the stamp impressed (as a mark of privacy, or genuineness), literally or figuratively

G2299.strongs: feminine of G2316 (θεός); a female deity

G3689.mounce: really, in truth, truly, Mk. 11:32; Lk. 23:47.
G3689.abbottSmith: really , actually , truly : Mk 11:32 , Lk 23:47 24:34 , Jo 8:36 , I Co 14:25 , Ga 3:21 ; ἡ ὄ. ζωή , I Ti 6:19 ; ἡ ὄ. χήρα , I Ti 5:3 , 5 , 16 .†
G3689.strongs: adverb of the oblique cases of G5607 (ὤν); really
G3689.kjv: certainly, clean, indeed, of a truth, verily

G571.thayer: unbelieving, incredulous, unchristian; sometimes subst: unbeliever.
G571.mounce: unbelieving, without confidence in any one, Mt. 17:17; violating one’s faith, unfaithful, false, treacherous, Lk. 12:46; an unbeliever, infidel, pagan, 1 Cor. 6:6; pass. incredible, Acts 26:8
G571.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4103 (πιστός); (actively) disbelieving, i.e. without Christian faith (specially, a heathen); (passively) untrustworthy (person), or incredible (thing)
G571.kjv: that believeth not, faithless, incredible thing, infidel, unbeliever(-ing)

G2497.strongs: perhaps for G2496 (Ἰωράμ); Jorim, an Israelite

G99.thayer: the Hadria, a name given by sailors not merely to the Adriatic Sea, to which it properly belonged, but also to the open Mediterranean to the south-east of Italy, to the sea that lay between Malta, Italy, Greece, and Crete.
G99.abbottSmith: the Adriatic sea, in later Greek usage extended to take in all the waters between Greece and Italy: Ac 27:27 .†
G99.strongs: from (a place near its shore); the Adriatic sea (including the Ionian)

G1946.thayer: an Epicurean, one who holds the tenets of Epicurus.
G1946.mounce: an Epicurean, follower of the philosophy of Epicurus, Acts 17:18*
G1946.abbottSmith: an Epicurean , a follower of Epicurus: Ac 17:18 .†
G1946.strongs: from (compare G1947 (ἐπικουρία)) (a noted philosopher); an Epicurean or follower of Epicurus

G3783.mounce: a debt; a due, Rom. 4:4, in NT a delinquency, offence, fault, sin, Mt. 6:12, cf. v. 14*
G3783.abbottSmith: that which is owed , a debt : Ro 4:4 ; metaph. (as Aram. חוֺבָא , חוֺב ), of sin as a debt, Mt 6:12 .†
G3783.strongs: from (the alternate of) G3784 (ὀφείλω); something owed, i.e. (figuratively) a due; morally, a fault

G4297.mounce: advance upon a way; met. progress, advancement, furtherance, Phil. 1:12, 25; 1 Tim. 4:15*
G4297.abbottSmith: progress (prop., on a journey, then generally): Phl 1:12 , 25 , I Ti 4:15 (condemned by Atticists, v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 158).†
G4297.strongs: from G4298 (προκόπτω); progress, i.e. advancement (subjectively or objectively)

G4990.mounce: a savior, preserver, deliverer, Lk. 1:47; 2:11; Acts 5:31
G4990.abbottSmith: saviour, deliverer, preserver , a freq. epithet of kings in the Ptolemaic and Rom. periods (Deiss., BS , 83; LAE , 368 f.; MM, xxiv), in NT,
G4990.strongs: from G4982 (σώζω); a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ

G1008.mounce: herb, herbage, produce of the earth, Heb. 6:7*
G1008.strongs: from G1006 (βόσκω); herbage (as if for grazing)

G387.thayer: (perhaps a political metaphor), I turn upside down, upset, unsettle.
G387.mounce: to lay waste, destroy; to disturb, throw into commotion, Acts 17:6; to excite to sedition and tumult, Acts 21:38; to disturb the mind of any one by doubts, etc.; to subvert, unsettle, Gal. 5:12*
G387.abbottSmith: to stir up , excite , unsettle : c. acc.; to tumult and sedition: Ac 17:6 21:38 ; by false teaching: Ga 5:12 (v. Milligan, NTD , 73 f.).†
G387.strongs: from a derivative of G450 (ἀνίστημι) (in the sense of removal); properly, to drive out of home, i.e. (by implication) to disturb (literally or figuratively)
G387.kjv: trouble, turn upside down, make an uproar

G147.thayer: greedily, in a spirit of eagerness for base gain.
G147.mounce: for the sake of base gain, greedily, 1 Pet. 5:2*
G147.abbottSmith: from eagerness for base gain : I Pe 5:2 (here only).†

G2704.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5351 (φθείρω); to spoil entirely, i.e. (literally) to destroy; or (figuratively) to deprave;

G457.mounce: an opening, act of opening, Eph. 6:19*
G457.abbottSmith: an opening (in MGr., springtime ): ἐν ἀ. , as often as I open , Eph 6:19 .†

G1847.thayer: I set at naught, ignore, despise.
G1847.mounce: also spelled ἐξουδενόω, to treat with contempt, Mk. 9:12*
G1847.abbottSmith: to despise, set at nought : Mk 9:12 (cf. ἐξουθενέω ).†
G1847.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of the neuter of G3762 (οὐδείς); to make utterly nothing of, i.e. despise

G2361.mounce: a lump; esp. a clot of blood, drop, Lk. 22:44*
G2361.abbottSmith: a lump , a clot of blood ( DCG , ii, 685 b ): αἵματος , Lk 22:44 (WH, R, mg. omit the passage, v. WH, App. , 64 ff.).†
G2361.strongs: perhaps from G5142 (τρέφω) (in the sense of thickening); a clot

G3362.strongs: i.e. G1437 (ἐάν) and G3361 (μή); if not, i.e. unless
G3362.kjv: X before, but, except, if, no, (if, + whosoever) not

G4859.mounce: agreeing in sound; met. harmonious, agreeing, accord, agreement, 1 Cor. 7:5*
G4859.abbottSmith: agreeing in sound . Metaph., harmonious, agreeing : ἐκ συμφώνου (for exx. from π. , v. Deiss., BS , 255), by agreement , I Co 7:5 .†
G4859.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5456 (φωνή); sounding together (alike), i.e. (figuratively) accordant (neuter as noun, agreement)

G2404.thayer: Hierapolis, a city of the Lycus valley in Phrygia, near Laodicea and Colossae.
G2404.mounce: Hierapolis, a city of Phrygia, Col. 4:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G2404.abbottSmith: Hierapolis : a city in the Lycus valley in the Province of Asia: Col 4:13 .†
G2404.strongs: from G2413 (ἱερός) and G4172 (πόλις); holy city; Hierapolis, a place in Asia Minor

G5611.mounce: timely, seasonable; in prime, blooming; in NT beautiful, Mt. 23:27; Acts 3:2, 10; Rom. 10:15*
G5611.abbottSmith: seasonable, timely , esp. of ripe fruits; hence, blooming, beautiful (both of things and persons): Mt 23:27 , Ac 3:2 , 10 , Ro 10:15 (LXX, ὥρα ).†
G5611.strongs: from G5610 (ὥρα); belonging to the right hour or season (timely), i.e. (by implication) flourishing (beauteous (figuratively))

G1581.thayer: I cut out (off, away), remove, prevent.
G1581.mounce: to cut out; to cut off, Mt. 3:10; 5:30; met. to cut off an occasion, remove, prevent, 2 Cor. 11:12; to render ineffectual, Mt. 7:19; 18:8; Lk. 3:9; 12:7, 9; Rom. 11:22, 24; 1 Pet. 3:7*
G1581.abbottSmith: in T for ἐνκοπή (q.v.): I Co 9:12 .†
G1581.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2875 (κόπτω); to exscind; figuratively, to frustrate
G1581.kjv: cut down (off, out), hew down, hinder

G3004.thayer: (denoting speech in progress), (a) I say, speak; I mean, mention, tell, (b) I call, name, especially in the pass., (c) I tell, command.
G3004.mounce: to lay, to arrange, to gather; to say, Mt. 1:20; to speak, make an address or speech, Acts 26:1; to say mentally, in thought, Mt. 3:9; Lk. 3:8; to say in written language, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 1:63; Jn. 19:37; to say, as distinguished from acting, Mt. 23:3; to mention, speak of, Mk. 14:71; Lk. 9:31; Jn. 8:27; to tell, declare, narrate, Mt. 21:27; Mk. 10:32; to express, Heb. 5:11; to put forth, propound, Lk. 5:36; 13:6; Jn. 16:29; to mean, to intend to signify, 1 Cor. 1:12; 10:29; to say, declare, affirm, maintain, Mt. 3:9; 5:18; Mk. 12:18; Acts 17:7; 26:22; 1 Cor. 1:10; to enjoin, Acts 15:24; 21:21; Rom. 2:22; to term, designate, cull, Mt. 19:17; Mk. 12:37; Lk. 20:37; 23:2; 1 Cor. 8:5; to call by a name, Mt. 2:23; pass. to be further named, to be surnamed, Mt. 1:16; to be explained, interpreted, Jn. 4:25; 20:16, 24; in NT σὺ λέγεις, you say, a form of affirmative answer to a question Mt. 27:11; Mk. 15:2; Jn. 18:37
G3004.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to "lay" forth, i.e. (figuratively) relate (in words (usually of systematic or set discourse; whereas G2036 (ἔπω) and G5346 (φημί) generally refer to an individual expression or speech respectively; while G4483 (ῥέω) is properly to break silence merely, and G2980 (λαλέω) means an extended or random harangue)); by implication, to mean
G3004.kjv: ask, bid, boast, call, describe, give out, name, put forth, say(-ing, on), shew, speak, tell, utter

G5071.strongs: plural from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); four hundred

G2956.mounce: a Cyrenian, an inhabitant of Cyrene, Mt. 27:32; Mk. 15:21; Lk. 23:26; Acts 6:9; 11:20; 13:1*
G2956.abbottSmith: of Cyrene , a Cyrenæan : Mt 27:32 , Mk 15:21 , Lk 23:26 , Ac 6:9 11:20 13:1 .†
G2956.strongs: from G2957 (Κυρήνη); i.e. Cyrenæan, i.e. inhabitant of Cyrene

G1720.mounce: to blow or breathe into, inflate; in NT to breathe upon, Jn. 20:22*
G1720.abbottSmith: to breathe into (cf. Ge 2:7 , Wi 15:11 , a1.), breathe upon : Jo 20:22 .†
G1720.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (to puff) (compare G5453 (φύω)); to blow at or on

G1480.mounce: to accustom; pass. to be customary, Lk. 2:27*
G1480.abbottSmith: to accustom : pass. pf. ptcp., τὸ εἰθισμένον , the established custom , Lk 2:27 .†
G1480.strongs: from G1485 (ἔθος); to accustom, i.e. (neuter passive participle) customary

G754.thayer: head of a custom-house, chief tax-gatherer or publican.
G754.mounce: a chief publican, chief collector of the customs or taxes, Lk. 19:2*
G754.abbottSmith: a chief tax-collector , chief publican : Lk 19:2 .†
G754.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G5057 (τελώνης); a principle tax-gatherer

G2489.thayer: (Hebrew), Joanna, Johanna, wife of Chuza, Herod's steward.
G2489.mounce: also spelled Ἰωάνα, Joanna, pr. name, Lk. 8:3; 24:10*
G2489.strongs: feminine of the same as G2491 (Ἰωάννης); Joanna, a Christian

G3046.thayer: a Roman pound, of about twelve ounces.
G3046.mounce: a pound, libra, equivalent to about twelve ounces (American), Jn. 12:3; 19:39*
G3046.abbottSmith: a Sicilian coin = Rom. libra or as .
G3046.strongs: of Latin origin (libra); a pound in weight

G4319.thayer: I beg, ask earnestly, ask for in addition.
G4319.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G154 (αἰτέω); to ask repeatedly (importune), i.e. solicit

G3516.thayer: unlearned, unenlightened; noun: an infant, child.
G3516.mounce: pr. not speaking, Latin infans; an infant, babe, child, Mt. 21:16; 1 Cor. 13:11; one below the age of manhood, a minor, Gal. 4:1; met. a babe in knowledge, unlearned, simple, Mt. 11:25; Rom. 2:20
G3516.abbottSmith: infant ; of children and minors: Mt 21:16 (LXX) , I Co 13:11 , Ga 4:1 (v. Lft., in l.). Metaph., childish , unskilled , simple ( Ps 18 (19):8 , Pr 1:32 , al.): Mt 11:25 , Lk 10:21 , Ro 2:20 , Ga 4:3 , Eph 4:14 , I Th 2:7 (WH, fox ἤπιοι ); opp. to τέλειος , He 5:13 ; ν. ἐν Χριστῷ , I Co 3:1 .†
G3516.strongs: from an obsolete particle (implying negation) and G2031 (ἔπος); not speaking, i.e. an infant (minor); figuratively, a simple-minded person, an immature Christian

G3773.thayer: Urbanus, a Christian in Rome, fellow-worker of Paul.
G3773.strongs: of Latin origin; Urbanus (of the city, "urbane"), a Christian

G23.mounce: to be pained; to be angry, vexed, indignant; to manifest indignation, Mk. 14:4; Lk. 13:14
G23.abbottSmith: to be indignant : Mt 21:15 , 26:8 , Mk 10:14 , 14:4 ; seq. περί , Mt 20:24 , Mk 10:41 ; seq. ὅτι , Lk 13:14 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G23.strongs: from (much) and (grief; akin to the base of G43 (ἀγκάλη)); to be greatly afflicted, i.e. (figuratively) indignant
G23.kjv: be much (sore) displeased, have (be moved with, with) indignation

G2184.thayer: for the day, daily, necessary for every day.
G2184.mounce: lasting for a day; daily sufficient for a day, necessary for every day, Jas. 2:15*
G2184.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); for a day ("ephemeral"), i.e. diurnal

G4013.thayer: I lead or carry about (or around), go about, traverse.
G4013.mounce: to lead around, carry about Acts 13:11 in one’s company, 1 Cor. 9:5; to traverse, Mt. 4:23; 9:35; 23:15; Mk. 6:6
G4013.abbottSmith: to lead about or around : c. acc. pers., I Co 9:5 .
G4013.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G71 (ἄγω); to take around (as a companion); reflexively, to walk around

G850.thayer: (poetical, lit: dry and parched; then: squalid and rough), dingy, murky, obscure, dark, funereal.
G850.mounce: squalid, filthy; by impl. dark, obscure, murky, 2 Pet. 1:19*
G850.strongs: from (probably from a base akin to that of G109 (ἀήρ)) (dust, as dried by wind); properly, dirty, i.e. (by implication) obscure

G1289.mounce: to scatter abroad or in every direction, as seen; to disperse, Acts 8:1, 4; 11:19*
G1289.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4687 (σπείρω); to sow throughout, i.e. (figuratively) distribute in foreign lands

G190.mounce: to follow; follow as a disciple; imitate
G190.abbottSmith: to accompany , follow : Mt 4:25 , al. Metaph., of discipleship: Mt 9:9 , Mk 9:38 , Jo 12:26 , al. Absol.: Mt 18:10 ; more freq., c. dat. (cl.), Mt 8:1 , al.; seq. μετά , c. gen, (cl.; Eutherford, N.Phr. , 458 f.), Lk 9:49 ; ὀπίσω , c. gen. (Heb. הָלַךְ אחֲֲרֵי ), Mt 10:38 (cf. ἐξ- , ἐπ- , κατ- , παρ- , συν-ἀκολουθέω ).
G190.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and (a road); properly, to be in the same way with, i.e. to accompany (specially, as a disciple)

G5428.thayer: understanding (which leads to right action), practical wisdom, prudence.
G5428.mounce: a thoughtful frame, sense, rightmindedness, Lk. 1:17; intelligence, Eph. 1:8*
G5428.abbottSmith: understanding, practical wisdom, prudence : Lk 1:17 , Eph 1:8 .†
G5428.strongs: from G5426 (φρονέω); mental action or activity, i.e. intellectual or moral insight

G2672.mounce: to curse, to wish evil to, imprecate evil upon, Mk. 11:21; Lk. 6:28; Rom. 12:14; Jas. 3:9; in NT pass. to be cursed, Mt. 25:41*
G2672.abbottSmith: to curse : c. acc., Mk 11:21 ; opp. to εὐλογέω , Lk 6:28 , Ro 12:14 , Ja 3:9 ; pass. pf. ptcp., accursed , under a curse (v. M, Pr. , 221): Mt 25:41 .†
G2672.strongs: middle voice from G2671 (κατάρα); to execrate; by analogy, to doom

G4515.mounce: in the Roman language, in Latin, Jn. 19:20*
G4515.strongs: adverb from a presumed derivative of G4516 (Ῥώμη); Romaistically, i.e. in the Latin language

G1007.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01160); Bosor (i.e. Beor), a Moabite

G3152.thayer: vain, unreal, ineffectual, unproductive; practically: godless.
G3152.mounce: idle, ineffective, worthless, 1 Cor. 3:20; groundless, deceptive, fallacious, 1 Cor. 15:17; useless, fruitless, unprofitable, Tit. 3:9; Jas. 1:26; from the Hebrew, erroneous in principle, corrupt, perverted, 1 Pet. 1:18; τὰ μάταια, superstition, idolatry, Acts 14:15*
G3152.abbottSmith: vain , useless : ἀνωφελὴς κ. μ. , Tit 3:9 ; πίστις , I Co 15:17 ; θρησκεία , Ja 1:26 ; διαλογισμοί , I Co 3:20 (LXX) ; ἀναστροφή , I Pe 1:18 ; of idols and heathen gods, τὰ μ. ( Je 2:5 , IV Ki 17:15 , al.): Ac 14:15 .
G3152.strongs: from the base of G3155 (μάτην); empty, i.e. (literally) profitless, or (specially), an idol

G648.thayer: I unroof, take the roof off.
G648.mounce: to remove or break through a covering or roof of a place, Mk. 2:4*
G648.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G4721 (στέγη); to unroof

G4941.thayer: Syntyche, a woman member of the church at Philippi.
G4941.strongs: from G4940 (συντυγχάνω); an accident; Syntyche, a Christian female

G2731.strongs: from G2730 (κατοικέω); residence (properly, the act; but by implication, concretely, the mansion)

G94.mounce: unjust, unrighteous, iniquitous, vicious; deceitful, fallacious
G94.abbottSmith: unjust : Ro 3:5 , He 6:10 . unrighteous, wicked : Lk 16:11 18:11 , Ac 24:15 , I Co 6:1 , 9 I Pe 3:18 ; opp. to δίκαιος , Mt 5:45 ; to εὐσεβής , II Pe 2:9 ; to πιστός , Lk 16:10 (Cremer, 200).†
G94.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1349 (δίκη); unjust; by extension wicked; by implication, treacherous; specially, heathen

G2471.mounce: equality, equal proportion, 2 Cor. 8:13, 14; fairness, equity, what is equitable, Col. 4:1*
G2471.strongs: likeness (in condition or proportion); by implication, equity

G4193.mounce: belonging to or an inhabitant of Pontus, Acts 18:2*
G4193.strongs: from G4195 (Πόντος); a Pontican, i.e. native of Pontus

G3722.mounce: the dawn; the morning, Jn. 8:2; Acts 5:21; ὄρθος βαθύς, the first streak of dawn, the early dawn, Lk. 24:1*
G3722.abbottSmith: daybreak , dawn : ὄρθρου βαθέως (M, Pr. , 73), at early dawn , Lk 24:1 ; ὄρθρου , Jo 8:[2] ; ὑπὸ τὸν ὄ. , Ac 5:21 (cf. MM, xviii).†
G3722.strongs: from the same as G3735 (ὄρος); dawn (as sun-rise, rising of light); by extension, morn

G360.thayer: I unloose, unloose for departure, depart, return.
G360.mounce: pr. to loose, dissolve; intrans. to loose in order to departure; to depart, Lk. 12:36; to depart from life, Phil. 1:23*
G360.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3089 (λύω); to break up, i.e. depart (literally or figuratively)

G4813.abbottSmith: c. acc. pers., to strip, plunder, spoil : II Co 11:8 .†
G4813.strongs: from a derivative of (to strip; probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); compare G4661 (σκῦλον)); to despoil

G2919.thayer: (a) I judge, whether in a law-court or privately: sometimes with cognate nouns emphasizing the notion of the verb, (b) I decide, I think (it) good.
G2919.mounce: pluperfect, κεκρίκει (3 sg), pr. to separate; to make a distinction between; to exercise judgment upon; to estimate, Rom. 14:5; to judge, to assume censorial power over, to call to account, Mt. 7:1; Lk. 6:37; Rom. 2:1, 3; 14:3, 4, 10, 13; Col. 2:16; Jas. 4:11, 12; to bring under question, Rom. 14:22; to judge judicially, to try as a judge, Jn. 18:31; to bring to trial, Acts 13:27; to sentence, Lk. 19:22; Jn. 7:51; to resolve on, decree, Acts 16:4; Rev. 16:5; absol. to decide, determine, resolve, Acts 3:13; 15:19; 27:1; to deem, Acts 13:46; to form a judgment, pass judgment, Jn. 8:15; pass. to be brought to trial, Acts 25:10, 20; Rom. 3:4; to be brought to account, to incur arraignment, be arraigned, 1 Cor. 10:29; mid. to go to law, litigate, Mt. 5:40; in NT to judge, to visit judicially, Acts 7:7; 1 Cor. 11:31, 32; 1 Pet. 4:6; to judge, to right, to vindicate, Heb. 10:30; to administer government over, to govern, Mt. 19:28; Lk. 22:30
G2919.abbottSmith: to separate , select , choose (cl.; in LXX: II Mac 13:15 ). to approve , esteem : Ro 14:5 . to be of opinion , judge , think : Lk 7:43 , I Co 11:13 ; seq. τοῦτο ὅτι , II Co 5:14 ; c. acc. et inf., Ac 16:15 ; c. acc. et pred., Ac 13:46 26:8 . to decide , determine , decree : c. acc., Ac 16:4 , Ro 14:13 , I Co 7:37 , II Co 2:1 ; c. inf. (Field, Notes , 167), Ac 20:16 25:25 , I Co 2:2 5:3 , Tit 3:12
G2919.strongs: properly, to distinguish, i.e. decide (mentally or judicially); by implication, to try, condemn, punish
G2919.kjv: avenge, conclude, condemn, damn, decree, determine, esteem, judge, go to (sue at the) law, ordain, call in question, sentence to, think

G1449.mounce: to engrave, inscribe, Lk. 10:20; met. ἐγγεγραμμένος, imprinted, 2 Cor. 3:2, 3*
G1449.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1125 (γράφω); to "engrave", i.e. inscribe

G1349.thayer: (a) (originally: custom, usage) right, justice, (b) process of law, judicial hearing, (c) execution of sentence, punishment, penalty, (d) justice, vengeance.
G1349.mounce: right, justice; in NT judicial punishment, vengeance, 2 Thess. 1:9; Jude 7; sentence of punishment, judgment, Acts 25:15; personified, the goddess of justice or vengeance, Nemesis, Paena, Acts 28:4
G1349.strongs: probably from G1166 (δεικνύω); right (as self-evident), i.e. justice (the principle, a decision, or its execution)

G86.mounce: the invisible abode or mansion of the dead; the place of punishment, hell; the lowest place or condition, Mt. 11:23; Lk. 10:15
G86.abbottSmith: in Hom., Hades (Pluto), the god of the underworld. the abode of Hades, the underivorld ; in NT, the abode of departed spirits, Hades : ἐν τ. ᾅ. , Lk 16:23 ; εἰς ᾅ. , Ac 2:27 , 31 ; πύλαι ᾅδου , Mt 16:18 ; κλεῖς τοῦ ᾅ. , Re 1:18 ; metaph., ἕως ᾅ. , Mt 11:23 , Lk 10:15 ; personified, Re 6:8 20:13 , 14 (Cremer, 67, 610; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G86.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1492 (εἴδω); properly, unseen, i.e. "Hades" or the place (state) of departed souls

G2541.thayer: Caesar, a surname of the gens Iulia, which became practically synonymous with the Emperor for the time being; in the Gospels it always refers to Tiberias.
G2541.abbottSmith: Cæsar: Mt 22:17 , Lk 2:1 , Jo 19:12 , Ac 25:8 ff., al.
G2541.strongs: of Latin origin; Cæsar, a title of the Roman emperor

G2469.abbottSmith: Iscariot : surname of Judas, Mk 3:19 14:10 , Lk 6:16 supr., also Mt 10:4 26:14 , Mk 14:43 (WH, R, omit), Lk 22:3 , Jo 12:4 13:2 14:22 ; of his father Simon, Jo 6:71 13:26 .†
G2469.strongs: of Hebrew origin (probably H0377 and H07149); inhabitant of Kerioth; Iscariotes (i.e. Keriothite), an epithet of Judas the traitor

G3630.mounce: wine-drinking; in a bad sense, a wine-bibber, tippler, Mt. 11:19; Lk. 7:34*
G3630.strongs: from G3631 (οἶνος) and a derivative of the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); a tippler

G3635.mounce: to be slow; to delay, hesitate, Acts 9:38*
G3635.abbottSmith: to shrink from doing, hesitate to do (Hom., Thuc., al.); hence, to delay c. inf., Ac 9:38 (cf. Nu 22:16 , and v. MM, xviii).†

G2581.abbottSmith: a Cananæan or Zealot (cf. ζηλωτής ): Mt 10:4 , Mk 3:18 (Rec. -νίτης ).†
G2581.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H07067); zealous; Cananites, an epithet
G2581.kjv: Canaanite (by mistake for a derivative from G5477 (Χαναάν))

G3322.thayer: I am in the middle, am advanced midway.
G3322.mounce: to be in the middle or midst; to be advanced midway, Jn. 7:14*
G3322.abbottSmith: to be in the middle , esp. of time: τ. ἑορτῆς μεσούσης , in the middle of the feast , Jo 7:14 .†
G3322.strongs: from G3319 (μέσος); to form the middle, i.e. (in point of time), to be half-way over

G2605.thayer: I declare openly, proclaim, preach, laud, celebrate.
G2605.mounce: to announce, proclaim, Acts 13:38; in NT to laud, celebrate, Rom. 1:8
G2605.abbottSmith: to proclaim , declare : c. acc. rei, Ac 3:24 16:21 17:23 ; τ. ἀνάστασιν , Ac 4:2 ; τ. λόγον τοῦ Θ. , τ. Κ. , Ac 13:5 15:36 ; ὁδὸν σωτηρίας , Ac 16:17 ; φῶς , Ac 26:23 ; τ. μυστήριον τοῦ Θ. , I Co 2:1 ; τ. εὐαγγέλιον , 9:14 ; τ. θάνατον τοῦ Κ. , 11:26 ; pass., ἄφεσις , Ac 13:38 ; ὁ λόγος τοῦ Θ. , Ac 17:13 ; ἡ πίστις ὑμῶν , Ro 1:8 ; c. acc. pers., of Christ, ὅν , Ac 17:3 , Col 1:28 ; τ. Χριστόν , Ph
G2605.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to proclaim, promulgate
G2605.kjv: declare, preach, shew, speak of, teach

G2242.mounce: Heli, the father of Joseph, Lk. 3:23*
G2242.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05941); Heli (i.e. Eli), an Israelite

G2705.mounce: to kiss affectionately or with a semblance of affection, to kiss with earnest gesture, Mt. 26:49; Mk. 14:45; Lk. 7:38, 45; 15:20; Acts 20:37*
G2705.abbottSmith: to kiss fervently , kiss affectionately : Mt 26:49 , Mk 14:45 , Lk 7:38 , 45 15:20 , Ac 20:37 .†

G4073.thayer: a rock, ledge, cliff, cave, stony ground.
G4073.mounce: a rock, Mt. 7:24, 25; met. Rom. 9:33; 1 Pet. 2:8; crags, clefts, Rev. 6:15, 16; stony ground, Lk. 8:6, 13
G4073.abbottSmith: a rock , i.e. a mass of live rock as distinct from πέτρος , a detached stone or boulder: Mt 7:24 , 25 27:51 , 60 , Mk 15:46 , Lk 6:48 8:6 , 13 ; of a hollow rock, a cave , Re 6:15 , 16 (cf. Is 2:10 , al.). Metaph., Mt 16:18 (on the meaning, v. Hort, Eccl. , 16 ff., but cf. also ICC , in l.), I Co 10:4 ; = πέτρος , Ro 9:33 , I Pe 2:8 (LXX) .†
G4073.strongs: feminine of the same as G4074 (Πέτρος); a (mass of) rock (literally or figuratively)

G2790.thayer: a harpist, one who sings with harp as accompaniment.
G2790.mounce: one who plays on the lyre and accompanies it with his voice, Rev. 14:2; 18:22*
G2790.abbottSmith: one who plays and sings to the lyre , a harper : Re 14:2 18:22 .†
G2790.strongs: from G2788 (κιθάρα) and a derivative of the same as G5603 (ᾠδή); a lyre-singer(-player), i.e. harpist

G2561.mounce: ill treatment, affliction, oppression, misery, Acts 7:34*

G3622.thayer: management of household affairs, stewardship, administration.
G3622.mounce: pr. the management of a household; a stewardship, Lk. 16:2, 3, 4; in NT an apostolic stewardship, a ministerial commission in the publication and furtherance of the Gospel, 1 Cor. 9:17; 3:2; Col. 1:25; or, an arranged plan, a scheme, Eph. 1:10; a due discharge of a commission, 1 Tim. 1:4, Eph. 3:9*
G3622.abbottSmith: prop. (Plat., Arist.), the office of οἰκονόμος , stewardship : Lk 16:2-4 .
G3622.strongs: from G3623 (οἰκονόμος); administration (of a household or estate); specially, a (religious) "economy"

G1717.mounce: apparent, conspicuous, obvious to the sight, Acts 10:40; met. manifest, known, comprehended, Rom. 10:20*
G1717.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G5316 (φαίνω); apparent in self

G2832.thayer: Clopas, husband of one Mary, who stood by the cross.
G2832.strongs: of Chaldee origin (corresponding to H0256); Clopas, an Israelite

G3838.thayer: complete, entire, perfect, through all time.
G3838.mounce: perfect, complete; εἰς τὸ παντελές, adverbially, throughout, through all time, ever, Heb. 7:25; with a negative, at all, Lk. 13:11*
G3838.abbottSmith: all-complete , entire , perfect ; εἰς τ. παντελές , completely , utterly : Lk 13:11 , He 7:25 (where perhaps in temp. sense, finally ; v. MM, xviii).†
G3838.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and G5056 (τέλος); full-ended, i.e. entire (neuter as noun, completion)

G1196.strongs: from G1199 (δεσμόν); to tie, i.e. shackle

G1994.thayer: (a) trans: I turn (back) to (towards), (b) intrans: I turn (back) (to [towards]); I come to myself.
G1994.mounce: trans. to turn towards; to turn round; to bring back, convert, Lk. 1:16, 17; Jas. 5:19, 20; intrans. and mid. to turn one’s self upon or towards, Acts 9:40; Rev. 1:12; to turn about, Mt. 9:22; to turn back, return, Mt. 12:44; met. to be converted, Acts 28:27
G1994.abbottSmith: trans., to turn about, round or towards , hence metaph., to turn, cause to return (to God, virtue, etc.): Lk 1:16 , 17 (cf. Ma 3:24 ), Ja 5:19 , 20 .
G1994.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4762 (στρέφω); to revert (literally, figuratively or morally)

G848.mounce: pr. caught in the act of theft, Jn. 8:4*
G848.strongs: contracted for G1438 (ἑαυτοῦ); self (in some oblique case or reflexively, relation)
G848.kjv: her (own), (of) him(-self), his (own), of it, thee, their (own), them(-selves), they

G4075.mounce: like rock; stony, rocky, Mt. 13:5, 20; Mk. 4:5, 16*
G4075.abbottSmith: rock-like , rocky , stony : τὸ, τὰ π. , of shallow soil with underlying rock, Mt 13:5 , 20 , Mk 4:5 , 16 .†
G4075.strongs: from G4073 (πέτρα) and G1491 (εἶδος); rock-like, i.e. rocky

G2929.thayer: I am clear as crystal, brilliant like crystal.
G2929.mounce: to be clear, brilliant like crystal, Rev. 21:11*
G2929.abbottSmith: to shine like crystal , be crystal-clear : Re 21:11 ( ἅπ. λεγ. ).†
G2929.strongs: from G2930 (κρύσταλλος); to make (i.e. intransitively, resemble) ice ("crystallize")

G1560.abbottSmith: given up, delivered over : Ac 2:23 (for construction, v. Field, Notes , 111 f.).†
G1560.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G1325 (δίδωμι); given out or over, i.e. surrendered

G1199.thayer: a bond, chain, imprisonment; a string or ligament, an impediment, infirmity.
G1199.mounce: a bond, anything by which one is bound, a cord, chain, fetters, etc.; and by meton. imprisonment, Lk. 8:29; Acts 16:26; 20:23; a string or ligament, as of the tongue, Mk. 7:35; met. an impediment, infirmity, Lk. 13:16
G1199.abbottSmith: a band, bond : metaph., Lk 13:16 ; ὁ δεσμὸς τ. γλώσσης , Mk 7:35 (for this expression in π. , v. Deiss., LAE , 306 ff.; of actual bonds, v. Ruth., Gr. , 9). Pl., δεσμοί , Phl 1:13 ; δεσμά (as also in cl.), Lk 8:29 , Ac 16:26 20:23 ; gen., dat., Ac 23:29 26:29 , 31 , Phl 1:7 , 14 , 17 , Col 4:18 , II Ti 2:9 , Phm 10 , He 11:36 , Ju 6 ; ἐν τοῖς δ. τοῦ εὐαγγελίου Phm 13 .†
G1199.strongs: neuter and masculine respectively from G1210 (δέω); a band, i.e. ligament (of the body) or shackle (of a prisoner); figuratively, an impediment or disability

G2252.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2252.strongs: a prolonged form of G2258 (ἦν); I was

G2860.mounce: to dive; in NT to swim, Acts 27:43*
G2860.abbottSmith: to dive , plunge into the sea : Ac 27:43 (cf. ἐκ-κολνμβάω ).†
G2860.strongs: from (a diver); to plunge into water

G3736.abbottSmith: to dig : c. acc., ληνόν , Mt 21:33 ; ὑπολήνιον , Mk 12:1 ; γῆν , Mt 25:18 (cf. δι- , ἐξ-ορύσσω ).†
G3736.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to "burrow" in the ground, i.e. dig

G121.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and probably a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι) (meaning a penalty); not guilty

G4078.mounce: to fasten; to pitch a tent, Heb. 8:2*
G4078.abbottSmith: to make fast , to fix ; of tents, to pitch : He 8:2 (cf. προσ-πήγνυμι ).†
G4078.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb (which in its simpler form occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses); to fix ("peg"), i.e. (specially) to set up (a tent)

G5303.thayer: (a) of things or persons: that which is lacking, a defect or shortcoming, (b) want, poverty.
G5303.mounce: a shortcoming, defect; personal shortcoming, 1 Cor. 16:17; Phil. 2:30; Col. 1:24; 1 Thess. 3:10; want, need, poverty, penury, Lk. 21:4; 2 Cor. 8:14; 9:12; 11:9*
G5303.abbottSmith: that which is lacking, deficiency, shortcoming : c. gen. poss. (pron. poss.), I Co 16:17 , Phl 2:30 ; c. gen. rei, Col 1:24 , I Th 3:10 ;
G5303.strongs: from G5302 (ὑστερέω); a deficit; specially, poverty
G5303.kjv: that which is behind, (that which was) lack(-ing), penury, want

G4213.mounce: drinking; drink, beverage, Jn. 6:55; Rom. 14:17; Col. 2:16*
G4213.abbottSmith: prop., drinking : Ro 14:17 , Col 2:16 (v. Lft.; ICC , in l.).
G4213.strongs: from the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); a drinking (the act), i.e. (concretely) a draught

G593.mounce: to reject upon trial; to reject, Mt. 21:42; Mk. 12:10; Lk. 20:17; 1 Pet. 2:4, 7; pass., to be disallowed a claim, declared useless, Lk. 9:22; 17:25; Heb. 12:17
G593.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1381 (δοκιμάζω); to disapprove, i.e. (by implication) to repudiate

G4708.mounce: earnestly, eagerly, diligently, Lk. 7:4; 2 Tim. 1:17; Tit. 3:13; compar. σπουδαιοτέρως, more earnestly, with special urgency, Phil. 2:28
G4708.strongs: adverb from G4707 (σπουδαιότερος); more speedily, i.e. sooner than otherwise

G4093.mounce: a small tablet for writing, Lk. 1:63*
G4093.strongs: diminutive of G4094 (πίναξ); a tablet (for writing on)

G4395.thayer: I foretell, prophesy; I set forth matter of divine teaching by special faculty.
G4395.mounce: to exercise the function of a προφήτης; to prophesy, to foretell the future, Mt. 11:13; to divine, Mt. 26:68; Mk. 14:65; Lk. 22:64; to prophesy, to set forth matter of divine teaching by special faculty, 1 Cor. 13:9; 14:1
G4395.abbottSmith: to be a προφήτης (q.v.), to prophesy : in the primary sense of telling forth the Divine counsels, Mt 7:22 26:68 , Mk 14:65 , Lk 1:67 22:64 , Ac 19:6 , I Co 11:4 , 5 13:9 14:1 , 3-5 , 24 , 31 , 39 , Re 11:3 ; with the idea of foretelling future events (an idea merely incidental, not essential; v. Lft., Notes , 83 f.), Mt 11:13 , Ac 2:17 , 18 (LXX) 21:9 ; seq. περί , Mt 15:7 , Mk 7:6 , I Pe 1:10 ; ἐ
G4395.strongs: from G4396 (προφήτης); to foretell events, divine, speak under inspiration, exercise the prophetic office

G1259.thayer: I change, exchange; I reconcile, change enmity for friendship.
G1259.mounce: to be reconciled to another, Mt. 5:24*
G1259.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); to change thoroughly, i.e. (mentally) to conciliate

G1591.mounce: to wipe off; to wipe dry, Lk. 7:38, 44; Jn. 11:2; 12:3; 13:5*
G1591.abbottSmith: to wipe off : c. acc, Lk 7:38 , 44 , Jo 11:2 12:3 13:5 .†
G1591.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and the base of G3145 (μασσάομαι); to knead out, i.e. (by analogy) to wipe dry

G1712.thayer: a place of traffic, mart, market, market-house.
G1712.mounce: a mart, marketplace, emporium; met. traffic, Jn. 2:16*
G1712.abbottSmith: a trading place, exchange : οἶκος ἐμπορίου , Jo 2:16 .†

G5416.strongs: neuter of a derivative from the base of G5417 (φραγελλόω); a whip, i.e. Roman lash as a public punishment

G1109.mounce: one acquainted with a thing, knowing, skilful, Acts 26:3*
G1109.abbottSmith: one who knows, an expert : Ac 26:3 .†

G4448.thayer: pass: I burn, am set on fire, am inflamed; glow with heat, am purified by fire.
G4448.mounce: to set on fire, burn; pass. to be kindled, be on fire, burn, flame, Eph. 6:16; 2 Pet. 3:12; Rev. 1:15; met. to fire with distressful feelings, 2 Cor. 11:29; of lust, to be inflamed, burn, 1 Cor. 7:9; to be tried with fire, as metals, Rev. 3:18*
G4448.abbottSmith: to set on fire , burn up . In NT always pass., to be set on fire , to burn : Eph 6:16 , II Pe 3:12 ; ptcp., glowing , Re 1:15 . Metaph., of grief or indignation, II Co 11:29 ; of lust, I Co 7:9 . Of metals (cf. Jb 22:25 , Ps 11 (12):7 , Za 13:9 ), to be refined or purified by fire: Re 3:18 (and so in RV, Re 1:15 , but v. supr.).†
G4448.strongs: from G4442 (πῦρ); to kindle, i.e. (passively) to be ignited, glow (literally), be refined (by implication), or (figuratively) to be inflamed (with anger, grief, lust)
G4448.kjv: burn, fiery, be on fire, try

G1074.thayer: a generation; if repeated twice or with another time word, practically indicates infinity of time.
G1074.mounce: pr. birth; hence, progeny; a generation of mankind, Mt. 11:16; 23:36, et al.; a generation, a step in a genealogy, Mt. 1:17; a generation, an interval of time, an age; in NT course of life, in respect of its events, interests, or character, Lk. 16:8; Acts 13:36
G1074.abbottSmith: race, stock, family (in NT, γέννημα , q.v.). generation ; of the contemporary members of a family: pl., Mt 1:17 (cf. Ge 31:3 , מוֹלֶדֶת ); metaph., of those alike in character, in bad sense, Mt 17:17 , Mk 9:19 , Lk 9:41 16:8 , Ac 2:40 ; of all the people of a given period: Mt 24:34 , Mk 13:30 , Lk 21:32 , Phl 2:15 ; pl., Lk 1:48 ; esp. of the Jewish people, Mt 11:16 12:39 , 41 , 42 , 45 16:4 23:36
G1074.strongs: from (a presumed derivative of) G1085 (γένος); a generation; by implication, an age (the period or the persons)

G1306.thayer: I shine through, dawn (of the light coming through the shadows).
G1306.mounce: to shine through, shine out, dawn, 2 Pet. 1:19*
G1306.abbottSmith: to shine through, to dawn : II Pe 1:19 (cf. ἕως οὗ διαπνεύσῃ ἡ ἡμέρα , Ca 2:17 ).†
G1306.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G826 (αὐγάζω); to glimmer through, i.e. break (as day)

G4459.thayer: how, in what manner, by what means.
G4459.abbottSmith: prop., in direct questions, how? : c. indic., Mt 12:29 , Mk 3:23 , Lk 11:18 , Jo 3:4 , al.; καὶ π. , Mk 4:13 , Lk 20:44 ; π. οὖν , Mt 12:26 ; π. οὐ , Mt 16:11 , Lk 12:56 ; in deliberative questions (cf. Bl., § 64, 6), c. subjc., Mt 23:33 26:54 ; π. οὖν , Ro 10:14 ; π. δέ , Ro 10:14 , 15 ; seq. ἄν , c. optat., Ac 8:31 .
G4459.strongs: adverb from the base of G4226 (ποῦ); an interrogative particle of manner; in what way? (sometimes the question is indirect, how?); also as exclamation, how much!

G2848.strongs: apparently a primary word; a kernel of seed

G4609.thayer: Silas, a pet-form of the name "Silvanus"; a Roman citizen and a helper of Paul.
G4609.mounce: Silas, pr. name, in Luke, Acts 15:22; see Σιλουανός
G4609.strongs: contraction for G4610 (Σιλουανός); Silas, a Christian

G3184.thayer: I am intoxicated with wine, am drunk.
G3184.mounce: to be intoxicated, be drunk, Mt. 24:49; Acts 2:15; 1 Cor. 11:21; 1 Thess. 5:7; Rev. 17:6*
G3184.abbottSmith: to be drunken : Mt 24:49 , Jo 2:10 , Ac 2:15 , I Co 11:21 , I Th 5:7 ; metaph., Re 17:2 , 6 .†
G3184.strongs: from another form of G3178 (μέθη); to drink to intoxication, i.e. get drunk

G50.thayer: I do not know, am ignorant of (a person, thing, or fact), sometimes with the idea of willful ignorance.
G50.mounce: to be ignorant; not to understand; sin through ignorance
G50.abbottSmith: to be ignorant , not to know : absol., I Ti 1:13 , He 5:2 ; c. acc., Ac 13:27 17:23 , Ro 10:3 , II Co 2:11 ; ἐν οἷς , II Pe 2:12 ; seq. ὅτι , Ro 2:4 6:3 7:1 , I Co 14:38 ; οὐ θέλω ὑμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν , a Pauline phrase: c. acc., Ro 11:25 ; seq. ὑπέρ , II Co 1:8 ; περί , I Co 12:1 , I Th 4:13 . ὅτι , Ro 1:13 , I Co 10:1 (for similar usage in π. , V. MM, VGT , s.v.). Pass.: I Co 14:38 , II Co 6:9 , Ga 1:22
G50.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3539 (νοιέω); not to know (through lack of information or intelligence); by implication, to ignore (through disinclination)
G50.kjv: (be) ignorant(-ly), not know, not understand, unknown

G1534.thayer: then, thereafter, next (marking a fresh stage); therefore, then, furthermore.
G1534.mounce: then, afterwards, Mk. 4:17, 28; Lk. 8:12; in the next place, 1 Cor. 12:28; besides, furthermore, Heb. 12:9
G1534.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a particle of succession (in time or logical enumeration), then, moreover

G1327.thayer: a meeting-place of roads, a public spot in a city.
G1327.mounce: a passage throughout; a line of road, a thoroughfare, Mt. 22:9*
G1327.abbottSmith: in π. of the conclusion of a trial (MM, Exp. , xi); a way out through, an outlet ; pl., δ. τῶν ὁδῶν , RV, the partings of the highways : Mt 22:9 .†
G1327.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1841 (ἔξοδος); an outlet through, i.e. probably an open square (from which roads diverge)

G769.thayer: want of strength, weakness, illness, suffering, calamity, frailty.
G769.mounce: want of strength, weakness, feebleness, 1 Cor. 15:43; bodily infirmity, state of ill health, sickness, Mt. 8:17; Lk. 5:15; met. infirmity, frailty, imperfection, intellectual and moral, Rom. 6:19; 1 Cor. 2:3; Heb. 5:2; 7:28; suffering, affliction, distress, calamity, Rom. 8:26
G769.strongs: from G772 (ἀσθενής); feebleness (of mind or body); by implication, malady; morally, frailty

G179.thayer: indissoluble, that cannot be broken up.
G179.mounce: incapable of dissolution, indissoluble; hence, enduring, everlasting, Heb. 7:16*
G179.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2647 (καταλύω); indissoluble, i.e. (figuratively) permanent

G5157.mounce: a turning round; a turning back, change, mutation, Jas. 1:17*
G5157.abbottSmith: a turning : esp. of the revolution of heavenly bodies, fig., Ja 1:17 (v.s. ἀποσκίασμα ).†
G5157.strongs: from an apparently primary to turn; a turn ("trope"), i.e. revolution (figuratively, variation)

G3967.thayer: ancestral, paternal, belonging to the fathers (ancestors).
G3967.mounce: from fathers or ancestors, ancestral, paternal, Gal. 1:14*

G4067.mounce: filth which is wiped off; met. 1 Cor. 4:13*
G4067.abbottSmith: that which is wiped off , offscouring : metaph. (assoc. with περικάθαρμα , q.v.), I Co 4:13 (and so prob., To 5:18 , as EV; but v. Thayer, s.v., for the meaning expiation , ransom , in To; and cf. LS, s.v. κάθαρμα ; Lft., Notes , 200 f., and on Ign., Eph. , 8).†
G4067.strongs: from a comparative of G4012 (περί) and (to rub); something brushed all around, i.e. off-scrapings (figuratively, scum)

G4656.mounce: to darken, shroud in darkness, Eph. 4:18; Rev. 9:2; 16:10
G4656.abbottSmith: to darken : Re 9:2 16:10 . Metaph., of the mind : pass., Eph 4:18 (cf. σκοτίζω ).†
G4656.strongs: from G4655 (σκότος); to obscure or blind (literally or figuratively)

G4512.thayer: a wrinkle; fig: a spiritual defect, flaw.
G4512.mounce: a wrinkle; met. a disfiguring wrinkle, flaw, blemish, Eph. 5:27*
G4512.strongs: from G4506 (ῥύομαι); a fold (as drawing together), i.e. a wrinkle (especially on the face)

G338.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G159 (αἴτιος) (in the sense of G156 (αἰτία)); innocent

G706.abbottSmith: number , a number : Lk 22:3 , Jo 6:10 , Ac 4:4 5:36 6:7 11:21 16:5 , Ro 9:27 , Re 5:11 7:4 9:16 13:17 , 18 15:2 20:8 (for exx. of mystical use in π. , v. MM, s.v.).†
G706.strongs: from G142 (αἴρω); a number (as reckoned up)

G2842.thayer: (lit: partnership) (a) contributory help, participation, (b) sharing in, communion, (c) spiritual fellowship, a fellowship in the spirit.
G2842.mounce: fellowship, partnership, Acts 2:42; 2 Cor. 6:14; 13:13; Gal. 2:9; Phil. 3:10; 1 Jn. 1:3; participation, communion, 1 Cor. 10:16; aid, relief, Heb. 13:16; contribution in aid, Rom. 15:26
G2842.abbottSmith: fellowship , communion : Ac 2:42 , II Co 6:14 , Phl 1:5 ; τ. υἱοῦ , I Co 1:9 ; τ. αἵματος, σώματος Χρ. , I Co 10:16 ; παθημάτων αὐτσῦ , Phl 3:10 ; τ. πνεύματος , II Co 13:13 , Phl 2:1 ; δεξιὰς κοινωνίας , Ga 2:9 ; τ. πίστεώς σου , Phm 6 ; seq. μετὰ : τ. πατρός , I Jo 1:3 , 6 ; ἡμῶν, ἀλλήλων , ib. 3 , 7 .
G2842.strongs: from G2844 (κοινωνός); partnership, i.e. (literally) participation, or (social) intercourse, or (pecuniary) benefaction

G4895.thayer: I am with, come together with.
G4895.abbottSmith: to be with : Lk 9:18 (WH, mg., συνήντησαν ), Ac 22:11 .†
G4895.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1510 (εἰμί) (including its various inflections); to be in company with, i.e. present at the time

G311.abbottSmith: delay : Ac 25:17 (for exx. of other meanings, v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G2720.thayer: (a) I make straight, (b) met: I put in the right way, direct.
G2720.mounce: optative, κατευθύναι (3rd sg), to make straight; to direct, guide aright, Lk. 1:79; 1 Thess. 3:11; 2 Thess. 3:5*
G2720.abbottSmith: to make or keep straight , direct , guide : metaph., τ. ὁδόν , I Th 3:11 ; τ. πόδας εἰς ὁδὸν εἰρήνης , Lk 1:79 ; τ. καρδίας εἰς τ. ἀγάπην , II Th 3:5 .†
G2720.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2116 (εὐθύνω); to straighten fully, i.e. (figuratively) direct

G1906.thayer: inquiry, request, appeal, demand; a profession, pledge.
G1906.mounce: pr. an interrogation, question; in NT profession, pledge, 1 Pet. 3:21*

G300.thayer: Amon (Amos), son of Manasseh and father of Josiah, an ancestor of Jesus.
G300.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0526); Amon, an Israelite

G1370.mounce: a standing apart; a division, dissension, Rom. 16:17; Gal. 5:20*
G1370.strongs: from a derivative of G1364 (δίς) and G4714 (στάσις); disunion, i.e. (figuratively) dissension

G4050.abbottSmith: abundance , superfluity : Ro 5:17 , II Co 8:2 10:15 , Ja 1:21 (so also Inscr., v. LAE , 80).

G1980a.abbottSmith: to equip, make ready ; mid., to make one’s preparations : Ac 21:15 .†

G4887.thayer: I bind together; pass: I am bound together with, as of prisoners in chains.
G4887.mounce: to bind together; in NT pass. to be in bonds together, Heb. 13:3*
G4887.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1210 (δέω); to bind with, i.e. (passively) be a fellow-prisoner (figuratively)

G3915.mounce: a wintering in a place, Acts 27:12*

G2591.strongs: perhaps for G2590 (καρπός); Carpus, probably a Christian

G2772.thayer: a small coin; plur: small change.
G2772.mounce: something clipped small; small change, small pieces of money, coin, Jn. 2:15*
G2772.strongs: from G2751 (κείρω); a clipping (bit), i.e. (specially) a coin

G1264.mounce: to fight out, to fight resolutely; met. to contend vehemently, insist, Acts 23:9*
G1264.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3164 (μάχομαι); to fight fiercely (in altercation)

G1934.thayer: I seek after, desire, search for, make inquiries about.
G1934.mounce: to seek for, make search for, Acts 12:19; to require, demand, Mt. 12:39; 16:4; Acts 19:39; to desire, endeavor to obtain, Rom. 11:7; Heb. 11:14; to seek with care and anxiety, Mt. 6:32
G1934.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2212 (ζητέω); to search (inquire) for; intensively, to demand, to crave

G4647.thayer: a stake or thorn; fig: a sharp affliction.
G4647.mounce: anything pointed; met. a thorn, a plague, 2 Cor. 12:7*
G4647.strongs: perhaps from the base of G4628 (σκέλος) and G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); withered at the front, i.e. a point or prickle (figuratively, a bodily annoyance or disability)

G5212.abbottSmith: poss. pron. of second pers. pl. (= emphasized gen., ὑμῶν ),
G5212.strongs: from G5210 (ὑμεῖς); yours, i.e. pertaining to you

G3784.mounce: to owe, be indebted, Mt. 18:28, 30, 34; to incur a bond, to be bound to make discharge, Mt. 23:16, 18; to be bound or obliged by what is due or fitting or consequently necessary, Lk. 17:10; Jn. 13:14; to incur desert, to deserve, Jn. 19:7; to be due or fitting, 1 Cor. 7:3, 36; from the Aramaic, to be delinquent, Lk. 11:4
G3784.abbottSmith: to owe , be a debtor : c. acc. rei, Mt 18:28 , Lk 7:41 16:7 , Phm 18 ; id. c. dat. pers., Mt 18:28 , Lk 16:5 . Pass., to be owed , to be due : τ. ὀφειλόμενον , Mt 18:30 , 34 . Metaph.: absol. (= Rabbinic חַיָּב ; v. McNeile, in l.), Mt 23:16 , 18 ; c. acc. rei et dat. pers., Ro 13:8 ; c. inf., to be bound or obliged to do (of. Westc., Epp. Jo. , 50), Lk 17:10 , Jo 13:14 19:7 , Ac 17:29 , Ro 15:1 ,
G3784.strongs: probably from the base of G3786 (ὄφελος) (through the idea of accruing); to owe (pecuniarily); figuratively, to be under obligation (ought, must, should); morally, to fail in duty
G3784.kjv: behove, be bound, (be) debt(-or), (be) due(-ty), be guilty (indebted), (must) need(-s), ought, owe, should

G1618.thayer: intent, constant, strenuous, intense; met: earnest, zealous.
G1618.mounce: pr. extended; met. intense, earnest, fervent, eager, 1 Pet. 4:8*
G1618.abbottSmith: stretched, strained . Metaph., earnest, zealous : I Pe 4:8 .†

G3664.thayer: like, similar to, resembling, of equal rank.
G3664.mounce: like, similar, resembling, Mt. 11:16; 13:31, 33, 44, 45, 47, 52; Jn. 8:55, et al. freq.; like, of similar drift and force, Mt. 22:39
G3664.abbottSmith: like , resembling , such as , the same as : c. dat., of form or appearance, Jo 9:9 , Re 1:13 (WH, mg., R, but v. infr.) Re 1:15 2:18 4:6 , 7 9:7 , 10 (Bl., § 37, 6 2 ) Re 9:19 11:1 13:2 , 11 ; ὁράσει , Re 4:3 ; of nature, condition, ability, etc., Mt 22:39 , Ac 17:29 , Ga 5:21 , I Jo 3:2 , Re 13:4 18:18 21:11 , 18 ; of comparison in parables, Mt 13:31 ff. 20:1 , Lk 13:18 , 19 , 21 ; of thinking, a
G3664.strongs: from the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ); similar (in appearance or character)

G2007.thayer: I put, place upon, lay on; I add, give in addition.
G2007.mounce: to put, place, or lay upon, Mt. 9:18; Lk. 4:40; to impose a name, Mk. 3:16, 17; to inflict, Acts 16:23; Lk. 10:30; Rev. 22:18; mid. to impose with authority, Acts 15:28; 28:10; to set or fall upon, assail, assault, attack, Acts 18:10
G2007.abbottSmith: to lay, set or place upon : c. acc rei, seq. ἐπί , c. acc rei, Mt 23:4 , Lk 15:5 , Jo 9:6 ( WH, txt.) , 15 , Ac 15:10 28:3 ; ἐπί , c. gen. rei, Mt 27:29 ; ἐν , ib.; c. dat. pers., σταυρόν , Lk 23:26 ; στέφανον , Jo 19:2 ; ὄνομα , Mk 3:16 , 17 ; πληγάς , Lk 10:30 , Ac 16:23 ; ἐπί c. acc pers., Re 22:18 ; of the laying on of hands, τ. χεῖρα ( -ας ), seq. ἐπί c. acc pers., Mt 9:18 , Mk 16:[18] , Ac 8
G2007.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5087 (τίθημι); to impose (in a friendly or hostile sense)
G2007.kjv: add unto, lade, lay upon, put (up) on, set on (up), + surname, X wound

G3095.mounce: pr. the system of the magians; magic, Acts 8:11*

G1959.thayer: I take care of, attend to.
G1959.mounce: gen., to take care of, Lk. 10:34f.; 1 Tim. 3:5*
G1959.abbottSmith: to take care of : c. gen., Lk 10:34 , 35 , I Ti 3:5 .†
G1959.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and the same as G3199 (μέλω); to care for (physically or otherwise)

G5299.thayer: I strike under the eye, hence: I bruise, treat severely, discipline by hardship, molest, annoy, harass, worry, exhaust.
G5299.mounce: pr. to strike one upon the parts beneath the eye; to beat black and blue; hence, to discipline by hardship, coerce, 1 Cor. 9:27; met. to weary by continual importunities, pester, Lk. 18:5*
G5299.abbottSmith: to strike under the eye , give a black eye :
G5299.strongs: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); to hit under the eye (buffet or disable an antagonist as a pugilist), i.e. (figuratively) to tease or annoy (into compliance), subdue (one's passions)

G4122.thayer: I take advantage of, overreach, defraud.
G4122.mounce: to have more than another; to take advantage of; to overreach, make gain of, 2 Cor. 7:2; 12:17, 18; to wrong, 1 Thess. 4:6; to get the better, or an advantage of, 2 Cor. 2:11*
G4122.abbottSmith: intrans., to have more , to have an advantage (cl., c. gen. pers.).
G4122.strongs: from G4123 (πλεονέκτης); to be covetous, i.e. (by implication) to over-reach
G4122.kjv: get an advantage, defraud, make a gain

G3602.mounce: bitter lamentation, wailing, Mt. 2:18; meton. sorrow, mourning, 2 Cor. 7:7*
G3602.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G1416 (δύνω); moaning, i.e. lamentation

G229.strongs: from the same as G224 (ἄλευρον); to grind

G961.strongs: from G960 (Βέροια); a Berœoean or native of Berœa

G1568.thayer: I am greatly astonished, am awe-struck.
G1568.mounce: pass., to be amazed, astonished, awe-struck, Mk. 9:15; 14:33; 16:5, 6*

G1636.thayer: an olive tree; the Mount of Olives.
G1636.mounce: an olive tree, Mt. 21:1; 24:3; an olive, fruit of the olive tree, Jas. 3:12, ὄρος τῶν ἐλαιῶν, the Mount of Olives, Mt. 21:1
G1636.abbottSmith: an olive tree : Ro 11:17 , 24 , Re 11:4 ; τ. ὄρος τῶν ἐ. ( הַר הַוֵּתִים , Za 14:4 ), the Mount of Olives : Mt 21:1 24:3 26:30 , Mk 11:1 13:3 14:26 , Lk 19:37 22:39 ; τὸ καλούμενον ἐ. (T, ἐλαιών , q.v.), Lk 19:29 21:37 .
G1636.strongs: feminine of a presumed derivative from an obsolete primary; an olive (the tree or the fruit)

G2123.abbottSmith: with easy labour, easy : compar., -ώτερόν ἐστι , c. inf., Mt 9:5 , Mk 2:9 , Lk 5:23 ; c. acc et inf., Mt 19:24 , Mk 10:25 , Lk 16:17 18:25 (Polyb.; the adv. -ως occurs in Aristoph., Fr. , 615).†
G2123.strongs: comparative of a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G2873 (κόπος); better for toil, i.e. more facile

G1872.thayer: I follow close after, accompany, dog; I imitate, pursue, am studious of.
G1872.mounce: to follow upon; to accompany, be attendant, Mk. 16:20; to appear later, 1 Tim. 5:24; met. to follow one’s steps, to imitate, 1 Pet. 2:21; to follow a work, pursue, prosecute, be studious of, devoted to, 1 Tim. 5:10*
G1872.abbottSmith: to follow after ; in NT metaph.; absol.: Mk 16:20 (illustrated by use in verifying accounts; v. MM, Exp. , xiv; Milligan; NTD , 78); c. dat. pers., of sins, I Ti 5:24 (cf. Ellic. and CGT , in l); τ. ἴχνεσιν , I Pe 2:21 ; ἔργῳ ἀγαθῷ , I Ti 5:10 .†
G1872.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to accompany

G857.mounce: pr. the disposition of one who is ἀφειδής, unsparing; hence, in NT unsparingness in the way of rigorous treatment, non-indulgence, Col. 2:23*
G857.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5339 (φείδομαι); unsparingness, i.e. austerity (asceticism)

G5312.thayer: (a) I raise on high, lift up, (b) I exalt, set on high.
G5312.mounce: to raise aloft, lift up, Jn. 3:14; 8:28; met. to elevate in condition, uplift, exalt, Mt. 11:23; 23:12; Lk. 1:52
G5312.abbottSmith: to lift or raise up : c. acc, Jo 3:14 8:28 12:32 , 34 ; ἕως τ. οὐρανοῦ , fig., pass., Mt 11:23 , Lk 10:15 . Metaph., to exalt, uplift : Ac 2:33 5:31 , II Co 11:7 , Ja 4:10 , I Pe 5:6 ; opp. to ταπεινῶ , Lk 1:52 ; ἑαυτόν , Mt 23:12 , Lk 4:11 18:14 (cf. ὑπερ-υψόω ).†
G5312.strongs: from G5311 (ὕψος); to elevate (literally or figuratively)

G412.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G1583 (ἐκλαλέω); not spoken out, i.e. (by implication) unutterable

G3679.mounce: to censure, inveigh against, Mt. 11:20; Mk. 16:14; to reproach or revile, Jas. 1:5; to revile, insult with insulting language, Mt. 5:11
G3679.abbottSmith: to reproach , upbraid : absol., Ja 1:5 ; c. acc. pers. (in cl. more freq. c. dat. pers.; Bl., § 34, 2; WM, 278), Mt 5:11 , Mk 15:32 , 34 , WH, mg., Lk 6:22 , Ro 15:3 (LXX) ; τ. πόλεις , Mt 11:20 ; pass., I Ti 4:10 , WH, mg., I Pe 4:14 ; c. acc. rei, Mk 16:[14] ; c. dupl. acc., Mt 27:44 .†
G3679.strongs: from G3681 (ὄνειδος); to defame, i.e. rail at, chide, taunt
G3679.kjv: cast in teeth, (suffer) reproach, revile, upbraid

G4578.thayer: a shaking (as an earthquake); a storm.
G4578.mounce: pr. a shaking, agitation, concussion; an earthquake, Mt. 24:7; 27:54; a tempest, Mt. 8:24
G4578.abbottSmith: a shaking, commotion , as a tempest at sea: Mt 8:24 ; esp. an earthquake : Mt 24:7 27:54 28:2 , Mk 13:8 , Lk 21:11 , Ac 16:26 , Re 6:12 8:5 11:13 , 19 16:18 .†
G4578.strongs: from G4579 (σείω); a commotion, i.e. (of the air) a gale, (of the ground) an earthquake

G2936.thayer: I create, form, shape, make, always of God.
G2936.mounce: pr. to reduce from a state of disorder and wildness; in NT to call into being, to create, Mk. 13:19; to call into individual existence, to frame, Eph. 2:15; to create spiritually, to invest with a spiritual frame, Eph. 2:10; 4:24
G2936.abbottSmith: in cl., to people or found a region or city ( I Es 4:53 ).
G2936.strongs: probably akin to G2932 (κτάομαι) (through the idea of proprietorship of the manufacturer); to fabricate, i.e. found (form originally)

G2798.thayer: a young tender shoot, then: a branch; met: of descendants.
G2798.mounce: a bough, branch, shoot, Mt. 13:32; 21:8; met. a branch of a family stock, Rom. 11:16, 21
G2798.abbottSmith: a young tender shoot broken off for grafting; then, a branch : Mt 13:32 21:8 24:32 , Mk 4:32 13:28 , Lk 13:19 ; metaph., of descendants (cf. Si 40:15 ), Ro 11:16-19 , 21 .†
G2798.strongs: from G2806 (κλάω); a twig or bough (as if broken off)

G4198.mounce: also listed as a deponent, πορεύομαι, to go, pass from one place to another, Mt. 17:27; 18:12; to go away, depart, Mt. 24:1; 25:41; Jn. 14:2, 3; trop. to go away, depart, from life, to die, Lk. 22:22; to go, pass on one’s way, journey, travel, Mt. 2:8, 9; Lk. 1:39; 2:41; πορεύομαι ὀπίσω, to go after, to become a follower or partisan, Lk. 21:8; or, to pursue after, be devoted to, 2 Pet. 2:10; from the Hebrew, to go or proceed in any way or course of life, live in any manner, Lk. 1:6; 8:14; Acts 9:31
G4198.abbottSmith: in cl. (the act. becomes obsolete in late Gk.; v. M, Pr. , 162), to cause to go over , carry , convey . Mid. (always in LXX and NT), -ομαι , to go , proceed , go on one's way : c. acc., ὁδόν , Ac 8:39 ; seq. ἐκεῖθεν , Mt 19:15 ; ἐντεῦθεν , Lk 13:31 ; ἀπό , Mt 25:41 , Lk 4:42 ; εἰς , Mt 2:20 , Mk 16:12 , Lk 1:39 , Jo 7:35 , al; εἰς εἰρήνην (cf. I Ki 1:17 ). Lk 7:50 ; ἐν εἰρήνῃ , Ac 16:36 ; ἐπί , c.
G4198.strongs: middle voice from a derivative of the same as G3984 (πεῖρα); to traverse, i.e. travel (literally or figuratively; especially to remove (figuratively, die), live, etc.);
G4198.kjv: --depart, go (away, forth, one's way, up), (make a, take a) journey, walk

G963.thayer: (a) Bethany, the home of Lazarus, Martha, and Mary, near Jerusalem, (b) Bethany, beyond Jordan.
G963.mounce: Bethany (1) A village near Jerusalem, at the Mount of Olives, Mt. 21:17; Mk. 11:1. (2) A village beyond the Jordan, Jn. 1:28. See PhotoGuide.
G963.abbottSmith: Bethany ; a village fifteen furlongs from Jerusalem, the modern El Azerîyeh : Mt 21:17 26:6 , Mk 8:22 (WH, mg.) 11:1 , 11 , 12 14:3 , Lk 19:29 24:50 , Jo 11:1 , 18 12:1 . A place on E. bank of Jordan: Jo 1:28 (R, mg., Βηθαβαρά , q.v.).†
G963.strongs: of Chaldee origin; date-house; Beth-any, a place in Palestine

G2097.thayer: I bring good news, preach good tidings, with or without an object, expressing either the persons who receive the good news or the good news itself (the good news being sometimes expressed as a person).
G2097.mounce: to address with good tidings, Rev. 10:7; 14:6; but elsewhere to proclaim as good tidings, to announce good tidings of, Lk. 1:19; to address with good tidings, Acts 13:32; 14:15; to address with the Gospel teaching, evangelize, Acts 16:10; Gal. 1:9; absol. to announce the good tidings of the Gospel, Lk. 4:18; 9:6; pass. to be announced as good tidings, Lk. 16:16; to be addressed with good tidings, Mt. 11:5; Lk. 7:22; Heb. 4:2
G2097.abbottSmith: to bring or announce glad tidings ;
G2097.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G32 (ἄγγελος); to announce good news ("evangelize") especially the gospel
G2097.kjv: declare, bring (declare, show) glad (good) tidings, preach (the gospel)

G4483.mounce: to lay, to arrange, to gather; to say, Mt. 1:20; to speak, make an address or speech, Acts 26:1; to say mentally, in thought, Mt. 3:9; Lk. 3:8; to say in written language, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 1:63; Jn. 19:37; to say, as distinguished from acting, Mt. 23:3; to mention, speak of, Mk. 14:71; Lk. 9:31; Jn. 8:27; to tell, declare, narrate, Mt. 21:27; Mk. 10:32; to express, Heb. 5:11; to put forth, propound, Lk. 5:36; 13:6; Jn. 16:29; to mean, to intend to signify, 1 Cor. 1:12; 10:29; to say, declare, affirm, maintain, Mt. 3:9; 5:18; Mk. 12:18; Acts 17:7; 26:22; 1 Cor. 1:10; to enjoin, Acts 15:24; 21:21; Rom. 2:22; to term, designate, cull, Mt. 19:17; Mk. 12:37; Lk. 20:37; 23:2; 1 Cor. 8:5; to call by a name, Mt. 2:23; pass. to be further named, to be surnamed, Mt. 1:16; to be explained, interpreted, Jn. 4:25; 20:16, 24; in NT σὺ λέγεις, you say, a form of affirmative answer to a question Mt. 27:11; Mk. 15:2; Jn. 18:37
G4483.abbottSmith: in Hom., to pick out , gather , reckon , recount . In Hdt. and Att., to say , speak , affirm , declare : absol., Ac 13:15 24:10 ; seq. orat. dir., Mt 9:34 , Mk 3:11 , Jo 1:29 , al.; seq. ὅτι recit., Mk 3:21 , Lk 1:24 , Jo 6:14 , al.; acc. et. inf., Lk 11:18 , Jo 12:29 , al.; after another verb of speaking, προσφωνεῖν κ. λέγειν , Mt 11:17 , al.; ἀπεκρίθη ( ἐλάλησεν ) λέγων ( καὶ λέγει ; Dalman, Wor
G4483.strongs: perhaps akin (or identical) with G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of pouring forth); to utter, i.e. speak or say

G82.mounce: not apparent or obvious; uncertain, not distinct, Lk. 11:44; 1 Cor. 14:8*
G82.abbottSmith: unseen , unobserved , not manifest ( Ps 50 (51):6 ): Lk 11:44 . uncertain , indistinct : I Co 14:8 .†
G82.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1212 (δῆλος); hidden, figuratively, indistinct

G708.thayer: Aristarchus, a Christian, belonging to Thessalonica in Macedonia.
G708.mounce: Aristarchus, pr. name, Acts 19:29; 20:4; 27:2; Col. 4:10; Phlm. 24*
G708.strongs: from the same as G712 (ἄριστον) and G757 (ἄρχω); best ruling; Aristarchus, a Macedonian

G353.thayer: I take up, raise; I pick up, take on board; I carry off, lead away.
G353.mounce: to take up, receive up, Mk. 16:19; to take up, carry, Acts 7:43; to take on board, Acts 20:13, 14; to take in company, Acts 23:31; 2 Tim. 4:11
G353.abbottSmith: to take up , raise : Mk 16:19 , Ac 1:2 , 11 1:22 10:16 , I Ti 3:16 .
G353.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take up

G1773.abbottSmith: nightly . Neut., adverbially, ἔννυχα (Rec. -χον ), by night : Mk 1:35 .†
G1773.strongs: neuter of a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G3571 (νύξ); (adverbially) by night

G2905.mounce: to cry out, exclaim, shout, Mt. 12:19; Acts 22:23
G2905.abbottSmith: of animals, to bay , to croak , etc.

G1218.thayer: properly: the people, especially citizens of a Greek city in popular assembly, but in NT, multitude, rabble.
G1218.abbottSmith: a district , country . the common people , the people generally; esp. the people assembled : Ac 12:22 17:5 19:30 , 33 .†
G1218.strongs: from G1210 (δέω); the public (as bound together socially)

G2175.mounce: a sweet smell, grateful odor, fragrance, 2 Cor. 2:15; Eph. 5:2; Phil. 4:18*
G2175.abbottSmith: fragrance : metaph., Χριστοῦ εὐ,. , II Co 2:15 ; ὀσμὴ εὐωδίας (a metaphor of sacrifice, most freq. in Pent. and Ez.), Eph 5:2 , Phl 4:18 .†
G2175.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G3605 (ὄζω); good-scentedness, i.e. fragrance

G3623.thayer: a household manager, a steward, guardian.
G3623.mounce: the manager of a household; a steward, Lk. 12:42; 16:1, 3, 8; 1 Cor. 4:2; a manager, trustee, Gal. 4:2; a public steward, treasurer, Rom. 16:23; a spiritual steward, the holder of a commission in the service of the Gospel, 1 Cor. 4:1; Tit. 1:7; 1 Pet. 4:10*
G3623.abbottSmith: prop., the manager of a household or estate , a house- or land-steward (usually a slave or freedman): Lk 12:42 16:1 , 3 , 8 , I Co 4:2 , Ga 4:2 ; ὀ οἰ. τ. πόλεως (RV, treasurer ; cf. I Es 4:49 ), Ro 16:23 .
G3623.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος) and the base of G3551 (νόμος); a house-distributor (i.e. manager), or overseer, i.e. an employee in that capacity; by extension, a fiscal agent (treasurer); figuratively, a preacher (of the Gospel)

G712.mounce: pr. the first meal, breakfast; afterwards extended to signify also a slight mid-day meal, luncheon, Mt. 22:4; Lk. 11:38; 14:12*
G712.strongs: apparently neuter of a superlative from the same as G730 (ἄῤῥην); the best meal (or breakfast; perhaps from ("early")), i.e. luncheon

G5493.abbottSmith: winter-flowing ; as subst., ὁ χ. (sc. ποταμός ), a torrent : Jo 18:1 .†
G5493.strongs: from the base of G5494 (χειμών) and G4482 (ῥέω); a storm-runlet, i.e. winter-torrent

G14.thayer: I work that which is good, perform good deeds.
G14.mounce: to do good, confer benefits, Acts 14:17; 1 Tim. 6:18*
G14.abbottSmith: to do good , show kindness : 1 Ti 6:18 (Cremer, 8).†
G14.strongs: from G18 (ἀγαθός) and G2041 (ἔργον); to work good

G3721.abbottSmith: early , in the early morning : Lk 24:22 , Rec. (v.s. ὀρθρινός ).†
G3721.strongs: from G3722 (ὄρθρος); in the dawn, i.e. up at day-break

G4255.thayer: I propose; mid: I propose to myself, purpose.
G4255.mounce: to prefer, choose; met. to purpose, intend considerately, 2 Cor. 9:7*
G4255.abbottSmith: to bring forth or forward . Most freq. in mid., to take by choice , prefer , propose : II Co 9:7 .†
G4255.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G138 (αἱρέομαι); to choose for oneself before another thing (prefer), i.e. (by implication) to propose (intend)
