G890.mounce: unuseful, useless, unprofitable, and by impl. detrimental, causing loss, Phlm. 11*
G890.abbottSmith: useless , unserviceable : opp. to εὔχρηστος , Phm 11 .†
G890.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5543 (χρηστός); inefficient, i.e. (by implication) detrimental

G5553.thayer: a piece of gold, golden ornament.
G5553.mounce: gold, Heb. 9:4; 1 Pet. 1:7; Rev. 3:18; 21:18, 21; spc. gold when coined or manufactured; golden ornaments, 1 Tim. 2:9; 1 Pet. 3:3; Rev. 17:4; 18:16; gold coin, money, Acts 3:6; 20:33; 1 Pet. 1:18*
G5553.abbottSmith: a piece of gold, gold : I Co 3:12 , He 9:4 , I Pe 1:7 , Re 3:18 21:18 , 21 ; of golden ornaments, I Ti 2:9 (WH, txt., RV), I Pe 3:3 , Re 17:4 18:16 (WH, txt., R); of gold coin, money, Ac 3:6 20:33 , I Pe 1:18 .†
G5553.strongs: diminutive of G5557 (χρυσός); a golden article, i.e. gold plating, ornament, or coin

G4926.mounce: pr. to be in company with; to talk or converse with, Acts 10:27*
G4926.abbottSmith: to converse with (absol., FlJ, BJ , v, 13, 1; seq. μετά , Cebes, 13): c. dat. pers., Ac 10:27 .†
G4926.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3656 (ὁμιλέω); to converse mutually

G4690.thayer: (a) seed, commonly of cereals, (b) offspring, descendents.
G4690.mounce: seed, Mt. 13:24, 27, 37, 38; semen virile, Heb. 11:11; offspring, posterity, Mt. 22:24, 25; Jn. 7:42; a seed of future generations, Rom. 9:29; in NT met. a seed or principle of spiritual life, 1 Jn. 3:9
G4690.strongs: from G4687 (σπείρω); something sown, i.e. seed (including the male "sperm"); by implication, offspring; specially, a remnant (figuratively, as if kept over for planting)

G4152.mounce: spiritual, pertaining to the soul, as distinguished from what concerns the body, Rom. 15:27; 1 Cor. 9:11; spiritual, pertaining to the nature of spirits, 1 Cor. 15:44; τὰ πνευματικά; τῆς πονηρίας, i.q. τὰ πνεύματα τὰ πονηρά, evil spirits, Eph. 6:12; spiritual, pertaining or relating to the influences of the Holy Spirit, of things, Rom. 1:11; 7:14; τὰ πνευματικά, spiritual gifts, 1 Cor. 12:1; 14:1; superior in process to the natural course of things, miraculous, 1 Cor. 10:3; of persons, gifted with a spiritual frame of mind, spiritually affected, 1 Cor. 2:13, 15; endowed with spiritual gifts, inspired, 1 Cor. 14:37
G4152.abbottSmith: of or caused by the wind , air or breath (Arist., al.).
G4152.strongs: from G4151 (πνεῦμα); non-carnal, i.e. (humanly) ethereal (as opposed to gross), or (dæmoniacally) a spirit (concretely), or (divinely) supernatural, regenerate, religious

G1184.mounce: accepted, acceptable, agreeable, approved, Lk. 4:24; Acts 10:35; Phil. 4:18; by impl. when used of a certain time, marked by divine acceptance, propitious, Lk. 4:19; 2 Cor. 6:2*
G1184.abbottSmith: acceptable : Lk 4:19 (LXX) 24 , Ac 10:35 , II Co 6:2 (LXX) , Phl 4:18 (cf. εὐπροσδ- ).†

G2077.strongs: second person singular present imperative of G1510 (εἰμί); be thou; also , third person of the same; let them be

G4290.thayer: readily, eagerly, with a ready mind, cheerfully.
G4290.mounce: promptly, readily, willingly, heartily, cheerfully, 1 Pet. 5:2*
G4290.abbottSmith: eagerly , readily , with a ready mind : I Pe 5:2 .†

G2027.thayer: I force forward, run (a ship) aground.
G2027.mounce: to push a ship to shore, Acts 27:41*
G2027.abbottSmith: of a ship, to run ashore : c. acc, Ac 27:41 .†
G2027.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and (to urge); to drive upon the shore, i.e. to beach a vessel

G4748.mounce: pr. to advance in a line; met. to frame one’s conduct by a certain rule, Acts 21:24; Rom. 4:12; Gal. 5:25; 6:16; Phil. 3:16*
G4748.abbottSmith: to be in rows (of waves, plants, etc., as well as of men), to walk in line (esp. of marching in file to battle; Xen., Cyr. , vi, 3, 34, al.). Metaph., in late writers, to walk by rule : Ac 21:24 ; c. dat., to walk by or in (as a rule of life), Ro 4:12 (cf. MM, xxiii), Ga 5:25 6:16 , Phl 3:16 (cf. συν-στοιχέω ).†
G4748.strongs: from a derivative of (to range in regular line); to march in (military) rank (keep step), i.e. (figuratively) to conform to virtue and piety

G5387.abbottSmith: tenderly loving, affectionate (Xen., Plut., al.): of Christians, Ro 12:10 .†
G5387.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and (cherishing one's kindred, especially parents or children); fond of natural relatives, i.e. fraternal towards fellow Christian

G2639.thayer: I enter in a list, register, enroll.
G2639.mounce: to select, enter in a list or catalog, enroll, 1 Tim. 5:9*
G2639.abbottSmith: to lay down ; mid. (in Hom.), to lie down .
G2639.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original meaning); to lay down, i.e. (figuratively) to enrol

G2725.mounce: an accuser, Rev. 12:10, a barbarous form for κατήγορος*
G2725.abbottSmith: an accuser : Ac 23:30 , 35 24:8 (WH, R, txt. om.) Ac 25:16 , 18 .†
G2725.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G58 (ἀγορά); against one in the assembly, i.e. a complainant at law; specially, Satan

G177.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G2572 (καλύπτω); unveiled

G2962.mounce: a lord, master, Mt. 12:8; an owner, possessor, Mt. 20:8; a potentate, sovereign, Acts 25:26; a power, deity, 1 Cor. 8:5; the Lord, Jehovah, Mt. 1:22; the Lord Jesus Christ, Mt. 24:42; Mk. 16:19; Lk. 10:1; Jn. 4:1; 1 Cor. 4:5; freq.; κύριε, a term of respect of various force, Sir, Lord, Mt. 13:27; Acts 9:6, et al. freq.
G2962.abbottSmith: having power ( κῦρος ) or authority ; as subst., ὁ κ. , lord , master ; in general: c. gen. rei, Mt 9:38 20:8 , Mk 12:9 13:35 , Lk 19:33 ; τ. σαββάτου , Mt 12:8 , Mk 2:28 , Lk 6:5 ; c. gen. pers., δούλου , etc., Mt 10:24 , Lk 14:21 , Ac 16:16 , al.; absol., opp. to οἱ δοῦλοι , Eph 6:5 , 9 , al.; of the Emperor (Deiss., LAE , 161), Ac 25:26 ; θεοὶ πολλοὶ καὶ κ. πολλοί , I Co 8:5 ; of a husband, I P
G2962.strongs: from (supremacy); supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

G3910.thayer: adv. with force of adj: present, immediate, for the moment.
G3910.mounce: instantly, immediately; ὁ, ἡ, τό, παραυτίκα, momentary, transient, 2 Cor. 4:17*
G3910.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and a derivative of G846 (αὐτός); at the very instant, i.e. momentary

G3858.mounce: to accept, receive, met. to receive, admit, yield assent to, Mk. 4:20; Acts 15:4; 16:21; 22:18; 1 Tim. 5:19; in NT to receive or embrace with favor, approve, love, Heb. 12:6*
G3858.abbottSmith: to receive , admit ; of things: Mk 4:20 , Ac 15:4 16:21 22:18 , I Ti 5:19 ; of persons: He 12:6 (LXX) .†
G3858.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to accept near, i.e. admit or (by implication) delight in

G285.abbottSmith: sand , sandy ground : Mt 7:26 , Ro 9:27 , He 11:12 , Re 18:1 , Re 20:8 .†
G285.strongs: perhaps from G260 (ἅμα); sand (as heaped on the beach)

G4364.thayer: I pretend, regard, make a show of.
G4364.mounce: to add or attach; mid. to attach to one’s self; to claim or arrogate to one’s self; to assume the appearance of, make a show of, pretend, Lk. 24:28*
G4364.abbottSmith: to make over to , add or attach to . Mid., to take to oneself , claim ; hence, to pretend ; c. inf. (cf. Xen., Anab. , iv, 3, 20), to make as if : Lk 24:28 .†
G4364.strongs: middle voice from G4314 (πρός) and G4160 (ποιέω); to do forward for oneself, i.e. pretend (as if about to do a thing)

G1169.mounce: timid, fearful, cowardly, Mt. 8:26; Mk. 4:40; Rev. 21:8*

G4621.strongs: of uncertain derivation; grain, especially wheat

G13.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H02285); Agabus, an Israelite

G2318.mounce: reverencing God, pious, godly, devout, a sincere worshipper of God, Jn. 9:31*
G2318.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and G4576 (σέβομαι); reverent of God, i.e. pious

G2613.abbottSmith: to pass sentence upon , condemn ; in cl., c. gen. pers. (acc. rei); in late writers (so LXX), c. acc. pers. ( θανάτῳ , Wi 2:20 ): Mt 12:7 , Ja 5:6 ; absol. (as Plat., Legg. , 958c), Lk 6:37 ; pass., Mt 12:37 , Lk 6:37 .†
G2613.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G1349 (δίκη); to adjudge against, i.e. pronounce guilty

G3424.thayer: one speaking with difficulty, a stammerer.
G3424.mounce: having an impediment in one’s speech, speaking with difficulty, a stammerer, Mk. 7:32*
G3424.abbottSmith: speaking with difficulty : Mk 7:32 (Tr., txt., μογγιλάλος , thick-voiced , v. Swete, in l.).†
G3424.strongs: from G3425 (μόγις) and G2980 (λαλέω); hardly talking, i.e. dumb (tongue-tied)
G3424.kjv: having an impediment in his speech

G3763.strongs: from G3761 (οὐδέ) and G4218 (ποτέ); not even at any time, i.e. never at all
G3763.kjv: neither at any time, never, nothing at any time

G358.mounce: without saltness, without the taste and pungency of salt, insipid, Mk. 9:50
G358.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G251 (ἅλς); saltless, i.e. insipid

G568.thayer: I have in full, am far, it is enough.
G568.mounce: trans. to have in full what is due or is sought, Mt. 6:2, 5, 16; Lk. 6:24; Phil. 4:18; to have altogether, Phlm. 15; hence, it is enough, Mk. 14:41; intrans. to be distant, Lk. 7:6; to be estranged, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6, mid. to abstain from, Acts 14:20
G568.abbottSmith: trans., to hold back , keep off ; to have in full , to have received (on the "punctiliar" force of the compound, v. M, Pr. , 109, 247): c. acc., Mt 6:2 , 5 , 16 , Lk 6:24 , Phl 4:18 , Phm 15 (for illustr. from π. , where it is used in receipts, v. Deiss., BS , 229; LAE , 110 f.; MM, s.v.); impers., ἀπέχει (Field, Notes , 39), it is enough : Mk 14:41 .
G568.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2192 (ἔχω); (actively) to have out, i.e. receive in full; (intransitively) to keep (oneself) away, i.e. be distant (literally or figuratively)

G1917.mounce: a purpose or design against any one; conspiracy, plot, Acts 9:24; 20:3, 19; 23:30*
G1917.abbottSmith: a plan against, a plot : Ac 9:24 20:3 , 19 23:30 .†
G1917.strongs: from a presumed compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G1014 (βούλομαι); a plan against someone, i.e. a plot

G3113.mounce: far off, at a distance, from afar, from a distance, Mk. 8:3; 11:13; preceded by ἀπό, in the same sense, Mt. 26:58
G3113.strongs: adverb from G3117 (μακρός); from a distance or afar

G3772.thayer: heaven, (a) the visible heavens: the atmosphere, the sky, the starry heavens, (b) the spiritual heavens.
G3772.mounce: heaven, the heavens, the visible heavens and all their phenomena, Mt. 5:18; 16:1; 24:29, et al. freq.; the air, atmosphere, in which the clouds and tempests gather, the birds fly, etc., Mt. 6:26; 16:2, 3; heaven as the peculiar seat and abode of God, of angels, of glorified spirits, etc., Mt. 5:34, 45, 48; 6:1, 9, 10; 12:50; Jn. 3:13, 31; 6:32, 38, 41, 42, 50, 51, 58; in NT heaven as a term expressive of the Divine Being, His administration, etc., Mt. 19:14; 21:25; Lk. 20:4, 5; Jn. 3:27
G3772.abbottSmith: heaven ; of the vault or firmament of heaven, the sky and the aerial regions above the earth: opp. to ἡ γῆ , He 1:10 , II Pe 3:5 , 10 ; ὁ οὐ. καὶ ἡ γῆ , i.e. the world, the universe, Mt 5:18 , Mk 13:31 , Lk 10:21 , Ac 4:24 , Re 10:6 , al.; ἀπ’ ἄκρων οὐ. ἕως ἄ. αὐτῶν (on the absence of art. aft. prep., v. Bl., § 46, 5), Mt 24:31 ; ὑπὸ τὸν οὐ. , Ac 2:5 , Col 1:23 ; ὑψωθῆναι ἕως τοῦ οὐ. , fig., Mt 11
G3772.strongs: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation); the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)

G5411.thayer: a tax, tribute, especially on persons.
G5411.mounce: tribute, tax, strictly such as is laid on dependent and subject people, Lk. 20:22; 23:2; Rom. 13:6, 7*
G5411.abbottSmith: tribute paid by a subject nation (cf. I Mac 10:33 ): φ. δοῦναι ( I Mac 8:4 , 7 ), Lk 20:22 23:2 ; ἀποδοῦναι , Ro 13:7 ; τελεῖν , Ro 13:6 (Hdt., al.).†
G5411.strongs: from G5342 (φέρω); a load (as borne), i.e. (figuratively) a tax (properly, an individual assessment on persons or property; whereas G5056 (τέλος) is usually a general toll on goods or travel)

G5055.thayer: (a) I end, finish, (b) I fulfill, accomplish, (c) I pay.
G5055.mounce: to finish, complete, conclude, an operation, Mt. 11:1; 13:53; 19:1; to finish a circuit, Mt. 10:23; to fulfil, to carry out into full operation, Rom. 2:27; Gal. 5:16; Jas. 2:8; to pay dues, Mt. 17:24; pass. to be fulfilled, realized, Lk. 12:50; 18:31; of time, to be ended, elapse, Rom. 15:8; 20:3, 5, 7
G5055.abbottSmith: to bring to an end, complete, finish : τ. δρόμον , II Ti 4:7 ; τ. λόγους , Mt 7:28 19:1 26:1 ; τ. παραβολάς , Mt 13:53 ; τ. πόλεις , Mt 10:23 ; pass., Re 15:8 20:3 , 5 , 7 ; c. ptcp., Mt 11:1 .
G5055.strongs: from G5056 (τέλος); to end, i.e. complete, execute, conclude, discharge (a debt)
G5055.kjv: accomplish, make an end, expire, fill up, finish, go over, pay, perform

G2289.thayer: I put to death, subdue; pass: I am in danger of death, am dead to, am rid of, am parted from.
G2289.mounce: to put to death, deliver to death, Mt. 10:21; 26:59; Mk. 13:12; pass. to be exposed to imminent danger of death, Rom. 8:36; in NT met. to subdue, Rom. 8:13; pass. to be dead to, to be rid, parted from, as if by the intervention of death, Rom. 7:4
G2289.abbottSmith: to put to death: c. acc., Mt 10:21 26:59 27:1 , Mk 13:12 14:55 , Lk 21:16 , II Co 6:9 , I Pe 3:18 ; pass., Ro 8:36 . Metaph.: Ro 8:13 ; pass., c. dat, (in relation to), Ro 7:4 .†
G2289.strongs: from G2288 (θάνατος) to kill (literally or figuratively)
G2289.kjv: become dead, (cause to be) put to death, kill, mortify

G4840.mounce: to be present with any one, Acts 25:24*
G4840.abbottSmith: to be present together or with : c. dat. pers., Ac 25:24 .†
G4840.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3918 (πάρειμι); to be at hand together, i.e. now present

G4476.strongs: from a primary (to sew; perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω) through the idea of puncturing); a needle

G5511.thayer: a short cloak, worn by military officers and soldiers.
G5511.mounce: chlamys, a type of cloak; a Roman military commander’s cloak, Mt. 27:28, 31*
G5511.abbottSmith: a chlamys , or short cloak worn over the χιτών (q.v.): Mt 27:28 , 31 (v. Tr., Syn. , § 1).†
G5511.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a military cloak

G5220.mounce: bound to a man, married, Rom. 7:2*
G5220.abbottSmith: under or subject to a man, married : γυνή , Ro 7:2 (Polyb., Diod., al.).†
G5220.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G435 (ἀνήρ); in subjection under a man, i.e. a married woman

G218.thayer: I anoint: festivally, in homage, medicinally, or in anointing the dead.
G218.mounce: to anoint with oil or ointment
G218.abbottSmith: to anoint , festally or in homage: c. acc rei or pers., Mt 6:17 , Jo 12:3 , Mk 16:1 ; seq. dat., ἐλαίῳ , Mk 6:13 , Ja 5:14 ; μύρῳ , Lk 7:38 , 46 , Jo 11:2 .†
G218.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as particle of union) and the base of G3045 (λιπαρός); to oil (with perfume)

G3527.strongs: probably from G3528 (νικάω); victorious; Nicanor, a Christian

G1912.thayer: I put a burden on, am burdensome.
G1912.mounce: to burden; met. to be burdensome, chargeable to, 1 Thess. 2:9; 2 Thess. 3:8; to bear hard upon, overcharge, 2 Cor. 2:5*
G1912.abbottSmith: to put a burden on, be burdensome : fig., absol., II Co 2:5 ; c. acc pers., I Th 2:9 , II Th 3:8 (cf. M, Th. , I, 2:9).†
G1912.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G916 (βαρέω); to be heavy upon, i.e. (pecuniarily) to be expensive to; figuratively, to be severe towards

G2966.thayer: a limb, member of a body; fig: a corpse, carcass.
G2966.mounce: lit., a member or limb of the body, fig., dead body, corpse, Heb. 3:17*
G2966.abbottSmith: a limb , member of a body, [but in LXX ( Le 26:30 , Nu 14:29 , 32 , 33 , I Ki 17:46 , Is 66:24 *) for פֶּגֶר ;] hence, carcase : He 3:17 (LXX) .†
G2966.strongs: from the base of G2849 (κολάζω); a limb of the body (as if lopped)

G1030.abbottSmith: a biting, a gnashing of teeth : Mt 8:12 13:42 , 50 22:13 24:51 25:30 , Lk 13:28 .†
G1030.strongs: from G1031 (βρύχω); a grating (of the teeth)

G5009.thayer: a store-chamber, secret chamber, closet; a granary, barn.
G5009.mounce: a storehouse, granary, barn, Lk. 12:24; a chamber, closet, place of retirement and privacy, Mt. 6:6; 24:26; Lk. 12:3*
G5009.strongs: neuter contraction of a presumed derivative of (a dispenser or distributor; akin to , to cut); a dispensary or magazine, i.e. a chamber on the ground-floor or interior of an Oriental house (generally used for storage or privacy, a spot for retirement)

G2466.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03485); Isachar (i.e. Jissaskar), a son of Jacob (figuratively, his descendant)

G138.mounce: some list as deponent αἱρέομαι, to take; mid. to choose
G138.abbottSmith: to take ; Mid., -ομαι (M, Pr ., 158 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.), to choose : Phl 1:22 , II Th 2:13 , He 11:25 . (Cf. ἀν- , ἀφ- , δι- , ἐξ- , καθ- , περι- , προ-αιρέω .)†
G138.strongs: probably akin to G142 (αἴρω); to take for oneself, i.e. to prefer

G91.thayer: I act unjustly towards, injure, harm.
G91.mounce: to act unjustly; wrong; injure; violate a law
G91.abbottSmith: intrans., to be ἄδικος , do wrong , act wickedly or criminally : Ac 25:11 , I Co 6:8 , II Co 7:12 , Col 3:25 , Re 22:11 ; to do hurt , Re 9:19 .
G91.strongs: from G94 (ἄδικος); to be unjust, i.e. (actively) do wrong (morally, socially or physically)
G91.kjv: hurt, injure, be an offender, be unjust, (do, suffer, take) wrong

G1459.thayer: I leave in the lurch, abandon (one who is in straits), desert.
G1459.mounce: to leave, leave behind; to forsake, abandon, Mt. 27:46; Mk. 15:34; Acts 2:27, 30; Rom. 9:29; 2 Cor. 4:9; 2 Tim. 4:10, 16; Heb. 10:25; 13:5* See abandon; desert; forsake.
G1459.abbottSmith: to leave behind : ἡμῖν σπέρμα , Ro 9:29 (LXX) . to abandon, desert, forsake : c. acc. pers., Mt 27:46 (LXX) , Mk 15:34 (ib.) , Ac 2:27 (LXX) (WH, ἐνκ- ), II Ti 4:10 , 16 He 13:5 (LXX) ; τ. ἐπισυναγωγήν (cf. MM, Exp. , xi), He 10:25 . Pass., Ac 2:31 (WH, ἐνκ- ), II Co 4:9 .†
G1459.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2641 (καταλείπω); to leave behind in some place, i.e. (in a good sense) let remain over, or (in a bad sense) to desert

G745.thayer: Archelaus, Herod Archelaus, son and successor of Herod I, reigned over Judea from 4 B.C. to A.D. 6 and died before A.D. 18.
G745.abbottSmith: Archelaus , son of Herod the Great, King of Judæa, Samaria and Idumæa: Mt 2:22 .†
G745.strongs: from G757 (ἄρχω) and G2994 (Λαοδικεύς); people-ruling; Archelaus, a Jewish king

G4148.thayer: I make rich, cause to abound in, enrich.
G4148.mounce: to make rich, enrich; met. to enrich spiritually, 1 Cor. 1:5; 2 Cor. 6:10; 9:11*
G4148.abbottSmith: to make rich , enrich : c. acc. pers., pass., II Co 9:11 . Metaph., of spiritual riches, c. acc. pers., II Co 6:10 ; seq. ἐν , pass., I Co 1:5 .†

G530.mounce: once, 2 Cor. 11:25; once for all, Heb. 6:4; 9:26, 28; 10:2; 1 Pet. 3:18, 20; Jude 3; εἰδὼς ἅπαξ, knowing once for ever, unfailingly, constantly, Jude 5
G530.abbottSmith: once : II Co 11:25 , He 9:26 , 27 ; ἔτι ἅ. , He 12:26 , 27 ; ἅ. τ. ἐνιαυτοῦ , He 9:7 ; καί ἅ. κ. δίς , twice : Phl 4:16 , I Th 2:18 ;
G530.strongs: probably from G537 (ἅπας); one (or a single) time (numerically or conclusively)

G120.thayer: I lose heart, am despondent, am disheartened.
G120.mounce: to be discouraged, lose heart, Col. 3:21*
G120.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2372 (θυμός); to be spiritless, i.e. disheartened

G4176.thayer: I live the life of a citizen, live.
G4176.mounce: intrans. to be a citizen; trans. to govern a city or state, administer the affairs of a state; pass. to be governed; in NT to order one’s life and conduct, converse, live, in a certain manner as to habits and principles, Acts 23:1; Phil. 1:27*
G4176.abbottSmith: to be a citizen , live as a citizen : metaph., of conduct as based on heavenly citizenship, Ac 23:1 , Phl 1:27 .†
G4176.strongs: middle voice of a derivative of G4177 (πολίτης); to behave as a citizen (figuratively)

G1399.strongs: feminine of G1401 (δοῦλος); a female slave (involuntarily or voluntarily)

G2890.thayer: Quartus, a Christian, brother of Erastus the Corinthian.
G2890.mounce: Quartus, a Latin pr. name, Rom. 16:23*
G2890.strongs: of Latin origin (fourth); Quartus, a Christian

G528.thayer: I go to meet, meet, encounter.
G528.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to meet away, i.e. encounter

G2558.mounce: to torment, afflict, harass; pass. to be afflicted, be oppressed with evils, Heb. 11:37 13:3*
G2558.abbottSmith: to ill-treat , hurt , torment : pass. ptcp., He 11:37 13:3 .†
G2558.strongs: from a presumed compound of G2556 (κακός) and G2192 (ἔχω); to maltreat

G4332.thayer: I wait upon, minister to, have charge of.
G4332.mounce: to sit near; to attend, serve, 1 Cor. 9:13*
G4332.strongs: from a compound of G4314 (πρός) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); to sit near, i.e. attend as a servant

G468.thayer: a gift in return (for another), a return, recompense, requital.
G468.abbottSmith: requital ; in favourable sense: Lk 14:12 ; in unfavourable sense: Ro 11:9 .†
G468.strongs: from G467 (ἀνταποδίδωμι); a requital (properly, the thing)

G1056.thayer: Galilee, a district towards the southern end of the Roman province Syria; the northern division of Palestine.
G1056.mounce: Galilee, a district of Palestine north of Samaria, Mt. 4:15
G1056.abbottSmith: Galilee , the name of the northern region of Palestine in NT times: Γ. τῶν ἐθνῶν , Mt 4:15 (LXX) ; θάλασσα τῆς Γ. , Mt 15:29 (cf. Jo 6:1 ).
G1056.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01551); Galilæa (i.e. the heathen circle), a region of Palestine

G3526.mounce: Niger, pr. name, probably not declined, Acts 13:1*
G3526.strongs: of Latin origin; black; Niger, a Christian

G2763.abbottSmith: a potter : Ro 9:21 ; ὁ ἀγρὸς τοῦ κ. , Mt 27:7 , 10 (LXX) .†

G5585.mounce: to reckon by means of pebbles, compute by counters; hence genr. to compute, reckon, calculate, Lk. 14:28; Rev. 13:18*
G5585.abbottSmith: to count (prop., with pebbles), reckon, calculate : τ. δαπάνην , Lk 14:28 ; τ. ἀριθμόν (i.e. calculate the number's meaning), Re 13:18 (in cl. chiefly mid., to vote by casting a pebble; cf. συγ-κατα- , συμ-φηφίζω ).†
G5585.strongs: from G5586 (ψῆφος); to use pebbles in enumeration, i.e. (generally) to compute

G2298.thayer: to be wondered at, wonderful, marvelous.
G2298.mounce: wondrous, glorious, 1 Pet. 2:9; Rev. 15:1; marvellous, strange, uncommon, Mt. 21:42; Mk. 12:11; Jn. 9:30; Rev. 15:3*
G2298.abbottSmith: wonderful , marvellous : Mt 21:42 (LXX) , Mk 12:11 (LXX) , Jo 9:30 , I Pe 2:9 , Re 15:1 , 3 .†
G2298.strongs: from G2296 (θαυμάζω); wondered at, i.e. (by implication) wonderful

G4539.thayer: Salome, wife of Zebedee and mother of James and John, the apostles.
G4539.mounce: Salome, pr. name, a Galilean woman who followed Jesus, Mt. 27:56; Mk. 15:40; 16:1*
G4539.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (feminine from H07965); Salome (i.e. Shelomah), an Israelitess

G2119.thayer: I have a good (favorable) opportunity, have leisure; I devote my leisure to.
G2119.mounce: to have convenient time or opportunity, have leisure, Mk. 6:31; 1 Cor. 16:12; to be at leisure for a thing, to be disposed to attend, to give time, Acts 17:21*
G2119.abbottSmith: to have leisure or opportunity I Co 16:12 ; c. inf., Mk 6:31 ; seq. εἰς , to devote one's leisure to , Ac 17:21 .†
G2119.strongs: from G2121 (εὔκαιρος); to have good time, i.e. opportunity or leisure

G4407.mounce: belonging to the morning, morning, Rev. 2:28; 22:16*
G4407.abbottSmith: at early morn , early : ἀστήρ , Re 2:28 22:16 .†
G4407.strongs: from G4404 (πρωΐ); pertaining to the dawn, i.e. matutinal

G479.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G2564 (καλέω); to invite in return

G4103.mounce: faithful, true, trusty, Mt. 24:45; 25:21, 23; Lk. 12:42; 2 Tim. 2:2; put in trust, 1 Cor. 7:25; true, veracious, Rev. 1:5; 2:13; credible, sure, certain, indubitable, Acts 13:34; 1 Tim. 1:15; believing, yielding belief and confidence, Jn. 20:27; Gal. 3:9; spc. a Christian believer, Acts 10:45; 16:1, 15; 2 Cor. 6:15; πιστόν, in a true-hearted manner, right-mindedly, 3 Jn. 5
G4103.abbottSmith: Pass., to be trusted or believed ; of persons, trusty , faithful : Mt 24:45 25:21 , 23 , Lk 12:42 , Ac 16:15 , I Co 4:2 , 17 7:25 , Eph 1:1 6:21 , Col 1:2 , 7 4:7 , 9 , I Ti 1:12 , II Ti 2:2 , He 2:17 3:2 , 5 , I Pe 5:12 , Re 2:13 19:11 ; of God, I Co 1:9 10:13 , II Co 1:18 , I Th 5:24 , II Th 3:3 , He 10:23 11:11 , II Ti 2:13 , I Jo 1:9 , I Pe 4:19 ; seq. ἐν , Lk 16:10-12 19:17 , I Ti 3:11 ; ἐπί
G4103.strongs: from G3982 (πείθω); objectively, trustworthy; subjectively, trustful

G5350.mounce: to emit a sound; to speak, Acts 4:18; 2 Pet. 2:16, 18*
G5350.abbottSmith: of men or animals, to utter a sound or voice: absol., Ac 4:18 ; seq. ἐν φωνῇ , II Pe 2:16 ; c. acc., ὑπέρογκα , ib. 18 .†
G5350.strongs: probably akin to G5338 (φέγγος) and thus to G5346 (φημί); to utter a clear sound, i.e. (generally) to proclaim

G4593.mounce: mid., to mark, inscribe marks upon; mid. to mark for one’s self, note, 2 Thess. 3:14*
G4593.abbottSmith: to mark, note . Mid., to note for oneself : II Th 3:14 (freq. in π. ; v. ICC , M, Th. , in l.).†
G4593.strongs: from G4592 (σημεῖον); to distinguish, i.e. mark (for avoidance)

G134.abbottSmith: to praise : c. acc., τ. θεόν , Lk 2:13 , 20 19:37 24:53 , Ac 2:47 3:8 , 9 , Ro 15:11 ; c. dat., τ. Θεῷ (Je 20:13, al. for הִלֵּל לְ ; v. Field, Notes , 245), Re 19:5 .†

G1861.mounce: to declare, to promise, undertake, Mk. 14:11; Rom. 4:21; to profess, 1 Tim. 2:10
G1861.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to announce upon (reflexively), i.e. (by implication) to engage to do something, to assert something respecting oneself

G1301.mounce: to watch carefully, guard with vigilance; to treasure up, Lk. 2:51; ἑαυτὸν ἐκ, to keep one’s self from, to abstain wholly from, Acts 15:29*
G1301.abbottSmith: to keep carefully : Lk 2:51 ; seq. ἐκ , Ac 15:29 .†
G1301.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5083 (τηρέω); to watch thoroughly, i.e. (positively and transitively) to observe strictly, or (negatively and reflexively) to avoid wholly

G1887.mounce: tomorrow; ἡ ἐπαύριον, i.e. ἡμέρα, the next or following day, Mt. 27:62; Mk. 11:12
G1887.abbottSmith: on the morrow : in NT, ἡ ἐ. (sc. ἡμέρα ), Mt 27:62 , Mk 11:12 , Jo 1:29 , Ac 10:9 , al
G1887.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G839 (αὔριον); occurring on the succeeding day, i.e. (G2250 (ἡμέρα) being implied) to-morrow

G3491.abbottSmith: a ship : Ac 27:41 (elsewhere in NT always τ. πλοῖον ; v. M, Pr. , 25 f.; Bl., Gosp. , 186 f.).†
G3491.strongs: from or (to float); a boat (of any size)

G810.abbottSmith: prodigality , wastefulness , profligacy : Eph 5:18 , Tit 1:6 , I Pe 4:4 (MM, s.v.).†
G810.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4982 (σώζω); properly, unsavedness, i.e. (by implication) profligacy

G3458.abbottSmith: = μύλη , a mill (Strab., Plut., LXX): Mt 24:41 , Re 18:22 .
G3458.strongs: probably ultimately from the base of G3433 (μόλις) (through the idea of hardship); a "mill", i.e. (by implication), a grinder (millstone)

G2793.thayer: I am in danger or peril.
G2793.mounce: to be in danger or peril, Lk. 8:23; Acts 19:27, 40; 1 Cor. 15:30*
G2793.kjv: be in danger, be (stand) in jeopardy

G3433.mounce: with difficulty, scarcely, hardly, Acts 14:18; 27:7, 8, 16; Rom. 5:7; 1 Pet. 4:18*
G3433.abbottSmith: with difficulty , hardly , scarcely : Lk 9:39 (T, μόγις ), Ac 14:8 27:7 , 8 , 16 , Ro 5:7 , I Pe 4:18 (LXX) .†
G3433.strongs: probably by variation for G3425 (μόγις); with difficulty

G4844.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4095 (πίνω); to partake a beverage in company

G2481.thayer: perhaps, equally; it may be that.
G2481.mounce: equally; perhaps, it may be that, Lk. 20:13*
G2481.strongs: adverb from G2470 (ἴσος); likely, i.e. perhaps

G5004.mounce: toil, difficulty, hardship; calamity, misery, distress, Rom. 3:16; Jas. 5:1*

G3465.thayer: Mysia, a country in the north-west of the Roman province Asia (and of Asia Minor).
G3465.mounce: Mysia, a province of Asia Minor, Acts 16:7f.*
G3465.abbottSmith: Mysia , a province of Asia Minor: Ac 16:7 , 8 .†
G3465.strongs: of uncertain origin; Mysia, a region of Asia Minor

G2180.strongs: from G2181 (Ἔφεσος); an Ephesian or inhabitant of Ephesus

G4937.thayer: I break by crushing, break in pieces, shatter, crush, bruise.
G4937.mounce: to rub together; to shiver, Mk. 14:3; Rev. 2:27; to break, break in pieces, Mk. 5:4; Jn. 19:36; to break down, crush, bruise, Mt. 12:20; met. to break the power of any one, deprive of strength, debilitate, Lk. 9:39; Rom. 16:20*
G4937.abbottSmith: to shatter, break in pieces : Mt 12:20 (LXX) , Mk 5:4 14:3 , Jo 19:36 (LXX) , Re 2:27 ; of persons and parts of the body, to break, crush, bruise : Lk 9:39 ; fig., Ro 16:20 .†
G4937.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to crush completely, i.e. to shatter (literally or figuratively)
G4937.kjv: break (in pieces), broken to shivers (+ -hearted), bruise

G5586.thayer: (a) a pebble, small stone, (b) hence, from their use in voting: a vote.
G5586.mounce: a small stone, pebble; a pebble variously employed, especially in a ballot; hence, a vote, suffrage, Acts 26:10; a pebble or stone; probably given as a token, Rev. 2:17 (2x)*
G5586.abbottSmith: a small smooth stone, a pebble : ψ. λευκή (for suggestions as to the meaning, v. Swete, in l.), Re 2:17 .
G5586.strongs: from the same as G5584 (ψηλαφάω); a pebble (as worn smooth by handling), i.e. (by implication, of use as a counter or ballot) a verdict (of acquittal) or ticket (of admission); a vote

G1722.mounce: followed by the dat., in, Mt. 8:6; Mk. 12:26; Rev. 6:6,; upon, Lk. 8:32; among, Mt. 11:11; before, in the presence of, Mk. 8:38; in the sight, estimation of, 1 Cor. 14:11; before, judicially, 1 Cor. 6:2; in, of state, occupation, habit, Mt. 21:22; Lk. 7:25; Rom. 4:10; in the case of, Mt. 17:12; in respect of, Lk. 1:7; 1 Cor. 1:7; on occasion of, on the ground of, Mt. 6:7; Lk. 1:21; used of the thing by which an oath is made, Mt. 5:34; of the instrument, means, efficient cause, Rom. 12:21; Acts 4:12; equipped with, furnished with, 1 Cor. 4:21; Heb. 9:25; arrayed with, accompanied by, Lk. 14:31; Jude 14; of time, during, in the course of, Mt. 2:1; in NT of demoniacal possession, possessed by, Mk. 5:2
G1722.abbottSmith: Of place, c. dat. rei, pers., in, within, on, at, by, among : ἐν τ. πόλει , Lk 7:37 ; τ. οφθαλμῷ , Mt 7:3 ; τ. κοιλίᾳ , Mt 12:40 ; τ. ὄρει , II Pe 1:18 ; τ. θρόνῳ , Re 3:21 ; τ. δεξιᾷ τ. θεοῦ , Ro 8:34 ; ἐν ἡμῖν Abbott-Smith has ὑμῖν . , Lk 1:1 ; of books, ἐν τ. βιβλίῳ , Ga 3:10 ; τ. νόμῳ , Mt 12:5 , al.; ἐν τοῖς τ. Πατρός , in my Father's house (RV; cf. M, Pr. , 103), Lk 2:49 ; trop., of the regi
G1722.strongs: a primary preposition denoting (fixed) position (in place, time or state), and (by implication) instrumentality (medially or constructively), i.e. a relation of rest (intermediate between G1519 (εἰς) and G1537 (ἐκ)); "in," at, (up-)on, by, etc.
G1722.kjv: about, after, against, + almost, X altogether, among, X as, at, before, between, (here-)by (+ all means), for (… sake of), + give self wholly to, (here-)in(-to, -wardly), X mightily, (because) of, (up-)on, (open-)ly, X outwardly, one, X quickly, X shortly, (speedi-)ly, X that, X there(-in, -on), through(-out), (un-)to(-ward), under, when, where(-with), while, with(-in)

G3891.mounce: to violate or transgress the law, Acts 23:3*
G3891.abbottSmith: to be a παράνομος , to transgress the law : Ac 23:3 .†
G3891.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G3551 (νόμος); to be opposed to law, i.e. to transgress

G562.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a secondary derivative of G4008 (πέραν); unfinished, i.e. (by implication) interminable

G3634.mounce: what, of what kind or sort, as, Mt. 24:21; Mk. 9:3; οὐχ οἶον, not so as, not as implying, Rom. 9:6
G3634.abbottSmith: qualitative (related to ὅς as qualis to qui ), what sort or manner of , such as , in NT usually without its correl. τοιοῦτος : Mt 24:21 , Mk 9:3 , II Co 12:20 , I Th 1:5 , II Ti 3:11 , Re 16:18 ; seq. τοιοῦτος , I Co 15:48 , II Co 10:11 ; id. redundant, Mk 13:19 ; τ. αὐτὸν . . . σἷον , Phl 1:30 ; οὐχ οἷον δὲ ὅτι , elliptically, but it is not as though (RV), Ro 9:6 .†
G3634.strongs: probably akin to G3588 (ὁ), G3739 (ὅς), and G3745 (ὅσος); such or what sort of (as a correlation or exclamation); especially the neuter (adverbially) with negative, not so
G3634.kjv: so (as), such as, what (manner of), which

G791.thayer: (lit: belonging to the city; then: witty, clever), elegant, pretty, fair, fine, beautiful.
G791.mounce: pr. belonging to a city; well bred, polite, polished; hence, elegant, fair, comely, beautiful, Acts 7:20; Heb. 11:23*
G791.strongs: from (a city); urbane, i.e. (by implication) handsome

G2193.thayer: (a) conj: until, (b) prep: as far as, up to, as much as, until.
G2193.mounce: can function as an improper prep., while, as long as, Jn. 9:4; until, Mt. 2:9; Lk. 15:4; as also in NT ἕως οὗ, ἕως ὅτου, Mt. 5:18, 26; ἕως ἄρτι, until now, Mt. 11:12; ἕως πότε, until when, how long, Mt. 17:17; ἕως σήμερον, until this day, to this time, 2 Cor. 3:15; as a prep. of time, until, Mt. 24:21; of place, unto, even to, Mt. 11:23; Lk. 2:15; ἕως ἄνω, to the brim, Jn. 2:7; ἕως εἰς, even to, as far as, Lk. 24:50; ἕως κάτω, to the bottom; ἕως ὥδε, to this place, Lk. 23:5; of state, unto, even to, Mt. 26:38; of number, even, so much as, Rom. 3:12, et al. freq.
G2193.abbottSmith: zeta , the sixth letter. As a numeral, ζ́ʹ = ἑπτά , ἕβδομος (the obsol. ϛ , Ϝ , vau , "digamma," representing ἕξ ), ζ́͵ = 7000.
G2193.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a conjunction, preposition and adverb of continuance, until (of time and place)
G2193.kjv: even (until, unto), (as) far (as), how long, (un-)til(-l), (hither-, un-, up) to, while(-s)

G5062.abbottSmith: forty : Mt 4:2 , Mk 1:13 , Lk 4:2 , Jo 2:20 , Ac 1:3 , al

G5488.mounce: Charran, a city in the northern part of Mesopotamia, Acts 7:2, 4*. See PhotoGuide.
G5488.abbottSmith: indecl. (in FlJ., Ant. , i, 16, 1, Κάρρα, -ας , ib. 6, 5, Χαρρά, -ᾶς ; so LXX: Ge 29:4 E, Ez 27:28 B, elsewhere -άν —Heb. חָרָן ), Haran , a town in N.W. Mesopotamia: Ac 7:2 , 4 .†
G5488.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02771); Charrhan (i.e. Charan), a place in Mesopotamia

G4771.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G4771.strongs: the personal pronoun of the second person singular; thou

G2051.mounce: to quarrel; to wrangle; to use the harsh tone of a wrangler or brawler, to grate, Mt. 12:19*

G2365.mounce: a little daughter, female child, Mk. 5:23; 7:25*
G2365.abbottSmith: a little daughter , a term of endearment used in late Gk. (cf. B1., § 27, 4): Mk 5:23 7:25 .†

G3476.thayer: Nahshon, son of Amminadab and father of Salmon, and one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3476.mounce: Naasson, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:4; Lk. 3:32*
G3476.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05177); Naasson (i.e. Nachshon), an Israelite

G5192.thayer: jacinth, a precious stone of the color of hyacinth.
G5192.mounce: a hyacinth, a gem resembling the color of the hyacinth flower, dark blue, Rev. 21:20*
G5192.strongs: of uncertain derivation; the "hyacinth" or "jacinth", i.e. some gem of a deep blue color, probably the zirkon

G4347.thayer: (lit: I glue one thing to another), I join (unite) closely, cleave (to), follow as an adherent.
G4347.mounce: pr. to glue to; to cleave closely to, Mk. 10:7; Eph. 5:31*
G4347.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2853 (κολλάω); to glue to, i.e. (figuratively) to adhere

G101.thayer: to be impossible; I am unable.
G101.mounce: not to be able; to be impossible, Mt. 17:20; Lk. 1:37*
G101.abbottSmith: Abbott-Smith omits this number to be unable (cl., Philo; π. , v. MM, VGT , s.v.).
G101.strongs: from G102 (ἀδύνατος); to be unable, i.e. (passively) impossible

G3450.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G3450.abbottSmith: I . The nom. is usually emphatic, when expressed as subjc, as in Mt 3:11 , Mk 1:8 , Lk 3:16 , al. But often there is no apparent emphasis, as Mt 10:16 , Jo 10:17 ; ἰδοὺ ἐ. (= Heb. הִנֵּנִי , cf. I Ki 3:8 ), Ac 9:10 ; ἐ. (like Heb. אֲנִי ), I am , Jo 1:23 (LXX) , Ac 7:32 (LXX) . The enclitic forms (v. supr.) are used with nouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs, where there is no emphasis: ἐν τ. πατρί μο
G3450.strongs: the simpler form of G1700 (ἐμοῦ); of me

G5330.thayer: a Pharisee, one of the Jewish sect so called.
G5330.mounce: a Pharisee, a follower of the sect of the Pharisees, a numerous and powerful sect of the Jews, distinguished for their ceremonial observances, and apparent sanctity of life, and for being rigid interpreters of the Mosaic law; but who frequently violated its spirit by their traditional interpretations and precepts, to which they ascribed nearly an equal authority with the OT Scriptures, Mt. 5:20; 12:2; 23:14
G5330.abbottSmith: a Pharisee : Mt 23:26 , Phl 3:5 ; usually in pl., Mt 9:11 , Mk 2:18 , al.; Φ. κ. γραμματεῖς , Mt 5:20 , Mk 2:16 , Lk 5:21 , al.; Φ. κ. Σαδδυκαῖοι , Mt 16:1 , Ac 23:6 , 7 al.; ἀρχιερεῖς , Mt 21:45 , Jo 7:32 , al. (v. DB , iii, 826 b ).
G5330.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H06567); a separatist, i.e. exclusively religious; a Pharisean, i.e. Jewish sectary

G4288.mounce: promptness, readiness, eagerness of mind, willingness, Acts 17:11; 2 Cor. 8:11, 12, 19; 9:2*
G4288.abbottSmith: eagerness , willingness , readiness : Ac 17:11 , II Co 8:11 , 12 , 19 9:2 .†
G4288.kjv: forwardness of mind, readiness (of mind), ready (willing) mind

G1791.abbottSmith: c. gen. pers., respect, reverence (Soph., Polyb., al.).

G641.thayer: I throw away from, throw overboard.
G641.mounce: to throw off, throw down, Acts 27:43*
G641.abbottSmith: to throw away , cast forth : reflexively, Ac 27:43 (EV, cast themselves overboard ; v. MM, s.v.).†
G641.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4496 (ῥίπτω); to hurl off, i.e. precipitate (oneself)

G3521.mounce: fasting, want of food, 2 Cor. 6:5; 11:27; a fast, religious abstinence from food, Mt. 17:21; Lk. 2:37; spc. the annual public fast of the Jews, the great day of atonement, occurring in the month Tisri, corresponding to the new moon of October, Acts 27:9*
G3521.abbottSmith: fasting , a fast ; of voluntary abstinence from food: Mt 17:21 (WH, R, txt., om.), Mk 9:29 (WH, txt., R, txt., om.), Lk 2:37 , Ac 14:23 ; of the Day of Atonement, Ac 27:9 ; of involuntary abstinence: II Co 6:5 11:27 .†
G3521.strongs: from G3522 (νηστεύω); abstinence (from lack of food, or voluntary and religious); specially, the fast of the Day of Atonement

G1891.thayer: Epaphras, Epaphroditus, a Colossian Christian, in captivity with Paul in Rome.
G1891.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) (in the sense of devoted to) and (Venus); Epaphroditus, a Christian

G1143.thayer: I bite; hence: I backbite, harm seriously.
G1143.mounce: to bite, sting; met. to molest, vex, injure, Gal. 5:15*
G1143.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary root; to bite, i.e. (figuratively) thwart

G5297.thayer: I bear up under, endure, suffer.
G5297.mounce: to bear under; to bear up under, support, sustain, 1 Cor. 10:13; to endure patiently, 1 Pet. 2:19; to undergo, 2 Tim. 3:11*
G5297.abbottSmith: to bear by being under . Metaph., to endure : c. acc rei, I Co 10:13 , II Ti 3:11 , I Pe 2:19 .†
G5297.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear from underneath, i.e. (figuratively) to undergo hardship

G4280.mounce: to tell beforehand, to foretell, Mt. 24:25; Acts 1:16; Rom. 9:29; 2 Cor. 13:2; Gal. 5:21; 1 Thess. 3:4
G4280.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2046 (ἐρέω); used as alternate of G4277 (προέπω); to say already, predict

G293.thayer: prop: something thrown around; a fishing-net, drag-net.
G293.abbottSmith: something thrown around , as a garment; spec., a casting-net : Mt 4:18 .†
G293.strongs: from a compound of the base of G297 (ἀμφότερος) and G906 (βάλλω); a (fishing) net (as thrown about the fish)

G4694.thayer: a hidden rock; fig: a flaw, stigma.
G4694.mounce: a sharply-cleft portion of rock; in NT a flaw, stigma, Jude 12*
G4694.abbottSmith: poët. in cl., a rock or reef over which the sea dashes (Polyb., FlJ, al.). Metaph., of men whose conduct causes danger to others, Ju 12 , R, txt. (but v. infr.).
G4694.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a ledge or reef of rock in the sea

G4477.thayer: Rahab, a Canaanitess, who rescued the Hebrew spies at Jericho.
G4477.strongs: from the same as G4460 (Ῥαάβ); Rachab, a Canaanitess

G2216.thayer: Zerubbabel, son of Shealtiel (Salathiel) and father of Abiud and Rhesa.
G2216.mounce: Zorobabel, pr. name, indecl. (Ezra 2:2; 3:8), Mt. 1:12, 13; Lk. 3:27*
G2216.abbottSmith: Zerubbabel ( I Ch 3:19 , al.): Mt 1:12 , 13 , Lk 3:27 .†
G2216.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02216); Zorobabel (i.e. Zerubbabel), an Israelite

G4810.mounce: a fig, a ripe fig, Mt. 7:16; Mk. 11:13; Lk. 6:44; Jas. 3:12*
G4810.strongs: apparently a primary word; a fig

G51.thayer: an offence committed through ignorance, an error due to (willful or culpable) ignorance.
G51.abbottSmith: a sin of ignorance (so in π. ; v. MM, VGT , s.v.): He 9:7 .†
G51.strongs: from G50 (ἀγνοέω); a thing ignored, i.e. shortcoming

G371.mounce: unworthily, in an improper manner, 1 Cor. 11:27*
G371.abbottSmith: in an unworthy manner : I Co 11:27 .†

G1640.mounce: ἐλάττων (G1784) is the Attic form of this word. Twice it is used with σσ (Jn. 2:10; Rom. 9:12) and twice with ττ (1 Tim. 5:9; Heb. 7:7). It is used as the comparative of μικρός, less; less in age, younger, Rom. 9:12; less in dignity, inferior, Heb. 7:7; less in quality, inferior, worse, Jn. 2:10; 1 Tim. 5:9*
G1640.abbottSmith: less , in age, rank or quality: Jo 2:19 , Ro 9:12 (LXX) , He 7:7 ; neut., -ον , adverbially: I Ti 5:9 .†
G1640.strongs: comparative of the same as G1646 (ἐλάχιστος); smaller (in size, quantity, age or quality)

G852.mounce: out of sight; not manifest, hidden, concealed, Heb. 4:13*
G852.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5316 (φαίνω); non-apparent)

G4426.mounce: to scare, terrify; pass. to be terrified, be in consternation, Phil. 1:28*
G4426.abbottSmith: to be startled , frightened : Phl 1:28 . (The active πτύρω is also found in some late writers.)†
G4426.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G4429 (πτύω) (and thus akin to G4422 (πτοέω)); to frighten

G239.mounce: (Hebrew) hallelujah, praise Yahweh or the Lord, Rev. 19:1, 3, 4, 6*
G239.strongs: of Hebrew origin (imperative of H01984 and H03050); praise ye Jah!, an adoring exclamation

G5161.thayer: Trophimus, a Christian of Ephesus in Asia.
G5161.mounce: Trophimus, pr. name, of Ephesus, a friend of Paul, Acts 20:4; 21:29; 2 Tim. 4:20*
G5161.strongs: from G5160 (τροφή); nutritive; Trophimus, a Christian

G3543.thayer: I practice, hold by custom; I deem, think, consider, suppose.
G3543.mounce: to own as settled and established; to deem, 1 Cor. 7:26; 1 Tim. 6:5; to suppose, presume, Mt. 5:17; 20:10; Lk. 2:44; to be usual, customary, Acts 16:13
G3543.abbottSmith: to practise , hold by custom : Ac 16:13 (Rec., but v. infr.).
G3543.strongs: from G3551 (νόμος); properly, to do by law (usage), i.e. to accustom (passively, be usual); by extension, to deem or regard

G3307.thayer: I divide into parts, divide, part, share, distribute; mid: I share, take part in a partitioning; I distract.
G3307.mounce: to divide; to divide out, distribute, Mk. 6:41; to assign, bestow, Rom. 12:3; 1 Cor. 7:17; 2 Cor. 10:13; Heb. 7:2; mid. to share, Lk. 12:13; pass. to be subdivided, to admit distinctions, Mk. 3:24-26; 1 Cor. 1:13; to be severed by discord, be at variance, Mt. 12:25, 26; to differ, 1 Cor. 7:34*
G3307.abbottSmith: to divide ; to divide into parts: metaph., pass., I Co 7:34 (WH, R, mg.), 34 (Rec., R, txt.) (on reading and punctuation, v. ICC , in l.); μεμέρισται ὁ Χριστός , I Co 1:13 ; as in late authors, of factional division (cf. Polyb., viii, 23, 9), καθ’ ἑαυτοῦ , Mt 12:25 ; ἐφ’ εαυτόν , Mt 12:26 , Mk 3:24-26 ; to distribute : c. acc. rei et dat. pers., Mk 6:41 ; as in later usage (cf. Polyb., xi, 28, 9),
G3307.strongs: from G3313 (μέρος); to part, i.e. (literally) to apportion, bestow, share, or (figuratively) to disunite, differ
G3307.kjv: deal, be difference between, distribute, divide, give participle

G3883.abbottSmith: in various senses (LS, s.v.), change : Ja 1:17 .†
G3883.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); transmutation (of phase or orbit), i.e. (figuratively) fickleness

G5270.mounce: can function as an improper prep., under, beneath, underneath, Mk. 6:11; 7:28; met. Heb. 2:8
G5270.abbottSmith: below, under : as prep. c. gen., Mt 22:44 , Mk 6:11 7:28 12:36 (Rec., R, txt., ὑποπόδιον , as in LXX), Lk 8:16 , Jo 1:51 , He 2:8 (LXX) , Re 5:3 , 13 6:9 12:1 .†
G5270.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2736 (κάτω); down under, i.e. beneath

G1352.mounce: inferential conj., on which account, wherefore, therefore, Mt. 27:8; 1 Cor. 12:3
G1352.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3739 (ὅς); through which thing, i.e. consequently

G1055.mounce: tranquillity of the sea, a calm, Mt. 8:26; Mk. 4:39; Lk. 8:24*

G2218.thayer: a yoke; hence met: (a Jewish idea) of a heavy burden, comparable to the heavy yokes resting on the bullocks' necks; a balance, pair of scales.
G2218.mounce: also spelled ζυγόν, ου, τό (n-2c), pr. a cross bar or band; a yoke; met. a yoke of servile condition, 1 Tim. 6:1; a yoke of service or obligation, Mt. 11:29, 30; Acts 15:10; Gal. 5:1; the beam of a balance; a balance, Rev. 6:5*
G2218.abbottSmith: a yoke ; metaph., of bondage or submission to authority: Mt 11:29 , 30 , Ac 15:10 , Ga 5:1 , I Ti 6:1 .
G2218.strongs: from the root of (to join, especially by a "yoke"); a coupling, i.e. (figuratively) servitude (a law or obligation); also (literally) the beam of the balance (as connecting the scales)

G1390.mounce: a gift, present, Mt. 7:11; Lk. 11:13; Eph. 4:8; Phil. 4:17*
G1390.abbottSmith: a gift : Mt 7:11 , Lk 11:13 , Eph 4:8 (LXX) , Phl 4:17 .†
G1390.strongs: from the base of G1325 (δίδωμι); a present

G1406.thayer: a drachma, a Greek silver coin.
G1406.mounce: a drachma, an Attic silver coin of nearly the same value as the Roman denarius, Lk. 15:8, 9*
G1406.abbottSmith: a drachma , nearly equal to the Roman denarius (v.s. δηνόριον ): Lk 15:8 , 9 ( DCG , ii, 200).†
G1406.strongs: from G1405 (δράσσομαι); a drachma or (silver) coin (as handled)

G1353.mounce: to travel through a place, traverse, Lk. 8:1; Acts 17:1
G1353.abbottSmith: to travel through : c. acc., Ac 17:1
G1353.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3593 (ὁδεύω); to travel through

G2555.abbottSmith: doing evil ; as susbt., an evil-doer : I Pe 2:12 , 14 4:15 .†
G2555.strongs: from G2556 (κακός) and G4160 (ποιέω); a bad-doer; (specially), a criminal

G3890.thayer: comfort, consolation, an exhortation, persuasion, encouragement.
G3890.abbottSmith: an exhortation , persuasion , encouragement : Phl 2:1 (cf. Plat., Legg. , vi, 773 E, al.; v. Lft. and ICC , Phl 2:1 ).

G2563.thayer: a reed; a reed-pen, reed-staff, measuring rod.
G2563.mounce: a reed, cane, Mt. 11:7; 12:20; Lk. 7:24; a reed in its various appliances, as, a wand, a staff, Mt. 27:29, 30, 48; Mk. 15:19, 36; a measuring-rod, Rev. 11:1; 21:15f.; a writer’s reed, 3 Jn. 13*
G2563.abbottSmith: a reed : Mt 11:7 12:20 (LXX) , Lk 7:24 ; hence, acc. to its various uses, a reed-pipe , flute ; a reed-staff , staff (cf. IV Ki 18:21 , ῥάβδος καλαμίνη ): Mt 27:29 , 30 , 48 , Mk 15:19 , 36 ; a measuring reed or rod ( Ez 40:3 ff.): Re 11:1 21:15 , 16 ; a writing reed , a pen : III Jo 13 (cf. Milligan, NTD , 9, 7).†
G2563.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a reed (the plant or its stem, or that of a similar plant); by implication, a pen

G4834.thayer: I sympathize with, have compassion on.
G4834.mounce: to sympathize with, Heb. 4:15; to be compassionate, Heb. 10:34*
G4834.abbottSmith: to have a fellow feeling for or with, sympathize with or in : c. dat. rei, He 4:15 ; dat. pers., ib. 10:34 (Arist., Plut., al.).†
G4834.strongs: from G4835 (συμπαθής); to feel "sympathy" with, i.e. (by implication) to commiserate
G4834.kjv: have compassion, be touched with a feeling of

G1388.mounce: pr. a bait or contrivance for entrapping, fraud, deceit, cunning, guile, Mt. 26:4; Mk. 7:22; 14:1
G1388.strongs: from an obsolete primary verb, (probably meaning to decoy; compare G1185 (δελεάζω)); a trick (bait), i.e. (figuratively) wile

G3969.strongs: perhaps a contraction for (a compound of G3962 (πατήρ) and G979 (βίος)); father's life; Patrobas, a Christian

G3962.abbottSmith: a father ; prop., of the male parent: Mt 2:22 , Lk 1:17 , Jo 4:53 ; anarth., He 12:7 (M, Pr. , 82 f.); pl., of both parents (cl.), He 11:23 ; οἱ π. τ. σαρκός , He 12:9 ; of a forefather or ancestor (in cl. usually in pl.; Hom., al.): Mt 3:9 , Lk 1:73 , Jo 8:39 , al.; pl., Mt 23:30 , 32 , Lk 6:23 , 26 , Jo 4:20 , I Co 10:1 , al. Metaph., of an author, originator, or archetype (= αἴτιος , ἀρχηγός ,
G3962.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "father" (literally or figuratively, near or more remote)

G2914.mounce: Crete, a large island in the eastern part of the Mediterranean, Acts 27:7, 12f., 21, Tit. 1:5*
G2914.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Crete, an island in the Mediterranean

G694.thayer: silver, a piece of silver, a shekel, money in general.
G694.abbottSmith: prop., a piece of silver (Lft., Notes , 191); in NT, silver : Ac 3:6 7:16 19:19 20:33 , I Co 3:12 , I Pe 1:18 ; money : Mt 25:18 , 27 , Mk 14:11 , Lk 9:3 19:15 , 23 22:5 , Ac 8:20 ; a silver coin : pl., Mt 26:15 27:3 , 5 , 6 , 9 28:12 , 15 , where the value is that of a shekel or tetradrachm; ἀργυρίου μυριάδες πέντε (prob. drachmas; MM, s.v.), Ac 19:19 .†
G694.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G696 (ἄργυρος); silvery, i.e. (by implication) cash; specially, a silverling (i.e. drachma or shekel)

G1254.abbottSmith: to hinder, prevent : c. acc, Mt 3:14 .†
G1254.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2967 (κωλύω); to hinder altogether, i.e. utterly prohibit

G4150.abbottSmith: to wash (inanimate objects, esp. clothing): τ. δίκτυα , Lk 5:2 . Fig., τ. στολάς , Re 7:14 22:14 (cf. Ps 50 (51):4 , 9 and v.s. ἀπο-πλύνω ).†
G4150.strongs: a prolonged form of an obsolete (to "flow"); to "plunge", i.e. launder clothing

G259.strongs: from a collateral form of G138 (αἱρέομαι); capture, be taken.

G4768.thayer: I am gloomy, have a somber countenance, am shocked.
G4768.mounce: to put on a gloomy and downcast look, to be shocked, appalled, Mk. 10:22; of the sky, to lower, Mt. 16:3*
G4768.abbottSmith: to have a sombre, gloomy appearance : of the human countenance (RV, his countenance fell ), Mk 10:22 ; of the sky (so στυγνότης , Polyb., iv, 21, 1), Mt 16:3 (|[WH]|, R, mg., om.).†
G4768.strongs: from the same as G4767 (στυγνητός); to render gloomy, i.e. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech)

G4618.strongs: from a derivative of G4621 (σῖτος); grain-fed, i.e. fattened

G2340.thayer: I hunt, seek to catch or entrap; met: I lay hold of.
G2340.mounce: to hunt, catch; met. to seize on, lay hold of, Lk. 11:54*
G2340.abbottSmith: to hunt , ensnare , catch : metaph., Lk 11:54 .†
G2340.strongs: from G2339 (θήρα); to hunt (an animal), i.e. (figuratively) to carp at

G2128.thayer: (used only of God), blessed (as entitled to receive blessing from man), worthy of praise.
G2128.mounce: worthy of praise or blessing, blessed, Mk. 14:61; Lk. 1:68

G3539.thayer: I understand, think, consider, conceive, apprehend; aor. possibly: realize.
G3539.mounce: to perceive, observe; to mark attentively, Mt. 24:15; Mk. 13:14; 2 Tim. 2:7; to understand, comprehend, Mt. 15:17; to conceive, Eph. 3:20
G3539.abbottSmith: to perceive with the mind, understand (for the phrase νοῶν κ. φρονῶν , in wills, v. MM, xvii): absol., Mt 16:9 , Mk 8:17 ; c. acc., Eph 3:4 , I Ti 1:7 ; c. dat. instr., τ. καρδίᾳ , Jo 12:40 ; pass., Ro 1:20 ; seq. ὅτι , Mt 15:17 16:11 , Mk 7:18 ; c. acc. et inf., He 11:8 .
G3539.strongs: from G3563 (νοῦς); to exercise the mind (observe), i.e. (figuratively) to comprehend, heed

G681.abbottSmith: prop., to fasten to ; hence, of fire, to kindle , light : Lk 8:16 11:33 15:8 , Ac 28:2 . Mid., c. gen., to fasten oneself to , cling to , lay hold of (so in π. ; MM, s.v.): Mt 8:3 , 15 , Jo 20:17 , al.; of carnal intercourse, I Co 7:1 ; with reference to levitical and ceremonial prohibitions, II Co 6:17 , Col 2:21 ; of hostile action, I Jo 5:18 (cf. ἀν- , καθ- , περι- άπτω ).
G681.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to fasten to, i.e. (specially) to set on fire

G5450.mounce: pr. inflation; met. inflation of mind, pride, 2 Cor. 12:20*
G5450.abbottSmith: a puffing up, swelling with pride: pl., II Co 12:20 .†

G2171.thayer: a prayer comprising a vow; a prayer, vow.
G2171.mounce: a wish, prayer, Jas. 5:15; a vow, Acts 21:23; Acts 18:18*
G2171.strongs: from G2172 (εὔχομαι); properly, a wish, expressed as a petition to God, or in votive obligation

G4605.thayer: Sidon, a great coast city of Phoenicia.
G4605.mounce: Sidon, a celebrated city of Phoenicia, Mt. 11:21f.; Mk. 3:8; 7:31; Lk. 6:17; Acts 27:3. See PhotoGuide.
G4605.abbottSmith: Sidon , a maritime city of Phœnicia: Mt 11:21, 22 15:21 , Mk 3:8 7:24 , 31 , Lk 6:17 10:13, 14 , Ac 27:3 .†
G4605.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06721); Sidon (i.e. Tsidon), a place in Palestine

G4846.thayer: I choke utterly, as weeds do plants; I crowd upon.
G4846.mounce: to throttle, choke; trop. to choke the growth or increase of seed or plants, Mt. 13:22; Mk. 4:7, 19; Lk. 8:14; to press upon, crowd, Lk. 8:42*
G4846.abbottSmith: to choke : of thronging by a crowd, Lk 8:42 . Fig., of seed, τ. λόγον , Mt 13:22 , Mk 4:7 , 19 , Lk 8:14 .†
G4846.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4155 (πνίγω); to strangle completely, i.e. (literally) to drown, or (figuratively) to crowd

G1850.thayer: I exercise (wield) power (authority), pass: I am ruled, am held under authority.
G1850.mounce: to have or exercise power or authority over anyone, Lk. 22:25; to possess independent control over, 1 Cor. 7:4 (2x); pass. to be subject to, under the power or influence of, 1 Cor. 6:12*
G1850.kjv: exercise authority upon, bring under the (have) power of

G4655.mounce: darkness, Mt. 27:45; Acts 2:20; gloom of punishment and misery, Mt. 8:12; 2 Pet. 2:17; met. moral or spiritual darkness, Mt. 4:16; Jn. 3:19; Eph. 5:11; a realm of moral darkness, Eph. 5:8; 6:12
G4655.strongs: from the base of G4639 (σκιά); shadiness, i.e. obscurity (literally or figuratively)

G2111.mounce: pr. well arranged, rightly disposed; fit, proper, adapted, Lk. 9:62; 14:35; useful, Heb. 6:7*
G2111.abbottSmith: ready for use, fit : of things, c. dat., He 6:7 ; seq. εἰς , Lk 14:35 ; of persons, c. dat., Lk 9:62 (for rabbinic parallels, v. Dalman, Words , 119 f.).†
G2111.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); well placed, i.e. (figuratively) appropriate

G4304.thayer: I meditate beforehand, prepare, get up, premeditate.
G4304.mounce: to practise beforehand; to premeditate, Lk. 21:14*
G4304.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3191 (μελετάω); to premeditate

G5523.mounce: also spelled Χωραζίν and Χοραζείν, Chorazin, a town of Galilee, probably near Bethsaida and Capernaum, indecl., Mt. 11:21; Lk. 10:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G5523.abbottSmith: Chorazin , a town of Galilee: Mt 11:21 , Lk 10:13 .†
G5523.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Chorazin, a place in Palestine

G1576.mounce: to shut out, exclude; to shut of, separate, insulate; Gal. 4:17; to leave no place for, eliminate, Rom. 3:27*
G1576.abbottSmith: to shut out : Ga 4:17 . Pass., Ro 3:27 .†
G1576.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2808 (κλείω); to shut out (literally or figuratively)

G2960.thayer: of the Lord, special to the Lord.
G2960.mounce: pertaining to the Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord’s, 1 Cor. 11:20; Rev. 1:10*
G2960.abbottSmith: as freq. in Inscr. (LS, s.v.; Deiss., BS , 217 f.), of the lord or master , imperial .
G2960.strongs: from G2962 (κύριος); belonging to the Lord (Jehovah or Jesus)

G1061.strongs: from G1062 (γάμος); to espouse (a daughter to a husband)

G3329.thayer: (usually: transfer, transport, and met: to a better mind), I turn about, change the position of; pass: I am brought back.
G3329.mounce: to lead or move from one place to another; to change direction, turn about, Jas. 3:3, 4*
G3329.abbottSmith: in Xen., Plut., and later writers, to transfer , transport (so LXX).
G3329.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G718 (ἁρμόζω); to lead over, i.e. transfer (direct)

G2005.mounce: to bring to an end; to finish, complete, perfect, Rom. 15:28; 2 Cor. 8:6, 11; to perform, Lk. 13:32; to carry into practice, to realize, 2 Cor. 7:1; to discharge, Heb. 9:6; to execute, Heb. 8:5; to carry out to completion, Phil. 1:6; mid. to end, make an end, Gal. 3:3; pass. to be fully undergone, endured, 1 Pet. 5:9
G2005.abbottSmith: to complete, accomplish, execute : c. acc rei, Ro 15:28 , II Co 7:1 8:6 , 11 , Phl 1:6 , He 8:5 ; of religious services (cf. Hdt., ii, 37, al.), He 9:6 ; art. inf., II Co 8:11 . Mid.,
G2005.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5055 (τελέω); to fulfill further (or completely), i.e. execute; by implication, to terminate, undergo

G991.thayer: (primarily physical), I look, see, perceive, discern.
G991.mounce: to have the faculty of sight, to see, Mt. 12:22; to exercise sight, to see, Mt. 6:4; to look towards or at, Mt. 22:16; to face, Acts 27:12; to take heed, Mt. 24:4; in NT, βλέπειν ἀπό, to beware of, shun, Mk. 8:15; trans., to cast a look on, Mt. 5:28; to see, behold, Mt. 13:17; to observe, Mt. 7:3; to have an eye to, see to, Mk. 13:9; Col. 4:17; 2 Jn. 8; to discern mentally, perceive, Rom. 7:23; 2 Cor. 7:8; Jas. 2:22; to guard against, Phil. 3:2; pass., to be an object of sight, be visible, Rom. 8:24
G991.abbottSmith: of bodily sight; to see, have sight (opp. to τυφλὸς εἶναι ): Mt 12:22 , Jo 9:7 , Ac 9:9 , Ro 11:8 , Re 3:18 , al.; to perceive, look (at), see : absol., Ac 1:9 ; c. acc., Mt 7:3 , Mk 5:31 , Lk 6:41 , Jo 1:29 , al.; ὅραμα , Ac 12:9 ; γυναῖκα , Mt 5:28 ; βιβλίον , Re 5:3 , 4 ; τ. βλεπόμενα , II Co 4:18 . Metaph., of mental vision; to see, perceive, discern : absol., Mt 13:13 , Lk 8:10 ; δι’ ἐσόπτρου
G991.strongs: a primary verb; to look at (literally or figuratively)
G991.kjv: behold, beware, lie, look (on, to), perceive, regard, see, sight, take heed

G4118.thayer: the greatest, the most, very great.
G4118.mounce: great in magnitude or quantity, much, large, Mt. 13:5; Jn. 3:23; 15:8; pl. many, Mt. 3:7; in time, long, Mt. 25:19; Mk. 6:35; Jn. 5:6; οἱ πολλοί, the many, the mass, Rom. 5:15; 12:5; 1 Cor. 10:33; τὸ πολύ, much, 2 Cor. 8:15; πολύ, as an adv., much, greatly, Mk. 12:27; Lk. 7:47; of time, ἐπὶ πολύ, a long time, Acts 28:6; μετ’ οὐ πολὺ, not long after, Acts 27:14; followed by a compar., much, 2 Cor. 8:22; πολλῷ, much, by much, Mt. 6:30; Mk. 10:48; τὰ πολλά, as an adv., most frequently, generally, Rom. 15:22; πολλά, as an adv., much, greatly, vehemently, Mk. 1:45; 3:12; of time, many times, frequently, often, Mt. 9:14
G4118.strongs: irregular superlative of G4183 (πολύς); the largest number or very large

G5445.thayer: a mass or lump, as of bread dough.
G5445.mounce: that which is mingled and reduced to a uniform consistence, by kneading, beating, treading, etc.; a mass of potter’s clay, Rom. 9:21; of dough, 1 Cor. 5:6, 7; Gal. 5:9; met. Rom. 11:16*
G5445.abbottSmith: that which is mixed or kneaded, a lump : of dough, Ro 11:16 , I Co 5:6 , 7 , Ga 5:9 ; of clay, Ro 9:21 .†
G5445.strongs: from a prolonged form of (to mix a liquid with a solid; perhaps akin to G5453 (φύω) through the idea of swelling in bulk), mean to knead; a mass of dough

G2964.thayer: I ratify, confirm, make valid, reaffirm, assure.
G2964.mounce: to confirm, ratify, Gal. 3:15; to reaffirm, assure, 2 Cor. 2:8*
G2964.abbottSmith: to confirm , ratify , make valid : II Co 2:8 ; pass., Ga 3:15 (cf. προ-κυρόω ).†
G2964.strongs: from the same as G2962 (κύριος); to make authoritative, i.e. ratify

G5002.mounce: pr. arranged; fixed, appointed, set, Acts 12:21*
G5002.strongs: from G5021 (τάσσω); arranged, i.e. appointed or stated

G2343.thayer: I store up, treasure up, save, lay up.
G2343.mounce: to collect and lay up stores or wealth, treasure, Mt. 6:19, 20; Lk. 12:21; 2 Cor. 12:14; Jas. 5:3; to heap up, accumulate, Rom. 2:5; 1 Cor. 16:2; to reserve, keep in store, 2 Pet. 3:7
G2343.abbottSmith: to lay up , store up : of riches, Ja 5:3 ; id. c. dat. pers., Lk 12:21 , II Co 12:14 ; c. acc. rei, I Co 16:2 ; θησαυροὺς ἑαυτῷ , Mt 6:19 , 20 ; pass., II Pe 3:7 ; metaph., ὀργὴν ἑαντῷ , Ro 2:5 (cf. Pr 1:18 , Pss. Sol 9:9 ).†
G2343.strongs: from G2344 (θησαυρός); to amass or reserve (literally or figuratively)
G2343.kjv: lay up (treasure), (keep) in store, (heap) treasure (together, up)

G2162.mounce: pr. use of words of good omen; hence, favorable expression, praise, commendation, 2 Cor. 6:8*
G2162.abbottSmith: good report, praise : opp. to δυσφημία , II Co 6:8 .†
G2162.strongs: from G2163 (εὔφημος); good language ("euphemy"), i.e. praise (repute)

G4529.thayer: Salamis, a city at the eastern end of Cyprus.
G4529.mounce: Saleim, also formed as Σαλίμ (G4890). John was baptizing at Aenon near Saleim, Jn. 3:26*. See PhotoGuide.
G4529.abbottSmith: Salamis , the chief city of Cyprus: Ac 13:5 .†
G4529.strongs: probably from G4535 (σάλος) (from the surge on the shore); Salamis, a place in Cyprus

G4725.abbottSmith: a wreath, garland : as used in sacrifices, Ac 14:13 .†
G4725.strongs: from the base of G4735 (στέφανος); a wreath for show

G4199.thayer: I lay waste, destroy, ravage, harass.
G4199.mounce: to lay waste, destroy; impl. to harass, ravage, Acts 9:21; Gal. 1:13, 23*

G2496.thayer: (Hebrew), Joram, Jehoram, son of Jehoshaphat and father of Uzziah.
G2496.mounce: Joram, king of Judah (2 Ki. 8:16ff.), pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:8*
G2496.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03141); Joram, an Israelite

G1330.thayer: I pass through, spread (as a report).
G1330.mounce: to pass through, Mk. 10:25; Lk. 4:30; to pass over, cross, Mk. 4:35; Lk. 8:22; to pass along, Lk. 19:4; to proceed, Lk. 2:15; Acts 9:38; to travel through or over a country, wander about, Mt. 12:43; Lk. 9:6; to transfix, pierce, Lk. 2:35; to spread abroad, be prevalent, as a rumor, Lk. 5:15; met. to extend to, Rom. 5:12
G1330.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to traverse (literally)
G1330.kjv: come, depart, go (about, abroad, everywhere, over, through, throughout), pass (by, over, through, throughout), pierce through, travel, walk through

G381.thayer: I kindle, set on fire, light.
G381.mounce: to light, kindle, set on fire, Lk. 12:49; Jas. 3:5*
G381.abbottSmith: to kindle : Lk 12:49 , Ja 3:5 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G381.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G681 (ἅπτω); to enkindle

G5225.thayer: I begin, am, exist, am in possession.
G5225.mounce: to begin; to come into existence; to exist; to be, subsist, Acts 19:40; 28:18; to be in possession, to belong, Acts 3:6; 4:37; goods, possessions, property, Mt. 19:21; Lk. 8:3; to be, Lk. 7:25; 8:41
G5225.abbottSmith: to begin, make a beginning (Hom., Hdt., al.).
G5225.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G756 (ἄρχομαι); to begin under (quietly), i.e. come into existence (be present or at hand); expletively, to exist (as copula or subordinate to an adjective, participle, adverb or preposition, or as an auxiliary to a principal (verb)

G2099.thayer: an evangelist, a missionary, bearer of good tidings.
G2099.mounce: pr. one who announces glad tidings; an evangelist, preacher of the Gospel, teacher of the Christian religion, Acts 21:8; Eph. 4:11; 2 Tim. 4:5*
G2099.strongs: from G2097 (εὐαγγελίζω); a preacher of the gospel

G3591.thayer: (properly: bulk, mass, hence) a weight, burden, encumbrance.
G3591.mounce: pr. bulk, weight; a burden, impediment, Heb. 12:1*
G3591.abbottSmith: bulk , mass ; metaph., an encumbrance : He 12:1 .†
G3591.strongs: probably from the same as G43 (ἀγκάλη); a mass (as bending or bulging by its load), i.e. burden (hindrance)

G730.mounce: male, of the male sex, Mt. 19:4; Mk. 10:6; Lk. 2:23; Rom. 1:27; Gal. 3:28; Rev. 12:5, 13*
G730.strongs: probably from G142 (αἴρω); male (as stronger for lifting)

G3398.mounce: little, small in size quantity, etc. Mt. 13:32; Lk. 12:32; Rev. 3:8; small, little in age, young, not adult, Mk. 15:40; little, short in time, Jn. 7:33; μικρόν, i.e. χρόνον, a little while, a short time, Jn. 13:33; μετὰ μικρόν, after a little while, a little while afterwards, Mt. 26:73; little in number, Lk. 12:32; small, little in dignity, low, humble, Mt. 10:42; 11:11; μικρόν, as an adv., little, a little, Mt. 26:39
G3398.abbottSmith: small , little ; of persons; lit., of stature: Mk 15:40 (MM, iii, xvi; on the view that age is meant, v. Deiss., BS , 144), Lk 19:3 ; οἱ μ. , the little ones , Mt 18:6 , 10 , 14 , Mk 9:42 ; hence metaph., of rank or influence (cf. Dalman, Words , 113 f.): Mt 10:42 , Lk 17:2 , Ac 8:10 26:22 , He 8:11 (LXX) , Re 11:18 13:16 19:5 , 18 20:12 ; compar., -ότερος , Mt 11:11 , Lk 7:28 9:48 . Of things; of
G3398.strongs: apparently a primary word; small (in size, quantity, number or (figuratively) dignity)

G3131.thayer: (Hebrew), manna, the supernatural food eaten by the Israelites in the desert: of spiritual food.
G3131.mounce: manna, the miraculous food of the Israelites while in the desert, Jn. 6:31, 49; Heb. 9:4; Rev. 2:17*
G3131.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04478); manna (i.e. man), an edible gum

G1739.thayer: the material of a building, a structure.
G1739.abbottSmith: a build­ing in : ἡ ἐ. τ. τείχους αὐτῆς ἴασπις , its wall had jasper built into it , Re 21:18 (v. MM, Exp. , xiii; Swete, Ap., l.c.).†
G1739.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of the base of G1218 (δῆμος); a housing in (residence), i.e. structure

G1286.mounce: pr. to shake thoroughly or violently; to harass, intimidate, extort from, Lk. 3:14*
G1286.abbottSmith: to shake violently ; metaph., to intimidate : Lk 3:14 .†
G1286.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4579 (σείω); to shake thoroughly, i.e. (figuratively) to intimidate

G2136.thayer: Euodia, or rather Euhodia, a Christian woman of Philippi.
G2136.abbottSmith: Euodia (not as AV, Euodias ), a Christian woman: Phl 4:2 .†
G2136.strongs: from the same as G2137 (εὐοδόω); fine travelling; Euodia, a Christian woman

G5099.mounce: to pay; to pay a penalty, incur punishment, 2 Thess. 1:9*
G5099.abbottSmith: to pay : τ. δίκην (cf. Pr, l.c.), to pay penalty , II Th 1:9 (v. M, Th. , in l.).†
G5099.strongs: strengthened for a primary (which is only used as an alternate in certain tenses); to pay a price, i.e. as a penalty

G4695.mounce: to spot, soil; to contaminate, defile, Jas. 3:6; Jude 23*
G4695.abbottSmith: to stain, spot, defile : c. acc., Ja 3:6 , Ju 23 .†
G4695.strongs: from G4696 (σπίλος); to stain or soil (literally or figuratively)

G4344.mounce: pr. a cushion for the head, pillow; also, a boat cushion, Mk. 4:38*
G4344.strongs: neuter of a presumed compound of G4314 (πρός) and G2776 (κεφαλή); something for the head, i.e. a cushion

G5462.mounce: illumination; a shining forth, bringing to light, enlightenment, 2 Cor. 4:4, 6*

G2461.mounce: equestrian; τὸ ἱππικόν, cavalry, horse, Rev. 9:16*
G2461.abbottSmith: of a horse or of horsemen , equestrian ; as subst., τὸ ἱ. (sc. στράτευμα ), cavalry : He 9:16 .†
G2461.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G2462 (ἵππος); the cavalry force

G565.thayer: I come or go away from, depart, return, arrive, go after, follow.
G565.mounce: to go away, depart, Mt. 8:18; to go forth, pervade, as a rumor, Mt. 4:24; to arrive at a destination, Lk. 23:33; to pass away, disappear, Rev. 21:4; ἀπέρχομαι ὀπίσω, to follow, Mk. 1:20
G565.abbottSmith: to go away , depart (also, in late writers, with "perfective" force, to arrive at a destination, the thought being carried on to the goal; M, Pr. , 111 f., 247; MM, s.v.); absol.: Mt 13:25 , al.; ptcp., ἀπελθών , used pleonastically with other verbs as in Heb. (Dalman, Words , 21), Mt 13:28 , al.; with mention of place or person: εἰς , Mt 14:15 ; ἐπί , Lk 24:24 ; πρός , Re 10:9 ; ἀπό , Lk 1:38 ; ἐ
G565.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to go off (i.e. depart), aside (i.e. apart) or behind (i.e. follow), literally or figuratively
G565.kjv: come, depart, go (aside, away, back, out, … ways), pass away, be past

G2109.thayer: I do good deeds, perform kind service, benefit.
G2109.mounce: to do good, exercise beneficence, Acts 10:38*
G2109.abbottSmith: to do good, bestow benefit : Ac 10:38 .†

G5208.mounce: wood, a forest; in NT firewood, a mass of fuel, Jas. 3:5*
G5208.strongs: perhaps akin to G3586 (ξύλον); a forest, i.e. (by implication) fuel

G1019.thayer: I am slow, I delay, tarry.
G1019.mounce: to be slow, to delay, 1 Tim. 3:15; 2 Pet. 3:9*
G1019.abbottSmith: trans., to retard (Soph., Is, l.c.). More freq. intrans., to be slow, to tarry : I Ti 3:15 , II Pe 3:9 .†

G1179.thayer: Decapolis, meaning a group or district of ten cities (of the Greek type) in Palestine, mostly south-east of the Lake of Tiberias; the names and number vary in ancient authorities.
G1179.mounce: Decapolis, a district of Palestine beyond Jordan, Mk. 5:20; 7:31; Mt. 4:25*
G1179.abbottSmith: Decapolis , a region east of the Jordan con­taining ten cities: Mt 4:25 , Mk 5:20 7:31 .†
G1179.strongs: from G1176 (δέκα) and G4172 (πόλις); the ten-city region; the Decapolis, a district in Syria

G2653.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) (intensive) and G332 (ἀναθεματίζω); to imprecate

G905.mounce: also spelled βαλάντιον, a bag, purse, Lk. 10:4; 12:33; 22:35, 36*
G905.strongs: probably remotely from G906 (βάλλω) (as a depository); a pouch (for money)

G3343.mounce: to send after; mid. to send after or for any one, invite to come to one’s self, Acts 10:5, 22, 29; 11:13; 20:1; 24:24, 26; 25:3*
G3343.abbottSmith: to send after or for : pass., Ac 10:29a . Chiefly in mid., to send for , summon Ac 10:5 , 22 , 29b 11:13 20:1 24:24 , 26 25:3 .†
G3343.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G3992 (πέμπω); to send from elsewhere, i.e. (middle voice) to summon or invite

G3178.mounce: strong drink; drunkenness, Lk. 21:34; an indulgence in drinking, Rom. 13:13; Gal. 5:21*
G3178.strongs: apparently a primary word; an intoxicant, i.e. (by implication) intoxication

G5396.thayer: I gossip against, talk idly, make empty charges against, talk nonsense.
G5396.mounce: to talk folly or nonsense; in NT trans., bring unjustified charges against, 3 Jn. 10*
G5396.abbottSmith: to talk nonsense : c. acc. pers., ἡμᾶς (EV, prating against us ), III Jo 10 .†
G5396.strongs: from G5397 (φλύαρος); to be a babbler or trifler, i.e. (by implication) to berate idly or mischievously

G3960.thayer: I smite, strike (as with a sword), smite to death, afflict.
G3960.mounce: to strike, beat upon; to smite, wound, Mt. 26:51; Lk. 22:49, 50; by impl. to kill, slay, Mt. 26:31; Mk. 14:27; Acts 7:24; to strike gently, Acts 12:7; from the Hebrew, to smite with disease, plagues, etc., Acts 12:23; Rev. 11:6; 19:15*
G3960.abbottSmith: in Hom., intrans., to beat (of the heart).
G3960.strongs: probably prolongation from G3817 (παίω); to knock (gently or with a weapon or fatally)

G2739.mounce: to scorch, burn, Mt. 13:6; Mk. 4:6; Rev. 16:8, 9*
G2739.abbottSmith: to burn or scorch up : c. acc. seq. ἐν πυρί , Re 16:8 . Pass., Mt 13:14 , Mk 4:6 ; seq. καῦμα μέγα , Re 16:9 .†

G2785.thayer: a sea monster, huge sea fish, whale.
G2785.mounce: a large fish, sea monster, whale, Mt. 12:40*
G2785.strongs: probably from the base of G5490 (χάσμα); a huge fish (as gaping for prey)

G5260.thayer: I throw or put under; met: I subject, submit, suggest, whisper, prompt, suborn, instigate.
G5260.mounce: to cast under; met. to suggest, instigate, Acts 6:11*
G5260.abbottSmith: to throw or put under . Metaph.,
G5260.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw in stealthily, i.e. introduce by collusion

G763.mounce: impiety, ungodliness; dishonesty, wickedness, Rom. 1:18; 11:26; 2 Tim. 2:16; Tit. 2:12; Jude 15, 18*
G763.abbottSmith: ungodliness , impiety : Ro 1:18 11:26 , II Ti 2:16 , Tit 2:12 ; ἔργα ἀσεβείας , ungodly deeds , Ju 15 ; ἐπιθυηίαι τ. ἀσεβειῶν , desires for ungodly things or deeds , Ju 18 ( DB , iv, 532; Cremer, 523; MM, s.v.).†
G763.strongs: from G765 (ἀσεβής); impiety, i.e. (by implication) wickedness

G5289.strongs: from G5288 (ὑποστέλλω); shrinkage (timidity), i.e. (by implication) apostasy

G798.mounce: a constellation; a star, Lk. 21:25; Acts 7:43; 27:20; Heb. 11:12*
G798.abbottSmith: mostly in pl. (as in cl.), the stars : Lk 21:25 , Ac 27:20 , He 11:12 ;
G798.strongs: neuter from G792 (ἀστήρ); properly, a constellation; put for a single star (natural or artificial)

G2871.mounce: a stroke, smiting; in NT slaughter, Heb. 7:1*
G2871.abbottSmith: in cl., a stroke , a pounding (as in a mortar).

G295.thayer: Amphipolis, a leading city of Macedonia.
G295.mounce: Amphipolis, a city of Thrace, on the river Strymon, Acts 17:1*. See PhotoGuide.
G295.abbottSmith: Amphipolis , in Macedonia, so called because the river Strymon flowed around it: Ac 17:1 .†
G295.strongs: from the base of G297 (ἀμφότερος) and G4172 (πόλις); a city surrounded by a river; Amphipolis, a place in Macedonia

G5418.mounce: a fence, hedge; a hedgeside path, Mt. 21:33; Mk. 12:1; Lk. 14:23; met. a parting fence, Eph. 2:14*
G5418.strongs: from G5420 (φράσσω); a fence, or inclosing barrier (literally or figuratively)

G840.thayer: grim, severe, strict, exacting, harsh, rigid.
G840.mounce: pr. harsh, sour in flavor; met. harsh, rigid, ungenerous, Lk. 19:21, 22*
G840.abbottSmith: prop., stringent , harsh to the taste. Metaph., in Inscr., of a rough country; of disposition and manners, strict , severe (as in π. , of an inspector; MM, s v): Lk 19:21 , 22 .†
G840.strongs: from a (presumed) derivative of the same as G109 (ἀήρ) (meaning blown); rough (properly as a gale), i.e. (figuratively) severe

G4454.mounce: a youngling; a foal or colt, Mt. 21:2, 5, 7; Mk. 11:2
G4454.abbottSmith: a foal , colt , prop., of a horse, then the young of other animals; in NT of the colt of an ass: Mt 21:2 , 5 (LXX) , 7 , Mk 11:2 , 4 , 5 , 7 , Lk 19:30 , 33 , 35 , Jo 12:15 (LXX) .†
G4454.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "foal" or "filly", i.e. (specially), a young ass

G2145.mounce: constantly attending; devoted to; τὸ εὐπάρεδρον, devotedness, 1 Cor. 7:35*
G2145.abbottSmith: Rec. for εὐπάρεδρος , q.v.: I Co 7:35 .†
G2145.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and the same as G4332 (προσεδρεύω); sitting well towards, i.e. (figuratively) assiduous (neuter, diligent service)

G863.thayer: (a) I send away, (b) I let go, release, permit to depart, (c) I remit, forgive, (d) I permit, suffer.
G863.mounce: to send away, dismiss, suffer to depart; to emit, send forth; τὴν φωνήν, the voice, to cry out, utter an exclamation, Mk. 15:37; τὸ πνεῦμα, the spirit, to expire, Mt. 27:50; to omit, pass over or by; to let alone, care not for, Mt. 15:14; 23:23; Heb. 6:1; to permit, suffer, let, forbid not; to give up, yield, resign, Mt. 5:40; to remit, forgive, pardon; to relax, suffer to become less intense, Rev. 2:4; to leave, depart from; to desert, forsake; to leave remaining or alone; to leave behind, i.e. at one’s death, Mk. 12:19, 20, 21, 22; Jn. 14:27
G863.abbottSmith: to send forth , send away , let go : of divorce ( DB , iii, 274 a ), τ. γυναῖκα (Hdt.), I Co 7:11-13 ; of death, τ. πνεῦμα ( Ge 35:18 , Hdt., al.), Mt 27:50 ; ( φωνήν , to utter a cry , Mk 15:37 ; of debts, to remit , forgive (cl.), τ. δανεῖον , Mt 18:27 ; τ. ὀφειλήν , Mt 18:32 ; esp. of sins (Cremer 296 f.), τ. ἁμαρτίας , ἁμαρτήματα , ἀνομίας , Mt 9:2 , Ro 4:7 (LXX) , I Jo 1:9 , al.; punctiliar a
G863.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to send; an intensive form of , to go); to send forth, in various applications (as follow)
G863.kjv: cry, forgive, forsake, lay aside, leave, let (alone, be, go, have), omit, put (send) away, remit, suffer, yield up

G3065.thayer: Lucas, Luke, Christian physician and writer of the Third Gospel and Acts.
G3065.abbottSmith: Luke : Lk, tit. , Col 4:14 , II Ti 4:11 , Phm 24 .†
G3065.strongs: contracted from Latin Lucanus; Lucas, a Christian

G2975.thayer: (a) I obtain (receive) by lot, my lot (turn) is, (b) I draw lots.
G2975.mounce: to have assigned to one, to obtain, receive, Acts 1:17; 2 Pet. 1:1; to have fall to one by lot, Lk. 1:9; absol. to cast lots, Jn. 19:24*
G2975.abbottSmith: to obtain by lot , to obtain (in cl., c. gen.): c. inf. art. (B1., § 36, 3; 71, 3), Lk 1:9 ; c. acc. rei, Ac 1:17 , II Pe 1:1 .
G2975.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb, which is only used as an alternate in certain tenses; to lot, i.e. determine (by implication, receive) especially by lot
G2975.kjv: his lot be, cast lots, obtain

G449.mounce: literally: unwashed; figuratively: ceremonially unclean Mt. 15:20; Mk. 7:2*
G449.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G3538 (νίπτω); without ablution

G2359.thayer: hair (of the head or of animals).
G2359.mounce: a hair; the hair of the head, Mt. 5:36; 10:30; of an animal, Mt. 3:4; Mk. 1:6
G2359.strongs: genitive case , etc.; of uncertain derivation; hair

G3725.thayer: the boundaries of a place, hence: districts, territory.
G3725.mounce: a limit, bound, border of a territory or country; pl. τὰ ὅρια, region, territory, district, Mt. 2:16; 4:13; 8:34
G3725.abbottSmith: a boundary , bound ; chiefly in pl., and so always in NT: Mt 2:16 4:13 8:34 15:22 , 39 19:1 , Mk 5:17 7:24 , 31 10:1 , Ac 13:50 .†
G3725.strongs: neuter of a derivative of an apparently primary (a bound or limit); a boundary-line, i.e. (by implication) a frontier (region)

G3406.mounce: a bestower of remuneration; recompenser, rewarder, Heb. 11:6*
G3406.abbottSmith: one who pays wages ; meton., a rewarder : He 11:6 .†
G3406.strongs: from G3409 (μισθόω) and G591 (ἀποδίδωμι); a renumerator

G2685.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) (intensive) and G4649 (σκοπός) (in the sense of a watcher); a reconnoiterer

G2477.mounce: to ascertain by inquiry and examination; to inquire of; in NT to visit in order to become acquainted with, Gal. 1:18*
G2477.abbottSmith: c. acc. rei, to inquire into , learn by inquiry .
G2477.strongs: from a derivative of G1492 (εἴδω); to be knowing (learned), i.e. (by implication) to visit for information (interview)

G1995.mounce: a turning towards, a turning about; in NT met. conversion, Acts 15:3*
G1995.abbottSmith: a turning about ; metaph., conversion (Field, Notes , 246): Ac 15:3 .†
G1995.strongs: from G1994 (ἐπιστρέφω); reversion, i.e. morally, revolution

G3084.thayer: I release on receipt of ransom; mid: I redeem, release by paying ransom, liberate.
G3084.mounce: to release for a ransom; mid, to ransom, redeem, deliver, liberate, Lk. 24:21; Tit. 2:14; 1 Pet. 1:18*
G3084.abbottSmith: to release on receipt of ransom ; mid., to release by paying ransom , to redeem : in spiritual sense, Tit 2:14 ; pass, I Pe 1:18 ; in general sense, to deliver (cf. Ex 6:6 , Ps 68 (69):18 , al.): Lk 24:21 .†
G3084.strongs: from G3083 (λύτρον); to ransom (literally or figuratively)

G2144.mounce: acceptable, grateful, pleasing, Rom. 15:16, 31; 2 Cor. 6:2; 8:12; 1 Pet. 2:5; in NT gracious*
G2144.abbottSmith: more usual than δέκτος , q.v., acceptable : Ro 15:16 , 31 , II Co 6:2 8:12 , I Pe 2:5 .†
G2144.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G4327 (προσδέχομαι); well-received, i.e. approved, favorable

G3711.mounce: prone to anger, irascible, passionate, Tit. 1:7*

G4490.thayer: expressly, explicitly, in so many words.
G4490.abbottSmith: in stated terms, expressly : I Ti 4:1 .†
G4490.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G4483 (ῥέω); out-spokenly, i.e. distinctly

G3030.mounce: arbor thurifera, the tree producing frankincense, growing in Arabia and Mount Lebanon; in NT frankincense, the transparent gum that distils from incisions in the tree, Mt. 2:11; Rev. 18:13*
G3030.strongs: of foreign origin (H03828); the incense-tree, i.e. (by implication) incense itself

G1117.mounce: the cargo of a ship, Acts 21:3; by impl. merchandise, Rev. 18:11, 12*
G1117.abbottSmith: a ship's freight, cargo : Ac 21:3 , Re 18:11 , 12 .†
G1117.strongs: from G1073 (γέμω); a load (as filling), i.e. (specially) a cargo, or (by extension) wares

G4676.mounce: a handkerchief, napkin, etc., Lk. 19:20; Jn. 11:44; 20:7; Acts 19:12*
