G4676.abbottSmith: handkerchief, kerchief : Lk 19:20 , Ac 19:12 ; used as a head covering for the dead, Jo 11:44 20:7 (cf. Deiss., BS , 223).†
G4676.strongs: of Latin origin; a sudarium (sweat-cloth), i.e. towel (for wiping the perspiration from the face, or binding the face of a corpse)

G1928.thayer: I make an additional testamentary disposition, I furnish with additions.
G1928.mounce: to enjoin anything additional, superadd an injunction, Gal. 3:15*
G1928.abbottSmith: to add provisions to a document: Ga 3:15 (cf. ἐπιδιαθήκη , a second will , F1J, BJ , ii, 2, 3; the word is used of wills in π. , cf. Deiss., LAE , 87).†
G1928.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1299 (διατάσσω); to appoint besides, i.e. supplement (as a codicil)

G5155.mounce: of hair, made of hair, Rev. 6:12*
G5155.abbottSmith: of hair: στόκος , Re 6:12 (Xen., Plat., al.).†
G5155.strongs: from G2359 (θρίξ); hairy, i.e. made of hair (mohair)

G552.abbottSmith: without experience of : c. gen. rei, He 5:13 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G552.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3984 (πεῖρα); inexperienced, i.e. ignorant

G4685.mounce: to draw, pull; to draw a sword, Mk. 14:47; Acts 16:27*
G4685.abbottSmith: in cl. poët. for ἕλκω ; mostly used in mid. (cf. M, Pr. , 157),

G1272.mounce: to open, Mk. 7:34, 35; Lk. 2:23; 24:31; Acts 7:56; met. to open the sense of a thing, explain, expound, Lk. 24:32; Acts 17:3; διανοίγειν τὸν νοῦν, τὴν καρδίαν, to open the mind, the heart, so as to understand and receive, Lk. 24:45; Acts 16:14*
G1272.abbottSmith: to open up completely, to open : Lk 2:23 (LXX) ; pass., Mk 7:34 , Ac 7:56 . Metaph., δ. τ. νοῦν , Lk 24:45 , τ. καρδίαν , Ac 16:14 , pass., οἱ ὀφθαλμοί , Lk 24:31 ; of explaining, τ. γραφάς , Lk 24:32 , Ac 17:3 .†
G1272.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G455 (ἀνοίγω); to open thoroughly, literally (as a first-born) or figuratively (to expound)

G5455.thayer: I give forth a sound, hence: (a) of a cock: I crow, (b) of men: I shout, (c) trans: I call (to myself), summon; I invite, address.
G5455.mounce: to sound, utter a sound; of the cock, to crow, Mt. 26:34, 74, 75; to call, or cry out, exclaim, Lk. 8:8, 54; 16:24; 23:46; to call to, Mt. 27:47; Mk. 3:31; to call, Jn. 13:13; to call, summon, Mt. 20:32; to invite to a feast, Lk. 14:12
G5455.strongs: from G5456 (φωνή); to emit a sound (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation

G4455.mounce: ever yet, ever, at any time, Lk. 19:30; Jn. 1:18
G4455.strongs: from G4452 (-πω) and G4218 (ποτέ); at any time, i.e. (with negative particle) at no time
G4455.kjv: at any time, + never (…to any man), + yet, never man

G4184.strongs: from G4183 (πολύς) and G4698 (σπλάγχνον) (figuratively); extremely compassionate

G4072.mounce: also spelled πετάομαι, to fly, Rev. 4:7; 8:13; 12:14; 14:6; 19:17*
G4072.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to fly

G4948.abbottSmith: a Syrian : Lk 4:27 , Mk 7:26 , WH, mg.†
G4948.strongs: from the same as G4947 (Συρία); a Syran (i.e. probably Tyrian), a native of Syria

G5579.thayer: a lie, falsehood, untruth; false religion.
G5579.mounce: falsehood, Jn. 8:44; Eph. 4:25; 2 Thess. 2:9, 11; 1 Jn. 2:21, 27; in NT religious falsehood, perversion of religious truth, false religion, Rom. 1:25; the practices of false religion, Rev. 14:15; 21:27; 22:15*
G5579.abbottSmith: a falsehood, untruth, lie : Re 14:15 ; opp. to ἡ ἀλήθεια , Jo 8:44 , Ro 1:25 , Eph 4:25 , II Th 2:11 , I Jo 2:21 ; to τὸ ἀληθές , ib. 27 ; ποιεῖν ψ. , Re 21:27 22:15 ; ἐν πάσῃ δυνάμει κ. σημείοις κ. τέρασιν ψεύδους (gen. qual., v. M, Th. , 104; and on the meaning and construction, v. also ICC , in l.), II Th 2:9 .†

G1672.thayer: a Hellene, the native word for a Greek; it is, however, a term wide enough to include all Greek-speaking (i.e. educated) non-Jews.
G1672.mounce: a Greek, Acts 18:17; Rom. 1:14; one not a Jew, a Gentile, Acts 14:1; 16:1, 3
G1672.abbottSmith: a Greek ; opp. to βάρβαρος , Ro 1:14 ; usually in ΝΤ of Greek Gentiles, opp. to Ἰουδαῖοι : Jo 7:35 , Ac 11:20 14:1 16:1 , 3 18:4 19:10 , 17 20:21 21:28 , Ro 1:16 2:9 , 10 3:9 10:12 , I Co 1:22 , 24 10:32 12:13 , Ga 2:3 3:28 , Col 3:11 ; of proselytes, Jo 12:20 , Ac 17:4 .†
G1672.strongs: from G1671 (Ἑλλάς); a Hellen (Grecian) or inhabitant of Hellas; by extension a Greek-speaking person, especially a non-Jew

G5446.thayer: natural, (a) according to nature, (b) merely animal.
G5446.mounce: natural, agreeable to nature, Rom. 1:26, 27; following the instinct of nature, as animals, 2 Pet. 2:12*

G2319.mounce: God-hated; in NT a hater and despiser of God, Rom. 1:30*
G2319.abbottSmith: passive, as freq. in cl., hateful to God : Ro 1:30 (R, txt., cf. Lit., Notes , 256).
G2319.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and the base of G4767 (στυγνητός); hateful to God, i.e. impious

G155.mounce: a thing asked or sought for; petition, request, Lk. 23:24; Phil. 4:6; 1 Jn. 5:15*
G155.abbottSmith: that which has been asked for, a petition , request : Lk 23:24 , Phl 4:6 , Jo 5:15 .†
G155.strongs: from G154 (αἰτέω); a thing asked or (abstractly) an asking

G2315.thayer: God-breathed, inspired by God, due to the inspiration of God.
G2315.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and a presumed derivative of G4154 (πνέω); divinely breathed in

G3401.mounce: to imitate, follow as an example, strive to resemble, 2 Thess. 3:7, 9; Heb. 13:7; 3 Jn. 11*
G3401.abbottSmith: to imitate : II Th 3:7 , 9 , He 13:7 , III Jo 11 .†

G1856.thayer: I drive out, expel, propel, thrust out; I drive out of the sea, drive on shore.
G1856.mounce: to expel, drive out, Acts 7:45; to propel, urge forward, Acts 27:39*
G1856.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and (to push); to expel; by implication, to propel

G434.abbottSmith: not tame , savage (MM, VGT , s.v.): II Ti 3:3 .†
G434.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and (lame); savage

G885.thayer: Achim, son of Zadok and father of Eliud.
G885.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H03137); Achim, an Israelite

G3531.thayer: a Nicolaitan, possibly a follower of Nicolaus (a heretic at Ephesus).
G3531.mounce: a Nicolaitan, or follower of Nicolaus, a heresy of the Apostolic age, Rev. 2:6, 15*
G3531.strongs: from G3532 (Νικόλαος); a Nicolaite, i.e. adherent of Nicolaus

G1454.mounce: pr. the act of waking or rising up; resurrection resuscitation, Mt. 27:53*
G1454.abbottSmith: a rousing (Plat.). a rising (Ps, l.c.): from death, Mt 27:53 .†

G1297.strongs: from G1299 (διατάσσω); an arrangement, i.e. (authoritative) edict

G1503.mounce: see εἴκω (1634a), dat., to be like, Jas. 1:6, 23*
G1503.abbottSmith: to be like : c. dat., Ja 1:6 , 23 .†
G1503.strongs: apparently a primary verb (perhaps akin to G1502 (εἴκω) through the idea of faintness as a copy); to resemble

G4256.mounce: pr. to charge beforehand; to convince beforehand, Rom. 3:9, since the charges in the case in question were drawn from Scripture.*
G4256.abbottSmith: to accuse or charge beforehand : Ro 3:9 (not elsewhere).†
G4256.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G156 (αἰτία); to accuse already, i.e. previously charge

G5086.thayer: Tiberius, the second Roman emperor (died A.D. 37).
G5086.mounce: Tiberius, the third Roman emperor, 14–37 A.D., Lk. 3:1*
G5086.strongs: of Latin origin; probably pertaining to the river Tiberis or Tiber; Tiberius, a Roman emperor

G762.mounce: unquenched; inextinguishable, unquenchable, Mt. 3:12; Mk. 9:43; Lk. 3:17*
G762.abbottSmith: unquenched , unquenchable : πῦρ , Mt 3:12 , Mk 9:43 , Lk 3:17 .†
G762.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4570 (σβέννυμι); not extinguished, i.e. (by implication) perpetual

G524.thayer: (lit: I cease to feel [my] pain), am past feeling, cease to care (suggesting sometimes despair, sometimes recklessness), become callous, reckless.
G524.mounce: pr. to desist from grief; hence, to become insensible or callous, Eph. 4:19*
G524.abbottSmith: prop., to cease to feel pain for (Thuc., ii, 61).
G524.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to smart); to grieve out, i.e. become apathetic

G1375.mounce: pr. chase, pursuit; persecution (specifically for religious reasons), Mt. 13:21; Mk. 4:17; 10:30
G1375.abbottSmith: persecution : Mt 13:21 , Mk 4:17 10:30 Ac 8:1 13:50 , Ro 8:35 , II Co 12:10 , II Th 1:4 , II Ti 3:11 .†

G3487.thayer: spikenard, a perfume made originally from a plant growing on the Himalayas.
G3487.mounce: spikenard, a species of aromatic plant with grassy leaves and a fibrous root, of which the best and strongest grows in India; in NT oil of spikenard, an oil extracted from the plant, which was highly prized and used as an ointment either pure or mixed with other substances, Mk. 14:3; Jn. 12:3*
G3487.abbottSmith: nard ; an Indian plant, the Nardostachys nardus jatamansi , used for the preparation of a fragrant ointment; ointment of nard : Mk 14:3 , Jo 12:3 .†

G262.abbottSmith: of amaranth (Inscr.); hence unfading : I Pe 5:4 .†

G1424.thayer: a setting (of the sun), hence: the West.
G1424.mounce: a sinking or setting; pl. δυσμαί, the setting of the sun; hence, the west, Mt. 8:11; 24:27
G1424.abbottSmith: mostly in pl., opp. to ἀνατολαί ;
G1424.strongs: from G1416 (δύνω); the sun-set, i.e. (by implication) the western region

G4032.thayer: I conceal, hide (by putting something around it).
G4032.mounce: also spelled περικρύπτω, to hide or keep from sight, to conceal by envelopment; to conceal, Lk. 1:24*
G4032.abbottSmith: to conceal entirely , keep hidden : late 2 aor., περιέκρυβσν (but v. Bl., § 17; Soph., Lex. , s.v. περικρύβω ), Lk 1:24 .†
G4032.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2928 (κρύπτω); to conceal all around, i.e. entirely

G3852.thayer: a command, charge, injunction; a precept, rule of living.
G3852.mounce: a command, order, charge, Acts 5:28; 16:24; direction, precept, 1 Thess. 4:2; 1 Tim. 1:5, 18*
G3852.abbottSmith: an instruction , charge , command : Ac 5:28 16:24 , I Th 4:2 , I Ti 1:5 , 18 . (In Xen., Polyb., of a military order.)†

G2082.thayer: inner; with the article: the part that is within.

G1426.thayer: evil report, defamation, words of ill omen.
G1426.mounce: ill words; words of ill omen; reproach, contumely, 2 Cor. 6:8*
G1426.strongs: from a compound of G1418 (δυσ-) and G5345 (φήμη); defamation

G3992.thayer: I send, transmit, permit to go, put forth.
G3992.mounce: to send, to despatch on any message, embassy, business, etc., Mt. 2:8; 11:2; 14:10; to transmit, Acts 11:29; Rev. 1:11; to dismiss, permit to go, Mk. 5:12; to send in or among, 2 Thess. 2:11; to thrust in, or put forth, Rev. 14:15, 18
G3992.abbottSmith: to send ; of persons: c. acc., absol., Mt 22:7 , Lk 7:19 , Jo 1:22 , II Co 9:3 , al.; ptcp. seq. verb., Mt 14:10 , Ac 19:31 , al.; ptcp. seq. διά (= Heb. בְּיוֹד probably misspelled בְּיַד , I Ki 16:20 , al.), Mt 11:2 (cf. Re 1:1 ); of teachers sent by God, Jo 1:33 4:34 , Ro 8:3 , al.; c. acc. et dat., I Co 4:17 , Phl 2:19 ; seq. πρός , c. acc., Lk 4:26 , Jo 16:7 , al.; seq. λέγων (cf. Heb. שָׁלַח
G3992.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to dispatch (from the subjective view or point of departure, whereas (as a stronger form of ) refers rather to the objective point or terminus ad quem, and G4724 (στέλλω) denotes properly, the orderly motion involved), especially on a temporary errand; also to transmit, bestow, or wield

G3893.mounce: pr. to incite to bitter feelings; to provoke; absol. to act provokingly, be rebellious, Heb. 3:16*
G3893.abbottSmith: to embitter , provoke : absol. (yet sc. τ. θεόν , cf. Ps 105 (106):7 , Ez 2:5-8 , al., and with τὸν θ. added, Ps 5:4 , Ez 20:21 , al.), He 3:16 (cf. παραπικρασμός ).†
G3893.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G4087 (πικραίνω); to embitter alongside, i.e. (figuratively) to exasperate

G5419.mounce: pr. to propound in distinct terms, to tell; in NT to explain, interpret, expound, Mt. 15:15*
G5419.abbottSmith: to show forth, tell, declare, explain : Mt 13:36 (WH, RV, διασάφησον ) 15:15 .†
G5419.strongs: probably akin to G5420 (φράσσω) through the idea of defining; to indicate (by word or act), i.e. (specially), to expound

G231.strongs: from G251 (ἅλς); a sailor (as engaged on the salt water), i.e. (by implication) a fisher

G1066.thayer: Gideon, one of the Judges of Israel.
G1066.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01439); Gedeon (i.e. Gid(e)on), an Israelite

G559.strongs: of Latin origin; Apelles, a Christian

G664.abbottSmith: abruptly , curtly , hence sharply , severely : II Co 13:10 , Tit 1:13 (MM, s.v.).†
G664.strongs: adverb from a derivative of a comparative of G575 (ἀπό) and (to cut); abruptly, i.e. peremptorily

G4159.mounce: whence? from where, used of place, etc., Mt. 15:33; met. of a state of dignity, Rev. 2:5; used of origin, Mt. 21:25; of cause, source, author, etc., Mt. 13:27, 54, 56; Lk. 1:43; how? in what way? Mk. 8:4; 12:37
G4159.abbottSmith: whence ; of place: Mt 15:33 , Lk 13:25 , 27 , Jo 3:8 6:5 8:14 9:29 , 30 19:9 , Re 7:13 ; metaph., of condition, Re 2:5 ; of origin: Mt 13:27 , 54 , 56 21:25 , Mk 6:2 , Lk 20:7 , Jo 2:9 , Ja 4:1 ; of parentage, Jo 7:27 , 28 ; of cause: Mk 8:4 12:37 , Lk 1:43 , Jo 1:49 4:11 .†
G4159.strongs: from the base of G4213 (πόσις) with enclitic adverb of origin; from which (as interrogative) or what (as relative) place, state, source or cause

G2676.mounce: pr. a complete adjustment; completeness of character, perfection, 2 Cor. 13:9*
G2676.abbottSmith: a strengthening , making fit : in ethical sense, II Co 13:9 .†

G4160.thayer: (a) I make, manufacture, construct, (b) I do, act, cause.
G4160.mounce: pluperf., πεποιήκειν, to make, form, construct, Mt. 17:4; Mk. 9:5; Jn. 2:15; of God, to create, Mt. 19:4; Acts 4:24; to make, prepare a feast, etc., Mt. 22:2; Mk. 6:21; met. to make, establish, ratify, a covenant, Heb. 8:9; to make, assume, consider, regard, Mt. 12:33; to make, effect, bring to pass, cause to take place, do, accomplish, Mt. 7:22; 21:21; Mk. 3:8; 6:5; 7:37; met. to perfect, accomplish, fulfil, put in execution a purpose, promise, etc., Lk. 16:4; 19:48; to cause, make, Mt. 5:32; Jn. 11:37; Acts 24:12; to make gain, gain, acquire, Mt. 25:16; Lk. 19:18; to get, procure, Lk. 12:33; to make, to cause to be or become a thing, Mt. 21:13; 23:15; to use, treat, Lk. 15:19; to make, constitute, appoint to some office, Mt. 4:19; Mk. 3:14; to make, declare to be, 1 Jn. 1:10; 5:10; to do, to perform, execute, practise, act, Mt. 5:46, 47, 6:2, 3; to commit evil, Mt. 13:41; 27:23; to be devoted to, follow, practise, Jn. 3:21; 5:29; Rom. 3:12; to do, execute, fulfil, keep, observe, obey, precepts, etc., Mt. 1:24; 5:19; 7:21, 24, 26; to bring evil upon, inflict, Acts 9:13; to keep, celebrate a festival, Mt. 26:18; to institute the celebration of a festival, Heb. 11:28; ποιεῖν τινα ἔχω, to cause to leave a place, i.q. ἔχω ἄγειν, to lead or conduct out, Acts 5:34; to pass, spend time, continue for a time, Mt. 20:12; Acts 15:33; 18:23; Jas. 4:13; to bear, as trees, yield, produce, Mt. 3:8, 10; 7:17, 18, 19; with a substantive or adjective it forms a periphrasis for the verb corresponding to the noun or adjective, e.g. δῆλον ποιεῖν, i.q. δηλοῦν, to make manifest, betray, Mt. 26:73; ἐκδίκησιν ποιεῖν, i.q. ἐδικεῖν, to vindicate, avenge, Lk. 18:7, 8; ἔκθετον ποιεῖν, i.q. ἐκτιθέναι, to expose infants, Acts 7:19; ἐνέδραν ποιεῖν, i.q. ἐνεδρεύειν, to lie in wait, Acts 25:3; ἐξουσίαν ποιεῖν, i.q. ἐξουσιάζειν, to exercise power or authority, Rev. 13:12; κρίσιν ποιεῖν, i.q. κρίνειν, to judge, act as judge, Jn. 5:27; λύτρωσιν ποιεῖν, i.q. λυτροῦν, to deliver, set free, Lk. 1:68; μονὴν ποιεῖν, i.q. μένειν, to remain, dwell, Jn. 14:23; πόλεμον ποιεῖν, i.q. πολεμεῖν, to make or wage war, fight, Rev. 11:7; συμβούλιον ποιεῖν, i.q. συμβουλεύεσθαι, to consult together, deliberate, Mk. 3:6; συνωμοσίαν ποιεῖν, i.q. συνομνύναι, and συστροφὴν ποιεῖν, i.q. συστρέφεσθαι, to conspire together, form a conspiracy, Acts 23:12, 13; φανερὸν ποιεῖν, i.q. φανεροῦν, to make known, betray, Mt. 12:16; ἀναβολὴν ποῖεσθαι, i.q. ἀναβάλλεσθαι, to delay, procrastinate, Acts 25:17; βέβαιον ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. βεβαιοῦν, to confirm, render firm and sure, 2 Pet. 1:10; δεήσεις ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. δεῖσθαι, to pray, offer prayer, Lk. 5:33; ἐκβολὴν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. ἐκβάλλειν, to cast out, throw overboard, Acts 27:18; καθαρισμὸν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. καθαρίζειν, to cleanse from sin, Heb. 1:3; κοινωνίαν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. κοινωνεῖν, to communicate in liberality, bestow alms, Rom. 15:26; κοπετὸν ποιεῖν, i.q. κόπτεσθαι, to lament, bewail, Acts 8:2; λόγον ποιεῖσθαι, to regard, make account of, Acts 20:24; μνείαν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. μνησθῆναι, to call to mind, Rom. 1:9; μνήμην ποιεῖσθαι, to remember, retain in memory, 2 Pet. 1:15; πορείαν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. πορεύεσθαι, to go, journey, travel, Lk. 13:22; πρόνοιαν ποιεῖσθαι, i.q. προνοεῖσθαι, to take care of, provide for, Rom. 13:14; σπουδὴν ποιεῖσθαι, to act with diligence and earnestness, Jude 3
G4160.abbottSmith: to make , produce , create , cause : c. acc. rei, Mt 17:4 , Mk 9:5 , Jo 9:11 , Ac 9:39 , Ro 9:20 , al.; of God as Creator (c. acc. pers. also), Mt 19:4 , Mk 10:6 , Lk 11:40 , Ac 4:24 , He 1:2 , al.; like Heb. עשׂה , absol. = ἐργάζομαι , to work , Mt 20:12 (cf. Ru 2:19 ; so AV, but v. infr.), Re 13:5 , R, mg. (but v. infr.); σκάνδαλα , Ro 16:17 ; εἰρήνην , Eph 2:15 , Ja 3:18 ; ἐπίστασιν , Ac 24:12
G4160.strongs: apparently a prolonged form of an obsolete primary; to make or do (in a very wide application, more or less direct)
G4160.kjv: abide, + agree, appoint, X avenge, + band together, be, bear, + bewray, bring (forth), cast out, cause, commit, + content, continue, deal, + without any delay, (would) do(-ing), execute, exercise, fulfil, gain, give, have, hold, X journeying, keep, + lay wait, + lighten the ship, make, X mean, + none of these things move me, observe, ordain, perform, provide, + have purged, purpose, put, + raising up, X secure, shew, X shoot out, spend, take, tarry, + transgress the law, work, yield

G4786.mounce: pluperf., συνεκέκρατο (3 sg), to mix with, mingle together; to blend, 1 Cor. 12:24; pass. to be combined, united, Heb. 4:2*
G4786.abbottSmith: to mix or blend together, compound : c. acc., I Co 12:24 ; ptcp. pf. pass., He 4:2 , T, WH, mg., R, mg.; id., of persons, c. dat. instr., to be united with, agree with , ib., WH, txt., R, txt. (v. Westc., in l.).†
G4786.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2767 (κεράννυμι); to commingle, i.e. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate

G2552.thayer: experience of evil, suffering, distress, affliction, perseverance.
G2552.mounce: a state of suffering, affliction, trouble, in NT endurance in affliction, perseverance, Jas. 5:10*
G2552.strongs: from a compound of G2256 (ἡμιώριον) and G3806 (πάθος); hardship

G1905.mounce: to interrogate, question, ask, Mt. 12:10; 17:10; in NT to request, require, Mt. 16:1; from the Hebrew, ἐπερωτᾶν τὸν θεόν, to seek after, desire an acquaintance with God, Rom. 10:20
G1905.abbottSmith: to in­quire of, consult, question : c. acc pers., Mk 12:34 , Lk 2:46 , al.; c. dupl. acc, Mk 7:17 11:29 , Lk 20:40 , al.; c. acc pers., seq. λέγων , Mt 12:18 , Mk 9:11 ; εἰ , Mk 8:23 , Lk 23:6 , al.; ἐ. θεόν , Ro 10:20 (LXX) .
G1905.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2065 (ἐρωτάω); to ask for, i.e. inquire, seek

G2414.thayer: the Greek form of the Hebrew name: Jerusalem.
G2414.abbottSmith: Jerusalem : Mt 2:1 , Mk 3:8 , Jo 1:19 , al.; its inhabitants, Mt 2:3 3:5 23:37 , Lk 13:34 . Symbolically, ἡ- ἄνω Ἱ. , Ga 4:26 , contrasted with ἡ νῦν Ἱ. , Ga 4:25 ; Ἱ. ἐπουράνιος , He 12:22 ; ἡ καινὴ Ἱ. , Re 3:12 21:2 , 10 .
G2414.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03389); Hierosolyma (i.e. Jerushalaim), the capitol of Palestine

G697.thayer: Areopagus, or Mar's Hill, an open space on a hill in Athens where the supreme court was held.
G697.mounce: a hill, Ἄρειος πάγος, Areopagus, the hill of Mars, at Athens, Acts 17:19, 22. See PhotoGuide.
G697.abbottSmith: Hill of Ares or Mars , Areopagus ; also, the Court of Areopagus , the highest tribunal of Athens: Ac 17:19 , 22 .†
G697.strongs: from (the name of the Greek deity of war) and a derivative of G4078 (πήγνυμι); rock of Ares, a place in Athens

G2887.abbottSmith: orderly , decent , modest : I Ti 2:9 (WH, mg., -ίως , q.v.) 3:2 .†
G2887.strongs: from G2889 (κόσμος) (in its primary sense); orderly, i.e. decorous

G461.thayer: I make upright (straight) again, rear again, restore.
G461.mounce: to restore to straightness or erectness, Lk. 13:13; to re-invigorate, Heb. 12:12; to re-erect, Acts 15:16*
G461.abbottSmith: to set upright or straight again , restore : of persons, Lk 13:13 , He 12:12 ; of things, σκηνήν , Ac 15:16 (MM, VGT , s.v.; Cremer, 807).†
G461.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a derivative of the base of G3717 (ὀρθός); to straighten up

G1238.mounce: pr. a band or fillet; a diadem, the badge of a sovereign, Rev. 12:3; 13:1; 19:12*
G1238.abbottSmith: the band round the τιάρα of a Persian king; a diadem , the badge of royalty: Re 12:3 13:1 19:12 .†
G1238.strongs: from a compound of G1223 (διά) and G1210 (δέω); a "diadem" (as bound about the head)

G5475.thayer: copper, brass, money; a brazen musical instrument.
G5475.mounce: copper, also, bronze, Rev. 18:12; a brazen musical instrument, 1 Cor. 13:1; copper money, Mt. 10:9; money in general, Mk. 6:8; 12:41*
G5475.abbottSmith: copper : I Co 13:1 , Re 18:12 ; by meton., of copper coin, Mt 10:9 , Mk 6:8 12:41 .†
G5475.strongs: perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of hollowing out as a vessel (this metal being chiefly used for that purpose); copper (the substance, or some implement or coin made of it)

G1647.mounce: used as the superlative of μικρός, smallest, least, Mt. 2:6; 5:19

G2052.thayer: (the seeking of followers and adherents by means of gifts, the seeking of followers, hence) ambition, rivalry, self-seeking; a feud, faction.
G2052.mounce: the service of a party, party spirit; feud, faction, 2 Cor. 12:20; contentious disposition, selfish ambition, Gal. 5:20; Phil. 1:17; 2:3; Jas. 3:14; by impl. untowardness, disobedience, Rom. 2:8; Jas. 3:16*
G2052.abbottSmith: ambition, self-seeking, rivalry : Ja 3:14 , 16 ; κατ’ ἐριθίαν , Phl 2:3 ; οἱ ἐξ ἐ. , Ro 2:8 , Phl 1:17 ; pl. (Bl., § 32, 6; WM, 220; Swete, Mk. , 153), II Co 12:20 , Ga 5:20 .†
G2052.strongs: perhaps as the same as G2042 (ἐρεθίζω); properly, intrigue, i.e. (by implication) faction

G2177.mounce: to leap or spring upon, assault, Acts 19:16*
G2177.abbottSmith: to leap upon : seq. ἐπί c. acc pers. Ac 19:16 .†
G2177.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G242 (ἅλλομαι); to spring upon

G3956.thayer: all, the whole, every kind of.
G3956.mounce: all; in the sg. the whole, entire, usually when the substantive has the article, Mt. 6:29; 8:32; Acts 19:26; every, only with an anarthrous subst., Mt. 3:10; 4:4; pl. all, Mt. 1:17, et al. freq.; πάντα, in all respects, Acts 20:35; 1 Cor. 9:25; 10:33; 11:2; by a Hebraism, a negative with πᾶς is sometimes equivalent to οὐδείς or μηδείς, Mt. 24:22; Lk. 1:37; Acts 10:14; Rom. 3:20; 1 Cor. 1:29; Eph. 4:29
G3956.abbottSmith: all , every . As adj., c. subst. anarth., all , every , of every kind : Mt 3:10 4:23 , Mk 9:49 , Lk 4:37 , Jo 2:10 , Ac 27:20 , Ro 7:8 , Re 18:17 , al. mult.; pl., all , Ac 22:15 , Ro 5:12 , He 1:6 , al.; of the highest degree, π. ἐξουσία ( προθυμία , χαρά ), Mt 28:18 , Ac 17:11 , Phl 2:29 , al.; also the whole (though in this sense more freq. c. art.), Mt 2:3 , Ac 2:36 , Ro 11:26 . C. art. (befor
G3956.strongs: including all the forms of declension; apparently a primary word; all, any, every, the whole
G3956.kjv: all (manner of, means), alway(-s), any (one), X daily, + ever, every (one, way), as many as, + no(-thing), X thoroughly, whatsoever, whole, whosoever

G44.strongs: from the same as G43 (ἀγκάλη); a hook (as bent)

G2227.thayer: I make that which was dead to live, cause to live, quicken.
G2227.mounce: pr. to engender living creatures; to quicken, make alive, Rom. 4:17; 8:11; 1 Cor. 15:36; in NT met. to quicken with the life of salvation, Jn. 6:63; 2 Cor. 3:6
G2227.abbottSmith: in cl. (= ζωογονέω ), to produce alive .
G2227.strongs: from the same as G2226 (ζῶον) and G4160 (ποιέω); to (re-)vitalize (literally or figuratively)

G2168.thayer: I thank, give thanks; pass. 3 sing: is received with thanks.
G2168.mounce: to thank, Lk. 17:16; absol. to give thanks, Mt. 15:36; 26:27; pass. to be made a matter of thankfulness, 2 Cor. 1:11
G2168.abbottSmith: to be thankful, give thanks (chiefly in late writers and Inscr.; cf. Milligan, Th. , 5; Ellic. on Col 1:12 ; Lft., Notes , 9): Ro 1:21 , I Co 14:17 , I Th 5:18 ; of giving thanks before meat, Mt 15:36 26:27 , Mk 8:6 14:23 , Lk 22:17 , 19 , Jo 6:11 , 23 , I Co 11:24 ; c. dat. pens., τ. θεῷ , Lk 17:16 , Ac 27:35 28:15 , Ro 14:6 16:4 , I Co 14:18 , Phl 1:3 , Col 1:3 , 12 , Phm 4 ; seq. διὰ Ἰ. Χριστοῦ
G2168.strongs: from G2170 (εὐχάριστος); to be grateful, i.e. (actively) to express gratitude (towards); specially, to say grace at a meal

G2972.thayer: Cos, an island in the Aegean Sea, south-west of Asia Minor.
G2972.abbottSmith: Cos , an island in the Ægean Sea: acc. Κῶ , Ac 21:1 ( Κῶν , Rec.).†
G2972.strongs: of uncertain origin; Cos, an island in the Mediterranean

G3730.mounce: impetus, impulse; assault, violent attempt, Acts 14:5; met. impulse of mind, purpose, will, Jas. 3:4
G3730.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a violent impulse, i.e. onset

G2243.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0452); Helias (i.e. Elijah), an Israelite

G3056.thayer: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy.
G3056.mounce: a word, a thing uttered, Mt. 12:32, 37; 1 Cor. 14:19; speech, language, talk, Mt. 22:15; Lk. 20:20; 2 Cor. 10:10; Jas. 3:2; converse, Lk. 24:17; mere talk, wordy show, 1 Cor. 4:19, 20; Col. 2:23; 1 Jn. 3:18; language, mode of discourse, style of speaking, Mt. 5:37; 1 Cor. 1:17; 1 Thess. 2:5; a saying, a speech, Mk. 7:29; Eph. 4:29; an expression, form of words, formula, Mt. 26:44; Rom. 13:9; Gal. 5:14; a saying, a thing propounded in discourse, Mt. 7:24; 19:11; Jn. 4:37; 6:60; 1 Tim. 1:15; a message, announcement, 2 Cor. 5:19; a prophetic announcement, Jn. 12:38; an account, statement, 1 Pet. 3:15; a story, report, Mt. 28:15; Jn. 4:39; 21:23; 2 Thess. 2:2; a written narrative, a treatise, Acts 1:1; a set discourse, Acts 20:7; doctrine, Jn. 8:31, 37; 2 Tim. 2:17; subject-matter, Acts 15:6; reckoning, account, Mt. 12:36; 18:23; 25:19; Lk. 16:2; Acts 19:40; 20:24; Rom. 9:28; Phil. 4:15, 17; Heb. 4:13; a plea, Mt. 5:32; Acts 19:38; a motive, Acts 10:29; reason, Acts 18:14; ὁ λόγος, the word of God, especially in the Gospel, Mt. 13:21, 22; Mk. 16:20; Lk. 1:2; Acts 6:4; ὁ λόγος, the divine WORD, or Logos, Jn. 1:1
G3056.abbottSmith: Of that by which the inward thought is expressed, Lat. oratio , sermo , vox , verbum . a word , not in the grammatical sense of a mere name ( ἔπος , ὄνομα , ῥῆμα ), but a word as embodying a conception or idea: Mt 8:8 , Lk 7:7 , I Co 14:9 , 19 , He 12:19 , al. a saying , statement , declaration : Mt 19:22 (T om.), Mk 5:36 7:29 , Lk 1:29 , Jo 2:22 6:60 , Ac 7:29 , al.; c. gen. attrib., Ac 13:15 , R
G3056.strongs: from G3004 (λέγω); something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expression (i.e. Christ)
G3056.kjv: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech, talk, thing, + none of these things move me, tidings, treatise, utterance, word, work

G2614.mounce: to follow hard upon; to track, search for, follow perseveringly, Mk. 1:36*
G2614.abbottSmith: "perfective" compound (M, Pr. , 116), to pursue closely , follow up ; with hostile intent (Thuc., i, 49, Ge 31:36 al.); with kindly intention ( Ps 22 (23):6 , I Ki 30:22 , cf. Si 27:17 ): Mk 1:36 .†
G2614.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1377 (διώκω); to hunt down, i.e. search for

G68.thayer: a field, especially as bearing a crop; the country, lands, property in land, a country estate.
G68.mounce: a field, especially a cultivated field; pl. the country; lands, farms, villages
G68.abbottSmith: a field : Mt 6:28 , al. the country : Mk 15:21 , al.; pl., country places, farms : Mk 5:14 6:36 56 , Lk 8:34 9:12 . = χωρίον a piece of ground : Mk 10:29 , Ac 4:27 , al. (On the occurrence of this word as compared with χώρα , χωρίον v. MM, VGT , s.v.).
G68.strongs: from G71 (ἄγω); a field (as a drive for cattle); genitive case, the country; specially, a farm, i.e. hamlet
G68.kjv: country, farm, piece of ground, land

G1875.mounce: with subj., whenever, as soon as, Mt. 2:8; Lk. 11:22, 34*
G1875.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G302 (ἄν); a particle of indefinite contemporaneousness; whenever, as soon as

G633.mounce: to cleanse a part of the body by washing; mid., of one’s self, Mt. 27:24*
G633.abbottSmith: to wash off : mid. (reflex.), τ. χεῖρας , Mt 27:24 .†
G633.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3538 (νίπτω); to wash off (reflexively, one's own hands symbolically)

G888.mounce: useless, unprofitable, worthless, Mt. 25:30; unmeritorious, Lk. 17:10*
G888.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5534 (χρή) (compare G5532 (χρεία)); useless, i.e. (euphemistically) unmeritorious

G511.thayer: higher, to a more honorable place (at the dinner table); previously, in an earlier passage (or a book), above.
G511.strongs: comparative degree of G507 (ἄνω); upper, i.e. (neuter as adverb) to a more conspicuous place, in a former part of the book

G556.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1643 (ἐλαύνω); to dismiss

G3954.thayer: freedom, openness, especially in speech; boldness, confidence.
G3954.mounce: freedom in speaking, boldness of speech, Acts 4:13; παρρησίᾳ, as an adv., freely, boldly, Jn. 7:13, 26; so μετὰ παρρησίας, Acts 2:29; 4:29, 31; license, authority, Phlm. 8; confidence, assurance, 2 Cor. 7:4; Eph. 3:12; Heb. 3:6; 10:19; openness, frankness, 2 Cor. 3:12; παρρησίᾳ, and ἐν παρρησίᾳ, adverbially, openly, plainly, perspicuously, unambiguously, Mk. 8:32; Jn. 10:24; publicly, before all, Jn. 7:4
G3954.abbottSmith: freedom of speech , plainness , openness , freedom in speaking (Eur., Plat., al.): Ac 4:13 , II Co 3:12 ; παρρησίᾳ , adverbially, freely , openly , plainly , Mk 8:32 , Jo 7:13 , 26 10:24 11:14 16:29 18:20 ; opp. to ἐν παροιμίαις , Jo 16:25 ; ἐν π. , Eph 6:19 ; μετὰ π. , Ac 2:29 4:29 , 31 28:31 .
G3954.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and a derivative of G4483 (ῥέω); all out-spokenness, i.e. frankness, bluntness, publicity; by implication, assurance
G3954.kjv: bold (X -ly, -ness, -ness of speech), confidence, X freely, X openly, X plainly(-ness)

G1604.mounce: pr. a filling up, completion; hence, a fulfilling, accomplishment, Acts 21:26*

G5354.abbottSmith: to envy : c. dat. (L, txt., Tr., mg., WH, mg., acc.), as in cl., Ga 5:26 .†
G5354.strongs: from G5355 (φθόνος); to be jealous of

G5348.thayer: (a) I anticipate, precede, (b) I come, arrive.
G5348.mounce: come before, precede, 1 Thess. 4:15; absol. to advance, make progress, 2 Cor. 10:14; Phil. 3:16; to come up with, come upon, be close at hand, Mt. 12:28; Lk. 11:20; 1 Thess. 2:16; to attain an object of pursuit, Rom. 9:31*
G5348.abbottSmith: to come before another, anticipate (cl.): c. acc. pers., I Th 4:15 .
G5348.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to be beforehand, i.e. anticipate or precede; by extension, to have arrived at

G4598.strongs: from G4597 (σής) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω); moth-eaten

G410.mounce: unblamable, irreproachable, 1 Cor. 1:8; Col. 1:22; 1 Tim. 3:10; Tit. 1:6, 7*
G410.abbottSmith: not to be called to account , unreprovable : I Co 1:8 , Col 1:22 , I Ti 3:10 , Tit 1:6 , 7 .†
G410.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G1458 (ἐγκαλέω); unaccused, i.e. (by implication) irreproachable

G2934.thayer: a beast of burden (generally, a horse or mule) either for riding or for carrying loads on its back, or for yoking to a cart or carriage.
G2934.mounce: pr. property, generally used in the plural, τὰ κτήνη; property in animals; a beast of burden, domesticated animal, Lk. 10:34; Acts 23:24; beasts, cattle, 1 Cor. 15:39; Rev. 18:13*
G2934.abbottSmith: a beast , (in late Gk. esp.) a beast of burden : Lk 10:34 pl. (as chiefly in cl.), Ac 23:24 , Re 18:13 ; of quadrupeds, as opp. to fishes and birds (cf. Ge 1:25 ), I Co 15:39 .†
G2934.strongs: from G2932 (κτάομαι); property, i.e. (specially) a domestic animal

G4831.mounce: to conform to, take on the same form as, Phil. 3:10*
G4831.abbottSmith: a fellow-imitator : c. gen. obj., Phl 3:17 (v. ICC , in l.). Not elsewhere.†
G4831.strongs: from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3401 (μιμέομαι); a co-imitator, i.e. fellow votary

G5153.strongs: from G5151 (τρίς) and G5507 (χίλιοι); three times a thousand

G5559.thayer: the skin, surface of the body.
G5559.mounce: the skin; the body surface; Acts 19:12*
G5559.abbottSmith: in cl. rare in prose, the surface of the body, skin : Ac 19:12 .†
G5559.strongs: probably akin to the base of G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of handling; the body (properly, its surface or skin)

G647.thayer: repudiation, divorce; met: bill of divorce.
G647.mounce: defection, desertion, as of a freedman from a patron; in NT the act of putting away a wife, repudiation, divorce, Mt. 19:7; Mk. 10:4; meton. a bill of repudiation, deed of divorce, Mt. 5:31*
G647.abbottSmith: in cl., only in phrase ἀποστασίου δίκη , an action against a freedman for forsaking his προστάτης (Dem.).
G647.strongs: neuter of a (presumed) adjective from a derivative of G868 (ἀφίστημι); properly, something separative, i.e. (specially) divorce

G5031.mounce: swift, speedy, 2 Pet. 2:1; near at hand, impending, 2 Pet. 1:14*
G5031.abbottSmith: poët. and late for ταχύς , swift : of swift approach, II Pe 1:14 2:1 .†

G1318.mounce: pr. taught, teachable, of things; in NT taught, of person, Jn. 6:45; 1 Cor. 2:13*
G1318.strongs: from G1321 (διδάσκω); (subjectively) instructed, or (objectively) communicated by teaching

G243.thayer: other, another (of more than two), different.
G243.mounce: another, some other; ὁ ἄλλος, the other; οἱ ἄλλοι, the others, the rest
G243.abbottSmith: other , another : absol., Mt 20:3 , al.; ἄ. δέ , I Co 3:10 12:8 ; pl., Mk 6:15 ; attached to a noun, Mt 2:12 4:21 , al.; c. art., ὁ ἄ. , the other , Mt 5:39 , Jo 19:32 (Bl., §47, 8); οἱ ἄ. , the others, the rest , Jo 20:25 , I Co 14:29 ; ἄ. πρὸς ἄλλον = πρὸς ἀλλήλους (BL, §48, 10), Ac 2:12 ; ἄλλ’ (i.e. ἄλλο ) ἤ (Bl., §77, 13), Lk 12:51 ; seq. πλήν , Mk 12:32 ; εἰ μή , Jo 6:22 ; παρά c. acc., I Co
G243.strongs: a primary word; "else," i.e. different (in many applications)

G2016.abbottSmith: renowned, illustrious, notable : Ac 2:20 (LXX) (cf. MM, Exp. , xiv).†

G390.thayer: I overturn; I turn back, return; I turn hither and thither; pass: I turn myself about; I sojourn, dwell; I conduct myself, behave, live.
G390.mounce: to overturn, throw down, to turn back, return, Acts 5:22; 15:16; to live, to conduct one’s self, 2 Cor. 1:12; Eph. 2:3; 1 Tim. 3:15; Heb. 13:18; 1 Pet. 1:17; 2 Pet. 2:18; to gaze, Heb. 10:33*
G390.abbottSmith: to overturn : Jo 2:15 . to turn back , return : Ac 5:22 15:16 . to turn hither and thither ; pass., to turn oneself about , sojourn , dwell : Mt 17:22 Rec.; metaph. (like Heb. הלךְ , in κοινή writers and in π. ; v. Deiss., LAE , 315; BS , 88, 194; MM, VGT , s.v.), to conduct oneself , behave , live : II Co 1:12 , Eph 2:3 , I Ti 3:15 , He 10:33 13:18 , I Pe 1:17 , II Pe 2:18 .†
G390.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4762 (στρέφω); to overturn; also to return; by implication, to busy oneself, i.e. remain, live
G390.kjv: abide, behave self, have conversation, live, overthrow, pass, return, be used

G476.mounce: an opponent in a lawsuit, Mt. 5:25 (2x); Lk. 12:58; 18:3; an adversary, 1 Pet. 5:8*
G476.abbottSmith: as subst., an opponent in a lawsuit , adversary : Mt 5:25 , Lk 12:58 18:3 , I Pe 5:8 (Cremer, 696; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G476.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G1349 (δίκη); an opponent (in a lawsuit); specially, Satan (as the arch-enemy)

G2211.strongs: probably contracted from a poetic form of G2203 (Ζεύς) and G1435 (δῶρον); Jove-given; Zenas, a Christian

G4109.mounce: a flat broad surface; a table, tablet, 2 Cor. 3:3; Heb. 9:4*
G4109.abbottSmith: anything flat and broad . a plain (poët.). In late writers (Luc., al.), a flat stone , a tablet : II Co 3:3 , He 9:4 .†
G4109.strongs: from G4111 (πλάσσω); a moulding-board, i.e. flat surface ("plate", or tablet, literally or figuratively)

G206.mounce: the top, tip, end, extremity, Mk. 13:27; Lk. 16:24; Heb. 11:21
G206.abbottSmith: highest , extreme ; as subst., τὸ ἄ. , the top , extremity : Mk 13:27 , Lk 16:24 , He 11:21 ; pl. (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.), Mt 24:31 .†
G206.strongs: neuter of an adjective probably akin to the base of G188 (ἀκμήν); the extremity
G206.kjv: one end… other, tip, top, uttermost participle

G2083.mounce: a companion, associate, fellow-comrade, friend, Mt. 20:13; 22:12; 26:50*
G2083.abbottSmith: a companion, comrade : Mt 11:16 (WH, ἑτέροις ); voc., as term of address, my friend : Mt 20:13 22:12 26:50 .†

G606.thayer: I have been put away, am stored, am reserved for.
G606.mounce: to be laid up, preserved, Lk. 19:20; to be in store, be reserved, await any one, Col. 1:5; 2 Tim. 4:8; Heb. 9:27*
G606.abbottSmith: to be laid up , in store , laid away : Lk 19:20 . Metaph., c. dat. pers., to be reserved (Dem., Plat.; and v. MM, s.v.): Col 1:5 , II Ti 4:8 , He 9:27 .†
G606.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to be reserved; figuratively, to await

G1271.mounce: pr. thought, intention; the mind, intellect, understanding, Mt. 22:37; Mk. 12:30; Lk. 10:27; an operation of the understanding, thought, imagination, Lk. 1:51; insight, comprehension, 1 Jn. 5:20; mode of thinking and feeling, disposition of mind and heart, the affection, Eph. 2:3; Col. 1:21
G1271.abbottSmith: the understanding, mind : Lk 1:51 , Eph 4:18 , Col 1:21 , I Pe 1:13 , II Pe 3:1 , I Jo 5:20 ; pl., Eph 2:3 ; in quotations from LXX, Mt 22:37 , Mk 12:30 , Lk 10:27 , He 8:10 10:16 (Cremer, 79, 438).†
G1271.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3563 (νοῦς); deep thought, properly, the faculty (mind or its disposition), by implication, its exercise

G1023.mounce: the arm; the arm as a symbol of power, Lk. 1:51; Jn. 12:38; Acts 13:17*
G1023.abbottSmith: the arm ; as in OT, β. Κυρίου , metaph., for the Divine power: Lk 1:51 , Jo 12:38 (LXX) , Ac 13:17 .†
G1023.strongs: properly, comparative of G1024 (βραχύς), but apparently in the sense of (to wield); the arm, i.e. (figuratively) strength

G667.thayer: I carry, bear away (sometimes with violence).
G667.mounce: to bear or carry away, conduct away, Mk. 15:1; Lk. 16:22; Acts 19:12; 1 Cor. 16:3; Rev. 17:3; 21:10*
G667.abbottSmith: to carry off , bear , or lead away : c. acc., Mk 15:1 , I Co 16:3 , Re 17:3 21:10 . Pass., Lk 16:22 , Ac 19:12 .†
G667.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear off (literally or relatively)

G3836.thayer: from all sides, from all parts.
G3836.strongs: adverb (of source) from G3837 (πανταχοῦ); from all directions

G3904.thayer: the day of preparation, the day before the Sabbath, Friday.
G3904.mounce: a getting ready, preparation, in NT preparation for a feast, day of preparation, Mt. 27:62; Mk. 15:42

G5347.abbottSmith: (Porcius), Festus , procurator of Judæa: Ac 24:27 25:1 ff. Ac 26:24 , 25 , 32 .†
G5347.strongs: of Latin derivation; festal; Phestus (i.e. Festus), a Roman

G3816.thayer: (a) a male child, boy, (b) a male slave, servant; thus: a servant of God, especially as a title of the Messiah, (c) a female child, girl.
G3816.mounce: a child in relation to parents, of either sex, Jn. 4:51; a child in respect of age, either male or female, and of all ages from infancy up to manhood, a boy, youth, girl, maiden, Mt. 2:16; 17:18; Lk. 2:43; 8:54; a servant, slave, Mt. 8:6, 8, 13, cf. v. 9; Lk. 7:7, cf. v. 3, 10; an attendant, minister, Mt. 14:2; Lk. 1:69; Acts 4:25; also, Lk. 1:54; or, perhaps, a child in respect of fatherly regard
G3816.abbottSmith: a child, boy , youth , maiden : ὁ π. , Mt 17:18 , Lk 2:43 9:42 , Ac 20:12 ; ἡ π. Lk 8:51 , 54 (on the artic. nom, of address, v. M, Pr. , 70 f., 235; Bl., § 33, 4); pl., Mt 2:16 21:15 ; of parentage, c. gen., Jo 4:51 .
G3816.strongs: perhaps from G3817 (παίω); a boy (as often beaten with impunity), or (by analogy), a girl, and (genitive case) a child; specially, a slave or servant (especially a minister to a king; and by eminence to God)
G3816.kjv: child, maid(-en), (man) servant, son, young man

G303.thayer: prep. Rare in NT; prop: upwards, up; among, between; in turn; apiece, by; as a prefix: up, to, anew, back.
G303.mounce: prep. used in the NT only in certain forms. ἀνὰ μέρος, in turn; ἀνὰ μέσον, through the midst, between; ἀνὰ δηνάριον, at the rate of a denarius; with numerals, ἀνὰ ἑκατόν, in parties of a hundred. In composition, step by step, up, back, again
G303.abbottSmith: upwards , up , always c. acc. In phrases: ἀ. μέσον , among , between , c. gen., Mt 13:25 , Mk 7:31 , I Co 6:5 (M, Pr. , 99), Re 7:17 [so in LXX for בְּתוֹךְ ]; ἀ. μέρος , in turn , I Co 14:27 (both found in Polyb.; cf. M Gr. ἀνάμεσα ). Distrib., apiece, by : Mt 20:9-10 , Lk 9:3 (WH om.), Lk 9:14 Lk 10:1 , Jo 2:6 , Re 4:8 . Adverbially ("a vulgarism," Bl., § 51, 5; cf. Deiss., BS, 139 f.), ἀ. εἷς ἕ
G303.strongs: a primary preposition and adverb; properly, up; but (by extension) used (distributively) severally, or (locally) at (etc.)
G303.kjv: and, apiece, by, each, every (man), in, through

G3550.strongs: from G3551 (νόμος) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); a legislator

G3515.abbottSmith: to be a babe : I Co 14:20 .†
G3515.strongs: from G3516 (νήπιος); to act as a babe, i.e. (figuratively) innocently

G2611.mounce: to bind up; to bandage a wound, Lk. 10:34*
G2611.abbottSmith: to bind up : τ. τραύματα (cf. Si 27:21 ), Lk 10:34 .†
G2611.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1210 (δέω); to tie down, i.e. bandage (a wound)

G1381.thayer: I put to the test, prove, examine; I distinguish by testing, approve after testing; I am fit.
G1381.abbottSmith: primarily of metals ( Pr 8:10 17:3 , Si 2:5 , Wi 3:6 ), to test, try, prove (in the hope and expectation that the test will prove successful, v. reff. s. Syn. ): χρυσίον , I Pe 1:7 ; other things, Lk 12:56 14:19 , II Co 8:8 , Ga 6:4 , I Th 2:4 5:21 ; τὰ διαφέροντα , Ro 2:18 , Phl 1:10 (R, mg., but v. infr.); πνεύματα , I Jo 4:1 ; of men, I Ti 3:10 (pass.); ἑαυτόν , I Co 11:28 , II Co 13:5 ; seq. s
G1381.strongs: from G1384 (δόκιμος); to test (literally or figuratively); by implication, to approve
G1381.kjv: allow, discern, examine, X like, (ap-)prove, try

G862.mounce: pr. incapability of decay; met. incorruptness, integrity, genuineness, purity, Tit. 2:7*
G862.abbottSmith: imperishable , immortal ; of things: I Co 9:25 , I Pe 1:4 , 23 3:4 ; of persons: of men, I Co 15:52 ; of God, Ro 1:23 , I Ti 1:17 (MM, s.v.).†
G862.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5351 (φθείρω); undecaying (in essence or continuance)

G2336.thayer: a repository, receptacle; a case, sheath, scabbard.
G2336.mounce: a repository, receptacle; a case, sheath, scabbard, Jn. 18:11*
G2336.abbottSmith: a receptacle , chest , case : of the sheath of a sword, Jo 18:11 .†
G2336.strongs: from G5087 (τίθημι); a receptacle, i.e. scabbard

G5616.thayer: as if, as it were, like; with numbers: about.
G5616.mounce: as if; as it were, as, like, Mt. 3:16; 9:36; with terms of number or quantity, about, Mt. 14:21; Lk. 1:56; 22:41, 59
G5616.abbottSmith: as if, .as it were, like as, like: Mt 3:16 9:36 , Ac 2:3 6:15 , Ro 6:13 , He 1:12 ; γίνεσθαι ( φαίνεσθαι ) ὡσεί , Mk 9:26 , Lk 22:44 ([WH] R, mg., om.), Lk 24:11 ;
G5616.strongs: from G5613 (ὡς) and G1487 (εἰ); as if
G5616.kjv: about, as (it had been, it were), like (as)

G279.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G3340 (μετανοέω); unrepentant

G49.strongs: from G48 (ἁγνίζω); a cleansing (the act), i.e. (ceremonially) lustration

G698.thayer: member of the Council of the Areopagus, an Areopagite.
G698.mounce: a judge of the court of Areopagus, Acts 17:34*
G698.abbottSmith: a judge of the Court of Areopagus: Ac 17:34 .†
G698.strongs: from G697 (Ἄρειος Πάγος); an Areopagite or member of the court held on Mars' Hill

G5621.mounce: in NT simply equivalent to οὖς, an ear, Mt. 26:51; Lk. 22:51; Jn. 18:26*
G5621.strongs: diminutive of G3775 (οὖς); an earlet, i.e. one of the ears, or perhaps the lobe of the ear

G225.thayer: truth, but not merely truth as spoken; truth of idea, reality, sincerity, truth in the moral sphere, divine truth revealed to man, straightforwardness.
G225.mounce: truth, Mk. 5:33; love of truth, sincerity, 1 Cor. 5:8; divine truth revealed to man, Jn. 1:17; practice in accordance with Gospel truth, Jn. 3:21; 2 Jn. 4
G225.abbottSmith: truth (v. DB , iv, 818f.). Objectively, "the reality lying at the basis of an appearance; the manifested, veritable essence of a matter" (Cremer, 86): Ro 9:1 , al.; of religious truth, Ro 1:25 , al.; esp. of Christian doctrine, Ga 2:5 , al.; ἀ. θεοῦ , Ro 15:8 . Subjectively, truthfulness, truth , not merely verbal (cl.), but sincerity and integrity of character: Jo 8:44 , III Jo 3 . In phrases (MM

G2722.thayer: (a) I hold fast, bind, arrest, (b) I take possession of, lay hold of, (c) I hold back, detain, restrain, (d) I hold a ship, keep its head.
G2722.mounce: (1) transitive, to hold down; to detain, retain, Lk. 4:42; Rom. 1:18; Phlm. 13; to hinder, restrain, 2 Thess. 2:6, 7; to hold downright, hold in a firm grasp, to have in full and secure possession, 1 Cor. 7:30; 2 Cor. 6:10; to come into full possession of, seize upon; to keep, retain, 1 Thess. 5:21; to occupy, Lk. 14:9; met. to hold fast mentally, retain, Lk. 8:15; 1 Cor. 11:2; 15:2; to maintain, Heb. 3:6, 14; 10:23; (2) intransitive, a nautical term, to land, touch, Acts 27:40; pass. to be in the grasp of, to be bound by, Rom. 7:6*
G2722.abbottSmith: perfective of ἔχω (M, Pr. , 116; M, Th. , 155), to possess , hold fast : Lk 8:15 , Jo 5:[4] , Ro 1:18 (Lft., Notes , 251), I Co 7:30 11:2 , II Co 6:10 , I Th 5:21 , He 3:6 , 14 10:23 ; to lay hold of , get possession of : Lk 14:9 .
G2722.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold down (fast), in various applications (literally or figuratively)
G2722.kjv: have, hold (fast), keep (in memory), let, X make toward, possess, retain, seize on, stay, take, withhold

G1089.mounce: to taste, Mt. 27:34; Jn. 2:9; absol. to take food, Acts 10:10, et al.; met. to have perception of, experience, Heb. 6:4, 5; 1 Pet. 2:3; θανάτου γεύεσθαι, to experience death, to die, Mt. 16:28, et al.
G1089.abbottSmith: to make to taste . Mid., to taste eat : absol., Ac 10:10 20:11 , Col 2:21 ; c. gen., Mt 27:34 , Lk 14:24 , Ac 23:14 ; c. acc. (not cl., but v. Westc, Heb. , 2:9 ; M, Pr. , 66, 245), Jo 2:9 . Metaph., He 6:4 ; ῥῆμα θεοῦ , He 6:5 (on case, v. supr., and cf. Milligan, NTD , 68) θανάτου (cf. Talmudic טַעַם מִיתָה ), Mt 16:28 , Mk 9:1 , Lk 9:27 , Jo 8:52 , He 2:9 ; seq. ὅτι , I Pe 2:3 (Cremer, 148).†
G1089.strongs: a primary verb; to taste; by implication, to eat; figuratively, to experience (good or ill)

G4410.mounce: the first or uppermost seat, the most honorable seat, Mt. 23:6; Mk. 12:39; Lk. 11:43; 20:46*
G4410.abbottSmith: the chief seat : Mt 23:6 , Mk 12:39 (v. Swete, in l.), Lk 11:43 20:46 .†
G4410.strongs: from G4413 (πρῶτος) and G2515 (καθέδρα); a sitting first (in the front row), i.e. preeminence in council

G5580.mounce: a false Christ, pretend Messiah, Mt. 24:24; Mk. 13:22*
G5580.abbottSmith: a false Christ or Messiah , "a pretender to the Messianic office" (Swete, Mk. , 309; cf. also Tr., Syn. , § xxx): Mt 24:24 , Mk 13:22 (cf. ἀντίχριστος ).†
G5580.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G5547 (Χριστός); a spurious Messiah

G897.thayer: (a) Babylon, the ancient city on the Euphrates, to which the people of Jerusalem, etc., were transported, (b) hence allegorically of Rome, from the point of view of the Christian people.
G897.abbottSmith: Babylon : Mt 1:11 , 12 , 17 , Ac 7:43 (LXX) ; symbolically, of Rome : Re 14:8 16:19 17:5 18:2 , 10 , 21 , and prob. also I Pe 5:13 .†
G897.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0894); Babylon, the capitol of Chaldæa (literally or figuratively (as a type of tyranny))

G607.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2776 (κεφαλή); to decapitate

G542.mounce: to view with undivided attention by looking away from every other object; to regard fixedly and earnestly, Heb. 12:2; to see distinctly, Phil. 2:23*
G542.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the same as G1492 (εἴδω); to see fully

G4416.abbottSmith: first-born : Lk 2:7 ; pl., He 11:28 . Metaph., of the priority of Christ (originally perh. a Messianic title, cf. Ps 88 (89):28 , He 1:6 ; v. ICC on Col 1:15 ): He 1:6 ; π. πάσης κτίσεως , Col 1:15 ; ἐν πολλοῖς ἀδελφοῖς , Ro 8:29 ; π. ( ἐκ ) τ. νεκρῶν , Col 1:18 , Re 1:5 ; pl., of the elect, ἐκκλησία πρωτοτόκων , He 12:23 .†
G4416.strongs: from G4413 (πρῶτος) and the alternate of G5088 (τίκτω); first-born (usually as noun, literally or figuratively)

G4830.mounce: a partaker with any one, a joint partaker, Eph. 3:6; 5:7*
G4830.abbottSmith: partaking together with ; as subst., ὁ σ. , a joint partaker (c. dat. pers. et gen. rei, FlJ, BJ , i, 24, 6): c. gen. rei, Eph 3:6 ; c. gen. pers., ib. 5:7 (Arist.).†
G4830.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3353 (μέτοχος); a co-participant

G4816.abbottSmith: to bring together, collect, gather up : c. acc. rei, Mt 13:28-30 ; pass., ib. 40 ; c. acc., seq. ἀπό , Mt 7:16 ; id. seq. ἐκ , Mt 13:41 , Lk 6:44 ; seq. εἰς , Mt 13:48 .†
G4816.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3004 (λέγω) in its original sense; to collect

G5402.thayer: Phoebe, a Christian woman in the church at Cenchreae.
G5402.abbottSmith: Phœbe , a deaconess of Cenchræa: Ro 16:1 .†
G5402.strongs: feminine of (bright; probably akin to the base of G5457 (φῶς)); Phœbe, a Christian woman

G1554.thayer: I give out, let; middle: I let out for my own advantage.
G1554.mounce: middle, to give out, to give up; to put out at interest; in NT to let out to tenants, Mt. 21:33, 41; Lark 12:1; Lk. 20:9*
G1554.abbottSmith: to surrender, give up, give out .
G1554.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give forth, i.e. (specially) to lease

G320.thayer: recognition, reading; public reading (of the law and prophets in synagogue or church).

G4060.thayer: I place or put around, clothe; fig: I bestow, confer.
G4060.mounce: to place around, put about or around, Mt. 21:33; 27:28; met. to attach, bestow, 1 Cor. 12:23
G4060.abbottSmith: to place or put around : c. acc. et dat., Mt 21:33 27:48 , Mk 12:1 15:36 , Jo 19:29 ; of garments, etc., to put on : Mt 27:28 Mk 15:17 ( Si 6:31 ). Metaph., like περιβάλλω , to bestow , confer (Thuc., al., Es 1:20 ): I Co 12:23 .†
G4060.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place around; by implication, to present
G4060.kjv: bestow upon, hedge round about, put about (on, upon), set about

G332.thayer: I curse, invoke curses, devote to destruction.
G332.mounce: to declare any one to be ἀνάθεμα; to curse, bind by a curse, Mk. 14:71; Acts 23:12, 14, 21*
G332.abbottSmith: to devote to destruction , declare or invoke anathema : absol., Mk 14:71 ; ἐαντόν , to bind oneself under a curse : Ac 23:12 , 14 23:21 (cf. καταναθεματίζω , and on the occurrence of the word in π. , v. Deiss., LAE , 92 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G332.strongs: from G331 (ἀνάθεμα); to declare or vow under penalty of execration
G332.kjv: (bind under a) curse, bind with an oath

G2617.thayer: I shame, disgrace, bring to shame, put to utter confusion, frustrate.
G2617.mounce: to humiliate, shame, put to shame, 1 Cor. 1:27; pass. to be ashamed, be put to shame, Lk. 13:17; to dishonor, disgrace, 1 Cor. 11:4, 5; from the Hebrew, to frustrate, disappoint, Rom. 5:5; 9:33; 1 Pet. 2:6
G2617.abbottSmith: to disgrace , dishonour , put to shame : c. acc. rei, I Co 11:4 , 5 ; c. acc. pers., I Co 1:27 11:22 ; pass., to be ashamed : Lk 13:17 , II Co 7:14 9:4 , I Pe 3:16 ; as in LXX ( Ps 21 (22):6 24 (25):2 , 3 , al.), of unfulfilled hopes: Ro 5:5 ; pass., Ro 9:33 10:11 , I Pe 2:6 (LXX) .†
G2617.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G153 (αἰσχύνομαι); to shame down, i.e. disgrace or (by implication) put to the blush

G396.thayer: I overturn (lit. or met.), subvert, overthrow, corrupt.
G396.mounce: pr. to overturn, overthrow; met. to subvert, corrupt, 2 Tim. 2:18; Tit. 1:11; Jn. 2:15*
G396.abbottSmith: to overturn , destroy : Jo 2:15 WH, txt.; metaph., to subvert (MM, VGT , s.v.): II Ti 2:18 , Tit 1:11 .†
G396.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to overturn (figuratively)

G4142.mounce: a small vessel, boat, Mk. 3:9; Jn. 6:22, 23, 24
G4142.abbottSmith: a boat : Mk 3:9 , Lk 5:2 ( πλοῖα , WH, txt., R), Jo 6:22 , 23 ( πλοῖα , WH) 24 21:8 .†
G4142.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4143 (πλοῖον); a boat

G2213.thayer: a question, subject of inquiry, dispute.
G2213.mounce: a question; a subject of debate or controversy, Acts 15:2; 18:15; 23:29; 25:19; 26:3*
G2213.strongs: from G2212 (ζητέω); a search (properly concretely), i.e. (in words) a debate

G4704.thayer: I hasten, am eager, am zealous.
G4704.mounce: to hurry; be bent upon, Gal. 2:10; to endeavor earnestly, strive, Eph. 4:3
G4704.abbottSmith: to make haste ; hence, to be zealous or eager, to give diligence : c. inf. (v. M, Pr. , 205 f.), Ga 2:10 , Eph 4:3 , I Th 2:17 , II Ti 2:15 4:9 , 21 , Tit 3:12 , He 4:11 , II Pe 1:10 3:14 ; c. acc. et inf. (on this construction v. Mayor in l.; MM, xxiii), II Pe 1:15 (for other constructions v. LS, s.v.).†
G4704.strongs: from G4710 (σπουδή); to use speed, i.e. to make effort, be prompt or earnest
G4704.kjv: do (give) diligence, be diligent (forward), endeavour, labour, study

G2046.thayer: (denoting speech in progress), (a) I say, speak; I mean, mention, tell, (b) I call, name, especially in the pass., (c) I tell, command.
G2046.mounce: to lay, to arrange, to gather; to say, Mt. 1:20; to speak, make an address or speech, Acts 26:1; to say mentally, in thought, Mt. 3:9; Lk. 3:8; to say in written language, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 1:63; Jn. 19:37; to say, as distinguished from acting, Mt. 23:3; to mention, speak of, Mk. 14:71; Lk. 9:31; Jn. 8:27; to tell, declare, narrate, Mt. 21:27; Mk. 10:32; to express, Heb. 5:11; to put forth, propound, Lk. 5:36; 13:6; Jn. 16:29; to mean, to intend to signify, 1 Cor. 1:12; 10:29; to say, declare, affirm, maintain, Mt. 3:9; 5:18; Mk. 12:18; Acts 17:7; 26:22; 1 Cor. 1:10; to enjoin, Acts 15:24; 21:21; Rom. 2:22; to term, designate, cull, Mt. 19:17; Mk. 12:37; Lk. 20:37; 23:2; 1 Cor. 8:5; to call by a name, Mt. 2:23; pass. to be further named, to be surnamed, Mt. 1:16; to be explained, interpreted, Jn. 4:25; 20:16, 24; in NT σὺ λέγεις, you say, a form of affirmative answer to a question Mt. 27:11; Mk. 15:2; Jn. 18:37
G2046.strongs: probably a fuller form of G4483 (ῥέω); an alternate for G2036 (ἔπω) in certain tenses; to utter, i.e. speak or say

G1831.mounce: to go or come out of; to come out, Mt. 5:26; 8:34; to proceed, emanate, take rise from, Mt. 2:6; 15:18; 1 Cor. 14:36; to come abroad, 1 Jn. 4:1; to go forth, go away, depart, Mt. 9:31; Lk. 5:8; to escape, Jn. 10:39; to pass away, come to an end, Acts 16:19
G1831.abbottSmith: to go, or come out of : Mt 10:11 , Mk 1:35 , Jo 13:30 , al.; c. inf., Mt 11:8 , Mk 3:21 , Lk 7:25 , 26 Ac 20:8 ; id. seq. ἐπί , Mt 26:55 , al.; εἰς , Mk 1:38 ; ἵνα , Re 6:2 ; ἐ. seq. ἐκ (cl. c. gen. loc.), Mk 5:2 , Jo 4:30 , al.; ἔξω , c. gen., Mt 21:17 , Mk 14:68 , Ac 16:13 , He 13:13 ; ἀπό , Mk 11:12 , Lk 9:5 , Phl 4:15 ; ἐκεῖθεν , Mt 15:21 , Mk 6:1 , Lk 9:4 , al.; of demons expelled, seq. ἐκ (ἀ
G1831.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to issue (literally or figuratively)
G1831.kjv: come (forth, out), depart (out of), escape, get out, go (abroad, away, forth, out, thence), proceed (forth), spread abroad

G3911.thayer: I turn aside, carry away, remove, cause to pass away; pass: I am misled, seduced.
G3911.mounce: to carry past; to cause to pass away, Mk. 14:36; Lk. 22:42; pass. to be swept along, Jude 12; to be led away, misled, seduced, Heb. 13:9*
G3911.abbottSmith: to bring to , set before , esp. of food (Hdt., al.).
G3911.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate forms); to bear along or aside, i.e. carry off (literally or figuratively); by implication, to avert

G1463.thayer: I clothe myself (originally: I tie round in a knot).
G1463.mounce: pr. to put on a garment which is to be tied; in NT to put on, clothe one’s self with; met. 1 Pet. 5:5*
G1463.abbottSmith: to put on oneself , as a garment, gird on : ἀλλήλοις τ. ταπεινοφροσύνην (as for service, RV, cf. Thayer, s.v., but cf. also ICC , in l.), I Pe 5:5 .†
G1463.strongs: middle voice from G1722 (ἐν) and (to gird); to engirdle oneself (for labor), i.e. figuratively (the apron as being a badge of servitude) to wear (in token of mutual deference)

G5409.mounce: to bear; to wear, Mt. 11:8; Jn. 19:5; Rom. 13:4; 1 Cor. 15:49; Jas. 2:3
G5409.abbottSmith: frequent. of φέρω , denoting repeated or habitual action (cf. Tr., Syn. , § lviii), most commonly used of clothing, weapons, etc., to bear constantly, wear : Mt 11:8 , Jo 19:5 , Ro 13:4 , I Co 15:49 , Ja 2:3 .†
G5409.strongs: from G5411 (φόρος); to have a burden, i.e. (by analogy) to wear as clothing or a constant accompaniment

G2814.mounce: a branch, shoot, twig, esp. of the vine, Jn. 15:2, 4-6*
G2814.strongs: from G2806 (κλάω); a limb or shoot (as if broken off)

G3142.mounce: testimony, evidence, Acts 4:33; 2 Cor. 1:12; Jas. 5:3; in NT testimony, mode of solemn declaration, Mt. 8:4; Lk. 9:5; testimony, matter of solemn declaration, 1 Cor. 1:6; 2:1; 1 Tim. 2:6; σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου, a title of the Mosaic tabernacle, Acts 7:44; Rev. 15:5
G3142.abbottSmith: a testimony , witness , proof (Hdt., Thuc., al.): c. gen. subj., II Co 1:12 , II Th 1:10 ; c. gen. obj., Ac 4:33 , I Co 1:6 2:1 (WH, txt., R, txt., μυστήριον ; II Ti 1:8 ; εἰς μ. , Mt 8:4 10:18 Mt 24:14 , Mk 1:44 6:11 13:9 , Lk 5:14 9:5 21:13 , He 3:5 , Ja 5:3 ; τὸ μ. καιροῖς ἰδίοις ( CGT , in l.), I Ti 2:6 ; ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μ. (LXX for אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד ), Ac 7:44 , Re 15:5 .†
G3142.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3144 (μάρτυς); something evidential, i.e. (genitive case) evidence given or (specially), the Decalogue (in the sacred Tabernacle)

G4404.thayer: early in the morning, at dawn.
G4404.mounce: in the morning, early, Mt. 16:3; 20:1; Mk. 15:1; Acts 28:23; the morning watch, which ushers in the dawn, Mk. 13:35
G4404.abbottSmith: in the morning , early : Mt 16:3 (R, txt.) 21:18 , Mk 1:35 11:20 13:35 15:1 16:[9] , Jo 18:28 20:1 ; λίαν π. , Mk 16:2 ; ἅμα π. , Mt 20:1 ; ἀπὸ π. (cf. ἀπὸ πρωΐθεν , Ex 18:13 ), Ac 28:23 .†
G4404.strongs: adverb from G4253 (πρό); at dawn; by implication, the day-break watch

G9.thayer: the Abilenian territory, the territory of Abila (in Syria), a small principality in the mountains of Damascus.
G9.mounce: Abilene, a district of the Syrian Decapolis; from Abila, the chief town, Lk. 3:1*
G9.strongs: of foreign origin (compare H058); Abilene, a region of Syria

G5216.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G5216.abbottSmith: thou, you , gen., σοῦ , dat., σοί , acc., σέ , pl., ὑμεῖς, -ῶν, -ῖν, -ᾶς (enclitic in oblique cases sing., except after prep. (BL, §48, 3), though πρὸς σέ occurs in Mt 25:39 ). Nom. for emphasis or contrast: Jo 1:30 , 42 4:10 5:33 , 39 , 44 , Ac 4:7 , Eph 5:32 ; so also perhaps σὺ εἶπας , Mt 26:64 , al. (M, Pr. , 86); before voc., Mt 2:6 , Lk 1:76 , Jo 17:5 , al.; sometimes without emphasis (M, Pr
G5216.strongs: genitive case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); of (from or concerning) you

G5317.thayer: Peleg, son of Eber, and one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G5317.mounce: Peleg, also spelled Φάλεγ, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:35*
G5317.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06389); Phalek (i.e. Peleg), a patriarch

G747.mounce: a chief, leader, prince, Acts 5:31; a prime author, Acts 3:15; Heb. 2:10; 12:2*
G747.abbottSmith: beginning , originating : more freq., as subst.; founder , author (Lat. auchor ; so sometimes in π. , v. MM, s.v.; Milligan, NTD , 75): Ac 3:15 (R, mg.), He 2:10 (R, txt.; but v. Westc., in l., and Page, Ac 3:15 ). prince , leader (so in MGr., v. Kennedy, Sources , 153): Ac 3:15 (R, txt.) 5:31 , He 2:10 (cf. R, mg.) 12:2 (Cremer, 117).†
G747.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G71 (ἄγω); a chief leader

G2412.thayer: suitable to a sacred character, reverent.
G2412.abbottSmith: suited to a sacred character , reverend (RV, reverent ): Tit 2:3 (cf. Tr., Syn. , § xcii).†
G2412.strongs: from G2413 (ἱερός) and the same as G4241 (πρέπω); reverent

G3742.mounce: piety, sacred observance of all duties towards God, holiness, Lk. 1:75; Eph. 4:24*
G3742.abbottSmith: piety , holiness : assoc. with δικαιοσύνη , Lk 1:75 , Eph 4:24 .†

G4659.mounce: of a stern, morose, sour, gloomy, or dejected countenance, Mt. 6:16; Lk. 24:17*
G4659.abbottSmith: of a gloomy countenance : Mt 6:16 , Lk 24:17 .†
G4659.strongs: from (sullen) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); angry-visaged, i.e. gloomy or affecting a mournful appearance

G965.mounce: Bethlehem, indecl., a town in Palestine. See PhotoGuide.
G965.abbottSmith: Bethlehem , a town 6 m. S. of Jerusalem: Mt 2:1 , 5 , 6 , 8 , 16 , Lk 2:4 , 15 , Jo 7:42 .†
G965.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01036); Bethleem (i.e. Beth-lechem), a place in Palestine

G171.thayer: unseasonably, out of due season, inopportunely.
G171.abbottSmith: out of season , unseasonably : opp. to εὐκαίρως (q.v.), II Ti 4:2 (cf. Cremer, 740; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G171.strongs: adverb from the same as G170 (ἀκαιρέομαι); inopportunely

G1127.thayer: (a) I am awake (in the night), watch, (b) I am watchful, on the alert, vigilant.
G1127.mounce: to be awake, to watch, Mt. 26:38, 40, 41; Mk. 14:34; 37, 38; to be alive, 1 Thess. 5:10; met. to be watchful, attentive, vigilant, circumspect, Mt. 25:13; Mk. 13:35, et al.
G1127.abbottSmith: to be awake ; metaph., of being alive, I Th 5:10 . to watch : Mt 24:43 26:38 , 40 Mk 13:34 14:34 , 37 Lk 12:37 , 39; metaph., Mt 24:42 25:13 26:41, Mk 13:35 , 37 14:38, Ac 20:31 , I Co 16:13 , I Th 5:6 , I Pe 5:8 , Re 3:2 , 3 16:15 ; seq. ἐν , Col 4:2 . (Cf. δια-γρηγορέω .†
G1127.strongs: from G1453 (ἐγείρω); to keep awake, i.e. watch (literally or figuratively)

G48.thayer: I cleanse, purify, either ceremonially, actually, or morally.
G48.mounce: to purify; to purify morally, reform, to live like one under a vow of abstinence, as the Nazarites
G48.abbottSmith: to purify , cleanse from defilement ; ceremonially: Jo 11:55 , Ac 21:24 , 26 24:18 ; morally: Ja 4:8 , I Pe 1:22 , I Jo 3:3 .†
G48.strongs: from G53 (ἁγνός); to make clean, i.e. (figuratively) sanctify (ceremonially or morally)

G1620.thayer: (a) I put out or expose a child, (b) mid: I set forth, expound, explain.
G1620.mounce: pr. to place outside, put forth; to expose an infant, Acts 7:21; met. to set forth, declare, explain, Acts 11:4; 18:26; 28:23*
G1620.abbottSmith: to set out, expose : Ac 7:21 , Metaph., to set forth, expound : Ac 11:4 28:23 ; c. acc rei, Ac 18:26 .†
G1620.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5087 (τίθημι); to expose; figuratively, to declare

G4634.thayer: the feast of tabernacles, the great festival of the Jews, held in October.
G4634.mounce: pr. a pitching of tents or booths; hence, the feast of tabernacles or booths, instituted in memory of the forty years’ wandering of the Israelites in the desert, and as a season of gratitude for the ingathering of harvest, celebrated for eight days, commencing on the 15th of Tisri, Jn. 7:2*
G4634.abbottSmith: prop., the setting up of tents or dwellings (Arist.); in LXX and NT, ἡ σ., ἡ ἑορτή τῆς σ. (called also ἑορτὴ σκηνῶν , Le 23:34 , al.; v. Deiss., LAE , 116 f.), the feast of Tabernacles : Jo 7:2 .†
G4634.strongs: from G4636 (σκῆνος) and G4078 (πήγνυμι); the Festival of Tabernacles (so called from the custom of erecting booths for temporary homes)

G5022.strongs: apparently a primary word (compare H08450, "steer"); a bullock

G657.thayer: I withdraw from, take leave of, renounce, send away.
G657.mounce: middle: to take leave of, bid farewell to, Lk. 9:61; Acts 18:18, 21; 2 Cor. 2:13; to dismiss, send away, Mk. 6:46; fig: to renounce, forsake, Lk. 14:33
G657.abbottSmith: to set apart . Mid., in late Gk. (Bl., § 37, 1; Swete, Mk. , 136 f.; MM, s.v.), c. dat., to take leave of : Mk 6:46 , Lk 9:61 , Ac 18:18 , 21 , II Co 2:13 ; to forsake : Lk 14:33 .†
G657.strongs: middle voice from G575 (ἀπό) and G5021 (τάσσω); literally, to say adieu (by departing or dismissing); figuratively, to renounce
G657.kjv: bid farewell, forsake, take leave, send away

G1676.mounce: in the Greek language, Jn. 19:20; Acts 21:37*
G1676.abbottSmith: in Greek : Jo 19:20 ; Ἑ. (sc. λαλεῖν ) γινώσκεις , Ac 21:37 (cf. Field, Notes , 135).†
G1676.strongs: adverb from the same as G1675 (Ἑλληνιστής); Hellenistically, i.e. in the Grecian language

G5199.mounce: sound, in health, Mt. 12:13; 15:31; met. of doctrine, sound, pure, wholesome, Tit. 2:8
G5199.abbottSmith: sound, whole, healthy : Mt 12:13 15:31 , Jo 5:[4] , 6 , 9 , 11 , 14 , 15 7:23 , Ac 4:10 ; seq. ἀπό , Mk 5:34 ; of words, opinions, etc. (as in cl.), metaph., λόγος , Tit 2:8 .†
G5199.strongs: from the base of G837 (αὐξάνω); healthy, i.e. well (in body); figuratively, true (in doctrine)

G4194.abbottSmith: Pontius , the prænomen of Pilate (v.s. Πειλᾶτος ), Mt 27:2 (Rec., WH, mg.), Lk 3:1 , Ac 4:27 , I Ti 6:13 .†
G4194.strongs: of Latin origin; apparently bridged; Pontius, a Roman

G2174.mounce: to be animated, encouraged, in good spirits, Phil. 2:19*
G2174.abbottSmith: to be of good courage : Phl 2:19 .†
G2174.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G5590 (ψυχή); to be in good spirits, i.e. feel encouraged

G2224.thayer: I gird, put on the girdle, especially as preparatory to active work.
G2224.mounce: also spelled ζωννύω, to gird, gird on, put on one’s girdle, Jn. 21:18 (2x), Acts 12:8*
G2224.abbottSmith: to gird : c. acc. pers., Jo 21:18 . Mid., to gird oneself : Ac 12:8 (cf. ἀνα- , δια- , περι- , ὑπο-ζώννυμι ).†
G2224.strongs: from G2223 (ζώνη); to bind about (especially with a belt)

G4752.thayer: warfare, military service; of Christian warfare.
G4752.mounce: a military expedition, campaign; and genr. military service, warfare; met. the Christian warfare, 2 Cor. 10:4; fight, 1 Tim. 1:18*
G4752.abbottSmith: an expedition, a campaign, warfare : metaph., II Co 10:4 ( -τιά , T), I Ti 1:18 .†
G4752.strongs: from G4754 (στρατεύομαι); military service, i.e. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger)

G1508.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G3361 (μή); if not
G1508.kjv: but, except (that), if not, more than, save (only) that, saving, till

G3330.thayer: (lit: I offer by way of change, offer so that a change of owner is produced), I share; sometimes merely: I impart, bestow.
G3330.mounce: to give a part, to share, Lk. 3:11; to impart, bestow, Rom. 1:11; 12:8; Eph. 4:28; 1 Thess. 2:8*
G3330.abbottSmith: to give a share of , impart : c. dat. pers. et acc. rei (in cl. more freq., c. gen. part., but acc. of that which is imparted, whether part or whole, so here, v. Bl., § 36, 1), Ro 1:11 , I Th 2:8 , and (with ellipse of acc.) Lk 3:11 ; c. dat. pers., Eph 4:28 ; absol., ὁ μεταδιδούς , Ro 12:8 .†
G3330.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give over, i.e. share

G4355.thayer: (a) I take to myself, (b) I take aside, (c) I welcome.
G4355.mounce: to take to one’s self, assume, take as a companion or associate, Acts 17:5; 18:26; to take, as food, Acts 27:33, 36; to receive kindly or hospitably, admit to one’s society and friendship, treat with kindness, Acts 28:2; Rom. 14:1, 3; 15:7; Phlm. 17; to take or draw to one’s self as a preliminary to an address of admonition, Mt. 16:22; Mk. 8:32*
G4355.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take to oneself, i.e. use (food), lead (aside), admit (to friendship or hospitality)

G4742.abbottSmith: a tattoed mark or brand : τὰ σ. τοῦ Ἰησοῦ , Ga 6:17 (v. Lft., in l.; Deiss., BS , 349; LAE , 303; MM, xxiii).†
G4742.strongs: from a primary (to "stick", i.e. prick); a mark incised or punched (for recognition of ownership), i.e. (figuratively) scar of service

G2308.abbottSmith: in colloq. and MGr. = τὸ θέλειν , will : He 2:4 .†
G2308.strongs: from G2309 (θέλω); determination (properly, the act), i.e. option

G4451.mounce: a burning, conflagration, Rev. 18:9, 18; met. a fiery test of trying circumstances, 1 Pet. 4:12*
G4451.strongs: from G4448 (πυρόω); ignition, i.e. (specially), smelting (figuratively, conflagration, calamity as a test)

G5093.mounce: precious, costly, of great price, 1 Cor. 3:12; Rev. 18:12; precious, dear, valuable, Acts 20:24; 1 Pet. 1:7, 19; honored, esteemed, respected, Acts 5:34; Heb. 13:4
G5093.strongs: from G5092 (τιμή); valuable, i.e. (objectively) costly, or (subjectively) honored, esteemed, or (figuratively) beloved
G5093.kjv: dear, honourable, (more, most) precious, had in reputation

G4116.abbottSmith: broad : Mt 7:13 . As subst., ἡ π. (sc. ὁδός ), [in LXX chiefly for רְחֹב ;] a street : Mt 6:5 12:19 (LXX) , Mk 6:56 , WH, mg., Lk 10:10 13:26 14:21 , Ac 5:15 , Re 11:8 21:21 22:2 .†
G4116.strongs: from G4111 (πλάσσω); spread out "flat" ("plot"), i.e. broad

G637.abbottSmith: to wash off : v.l. for πλύνω , Lk 5:2 , Rec.†
G637.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4150 (πλύνω); to rinse off

G1193.mounce: made of skin, leathern, Mt. 3:4; Mk. 1:6*
G1193.abbottSmith: of skin, leathern : Mt 3:4 , Mk 1:6 (cf. IV Ki 1:8 ).†

G5158.thayer: (a) way, manner, (b) manner of life, character.
G5158.mounce: a turn; mode, manner, way, Jude 7; ὁν τρόπον, + καθ’ ὁν τρόπον, in which manner, as, even as, Mt. 23:37; Acts 15:11; κατὰ μηδένα τρόπον, in no way, by no means, 2 Thess. 2:3; ἐν παντὶ τρόπῳ, and παντὶ τρόπῳ in every way, by every means, Phil. 1:18; 2 Thess. 3:16; turn of mind or action, habit, disposition, Heb. 13:5
G5158.abbottSmith: a way, manner, fashion : Mt 23:37 , Lk 13:34 , Ac 1:11 7:28 , II Ti 3:8 , Ju 7 ; καθ’ ὃν τ. , Ac 15:11 27:25 (cf. MM, xxv); κατὰ πάντα τ. , Ro 3:2 ; κατὰ μηδένα τ. ( IV Mac 4:24 , al.), II Th 2:3 ; ( ἐν ) παντὶ τ. , Phl 1:18 , II Th 3:16
G5158.strongs: from the same as G5157 (τροπή); a turn, i.e. (by implication) mode or style (especially with preposition or relative prefix as adverb, like); figuratively, deportment or character
G5158.kjv: (even) as, conversation, (+ like) manner, (+ by any) means, way

G132.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Ænĕas, an Israelite

G4381.abbottSmith: a respecter of persons : Ac 10:34 (on this group of cognate forms, v. Mayor, ja. , 78 f.; and cf. Thackeray, Gr. , 44).†
G4381.strongs: from G4383 (πρόσωπον) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); an accepter of a face (individual), i.e. (specially), one exhibiting partiality

G391.thayer: dealing with other men, conduct, life, behavior, manner of life.
G391.mounce: conversation, mode of life, conduct, deportment, Gal. 1:13
G391.abbottSmith: a turning down or back , a wheeling about (Soph., Thuc., al.).

G1598.thayer: I put to the test, make trial of, tempt, try.
G1598.mounce: to tempt, put to the test, Mt. 4:7; Lk. 4:12; 1 Cor. 10:9; to try, sound, Lk. 10:25*
G1598.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3985 (πειράζω); to test thoroughly

G307.thayer: I draw up, as a net to shore.
G307.mounce: to cause to come up or ascend, draw or bring up, Mt. 13:48*
G307.abbottSmith: to make go up , draw up , as a ship (Xen.): σαγήνην , Mt 13:48 (metaph., MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G307.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a derivative of the base of G939 (βάσις); to cause to go up, i.e. haul (a net)

G1965.mounce: one who violates his oath, perjured, 1 Tim. 1:10*
G1965.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3727 (ὅρκος); on oath, i.e. (falsely) a forswearer

G2314.mounce: fighting against God, in conflict with God, Acts 5:39*
G2314.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and G3164 (μάχομαι); an opponent of deity

G1447.thayer: in the Hebrew, or rather, in the Aramaic dialect.
G1447.abbottSmith: in Hebrew : Re 9:11 ; elsewhere, in Aramaic (v. supr.): Jo 5:2 19:13 , 17 , 20 20:16 , Re 16:16 .†
G1447.strongs: adverb from G1446 (Ἑβραΐς); Hebraistically or in the Jewish (Chaldee) language

G1961.thayer: (a) I remain, tarry, (b) I remain in, persist in.
G1961.mounce: to stay longer, prolong a stay, remain on, Acts 10:48; 15:34; to continue, persevere, Jn. 8:7; Acts 12:16; to adhere to, continue to embrace, Acts 13:43; Rom. 11:22; to persist in, Rom. 6:1; 1 Cor. 16:8
G1961.abbottSmith: to stay on, tarry or abide still : seq. έν , I Co 16:8 ; αὐτοῦ , there, Ac 15:34 (WH, txt., RV, txt., omit) 21:4 ; c. dat., τ. σαρκί , Phl 1:24 ; seq. παρά , c. dat. pers., Ac 28:14 ; πρός , c. acc pers., I Co 16:7 , Ga 1:18 ; c. acc temp., Ac 10:48 21:4 , 10 28:12 , 14 , I Co 16:7 . Metaph., to continue in a pursuit or state: c. dat., τ. ἀμαρτίᾳ , Ro 6:1 ; τ. ὰπιστίᾳ , 11:23 ; τ. πίστει , Col 1:2
G1961.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3306 (μένω); to stay over, i.e. remain (figuratively, persevere)

G625.strongs: probably from the same as G624 (Ἀπολλωνία); Apollos, an Israelite

G4062.mounce: to turn about; to bring round into any state, Acts 26:24*
G4062.abbottSmith: to turn about , turn : c. acc. et, dat., Ac 26:24 (cf. FlJ, Ant. , ix, 4, 4).†
G4062.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to turn around, i.e. (mentally) to craze

G5126.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5126.strongs: accusative case singular masculine of G3778 (οὗτος); this (person, as objective of verb or preposition)

G2794.abbottSmith: danger , peril : Ro 8:35 ; pl., II Co 11:26 .†

G1081a.abbottSmith: of fruit, produce of the earth : Mt 26:29 , Mk 14:25 , Lk 12:18 22:18 , II Co 9:10 (Rec. γέννημα ).†

G4414.thayer: one who stands in the front rank, hence: a leader, ringleader, chief.
G4414.mounce: pr. one stationed in the first rank of an army; a leader; a chief, ringleader, Acts 24:5*
G4414.abbottSmith: prop., of soldiers, one who stands first , one in the front rank (Thuc., Xen.); hence, metaph., a leader : Ac 24:5 .†
G4414.strongs: from G4413 (πρῶτος) and G2476 (ἵστημι); one standing first in the ranks, i.e. a captain (champion)

G2396.mounce: the imperative of εἶδον used as a particle, Lo! Behold! Jn. 16:29; 19:4, 5
G2396.abbottSmith: prop., 2 aor. imperat. of ὁράω , q.v.
G2396.strongs: second person singular imperative active of G1492 (εἴδω); used as an interjection to denote surprise; lo!

G2114.thayer: I keep up spirit, am cheerful, am of good courage.
G2114.mounce: to be cheerful, be in good spirits, take courage, Acts 27:22, 25; Jas. 5:13*
G2114.strongs: from G2115 (εὔθυμος); to cheer up, i.e. (intransitively) be cheerful; neuter comparative (adverbially) more cheerfully

G2391.thayer: (Hebrew), Jared, son of Mahalalel and father of Enoch.
G2391.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03382); Jared (i.e. Jered), an antediluvian

G1247.thayer: I wait at table (particularly of a slave who waits on guests); I serve (generally).
G1247.mounce: to wait, attend upon, serve, Mt. 8:15; Mk. 1:31; Lk. 4:39; to be an attendant or assistant, Acts 19:22; to minister to, relieve, assist, or supply with the necessaries of life, provide the means of living, Mt. 4:11; 27:55; Mk. 1:13; 15:41; Lk. 8:3; to fill the office of διάκονος, deacon, perform the duties of deacon, 1 Tim. 3:10, 13; 1 Pet. 4:11; to convey in charge, administer, 2 Cor. 3:3; 8:19, 20; 1 Pet. 1:12; 4:10; pass. to receive service, Mt. 20:28; Mk. 10:45
G1247.abbottSmith: generally, to minister, serve, wait upon , especially at table, to do one a service, care for one's needs : absol., Mt 20:28 , Mk 10:45 , Lk 10:40 22:26 , 27 , Jo 12:2 , I Pe 4:11 ; ὅσα διηκόνησεν , II Ti 1:18 ; c. dat. pers., Mt 4:11 8:15 25:44 27:55 , Mk 1:13 , 31 15:41 , Lk 4:39 8:3 12:37 17:8 , Jo 12:26 , Ac 6:2 19:22 , Ro 15:25 , Phm 13 , He 6:10 .
G1247.strongs: from G1249 (διάκονος); to be an attendant, i.e. wait upon (menially or as a host, friend, or (figuratively) teacher); technically, to act as a Christian deacon
G1247.kjv: (ad-)minister (unto), serve, use the office of a deacon

G5267.thayer: answerable to, liable to (brought under) the judgment of.
G5267.mounce: under a legal process; also, under a judicial sentence; under verdict to an opposed party in a suit, liable to penalty, Rom. 3:19*
G5267.abbottSmith: brought to trial, answerable to : c. dat. pers., τ. θεῷ , Ro 3:19 (v. MM, xxv).†
G5267.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1349 (δίκη); under sentence, i.e. (by implication) condemned

G5175.thayer: Trogyllium, a promontory somewhat to the south of Ephesus.
G5175.abbottSmith: Trogyllium , a city of Ionia: Ac 20:15 (WH, txt., R, txt., om.).†
G5175.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Trogyllium, a place in Asia Minor

G2679.thayer: I dig down under, demolish, undermine.
G2679.mounce: pr. to dig down under, undermine; by impl. to overthrow; demolish, raze, Rom. 11:3; τὰ κατεσκαμμένα, ruins, Acts 15:16*
G2679.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4626 (σκάπτω); to undermine, i.e. (by implication) destroy

G2217.mounce: gloom, thick darkness, Heb. 12:18; 2 Pet. 2:4, 17; Jude 6, 13*
G2217.abbottSmith: in Hom. the gloom of the under-world ; hence, darkness , deep gloom (poët. and late prose writers): He 12:18 , II Pe 2:4 , 17 , Ju 6 , 13 .†
G2217.strongs: akin to the base of G3509 (νέφος); gloom (as shrouding like a cloud)

G4010.thayer: Pergamum, an important city of the Roman province Asia.
G4010.mounce: Pergamus, a city of Mysia, in Asia Minor, Rev. 1:11; 2:12*. See PhotoGuide.
G4010.abbottSmith: Pergamum , a city of Mysia: Re 1:11 2:12 .†
G4010.strongs: from G4444 (πύργος); fortified; Pergamus, a place in Asia Minor

G1427.thayer: twelve; the usual way in which the Twelve apostles of Jesus are referred to.
G1427.mounce: twelve, Mt. 9:20; 10:1; οἱ δώδεκα, the twelve apostles, Mt. 26:14, 20
G1427.abbottSmith: numeral, twelve : Mt 9:20 10:1 , al.; οἱ δ. , the apostles , Mt 10:5 , Mk 4:10 , al.; in Ac 19:7 24:11 , for Rec. δεκαδύο.
G1427.strongs: from G1417 (δύο) and G1176 (δέκα); two and ten, i.e. a dozen

G5043.mounce: a child, a son or daughter, Mt. 2:18; Lk. 1:7; pl. descendants, posterity, Mt. 3:9; Acts 2:39; child, son, as a term of endearment, Mt. 9:2; Mk. 2:5; 10:24; pl. children, inhabitants, people, of a city, Mt. 23:37; Lk. 19:44; from the Hebrew, met. a child or son in virtue of discipleship, 1 Cor. 4:17; 1 Tim. 1:2; 2 Tim. 1:2; Tit. 1:4; Phlm. 10; 3 Jn. 4; a child in virtue of gracious acceptance, Jn. 1:12; 11:52; Rom. 8:16, 21; 1 Jn. 3:1; a child in virtue of spiritual conformity, Jn. 8:39; Phil. 2:15; 1 Jn. 3:10; a child of, one characterized by some condition or quality, Mt. 11:19; Eph. 2:3; 5:8; 1 Pet. 1:14; 2 Pet. 2:14
G5043.abbottSmith: that which is begotten, born (cf. Scottish bairn ), a child of either sex: Mk 13:12 , Lk 1:7 , Ac 7:5 ; pl., Mt 7:11 , Mk 7:27 , Lk 1:17 , Eph 6:1 , al.; τέκνα ἐπαγγελίας , Ro 9:8 ; τ. τῆς σαρκός , ib. ; in a wider sense (as Heb. בָּנִים ), of posterity, Mt 2:18 , Lk 3:8 , al.; specif., of a male child, Mt 21:28 , Ac 21:21 , a,l.; in voc. as a form of kindly address from an elder to a junior or fr
G5043.strongs: from the base of G5098 (τιμωρία); a child (as produced)

G1012.mounce: counsel, purpose, design, determination, decree, Lk. 7:30; 23:51, et al. freq.; by impl. secret thoughts, cogitations of the mind, 1 Cor. 4:5
G1012.abbottSmith: counsel, purpose (in cl., esp. of the gods): Lk 23:51 , Ac 4:28 5:38 19:1 27:12 , 42 ; pl., I Co 4:5 ; of the Divine purpose, He 6:17 ; τ. βουλὴν τ. θελήματος αὐτοῦ , Eph 1:11 ; β. τ. Θεοῦ , Lk 7:30 , Ac 2:23 13:36 20:27 .†
G1012.strongs: from G1014 (βούλομαι); volition, i.e. (objectively) advice, or (by implication) purpose
