G766.thayer: (outrageous conduct, conduct shocking to public decency, a wanton violence), wantonness, lewdness.
G766.mounce: intemperance; licentiousness, lasciviousness, Rom. 13:13; insolence, outrageous behavior, Mk. 7:22
G766.abbottSmith: licentiousness , wantonness , excess : Mk 7:22 , Ro 13:13 , II Co 12:21 , Ga 5:19 (Lft., in l.), Eph 4:19 , I Pe 4:3 , II Pe 2:2 , 7 , 18 , Ju 4 .†
G766.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed (of uncertain derivation, but apparently meaning continent); licentiousness (sometimes including other vices)

G2944.mounce: to encircle, surround, encompass, come around. Jn. 10:24; Acts 14:20; spc. to lay siege to, Lk. 21:20; to march round, Heb. 11:30*
G2944.abbottSmith: to move in a circle , revolve .
G2944.strongs: from the same as G2945 (κύκλῳ); to encircle, i.e. surround

G3580.mounce: to receive and entertain strangers, exercise hospitality, 1 Tim. 5:10*
G3580.strongs: from a compound of G3581 (ξένος) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to be hospitable

G3750.mounce: smell, the sense of smelling, 1 Cor. 12:17*
G3750.abbottSmith: the sense of smell , smelling : I Co 12:17 .†
G3750.strongs: from a derivative of G3605 (ὄζω); smell (the sense)

G172.mounce: free from evil, innocent, blameless; simple, Rom. 16:18; Heb. 7:26*
G172.abbottSmith: as in cl. (Æsch., Plat., al.), of persons, simple , guileless : Ro 16:18 , He 7:26 (cf. Cremer, 327); of things, undamaged (? MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G172.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2556 (κακός); not bad, i.e. (objectively) innocent or (subjectively) unsuspecting

G1485.thayer: a custom, habit; an institute, rite.
G1485.mounce: a custom, usage, habit, Lk. 2:42; 22:39; an institute, rite, Lk. 1:9; Acts 6:14; 15:1
G1485.abbottSmith: habit, custom ; Lk 22:39 , Jo 19:40 , Ac 16:21 25:16 , He 10:25 ; in Lk 1:9 2:42 , Ac 6:14 15:1 21:21 26:3 28:17 , almost in the narrower sense of law (Deiss., BS , 251 f.).†
G1485.strongs: from G1486 (ἔθω); a usage (prescribed by habit or law)

G837.thayer: (a) I cause to increase, become greater (b) I increase, grow.
G837.abbottSmith: trans., to make to grow : I Co 3:6 , 7 , II Co 9:10 . Pass., to grow , increase , become greater : Mt 13:32 , Mk 4:8 , II Co 10:15 , Col 1:6 ; τῇ ἐπιγνώσει τ. θεοῦ , Col 1:10 ; εἰς σωτηρίαν , I Pe 2:2 .
G837.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb; to grow ("wax"), i.e. enlarge (literal or figurative, active or passive)

G198.thayer: I learn carefully, inquire with exactness.
G198.mounce: to inquire accurately or diligently, Mt. 2:7, 16 (see v. 8)*
G198.abbottSmith: to enquire with exactness , learn carefully : Mt 2:7 , 16 (for similar ex., v. MM, VGT , sv.).†
G198.strongs: from the same as G196 (ἀκριβέστατος); to be exact, i.e. ascertain

G4873.mounce: to recline with someone at table, Mt. 9:10; 14:9; Mk. 2:15; 6:22; Lk. 7:49; 14:10, 15*
G4873.abbottSmith: to recline with or together at table: Mt 14:9 , Mk 6:22 , Lk 7:49 14:15 ; c. dat. pers., Mt 9:10 , Mk 2:15 , Lk 14:10 .†
G4873.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G345 (ἀνακεῖμαι); to recline in company with (at a meal)
G4873.kjv: sit (down, at the table, together) with (at meat)

G3826.mounce: the whole multitude together, all at once, Lk. 23:18*
G3826.abbottSmith: = cl. παμπληθές , with the whole multitude , all together : Lk 23:18 .†
G3826.strongs: dative case (adverb) of a compound of G3956 (πᾶς) and G4128 (πλῆθος); in full multitude, i.e. concertedly or simultaneously

G474.thayer: I throw at in opposition, exchange (words) with, perhaps: I compare.
G474.mounce: pr. to throw or toss from one to another; met. to agitate, to converse or discourse about, Lk. 24:17*
G474.abbottSmith: to throw in turn , exchange : metaph., λόγους (cf. Lat. conferre sermones ; v. Field, Notes , 81), Lk 24:17 .†
G474.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G906 (βάλλω); to bandy

G482.thayer: I take hold of, help, share in, partake of, enjoy.
G482.mounce: to aid, assist, help, Lk. 1:54; Acts 20:35; to be a recipient, 1 Tim. 6:2*
G482.abbottSmith: to take instead of or in turn . Mid., c. gen., to take hold of ; of persons, to help (v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 1:54 , Ac 20:35 ; of things, to partake of : I Ti 6:2 (v. Field, Notes , 210; Cremer, 386; and cf. συν-αντιλαμβάνω ).†
G482.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and the middle voice of G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take hold of in turn, i.e. succor; also to participate

G2258.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2258.strongs: imperfect of G1510 (εἰμί); I (thou, etc.) was (wast or were)
G2258.kjv: + agree, be, X have (+ charge of), hold, use, was(-t), were

G3919.thayer: I bring in secretly, am at hand.
G3919.abbottSmith: to lead in by one's side , bring forward , introduce .
G3919.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1521 (εἰσάγω); to lead in aside, i.e. introduce surreptitiously

G4964.mounce: to fashion in accordance with; mid/pass. to conform or assimilate one’s self to, met. Rom. 12:2; 1 Pet. 1:14*
G4964.abbottSmith: to conform to (Arist.). Pass., to be conformed to, conform oneself to : Ro 12:2 , I Pe 1:14 .†
G4964.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4976 (σχῆμα); to fashion alike, i.e. conform to the same pattern (figuratively)
G4964.kjv: conform to, fashion self according to

G4562.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08286); Saruch (i.e. Serug), a patriarch

G4074.mounce: a stone; in NT the Greek rendering of the surname Cephas, given to the Apostle Simon, and having, therefore, the same sense as πέτρα, Peter, Mt. 4:18; 8:14
G4074.abbottSmith: Simon Peter , the Apostle: Mt 4:18 10:2 , Mk 3:16 , Lk 5:8 , Jo 1:41 , 43 , al.
G4074.strongs: apparently a primary word; a (piece of) rock (larger than G3037 (λίθος)); as a name, Petrus, an apostle

G2131.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G3529 (νίκη); victorious; Eunice, a Jewess

G4225.thayer: where, somewhere, anywhere; with numerals: somewhere about.
G4225.strongs: genitive case of an indefinite pronoun (some) otherwise obsolete (compare G4214 (πόσος)); as adverb of place, somewhere, i.e. nearly

G4619.abbottSmith: = σιτευτός , fattened ; as subst., pl., τὰ σ. , fatlings : Mt 22:4 .†
G4619.strongs: from a derivative of G4621 (σῖτος); grained, i.e. fatted

G2714.thayer: before the face of, over against.
G2714.mounce: can function as an improper prep., in the presence of, in the sight of, before; Eph. 1:4; Col. 1:22; Jude 24*
G2714.abbottSmith: over against , before : c. gen., τ. δόξης , Ju 24 ; metaph. (cf. κατέναντι , and v. Lft., Col 1:22 ), τ. θεοῦ , Eph 1:4 , Col 1:22 .†
G2714.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1799 (ἐνώπιον); directly in front of
G2714.kjv: before (the presence of), in the sight of

G294.mounce: also spelled ἀμφιέζω and ἀμφιέννυμι, to clothe, invest, Mt. 6:30; 11:8; Lk. 7:25*
G294.strongs: from the base of G297 (ἀμφότερος) and (to invest); to enrobe

G5250.thayer: I abound exceedingly, am exceedingly abundant.
G5250.mounce: to superabound, be in exceeding abundance, over exceed, 1 Tim. 1:14*
G5250.abbottSmith: to abound exceedingly : I Ti 1:14 (Ps Sol 5:19; Herm., Mand. , v, 2, 5).†
G5250.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4121 (πλεονάζω); to superabound

G1175.thayer: religion in general; in a bad sense: superstition.
G1175.mounce: fear of the gods; in a bad sense, superstition; a form of religious belief, Acts 25:19*
G1175.abbottSmith: fear of the gods ; piety, religion ; superstition . Objectively, a religion : Ac 25:19 (Cremer, 72, 682).†
G1175.strongs: from the same as G1174 (δεισιδαιμονέστερος); religion

G1314.mounce: to keep or guard carefully or with vigilance; to guard, protect, Lk. 4:10*
G1314.abbottSmith: to guard carefully (M, Pr. , 116), defend : c. acc, Lk 4:10 (LXX) .†
G1314.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5442 (φυλάσσω); to guard thoroughly, i.e. protect

G1274.abbottSmith: to accomplish fully, finish, complete : Ac 21:7 (EV).
G1274.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and (to effect); to accomplish thoroughly

G4931.thayer: I bring to an end, fulfill, accomplish.
G4931.mounce: pr. to bring to an end altogether; to finish, end, Lk. 4:13; to consummate, Rom. 9:28; to ratify a covenant, Heb. 8:8; pass. to be terminated, Lk. 4:2; Acts 21:27; to be fully realized, Mk. 13:4*
G4931.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5055 (τελέω); to complete entirely; generally, to execute (literally or figuratively)

G183.abbottSmith: that cannot be restrained : Ja 3:8 Rec. (for -στατος , q.v.).†
G183.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2722 (κατέχω); unrestrainable

G2968.mounce: a village, a country town, Mt. 9:35; 10:11; Lk. 8:1
G2968.abbottSmith: a village or country town , prop, as opp. to a walled city: Mt 14:15 , Mk 6:6 , al.; πόλεις καὶ κ. , Mt 9:35 , al.; with the name added, Βηθλεέμ , Jo 7:42 ; Βηθανΐα , Jo 11:1 ; with the name of the district, τὰς κ. Καισαρίας , Mk 8:27 ; Σαμαρειτῶν , Lk 9:52 , Ac 8:25 .
G2968.strongs: from G2749 (κεῖμαι); a hamlet (as if laid down)

G4769.mounce: a pillar, column, Rev. 10:1; used of persons of authority, influence, etc., a support or pillar of the Church, Gal. 2:9; Rev. 3:12; a support of true doctrine, 1 Tim. 3:15*
G4769.abbottSmith: a pillar , regarded especially as a support: σ. πυρός , Re 10:1 ; σ. ἐν τ. ναῷ , fig., Re 3:12 , Metaph., Ga 2:9 ; τ. ἀληθείας , I Ti 3:15 .†
G4769.strongs: from (to stiffen; properly akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι)); a post ("style"), i.e. (figuratively) support

G1741.mounce: honored, 1 Cor. 4:10; notable, memorable, Lk. 13:17; splendid, gorgeous, Lk. 7:25; in unsullied array, Eph. 5:27*
G1741.abbottSmith: held in honour, of high repute : I Co 4:10 .
G1741.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1391 (δόξα); in glory, i.e. splendid, (figuratively) noble

G4785.thayer: I am voted or classed with.
G4785.mounce: to count, number with, be chosen together with, Acts 1:26*
G4785.abbottSmith: to condemn with or together (Plut., Themist. , 21).
G4785.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G5585 (ψηφίζω); to count down in company with, i.e. enroll among

G1213.mounce: to render manifest or evident; to make known, to tell, relate, declare, 1 Cor. 1:11; Col. 1:8; to show, point out, bring to light, 1 Cor. 3:13; to indicate, signify, Heb. 9:8; 12:27; 1 Pet. 1:11
G1213.abbottSmith: to make plain, declare : c. acc., I Co 3:13 , Col 1:8 , He 9:8 12:27 ; c. dat. pers., II Pe 1:14 ; c. dat. pers., seq. περί (pass.), I Co 1:11 ; seq. εἰς , I Pe 1:11 .†
G1213.strongs: from G1212 (δῆλος); to make plain (by words)

G1539.strongs: as if from G1538 (ἕκαστος) and G5119 (τότε); at every time

G945.thayer: I chatter, am long-winded, utter empty words, stammer, repeat.
G945.mounce: also spelled βαττολογέω, pr. to stammer; hence, to babble; to use vain repetitions, Mt. 6:7*
G945.strongs: from (a proverbial stammerer) and G3056 (λόγος); to stutter, i.e. (by implication) to prate tediously

G2806.mounce: to break off; in NT to break bread, Mt. 14:19; with figurative reference to the violent death of Christ, 1 Cor. 11:24
G2806.abbottSmith: to break , break in pieces : of bread, Mt 14:19 15:36 26:26 , Mk 8:6 14:22 , Lk 22:19 24:30 , Ac 2:46 20:7 , 11 27:35 , I Co 10:16 11:24 ; seq. εἰς , c. acc. pers., Mk 8:19 (cf. ἐκ- , κατα-κλάω ).†
G2806.strongs: a primary verb; to break (specially, of bread)

G80.thayer: a brother, member of the same religious community, especially a fellow-Christian.
G80.mounce: a brother, near kinsman or relative; one of the same nation or nature; one of equal rank and dignity; an associate, a member of the Christian community
G80.abbottSmith: in cl., a brother , born of the same parent or parents. [In LXX (Hort, Ja. , 102 f.), for אָח ;] lit. of a brother ( Ge 4:2 , al.). Of a neighbour ( Le 19:17 ). Of a member of the same nation ( Ex 2:14 , De 15:3 ). In NT in each of these senses (1. Mt 1:2 , al.; 2. Mt 7:3 ; 3. Ro 9:3 ) and also, of a fellow-Christian: I Co 1:1 , Ac 9:30 . This usage finds illustration in π. , where ἀ. is used of m
G80.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a connective particle) and (the womb); a brother (literally or figuratively) near or remote (much like G1 (Α))

G1079.strongs: feminine of a presumed derivative of the base of G1074 (γενεά); birth

G1057.thayer: a Galilean, an inhabitant of Galilee.
G1057.mounce: a native of Galilee, Mt. 26:69; Mk. 14:70; Lk. 13:1; Jn. 4:45; Acts 1:11
G1057.strongs: from G1056 (Γαλιλαία); Galilean or belonging to Galilea

G2494.strongs: probably for G2491 (Ἰωάννης) or G2495 (Ἰωνᾶς); Jonan, an Israelite

G1107.thayer: I make known, declare, know, discover.
G1107.mounce: to make known, reveal, declare, Jn. 15:15; 17:26, et al.; to know, Phil. 1:22
G1107.abbottSmith: as most commonly in cl., to come to know, discover, know : Phl 1:22 (but cf. R, mg.). to make known : c. acc., Ro 9:22 , 23 ; c. acc. rei da.t. pers., Lk 2:15 , Jo 15:15 17:26 , Ac 2:28 (LXX) , I Co 15:1 , II Co 8:1 , Ga 1:11 , Eph 1:9 6:19 , 21 Col 4:7 , 9 II Pe 1:16 ; c. dat., seq. ὅτι , I Co 12:3 ; seq. τί , Col 1:27 ; περί , Lk 2:17 . Pass., Ac 7:13 , Ro 16:26 , Eph 3:3 , 6 , 10 , Phl 4:6 (Cre
G1107.strongs: from a derivative of G1097 (γινώσκω); to make known; subjectively, to know
G1107.kjv: certify, declare, make known, give to understand, do to wit, wot

G3048.thayer: a collection, collecting (of money), particularly of an irregular local contribution for religious purposes.
G3048.strongs: from G3056 (λόγος) (in the commercial sense); a contribution

G1251.thayer: I hear throughout, of a judicial hearing.
G1251.mounce: to hear a thing through; to hear judicially, Acts 23:35*
G1251.abbottSmith: to hear through, hear fully ; technically, to hear judicially (as De, l.c. ; of. Deiss., BS , 230): Ac 23:35 .†
G1251.strongs: middle voice from G1223 (διά) and G191 (ἀκούω); to hear throughout, i.e. patiently listen (to a prisoner's plea)

G2425.thayer: (a) considerable, sufficient, of number, quantity, time, (b) of persons: sufficiently strong (good, etc.), worthy, suitable, with various constructions, (c) many, much.
G2425.mounce: befitting; sufficient, enough, Lk. 22:38; ἱκανὸν ποιεῖν τινί, to satisfy, gratify, Mk. 15:15; τὸ ἱκανὸν λαμβάνειν, to take security or bail of any one, Acts 17:9; or persons, adequate, competent, qualified, 2 Cor. 2:16; fit, worthy, Mt. 3:11; 8:8; of number or quantity, considerable, large, great, much, and pl. many, Mt. 28:12; Mk. 10:46
G2425.abbottSmith: of persons, suffcient , competent , fit : c. inf., Mt 3:11 , Mk 1:7 , Lk 3:16 , I Co 15:9 , II Co 3:5 , II Ti 2:2 ; seq. πρός , II Co 2:16 ; seq. ἵνα , Mt 8:8 , Lk 7:6 .
G2425.strongs: from ( or , akin to G2240 (ἥκω)) (to arrive); competent (as if coming in season), i.e. ample (in amount) or fit (in character)
G2425.kjv: able, + content, enough, good, great, large, long (while), many, meet, much, security, sore, sufficient, worthy

G12.thayer: the abyss, unfathomable depth, an especially Jewish conception, the home of the dead and of evil spirits.
G12.mounce: bottomless; place of the dead, Lk. 8:31; Rom. 10:7
G12.abbottSmith: in cl., boundless, bottomless (e.g. ἄ. πέλαγος, ἄ. πλοῦτος Æsch.).
G12.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a variation of G1037 (βυθός); depthless, i.e. (specially) (infernal) "abyss"

G2553.mounce: to suffer evil or afflictions, 2 Tim. 2:9; to be afflicted, troubled, dejected, Jas. 5:13; in NT to show endurance in trials and afflictions, 2 Tim. 4:5*
G2553.abbottSmith: to suffer evil , endure affliction : II Ti 2:9 4:5 , Ja 5:13 .†
G2553.strongs: from the same as G2552 (κακοπάθεια); to undergo hardship
G2553.kjv: be afflicted, endure afflictions (hardness), suffer trouble

G1602.thayer: I sail out (of harbor), sail away.
G1602.mounce: to sail out of or from a place, Acts 15:39; 18:18; 20:6*
G1602.abbottSmith: to sail away : Ac 20:6 ; seq. εἰς , Ac 15:39 18:18 .†
G1602.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4126 (πλέω); to depart by ship

G4155.thayer: I choke, throttle, strangle; hence: I drown.
G4155.mounce: to stifle, suffocate, choke, Mk. 5:13; to seize by the throat, Mk. 13:17; 18:28*
G4155.abbottSmith: to choke : c. acc., Mt 13:7 (WH, mg.); impf. (conative), Mt 18:28 ; of drowning, pass., Mk 5:13 (cf. ἀπο- , ἐπι- , συν-πνίγω ).†
G4155.strongs: strengthened from G4154 (πνέω); to wheeze, i.e. (causative, by implication) to throttle or strangle (drown)

G4089.mounce: bitter, Jas. 3:11; met. bitter, harsh, Jas. 3:14*
G4089.strongs: perhaps from G4078 (πήγνυμι) (through the idea of piercing); sharp (pungent), i.e. acrid (literally or figuratively)

G4729.thayer: (I keep some one in a tight place), I press upon, cramp, restrain.
G4729.mounce: to crowd together into a narrow place, straiten; pass. met. to be in straits, to be cooped up, to be cramped from action, 2 Cor. 4:8; to be cramped in feeling, 2 Cor. 6:12*
G4729.abbottSmith: to be straitened (cf. Is 49:19 ); metaph., to be anxious (Hipp., al.).
G4729.strongs: from the same as G4730 (στενοχωρία); to hem in closely, i.e. (figuratively) cramp

G3090.mounce: Lois, pr. name of a woman, 2 Tim. 1:5*
G3090.strongs: of uncertain origin; Lois, a Christian woman

G2911.mounce: a hanging steep, precipice, a steep bank, Mt. 8:32; Mk. 5:13; Lk. 8:33*
G2911.abbottSmith: a steep bank : Mt 8:32 , Mk 5:13 , Lk 8:33 .†
G2911.strongs: from G2910 (κρεμάννυμι); overhanging, i.e. a precipice

G5385.thayer: love of wisdom, philosophy, in the NT of traditional Jewish theology.
G5385.mounce: a love of science; systematic philosophy; in NT the philosophy of the Jewish gnosis, Col. 2:8*
G5385.abbottSmith: the love and pursuit of wisdom ; hence, philosophy, investi­gation of truth and nature: of the so-called philosophy of false teachers, Col 2:8 (v. Lft., ICC , in l.).†

G2799.thayer: I weep, weep for, mourn, lament.
G2799.mounce: intrans. to weep, shed tears, Mt. 26:75; Mk. 5:38, 39; Lk. 19:41; 23:28; trans. to weep for, bewail, Mt. 2:18
G2799.abbottSmith: of any loud expression of pain or sorrow, esp. for the dead, to weep , lament ; intrans.: Mk 5:38 , 39 14:72 (M, Pr. , 131) Mk 16:[10] , Lk 7:13 , 32 , 38 8:52 , Jo 11:31 , 33 16:20 20:11 , 13 , 15 Ac 9:39 21:13 , I Co 7:30 , Phl 3:18 , Ja 4:9 5:1 , Re 5:5 18:15 , 19 ; πολύ , Re 5:4 ; πολλά , Ac 8:24 (WH, mg.); πικρῶς , Mt 26:75 , Lk 22:62 ; opp. to γελάω , Lk 6:21 , 25 ; χαίρω , Ro 12:15 ; seq. ἐ
G2799.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to sob, i.e. wail aloud (whereas 1145 is rather to cry silently)

G75.thayer: I am struggling, striving (as in an athletic contest or warfare); I contend, as with an adversary.
G75.mounce: to be a combatant in the public games; to contend, fight, strive earnestly
G75.abbottSmith: to contend for a prize : I Co 9:25 , to fight, struggle, strive : Jo 18:36 ; metaph. (MM, VGT , s.v.). Col 1:29 4:12 , I Ti 4:10 6:12 , II Ti 4:7 ; c. inf. (Field, Notes , 66), Lk 13:24 (Cremer, 609).†
G75.strongs: from G73 (ἀγών); to struggle, literally (to compete for a prize), figuratively (to contend with an adversary), or genitive case (to endeavor to accomplish something)

G1567.thayer: I seek out, seek out after, require.
G1567.abbottSmith: to seek out or after, search for : c. acc pers. ( I Mac 9:26 ); fig., τ. κύριον, θεόν (cf. Ps 14:2 , Am 5:4 , al.), Ac 15:17 , Ro 3:11 , He 11:6 ; εὐλογίαν , He 12:17 ; ἐξεζήτησαν κ. ἐξηραύνησαν (as in I Mac l.c.), sought and searched out : I Pe 1:10 .
G1567.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2212 (ζητέω); to search out, i.e. (figuratively)investigate, crave, demand, (by Hebraism) worship

G2047.thayer: a desert place, desert, uninhabited region.
G2047.mounce: a solitude, uninhabited region, waste, desert, Mt. 15:33; Mk. 8:4; 2 Cor. 11:26; Heb. 11:38*
G2047.abbottSmith: a solitude, wilderness : Mt 15:33 , Mk 8:4 , II Co 11:26 , He 11:38 .†

G2415.mounce: a native of Jerusalem, Mk. 1:5; Jn. 7:25*
G2415.abbottSmith: an inhabitant of Jerusalem : Mk 1:5 , Jo 7:25 .†
G2415.strongs: from G2414 (Ἱεροσόλυμα); a Hierosolymite, i.e. inhabitant of Hierosolyma

G1065.thayer: an enclitic, emphasizing particle: at least, indeed, really, but generally too subtle to be represented in English.
G1065.mounce: an enclitic particle imparting emphasis; indicating that a particular regard is to be had to the term to which it is attached. Its force is to be conveyed, when this is possible, by various expression; at least, indeed, even
G1065.abbottSmith: indeed, at least, even (but not always translatable into English); used alone: Lk 11:8 18:5 Ro 8:32 . More freq. with other particles: αλλά γε , Lk 24:21 , I Co 9:2 ; ἄρα γε , Mt 7:20 17:26 , Ac 17:27 ; ἆρά γε , Ac 8:30 ; εἴ γε (Rec. εἴγε ), II Co 5:3 , Ga 3:4 , Eph 3:2 4:21 , Col 1:23 (v. Meyer, Ellic., on Ga, Eph, ll. c.; Lft., on Ga, Col, ll. c.); εἰ δὲ μήγε , following an affirmation, Mt 6:1 ,
G1065.strongs: a primary particle of emphasis or qualification (often used with other particles prefixed)
G1065.kjv: and besides, doubtless, at least, yet

G1395.strongs: from the base of G1325 (δίδωμι); a giver

G5451.thayer: a plant, that which is planted.
G5451.mounce: plantation, the act of planting; a plant, met. Mt. 15:13*
G5451.strongs: from G5452 (φυτεύω); trans-planting, i.e. (concretely) a shrub or vegetable

G5502.mounce: also spelled Χερουβείν and Χερουβίμ, indecl. cherub, a two-winged figure over the ark of the covenant, Heb. 9:5*
G5502.abbottSmith: Cherubim : He 9:6 (cf. Ex 25:17 (18) , al.; v. DB , i, 377 ff.).†
G5502.strongs: plural of Hebrew origin (H03742); "cherubim" (i.e. cherubs or kerubim)

G3618.thayer: I erect a building, build; fig. of the building up of character: I build up, edify, encourage.
G3618.abbottSmith: to build a house , to build : absol., Lk 11:48 14:30 17:28 ; οἱ οἰκοδομοῦντες , the builders (as Ps 117 (118):22 , הַבּוֹנִים ), Mt 21:42 , Mk 12:10 , Lk 20:17 , I Pe 2:7 (LXX) ; ἐπ’ ἀλλότριον θεμέλιον οἰ. , proverb., Ro 15:20 ; c. acc. rei, Ga 2:18 ; πύργον , Mt 21:33 , Mk 12:1 , Lk 14:28 ; ἀποθήκας , Lk 12:18 ; ναόν , Mk 14:58 ; pass., Jo 2:20 ; οἰκία , Lk 6:48 ; c. acc. rei seq. dat. pers. (cf.
G3618.strongs: from the same as G3619 (οἰκοδομή); to be a house-builder, i.e. construct or (figuratively) confirm

G5254.mounce: pr. to hold under; to render, undergo, suffer, Jude 7*
G5254.abbottSmith: to hold or put under . Metaph., to undergo, suffer : δίκην , Ju 7 (Soph., Eur., al.).†
G5254.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself under, i.e. endure with patience

G1646.thayer: least, smallest, but perhaps oftener in the weaker sense: very little, very small.
G1646.mounce: used as the superlative of μικρός, smallest, least, Mt. 2:6; 5:19
G1646.abbottSmith: smallest, least : as proper superlat., I Co 15:9 ; elsewhere, as usually in late Gk., intensive (B1., § 11, 3); Mt 2:6 (LXX) 25:40 , 45 , Lk 12:26 16:10 19:17 , I Co 4:3 6:2 , Ja 3:4 ; ἐ. ἐν τ. βασιλείᾳ τ. οὐρανῶν , Mt 5:19 (v. Dalman, Words , 113). Compar., ἐλαχιστότερος (for corresp. superl., v. LS; v. also Bl., § 44, 3); less than the least : Eph 3:8 .†
G1646.strongs: superlative of (short); used as equivalent to G3398 (μικρός); least (in size, amount, dignity, etc.)

G4340.mounce: opportune, in NT continuing for a limited time, temporary, transient, Mt. 13:21; Mk. 4:17; 2 Cor. 4:18; Heb. 11:25*
G4340.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2540 (καιρός); for the occasion only, i.e. temporary
G4340.kjv: dur-(eth) for awhile, endure for a time, for a season, temporal

G3973.thayer: (a) act: I cause to cease, restrain, hinder, (b) mid: I cease, stop, leave off.
G3973.mounce: to cause to pause or cease, restrain, prohibit, 1 Pet. 3:10; mid. perf. πέπαυται, to cease, stop, leave off, desist, refrain, 1 Pet. 4:1
G3973.abbottSmith: to make to cease , restrain , hinder : c. acc. rei, seq. ἀπό , I Pe 3:10 (LXX) . Mid., to cease , leave off : Lk 8:24 11:1 , Ac 20:1 , I Co 13:8 ; c. ptcp., Lk 5:4 , Ac 5:42 6:13 13:10 20:31 21:32 , Eph 1:16 , Col 1:9 , He 10:2 ; c. gen., ἀμαρτίας , I Pe 4:1 (WH, mg., R, txt.); c. dat., I Pe 4:1 (WH, txt., R, mg.).†
G3973.strongs: a primary verb ("pause"); to stop (transitively or intransitively), i.e. restrain, quit, desist, come to an end

G4382.mounce: respect of persons, partiality, Rom. 2:11; Eph. 6:9; Col. 3:25; Jas. 2:1*
G4382.abbottSmith: respect of persons : Ro 2:11 , Eph 6:9 , Col 3:25 , Ja 2:1 (v. Mayor, in l.).†

G2540.thayer: fitting season, season, opportunity, occasion, time.
G2540.mounce: pr. fitness, proportion, suitableness; a fitting situation, suitable place, 1 Pet. 4:17; a limited period of time marked by a suitableness of circumstances, a fitting season, 1 Cor. 4:5; 1 Tim. 2:6; 6:15; Tit. 1:3; opportunity, Acts 24:25; Gal. 6:10; Heb. 11:15; a limited period of time marked by characteristic circumstances, a signal juncture, a marked season, Mt. 16:3; Lk. 12:56; 21:8; 1 Pet. 1:11; a destined time, Mt. 8:29; 26:18; Mk. 1:15; Lk. 21:24; 1 Thess. 5:1; a season in ordinary succession, equivalent to ὥρα, Mt. 13:30; Acts 14:17; in NT a limited time, a short season, Lk. 4:13; simply, a point of time, Mt. 11:25; Lk. 13:1
G2540.abbottSmith: due measure , fitness , proportion (Eur., Xen., al.).
G2540.strongs: of uncertain affinity; an occasion, i.e. set or proper time
G2540.kjv: X always, opportunity, (convenient, due) season, (due, short, while) time, a while

G2895.thayer: a bed, mattress, mat of a poor man.
G2895.mounce: also spelled κράββατος, mattress, pallet, bed, Mk. 2:4, 9, 11f.; 6:55; Jn. 5:8-11; Acts 5:15; 9:33*
G2895.abbottSmith: a camp bed , pallet : Mk 2:4 , 9 , 11 , 12 6:55 , Jo 5:8-11 , Ac 5:15 9:33 (v. Swete, Mk. , 2:4 , and cf. κλίνη ).†
G2895.strongs: probably of foreign origin; a mattress

G226.thayer: I say (speak) truth, do truth, maintain truth (the truth).
G226.mounce: to speak or maintain the truth; to act truly or sincerely, Gal. 4:16; Eph. 4:15*
G226.abbottSmith: to speak the truth (R, mg., deal truly ; Field, Notes , 192): Ga 4:16 , Eph 4:15 .†
G226.strongs: from G227 (ἀληθής); to be true (in doctrine and profession)

G946.thayer: an abominable thing, an accursed thing.
G946.mounce: an abomination, an abominable thing, Mt. 24:15; Mk. 13:14; idolatry with all its pollution, Lk. 16:15; Rev. 17:4, 5; 21:27*
G946.abbottSmith: an abomination , a detestable thing : Lk 16:15 , Re 17:4 , 5 21:27 ; τὸ β. τ. ἐρημώσεως ( Da LXX 12:11 , cf . I Mac 1:54 ; DB , i, 12 f.; DCG , i, 6 f.), Mk 13:14 , Mt 24:15 (Cremer, 138).†
G946.strongs: from G948 (βδελύσσω); a detestation, i.e. (specially) idolatry

G4337.thayer: (a) I attend to, pay attention to, (b) I beware, am cautious, (c) I join, devote myself to.
G4337.mounce: to have in addition; to hold to, bring near; absol. to apply the mind to a thing, to give heed to, attend to, observe, consider, Acts 5:35; Heb. 2:1; 2 Pet. 1:19; to take care of, provide for, Acts 20:28; when followed by ἀπό, μή, or μήποτε, to beware of, take heed of, guard against, Mt. 6:1; 7:15; to assent to, yield credence to, follow, adhere or be attached to, Acts 8:6, 10, 11; 16:14; to give one’s self up to, be addicted to, engage in, be occupied with, 1 Tim. 1:4, 3:8
G4337.abbottSmith: to turn to , bring to (freq. ναῦν , expressed or understood, to bring to port , land ; Hdt., al.).
G4337.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2192 (ἔχω); (figuratively) to hold the mind (3563 implied) towards, i.e. pay attention to, be cautious about, apply oneself to, adhere to
G4337.kjv: (give) attend(-ance, -ance at, -ance to, unto), beware, be given to, give (take) heed (to unto); have regard

G2732.strongs: from a derivative of G2730 (κατοικέω); a dwelling-place

G1139.thayer: I am possessed, am under the power of an evil-spirit or demon.
G1139.mounce: in NT to be possessed, afflicted, vexed, by a demon or evil spirit, i.q. δαιμόνιον ἔχειν, Mt. 4:24; 8:16, 28, 33
G1139.abbottSmith: to be under the power of a δαίμων ( δαιμόνιον , q.v.), to be possessed : Mt 15:22 ; elsewhere always ptcp., -ιζόμενος , -ισθείς : Mt 4:24 ; 8:16 , 28 , 33 9:32 12:22 , Mk 1:32 5:15 , 16 , 18 , Lk 8:36 , Jo 10:21 (Cremer, 171).†
G1139.strongs: middle voice from G1142 (δαίμων); to be exercised by a dæmon
G1139.kjv: have a (be vexed with, be possessed with) devil(-s)

G858.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and (in the sense of a stone as stubbing the foot); smoothness, i.e. (figuratively) simplicity

G4020.thayer: I overdo, am a busybody, waste my labor about (a thing).
G4020.mounce: to do a thing with excessive or superfluous care; to be a busybody, 2 Thess. 3:11*
G4020.abbottSmith: to waste one's labour about a thing.
G4020.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2038 (ἐργάζομαι); to work all around, i.e. bustle about (meddle)

G2239.mounce: pr. a place of customary resort; hence, a settled habit of mind and manners, 1 Cor. 15:33*
G2239.strongs: a strengthened form of G1485 (ἔθος); usage, i.e. (plural) moral habits

G4153.thayer: spiritually, in a spiritual way; from a spiritual point of view.
G4153.mounce: spiritually, through spiritual views and affections, 1 Cor. 2:14; spiritually, in a spiritual sense, allegorically, Rev. 11:8*
G4153.abbottSmith: spiritually ; by the aid of the Holy Spirit: I Co 2:13 (WH, mg.) , 14 ; in a spiritual sense: Re 11:8 .†
G4153.strongs: adverb from G4152 (πνευματικός); non-physically, i.e. divinely, figuratively

G244.thayer: one who meddles in things alien to his calling or in matters belonging to others; factious.
G244.mounce: pr. one who meddles with the affairs of others, a busybody in other men’s matters; factious, 1 Pet. 4:15*
G244.abbottSmith: one who meddles in things alien to his calling : I Pe 4:15 (v. ICC , in l.; Deiss., BS, 224-4; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G244.strongs: from G245 (ἀλλότριος) and G1985 (ἐπίσκοπος); overseeing others' affairs, i.e. a meddler (specially, in Gentile customs)

G4642.mounce: dry, hard to the touch; met. harsh, severe, stern, Mt. 25:24; vehement, violent, fierce, Jas. 3:4; grievous, painful, Acts 26:14; grating to the mind, repulsive, offensive, Jn. 6:60; stubborn, resistance to authority, Jude 15*
G4642.abbottSmith: hard to the touch, rough, harsh , (opp. to μαλακός ); metaph., in various uses;
G4642.strongs: from the base of G4628 (σκέλος); dry, i.e. hard or tough (figuratively, harsh, severe)

G3548.mounce: legislation; ἡ νομοθεσία, the gift of the Divine law, or the Mosaic law itself, Rom. 9:4*
G3548.strongs: from G3550 (νομοθέτης); legislation (specially, the institution of the Mosaic code)

G4823.thayer: I give advice, exhort; mid: I take counsel together, consult.
G4823.mounce: to counsel, advise, exhort, Jn. 18:14; Rev. 3:18; mid. to consult together, plot, Mt. 26:4; Acts 9:23*
G4823.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1011 (βουλεύω); to give (or take) advice jointly, i.e. recommend, deliberate or determine

G1484.thayer: a race, people, nation; the nations, heathen world, Gentiles.
G1484.mounce: a multitude, company, Acts 17:26; 1 Pet. 2:9; Rev. 21:24; a nation, people, Mt. 20:25; 21:43; pl. ἔθνη, from the Hebrew, nations or people as distinguished from the Jews, the heathen, Gentiles, Mt. 4:15; 10:5; Lk. 2:32
G1484.abbottSmith: a multitude, a company , whether of beasts or men (Hom.). a nation, people : Mt 21:43 24:7 , Mk 13:8 , Lk 22:25 , Ac 10:35 , al.; in sing., of the Jewish people, Lk 7:5 23:2 , Jo 11:48 , 50-53 18:35 , Ac 10:22 24:3 , 10 26:4 28:19 . In pl., as in OT, τὰ ἔ. (like Heb. הַגּוֹיִם ), the nations , as distinct from Israel, Gentiles : Mt 4:15 6:32 , Ac 26:17 , Ro 3:29 11:11 15:10 , Ga 2:8 , al.; of Gent
G1484.strongs: probably from G1486 (ἔθω); a race (as of the same habit), i.e. a tribe; specially, a foreign (non-Jewish) one (usually, by implication, pagan)

G2015.mounce: appearance, manifestation, 1 Tim. 6:14; 2 Tim. 1:10; glorious display, 2 Thess. 2:8; 2 Tim. 4:1, 8; Tit. 2:13*
G2015.abbottSmith: a manifestation, appear­ance : II Th 2:8 , I Ti 6:14 , II Ti 1:10 4:1 , 8 , Tit 2:13 (cf. M, Th. , 148 f.).†
G2015.strongs: from G2016 (ἐπιφανής); a manifestation, i.e. (specially) the advent of Christ (past or future)

G5100.thayer: any one, some one, a certain one or thing.
G5100.mounce: enclitic, indefinite pronoun, a certain one, someone, Mt. 12:47; pl. some, certain, several, Lk. 8:2; Acts 9:19; 2 Pet. 3:16; one, a person, Mt. 12:29; Lk. 14:8; Jn. 6:50; combined with the name of an individual, one, Mk. 15:21; as it were in a manner, a kind of, Heb. 10:27; Jas. 1:18; any whatever, Mt. 8:28; Lk. 11:36; Rom. 8:39; τις, somebody of consequence, Acts 5:36; τι, something of consequence, Gal. 2:6; 6:3; τι, anything at all, anything worth account, 1 Cor. 3:7; 10:19; τι at all, Phil. 3:15; Phlm. 18
G5100.abbottSmith: As subst., one, a certain one : Lk 9:49 , Jo 11:1 , Ac 5:25 , al.; pl., τίνες , certain, some : Lk 13:1 , Ac 15:1 , Ro 3:8 , al. someone, anyone, something, anything : Mt 12:29 , Mk 9:30 , Lk 8:46 , Jo 2:25 , Ac 17:25 , Ro 5:7 , al.; = indef., one (French on ), Mk 8:4 , Jo 2:25 , Ro 8:24 , al.; pl., τινες , some , Mk 14:4 , al. As adj., a certain : Mt 18:12 , Lk 1:5 8:27 , Ac 3:2 , al.; with prope
G5100.strongs: an enclitic indefinite pronoun; some or any person or object
G5100.kjv: a (kind of), any (man, thing, thing at all), certain (thing), divers, he (every) man, one (X thing), ought, + partly, some (man, -body, - thing, -what), (+ that no-)thing, what(-soever), X wherewith, whom(-soever), whose(-soever)

G3133.thayer: pass: I die, wither (like the grass).
G3133.mounce: to quench, cause to decay, fade, or wither; pass. to wither, waste away, met. to fade away, disappear, perish, Jas. 1:11*
G3133.abbottSmith: prop., to quench fire; pass., of fire, to die away , go out .
G3133.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to extinguish (as fire), i.e. (figuratively and passively) to pass away

G2953.mounce: a Cypriot, an inhabitant of Cyprus, Acts 4:36; 11:20; 21:16*
G2953.strongs: from G2954 (Κύπρος); a Cyprian (Cypriot), i.e. inhabitant of Cyprus

G1696.thayer: I remain (abide) in, abide by, maintain, persevere in.
G1696.mounce: pr. to remain in a place; met. to abide by, to continue firm in, persevere in, Acts 14:22; 28:30; Gal. 3:10; Heb. 8:9*
G1696.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3306 (μένω); to stay in the same place, i.e. (figuratively) persevere

G5103.thayer: Titus, a Greek Christian, helper of Paul, perhaps also brother of Luke.
G5103.mounce: Titus, pr. name, friend and helper of Paul, 2 Cor. 2:13; 7:6; Gal. 2:1; 2 Tim. 4:10; Tit. 1:4
G5103.strongs: of Latin origin but uncertain significance; Titus, a Christian

G5316.thayer: (a) act: I shine, shed light, (b) pass: I shine, become visible, appear, (c) I become clear, appear, seem, show myself as.
G5316.mounce: to cause to appear, bring to light; absol. to shine, Jn. 1:5; 5:35; 2 Pet. 1:19; 1 Jn. 2:8; Rev. 1:16; 8:12; 21:23; mid./pass. to be seen, appear, be visible, Mt. 1:20; 2:7, 13, 19; τὰ φαινόμενα, things visible, things obvious to the senses, Heb. 11:3; φαίνομαι, to appear, seen, be in appearance, Mt. 23:27; Lk. 24:11; to appear in thought, seen in idea, be a notion, Mk. 14:64
G5316.strongs: prolongation for the base of G5457 (φῶς); to lighten (shine), i.e. show (transitive or intransitive, literal or figurative)
G5316.kjv: appear, seem, be seen, shine, X think

G260.thayer: at the same time, therewith, along with, together with.
G260.mounce: also functions as an improper preposition with the genitive (2x), with, together with; at the same time
G260.abbottSmith: at once (Lat. simul ): Ac 24:26 27:40 , Ro 3:12 ( one and all = יַחַד , Ps 14:3 ), Col 4:3 , I Ti 5:13 , Phm 22 ; seq. σύν , I Th 4:17 5:10 ; as prep. c. dat., together with : Mt 13:29 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.); also, c. adv., ἅ. πρωΐ (cl., ἅ. ἕω , etc.), early in the morning : Mt 20:1 .†
G260.strongs: a primary particle; properly, at the "same" time, but freely used as a preposition or adverb denoting close association

G4989.strongs: prolongation for G4986 (Σώπατρος); Sosipatrus, a Christian

G1766.abbottSmith: suborned to lie in wait, lying in wait : as subst., Lk 20:20 .†

G3157.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04977); Matthan (i.e. Mattan), an Israelite

G41.thayer: holiness, sanctity, as an abstract quality.
G41.abbottSmith: sanctity , holiness , regarded, properly, as an abstract quality (v. next word, and cf. Lft. Notes, 49; MM, VGT , s.v.): II Co 1:12 , He 12:10 .†
G41.strongs: from G40 (ἅγιος); sanctity (i.e. properly, the state)

G1551.mounce: pr. to receive from another; to expect, look for, Acts 17:16; to wait for, to wait, 1 Cor. 11:33; 16:11; Heb. 11:10; 10:13; Jas. 5:7*
G1551.abbottSmith: to take or receive from (Hom., Hdt., a1.).
G1551.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to accept from some source, i.e. (by implication) to await

G4583.thayer: I am a lunatic, am moonstruck, epileptic.
G4583.abbottSmith: to be moonstruck , i.e. epileptic (epilepsy being supposed to be influenced by the moon): Mt 4:24 17:15 .†
G4583.strongs: middle voice or passive from a presumed derivative of G4582 (σελήνη); to be moon-struck, i.e. crazy

G321.thayer: I lead up, bring up, offer, produce, put to sea, set sail.
G321.mounce: to conduct; to lead or convey up from a lower place to a higher, Lk. 4:5; to offer up, as a sacrifice, Acts 7:41; to lead out, produce, Acts 12:4; as a nautical term (in the middle or passive), to set sail, put to sea, Lk. 8:22
G321.abbottSmith: to lead or bring up : seq. είς , c. acc. loc., Mt 4:1 , Lk 2:22 4:5 (WH om. είς , κ.τ.λ. ), Ac 9:39 16:34 ; of raising the dead (cl.), ἐκ νεκρῶν , Ro 10:7 , He 13:20 ; to produce and set before , τ. λαῷ , Ac 12:4 (MM, VGT , s.v.); in sacrificial sense (MM, VGT , s.v.), to offer , θυσίαν , Ac 7:41 . Mid., in nautical sense (Hom., Hdt., Thuc., al.), to put to sea : Lk 8:22 , Ac 13:13 16:11 18:21 20:
G321.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead up; by extension to bring out; specially, to sail away
G321.kjv: bring (again, forth, up again), depart, launch (forth), lead (up), loose, offer, sail, set forth, take up

G4187.mounce: in many ways, in various modes, Heb. 1:1*
G4187.abbottSmith: in many ways or manners : He 1:1 (Philo).†
G4187.strongs: adverb from a compound of G4183 (πολύς) and G5158 (τρόπος); in many ways, i.e. variously as to method or form

G2688.mounce: determinate state, behavior, condition; personal appearance, Tit. 2:3*
G2688.strongs: from G2525 (καθίστημι); properly, a position or condition, i.e. (subjectively) demeanor

G2468.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G2468.strongs: second person imperative present of G1510 (εἰμί); be thou
G2468.kjv: + agree, be, X give thyself wholly to

G4868.thayer: I compare (settle) accounts, make a reckoning.
G4868.mounce: to take up a thing with any one; in NT συναίρειν λόγον, to settle accounts, reckon in order to payment, Mt. 18:23, 24; 25:19*
G4868.abbottSmith: to take up together : σ. λόγον (of which there are several exx. in π. ; v. Deiss., LAE , 118 f.; MM, i, xxiv; M, Pr. , 160), to settle accounts , Mt 18:23, 24 ; seq. μετά , C. gen. pers., Mt 25:19 .†
G4868.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G142 (αἴρω); to make up together, i.e. (figuratively) to compute (an account)

G4352.thayer: I go down on my knees to, do obeisance to, worship.
G4352.mounce: to do reverence or homage by kissing the hand; in NT to do reverence or homage by prostration, Mt. 2:2, 8, 11; 20:20; Lk. 4:7; 24:52; to pay divine homage, worship, adore, Mt. 4:10; Jn. 4:20, 21; Heb. 1:6; to bow one’s self in adoration, Heb. 11:21
G4352.abbottSmith: to make obeisance , do reverence to , worship ; prop. (as in cl., of the gods: Hdt., Æsch., Plat., al.), of God, Christ and supra-mundane beings: absol., Jo 4:20 12:20 , Ac 8:27 24:11 , He 11:21 (Westc., in l.), Re 11:1 ; πίπτειν καὶ π. , Re 5:14 ; c. dat. (of the significance of this constr. as compared with the usual cl., c. acc., v. Abbott, JG , 78 f.; JV , 133 ff.), Jo 4:21 , 23 , Ac 7:43 , I
G4352.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and a probable derivative of G2965 (κύων) (meaning to kiss, like a dog licking his master's hand); to fawn or crouch to, i.e. (literally or figuratively) prostrate oneself in homage (do reverence to, adore)

G187.thayer: I reach maturity, become ripe, am in full vigor.
G187.mounce: to flourish, ripen, be in one’s prime, Rev. 14:18*
G187.abbottSmith: to be at the prime ; of produce of the ground, to be ripe (Thuc.): Re 14:18 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G187.strongs: from the same as G188 (ἀκμήν); to make a point, i.e. (figuratively) mature

G4351.thayer: I roll to, roll up against.
G4351.mounce: to roll to or against, Mt. 27:60; Mk. 15:46*
G4351.abbottSmith: to roll up , roll to : c. acc. et dat., Mt 27:60 ; acc. seq. ἐπί , Mk 15:46 .†
G4351.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2947 (κυλιόω); to roll towards, i.e. block against

G4027.mounce: pr. filth; met. refuse, outcast, 1 Cor. 4:13*
G4027.strongs: from a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2508 (καθαίρω); something cleaned off all around, i.e. refuse (figuratively)

G1135.mounce: a woman, Mt. 5:28, et al.; a married woman, wife, Mt. 5:31, 32; 14:3, et al.; in the voc. ὦ γύναι, O woman! an ordinary mode of addressing females under every circumstance; met. used of the Church, as united to Christ, Rev. 19:7; 21:9
G1135.abbottSmith: a woman , married or unmarried: Mt 11:11 14:21 , al.; ὕπανδρος γ. , Ro 7:2 ; γ. χήρα , Lk 4:26 ; in vocat., γύναι implies neither reproof nor severity, but is used freq. as a term of respect and endearment, Mt 15:28 , Jo 2:4 , 4:21 19:26 . a wife : Mt 1:20 , I Co 7:3 , 4 al.; γ. ἀπολύειν , Mk 10:2 , al.; γ. ἔχειν Mk 6:18 ; γ. λαβεῖν , Mk 12:19 ; γ. γαμεῖν , Lk 14:20 . a deaconess , I Ti 3:11 ( CGT
G1135.strongs: probably from the base of G1096 (γίνομαι); a woman; specially, a wife

G3063.thayer: finally, from now on, henceforth, beyond that.
G3063.mounce: remaining; the rest, remainder, Mt. 22:6; as an adv., οῦ λοιποῦ, henceforth, Gal. 6:17; τὸ λοιπόν, or λοιπόν, henceforward, Mt. 26:45; 2 Tim. 4:8; Acts 27:20; as to the rest, besides, 1 Cor. 1:16; finally, Eph. 6:10; ὁ δὲ λοιπόν, but, now, furthermore, 1 Cor. 4:2
G3063.strongs: neuter singular of the same as G3062 (λοιποί); something remaining (adverbially)
G3063.kjv: besides, finally, furthermore, (from) henceforth, moreover, now, + it remaineth, then

G3443.mounce: to leave alone; pass. to be left alone, be lone, 1 Tim. 5:5*
G3443.abbottSmith: to leave alone , forsake : of a childless widow, pf. ptcp. pass., I Ti 5:5 .†
G3443.strongs: from G3441 (μόνος); to isolate, i.e. bereave

G3696.mounce: an implement, Rom. 6:13; pl. τὰ ὅπλα, arms, armor, weapons, whether offensive or defensive, Jn. 18:3; Rom. 13:12; 2 Cor. 6:7; 10:4*
G3696.abbottSmith: a tool , implement , instrument : ὅπλα ἀδικίας (opp. to ὅ. δικαιοσύνης ), Ro 6:13 .
G3696.strongs: probably from a primary (to be busy about); an implement or utensil or tool (literally or figuratively, especially, offensive for war)

G3019.thayer: a Levite, properly a man of the tribe of Levi; hence: a priest's assistant, an under priest, as the members of that tribe were charged with this duty.
G3019.mounce: a Levite, one of the posterity of Levi, Jn. 1:19; Lk. 10:32; Acts 4:36*
G3019.strongs: from G3017 (Λευΐ); a Levite, i.e. descendant of Levi

G5135.strongs: from G5134 (τραῦμα); to inflict a wound

G5244.mounce: in the highest degree, preeminently, especially, superlatively, 2 Cor. 11:5; 12:11*
G5244.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5316 (φαίνω); appearing above others (conspicuous), i.e. (figuratively) haughty

G4446.mounce: scorching and noxious heat; a fever, Mt. 8:15; Mk. 1:31
G4446.abbottSmith: a fever : Mt 8:15 , Mk 1:31 , Lk 4:39 , Jo 4:52 , Ac 28:8 ; π. μέγας , a high fever : Lk 4:38 (on the technical phrase here, v. MM, xxii).†
G4446.strongs: from G4445 (πυρέσσω); inflamed, i.e. (by implication) feverish (as noun, fever)

G65.abbottSmith: of the wild olive (Anth.). As subst., the wild olive : Ro 11:17 , 24 ( CGT , in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G65.strongs: from G66 (ἄγριος) and G1636 (ἐλαία); an oleaster

G5128.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5128.strongs: accusative case plural masculine of G3778 (οὗτος); these (persons, as objective of verb or preposition)

G4064.thayer: I carry around; pass: I am driven to and fro.
G4064.mounce: to bear or carry about, Mk. 6:55; 2 Cor. 4:10; pass. to be borne about hither and thither, driven to and fro, Eph. 4:14*
G4064.abbottSmith: to carry about : Mk 6:55 , II Co 4:10 . Pass., metaph.: Eph 4:14 .†
G4064.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G5342 (φέρω); to convey around, i.e. transport hither and thither

G4879.thayer: I lead away with, carry along with (in good or bad sense according to context); mid: I conform myself willingly to.
G4879.mounce: to lead or carry away with; to seduce; pass. to be led away
G4879.abbottSmith: to lead away with or together ( Ex, l.c. ). Pass., metaph. (as συμπεριφέρομαι , v. LS, s.v.), to be carried away with : c. dat., Ga 2:13 , II Pe 3:17 ; of accommodating one­self to (EV, condescend to ) things or persons (on the meaning, v. ICC , in l.; Field, Notes , 163), Ro 12:16 .†
G4879.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G520 (ἀπάγω); to take off together, i.e. transport with (seduce, passively, yield)

G2984.thayer: (Hebrew), Lamech, son of Methuselah and father of Noah.
G2984.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03929); Lamech (i.e. Lemek), a patriarch

G4596.mounce: see also σηρικός, silk, of silk, silken; τὸ σηρικόν, silken stuff, Rev. 18:12*
G4596.abbottSmith: silk, silken ; as subst., τὸ σ. , silken fabric, silk : Re 18:12 (cf. FlJ, B.J. , vii, 5, 4).†
G4596.strongs: from (an Indian tribe from whom silk was procured; hence the name of the silk-worm); Seric, i.e. silken (neuter as noun, a silky fabric)

G1715.thayer: in front, before the face; sometimes made a subst. by the addition of the article: in front of, before the face of.
G1715.mounce: also an improper prep., before, in front of, Lk. 19:4; Phil. 3:14; before, in the presence of, in the face of, Mt. 5:24; 23:14; before, previous to, Jn. 1:15, 27, 30; from the Hebrew, in the sight or estimation of, Mt. 11:26; 18:14
G1715.abbottSmith: of place (in cl. also of time),
G1715.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4314 (πρός); in front of (in place (literally or figuratively) or time)
G1715.kjv: against, at, before, (in presence, sight) of

G5537.thayer: (originally: I transact business), (a) act. of God: I warn; pass: I am warned by God (probably in response to an inquiry as to one's duty), (b) (I take a name from my public business, hence) I receive a name, am publicly called.
G5537.mounce: to have dealings, transact business; to negotiate; to give answer on deliberation; in NT to utter a divine communication, Heb. 12:25; pass. to be divinely instructed, receive a revelation or warning from God, Mt. 2:12, 22; Lk. 2:26; Acts 10:22; Heb. 8:5; 11:7; intrans. to receive an appellation, Acts 11:26; Rom. 7:3*
G5537.abbottSmith: to transact business , hence, to consult, deliberate (Thuc., Dem., al.); hence in later writers,
G5537.strongs: from G5536 (χρῆμα); to utter an oracle (compare the original sense of G5530 (χράομαι)), i.e. divinely intimate; by implication, (compare the secular sense of G5532 (χρεία)) to constitute a firm for business, i.e. (generally) bear as a title
G5537.kjv: be called, be admonished (warned) of God, reveal, speak

G4349.thayer: a cause of stumbling, offense, shock.
G4349.mounce: pr. a stumbling; offense; in NT an offense, shock, ground of exception, 2 Cor. 6:3*
G4349.abbottSmith: an occasion of stumbling , offence : II Co 6:3 .†
G4349.strongs: from G4350 (προσκόπτω); a stumbling, i.e. (figuratively and concretely) occasion of sin

G1729.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G1210 (δέω) (in the sense of lacking); deficient in

G4493.thayer: a glance, twinkle or flash of the eye; a rush of wind or flame; any rapid movement.
G4493.mounce: pr. a rapid sweep, jerk; a wink, twinkling of the eye, 1 Cor. 15:52*
G4493.abbottSmith: poët. in cl., any rapid movement such as the throw or flight of a javelin, the rush of wind or flame, the flapping of wings, the twinkling of lights; ῥ. ὀφθαλμοῦ , the twinkling of an eye: I Co 15:52 (L, mg., ῥοπή , q.v.).†
G4493.strongs: from G4496 (ῥίπτω); a jerk (of the eye, i.e. (by analogy) an instant)

G3640.mounce: scant of faith, of little faith, one whose faith is small and weak, Mt. 6:30; 8:26; 14:31; 16:18; Lk. 12:28*
G3640.abbottSmith: of little faith or trust : Mt 6:30 8:26 14:31 16:8 , Lk 12:28 .†
G3640.strongs: from G3641 (ὀλίγος) and G4102 (πίστις); incredulous, i.e. lacking confidence (in Christ)

G727.thayer: rapacious, ravenous; a robber, an extortioner.
G727.mounce: pr. raveneous, ravening, as a wild beast, Mt. 7:15; met. rapacious, given to extortion and robbery, an extortioner, Lk. 18:11; 1 Cor. 5:10, 11; 6:10*
G727.abbottSmith: rapacious : Mt 7:15 , Lk 18:11 ; as subst., a swindler , an extortioner (MM, s.v.), I Co 5:10 , 11 6:10 .†

G2557.mounce: an evil-doer, malefactor, criminal, Lk. 23:32, 33, 39; 2 Tim. 2:9*
G2557.abbottSmith: a malefactor , criminal : Lk 23:32 , 33 , 39 , II Ti 2:9 .†
G2557.strongs: from G2556 (κακός) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); a wrong-doer, i.e. criminal

G1623.abbottSmith: as adv., outside, beyond : τὸ ἐ. , c. poss. gen., the outside , Mt 23:26 ; in late Gk. (v. Deiss., BS, 118), pleonastic, ἐκτός εἰ μή , I Co 14:5 15:2 , I Ti 5:19 .

G4389.mounce: to turn forwards; to impel; to excite, urge, exhort, Acts 18:27*
G4389.abbottSmith: to urge forwards , exhort , persuade . Mid., in same sense (as also in cl.): Ac 18:27 .†
G4389.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to turn forward for oneself, i.e. encourage

G1091.mounce: cultivated field or ground, a farm, 1 Cor. 3:9*
G1091.abbottSmith: a field ( Pr 24:5 , 30 , Strabo). cultivation, husbandry, tillage ( Pr 6:7 9:12 , Je 28:23 , Si 27:6 ): I Co 3:9 .†
G1091.strongs: neuter of a (presumed) derivative of G1092 (γεωργός); cultivable, i.e. a farm

G4217.thayer: of what kind, of what manner.
G4217.mounce: Of what country? in NT equivalent to ποῖος, What? Of what manner? Of what kind or sort? Lk. 1:29; 7:37; denoting admiration, What? What kind of? How great? Mt. 8:27; Mk. 13:1
G4217.strongs: apparently from G4219 (πότε) and the base of G4226 (ποῦ); interrogatively, whatever, i.e. of what possible sort

G1583.mounce: to speak out; to tell, utter, divulge, Acts 23:22*
G1583.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2980 (λαλέω); to divulge

G3887.thayer: I remain by, abide with; met: I persevere in.
G3887.mounce: to stay beside; to continue, stay, abide, 1 Cor. 16:6; Heb. 7:23; met. to remain constant in, persevere in, Phil. 1:25; Jas. 1:25*
G3887.abbottSmith: to remain beside or near : He 7:23 , Ja 1:25 ; of remaining alive, I Co 16:6 (WH, κατα- ), Phl 1:25 (cf. συμ-παραμένω ).†
G3887.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3306 (μένω); to stay near, i.e. remain (literally, tarry; or figuratively, be permanent, persevere)

G4166.thayer: a shepherd; hence met: of the feeder, protector, and ruler of a flock of men.
G4166.mounce: one who tends flocks or herds, a shepherd, herdsman, Mt. 9:36; 25:32; met. a pastor, superintendent, guardian, Jn. 10:11, 14, 16
G4166.abbottSmith: a shepherd : Mt 9:36 25:32 26:31 (LXX) , Mk 6:34 14:27 , Lk 2:8 , 15 , 18 , 20 , Jo 10:2 , 12 . Metaph., of Christ; Jo 10:11 , 14 , 16 , He 13:20 , I Pe 2:25 ; of Christian pastors, Eph 4:11 (cf. Hom., Il. , i, 263, ποιμένα λαῶν ).†
G4166.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a shepherd (literally or figuratively)

G4663.mounce: a worm; met. gnawing anguish, Mk. 9:48*
G4663.abbottSmith: a worm : of the kind which preys upon dead bodies, metaph., of fut. punishment, Mk 9:48 (LXX, Is 66:24 ; cf. Si 7:17 , Jth 16:17 ).†
G4663.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a grub, maggot or earth-worm

G1324.thayer: the Twin; Didymus, the Greek name equivalent to Thomas.
G1324.mounce: a twin; Didymus, the Greek equivalent to the name Thomas, Jn. 11:16; 20:24; 21:2*
G1324.abbottSmith: Didymus , surname of the apostle Thomas: Jo 11:16 20:24 21:2 .†
G1324.strongs: prolongation from G1364 (δίς); double, i.e. twin; Didymus, a Christian

G2073.strongs: feminine of an adjective hesperos (evening); the eve (G5610 (ὥρα) being implied)

G5184.thayer: Tyre, an ancient city, the capital of Phoenicia.
G5184.mounce: Tyre, a celebrated and wealthy commercial city of Phoenicia, Mt. 11:21; 15:21; Mk. 7:24; Acts 21:3, 7. See PhotoGuide.
G5184.abbottSmith: Tyre , a maritime city of Phænicia: Mk 7:31 , Ac 21:3 , 7 ; Τ. κ, Σιδών , Mt 11:21 , 22 15:21 , Mk 3:8 7:24 , Lk 6:17 10:13 , 14 .†
G5184.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06865): Tyrus (i.e. Tsor), a place in Palestine

G4751.mounce: pr. the gullet leading to the stomach; hence, later, the stomach itself, 1 Tim. 5:23*
G4751.strongs: from G4750 (στόμα); an orifice (the gullet), i.e. (specially), the "stomach"

G4133.thayer: however, nevertheless, but, except that, yet.
G4133.mounce: can function as an improper prep., besides, except, Mk. 12:32; Acts 8:1; 20:23; as a conj. but, however, nevertheless, Mt. 18:7; Lk. 19:27; Eph. 5:33; equivalent to ἀλλά, Lk. 6:35; 12:31; Acts 27:22
G4133.abbottSmith: introducing a clause (= ἀλλά , δέ ; "it is obvious that πλήν was the regular word in the vulgar language": Bl., § 77, 13), yet , howbeit , only : Mt 11:22 , 24 18:7 26:39 , 64 (M, Pr. , 86), Lk 6:24 , 35 10:11 , 14 , 20 11:41 12:31 13:33 17:1 18:8 19:27 22:21 , 22 , 42 (WH, mg. om.) 23:28 , I Co 11:11 , Eph 5:33 , Phl 3:16 4:14 , Re 2:25 ; π. ὅτι (Hdt., Plat., al.), except that , save that , Ac 20
G4133.strongs: from G4119 (πλείων); moreover (besides), i.e. albeit, save that, rather, yet
G4133.kjv: but (rather), except, nevertheless, notwithstanding, save, than

G3122.mounce: most, most of all, chiefly, especially, Acts 20:38; 25:26
G3122.strongs: neuter plural of the superlative of an apparently primary adverb (very); (adverbially) most (in the greatest degree) or particularly

G2768.thayer: (a) a horn, (b) a horn-like projection at the corner of an altar, (c) a horn as a symbol of power.
G2768.mounce: a horn, Rev. 5:6; 12:3; a horn-like projection at the corners of an altar, Rev. 9:13; from the Hebrew, a horn as a symbol of power, Lk. 1:69
G2768.abbottSmith: a horn : Re 5:6 12:3 13:1 , 11 17:3 , 7 , 12 , 16 ; of the projections at the corners of the altar ( Ex 29:12 , al.): Re 9:13 . Metaph., as symbol of strength, κ. σωτηρίας (cf. Ps 17 (18):3 , al.): Lk 1:69 .†
G2768.strongs: from a primary (the hair of the head); a horn (literally or figuratively)

G5522.mounce: uncontracted form χόος, dust, acc., χοῦν, Mk. 6:11; Rev. 18:19*
G5522.strongs: from the base of G5494 (χειμών); a heap (as poured out), i.e. rubbish; loose dirt

G324.thayer: I welcome, receive kindly; I undertake, assume the responsibility of.
G324.mounce: to receive, as opposed to shunning or refusing; to receive with hospitality, Acts 28:7; to embrace a proffer or promise, Heb. 11:17*
G324.abbottSmith: to assume , undertake (in π. freq. as legal term: MM, VGT , s.v.): ἐπαγγελίας , He 11:17 .
G324.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to entertain (as a guest)

G214.thayer: I cry aloud, raise a war-cry; a clanging or clashing cymbal.
G214.mounce: pr. to raise the war-cry, ἀλαλά: hence, to utter other loud sounds; to wail, Mk. 5:38; to tinkle, ring, 1 Cor. 13:1*
G214.abbottSmith: prop, to raise a war-cry , shout with triumph or joy ; rarely of grief, to wail : Mk 5:38 (cf. Je 4:8 ); of a cymbal, ἀλαλάζον (RV. clanging ), I Co 13:1 (cf. ὀλολύζω ).†
G214.strongs: from ( a shout, "halloo"); to vociferate, i.e. (by implication) to wail; figuratively, to clang

G4806.mounce: to make alive together with another; to make a sharer in the quickening of another, Eph. 2:5; Col. 2:13*
G4806.abbottSmith: to make alive or quicken together with ; metaph., of the spiritual life: τ. Χριστῷ , Eph 2:5 ; σὺν τ. Χ. , Col 2:13 .†
G4806.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2227 (ζωοποιέω); to reanimate conjointly with (figuratively)

G3036.thayer: I stone, cast stones (at), kill by stoning.
G3036.mounce: to stone, pelt with stones, in order to kill, Mt. 21:35; 23:37
G3036.abbottSmith: to pelt with stones , to kill by stoning , to stone (cf. λιθάζω ): c. acc. pers., Mt 21:35 23:37 , Lk 13:34 , Ac 7:58 , 59 14:5 ; pass., He 12:20 (LXX) .†
G3036.strongs: from a compound of G3037 (λίθος) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw stones, i.e. lapidate

G4524.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06659); Sadoc (i.e. Tsadok), an Israelite

G3662.abbottSmith: to be like : Mt 23:27 (L, Tr., txt., WH, mg.) Mt 26:73 (WH, mg.) (not found elsewhere. Cf. παρ-ομοιάζω .)†

G5234.strongs: adverb from present participle active of G5235 (ὑπερβάλλω); excessively

G4611.thayer: Siloam, a spring within the walls, in the south-east corner of Jerusalem.
G4611.mounce: Siloam, a pool or fountain near Jerusalem, Lk. 13:4; Jn. 9:7, 11*
G4611.abbottSmith: Siloam (v. DB , iii, 515 f.): Lk 13:4 , Jo 9:7 , 11 .†
G4611.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07975); Siloam (i.e. Shiloach), a pool of Jerusalem

G3172.mounce: greatness, majesty, Heb. 1:3; 8:1; ascribed majesty, Jude 25*
G3172.abbottSmith: greatness , majesty : He 1:3 8:1 , Ju 25 (elsewhere Eccl. only).†
G3172.strongs: from G3173 (μέγας); greatness, i.e. (figuratively) divinity (often God himself)

G4011.thayer: Perga, a city on the river Cestrus in the Roman province Pamphylia.
G4011.mounce: Perga, the chief city of Pamphylia, in Asia Minor, Acts 13:13f.; 14:25. See PhotoGuide.
G4011.abbottSmith: Perga , a city of Pamphylia: Ac 13:13 , 14 14:25 .†
G4011.strongs: probably from the same as G4010 (Πέργαμος); a tower; Perga, a place in Asia Minor

G3642.strongs: from G3641 (ὀλίγος) and G6590; little-spirited, i.e. faint-hearted

G2013.mounce: to light upon, find; to hit, reach; to acquire, obtain, attain, Rom. 11:7 (2x); Heb. 6:15; 11:33; Jas. 4:2*
G2013.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5177 (τυγχάνω); to chance upon, i.e. (by implication) to attain

G4412.thayer: first, in the first place, before, formerly.
G4412.strongs: neuter of G4413 (πρῶτος) as adverb (with or without G3588 (ὁ)); firstly (in time, place, order, or importance)
G4412.kjv: before, at the beginning, chiefly (at, at the) first (of all)

G297.mounce: both. Only plural in the NT.

G974.mounce: a small volume or scroll, a little book, Rev. 10:2, 9, 10*
G974.abbottSmith: a little book : Re 10:2 , 8 (WH, βιβλιόν , T 2, 7 βιβλιδάριον ) 9 , 10 . Not hitherto found elsewhere.†
G974.strongs: a diminutive of G975 (βιβλίον); a booklet

G1641.mounce: trans. to make less; intrans. to be less, inferior; to have too little, want, lack, 2 Cor. 8:15*
G1641.abbottSmith: to be less (RV, had no lack ): II Co 8:15 (LXX) (a rare word; cf. MM, Exp. , xii).†
G1641.strongs: from G1640 (ἐλάσσων); to diminish, i.e. fall short

G645.thayer: lit: I wrench away from, drag away, but perhaps sometimes in the well-attested weakened sense: I am parted or withdrawn.
G645.mounce: to draw away from; to draw out or forth, Mt. 26:51; to draw away, seduce, Acts 20:30; to separate one’s self, to part, Lk. 22:41; Acts 21:1*
G645.abbottSmith: to draw off or away , tear away (MM, s.v.): Ac 20:30 ; μάχαιραν , draw a sword, Mt 26:51 ; pass., to be parted or withdrawn (Field, Notes , 134; but v. Thayer, s.v.), Lk 22:41 , Ac 21:1 .†
G645.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4685 (σπάω); to drag forth, i.e. (literally) unsheathe (a sword), or relatively (with a degree of force implied) retire (personally or factiously)

G1060.thayer: I marry, used of either sex.
G1060.mounce: to marry, Mt. 5:32, et al.; absol. to marry, enter the marriage state, Mt. 19:10, et al.; mid. to marry, be married, Mk. 10:12; 1 Cor. 7:39
G1060.abbottSmith: to marry ; of the man, to marry, take to wife ( ducere ): absol., Mt 19:10 22:25 , 30 24:38 , Mk 12:25 , Lk 17:27 , 20:34 , 35 , I Co 7:28 , 33 ; c. acc.: Mt 5:32 19:9 , Mk 6:17 10:11 , Lk 14:20 16:18 . Of the woman, mid. (and in late writers, pass.), to give oneself in marriage, marry ( nubere ): I Co 7:39 ; in Hellenistic (M, Pr. , 159), act. (as of the man), to marry : absol., I Co 7:28 , 34 ,
G1060.strongs: from G1062 (γάμος); to wed (of either sex)

G460.mounce: without the intervention of law, Rom. 2:12 (2x)
G460.strongs: adverb from G459 (ἄνομος); lawlessly, i.e. (specially) not amenable to (the Jewish) law

G5076.thayer: a tetrarch, ruler over a fourth part of a region.
G5076.mounce: also spelled τετράρχης, a tetrarch, title of a prince, whose rank was lower than a king, Mt. 14:1; Lk. 3:19; 9:7; Acts 13:1*
G5076.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G757 (ἄρχω); the ruler of a fourth part of a country ("tetrarch")

G3336.mounce: a partaking of, a being partaken of, 1 Tim. 4:3*
G3336.abbottSmith: participation , taking , receiving : of food, I Ti 4:3 .†

G3000.thayer: I serve, especially God, perhaps simply: I worship.
G3000.mounce: to be a servant, to serve, Acts 27:23; to render religious service and homage, worship, Mt. 4:10; Lk. 1:74; spc. to offer sacrifices, present offerings, Heb. 8:5; 9:9
G3000.strongs: from (a hired menial); to minister (to God), i.e. render religious homage

G854.mounce: a disappearing, vanishing away; met. destruction, abolition, abrogation, Heb. 8:13*

G264.thayer: originally: I miss the mark, hence (a) I make a mistake, (b) I sin, commit a sin (against God); sometimes the idea of sinning against a fellow-creature is present.
G264.mounce: pr. to miss a mark; to be in error, 1 Cor. 15:34; Tit. 3:11; to sin, Jn. 5:14; to be guilty of wrong, Mt. 18:15
G264.abbottSmith: to miss the mark (Hom., Æsch., al.), hence metaph. (Hom., al.), to err , do wrong .
G264.strongs: perhaps from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G3313 (μέρος); properly, to miss the mark (and so not share in the prize), i.e. (figuratively) to err, especially (morally) to sin
G264.kjv: for your faults, offend, sin, trespass

G3649.thayer: I howl, lament loudly, cry aloud, bewail.
G3649.mounce: pr. to cry aloud in invocation; to howl, utter cries of distress, lament, bewail, Jas. 5:1*
G3649.strongs: a reduplicated primary verb; to "howl" or "halloo", i.e. shriek

G4865.thayer: I struggle in company with, aid.
G4865.mounce: to combat in company with any one; to exert one’s strength with, to be earnest in aiding, help, Rom. 15:30*
G4865.abbottSmith: to strive together with, to help (prop., of sharing in a contest): c. dat. pers. et rei, Ro 15:30 .†
G4865.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G75 (ἀγωνίζομαι); to struggle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to be a partner (assistant)

G5136.thayer: I am laid bare, laid open.
G5136.mounce: pr. to grip the neck; to bend the neck back, so as to make bare or expose the throat, as in slaughtering animals, etc.; met. to lay bare in view, Heb. 4:13*
G5136.strongs: from G5137 (τράχηλος); to seize by the throat or neck, i.e. to expose the gullet of a victim for killing (generally, to lay bare)

G4017.mounce: trans. to look around upon, Mk. 3:5, 34; 11:11; Lk. 6:10; absol. to look around, Mk. 5:32; 9:8; 10:23*
G4017.abbottSmith: to look around ( at ). Mid., to look about one ( at ): absol., Mk 9:8 10:23 ; c. inf., Mk 5:32 ; c. acc. pers., Mk 3:5 , 34 , Lk 6:10 ; πάντα , Mk 11:11 .†
G4017.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G991 (βλέπω); to look all around

G5323.thayer: Phanuel, father of Anna the prophetess.
G5323.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06439); Phanuel (i.e. Penuel), an Israelite

G2037.thayer: Erastus, steward of Corinth, a Christian.
G2037.mounce: to search, examine, investigate, Jn. 5:39; 7:52; Rom. 8:27; 1 Cor. 2:10; 1 Pet. 1:11; Rev. 2:23*
G2037.strongs: from (to love); beloved; Erastus, a Christian

G5437.strongs: from G5343 (φεύγω); a fleeing, i.e. escape

G1015.strongs: probably of foreign origin; a hillock

G1542.strongs: from G1540 (ἑκατόν) and a presumed derivative of G4111 (πλάσσω); a hundred times

G18.thayer: intrinsically good, good in nature, good whether it be seen to be so or not, the widest and most colorless of all words with this meaning.
G18.abbottSmith: in general, good , in physical and in moral sense, used of persons, things, acts, conditions, etc., applied to that which is regarded as "perfect in its kind, so as to produce pleasure and satisfaction, . . . that which, in itself good, is also at once for the good and the advantage of him who comes in contact with it" (Cremer, 3): γῆ , Lk 8:8 ; δένδρον , Mt 7:18 ; καρδία , Lk 8:15 ; δόσις , Ja 1:
G18.strongs: a primary word; "good" (in any sense, often as noun)

G119.thayer: a struggling (as in an athletic contest).
G119.abbottSmith: a contest , combat , esp. of athletes; fig., a struggle : He 10:32 (for exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G1073.mounce: to be full, Mt. 23:27; Lk. 11:39, et al.
G1073.abbottSmith: to be full ; properly, of a ship (Xen.). Generally (Plat., al.), c. gen. rei: Mt 23:27 , Lk 11:39 , Ro 3:14 (LXX) , Re 4:6 , 8 5:8 15:7 17:3 (Rec.) 4 21:9 seq. ἐκ : Mt 23:25 ; c. acc. rei (called a solecism in WM, 251; Bl., §36, 4), as in later Gk. from Byz. to Mod. times (Jannaris, Gr. , 1319): Re 17:3 . †
G1073.strongs: a primary verb; to swell out, i.e. be full

G3849.thayer: I urge, press, constrain by entreaties.
G3849.mounce: also spelled παραβουλεύομαι, to stake or risk one’s self, Phil. 2:30*
G3849.abbottSmith: prop., to force against nature or law .
G3849.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the middle voice of G971 (βιάζω); to force contrary to (nature), i.e. compel (by entreaty)

G3912.thayer: I am out of my senses, am beside myself.
G3912.mounce: to be beside one’s wits; παραφρονῶν, in foolish style, 2 Cor. 11:23*
G3912.abbottSmith: to be beside oneself , be deranged : II Co 11:23 .†
G3912.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5426 (φρονέω); to misthink, i.e. be insane (silly)

G327.thayer: I seek out, search for (implying the difficulty of the task).
G327.mounce: to track; to seek diligently, inquire after, search for, Lk. 2:44, 45; Acts 11:25*
G327.abbottSmith: to look for or seek carefully ("specially of searching for human beings, with an implication of difficulty": MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 2:44 , 45 , Ac 11:25 .†
G327.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2212 (ζητέω); to search out

G3646.mounce: a holocaust, whole burnt-offering, Mk. 12:33; Heb. 10:6, 8*
G3646.abbottSmith: a whole burnt offering : Mk 12:33 , He 10:6 , 8 (LXX) (cf. Kennedy, Sources , 113 f.).†
G3646.strongs: from a derivative of a compound of G3650 (ὅλος) and a derivative of G2545 (καίω); a wholly-consumed sacrifice ("holocaust")

G71.thayer: I lead, lead away, bring (a person, or animal), guide, spend a day, go.
G71.mounce: to lead. bring; lead away, drive off, as a booty of cattle; conduct, accompany; lead out, produce; conduct with force, drag, hurry away; guide, incite, entice; convey one’s self, go, go away; pass or spend as time; celebrate
G71.abbottSmith: to lead, bring, carry : c. acc., seq. ἐπί , εἰς , ἕως , πρός and simple dat.; metaph., to lead, guide, impel : Jo 10:16 Ro 2:4 , He 2:10 , II Ti 3:6 , al. to spend or keep a day: Lk 24:21 , Ac 19:38 Intrans., to go : subjunc., ἄγωμεν , Mt 2:46 , al. (Cramer, 61; MM, VGT , s.v.).
G71.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to lead; by implication, to bring, drive, (reflexively) go, (specially) pass (time), or (figuratively) induce
G71.kjv: be, bring (forth), carry, (let) go, keep, lead away, be open

G2492.thayer: (Hebrew), Job, the hero of the book of that name in the Old Testament.
G2492.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0347); Job (i.e. Ijob), a patriarch

G1466.mounce: self-control, continence, temperance, Acts 24:25; Gal. 4:23; 2 Pet. 1:6*
G1466.abbottSmith: prop., mastery, control . (sc. ἑαθτοῦ ) self-control : Ac 24:25 , Ga 5:23 , II Pe 1:6 (v. DB , iv, 558 b 695 a ; Page on Ac, l.c.).†

G11.thayer: Abraham, progenitor of the Hebrew race.
G11.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H085); Abraham, the Hebrew patriarch

G4672.thayer: Solomon, son of David King of Israel, and Bathsheba.
G4672.mounce: Solomon, also spelled Σολομῶν, pr. name, son and successor of David, Mt. 1:6f.; 6:29; Lk. 11:31; Jn. 10:23; Acts 3:11; 7:47
G4672.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08010); Solomon (i.e. Shelomoh), the son of David

G480.thayer: I resist, oppose, withstand, lie opposite to.
G480.mounce: pr. occupy an opposite position; met. to oppose, be adverse to, Gal. 5:17; 1 Tim. 1:10; opponent, hostile, Lk. 13:7
G480.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to lie opposite, i.e. be adverse (figuratively, repugnant) to

G1394.mounce: pr. giving, outlay; Phil. 4:15; a donation, gift, Jas. 1:17*
G1394.abbottSmith: properly, the act of giving : Phl 4:15 .
G1394.strongs: from the base of G1325 (δίδωμι); a giving; by implication, (concretely) a gift

G4749.thayer: a long robe, worn by the upper classes in the East.
G4749.mounce: equipment; dress; a long garment, flowing robe, worn by priests, kings, and persons of distinction, Mt. 12:38; 16:5; Lk. 15:22; Rev. 6:11
G4749.strongs: from G4724 (στέλλω); equipment, i.e. (specially), a "stole" or long-fitting gown (as a mark of dignity)

G4474.thayer: I slap, strike, smite with the hand.
G4474.mounce: to beat with rods; to strike with the palm of the hand, cuff, clap, Mt. 5:39; 26:67*
G4474.abbottSmith: prop., to strike with a rod .
G4474.strongs: from a derivative of a primary (to let fall, "rap"); to slap
G4474.kjv: smite (with the palm of the hand)

G4226.mounce: interrogative, where? In what place? direct, Mt. 2:2; Lk. 8:25; Jn. 1:39; indirect, Mt. 2:4; Jn. 1:40; whither, Jn. 3:8; 7:35; 13:36
G4226.abbottSmith: prop., where? : Mt 2:2 26:17 , Mk 14:12 , 14 , Lk 17:17 , 37 22:9 , 11 , Jo 1:39 7:11 8:[10] , 19 9:12 11:34 ; ποῦ ( ἐστιν ), indicating that the subject in question is not to be found, Lk 8:25 , Ro 3:27 , I Co 1:20 12:17 , 19 15:55 , Ga 4:15 , II Pe 3:4 ; ποῦ φανεῖται , I Pe 4:18 .
G4226.strongs: genitive case of an interrogative pronoun (what) otherwise obsolete (perhaps the same as G4225 (πού) used with the rising slide of inquiry); as adverb of place; at (by implication, to) what locality

G3070.thayer: Lydia, a lady resident of Philippi, native of Thyatira in Lydia (Asia Minor), and engaged in the clothing trade.
G3070.mounce: Lydia, pr. name of a woman, Acts 16:14, 40*
G3070.abbottSmith: Lydia , a woman of Thyatira: Ac 16:14 , 40 .†
G3070.strongs: properly, feminine of Ludios (of foreign origin) (a Lydian, in Asia Minor); Lydia, a Christian woman

G5150.mounce: the space of three months, Heb. 11:23*
G5150.abbottSmith: of three months ; as subst., τὸ τ. (= cl. ἡ τ. ), a space of three months (Polyb., al.; LXX): He 11:23 .†
G5150.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G3376 (μήν) as noun; a three months' space

G980.abbottSmith: to spend life, to live : τ. χρόνον βιῶσαι (cl., more freq. 2 aor., -ναι ), I Pe 4:2 (cf. Jb, l.c.).†

G4239.mounce: also spelled πρᾶος, meek, gentle, kind, forgiving, Mt. 5:5; mild, benevolent, humane, Mt. 11:29; 21:5; 1 Pet. 3:4*
G4239.strongs: apparently a primary word; mild, i.e. (by implication) humble

G5594.thayer: I cool, pass: I grow cold.
G5594.mounce: to breathe; to cool; pass. to be cooled; met. of affection, Mt. 24:12*
G5594.abbottSmith: to breathe, blow ; hence, to make cool . Pass., to grow cool : metaph., Mt 24:12 .†
G5594.strongs: a primary verb; to breathe (voluntarily but gently, thus differing on the one hand from G4154 (πνέω), which denotes properly a forcible respiration; and on the other from the base of G109 (ἀήρ), which refers properly to an inanimate breeze), i.e. (by implication, of reduction of temperature by evaporation) to chill (figuratively)

G3370.thayer: a Mede, a Median, from east of Assyria.
G3370.mounce: a Mede, a native of Media in Asia, Acts 2:9*
G3370.strongs: of foreign origin (compare H04074); a Median, or inhabitant of Media

G864.thayer: I arrive at, reach, come to.
G864.mounce: to come, arrive at; to reach as a report, Rom. 16:19*
G864.abbottSmith: to come (M, Pr. , 247), to arrive at , come to , reach : metaph. (MM, s.v.), Ro 16:19 .†
G864.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G2425 (ἱκανός); to go (i.e. spread) forth (by rumor)

G4204.thayer: a prostitute; met: an idolatrous community.
G4204.mounce: a prostitute, a whore, harlot, an unchaste female, Mt. 21:31, 32; from the Hebrew, an idolatress, Rev. 17:1, 5, 15
G4204.abbottSmith: a prostitute , harlot : Mt 21:31 , 32 , Lk 15:30 , I Co 6:15 , 16 , He 11:31 , Ja 2:25 . Metaph. (v. Swete, Ap. , 180 f.), of Babylon (i.e. Rome): Re 17:1 , 5 , 17:15 , 16 19:2 .†
G4204.strongs: feminine of G4205 (πόρνος); a strumpet; figuratively, an idolater

G1282.thayer: (lit: I saw through), I cut to the quick (with indignation and envy).
G1282.mounce: to divide with a saw, saw asunder; to grate the teeth in a rage; pass. met. to be cut to the heart, to be enraged, Acts 5:33; 7:54*
G1282.abbottSmith: to saw asunder . Pass., metaph. (vernacular?), EV, cut to the heart : Ac 5:33 ; seq. τ. καρδίαις αὐτῶν , Ac 7:54 .†
G1282.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G4249 (πρίζω); to saw asunder, i.e. (figuratively) to exasperate

G5001.mounce: pr. anything placed in order; in NT order of succession, class, group, 1 Cor. 15:23*
G5001.abbottSmith: that which has been arranged or placed in order ; esp. as military term, a company, troop, division, rank : metaph., I Co 15:23 (v. ICC , in l.).†
G5001.strongs: from G5021 (τάσσω); something orderly in arrangement (a troop), i.e. (figuratively) a series or succession

G4049.mounce: to draw off from around; to wheel about; to distract; pass. to be distracted, over busied, Lk. 10:40*
G4049.abbottSmith: to draw around , draw off or away . Metaph., to distract : pass., Lk 10:40 .†
G4049.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G4685 (σπάω); to drag all around, i.e. (figuratively) to distract (with care)

G3504.thayer: (lit: newly-planted), newly converted to Christianity, recent convert.
G3504.mounce: newly or recently planted met. a neophyte, one newly implanted into the Christian Church, a new convert, 1 Tim. 3:6*
G3504.abbottSmith: newly planted (LXX). Metaph., as subst., ὁ ν. , a new convert , neophyte , novice : I Ti 3:6 .†
G3504.strongs: from G3501 (νέος) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω); newly planted, i.e. (figuratively) a young convert ("neophyte")

G1166.thayer: I point out, show, exhibit; met: I teach, demonstrate, make known.
G1166.abbottSmith: to show , c. acc rei (pers.), dat. pers.;
G1166.strongs: a prolonged form of an obsolete primary of the same meaning; to show (literally or figuratively)

G4985.thayer: bodily, corporeally, belonging to the body.
G4985.mounce: bodily, in a bodily frame, Col. 2:9*
G4985.abbottSmith: bodily, corporeally : Col 2:9 (v. ICC , Lft., in l.).†
G4985.strongs: adverb from G4984 (σωματικός); corporeally or physically

G787.thayer: a small coin equal to the tenth part of a drachma.
G787.mounce: dimin. of the Latin, as, a Roman brass coin with the value of one-tenth of a denarius, or δραχμή, used to convey the idea of a trifle or very small sum, Mt. 10:29; Lk. 12:6*
G787.abbottSmith: an assarion , a farthing , one-tenth of a drachma: Mt 10:29 , Lk 12:6 (MM, s.v.; DB , iii, 428; DCG , ii, 200).†
G787.strongs: of Latin origin; an assarius or as, a Roman coin

G2125.thayer: I fear, am anxious, am cautious; I reverence.
G2125.mounce: to fear, be afraid or apprehensive; in NT absol. to reverence God, to be influenced by pious awe, Heb. 11:7*
G2125.abbottSmith: to be cautious, to beware : Ac 23:10 (Rec.; φοβηθείς , WH, RV).
G2125.strongs: middle voice from G2126 (εὐλαβής); to be circumspect, i.e. (by implication) to be apprehensive; religiously, to reverence

G1949.thayer: I lay hold of, take hold of, seize (sometimes with beneficent, sometimes with hostile, intent).
G1949.mounce: to take hold of, Mt. 14:31; Mk. 8:23; to lay hold of, seize, Lk. 23:26; Acts 16:19; met. to seize on as a ground of accusation, Lk. 20:20, 26; to grasp, obtain as if by seizure, 1 Tim. 6:12, 19; to assume a portion of, to assume the nature of, or, to attach or ally one’s self to, Heb. 2:16
G1949.abbottSmith: always mid. in LXX and NT (v. Cremer, 758), to lay hold of : c. gen. pers., Mt 14:31 , Ac 17:19 21:30 , 33 ; c. acc pers. (not cl.), Lk 9:47 14:4 23:26 (WH, but v. Bl., 101 5 ), Ac 9:27 16:9 18:17 ; c. gen. rei, Mk 8:23 , Ac 23:19 , He 8:9 (LXX) ; c. gen. pers. et rei, Lk 20:20 , 26 ; τ. αἰωνίου ( ὄντως ) ζωῆς , I Ti 6:12 , 19 . Metaph. (as in Si 4:11 ), He 2:16 (v. Westc., in l.).†
G1949.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to seize (for help, injury, attainment, or any other purpose; literally or figuratively)
G1949.kjv: catch, lay hold (up-)on, take (by, hold of, on)

G1422.thayer: difficult, hard; of persons: hard to please.
G1422.mounce: pr. peevish about food; hard to please, disagreeable; in NT, difficult, Mk. 10:24*
G1422.abbottSmith: properly, of persons, hard to satisfy with food , hence, generally, hard to please (Eur., Plat.).
G1422.strongs: from G1418 (δυσ-) and (food); properly, fastidious about eating (peevish), i.e. (genitive case) impracticable

G3600.thayer: I torment, pain; mid. and pass: I am tormented, pained; I suffer acute pain, physical or mental.
G3600.mounce: to pain either bodily or mentally; pass. to be in an agony, be tormented, Lk. 2:48; 16:24, 25; to be distressed, grieved, Acts 20:38*
G3600.abbottSmith: to cause pain or suffering ; pass. and mid., to suffer pain , be tormented or greatly distressed : Lk 2:48 16:24 , 25 ( ὀδυνᾶσαι , v. M, Pr. , 53 f.); seq. ἐπί , Ac 20:38 .†

G5610.thayer: (a) a definite space of time, a season, (b) an hour, (c) the particular time for anything.
G5610.mounce: a limited portion of time, marked out by part of a settled routine or train of circumstances; a season of the year; time of day, Mt. 14:15; Mk. 6:35; 11:11; an hour, Mt. 20:3; Jn. 11:9; in NT an eventful season, 1 Jn. 2:18 (2x); Rev. 3:10; 14:7; due time, Jn. 16:21; Rom. 13:11; a destined period, hour, Mt. 26:45; Mk. 14:35; Jn. 2:4; 7:30; a short period, Mt. 26:40; Jn. 5:35; 2 Cor. 7:8; Gal. 2:5; 1 Thess. 2:17; Phlm. 15; a point of time, time, Mt. 8:13; 24:42; Lk. 2:38
G5610.abbottSmith: any time or period fixed by nature, esp. a season (Hom., Hdt., Plat., al.). A part of the day, and esp. a twelfth part of day or night, an hour : Mt 24:36 , Mk 13:32 , Ac 10:3 , al.; accus. in ans. to "when"? (M, Pr. , 63, 215; Bl., § 34, 8), Jo 4:52 , Ac 10:3 , 30 I Co 15:30 , Re 3:3 ; acc. of duration, Mt 20:12 26:40 , Mk 14:37 ; inexactly, πρὸς ὥραν , for a season, for a time , Jo 5:35 , II Co
G5610.strongs: apparently a primary word; an "hour" (literally or figuratively)
G5610.kjv: day, hour, instant, season, X short, (even-)tide, (high) time

G5123.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5123.strongs: contraction for G5124 (τοῦτο) and G2076 (ἐστί); that is

G1652.abbottSmith: pitiable, miserable : Re 3:17 ; comparat., I Co 15:19 .†

G604.thayer: I reconcile, change from one state of feeling to another.
G604.mounce: to transfer from a certain state to another which is quite different; hence, to reconcile, restore to favor, Eph. 2:16; Col. 1:20, 22*
