G604.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2644 (καταλλάσσω); to reconcile fully

G623.thayer: Apollyon, The Destroying One, a Greek translation of the Hebrew: Abaddon.
G623.abbottSmith: Apollyon , i.e. the Destroyer: Re 9:11 (cf. Ἀβάδδων ). (Cremer, 453; DB , i, 125, 172.)†
G623.strongs: active participle of G622 (ἀπόλλυμι); a destroyer (i.e. Satan)

G2816.thayer: I inherit, obtain (possess) by inheritance, acquire.
G2816.mounce: pr. to acquire by lot; to inherit, obtain by inheritance; in NT to obtain, acquire, receive possession of, Mt. 5:5; 19:29; absol. to be heir, Gal. 4:30
G2816.strongs: from G2818 (κληρονόμος); to be an heir to (literally or figuratively)

G3984.mounce: a trial, attempt, endeavor; λαμβάνειν πεῖραν, to attempt, Heb. 11:29; also, to experience, Heb. 11:36*
G3984.abbottSmith: a trial , experiment ; π. λαμβάνειν , to make trial , have experience of : He 11:29 , 36 (for exx., v. Field, Notes , 232 f.).†
G3984.strongs: from the base of G4008 (πέραν) (through the idea of piercing); a test, i.e. attempt, experience

G5017.mounce: agitation, commotion; consternation, terror, Acts 12:18; excitement, tumult, public contention, Acts 19:23*
G5017.abbottSmith: later form of ταραχή (Xen., al.; v. Thackeray, Gr. , 159): Ac 12:18 19:23 .†
G5017.strongs: masculine from G5015 (ταράσσω); a disturbance, i.e. (popular) tumult

G2664.thayer: (a) trans: I cause to rest, bring to rest; I cause to refrain, (b) intrans: I rest.
G2664.mounce: to cause to cease, restrain, Acts 14:18; to cause to rest, give final rest to, settle finally, Heb. 4:8; intrans. to rest, desist from, Heb. 4:4, 10*
G2664.abbottSmith: trans., to cause to cease , restrain : Ac 14:18 ; to cause to rest : He 4:8 .
G2664.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3973 (παύω); to settle down, i.e. (literally) to colonize, or (figuratively) to (cause to) desist

G4135.thayer: (lit: I carry full), (a) I complete, carry out fully, (b) I fully convince, satisfy fully, (c) I fully believe.
G4135.mounce: to bring full measure, to give in full; to carry out fully, to discharge completely, 2 Tim. 4:5, 17; pass. of things, to be fully established as a matter of certainty, Lk. 1:1; of persons, to be fully convinced, assured, Rom. 4:21; 14:15; Col. 4:12*
G4135.abbottSmith: to bring in full measure , hence, to fulfil , accomplish : Lk 1:1 , II Ti 4:5 , 17 .
G4135.strongs: from G4134 (πλήρης) and G5409 (φορέω); to carry out fully (in evidence), i.e. completely assure (or convince), entirely accomplish
G4135.kjv: most surely believe, fully know (persuade), make full proof of

G1842.thayer: I destroy utterly, annihilate, exterminate, root out.
G1842.mounce: to destroy utterly, root out, Acts 3:23*
G1842.abbottSmith: to destroy utterly : seq. ἐκ τ. λαοῦ , Ac 3:23 (LXX) .†
G1842.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3645 (ὀλοθρεύω); to extirpate

G4495.thayer: I throw off or away, cast, hurl.
G4495.mounce: also spelled ῥίπτω, frequent and repeated action, to toss repeatedly, toss up with violent gesture, Acts 22:23 (ῥιπτούντων)*
G4495.strongs: from a derivative of G4496 (ῥίπτω); to toss up

G3938.mounce: a way by; a passing by; ἐν παρόδῳ, in passing, by the way, 1 Cor. 16:7*
G3938.abbottSmith: a passing or passage : ἐν π. , in passing , I Co 16:7 .†
G3938.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3598 (ὁδός); a by-road, i.e. (actively) a route

G2085.thayer: I teach different things, that is, different from the true or necessary teaching.
G2085.mounce: to teach other or different doctrine, and spc. what is foreign to the Christian religion, 1 Tim. 1:3; 6:3*
G2085.abbottSmith: to teach other or different doctrine : I Ti 1:3 6:3 (cf. CGT , in l.; Milligan, NTD , 102).†
G2085.strongs: from G2087 (ἕτερος) and G1320 (διδάσκαλος); to instruct differently

G812.thayer: (lit: I march out of order; then: I riot, rebel), behave disorderly, neglect my duty, am careless (or idle) in habits.
G812.mounce: pr. to infringe military order; met. to be irregular, behave disorderly, to be lazy, 2 Thess. 3:7*
G812.abbottSmith: primarily, of soldiers marching, to be out of order , to quit the ranks ; hence, metaph., to be remiss , fail in the performance of duty (in π. , of truancy on the part of an apprentice): II Th 3:7 (on ἀ. and its cognates, v. M, Th. , 152 ff.; MM, s.vv.).†
G812.strongs: from G813 (ἄτακτος); to be (i.e. act) irregular

G859.thayer: a sending away, a letting go, a release, pardon, complete forgiveness.
G859.mounce: dismission, deliverance, from captivity, Lk. 4:18 (2x); remission, forgiveness, pardon, Mt. 26:28

G3309.thayer: I am over-anxious; with acc: I am anxious about, distracted; I care for.
G3309.mounce: to be anxious, or solicitous, Phil. 4:6; to expend careful thought, Mt. 6:27, 28, 31, 34a; 10:19; Lk. 10:41; 12:11, 22, 25, 26; to concern one’s self, Mt. 6:25; 1 Cor. 12:25; to have the thoughts occupied with, 1 Cor. 7:32, 33, 34; to feel an interest in, Phil. 2:20*
G3309.abbottSmith: to be anxious : absol., Mt 6:27 , 31 , Lk 12:25 ; μηδὲν μ. , Phl 4:6 ; c. dat. rei, Mt 6:25 , Lk 12:22 ; seq. περί , Mt 6:28 , Lk 10:41 12:26 ; πῶς , Mt 10:19 , Lk 12:11 ; εἰς τὴν αὔριον , Mt 6:34 .
G3309.strongs: from G3308 (μέριμνα); to be anxious about

G4802.mounce: to seek, ask, or inquire with another; to deliberate, debate, Mk. 1:27; 9:10; Lk. 24:15; to hold discourse with, argue, reason, Mk. 8:11; 12:28; Lk. 22:23; Acts 6:9; 9:29; to question, dispute, quibble, Mk. 9:14, 16*
G4802.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2212 (ζητέω); to investigate jointly, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil

G2164.thayer: I bear well, bring a good harvest, yield abundantly.
G2164.mounce: to bear or bring forth well or plentifully, yield abundantly, Lk. 12:16*
G2164.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G5409 (φορέω); to bear well, i.e. be fertile

G3165.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G3165.strongs: a shorter (and probably original) form of G1691 (ἐμέ); me

G1497.mounce: pr. a form, shape, figure; image or statue; hence, an idol, image of a god, Acts 7:41; a heathen god, 1 Cor. 8:4, 7; for εἰδωλόθυτον, the flesh of victims sacrificed to idols, Acts 15:20; Rom. 2:22; 1 Cor. 10:19; 12:12; 2 Cor. 6:16; 1 Thess. 1:9; 1 Jn. 5:21; Rev. 9:20*
G1497.abbottSmith: in cl. a phantom, image, likeness ; an image in the mind, an idea, fancy . In LXX and NT, an image of a god, an idol (cf. Polyb., xxxi, 3, 13): Ac 7:41 , I Co 12:2 , Re 9:20 ; the false god or idol worshipped in an image ( ICC , on I Th, l.c.): Ac 15:20 , Ro 2:22 , I Co 8:4 , 7 10:19 II Co 6:18 , I Th 1:9 , I Jo 5:21 .†
G1497.strongs: from G1491 (εἶδος); an image (i.e. for worship); by implication, a heathen god, or (plural) the worship of such

G4233.thayer: an officer employed to execute judicial sentences.
G4233.mounce: an exactor of dues or penalties; an officer who enforced payment of debts by imprisonment, Lk. 12:58*
G4233.strongs: from a derivative of G4238 (πράσσω); a practiser, i.e. (specially), an official collector

G492.thayer: I pass opposite, on the opposite side of the road.
G492.mounce: to pass over against, to pass along without noticing, Lk. 10:31, 32*
G492.abbottSmith: to pass by opposite to : Lk 10:31 , 32 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G492.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G3928 (παρέρχομαι); to go along opposite
G492.kjv: pass by on the other side

G1792.mounce: to live luxuriously, riot, revel, 2 Pet. 2:13*
G1792.abbottSmith: to revel in : ἐν-τ. ἀπάταις , II Pe 2:13 (v. Mayor, in l).†
G1792.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5171 (τρυφάω); to revel in

G979.mounce: life; means of living; sustenance, maintenance, substance, goods, Mk. 12:44, Lk. 8:14, 43; 15:12, 30; 21:4; 1 Tim. 2:2; 2 Tim. 2:4; 1 Jn. 2:16; 3:17*
G979.abbottSmith: period or course of life , life : Lk 8:14 , I Ti 2:2 , II Ti 2:4 , I Jo 2:16 .
G979.strongs: a primary word; life, i.e. (literally) the present state of existence; by implication, the means of livelihood

G4556.mounce: carnelian, a reddish precious stone, Rev. 4:3; 21:20*
G4556.abbottSmith: the sardian stone, sard (of which carnelian is one variety): Re 4:3 21:20 .†
G4556.strongs: properly, an adjective from an uncertain base; sardian (3037 being implied), i.e. (as noun) the gem so called

G5110.mounce: a bringing forth; offspring; met. produce of money lent, interest, usury, Mt. 25:27; Lk. 19:23*
G5110.strongs: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); interest on money loaned (as a produce)

G266.thayer: prop: missing the mark; hence: (a) guilt, sin, (b) a fault, failure (in an ethical sense), sinful deed.
G266.mounce: error; offence, sin, Mt. 1:21; a principle or cause of sin, Rom. 7:7; proneness to sin, sinful propensity, Rom. 7:17, 20; guilt or imputation of sin, Jn. 9:41; Heb. 9:26; a guilty subject, sin-offering, expiatory victim, 2 Cor. 5:21
G266.abbottSmith: prop. a missing the mark ; in cl. (v. reff. to CR in MM, VGT , s.v.); guilt , sin (Plat., Arist., al.); more freq., from Æsch. down, a fault, failure . In NT (as LXX) always in ethical sense; as a principle and quality of action, = τὸ ἁμαρτάνειν , a sinning, sin : Ro 5:12 , 13 20 ; ὑφ’ ἁμαρτίαν εἶναι , Ro 3:9 ; ἐπιμένειν , Ro 6:1 ; ἀποθνήσκειν, νεκρὸν εἶναι τῇ ἁ. , Ro 6:2 , 11 ; τὴν ἀ. γινώσκειν ,

G440.abbottSmith: coal , charcoal : . ἄ. πυρός , a burning coal , Ro 12:20 .†
G440.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a live coal

G4880.mounce: to die together with any one, Mk. 14:31; 2 Cor. 7:3; met. to die with, in respect of a spiritual likeness, 2 Tim. 2:11*
G4880.abbottSmith: to die with or together : Mk 14:31 , II Co 7:3 (v. Meyer, in l.), II Ti 2:11 .†
G4880.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G599 (ἀποθνήσκω); to decease (literally) in company with, or (figuratively), similarly to

G3918.thayer: I am present, am near; I have come, arrived.
G3918.mounce: to be beside; to be present, Lk. 13:1; to have come, Mt. 26:50; Jn. 7:6; 11:28; Col. 1:6; to be in possession, Heb. 13:5; 2 Pet. 1:9, 12; part. παρών, οῦσα, όν, present, 1 Cor. 5:3; τὸ παρόν, the present time, the present, Heb. 12:11
G3918.abbottSmith: to be by , at hand or present ; of persons: Re 17:8 ; παρών (opp. to ἀπών ), I Co 5:3 , II Co 10:2 , 11 13:2 , 10 ; seq. ἐπί , c. gen., Ac 24:19 ; ἐνώπιον , Ac 10:33 ; ἐνθάδε , Ac 17:6 ; πρός , c. acc. pers., Ac 12:20 , II Co 11:8 , Ga 4:18 , 20 ; of things: of time, ὁ καιρός , Jo 7:6 ; τ. παρόν , He 12:11 ; ἡ ἀλήθεια , II Pe 1:12 ; ταῦτα , II Pe 1:9 ; τ. παρόντα , He 13:5 .
G3918.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1510 (εἰμί) (including its various forms); to be near, i.e. at hand; neuter present participle (singular) time being, or (plural) property
G3918.kjv: come, X have, be here, + lack, (be here) present

G2734.mounce: to show in a mirror; to present a clear and correct image of a thing, mid. to have presented in a mirror, to have a clear image presented, or, to reflect, 2 Cor. 3:18*
G2734.abbottSmith: to show as in a mirror . Mid., to see oneself mirrored (v. MM, Exp. , xv); c. acc. rei (R, txt., but v. mg.), to reflect as a mirror : II Co 3:18 (cf. Abbott, Essays , 94).†
G2734.strongs: middle voice from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (compare G2072 (ἔσοπτρον)); to mirror oneself, i.e. to see reflected (figuratively)

G4363.thayer: (a) I fall down before, (b) I beat against, rush violently upon.
G4363.mounce: to fall or impinge upon or against a thing; to fall down to any one, Mk. 3:11; 7:25; to rush violently upon, beat against, Mt. 7:25
G4363.abbottSmith: to fall upon , strike against : c. dat., of wind, Mt 7:25 .
G4363.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G4098 (πίπτω); to fall towards, i.e. (gently) prostrate oneself (in supplication or homage), or (violently) to rush upon (in storm)

G5272.thayer: (lit: stage-playing), a response, answer, hypocrisy, dissembling.
G5272.mounce: a response, answer; an over-acting personification, acting; hypocrisy, simulation, Mt. 23:28; Mk. 12:15; Lk. 12:1; Gal. 2:13; 1 Tim. 4:2; 1 Pet. 2:1*
G5272.strongs: from G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); acting under a feigned part, i.e. (figuratively) deceit ("hypocrisy")

G725.thayer: spoil, an object of eager desire, a prize.
G725.mounce: eager seizure; in NT, a thing retained with an eager grasp, or eagerly claimed and conspicuously exercised, Phil. 2:6*
G725.abbottSmith: prop., acc., to the rule of its formation (Bl., § 27, 2), actively, the act of seizing , robbery (Plut., de Puer. Educ. , p. 12A), Phl 2:6 , AV (Waterland, Works , II, 108; Cremer, 649 f.; Meyer, in l.; cf. also JThS , July, 1909, April, 1911; MM, s.v.).

G1185.mounce: pr. to entrap, take or catch with a bait; met. allure, entice, Jas. 1:14; 2 Pet. 2:14, 18*
G1185.abbottSmith: to allure by a bait : metaph., to allure, entice : c. acc, II Pe 2:14 , 18 ; pass., Ja 1:14 .†
G1185.strongs: from the base of G1388 (δόλος); to entrap, i.e. (figuratively) delude

G4947.thayer: Syria, a great Roman imperial province, united with Cilicia.
G4947.mounce: Syria, an extensive country of Asia, Mt. 4:24; Lk. 2:2; Acts 15:23
G4947.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (H06865); Syria (i.e. Tsyria or Tyre), a region of Asia

G2867.mounce: to whitewash, or, plaster, Mt. 23:27; Acts 23:3*
G2867.abbottSmith: to plaster or whiten over : of tombs, Mt 23:27 ; fig., of a hypocrite, Ac 23:3 .†
G2867.strongs: from (dust; by analogy, lime); to whitewash

G2503.thayer: iota, a small letter of the Greek alphabet, used in the NT (like yod, the Hebrew or rather Aramaic letter which was the smallest of all) to indicate the smallest part.
G2503.mounce: iota; in NT used like the Hebrew/Aramaic yod, the smallest letter in the Hebrew/Aramaic alphabet, as an expression for the least or minutest part; a jot, Mt. 5:18*
G2503.abbottSmith: iota (Heb. י , i.e. the smallest letter): Mt 5:18 .†
G2503.strongs: of Hebrew origin (the tenth letter of the Hebrew alphabet); "iota", the name of the eighth letter of the Greek alphabet, put (figuratively) for a very small part of anything

G3534.abbottSmith: victory : Mt 12:20 ( Is 42:3 , LXX ἀλήθεια ), I Co 15:54 ( Is 25:8 , Aq., Th.), I Co 15:55 ( Ho 13:14 , LXX δίκη ), I Co 15:57 .†
G3534.strongs: from G3529 (νίκη); a conquest (concretely), i.e. (by implication) triumph

G4329.mounce: a looking for, expectation, anticipation, Lk. 21:26; meton. expectation, what is expected or anticipated, Acts 12:11*
G4329.abbottSmith: expectation : c. gen. obj., Lk 21:26 ; c. gen. subjc., Ac 12:11 .†
G4329.strongs: from G4328 (προσδοκάω); apprehension (of evil); by implication, infliction anticipated

G1141.mounce: pertaining to or proceeding from demons; demonic, devilish, Jas. 3:15*

G4731.mounce: stiff, hard; of food, solid, as opposed to what is liquid and light, Heb. 5:12, 14; firm, steadfast, 2 Tim. 2:19; 1 Pet. 5:9*
G4731.abbottSmith: hard, firm, solid : τροψή , He 5:12 , 14 ; θεμέλιος , fig., II Ti 2:19 . Metaph.,
G4731.strongs: from G2476 (ἵστημι); stiff, i.e. solid, stable (literally or figuratively)

G4838.thayer: I take along as a companion.
G4838.mounce: to take along with, take as a companion, Acts 12:25; 15:37, 38; Gal. 2:1*
G4838.abbottSmith: to take along with : as a companion, c. acc., Ac 12:25 15:37, 38 (on the tenses, v. M, Pr. , 130), Ga 2:1 .†
G4838.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3880 (παραλαμβάνω); to take along in company

G4021.thayer: of persons: over-careful; curious, meddling, a busy-body; of things: over-wrought; superfluous; curious, uncanny; subst: curious arts, magic.
G4021.mounce: over careful; officious, a busybody, 1 Tim. 5:13; in NT περίεργα, magic arts, sorcery, Acts 19:19*
G4021.abbottSmith: of persons; over careful . curious , meddling , a busybody : I Ti 5:13 .
G4021.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2041 (ἔργον); working all around, i.e. officious (meddlesome, neuter plural magic)

G1378.mounce: a decree, statute, ordinance, Lk. 2:1; Acts 16:4; 17:7; Eph. 2:15; Col. 2:14*
G1378.strongs: from the base of G1380 (δοκέω); a law (civil, ceremonial or ecclesiastical)

G3009.thayer: a charitable gift, public service in the widest sense; service as of priest or Levite ritual.
G3009.mounce: pr. a public service discharged by a citizen at his own expense; in NT a sacred ministration, Lk. 1:23; Phil. 2:17; Heb. 8:6; 9:21; a kind office, aid, relief, 2 Cor. 9:12; Phil. 2:30*
G3009.abbottSmith: in cl. (chiefly of Athens), the discharge of a public office at one's own expense (v. LS, s.v.), hence,
G3009.strongs: from G3008 (λειτουργέω); public function (as priest ("liturgy") or almsgiver)

G1248.thayer: waiting at table; in a wider sense: service, ministration.
G1248.mounce: serving, service, waiting, attendance, the act of rendering friendly offices, Lk. 10:40; 2 Tim. 4:11; Heb. 1:14; relief, aid, Acts 6:1; 11:29; 2 Cor. 8:4; 9:1, 12, 13; a commission, Acts 12:25; Rom. 15:31; a commission or ministry in the service of the Gospel, Acts 1:17, 25; 20:24; Rom. 11:13; 2 Cor. 4:1; 5:18; 1 Tim. 1:12; service in the Gospel, Acts 6:4; 21:19; 1 Cor. 16:15; 2 Cor. 6:3; 11:8; Eph. 4:12; Rev. 2:19; a function, ministry, or office in the Church, Rom. 12:7; 1 Cor. 12:5; Col. 4:17; 2 Tim. 4:5; a ministering in the conveyance of a revelation from God, 2 Cor. 3:7, 8, 9
G1248.abbottSmith: the office and work of a διάκονος , service, ministry ;
G1248.strongs: from G1249 (διάκονος); attendance (as a servant, etc.); figuratively (eleemosynary) aid, (official) service (especially of the Christian teacher, or technically of the diaconate)

G96.thayer: failing to pass the test, unapproved, counterfeit.
G96.mounce: unable to stand test, rejected, refuse, worthless
G96.abbottSmith: of things (prop. of metals: LXX, Pr 25:4 , Is 1:22 ), not standing the test , rejected : γῆ , He 6:8 . Of persons, rejected after testing , reprobate : Ro 1:28 , I Co 9:27 , II Co 13:5-7 , II Ti 3:8 , Tit 1:16 (Cremer, 212).†
G96.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1384 (δόκιμος); unapproved, i.e. rejected; by implication, worthless (literally or morally)

G3606.mounce: whence, Mt. 12:44; Acts 14:26; from the place where, Mt. 25:24, 26; whence, from which circumstance, 1 Jn. 2:18; wherefore, whereupon, Mt. 14:7
G3606.abbottSmith: whence ; of direction or source: Mt 12:44 , Lk 11:24 , Ac 14:26 28:13 ; = ἐκεῖθεν ὅπου , Mt 25:24 , 26 (cf. Thuc., i, 89, 3); ὅ. γινώσκομεν , I Jo 2:18 ; of cause, whence , wherefore : Mt 14:7 , Ac 26:19 , He 2:17 3:1 7:25 8:3 9:18 11:19 .†
G3606.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) with the directive enclitic of source; from which place or source or cause (adverb or conjunction)

G5482.mounce: a stake; a military palisade, rampart, formed from the earth thrown out of the ditch, and stuck with sharp stakes or palisades, Lk. 19:43*
G5482.strongs: from (to sharpen to a point; akin to G1125 (γράφω) through the idea of scratching); a stake, i.e. (by implication) a palisade or rampart (military mound for circumvallation in a siege)

G3117.thayer: long, distant, far; of long duration.
G3117.mounce: long; of space, far, distant, remote, Lk. 15:13; 19:12; of time, of long duration, Mk. 12:40; Lk. 20:47*
G3117.abbottSmith: of space and time, long : μακρὰ προσεύχεσθαι , Mk 12:40 , Lk 20:47 .
G3117.strongs: from G3372 (μῆκος); long (in place (distant) or time (neuter plural))

G5139.thayer: Trachonitis, the north-east of the territory beyond Jordan.
G5139.mounce: Trachonitis, part of the tetrarchy of Herod Antipas, the north-easternmost habitable district east of the Jordan, Lk. 3:1*
G5139.abbottSmith: Trachonitis , a rough region S. of Damascus (in FlJ, sometimes ἡ Τ. , sometimes ό Τράχων ): ἡ Τ. χώρα , Lk 3:1 .†
G5139.strongs: from a derivative of G5138 (τραχύς); rough district; Trachonitis, a region of Syria

G1013.mounce: purpose, will, determination, Acts 27:43; Rom. 9:19; 1 Pet. 4:3*
G1013.abbottSmith: purpose, will : Ac 27:43 , Ro 9:19 , I Pe 4:3 .†

G5197.thayer: an insolent, insulting, or violent man.
G5197.mounce: an overbearing, violent person, Rom. 1:30; 1 Tim. 1:13*
G5197.abbottSmith: a violent, insolent man : Ro 1:20 , I Ti 1:13 (EV, injurious ).†
G5197.strongs: from G5195 (ὑβρίζω); an insulter, i.e. maltreater

G2695.mounce: also spelled κατασφάττω, to slaughter, slay, Lk. 19:27*
G2695.abbottSmith: to kill off , slay : c. acc., Lk 19:27 .†
G2695.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4969 (σφάζω); to kill down, i.e. slaughter

G3812.mounce: from childhood, from a child, Mk. 9:21*
G3812.abbottSmith: = cl. ἐκ παιδός , παιδίου , from childhood : Mk 9:21 .†
G3812.strongs: adverb (of source) from G3813 (παιδίον); from infancy

G4700.abbottSmith: ashes : He 9:13 ; ἐν σάκκῳ καὶ σ. ( Is 58:5 , Jo 3:6 , Da LXX 9:3 , al.), Mt 11:21 , Lk 10:13 .†

G3087.mounce: a candlestick, lampstand, Mt. 5:15; met. a candlestick, as a figure of a Christian church, Rev. 1:12, 13, 20; of a teacher or prophet, Rev. 11:4
G3087.abbottSmith: a lampstand : Mt 5:15 , Mk 4:21 , Lk 8:16 11:33 ; of that in the Tabernacle, He 9:2 ; metaph., of the two witnesses, Re 11:4 ; of the seven churches of Asia, Re 1:12 , 13 , 20 2:1 ; of the removal of a church from its position, κινεῖν τ. λυχνίαν κ.τ.λ. , Re 2:5 .†
G3087.strongs: from G3088 (λύχνος); a lamp-stand (literally or figuratively)

G4761.thayer: I twist, torture; met: I twist or pervert language.
G4761.mounce: pr. to distort the limbs on a rack; met. to wrench, distort, pervert, 2 Pet. 3:16*
G4761.abbottSmith: to twist, torture . Metaph. (cf. II Ki, l.c. ), to twist or pervert language: II Pe 3:16 .†
G4761.strongs: from a derivative of G4762 (στρέφω); to wrench, i.e. (specially), to torture (by the rack), but only figuratively, to pervert

G5587.mounce: a whispering; a calumnious whispering, gossip, 2 Cor. 12:20*
G5587.strongs: from a derivative of (a whisper; by implication, a slander; probably akin to G5574 (ψεύδομαι)); whispering, i.e. secret detraction

G5089.mounce: to pull, pluck off, Mt. 12:1; Mk. 2:23; Lk. 6:1*
G5089.abbottSmith: to pluck, pluck off : στάχυας , Mt 12:1 , Mk 2:23 , Lk 6:1 .†
G5089.strongs: perhaps akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), and thus to G4951 (σύρω); to pull off

G4400.thayer: I appoint, choose, elect, take into hand.
G4400.mounce: also listed as a deponent, προχειρίζομαι, to take into the hand, to make ready for use or action; to constitute, destine, Acts 3:20; 22:14; 26:16*
G4400.abbottSmith: to put into the hand , deliver up : pass., Ac 3:20 .
G4400.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G5495 (χείρ); to handle for oneself in advance, i.e. (figuratively) to purpose

G1575.mounce: to break off, pass., be broken, Rom. 11:17, 19, 20*
G1575.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2806 (κλάω); to exscind

G3613.mounce: a habitation, dwelling, an abode, Jude 6; trop. the personal abode of the soul, 2 Cor. 5:2*
G3613.abbottSmith: a habitation : Ju 6 ; trop., II Co 5:2 .†
G3613.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3611 (οἰκέω) (equivalent to G3612 (οἴκημα)); a residence (literally or figuratively)

G1346.mounce: justly, with strict justice, 1 Pet. 2:23; deservedly, Lk. 23:41; as it is right, fit or proper, 1 Cor. 15:34; uprightly, honestly, piously, religiously, 1 Thess. 2:10; Tit. 2:12*
G1346.abbottSmith: righteously : I Co 15:34 , I Th 2:10 , Tit 2:12 .

G4208.strongs: adverb comparative of G4206 (πόῤῥω); further, i.e. a greater distance

G4565.thayer: Sharon, the maritime plain between Carmel and Joppa.
G4565.mounce: Saron, a level tract of Palestine, between Caesarea and Joppa, Acts 9:35*
G4565.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08289); Saron (i.e. Sharon), a district of Palestine

G1279.mounce: to go or pass through, Lk. 6:1; 13:22; Acts 16:4; to pass by, Lk. 18:36
G1279.abbottSmith: to carry over . Pass., to pass across, journey through : absol., Lk 18:36 , Ro 15:24 ; seq. κατὰ πόλεις κ. κώμας , Lk 13:22 ; διά , c. gen., Mk 2:23 , Lk 6:1 (cf. Pr 9:12 c , Wi 3:1 ); c. acc, Ac 16:4 .†
G1279.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to travel through
G1279.kjv: go through, journey in, pass by

G2910.thayer: I hang, hang up, suspend; mid: I am hanging, hang.
G2910.mounce: also spelled κρέμαμαι and κρεμάζω, to hang, suspend, Acts 5:30; 10:39; pass. to be hung, suspended, Mt. 18:6; Lk. 23:39; mid. κρέμαμαι, to hang, be suspended, Acts 28:4; Gal. 3:13; met. κρέμαμαι ἐν, to hang upon, to be referable to as an ultimate principle, Mt. 22:40
G2910.abbottSmith: trans., to hang , suspend : c. acc. pers., seq. ἐπὶ ξύλου , Ac 5:30 10:39 ; pass., Lk 23:39 ; seq. περί , Mt 18:6 . Mid., κρέμαμαι , intrans., to hang : seq. ἐκ , Ac 28:4 ; ἐπὶ ξύλου , Ga 3:13 (LXX) ; metaph., seq. ἐν , Mt 22:40 .†
G2910.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb; to hang

G4082.thayer: a sack, wallet for carrying provisions.
G4082.mounce: a leather bag or sack for provisions, wallet, Mt. 10:10; Mk. 6:8
G4082.abbottSmith: a leathern pouch for victuals, etc., a wallet (Deiss. thinks an alms-bag , v. LAE , 108 ff.): Mt 10:10 , Mk 6:8 , Lk 9:3 10:4 22:35 , 36 .†
G4082.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a wallet or leather pouch for food

G678.thayer: (literary and Jewish), without any preference (undue favor, partiality) for a person.
G678.mounce: without respect of persons, impartially, 1 Pet. 1:17*
G678.abbottSmith: without respect of persons , impartially : I Pe 1:17 .†
G678.strongs: adverb from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a presumed compound of G4383 (πρόσωπον) and G2983 (λαμβάνω) (compare G4381 (προσωπολήπτης)); in a way not accepting the person, i.e. impartially

G2160.mounce: facetiousness, pleasantry; hence, buffoonery, coarse laughter, Eph. 5:4*
G2160.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of the base of G5157 (τροπή) (meaning well-turned, i.e. ready at repartee, jocose); witticism, i.e. (in a vulgar sense) ribaldry

G1691.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G1691.strongs: a prolonged form of G3165 (μέ); me

G4264.thayer: I lead forward, lead on; met: I induce, incite, urge.
G4264.mounce: to cause any one to advance, to lead forward; met. to incite, instigate, Mt. 14:8*
G4264.abbottSmith: to lead forward , lead on ; metaph., to induce , incite , urge : Mt 14:8 .†
G4264.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and a reduplicated form of G971 (βιάζω); to force forward, i.e. bring to the front, instigate

G2616.mounce: to tyrannize over, oppress, exploit, Acts 10:38; Jas. 2:6*
G2616.abbottSmith: to exercise power over , oppress : c. acc. pers. (Xen.; LXX, Mi 2:2 , Wi 2:10 , al.); c. gen. pers., Ja 2:6 ( ὑμᾶς , T); pass., Ac 10:38 (cf. MM, Exp. , xv).†
G2616.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G1413 (δυνάστης); to exercise dominion against, i.e. oppress

G3079.thayer: Claudius Lysias, a Roman tribune of the soldiers in Jerusalem.
G3079.strongs: of uncertain affinity; Lysias, a Roman

G4442.thayer: fire; the heat of the sun, lightning; fig: strife, trials; the eternal fire.
G4442.mounce: fire, Mt. 3:10; 7:19; 13:40, et al. freq.; πυρός, used by Hebraism with the force of an adjective, fiery, fierce, Heb. 10:27; fire used figuratively to express various circumstances of severe trial, Lk. 12:49; 1 Cor. 3:13; Jude 23
G4442.abbottSmith: fire : Mk 9:22 , Lk 3:9 , Jo 15:6 , Ac 2:19 , I Co 3:13 , Ja 3:5 , Re 8:5 , al; π. καὶ θεῖον , Lk 17:29 ; κατακαίειν ( ἐν ) π. , Mt 13:40 , Re 17:16 18:8 ; καίεσθαι πυρί , He 12:18 , Re 8:8 21:8 ; φλὸξ πυρός , Ac 7:30 , II Th 1:8 , He 1:7 , Re 1:14 2:18 19:12 ; λαμπάδες πυρός , Re 4:5 ; στῦλοι πυρός , Re 10:1 ; ἄνθρακες πυρός , Ro 12:20 (LXX) ; γλῶσσαι ὡσεὶ πυρός , Ac 2:3 ; δοκιμάζειν ( πυροῦσθαι
G4442.strongs: a primary word; "fire" (literally or figuratively, specially, lightning)

G902.strongs: a diminutive of a derivative probably of the base of G939 (βάσις); a palm twig (as going out far)

G283.mounce: pr. unstained, unsoiled; met. undefiled, chaste, Heb. 7:26; 13:4; pure, sincere, Jas. 1:27; undefiled, unimpaired, 1 Pet. 1:4*
G283.abbottSmith: undefiled , free from contamination (in π. , of αἰθήρ ; MM, VGT , s.v.): He 7:26 13:4 , I Pe 1:4 , Ja 1:27 .†
G283.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3392 (μιαίνω); unsoiled, i.e. (figuratively) pure

G1675.thayer: a Hellenist, Grecian Jew, a Greek-speaking Jew, that is one who can speak Greek only and not Hebrew (or Aramaic).
G1675.mounce: pr. one who uses the language and follows the customs of the Greeks; in NT a Jew by blood, but a native of a Greek-speaking country, Hellenist, Acts 6:1; 9:29; 11:20*
G1675.strongs: from a derivative of G1672 (Ἕλλην); a Hellenist or Greek-speaking Jew

G2820.thayer: I cast lots, choose by lot, assign by lot, assign a portion, receive a share.
G2820.mounce: to obtain by lot or assignment; to obtain a portion, receive a share, Eph. 1:11*
G2820.strongs: from G2819 (κλῆρος); to allot, i.e. (figuratively) to assign (a privilege)

G5600.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G5600.strongs: the subjunctive of G1510 (εἰμί); (may, might, can, could, would, should, must, etc.; also with G1487 (εἰ) and its comparative, as well as with other particles) be
G5600.kjv: + appear, are, (may, might, should) be, X have, is, + pass the flower of her age, should stand, were

G4896.mounce: from εἶμι, to come together, Lk. 8:4*
G4896.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to go); to assemble

G3496.mounce: Neapolis, a city of Thrace on the Strymonic gulf, Acts 16:11*. See PhotoGuide.
G3496.abbottSmith: Neapolis , a maritime city of Macedonia: Ac 16:11 .†
G3496.strongs: from G3501 (νέος) and G4172 (πόλις); new town; Neapolis, a place in Macedonia

G2939.thayer: (often of the founder of a city), creator, God.
G2939.mounce: a founder; in NT a creator, 1 Pet. 4:19*
G2939.strongs: from G2936 (κτίζω); a founder, i.e. God (as author of all things)

G758.thayer: a ruler, governor, leader, leading man; with the Jews, an official member (a member of the executive) of the assembly of elders.
G758.mounce: one invested with power and dignity, chief, ruler, prince, magistrate, Mt. 9:23; 20:25
G758.abbottSmith: a ruler , chief : Jesus, Re 1:5 ; rulers of nations, Mt 20:25 , Ac 4:26 7:35 ; magistrates, Ac 23:5 , Ro 13:3 ; judges, Lk 12:58 , Ac 7:27 , 35 16:19 ; members of the Sanhedrin, Lk 14:1 23:13 , 35 24:20 , Jo 3:1 7:26 , 48 12:42 , Ac 3:17 4:5 , 8 13:27 14:5 ; rulers of synagogues, Mt 9:18 , 23 , Lk 8:41 18:18 ; οἱ ἄ. τ. αἰῶνος τούτου , I Co 2:6 , 8 ; of the devil: ἄ. τῶν δαιμονίων , Mt 9:34 12:24 ,
G758.strongs: present participle of G757 (ἄρχω); a first (in rank or power)

G3337.mounce: to exchange, change for or into, transmute, Rom. 1:25, 26*
G3337.abbottSmith: to exchange : τ. ἀλήθειαν . . . ἐν τ. ψεύδει , the truth for a lie (v. Bl., § 36, 8), Ro 1:25 .
G3337.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); to exchange

G2743.thayer: I cauterize, burn with a hot iron; hence met: I sear.
G2743.mounce: also spelled καυτηριάζω, to cauterize, brand; pass. met. to be branded with marks of guilt, or, to be seared into insensibility, 1 Tim. 4:2*
G2743.abbottSmith: to mark by branding , brand (AV, scar , but v. CGT , in l.): metaph., pass. pf. ptcp., I Ti 4:2 (cf. καυτήριον , IV Mac 15:22 *).†
G2743.strongs: from a derivative of G2545 (καίω); to brand ("cauterize"), i.e. (by implication) to render unsensitive (figuratively)

G3776.strongs: from the feminine of G5607 (ὤν); substance, i.e. property (possessions)

G891.thayer: as far as, up to, until, during.
G891.mounce: improper prep with the gen., also functioning as a conj, also spelled ἄχρις (Gal. 3:19; Heb. 3:13; Rev. 2:25), with respect to place, as far as; to time, until, during; as a conj., until
G891.strongs: akin to G206 (ἄκρον) (through the idea of a terminus); (of time) until or (of place) up to
G891.kjv: as far as, for, in(-to), till, (even, un-)to, until, while

G3690.thayer: vinegar, sour wine mixed with water, a common drink of Roman soldiers.
G3690.mounce: vinegar; a wine of sharp flavor, posca, which was an ordinary beverage, and was often mixed with bitter herbs, etc., and this given to the condemned criminals in order to stupefy them, and lessen their sufferings, Mt. 27:48; Mk. 15:36; Lk. 23:36; Jn. 19:29, 30
G3690.abbottSmith: sour wine ( posca , vin-de-pays ), the ordinary drink of labourers and common soldiers: Mt 27:48 , Mk 15:36 , Lk 23:36 , Jo 19:29 , 30 .†
G3690.strongs: from G3691 (ὀξύς); vinegar, i.e. sour wine

G1787.mounce: improper prep., gen., inside, within, Lk. 17:21; τὸ ἐντός, the interior, inside, Mt. 23:26*
G1787.abbottSmith: within : c. gen., ἐ. ὑμῶν , within you (i.e. in your hearts, R, txt.), or among you (R, mg.), Lk 17:21 (cf. Field, Notes , 71; Thayer, s.v.; ICC , Lk, l.c.; Dalman, Words , 145 ff.); τὸ ἐ. , Mt 23:26 .†

G2042.thayer: I stir up, arouse to anger, provoke, irritate, incite.
G2042.mounce: to provoke, to irritate, exasperate, Col. 3:21; to incite, stimulate, 2 Cor. 9:2*
G2042.abbottSmith: to stir up, provoke (as in cl.): Col 3:21 .
G2042.strongs: from a presumed prolonged form of G2054 (ἔρις); to stimulate (especially to anger)

G403.mounce: pr. a refreshing coolness after heat; met. refreshing, recreation, rest, Acts 3:20*
G403.strongs: from G404 (ἀναψύχω); properly, a recovery of breath, i.e. (figuratively) revival

G2573.mounce: well, rightly, suitable, with propriety, becomingly, 1 Cor. 7:37; 14:17; Gal. 4:17; 5:7; truly, justly, correctly, Mk. 12:32; Lk. 20:39; Jn. 4:17; appositely, Mt. 15:7; Mk. 7:6; becomingly, honorably, Jas. 2:3; well, effectually, Mk. 7:9, 37; καλῶς εἰπεῖν, to speak well, praise, applaud, Lk. 6:26; καλῶς ἔχειν, to be convalescent, Mk. 16:18; καλῶς ποιεῖν, to do good, confer benefits, Mt. 5:44; 12:12; to do well, act virtuously, Phil. 4:14
G2573.abbottSmith: finely , rightly , well : Lk 6:48 , I Co 14:17 , Ga 4:17 5:7 , Ja 2:3 ; λέγειν , λαλεῖν , etc., Mt 15:7 , Mk 7:6 12:28 , Lk 6:26 20:39 , Jo 4:17 8:48 13:13 18:23 , Ac 28:25 ; as exclamation of approval, Mk 12:32 , Ro 11:20 ; κ. ποιεῖν , Mt 12:12 , I Co 7:37 , 38 , Ja 2:8 , 19 ; c. dat. pers. (cl. acc.; WM, § 32, 1 β ), Lk 6:27 ; c. acc. rei, Mk 7:37 ; c. ptcp., Ac 10:33 (M, Pr. , 131), Phl 4:14 ,

G5368.thayer: I love (of friendship), regard with affection, cherish; I kiss.
G5368.mounce: pr. to manifest some act or token of kindness or affection; to kiss, Mt. 26:48; Mk. 14:44; Lk. 22:47; to love, regard with affection, have affection for, Mt. 10:37; Jn. 5:20; to like, be fond of, delight in a thing, Mt. 23:6; Rev. 22:15; to cherish inordinately, set store by, Jn. 12:25
G5368.abbottSmith: to love (with the love of emotion and friendship, Lat, amare ; v. SYN. ): c. acc. pers., Mt 10:37 , Jo 5:20 ; Jo 11:3 , 36 15:19 16:27 20:2 21:15-17 , I Co 16:22 , Re 3:19 ; ἐν πίστει , Tit 3:15 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 23:6 , Lk 20:46 , Jo 12:25 , Re 22:15 ; c. inf. ( Is 56:10 ; cf. Bl., § 69, 4), Mt 6:5 .
G5368.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος); to be a friend to (fond of (an individual or an object)), i.e. have affection for (denoting personal attachment, as a matter of sentiment or feeling; while G25 (ἀγαπάω) is wider, embracing especially the judgment and the deliberate assent of the will as a matter of principle, duty and propriety: the two thus stand related very much as G2309 (θέλω) and G1014 (βούλομαι), or as G2372 (θυμός) and G3563 (νοῦς) respectively; the former being chiefly of the heart and the latter of the head); specially, to kiss (as a mark of tenderness)

G4905.thayer: I come or go with, accompany; I come together, assemble.
G4905.mounce: pluperf., συνεληλύθεισαν (3 pl), to come together; to assemble, Mk. 3:20; 6:33; 14:53; to cohabit matrimonially, Mt. 1:18; 1 Cor. 7:5; to go or come with any one, to accompany, Lk. 23:55; Acts 9:39; to company with, associate with, Acts 1:21
G4905.abbottSmith: (cl.) to come together, assemble : Mk 3:20 14:53 , T, WH, txt., Ac 1:6 2:6 10:27 16:13 19:32 21:22 22:30 28:17 , I Co 14:26 ; seq. εἰς , c. acc loc., Ac 5:16 ; ἐπὶ τ. αὐτό , I Co 11:20 14:23 ; c. dat. pers., Mk 14:53 , WH, mg., R ( πρὸς αὐτόν , v. Field, Notes , 40), Jo 11:33 ; ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ , I Co 11:18 ; ἐνθάδε , Ac 25:17 ; αὐτοῦ , Mk 6:33 , WH, mg. (cf. WH, Intr. , 95 ff.); ὅπου , Jo 18:20 ; c. i
G4905.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to convene, depart in company with, associate with, or (specially), cohabit (conjugally)
G4905.kjv: accompany, assemble (with), come (together), come (company, go) with, resort

G4558.thayer: Zarephath, a town in the district of Sidon in Phoenicia.
G4558.mounce: Sarepta, a city of Phoenicia, between Tyre and Sidon, Lk. 4:26*
G4558.abbottSmith: Sarepta , a city of Sidon: Lk 4:26 .†
G4558.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06886); Sarepta (i.e. Tsarephath), a place in Palestine

G3614.thayer: a house, household, dwelling; meton: goods, property, means.
G3614.mounce: a house, dwelling, an abode, Mt. 2:11; 7:24, 27; trop. the bodily abode of the soul, 2 Cor. 5:1; meton. a household, family, Mt. 10:13; 12:25; meton. goods, property, means, Mt. 23:13
G3614.abbottSmith: a house , dwelling : Mt 2:11 7:24-27 , Mk 1:29 , al.; ἐν οἰκίᾳ (= cl. κατ ̓ οἰκίαν ), at home , Lk 8:27 ; εἰς οἰ. , II Jo 10 ; οἰ , c. gen. pers., usually has the art. ( Mt 8:14 , al., but cf. οἶκος and v. Bl., § 46, 9); ἡ οἰ. τ. πατρός μου , Jo 14:2 . Metaph., of the body as the dwelling of the soul: II Co 5:1 ; of property (as בַּיִת , Ge 45:18 , LXX, τ. ὑπάρχοντα ; III Ki 13:8 , LXX, οἶκος ) =
G3614.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος); properly, residence (abstractly), but usually (concretely) an abode (literally or figuratively); by implication, a family (especially domestics)

G4980.thayer: (a) I have leisure, (b) I stand empty (of a house).
G4980.mounce: to be unemployed, to be at leisure; to be at leisure for a thing, to devote one’s self entirely to a thing, 1 Cor. 7:5; to be unoccupied, empty, Mt. 12:44*
G4980.abbottSmith: to be at leisure , hence, to have time or opportunity for, to devote oneself to, be occupied in : I Co 7:5 ; of things, to be unoccupied, empty (Plut., Eur., al.): οἶκος , Mt 12:44 , Lk 11:25 (T [WH], R, om.).†
G4980.strongs: from G4981 (σχολή); to take a holiday, i.e. be at leisure for (by implication, devote oneself wholly to); figuratively, to be vacant (of a house)

G1230.thayer: I pass (of time); I continue through, intervene.
G1230.mounce: pass., to continue through; to intervene, elapse of time, Mk. 16:1; Acts 25:13; 27:9*
G1230.abbottSmith: to go through, to pass , e.g. τ. νύκτα ; absol., to live .
G1230.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1096 (γίνομαι); to elapse meanwhile
G1230.kjv: X after, be past, be spent

G1780.thayer: embalming, preparation of a body for burial.
G1780.mounce: preparation of a corpse for burial, burial itself, Mk. 14:8; Jn. 12:7*

G1138.mounce: also spelled Δανείδ and Δαβίδ, David, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:6; Lk. 1:27; Acts 2:29; Rom. 1:3; 2 Tim. 2:28
G1138.abbottSmith: David , King of Israel: Mt 1:6 ; 12:3 , et al.; σκηνὴ Δ. , Ac 15:16 ; κλεὶς Δ. , Re 3:7 ; θρόνος Δ. , Lk 1:32 ; ῥίζα Δ. , Re 5:5 ; βασιλεία Δ. , Mk 11:10 ; υἱὸς Δ. , the Messiah ( Ps. Sol., 17:23 ; for other reff. in Jewish lit., v. Dalman, Words , 317), Mt 1:1 9:27 , et al.; ἐν Δ. , i.e. the Psalter, He 4:7
G1138.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01732); Dabid (i.e. David), the Israelite king

G92.thayer: a legal wrong, crime (with which one is charged), misdeed, crime against God, a sin.
G92.mounce: an act of injustice, crime, Acts 18:14; 24:20; Rev. 18:5*
G92.abbottSmith: a wrong , injury , misdeed (MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 18:14 24:20 , Re 18:5 .†
G92.kjv: evil doing, iniquity, matter of wrong

G800.mounce: discordant in sound; met. discordant, at difference, Acts 28:25*
G800.abbottSmith: dissonant , discordant ; metaph., at variance : πρὸς ἀλλήλους , Ac 28:25 .†
G800.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4859 (σύμφωνος); inharmonious (figuratively)

G4951.mounce: to draw, drag, Jn. 21:8; Rev. 12:4; to force away, hale before magistrates, etc., Acts 8:3; 14:19; 17:6*
G4951.abbottSmith: to draw, drag : c. acc. rei, Jo 21:8 , Re 12:4 ; c. acc. pers., of taking to trial or punish­ment, Ac 8:3 ; ἔξω τ. πόλεως , Ac 14:19 ; ἐπὶ τ. πολιτάρχας (cf. IV Mac, l.c. ), Ac 17:6 (cf. κατα-σύρω ).†
G4951.strongs: probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); to trail

G5514.thayer: Chloe, probably with business connections either in Corinth or in Ephesus or in both.
G5514.strongs: feminine of apparently a primary word; "green"; Chloë, a Christian female

G580.thayer: a casting away, rejection, a loss.
G580.mounce: a casting off; rejection, reprobation, Rom. 11:15; loss, deprivation, of life, etc., Acts 27:22*
G580.abbottSmith: a throwing away , rejection : opp. to πρόσλημψις , Ro 11:15 .

G4774.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1097 (γινώσκω); fellow knowledge, i.e. concession

G1536.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G5100 (τὶς); if any
G1536.kjv: he that, if a(-ny) man('s thing, from any, ought), whether any, whosoever

G5513.abbottSmith: warm, tepid ; metaph., of persons, luke-warm : Re 3:16 .†

G539.abbottSmith: deceit , deceitfulness : Col 2:8 ; τοῦ πλούτου , Mt 13:22 , Mk 4:19 (MM, VGT , s.v.); τῆς ἀδικίας , II Th 2:10 ; τῆς ἁμαρτίας , He 3:13 ; αἱ ἐπιθυμίαι τῆς ἀ. , Eph 4:22 . pl., ἀπάται (v. M, II Th. 2:10 ; NTD , 75; MM, VGT , s.v.): II Pe 2:13 (WH, mg., R., txt., ἐν ἀγάπαις ).†

G1183.mounce: to cause to pay tithes; pass. to be tithed, pay tithes, Heb. 7:6, 9*
G1183.abbottSmith: to take tithe of : c. acc. pers., He 7:6 ; pass. to pay tithe : He 7:9 .†
G1183.strongs: from G1181 (δεκάτη); to tithe, i.e. to give or take a tenth

G1096.thayer: I come into being, am born, become, come about, happen.
G1096.mounce: pluperfect, ἐγενόει (3rd sg), to come into existence; to be created, exist by creation, Jn. 1:3, 10; Heb. 11:3; Jas. 3:9; to be born, produced, grow, Mt. 21:19; Jn. 8:58, et al.; to arise, come on, occur, as the phenomena of nature, etc.; Mt. 8:24, 26; 9:16, et al.; to come, approach, as morning or evening, Mt. 8:16; 14:15, 23; to be appointed, constituted, established, Mk. 2:27; Gal. 3:17, et al.; to take place, come to pass, happen, occur, Mt. 1:22; 24:6, 20, 21, 34, et al. freq.; to be done, performed, effected, Mt. 21:42, et al.; to be fulfilled, satisfied, Mt. 6:10; 26:42, et al.; to come into a particular state or condition; to become, assume the character and appearance of anything, Mt. 5:45, et al.; to become or be made anything, be changed or converted, Mt. 4:3; 21:42; Mk. 1:17, et al.; to be, Mt. 11:26; 19:8; γίνεσθαι ὑπό τινα, to be subject to, Gal. 4:4; γίνεσθαι ἐν ἑαυτῷ, to come to one’s self, to recover from a trance or surprise, Acts 12:11; μὴ γένοιτο, let it not be, far be it from, God forbid, Lk. 20:16; Rom. 3:4, 31, et al.; to be kept, celebrated, solemnized, as festivals, Mt. 26:2, et al.; to be finished, completed, Heb. 4:3
G1096.abbottSmith: of persons, things occurrences, to come into being, be born, arise, come on : Jo 1:15 8:58 , I Co 15:37 ; a first appearance in public, Mk 1:4 , Jo 1:6 , al.; seq. ἐκ (of birth), Ro 1:3 , Ga 4:4 ; διά , Jo 1:3 ; βροντή , Jo 12:29 ; σεισμός , Re 6:12 ; γογγυσμός , Ac 6:1 ; χαρά , Ac 8:8 , many other similar exx.; ἡμέρα , Lk 22:66 , al.; ὀψέ , Mk 11:19 ; πρωΐα , Mt 27:1 ; νύξ , Ac 27:27 . Of events,
G1096.strongs: a prolongation and middle voice form of a primary verb; to cause to be ("gen"-erate), i.e. (reflexively) to become (come into being), used with great latitude (literal, figurative, intensive, etc.)
G1096.kjv: arise, be assembled, be(-come, -fall, -have self), be brought (to pass), (be) come (to pass), continue, be divided, draw, be ended, fall, be finished, follow, be found, be fulfilled, + God forbid, grow, happen, have, be kept, be made, be married, be ordained to be, partake, pass, be performed, be published, require, seem, be showed, X soon as it was, sound, be taken, be turned, use, wax, will, would, be wrought

G987.thayer: I speak evil against, blaspheme, use abusive or scurrilous language about (God or men).
G987.mounce: to defame, revile, slander, Mt. 27:39; to speak of God or divine things in terms of impious irreverence, to blaspheme, Mt. 9:3; 26:65
G987.abbottSmith: to speak lightly or profanely of sacred things (in cl., opp. of εὐφημέω ), esp. to speak impiously of God, to blaspheme, speak blasphemously : absol., Mt 9:3 26:65 , Mk 2:7 , Jo 10:36 , Ac 26:11 , I Ti 1:20 , II Pe 2:12 ; τ. θεόν , Ac 19:37 , Re 16:11 , 21 ; τὸ ὄνομα τ. θεοῦ , Re 13:6 16:9 ; δόξας , Ju 3 , II Pe 2:10 ; εἰς τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον , Mk 3:29 , Lk 12:10 . to revile, rail at, sla
G987.strongs: from G989 (βλάσφημος); to vilify; specially, to speak impiously
G987.kjv: (speak) blaspheme(-er, -mously, -my), defame, rail on, revile, speak evil

G1098.thayer: the unfermented juice of grapes; hence: sweet new wine.
G1098.mounce: pr. the unfermented juice of grapes; hence, sweet new wine, Acts 2:13*
G1098.strongs: akin to G1099 (γλυκύς); sweet wine, i.e. (properly) must (fresh juice), but used of the more saccharine (and therefore highly inebriating) fermented wine

G2151.thayer: I am dutiful, pious, show piety towards, worship.
G2151.mounce: to exercise piety; towards a deity, to worship, Acts 17:23; towards relatives, to be dutiful towards, 1 Tim. 5:4*
G2151.abbottSmith: to reverence, show piety towards ; c. acc (else­where more freq. seq. εἰς., περί, πρός ): οἶκον , I Ti 5:4 ; θεόν , Ac 17:23 (Cremer, 525).†
G2151.strongs: from G2152 (εὐσεβής); to be pious, i.e. (towards God) to worship, or (towards parents) to respect (support)

G2965.thayer: a dog, universally despised in the East.
G2965.mounce: a dog, Mt. 7:6; Lk. 16:21; 2 Pet. 2:22; met. a dog, a religious corrupter, Phil. 3:2; miscreant, Rev. 22:15*
G2965.abbottSmith: a dog : Lk 16:21 , II Pe 2:22 ; metaph., as a word of reproach, Mt 7:6 , Phl 3:2 , Re 22:15 .†
G2965.strongs: a primary word; a dog ("hound") (literally or figuratively)

G2420.thayer: the abstract notion of the priestly office.
G2420.strongs: from G2413 (ἱερός); sacredness, i.e. (by implication) the priestly office

G5206.thayer: adoption, as a son into the divine family.
G5206.mounce: adoption, a placing in the condition of a son, Rom. 8:15, 23; 9:4; Gal. 4:5; Eph. 1:5*
G5206.strongs: from a presumed compound of G5207 (υἱός) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); the placing as a son, i.e. adoption (figuratively, Christian sonship in respect to God)

G2708.mounce: to pour out or down upon, Mt. 26:7; Mk. 14:3*
G2708.abbottSmith: to pour down upon : c. gen. (cl.), Mk 14:3 ; seq. ἐπί , c. gen., Mt 26:7 .†
G2708.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and cheo (to pour); to pour down (out)

G4125.mounce: pr. a rib; the side of the body, Jn. 19:34; 20:20, 25, 27; Acts 12:7*
G4125.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a rib, i.e. (by extension) side

G3979.mounce: on foot, or, by land, Mt. 14:13; Mk. 6:33
G3979.abbottSmith: on foot : Mt 14:13 (WH, mg., R, txt.).
G3979.strongs: dative case feminine of a derivative of G4228 (πούς) (as adverb); foot-wise, i.e. by walking

G692.mounce: pr. inactive, unemployed, Mt. 20:3, 6; idle, averse from labor, 1 Tim. 5:13; Tit. 1:12; met. 2 Pet. 1:8; unprofitable, hollow, or by impl., injurious, Mt. 12:36; Jas. 2:20*
G692.abbottSmith: inactive , idle : Mt 20:3 , 6 , I Ti 5:13 , Tit 1:12 , II Pe 1:8 . Metaph., of things, inactive , ineffective , worthless : ῥῆμα , Mt 12:36 ; πίστις , Ja 2:20 (v. Cremer, 259 f.).†
G692.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2041 (ἔργον); inactive, i.e. unemployed; (by implication) lazy, useless

G4399.mounce: to outstrip, anticipate; to anticipate any one in doing or saying a thing, be beforehand with, Mt. 17:25*
G4399.abbottSmith: c. ptcp. (as in cl., but more freq. the simple φθάνω , q.v.), to anticipate : Mt 17:25 .†
G4399.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5348 (φθάνω); to get an earlier start of, i.e. anticipate

G2920.thayer: judging, judgment, decision, sentence; generally: divine judgment; accusation.
G2920.mounce: pr. distinction; discrimination; judgment, decision, award, Jn. 5:30; 7:24; 8:16; a judicial sentence, Jn. 3:19; Jas. 2:13; an adverse sentence, Mt. 23:33; Mk. 3:29; judgment, judicial process, trial, Mt. 10:15; Jn. 5:24; 12:31; 16:8; judgment, administration of justice, Jn. 5:22, 27; in NT a court of justice, tribunal, Mt. 5:21, 22; an impeachment, 2 Pet. 2:11; Jude 9; from the Hebrew, justice, equity, Mt. 12:18, 20; 23:23; Lk. 11:42
G2920.strongs: decision (subjectively or objectively, for or against); by extension, a tribunal; by implication, justice (especially, divine law)

G4488.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (apparently for H07509); Resa (i.e. Rephajah), an Israelite

G425.mounce: pr. the relaxing of a state of constraint; relaxation of rigor of confinement, Acts 24:23; met. ease, rest, peace, tranquility, 2 Cor. 2:13; 7:5; 8:13; 2 Thess. 1:7*
G425.abbottSmith: a loosening, relaxation : Ac 24:23 (RV, indulgence ; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.); by St. Paul, opp. to θλίψις , expressed or understood, relief : II Co 2:12 7:5 8:13 , II Th 1:7 .†

G3085.thayer: (in the Old Testament: ransoming from imprisonment for debt, or from slavery, release from national misfortune, etc.), liberation, deliverance, release.
G3085.abbottSmith: a ransoming , redemption ( αἰχμαλώτων , Plut., Arat. , 11): of the mediatorial work of Christ, He 9:12 ; in general sense, deliverance (cf. Ps 48 (49):8 ): Lk 1:68 2:38 .†

G2159.strongs: adverb from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of (to stretch); in a well-strung manner, i.e. (figuratively) intensely (in a good sense, cogently; in a bad one, fiercely)

G2575.mounce: a furnace, oven, kiln, Mt. 13:42, 50; Rev. 1:15; 9:2*

G579.thayer: worthy to be cast away, worthless, regarded as vile.
G579.mounce: pr. to be cast away; met. to be condemned, regarded as vile, 1 Tim. 4:4*
G579.abbottSmith: to be thrown away , rejected : I Ti 4:4 (Hom., Plut.).†
G579.strongs: from G577 (ἀποβάλλω); cast off, i.e. (figuratively) such as to be rejected

G1617.mounce: intensely, fervently, earnestly, Lk. 22:44; Acts 12:5; 1 Pet. 1:22*
G1617.strongs: neuter of the comparative of G1618 (ἐκτενής); more intently

G930.thayer: one who tortures, a tormentor, jailor.
G930.mounce: pr. an inquisitor, tormentor; in NT a keeper of a prison, jailer, Mt. 18:34*
G930.abbottSmith: prop., a torturer ; used of a gaoler, Mt 18:34 .†

G720.thayer: (a) I deny (a statement), (b) I repudiate (a person, or belief).
G720.abbottSmith: to deny , say no , opp. to εἰπεῖν : absol., Mt 26:70 , Lk 8:45 ; seq. ὅτι , I Jo 2:22 ; c. inf., He 11:24 .
G720.strongs: perhaps from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the middle voice of G4483 (ῥέω); to contradict, i.e. disavow, reject, abnegate

G240.abbottSmith: of one another , mutually : Mt 25:32 , Mk 4:41 , Jo 13:22 , al.
G240.strongs: Genitive plural from G243 (ἄλλος) reduplicated; one another
G240.kjv: each other, mutual, one another, (the other), (them-, your-)selves, (selves) together (sometimes with G3326 (μετά) or G4314 (πρός))

G5992.mounce: Three Taverns, the name of a station on the Appian Way in Acts 28:15 (Τριῶν ταβερνῶν). The Latin taberna is an inn or shop

G4520.thayer: a keeping of the Sabbath, a Sabbath rest.
G4520.mounce: pr. a keeping of a sabbath; a state of rest, a sabbath-state, Heb. 4:9*
G4520.abbottSmith: a keeping sabbath, a sabbath rest : metaph., as in Mishna (Zorell, s.v.), He 4:9 .†
G4520.strongs: from a derivative of G4521 (σάββατον); a "sabbatism", i.e. (figuratively) the repose of Christianity (as a type of heaven)

G2682.mounce: pr. the pitching a tent; a tent; in NT a dwelling place, Mt. 8:20; Lk. 9:58*
G2682.abbottSmith: prop., an encamping , taking up one's quarters (Polyb., al., v. LS; and cf. LXX, I Ch 28:2 , Ez 37:27 , To 1:4 , Wi 9:8 , II Mac 14:35 ).
G2682.strongs: from G2681 (κατασκηνόω); an encamping, i.e. (figuratively) a perch

G1984.mounce: inspection, oversight, visitation; of God, visitation, interposition, whether in mercy or judgment, Lk. 19:44; 1 Pet. 2:12; the office of an ecclesiastical overseer, 1 Tim. 3:1; from the Hebrew, charge, function, Acts 1:20*
G1984.abbottSmith: a visiting, visitation ( εἰς ἐ. τοῦ παιδός , Lucian, dial. deor., 20, 6); as in LXX (after Heb.), of God's visitation in mercy, or in judgment ( Le 19:20 Jb 10:12 , Je 6:15 , Wi 2:20 , al.): Lk 19:44 , I Pe 2:12 (v. Hort, in l.).
G1984.strongs: from G1980 (ἐπισκέπτομαι); inspection (for relief); by implication, superintendence; specially, the Christian "episcopate"
G1984.kjv: the office of a "bishop", bishoprick, visitation

G2737.abbottSmith: lower ; τὰ. κ. τῆς γῆς (v. AR, Eph 4:9 ; for rendering this lower earth , v. ICC , Eph 4:9 ), the lower parts of the earth (cf. Ps 138 (139):15 ; cf. also MGr., ἡ κάτου γῆ , the underworld , Thumb, MGV , 334): Eph 4:9 .†
G2737.strongs: comparative from G2736 (κάτω); inferior (locally, of Hades)

G2675.thayer: (a) I fit (join) together; met: I compact together, (b) act. and mid: I prepare, perfect, for his (its) full destination or use, bring into its proper condition (whether for the first time, or after a lapse).
G2675.mounce: to adjust thoroughly; to knit together, unite completely, 1 Cor. 1:10; to frame, Heb. 11:3; to prepare, provide, Mt. 21:16; Heb. 10:5; to qualify fully, to complete in character, Lk. 6:40; Heb. 13:21; 1 Pet. 5:10; perf. pass. κατηρτισμένα, fit, ripe, Rom. 9:22; to repair, refit, Mt. 4:21; Mk. 1:19; to supply, make good, 1 Thess. 3:10; to restore to a forfeited condition, to reinstate, Gal. 6:1; 2 Cor. 13:11*
G2675.abbottSmith: to render ἄρτιος , i.e. fit , complete ; to mend , repair : Mt 4:21 , Mk 1:19 ; to furnish completely , complete , equip , prepare : pass., Lk 6:40 , Ro 9:22 , He 11:3 ; mid., Mt 21:16 (LXX) , He 10:5 (LXX) ; in ethical sense, to prepare , complete , perfect : Ga 6:1 (EV, restore ), I Pe 5:10 ; pass., I Co 1:10 (Field, Notes , 167), II Co 13:11 , He 13:21 (cf. προ-καταρτίζω ).†
G2675.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G739 (ἄρτιος); to complete thoroughly, i.e. repair (literally or figuratively) or adjust
G2675.kjv: fit, frame, mend, (make) perfect(-ly join together), prepare, restore

G4508.mounce: filthy, squalid, sordid, dirty, Jas. 2:2; met. defiled, polluted, Rev. 22:11*
G4508.abbottSmith: filthy, dirty : of old, shabby clothing ( Za, l.c. ), Ja 2:2 ; metaph., of moral defilement, Re 22:11 .†
G4508.strongs: from G4509 (ῥύπος); dirty, i.e. (relatively) cheap or shabby; morally, wicked

G74.thayer: (properly the feeling of the athlete before a contest), great fear, terror, of death; anxiety, agony.
G74.mounce: contest, violent struggle; agony, anguish, Lk. 22:44*
G74.abbottSmith: a contest, wrestling (Eur., Xen.). Of the mind, great fear, agony, anguish (Dem., Arist.): Lk 22:44 (cf . Field, Notes , 77 f. ; Abbott, Essays , 101 f. ; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G74.strongs: from G73 (ἀγών); a struggle (properly, the state), i.e. (figuratively) anguish

G4205.thayer: a fornicator, man who prostitutes himself.
G4205.mounce: a catamite; in NT a fornicator, impure person, 1 Cor. 5:9, 10, 11; 6:9
G4205.strongs: from (to sell; akin to the base of G4097 (πιπράσκω)); a (male) prostitute (as venal), i.e. (by analogy) a debauchee (libertine)

G2453.mounce: Jewish, Mk. 1:5; Jn. 3:22; Acts 16:1; 24:24; pr. one sprung from the tribe of Judah, or a subject of the kingdom of Judah; in NT a descendant of Jacob, a Jew, Mt. 28:15; Mk. 7:3; Acts 19:34; Rom. 2:28, 29
G2453.abbottSmith: Jewish : ἀνήρ , Ac 10:28 22:3 ; ἄνθρωπος , Ac 21:39 ; ψευδοπροφήτης , 13:6 ; ἀσχιερεύς , 19:14 ; γυνή , 16:1 24:24 ; γῆ , Jo 3:22 ; χώρα , Mk 1:5 . Substantively, Ἰουδαῖος, ὁ , a Jew : Jo 4:9 , Ac 18:24 , Ro 2:28 ; pl., Re 2:9 3:9 ; οἱ Ἰ. , Mt 2:2 , Mk 7:3 , Jo 2:6 , al.; Ἰ. τε καὶ Ἕλληνες , Ac 14:1 , al.; κ. προσήλυτοι , Ac 2:10 ; ἔθνη τε κ. Ἰ. , Ac 14:5 ; οἱ κατὰ τὰ ἔθνη Ἰ. , Ac 21:21 ; of Jewis
G2453.strongs: from G2448 (Ἰουδά) (in the sense of G2455 (Ἰούδας) as a country); Judæan, i.e. belonging to Jehudah

G5374.thayer: a Philippian, an inhabitant of Philippi.
G5374.mounce: a Philippian, a citizen of Φίλιπποι, Philippi, Phil. 4:15; title*
G5374.strongs: from G5375 (Φίλιπποι); a Philippesian (Philippian), i.e. native of Philippi

G4978.thayer: a rent, as in a garment; a division, dissention.
G4978.mounce: a split, Mt. 9:16; Mk. 2:21; met. a division into parties, schism, Jn. 7:43; 9:16; 10:19; 1 Cor. 1:10; 11:18; 12:25*
G4978.abbottSmith: a rent (Arist., al.): Mt 9:16 , Mk 2:21 . Metaph., a dissension, division : Jo 7:43 9:16 10:19 , I Co 1:10 11:18 12:25 .†
G4978.strongs: from G4977 (σχίζω); a split or gap ("schism"), literally or figuratively

G1050.thayer: Gaius, (a) a Corinthian, (b) a Macedonian, (c) a citizen of Derbe, (d) an Ephesian.
G1050.mounce: Gaius, pr. name. (1) Of Macedonia, Acts 19:29. (2) Of Corinth, 1 Cor. 1:14. (3) Of Derbe, Acts 20:4. (4) A Christian to whom John addressed his third Epistle, 3 Jn. 1; Rom. 16:23*
G1050.abbottSmith: Gaius , the name of a Christian; of Macedonia: Ac 19:29 . Of Derbe: Ac 20:4 . Of Corinth: Ro 16:23 , I Co 1:14 . The one to whom III Ep. Jo is addressed: III Jo 1 .†
G1050.strongs: of Latin origin; Gaius (i.e. Caius), a Christian

G1250.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); two hundred

G1290.thayer: lit: scattering abroad of seed by the sower, hence: dispersion, used especially of the Jews who had migrated and were scattered over the ancient world.
G1290.mounce: pr. a scattering, as of seed; dispersion; in NT meton. the dispersed portion of the Jews, specially termed the dispersion, Jn. 7:35; Jas. 1:1; 1 Pet. 1:1*
G1290.abbottSmith: a dispersion : δ. τῶν Ἑλλήνων , Jo 7:35 ; metaph., of Christians ( DB , iii, 782 f.), Ja 1:1 , I Pe 1:1 (v. Hort, in ll.).†
G1290.strongs: from G1289 (διασπείρω); dispersion, i.e. (specially and concretely) the (converted) Israelite resident in Gentile countries

G2163.thayer: well reported of, spoken in a kindly spirit, laudable, reputable.
G2163.mounce: pr. of good omen, auspicious; hence, of good report, commendable, laudable, reputable, Phil. 4:8*
G2163.abbottSmith: primarily, utter­ing words or sounds of good omen , hence,
G2163.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G5345 (φήμη); well spoken of, i.e. reputable

G5202.thayer: I am only a water drinker.
G5202.mounce: to be only a water drinker, 1 Tim. 5:23*
G5202.abbottSmith: to drink water , opp. to οἴνῳ χρῆσθαι : I Ti 5:23 (Hdt., i, 71; Plat., Rep. , 561 c, al.).†
G5202.strongs: from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G4095 (πίνω); to be a water-drinker, i.e. to abstain from vinous beverages

G3380.mounce: not yet, not as yet, Rom. 9:11; Heb. 9:8*
G3380.abbottSmith: not yet : c. ptcp., Ro 9:11 ; c. acc. et inf., He 9:8 .†
G3380.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G4452 (-πω); not yet

G2755.strongs: from G2756 (κενός) and G1391 (δόξα); vainly glorifying, i.e. self-conceited

G906.thayer: (a) I cast, throw, rush, (b) often, in the weaker sense: I place, put, drop.
G906.mounce: pluperfect, ἐβεβλήμην, to throw, cast; to lay, Rev. 2:22; Mt. 8:6, 14; to put, place, Jas. 3:3; to place, deposit, Mt. 27:6; Mk. 12:41-44; Lk. 21:1-4; Jn. 12:6; to pour, Jn. 13:5; to thrust, Jn. 18:11; 20:27; Mk. 7:33; Rev. 14:19; to send forth, Mt. 10:34; to assault, strike, Mk. 14:65; met. to suggest, Jn. 13:2; intrans. to rush, beat, as the wind, Acts 27:14
G906.abbottSmith: prop., of a weapon or missile; then generally, of things and persons, lit. and metaph., to throw, cast, put, place : c. acc., seq. εἰς , Mt 4:18 , and freq. ἐπί , Mt 10:34 ; κάτω , Mt 4:6 ; ἔξω , Mt 5:13 ; ἀπό , Mt 5:29 ; ἐκ , Mk 12:44 ; δρέπανον , Re 14:19 ; μάχαιραν , Mt 10:34 ; κλῆρον , Mt 27:35 ; of fluids, to pour : Mt 9:17 , Jo 13:5 ; pass., to be laid, to lie ill : Mt 9:2 ; ἐβλήθη (timeless
G906.strongs: a primary verb; to throw (in various applications, more or less violent or intense)
G906.kjv: arise, cast (out), X dung, lay, lie, pour, put (up), send, strike, throw (down), thrust

G205.thayer: (lit: top of a heap), first-fruits, spoil, treasure (taken in war).
G205.mounce: the first-fruits of the produce of the ground, which were taken from the top of the heap and offered to the gods; the best and choicest of the spoils of war, usually collected in a heap, Heb. 7:4*
G205.abbottSmith: prop., the top of a heap , hence, in pl., first-fruits (Xen.; MM, VGT , s.v.). In war, the choicest spoils (cf. Hdt., viii, 121 f.): He 7:4 .†
G205.strongs: from G206 (ἄκρον) and (a heap); properly (in the plural) the top of the heap, i.e. (by implication) best of the booty

G401.thayer: outpouring, excess, overflow, a pouring out.
G401.mounce: a pouring out; met. excess, stream, flood, 1 Pet. 4:4*
G401.abbottSmith: a pouring out , overflowing , excess : metaph., I Pe 4:4 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G401.strongs: from a comparative of G303 (ἀνά) and (to pour); properly, effusion, i.e. (figuratively) license

G3983.thayer: I am hungry, needy, desire earnestly.
G3983.mounce: to hunger, be hungry, Mt. 4:2; Mk. 11:12; to be exposed to hunger, be famished, 1 Cor. 4:11; Phil. 4:12; met. to hunger after, desire earnestly, long for, Mt. 5:6
G3983.strongs: from the same as G3993 (πένης) (through the idea of pinching toil; "pine"); to famish (absolutely or comparatively); figuratively, to crave

G2271.mounce: rest, quiet, tranquillity; a quiet, tranquil life, 2 Thess. 3:12; silence, silent attention, Acts 22:2; 1 Tim. 2:11, 12*
G2271.strongs: feminine of G2272 (ἡσύχιος); (as noun) stillness, i.e. desistance from bustle or language

G5383.thayer: I love the chief place, desire preeminence.
G5383.mounce: to love or desire to be first or chief, affect pre-eminence, 3 Jn. 9*
G5383.abbottSmith: to strive to be first : III Jo 9 (eccl.).†
G5383.strongs: from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G4413 (πρῶτος); to be fond of being first, i.e. ambitious of distinction

G992.thayer: a verbal adj: one must put, that ought to be put.
G992.mounce: requiring to be cast or put, Lk. 5:38*
G992.strongs: from G906 (βάλλω); fit to be cast (i.e. applied)

G3854.thayer: (a) I come on the scene, appear, come, (b) with words expressing destination: I present myself at, arrive at, reach.
G3854.mounce: pluperfect, παραγεγόνει (3 sg), to be by the side of; to come, approach, arrive, Mt. 2:1; 3:13; Mk. 14:43; Lk. 7:4; seq. ἐπί, to come upon in order to seize, Lk. 22:52; to come forth in public, make appearance, Mt. 3:1; Heb. 9:11
G3854.abbottSmith: to be beside or at hand (Hom., Hdt., al.), hence, to stand by , support (Æsch., Thuc., al.): c. dat. pers., II Ti 4:16 .
G3854.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1096 (γίνομαι); to become near, i.e. approach (have arrived); by implication, to appear publicly

G4772.mounce: kindred; kinsfolk, kinsmen, relatives, Lk. 1:61; Acts 7:3, 14*

G184.strongs: of Chaldee origin (meaning field of blood; corresponding to H02506 and H01818); Akeldama, a place near Jerusalem

G1409.thayer: Drusilla (born A.D. 39), daughter of Herod Agrippa I.
G1409.strongs: a feminine diminutive of Drusus (a Roman name); Drusilla, a member of the Herodian family

G3200.thayer: a parchment leaf, perhaps for notes.

G2696.thayer: I seal up, secure with a seal.
G2696.abbottSmith: to seal up , secure with a seal : Re 5:1 .†
G2696.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4972 (σφραγίζω); to seal closely

G2498.thayer: (Hebrew), Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, son of Asaph, father of Joram, an ancestor of Jesus.
G2498.mounce: Josaphat, king of Judah (1 Ki. 22:41), pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:8*
G2498.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03092); Josaphat (i.e. Jehoshaphat), an Israelite

G1154.mounce: Damascus, the capital city of Syria
G1154.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01834); Damascus, a city of Syria

G2513.thayer: clean, pure, unstained, either literally or ceremonially or spiritually; guiltless, innocent, upright.
G2513.mounce: clean, pure, unsoiled, Mt. 23:26; 27:59; met. clean from guilt, guiltless, innocent, Acts 18:6; 20:26; sincere, upright, virtuous, void of evil, Mt. 5:8; Jn. 15:3; clean ceremonially and morally, Lk. 11:41
G2513.abbottSmith: pure , clean . Physically: Mt 23:26 27:59 , Jo 13:10 , 11 (fig.), Jo 15:3 (fig., as of a vine cleansed by pruning), He 10:22 , Re 15:6 19:8 , 14 21:18 , 21 . Ceremonially: Lk 11:41 , Ro 14:20 , Tit 1:15 . Ethically; of persons: Jo 13:16 , Ac 18:6 , Tit 1:15 ; ὁ κ. τῇ καρδίᾳ ( καθαρὸς χειρας , Hdt., i, 35), Mt 5:8 ; seq. ἀπό (cl. c. gen. simp.; Bl., § 36, 11; Deiss., BS , 196; MM, Exp. , xv), Ac 20
G2513.strongs: of uncertain affinity; clean (literally or figuratively)

G2485.strongs: diminutive from G2486 (ἰχθύς); a petty fish

G2155.abbottSmith: in Hippocr., as medical term (LS, s.v.).
G2155.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G4698 (σπλάγχνον); well compassioned, i.e. sympathetic

G4383.mounce: the face, countenance, visage, Mt. 6:16, 17; 17:2, 6; according to late usage, a person, individual, 2 Cor. 1:11; hence, a personal presence, 1 Thess. 2:17; from the Hebrew, πρόσωπον πρὸς πρόσωπον, face to face, clearly, perfectly, 1 Cor. 13:12; face, surface, external form, figure, appearance, Mt. 16:3; Lk. 12:56; external circumstances, or condition of any one, Mt. 22:16; Mk. 12:14; πρόσωπον λαμβάνειν, to have respect to the external circumstances of any one, Lk. 20:21; Gal. 2:6; ἐν προσώπῳ, in presence of, 2 Cor. 2:10; ἀπὸ προσώπου, from the presence of, from, Acts 3:19; also, from before, Acts 7:45; εἰς πρόσωπον, and κατὰ πρόσωπον, in the presence of, before, Acts 3:13; 2 Cor. 8:24; also, openly, Gal. 2:11; κατὰ πρόσωπον, ἔχειν, to have before one’s face, to have any one present, Acts 25:16; ἀπὸ προσώπου, from, Rev. 12:14; πρὸ προσώπου, before, Acts 13:24
G4383.abbottSmith: prop., of persons (so always in cl.); the face , countenance : Mt 6:16 , 17 , Mk 14:65 , Lk 9:29 , II Co 3:7 , Re 4:7 , al.; τὸ π. τῆς γενέσεως (EV, natural face ), Ja 1:23 ; πίπτειν ἐπὶ ( τὰ ) π. , Mt 17:6 , Lk 5:12 , Re 7:11 , al.; ἀγνοούμενος τῷ π. , Ga 1:22 ; π., οὐ καρδίᾳ , I Th 2:17 ; κατὰ πρόσωπον ( in front , facing : Thuc., Xen., al.), when present , face to face , Ac 25:16 , II Co 10:1 ,
G4383.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and (the visage, from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)); the front (as being towards view), i.e. the countenance, aspect, appearance, surface; by implication, presence, person
G4383.kjv: (outward) appearance, X before, countenance, face, fashion, (men's) person, presence

G1021.mounce: slow, not hasty, Jas. 1:19; slow of understanding, heavy, stupid, Lk. 24:25*
G1021.abbottSmith: slow : εἰς τὸ λαλῆσαι , Ja 1:19 ; metaph., of the understanding: β. τ. καρδίᾳ , assoc. with ἀνόητος , Lk 24:25 .†
G1021.strongs: of uncertain affinity; slow; figuratively, dull

G194.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G2767 (κεράννυμι); undiluted

G5379.mounce: a love of contention; rivalry, contention, Lk. 22:24*
G5379.abbottSmith: love of strife, rivalry, emulation (Plat., al.), but mostly in bad sense (Thuc., al.), contentiousness, contention : Lk 22:24 (but v. Field, Notes , 75 f.).†
G5379.strongs: from G5380 (φιλόνεικος); quarrelsomeness, i.e. a dispute

G1367.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G5507 (χίλιοι); two thousand

G4253.thayer: (a) of place: before, in front of, (b) of time: before, earlier than.
G4253.mounce: before, of place, in front of, in advance of, Mt. 11:10; Lk. 1:76; Acts 5:23; before, of time, Mt. 5:12; Lk. 11:38; before an infin. with the gen. of the article, before, before that, Mt. 6:8; Lk. 2:21; before, above, in preference, Jas. 5:12; 1 Pet. 4:8
G4253.abbottSmith: before ; of place: Ac 12:6 , 14 14:13 , Ja 5:9 ; π. προσώπου (= Heb. לִפְנֵי , De 3:18 , Ma 3:1 , al.; Bl., § 40, 9), Mt 11:10 , Mk 1:2 , Lk 1:76 7:27 9:52 10:1 , Ac 14:13 ; of time: Mt 8:29 24:38 , Lk 11:38 21:12 , Jo 11:55 13:1 17:24 , Ac 5:36 21:38 , I Co 2:7 4:5 , Eph 1:4 , Col 1:17 , II Ti 1:9 4:21 , Tit 1:2 , He 11:5 , I Pe 1:20 , Ju 25 ; π. ἐτῶν δεκατεσσάρων , fourteen years ago , II Co 12:
G4253.strongs: a primary preposition; "fore", i.e. in front of, prior (figuratively, superior) to

G3561.thayer: the new moon, first of the month.
G3561.abbottSmith: new moon : of the Jewish festival, Col 2:16 .†
G3561.strongs: feminine of a compound of G3501 (νέος) and G3376 (μήν) (as noun by implication, of G2250 (ἡμέρα)); the festival of new moon

G3755.mounce: whoever, whatever; whosoever, whatsoever, Mt. 5:39, 41; 13:12; 18:4; its use in place of the simple relative is also required in various cases, which may be learned from the grammars; ἕως ὅτου, i.e. χρόνου, until, Lk. 13:8; while, Mt. 5:25
G3755.strongs: for the genitive case of G3748 (ὅστις) (as adverb); during which same time, i.e. whilst

G398.thayer: (a) a nautical term: I sight (a place), (b) mid: I appear (as it were, out of the unseen), (c) I bring to light, make to appear.
G398.mounce: to bring to light, display; mid. and pass. to appear, Lk. 19:11; a nautical term, to come in sight of, Acts 21:3*
G398.abbottSmith: to bring to light , make to appear : ἀναφάναντες τ. Κύπρον , i.e. having sighted C.: Ac 21:3 WH; pass., to appear , be made manifest : Lk 19:11 .
G398.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5316 (φαίνω); to show, i.e. (reflexively) appear, or (passively) to have pointed out

G915.thayer: a foreigner, one who speaks neither Greek nor Latin; as adj: foreign.
G915.mounce: pr. one to whom a pure Greek dialect is not native; one who is not a proper Greek, a barbarian, Rom. 1:14; Col. 3:11; Acts 28:2, 4; a foreigner speaking a strange language, 1 Cor. 14:11*
G915.abbottSmith: barbarous , barbarian , strange to Greek language and culture (and also, after the Persian war, with the added sense of brutal , rude ): Ac 28:2 , 4 , Ro 1:14 , I Co 14:11 , Col 3:11 (v. Lft., in l., and Notes , 249).†
G915.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a foreigner (i.e. non-Greek)

G2019.thayer: I call out, shout, clamor at.
G2019.mounce: to cry aloud, raise a shout at a speaker, whether applaudingly, Acts 12:22; or the contrary, to clamor at, Lk. 23:21; Acts 21:34; 22:24*
G2019.abbottSmith: to call out, shout : c. acc rei, Ac 21:34 ; c. dat. pers., ib. 22:24 ; seq. λέγοντες , Lk 23:21 ; orat. rect., Ac 12:22 .†
G2019.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5455 (φωνέω); to call at something, i.e. exclaim

G5362.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G435 (ἀνήρ); fond of man, i.e. affectionate as a wife

G1455.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G2524 (καθίημι); subinduced, i.e. surreptitiously suborned as a lier-in-wait

G1420.strongs: from G1418 (δυσ-) and a comparative of G1787 (ἐντός) (meaning a bowel); a "dysentery"

G5215.thayer: a hymn, sacred song, song of praise to God.
G5215.mounce: a song; a hymn, song of praise to God, Eph. 5:19; Col. 3:16*
G5215.abbottSmith: a hymn ; in cl. a festal song in praise of gods or heroes; in LXX and NT a song of praise addressed to God: Eph 5:19 , Col 3:16 .†
G5215.strongs: apparently from a simpler (obsolete) form of (to celebrate; probably akin to G103 (ᾄδω); compare H05667); a "hymn" or religious ode (one of the Psalms)

G5190.thayer: Tychicus, a Christian of the Roman province Asia.
G5190.mounce: Tychicus, pr. name, a friend or companion of Paul, Acts 20:4; Eph. 6:21; Col. 4:7; 2 Tim. 4:12; Tit. 3:12; Eph. subscr.; Col. subscr.*
G5190.abbottSmith: Tychicus : Ac 20:4 , Eph 6:21 , Col 4:7 , II Ti 4:12 , Tit 3:12 .†
G5190.strongs: from a derivative of G5177 (τυγχάνω); fortuitous, i.e. fortunate; Tychicus, a Christian

G4100.thayer: I believe, have faith in, trust in; pass: I am entrusted with.
G4100.mounce: pluperfect, πεπιστεύκειν, to believe, give credit to, Mk. 1:15; 16:13; Lk. 24:25; intrans. to believe, have a mental persuasion, Mt. 8:13; 9:28; Jas. 2:19; to believe, be of opinion, Rom. 14:2; in NT πιστεύειν ἐν, εἰς, ἐπί, to believe in or on, Mt. 18:6; 27:42; Jn. 3:15, 16, 18; absol. to believe, be a believer, Acts 2:44; 4:4, 32; 13:48; trans. to intrust, commit to the charge or power of, Lk. 16:11; Jn. 2:24; pass. to be intrusted with, Rom. 3:2; 1 Cor. 9:17
G4100.abbottSmith: intrans., to have faith (in), to believe ; in cl., c. acc., dat., in NT also c. prep. (on the significance of the various constructions, v. M, Pr. , 67 f.; Vau. on Ro 4:5 ; Ellic. on I Ti 1:16 ; Abbott, JV , 19-80): absol., Mt 24:23 , 26 , Mk 13:21 , I Co 11:18 ; c. acc. rei, Ac 13:41 , I Co 13:7 ; c. dat. pers. (to believe what one says), Mk 16:[13 , 14] , I Jo 4:1 ; τ. ψεύδει , II Th 2:11 ; περί
G4100.strongs: from G4102 (πίστις); to have faith (in, upon, or with respect to, a person or thing), i.e. credit; by implication, to entrust (especially one's spiritual well-being to Christ)
G4100.kjv: believe(-r), commit (to trust), put in trust with

G5054.mounce: a finishing, end; hence, end of life, death, decease, Mt. 2:15*

G1076.mounce: genealogy, catalogue of ancestors, history of descent, 1 Tim. 1:4; Tit. 3:9*
G1076.abbottSmith: the making of a pedigree . a genealogy : pl., I Ti 1:4 , Tit 3:9 (v. CGT , in l., and ib. Intr. , xlix ff.; DB , ii, p. 141).†
G1076.strongs: from the same as G1075 (γενεαλογέω); tracing by generations, i.e. "genealogy"

G5176.thayer: I eat, partake of a meal.
G5176.mounce: pr. to crunch; to eat, Mt. 24:38; from the Hebrew, ἄρτον τρώγειν, to take food, partake of a meal, Jn. 6:54, 56-58; 13:18*
G5176.abbottSmith: prop., of animals, to gnaw, munch, crunch (Hom., al.).
G5176.strongs: probably strengthened from a collateral form of the base of G5134 (τραῦμα) and G5147 (τρίβος) through the idea of corrosion or wear; or perhaps rather of a base of G5167 (τρυγών) and G5149 (τρίζω) through the idea of a craunching sound; to gnaw or chew, i.e. (generally) to eat

G5237.thayer: I overlook, take no notice of, disregard.
G5237.abbottSmith: to overlook : c. acc rei, Ac 17:30
G5237.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1492 (εἴδω); to overlook, i.e. not punish

G3092.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin; Maath, an Israelite

G3655.mounce: also spelled ἱμείρομαι, to desire earnestly, have a strong affection for, 1 Thess. 2:8*
G3655.abbottSmith: a storm of rain , a shower : Lk 12:54 .†
G3655.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a thunder storm

G4040.mounce: one who dwells in the vicinity, a neighbor, Lk. 1:58*
G4040.abbottSmith: dwelling around ; as subst., ὁ π. , a neighbour : Lk 1:58 (cf. πλησίον ).†
G4040.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3624 (οἶκος); housed around, i.e. neighboring (used elliptically as a noun)

G2441.mounce: garment; raiment, apparel, clothing, Lk. 7:25; 9:29; Jn. 19:24; Acts 20:33; 1 Tim. 2:9*
G2441.abbottSmith: clothing , apparel (usually of sumptuous attire; v. Tr., Syn. , § 1): Lk 7:25 9:29 , Jo 19:24 ( Ps 21:18 (22:19) , LXX for לְבוּשׁ ), Ac 20:33 , I Ti 2:9 .†

G1565.mounce: demonstrative adjective or noun, that, this, he, etc., Mt. 17:27; 10:14; 2 Tim. 4:8; in contrast with οὗτος, referring to the former of two things previously mentioned, Lk. 18:14
G1565.abbottSmith: that person or thing ( ille ), implying remoteness as compared with οὗτος ( hic ); absol., emphatic he, she, it : opp. to οὗτος , Lk 18:14 , Ja 4:15 ; ἡμεῖς , He 12:25 ; ὑμεῖς , Mt 13:11 , Mk 4:11 ; ἄλλοι , Jo 9:9 ; ἐγώ , Jo 3:30 ; to persons named, Mk 16:[ 10 , 13 , 20 ], Jo 2:21 ; of one (absent) who is not named, contemptuously (Abbott, JG , §§2385, 2732), Jo 7:11 9:28 ; with respect, of Christ
G1565.strongs: from G1563 (ἐκεῖ); that one (or (neuter) thing); often intensified by the article prefixed
G1565.kjv: he, it, the other (same), selfsame, that (same, very), X their, X them, they, this, those

G3467.thayer: I am short-sighted, blink, see dimly.
G3467.mounce: pr. to close the eyes, contract the eyelids, wink; to be nearsighted, partially blinded, slow to understand, 2 Pet. 1:9*
G3467.abbottSmith: to be short-sighted : II Pe 1:9 (R, mg., closing his eyes ; v. ICC , in l.).†
G3467.strongs: from a compound of the base of G3466 (μυστήριον) and (the face; from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)); to shut the eyes, i.e. blink (see indistinctly)

G3186.strongs: continued comparative of G3187 (μείζων); still larger (figuratively)

G2246.mounce: the sun, Mt. 13:43; 17:2; Mk. 1:32; meton. light of the sun, light, Acts 13:11
G2246.strongs: from (a ray; perhaps akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι)); the sun; by implication, light

G2325.mounce: to gather in harvest, reap, Mt. 6:26; 25:24, 26; met. to reap the reward of labor, 1 Cor. 9:11; 2 Cor. 9:6; to reap the harvest of vengeance, Rev. 14:15, 16
G2325.abbottSmith: to reap : Mt 6:26 , Lk 12:24 , Ja 5:4 ; fig., Mt 25:24 , 26 , Lk 19:21 , 22 , Jo 4:36-38 , II Co 9:6 , Ga 6:7 , 9 , Re 14:15 ; c. acc., τ. σαρκικά , I Co 9:11 ; φθοράν, ζωὴν αἰώνιον , Ga 6:8 ; τ. γῆν , Re 14:16 .†
G2325.strongs: from G2330 (θέρος) (in the sense of the crop); to harvest

G1487.mounce: if, Mt. 4:3, 6; 12:7; Acts 27:39, freq.; since, Acts 4:9; whether, Mk. 9:23; Acts 17:11; that, in certain expressions, Acts 26:8, 23; Heb. 7:15; by a suppression of the apodosis of a sentence, εἰ serves to express a wish; O if! O that! Lk. 19:42; 22:42; also a strong negation, Mk. 8:12; Heb. 3:11; 4:3; εἰ καί, if even, though, although, Lk. 18:4; εἰ μή, unless, except, Mt. 11:27; also equivalent to ἀλλά, but, Mt. 12:4; Mk. 13:32; Lk. 4:26, 27; εἰ μήτι, unless perhaps, unless it be, Lk. 9:13; εἴ τις, εἴ τι, pr. if any one; whosoever, whatsoever, Mt. 18:28. The syntax of this particle must be learned from the grammars. As an interrogative particle, whether, Acts 17:11; in NT as a mere note of interrogation, Lk. 22:49
G1487.strongs: a primary particle of conditionality; if, whether, that, etc.

G1758.thayer: (a) I have a grudge against, am angry (with), (b) pass. or mid: I am entangled, entangle myself.
G1758.mounce: to hold within; to fix upon; in NT intrans. (i.e. χόλον) to entertain a grudge against, Mk. 6:19; to be exasperated against, Lk. 11:53; pass. to be entangled, held fast in, Gal. 5:1*
G1758.abbottSmith: to hold in ; pass., to be held, entangled : c. dat. rei; fig., ζυγῷ δουλείας , Ga 5:1 (cf. MM, Exp. , xii); θλίψρεσιν , II Th 1:4 (cf. ἀσεβείσιν , III Mac 6:10 ).
G1758.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold in or upon, i.e. ensnare; by implication, to keep a grudge
G1758.kjv: entangle with, have a quarrel against, urge

G1903.thayer: a coat, outer wrap or tunic.
G1903.mounce: the outer or upper tunic, worn between the inner tunic and the external garments, Jn. 21:7*
G1903.strongs: from G1902 (ἐπενδύομαι); a wrapper, i.e. outer garment

G749.abbottSmith: high-priest : Mk 2:26 14:47 , al.; of Christ: He 2:17 3:1 , al. In pl., chief priests , including ex-high-priests and members of high-priestly families: Mt 2:4 , Mk 8:31 , al. (Cremer, 294; DCG , i, 297 f.; MM, s.v.).
G749.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G2409 (ἱερεύς); the high-priest (literally, of the Jews, typically, Christ); by extension a chief priest
G749.kjv: chief (high) priest, chief of the priests

G3571.mounce: night, Mt. 2:14; 28:13; Jn. 3:2; met. spiritual night, moral darkness, Rom. 13:12; 1 Thess. 5:5
G3571.abbottSmith: night : Mt 12:40 , Mk 6:48 , Jo 13:30 , al.; gen. temp. (of the time within which something happens; M, Pr. , 73; B1., § 36, 13), νυκτός , by night , Mt 2:14 , Jo 3:2 , I Th 5:7 , al.; ν. κ. ἡμέρας , Mk 5:5 , I Th 2:9 , al.; ἡμέρας κ. ν. , Lk 18:7 , Re 4:8 , al.; μέσης ν. , Mt 25:6 ; dat., νυκτί , in ans. to the question, "when?" (rare in cl.; Hdt., Soph.), ταύτῃ τ. ν. , Lk 12:20 , al.; ἐκείνῃ , A

G577.thayer: I throw away from, throw overboard, cast aside.
G577.mounce: to cast or throw off, cast aside, Mk. 10:50; Heb. 10:35*
G577.abbottSmith: to throw off : Mk 10:50 ; metaph., to lose , let go (Field, Notes , 231 f.; MM, s.v.): He 10:35 .†
G577.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw off; figuratively, to lose

G1802.thayer: Enoch, son of Jared and father of Methuselah.
G1802.mounce: Enoch, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:37; Heb. 11:5; 1 Pet. 3:19; Jude 14*
G1802.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02585); Enoch (i.e. Chanok), an antediluvian

G1611.thayer: (properly: distraction or disturbance of mind caused by shock), bewilderment, amazement; a trance.
G1611.mounce: pr. a displacement; hence, a displacement of the mind from its ordinary state and self-possession; amazement, astonishment, Mk. 5:42; excess of fear; fear, terror, Mk. 16:8; Lk. 5:26; Acts 3:10; in NT an ecstasy, a trance, Acts 10:10; 11:5; 22:17*
G1611.strongs: from G1839 (ἐξίστημι); a displacement of the mind, i.e. bewilderment, "ecstasy"
