G4394.thayer: prophecy, prophesying; the gift of communicating and enforcing revealed truth.
G4394.mounce: prophecy, a prediction of future events, Mt. 13:14; 2 Pet. 1:20, 21; prophecy, a gifted faculty of setting forth and enforcing revealed truth, 1 Cor. 12:10; 13:2; prophecy, matter of divine teaching set forth by special gift, 1 Tim. 1:18
G4394.abbottSmith: the gift (and its exercise) of interpreting the Divine will and purpose, prophecy , prophesying : of OT prophecy, Mt 13:14 , II Pe 1:20 , 21 ; of NT prophecy, Ro 12:6 , I Co 12:10 13:2 14:6 , 22 , I Ti 4:14 , Re 11:6 22:19 ; pl., I Co 13:8 , I Th 5:20 , I Ti 1:18 ; οἱ λόγοι τῆς π. , Re 1:3 22:7 , 10 , 18 ; τ. πνεῦμα τῆς π. , Re 19:10 (Luc., FlJ, LXX, π. ; v. Deiss., BS , 235 f.; MM, xxii).†

G2275.mounce: an inferiority, to a particular standard; default, defeat, failure, shortcoming, Rom. 11:12; 1 Cor. 6:7*
G2275.abbottSmith: defect , loss , defeat : Ro 11:12 (cf. Is 31:9 ( 8 ), I Co 6:7 (cf. Field, Notes , 160 f., 171 f.; Lft., Notes , 212).†
G2275.strongs: from G2274 (ἡττάω); a deterioration, i.e. (objectively) failure or (subjectively) loss

G304.thayer: a step, a flight of steps; the well-known "stairs" leading up from the temple to the tower of Antonia at Jerusalem.
G304.mounce: the act of ascending; means of ascent, steps, stairs, Acts 21:35, 40*
G304.abbottSmith: a going up , an ascent (Pss, tit. 119(120)-133(134) ?).

G355.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a form of the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι); properly, to use up, i.e. destroy

G3116.strongs: adverb of a compound of G3117 (μακρός) and G2372 (θυμός); with long (enduring) temper, i.e. leniently

G2002.thayer: I heap up, obtain a multitude of.
G2002.mounce: to heap up, accumulate largely; met. to procure in abundance, 2 Tim. 4:3*
G2002.abbottSmith: to heap together ; metaph., διδασκάλους , II Ti 4:3 .†
G2002.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4987 (σωρεύω); to accumulate further, i.e. (figuratively) seek additionally

G394.thayer: I lay (a case) before, impart, communicate, declare, relate (with a view to consulting).
G394.mounce: in NT only mid., to submit to a person’s consideration, statement, or report of matters, Acts 25:14; Gal. 2:2*
G394.abbottSmith: to lay upon , set up , etc. Mid. -εμαι , in late writers (Plut., al.; v. also MM, VGT , s.v.), to set forth , declare : Ac 25:14 , Ga 2:2 .†
G394.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the middle voice of G5087 (τίθημι); to set forth (for oneself), i.e propound

G3051.thayer: plur: oracles, divine responses or utterances (it can include the entire Old Testament).
G3051.mounce: an oracle, a divine communication or revelation, Acts 7:38; Rom. 3:2; Heb. 5:12; 1 Pet. 4:11*
G3051.abbottSmith: an oracle : Ac 7:38 , Ro 3:2 , He 5:12 , I Pe 4:11 (on the eccl., λόγια τ. Κυρίου , v. Lft., Essay on Sup. Rel. , 172 ff.).†
G3051.strongs: neuter of G3052 (λόγιος); an utterance (of God)

G949.mounce: firm, stable, steadfast, Heb. 3:14; 6:19; sure, certain, established, Rom. 4:16
G949.abbottSmith: firm , secure : ἄγκυρα , He 6:19 ; metaph., sure (esp. "in the sense of legally guaranteed security," Deiss., BS , 109; cf . two foll. words): ἐπαγγελία , Ro 4:16 ; ἐλπίς , II Co 1:6 ; λόγος , He 2:2 ; παρρησία , He 3:6 ; ἀρχή ( τ. ὑποστάσεως ), He 3:14 ; διαθήκη , He 9:17 ; κλῆσις κ. ἐκλογή , II Pe 1:10 ; comp. ( -ότερος ), προφητικὸς λόγος , II Pe 1:19 .†
G949.strongs: from the base of G939 (βάσις) (through the idea of basality); stable (literally or figuratively)

G4845.thayer: I fill completely; pass: I am completed.
G4845.mounce: to fill, fill up, Lk. 8:23; pass., of time, to be completed, have fully come, Lk. 9:51; Acts 2:1*
G4845.abbottSmith: to fill up completely (Thuc., of manning ships): of a ship filling with water, and by meton., of those on board, Lk 8:23 .
G4845.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4137 (πληρόω); to implenish completely, i.e. (of space) to swamp (a boat), or (of time) to accomplish (passive, be complete)

G823.thayer: (lit: that cannot be cut), an indivisible part of time, a moment.
G823.mounce: indivisible, and by impl. exceedingly minute; ἐν ἀτόμῳ, i.e. χρόνῳ, in an indivisible point of time, in an instant or moment, 1 Cor. 15:52*
G823.abbottSmith: indivisible ; of time, ἄτομον , a moment : ἐν ἄ. , I Co 15:52 .†
G823.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); uncut, i.e. (by implication) indivisible (an "atom" of time)

G4968.mounce: a victim slaughtered in sacrifice, offering, Acts 7:42*
G4968.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G4967 (σφαγή); a victim (in sacrifice)

G3922.thayer: I enter secretly, come in from the side.
G3922.mounce: to supervene, Rom. 5:20; to steal in, Gal. 2:4*
G3922.abbottSmith: to come in beside or by the way : Ro 5:20 .
G3922.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1525 (εἰσέρχομαι); to come in alongside, i.e. supervene additionally or steathily

G5541.thayer: I am kind (full of service to others), gentle.
G5541.mounce: to be gentle, benign, kind, 1 Cor. 13:4*
G5541.strongs: middle voice from G5543 (χρηστός); to show oneself useful, i.e. act benevolently

G5598.thayer: omega, the last letter of the Greek alphabet.
G5598.abbottSmith: omega , ô, the twenty-fourth and last letter. As a numeral, ωʹ = 800, ω͵ = 800,000. As a symbol of the last (= τὸ τέλος ), τὸ Ὦ (Rec. Ω , L, ὦ , T, ω ), the Omega : Re 1:8 21:6 22:13 (cf. Ἄλφα ).†
G5598.strongs: the last letter of the Greek alphabet, i.e. (figuratively) the finality

G305.thayer: I go up, mount, ascend; of things: I rise, spring up, come up.
G305.mounce: to go up, ascend, Mt. 5:1; to climb, Lk. 19:4; to go on board, Mk. 6:51; to rise, mount upwards, as smoke, Rev. 8:4; to grow or spring up, as plants, Mt. 13:7; to spring up, arise, as thoughts, Lk. 24:38
G305.abbottSmith: to go up , ascend , of persons: ἐπί συκομωρέαν , Lk 19:4 ; εἰς τ. πλοῖον , Mk 6:51 ; εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα , Mt 20:17 ; εἰς τ. ἱερόν , c. inf. (M, Pr. , 205), Lk 18:10 ; with mention of place of departure, Mt 3:16 ( ἀπό ), Ac 8:39 ( ἐκ ); of things, to rise , spring up , come up : a fish, Mt 17:27 ; smoke, Re 8:4 ; plants growing, Mt 13:7 ; metaph., of things coming up in one's mind (as Heb. עָלָה אֶל לֵ
G305.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to go up (literally or figuratively)
G305.kjv: arise, ascend (up), climb (go, grow, rise, spring) up, come (up)

G3473.strongs: from a compound of G3474 (μωρός) and G3004 (λέγω); silly talk, i.e. buffoonery

G4465.thayer: a holder of the rods, a lictor, Roman officer, judge.
G4465.mounce: the bearer of a wand of office; a lictor, sergeant, a public servant who bore a bundle or rods before the magistrates as insignia of their office, and carried into execution the sentences they pronounced, Acts 16:35, 38*
G4465.abbottSmith: one who carries a rod or staff of office;
G4465.strongs: from G4464 (ῥάβδος) and G2192 (ἔχω); a rod- (the Latin fasces) holder, i.e. a Roman lictor (constable or executioner)

G275.thayer: free from anxiety (though anxiety is rather too strong a word) or care.
G275.mounce: free from care or solicitude, Mt. 28:14; 1 Cor. 7:32*
G275.abbottSmith: free from anxiety or care : Mt 28:14 , I Co 7:32 (for exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G275.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3308 (μέριμνα); not anxious

G88.abbottSmith: unremitting , incessant : Ro 9:2 , II Ti 1:3 . (For exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.)†
G88.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of a compound of G1223 (διά) and G3007 (λείπω); unintermitted, i.e. permanent

G548.abbottSmith: to be absent : I Co 5:3 , II Co 10:1 , 11 13:2 , 10 , Phl 1:27 , Col 2:5 .†
G548.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1510 (εἰμί); to be away

G3105.thayer: I am raving mad, speak as a madman.
G3105.mounce: to be disordered in mind, mad, Jn. 10:20; Acts 12:15; 26:24, 25; 1 Cor. 14:23*
G3105.strongs: middle voice from a primary (to long for; through the idea of insensate craving); to rave as a "maniac"

G3062.thayer: left, left behind, the remainder, the rest, the others.
G3062.mounce: remaining; the rest, remainder, Mt. 22:6; as an adv., οῦ λοιποῦ, henceforth, Gal. 6:17; τὸ λοιπόν, or λοιπόν, henceforward, Mt. 26:45; 2 Tim. 4:8; Acts 27:20; as to the rest, besides, 1 Cor. 1:16; finally, Eph. 6:10; ὁ δὲ λοιπόν, but, now, furthermore, 1 Cor. 4:2
G3062.strongs: masculine plural of a derivative of G3007 (λείπω); remaining ones
G3062.kjv: other, which remain, remnant, residue, rest

G2473.thayer: like-minded, of the same mind or spirit.
G2473.mounce: likeminded, of the same mind and spirit, Phil. 2:20*
G2473.strongs: from G2470 (ἴσος) and G5590 (ψυχή); of similar spirit

G3524.thayer: sober, not intoxicated (with wine), temperate, vigilant.
G3524.mounce: somber, temperate, abstinent in respect to wine, etc.; in NT met., vigilant, circumspect, self-controlled, 1 Tim. 3:2, 11; Tit. 2:2*
G3524.abbottSmith: in cl., of drink, not mixed with wine .

G3086.abbottSmith: a redeemer , deliverer : Ac 7:35 (not found elsewhere).†

G3316.thayer: (a) a mediator, intermediary, (b) a go-between, arbiter, agent of something good.
G3316.mounce: one that acts between two parties; a mediator, one who interposes to reconcile two adverse parties, 1 Tim. 2:5; an arbitrator, one who is the medium of communication between two parties, a mid-party, Gal. 3:19, 20; Heb. 8:6; 9:15; 12:24*
G3316.abbottSmith: an arbitrator , mediator : Ga 3:19 ; c. dupl. gen. pers., θεοῦ κ. ἀνθρώπων , I Ti 2:5 ; c. gen. rei, διαθήκης , He 8:6 9:15 12:24 ; ὁ δε μ. ἑνὸς οὐκ ἔστιν , Ga 3:20 (v. Lft., in l.; and for exx. of this word in π. , v. MM, xvi).†
G3316.strongs: from G3319 (μέσος); a go-between, i.e. (simply) an internunciator, or (by implication) a reconciler (intercessor)

G2858.abbottSmith: a Colossian : pl., Col., tit . (and subscr. , Rec., Tr.).†
G2858.strongs: from G2857 (Κολοσσαί); a Colossæan, (i.e. inhabitant of Colossæ

G2638.thayer: (a) I seize tight hold of, arrest, catch, capture, appropriate, (b) I overtake, (c) mid. aor: I perceived, comprehended.
G2638.mounce: to lay hold of, grasp; to obtain, attain, Rom. 9:30; 1 Cor. 9:24; Phil. 3:12, 13; to seize, to take possession of, Mk. 9:18; to come suddenly upon; overtake, surprise, Jn. 12:35; 1 Thess. 5:4; to detect in the act, seize, Jn. 8:3, 4; met. to comprehend, apprehend, Jn. 1:5; mid. to understand, perceive, Acts 4:13; 10:34; 25:25; Eph. 3:18*
G2638.abbottSmith: to lay hold of , seize , appropriate : Mk 9:18 ; c. acc. rei, I Co 9:24 , Phl 3:12 , 13 .
G2638.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take eagerly, i.e. seize, possess, etc. (literally or figuratively)
G2638.kjv: apprehend, attain, come upon, comprehend, find, obtain, perceive, (over-)take

G1005.thayer: the north wind, hence: the North.
G1005.mounce: pr. the north or N.N.E. wind; meton. the north, Lk. 13:29; Rev. 21:13*
G1005.abbottSmith: Boreas, the North wind . the north : Lk 13:29 , Re 21:13 .†
G1005.strongs: of uncertain derivation; the north (properly, wind)

G1707.thayer: I enfold, entangle; pass: I am involved in.
G1707.mounce: pr. to intertwine; met. to implicate, entangle, involve; pass. to be implicated, involved, or to entangle one’s self in, 2 Tim. 2:4; 2 Pet. 2:20*
G1707.abbottSmith: to weave in, entwine ; pass., metaph., to be involved, entangled in : II Ti 2:4 , II Pe 2:20 .†
G1707.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4120 (πλέκω); to entwine, i.e. (figuratively) involve with

G4291.thayer: I preside, rule over, give attention to, direct, maintain, practice diligently.
G4291.mounce: to set before; met. to set over, appoint with authority; intrans. 2 aor. προὔστην, perf. προέστηκα, part. προεστώς, and mid. προίσταμαι, to preside, govern, superintend, Rom. 12:8; 1 Thess. 5:12; 1 Tim. 3:4, 5, 12; 5:17; mid. to undertake resolutely, to practise diligently, to maintain the practice of, Tit. 3:8, 14*
G4291.abbottSmith: trans. in fut., 1 aor., and mid. 1 aor., to put before , set over (Plat., al.).
G4291.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand before, i.e. (in rank) to preside, or (by implication) to practise

G3474.thayer: (a) adj: stupid, foolish, (b) noun: a fool.
G3474.mounce: pr. dull; foolish, Mt. 7:26; 23:17; 25:2f., 8; 1 Cor. 1:25, 27; 3:18; 4:10; 2 Tim. 2:23; Tit. 3:9; from the Hebrew, a fool in senseless wickedness, Mt. 5:22*
G3474.abbottSmith: prop., of the nerves, dull , sluggish (Hipp., Arist.).
G3474.strongs: probably from the base of G3466 (μυστήριον); dull or stupid (as if shut up), i.e. heedless, (morally) blockhead, (apparently) absurd

G5404.abbottSmith: the date-palm, palm : τὰ βαΐα τῶν φ. , Jo 12:13 ; of palm branches, φοίνικες (as Arist., II Mac 10:7 , al.), Re 7:9 .†

G1556.thayer: I give justice over, defend, avenge, vindicate.
G1556.mounce: pr. to execute right and justice; to punish, 2 Cor. 10:6; Rev. 6:10; 19:2; in NT to right, avenge a person, Lk. 18:3, 5; Rom. 12:9*
G1556.abbottSmith: to vindicate : c. acc pers., Lk 18:3 , 5 .
G1556.strongs: from G1558 (ἔκδικος); to vindicate, retaliate, punish

G517.abbottSmith: unseen , invisible : Ro 1:20 , Col 1:15 , 16 , I Ti 1:17 , He 11:27 .†
G517.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3707 (ὁρατός); invisible

G3509.thayer: a cloud; met: a dense crowd, a multitude, great company.
G3509.mounce: a cloud; trop. a cloud, a throng of persons, Heb. 12:1*
G3509.abbottSmith: a mass of clouds , a cloud (cf. νεφέλη ); metaph. (as in Hom., Hdt., al.), of a dense throng: He 12:1 .†
G3509.strongs: apparently a primary word; a cloud

G4398.mounce: a prophetess, a divinely gifted female teacher, Lk. 2:36; Rev. 2:20*
G4398.strongs: feminine of G4396 (προφήτης); a female foreteller or an inspired woman

G3305.abbottSmith: yet , however : Jo 4:27 , al.; εἰ μ. , Ja 2:8 ( if indeed ).
G3305.strongs: from G3203 and G5104 (τοί); indeed though, i.e. however

G1876.mounce: of necessity, necessarily; τὰ ἐπάναγκες, necessary things, Acts 15:28*
G1876.abbottSmith: ἐπάναγκες (sc. ἐστί ), it is compulsory, necessary .
G1876.strongs: neuter of a presumed compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G318 (ἀνάγκη); (adverbially) on necessity, i.e. necessarily

G5310.mounce: highest, loftiest, most elevated; τὰ ὕψιστα, from the Hebrew, the highest heaven, Mt. 21:9; Mk. 11:10; met. ὁ ὕψιστος, the Most High, Mk. 5:7
G5310.abbottSmith: highest, most high : of place, τὰ ὕ. (of the heavens), Mt 21:9 , Mk 11:10 , Lk 2:14 19:38 (cf. Jos 16:19 , Is 57:15 ); of God (in cl., of Zeus; Pind., Æsch., al.), ὕ. , Lk 1:32 , 35 , 76 6:35 (as freq. in Si 4:10 , al.); ὁ ὕ. , Ac 7:48 ; ὁ θεὸς ὁ ὕ. , Mk 5:7 , Lk 8:28 , Ac 16:17 , He 7:1 (cf. Ge 14:18 ).†
G5310.strongs: superlative from the base of G5311 (ὕψος); highest, i.e. (masculine singular) the Supreme (God), or (neuter plural) the heavens

G2706.thayer: I despise, scorn, and show it by active insult, disregard.
G2706.mounce: pr. to look down on; to scorn, despise, Mt. 18:10; Rom. 2:4; to slight, Mt. 6:24; Lk. 16:13; 1 Cor. 11:22; 1 Tim. 4:12; 6:2; 2 Pet. 2:10; to disregard, Heb. 12:2*
G2706.abbottSmith: to think little of , despise : c. gen., Mt 6:24 18:10 , Lk 16:13 , Ro 2:4 , I Co 11:22 , I Ti 4:12 6:2 , He 12:2 , II Pe 2:10 .†
G2706.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5426 (φρονέω); to think against, i.e. disesteem

G4764.abbottSmith: insolent luxury, wantonness : Re 18:3 (a late word, first found in a Comic poet, B.C. 300, v. Kennedy, Sources , 41, cf. στρηνιάω ).†
G4764.strongs: akin to G4731 (στερεός); a "straining", "strenuousness" or "strength", i.e. (figuratively) luxury (voluptuousness)

G189.thayer: hearing, faculty of hearing, ear; report, rumor.
G189.mounce: hearing; the act or sense of hearing, 1 Cor. 12:17; 2 Pet. 2:8; the instrument of hearing, the ear, Mk. 7:35; a thing heard; announcement, instruction, doctrine, Jn. 12:38; Rom. 10:16; report, Mt. 4:24, et al
G189.abbottSmith: hearing , the sense of hearing : I Co 12:17 , II Pe 2:8 ; "Hebraic dative," ἀκοῇ ακούειν (freq. in LXX; V. M, Pr. , 14, 75), Mt 13:14 , Ac 28:26 .
G189.strongs: from G191 (ἀκούω); hearing (the act, the sense or the thing heard)
G189.kjv: audience, ear, fame, which ye heard, hearing, preached, report, rumor

G2438.thayer: a thong, strap, (a) for binding a man who is to be flogged, (b) for fastening a sandal or shoe.
G2438.mounce: a strap or thong of leather, Acts 22:25; a shoe-latchet, Mk. 1:7; Lk. 3:16; Jn. 1:27*
G2438.abbottSmith: a thong , strap : for binding prisoners ( IV Mac 9:11 , Si 30:35 ( 33:26 )), Ac 22:25 ; for fastening sandals, Mk 1:7 , Lk 3:16 , Jo 1:27 .†
G2438.strongs: perhaps from the same as G260 (ἅμα); a strap, i.e. (specially) the tie (of a sandal) or the lash (of a scourge)

G1237.thayer: I receive in my turn, receive through another.
G1237.mounce: to receive by transmission; to receive in return, Acts 7:45*
G1237.abbottSmith: to receive through another, receive in turn : Ac 7:45 (RV, in their turn ; v. Field, Notes , 116).†
G1237.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to receive in turn, i.e. (figuratively) succeed to

G2594.thayer: I persevere, endure, am steadfast, patient.
G2594.mounce: to be stout; to endure patiently, persevere, bear up with fortitude, Heb. 11:27*
G2594.abbottSmith: to be steadfast , patient : He 11:27 (cf. προσ-καρτερέω ).†
G2594.strongs: from a derivative of G2904 (κράτος) (transposed); to be strong, i.e. (figuratively) steadfast (patient)

G4234.thayer: (a) a doing, action, mode of action; plur: deeds, acts, (b) function, business.
G4234.mounce: operation, business, office, Rom. 12:4; πρᾶξις, and πράξεις, actions, mode of acting, ways, deeds, practice, behavior, Mt. 16:27; Lk. 23:51
G4234.abbottSmith: a doing , deed , act: Mt 16:27 ; pl., π. ἀποσπτόλων , Ac, tit. ; in late writers especially of wicked deeds or practices (freq. in Polyb.): Lk 23:51 ; p1., Ro 8:13 , Col 3:9 ; with ref. to magic (v. BS , 323 5 ), Ac 19:18 .
G4234.strongs: from G4238 (πράσσω); practice, i.e. (concretely) an act; by extension, a function

G4688.mounce: a sentinel, life-guardsman, a kind of soldiers who formed the body-guard of princes, etc., one of whose duties was to put criminals to death, Mk. 6:27*
G4688.strongs: of Latin origin; a speculator, i.e. military scout (spy or (by extension) life-guardsman)

G2506.abbottSmith: a pulling down , destruction : fig., κ. ὀχυρωμάτων , II Co 10:4 ; opp. to οἰκοδομή , II Co 10:8 13:10 .†

G4776.thayer: I cause to sit down with, sit down together.
G4776.mounce: trans. to cause to sit down with, seat in company with, Eph. 2:6; intrans. to sit in company with; to sit down together, Lk. 22:55*
G4776.abbottSmith: to invest , besiege (Diod., IV Ki 6:24 , I Mac 11:61 , al.).
G4776.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2523 (καθίζω); to give (or take) a seat in company with

G3748.mounce: whoever, whatever; whosoever, whatsoever, Mt. 5:39, 41; 13:12; 18:4; its use in place of the simple relative is also required in various cases, which may be learned from the grammars; ἕως ὅτου, i.e. χρόνου, until, Lk. 13:8; while, Mt. 5:25
G3748.abbottSmith: whoever , anyone who ; of an indef. person or thing: in general statements, Mt 5:39 , 41 13:12 , and freq., Lk 14:27 , Ga 5:4 , al.; in relative sentences, Mt 7:26 , Lk 15:7 , Phl 3:7 , al.; πᾶς ὅ. , c. indic., Mt 7:24 10:32 ; ὅ ἄν ( ἐάν ), c. subjc., Mt 12:50 13:12 , Jo 14:13 , I Co 16:2 , Ga 5:10 , al.; of a definite person or thing, indicating quality, "either generic, which , as other like thi
G3748.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G5100 (τὶς); which some, i.e. any that; also (definite) which same
G3748.kjv: X and (they), (such) as, (they) that, in that they, what(-soever), whereas ye, (they) which, who(-soever)

G90.strongs: from a derivative of a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G1311 (διαφθείρω); incorruptibleness, i.e. (figuratively) purity (of doctrine)

G2593.mounce: fruitful, adapted to bring forth fruit, Acts 14:17*

G322.thayer: I show forth, show clearly; hence: I proclaim (a person's appointment to an office), appoint.
G322.mounce: pr. to show anything by raising it aloft, as a torch; to display, manifest, show plainly or openly, Acts 1:24; to mark out, constitute, appoint by some outward sign, Lk. 10:1*
G322.abbottSmith: to lift up and show , show forth , declare (cf. II Mac 2:8 , v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 1:24 .
G322.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to exhibit, i.e. (by implication) to indicate, appoint

G1514.thayer: I am peaceful, keep the peace, am at peace.
G1514.mounce: to be at peace; to cultivate peace, concord, or harmony, Mt. 9:50; Rom. 12:18; 2 Cor. 13:11; 1 Thess. 5:13*
G1514.abbottSmith: to bring to peace, reconcile (so I Mac 6:60 ). to keep peace, be at peace : Mk 9:50 , Ro 12:18 , II Co 13:11 , I Th 5:13 (cf. Si 28:9 ; Cremer, 246).†
G1514.kjv: be at (have, live in) peace, live peaceably

G4534.thayer: Salmone, a promontory on the east of Crete.
G4534.mounce: Salmone, a promontory, the eastern extremity of Crete, Acts 27:7*
G4534.abbottSmith: Salmone , a promontory of Crete: Ac 27:7 .†
G4534.strongs: perhaps of similar origin to G4529 (Σαλαμίς); Salmone, a place in Crete

G1334.thayer: I relate in full, describe, narrate.
G1334.mounce: pr. to lead throughout; to declare thoroughly, detail, recount, relate, tell, Mk. 5:16; 9:9; Lk. 8:39; Acts 8:33; Heb. 11:32
G1334.abbottSmith: to set out in detail, recount, describe : absol., He 11:32 ; c. acc rei, Ac 8:33 ; c. dat. pers., seq. πῶς , Mk 5:16 , Ac 9:27 12:17 ; ἅ εἶδον , Mk 9:9 ; ὅσα ἐποίησε, -αν , Lk 8:39 9:10 .†
G1334.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); to relate fully

G236.mounce: to change, alter, transform, Acts 6:14; Rom. 1:23; 1 Cor. 15:51, 52; Gal. 4:20; Heb. 1:12

G272.thayer: I neglect, am careless of, disregard.
G272.mounce: not to care for, to neglect, disregard, Mt. 22:5; 1 Tim. 4:14; Heb. 2:3; 8:9*
G272.abbottSmith: absol., to be careless , not to care : Mt 22:5 ;
G272.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3199 (μέλω); to be careless of
G272.kjv: make light of, neglect, be negligent, no regard

G4104.thayer: I convince, establish, give assurance to; pass: I am assured of.
G4104.mounce: to make trustworthy; pass. to be assured, feel sure belief, 2 Tim. 3:14*
G4104.abbottSmith: to make trustworthy (Thuc., III Ki 1:36 ), hence, to establish ( I Ch 17:14 ). Pass. and mid., to be assured of : c. acc. rei, II Ti 3:14 .†

G1031.thayer: I grind or gnash, as with the teeth for rage or pain.
G1031.mounce: to grate or gnash the teeth, Acts 7:54*
G1031.abbottSmith: to bite or eat greedily . to gnash, grind , with the teeth: Ac 7:54 .†
G1031.strongs: a primary verb; to grate the teeth (in pain or rage)

G4662.mounce: eaten of worms, consumed by worms, Acts 12:23*
G4662.abbottSmith: eaten of worms : Ac 12:23 (cf. II Mac 9:9 , and v. MM, xxiii) †
G4662.strongs: from G4663 (σκώληξ) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω); worm-eaten, i.e. diseased with maggots

G4492.thayer: I cause to take root; met: I plant, fix firmly, establish.
G4492.mounce: to root, cause to take root; firmly rooted, strengthened with roots; met. firm, constant, firmly fixed, Eph. 3:17; Col. 2:7*
G4492.abbottSmith: to cause to take root . Metaph., to plant, fix firmly, establish : pass. (EV, rooted ), ἐν ἀγάπῃ , Eph 3:18 (17) ; ἐν Χριστῷ , Col 2:7 (cf. ἐκ ριζόω ).†
G4492.strongs: from G4491 (ῥίζα); to root (figuratively, become stable)

G5269.thayer: I under-gird, as to strengthen a ship against the waves.
G5269.mounce: to gird under, of persons; to undergird a ship with cables, chains, etc., Acts 27:17*
G5269.abbottSmith: to undergird (Hdt., al.; ὑπὸ τ. μάστους , II Mac, l.c. ); of a ship, to undergird or frap : Ac 27:17 (v. DB , ext. 367 a ).†
G5269.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2224 (ζώννυμι); to gird under, i.e. frap (a vessel with cables across the keel, sides and deck)

G5083.thayer: I keep, guard, observe, watch over.
G5083.mounce: to keep watch upon, guard, Mt. 27:36, 54; 28:4; Acts 12:6; to watch over protectively, guard, 1 Jn. 5:18; Rev. 16:15; to mark attentively, to heed, Rev. 1:3; to observe practically, keep strictly, Mt. 19:17; 23:3; 28:20; Mk. 7:9; Jn. 8:51; to preserve, shield, Jn. 17:15; to store up, reserve, Jn. 2:10; 12:7; 1 Pet. 1:4; 2 Pet. 2:4, 9, 17; to keep in custody, Acts 12:5; 16:23; to maintain, Eph. 4:3; 2 Tim. 4:7; to keep in a condition, Jn. 17:11, 12; 1 Cor. 7:37; 2 Cor. 11:9; 1 Tim. 5:22; Jas. 1:27
G5083.abbottSmith: to watch over, guard, keep, preserve : Mt 27:36 , Ac 16:23 , al.; ἑαυτόν , II Co 11:9 , I Ti 5:22 , Ja 1:27 , Ju 21 ; seq. εἰς , Jo 12:7 , Ac 25:21 , I Pe 1:4 , II Pe 2:4 , 9 3:7 , Ju 6 ; ἐν , Jo 17:11 , 12 , Ju 21 ; ἐκ , Jo 17:15 , Re 3:10 ; τ. πίστιν , II Ti 4:7 , Re 14:12 ; τ. ἑνότητα τ. πνεύματος , Eph 4:3 .
G5083.strongs: from (a watch; perhaps akin to G2334 (θεωρέω)); to guard (from loss or injury, properly, by keeping the eye upon; and thus differing from G5442 (φυλάσσω), which is properly to prevent escaping; and from G2892 (κουστωδία), which implies a fortress or full military lines of apparatus), i.e. to note (a prophecy; figuratively, to fulfil a command); by implication, to detain (in custody; figuratively, to maintain); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried)
G5083.kjv: hold fast, keep(- er), (pre-, re-)serve, watch

G2337.mounce: to suckle, give suck, Mt. 24:19; Mk. 13:17; Lk. 21:23; to suck, Mt. 21:16; Lk. 11:27*
G2337.abbottSmith: of the mother, to suckle : Mt 24:19 , Mk 13:17 , Lk 21:23 .
G2337.strongs: from (the nipple); to suckle, (by implication) to suck

G572.mounce: simplicity, sincerity, purity of mind, Rom. 12:8; 11:3; Eph. 6:5; Col. 3:22; liberality, as arising from simplicity and frankness of character, 2 Cor. 8:2; 9:11, 13; 11:3*
G572.abbottSmith: simplicity , sincerity : Ro 12:8 , II Co 11:3 , Eph 6:5 , Col 3:22 ; as manifested in generous, unselfish giving, liberality , graciousness : II Co 8:2 9:11 , 13 (v. ICC , Ro. , 12:8 ; Hort, Ja. , 1:5 , and v.s. ἁπλῶς ).†
G572.strongs: from G573 (ἁπλοῦς); singleness, i.e. (subjectively) sincerity (without dissimulation or self-seeking), or (objectively) generosity (copious bestowal)

G515.thayer: I account or treat as worthy.
G515.mounce: to judge or esteem worthy or deserving; to deem fitting, to require, Acts 15:38; 28:22
G515.abbottSmith: to deem worthy : c. acc. et inf. (v. MM, VGT , s.v., and cf. κατ-αξιόω ), Lk 7:7 ; id. et gen. rei, II Th 1:11 ; pass. c. gen. rei, I Ti 5:17 , He 3:3 10:29 ;
G515.strongs: from G514 (ἄξιος); to deem entitled or fit

G5476.mounce: on the ground, to the earth, Jn. 9:6; 18:6*
G5476.abbottSmith: on or to the ground : Jo 9:6 18:6 .†
G5476.strongs: adverb perhaps from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of a fissure in the soil; earthward, i.e. prostrate

G3383.mounce: neither; μήτε ... μήτε, or μὴ ... μήτε, or μηδὲ ... μητέ, neither ... nor, Mt. 5:34, 35, 36; Acts 23:8; 2 Thess. 2:2; in NT also equivalent to μηδέ, not even, not so much as, Mk. 3:20
G3383.abbottSmith: neither , nor : μήτε . . . μήτε , neither . . . nor , Mt 11:18 , Lk 7:33 9:3 , Ac 23:12 , 21 27:20 , He 7:3 ; μὴ ( μηδὲ ) . . . μήτε . . . μήτε , Mt 5:34-36 , Mk 3:20 T, Ac 23:8 , II Th 2:2 , I Ti 1:7 , Ja 5:12 , Re 7:1 , 3 .†
G3383.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G5037 (τέ); not too, i.e. (in continued negation) neither or nor; also, not even

G2122.abbottSmith: seasonably, in season : Mk 14:11 ; opp. to ἀκ- (Kühner 3 , iv, 346 d), II Ti 4:2 .†

G4327.thayer: (a) I await, expect, (b) I receive, welcome (originally: to my house), (c) I accept.
G4327.mounce: to receive, accept; to receive, admit, grant access to, Lk. 15:2; to receive, admit, accept, and with οὐ, to reject, Heb. 11:35; to submit to, Heb. 10:34; to receive kindly, as a guest, entertain, Rom. 16:2; to receive, admit, as a hope, Acts 24:15; to look or wait for, expect, await, Mk. 15:43; Lk. 2:25
G4327.abbottSmith: to receive to oneself , receive favourably , admit , accept : c. acc. pers., Lk 15:2 , Ro 16:2 , Phl 2:29 ; c. acc. rei, Ac 24:15 (R, mg.), He 10:34 11:35 .
G4327.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to admit (to intercourse, hospitality, credence, or (figuratively) endurance); by implication, to await (with confidence or patience)

G4888.mounce: in NT to glorify together with, to exalt to a state of dignity and happiness in company with, to make to partake in the glorification of another, Rom. 8:17*
G4888.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1392 (δοξάζω); to exalt to dignity in company (i.e. similarly) with

G4519.abbottSmith: Sabaoth , i.e. hosts or armies (v. DB , l.c.): Ro 9:29 (LXX) , Ja 5:4 .†
G4519.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06635 in feminine plural); armies; sabaoth (i.e. tsebaoth), a military epithet of God

G3990.mounce: to strike or cut with an axe; to behead, Rev. 20:4*
G3990.abbottSmith: to out off with an axe , esp. to behead : c. acc., Re 20:4 (Polyb., al.).†
G3990.strongs: from a derivative of G4141 (πλήσσω) (meaning an axe); to chop off (the head), i.e. truncate

G4346.mounce: pr. a leaning upon or towards a thing; met. a leaning towards any one, inclination of mind towards, partiality, 1 Tim. 5:21*
G4346.strongs: from a compound of G4314 (πρός) and G2827 (κλίνω); a leaning towards, i.e. (figuratively) proclivity (favoritism)

G3452.strongs: perhaps a primary word; the marrow

G3082.thayer: Lystra, a Lycaonian city in the southern part of the Roman province Galatia.
G3082.mounce: Lystra, a city of Lycaonia, in Asia Minor, Acts 14:6, 8, 21; 16:1f.; 2 Tim. 3:11*. See PhotoGuide.
G3082.abbottSmith: Lystra , a city of Lycaonia: Ac 14:6 , 8 , 21 16:1 , 2 , II Ti 3:11 .†
G3082.strongs: of uncertain origin; Lystra, a place in Asia Minor

G2120.mounce: convenient opportunity, favorable occasion, Mt. 26:16; Lk. 22:6*
G2120.abbottSmith: fitting time, opportunity : seq. ἵνα , Mt 26:16 ; τοῦ , c. inf., Lk 22:6 .†

G4144.strongs: from G4126 (πλέω); a sail, i.e. navigation

G1097.thayer: I am taking in knowledge, come to know, learn; aor: I ascertained, realized.
G1097.mounce: to know, whether the action be inceptive or complete and settled; to perceive, Mt. 22:18; Mk. 5:29; 8:17; 12:12; Lk. 8:46; to mark, discern, Mt. 25:24; Lk. 19:44; to ascertain by examination, Mk. 6:38; Jn. 7:51; Acts 23:28; to understand, Mk. 4:13; Lk. 18:34; Jn. 12:16; 13:7; Acts 8:30; 1 Cor. 14:7, 9; to acknowledge, Mt. 7:23; 2 Cor. 3:2; to resolve, conclude, Lk. 16:4; Jn. 7:26; 17:8; to be assured, Lk. 21:20; Jn. 6:69; 8:52; 2 Pet. 1:20; to be skilled, to be master of a thing, Mt. 16:3; Acts 21:37; to know carnally, Mt. 1:25; Lk. 1:34; from the Hebrew, to view with favor, 1 Cor. 8:3; Gal. 4:9
G1097.abbottSmith: to be taking in knowledge, come to know, recognize, perceive, understand ; in past tenses to know, realize ; pass., to become known : c. acc., Mt 22:18 , Mk 5:43 , Col 4:8 , I Th 3:5 , al. Pass., Mt 10:26 , Phl 4:5 , al.; seq. ὅτι , Mt 21:45 , Jo 4:1 , al.; τί , Mt 6:3 ; ἀπό , Mk 15:45 ; ὅ , Ro 7:15 ; τ. λεγόμενα , Lk 18:34 ; τ. θέλημα , Lk 12:47 ; τ. καρδίας , Lk 16:15 ; c acc. pers., of recognit
G1097.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb; to "know" (absolutely) in a great variety of applications and with many implications (as follow, with others not thus clearly expressed)
G1097.kjv: allow, be aware (of), feel, (have) know(-ledge), perceived, be resolved, can speak, be sure, understand

G4026.thayer: in intrans. act. tenses: I stand around; mid: I avoid, shun.
G4026.mounce: to place around; intrans. 2 aor. περιέστην, perf. part. περιεστώς, to stand around, Jn. 11:42; Acts 25:7; mid. to keep aloof from, avoid, shun, 2 Tim. 2:16; Tit. 3:9*
G4026.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand all around, i.e. (near) to be a bystander, or (aloof) to keep away from

G1653.mounce: also formed as ἐλεάω   4x, to pity, have compassion on; pass. to receive pity, experience compassion, Mt. 5:7; 9:27; 15:22; to be gracious to any one, show gracious favor and saving mercy towards; pass. to be an object of gracious favor and saving mercy, Rom. 11:30, 31; spc. to obtain pardon and forgiveness, 1 Tim. 1:13, 16
G1653.abbottSmith: to have pity or mercy on, to show mercy : absol., Ro 9:16 12:8 ; c. acc, Mt 9:27 15:22 17:15 18:33 20:30 , 31 , Mk 5:19 10:47 , 48 , Lk 16:24 17:13 18:38 , 39 , Ro 9:15 , 18 11:32 , Phl 2:27 , Ju 22 . Pass., to have pity or mercy shown one (EV, obtain mercy): Mt 5:7 , Ro 11:30 , 31 , I Co 7:25 , II Co 4:1 , I Ti 1:13 , 16 , I Pe 2:10 .†
G1653.strongs: from G1656 (ἔλεος); to compassionate (by word or deed, specially, by divine grace)
G1653.kjv: have compassion (pity on), have (obtain, receive, shew) mercy (on)

G2124.mounce: the disposition of one who is εὐλαβής, caution, circumspection; in NT reverence to God, piety, Heb. 5:7; 12:28*
G2124.strongs: from G2126 (εὐλαβής); properly, caution, i.e. (religiously) reverence (piety); by implication, dread (concretely)

G2165.thayer: I cheer, make glad; generally mid. or pass: I am glad, make merry, revel, feast.
G2165.mounce: to gladden, 2 Cor. 2:2; pass. to be glad, exult, rejoice, Lk. 12:19; Acts 2:26; mid. to feast in token of joy, keep a day of rejoicing, Lk. 15:23, 24, 29, 32
G2165.abbottSmith: to cheer, gladden : c. acc pers., opp. to λυπεῖν , II Co 2:2 . Pass., to be happy, rejoice, make merry : Lk 15:32 , Ac 2:26 (LXX) Ro 15:10 (LXX) , Ga 4:27 (LXX) , Re 11:10 12:12 ; seq. ἐν , Ac 7:41 ; ἐπί c. dat., Re 18:20 ; of merry-making at a feast ( III Ki 4:20 ; cf. Kennedy, Sources , 155; Field, Notes , 69 f.), Lk 12:19 15:23 , 24 , 29 (LXX) ( λαμπρῶς ) Lk 16:19 .†
G2165.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G5424 (φρήν); to put (middle voice or passively, be) in a good frame of mind, i.e. rejoice
G2165.kjv: fare, make glad, be (make) merry, rejoice

G1846.thayer: (a) I dig out, hence: I open up, (b) I gouge.
G1846.mounce: to dig out or through, force up, Mk. 2:4; to pluck out the eyes, Gal. 4:15*
G1846.abbottSmith: to dig out, dig up : στέγην , Mk 2:4 ; metaph., ὀφθαλμούς (cf. LXX, ll c.; Herod., viii, 116), Ga 4:15 .†
G1846.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3736 (ὀρύσσω); to dig out, i.e. (by extension) to extract (an eye), remove (roofing)

G39.mounce: separate from common condition and use; dedicated. Lk. 2:23; hallowed; used of things, τὰ ἅγια, the sanctuary; and of persons, saints, e.g., members of the first Christian communities; pure, righteous, ceremonially or morally; holy
G39.abbottSmith: primarily, dedicated to the gods , sacred (Hdt.; rare in Att., never in Hom., Hes. and Trag., who use ἁγνός ), hence, holy , characteristic of God, separated to God, worthy of veneration. Its highest application is to God himself, in his purity, majesty, and glory: Lk 1:49 , Jo 17:11 , Re 4:8 . Hence of things and places which have a claim to reverence as sacred to God, e.g. the Temple: Mt 24:15 ,
G39.strongs: neuter of G40 (ἅγιος); a sacred thing (i.e. spot)

G3144.thayer: a witness; an eye- or ear-witness.
G3144.mounce: (1) a judicial witness, deponent, Mt. 18:16; Heb. 10:28; (2) generally, a witness to a circumstance, Lk. 24:48; Acts 10:41; in NT a witness, a testifier, of a doctrine, Rev. 1:5; 3:14; 11:3; (3) a martyr, Acts 22:20; Rev. 2:13
G3144.abbottSmith: a witness : Ac 10:41 , I Ti 6:12 , II Ti 2:2 , He 12:1 (Westc., in l.); in forensic sense, Mt 18:16 26:65 , Mk 14:63 , Ac 6:13 7:58 , II Co 13:1 , I Ti 5:19 , He 10:28 ; c. gen. obj., Lk 24:48 , Ac 1:22 2:32 3:15 5:32 10:39 26:16 , I Pe 5:1 ; c. gen. poss., Ac 1:8 13:31 , Re 11:3 ; c. dat. pers., Lk 11:48 , Ac 22:15 ; of Christ, Re 1:5 3:14 ; of God, Ro 1:9 , II Co 1:23 , Phl 1:8 , I Th 2:5 , 10 ;
G3144.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

G1303.thayer: (a) I appoint, make (of a covenant), (b) I make (a will).
G1303.mounce: in NT only mid., so some list as διατίθεμαι, to arrange; to arrange according to one’s own mind; to make a disposition, to make a will; to settle the terms of a covenant, to ratify, Acts 3:25; Heb. 8:10; 10:16; to assign, Lk. 22:29
G1303.abbottSmith: to place separately, arrange, dispose . Mid, only in NT;
G1303.strongs: middle voice from G1223 (διά) and G5087 (τίθημι); to put apart, i.e. (figuratively) dispose (by assignment, compact, or bequest)

G3194.mounce: also spelled Μελιτήνη, Malta, an island in the Mediterranean, Acts 28:1*
G3194.strongs: of uncertain origin; Melita, an island in the Mediterranean

G4924.thayer: I dwell with, live in wedlock with.
G4924.mounce: to dwell with; to live or cohabit with, 1 Pet. 3:7*
G4924.abbottSmith: to dwell together : of man and wife (Hdt., al.), c. dat. pers., I Pe 3:7 ,†
G4924.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to reside together (as a family)

G3650.abbottSmith: of persons and things, whole , entire , complete ; of indefinite ideas, c. subst. anarth.: Lk 5:5 , Ac 11:26 28:30 , Tit 1:11 ; ὅλον ἄνθρωπον ( an entire man ; v. Field, Notes , 93), Jo 7:23 ; ὅλη Ἰερουσαλήμ (= πᾶσα Ἰ , Mt 2:3 ; v. Bl., § 47, 9), Ac 21:31 . Definite, c. art.; preceding subst.: Mt 4:23 , 24 , Lk 8:39 , I Co 12:17 , al.; following subst.: Mk 1:33 , Lk 9:25 , Jo 4:53 , Ac 21:30 , al.
G3650.strongs: a primary word; "whole" or "all", i.e. complete (in extent, amount, time or degree), especially (neuter) as noun or adverb
G3650.kjv: all, altogether, every whit, + throughout, whole

G446.abbottSmith: supreme , a consul , one acting in place of a consul, a proconsul , the administrator of a senatorial province (cf. ἡγεμών , and v. MM, VGT , 44): Ac 13:7 , 8 , 12 18:12 19:38 .†
G446.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and a superlative of G5228 (ὑπέρ); instead of the highest officer, i.e. (specially) a Roman proconsul

G740.mounce: bread; a loaf or thin cake of bread, Mt. 26:26; food, Mt. 15:2; Mk. 3:20; bread, maintenance, living, necessaries of life, Mt. 6:11; Lk. 11:3; 2 Thess. 3:8
G740.abbottSmith: bread , a loaf : Mt 4:3 , 4 , Mk 3:20 , al.; ἄρτοι τ. προθέσεως , bread of the setting forth , i.e. the shewbread. Metaph., ὁ ἄ. τ. θεοῦ, τ. ζωῆς , ref. to Christ, Jo 6:33 , 35 ; in general, food : Mt 6:11 , al.; ἄ. φαγεῖν (Heb. אֲכֹל לֶחֶם ), to eat (MM, s.v.), Lu 14:1 , al.
G740.strongs: from G142 (αἴρω); bread (as raised) or a loaf

G160.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5316 (φαίνω) (compare G1810 (ἐξαίφνης)) (meaning non-apparent); unexpected, i.e. (adverbially) suddenly

G1547.abbottSmith: Rec. for γαμίζω , q.v.: Mt 22:30 24:38 , Lk 17:27 , I Co 7:38 . Not elsewhere.†
G1547.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a form of G1061 (γαμίσκω) (compare G1548 (ἐκγαμίσκω)); to marry off a daughter

G3744.mounce: smell, odor; fragrant odor, Jn. 12:3; Eph. 5:2; Phil. 4:18; met. 2 Cor. 2:14, 16*
G3744.abbottSmith: a smell , odour : Jo 12:3 ; metaph. (EV, savour ), II Co 2:14 , 16 ; of the effect of sacrifice (cf. רֵיחַ הַגִּיחֹחַ , Ge 8:21 , al., v.s. εὐωδία ), ὀ. εὐωδίας , Eph 5:2 , Phl 4:18 .†

G1622.thayer: (a) adv: (1) without, outside, beyond, (2) except, (3) subst: the outside, (b) prep: outside, apart from.
G1622.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ); the exterior; figuratively (as a preposition) aside from, besides
G1622.kjv: but, except(-ed), other than, out of, outside, unless, without

G4085.thayer: I press down, press together, as in a measure.
G4085.mounce: to press, to press or squeeze down, make compact by pressure, Lk. 6:38*
G4085.abbottSmith: to press , press down or together : Lk 6:38 (cl).
G4085.strongs: another form for G4084 (πιάζω); to pack

G4222.thayer: I cause to drink, give to drink; irrigate, water.
G4222.mounce: to cause to drink, give drink to, Mt. 10:42; met. 1 Cor. 3:2; Rev. 14:8; to water, irrigate, met. 1 Cor. 3:6, 7, 8
G4222.abbottSmith: to give to drink : c. acc. pers., Mt 25:35 , 37 , 42 27:48 , Mk 15:36 , Lk 13:15 , Ro 12:20 ; c. dupl. acc., Mt 10:42 , Mk 9:41 ; fig., γάλα , I Co 3:2 ; ἐκ τ. οἴνου , Re 14:8 ; of plants, to water (Xen., Strab., al.; Ge 13:10 ), fig., I Co 3:6-8 . Metaph. (cf. Is 29:10 , Si 15:3 ), of the Spirit, I Co 12:13 .†
G4222.strongs: from a derivative of the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); to furnish drink, irrigate

G4342.thayer: I persist, persevere in, continue steadfast in; I wait upon.
G4342.mounce: to persist in adherence to a thing; to be intently engaged in, attend constantly to, Acts 1:14; 2:42; Rom. 13:6; to remain constantly in a place, Acts 2:46; to constantly attend upon, continue near to, be at hand, Mk. 3:9; Acts 8:13; 10:7
G4342.abbottSmith: to attend constantly , continue stedfastly , adhere to , wait on : c. dat. pers., Mk 3:9 , Ac 8:13 10:7 ; c. dat. rei, Ac 1:14 2:42 6:4 , Ro 12:12 , Col 4:2 ; seq. ἐν , Ac 2:46 ; εἰς , Ro 13:6 .†
G4342.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2594 (καρτερέω); to be earnest towards, i.e. (to a thing) to persevere, be constantly diligent, or (in a place) to attend assiduously all the exercises, or (to a person) to adhere closely to (as a servitor)
G4342.kjv: attend (give self) continually (upon), continue (in, instant in, with), wait on (continually)

G5405.thayer: Phoenix, a bay on the south coast of Crete.
G5405.mounce: Phoenix, Phoenice, a city, with a harbor, on the southeast coast of Crete, Acts 27:12*
G5405.abbottSmith: a Phœnician (also with fem., Φοίνισσα , Hom., al.);
G5405.strongs: probably the same as G5404 (φοῖνιξ); Phœnix, a place in Crete

G4927.thayer: I am contiguous with, am next door to.
G4927.mounce: to be next to, be next door, Acts 18:7*
G4927.abbottSmith: to border on : c. dat., Ac 18:7 (Byz.).†
G4927.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of the base of 3674 and the base of G3725 (ὅριον); to border together, i.e. adjoin

G4163.thayer: (a) a "maker," poet, (b) a doer, carrier out, performer.
G4163.mounce: a maker; the maker or author of a song or poem, a poet, Acts 17:28; a doer; a performer of the enactments of a law, Rom. 2:13
G4163.strongs: from G4160 (ποιέω); a performer; specially, a "poet";

G3563.thayer: the mind, the reason, the reasoning faculty, intellect.
G3563.mounce: the mind, intellect, 1 Cor. 14:15, 19; understanding, intelligent faculty, Lk. 24:45; intellect, judgment, Rom. 7:23, 25; opinion, sentiment, Rom. 14:5; 1 Cor. 1:10; mind, thought, conception, Rom. 11:34; 1 Cor. 2:16; Phil. 4:7; settled state of mind, 2 Thess. 2:2; frame of mind, Rom. 1:28; 12:2; Col. 2:18; Eph. 4:23; 1 Tim. 6:5; 2 Tim. 3:8; Tit. 1:15
G3563.abbottSmith: prop., of the ruling faculty, mind , understanding , reason (v. Lft., Notes , 88 f.; Vaughan on Ro 7:23 ): Lk 24:45 , Ro 1:28 7:23 12:2 14:5 , Eph 4:17 , 23 , Phl 4:7 , II Th 2:2 , I Ti 6:5 , II Ti 3:8 , Tit 1:15 , Re 13:18 17:9 ; ν. τ. σαρκός ( ICC , in l.), Col 2:18 ; opp. to σάρξ , Ro 7:25 ; to πνεῦμα , I Co 14:14 , 15 ; to γλῶσσα , I Co 14:19 .
G3563.strongs: probably from the base of G1097 (γινώσκω); the intellect, i.e. mind (divine or human; in thought, feeling, or will); by implication, meaning

G3617.mounce: the master or head of a house or family, Mt. 10:25; 13:27, 52
G3617.abbottSmith: the master of a house , a householder : Mt 10:25 13:27 20:11 24:43 , Mk 14:14 , Lk 12:39 13:25 14:21 ; ἄνθρωπος οἰ. , Mt 13:52 20:1 21:33 ; pleonast., οἰ. τ. οἰκίας , Lk 22:11 (v. Bl., § 81, 4).†
G3617.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος) and G1203 (δεσπότης); the head of a family
G3617.kjv: goodman (of the house), householder, master of the house

G3020.thayer: belonging to the tribe of Levi; Levitical.
G3020.mounce: Levitical, pertaining to the Levites, Heb. 7:11*
G3020.strongs: from G3019 (Λευΐτης); Levitic, i.e. relating to the Levites

G3833.mounce: panoply, complete armor, a complete suit of armor, both offensive and defensive, as the shield, sword, spear, helmet, breastplate, etc., Lk. 11:22; Eph. 6:11, 13*
G3833.abbottSmith: full armour : Lk 11:22 ; metaph. (cf. Wi 5:17 ), τ. θεοῦ , Eph 6:11 , 13 .†
G3833.strongs: from a compound of G3956 (πᾶς) and G3696 (ὅπλον); full armor ("panoply")

G3045.mounce: lit., fat; fig., rich, sumptuous, Rev. 18:14*
G3045.abbottSmith: oily , fatty ; metaph., of living, rich , dainty : τὰ λ. καὶ τ. λαμπρά , Re 18:14 .†

G370.abbottSmith: unworthy : c. gen., I Co 6:2 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G370.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G514 (ἄξιος); unfit

G1309.mounce: to flee through, escape by flight, Acts 27:42*
G1309.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5343 (φεύγω); to flee through, i.e. escape

G5057.mounce: one who farms the public revenues; in NT a publican, collector of imposts, revenue officer, tax gatherer, Mt. 5:46; 9:10, 11; 10:3; Mk. 2:15f.; Lk. 3:12
G5057.strongs: from G5056 (τέλος) and G5608 (ὠνέομαι); a tax-farmer, i.e. collector of public revenue

G5326.abbottSmith: = φάσμα , an appearance, apparition (Æsch., al.): Mt 14:26 , Mk 6:49 (v. DCG , i, 111 b ).†
G5326.strongs: from G5324 (φαντάζω); (properly concrete) a (mere) show ("phantasm"), i.e. spectre

G1737.thayer: (somewhat rare) (of clothing: I put on another); mid: I put on (myself).
G1737.mounce: a later form, equivalent to ἐνδύω, to dress (oneself), Mark 15:17; 16:19*
G1737.abbottSmith: to put on : c. dupl. acc, Mk 15:17 ( ἐνδύουσι , Rec.). Mid., to put on oneself, be clothed in : c. acc rei, Lk 16:19 (cf. MM, Exp. , xii).†
G1737.strongs: a prolonged form of G1746 (ἐνδύω); to invest (with a garment)

G1017.mounce: a prize bestowed on victors in the public games, such as a crown, wreath, chaplet, garland, etc., 1 Cor. 9:24; Phil. 3:14*
G1017.abbottSmith: a prize in the games: I Co 9:24 ; metaph., of the Christian's reward, Phl 3:14 .†
G1017.strongs: from (an umpire of uncertain derivation); an award (of arbitration), i.e. (specially) a prize in the public games

G423.mounce: also spelled ἀνεπίληπρος. pr. not to be laid hold of; met. beyond reproach, unblamable, 1 Tim. 3:2; 5:7; 6:14*
G423.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G1949 (ἐπιλαμβάνομαι); not arrested, i.e. (by implication) inculpable

G3596.thayer: I travel, pursue a way, journey.
G3596.strongs: from a compound of G3598 (ὁδός) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to be a wayfarer, i.e. travel

G176.thayer: not open to just rebuke; irreprehensible.
G176.mounce: pr. not worthy of condemnation by a judge; hence, irreprehensible, Tit. 2:8*
G176.abbottSmith: not open to just rebuke , irreprehensible : Tit 2:8 (v. Cremer, 676; and for other exx., MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G176.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2607 (καταγινώσκω); unblamable

G2521.thayer: I sit, am seated, enthroned; I dwell, reside.
G2521.mounce: to sit, be sitting, Mt. 9:9; Lk. 10:13; to be seated, 1 Cor. 14:30; to be enthroned, Rev. 18:7; to dwell, reside, Mt. 4:16; Lk. 1:79; 21:35
G2521.abbottSmith: in cl. pres. and impf. only (prop. pf. and plpf. of καθέζομαι ; cf. Bl., § 24), in LXX and NT fut. also, to sit , be seated : Mt 11:16 22:44 (LXX) , Mk 2:6 , Jo 6:3 , Ac 2:2 , Ja 2:3 (on the vulgar imper. κάθου , v. Kennedy, Sources , 162), Re 4:3 , al.; seq. prep. c. acc., ἐπί , Mt 9:9 , Mk 2:14 , Jo 12:15 ; παρά , Mt 13:1 , Mk 10:46 ; περί , Mk 3:32 , 34 ; πρός , Lk 22:56 ; ὑπό , Ja 2:3 ; εἰς ,
G2521.strongs: from G2596 (κατά); and (to sit; akin to the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος)); to sit down; figuratively, to remain, reside

G19.thayer: intrinsic goodness, especially as a personal quality, with stress on the kindly (rather than the righteous) side of goodness.
G19.mounce: goodness, virtue, beneficence, Rom. 5:14; Eph. 5:9; 2 Thess. 1:11; generosity, Gal. 5:22*
G19.abbottSmith: goodness (representing "the kindlier, as δικαιοσύνη , the sterner element in the ideal character," AR, Eph., 5:9 ; on its relation to χρηστότης , v. Tr., Syn ., §lxiii): Ro 15:14 , Ga 5:22 , II Th 1:11 .†
G19.strongs: from G18 (ἀγαθός); goodness, i.e. virtue or beneficence

G5334.abbottSmith: information , esp. against fraud or other crime: Ac 21:31 .†
G5334.strongs: from G5346 (φημί) (not the same as "phase", which is from G5316 (φαίνω)); a saying, i.e. report

G5164.mounce: pr. a runner; anything spherical, a wheel; drift, course, with which signification the word is usually written τρόχος, Jas. 3:6*
G5164.abbottSmith: a wheel : Ja 3:6 (v. Mayor, Hort, in l.).†
G5164.strongs: from G5143 (τρέχω); a wheel (as a runner), i.e. (figuratively) a circuit of physical effects

G3306.thayer: I remain, abide, stay, wait; with acc: I wait for, await.
G3306.mounce: pluperfect, μεμενήκειν, to stay, Mt. 26:38; Acts 27:31; to continue; 1 Cor. 7:11; 2 Tim. 2:13; to dwell, lodge, sojourn, Jn. 1:39; Acts 9:43; to remain, Jn. 9:41; to rest, settle, Jn. 1:32, 33; 3:36; to last, endure, Mt. 11:23; Jn. 6:27; 1 Cor. 3:14; to survive, 1 Cor. 15:6; to be existent, 1 Cor. 13:13; to continue unchanged, Rom. 9:11; to be permanent, Jn. 15:16; 2 Cor. 3:11; Heb. 10:34; 13:14; 1 Pet. 1:23; to persevere, be constant, be steadfast, 1 Tim. 2:15; 2 Tim. 3:14; to abide, to be in close and settled union, Jn. 6:56; 14:10; 15:4; to indwell, Jn. 5:38; 1 Jn. 2:14; trans. to wait for, Acts 20:5, 23 See abide; await; remain; stay; wait for.
G3306.abbottSmith: to stay , abide , remain . Intrans.; of place: seq. ἐν , Lk 8:27 , al.; παρά , c. dat. pers., Jo 1:40 , al; σύν , Lk 1:56 ; καθ’ ἑαυτόν , Ac 28:16 ; c. adv., ἐκεῖ , Mt 10:11 ; ὧδε , Mt 26:38 ; metaph., I Jo 2:18 ; of the Holy Spirit, Jo 1:32 , 33 14:17 ; of Christ, Jo 6:56 15:4 , al.; ὁ θεός , I Jo 4:15 , conversely, of Christians, Jo 6:56 15:4 , I Jo 4:15 , al.; ὁ λόγος τ. θεοῦ , I Jo 2:14 ; ἡ ἀλ
G3306.strongs: a primary verb; to stay (in a given place, state, relation or expectancy)
G3306.kjv: abide, continue, dwell, endure, be present, remain, stand, tarry (for), X thine own

G2011.mounce: a trust; a commission, permission, Acts 26:12*
G2011.strongs: from G2010 (ἐπιτρέπω); permission, i.e. (by implication) full power

G2307.thayer: an act of will, will; plur: wishes, desires.
G2307.mounce: will, bent, inclination, 1 Cor. 16:12; Eph. 2:3; 1 Pet. 4:3; resolve, 1 Cor. 7:37; will, purpose, design, 2 Tim. 2:26; 2 Pet. 1:21; will, sovereign pleasure, behest, Mt. 18:14; Lk. 12:47; Acts 13:22, et al. freq.; ἐν τῷ θελήματι θεοῦ, Deo permittente, if God please or permit, Rom. 1:10
G2307.strongs: from the prolonged form of G2309 (θέλω); a determination (properly, the thing), i.e. (actively) choice (specially, purpose, decree; abstractly, volition) or (passively) inclination

G4424.thayer: Ptolemais, a coast city of Phoenicia, midway between Tyre and Caesarea.
G4424.mounce: Ptolemais, a city on the seacoast of Galilee: the modern Acre, Acts 21:7*. See PhotoGuide.
G4424.abbottSmith: Ptolemais , a maritime city of Phœnicea: Ac 21:7 .†
G4424.strongs: from (Ptolemy, after whom it was named); Ptolemais, a place in Palestine

G655.mounce: to shrink from with abhorrence, detest, Rom. 12:9*
G655.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G4767 (στυγνητός); to detest utterly

G3633.mounce: to think, suppose, imagine, presume, Jn. 21:25; Phil. 1:17. οἰέσθω in Jas. 1:7 formed from the contracted form οἶμαι*
G3633.abbottSmith: to suppose , expect , imagine ; c. acc. et inf., Jo 21:25 ; c. inf., Phl 1:17 ; seq. ὅτι , Ja 1:7 .†
G3633.strongs: middle voice apparently from G3634 (οἷος); to make like (oneself), i.e. imagine (be of the opinion)

G1643.thayer: (a) trans: I drive (on), propel, (b) intrans: I row.
G1643.mounce: to drive, urge forward, spur on, Lk. 8:29; Jas. 3:4; 2 Pet. 2:17; to impel a vessel by oars, to row, Mk. 6:48; Jn. 6:19*
G1643.abbottSmith: to drive : of the wind, Ja 3:4 , II Pe 2:17 ; of sailors rowing or sailing a boat, Mk 6:48 , Jo 6:19 ; of demons, Lk 8:29 (cf. ἀπ- , συν-ελαύνω ).†
G1643.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb (obsolete except in certain tenses as an alternative of this) of uncertain affinity; to push (as wind, oars or dæmonical power)

G5495.mounce: a hand, Mt. 3:12; 4:6; 8:15 freq.; from the Hebrew, χεὶρ Κυρίου, a special operation of God, Acts 11:21; 13:3; ἐν χειρί, by agency, Acts 7:35; Gal. 3:19
G5495.abbottSmith: the hand : Mt 3:12 , Mk 3:1 , Lk 6:6 , al. mult.; ἡ χ. , acting subject, Lk 22:21 ; pl., Ac 17:25 20:34 , I Jo 1:1 ; τ. ἔργα τῶν χ. , Ac 7:41 , Re 9:20 ; ὁ ἀσπασμὸς τ. ἐμῇ χ. , I Co 16:21 , Col 4:18 , II Th 3:17 ; prepositional phrases, esp. those without art., similar to Heb. constructions (Bl., §32, 4; 40, 9; 46, 9), ἐν χ. ; c gen. (Lft., in l.), Ga 3:19 ; σὺν χ. ἀγγέλου , Ac 7:35 ; διὰ (τῶν) χε
G5495.strongs: perhaps from the base of G5494 (χειμών) in the sense of its congener the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (through the idea of hollowness for grasping); the hand (literally or figuratively (power); especially (by Hebraism) a means or instrument)

G4310.mounce: a grandfather; a progenitor, or ancestor, Rom. 4:1*
G4310.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3958 (πάσχω); to undergo hardship previously

G2148.mounce: also spelled εὐρυκλύδων and εὐροκλύδων, which BDAG 411, says was probably due to scribal error, euracylon, the name of a tempestuous southeast wind, Acts 27:14*
G2148.abbottSmith: the Euraquilo , a N.E. wind (i.e. between Eurus and Aquilo): Ac 27:14 .†
G2148.strongs: from (the east wind) and G2830 (κλύδων); a storm from the East (or southeast), i.e. (in modern phrase) a Levanter

G4252.thayer: Prisca, Priscilla, the former being the more correct and formal name, the latter a diminutive and more familiar; a Roman lady, probably of good birth, wife of the Jewish Christian Aquila.
G4252.mounce: Priscilla, pr. name, the diminutive form of Πρίσκα, the wife of Apollos, Acts 18:2, 18, 26*
G4252.strongs: diminutive of G4251 (Πρίσκα); Priscilla (i.e. little Prisca), a Christian woman

G2210.thayer: I inflict loss (damage) upon, fine, punish, sometimes with the acc. of the penalty, even when the verb is passive.
G2210.abbottSmith: to damage . Pass., to suffer loss , forfeit , lose : absol., I Co 3:15 ; seq. ἐν , II Co 7:9 ; c. acc. rei (v. B1., § 34, 6), τ. ψυχήν , Mt 16:26 , Mk 8:36 ; ἑαυτόν , Lk 9:25 ; τ. πάντα , Phl 3:8 .†
G2210.strongs: from G2209 (ζημία); to injure, i.e. (reflexively or passively) to experience detriment
G2210.kjv: be cast away, receive damage, lose, suffer loss

G146.mounce: eager for dishonorable gain, greedy, 1 Tim. 3:8; Tit. 1:7*
G146.abbottSmith: greedy of base gains : I Ti 3:8 , Tit 1:7 .†

G4096.thayer: fatness, as of the olive; richness.
G4096.strongs: from (fat; perhaps akin to the alternate of G4095 (πίνω) through the idea of repletion); plumpness, i.e. (by implication) richness (oiliness)

G5355.mounce: envy, jealously, spite, Mt. 27:18; Mk. 15:10; Rom. 1:29; Gal. 5:21; Phil. 1:15; 1 Tim. 6:4; Tit. 3:3; Jas. 4:5; 1 Pet. 2:1*
G5355.abbottSmith: envy : Ro 1:29 , Ga 5:21 , I Ti 6:4 , Tit 3:3 , I Pe 2:1 ; διὰ φθόνον , Mt 27:18 , Mk 15:10 , Phl 1:15 ; πρὸς φθόνον ἐπιποθεῖ τ. πνεῦμα (on the meaning, v. R, txt., mg. 1, 2 ; Hort, Ja. , 93 f.), Ja 4:5 .†
G5355.strongs: probably akin to the base of G5351 (φθείρω); ill-will (as detraction), i.e. jealousy (spite)

G4875.mounce: to experience refreshment or rest in company with someone, Rom. 15:32*
G4875.abbottSmith: c. dat. pers., to lie down to rest with, sleep with . Metaph., to be refreshed in spirit with : Ro 15:32 (L, om.).†
G4875.strongs: middle from G4862 (σύν) and G373 (ἀναπαύω); to recruit oneself in company with

G208.thayer: I annul, make of no effect, cancel.
G208.mounce: to deprive of authority, annul, cancel, Mt. 15:6; Mk. 7:13; Gal. 3:17*
G208.abbottSmith: to revoke , invalidate (MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 15:6 , Mk 7:13 , Ga 3:17 (Plut.).†
G208.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2964 (κυρόω); to invalidate

G1921.thayer: I come to know by directing my attention to him or it, I perceive, discern, recognize; aor: I found out.
G1921.mounce: pr. to make a thing a subject of observation; hence, to arrive at knowledge from preliminaries; to attain to a knowledge of, Mt. 11:27; to ascertain, Lk. 7:37; 23:7; to perceive, Mk. 2:8; 5:30; to discern, detect, Mt. 7:16, 20; to recognize, Mk. 6:33; Lk. 24:16, 31; Acts 3:10; to acknowledge, admit, 1 Cor. 14:37; 1 Tim. 4:3; pass. to have one’s character discerned and acknowledged, 2 Cor. 6:9; from the Hebrew, to regard with favor and kindness, 1 Cor. 16:18
G1921.abbottSmith: "directive" of γινώσκω (AR, Eph. , 249), as in cl.;
G1921.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1097 (γινώσκω); to know upon some mark, i.e. recognize; by implication, to become fully acquainted with, to acknowledge

G1593.thayer: I escape, get clear of, deviate, withdraw.
G1593.mounce: pr. to swim out, to escape by swimming; hence, generally, to escape, get clear of a place, Jn. 5:13; though ἐκνεύσας, in this place, may be referred to ἐκνεύω, to deviate, withdraw*
G1593.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3506 (νεύω); (by analogy) to slip off, i.e. quietly withdraw

G1785.mounce: an injunction; a precept, commandment, law, Mt. 5:19; 15:3, 6; an order, direction, Acts 17:15; an edict, Jn. 11:57; a direction, Mk. 10:5; a commission, Jn. 10:18, a charge of matters to be proclaimed or received, Jn. 12:49, 50; 1 Tim. 6:14; 2 Pet. 2:21
G1785.abbottSmith: generally, a charge, injunction, order, command : Lk 15:29 , Jo 10:18 11:57 12:49 , 50 14:31 , Ac 17:15 , Col 4:10 ; ἐ. σαρκίνη , He 7:16 , 18
G1785.strongs: from G1781 (ἐντέλλομαι); injunction, i.e. an authoritative prescription

G4692.thayer: I hasten, urge on, desire earnestly.
G4692.mounce: trans. to urge on, impel, quicken; to quicken in idea, to be eager for the arrival of, 2 Pet. 3:12; intrans. to hasten, make haste, Acts 20:16; 22:18; the part. has the force of an adverb, quickly, hastily, Lk. 2:16; 19:5, 6*
G4692.abbottSmith: most freq. intrans., to hasten : c. inf. (as freq. in cl.), Ac 20:16 ; ptcp. c. indic., Lk 2:16 ; c. imperat., Lk 19:5, 6 ; σπεῦσον κ. ἔξελθε , Ac 22:18
G4692.strongs: probably strengthened from G4228 (πούς); to "speed" ("study"), i.e. urge on (diligently or earnestly); by implication, to await eagerly

G5356.mounce: corruption, decay, ruin, corruptibility, mortality, Rom. 8:21; 1 Cor. 15:42; meton. corruptible, perishable substance, 1 Cor. 15:50; killing, slaughter, 2 Pet. 2:12; spiritual ruin, Gal. 6:8; Col. 2:22; met. moral corruption, depravity, 2 Pet. 1:4; 2:19*
G5356.abbottSmith: destruction, corruption, decay (v. Mayor on II Pe, App. ,175 ff.): Ro 8:21 , I Co 15:42 , Col 2:22 , II Pe 2:12 ; opp. to ζωὴ αἰώνιος , Ga 6:8 ; by meton., of that which is subject to corruption, I Co 15:50 ; of moral decay, II Pe 1:4 2:12 , 19 (cf. Wi 14:12 ).†
G5356.strongs: from G5351 (φθείρω); decay, i.e. ruin (spontaneous or inflicted, literally or figuratively)

G5005.mounce: pr. enduring severe effort and hardship; hence, wretched, miserable, afflicted, Rom. 7:24; Rev. 3:17*
G5005.strongs: from the base of G5007 (τάλαντον) and a derivative of the base of G3984 (πεῖρα); enduring trial, i.e. miserable

G3665.abbottSmith: likeness : καθ’ ὁμοιότητα , in like manner , He 4:15 ; id. c. gen. (MM, xviii), after the likeness (of), He 7:15 .†

G532.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3903 (παρασκευάζω); unready

G4430.thayer: a fall; a carcass, corpse, dead body.
G4430.mounce: a fall; a dead body, carcass, corpse, Mt. 24:28; Mk. 6:29
G4430.abbottSmith: a fall , metaph., a misfortune , calamity (Trag., Plat., Polyb., al.; LXX).
G4430.strongs: from the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω); a ruin, i.e. (specially), lifeless body (corpse, carrion)

G5436.thayer: Phygelus, a Christian of the Roman province Asia who deserted Paul.
G5436.strongs: probably from G5343 (φεύγω); fugitive; Phygellus, an apostate Christian

G3501.mounce: recent, new, fresh, Mt. 9:17; 1 Cor. 5:7; Col. 3:10; Heb. 12:24; young, youthful, Tit. 2:4. In Acts 16:11 is used in the name Νέαν πόλις, which some lexicons list as its own lexical form. This occurrence is not included in the word’s frequency count.
G3501.strongs: including the comparative ; a primary word; "new", i.e. (of persons) youthful, or (of things) fresh; figuratively, regenerate

G5209.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G5209.strongs: accusative case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); you (as the objective of a verb or preposition)

G2943.thayer: in a circle round, round about.
G2943.abbottSmith: from all sides , round about : Re 4:3 , 4 , 8 .†
G2943.strongs: adverb from the same as G2945 (κύκλῳ); from the circle, i.e. all around

G5395.abbottSmith: a flame : Lk 16:24 ; φ, πυρός , Ac 7:30 , II Th 1:8 , He 1:7 (LXX) , Re 1:14 2:18 19:12 .†
G5395.strongs: from a primary (to "flash" or "flame"); a blaze

G3850.thayer: (a) a comparison, (b) a parable, often of those uttered by our Lord, (c) a proverb, an adage.
G3850.mounce: a placing one thing by the side of another; a comparing; a parallel case cited in illustration; a comparison, simile, similitude, Mk. 4:30; Heb. 11:19; a parable, a short relation under which something else is figured, or in which that which is fictitious is employed to represent that which is real, Mt. 13:3, 10, 13, 18, 24, 31, 33, 34, 36, 53; 21:33, 45; 22:1; 24:32; in NT a type, pattern, emblem, Heb. 9:9; a sentiment, grave and significant precept, maxim, Lk. 14:7; an obscure and enigmatical saying, anything expressed in remote and ambiguous terms, Mt. 13:35; Mk. 7:17; a proverb, adage, Lk. 4:23
G3850.strongs: from G3846 (παραβάλλω); a similitude ("parable"), i.e. (symbolic) fictitious narrative (of common life conveying a moral), apothegm or adage

G2419.thayer: (Aramaic form), Jerusalem, the capital of Palestine: hence Judaism, and allegorically, Christendom, the Christian Church.
G2419.mounce: Jerusalem, pr. name, indecl., also spelled Ἱεροσόλυμα   62x in our text. See PhotoGuide.
G2419.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03389); Hierusalem (i.e. Jerushalem), the capitol of Palestine

G3594.thayer: I lead, guide; met: I instruct, teach.
G3594.mounce: to lead, guide, Mt. 15:14; Lk. 6:39; Rev. 7:17; met. to instruct, teach, Jn. 16:13; Acts 8:31*
G3594.abbottSmith: to lead on one's way, to guide : c. acc. pers., Mt 15:14 , Lk 6:39 ; id. seq. ἐπί , Re 7:17 . Metaph., to guide , instruct , teach : Ac 8:31 ; seq. εἰς τ. ἀλήθειαν ( ἐν τ. ἀ. , WH, mg.), Jo 16:13 (cf. Ps 24 (25):5 ).†
G3594.strongs: from G3595 (ὁδηγός); to show the way (literally or figuratively (teach))

G584.thayer: I show by proof, demonstrate, set forth, proclaim to an officer.
G584.mounce: to point out, display; to prove, evince, demonstrate, Acts 25:7; to designate, proclaim, hold forth, 2 Thess. 2:4; to constitute, appoint, Acts 2:22; 1 Cor. 4:9*
G584.abbottSmith: to bring out , show forth , exhibit (Lft., Notes , 200; ICC , in l.): I Co 4:9 .
G584.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to show off, i.e. exhibit; figuratively, to demonstrate, i.e. accredit

G5109.mounce: a wall of a building, as distinct from a city wall or fortification (τεῖχος) Acts 23:3*
G5109.abbottSmith: a wall , esp. of a house: fig., Ac 23:3 (cf. τεῖχος ).†
G5109.strongs: another form of G5038 (τεῖχος); a wall

G3006.strongs: apparently a primary word; smooth, i.e. "level"

G4615.thayer: mustard (probably the shrub, not the herb).
G4615.mounce: mustard; in NT probably the shrub, not the herb, Khardal, Salvadora Persica L., the fruit of which possesses the pungency of mustard, Mt. 13:31; 17:20; Mk. 4:31; Lk. 13:19; 17:6*
G4615.strongs: perhaps from (to hurt, i.e. sting); mustard (the plant)

G5412.thayer: I load, burden; pass: I am laden.
G5412.mounce: to load, burden; met. Mt. 11:28; Lk. 11:46*
G5412.abbottSmith: to load : c. dupl. acc., Lk 11:46 ; pass., to be laden : metaph. (EV, heavy laden ), Mt 11:28 .†
G5412.strongs: from G5414 (φόρτος); to load up (properly, as a vessel or animal), i.e. (figuratively) to overburden with ceremony (or spiritual anxiety)

G3766.abbottSmith: with the negative element lost, therefore , so then : Jo 18:37 .†
G3766.strongs: from G3756 (οὐ) and G3767 (οὖν); is it not therefore that, i.e. (affirmatively) hence or so

G2031.mounce: a word, that which is expressed by words; ὡς ἔπος εἰπεῖν, so to say, if the expression may be allowed, Heb. 7:9*
G2031.abbottSmith: a word : ὡς ἔ. εἰπεῖν (cl.), so to speak: He 7:9 .†

G1242.thayer: (a) a covenant between two parties, (b) (the ordinary, everyday sense [found a countless number of times in papyri]) a will, testament.
G1242.mounce: a testamentary disposition, will; a covenant, Heb. 9:16, 17; Gal. 3:15; in NT, a covenant of God with men, Gal. 3:17; 4:24; Heb. 9:4; Mt. 26:28; the writings of the old covenant, 2 Cor. 3:14
G1242.abbottSmith: as usually in cl., a disposition, testament, will (Plat., al.): Ga 3:15 (R, mg., but v. Lft., in l.), He 9:16 , 17 (R, txt.; MM, Exp. , xi,; Milligan, NTD , 75; Abbott, Essays , 107; Deiss., LAE , 341; but v. infr.).
G1242.strongs: from G1303 (διατίθεμαι); properly, a disposition, i.e. (specially) a contract (especially a devisory will)

G4743.thayer: a moment, point of time, an instant.
G4743.mounce: pr. a point; met. a point of time, moment, instant, Lk. 4:5*
G4743.abbottSmith: a prick, a point ; metaph., σ. χρόνου , a moment : Lk 4:5 .†
G4743.strongs: feminine of G4742 (στίγμα); a point of time, i.e. an instant

G5087.thayer: I put, place, lay, set, fix, establish.
G5087.mounce: by-form of τιθέω, to place, set, lay, Mt. 5:15; Mk. 6:56; Lk. 6:48; to produce at table, Jn. 2:10; to deposit, lay, Mt. 27:60; Lk. 23:53; Acts 3:2; to lay down, Lk. 19:21, 22; Jn. 10:11, 15, 17, 18; 1 Jn. 3:16; to lay aside, put off, Jn. 13:4; to allocate, assign, Mt. 24:51; Lk. 12:46; to set, appoint, Jn. 15:16; Acts 13:47; Heb. 1:2; to render, make, Mt. 22:44; Rom. 4:17; 1 Cor. 9:18; mid. to put in custody, Mt. 14:3; Acts 4:3; to reserve, Acts 1:7; to commit as a matter of charge, 2 Cor. 5:19; to set, with design, in a certain arrangement or position, Acts 20:28; 1 Cor. 12:18, 28; 1 Thess. 5:9; 1 Tim. 1:12; pass. 1 Tim. 2:7; 2 Tim. 1:11; 1 Pet. 2:8; τιθέναι τὰ γόνατα, to kneel down, Mk. 15:19; Lk. 22:41; Acts 7:60; 9:40; 20:36; 21:5; τίθεσθαι ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ, to lay to heart, ponder, Lk. 1:66; also, εἰς τὰς καρδίας, Lk. 21:14; to design, resolve, Acts 5:4; also, ἐν πνεύματι, Acts 19:21; also, βουλήν, Acts 17:12; τίθεσθαι εἰς τὰ ὦτα, to give attentive audience to, to listen to retentively, Lk. 9:44
G5087.abbottSmith: causative of κεῖμαι , to place, lay, set : Lk 6:48 , Ro 9:33 , al.; of laying the dead to rest, Mk 15:47 , Lk 23:55 , Jo 11:34 , Ac 7:16 , al.; seq. ἐπί , c. gen., Lk 8:16 , Jo 19:19 , al.; id. c. acc., Mk 4:21 , II Co 3:13 , al.; ὑπό , Mt 5:15 , al.; παρά , Ac 4:35 , 37 . Mid., to have put or placed, to place for oneself : of putting in prison, Ac 4:3 , 5:18 , 25 , al.; of giving counsel, βουλήν
G5087.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses); to place (in the widest application, literally and figuratively; properly, in a passive or horizontal posture, and thus different from G2476 (ἵστημι), which properly denotes an upright and active position, while G2749 (κεῖμαι) is properly reflexive and utterly prostrate)
G5087.kjv: + advise, appoint, bow, commit, conceive, give, X kneel down, lay (aside, down, up), make, ordain, purpose, put, set (forth), settle, sink down

G1144.abbottSmith: a tear : Mk 9:24 (WH, txt., R, txt. omit), Ac 20:19 , 31 , II Co 2:4 , II Ti 1:4 , He 5:7 12:17 , Re 7:17 21:4 ; metaph., dat. pl., δάκρυσι ( La 2:11 , Thuc., vii, 75; Bl., § 9, 3), Lk 7:38 , 44 .†

G2691.mounce: to be headstrong or wanton towards, 1 Tim. 5:11*
G2691.abbottSmith: to wax wanton against : c. gen., I Ti 5:11 .
G2691.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4763 (στρηνιάω); to become voluptuous against

G753.abbottSmith: a master-builder , architect : I Co 3:10 (in π. of building contractors, MM, s.v.).†
G753.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G5045 (τέκτων); a chief constructor, i.e. "architect"

G4296.thayer: I announce or preach beforehand, announce by herald.
G4296.mounce: to announce publicly; in NT to announce before, Acts 13:24*
G4296.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2784 (κηρύσσω); to herald (i.e. proclaim) in advance

G4597.abbottSmith: a moth, clothes moth : Mt 6:19, 20 , Lk 12:33 .†
G4597.strongs: apparently of Hebrew origin (H05580); a moth

G1947.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and a (prolonged) form of the base of G2877 (κοράσιον) (in the sense of servant); assistance

G2296.thayer: (a) intrans: I wonder, marvel, (b) trans: I wonder at, admire.
G2296.mounce: to admire, regard with admiration, wonder at, Lk. 7:9; Acts 7:31; to reverence, adore, 2 Thess. 1:10; absol. to wonder, be filled with wonder, admiration, or astonishment, Mt. 8:10; Lk. 4:22
G2296.abbottSmith: to marvel , wonder , wonder at : absol., Mt 8:10 , 27 9:33 15:31 21:20 22:22 27:14 , Mk 5:20 15:5 , Lk 1:21 (R, txt.; ICC , in l., but v. infr.), Lk 1:63 8:25 11:14 24:41 , Jo 5:20 7:15 , Ac 2:7 4:13 13:41 , Re 17:7 , 8 ; c. acc pers., Lk 7:9 ; c. acc. rei, Lk 24:12 (WH, R, mg. om.), Jo 5:28 , Ac 7:31 ; θαῦμα μέγα , Re 17:6 ; πρόσωπον (LXX for נָשָׂא פָּנִים , De 10:17 , al.), Ju 16 ; seq. διά , c
G2296.strongs: from G2295 (θαῦμα); to wonder; by implication, to admire
G2296.kjv: admire, have in admiration, marvel, wonder

G5008.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H02924); the fresh, i.e. young girl; talitha (O maiden)

G4025.thayer: a putting around or on (as of ornaments).
G4025.mounce: a putting on, wearing of dress, etc., 1 Pet. 3:3*
G4025.abbottSmith: a putting around , putting on : I Pe 3:3 .†
G4025.strongs: from G4060 (περιτίθημι); a putting all around, i.e. decorating oneself with

G3729.mounce: pr. trans. to put in motion, incite; intrans. to rush, Mt. 8:32; Mk. 5:13; Lk. 8:33
G3729.abbottSmith: causal, to set in motion , urge on .
G3729.strongs: from G3730 (ὁρμή); to start, spur or urge on, i.e. (reflexively) to dash or plunge

G1840.thayer: I have strength for (a difficult task), am perfectly able.
G1840.mounce: to be fully able, be strong, Eph. 3:18*
G1840.abbottSmith: to have strength enough, to be quite able : c. inf., Eph 3:18 .†
G1840.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2480 (ἰσχύω); to have full strength, i.e. be entirely competent

G4532.mounce: (Hebrew, meaning peace), Salem, pr. name, indecl., Heb. 7:1f.*
G4532.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08004); Salem (i.e. Shalem), a place in Palestine

G751.mounce: Archippus, pr. name, Col. 4:17; Phlm. 2, inscr. and subscr.*
G751.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G2462 (ἵππος); horse-ruler; Archippus, a Christian

G4219.strongs: from the base of G4226 (ποῦ) and G5037 (τέ); interrogative adverb, at what time

G3604.thayer: Uzziah, son of Joram and father of Jotham, and king of Judah from about 785 to 746 B.C., an ancestor of Jesus.
G3604.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05818); Ozias (i.e. Uzzijah), an Israelite

G3678.mounce: a young donkey, a donkey’s colt, Jn. 12:14*
G3678.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3688 (ὄνος); a little ass

G67.strongs: apparently from G66 (ἄγριος) and G2462 (ἵππος); wild-horse tamer; Agrippas, one of the Herods

G362.thayer: I await (one whose coming is expected).
G362.mounce: to await, wait for, expect, 1 Thess. 1:10*
G362.abbottSmith: to await "one whose coming is expected, perhaps with the added idea of patience and confidence" c. acc., I Th 1:10 (v. M, Th. , in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G362.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3306 (μένω); to await

G5010.thayer: order, (a) regular arrangement, (b) appointed succession, (c) position, rank.
G5010.mounce: order, regular disposition, arrangement; order, series, succession, Lk. 1:8; an order, distinctive class, as of priests, Heb. 5:6, 10; 6:20; 7:11(2x), 17; order, good order, 1 Cor. 14:40; orderliness, well-regulated conduct, Col. 2:5*
G5010.abbottSmith: an arranging , hence, in military sense, disposition of an army, battle array (Thuc., al.).
G5010.strongs: from G5021 (τάσσω); regular arrangement, i.e. (in time) fixed succession (of rank or character), official dignity

G2786.thayer: Cephas (Aramaic for rock), the new name given to Simon Peter, the apostle.
G2786.mounce: Cephas, Rock, rendered into Greek by Πέτρος, Jn. 1:42; 1 Cor. 1:12; 3:22; 9:5; 15:5; Gal. 1:18; 2:9, 11, 14*
G2786.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H03710); the Rock; Cephas (i.e. Kepha), a surname of Peter

G142.thayer: I raise, lift up, take away, remove.
G142.mounce: to take up, lift, raise; bear, carry; take away, remove; destroy, kill
G142.abbottSmith: to raise , take up , lift or draw up : Jo 8:59 11:41 , Ac 27:17 , al.
G142.strongs: a primary root; to lift up; by implication, to take up or away; figuratively, to raise (the voice), keep in suspense (the mind), specially, to sail away (i.e. weigh anchor); by Hebraism (compare H05375) to expiate sin
G142.kjv: away with, bear (up), carry, lift up, loose, make to doubt, put away, remove, take (away, up)

G914.thayer: Barachiah; his identity is uncertain, perhaps father of the Zechariah killed by the Zealots in the last Jewish War.
G914.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01296); Barachias (i.e. Berechijah), an Israelite

G1677.thayer: I charge to, put to one's account, impute.
G1677.mounce: to enter in an account, to put or charge to one’s account, Phlm. 18; in NT to impute, Rom. 5:13*
G1677.abbottSmith: to charge to one's account, impute : Phm 18 (on parallels, cf. Deiss., LAE , 79 f., 335 f.; Milligan, NTD , 73; MM, Exp. , xii); of sin, Ro 5:13 (Cremer, 400).†
G1677.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3056 (λόγος) (in the sense of account); to reckon in, i.e. attribute

G3877.thayer: I accompany, follow closely, characterize, both lit. and met.; I investigate.
G3877.mounce: to follow or accompany closely; to accompany, attend, characterize, Mk. 16:17; to follow with the thoughts, trace, Lk. 1:3; to conform to, 1 Tim. 4:6; 2 Tim. 3:10*
G3877.abbottSmith: c. dat., to follow closely , accompany . Metaph., in various senses, to result : Mk 16:[17] ; to follow up , trace , investigate : Lk 1:3 (so freq. in cl.); to follow as a standard of conduct: I Ti 4:6 , II Ti 3:10 . (For exx. from π. , v. MM, xviii.)†
G3877.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to follow near, i.e. (figuratively) attend (as a result), trace out, conform to
G3877.kjv: attain, follow, fully know, have understanding

G1716.mounce: followed by the dat., or εἰς and the acc., to spit upon, Mt. 26:67; 27:30
G1716.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4429 (πτύω); to spit at or on

G1952.abbottSmith: to fail : c. acc pers., He 11:32 .†
G1952.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave upon, i.e. (figuratively) to be insufficient for

G3345.thayer: I change the outward appearance (the dress, the form of presentment) of something, transfigure; I adapt.
G3345.mounce: to remodel, transfigure, Phil. 3:21; mid. to transform one’s self, 2 Cor. 11:13, 14, 15; to transfer an imagination, 1 Cor. 4:6*
G3345.abbottSmith: to change in fashion or appearance : c. acc. rei, τ. σῶμα , Phl 3:21 ; mid., seq. εἰς , II Co 11:13 , 14 ; seq. ὡς , II Co 11:15 ; of a rhetorical device, to transfer by a fiction (Field, Notes , in l.), seq. εἰς , I Co 4:6 .†
G3345.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and a derivative of G4976 (σχῆμα); to transfigure or disguise; figuratively, to apply (by accommodation)

G2387.thayer: Jambres, a sorcerer at the court of the Pharaoh.
G2387.strongs: of Egyptian origin; Jambres, an Egyptian

G1099.abbottSmith: Ja 3:11 , 12 ; opp. to πικρόν, ἁλυκόν , Re 10:9 , 10 .†
G1099.strongs: of uncertain affinity; sweet (i.e. not bitter nor salt)

G1686.mounce: to dip in, Mt. 26:23; to dip for food in a dish, Mk. 14:20*
G1686.abbottSmith: to dip in : τ. χεῖρα ἐν τ. τρυβλίῳ , Mt 26:23 ; mid., seq. εἰς , Mk 14:20 .†
G1686.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G911 (βάπτω); to whelm on, i.e. wet (a part of the person, etc.) by contact with a fluid

G2761.thayer: falsely, in vain, to no purpose.
G2761.mounce: in vain, to no purpose, unmeaning, Jas. 4:5*
G2761.abbottSmith: in vain , to no purpose : Ja 4:5 .†
G2761.strongs: adverb from G2756 (κενός); vainly, i.e. to no purpose

G4572.mounce: of yourself, to yourself, etc. Mt. 4:6; 8:4; 19:19
G4572.abbottSmith: ( of, to ) thyself : Mt 4:6 , Jo 8:13 , I Ti 4:16 , al. (In Hellenistic, the pl. is ἑαυτῶν (q.v.), not ὑμῶν αὐτῶν as in Attic, v. Bl., § 13, 1.) †
G4572.strongs: genitive case from G4571 (σέ) and G846 (αὐτός), also dative case of the same, , and accusative case , likewise contracted , , and , respectively; of (with, to) thyself

G761.mounce: unshaken, immovable, Acts 27:41; met. firm, stable, enduring, Heb. 12:28*
G761.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4531 (σαλεύω); unshaken, i.e. (by implication) immovable (figuratively)

G4884.mounce: pluperf., συνηρπάκειν, to snatch up, clutch; to seize and carry off suddenly, Acts 6:12; to seize with force and violence, Lk. 8:29; Acts 19:29; pass. of a ship, to be caught and swept on by the wind, Acts 27:15*
G4884.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G726 (ἁρπάζω); to snatch together, i.e. seize

G1120.thayer: I fall on my knees before (in supplication), supplicate, entreat.
G1120.mounce: to fall upon one’s knees, to kneel before, Mt. 17:14; 27:29; Mk. 1:40; 10:17*
G1120.abbottSmith: to fall on the knees : seq. ἔμπροσθεν , Mt 27:29 . to fall down before one: c. acc., Mt 17:14 , Mk 1:40 , R, txt., 10:17 .†
G1120.strongs: from a compound of G1119 (γόνυ) and the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω); to fall on the knee

G4930.mounce: a complete combination, a completion, consummation, end, Mt. 13:39, 40, 49; 24:3; 28:20; Heb. 9:26*
G4930.abbottSmith: in cl., a joint payment or contribution for public service; hence, generally, joint action (Plat.).
G4930.strongs: from G4931 (συντελέω); entire completion, i.e. consummation (of a dispensation)

G687.thayer: a particle asking a question, to which a negative answer is expected.
G687.mounce: inferential particle, used mainly in interrogations, Lk. 18:8; Acts 8:30; Gal. 2:17*
G687.abbottSmith: ( num igitur ) expecting a neg. reply, Lk 18:8 ; ἆρά γε , Ac 8:30 .
G687.strongs: a form of G686 (ἄρα), denoting an interrogation to which a negative answer is presumed

G1436.thayer: an interjection, Ho! Ah! Ha! It is supposed to imply surprise, fear and indignation.
G1436.mounce: Ha! an expression of surprise or displeasure, Lk. 4:34*
G1436.abbottSmith: expressing surprise, indignation, fear (in cl. chiefly in poët.), ah! ha! : Lk 4:34 .†
G1436.strongs: apparent imperative of G1439 (ἐάω); properly, let it be, i.e. (as interjection) aha!

G2837.thayer: I fall asleep, am asleep, sometimes of the sleep of death.
G2837.mounce: to lull to sleep; pass. to fall asleep, be asleep, Mt. 28:13; Lk. 22:45; met. to sleep in death, Acts 7:60; 13:36; 2 Pet. 3:4
G2837.abbottSmith: to lull to sleep , put to sleep . Mid and pass., to fall asleep (M, Pr. , 162; M, Th. , 1, 4, 13): Mt 28:13 , Lk 22:45 , Jo 11:12 , Ac 12:6 . Metaph, of death: Mt 27:52 , Jo 11:11 Ac 7:60 13:36 , I Co 7:39 11:30 15:6 , 18 , 20 , 51 , I Th 4:13-15 , II Pe 3:4 (cf. Is 14:8 43:17 , II Mac 12:45 ).†
G2837.strongs: from G2749 (κεῖμαι); to put to sleep, i.e. (passively or reflexively) to slumber; figuratively, to decease
G2837.kjv: (be a-, fall a-, fall on) sleep, be dead

G5427.mounce: frame of thought, will, aspirations, Rom. 8:6, 7, 27*
G5427.abbottSmith: that which is in the mind (the content of φρονεῖν , ICC, Ro. , 8 6 ), the thought : Ro 8:6 , 7 , 27 .†

G1516.mounce: pertaining to peace; peaceable, disposed to peace, Jas. 3:17; from the Hebrew, profitable, blissful, Heb. 12:11*
G1516.strongs: from G1515 (εἰρήνη); pacific; by implication, salutary

G4563.mounce: to sweep, to clean with a broom, Mt. 12:44; Lk. 11:25; 15:8*
G4563.strongs: from a derivative of (to brush off; akin to G4951 (σύρω)); meaning a broom; to sweep

G3016.thayer: a small piece of money, probably the smallest piece of money.
G3016.strongs: neuter of a derivative of the same as G3013 (λεπίς); something scaled (light), i.e. a small coin

G4479.thayer: Rebecca, wife of the patriarch Isaac.
G4479.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07259); Rebecca (i.e. Ribkah), the wife of Isaac

G2588.thayer: lit: the heart; mind, character, inner self, will, intention, center.
G2588.mounce: the heart, regarded as the seat of feeling, impulse, affection, desire, Mt. 6:21; 22:37; Phil. 1:7; the heart, as the seat of intellect, Mt. 13:15; Rom. 1:21; the heart, as the inner and mental frame, Mt. 5:8; Lk. 16:15; 1 Pet. 3:4; the conscience, 1 Jn. 3:20, 21; the heart, the inner part, middle, center, Mt. 12:40
G2588.abbottSmith: the heart , the bodily organ which is regarded as the seat of life ( II Ki 18:14 , IV Ki 9:24 , al.).
