In a psychological sense, the seat of man's collective energies, the focus of personal life, the seat of the rational as well as the emotional and volitional elements in human life, hence that wherein lies the moral and religious condition of the man ( DB , ii, 317 f.; DGG , ii, 344 a ); of the se
G2588.strongs: prolonged from a primary (Latin cor, "heart"); the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle

G921.thayer: Barnabas, a Cypriote Jew, uncle of John Mark; his other name was Joseph.
G921.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and H05029); son of Nabas (i.e. prophecy); Barnabas, an Israelite

G2178.thayer: once, once for all; at once.
G2178.mounce: once for all, Rom. 6:10; Heb. 7:27; 9:12; 10:10; at once, 1 Cor. 15:6*
G2178.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G530 (ἅπαξ); upon one occasion (only)

G2482.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Italia, a region of Europe

G437.thayer: I make a mutual agreement; I confess, acknowledge, formally admit, give thanks to.
G437.mounce: pr. to come to an agreement; hence, to confess openly what is due; to confess, give thanks, render praise, Lk. 2:38*
G437.abbottSmith: to make a mutual agreement (Dem., Polyb.).
G437.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and the middle voice of G3670 (ὁμολογέω); to confess in turn, i.e. respond in praise

G3040.mounce: a port, haven, harbor, Καλὰ Λιμένες, Acts 27:8, 12*
G3040.abbottSmith: a harbour , haven : Ac 27:8 , 12 (cf. Καλοὶ Λιμένες ).†
G3040.strongs: apparently a primary word; a harbor

G5163.thayer: the track of a wheel, a path.
G5163.mounce: a track, way, path, met. Heb. 12:13*
G5163.abbottSmith: the track of a wheel , hence, a track, path : fig., He 12:13 (LXX) †
G5163.strongs: from G5164 (τροχός); a track (as a wheel-rut), i.e. (figuratively) a course of conduct

G2095.thayer: well, well done, good, rightly; also used as an exclamation.
G2095.abbottSmith: in replies, well! good! well done! : Lk 19:17 (WH for Rec. εὖ , q.v.).†

G1768.strongs: from a (tenth) multiple of G1767 (ἐννέα) and G1767 (ἐννέα) itself; ninety-nine

G4117.mounce: anything plaited or intertwined; a braid of hair, 1 Tim. 2:9*
G4117.abbottSmith: what is woven or twisted (as basket-work, nets, etc.); a braiding (sc. τριχῶν , cf. I Pe 3:3 ): pl., I Ti 2:9 .†

G22.thayer: unmarried, of a person not in a state of wedlock, whether he or she has formerly been married or not.
G22.abbottSmith: unmarried : I Co 7:8 , 32 ; fem. (= cl. ἄνανδρος ), I Co 7:11 , 34 .†
G22.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1062 (γάμος); unmarried

G5589.mounce: a morsel, crumb, bit, Mt. 15:27; Mk. 7:28*
G5589.strongs: diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5567 (ψάλλω) (meaning a crumb); a little bit or morsel

G752.thayer: a leader of the synagogue, a leader connected with the synagogue: sometimes there was only one, and the name was in some cases merely honorary.
G752.mounce: a president or moderating elder of a synagogue, Mk. 5:22, 35, 36, 38; Lk. 8:49
G752.abbottSmith: ruler of a synagogue , an administrative officer, supervising the worship ( רֹאשׁ הַכְּנֵםֶת ): Mk 5:22 , 35 , 36 , 38 , Lk 8:49 13:14 , Ac 13:15 18:8 , 17 (Inscr., v. MM, s.v.; cf. also DB , ext. , 101).†
G752.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G4864 (συναγωγή); director of the synagogue services

G1662.thayer: Eliakim, son of Abiud and father of Azor; son of Melea and father of Jonam.
G1662.mounce: also spelled Ἐλιακείμ, Eliakim, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:13; Lk. 3:30*
G1662.abbottSmith: Eliakim , an ancestor of Jesus: Mt 1:13 , Lk 3:30 .†
G1662.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0471); Eliakim, an Israelite

G4059.mounce: to cut around; to circumcise, remove the prepuce, Lk. 1:59; 2:21; met. Col. 2:11; mid. to submit to circumcision, Acts 15:1
G4059.abbottSmith: to cut around (Hdt.), hence, to circumcise ( π. τὰ αἰδοῖα , Hdt.): Lk 1:59 2:21 , Jo 7:22 , Ac 7:8 15:5 16:3 21:21 . Pass. and mid., to be circumcised , receive circumcision : Ac 15:1 , I Co 7:18 , Ga 2:3 5:2 , 3 6:12 , 13 . Metaph. (cf. De 10:16 , Je 4:4 , al.), Col 2:11 (v. Deiss., BS , 151 f.; MM, xix).†
G4059.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); to cut around, i.e. (specially) to circumcise

G1249.thayer: a waiter, servant; then of any one who performs any service, an administrator.
G1249.mounce: one who renders service to another; an attendant, servant, Mt. 20:26; 22:13; Jn. 2:5, 9; one who executes a commission, a deputy, Rom. 13:4; Χριστοῦ, Θεοῦ, ἐν κυρίῳ, etc. a commissioned minister or preacher of the Gospel, 1 Cor. 3:5; 2 Cor. 6:4; a minister charged with an announcement or sentence, 2 Cor. 3:6; Gal. 2:17; Col. 1:23; a minister charged with a significant characteristic, Rom. 15:8; a servitor, devoted follower, Jn. 12:26; a deacon or deaconess, whose official duty was to superintend the alms of the Church, with other kindred services, Rom. 16:1; Phil. 1:1; 1 Tim. 3:8, 12
G1249.abbottSmith: in general, a servant, attendant, minister : Mt 20:26 22:13 23:11 , Mk 9:35 10:43 , Jo 2:5 , 9 , I Co 3:5 , Ga 2:17 , Eph 6:21 , Col 4:7 ; δ. θεοῦ , Ro 13:4 , II Co 6:4 , I Th 3:2 ; δ. Χριστοῦ , II Co 11:23 , Col 1:7 , I Ti 4:6 ; cf. ὁ δ. ὁ ἐμός , Jo 12:26 ; δ. περιτομῆς , Ro 15:8 ; δ. καινῆς διαθήκης , II Co 3:6 ; δ. δικαιοσύνης , II Co 11:15 ; δ. [εὐαγγελίου] , Eph 3:7 , Col 1:23 ; δ. [ἐκκλησίας
G1249.strongs: probably from an obsolete (to run on errands; compare G1377 (διώκω)); an attendant, i.e. (genitive case) a waiter (at table or in other menial duties); specially, a Christian teacher and pastor (technically, a deacon or deaconess)

G4770.strongs: from G4745 (στοά); a "Stoic" (as occupying a particular porch in Athens), i.e. adherent of a certin philosophy

G175.mounce: without fruit, unfruitful, barren; by impl. noxious
G175.abbottSmith: unfruitful , barren : fig., Mt 13:22 , Mk 4:19 , I Co 14:14 , Eph 5:11 , Tit 3:14 , II Pe 1:8 , Ju 12 .†
G175.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2590 (καρπός); barren (literally or figuratively)

G3601.thayer: pain, sorrow, distress, of body or mind.
G3601.mounce: pain of body of mind; sorrow, grief, Rom. 9:2; 1 Tim. 6:10*
G3601.abbottSmith: pain , distress , of body or mind: Ro 9:2 , I Ti 6:10 .†

G1963.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3563 (νοῦς); attention of the mind, i.e. (by implication) purpose

G2049.thayer: (a) I make desolate, bring to desolation, destroy, waste, (b) of a person: I strip, rob.
G2049.mounce: to lay waste, make desolate, bring to ruin, Mt. 12:25; Lk. 11:17; Rev. 17:16; 18:17, 19*
G2049.abbottSmith: to desolate, lay waste : Mt 12:25 , Lk 11:17 , Re 17:16 18:16 , 19 .†
G2049.strongs: from G2048 (ἔρημος); to lay waste (literally or figuratively)
G2049.kjv: (bring to, make) desolate(-ion), come to nought

G2373.thayer: I provoke to anger; pass: I am wroth or very angry.
G2373.mounce: to provoke to anger; pass. to be angered, enraged, Mt. 2:16*
G2373.abbottSmith: to be wroth or very angry : Mt 2:16 .†
G2373.strongs: from G2372 (θυμός); to put in a passion, i.e. enrage

G5188.mounce: to raise a smoke; pass. to emit smoke, smoke, smoulder, Mt. 12:20*
G5188.abbottSmith: to raise a smoke ; pass., to smoke : Mt 12:20 (LXX, καπνιζόμενον ).†
G5188.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to make a smoke, i.e. slowly consume without flame

G1321.mounce: to teach, Mt. 4:23; 22:16; to teach or speak in a public assembly, 1 Tim. 2:12; to direct, admonish, Mt. 28:15; Rom. 2:21
G1321.abbottSmith: to teach (i.e. instruct ) a person, teach a thing; trans.: c. acc. pers., Mt 5:2 , Mk 1:22 , al.; seq. ὅτι , Mk 8:31 ; περί , I Jo 2:27 ; c. inf., Lk 11:1 ; c. acc. rei, Mk 6:30 12:14 ; c. cogn. acc., Mt 15:9 (LXX); c. dupl. acc, Mk 4:2 , Jo 14:26 ; pass., Ga 1:12 , II Th 2:15 ; c. dat. pers. (like Heb., cf. Jb 21:22 , but prob. a vernac. usage, v. Swete, in l.), Re 2:14 . Absol., to teach, give i
G1321.strongs: a prolonged (causative) form of a primary verb (to learn); to teach (in the same broad application)

G2824.thayer: a small geographical division, district, or territory.
G2824.mounce: pr. a slope; a portion of the ideal slope of the earth’s surface; a tract or region of country, Rom. 15:23; 2 Cor. 11:10; Gal. 1:21*
G2824.abbottSmith: an inclination , slope , esp. the slope from the equator to the pole (Arist).
G2824.strongs: from G2827 (κλίνω); a slope, i.e. (specially) a "clime" or tract of country

G1925.thayer: I show, display, point out, indicate; I prove, demonstrate.
G1925.mounce: to exhibit, Mt. 16:1; Acts 9:39; to show, Mt. 22:19; Lk. 17:14; to point out, Mt. 24:1; to demonstrate, prove, Acts 18:28; Heb. 6:17*
G1925.abbottSmith: to show, exhibit, display : c. acc et dat., Mt 16:1 22:19 24:1 , Lk 17:14 . Mid., to display for oneself or as one's own (but cf. Bl., § 55, 1): Ac 9:39 .
G1925.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to exhibit (physically or mentally)

G4852.thayer: I assent to, consent, confess, agree with.
G4852.abbottSmith: to consent, confess : c. dat., seq. ὅτι , Ro 7:16 .†
G4852.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5346 (φημί); to say jointly, i.e. assent to

G5425.mounce: to be ruffled, to bristle; to shiver, shudder from fear, Jas. 2:19*
G5425.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to "bristle" or chill, i.e. shudder (fear)

G1142.mounce: a god, a superior power; in NT a malignant demon, evil angel, Mt. 8:31*
G1142.abbottSmith: in cl. and NT = δαιμόνιον ; a demon : Mt 8:31 .†
G1142.strongs: from (to distribute fortunes); a dæmon or supernatural spirit (of a bad nature)

G54.strongs: from G53 (ἁγνός); cleanness (the state), i.e. (figuratively) blamelessness

G652.thayer: a messenger, envoy, delegate, one commissioned by another to represent him in some way, especially a man sent out by Jesus Christ Himself to preach the Gospel; an apostle.
G652.mounce: one sent as a messenger or agent, the bearer of a commission, messenger, Jn. 13:16; an apostle, Mt. 10:2
G652.strongs: from G649 (ἀποστέλλω); a delegate; specially, an ambassador of the Gospel; officially a commissioner of Christ ("apostle") (with miraculous powers)
G652.kjv: apostle, messenger, he that is sent

G5366.abbottSmith: loving money, avaricious : Lk 16:14 , II Ti 3:2 .†
G5366.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G696 (ἄργυρος); fond of silver (money), i.e. avaricious

G2493.mounce: Joel, an Old Testament prophet, pr. name, indecl., Acts 2:16*
G2493.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03100); Joel, an Israelite

G4282.thayer: I prepare or appoint beforehand, predestine.
G4282.mounce: to prepare beforehand; in NT to appoint beforehand, Rom. 9:23; Eph. 2:10*
G4282.abbottSmith: to prepare before : c. acc. rei, Ro 9:23 ( οἷς for ἅ by attraction), Eph 2:10 .†
G4282.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2090 (ἑτοιμάζω); to fit up in advance (literally or figuratively)

G3921.thayer: I enter secretly, come in by stealth.
G3921.abbottSmith: to slip in secretly , steal in : παρεισεδύησαν (vulgar aor.; pass. for act., Bl., § 19, 2), Ju 4 (cf. also MM, xix).†
G3921.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and a compound of G1519 (εἰς) and G1416 (δύνω); to settle in alongside, i.e. lodge stealthily

G2248.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G2248.strongs: accusative case plural of G1473 (ἐγώ); us

G663.thayer: (lit: sheerness, of a rock), abruptness, harshness, severity, rigor.
G663.mounce: pr. abruptness; met. severity, rigor, Rom. 11:22 (2x)*
G663.abbottSmith: steepness , sharpness ; metaph., severity (MM, s.v.): Ro 11:22 .†
G663.strongs: from the base of G664 (ἀποτόμως); (figuratively) decisiveness, i.e. rigor

G1223.thayer: (a) gen: through, throughout, by the instrumentality of, (b) acc: through, on account of, by reason of, for the sake of, because of.
G1223.mounce: (1) gen., through, used of place or medium, Mt. 7:13; Lk. 6:1; 2 Cor. 11:33; through, of time, during, in the course of, Heb. 2:15; Acts 5:19; through, of immediate agency, causation, instrumentality, by means of, by, Jn. 1:3; Acts 3:18; of means or manner, through, by, with, Lk. 8:4; 2 Cor. 5:7; 8:8; of state or condition, in a state of, Rom. 4:11; (2) acc., used of causation which is not direct and immediate in the production of a result, on account of, because of, for the sake of, with a view to, Mk. 2:27; Jn. 1:31; rarely, through, while subject to a state of untoward circumstances, Gal. 4:13
G1223.abbottSmith: c. gen., through ; of Place, after verbs of motion or action: Mt 2:12 12:43 , Mk 2:23 , Lk 4:30 , Jo 4:4 , II Co 11:33 , al.; σώζεσθαι (διας-) δ. πυρός, ὕδατος , I Co 3:15 , I Pe 3:20 ; βλέπειν δ. ἐσόπτρου , I Co 13:12 ; metaph., of a state or condition: Ro 14:20 , II Co 2:4 5:7 , 10; δ. γράμματος, ἀκροβυστίας (Lft., Notes , 263, 279), Ro 2:27 4:11 ; δι’ ὑπομενῆς , Ro 8:25 . Of Time; during which:
G1223.strongs: a primary preposition denoting the channel of an act; through (in very wide applications, local, causal, or occasional)
G1223.kjv: after, always, among, at, to avoid, because of (that), briefly, by, for (cause) … fore, from, in, by occasion of, of, by reason of, for sake, that, thereby, therefore, X though, through(-out), to, wherefore, with (-in)

G573.mounce: pr. single; hence, simple, uncompounded; sound, perfect, Mt. 6:22; Lk. 11:34*
G573.abbottSmith: simple , single : in a moral sense ( DCG , ii, 628 f.), ἀφθαλμός , Mt 6:22 , Lk 11:34 . (In π. of a marriage dowry, v. MM, s.v.).†
G573.strongs: probably from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and the base of G4120 (πλέκω); properly, folded together, i.e. single (figuratively, clear)

G3740.thayer: as often as, as many times as.
G3740.abbottSmith: relat. adv., as often as : seq. ἐάν (q.v.), I Co 11:25 , 26 , Re 11:6 .†
G3740.strongs: multiple adverb from G3739 (ὅς); how (i.e. with G302 (ἄν), so) many times as

G5624.mounce: profitable, useful, beneficial; serviceable, 1 Tim. 4:8 (2x); 2 Tim. 3:16; Tit. 3:8*
G5624.abbottSmith: useful, serviceable, profitable : c. dat. commod., Tit 3:8 ; seq. πρός , c. acc, I Ti 4:8 , II Ti 3:16 .†
G5624.strongs: from a form of G3786 (ὄφελος); helpful or serviceable, i.e. advantageous

G1040.mounce: byssus, a species of fine cotton highly prized by the ancients, Lk. 16:19*
G1040.abbottSmith: byssus , a fine species of flax, also the linen made from it: Lk 16:19 .†

G4475.thayer: a slap, blow on the cheek with the open hand.
G4475.mounce: a blow with the palm of the hand, cuff, slap, Mk. 14:65; Jn. 18:22; 19:3*
G4475.kjv: (+ strike with the) palm of the hand, smite with the hand

G245.thayer: belonging to another person, belonging to others, foreign, strange.
G245.mounce: belonging to another, Lk. 16:12; foreign, Acts 7:6; Heb. 11:9; a foreigner, alien, Mt. 17:25
G245.abbottSmith: belonging to another , not one's own (opp. to ἴδιος ): Lk 16:12 , Ro 14:4 15:20 (Field, Notes , 165 f.), II Co 10:15 , 16 , I Ti 5:22 , He 9:25 .
G245.strongs: from G243 (ἄλλος); another's, i.e. not one's own; by extension foreign, not akin, hostile

G2435.thayer: (a) a sin offering, by which the wrath of the deity shall be appeased; a means of propitiation, (b) the covering of the ark, which was sprinkled with the atoning blood on the Day of Atonement.
G2435.mounce: the cover of the ark of the covenant, the mercy-seat, the place of propitiation, Rom. 3:25; Heb. 9:5*
G2435.abbottSmith: propitiatory ( μνῆμα , FlJ, Ant. , xvi, 7, 1; θάνατος , II Mac, l.c.): of Christ, Ro 3:25 ; as subst., το ἱ. (sc. ἐπίθεμα , Ex 25:16 , 17 , where the word first occurs in LXX and where ἱ. ἐ. = כַּפֹּרֶת (q.v. in BDB, s.v.), elsewhere rendered simply τὸ ἱ ; cf. Deiss., 124 ff.; Westc., He. , in l.), He 9:5 .†
G2435.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G2433 (ἱλάσκομαι); an expiatory (place or thing), i.e. (concretely) an atoning victim, or (specially) the lid of the Ark (in the Temple)

G4679.thayer: I make wise, instruct; pass: I am skillfully devised.
G4679.mounce: to make wise, enlighten, 2 Tim. 3:15; mid. to invent skilfully, devise artfully, pass. 2 Pet. 1:16*
G4679.abbottSmith: to make wise, instruct : c. acc. pers., II Ti 3:15 (cf. Ps 18 (19):8 ( Ps 118 (119):98 ). As depon., -ομαι ;
G4679.strongs: from G4680 (σοφός); to render wise; in a sinister acceptation, to form "sophisms", i.e. continue plausible error

G5222.mounce: a meeting, act of meeting, Mt. 8:34; 25:1; Jn. 12:13*
G5222.abbottSmith: a going to meet : εἰς ὑ. (v. M, Pr., 14 n ), Mt 8:34 25:1 , Jo 12:13 .†
G5222.strongs: from G5221 (ὑπαντάω); an encounter or concurrence (with G1519 (εἰς) for infinitive, in order to fall in with)

G924.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and H02931); son of Timæus (or the unclean); Bar-timæus, an Israelite

G1958.strongs: from G1959 (ἐπιμελέομαι); carefulness, i.e. kind attention (hospitality)

G2245.thayer: of which size, of what size, how small, how much.
G2245.mounce: as great as; how great, Col. 2:1; Jas. 3:5 (2x)*
G2245.abbottSmith: prop., as big as , as old as .
G2245.strongs: from (a comrade, i.e. one of the same age); as big as, i.e. (interjectively) how much

G448.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2436 (ἵλεως); inexorable

G1085.mounce: also spelled Γεργεσηνός, from Gerasene, belonging to the city of Gerasa, Mk. 5:1; Lk. 8:26, 37*. See PhotoGuide
G1085.strongs: from G1096 (γίνομαι); "kin" (abstract or concrete, literal or figurative, individual or collective)
G1085.kjv: born, country(-man), diversity, generation, kind(-red), nation, offspring, stock

G540.mounce: pr. without a father, fatherless; hence, independent of paternal descent, Heb. 7:3*
G540.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3962 (πατήρ); fatherless, i.e. of unrecorded paternity

G2715.mounce: to exercise lordship or authority over, domineer over, Mt. 20:25; Mk. 10:42*
G2715.abbottSmith: to exercise authority over : c. gen. pers., Mt 20:25 , Mk 10:42 .†
G2715.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1850 (ἐξουσιάζω); to have (wield) full privilege over

G2620.mounce: to boast, glory over, assume superiority over, Rom. 11:18 (2x); Jas. 2:13; 3:14*
G2620.abbottSmith: to boast against , exult over : c. gen., Ro 11:18 , Ja 2:13 ; seq. κατά , c. gen., Ja 3:14 (T, om. κατά ).
G2620.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2744 (καυχάομαι); to exult against (i.e. over)

G4885.mounce: pass., to grow together in company, Mt. 13:30*
G4885.abbottSmith: to cause to increase or grow together . Pass., to grow together : Mt 13:30 (Xen., al.).†
G4885.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G837 (αὐξάνω); to increase (grow up) together

G2003.mounce: injunction, 1 Cor. 7:6, 25; 2 Cor. 8:8; a decree, Rom. 16:26; 1 Tim. 1:1; Tit. 1:3; authoritativeness, strictness, Tit. 2:15*
G2003.strongs: from G2004 (ἐπιτάσσω); an injunction or decree; by implication, authoritativeness

G4714.thayer: an insurrection, dissension; originally: standing, position, place.
G4714.mounce: a setting; a standing; an effective position, an unimpaired standing or dignity, Heb. 9:8; a gathered party, a group; hence, a tumultuous assemblage, popular outbreak, Mk. 15:7; Acts 19:40; Lk. 23:19, 25; seditious movement, Acts 24:5; discord, dispute, dissension, Acts 15:2; 23:7, 10*
G4714.abbottSmith: a standing, place, status : σ. ἔχειν (Polyb., v, 5, 3; and cf. Lat. locum habere ), He 9:8 .
G4714.strongs: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a standing (properly, the act), i.e. (by analogy) position (existence); by implication, a popular uprising; figuratively, controversy

G253.mounce: free from grief or sorrow, Phil. 2:28*
G253.strongs: comparative of a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3077 (λύπη); more without grief

G2495.mounce: Jonas, pr. name Jonah, the prophet, Mt. 12:39; Lk. 11:29
G2495.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03124); Jonas (i.e. Jonah), the name of two Israelites

G1784.thayer: (held precious, hence) precious, honored, honorable in rank.
G1784.mounce: honored, estimable, dear, Lk. 7:2; 14:8; Phil. 2:29; highly-valued, precious, costly, 1 Pet. 2:4, 6*
G1784.abbottSmith: honoured, prized, precious : of persons, Lk 7:2 , Phl 2:29 ; compar., Lk 14:8 ; of things, metaph., λίθος , I Pe 2:4 , 6 (LXX) .†
G1784.kjv: dear, more honourable, precious, in reputation

G3127.thayer: (Graecized form of Aramaic Menahem), Manaen, probably a member of Herod Antipas' court.
G3127.strongs: of uncertain origin; Manaën, a Christian

G1698.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G1698.strongs: a prolonged form of G3427 (μοί); to me

G1862.mounce: a promise, 2 Pet. 3:13; meton. promised favor or blessing, 2 Pet. 1:4*
G1862.strongs: from G1861 (ἐπαγγέλλω); a self-committal (by assurance of conferring some good)

G1770.thayer: I make a sign to by nodding.
G1770.mounce: to nod at, signify by a nod; to make signs; to intimate by signs, Lk. 1:62*
G1770.abbottSmith: to nod to, make a sign to : c. dat. pers., Lk 1:62 .†
G1770.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3506 (νεύω); to nod at, i.e. beckon or communicate by gesture

G1440.mounce: seventy, indecl., Acts 7:14; οἱ ἑβδομήκοντα, the seventy disciples, Lk. 10:1, 17; Acts 23:23*
G1440.strongs: from G1442 (ἕβδομος) and a modified form of G1176 (δέκα); seventy

G2991.mounce: cut in stone, hewn out of stone or rock, Lk. 23:53*
G2991.abbottSmith: hewn (in stone); Lk 23:53 (elsewhere κοινή writers use λατομητός , IV Ki 12:12 , al.; cf. λατομέω ).†
G2991.strongs: from a compound of (a stone) and the base of G3584 (ξηρός) (in its original sense of scraping); rock-quarried

G2377.abbottSmith: a door-keeper , porter : ὁ , Mk 13:34 , Jo 10:3 ; ἡ , Jo 18:16 , 17 .†
G2377.strongs: from G2374 (θύρα) and (a watcher); a gate- warden

G3355.thayer: a measure, amphora, about 39.39 liters or 8.75 gallons.
G3355.mounce: pr. a measurer; also, metretes, Latin metreta, equivalent to the Attic ἀμφορεύς, i.e., three-fourths of the Attic μέδιμνος, and therefore equal to about nine gallons, Jn. 2:6*
G3355.strongs: from G3354 (μετρέω); a measurer, i.e. (specially), a certain standard measure of capacity for liquids

G2023.mounce: to supply further; to superadd, 2 Pet. 1:5; to supply, furnish, give, 2 Cor. 9:10; Gal. 3:5; 2 Pet. 1:11; pass. to gather vigor, Col. 2:19*
G2023.abbottSmith: to supply, provide : c. acc rei, II Pe 1:5 ; id. c. dat. pers., II Co 9:10 , Ga 3:5 ; pass., Col 2:19 , II Pe 1:11 (cf. χορηγέω , and v. MM, Exp. , xiv).†
G2023.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5524 (χορηγέω); to furnish besides, i.e. fully supply, (figuratively) aid or contribute

G828.thayer: Augustus, a title conferred on the first Roman Emperor, C. Iulius Octauianus, denoting sanctity (almost divinity).
G828.abbottSmith: (Lat.), Augustus , the Roman Emperor: Lk 2:1 (cf. Σεβαστός ; and v. MM, s.v.).†
G828.strongs: from Latin ("august"); Augustus, a title of the Roman emperor

G4456.mounce: to petrify; to harden; in NT to harden the feelings, Jn. 12:40; pass. to become callous, unimpressible, Mk. 6:52; 8:17; Rom. 11:7; 2 Cor. 3:14*
G4456.abbottSmith: to petrify , harden , form a callus . Metaph., π. τ. καρδίαν , Jo 12:40 . Pass., Ro 11:7 ; τ. νοήματα , II Co 3:14 ; ἡ καρδία , Mk 6:52 8:17 .†
G4456.strongs: apparently from (a kind of stone); to petrify, i.e. (figuratively) to indurate (render stupid or callous)

G2949.mounce: a wave, surge, billow, Mt. 8:24; 14:24; Mk. 4:37; Acts 27:41; Jude 13*
G2949.abbottSmith: a wave : pl., Mt 8:24 14:24 , Mk 4:37 ; κ. θαλάσσης , fig., Ju 13 .†
G2949.strongs: from (to swell (with young), i.e. bend, curve); a billow (as bursting or toppling)

G3702.mounce: dressed by fire, roasted, broiled, etc., Lk. 24:42*
G3702.strongs: from an obsolete verb akin to (to "steep"); cooked, i.e. roasted

G3358.thayer: a measure, whether lineal or cubic; a measuring rod.
G3358.mounce: measure, Mt. 7:2; Mk. 4:24; Lk. 6:38; Rev. 21:17; measure, standard, Eph. 4:13; extent, compass, 2 Cor. 10:13; allotted measure, specific portion, Rom. 12:3; Eph. 4:7, 16; ἐκ μέτρον, by measure, with definite limitation, Jn. 3:34
G3358.abbottSmith: that which is used for measuring, a measure ; a vessel: fig., Mt 23:32 , Lk 6:38 ; ἐκ μ. , by measure , Jo 3:34 ; a rod or rule: Re 21:15 , 17 ; fig., Mt 7:2 , Mk 4:24 .
G3358.strongs: an apparently primary word; a measure ("metre"), literally or figuratively; by implication, a limited portion (degree)

G1176.mounce: ten, Mt. 20:24; 25:1; ἡμερῶν δέκα, ten days, a few days, a short time, Rev. 2:10
G1176.abbottSmith: ten : Mt 20:24 , al.; θλίψις ἡμερῶν δ. , i.e. of brief duration: Re 2:10 .†

G2205.mounce: generous rivalry; noble aspiration; in NT zeal, ardor in behalf of, ardent affection, Jn. 2:17; Rom. 10:2; in a bad sense, jealousy, envy, malice, Acts 13:45; Rom. 13:13; indignation, wrath, Acts 5:17
G2205.abbottSmith: zeal : II Co 7:11 9:2 ; κατὰ ζῆλος , Phl 3:6 ; c. gen. obj., Jo 2:17 (LXX) , Ro 10:2 ; seq. ὑπέρ , II Co 7:7 ; c. gen. subj., θεοῦ , II Co 11:2 ; πυρός , He 10:27 (cf. Is 26:11 , Wi 5:18 , and cf. Westc., in l.).
G2205.strongs: from G2204 (ζέω); properly, heat, i.e. (figuratively) "zeal" (in a favorable sense, ardor; in an unfavorable one, jealousy, as of a husband (figuratively, of God), or an enemy, malice)
G2205.kjv: emulation, envy(-ing), fervent mind, indignation, jealousy, zeal

G5041.thayer: I bear children, beget, rear a family.
G5041.mounce: to bear children, to rear a family, 1 Tim. 5:14*
G5041.abbottSmith: to beget or bear children : I Ti 5:14 (Anthol.).†
G5041.strongs: from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and the base of G1096 (γίνομαι); to be a child-bearer, i.e. parent (mother)

G4750.thayer: the mouth, speech, eloquence in speech, the point of a sword.
G4750.mounce: the mouth, Mt. 12:34; 15:11, 17, 18; 21:16; speech, words, Mt. 18:16; 2 Cor. 13:1; command of speech, facility of language, Lk. 21:15; from the Hebrew, ἀνοίγειν τὸ στόμα, to utter, to speak, Mt. 5:2, 13:35; also, used of the earth, to rend, yawn, Rev. 12:16; στόμα πρὸς στόμα λαλεῖν, to speak mouth to mouth, face to face, 2 Jn. 12; 3 Jn. 14; the edge or point of a weapon, Lk. 21:24; Heb. 11:34
G4750.abbottSmith: the mouth : of man, Mt 15:11 , Jo 19:29 , Ac 11:8 , al.; of animals, Mt 17:27 , II Ti 4:17 (fig.), He 11:33 , Ja 3:3 , al.; fig., of inanimate things ( ποταμοῦ , Hom.), ἤνοιξεν ἡ γῆ τὸ σ. , Re 12:16 ; σ. μαχαίρας (Heb. פִּי ־ חֶרֶב , Ge 34:26 al.), the edge of the sword, Lk 21:24 , He 11:34 ; esp. of the mouth as the organ of speech: opp. to καρδία , Mt 12:34 , Ro 10:8 , 10 ; in various phrases (s
G4750.strongs: probably strengthened from a presumed derivative of the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); the mouth (as if a gash in the face); by implication, language (and its relations); figuratively, an opening (in the earth); specially, the front or edge (of a weapon)

G5349.abbottSmith: perishable, corruptible : ἄνθρωπος , Ro 1:23 (opp. to ἄφθαρτος θεός ); στέφανος , I Co 9:25 (opp. to ἄφθαρτος ); σπσρά , I Pe 1:23 (opp. to ἄφθ. ); neut., τὸ φ. τοῦτο , I Co 15:53 , 54 ; pl., I Pe 1:18 .†
G5349.strongs: from G5351 (φθείρω); decayed, i.e. (by implication) perishable

G670.abbottSmith: to discharge a cargo (Field, Notes , 134), to unlade : c. acc., τ. γόμον , Ac 21:3 .†
G670.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the middle voice of G5412 (φορτίζω); to unload

G2285.thayer: astonishment, amazement (allied to terror or awe).
G2285.strongs: akin to an obsolete (to dumbfound); stupefaction (by surprise), i.e. astonishment

G5536.mounce: anything useful, or needful; pl. wealth, riches, Mk. 10:23; Lk. 18:24; money, Acts 8:18, 20; 24:26; sg. price, Acts 4:37*
G5536.abbottSmith: a thing that one uses or needs (and generally, a matter, event, business ); hence in pl.,
G5536.strongs: something useful or needed, i.e. wealth, price

G1936.thayer: a laying on; an attack, assault.
G1936.mounce: the act of placing upon, imposition of hands, Acts 8:18; 1 Tim. 4:14; 2 Tim. 1:6; Heb. 6:2*
G1936.abbottSmith: a laying on : χειρῶν , Ac 8:18 , I Ti 4:14 , II Ti 1:6 , He 6:2 (cf. Westc., He ., l.c. ; CGT on 1Ti, l.c. ).
G1936.strongs: from G2007 (ἐπιτίθημι); an imposition (of hands officially)

G2156.mounce: in a becoming manner, with propriety, decently, gracefully, Rom. 13:13; 1 Cor. 14:40; 1 Thess. 4:12*
G2156.abbottSmith: decorously, becomingly : I Co 14:40 ; περιπατεῖν , Ro 13:13 , I Th 4:12 .†

G777.abbottSmith: fasting , without eating (cf. MM, s.v.): Ac 27:33 .†
G777.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4621 (σῖτος); without (taking) food

G4584.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08096); Semei (i.e. Shimi), an Israelite

G2056.abbottSmith: a kid : Mt 25:32 , Lk 15:29 , WH, txt.†
G2056.strongs: perhaps from the same as G2053 (ἔριον) (through the idea of hairiness); a kid or (genitive case) goat

G4162.mounce: a making; an acting, doing, performance; observance of a law, Jas. 1:25*
G4162.strongs: from G4160 (ποιέω); action, i.e. performance (of the law)

G1130.thayer: I am poorly clad; I am habitually wearing an under-garment only.
G1130.abbottSmith: to be naked or scantily clad : I Co 4:11 (cf. ἐν ψύχει κ. γυμνότητι , II Co 11:27 ; γυμνοί , Ja 2:15 . In κοινή writers, γ. also means to go light-armed .†
G1130.strongs: from a derivative of G1131 (γυμνός); to strip, i.e. (reflexively) go poorly clad

G136.strongs: apparently a prime word; properly, a story, but used in the sense of G1868 (ἔπαινος); praise (of God)

G5131.strongs: from the base of G5176 (τρώγω); a he-goat (as a gnawer)

G5292.mounce: subordination, 1 Tim. 3:4; submissiveness, obedience, 2 Cor. 9:13; Gal. 2:5; 1 Tim. 2:11*
G5292.abbottSmith: subjection : II Co 9:13 , Ga 2:5 . I Ti 2:11 3:4 .†

G4560.mounce: of flesh, fleshly, 2 Cor. 3:3; Rom. 7:14; 1 Cor. 3:1; Heb. 7:16*
G4560.abbottSmith: = Lat. carneus (the termination -ινος denoting the substance or material of a thing; v. Tr., Syn. , § lxxii; Lft., Notes , 184; and for illustrations from π. , v. MM, xxii), of the flesh, of flesh, fleshy (Plat., Arist., Plut., al.): Ro 7:14 , I Co 3:1 , II Co 3:3 , He 7:16 . (Rec. has -ικός in all these passages except II Co, l.c. , but the evidence is decisive against it.)†
G4560.strongs: from G4561 (σάρξ); similar to flesh, i.e. (by analogy) soft

G546.mounce: to threaten, menace, rebuke, Acts 4:17; 1 Pet. 2:23*
G546.abbottSmith: to threaten : I Pe 2:23 ; mid., Ac 4:17 (v. MM, VGT , s.v., and cf. προσαπειλέω ).†
G546.strongs: of uncertain derivation; to menace; by implication, to forbid

G4221.thayer: a drinking cup, the contents of the cup; fig: the portion which God allots.
G4221.mounce: a vessel for drinking, cup, Mt. 10:42; 23:25, 26; meton. the contents of a cup, liquor contained in a cup, Lk. 22:20; 1 Cor. 10:16; from the Hebrew, the cup or potion of what God’s administration deals out, Mt. 20:22, 23; Rev. 14:10
G4221.abbottSmith: a wine cup : Mt 23:25 , 26 26:27 , Mk 7:4 14:23 , Lk 11:39 22:17 , 20a (WH, R, mg., om.), I Co 11:25 , Re 17:4 ; πίνειν ἐκ τοῦ π. , I Co 11:28 ; c. gen. rei, Mt 10:42 , Mk 9:41 ; τ. εὐλογίας , I Co 10:16 ; by meton., of the contents of the cup, Lk 22:20b (WH, R, mg., om.), I Co 11:25 , 26 ; c. gen. pers., I Co 10:21 11:27 . Metaph., of experience of divine providence; of prosperity ( Ps 15 (16):5
G4221.strongs: neuter of a derivative of the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); a drinking-vessel; by extension, the contents thereof, i.e. a cupful (draught); figuratively, a lot or fate

G2270.thayer: I rest from work, cease from altercation, am silent, live quietly.
G2270.mounce: to be still, at rest; to live peaceably, be quiet, 1 Thess. 4:11; to rest from labor, Lk. 23:56; to be silent or quiet, acquiesce, to desist from discussion, Lk. 14:4; Acts 11:18; 21:14*
G2270.abbottSmith: to be still ; to rest from labour : Lk 23:56 ; to live quietly : I Th 4:11 ; to be silent : Lk 14:3 , Ac 11:18 21:14 (cf. Jb 32:7 , Ne 5:8 ).†
G2270.strongs: from the same as G2272 (ἡσύχιος); to keep still (intransitively), i.e. refrain from labor, meddlesomeness or speech
G2270.kjv: cease, hold peace, be quiet, rest

G5105.thayer: consequently, therefore, well then, so then.
G5105.mounce: well then, so then, wherefore, for that reason, 1 Thess. 4:8; Heb. 12:1*
G5105.abbottSmith: wherefore then, so therefore : I Th 4:8 , He 12:1 (Hdt., Plat., al.).†
G5105.strongs: from G5104 (τοί) and G1063 (γάρ) and G3767 (οὖν); truly for then, i.e. consequently

G2268.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03470); Hesaias (i.e. Jeshajah), an Israelite

G2736.thayer: (a) down, below, also: downwards, (b) lower, under, less, of a length of time.
G2736.mounce: lower, farther down; of time, under, Mt. 2:16*
G2736.abbottSmith: down , downwards (with verbs of motion), Mt 4:6 , Lk 4:9 , Jo 8:[6 , 8] , Ac 20:9 .

G1226.mounce: to assert strongly, insist, 1 Tim. 1:7; Tit. 3:8*
G1226.abbottSmith: to affirm confidently : Tit 3:8 ; seq. περί , I Ti 1:7 (Cremer, 140).†
G1226.strongs: middle voice of a compound of G1223 (διά) and G950 (βεβαιόω); to confirm thoroughly (by words), i.e. asseverate

G429.thayer: I find by seeking out, discover.
G429.mounce: to find by diligent search, Lk. 2:16; Acts 21:4*
G429.abbottSmith: to find out by search, discover (v. Field, Notes , 47 f.): Lk 2:16 , Ac 21:4 .†
G429.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2147 (εὑρίσκω); to find out

G3578.mounce: pr. state of being a guest; then, the reception of a guest or stranger, hospitality, in NT a lodging, Acts 28:23; Phlm. 22*
G3578.abbottSmith: hospitality , entertainment : Phm 22 (cf. ICC , in l., but v. infr.). By meton., a place of entertainment , a lodging-place : Ac 28:23 , and so perh. Phm 22 (Lft., in l., Phl. , p. 9; but v. supr., and cf. MM, xvii).†
G3578.strongs: from G3581 (ξένος); hospitality, i.e. (by implication) a place of entertainment

G5479.thayer: joy, gladness, a source of joy.
G5479.mounce: joy, gladness, rejoicing, Mt. 2:10; 13:20, 44; 28:8; meton, joy, cause of joy, occasion of rejoicing, Lk. 2:10; Phil. 4:1; 1 Thess. 2:19, 20; bliss, Mt. 25:21, 23
G5479.abbottSmith: joy, delight : Lk 1:14 15:7 , 10 , Jo 15:11 16:22 , 24 17:13 , Ac 8:8 , II Co 1:24 7:13 8:2 , Ga 5:22 , Col 1:11 , Phl 2:2 , I Jo 1:4 , II Jo 12 ; opp. to κατήφεια , Ja 4:9 ; to λύπη , Jo 16:20 ; χ. τῆς πίστεως , Phl 1:25 ; ἀγαλλιᾶσθαι ( χαίρειν , q.v.), χαρᾷ , Jo 3:29 , I Pe 1:8 ; χ. ἔχειν , Phm 7 ; πληροῦν ( -οῦσθαι ) χαρᾶς , Ac 13:52 , Ro 15:13 , II Ti 1:4 ; ποιεῖν χ. μεγάλην , Ac 15:3 ; ἀπὸ τῆ
G5479.strongs: from G5463 (χαίρω); cheerfulness, i.e. calm delight
G5479.kjv: gladness, X greatly, (X be exceeding) joy(-ful, -fully, -fulness, -ous)

G640.abbottSmith: perplexity : Lk 21:25 (MM, s.v.; on the construction, v. Field, Notes , 74 f.).†
G640.strongs: from the same as G639 (ἀπορέω); a (state of) quandary

G3151.mounce: a vain talker, given to vain talking or trivial disputation, Tit. 1:10*
G3151.strongs: from G3152 (μάταιος) and G3004 (λέγω); an idle (i.e. senseless or mischievous) talker, i.e. a wrangler

G5106.thayer: indeed now, therefore, accordingly, well then.
G5106.abbottSmith: accordingly, therefore : as in cl., after the first word in a sentence, I Co 9:26 ; acc to later usage, at the beginning, Lk 20:25 , He 13:13 .†
G5106.strongs: from G5104 (τοί) and G3568 (νῦν); truly now, i.e. accordingly

G4276.thayer: I hope before, am the first to hope.
G4276.mounce: to have hope and confidence in a person or thing beforehand, Eph. 1:12*
G4276.abbottSmith: to hope before : seq. ἐν , Eph 1:12 .†
G4276.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1679 (ἐλπίζω); to hope in advance of other confirmation

G636.mounce: to depart by ship, sail away, Acts 13:4; 14:26; 20:15; 27:1*
G636.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4126 (πλέω); to set sail

G5465.thayer: I let down, lower, slacken, loosen.
G5465.mounce: to slacken; to let down, lower, Mk. 2:4; Lk. 5:4, 5; Acts 9:25; 27:17, 30; 1 Cor. 11:33*
G5465.strongs: from the base of G5490 (χάσμα); to lower (as into a void)

G3928.thayer: I pass by, pass away, pass out of sight; I am rendered void, become vain, neglect, disregard.
G3928.mounce: to pass beside, pass along, pass by, Mt. 8:28; Mk. 6:48; to pass, elapse, as time, Mt. 14:15; Acts 27:19; to pass away, be removed, Mt. 26:39, 42; Mk. 14:35; met. to pass away, disappear, vanish, perish, Mt. 5:18; 24:34, 35; to become vain, be rendered void, Mt. 5:18; Mk. 13:31; trans. to pass by, disregard, neglect, Lk. 11:42; 15:29; to come to the side of, come to, Lk. 12:37; 17:7
G3928.abbottSmith: to pass , pass by ; of persons: absol., Lk 18:37 ; c. acc. pers., Mk 6:48 ; c. acc. lot., Ac 16:8 ; seq. διά , c. gen., Mt 8:28 ; of things: τ. ποτήριον , Mt 26:39 ( ἀπ’ ἐμοῦ ), Mt 26:42 ; of time, Mt 14:15 , Mk 14:35 , Ac 27:9 , I Pe 4:3 . Metaph., to pass away , perish : Mt 5:18 24:34 , 35 , Mk 13:30 , 31 , Lk 16:17 21:32 , 33 , II Co 5:17 , Ja 1:10 , II Pe 3:10 ; to pass by , neglect , disregar
G3928.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to come near or aside, i.e. to approach (arrive), go by (or away), (figuratively) perish or neglect, (causative) avert
G3928.kjv: come (forth), go, pass (away, by, over), past, transgress

G1236.thayer: (either trans. or intrans.), I spend time, pass time, live.
G1236.mounce: to conduct or carry through or over; to pass or spend time, live, 1 Tim. 2:2; Tit. 3:3*
G1236.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G71 (ἄγω); to pass time or life

G833.thayer: court-yard, fore-court, sheep-fold; but it may be understood as: palace, house.
G833.mounce: pr. an unroofed enclosure; court-yard; sheepfold, Jn. 10:1, 16; an exterior court, i.q. προαύλιον, an enclosed place between the door and the street, Rev. 11:2; an interior court, quadrangle, the open court in the middle of Oriental houses, which are commonly built in the form of a square enclosing this court, Mt. 26:58, 69; by synec. a house, mansion, palace, Mt. 26:3; Lk. 11:21
G833.abbottSmith: in Hom., an open courtyard before a house, hence, an enclosure in the open , a sheepfold : Jo 10:1 , 16 .
G833.strongs: from the same as G109 (ἀήρ); a yard (as open to the wind); by implication, a mansion

G4925.mounce: to build in company with someone; pass. to be built up, form a constituent part of a structure, Eph. 2:22*
G4925.abbottSmith: to build together or with (c. dat. pers., I Es, l.c. ).
G4925.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3618 (οἰκοδομέω); to construct, i.e. (passively) to compose (in company with other Christians, figuratively)

G3806.mounce: suffering; an affection, passion, especially sexual, Rom. 1:26
G3806.abbottSmith: that which befalls one , that which one suffers .
G3806.strongs: from the alternate of G3958 (πάσχω); properly, suffering ("pathos"), i.e. (subjectively) a passion (especially concupiscence)

G1777.thayer: involved in, held in, hence: liable, generally with dat. (or gen.) of the punishment.
G1777.mounce: held in or by; subjected to, Heb. 2:15; subject to, liable to, guilty, deserving, Mt. 5:21, 22; 26:66; Mk. 3:29; 14:64; an offender against, 1 Cor. 11:27; Jas. 2:10*
G1777.abbottSmith: held in, bound by : c. gen. (cl. c. dat.), δουλείας , He 2:15 .
G1777.strongs: from G1758 (ἐνέχω); liable to (a condition, penalty or imputation)
G1777.kjv: in danger of, guilty of, subject to

G3451.thayer: skilled in music; subst: a musician, singer.
G3451.mounce: pr. devoted to the arts of the Muses; a musician; in NT, perhaps, a singer, Rev. 18:22*
G3451.abbottSmith: skilled in the arts , esp. in music ; as subst., ὁ μ. , a minstrel , musician : Re 18:22 .†
G3451.strongs: from (a Muse); "musical", i.e. (as noun) a minstrel

G3735.abbottSmith: a mountain : Mt 4:8 17:1 , Mk 9:2 , Jo 4:20 , II Pe 1:18 , al.; opp. to βουνός , Lk 3:5 (LXX) ; ὄ. τ. ἐλαιῶν , Mt 21:1 24:3 , al. (v.s. ἐ. ); ὄ. Σιών ( Σ. , prob. in gem appos.), He 12:22 , Re 14:1 ; ὄ. Σινά , Ac 7:30 , 38 , Ga 4:24 , 25 ; τὸ ὄ. , of the hill district as distinct from the lowlands, esp. the hills above the Sea of Galilee, Mt 5:1 8:1 , Mk 3:13 6:46 , al.; τὰ ὄ. , Mt 18:12 , Mk 5:5
G3735.strongs: probably from an obsolete (to rise or "rear"; perhaps akin to G142 (αἴρω); compare G3733 (ὄρνις)); a mountain (as lifting itself above the plain)

G2556.thayer: bad, evil, in the widest sense.
G2556.mounce: bad, of a bad quality or disposition, worthless, corrupt, depraved, Mt. 21:41; 24:48; Mk. 7:21; wicked, criminal, morally bad; τὸ κακόν, evil, wickedness, crime, Mt. 27:23; Acts 23:9; deceitful, 1 Pet. 3:10; mischievous, harmful, destructive; τὸ κακόν, evil mischief, harm, injury, Tit. 1:12; afflictive; τὸ κακόν, evil, misery, affliction, suffering, Lk. 16;25
G2556.abbottSmith: in general, opp. to ἀγαθός , καλός , in various senses, bad , mean , base , worthless (cl.).
G2556.strongs: apparently a primary word; worthless (intrinsically, such; whereas G4190 (πονηρός) properly refers to effects), i.e. (subjectively) depraved, or (objectively) injurious
G2556.kjv: bad, evil, harm, ill, noisome, wicked

G1147.mounce: a finger, Mt. 23:4; Mk. 7:33; from Hebrew, power, Lk. 11:20

G2681.thayer: I encamp, take up my quarters, tabernacle, pitch my tent, dwell.
G2681.mounce: to pitch one’s tent; in NT to rest in a place, settle, abide, Acts 2:26; to haunt, roost, Mt. 13:32; Mk. 4:32; Lk. 13:19*
G2681.abbottSmith: to pitch one's tent , lodge , dwell : seq. ἐν , Mt 13:32 , Lk 13:19 ; ὑπό , c. acc., Mk 4:32 ; ἐπ’ ἐλπιδι , Ac 2:26 (LXX) .†
G2681.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4637 (σκηνόω); to camp down, i.e. haunt; figuratively, to remain

G4686.thayer: a cohort, the tenth part of a legion; a military guard.
G4686.mounce: anything twisted or wreathed, a cord, coil, band, etc.; a band of soldiers, company, troop; used for a Roman cohort, about 600 soldiers, Mt. 27:27; Acts 10:1; the temple guard, Mk. 15:16; Jn. 18:3, 12; Acts 21:31; 27:1*
G4686.abbottSmith: (= Lat. spira ) anything or rolled up, a coil .
G4686.strongs: of immediate Latin origin, but ultimately a derivative of G138 (αἱρέομαι) in the sense of its cognate G1507 (εἱλίσσω); a coil (spira, "spire"), i.e. (figuratively) a mass of men (a Roman military cohort; also (by analogy) a squad of Levitical janitors)

G2597.thayer: I go down, come down, either from the sky or from higher land, descend.
G2597.mounce: to come or go down, descend, Mt. 8:1; 17:9; to lead down, Acts 8:26; to come down, fall, Mt. 7:25, 27; to be let down, Acts 10:11; 11:5
G2597.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to descend (literally or figuratively)

G5560.thayer: lame, deprived of a foot, limping.
G5560.mounce: crippled in the feet, limping, halting, lame, Mt. 11:5; 15:30, 31; met. limping, weak, spiritually, Heb. 12:13; maimed, deprived of a foot, for ἀναπηρός, Mk. 9:45
G5560.strongs: apparently a primary word; "halt", i.e. limping

G1351.mounce: pr. saying the same thing twice; in NT double-tongued, speaking one thing and meaning another, deceitful in words, 1 Tim. 3:8*
G1351.abbottSmith: in sense of διλογεῖν, -ία (Xen.), given to repetition .
G1351.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G3056 (λόγος); equivocal, i.e. telling a different story

G2735.thayer: anything happily and successfully accomplished, a beneficial and worthy deed.
G2735.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G3717 (ὀρθός) (compare G1357 (διόρθωσις)); something made fully upright, i.e. (figuratively) rectification (specially, good public administration)

G2408.abbottSmith: Jeremiah the prophet: Mt 2:17 16:14 27:9 (a ref. to Za 11:13 ).†
G2408.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03414); Hieremias (i.e. Jermijah), an Israelite

G1970.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4155 (πνίγω); to throttle upon, i.e. (figuratively) overgrow

G5085.thayer: Tiberias, a town in Galilee on the western border of the sea called after it.
G5085.mounce: Tiberias, a city of Galilee, built by Herod Antipas, and named in honor of Tiberius, Jn. 6:1, 23; 21:1*
G5085.abbottSmith: Tiberias , a city of Galilee: Jo 6:23 ; θάλασσα τῆς , T., Jo 6:1 21:1 (cf Γεννησσαρέτ, Γαλιλαία ).†
G5085.strongs: from G5086 (Τιβέριος); Tiberias, the name of a town and a lake in Palestine

G3187.strongs: irregular comparative of G3173 (μέγας); larger (literally or figuratively, specially, in age)

G4527.thayer: Sala, the name of two of the ancestors of Jesus.
G4527.abbottSmith: Sala, Shalah : Lk 3:32 (R, txt., Σαλμών , q.v.), ib. 35 .†
G4527.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07974); Sala (i.e. Shelach), a patriarch

G319.thayer: I recognize; pass: I am made known, cause myself to be recognized.
G319.mounce: to recognize; pass. to be made known, or to cause one’s self to be recognized, Acts 7:13*
G319.strongs: middle voice from G303 (ἀνά) and G1107 (γνωρίζω); to make (oneself) known

G1718.thayer: I make visible (manifest); hence: I report (inform) against; pass: I appear before.
G1718.mounce: to cause to appear clearly; to communicate, report, Acts 23:15, 22; to bring charges against, Acts 24:1; 25:2, 15, to manifest, intimate plainly, Heb. 11:14; to reveal, make known, Jn. 14:21, 22; pass. to appear, be visible, Mt. 27:53; to present one’s self, Heb. 9:24*
G1718.abbottSmith: to manifest, exhibit : ἐαυτόν , c. dat pers., Jo 14:21 , 22 ( DCG , ii, 112b). Pass. and mid., to show oneself, appear : Mt 27:53 , He 9:24 (cf. MM, Exp. , xii).
G1718.strongs: from G1717 (ἐμφανής); to exhibit (in person) or disclose (by words)
G1718.kjv: appear, declare (plainly), inform, (will) manifest, shew, signify

G475.mounce: to set opposite; mid. (only in the NT) to be of an opposite opinion, to be adverse; opponent, 2 Tim. 2:25*
G475.abbottSmith: in mid, to place oneself in opposition , oppose : II Ti 2:25 (EV; but v. Field, Notes , 215 f.; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.).
G475.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G1303 (διατίθεμαι); to set oneself opposite, i.e. be disputatious

G4313.thayer: I precede, pass on before, go before.
G4313.mounce: to precede, go before, Acts 7:40; Lk. 1:76*
G4313.abbottSmith: to make to go before . Pass. and mid., to go before : Lk 1:76 , Ac 7:40 (LXX) .†
G4313.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to precede (as guide or herald)

G611.thayer: I answer, reply, take up the conversation.
G611.mounce: to answer, Mt. 3:15; in NT to respond to certain present circumstances, to avow, Mt. 11:25
G611.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and ; to conclude for oneself, i.e. (by implication) to respond; by Hebraism (compare H06030) to begin to speak (where an address is expected)

G1054.thayer: Galatic, belonging to the province Galatia.
G1054.strongs: from G1053 (Γαλατία); Galatic or relating to Galatia

G4898.mounce: one who accompanies another to foreign countries, fellow traveller, Acts 19:29; 2 Cor. 8:19*
G4898.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1553 (ἐκδημέω); a co-absentee from home, i.e. fellow-traveller

G2449.thayer: Judea, a Roman province, capital Jerusalem.
G2449.mounce: Judea, the southern party of the country, below Samaria, Mt. 2:1, 5, 22; 3:1; meton. the inhabitants of Judea
G2449.strongs: feminine of G2453 (Ἰουδαῖος) (with G1093 (γῆ) implied); the Judæan land (i.e. Judæa), a region of Palestine

G2247.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a stud, i.e. spike

G827.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a ray of light, i.e. (by implication) radiance, dawn

G2795.thayer: I set in motion, move, remove, excite, stir up.
G2795.mounce: to move, Mt. 23:4; to excite, agitate, Acts 21:30; 24:5; to remove, Rev. 2:5; 6:14; in NT κεφαλήν, to shake the head in derision, Mt. 27:39; Mk. 15:29; mid., to move, possess the faculty of motion, exercise the functions of life, Acts 17:28*
G2795.abbottSmith: to set in motion , move : Mt 23:4 ; τ. κεφαλήν ( Ps 21 (22):8 , al.), Mt 27:39 , Mk 15:29 ; pass. (cf. Ge 7:21 ), Ac 17:28 .
G2795.strongs: from (poetic for , to go); to stir (transitively), literally or figuratively

G5027.mounce: burial, the act of burying, burial place, Mt. 27:7*
G5027.abbottSmith: burial : Mt 27:7 (Hdt., al.; for other meanings, v. LS, s.v., Deiss., BS , 355 f., MM xxiv).†

G5039.mounce: a sign, indubitable token, clear proof, Acts 1:3*
G5039.abbottSmith: a sure sign, a positive proof : Ac 1:3 (for exx., v. MM, xxiv).†
G5039.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a goal or fixed limit); a token (as defining a fact), i.e. criterion of certainty

G5342.thayer: I carry, bear, bring; I conduct, lead; perhaps: I make publicly known.
G5342.mounce: to bear, carry, Mk. 2:3; to bring, Mt. 14:11, 18; to conduct, Mt. 17:17; Jn. 21:18; to bear, endure, Rom. 9:22; Heb. 12:20; 13:13; to uphold, maintain, conserve, Heb. 1:3; to bear, bring forth, produce, Mk. 4:8; Jn. 12:24; 15:2; to bring forward, advance, allege, Jn. 18:29; Acts 25:7; 2 Pet. 2:11; to offer, ascribe, Rev. 21:24, 26; absol. used of a gate, to lead, Acts 12:10; pass. to be brought within reach, offered, 1 Pet. 1:13; to be brought in, to enter, Heb. 9:16; to be under a moving influence, to be moved, 2 Pet. 1:21; mid. to rush, sweep, Acts 2:2; to proceed, come forth, have utterance, 2 Pet. 1:17, 18, 21; to proceed, make progress, Heb. 6:1; used of a ship, to drive before the wind, Acts 27:15, 17
G5342.strongs: a primary verb (for which other, and apparently not cognate ones are used in certain tenses only; namely, ; and ; to "bear" or carry (in a very wide application, literally and figuratively, as follows)
G5342.kjv: be, bear, bring (forth), carry, come, + let her drive, be driven, endure, go on, lay, lead, move, reach, rushing, uphold

G4855.thayer: I grow at the same time.
G4855.mounce: to make to grow together; pass. to grow or spring up with, Lk. 8:7*
G4855.abbottSmith: to cause to grow together . Pass., to grow together or with (Plat. al.): Lk 8:7 .†
G4855.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5453 (φύω); passive, to grow jointly

G877.thayer: want of sense, foolishness, impiety, wickedness.
G877.mounce: inconsiderateness, folly; boastful folly, 2 Cor. 11:1, 17, 21; in NT foolishness, levity, wickedness, impiety, Mk. 7:22*
G877.strongs: from G878 (ἄφρων); senselessness, i.e. (euphemistically) egotism; (morally) recklessness

G2063.abbottSmith: red : ἡ ἐ. θάλασσα , the Red Sea , Ac 7:36 , He 11:29 .†
G2063.strongs: of uncertain affinity; red, i.e. (with G2281 (θάλασσα)) the Red Sea

G235.mounce: but; however; but still more; ἀλλάγε, at all events; ἀλλή, unless, except. Ἀλλά also serves to introduce a sentence with keenness and emphasis, Jn. 16:2; Rom. 6:5; 7:7; Phil. 3:8
G235.abbottSmith: stronger than δέ ; prop. neuter pl. of ἄλλος , used adverbially, with changed accent; hence prop. otherwise , on the other hand (cf. Ro 3:31 ); opposing a previous negation, but : οὐ (μὴ) . . . ἀ. , Mt 5:15 , 17 Mk 5:39 , Jo 7:16 , al.; rhetorically subordinating but not entirely negativing what precedes, οὐ . . . ἀ. , not so much . . . as , Mk 9:37 , Mt 10:20 , Jo 12:44 , al.; with ellipse of the
G235.strongs: neuter plural of G243 (ἄλλος); properly, other things, i.e. (adverbially) contrariwise (in many relations)
G235.kjv: and, but (even), howbeit, indeed, nay, nevertheless, no, notwithstanding, save, therefore, yea, yet

G3175.thayer: a great one, a lord; a courtier, satrap, nobleman.
G3175.mounce: great men, lords, chiefs, nobles, princes, Mk. 6:21; Rev. 6:15; 18:23*
G3175.abbottSmith: the chief men , nobles (Manetho, FlJ, al.): Mk 6:21 , Re 6:15 18:23 .†

G4315.mounce: the day before the sabbath, sabbath-eve, Mk. 15:42*
G4315.abbottSmith: the day before the Sabbath : Mk 15:42 (L, Tr., txt., πρὸς σ. ).†
G4315.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G4521 (σάββατον); a fore-sabbath, i.e. the Sabbath-eve

G3588.mounce: the prepositive article, answering, to a considerable extent, to the English definite article; but, for the principle and facts of its usage, consult a grammar; ὁ μὲν ... ὁ δέ, the one ... the other, Phil. 1:16, 17; Heb. 7:5, 6, 20, 21, 23, 24; pl. some ... others, Mt. 13:23; 22:5, 6; ὁ δέ, but he, Mt. 4:4; 12:48; οἱ δέ, but others, Mt. 28:17; used, in a poetic quotation, for a personal pronoun, Acts 17:28
G3588.abbottSmith: As demonstr. pron. As freq. in Hom., absol., he ( she , it ), his (etc.): Ac 17:28 (quoted from the poet Aratus). Distributive, ὁ μὲν . . . ὁ δέ , the one . . . the other : I Co 7:7 , Ga 4:22 ; pl., Ac 14:4 , 17:32 , Phl 1:16 , al.; οἱ μὲν . . . ἄλλοι δέ , Mt 16:14 , Jo 7:12 ; οἱ μεν̀ . . . ὁ δέ , He 7:21 , 23 . In narration (without ὁ μὲν preceding), ὁ δέ , but he : Mt 2:14 , Mk 1:45 , Lk 8:21 ,
G3588.strongs: the definite article; the (sometimes to be supplied, at others omitted, in English idiom)
G3588.kjv: the, this, that, one, he, she, it, etc

G2654.thayer: I use up, spend, consume (as with fire).
G2654.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G355 (ἀναλίσκω); to consume utterly

G2915.abbottSmith: barley : pl. (as usually in cl.), Re 6:6 .†

G3823.thayer: wrestling, a wrestling bout; hence: a struggle, fight, conflict, contest.
G3823.abbottSmith: wrestling , hence, generally, fight , contest: fig., of the spiritual combat of Christians, Eph 6:12 .†
G3823.strongs: from (to vibrate; another form for G906 (βάλλω)); wrestling

G4503.thayer: Ruth, wife of Boaz and mother of Obed.
G4503.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07327); Ruth, a Moabitess

G3547.thayer: a teacher and interpreter of the Mosaic Law.
G3547.mounce: a teacher and interpreter of the Mosaic law, Lk. 5:17; Acts 5:34; 1 Tim. 1:7
G3547.abbottSmith: a teacher of the law : Lk 5:17 , Ac 5:34 , I Ti 1:7 (NT and eccl. only; cf. νομοδείκτης , -διδάκτης , Plut.).†
G3547.strongs: from G3551 (νόμος) and G1320 (διδάσκαλος); an expounder of the (Jewish) law, i.e. a Rabbi

G4573.thayer: I worship, stand in awe of.
G4573.mounce: to feel dread of a thing; to venerate, adore, worship, Rom. 1:25*
G4573.strongs: middle voice from a derivative of G4576 (σέβομαι); to venerate, i.e. adore

G402.thayer: I return, retire, withdraw, depart (underlying idea perhaps of taking refuge from danger or of going into retirement).
G402.mounce: to go backward; to depart, go away, Mt. 2:12; to withdraw, retire, Mt. 9:24; Acts 23:19; 26:31
G402.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5562 (χωρέω); to retire
G402.kjv: depart, give place, go (turn) aside, withdraw self

G3851.thayer: I expose myself to danger; I am adventuresome, reckless.
G3851.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the middle voice of G1011 (βουλεύω); to misconsult, i.e. disregard

G4008.thayer: over, on the other side, beyond.
G4008.mounce: can function as an improper prep., across, beyond, over, on the other side, Mt. 4:15, 25; 19:1; Jn. 6:1, 17; ὁ, ἡ, τό, πέραν, farther, on the farther side, and τὸ πέραν, the farther side, the other side, Mt. 8:18, 28; 14:22
G4008.abbottSmith: on the other side , across (usually with the idea of water lying between); as in the older poets, as prep. c. gen.: τ. θαλάσσης , Jo 6:1 , 17 , 22 , 25 ; τ. Ἰορδάνου , Mt 4:15 (LXX) , 25 19:1 , Mk 3:8 10:1 , Jo 1:28 3:26 10:40 ; τ. χειμάρρου τ. Κέδρων , Jo 18:1 ; τὸ π. , the region beyond , the other side : Mt 8:18 , 28 14:22 16:5 , Mk 4:35 5:21 6:45 8:13 ; τ. θαλάσσης , Mk 5:1 ; τ. λίμνης , Lk 8:
G4008.strongs: apparently accusative case of an obsolete derivative of (to "pierce"); through (as adverb or preposition), i.e. across

G3109.thayer: (Hebrew), Macedonia, a Roman province north of Achaia (Greece).
G3109.mounce: Macedonia, Acts 16:9; Rom. 15:26; 1 Cor. 16:5; 1 Thess. 1:7; 1 Tim. 1:3
G3109.abbottSmith: Macedonia : Ac 16:9 , 10 , I Co 16:5 , II Co 1:16 , Phl 4:15 , al.; M. καὶ Ἀχαία , Ac 19:21 , Ro 15:26 , I Th 1:7 , 8 .
G3109.strongs: from G3110 (Μακεδών); Macedonia, a region of Greece

G4653.mounce: darkness, Jn. 6:17; 20:1; privacy, Mt. 10:27; Lk. 12:3; met. moral or spiritual darkness, Jn. 1:5 (2x); 8:12; 12:35, 46; 1 Jn. 1:5; 2:8, 9, 11*
G4653.strongs: from G4655 (σκότος); dimness, obscurity (literally or figuratively)

G700.thayer: I please, with the idea of willing service rendered to others; hence almost: I serve.
G700.mounce: to please, Mt. 14:6; to be pleasing, acceptable, Acts 6:5; to consult the pleasure of any one, Rom. 15:1, 2, 3; 1 Cor. 10:33; to seek favor with, Gal. 1:10; 1 Thess. 2:4
G700.abbottSmith: to please (Hom., Hdt., al.): c. dat. pers., Mt 14:6 , Mk 6:22 , Ro 8:8 15:2 , I Th 2:15 4:1 , I Co 7:32 , 33 , 34 , Ga 1:10 , II Ti 2:4 ; seq. ἐνώπιον (= Heb. בּעֵנֵי , Bl., § 37, 1; 40, 7), Ac 6:5 .
G700.strongs: probably from G142 (αἴρω) (through the idea of exciting emotion); to be agreeable (or by implication, to seek to be so)

G5415.abbottSmith: (Lat.) Fortunatus : I Co 16:17 (v. Lft., Cl. Ro., 1Co. , 59).†
G5415.strongs: of Latin origin; "fortunate"; Fortunatus, a Christian

G5544.mounce: pr. goodness, kindness, gentleness, Rom. 2:4; 11:22(3x); 2 Cor. 6:6; Gal. 5:22; Col. 3:12; Tit. 3:4; kindness shown, beneficence, Eph. 2:7; goodness, virtue, Rom. 3:12*
G5544.abbottSmith: goodness, excellence, uprightness : Ro 3:12 (LXX) . goodness of heart, kindness : Ro 2:4 , II Co 6:6 , Ga 5:22 , Col 3:12 , Tit 3:4 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc. pers., Eph 2:7 ; id., opp. to ἀποτομία , Ro 11:22 .
G5544.strongs: from G5543 (χρηστός); usefulness, i.e. morally, excellence (in character or demeanor)

G2293.thayer: I am of good courage, good cheer, am bold.
G2293.mounce: to be of good courage, be of good cheer, Mt. 9:2; to be confident, hopeful; to be bold, maintain a bold bearing, Mt. 9:22; 14:27; Mk. 6:50; 10:49; Jn. 16:33; Acts 23:11*
G2293.abbottSmith: to be of good courage : imperat., θάραει, -εῖτε , Mt 9:2 , 22 14:27 , Mk 6:50 10:49 , Jo 16:33 , Ac 23:11 .†

G968.thayer: an elevated place ascended by steps, a throne, tribunal.
G968.mounce: a step, footstep, foot-breadth, space to set the foot on, Acts 7:5; an elevated place ascended by steps, tribunal, throne, Mt. 27:19; Acts 12:21
G968.strongs: from the base of G939 (βάσις); a step, i.e. foot-breath; by implication, a rostrum, i.e. a tribunal

G3058.thayer: I revile a person to his face, abuse insultingly.
G3058.mounce: to revile, rail at, abuse, Jn. 9:28; Acts 23:4; 1 Cor. 4:12; 1 Pet. 2:23*
G3058.abbottSmith: to abuse , revile : c. acc. pers., Jo 9:28 , Ac 23:4 ; pass., I Co 4:12 , I Pe 2:23 (cf. ἀντι-λοιδορέω ).†
G3058.strongs: from G3060 (λοίδορος); to reproach, i.e. vilify

G3505.strongs: of Latin origin; Neron (i.e. Nero), a Roman emperor

G1518.mounce: a peace-maker, one who cultivates peace and concord, Mt. 5:9*
G1518.strongs: from G1515 (εἰρήνη) and G4160 (ποιέω); pacificatory, i.e. (subjectively) peaceable

G1377.mounce: to put in rapid motion; to pursue; to follow, pursue the direction of, Lk. 17:23; to follow eagerly, endeavor earnestly to acquire, Rom. 9:30, 31; 12:13; to press forwards, Phil. 3:12, 14; to pursue with malignity, persecute, Mt. 5:10, 11, 12, 44
G1377.abbottSmith: to put to flight, drive away : Mt 23:34 .
G1377.strongs: a prolonged (and causative) form of a primary verb (to flee; compare the base of G1169 (δειλός) and G1249 (διάκονος)); to pursue (literally or figuratively); by implication, to persecute
G1377.kjv: ensue, follow (after), given to, (suffer) persecute(-ion), press forward

G4.thayer: not burdensome, bringing no weight or oppression upon.
G4.mounce: literally: weightless; figuratively: not burdensome, 2 Cor. 11:9*
G4.abbottSmith: without weight ; metaph. (MM, VGT , s.v.) not burdensome : II Co 11:9 .†
G4.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G922 (βάρος); weightless, i.e. (figuratively) not burdensome

G2651.abbottSmith: Rec. for κατὰ μόνας , v.s. μόνος .
G2651.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and accusative case plural feminine of G3441 (μόνος) (with G5561 (χώρα) implied); according to sole places, i.e. (adverbially) separately

G847.mounce: self, very; alone, Mk. 6:31; 2 Cor. 12:13; of one’s self, of one’s own motion, Jn. 16:27; used also in the oblique cases independently as a personal pron. of the third person; ὁ αὐτός, the same; unchangeable, Heb. 1:12; κατὰ τὸ αὐτό, at the same time, together, Acts 14:1; ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό, in one and the same place, Mt. 22:34; at the same time, together, Acts 3:1
G847.strongs: genitive (i.e. possessive) of G846 (αὐτός), used as an adverb of location; properly, belonging to the same spot, i.e. in this (or that) place

G2901.thayer: I strengthen, confirm; pass: I grow strong, become strong.
G2901.mounce: to strengthen, render strong, corroborate, confirm; pass. to grow strong, acquire strength, Lk. 1:80; 2:40; Eph. 3:16; to be firm, resolute, 1 Cor. 16:13*
G2901.abbottSmith: to strengthen ; pass., to wax strong : Lk 2:40 , I Co 16:13 ; πνεύματι , Lk 1:80 ; δυνάμει κ. διὰ τ. πνεύματος , Eph 3:16 .†
G2901.strongs: from G2900 (κραταιός); to empower, i.e. (passively) increase in vigor

G743.thayer: a ruler of angels, a superior angel, an archangel.
G743.mounce: an archangel, chief angel, 1 Thess. 4:16; Jude 9*
G743.abbottSmith: archangel , a chief angel : I Th 4:16 , Ju 9 (Cremer, 24; MM, s.v.).†
G743.strongs: from G757 (ἄρχω) and G32 (ἄγγελος); a chief angel

G932.thayer: kingship, sovereignty, authority, rule, especially of God, both in the world, and in the hearts of men; hence: kingdom, in the concrete sense.
G932.mounce: a kingdom, realm, the region or country governed by a king; kingly power, authority, dominion, reign; royal dignity, the title and honor of king; ἡ βασιλεία, Mt. 9:35, ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ or τοῦ Χριστοῦ or τοῦ οὐρανοῦ or τῶν οὐρανῶν, the reign or kingdom of the Messiah, both in a false and true conception of it; used also with various limitation, of its administration and coming history, as in the parables; its distinctive nature, Rom. 14:17; its requirements, privileges, rewards, consummation
G932.abbottSmith: prop. abstract, sovereignty , royal power , dominion : Lk 1:33 22:29 , Jo 18:36 , Ac 1:6 , He 1:8 , I Co 15:24 ; λαβεῖν β. , Lk 19:12 , 15 , Re 17:12 ; δοῦναι τὴν , Re 17:17 ; ἔχειν β. , Re 17:18 ; ἔρχεσθαι ἐν τ. ( εἰς τὴν ) β. , Mt 16:28 , Lk 23:42 ; β. τ. θεοῦ , Re 12:10 .
G932.strongs: from G935 (βασιλεύς); properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

G775.thayer: an Asiarch, an official connected with the worship of Rome and the Emperor in the Roman province Asia.
G775.mounce: an Asiarch, an officer in the province of Asia, as in other eastern provinces of the Roman empire, selected, with others, from the more opulent citizens, to preside over the things pertaining to religious worship, and to exhibit annual public games at their own expense in honor of the gods, in the manner of the aediles at Rome, Acts 19:31*
G775.abbottSmith: an Asiarch , one of ten officers elected by the various cities in the province of Asia whose duty it was to celebrate at their own charges the public games and festivals: Ac 19:31 (Strab., Inscr.; DB , s.v.).†
G775.strongs: from G773 (Ἀσία) and G746 (ἀρχή); an Asiarch or president of the public festivities in a city of Asia Minor

G861.mounce: incorruptibility, 1 Cor. 15:42, 53, 54; immortality, Rom. 2:7; 2 Tim. 1:10; soundness, purity; ἐν ἀφθαρσίᾳ, purely, sincerely or constantly, unfailingly, Eph. 6:24
G861.abbottSmith: incorruptibility , immortality : Ro 2:7 , I Co 15:42 , 50 , 53 , 54 , II Ti 1:10 ; ἀγαπώντων . . . ἐν ἀ. , Eph 6:24 (v. AR, in l.).†
G861.strongs: from G862 (ἄφθαρτος); incorruptibility; genitive, unending existence; (figuratively) genuineness

G4001.strongs: from G4002 (πέντε) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); five hundred

G704.strongs: perhaps the same as G730 (ἄῤῥην); a lamb (as a male)

G3804.thayer: (a) suffering, affliction, (b) passion, emotion, (c) an undergoing, an enduring.
G3804.mounce: what is suffered; suffering, affliction, Rom. 8:18; 2 Cor. 1:5, 6, 7; Phil. 3:10; emotion, passion, Rom. 7:5; Gal. 5:24
G3804.abbottSmith: that which befalls one , a suffering , affliction : pl., Ro 8:18 , II Co 1:6 , 7 , Col 1:24 , II Ti 3:11 , He 2:10 10:32 , I Pe 5:9 ; of Christ's sufferings: τὰ εἰς Χ. , I Pe 1:11 ; τ. Χριστσῦ , I Pe 5:1 ; id. as shared by Christians, II Co 1:5 , Phl 3:10 , I Pe 4:13 .
G3804.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3806 (πάθος); something undergone, i.e. hardship or pain; subjectively, an emotion or influence

G3758.mounce: expressive of insult and derision, Ah! Ah!, Mk. 15:29*

G3393.abbottSmith: a stain , defilement : pl., II Pe 2:20 .†

G804.thayer: (lit: unfailing), safe, reliable, trustworthy, certain, sure.
G804.mounce: pr. firm, secure from falling; firm, sure, steady, immovable, Heb. 6:19; met. certain, sure, Acts 21:34; 22:30; 25:26; safe, making secure, Phil. 3:1*
G804.abbottSmith: certain , secure , safe : Ac 21:34 22:30 25:26 , Phl 3:1 , He 6:19 (MM, s.v.).†
G804.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and sphallo (to "fail"); secure (literally or figuratively)

G964.thayer: Bethesda, name of a pool in Jerusalem.
G964.abbottSmith: Bethesda , a pool in Jerusalem: Jo 5:2 , Rec. ( Βηθζαθά , WH, Βηθσαϊδά , WH, mg.).†
G964.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01004 and H02617); house of kindness; Beth-esda, a pool in Jerusalem

G3577.mounce: the back of men or animals, Rom. 11:10*

G4120.thayer: I weave together, plait, twist, braid.
G4120.mounce: to interweave, weave, braid, plait, Mt. 27:29; Mk. 15:17; Jn. 19:2*
G4120.abbottSmith: to plait , twist , weave : Mt 27:29 , Mk 15:17 , Jo 19:2 .†
G4120.strongs: a primary word; to twine or braid

G3708.thayer: I see, look upon, experience, perceive, discern, beware.
G3708.mounce: pluperfect, ἑωράκειν, some list εἶδον as the second aorist of ὁράω, to see, behold, Mt. 2:2, et al. freq.; to look, Jn. 19:37; to visit, Jn. 16:22; Heb. 13:23; to mark, observe, Acts 8:23; Jas. 2:24; to be admitted to witness, Lk. 17:22; Jn. 3:36; Col. 2:18; with θεόν, to be admitted into the more immediate presence of God, Mt. 5:8; Heb. 12:14; to attain to a true knowledge of God, 3 Jn. 11; to see to a thing, Mt. 27:4; Acts 18:15; ὅρα, see, take care, Mt. 8:4; Heb. 8:5; pass. to appear, Lk. 1:11; Acts 2:3; to reveal one’s self, Acts 26:16; to present one’s self, Acts 7:26
G3708.abbottSmith: in "durative" sense (hence aor. act., εἶδον , pass., ὤφθην , fut., ὄψομαι , from different roots; v. M, Pr. , 110 f.), to see (in colloq. even the pres. is rare, its place being generally taken by βλέτω , θεωρέω , v. Bl., § 24). Of bodily vision, to see , perceive , behold : absol., Mk 6:38 , al.; ἔρχου καὶ ἴδε , Jo 1:46 , al.; seq. ὅτι , Mk 2:16 , al.; c. acc., Mt 2:2 , Mk 1:10 16:7 , Ga 1:19 , a
G3708.strongs: properly, to stare at (compare G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)), i.e. (by implication) to discern clearly (physically or mentally); by extension, to attend to; by Hebraism, to experience; passively, to appear

G4172.thayer: a city, the inhabitants of a city.
G4172.mounce: a city, an enclosed and walled town, Mt. 10:5, 11; 11:1; meton. the inhabitants of a city, Mt. 8:34; 10:15; with a gen. of person, or a personal pronoun, the city of any one, the city of one’s birth or residence, Mt. 9:1; Lk. 2:4, 11; ἡ πόλις, the city, κατ’ ἐξοχήν, Jerusalem, Mt. 21:18; 28:11; met. a place of permanent residence, abode, home, Heb. 11:10, 16; 13:14. The frequency count does not include its occurrence in the name Νέαν πόλιν in Acts 16:11
G4172.abbottSmith: a city : Mt 2:23 , Mk 1:45 , Lk 4:29 , Jo 4:8 , al. mult.; opp. to κῶμαι ( κ. καὶ ἀγροί ), Mt 9:35 10:11 , Mk 6:56 , Lk 8:1 13:22 ; c. nom. propr. in appos. (cl.), Ac 11:5 16:14 ; c. id. in gen. appos. (Bl., § 35, 5), Ac 8:5 , II Pe 2:6 ; gen., of the region, Lk 1:26 4:31 , Jo 4:5 ; of the inhabitants, Mt 10:5 , 23 , Lk 23:51 , Ac 19:35 , II Co 11:32 ; c. gen. pers., of one's residence or native p
G4172.strongs: probably from the same as G4171 (πόλεμος), or perhaps from G4183 (πολύς); a town (properly, with walls, of greater or less size)

G2094.abbottSmith: a year : Lk 3:1 , He 1:12 , Re 20:3 , al.; ἔτη ἔχειν , Jo 5:5 8:57 ; εἶναι, γίνεσθαι, ἐτῶν , Mk 5:42 , Lk 2:42 , Ac 4:22 , I Ti 5:9 ; dat. pl. of space of time, Jo 2:20 , Ac 13:20 ; acc, in ans. to how long? Mt 9:20 , Mk 5:25 , Lk 2:36 , Ac 7:6 , He 3:9 , al.; preceded by a prep.: ἀπό , Lk 8:43 , Ro 15:23 ; διά , c. gen. (v.s. διά ), Ac 24:17 , Ga 2:1 ; ἐκ , Ac 24:10 ; εἰς , Lk 12:19 ; ἐπί , c. ac
G2094.strongs: apparently a primary word; a year

G1108.mounce: knowledge, Lk. 1:77; knowledge of an especial kind and relatively high character, Lk. 11:52; Rom. 2:20; 1 Tim. 6:20; more particularly in respect of Christian enlightenment, Rom. 15:14; 1 Cor. 8:10; 12:8; 2 Cor. 11:6, et al.
G1108.abbottSmith: a seeking to know, inquiry, investigation . knowledge , in NT, specially of the kn. of spiritual truth: absol., Lk 11:52 , Ro 2:20 15:14 , I Co 1:5 (Lft., Notes , 147) 8:1 , 7 , 10 , 11 , 13:2 , 8 14:6 , II Co 6:6 8:7 11:6 , Eph 3:19 , Col 2:3 , I Pe 3:7 , II Pe 1:5 , 6 ; c. gen. obj., σωτηρίας , Lk 1:77 ; τ. δόξης τ. θεοῦ , II Co 4:6 ; τ. θεοῦ , II Co 2:14 10:5 ; Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ , Phl 3:8 (v. Deiss
G1108.strongs: from G1097 (γινώσκω); knowing (the act), i.e. (by implication) knowledge

G4374.thayer: (a) I bring to, (b) characteristically: I offer (of gifts, sacrifices, etc).
G4374.mounce: to bear or bring to, Mt. 4:24; 25:20; to bring to or before magistrates, Lk. 12:11; 23:14; to bring near to, apply to, Jn. 19:29; to offer, tender, proffer, as money, Acts 8:18; to offer, present, as gifts, oblations, etc., Mt. 2:11; 5:23; Heb. 5:7; to offer in sacrifice, Mk. 1:44; Lk. 5:14; to offer up any one as a sacrifice to God, Heb. 9:25, 28; 11:17; mid. to bear one’s self towards, behave or conduct one’s self towards, to deal with, treat any one, Heb. 12:7
G4374.abbottSmith: to bring to , lead to : c. acc. et dat. pers., Mt 4:24 8:16 9:2 , 32 14:35 17:16 , Mk 2:4 (WH, R, txt.; sc. αὐτόν ) 10:13 , Lk 18:15 23:14 ; pass., Mt 12:22 (act., WH, txt.) 18:24 ( προσήχθη , WH) 19:13 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 25:20 ; id. c. dat. pers., Mt 22:19 , Lk 23:36 ; τ. στόματι , Jo 19:29 ; metaph., c. dat pers., to deal with , He 12:7 (cl.).
G4374.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate); to bear towards, i.e. lead to, tender (especially to God), treat
G4374.kjv: bring (to, unto), deal with, do, offer (unto, up), present unto, put to

G3148.thayer: (a) a scourge, lash, of leathern thongs with pieces of metal sewn up in them, (b) met: severe pains (sufferings), disease.
G3148.mounce: a scourge, whip, Acts 22:24; Heb. 11:36; met. a scourge of disease, Mk. 3:10; 5:29, 34; Lk. 7:21*
G3148.abbottSmith: a whip , scourge : Ac 22:24 , He 11:36 ; metaph., of disease or suffering as a divine chastisement (cf. Ps 88 (89):33 , Pr 3:12 , II Mac 9:11 ), Mk 3:10 5:29 , 34 , Lk 7:21 .†
G3148.strongs: probably from the base of G3145 (μασσάομαι) (through the idea of contact); a whip (literally, the Roman flagellum for criminals; figuratively, a disease)

G853.thayer: I cause to disappear, hide, remove; I disfigure (probably by leaving unwashed for a long period), destroy.
G853.mounce: to remove out of sight, cause to disappear; pass. to disappear, vanish, Jas. 4:14; by impl. to destroy, consume, so that nothing shall be left visible, Mt. 6:19, 20; met. to spoil, deform, disfigure, Mt. 6:16; to perish, Acts 13:41*
G853.abbottSmith: to make unseen , hide from sight (Xen., al.).
G853.strongs: from G852 (ἀφανής); to render unapparent, i.e. (actively) consume (becloud), or (passively) disappear (be destroyed)

G1727.mounce: opposite to, over against, Mk. 15:39; contrary, as the wind, Mt. 14:24; Acts 26:9; 28:17; ὁ ἐξ ἐναντίας, an adverse party, enemy, Tit. 2:8; adverse, hostile, counter, 1 Thess. 2:15

G3714.abbottSmith: mountainous , hilly ; ἡ ὀ. (sc. χώρα ), the hill-country (LXX for הַר ): Lk 1:39 , 65 .†
G3714.strongs: from G3735 (ὄρος); mountainous, i.e. (feminine by implication, of G5561 (χώρα)) the Highlands (of Judæa)

G1095.mounce: to be or become old, Jn. 21:18; Heb. 8:13*

G4666.abbottSmith: myrrh , a resinous gum used as an unguent and for em­balming: Mt 2:11 , Jo 19:39 .†

G2167.strongs: from the same as G2165 (εὐφραίνω); joyfulness

G2235.thayer: already; now at length, now after all this waiting.
G2235.mounce: before now, now, already, Mt. 3:10; 5:28; ἤδη ποτέ, at length, Rom. 1:10; Phil. 4:10
G2235.abbottSmith: in NT, always of time; now , already : Mt 3:10 , Mk 4:37 , Lk 7:6 , Jo 4:36 , al.; νῦν . . . ἤ. , now already , I Jo 4:3 ; ἤ. ποτέ , now at length , c. fut., Ro 1:10 (cf. ἄρτι ).
G2235.strongs: apparently from G2228 (ἤ) (or possibly G2229 (ἦ)) and G1211 (δή); even now

G1849.thayer: (a) power, authority, weight, especially: moral authority, influence, (b) in a quasi-personal sense, derived from later Judaism, of a spiritual power, and hence of an earthly power.
G1849.mounce: power, ability, faculty, Mt. 9:8; 10:1; efficiency, energy, Lk. 4:32; liberty, licence, Jn. 10:18; Acts 5:4; authority, rule, dominion, jurisdiction, Mt. 8:9; 28:18; meton. pl. authorities, potentates, powers, Lk. 12:11; 1 Cor. 15:24; Eph. 1:21; right, authority, full power, Mt. 9:6; 21:23; privilege, prerogative, Jn. 1:12; perhaps, a veil, 1 Cor. 11:10
G1849.abbottSmith: prop., liberty or power to act , freedom to exercise the inward force or faculty expressed by δύναμις (q.v.): I Co 9:12 ; ἐ. ἔχειν , II Th 3:9 ; id. seq. inf., Jo 10:18 , I Co 9:4 , 5 ; c. gen. obj., Ro 9:21 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc., Re 22:14 ; περί , I Co 7:37 . Later (cf. Milligan, Th. , 114; MM, Exp. , xiv), of the power of right, authority : Mt 21:33 , Mk 11:28 , Lk 20:2 ; of Messianic authority,
G1849.strongs: from G1832 (ἔξεστι) (in the sense of ability); privilege, i.e. (subjectively) force, capacity, competency, freedom, or (objectively) mastery (concretely, magistrate, superhuman, potentate, token of control), delegated influence
G1849.kjv: authority, jurisdiction, liberty, power, right, strength

G2430.thayer: Iconium, a Phrygian city of the Roman province Galatia (modern Konia).
G2430.mounce: Iconium, a city of Lycaonia, in Asia Minor, Acts 13:51; 14:1, 19, 21; 16:2; 2 Tim. 3:11*. See PhotoGuide.
G2430.abbottSmith: Iconium , a city of the province of Galatia: Ac 13:51 14:1 , 19 , 21 16:2 , II Ti 3:11 .†
G2430.strongs: perhaps from G1504 (εἰκών); image-like; Iconium, a place in Asia Minor

G3892.mounce: violation of the law, transgression, 2 Pet. 2:16*
G3892.strongs: from the same as G3891 (παρανομέω); transgression

G2885.thayer: I put into order; I decorate, deck, adorn.
G2885.mounce: pluperfect, ἐκεκόσμητο (3 sg), to arrange, set in order; to adorn, decorate, embellish, Mt. 12:44; 23:29; to prepare, put in readiness, trim, Mt. 25:7; met. to honor, dignify, Tit. 2:10
G2885.abbottSmith: to order , arrange , prepare (in Hom. esp. of marshalling armies): Mt 25:7 (cf. Si 50:9 , al.).
G2885.strongs: from G2889 (κόσμος); to put in proper order, i.e. decorate (literally or figuratively); specially, to snuff (a wick)

G3499.thayer: lit. and met: I put to death, make as dead; I render weak, impotent.
G3499.mounce: pr. to put to death, kill; in NT met. to deaden, mortify, Col. 3:5; pass. to be rendered impotent, Rom. 4:19; Heb. 11:12*
G3499.abbottSmith: to make dead , put to death ; pass., to be dead : hyperbolically, of impotent age, He 11:12 ; σῶμα , Ro 4:19 . Trop., of carnal impulses, τὰ μέλη , Col 3:5 .†
G3499.strongs: from G3498 (νεκρός); to deaden, i.e. (figuratively) to subdue

G3405.thayer: (lit: repayment of price or payment of price due), reward, due punishment.
G3405.mounce: pr. the discharge of wages; requital; reward, Heb. 10:35; 11:26; punishment, Heb. 2:2*
G3405.abbottSmith: payment of wages , recompense ; meton., of reward: He 10:35 11:26 ; of punishment: He 2:2 .†

G2422.thayer: (Hebrew), Jephthah, one of the Judges of Israel.
G2422.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03316); Jephthaë (i.e. Jiphtach), an Israelite

G2310.thayer: (properly, an adj: belonging to the foundation), a foundation stone.
G2310.mounce: see θεμέλιον (G2528) a foundation, Lk. 6:48, 49; Heb. 11:10; met. a foundation laid in elementary instruction, Heb. 6:1; a foundation of a superstructure of faith, doctrine, or hope, 1 Cor. 3:10, 11, 12; Eph. 2:20; 1 Tim. 6:19; a foundation laid in the commencement of the preaching of the Gospel, Rom. 15:20*
G2310.abbottSmith: of or for a foundation ; as subst., ὁ θ. (sc. λίθος ), a foundation stone , foundation : Lk 6:48 , 49 14:29 (but v. Deiss., BS , 123); pl., οἰ θ. (cl.), He 11:10 , Re 21:14 , 19 ; neut., τὸ θ. (Arist., Phys. , vi, 6, 10, LXX and later writers), Ac 16:26 ; metaph., Ro 15:20 , I Co 3:10-12 , Eph 2:20 , I Ti 6:19 , II Ti 2:19 , He 6:1 .†
G2310.strongs: from a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); something put down, i.e. a substruction (of a building, etc.), (literally or figuratively)

G3768.strongs: from G3756 (οὐ) and G4452 (-πω); not yet
G3768.kjv: hitherto not, (no…) as yet, not yet

G4312.mounce: falling forwards; meton. precipitate, rash, Acts 19:36; 2 Tim. 3:4*
G4312.abbottSmith: falling forwards , headlong . Metaph., precipitate , rash , reckless : of persons, II Ti 3:4 ; of things, Ac 19:36 .†
G4312.strongs: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G4098 (πίπτω); falling forward, i.e. headlong (figuratively, precipitate)

G3712.thayer: a fathom, about five or six feet.
G3712.mounce: the space measured by the arms outstretched; a fathom, Acts 27:28 (2x)*
G3712.abbottSmith: the length of the outstretched arms, a fathom : Ac 27:28 .†
G3712.strongs: from G3713 (ὀρέγομαι); a stretch of the arms, i.e. a fathom

G4933.thayer: I preserve, keep safe, keep in mind, keep close.
G4933.mounce: to keep safe and sound, Mt. 9:17; to observe strictly, or, to secure from harm, protect, Mk. 6:20; to preserve in memory, keep carefully in mind, Lk. 2:19*
G4933.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5083 (τηρέω); to keep closely together, i.e. (by implication) to conserve (from ruin); mentally, to remember (and obey)

G2748.mounce: indecl. pr. name, Kidron, a valley near Jerusalem, Jn. 18:1*
G2748.abbottSmith: Cedron (OT, Kidron ): χείμαρρος τοῦ Κ. , Jo 18:1 (L, Tr., mg., R, txt.; cf. Bl., § 10, 5; Lft., Essays , 172 ff).†
G2748.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06939); Cedron (i.e. Kidron), a brook near Jerusalem

G1467.mounce: to possess the power of self-control or continence, 1 Cor. 7:9; to practise abstinence, 1 Cor. 9:25*
G1467.abbottSmith: to exercise self-control: I Co 7:9 ; c. acc., πάντα (v. Bl., 91), I Co 9:25 .†
G1467.strongs: middle voice from G1468 (ἐγκρατής); to exercise self-restraint (in diet and chastity)

G1902.thayer: I have on over (as a garment); mid: I put on myself in addition.
G1902.mounce: to put on over or in addition to; mid. to put on one’s self in addition; to be further invested, 2 Cor. 5:2, 4*
G1902.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1746 (ἐνδύω); to invest upon oneself

G488.thayer: I measure in return, give equivalent measure.
G488.abbottSmith: to measure in return : Lk 6:38 (WH, mg., μετρέω ).†
G488.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G3354 (μετρέω); to mete in return

G1870.thayer: I am ashamed, am ashamed of.
G1870.abbottSmith: to be ashamed ( of ): absol., II Ti 1:12 ; c. acc pers., Mk 8:38 , Lk 9:26 ; c. acc rei, Ro 1:16 , II Ti 1:8 , 16 ; ἐπί , c. dat. rei, Ro 6:21 ; c. inf., He 2:11 ; c, acc pers, et inf., He 11:16 ,†
G1870.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G153 (αἰσχύνομαι); to feel shame for something

G3761.thayer: neither, nor, not even, and not.
G3761.mounce: negative conj., neither, nor, and not, also not, Mt. 5:15; 6:15, 20, 26, 28; when single, not even Mt. 6:29; 8:10
G3761.abbottSmith: As conjc., and not , also not , neither , nor : Mt 6:15 , Lk 16:31 , Ro 4:15 , al.; οὐ . . . οὐδέ , Mt 5:15 10:24 , Mk 4:22 , Lk 6:43 , 44 , Jo 6:24 , Ac 2:27 , Ro 2:28 , al. (v. Bl., § 77, 10).
G3761.strongs: from G3756 (οὐ) and G1161 (δέ); not however, i.e. neither, nor, not even
G3761.kjv: neither (indeed), never, no (more, nor, not), nor (yet), (also, even, then) not (even, so much as), + nothing, so much as

G1736.thayer: I am at home, live in a place.
G1736.mounce: to dwell in a place, be at home, 2 Cor. 5:6, 8, 9*
G1736.abbottSmith: to live in a place, be at home : ἐν τ. σώματι , II Co 5:6 5:9 ; πρὸς τ. Κύριον , II Co 5:8 .†
G1736.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G1218 (δῆμος); to be in one's own country, i.e. home (figuratively)

G1450.abbottSmith: a surety : He 7:22 (exx. from π. , v. MM, Exp. , xi; cf. Cremer, 222).†
G1450.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (a limb); pledged (as if articulated by a member), i.e. a bondsman

G3445.mounce: to give shape to, mold, fashion, Gal. 4:19*
G3445.abbottSmith: to form : fig., Ga 4:19 (cf. μετα- , συμ-μορφόω ).†
G3445.strongs: from the same as G3444 (μορφή); to fashion (figuratively)
