G4151.mounce: wind, air in motion, Jn. 3:8; breath, 2 Thess. 2:8; the substance spirit, Jn. 3:6; a spirit, spiritual being, Jn. 4:24; Acts 23:8, 9; Heb. 1:14; a bodiless spirit, specter, Lk. 24:37; a foul spirit, δαιμόνιον, Mt. 8:16; Lk. 10:20; spirit, as a vital principle, Jn. 6:63; 1 Cor. 15:45; the human spirit, the soul, Mt. 26:41; 27:50; Acts 7:59; 1 Cor. 7:34; Jas. 2:26; the spirit as the seat of thought and feeling, the mind, Mk. 8:12; Acts 19:21; spirit, mental frame, 1 Cor. 4:21; 1 Pet. 3:4; a characteristic spirit, an influential principle, Lk. 9:55; 1 Cor. 2:12; 2 Tim. 1:7; a pervading influence, Rom. 11:8; spirit, frame of mind, as distinguished from outward circumstances and action, Mt. 5:3; spirit as distinguished from outward show and form. Jn. 4:23; spirit, a divinely bestowed spiritual frame, characteristic of true believers, Rom. 8:4; Jude 19; spirit, latent spiritual import, spiritual significance, as distinguished from the mere letter, Rom. 2:29; 7:6; 2 Cor. 3:6, 17; spirit, as a term for a process superior to a merely natural or carnal course of things, by the operation of the Divine Spirit, Rom. 8:4; Gal. 4:29; a spiritual dispensation, or a sealing energy of the Holy Spirit, Heb. 9:14; the Holy Spirit, Mt. 3:16; 12:31; Jn. 1:32, 33; a gift of the Holy Spirit, Jn. 7:39; Acts 19:2; 1 Cor. 14:12; an operation or influence of the Holy Spirit, 1 Cor. 12:3; a spiritual influence, an inspiration, Mt. 22:43; Lk. 2:27; Eph. 1:17; a professedly divine communication, or, a professed possessor of a spiritual communication, 1 Cor. 12:10; 2 Thess. 2:2; 1 Jn. 4:1, 2, 3
G4151.abbottSmith: of air in motion; wind : Jo 3:8 ; pl., He 1:7 (LXX) ; breath : π. ζωῆς , Re 11:11 ; π. τοῦ στόματος , fig., II Th 2:8 (cf. Ps 32 (33):6 ).
G4151.strongs: from G4154 (πνέω); a current of air, i.e. breath (blast) or a breeze; by analogy or figuratively, a spirit, i.e. (human) the rational soul, (by implication) vital principle, mental disposition, etc., or (superhuman) an angel, demon, or (divine) God, Christ's spirit, the Holy Spirit

G1119.mounce: the knee, Lk. 22:41; Heb. 12:12, et al.
G1119.abbottSmith: the knee : He 12:12 ; of a suppliant, προσπίπτειν τοῖς γ. , Lk 5:8 ; so also τιθέναι τὰ γ. , to kneel : Lk 22:41 , Ac 7:60 9:40 20:36 21:5 ; in mockery, Mk 15:19 ; κάμπτειν τὰ γ. , to bend the knee : Ro 11:4 (LXX) 14:11 (LXX) , Eph 3:14 , Phl 2:10 .†

G1203.mounce: a lord, master, especially of slaves, 1 Tim. 6:1, 2; 2 Tim. 2:21; Tit. 2:9; 1 Pet. 2:18; by impl. as denoting the possession of supreme authority, Lord, sovereign, used of God, Lk. 2:29; Acts 4:24; Rev. 6:10; and of Christ, 2 Pet. 2:1; Jude 4
G1203.abbottSmith: a master, lord , correlative of δοῦλος , οἰκέτης : I Ti 6:1 , 2 , II Ti 2:21 , Tit 2:9 , I Pe 2:18 ; as title of God, voc., δέσποτα (so usually in LXX), Lk 2:29 , Ac 4:24 ; ὁ δ. = voc. δέσποτα (cf. B1., § 33, 4), Re 6:10 ; of Christ, II Pe 2:1 , Ju 4 , R, txt. (but cf. mg.).†
G1203.strongs: perhaps from G1210 (δέω) and (a husband); an absolute ruler ("despot")

G3739.mounce: who, which, what, that, Mt. 1:16, 23, 25; in NT interrog. ἐφ’ ὅ, wherefore, why, Mt. 26:50; in NT ὅς μὲν ... ὅς δέ, for ὁ μὲν ... ὁ δέ, Mt. 21:35; 2 Cor. 2:16
G3739.abbottSmith: As demonstr. pron. = οὗτος , ὅδε , this , that , also for αὐτός , chiefly in nom.: ὅς δέ , but he (cf. ἦ δὲ ὅς , freq. in Plat.), Mk 15:23 , Jo 5:11 ; ὃς μὲν . . . ὃς δέ , the one . . . the other , Mt 21:35 22:5 25:15 , Lk 23:33 , Ac 27:14 , Ro 14:5 , I Co 11:21 , II Co 2:16 , Ju 22 ; neut., ὃ μὲν . . . ὃ δέ , the one . . . the other , some . . . some , Mt 13:8 , 23 , Ro 9:21 ; ὃς ( ὃ ) μὲν . . .
G3739.strongs: probably a primary word (or perhaps a form of the article G3588 (ὁ)); the relatively (sometimes demonstrative) pronoun, who, which, what, that
G3739.kjv: one, (an-, the) other, some, that, what, which, who(-m, -se), etc

G58.thayer: market-place, forum, public place of assembly.
G58.mounce: a place of public concourse, forum, market-place; things said in the market, provision
G58.strongs: from (to gather; probably akin to G1453 (ἐγείρω)); properly, the town-square (as a place of public resort); by implication, a market or thoroughfare

G1930.thayer: I set in order besides, put in order, correct.
G1930.mounce: to set further to rights, to carry on an amendment, correct, Tit. 1:5*
G1930.abbottSmith: to set in order further : Tit 1:5 (Inscr.; Cremer, 808).†
G1930.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G3717 (ὀρθός); to straighten further, i.e. (figuratively) arrange additionally

G4372.thayer: (originally: newly slaughtered, freshly killed), recent, new.
G4372.mounce: pr. recently killed; hence, genr. recent, new, newly or lately made, Heb. 10:20*
G4372.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G4969 (σφάζω); previously (recently) slain (fresh), i.e. (figuratively) lately made

G1495.mounce: idolatry, worship of idols, 1 Cor. 10:14; Gal. 5:20; Col. 3:5; 1 Pet. 4:3*
G1495.abbottSmith: idolatry : I Co 10:14 , Ga 5:20 , Col 3:5 ; pl. (Bl, §32, 6), I Pe 4:3 (Cremer, 390).†
G1495.strongs: from G1497 (εἴδωλον) and G2999 (λατρεία); image-worship (literally or figuratively)

G4671.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G4671.strongs: dative case of G4771 (σύ); to thee

G5074.abbottSmith: four-footed : of beasts, neut. pl., Ac 10:12 11:6 , Ro 1:23 .†
G5074.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G4228 (πούς); a quadruped

G1664.thayer: Eliud, son of Achim, and father of Eleazar.
G1664.mounce: Eliud, the father of Eleazar, Mt. 1:14, 15*
G1664.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0410 and H01935); God of majesty; Eliud, an Israelite

G399.thayer: (a) I carry up, lead up, (b) I offer up (on a high altar) as a sacrifice, offer up to God on high.
G399.mounce: to bear or carry upwards, lead up, Mt. 17:1; to offer sacrifices, Heb. 7:27; to bear aloft or sustain a burden, as sins, 1 Pet. 2:24; Heb. 9:28
G399.abbottSmith: to carry or lead up : c. acc. pers., Mt 17:1 , Mk 9:2 ; pass., Lk 24:51 (WH, rejects, R, mg. omits); ἀ. τ. ἁμαρτίας ἐπὶ τ. ξύλον (v. Deiss., BS , 88 f.; ICC , in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.): I Pe 2:24 .
G399.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5342 (φέρω); to take up (literally or figuratively)

G707.mounce: Arimathea, a town of Palestine, Mt. 27:57; Mk. 15:43; Lk. 23:51; Jn. 19:38*. See PhotoGuide.
G707.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07414); Arimathæa (or Ramah), a place in Palestine

G4962.mounce: to turn or roll together; to collect, gather, Acts 28:3; Mt. 17:22*
G4962.abbottSmith: to twist together : c. acc. rei, Ac 28:3 .
G4962.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4762 (στρέφω); to twist together, i.e. collect (a bundle, a crowd)

G2776.thayer: (a) the head, (b) met: a corner stone, uniting two walls; head, ruler, lord.
G2776.mounce: the head, Mt. 5:36; 6:17; the head, top; κεφαλὴ γωνίας, the head of the corner, the chief corner-stone, Mt. 21:42; Lk. 20:17; met. the head, superior, chief, principal, one to whom others are subordinate, 1 Cor. 11:3; Eph. 1:22
G2776.abbottSmith: the head : Mt 5:36 , Mk 6:24 , Re 1:14 9:7 , al. mult.; fig., ἄνθρακες ἐπὶ τὴν κ. , Ro 12:20 (LXX) ; αἷμα , Ac 18:6 ; metaph., of a husband, c. gen., I Co 11:3 , Eph 5:23 ; of Christ, I Co 11:3 , Eph 4:15 5:23 , Col 1:18 2:10 , 19 ; of things, κ. γωνίας , Mt 21:42 (LXX) .
G2776.strongs: from the primary (in the sense of seizing); the head (as the part most readily taken hold of), literally or figuratively

G3120.thayer: (a) soft, (b) of persons: soft, delicate, effeminate.
G3120.mounce: soft; soft to the touch, delicate, Mt. 11:8; Lk. 7:25; met. an instrument of unnatural lust, effeminate, 1 Cor. 6:9*
G3120.abbottSmith: soft ; prop., to the touch (opp. to σκληρός ): of clothing, pl., Mt 11:8 , Lk 7:25 . Of persons and their mode of living; mild , gentle ; soft , effeminate : I Co 6:9 (prob. in obscene sense, cf. Deiss., LAE , 150 4 ; MM, xvi; Zorell, s.v.).†
G3120.strongs: of uncertain affinity; soft, i.e. fine (clothing); figuratively, a catamite

G1361.abbottSmith: as proper name, Diotrephes : III Jo 9 .†
G1361.strongs: from the alternate of G2203 (Ζεύς) and G5142 (τρέφω); Jove-nourished; Diotrephes, an opponent of Christianity

G4585.strongs: probably of foreign origin; fine wheaten flour

G1892.mounce: to raise or stir up against, excite or instigate against, Acts 13:50; 14:2*
G1892.abbottSmith: to rouse up, excite : c. acc rei, διωγμόν , Ac 13:50 ; ψυχάς , Ac 14:2 .†
G1892.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1453 (ἐγείρω); to rouse upon, i.e. (figuratively) to excite against

G5499.thayer: done or made with hands, artificial.
G5499.mounce: made by hand, artificial, material, Mk. 14:58; Acts 7:48; 17:24; Eph. 2:11; Heb. 9:11, 24*
G5499.abbottSmith: made by hand (i.e. of human handiwork): of temples, Mk 14:58 , Ac 7:48 17:24 , He 9:11 , 24 ; of circumcision, Eph 2:11 .†
G5499.strongs: from G5495 (χείρ) and a derivative of G4160 (ποιέω); manufactured, i.e. of human construction

G251.abbottSmith: variant for ἅλας (q.v.): Mk 9:49 , Rec. WH, mg., R, mg.†

G2930.strongs: from a derivative of (frost); ice, i.e. (by analogy) rock "crystal"

G901.thayer: deep (lit. and met.); in the depths of the early morning, while still very early; profound.
G901.mounce: deep, Jn. 4:11; met. deep, profound, Acts 20:9; Rev. 2:24; ὄρθρου βαθέος, lit. at deep morning twilight, at the earliest dawn, Lk. 24:1*
G901.abbottSmith: deep ; Jo 4:11 ; metaph., ὄρθρου βαθέως (v. supr.), early dawn , Lk 24:1 ; ὕπνος , Ac 20:9 ; τὰ β. τοῦ Σατανᾶ , Re 2:24 .†
G901.strongs: from the base of G939 (βάσις); profound (as going down), literally or figuratively

G42.mounce: sanctification, sanctity, holiness, Rom. 1:4; 2 Cor. 7:1; 1 Thess. 3:13*
G42.abbottSmith: holiness , the state in man resulting from ἁγιασμός , q.v.: Ro 1:4 , II Co 7:1 , I Th 3:13 (Cremer, 52; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G42.strongs: from G40 (ἅγιος); sacredness (i.e. properly, the quality)

G4842.mounce: to send with any one, 2 Cor. 8:18, 22*
G4842.abbottSmith: to send together with : c. acc. et dat., II Co 8:22 ; id. seq. μετά , c. gen., ib. 18 .†
G4842.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3992 (πέμπω); to despatch in company

G3483.mounce: also spelled Νεεμάν, Naaman, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 4:27*
G3483.abbottSmith: yea , verily , even so ; in answer to a question: Mt 9:28 13:51 17:25 21:16 , Jo 11:27 21:15 , 16 , Ac 5:8 22:27 , Ro 3:29 ; seq. λέγω ὑμῖν , Mt 11:9 , Lk 7:26 ; repeated for emphasis, ναὶ ναί (opp. to οὒ οὔ ): Mt 5:37 ; ἤτω ὑμῶν τὸ ναὶ ναί , Ja 5:12 ; ν. καὶ οὔ , II Co 1:18 , 19 ; ἵνα ᾖ . . . τὸ ναὶ ναί , II Co 1:17 ; τὸ ν. , II Co 1:20 ; in assent to an assertion: Mt 15:27 , Mk 7:28 , Re 14:13 1
G3483.strongs: a primary particle of strong affirmation; yes
G3483.kjv: even so, surely, truth, verily, yea, yes

G3310.thayer: (a) a part, division of a country, (b) a share, portion.
G3310.mounce: a part; a division of a country, district, region, tract, Acts 16:12; a portion, Lk. 10:42; an allotted portion, Col. 1:12; a portion in common, share, Acts 8:21; 2 Cor. 6:15*
G3310.abbottSmith: (as in cl.) a part , portion : Lk 10:42 , Ac 8:21 , II Co 6:15 , Col 1:12 .
G3310.strongs: feminine of G3313 (μέρος); a portion, i.e. province, share or (abstractly) participation

G3663.thayer: of like feelings, having similar passions and feelings, of like infirmities.
G3663.mounce: being affected in the same way as another, subject to the same incidents, of like infirmities, subject to the same frailties and evils, Acts 14:15; Jas. 5:17*
G3663.abbottSmith: of like feelings or affections : c. dat., Ac 14:15 , Ja 5:17 .†
G3663.strongs: from G3664 (ὅμοιος) and the alternate of G3958 (πάσχω); similarly affected

G1914.thayer: I look with favor on, regard.
G1914.mounce: to look upon; to regard with partiality, Jas. 2:3; to regard with kindness and favor, Lk. 1:48; 9:38*
G1914.abbottSmith: to look upon . In NT, as in LXX ( I Ki 1:11 9:16 , Ps 24(25):16 ; To 3:3 , al.), to look on with favour : seq. ἐπί c. acc pers., Lk 1:48 9:38 , Ja 2:3 .†
G1914.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G991 (βλέπω); to gaze at (with favor, pity or partiality)
G1914.kjv: look upon, regard, have respect to

G5400.thayer: a terrible sight, cause of terror, an object of fear.
G5400.mounce: something which inspires terror; terrible sight or event, Lk. 21:11*
G5400.abbottSmith: that which causes fright, a terror : pl. (as always, exc. Is, l.c. ), Lk 21:11 (Hipp., Plat.).†
G5400.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G5399 (φοβέω); a frightening thing, i.e. terrific portent

G5195.mounce: to run riot; trans. to outrage, to treat in an arrogant or spiteful manner, Mt. 22:6; Lk. 11:45; 18:32; Acts 14:5; 1 Thess. 2:2*
G5195.abbottSmith: intrans., to wax wanton, run riot .
G5195.strongs: from G5196 (ὕβρις); to exercise violence, i.e. abuse

G3717.mounce: erect, upright, Acts 14:10; plain, level, straight, Heb. 12:13*
G3717.abbottSmith: straight ; in height, straight , upright : Ac 14:10 ; in line, straight , direct : fig., He 12:13 (LXX) .†
G3717.strongs: probably from the base of G3735 (ὄρος); right (as rising), i.e. (perpendicularly) erect (figuratively, honest), or (horizontally) level or direct

G3769.strongs: apparently a primary word; a tail

G4405.mounce: morning, the morning hour, Mt. 27:1; Jn. 21:4*
G4405.strongs: feminine of a derivative of G4404 (πρωΐ) as noun; day-dawn

G1926.mounce: to admit; to receive kindly, welcome, entertain, 3 Jn. 10; met. to admit, approve, assent to, 3 Jn. 9*
G1926.abbottSmith: in cl., of things, to allow of, admit of (Dem., Arist., al.). In late writers,
G1926.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to admit (as a guest or (figuratively) teacher)

G1931.thayer: I sink, set, set during, go down.
G1931.mounce: to set upon, to set during, Eph. 4:26*
G1931.abbottSmith: to go down, set (of the sun): Eph 4:26 .†
G1931.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1416 (δύνω); to set fully (as the sun)

G5007.thayer: a talent of silver or gold.
G5007.mounce: the scale of a balance; a talent, which as a weight was among the Jews equivalent to 3000 shekels, i.e., as usually estimated, 114 lbs. 15 dwts. Troy; while the Attic talent, on the usual estimate, was only equal to 56 lbs. 11 oz. Troy, Mt. 18:24; 25:15, 16, 20, 22, 24, 25, 28*
G5007.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the original form of (to bear; equivalent to G5342 (φέρω)); a balance (as supporting weights), i.e. (by implication) a certain weight (and thence a coin or rather sum of money) or "talent"

G1035.thayer: (a) abstr: eating, (b) food, a meal, (c) rust.
G1035.mounce: eating, the act of eating, Rom. 14:17; 1 Cor. 8:4; meat, food, Jn. 6:27; Heb. 12:16; a canker or rust, Mt. 6:19, 20
G1035.abbottSmith: eating : β. καὶ πόσις , Ro 14:17 , Col 2:16 (v. Lft., ICC , in l.); c. gen. obj., I Co 8:4 ; metaph., corrosion, rust : Mt 6:19 , 20 . As also in cl. (Hom., al.) = βρῶμα , food : Jo 6:27a , II Co 9:10 , Col 2:16 (EV, but v. supr.). He 12:16 ; metaph., of spiritual nourishment, Jo 4:32 6:27b , 55 .†
G1035.strongs: from the base of G977 (βιβρώσκω); (abstractly) eating (literally or figuratively); by extension (concretely) food (literally or figuratively)

G1292.abbottSmith: an interval, space : of time (Si, prol. 24 ), Ac 5:7 .†

G3575.mounce: Noah, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 24:37ff; Lk. 3:36; 17:26f.; Heb. 11:7; 1 Pet. 3:20; 2 Pet. 2:25*
G3575.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05146); Noë, (i.e. Noäch), a patriarch

G4710.mounce: haste; μετὰ σπουδῆς, with haste, hastily, quickly, Mk. 6:25; Lk. 1:39; earnestness, earnest application, diligence, enthusiasm, Rom. 12:8, 11; 2 Cor. 7:11, 12; 8:16; 8:7f.
G4710.abbottSmith: haste, speed : μετὰ σ. ( Wi 19:2 ), Mk 6:25 , Lk 1:39 .
G4710.strongs: from G4692 (σπεύδω); "speed", i.e. (by implication) despatch, eagerness, earnestness

G2044.thayer: (lit: I belch forth, hence) I utter, declare.
G2044.mounce: to vomit; met. to utter, declare openly, Mt. 13:35*
G2044.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to belch, i.e. (figuratively) to speak out

G2623.mounce: to close, shut fast; to shut up, confine, Lk. 3:20; Acts 26:10*
G2623.abbottSmith: to shut up :. c. acc. pers., seq. ἐν φυλακῇ ( -αῖς ), Lk 3:20 , Ac 26:10 .†
G2623.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2808 (κλείω); to shut down (in a dungeon), i.e. incarcerate

G6.thayer: Abel, second son of Adam and Eve, brother of Cain.
G6.mounce: Abel, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 23:35; Lk. 11:51; Heb. 11:4; 12:24
G6.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01893); Abel, the son of Adam

G627.thayer: a verbal defense (particularly in a law court).
G627.abbottSmith: a speech in defence : Ac 25:16 , II Co 7:11 , Phl 1:7 , 16 , II Ti 4:16 ; c. dat. pers., I Co 9:3 , I Pe 3:15 ; seq. πρός , Ac 22:1 .†
G627.strongs: from the same as G626 (ἀπολογέομαι); a plea ("apology")
G627.kjv: answer (for self), clearing of self, defence

G1546.thayer: a throwing out, a jettisoning of cargo to lighten a ship.
G1546.mounce: a casting out; especially, a throwing overboard of a cargo, Acts 27:18*
G1546.strongs: from G1544 (ἐκβάλλω); ejection, i.e. (specially) a throwing overboard of the cargo

G527.thayer: soft, tender, as a shoot of a tree.

G2801.mounce: a piece broken off, fragment, Mt. 14:20; 15:37; Mk. 6:43; 8:8, 19, 20; Lk. 9:17; Jn. 6:12f.*
G2801.abbottSmith: a broken piece , fragment : Mt 14:20 15:37 , Mk 6:43 8:8 , 19 , 20 , Lk 9:17 , Jo 6:12 , 13 .†

G5612.thayer: I roar, howl, as a beast.
G5612.mounce: to howl; to roar, as a lion, 1 Pet. 5:8*
G5612.abbottSmith: of animals (also of men, Hdt., al.), to roar, howl : λέων , I Pe 5:8 .†
G5612.strongs: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to "roar"

G1235.thayer: I awake out of sleep, am thoroughly awake.
G1235.mounce: to remain awake; to wake thoroughly, Lk. 9:32*
G1235.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1127 (γρηγορεύω); to waken thoroughly

G4991.thayer: welfare, prosperity, deliverance, preservation, salvation, safety.
G4991.mounce: a saving, preservation, Acts 27:34; Heb. 11:7; deliverance, Lk. 1:69, 71; Acts 7:25; salvation, spiritual and eternal, Lk. 1:77; 19:9; Acts 4:12; Rev. 7:10; a being placed in a condition of salvation by an embracing of the Gospel, Rom. 10:1, 10; 2 Tim. 3:15; means or opportunity of salvation, Acts 13:26; Rom. 11:11; Heb. 2:3; ἡ σωτηρία, the promised deliverance by the Messiah, Jn. 4:22
G4991.abbottSmith: deliverance, preservation, salvation, safety (Lat. salus ): Ac 7:25 27:34 , He 11:7 ; ἐξ ἐχθρῶν , Lk 1:71 . In NT esp. of Messianic and spiritual salvation (v.s. σώζω ): Lk 19:9 , Jo 4:22 , Ac 4:12 13:47 , Ro 11:11 , II Th 2:13 , II Ti 3:15 , He 2:3 6:9 , II Pe 3:15 , Ju 3 ; opp. to ἀπηώλεια , Phl 1:28 ; αἰώνιος-. , He 5:9 ; ὁ λόγος ( τὸ εὐαγγέλιον ) τῆς σ. , Ac 13:26 , Eph 1:13 ; ὁδὸς σωτηρίας ,
G4991.strongs: feminine of a derivative of G4990 (σωτήρ) as (properly, abstract) noun; rescue or safety (physically or morally)

G750.abbottSmith: found on an Egyptian mummy label (Deiss., LAE , 97 ff.; cf. MM, s.v.); used by modem Greeks of tribal chiefs; chief shepherd . of Christ, I Pe 5:4 .†
G750.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and G4166 (ποιμήν); a head shepherd

G5360.thayer: brotherly love, love of Christian brethren.
G5360.mounce: brotherly love; in NT love of the Christian brotherhood, Rom. 12:10; 1 Thess. 4:9; Heb. 13:1; 1 Pet. 1:22; 2 Pet. 1:7*
G5360.abbottSmith: the love of brothers, brotherly love : of Christians' mutual love as brethren (v.s. ἀδελφός ), Ro 12:10 , I Th 4:9 , He 13:1 , I Pe 1:22 , II Pe 1:7 .†
G5360.kjv: brotherly love (kindness), love of the brethren

G3031.mounce: frankincense; in NT a censer, Rev. 8:3, 5*
G3031.abbottSmith: in cl. (and LXX), frankincense , the gum of the libanus .
G3031.strongs: from G3030 (λίβανος); frankincense, i.e. (by extension) a censer for burning it

G927.abbottSmith: of great value , very costly : Mt 26:7 (T, πολυτίμου ).†
G927.strongs: from G926 (βαρύς) and G5092 (τιμή); highly valuable

G1192.thayer: the skin or hide of an animal.
G1192.mounce: the skin of an animal, Heb. 11:37*
G1192.abbottSmith: the skin, hide of beasts: ἐν αἰγίοις δ. , He 11:37 .†

G1811.mounce: to follow out; to imitate, 2 Pet. 2:2, 15; to observe as a guide, 2 Pet. 1:16*
G1811.abbottSmith: to follow, follow up (in various senses): metaph., II Pe 1:16 2:2 , 15 †
G1811.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to follow out, i.e. (figuratively) to imitate, obey, yield to

G186.abbottSmith: unbending , firm : metaph. (MM, VGT , s.v.). He 10:23 .†
G186.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2827 (κλίνω); not leaning, i.e. (figuratively) firm

G3373.mounce: to lengthen, prolong; mid. to grow up, as plants, Mk. 4:27*
G3373.abbottSmith: to lengthen , extend : of causing plants to grow, Is 44:14 ; pass. (mid., Swete, in l.), to grow : Mk 4:27 .†
G3373.strongs: from G3372 (μῆκος); to lengthen, i.e. (middle voice) to enlarge

G2424.thayer: Jesus; the Greek form of Joshua; Jesus, son of Eliezer; Jesus, surnamed Justus.
G2424.mounce: a Savior, Jesus, Mt. 1:21, 25; 2:1, et al. freq.; Joshua, Acts 7:45; Heb. 4:8; Jesus, a Jewish Christian, Col. 4:11
G2424.abbottSmith: JESUS : Mt 1:21 , al.; ὁ Ἰ , Mt 3:13 , al.; Ἰ. Χριστός , Mt 1:1 , Mk 1:1 , al.; Χρ. Ἰ. , Ro 2:26 , al.; κύριος Ἰ. , Ac 28:31 , al.; Ἰησοῦ , voc., Mk 1:24 , al. Joshua : Ac 7:45 , He 4:8 . Jesus , son of Eliezer: Lk 3:29 . Jesus , surnamed Justus: Col 4:11 . v.s. βαραββᾶς .
G2424.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03091); Jesus (i.e. Jehoshua), the name of our Lord and two (three) other Israelites

G1476.mounce: sedentary; met. settled, steady, firm, steadfast, constant, 1 Cor. 7:37; 15:58; Col. 1:23*
G1476.abbottSmith: sitting, seated . steadfast, firm ; metaph., of moral fixity: I Co 7:37 15:58 , Col 1:23 .†
G1476.strongs: from a derivative of (to sit); sedentary, i.e. (by implication) immovable

G3807.thayer: a boy's guardian or tutor, a slave who had charge of the life and morals of the boys of a family, not strictly a teacher.
G3807.mounce: a pedagogue, childtender, a person, usually a slave or freedman, to whom the care of the boys of a family was committed, whose duty it was to attend them at their play, lead them to and from the public school, and exercise a constant superintendence over their conduct and safety; in NT an ordinary director or minister contrasted with an Apostle, as a pedagogue occupies an inferior position to a parent, 1 Cor. 4:15; a term applied to the Mosaic law, as dealing with men as in a state of mere childhood and tutelage, Gal. 3:24, 25*
G3807.abbottSmith: a guide, guardian, trainer of boys, a tutor (disting. from διδάσκαλος , Xen., Lac. , 3, 1), usually a trusty slave: opp. to πατήρ , I Co 4:15 ; fig., of the Law, π. εἰς Χριστόν , Ga 3:24 , 25 .†
G3807.strongs: from G3816 (παῖς) and a reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω); a boy-leader, i.e. a servant whose office it was to take the children to school; (by implication, (figuratively) a tutor ("pædagogue"))

G4778.mounce: to encounter adversity along with any one, Heb. 11:25*
G4778.abbottSmith: to endure adversity with : c. dat. pers., He 11:25 . Not elsewhere.†
G4778.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2558 (κακουχέω); to maltreat in company with, i.e. (passively) endure persecution together

G4866.thayer: I compete together with others, cooperate vigorously with.
G4866.mounce: pr. to fight or work on the side of any one; in NT to cooperate vigorously with a person, Phil. 4:3; to make effort in the cause of, in support of a thing, Phil. 1:27*
G4866.abbottSmith: = συναγωνίζομαι : c. dat. commod., Phl 1:27 ; c. dat. pers., seq. ἐν , Phl 4:3 .†
G4866.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G118 (ἀθλέω); to wrestle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to seek jointly

G4251.thayer: Prisca, Priscilla, the former being the more correct and formal name, the latter a diminutive and more familiar; a Roman lady, probably of good birth, wife of the Jewish Christian Aquila.
G4251.mounce: see also Πρόσκιλλα, Prisca, pr. name, Rom. 16:3; 1 Cor. 16:19; 2 Tim. 4:19*
G4251.abbottSmith: Prisca : Ro 16:3 , I Co 16:19 , II Ti 4:19 ; Priscilla : Ac 18:2 , 18 , 26 ; the wife of Aquila, v.s. Ἀκύλας .†
G4251.strongs: of Latin origin; feminine of Priscus, ancient; Priska, a Christian woman

G1726.thayer: before, in the presence of, in the eyes of.
G1726.mounce: acc sg neut of ἐναντίος used adverbially; the adj. does not appear in the NT, improper prep., before, in the presence of, Lk. 1:6; 20:26; Acts 8:32; 2 Cor. 2:7; Gal. 2:7; 1 Pet. 3:9; from the Hebrew, in the sight or estimation of, Acts 7:10; with τοῦ θεοῦ, an intensive expression, Lk. 24:19*
G1726.abbottSmith: over against, opposite, contrary : ἄνεμος , Mt 14:24 , Mk 6:48 , Ac 27:4 ; ἐξ ἐναντίας (ellipse obscure, v. Bl., § 44,1; Mozley, Ps 42:1-11 ), c. gen., Mk 15:39 . Metaph., opposed, hostile : I Th 2:15 , Ac 26:9 28:17 ; ὁ ἐξ ἐ. , Tit 2:8 . Neuter., -ίον , adv., as prep. c. gen., before, in the presence of : Lk 1:6 20:26 24:19 Ac 7:10 ( ἔναντι , T), Ac 8:32 (LXX) .†
G1726.strongs: neuter of G1727 (ἐναντίος); (adverbially) in the presence (view) of

G4506.thayer: I rescue, deliver (from danger or destruction).
G4506.mounce: to drag out of danger, to rescue, save, Mt. 6:13; 27:43; to be rescued, delivered, Lk. 1:74; Rom. 15:31; 2 Thess. 3:2; 2 Tim. 4:17
G4506.abbottSmith: to draw to oneself , hence, to rescue, deliver : c. acc. pers., Mt 27:43 , II Pe 2:7 ; id. seq. ἀπό , Mt 6:13 , Lk 11:4 (Rec., R, mg.), II Ti 4:18 ; seq. ἐκ , Ro 7:24 , II Co 1:10 , Col 1:13 , I Th 1:10 , II Ti 3:11 , II Pe 2:9 ; absol., ὁ ῥυύμενος , the deliverer , Ro 11:26 . Passive: seq. ἀπό , Ro 15:31 , II Th 3:2 ; seq. ἐκ , Lk 1:74 , II Ti 4:17 .†
G4506.strongs: middle voice of an obsolete verb, akin to G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of a current; compare G4511 (ῥύσις)); to rush or draw (for oneself), i.e. rescue

G3813.thayer: a little child, an infant, little one.
G3813.mounce: an infant, babe, Mt. 2:8; but usually in NT as equiv. to παῖς, Mt. 14:21; Mk. 7:28, et al. freq.; pl. voc. used by way of endearment, my dear children, 1 Jn. 2:18; also as a term of familiar address, children, my lads, Jn. 21:5
G3813.strongs: neuter diminutive of G3816 (παῖς); a childling (of either sex), i.e. (properly), an infant, or (by extension) a half-grown boy or girl; figuratively, an immature Christian

G1745.mounce: a putting on, or wearing of clothes, 1 Pet. 3:3*
G1745.abbottSmith: a putting on : ίματίων , I Pe 3:3 .†

G296.thayer: prop: a road around anything; the street of a village.
G296.mounce: pr. a road leading round a town or village; the street of a village, Mk. 11:4*
G296.abbottSmith: prop., a road around anything (RV, the open street ): Mk 11:4 , Ac 19:28 , WH, mg.†
G296.strongs: from the base of G297 (ἀμφότερος) and G3598 (ὁδός); a fork in the road

G695.mounce: a forger of silver, silversmith, Acts 19:24*
G695.abbottSmith: a silversmith : Ac 19:24 (Plut.; π. , v. MM, s.v.).†
G695.strongs: from G696 (ἄργυρος) and G2875 (κόπτω); a beater (i.e. worker) of silver

G3636.thayer: slothful, backward, hesitating; of things: irksome.
G3636.mounce: slow; slothful, indolent, idle, Mt. 25:26; Rom. 12:11; tedious, troublesome, Phil. 3:1*
G3636.abbottSmith: shrinking , hesitating , timid : c. dat. (Bl., § 38, 2), Ro 12:11 ; hence, slothful : Mt 25:26 . Of things, that which causes shrinking , irksome : Phl 3:1 .†
G3636.strongs: from G3635 (ὀκνέω); tardy, i.e. indolent; (figuratively) irksome

G5581.thayer: falsely named, under a false name.
G5581.abbottSmith: under a false name, falsely called : I Ti 6:20 (Æsch., Plut., al.).†
G5581.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G3686 (ὄνομα); untruly named

G5134.strongs: from the base of (to wound; akin to the base of G2352 (θραύω), G5147 (τρίβος), G5149 (τρίζω), etc.); a wound

G430.thayer: I endure, bear with, have patience with, suffer, admit, persist.
G430.mounce: also listed as ἀνέχω, but it is always in the middle in our literature, to endure patiently, 1 Cor. 4:12; 2 Cor. 11:20; 2 Thess. 1:4; to bear with, Matt 17:7; to suffer, admit, permit, Acts 18:14; 2 Cor. 11:4; 2 Tim. 4:3; Heb. 13:22
G430.abbottSmith: to hold up ; in NT always mid., to bear with , endure : in cl. most freq. c. acc., but in NT c. gen. pers., Mt 17:17 , Mk 9:19 , Lk 9:41 , II Co 11:1 , 19 , Eph 4:2 , Col 3:13 ; seq. μικρόν τι , c. gen. pers. and c. gen. rei, II Co 11:1 ; c. dat. rei, II Th 1:4 (v M, Th. , in l.); seq. εἴ τις , II Co 11:20 ; absol., I Co 4:12 , II Co 11:4 ; to bear with = to listen to , c. gen. pers., Ac 18:14 ; c
G430.strongs: middle voice from G303 (ἀνά) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself up against, i.e. (figuratively) put up with

G2134.thayer: I make into a eunuch, emasculate, castrate.
G2134.abbottSmith: to make a eunuch of, castrate : pass., Mt 19:12 ; metaph., εὐ. έαυτόν , ib. †
G2134.strongs: from G2135 (εὐνοῦχος); to castrate (figuratively, live unmarried)

G898.thayer: a step (of a stairway); hence: a stage in a career, a position.
G898.mounce: pr. a step, stair; met. grade of dignity, degree, rank, standing, 1 Tim. 3:13*
G898.abbottSmith: a step ( IV Ki 20:9 , 10 , 11 , of degrees of a dial); metaph., a degree , standing : I Ti 3:13 .†
G898.strongs: from the same as G899 (βάθος); a step, i.e. (figuratively) grade (of dignity)

G5497.thayer: one who leads a helpless person by the hand.
G5497.mounce: one who leads another by the hand, Acts 13:11*
G5497.abbottSmith: leading by the hand . As subst., ὁ χ. , one to lead by the head, a guide : Ac 13:11 .†
G5497.strongs: from G5495 (χείρ) and a reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω); a hand-leader, i.e. personal conductor (of a blind person)
G5497.kjv: some to lead by the hand

G1294.mounce: to distort, turn away; met. to pervert, corrupt, Mt. 17:17; Lk. 9:41; to turn out of the way, cause to make defection, Lk. 23:2; Acts 13:8; διεστραμμένος, perverse, corrupt, erroneous
G1294.abbottSmith: to distort, twist ; metaph., to distort, pervert : Lk 23:2 , Ac 13:8 , 10 ; διεστραμμένος , perverse : Mt 17:17 , Lk 9:41 , Ac 20:30 , Phl 2:15 .†
G1294.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4762 (στρέφω); to distort, i.e. (figuratively) misinterpret, or (morally) corrupt

G5517.abbottSmith: earthy, made of dust : I Co 15:47-49 (v. Field, Notes , 179 f.).†
G5517.strongs: from G5522 (χόος); dusty or dirty (soil-like), i.e. (by implication) terrene

G4658.thayer: a Scythian, as typical of the uncivilized.
G4658.mounce: A Scythian, a native of Scythia, the modern Mongolia and Tartary, Col. 3:11*
G4658.abbottSmith: Scythian , an inhabitant of Scythia, i.e. Russia and Siberia, a synonym with the Greeks for the wildest of barbarians: Col 3:11 .†
G4658.strongs: probably of foreign origin; a Scythene or Scythian, i.e. (by implication) a savage

G3137.thayer: Mary, Miriam, (a) the mother of Jesus, (b) of Magdala, (c) sister of Martha and Lazarus, (d) wife of Cleopas, (e) mother of John Mark, (f) a Christian woman in Rome.
G3137.abbottSmith: Mary . In NT; the mother of Jesus: Mt 1:16 ff. 2:11 13:55 , Mk 6:3 , Lk 1:27 ff. 2:5 , 16 , 19 , 34 , Ac 1:14 . M. Magdalene (q.v.). The wife of Clopas ( Jo 19:25 ) and mother of James the little, and Joses: Mt 27:56 , 61 28:1 , Mk 15:40 , 47 16:1 , Lk 24:10 . The sister of Martha and Lazarus: Lk 10:39 , 42 , Jo 11:1 , 2 , 19 ff. 12:3 . The mother of John Mark: Ac 12:12 . A Christian greeted by St
G3137.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04813); Maria or Mariam (i.e. Mirjam), the name of six Christian females

G5614.thayer: (Aramaic and Hebrew, originally a cry for help), hosanna!, a cry of happiness.
G5614.mounce: Hosanna! save now, help now, Mt. 21:9, 15; Mk. 11:9, 10; Jn. 12:13*
G5614.abbottSmith: hosanna: Mt 21:9 , Mk 11:9-10 , Jo 12:13 ; τ. υἱῷ Δανείδ , Mt 21:9 , 15 .†
G5614.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03467 and H04994); oh save!; hosanna (i.e. hoshia-na), an exclamation of adoration

G5265.thayer: (lit: I bind under), mid: I put on my feet, pass: I am shod.
G5265.mounce: to bind under, mid. to bind under one’s self, put on one’s own feet, Acts 12:8; to shoe, Eph. 6:15; pass. to be shod, Mk. 6:9*
G5265.abbottSmith: to bind under , esp. of foot gear. Most freq. in mid, and pass. c. acc;
G5265.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1210 (δέω); to bind under one's feet, i.e. put on shoes or sandals

G554.mounce: to put off, renounce, Col. 3:9; to despoil a rival, Col. 2:15*
G554.abbottSmith: to strip off clothes or arms; mid., to strip off from oneself : Col 3:9 ; to strip , despoil (mid, for act., ICC , in l.; but cf. Lft., Ellic.), Col 2:15 .†
G554.strongs: middle voice from G575 (ἀπό) and G1562 (ἐκδύω); to divest wholly oneself, or (for oneself) despoil

G4911.mounce: to set together upon, join in an attack, Acts 16:22*
G4911.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2186 (ἐφίστημι); to stand up together, i.e. to resist (or assault) jointly

G2330.mounce: the warm season of the year, summer, Mt. 24:32; Mk. 13:28; Lk. 21:30*
G2330.strongs: from a primary (to heat); properly, heat, i.e. summer

G4824.thayer: (a) a body of advisers (assessors) in a court, a council, (b) abstr: consultation, counsel, advice; resolution, decree.
G4824.mounce: counsel, consultation, mutual consultation, Mt. 12:14; 22:15; 27:1, 7; 28:12; Mk. 3:6; Acts 27:1, 7; 28:12; a council of counsellors, Acts 25:12*
G4824.abbottSmith: a word of the Græco-Roman period (of. Lat. consilium , and v. Deiss., BS , 238) = cl. συμβουλία (Arist., Xen.),
G4824.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4825 (σύμβουλος); advisement; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court

G2371.thayer: (lit: I fight desperately, hence) I am furiously angry with.
G2371.mounce: to wage war fiercely; to be warmly hostile to, be enraged against, Acts 12:20*
G2371.abbottSmith: to fight desperately , have a hot quarrel : c. dat. pers., Ac 12:20 .†
G2371.strongs: from a presumed compound of G2372 (θυμός) and G3164 (μάχομαι); to be in a furious fight, i.e. (figuratively) to be exasperated

G3562.strongs: adverb from a comparative of the accusative case of G3563 (νοῦς) and G2192 (ἔχω); in a mind-having way, i.e. prudently

G463.thayer: forbearance, suspense or delay (of punishment), patience.
G463.abbottSmith: in cl., a holding back , delaying (MM, VGT , s.v.).

G1190.strongs: from G1191 (Δέρβη); a Derbæan or inhabitant of Derbe

G3439.mounce: only-begotten, only-born, Lk. 7:12; 8:42; 9:38; Heb. 11:17; only-begotten in respect of peculiar generation, unique, Jn. 1:14, 18; 3:16, 18; 1 Jn. 4:9*
G3439.abbottSmith: only , only begotten ( DCG , ii, 281), of sons and daughters: Lk 7:12 8:42 9:38 , He 11:17 ; of Christ, Jo 3:16 , 18 , I Jo 4:9 ; μ. παρὰ πατρός , Jo 1:14 ; μ. θεός , Jo 1:18 .†
G3439.strongs: from G3441 (μόνος) and G1096 (γίνομαι); only-born, i.e. sole

G2173.mounce: highly useful, very profitable, 2 Tim. 2:21; 4:11; Phlm. 11*
G2173.abbottSmith: useful, serviceable : c. dat. pers., II Ti 2:21 ; id. seq. εἰς , c. dat. rei, II Ti 4:11 ; opp. to ἄχρηστος , Phm 11 .†
G2173.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G5543 (χρηστός); easily used, i.e. useful

G651.mounce: a sending, expedition; office or duty of one sent as a messenger or agent; office of an apostle, apostleship, Acts 1:25; Rom. 1:5; 1 Cor. 9:2; Gal. 2:8*
G651.abbottSmith: In cl., a sending away (MM, s.v.), as, an expedition (Hdt.).

G2779.thayer: a garden, any place planted with trees and herbs.
G2779.mounce: a garden, any place planted with trees and herbs, Lk. 13:19; Jn. 18:1, 26; 19:41*

G158.mounce: causative; αἴτιος, an author or causer, Heb. 5:9; τὸ αἴτιον, equivalent to αἰτία
G158.strongs: neuter of G159 (αἴτιος); a reason or crime (like G156 (αἰτία))

G24.abbottSmith: indignation : II Co 7:11 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G2590.thayer: (a) fruit, generally vegetable, sometimes animal, (b) met: fruit, deed, action, result, (c) profit, gain.
G2590.mounce: fruit, Mt. 3:10; 21:19, 34; from the Hebrew, καρπὸς κοιλίας, fruit of the womb, offspring, Lk. 1:42; καρπὸς ὀσφύος, fruit of the loins, offspring, posterity, Acts 2:30; καρπὸς χειλέων, fruit of the lips, praise, Heb. 13:15; met. conduct, actions, Mt. 3:8; 7:16; Rom. 6:22; benefit, profit, Rom. 1:13; 6:21; reward, Phil. 4:17
G2590.abbottSmith: fruit : of trees, Mt 12:33 21:19 , Mk 11:14 , Lk 6:44 13:6 , 7 ; of vines, Mt 21:34 , Mk 12:2 , Lk 20:10 , I Co 9:7 ; of fields, Mk 4:29 , Lk 12:17 , II Ti 2:6 , Ja 5:7 ; βλαστάνειν , Ja 5:18 ; ποιεῖν (cf. Heb. עָשָׂה פְּרִי ), Mt 3:10 7:17-19 13:26 , Lk 3:9 6:43 8:8 13:9 , Re 22:2 ; διδόναι , Mt 13:8 , Mk 4:7 , 8 ; φέρειν , Mt 7:18 , Jo 12:24 15:2 , 4 , 5 , 8 , 16 ; ἀποδιδόναι , Mt 21:41 , Re 22:
G2590.strongs: probably from the base of G726 (ἁρπάζω); fruit (as plucked), literally or figuratively

G2826.thayer: a couch or litter of a sick person.
G2826.mounce: a small couch or bed, Lk. 5:19, 24*
G2826.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G2825 (κλίνη); a pallet or little couch

G5309.mounce: to have lofty thoughts, be proud, haughty, 1 Tim. 6:17*
G5309.abbottSmith: to be high-minded : I Ti 6:17 (WH, mg., ὑψηλὰ, φρονεῖν ).†
G5309.strongs: from a compound of G5308 (ὑψηλός) and G5424 (φρήν); to be lofty in mind, i.e. arrogant

G36.thayer: lit: without family, hence: ignoble, base.
G36.mounce: lit., without kin; fig., base, low, insignificant, 1 Cor. 1:28*
G36.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1085 (γένος); properly, without kin, i.e. (of unknown descent, and by implication) ignoble

G3540.thayer: a thought, purpose, design; the mind; the heart, soul, feelings.
G3540.mounce: the mind, the understanding, intellect, 2 Cor. 3:14; 4:4; Phil. 4:7; the heart, soul, affections, feelings, disposition, 2 Cor. 11:3; a conception of the mind, thought, purpose, device, 2 Cor. 2:11; 10:5*
G3540.abbottSmith: a thought , purpose , design : II Co 2:11 10:5 11:3 , Phl 4:7 . Meton., of the mind, II Co 3:14 4:4 .†
G3540.strongs: from G3539 (νοιέω); a perception, i.e. purpose, or (by implication) the intellect, disposition, itself

G2571.thayer: a covering, especially a covering of head and face, a veil.
G2571.mounce: a covering; a veil, 2 Cor. 3:13; met. a veil, a blind to spiritual vision, 2 Cor. 3:14, 15, 16*
G2571.strongs: from G2572 (καλύπτω); a cover, i.e. veil

G3616.mounce: pr. to be master of a household; to occupy one’s self in the management of a household, 1 Tim. 5:14*
G3616.abbottSmith: to rule a household : I Ti 5:14 .†
G3616.strongs: from G3617 (οἰκοδεσπότης); to be the head of (i.e. rule) a family

G4505.thayer: a narrow street or lane in a town or city.
G4505.mounce: pr. a rush or sweep of a body in motion; a street, Acts 9:11; 12:10; a narrow street, lane, alley, as distinguished from πλατεῖα, Mt. 6:2; Lk. 14:21*
G4505.abbottSmith: in cl., the force, rush, swing , of a moving body; esp. of a charge of soldiers.
G4505.strongs: prolongation from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in its original sense; an alley or avenue (as crowded)

G3672.strongs: adverb of present passive participle of G3670 (ὁμολογέω); confessedly

G4139.mounce: can function as an improper prep., near, near by, Jn. 4:5; ὁ πλησίον, a neighbor, Mt. 19:19; Rom. 15:2; a friendly neighbor, Mt. 5:43
G4139.abbottSmith: near , close by , neighbouring . As adv., πλησίον = πέλας , near : c. gen., Jo 4:5 ; c. art., ὁ π. , one's neighbour [in LXX chiefly for רֵעַ ]: Lk 10:29 , 36 , Ac 7:27 , Ro 13:10 15:2 , Eph 4:25 , Ja 4:12 ; ἀγαπήσεις τὸν π. σου ὡς σεαυτόν ( Le 19:18 , LXX), Mt 5:43 19:19 22:39 , Mk 12:31 , 33 , Lk 10:27 , Ro 13:9 , Ga 5:14 , Ja 2:8 (on the various senses of ὁ π. in OT, NT, v. DB , iii, 511; DCC ,
G4139.strongs: neuter of a derivative of (near); (adverbially) close by; as noun, a neighbor, i.e. fellow (as man, countryman, Christian or friend)

G4043.thayer: I walk, hence Hebraistically (in an ethical sense): I conduct my life, live.
G4043.mounce: pluperfect, περι[ε]πεπατήκει (3 sg), to walk, walk about, Mt. 9:5; 11:5; 14:25, 26, 29; to rove, roam, 1 Pet. 5:8; with μετά, to accompany, follow, Jn. 6:66; Rev. 3:4; to walk, frequent a locality, Jn. 7:1; 11:54; from the Hebrew, to maintain a certain walk of life and conduct, Gal. 5:16; Eph. 2:10
G4043.abbottSmith: to walk : absol., Mt 9:5 , Mk 5:42 , Lk 5:23 , Jo 1:36 ; c. pred., γυμνός , Re 16:15 ; ἐπάνω Lk 11:44 ; διά , c. gen., Re 21:24 ; ἐν , Mk 11:27 12:38 , Jo 7:1 11:54 , Re 2:1 , al.; ἐν τ. σκοτίᾳ , fig., Jo 8:12 12:35 , I Jo 1:6 , 7 2:11 ; ἐπί , c. gen., Mt 14:26 ; id. c. dat., Mt 14:25 , 29 ; μετά , Jo 6:66 , Re 3:4 ; παρά , c. acc., Mt 4:18 . Metaph., of living, passing one's life, conducting ones
G4043.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3961 (πατέω); to tread all around, i.e. walk at large (especially as proof of ability); figuratively, to live, deport oneself, follow (as a companion or votary)

G1639.thayer: an Elamite, one of a people living to the north of the Persian Gulf in the southern part of Persia.
G1639.mounce: an Elamite; an inhabitant of Elam, a province of Persia, Acts 2:9*
G1639.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05867); an Elamite or Persian

G5203.strongs: from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (as if looking watery); to be "dropsical"

G668.mounce: to flee from, escape; met. to be rid, be freed from, 2 Pet. 1:4; 2:18, 20*
G668.abbottSmith: to flee from , escape : c. acc., II Pe 2:18 , 20 ; c. gen., II Pe 1:4 .†
G668.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5343 (φεύγω); (figuratively) to escape

G2545.thayer: I ignite, light, burn, lit. and met; I consume with fire.
G2545.mounce: to cause to burn, kindle, light, Mt. 5:15; pass. to be kindled, burn, flame, Lk. 12:35; met. to be kindled into emotion, Lk. 24:32; to consume with fire, Jn. 15:6; 1 Cor. 13:3
G2545.abbottSmith: to kindle , light : c. acc., λύχνον , Mt 5:15 ; pass., to be lighted , to burn : ptcp., Lk 12:35 , Re 4:5 8:10 19:20 ; πυρί , He 12:18 , Re 8:8 21:8 ; metaph., Lk 24:32 , Jo 5:35 .
G2545.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to set on fire, i.e. kindle or (by implication) consume

G3681.mounce: pr. fame, report, character; usually, reproach, disgrace, Lk. 1:25*
G3681.strongs: probably akin to the base of G3686 (ὄνομα); notoriety, i.e. a taunt (disgrace)

G3528.thayer: I conquer, am victorious, overcome, prevail, subdue.
G3528.mounce: to conquer, overcome, vanquish, subdue, Lk. 1:22; Jn. 16:33; absol. to overcome, prevail, Rev. 5:5; to come off superior in a judicial cause, Rom. 3:4
G3528.abbottSmith: to conquer , prevail : absol., of Christ, Re 3:21 6:2 ; c. inf., Re 5:5 ; of Christians, Re 2:7 , 11 , 17 , 26 3:5 , 12 , 21 21:7 ; seq. ἐκ (RV, come victorious from ), Re 15:2 ; as law-term (cl.), Ro 3:4 (LXX) ; c. acc. pers., Lk 11:22 , Re 11:7 13:7 ([WH], R, mg., om.); of Christ, Jo 16:33 ( τ. κόσμον ), Re 17:14 ; of Christians, I Jo 4:4 ; τ. πονηρόν , I Jo 2:13 , 14 ; αὐτόν (ref. to ὁ κατήγωρ
G3528.strongs: from G3529 (νίκη); to subdue (literally or figuratively)
G3528.kjv: conquer, overcome, prevail, get the victory

G1704.thayer: I walk about in a place, live among, am conversant with.
G1704.mounce: pr. to walk about in a place; met. in NT to live among, be conversant with, 2 Cor. 6:16*
G1704.abbottSmith: to walk about in or among : seq. ἐν. , dat. pers., II Co 6:16 (LXX) ,†
G1704.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4043 (περιπατέω); to perambulate on a place, i.e. (figuratively) to be occupied among persons

G5305.mounce: posterior in place or time; subsequent, later, last, finally, Mt. 21:31; 1 Tim. 4:1
G5305.strongs: neuter of G5306 (ὕστερος) as adverb; more lately, i.e. eventually

G510.strongs: from G511 (ἀνώτερος); superior, i.e. (locally) more remote

G2190.mounce: hated, under disfavor, Rom. 11:28; inimical, hostile, Mt. 13:28; Col. 1:21; as a subst., an enemy, adversary, Mt. 5:43, 44; 10:36; Lk. 27:35
G2190.abbottSmith: hated , hateful (Hom.): opp. to ἀγαπητός , Ro 11:28 .
G2190.strongs: from a primary (to hate); hateful (passively, odious, or actively, hostile); usually as a noun, an adversary (especially Satan)

G72.thayer: a leading, guiding; hence: mode of life, conduct.
G72.mounce: guidance, mode of instruction, discipline, course of life, 2 Tim. 3:10*
G72.abbottSmith: a carrying away . a leading, guiding ; metaph., training ; hence, from the expression ἀ. τοῦ βίου , absol., conduct, way of life : II Ti 3:10 (Cremer, 61; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G72.strongs: reduplicated from G71 (ἄγω); a bringing up, i.e. mode of living

G1202.strongs: from the same as G1201 (δεσμωτήριον); (passively) a captive

G4370.mounce: to run to, or up, Mk. 9:15; 10:17; Acts 8:30*
G4370.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to run towards, i.e. hasten to meet or join

G3181.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G3725 (ὅριον); bounded alongside, i.e. contiguous (neuter plural as noun, frontier)

G5557.thayer: gold, anything made of gold, a gold coin.
G5557.mounce: to gild, overlay with gold, adorn or deck with gold, Rev. 17:4; 18:16*
G5557.abbottSmith: gold : Mt 2:11 , Re 9:7 ; of golden ornaments, Mt 23:16, 17 , I Ti 2:9 (Rec., WH, mg.), Ja 5:3 , Re 17:4 (Rec., WH, mg.) Re 18:12 , 16 (Rec., WH, mg.) ; of images, Ac 17:29 ; of gold coin, Mt 10:9 .†
G5557.strongs: perhaps from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) (through the idea of the utility of the metal); gold; by extension, a golden article, as an ornament or coin

G1594.thayer: I return to soberness of mind.
G1594.mounce: pr. to awake sober after intoxication; met. to shake off mental bewilderment, to wake up from delusion and folly, 1 Cor. 15:34*
G1594.abbottSmith: to become sober after drunkenness : metaph., of sobriety of mind, I Co 15:34 .†
G1594.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3525 (νήφω); (figuratively) to rouse (oneself) out of stupor

G5588.mounce: a whisperer; a whisperer, gossip, Rom. 1:29*
G5588.abbottSmith: a whisperer (as epithet of Hermes, Dem., 1358, 6): in bad sense, Ro 1:30 .†
G5588.strongs: from the same as G5587 (ψιθυρισμός); a secret calumniator

G4039.thayer: I dwell around, am neighboring to.
G4039.mounce: to dwell around, or in the vicinity; to be a neighbor, Lk. 1:65*
G4039.abbottSmith: to dwell round about : c. acc., Lk 1:65 .†
G4039.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to reside around, i.e. be a neighbor

G3706.mounce: seeing, sight; appearance, aspect, a vision, Acts 2:17; Rev. 9:17; 4:3*
G3706.abbottSmith: in Arist. and later writers, the act of seeing , the sense of sight , and by meton., pl., the eyes .
G3706.strongs: from G3708 (ὁράω); the act of gazing, i.e. (externally) an aspect or (internally) an inspired appearance

G438.thayer: bloom, possibly a reference to the bright flowers, such as poppies (among the grass).

G966.thayer: Bethsaida, (a) a city of Galilee, (b) a city east of the Jordan.
G966.mounce: also spelled Βηθσαϊδάν (G1035) Bethsaida, indecl. (1) A city of Galilee, Mt. 11:21; Mk. 6:45,. (2) A city of Lower Gaulanitis, near the Lake of Gennesareth, Lk. 9:10. See PhotoGuide.
G966.abbottSmith: Bethsaida , a town on NE. shore of the Sea of Galilee: Lk 9:10 . It is generally supposed that a second Β. on the W. shore is referred to in Mt 11:21 , Mk 6:45 8:22 , Lk 10:13 , Jo 1:45 12:21 ( DB , i, 282 f.; but v. Swete on Mk 6:45 ). v.l. for Βηθζαθά , Βηθεσδά , Jo 5:2 .†
G966.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01004 and H06719); fishing-house; Bethsaida, a place in Palestine

G3303.thayer: an untranslatable particle, generally answered by "de", each of the two introducing a clause intended to be contrasted with the other.
G3303.abbottSmith: It is generally untranslatable and is not nearly so frequent in NT as in cl. Like δέ , it never stands first in a clause. Answered by δέ or some other particle: μὲν . . . δέ , indeed . . . but , Mt 3:11 , Lk 3:16 , al.; with pronouns, ὃς μὲν . . . ὃς δέ , one . . . another , Mt 21:35 , al.; pl., Phl 1:16 , 17 ; ὃ μὲν . . . ὃ δὲ . . . ὃ δέ , some . . . some . . . some , Mt 13:8 ; τοῦτο μὲν . . . το
G3303.strongs: a primary particle; properly, indicative of affirmation or concession (in fact); usually followed by a contrasted clause with G1161 (δέ) (this one, the former, etc.)
G3303.kjv: even, indeed, so, some, truly, verily

G2643.mounce: pr. an exchange; reconciliation, restoration to favor, Rom. 5:11; 11:15; 2 Cor. 5:18, 19*
G2643.strongs: from G2644 (καταλλάσσω); exchange (figuratively, adjustment), i.e. restoration to (the divine) favor

G5098.strongs: from G5097 (τιμωρέω); vindication, i.e. (by implication) a penalty

G1364.mounce: twice ten thousand, two myriads, Rev. 9:16*
G1364.abbottSmith: twice : Mk 14:30 , 72 ; δ. τ. σαββάτου , Lk 18:12 ; καὶ ἅπαξ κ. δ. , Phl 4:16 , I Th 2:18 ; δ. ἀποθανόντα , Ju 12 (v. Mayor, ICC , in l.); δ. μυριάδες , Re 9:16 .†

G5466.mounce: a Chaldean, a native of Chaldea, a country of central Asia, which seems to have included Mesopotamia, Acts 7:4*
G5466.strongs: probably of Hebrew or (H03778); a Chaldæan (i.e. Kasdi), or native or the region of the lower Euphrates

G4084.thayer: I lay hold of, apprehend, catch, arrest.
G4084.mounce: to press; in NT to take or lay hold of, Acts 3:7; to take, catch fish, etc., Jn. 21:3, 10; Rev. 19:20; to take, seize, apprehend, arrest, Jn. 7:30, 32, 44
G4084.strongs: probably another form of G971 (βιάζω); to squeeze, i.e. seize (gently by the hand (press), or officially (arrest), or in hunting (capture))
G4084.kjv: apprehend, catch, lay hand on, take

G5061.thayer: Tertullus, a barrister acting as professional prosecutor of Paul at Caesarea.
G5061.mounce: Tertullus, pr. name, an attorney, Acts 24:1f.*
G5061.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Tertullus, a Roman

G5456.thayer: a sound, noise, voice, language, dialect.
G5456.mounce: a sound, Mt. 24:31; Jn. 3:8; Rev. 4:5; 8:5; a cry, Mt. 2:18; an articulate sound, voice, Mt. 3:3, 17; 17:5; 27:46, 50; voice, speech, discourse, Jn. 10:16, 27; Acts 7:31; 12:22; 13:27; Heb. 3:7, 15; tone of address, Gal. 4:20; language, tongue, dialect, 1 Cor. 14:10
G5456.abbottSmith: a voice ; prop., of persons, Mt 2:18 (LXX) , al.; φ. αἴρειν (ἐπαίρειν) , Lk 17:13 , Ac 2:14 , al.; φ. μεγάλῃ εἰπεῖν ( λέγειν, φωνεῖν , etc.), Lk 8:28 , Ac 7:57 , Re 5:12 , al.; γίνεται (ἔρχεται) φ. ἐκ τ. οὐρανῶν (ἐξ οὐρανοῦ) , Mk 1:11 , Lk 3:22 , Jo 12:28 , al. (cf. DCG , ii, 810 a ; Dalman, Words , 204 f.); ἀκούειν φωνήν ( -ῆς ; v.s. ἀκούω ), Ac 9:4 , 7 al.; φ. βοῶντος , Mt 3:3 , Mk 1:3 , Lk 3:4
G5456.strongs: probably akin to G5316 (φαίνω) through the idea of disclosure; a tone (articulate, bestial or artificial); by implication, an address (for any purpose), saying or language

G4857.abbottSmith: concord, agreement : seq. πρός , c. acc. pers., II Co 6:15 .†

G2040.thayer: a field-laborer; then: a laborer, workman in general.
G2040.mounce: a workman, laborer, Mt. 9:37, 38; 20:1, 2, 8; met. a spiritual workman or laborer, 2 Cor. 11:13; an artisan, artificer, Acts 19:25; a worker, practicer, Lk. 13:27
G2040.abbottSmith: prop., a field labourer, husbandman : Mt 9:37 , 38 20:1 , 2 , 8 , Lk 10:2 , Ja 5:4 (cf. Wi, l.c. ).
G2040.strongs: from G2041 (ἔργον); a toiler; figuratively, a teacher

G1462.strongs: from G1458 (ἐγκαλέω); an accusation, i.e. offence alleged
G1462.kjv: crime laid against, laid to charge

G677.thayer: (free from hurt or harm, hence) not offending, not causing offence, blameless.
G677.mounce: not stumbling or jarring; met. not stumbling or jarring against moral rule, unblamable, clear, Acts 24:16; Phil. 1:10; free from offensiveness, 1 Cor. 10:32*
G677.abbottSmith: act., not causing to stumble : metaph., of not leading others into sin, I Co 10:32 .
G677.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4350 (προσκόπτω); actively, inoffensive, i.e. not leading into sin; passively, faultless, i.e. not led into sin

G1860.mounce: annunciation, 2 Tim. 1:1; a promise, act of promising, Acts 13:23, 32; 23:21; meton. the thing promised, promised favor and blessing, Lk. 24:49; Acts 1:4
G1860.abbottSmith: a summons (as Attic law-term, Dem., al.).
G1860.strongs: from G1861 (ἐπαγγέλλω); an announcement (for information, assent or pledge; especially a divine assurance of good)

G4309.thayer: I foreordain, predetermine, mark out beforehand.
G4309.mounce: to limit or mark out beforehand; to design definitely beforehand, ordain beforehand, predestine, Acts 4:28; Rom. 8:29, 30
G4309.abbottSmith: to predetermine , foreordain : c. acc., Ro 8:30 ; id. et inf., Ac 4:28 ; id. seq. εἰς , I Co 2:7 , Eph 1:5 ; c. dupl. acc., Ro 8:26 ; pass., Eph 1:11 .†
G4309.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3724 (ὁρίζω); to limit in advance, i.e. (figuratively) predetermine

G4589.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08352); Seth (i.e. Sheth), a patriarch

G456.thayer: I rebuild, build up (what has fallen or been razed to the ground), sometimes merely: I build.
G456.abbottSmith: to build again , rebuild (MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 15:16 .†
G456.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3618 (οἰκοδομέω); to rebuild

G1211.thayer: (a) in a clause expressing demand: so, then, (b) indeed, (c) truly.
G1211.mounce: a particle that adds an intensity of expression to a term or clause. Its simplest and most ordinary uses are when it gives impressiveness to an affirmation, indeed, really, doubtless, Mt. 13:23; or earnestness to a call, injunction, or entreaty, Lk. 2:15; Acts 13:2; 15:36; 1 Cor. 16:20*
G1211.abbottSmith: consecutive co-ordinating particle with no exact equiv. in Eng., giving greater exactness and emphasis to the word or words to which it is attached; sometimes translatable as now therefore, then, verily, certainly .
G1211.strongs: probably akin to G1161 (δέ); a particle of emphasis or explicitness; now, then, etc.

G135.mounce: an enigma, riddle, any thing obscurely expressed or intimated, 1 Cor. 13:12*
G135.abbottSmith: a dark saying , riddle : I Co 13:12 (cf. Nu 12:8 ).†
G135.strongs: from a derivative of G136 (αἶνος) (in its primary sense); an obscure saying ("enigma"), i.e. (abstractly) obscureness

G2861.thayer: (lit: a diving or swimming place), a pool.
G2861.mounce: a place where any one may swim; a pond, pool, Jn. 5:2, 4, 7; 9:7*
G2861.strongs: from G2860 (κολυμβάω); a diving-place, i.e. pond for bathing (or swimming)

G4521.mounce: pr. cessation from labor, rest; the Jewish sabbath, both in the sg. and pl., Mt. 12:2, 5, 8; 28:1; Lk. 4:16; a week, sg. and pl., Mt. 28:1; Mk. 16:9; pl. sabbaths, or times of sacred rest, Col. 2:16
G4521.abbottSmith: the seventh day of the week, the sabbath
G4521.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07676); the Sabbath (i.e. Shabbath), or day of weekly repose from secular avocations (also the observance or institution itself); by extension, a se'nnight, i.e. the interval between two Sabbaths; likewise the plural in all the above applications

G5228.thayer: gen: in behalf of; acc: above.
G5228.mounce: (1) gen., above, over; met. in behalf of, Mt. 5:44; Mk. 9:40; Jn. 17:19; instead of beneficially, Phlm. 13; in maintenance of, Rom. 15:8; for the furtherance of, Jn. 11:4; 2 Cor. 1:6, 8; for the realization of, Phil. 2:13; equivalent to περί, about, concerning, with the further signification of interest or concern in the subject, Acts 5:41; Rom. 9:27; 2 Cor. 5:12; 8:23; 2 Thess. 2:1. (2) acc., over, beyond; met. beyond, more than, Mt. 10:37; 2 Cor. 1:8; used after comparative terms, Lk. 16:8; 2 Cor. 12:13; Heb. 4:12. (3) in NT as an adv., in a higher degree, in fuller measure, 2 Cor. 11:23
G5228.abbottSmith: C. gen., primarily of place (rest or motion), over, above, across, beyond , hence, metaph., for, on behalf of : of prayer, Mt 5:44 , Ac 8:24 , Ro 10:1 , Ja 5:16 , al.; of laying down life, Jo 10:11 , Ro 9:3 , al.; esp. of Christ giving his life for man's redemption, Mk 14:24 , Jo 10:15 , Ac 21:13 , Ro 5:6-8 , al.; opp. to κατά , Mk 9:40 , Lk 9:50 , Ro 8:31 . Causal, for, because of, for the sake o
G5228.strongs: a primary preposition; "over", i.e. (with the genitive case) of place, above, beyond, across, or causal, for the sake of, instead, regarding; with the accusative case superior to, more than
G5228.kjv: (+ exceeding, abundantly) above, in (on) behalf of, beyond, by, + very chiefest, concerning, exceeding (above, -ly), for, + very highly, more (than), of, over, on the part of, for sake of, in stead, than, to(-ward), very

G3081.thayer: it is advantageous to, it profits.
G3081.mounce: pr. to compensate for incurred expense; by impl. to be advantageous to, to profit, advantage; impers. Lk. 17:2*
G3081.strongs: third person singular present indicative active of a derivative of a compound of G3080 (λύσις) and G5056 (τέλος); impersonally, it answers the purpose, i.e. is advantageous

G4216.mounce: borne along or carried away by a flood or torrent, Rev. 12:15*
G4216.abbottSmith: carried away by a stream : Re 12:15 (for two exx. in π. , v. MM, xxi).†
G4216.strongs: from G4215 (ποταμός) and a derivative of G5409 (φορέω); river-borne, i.e. overwhelmed by a stream

G2874.abbottSmith: a dung hill ( I Ki 2:8 , Ps 112 (113):7 al.): Lk 14:35 .†
G2874.strongs: from (ordure; perhaps akin to G2875 (κόπτω)); manure

G125.abbottSmith: Egypt : Mt 2:13-15 , 19 , Ac 2:10 7:9-39 , He 3:16 11:26 , 27 ; γῆ Αἴ. , Ac 7:40 13:17 , He 8:9 , Ju 5 (cf. Ex 5:12 , al.); ἡ Αἴ. , Ac 7:11 ; fig., of Jerusalem as hostile to God, Re 11:8 .†
G125.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Ægyptus, the land of the Nile

G1919.thayer: (a) on the earth, belonging to the earth (as opposed to the sky), (b) in a spiritual sense, belonging to the earthly sphere, earthly (as opposed to heavenly).
G1919.mounce: pr. on the earth, Phil. 2:10; earthly, terrestrial, Jn. 3:12; 1 Cor. 15:40; 2 Cor. 5:1; Phil. 3:19; earthly, low, grovelling, Jas. 3:15*
G1919.abbottSmith: of the earth, earthly : τὰ ἐ. , Jo 3:12 Phl 2:10 3:19 (anarth.); σώματα , I Co 15:40 ; οἰκία , II Co 5:1 ; σοφία , Ja 3:15 (Cremer, 153).†
G1919.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1093 (γῆ); worldly (physically or morally)

G2749.thayer: I lie, recline, am placed, am laid, set, specially appointed, destined.
G2749.mounce: to lie, to be laid; to recline, to be lying, to have been laid down, Mt. 28:6; Lk. 2:12; to have been laid, placed, set, Mt. 3:10; Lk. 3:9; Jn. 2:6; to be situated, as a city, Mt. 5:14; Rev. 21:16; to be in store, Lk. 12:19; met. to be constituted, established as a law, 1 Tim. 1:9; in NT of persons, to be specially set, solemnly appointed, destined, Lk. 2:34; Phil. 1:16; 1 Thess. 3:3; to lie under an influence, to be involved in, 1 Jn. 5:19
G2749.abbottSmith: used as passive of τίθημι (LS, s.v.), to be laid , to lie ; of persons: an infant, Lk 2:12 , 16 ; a dead body, Mt 28:6 , Lk 23:53 , Jo 20:12 ; of things, to lie , be laid or set , stand : Mt 5:14 , Jo 2:6 19:29 20:5-7 21:9 , Re 4:2 21:16 ; trop., Θεμέλιος , I Co 3:11 ; seq. prep. c. acc., πρός , Mt 3:10 , Lk 3:9 ; εἰς , Lk 12:19 ; ἐπί , trop., κάλυμμα , II Co 3:15 . Metaph., ὁ κόσμος ἐν τ. πονηριῷ
G2749.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to lie outstretched (literally or figuratively)
G2749.kjv: be (appointed, laid up, made, set), lay, lie

G5058.mounce: a custom-house, toll house; collector’s office, Mt. 9:9; Mk. 2:14; Lk. 5:27*
G5058.abbottSmith: a custom house (so in MGr.), tollhouse, place of toll : Mt 9:9 , Mk 2:14 , Lk 5:27 .†
G5058.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G5057 (τελώνης); a tax-gatherer's place of business

G4083.thayer: a cubit, about a foot and a half.
G4083.mounce: pr. cubitus, the forearm; hence, a cubit, a measure of length, equal to the distance from the elbow to the extremity of the middle finger, usually considered as equivalent to a foot and one half, or 17 inches and one half, Jn. 21:8; Rev. 21:17; met. of time, a span, Mt. 6:27; Lk. 12:25*
G4083.strongs: of uncertain affinity; the fore-arm, i.e. (as a measure) a cubit

G5622.mounce: help; profit, gain, advantage, benefit, Rom. 3:1; Jude 16*
G5622.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G5624 (ὠφέλιμος); usefulness, i.e. benefit

G4684.thayer: I live extravagantly, luxuriously; I am wanton.
G4684.mounce: to live luxuriously, voluptuously, wantonly, 1 Tim. 5:6; Jas. 5:5*
G4684.abbottSmith: to live riotously : I Ti 5:6 , Ja 5:5 (Polyb.).†

G3668.thayer: in like manner, similarly, in the same way, equally.
G3668.mounce: likewise, in a similar manner, Mt. 22:26; 27:41
G3668.abbottSmith: likewise , in like manner , equally : Mt 22:26 , Mk 4:16 , Lk 10:37 , al.; c. dat., Mt 22:39 , Lk 6:31 ; ὁ. καί , Mt 22:26 , Mk 15:31 , al.; ὁ. καθώς , Lk 17:28 ; καθὼς . . . ὁ. , Lk 6:31 ; ὁ. μέντοι καί , Ju 8 .

G5263.thayer: I indicate, intimate, suggest, show, prove.
G5263.mounce: also spelled ὑποδεικνύω, to indicate, Acts 20:35; to intimate, suggest, show, prove, Mt. 3:7; Lk. 3:7; 6:47; 12:5; Acts 9:16*
G5263.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to exhibit under the eyes, i.e. (figuratively) to exemplify (instruct, admonish)

G5529.thayer: Chuza, a steward of Herod Antipas.
G5529.strongs: of uncertain origin; Chuzas, an officer of Herod

G2233.thayer: (a) I lead, (b) I think, am of opinion, suppose, consider.
G2233.mounce: to lead the way; to take the lead, Acts 14:12; to be chief, to preside, govern, rule, Mt. 2:6; Acts 7:10; ἡγούμενος, a chief officer in the church, Heb. 13:7, 17, 24; also, to think, consider, count, esteem, regard, Acts 26:2; 2 Cor. 9:5
G2233.strongs: middle voice of a (presumed) strengthened form of G71 (ἄγω); to lead, i.e. command (with official authority); figuratively, to deem, i.e. consider
G2233.kjv: account, (be) chief, count, esteem, governor, judge, have the rule over, suppose, think

G3346.thayer: (a) I transfer, mid: I go over to another party, desert, (b) I change.
G3346.mounce: to turn around, change, alter, Jas. 4:9*
G3346.abbottSmith: to transfer to another place: c. acc., pass., He 11:5 (LXX) ; seq. εἰς , Ac 7:16 .
G3346.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G5087 (τίθημι); to transfer, i.e. (literally) transport, (by implication) exchange, (reflexively) change sides, or (figuratively) pervert
G3346.kjv: carry over, change, remove, translate, turn

G3481.thayer: Nathan, son of David, and an ancestor of Jesus.
G3481.mounce: also spelled Ναθάν, Nathan, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:31*
G3481.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05416); Nathan, an Israelite

G3484.thayer: Nain, a city south-west of the Sea of Galilee.
G3484.mounce: Nain, a town of Palestine, indecl., Lk. 7:11*. See PhotoGuide.
G3484.abbottSmith: Nain , a village of Galilee: Lk 7:11 .†
G3484.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H04999); Nain, a place in Palestine

G5286.abbottSmith: a footstool (= cl. θρᾶνος ): Ja 2:3 ; metaph., Mt 5:35 , Mk 12:36 ( ὑποκάτω , WH, R, mg.), Lk 20:43 , Ac 2:35 7:49 , He 1:13 10:13 (all, except Mt, l.c. , from LXX, Ps 109 (110):1 , Is 66:1 ) (for exx., v. Deiss., BS , 223).†
G5286.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G4228 (πούς); something under the feet, i.e. a foot-rest (figuratively)

G143.abbottSmith: to perceive : c.acc. rei (Bl., §36, 5; MM, VGT , s.v.), Lk 9:45 (Cremer, 619 f.).†
G143.strongs: of uncertain derivation; to apprehend (properly, by the senses)

G1393.thayer: Dorcas, the Greek name of Tabitha.
G1393.mounce: Dorcas, pr. name, signifying a gazelle or antelope, Acts 9:36, 39*
G1393.abbottSmith: Dorcas , also called Ταβειθά , q.v.: Ac 9:36 , 39 .†

G4745.mounce: a colonnade, cloister, covered walk supported by columns, Jn. 5:2; 10:23; Acts 3:11; 5:12*
G4745.abbottSmith: a portico : Jo 5:2 ; used of the covered colonnade in the Temple (EV, porch ), Jo 10:23 , Ac 3:11 5:12 †
G4745.strongs: probably from G2476 (ἵστημι); a colonnade or interior piazza

G1627.thayer: I bring out, carry out, sometimes out of the city for burial; I bring forth, bear, produce.
G1627.mounce: to bring forth, carry out, Lk. 15:22; Acts 5:15; 1 Tim. 6:7; to carry out for burial, Acts 5:6, 9, 10; to produce, yield, Mk. 8:23; Heb. 6:8*
G1627.abbottSmith: to carry out, bring out : cc. acc rei, Lk 15:22 , I Ti 6:7 ; c. acc pers., Mk 8:23 , Ac 5:15 ; of the dead for burial (cf. κομίζω ), Ac 5:6 , 9 , 10 .
G1627.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear out (literally or figuratively)

G3757.thayer: where, whither, when, in what place.
G3757.abbottSmith: where , whither ; in answer to the question "where?" (= ubi ): Mt 2:9 18:20 , Lk 4:16 , 17 23:53 , Ac 1:13 2:2 7:29 12:12 16:13 20:6 , 8 25:10 28:14 , Ro 9:26 (LXX) , Col 3:1 , He 3:9 (LXX) , Re 17:15 ; of condition, Ro 4:15 5:20 , II Co 3:17 ; in answer to the question "whither?" (= quo ): Mt 28:16 , Lk 10:1 24:28 ; seq. ἐάν , c. subjc., I Co 16:6 .†
G3757.strongs: genitive case of G3739 (ὅς) as adverb; at which place, i.e. where

G2566.mounce: pr. beautiful; good, of good quality or disposition; fertile, rich, Mt. 13:8, 23; useful, profitable, Lk. 14:34; καλόν ἐστι[ν], it is profitable, it is well, Mt. 18:8, 9; excellent, choice, select, goodly, Mt. 7:17, 19; καλόν ἐστι[ν], it is pleasant, delightful, Mt. 17:4; just, full measure, Lk. 6:38; honorable, distinguished, Jas. 2:7; good, possessing moral excellence, worthy, upright, virtuous, Jn. 10:11, 14; 1 Tim. 4:6; τὸ καλόν, and τὸ καλόν ἔργον, what is good and right, a good deed, rectitude, virtue, Mt. 5:16; Rom. 7:18, 21; right, duty, propriety, Mt. 15:26; benefit, favor, Jn. 10:32, 33
G2566.strongs: neuter of the (irregular) comparative of G2570 (καλός); (adverbially) better than many

G3436.abbottSmith: defilement : c. gen. obj., II Co 7:1 (Plut., FlJ).†
G3436.strongs: from G3435 (μολύνω); a stain; i.e. (figuratively) immorality

G5300.mounce: a hog, swine, boar, sow, 2 Pet. 2:22*
G5300.strongs: apparently a primary word; a hog ("swine")

G1504.mounce: a material image, likeness, effigy, Mt. 22:20; Mk. 12:16; a representation, exact image, 1 Cor. 11:7; 15:49; Rev. 13:14f.; resemblance, Rom. 1:23; 8:29; Col. 3:10; Heb. 10:1
G1504.abbottSmith: an image, likeness : Mt 22:20 , Mk 12:16 , Lk 20:24 , Ro 1:23 , I Co 15:49 , Re 13:14 , 15 14:9 , 11 15:2 16:2 19:20 20:4 ; opp. to σκιά , He 10:1 ; of man, εἰ. θεοῦ , I Co 11:7 ; of the regenerate, εἰ. τ. θεοῦ Col 3:10 (v. Lft., in l.); εἰ. τ. υἱοῦ τ. θεοῦ , Ro 8:29 , II Co 3:18 ; of Christ, εἰ. τ. θεοῦ , II Co 4:4 , Col 1:15 .† SYN. : ὁμοίωμα , denoting resemblance, which may however be merely a
G1504.strongs: from G1503 (εἴκω); a likeness, i.e. (literally) statue, profile, or (figuratively) representation, resemblance

G3511.mounce: pr. one who sweeps or cleanses a temple; generally, one who has the charge of a temple; in NT a devotee city, as having specially dedicated a temple to some deity, Acts 19:35*
G3511.abbottSmith: a temple-keeper ; as honorary title given to a city (v. DB , i, 722 b): Ac 19:35 .†
G3511.strongs: from a form of G3485 (ναός) and (to sweep); a temple-servant, i.e. (by implication) a votary

G1607.thayer: I depart from; I am voided, cast out; I proceed from, am spoken; I burst forth, flow out, am spread abroad.
G1607.mounce: to go from or out of a place, depart from, Mk. 11:19; 13:1; to be voided, Mk. 7:19; to be cast out, Mt. 17:21; to proceed from, be spoken, Mt. 4:4; 15:11; to burst forth, Rev. 4:5; to be spread abroad, Lk. 4:37; to flow out, Rev. 22:1; from the Hebrew, ἐκπορεύομαι καὶ εἰσπορεύομαι. see εἰσέρχομαι, Acts 9:28
G1607.abbottSmith: to make to go out ; pass. and mid., to go forth : Lk 3:7 , Ac 25:4 ; of demons leaving one possessed, Mt 17:21 (WH om.), Ac 19:12 ; of excrement, Mk 7:19 ; seq. ἀπό , Mt 20:29 , Mk 10:46 ; ἐκ , Mk 13:1 (of the dead rising, Jo 5:29 ); ἐκεῖθεν , Mk 6:11 ; ἔξω , Mk 11:19 ; είς , Mk 10:17 , Jo 5:29 ; ἐπί , c. acc pers., Re 16:14 ; πρός , c. acc pers., Mt 3:5 , Mk 1:5 ; εἰσπορ- (q.v.) καὶ ἐ. , Ac 9:28
G1607.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to depart, be discharged, proceed, project
G1607.kjv: come (forth, out of), depart, go (forth, out), issue, proceed (out of)

G3896.mounce: to sail by or past a place, Acts 20:16*
G3896.abbottSmith: to sail by or past : c. acc., Ac 20:16 .†
G3896.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G4126 (πλέω); to sail near

G1578.thayer: (lit: I bend away from), I fall away from, turn away (from), deviate.
G1578.mounce: to deflect, deviate, Rom. 3:12; to decline or turn away from, avoid, Rom. 16:17; 1 Pet. 3:11
G1578.abbottSmith: intrans., to turn aside, turn away : metaph., from the right path , absol., Ro 3:12 (LXX) ; from evil, absol., I Pe 3:11 ; seq. ἀπό , c. gen. pers., Ro 16:17 .†
G1578.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2827 (κλίνω); to deviate, i.e. (absolutely) to shun (literally or figuratively), or (relatively) to decline (from piety)
G1578.kjv: avoid, eschew, go out of the way

G470.thayer: I contradict, reply against, give a hostile answer.
G470.mounce: occurs in the NT only in the middle, to answer, speak in answer, Lk. 14:6; to reply against, contradict, dispute, Rom. 9:20*
G470.abbottSmith: to answer again , reply against : seq. πρός , c. acc. rei, Lk 14:6 ; c. dat. pers., Ro 9:20 .†
G470.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G611 (ἀποκρίνομαι); to contradict or dispute

G1222.thayer: of course, indeed, qualifying and yet strengthening the assertion.
G1222.mounce: now in some way, surely, Heb. 2:16*
G1222.abbottSmith: mostly in sense of surely, of course, we know : He 2:16 T (WH, δή που ).†
G1222.strongs: from G1211 (δή) and G4225 (πού); a particle of asseveration; indeed doubtless

G4738.abbottSmith: the breast : Jo 13:25 21:10 , Re 15:6 ; as a sign of penitence, τύπτειν ( εἰς ) τὸ σ. , Lk 18:13 23:48 ,†
G4738.strongs: from G2476 (ἵστημι) (as standing prominently); the (entire external) bosom, i.e. chest

G2212.thayer: I seek, search for, desire, require, demand.
G2212.mounce: to seek, look for, Mt. 18:12; Lk. 2:48, 49; to search after, Mt. 13:45; to be on the watch for, Mt. 26:16; to pursue, endeavor to obtain, Rom. 2:7; 1 Pet. 3:11; to desire, wish, want, Mt. 12:47; to seek, strive for, Mt. 6:33; to endeavor, Mt. 21:46; to require, demand, ask for, Mk. 8:11; Lk. 11:16; 12:48; to inquire or ask questions, question, Jn. 16:19; to deliberate, Mk. 11:18; Lk. 12:29; in NT from Hebrew, ζητεῖν τὴν ψυχήν, to seek the life of any one, to seek to kill, Mt. 2:20
G2212.abbottSmith: to seek , seek for : Mt 7:7 , 8 , Lk 11:9 , 10 ; c. acc. pers., Mk 1:37 , Lk 2:48 , Jo 6:24 , al.; id. seq. ἐν , Ac 9:11 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 13:45 , Lk 19:10 ; seq. ἐν , Lk 13:6 , 7 ; ψυχήν , of plotting against one's life ( Ex 4:19 , al.), Mt 2:20 , Ro 11:3 (LXX) . Metaph., to seek by thinking, search after , inquire into : Mk 11:18 , Lk 12:28 , Jo 16:19 ; τ. θεόν , Ac 17:27 . to seek or strive aft
G2212.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to seek (literally or figuratively); specially, (by Hebraism) to worship (God), or (in a bad sense) to plot (against life)
G2212.kjv: be (go) about, desire, endeavour, enquire (for), require, (X will) seek (after, for, means)

G4248.mounce: prone, head-foremost; πρηνὴς γενόμενος, falling head-long, Acts 1:18*
G4248.strongs: from G4253 (πρό); leaning (falling) forward ("prone"), i.e. head foremost

G2238.abbottSmith: sweet-smelling ; as subst., τὸ ἡ. , mint : Mt 23:23 , Lk 11:42 .†
G2238.strongs: neuter of the compound of the same as G2234 (ἡδέως) and G3744 (ὀσμή); a sweet-scented plant, i.e. mint

G4674.mounce: yours, Mt. 7:3, 22; οἱ σοί, your kindred, friends, etc., Mk. 5:19; τὸ σόν and τὰ σά, what is yours, your property, goods, etc., Mt. 20:14; 25:25; Lk. 6:30

G1513.abbottSmith: Conditional, if ; c. indic., expressing a general assumption; pres.: seq. indic, pres., Mt 11:14 , Ro 8:25 , al.; seq. imperat., Mk 4:23 9:22 , Jo 15:18 , I Co 7:9 , al.; seq. fut. indic., Lk 16:31 , Ro 8:11 , al.; seq. pf. or aor., with negation in apodosis, Mt 12:26 , Ro 4:14 , al.; similarly, seq. impf., Lk 17:6 , Jo 8:39 ; seq. quæst., Mt 6:23 , Jo 5:47 7:23 8:46 , I Pe 2:20 ; fut.: Mt 26:33 ,
G1513.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G4458 (-πώς); if somehow

G612.abbottSmith: an answering , an answer : Lk 2:47 20:26 , Jo 1:22 19:9 .†

G1260.mounce: pr. to make a settlement of accounts; to reason, deliberate, ponder, consider, Mt. 16:7, 8; Mk. 2:6, 8; Jn. 11:50; to dispute, contend, Mk. 9:33
G1260.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3049 (λογίζομαι); to reckon thoroughly, i.e. (genitive case) to deliberate (by reflection or discussion)
G1260.kjv: cast in mind, consider, dispute, muse, reason, think

G102.thayer: of persons: incapable; of things: impossible; either the inability, or that which is impossible.
G102.abbottSmith: of persons, unable , powerless : Ac 14:8 ; fig. Ro 15:1 (MM, VGT , s.v.). Of things, impossible : Mt 19:26 , Mk 10:27 , Lk 18:27 , Ro 8:3 , He 6:4 , 18 10:4 11:6 .†
G102.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1415 (δυνατός); unable, i.e. weak (literally or figuratively); passively, impossible
G102.kjv: could not do, impossible, impotent, not possible, weak

G4035.thayer: I leave behind; pass: I am left behind, remain, survive.
G4035.mounce: to leave remaining; pass. to remain, survive, 1 Thess. 4:15, 17*
G4035.abbottSmith: to be left remaining , remain over , survive : I Th 4:15 , 17 .†
G4035.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave all around, i.e. (passively) survive

G2348.thayer: I die, am dying, am dead.
G2348.mounce: to die; in NT to be dead, Mt. 2:20; Mk. 15:44
G2348.abbottSmith: to die ; pf. (M, Pr. , 114), to be dead : Mt 2:20 , Mk 15:44 , Lk 7:12 8:49 , Jo 11:44 19:33 , Ac 14:19 25:19 ; metaph., of spiritual death, I Ti 5:6 (cf. ἀπο- , συν-απο-θνήσκω ).†
G2348.strongs: a strengthened form of a simpler primary (which is used for it only in certain tenses); to die (literally or figuratively)

G1084.mounce: born or produced of, Mt. 11:11; Lk. 7:28*
G1084.abbottSmith: begotten, born : pl., γ. γυναικῶν (cf. יְלוּד אִשָּׁה , Jb 14:1 ), periphrasis for mankind, Mt 11:11 , Lk 7:28 (Cremer, 147).

G2565.mounce: pr. adj. productive of good oil; as subst. a cultivated olive tree, Rom. 11:24*
G2565.abbottSmith: the garden olive (opp. to ἀγριέλ- ): Ro 11:24 .†
G2565.strongs: from the base of G2566 (καλλίον) and G1636 (ἐλαία); a cultivated olive tree, i.e. a domesticated or improved one

G3894.abbottSmith: provocation : ἐν τῷ π. (Heb. at Meribah ), He 3:8 , 15 (LXX) .†

G2472.mounce: of equal price, equally precious or valuable, 2 Pet. 1:1*
G2472.abbottSmith: equally privileged , held in equal honour : πίστις , II Pe 1:1 (R, mg., equally precious , but v Field, Notes , 240; Mayor, in l.).†
G2472.strongs: from G2470 (ἴσος) and G5092 (τιμή); of equal value or honor

G2129.mounce: pr. good speaking; fair speech, flattery, Rom. 16:18; in NT blessing, praise, celebration, 1 Cor. 10:16; Rev. 5:12, 13; invocation of good, benediction, Jas. 3:10; a divine blessing, Rom. 15:29; a gift, benevolence, 2 Cor. 9:5; a frank gift, as opposed to πλεονεξία, 2 Cor. 9:5; ἐπ’ εὐλογίαις, liberally, 2 Cor. 9:6
G2129.abbottSmith: fair speaking, flattering speech : χρηστολογίας καὶ εὐ. , Ro 16:18 .
G2129.strongs: from the same as G2127 (εὐλογέω); fine speaking, i.e. elegance of language; commendation ("eulogy"), i.e. (reverentially) adoration; religiously, benediction; by implication, consecration; by extension, benefit or largess
G2129.kjv: blessing (a matter of) bounty (X -tifully), fair speech

G884.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G5483 (χαρίζομαι); thankless, i.e. ungrateful

G1937.thayer: I long for, covet, lust after, set the heart upon.
G1937.mounce: with the gen. or acc., to set the heart upon; to desire, long for, have earnest desire, Mt. 13:17; Lk. 15:16; to lust after, Mt. 5:28; spc. to covet, Rom. 13:9
G1937.abbottSmith: to set one's heart upon, desire, lust after, covet : absol., Ja 4:2 , Ro 7:7 13:9 (LXX) , I Co 10:6 (of. IV Mac 2:6 ); seq. κατά (against, in opposition to), Ga 5:17 ; prop. (as in cl.) c. gen., Ac 20:33 , I Ti 3:1 ; in late Gk. also c. acc (M, Pr. , 65), Mt 5:28 (Rec. αὐτῆς ; T omits; cf. Ex 20:17 , Mi 2:2 , Wi 16:3 , al.); c. inf., Mt 13:17 , Lk 15:16 16:21 17:22 , I Pe 1:12 , Re 9:6 ; c. acc et
G1937.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2372 (θυμός); to set the heart upon, i.e. long for (rightfully or otherwise)

G2952.thayer: a little dog, a house dog.
G2952.mounce: a little or worthless dog, Mt. 15:26, 27; Mk. 7:27, 28*
G2952.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G2965 (κύων); a puppy

G818.thayer: I disgrace, treat disgracefully, dishonor, insult; I despise.
G818.mounce: also spelled ἀτιμάω and ἀντιμόω, to dishonor, slight, Jn. 8:49; Rom. 2:23; Jas. 2:6; to treat with indignity, Mk. 12:4; Lk. 20:11; Acts 5:41; to abuse, debase, Rom. 1:24*
G818.abbottSmith: to dishonour , insult : Mk 12:4 , TTr., mg., WH, Lk 20:11 , Jo 8:49 , Ro 2:23 , Ja 2:6 ; pass.: Ac 5:41 , Ro 1:24 (cf ἀτιμάω ).†
G818.strongs: from G820 (ἄτιμος); to render infamous, i.e. (by implication) contemn or maltreat
G818.kjv: despise, dishonour, suffer shame, entreat shamefully

G1866.abbottSmith: Epænetus , a Christian of Rome: Ro 16:5 .†
G1866.strongs: from G1867 (ἐπαινέω); praised; Epænetus, a Christian

G4393.mounce: to bring before, present; to bring forth or out, produce, Lk. 6:45 (2x)*
G4393.abbottSmith: to bring forth : c. acc. rei, seq. ἐκ , Lk 6:45 .†
G4393.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear forward, i.e. produce

G1059.thayer: Gamaliel, a noted Pharisee, teacher of Saul.
G1059.abbottSmith: Gamaliel the elder, a Pharisee and Doctor of the Law: Ac 5:34 22:3 .†
G1059.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01583); Gamaliel (i.e. Gamliel), an Israelite

G1573.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2556 (κακός); to be (bad or) weak, i.e. (by implication) to fail (in heart)

G5077.thayer: I reduce to ashes, consume, destroy.
G5077.mounce: to reduce to ashes, to consume, destroy, 2 Pet. 2:6*
G5077.abbottSmith: to burn to ashes : II Pe 2:6 .†

G4108.thayer: adj: misleading, deceiving, wandering; as subst: a deceiver, imposter.
G4108.mounce: a wanderer, vagabond; also act. deceiving, seducing; a deceiver, impostor, Mt. 27:63; 2 Cor. 6:8; 1 Tim. 4:1; 2 Jn. 7*
G4108.strongs: of uncertain affinity; roving (as a tramp), i.e. (by implication) an impostor or misleader;

G3777.mounce: neither, nor, Lk. 20:36; οὔτε ... οὔτε, or οὐτὲ ... οὔτε, neither ... nor, Lk. 20:35; Gal. 1:12; in NT also used singly in the sense of οὐδέ, not even, Mk. 5:3; Lk. 12:26; 1 Cor. 3:2
G3777.abbottSmith: and not , neither , nor : οὐδεὶς . . . οὔτε , Re 5:4 ; οὐδὲ . . . οὔτε , Ga 1:12 ; οὔτε . . . καί , Jo 4:11 ; after a question with μή interrog., Ja 3:12 ; οὔτε . . . οὔτε , neither . . . nor , Mt 6:20 , Mk 12:25 , Jo 4:21 , Ac 15:10 , Ro 8:38 , 39 , Ga 5:6 , al.
G3777.strongs: from G3756 (οὐ) and G5037 (τέ); not too, i.e. neither or nor; by analogy, not even
G3777.kjv: neither, none, nor (yet), (no, yet) not, nothing

G1969.thayer: I rebuke, chide, reprove, strike at.
G1969.mounce: pr. to inflict blows upon; met. to chide, reprove, 1 Tim. 5:1*
G1969.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4141 (πλήσσω); to chastise, i.e. (with words) to upbraid

G5426.thayer: (a) I think, (b) I think, judge, (c) I direct the mind to, seek for, (d) I observe, (e) I care for.
G5426.mounce: to think, to mind; to be of opinion, Acts 28:22; Phil. 1:7; to take thought, be considerate, Phil. 4:10; to entertain sentiments or inclinations of a specific kind, to be minded, Rom. 12:16; 15:5; 1 Cor. 13:11; 2 Cor. 13:11; Gal. 5:10; Phil. 2:2; 3:16; 4:2; to be in a certain frame of mind, Rom. 12:3; Phil. 2:5; to imagine, entertain conceit, 1 Cor. 4:6; to heed, pay regard to, Rom. 14:6; to incline to, be set upon, mind, Mt. 16:23; Mk. 8:33; Rom. 8:5; Phil. 3:15, 19; Col. 3:2
G5426.strongs: from G5424 (φρήν); to exercise the mind, i.e. entertain or have a sentiment or opinion; by implication, to be (mentally) disposed (more or less earnestly in a certain direction); intensively, to interest oneself in (with concern or obedience)
G5426.kjv: set the affection on, (be) care(-ful), (be like-, + be of one, + be of the same, + let this) mind(-ed), regard, savour, think

G5129.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5129.strongs: dative case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); to (in, with or by) this (person or thing)
G5129.kjv: here(-by, -in), him, one, the same, there(-in), this

G1890.thayer: I foam out (a metaphor from the seaweed and refuse borne on the crest of waves), vomit forth.
G1890.mounce: to foam out; to pour out like foam, vomit forth, Jude 13*
G1890.abbottSmith: to foam up : metaph., τ. αἰσχύνας , Ju 13 .†
