G1890.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G875 (ἀφρίζω); to foam upon, i.e. (figuratively) to exhibit (a vile passion)

G4917.thayer: I break, break in pieces, crush, shatter.
G4917.mounce: to crush together; to break in pieces, shatter, Mt. 21:44; Lk. 20:18*
G4917.abbottSmith: to crush together, crush : Mt 21:44 ([WH], R, mg., om.), Lk 20:18 .†
G4917.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and thlao (to crush); to dash together, i.e. shatter

G5606.strongs: perhaps from the alternate of G5342 (φέρω); the shoulder (as that on which burdens are borne)

G3052.mounce: gifted with learning or eloquence, Acts 18:24*
G3052.abbottSmith: in cl., learned ( Ac 18:24 , R, txt.).
G3052.strongs: from G3056 (λόγος); fluent, i.e. an orator

G3001.mounce: a garden herb, vegetable, Mt. 13:32; Mk. 4:32; Lk. 11:42; Rom. 14:2*
G3001.abbottSmith: a garden herb , vegetable : Lk 11:42 ; usually in pl., Mt 13:32 , Mk 4:32 , Ro 14:2 .†

G3565.thayer: (a) a bride, young wife, young woman, (b) a daughter-in-law.
G3565.mounce: a bride, Jn. 3:29; Rev. 18:23; 21:2, 9; 22:17; opposed to πενθερά, a daughter-in-law, Mt. 10:35; Lk. 12:53*
G3565.abbottSmith: cl., a bride , young wife , young woman : Mt 25:1 , WH, mg., Jo 3:29 , Re 18:23 21:2 , 9 22:17 .
G3565.strongs: from a primary but obsolete verb (to veil as a bride; compare Latin "nupto," to marry); a young married woman (as veiled), including a betrothed girl; by implication, a son's wife

G185.mounce: pr. unmixed: hence, without mixture of vice or deceit, sincere, blameless, Mt. 10:16; Rom. 16:19; Phil. 2:15*
G185.abbottSmith: unmixed , pure , hence, metaph. (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.), guileless , simple : Mt 10:16 , Ro 16:19 , Phl 2:15 .†
G185.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G2767 (κεράννυμι); unmixed, i.e. (figuratively) innocent

G4223.thayer: Puteoli, the great harbor for traffic with Alexandria on the Bay of Naples.
G4223.mounce: Puteoli, a town of Italy, Acts 28:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G4223.abbottSmith: Puteoli (mod. Pozzuoli ), a city on the Bay of Naples: Ac 28:13 .†
G4223.strongs: of Latin origin; little wells, i.e. mineral springs; Potioli (i.e. Puteoli), a place in Italy

G3034.mounce: to stone, pelt or kill with stones, Jn. 8:5; 10:31, 32, 33; 11:8; Acts 5:26; 14:19; 2 Cor. 11:25; Heb. 11:37*

G4624.thayer: I cause to stumble, cause to sin, cause to become indignant, shock, offend.
G4624.mounce: pr. to cause to stumble; met. offend, Mt. 17:27; to offend, shock, excite feeling of repugnance, Jn. 6:61; 1 Cor. 8:13; pass. to be offended, shocked, pained, Mt. 15:12; Rom. 14:21; 2 Cor. 11:29; σκανδαλίζεσθαι ἔν τινι, to be affected with scruples of repugnance towards any one as respects his claims or pretensions, Mt. 11:6; 13:57; met. to cause to stumble morally, to cause to falter or err, Mt. 5:29; 18:6; pass. to falter, fall away, Mt. 13:21
G4624.abbottSmith: prop., to put a snare or stumbling-block in the way; in NT always metaph. of that which hinders right conduct or thought, to cause to stumble : c. acc. pers., Mt 5:29, 30 17:27 18:6 , 8, 9 Mk 9:42, 43 , 45 , 47 Lk 17:2 , Jo 6:61 , I Co 8:13 . Pass., to be made to stumble, to stumble : Mt 11:6 13:21 , 57 15:12 24:10 26:31 , 33 , Mk 4:17 6:3 14:27 , 29 , Lk 7:23 , Jo 16:1 , Ro 14:21 (WH, R, txt., om
G4624.strongs: from G4625 (σκάνδαλον); to entrap, i.e. trip up (figuratively, stumble (transitively) or entice to sin, apostasy or displeasure)

G1313.mounce: different, diverse, of different kinds, Rom. 12:6; Heb. 9:10; excellent, superior, Heb. 1:4; 8:6*

G4161.thayer: a thing made, a work, workmanship.
G4161.mounce: that which is made or done; a work, workmanship, creation, Rom. 1:20; met. Eph. 2:10*
G4161.abbottSmith: that which is made or done , a work : Ro 1:20 , Eph 2:10 .†
G4161.strongs: from G4160 (ποιέω); a product, i.e. fabric (literally or figuratively)

G1468.mounce: strong, stout; possessed of mastery; master of self, Tit. 1:8*
G1468.abbottSmith: strong, powerful. C. gen. rei, master of , hence, (sc. ἑαυτοῦ ), self -controlled, exercising self-control : Tit 1:8 .†
G1468.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2904 (κράτος); strong in a thing (masterful), i.e. (figuratively and reflexively) self-controlled (in appetite, etc.)

G1397.mounce: slavery, bondage, servile condition; in NT met. with reference to degradation and unhappiness, Rom. 8:15, 21; Gal. 4:24; 5:1; Heb. 2:15*

G5519.mounce: pr. a young swine; a swine, hog, or sow, Mt. 8:30, 31, 32

G2110.abbottSmith: a benefactor : Lk 22:25 (for contemp. usage, v. Deiss., LAE , 248).†
G2110.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); a worker of good, i.e. (specially) a philanthropist

G4181.thayer: in many parts (one at one time, another at another, and so on).
G4181.mounce: in many parts or ways, Heb. 1:1*
G4181.abbottSmith: in many parts or portions : He 1:1 (Plut., al.).†
G4181.strongs: adverb from a compound of G4183 (πολύς) and G3313 (μέρος); in many portions, i.e. variously as to time and agency (piecemeal)

G3094.strongs: feminine of a derivative of G3093 (Μαγδαλά); a female Magdalene, i.e. inhabitant of Magdala

G1000.thayer: a casting, throw; in acc: as measure of distance.
G1000.mounce: a cast, a throw; the distance to which a thing can be thrown, Lk. 22:41*
G1000.abbottSmith: a throw ; λίθου β. (Thuc., v, 65, 2), a stone's throw : Lk 22:41 .†
G1000.strongs: from G906 (βάλλω); a throw (as a measure of distance)

G3091.mounce: Lot, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 17:28, 29, 32; 2 Pet. 2:7*
G3091.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03876); Lot, a patriarch

G4894.thayer: I know, consider, am privy to.
G4894.mounce: perceive, become aware of, realize, Acts 12:12; 14:6*
G4894.abbottSmith: to see together or at the same time (Arist., Xen.).
G4894.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1492 (εἴδω); to see completely; used (like its primary) only in two past tenses, respectively meaning to understand or become aware, and to be conscious or (clandestinely) informed of
G4894.kjv: consider, know, be privy, be ware of

G4481.thayer: Rephan, the Saturn of later mythology.
G4481.strongs: by incorrect transliteration for a word of Hebrew origin (H03594); Remphan (i.e. Kijun), an Egyptian idol

G2718.thayer: I come down from sky to earth, or from high land to lower land (or to the coast), or from the high seas to the shore.
G2718.mounce: to come or go down, Lk. 4:31; 9:37; Acts 8:5; 9:32; to land at, touch at, Acts 18:22; 27:5
G2718.abbottSmith: to come down , go down : seq. εἰς , Lk 4:31 , Ac 8:5 13:4 15:30 19:1 , Τ; ἀπό , Lk 9:37 , Ac 15:1 18:5 21:10 ; ἀπό et εἰς , Ac 11:27 12:10 ; of coming to port by ship, Ac 18:22 21:3 27:5 ; seq. πρός , c. acc. pers., Ac 9:32 ; metaph., of gifts from God, Ja 3:15 .†
G2718.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι) (including its alternate); to come (or go) down (literally or figuratively)
G2718.kjv: come (down), depart, descend, go down, land

G4500.thayer: with a great noise, with a rushing sound.
G4500.mounce: with a noise, with a crash, etc., 2 Pet. 3:10*
G4500.abbottSmith: with rushing sound (as of roaring flames): II Pe 3:10 .†
G4500.strongs: adverb from a derivative of (a whir); whizzingly, i.e. with a crash

G3795.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G3702 (ὀπτός); a relish to other food (as if cooked sauce), i.e. (specially), fish (presumably salted and dried as a condiment)

G4501.thayer: a sword, scimitar; fig: war, piercing grief.
G4501.mounce: pr. a Thracian broad-sword; a sword, Rev. 1:16; 2:12; by meton. war, Rev. 6:8; met. a thrill of anguish, Lk. 2:35
G4501.abbottSmith: a large broad sword , used by the Thracians (v. DB , iv, 634); then generally (in LXX used interchangeably with μάχαιρα , q.v.), a sword : Re 1:16 2:12 , 16 6:8 19:15 , 21 ; metaph., Lk 2:35 .†
G4501.strongs: probably of foreign origin; a sabre, i.e. a long and broad cutlass (any weapon of the kind, literally or figuratively)

G393.thayer: I make to rise, I rise, shine (generally of the sun, and hence met.).
G393.mounce: to cause to rise, Mt. 5:45; intrans. to rise, as the sun, stars, etc., Mt. 4:16; to spring by birth, Heb. 7:14
G393.abbottSmith: trans., to cause to rise : Mt 5:45 .
G393.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the base of G5056 (τέλος); to (cause to) arise
G393.kjv: (a-, make to) rise, at the rising of, spring (up), be up

G2670.thayer: I sink in the sea, pass: I drown, am submerged.
G2670.mounce: to sink in the sea; pass. to sink, Mt. 14:30; to be plunged, submerged, drowned, Mt. 18:6*
G2670.abbottSmith: to throw into the sea , sink or drown therein: Mt 14:30 ; pass., 18:6 .†
G2670.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of the same as G4195 (Πόντος); to plunge down, i.e. submerge

G3934.thayer: a Parthian, an inhabitant of the country beyond the eastern boundary of the Roman Empire between the Caspian Sea and the Persian Gulf.
G3934.mounce: a Parthian, a native of Parthia, in central Asia, Acts 2:9
G3934.strongs: probably of foreign origin; a Parthian, i.e. inhabitant of Parthia

G2333.thayer: Theudas, a Jewish pretender of date about 4 B.C., otherwise unknown.
G2333.strongs: of uncertain origin; Theudas, an Israelite

G2649.mounce: to witness or testify against, Mt. 26:62; 27:13; Mk. 14:60*
G2649.abbottSmith: to bear witness againt : c. acc. rei et gen. pers., Mt 26:62 27:13 , Mk 14:60 .†
G2649.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3140 (μαρτυρέω); to testify against

G4094.mounce: pr. a board or plank; in NT a plate, platter, dish on which food was served, Mk. 14:8, 11
G4094.abbottSmith: prop., a board , plank ; hence, of various at wooden articles; a tablet ; a disc , a dish : Mt 14:8 , 11 , Mk 6:25 , 28 , Lk 11:39 .†
G4094.strongs: apparently a form of G4109 (πλάξ); a plate

G1214.thayer: Demas, a helper of Paul in Rome.
G1214.mounce: Demas, pr. name, Col. 4:14; 2 Tim. 4:10; Phlm. 24*
G1214.abbottSmith: Demas , a companion of St. Paul: Col 4:14 , Phm 24 , II Ti 4:10 .†
G1214.strongs: probably for G1216 (Δημήτριος); Demas, a Christian

G4977.mounce: to split, Mt. 27:51; Mk. 15:38; to rend, tear asunder, Mt. 27:51; Lk. 5:36; 23:45; Jn. 19:24; 21:11); mid. to open or unfold with a chasm, Mk. 1:10; pass. met. to be divided into parties or factions, Acts 14:4; 23:7*
G4977.abbottSmith: to cleave, rend : Mt 27:51 , Mk 1:10 15:38 , Lk 5:36 23:45 , Jo 19:24 21:11 ; metaph., in pass., to be divided into factions, Ac 14:4 23:7 .†
G4977.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to split or sever (literally or figuratively)
G4977.kjv: break, divide, open, rend, make a rent

G2840.thayer: I make unclean, pollute, desecrate, mid: I regard (treat) as unclean.
G2840.mounce: to make common, in NT to profane, desecrate, Acts 21:28; to render ceremonially unclean, defile, pollute, Mt. 15:11, 18, 20; 7:15, 18, 20, 23; Heb. 9:13; to pronounce unclean ceremonially, Acts 10:15; 11:9*
G2840.strongs: from G2839 (κοινός); to make (or consider) profane (ceremonially)

G894.abbottSmith: wormwood : as a proper name, Re 8:11 .†
G894.strongs: of uncertain derivation; wormwood (as a type of bitterness, i.e. (figuratively) calamity)

G781.mounce: unwise; destitute of Christian wisdom, Eph. 5:15*
G781.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G4680 (σοφός); unwise

G1002.strongs: from G906 (βάλλω); a missile, i.e. javelin

G1942.mounce: a covering, veil; met. a cloak, 1 Pet. 2:16*
G1942.abbottSmith: a cover, veil : metaph., τ. κακίας , I Pe 2:16 .†
G1942.strongs: from G1943 (ἐπικαλύπτω); a covering, i.e. (figuratively) pretext

G5358.mounce: a lover of goodness, or, of the good, a fosterer of virtue, Tit. 1:8*
G5358.abbottSmith: loving that which is good, loving goodness : Tit 1:8 (Arist., Polyb., al.).†
G5358.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G18 (ἀγαθός); fond to good, i.e. a promoter of virtue

G1049.mounce: also spelled γαζοφυλακεῖον, a treasury; the sacred treasure, Mk. 12:41, 43; Lk. 21:1; Jn. 8:20*
G1049.abbottSmith: treasury : also, apparently, the trumpet-shaped chests into which the peoples' temple-offerings were thrown ( DB , iv, 96; DCG , ii, 748): Mk 12:41 , 43 , Lk 21:1 , Jo 8:20 .†
G1049.strongs: from G1047 (γάζα) and G5438 (φυλακή); a treasure-house, i.e. a court in the temple for the collection-boxes

G3466.thayer: a mystery, secret, of which initiation is necessary; in the NT: the counsels of God, once hidden but now revealed in the Gospel or some fact thereof; the Christian revelation generally; particular truths or details of the Christian revelation.
G3466.mounce: a matter to the knowledge of which initiation is necessary; a secret which would remain such but for revelation, Mt. 3:11; Rom. 11:25; Col. 1:26; a concealed power or principle, 2 Thess. 2:7; a hidden meaning of a symbol, Rev. 1:20; 17:7
G3466.abbottSmith: that which is known to the μύστης ( initiated ), a mystery or secret doctrine , mostly in pl., τὰ μ. (Æsch., Hdt., al.).
G3466.strongs: from a derivative of (to shut the mouth); a secret or "mystery" (through the idea of silence imposed by initiation into religious rites)

G1365.strongs: from G1364 (δίς); properly, to duplicate, i.e. (mentally) to waver (in opinion)

G2641.thayer: I leave behind, desert, abandon, forsake; I leave remaining, reserve.
G2641.mounce: to leave behind; to leave behind at death, Mk. 12:19; to relinquish, let remain, Mk. 14:52; to quit, depart from, forsake, Mt. 4:13; 16:4; to neglect, Acts 6:2; to leave alone, or without assistance, Lk. 10:40; to reserve, Rom. 11:4
G2641.abbottSmith: to leave behind , leave : c. acc. pers. (rei), Mt 4:13 , Mk 12:19 , 21 , Lk 20:31 , Ac 24:27 25:14 , He 11:27 ; of sailing by a place, Ac 21:3 ; ptcp., καταλιπών , redundant (Dalman, Words , 21 f.), Mt 16:4 21:17 ; pass., Jo 8:[9] , I Th 3:1 ; metaph., εὐθεῖαν ὁδόν , II Pe 2:15 .
G2641.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave down, i.e. behind; by implication, to abandon, have remaining

G4504.thayer: Rufus, a Christian man in Rome, probably to be identified with the brother of Alexander and son of Simon of Cyrene.
G4504.strongs: of Latin origin; red; Rufus, a Christian

G5069.thayer: a quaternion, a group of four soldiers.
G5069.mounce: a set of four; a detachment of four men, Acts 12:4*
G5069.abbottSmith: a quarternion, a group of four : στρατιωτῶν , Ac 12:4 (Philo).
G5069.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a tetrad; from G5064 (τέσσαρες)); a quaternion or squad (picket) of four Roman soldiers

G770.thayer: I am weak (physically: then morally), I am sick.
G770.mounce: to be weak, infirm, deficient in strength; to be inefficient, Rom. 8:3; 2 Cor. 13:3; to be sick, Mt. 25:36; met. to be weak in faith, to doubt, hesitate, be unsettled, timid, Rom. 14:1; 1 Cor. 8:9, 11, 12; 2 Cor. 11:29; to be deficient in authority, dignity, or power, be contemptible, 2 Cor. 11:21; 13:3, 9; to be afflicted, distressed, needy, Acts 20:35; 2 Cor. 12:10; 13:4, 9
G770.abbottSmith: to be weak , feeble : Ac 20:35 , Ro 8:3 , II Co 11:21 12:10 13:4 , 9 ; c. dat., πίστει (Cremer, 527), Ro 4:19 14:1 ; same implied, Ro 14:2 , 21 , I Co 8:11 , 12 , II Co 11:29 ; εἰς , II Co 13:3 . Specif., of bodily debility, to be sick : Mt 25:36 , 39 , Lk 4:40 , Jo 4:46 5:3 , 7 , 13 11:1-3 , 6 , Ac 9:37 , Phl 2:26 , 27 , II Ti 4:20 , Ja 5:14 ; οἱ ἀσθενοῦντες , the sick : Mt 10:8 (MM, s.v.), Mk 6:
G770.strongs: from G772 (ἀσθενής); to be feeble (in any sense)
G770.kjv: be diseased, impotent folk (man), (be) sick, (be, be made) weak

G895.mounce: void of life or sense, inanimate, 1 Cor. 14:7*
G895.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5590 (ψυχή); lifeless, i.e. inanimate (mechanical)

G2143.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4241 (πρέπω); good suitableness, i.e. gracefulness

G1708.mounce: braiding or plaiting of hair, 1 Pet. 3:3*
G1708.strongs: from G1707 (ἐμπλέκω); elaborate braiding of the hair

G3149.abbottSmith: the breast : pl., Lk 11:27 23:29 , Re 1:13 (T, μασθοῖς ; WH, App. , 149; L, μαζοῖς ).†
G3149.strongs: from the base of G3145 (μασσάομαι); a (properly, female) breast (as if kneaded up)

G2264.thayer: Herod; four persons are called by this name: Herod the Great, Herod Antipas, Herod Agrippa, and Herod Agrippa the younger.
G2264.mounce: Herod, pr. name. (1) Herod the Great, Mt. 2:1. (2) Herod Antipas, tetrarch of Galilee and Peraea, Mt. 14:1. (3) Herod Agrippa, Acts 12:1
G2264.abbottSmith: Herod ; Herod the Great : Mt 2 , Ac 23:35 . Herod Antipas ( ὁ τετραάρχης , Mt 14:1 ): Mt 14:1 , 3 , 6 , Mk 6 8:15 , Lk 1:5 3:1 , 19 8:3 9:7 , 9 13:31 23 , Ac 4:27 13:1 . Herod Agrippa I : Ac 12:1 , 6 , 11 , 19 , 21 (cf. Ἀγρίππας (II)).†
G2264.strongs: compound of (a "hero") and G1491 (εἶδος); heroic; Herod, the name of four Jewish kings

G759.mounce: an aromatic substance, spice, etc., Mk. 16:1; Lk. 23:56; 24:1; Jn. 19:40*
G759.strongs: from G142 (αἴρω) (in the sense of sending off scent); an aromatic

G2746.thayer: the act of boasting, glorying, exultation.
G2746.mounce: boasting, pride, a later equivalent to καύχημαι, Rom. 3:27; 2 Cor. 7:4, 14; 11:10
G2746.abbottSmith: a boasting , glorying : Ro 3:27 , II Co 11:10 , 17 , Ja 4:16 ; seq. ὑπέρ , II Co 7:4 8:24 ; ἐτί , c. gen., II Co 7:14 ; ἔχω τὴν κ. ἐν Χρ. Ἰησ. , Ro 15:17 ; στέφανος καυχήσεως ( Ez 16:12 , al.), I Th 2:19 ; of the cause of glorying, a boast ( = καύχημα ), II Co 1:12 .†
G2746.strongs: from G2744 (καυχάομαι); boasting (properly, the act; by implication, the object), in a good or a bad sense
G2746.kjv: boasting, whereof I may glory, glorying, rejoicing

G2358.thayer: (properly: I lead one as my prisoner in a triumphal procession, hence) I lead around, make a show (spectacle) of, cause to triumph.
G2358.mounce: pr. to celebrate a triumph; trans. to lead in triumph, celebrate a triumph over, Col. 2:15; in NT to cause to triumph, or, to render conspicuous, 2 Cor. 2:14*
G2358.abbottSmith: to triumph (and rarely, c. acc., to triumph over ; so perh. Col 2:15 , but v. infr.).
G2358.strongs: from a prolonged compound of the base of G2360 (θροέω); and a derivative of G680 (ἅπτομαι) (meaning a noisy iambus, sung in honor of Bacchus); to make an acclamatory procession, i.e. (figuratively) to conquer or (by Hebraism) to give victory

G1464.thayer: an incision, a cutting, break; met: an interruption, a hindrance.
G1464.mounce: alsn spelled ἐκκοπή, pr. an incision, e.g. a trench, etc., cut in the way of an enemy; an impediment, hindrance, 1 Cor. 9:12*

G892.mounce: chaff, straw broken up by treading out the grain, Mt. 3:12; Lk. 3:17*
G892.strongs: perhaps remotely from (to shed forth); chaff (as diffusive)

G3415.strongs: middle voice of a derivative of G3306 (μένω) or perhaps of the base of G3145 (μασσάομαι) (through the idea of fixture in the mind or of mental grasp); to bear in mind, i.e. recollect; by implication, to reward or punish
G3415.kjv: be mindful, remember, come (have) in remembrance

G5554.mounce: having rings of gold on the fingers, Jas. 2:2*
G5554.strongs: from G5557 (χρυσός) and G1146 (δακτύλιος); gold-ringed, i.e. wearing a golden finger-ring or similar jewelry

G5230.thayer: past the bloom of youth, of full age.
G5230.mounce: past the bloom of life, past one’s prime, 1 Cor. 7:36*
G5230.abbottSmith: past the bloom of youth : I Co 7:36 (Eustath.).†
G5230.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G188 (ἀκμήν); beyond the "acme", i.e. figuratively (of a daughter) past the bloom (prime) of youth

G2726.thayer: a downcast countenance as a sign of sorrow, gloominess, gloom, dejection.
G2726.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and perhaps a derivative of the base of G5316 (φαίνω) (meaning downcast in look); demureness, i.e. (by implication) sadness

G363.thayer: I remind, admonish, am reminded, remind myself; pass: I remember, recall.
G363.mounce: to remind, cause to remember, 1 Cor. 4:17; to exhort, 2 Tim. 1:6; to call to mind, recollect, remember, Mk. 11:21; 14:72; 2 Cor. 7:15; Heb. 10:32*
G363.abbottSmith: to remind , call to one's remembrance : c. acc. rei, I Co 4:17 ; c. inf., II Ti 1:6 . Pass., to remember , call to mind : Mk 11:21 14:72 , II Co 7:15 , He 10:32 .†
G363.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3403 (μιμνήσκω); to remind; (reflexively) to recollect
G363.kjv: call to mind, (bring to , call to, put in), remember(-brance)

G4809.mounce: the fig mulberry tree, sycamore fig, Lk. 19:4*
G4809.abbottSmith: a fig-mulberry, a sycamore : Lk 19:4 (cf. συκάμινος ).†
G4809.strongs: from G4810 (σῦκον) and (the mulberry); the "sycamore"-fig tree

G1786.thayer: belonging to the place, native, resident; subst: a citizen.
G1786.mounce: in or of a place; an inhabitant, citizen, Acts 21:12*
G1786.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5117 (τόπος); a resident

G5403.thayer: Phoenicia, a northern coast strip of the Roman province Syria.
G5403.mounce: Phoenice, Phoenicia, a country on the east of the Mediterranean, between Palestine and Syria, anciently celebrated for commerce, Acts 11:19; 15:3; 21:2*
G5403.abbottSmith: Phœnicia : Ac 11:19 15:3 21:2 (v. DB , iii, 856 b , 857 a n ).†
G5403.strongs: from G5404 (φοῖνιξ); palm-country; Phœnice (or Phœnicia), a region of Palestine

G601.thayer: I uncover, bring to light, reveal.
G601.mounce: pr. uncover; to reveal, Mt. 11:25; pass. to be disclosed, Lk. 2:35; Eph. 3:5; to be plainly signified, distinctly declared, Rom. 1:17, 18; to be set forth, announced, Gal. 3:23; to be discovered in true character, 1 Cor. 3:13; to be manifested, appear, Jn. 12:38;Rom. 8:18; 2 Thess. 2:3, 6, 8; 1 Pet. 1:5; 5:1
G601.abbottSmith: in general sense (cl.), to reveal , uncover , disclose , of things: Mt 10:26 , Lk 2:35 12:2 , I Co 3:13 ; of persons: pass., Christ, Lk 17:30 ; Antichrist, II Th 2:3 , 6 , 8 .
G601.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to take off the cover, i.e. disclose

G2327.mounce: also formed as θέρμα, heat, warmth, Acts 28:3*

G5198.thayer: I am well, am in good health; I am right, reasonable, sound, pure, uncorrupted.
G5198.mounce: to be sound, in health, Lk. 5:31; 7:10; to be safe and sound, Lk. 15:27; 3 Jn. 2; met. to be healthful or sound in faith, doctrine, etc., Tit. 1:9, 13; 2:1, 2; sound, pure, uncorrupted, 1 Tim. 1:10; 6:3; 2 Tim. 1:13; 4:3*
G5198.abbottSmith: to be sound, healthy, in good health : Lk 5:31 7:10 15:27 , III Jo 2 . In Past. Epp. (as also in cl.) metaph., ὑ. ἐν τ. πίστει , Tit 1:13 ; τ. πίστει , τ. ἀγάπῃ , τ. ὑπομονῇ , Tit 2:2 ; ἡ ὑγιαίνουσα διδασκαλία , I Ti 1:10 , II Ti 4:3 , Tit 1:9 2:1 ; λόγοι ὑγιαίνοντες , I Ti 6:3 , II Ti 1:13 .†
G5198.strongs: from G5199 (ὑγιής); to have sound health, i.e. be well (in body); figuratively, to be uncorrupt (true in doctrine)
G5198.kjv: be in health, (be safe and) sound, (be) whole(-some)

G5506.thayer: a commander of a thousand men, a military tribune.
G5506.mounce: commander of a thousand men; hence, genr. a commander, military chief, Mk. 6:21; Rev. 6:15; 19:18; spc. a legionary tribune, Acts 21:31, 32, 33, 37; the prefect of the temple, Jn. 18:12
G5506.abbottSmith: a chiliarch, the commander of a thousand , esp. a Roman military tribune, the commander of a cohort : Jo 18:12 , Ac 21:31-33 , 37 22:24-29 23:10 , 15 , 17-19 , 22 24:7 , 22 25:23 ; more generally, of officers of similar rank, Mk 6:21 , Re 6:15 19:18 (v., DB , i, 352 b ; DCG , i, 271 a , 307 a ).†
G5506.strongs: from G5507 (χίλιοι) and G757 (ἄρχω); the commander of a thousand soldiers ("chiliarch"; i.e. colonel

G3191.thayer: I devise, plan; practice, exercise myself in, study, ponder.
G3191.mounce: to care for; to bestow careful thought upon, to give painful attention to, be earnest in, 1 Tim. 4:15; to devise, Acts 4:25*
G3191.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3199 (μέλω); to take care of, i.e. (by implication) revolve in the mind

G3612.mounce: a dwelling; used in various conventional senses, and among them, a prison, cell, Acts 12:7*
G3612.abbottSmith: a dwelling . As a euphemism for other definite terms (e.g. brothel . Hdt., ii, 121; cf. Ez 16:24 ), a prison (Thuc., iv, 47 f.): Ac 12:7 .†
G3612.strongs: from G3611 (οἰκέω); a tenement, i.e. (specially), a jail

G824.thayer: (lit: out of place, unusual, unbecoming), improper, unrighteous, perverse.
G824.mounce: pr. out of place; inopportune, unsuitable, absurd; new, unusual, strange; in NT improper, amiss, wicked, Lk. 23:41; Acts 25:5; 2 Thess. 3:2; noxious, harmful, Acts 28:6*
G824.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5117 (τόπος); out of place, i.e. (figuratively) improper, injurious, wicked

G3342.mounce: can function as an improper prep., between, Mt. 23:35; Lk. 11:51; 16:26; Acts 15:9; ἐν τῷ μεταξύ, i.e. χρόνῷ, in the meantime, meanwhile, Jn. 4:31; in NT ὁ μεταξύ, following, succeeding, Acts 13:42
G3342.abbottSmith: adv. of place and time (in NT time only); between : ἐν τῷ μ. (s.c. χρόνῳ ), Jo 4:31 ; in late writers (FlJ, Plut., al.), like μετά (adv.), after , afterwards : τὸ μ. σάββατον , Ac 13:42 (cf. Cl., Ro. , I Co. , 44, 2).
G3342.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and a form of G4862 (σύν); betwixt (of place or person); (of time) as adjective, intervening, or (by implication) adjoining

G2105.mounce: serenity of the heavens, a cloudless sky, fair or fine weather, Mt. 16:2*
G2105.strongs: feminine from G2095 (εὖ) and the alternate of G2203 (Ζεύς) (as the god of the weather); a clear sky, i.e. fine weather

G1680.mounce: pr. expectation; hope, Acts 24:15; Rom. 5:4; meton. the object of hope, thing hoped for, Rom. 8:24; Gal. 5:5; the author or source of hope, Col. 1:27; 1 Tim. 1:1; trust, confidence, 1 Pet. 1:21; ἐπ’ ἐλπίδι, in security, with a guarantee, Acts 2:26; Rom. 8:20
G1680.abbottSmith: expectation (in cl., rarely of evil, mostly of good, and so always in NT), hope ;
G1680.strongs: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure); expectation (abstractly or concretely) or confidence

G4243.thayer: I am aged, act as an ambassador.
G4243.mounce: to be elder; to be an ambassador, perform the duties of an ambassador, 2 Cor. 5:20; Eph. 6:20*
G4243.abbottSmith: to be the elder , to take precedence .
G4243.strongs: from the base of G4245 (πρεσβύτερος); to be a senior, i.e. (by implication) act as a representative (figuratively, preacher)

G4408.mounce: the forepart of a vessel, prow, Acts 27:30, 41*
G4408.abbottSmith: the forward part of a ship, the prow : Ac 27:30 ; opp. to πρύμνα , Ac 27:41 .†
G4408.strongs: feminine of a presumed derivative of G4253 (πρό) as noun; the prow, i.e. forward part of a vessel

G1924.thayer: I write upon, inscribe, imprint a mark on.
G1924.mounce: pluperfect pass., ἐπεγεγράμμην, to imprint a mark on; to inscribe, engrave, write on, Mk. 15:26; Acts 17:23; Rev. 21:12; met. to imprint, impress deeply on, Heb. 8:10; 10:16*
G1924.abbottSmith: to write upon, inscribe : Mk 15:26 , Ac 17:23 , Re 21:12 ; fig., He 8:10 (LXX) He 10:16 (ib) .†
G1924.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1125 (γράφω); to inscribe (physically or mentally)

G1978.mounce: pr. bearing a distinctive mark or device; noted, eminent, Rom. 16:7; notorious, Mt. 27:16*
G1978.abbottSmith: bearing a mark ; of money, stamped, coined .
G1978.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and some form of the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); remarkable, i.e. (figuratively) eminent

G1435.mounce: a gift, present, Mt. 2:11; Eph. 2:8; Rev. 11:10; an offering, sacrifice, Mt. 5:23, 24; 8:4; δῶρον, i.e. ἐστι[ν], it is consecrated to God, Mt. 15:5; Mk. 7:11; contribution to the temple, Lk. 21:1, 4
G1435.abbottSmith: a gift, present : Mt 2:11 , Re 11:10 ; of gifts and sacrifices to God, Mt 5:23 , 24 8:4 15:5 23:18 , 19 , Mk 7:11 , Lk 21:1 , 4 , He 5:1 8:3 , 4 9:9 11:4 ; δ. θεοῦ , Eph 2:8 .†

G830.mounce: pr. one who chooses his own course of action; acting spontaneously, of one’s own accord, 2 Cor. 8:3, 17*
G830.strongs: from G846 (αὐτός) and the same as G140 (αἱρετίζω); self-chosen, i.e. (by implication) voluntary
G830.kjv: of own accord, willing of self

G3692.thayer: a crevice (in a rock), a cave, an opening, hole.
G3692.mounce: a hole; a hole, vent, opening, Jas. 3:11; a hole, cavern, Heb. 11:38*
G3692.abbottSmith: an opening , a hole : Ja 3:11 (cf. Ex 33:22 ), He 11:33 (cf. Ob 3 ).†
G3692.strongs: probably from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); a hole (as if for light), i.e. cavern; by analogy, a spring (of water)

G4092.mounce: to set on fire, burn, inflame; in NT pass., to swell from inflamation, Acts 28:6*
G4092.strongs: a reduplicated and prolonged form of a primary ; which occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses); to fire, i.e. burn (figuratively and passively, become inflamed with fever)

G2526.thayer: according to which thing, as, according as.
G2526.mounce: as, Rom. 8:26; according as, in proportion as, to the degree that, 2 Cor. 8:12; 1 Pet. 4:13*
G2526.abbottSmith: as , according as : Ro 8:26 , II Co 8:12 , I Pe 4:13 .†
G2526.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3739 (ὅς); according to which thing, i.e. precisely as, in proportion as

G1078.mounce: birth, nativity, Mt. 1:18; Lk. 1:14; Jas. 1:23; successive generation, descent, lineage, Mt. 1:1; meton. life, Mt. 1:18; Jas. 3:6*
G1078.abbottSmith: origin, lineage : βίβλος γενέσεως (as in Ge 2:4 a 5:1 ), Mt 1:1 . birth : Mt 1:18 , Lk 1:14 (Rec. γέννησις ); πρόσωπον τῆς γ. , face of his birth ("what God made him to be," Hort., in l.), Ja 1:23 ; ὁ τροχὸς τῆς γ. , the wheel (course) of birth or creation ("the wheel of man's nature according to its original Divine purpose," Hort), Ja 3:6 .†
G1078.strongs: from the same as G1074 (γενεά); nativity; figuratively, nature

G204.thayer: in the corner (of a building); subst: corner-(stone).
G204.mounce: literally: lying at the extreme corner; with λίθος, corner or foundation stone, Eph. 2:20; 1 Pet. 2:6*
G204.abbottSmith: at the extreme angle : ὁ ἀ. , the corner foundation stone , Eph 2:20 , I Pe 2:6 .†
G204.strongs: from G206 (ἄκρον) and G1137 (γωνία); belonging to the extreme corner

G5512.strongs: from a derivative probably of G5491 (χεῖλος); to throw out the lip, i.e. jeer at

G713.abbottSmith: sufficient : Mt 6:34 (on the neut., v. Bl., § 31, 2); seq. ἵνα (M, Pr. , 210), Mt 10:25 ; c. inf., I Pe 4:3 (for exx., v. Deiss., BS , 257; MM, s.v.).†

G2750.thayer: a kind of girdle made of cords; a bandage, grave clothes.
G2750.mounce: a bandage, swath, in NT pl. graveclothes, Jn. 11:44*
G2750.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a swathe, i.e. winding-sheet

G5359.thayer: Philadelphia, a city of the Roman province Asia.
G5359.mounce: Philadelphia, a city of Lydia, near Mount Tmolus, Rev. 1:11; 3:7*. See PhotoGuide.
G5359.abbottSmith: Philadelphia , a city of Lydia: Re 1:11 3:7 .†
G5359.strongs: from (the same as G5361 (φιλάδελφος)), a king of Pergamos; Philadelphia, a place in Asia Minor

G4443.thayer: a fire, pile of burning fuel.
G4443.mounce: a fire, heap of combustibles, Acts 28:2, 3*

G1067.thayer: Gehenna, and originally the name of a valley or cavity near Jerusalem, a place underneath the earth, a place of punishment for evil.
G1067.mounce: Gehenna, pr. the valley of Hinnom, south of Jerusalem, once celebrated for the horrid worship of Moloch, and afterwards polluted with every species of filth, as well as the carcasses of animals, and dead bodies of malefactors; to consume which, in order to avert the pestilence which such a mass of corruption would occasion, constant fires were kept burning; hence, hell, the fires of Tartarus, the place of punishment in Hades, Mt. 5:22, 29, 30; 10:28; 18:9, et al.
G1067.abbottSmith: Gehenna , a valley W. and S. of Jerusalem, which as the site of fire-worship from the time of Ahaz, was desecrated by Josiah and became a dumping-place for the offal of the city. Later, the name was used as a symbol of the place of future punishment, as in NT: Mt 5:29 , 30 10:28 , Mk 9:43 , 45 , 47 , Lk 12:5 , Ja 3:6 ; γ. τ. πυρός , Mt 5:22 18:19 , prob. with ref. to fires of Moloch ( DB , ii, 119
G1067.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01516 and H02011); valley of (the son of) Hinnom; ge-henna (or Ge-Hinnom), a valley of Jerusalem, used (figuratively) as a name for the place (or state) of everlasting punishment

G4967.abbottSmith: slaughter : Ac 8:32 (LXX) ; πρόβατα σφαγῆς , Ro 8:36 (LXX) ; ἡμέρα σφαγῆς , Ja 5:5 .†
G4967.strongs: from G4969 (σφάζω); butchery (of animals for food or sacrifice, or (figuratively) of men (destruction))

G3179.thayer: I cause to change its place, move out of its place; I translate, transfer, remove.
G3179.mounce: to cause a change of position; to remove, transport, 1 Cor. 13:2; to transfer, Col. 1:13; met. to cause to change sides; by impl. to pervert, mislead, Acts 19:26; to remove from office, dismiss, discard, Lk. 16:4; Acts 13:22*
G3179.abbottSmith: to change , remove : c. acc. rei, ὄρη , I Co 13:2 (cf. Is 54:10 ); c. acc. pers.: seq. εἰς , Col 1:13 ; seq. ἐκ , pass., Lk 16:4 ; of causing death (cf. similar intrans. sense, Eur., Alc. , 21, al.), Ac 13:22 . Metaph. (cf. τ. καρδίαν μ. , Jos 14:8 ), c. acc. pers., to pervert : Ac 19:26 .†
G3179.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to transfer, i.e. carry away, depose or (figuratively) exchange, seduce
G3179.kjv: put out, remove, translate, turn away

G3522.strongs: from G3523 (νῆστις); to abstain from food (religiously)

G3044.mounce: some accent as Λῖνος, Linus, pr. name, 2 Tim. 4:21*
G3044.strongs: perhaps from G3043 (λίνον); Linus, a Christian

G1886.mounce: pr. a place to pass the night in; cottage, farm; in NT a dwelling, habitation, farm, Acts 1:20*
G1886.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and an equivalent of G833 (αὐλή); a hut over the head, i.e. a dwelling

G686.abbottSmith: then : prop. (as in cl.), the second word in the sentence, Ro 7:21 8:1 , Ga 3:7 ; ἐπεὶ ἄρα , I Co 7:14 (with another word between) 5:10 ; as the first word, Lk 11:48 , Ac 11:18 , Ro 10:17 , I Co 15:18 , II Co 5:15 7:12 , He 4:9 ; so prop. in apodosis after protasis with εἰ , Mt 12:28 , Lk 11:20 , Ga 2:21 3:29 5:11 , He 12:8 ( κενὸν ἄρα ), I Co 15:14 ; often in interrogations, direct and indirect,
G686.strongs: probably from G142 (αἴρω) (through the idea of drawing a conclusion); a particle denoting an inference more or less decisive (as follows)
G686.kjv: haply, (what) manner (of man), no doubt, perhaps, so be, then, therefore, truly, wherefore

G2157.mounce: comeliness, gracefulness; artificial comeliness, ornamental array, embellishment, 1 Cor. 12:23*

G1077.mounce: pr. a day observed in memory of the dead; in NT equivalent to γενέθλια, celebration of one’s birthday, birthday-festival, Mt. 14:6; Mk. 6:21*
G1077.abbottSmith: in Attic Gk., a commemoration of the dead . In late Gk., a birthday feast (= cl. γενέθλια ; so in π. , v. MM, Exp. , x; cf. also Rutherford, NPhr. , 184): Mt 14:6 , Mk 6:21 .†
G1077.strongs: neuter plural of a derivative of G1078 (γένεσις); birthday ceremonies

G83.thayer: the quality of being unseen (of disappearing), indefiniteness, uncertainty.

G3552.thayer: I am diseased, hence of mental or spiritual disease.
G3552.mounce: to be sick; met. to have a diseased appetite or craving for a thing, have an excessive and vicious fondness for a thing, 1 Tim. 6:4*
G3552.abbottSmith: to be sick : metaph., of mental ailment, seq. περί , I Ti 6:4 (cf. Plat., Mor. , 546 d).†
G3552.strongs: from G3554 (νόσος); to be sick, i.e. (by implication, of a diseased appetite) to hanker after (figuratively, to harp upon)

G3656.thayer: I consort with, associate with, commune with; particularly, I talk (converse) with.
G3656.mounce: to be in company with, associate with; to converse with, talk with, Lk. 24:14, 15; Acts 20:11; 24:26*
G3656.abbottSmith: to be in company with , consort with ; hence, to converse with : Ac 20:11 ; c. dat., Ac 24:26 ; seq. πρός , Lk 24:14 , 15 .†
G3656.strongs: from G3658 (ὅμιλος); to be in company with, i.e. (by implication) to converse

G3610.mounce: pr. an inmate of a house; a domestic servant, household slave, Lk. 16:13; Acts 10:7; Rom. 14:4; 1 Pet. 2:18*
G3610.abbottSmith: a house-servant : Lk 16:13 , Ro 14:4 ; pl., Ac 10:7 , I Pe 2:18 . (In Plat., Hdt., Si 4:30 6:11 , the pl. includes all the inmates of the house, the familia , οἰκετεία. .)†
G3610.strongs: from G3611 (οἰκέω); a fellow resident, i.e. menial domestic

G4944.mounce: pr. to travail at the same time with; trop. suffer together, Rom. 8:22*
G4944.abbottSmith: to be in travail together (Arist.): metaph. (Eur.), Ro 8:22 .†
G4944.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5605 (ὠδίνω); to have (parturition) pangs in company (concert, simultaneously) with, i.e. (figuratively) to sympathize (in expectation of relief from suffering)

G4526.mounce: sackcloth, a coarse black cloth made of hair (goat or camel), Rev. 6:12; a mourning garment of sackcloth, Mt. 11:21; Lk. 10:13; Rev. 11:3*
G4526.abbottSmith: a coarse cloth, sackcloth , usually made of hair: Re 6:12 .
G4526.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08242); "sack"-cloth, i.e. mohair (the material or garments made of it, worn as a sign of grief)

G3790.thayer: the brow, a ridge (of a mountain).
G3790.mounce: a brow, eye-brow; the brow of a mountain, edge of a precipice, Lk. 4:29*
G3790.abbottSmith: an eyebrow , the brow of a hill: Lk 4:29 .†
G3790.strongs: perhaps from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (through the idea of the shading or proximity to the organ of vision); the eye-"brow" or forehead, i.e. (figuratively) the brink of a precipice

G163.thayer: I take captive (in war); I subdue, ensnare.
G163.mounce: to lead captive; by impl. to subject, Lk. 21:24; Rom. 7:23; 2 Cor. 10:5; 2 Tim. 3:6*
G163.abbottSmith: to take or lead captive : seq. εἰς , Lk 21:24 (cf. To 1:10 ); metaph., Ro 7:23 , II Co 10:5 , II Ti 3:6 .†
G163.kjv: lead away captive, bring into captivity

G5068.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G1137 (γωνία); four-cornered, i.e. square

G3568.thayer: adv. (a) of time: just now, even now; just at hand, immediately, (b) of logical connection: now then, (c) in commands and appeals: at this instant.
G3568.mounce: now, at the present time, Mk. 10:30; Lk. 6:21, et al. freq.; just now, Jn. 11:8; forthwith, Jn. 12:31; καὶ νῦν, even now, as matters stand, Jn. 11:22; now, expressive of a marked tone of address, Acts 7:34; 13:11; Jas. 4:13; 5:1; τὸ νῦν, the present time, Lk. 1:48; τανῦν, or τὰ νῦν, now, Acts 4:29
G3568.abbottSmith: prop., of time, now , i.e. at the present time: as opp. to past, Jo 4:18 , Ac 7:52 , Ro 13:11 , II Co 7:9 , Col 1:24 , al.; opp. to fut., Jo 12:27 , Ro 11:31 , al.; c. art., ὁ ( ἡ , τὸ ) et subst., the present : Ro 3:26 , Ga 4:25 , I Ti 6:17 , Tit 2:12 , al.; ἀπὸ τοῦ ν. (LXX for מֵעַתָּה ), Lk 1:48 , Ac 18:6 , al.; ἄχρι τοῦ ν. , Ro 8:22 , Phl 1:5 ; ἕως τοῦ ν. (LXX for עַתָּה עַד ), Mt 24:21 , Mk 1
G3568.strongs: a primary particle of present time; "now" (as adverb of date, a transition or emphasis); also as noun or adjective present or immediate
G3568.kjv: henceforth, + hereafter, of late, soon, present, this (time)

G2423.thayer: (Hebrew), Jechoniah, son of Josiah and father of Salathiel.
G2423.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03204); Jechonias (i.e. Jekonjah), an Israelite

G802.thayer: not covenanting, untrue to an agreement, treacherous.
G802.mounce: unable to be trusted, undutiful, Rom. 1:31*
G802.abbottSmith: false to engagements , not keeping covenant , faithless (MM, s.v.): Ro 1:31 .†
G802.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4934 (συντίθεμαι); properly, not agreed, i.e. treacherous to compacts

G357.strongs: middle voice from G356 (ἀναλογία); to estimate, i.e. (figuratively) contemplate

G3667.thayer: (originally: a thing made like something else), a likeness, or rather: form; a similitude.
G3667.mounce: pr. that which is conformed or assimilated; form, shape, figure, Rev. 9:7; likeness, resemblance, similitude, Rom. 1:23; 5:14; 6:5; 8:3; Phil. 2:7*
G3667.abbottSmith: that which is made like something; concrete, an image , likeness ( Ps 105 (106):20 , Ez 1:5 , I Mac 3:48 , al.): Re 9:7 ; abstract, likeness , resemblance : Ro 5:14 6:5 8:3 , Phl 2:7 ; ἐν ὁ. εἰκόνος , Ro 1:23 .†
G3667.strongs: from G3666 (ὁμοιόω); a form; abstractly, resemblance
G3667.kjv: made like to, likeness, shape, similitude

G2958.thayer: Cyrenius or Quirinius, governor of Syria.
G2958.mounce: Cyrenius (perhaps Quirinus) pr. name, the governor of Syria, Lk. 2:2*
G2958.strongs: of Latin origin; Cyrenius (i.e. Quirinus), a Roman

G124.strongs: from G125 (Αἴγυπτος); an Ægyptian or inhabitant of Ægyptus

G2137.thayer: I cause to prosper, pass: I have a happy (successful) journey, hence: I prosper.
G2137.mounce: to give a prosperous journey; cause to prosper or be successful; pass. to have a prosperous journey, to succeed in a journey, Rom. 1:10; met. to be furthered, to prosper, temporally or spiritually, 1 Cor. 16:2; 3 Jn. 2 (2x)*
G2137.abbottSmith: to help on one's way (Soph., al.). Pass., to have a prosperous journey ; metaph. (Hdt., al.), to prosper, be prospered, be successful : III Jo 2 , Ro 1:10 , I Co 16:2 (on the tense, v. M, Pr. , 54; ICC , in l.).†
G2137.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3598 (ὁδός); to help on the road, i.e. (passively) succeed in reaching; figuratively, to succeed in business affairs

G1986.thayer: (lit: I draw over), mid: I become as uncircumcised.
G1986.mounce: to sow in or among, Mt. 13:25*
G1986.abbottSmith: to draw on : mid., in peculiar sense of effacing signs of Judaism (cf. I Mac 1:15 , FlJ, Ant. , xii, 5, i; v. Thayer, s.v.), to become as uncircumcised : I Co 7:18 .†
G1986.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4685 (σπάω); to draw over, i.e. (with G203 (ἀκροβυστία) implied) efface the mark of circumcision (by recovering with the foreskin)

G1512.mounce: if indeed, if it be so that, granted, Rom. 8:9; 1 Cor. 15:15; since indeed, since, 2 Thess. 1:6; 1 Pet. 2:3; although indeed, 1 Cor. 8:5
G1512.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G4007 (περ); if perhaps

G268.mounce: one who deviates from the path of virtue, a sinner, Mk. 2:17; depraved, Mk. 8:38; sinful, detestable, Rom. 7:13
G268.abbottSmith: sinful , a sinner : of all men, I Ti 1:15 ; of those especially wicked, I Ti 1:9 , I Pe 4:18 ; p1., Mt 9:10-13 11:19 26:45 , al. (v. MM, VGT , s.v.; Cremer, 102, 634)
G268.strongs: from G264 (ἁμαρτάνω); sinful, i.e. a sinner

G1533.thayer: I lead into, bring in, announce.
G1533.mounce: to bring in or into, Lk. 5:18, 19; 1 Tim. 6:7; Heb. 13:11; to bring to the ears of any one, to announce, Acts 17:20; to lead into, Mt. 6:13; Lk. 11:4; drag in, Lk. 12:11*
G1533.abbottSmith: to bring in, into : c. acc pers., Lk 5:18 , 19 ; seq. εἰς , Mt 6:13 , Lk 11:4 ; ἐπί , Lk 12:11 ; c. acc rei, seq. εἰς , I Ti 6:7 ; pass., He 13:11 .†
G1533.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G5342 (φέρω); to carry inward (literally or figuratively)

G659.thayer: I lay off or aside, renounce, stow away, put.
G659.mounce: mid: to lay off, lay down or aside, as garments, Acts 7:58; me
G659.abbottSmith: to put off or aside ; in NT always mid., to put off from oneself as a garment: τ. ἱμάτια , Ac 7:58 ; metaph., in ethical sense, to put of , lay aside : Ro 13:12 , Eph 4:22 , 25 , Col 3:8 , He 12:1 , Ja 1:21 , I Pe 2:1 ; to stow away , put : ἐν τ. φυλακῇ (MM, s.v.), Mt 14:3 .†
G659.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5087 (τίθημι); to put away (literally or figuratively)
G659.kjv: cast off, lay apart (aside, down), put away (off)

G621.thayer: I lick off, lick clean, lick up.
G621.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to "lick"); to lick clean

G2300.thayer: I see, behold, contemplate, look upon, view; I see, visit.
G2300.mounce: to gaze upon, Mt. 6:1; 23:5; Lk. 7:24; to see, discern with the eyes, Mk. 16:11, 14; Lk. 5:27; Jn. 1:14, 32, 38; to see, visit, Rom. 15:24
G2300.abbottSmith: to behold , look upon , contemplate , view (in early writers with a sense of wondering ), in NT apparently always in literal, physical sense of "careful and deliberate vision which interprets . . . its object": c. acc. rei, Mt 11:7 , Lk 7:24 23:55 , Jo 1:14 , 32 4:35 11:45 , Ac 22:9 , I Jo 1:1 ; c. acc. pers., Mt 22:11 , Ac 21:27 , Ro 15:24 , I Jo 4:12 ; c. ptcp., Mk 16:[14] , Lk 5:27 , Jo 1:38 ,
G2300.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb; to look closely at, i.e. (by implication) perceive (literally or figuratively); by extension to visit

G188.mounce: pr. the point of a weapon; point of time: ἀκμήν, for κατἀκμήν, adv., yet, still, even now, Mt. 15:16*
G188.abbottSmith: a point , used as adv., at the present point of time , even now , even yet : Mt 15:16 .†
G188.strongs: accusative case of a noun ("acme") akin to (a point) and meaning the same; adverbially, just now, i.e. still

G4665.mounce: smaragdus, the emerald, a gem of a pure green color; but under this name the ancients probably comprised all stones of a fine green color, Rev. 21:19*
G4665.abbottSmith: emerald or other trans parent green stone (LS, s.v.; DB , iv, 620; Swete, Ap. , 67, 288): Re 21:19 .†
G4665.strongs: of uncertain derivation; the emerald or green gem so called

G3408.thayer: (a) pay, wages, salary, (b) reward, recompense, punishment.
G3408.mounce: hire, wages, Mt. 20:8; Jas. 5:4; reward, Mt. 5:12, 46; 6:1, 2, 5, 16; punishment, 2 Pet. 2:13
G3408.abbottSmith: prop., wages , hire : Mt 20:8 , Lk 10:7 , Ro 4:4 , I Ti 5:18 , Ja 5:4 , Ju 11 ; μ. ἀδικίας , Ac 1:18 , II Pe 2:13 (but v. Mayor and ICC , in l.), II Pe 2:15 .
G3408.strongs: apparently a primary word; pay for service (literally or figuratively), good or bad

G3743.abbottSmith: piously , holily : ὁ. καὶ δικαίως κ. ἀμέμπτως (on the distinction here between these synonyms, v. M, Th. , 24 f.), I Th 2:10 .†

G2344.thayer: a store-house for precious things; hence: a treasure, a store.
G2344.mounce: a treasury, a store, treasure, precious deposit, Mt. 6:19, 20, 21; a receptacle in which precious articles are kept, a casket, Mt. 2:11; a storehouse, Mt. 12:35
G2344.abbottSmith: a place of safe keeping ; a casket : Mt 2:11 ; a treasury ( I Mac 3:29 and freq. in cl.); a storehouse ( Ne 13:12 , De 28:12 , al.): Mt 13:52 ; metaph., of the soul, Mt 12:35 ; τ. καρδίας , Lk 6:45 .
G2344.strongs: from G5087 (τίθημι); a deposit, i.e. wealth (literally or figuratively)

G2648.thayer: I understand, take in a fact about, consider carefully.
G2648.mounce: to learn or observe thoroughly; to consider accurately and diligently, contemplate, Mt. 6:28*
G2648.abbottSmith: to learn thoroughly , observe well , consider carefully (on distinction bet. κ. and μανθάνω , cf. M, Pr. , 117): Mt 6:28 .†
G2648.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3129 (μανθάνω); to learn thoroughly, i.e. (by implication) to note carefully

G1481.thayer: an ethnarch, tribal lord, a subordinate ruler.
G1481.mounce: a governor, chief of any tribe or nation, 2 Cor. 11:32*
G1481.abbottSmith: an ethnarch, a provincial governor (cf. I Mac, ll. c.; FlJ, Ant. , xiii, 6, 6; Dalman, 332): II Co 11:32 .†
G1481.strongs: from G1484 (ἔθνος) and G746 (ἀρχή); the governor (not king) of a district

G3143.thayer: (properly: I call (summon) to witness, and then absolutely) I testify, protest, asseverate; I conjure, solemnly charge.
G3143.mounce: to call to witness; intrans. to make a solemn affirmation or declaration, Acts 20:26; 26:22; Gal. 5:3; to make a solemn appeal, Eph. 4:17; 1 Thess. 2:12*
G3143.abbottSmith: to summon as witness (M, Th. , 25 f.; Hort., 1Pe. , 53 f.; Lft., Notes , 29; Ga 203), hence, to protest , affirm solemnly : seq. ὅτι , Ac 20:26 , Ga 5:3 ; to adjure , beseech : c. dat. pers., Ac 26:22 ; c. acc. et inf., Eph 4:17 ; seq. εἰς , I Th 2:12 (cf. δια- , προ-μαρτύρομαι ).†
G3143.strongs: middle voice from G3144 (μάρτυς); to be adduced as a witness, i.e. (figuratively) to obtest (in affirmation or exhortation)

G5082.mounce: so great, large, important, 2 Cor. 1:10; Heb. 2:3; Jas. 3:4; Rev. 16:18*
G5082.strongs: from a compound of G3588 (ὁ) with G2245 (ἡλίκος) and G3778 (οὗτος); such as this, i.e. (in (figurative) magnitude) so vast

G5261.mounce: pr. a copy to write after; met. an example for imitation, pattern, 1 Pet. 2:21*
G5261.strongs: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G1125 (γράφω); an underwriting, i.e. copy for imitation (figuratively)

G3775.thayer: (a) the ear, (b) met: the faculty of perception.
G3775.abbottSmith: the ear : Mt 13:16 , Mk 7:33 , Lk 22:50 , I Co 2:9 12:16 , I Pe 3:12 ; ἐν τ. ὠσί , Lk 4:21 ; εἰς τ. ὦτα ἀκούεσθαι , Ac 7:57 ; γίνεσθαι , Lk 1:44 ; εἰσέρχεσθαι , Ja 5:4 ; εἰς τ. οὖς ἀκούειν , Mt 10:27 ; πρὸς τ. οὖς λαλεῖν , Lk 12:3 ; τὰ ὦ. συνέχειν (MM, xviii), Ac 7:57 . Metaph., of understanding, perceiving, knowing: Mt 13:16 ; ὁ ἔχων ( εἴ τις ἔχει ) οὖς ἀκουσάτω , Re 2:7 , 11 , 17 , 29 3:6 , 13 ,
G3775.strongs: apparently a primary word; the ear (physically or mentally)

G5042.thayer: child bearing, the rearing of a family.
G5042.mounce: the bearing of children, the rearing of a family, 1 Tim. 2:15*
G5042.strongs: from the same as G5041 (τεκνογονέω); childbirth (parentage), i.e. (by implication) maternity (the performance of maternal duties)

G263.abbottSmith: unfading (whence ὁ ἀ. , the amaranth , an unfading flower): I Pe 1:4 (cf. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G263.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G3133 (μαραίνω); unfading, i.e. (by implication) perpetual

G880.mounce: dumb, destitute of the power of speech, 1 Cor. 12:2; 2 Pet. 2:16; silent, mute, uttering no voice, Acts 8:32; inarticulate, consisting of inarticulate sounds, unmeaning, 1 Cor. 14:10*
G880.abbottSmith: dumb , speechless : Ac 8:32 (LXX) ; of idols (MM, s.v.), I Co 12:2 ; of beasts, II Pe 2:19 ; τοσαῦτα γένη φωνῶν καὶ οὐδὲν ἄ. —so many kinds of voices and none voiceless, i.e. without signification, unintelligible : I Co 14:10 .†
G880.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5456 (φωνή); voiceless, i.e. mute (by nature or choice); figuratively, unmeaning

G4441.thayer: I ask, inquire, ascertain by inquiry, understand.
G4441.mounce: to ask, inquire, Mt. 2:4; Lk. 15:26; to investigate, examine judicially, Acts 23:20; to ascertain by inquiry, understand, Acts 23:34
G4441.abbottSmith: to inquire : c. acc. rei, Jo 4:42 , Ac 23:20 ; seq. quæst. indir., Mt 2:4 , Lk 15:26 18:36 , Ac 10:18 21:33 ; quæst. dir., Ac 4:7 10:29 23:19 ; seq. παρά , c. gen. pers., Mt 2:4 , Jo 4:52 .
G4441.strongs: middle voice prolonged from a primary (which occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses); to question, i.e. ascertain by inquiry (as a matter of information merely; and thus differing from G2065 (ἐρωτάω), which properly means a request as a favor; and from G154 (αἰτέω), which is strictly a demand for something due; as well as from G2212 (ζητέω), which implies a search for something hidden; and from G1189 (δέομαι), which involves the idea of urgent need); by implication, to learn (by casual intelligence)

G1498.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G1498.strongs: optative (i.e. English subjunctive) present of G1510 (εἰμί) (including the other person); might (could, would, or should) be
G1498.kjv: mean, + perish, should be, was, were

G1738.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1349 (δίκη); in the right, i.e. equitable

G502.thayer: what is drawn; a vessel or bucket to draw with.
G502.mounce: pr. that which is drawn; a bucket, vessel for drawing water, Jn. 4:11*

G4263.abbottSmith: in Hom., Hdt., cattle , esp. of small cattle, sheep and goats .
G4263.strongs: probably neuter of a presumed derivative of G4260 (προβαίνω); something that walks forward (a quadruped), i.e. (specially), a sheep (literally or figuratively)

G4070.mounce: last year, a year ago, 2 Cor. 8:10; 9:2*
G4070.abbottSmith: last year , a year ago : ἀπὸ π. (as in π. v. Deiss., BS , 221; LAE , 70), II Co 8:10 9:2 .†
G4070.strongs: adverb from G4009 (πέρας); the by-gone, i.e. (as noun) last year

G3512.mounce: juvenile, natural to youth, youthful, 2 Tim. 2:22*
G3512.abbottSmith: = νεανικός , youthful , esp. of qualities: ἐπιθυμίαι , II Ti 2:22 (Polyb.).†
G3512.strongs: from the comparative of G3501 (νέος); appertaining to younger persons, i.e. juvenile

G3166.abbottSmith: to boast great things : Ja 3:5 (Rec. for μεγάλα αὐχεῖ , WH).†
G3166.strongs: from a compound of G3173 (μέγας) and (to boast; akin to G837 (αὐξάνω) and G2744 (καυχάομαι)); to talk big, i.e. be grandiloquent (arrogant, egotistic)

G2334.thayer: I look at, gaze, behold; I see, experience, discern; I partake of.
G2334.mounce: to be a spectator, to gaze on, contemplate; to behold, view with interest and attention, Mt. 27:55; 28:1; to contemplate mentally, consider, Heb. 7:4; in NT to see, perceive, Mk. 3:11; to come to a knowledge of, Jn. 6:40; from the Hebrew, to experience, undergo, Jn. 8:51
G2334.strongs: from a derivative of G2300 (θεάομαι) (perhaps by addition of G3708 (ὁράω)); to be a spectator of, i.e. discern, (literally, figuratively (experience) or intensively (acknowledge))
G2334.kjv: behold, consider, look on, perceive, see

G5245.thayer: I am more than conqueror, prevail mightily.
G5245.mounce: to overpower in victory; to be abundantly victorious, prevail mightily, Rom. 8:37*
G5245.abbottSmith: to be more than conqueror : Ro 8:37 (eccl.).†
G5245.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G3528 (νικάω); to vanquish beyond, i.e. gain a decisive victory

G3957.thayer: the feast of Passover, the Passover lamb.
G3957.mounce: the passover, the paschal lamb, Mt. 26:17; Mk. 14:12; met. used of Christ, the true paschal lamb, 1 Cor. 5:7; the feast of the passover, the day on which the paschal lamb was slain and eaten, the 14th of Nisan, Mt. 26:18; Mk. 14:1; Heb. 11:28; more genr. the whole paschal festival, including the seven days of the feast of unleavened bread, Mt. 26:2; Lk. 2:41; Jn. 2:13
G3957.abbottSmith: the festival of the Passover : Mt 26:2 , Mk 14:1 , Lk 22:1 , Jo 2:13 , 23 6:4 11:55 12:1 18:39 19:14 , Ac 12:4 , He 11:28 ; . ἡ ἐορτὴ τοῦ π. , Lk 2:41 , Jo 13:1 .
G3957.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H06453); the Passover (the meal, the day, the festival or the special sacrifices connected with it)

G3464.mounce: pr. aromatic juice which distills from trees; ointment, unguent, usually perfumed, Mt. 26:7, 12; Mk. 14:3, 4
G3464.strongs: probably of foreign origin (compare H04753, G4666 (σμύρνα)); "myrrh", i.e. (by implication) perfumed oil

G4053.thayer: more, greater, excessive, abundant, exceedingly, vehemently; noun: preeminence, advantage.
G4053.mounce: over and above, Mt. 5:37; superfluous, 2 Cor. 9:1; extraordinary, Mt. 5:47; compar. more, greater, Mt. 11:9; 23:14; excessive, 2 Cor. 2:7; adverbially, περισσόν, in full abundance, Jn. 10:10; περισσότερον, and ἐκ περισσοῦ, exceedingly, vehemently, Mk. 6:51; 7:36; 1 Cor. 15:10; Eph. 3:20; τὸ περισσόν, preeminence, advantage, Rom. 3:1
G4053.abbottSmith: more than sufficient , over and above , abundant : Jo 10:10 , II Co 9:1 ; c. gen. (a popular substitute for πλειών , Bl., § 11, 3 4 ), Mt 5:37 ; id. c. ellips. gen., Mt 5:47 (EV, more than others ; but v. infr.); ἐκ περισσοῦ , Mk 6:51 , Rec., T. (on ὑπὲρ ὲκ π. , v.s. ὑπερεκπερισσοῦ ). Compar. neut., -ότερον : Lk 12:4 , 48 ; c. gen., Mk 12:33 ; adverbially (cf. περισσῶς ), more abundantly , II Co 1
G4053.strongs: from G4012 (περί) (in the sense of beyond); superabundant (in quantity) or superior (in quality); by implication, excessive; adverbially (with G1537 (ἐκ)) violently; neuter (as noun) preeminence
G4053.kjv: exceeding abundantly above, more abundantly, advantage, exceedingly, very highly, beyond measure, more, superfluous, vehement(-ly)

G4267.mounce: to know beforehand, to be previously acquainted with, Acts 26:5; 2 Pet. 3:17; to determine on beforehand, to foreordain, 1 Pet. 1:20; in NT, from the Hebrew, to foreknow, to appoint as the subject of future privileges, Rom. 8:29; 11:2*
G4267.abbottSmith: to know beforehand , foreknow : II Pe 3:17 ; c. acc. pers., Ac 26:5 ; of the Divine foreknowledge, Ro 8:29 11:2 , I Pe 1:20 .†
G4267.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1097 (γινώσκω); to know beforehand, i.e. foresee

G5551.thayer: I waste time, spend time, delay.
G5551.mounce: to spend time, waste time, linger, delay, Acts 20:16*
G5551.strongs: from a presumed compound of G5550 (χρόνος) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to be a time-wearer, i.e. to procrastinate (linger)

G1845.thayer: an exorcist, a caster out of evil spirits by the use of names or spells.
G1845.mounce: pr. one who puts an oath; in NT an exorcist, one who by various kinds of incantations, etc., pretended to expel demons, Acts 19:13*
G1845.strongs: from G1844 (ἐξορκίζω); one that binds by an oath (or spell), i.e. (by implication) an "exorcist" (conjurer)

G3390.abbottSmith: a metropolis , chief city : ITi, subscr. (Rec.).†
G3390.strongs: from G3384 (μήτηρ) and G4172 (πόλις); a mother city, i.e. "metropolis"

G561.thayer: against, over against, opposite, in view of, in the presence of.
G561.mounce: some classify as an improper preposition, opposite to, over against, Mt. 27:61; contrary to, in oppositition to, against, Acts 17:7; before, in the presence of, Mt. 27:24; Rom. 3:18; Acts 3:16*
G561.abbottSmith: over against (MM, s.v.): Mt 27:61 , Mk 12:41 , WH, mg.
G561.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1725 (ἔναντι); from in front, i.e. opposite, before or against
G561.kjv: before, contrary, over against, in the presence of

G3378.strongs: i.e. G3361 (μή) and G3756 (οὐ); as interrogative and negative, is it not that?

G3693.mounce: can function as an improper prep., from behind, behind, after, at the back of, Mt. 9:20; 15:23
G3693.abbottSmith: behind , after : Mt 9:20 , Mk 5:27 , Lk 8:44 , Re 4:6 5:1 . As prep. c. gen.: Mt 15:23 , Lk 23:26 , Re 1:10 (WH, mg.).†
G3693.strongs: from (regard; from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)) with enclitic of source; from the rear (as a secure aspect), i.e. at the back (adverb and preposition of place or time)

G693.mounce: made of silver, Acts 19:24; 2 Tim. 2:20; Rev. 9:20*
G693.abbottSmith: of silver : Ac 19:24 (WH, br.), II Ti 2:20 , Re 9:20 .†

G3453.thayer: I initiate, instruct; pass: I am disciplined, learn (a lesson).
G3453.mounce: to initiate, instruct in the sacred mysteries; in NT pass. to be disciplined in a practical lesson, to learn a lesson, Phil. 4:12*
G3453.abbottSmith: to initiate into the mysteries (so chiefly in cl.; LXX, III Mac 2:30 ); hence, to instruct : pass., Phl 4:12 (RV, I have learned the secret ).†
G3453.strongs: from the base of G3466 (μυστήριον); to initiate, i.e. (by implication) to teach

G1228.thayer: (adj. used often as a noun), slanderous; with the article: the Slanderer (par excellence), the Devil.
G1228.mounce: slanderer, 1 Tim. 3:11; 2 Tim. 3:3; Tit. 2:3; a treacherous informer, traitor, Jn. 6:70; ὁ διάβολος, the devil
G1228.abbottSmith: slanderous, accusing falsely . As subst., ὁ, ἡ, δ. ;
G1228.strongs: from G1225 (διαβάλλω); a traducer; specially, Satan (compare H07854)

G258.thayer: a fox; a fox-like, crafty person.
G258.mounce: a fox, Mt. 8:20; Lk. 9:58; met. a fox-like, crafty man, Lk. 13:32*
G258.abbottSmith: a fox : Mt 8:20 , Lk 9:58 ; metaph., of Herod, Lk 13:32 .†
G258.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a fox, i.e. (figuratively) a cunning person

G257.mounce: a threshing-floor, a place where corn is trodden out; meton. the corn which is trodden out, Mt. 3:12; Lk. 3:17*
G257.abbottSmith: a threshing floor : Mt 3:12 , Lk 3:17 (here prob. by meton. = the grain on the threshing floor).†
G257.strongs: probably from the base of G1507 (εἱλίσσω); a threshing-floor (as rolled hard), i.e. (figuratively) the grain (and chaff, as just threshed)

G729.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of the same as G4476 (ῥαφίς); unsewed, i.e. of a single piece

G4260.mounce: to go forward, advance, Mt. 4:21; Mk. 1:19; to advance in life, Lk. 1:7, 18; 2:36*
G4260.abbottSmith: to go forwards , go on , advance : Mt 4:21 , Mk 1:19 . Metaph., of age ( Ge 18:11 , al., Lys., Diod., al.), ἐν τ. ἡμέραις , Lk 1:7 , 18 2:36 .†
G4260.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to walk forward, i.e. advance (literally, or in years)
G4260.kjv: + be of a great age, go farther (on), be well stricken

G4848.thayer: I journey together with; I come together, assemble.
G4848.mounce: to go with, accompany, Lk. 7:11; 14:25; 24:15; to come together, assemble, Mk. 10:1*
G4848.abbottSmith: to journey together (Plat., al.): c. dat. pers., Lk 7:11 14:25 24:15 .
G4848.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to journey together; by implication, to assemble

G464.abbottSmith: to struggle against : seq. πρός , c. acc., He 12:4 .†
G464.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G75 (ἀγωνίζομαι); to struggle against (figuratively) ("antagonize")

G1493.thayer: a temple for (containing) an image (an idol).
G1493.abbottSmith: an idol's temple : I Co 8:10 (cf. Ἀσταρτεῖον , I Ki 31:10 ; v. ICC , in I Co, l.c.).†
G1493.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G1497 (εἴδωλον); an image-fane

G2152.mounce: reverent; pious, devout, religious, Acts 10:2, 7; 2 Pet. 2:9*
G2152.abbottSmith: pious, godly, devout : Ac 10:2 , 7 , II Pe 2:9 .†
G2152.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G4576 (σέβομαι); well-reverent, i.e. pious

G5279.thayer: I remind; pass: I remember, call to mind.
G5279.mounce: remember, remind, Jn. 14:26; Tit. 3:1; 2 Pet. 1:12; Jude 5; to suggest recollection of, remind others of, 2 Tim. 2:14; 3 Jn. 10; to call to mind, recollect, remember, Lk. 22:61*
G5279.abbottSmith: to cause one to remember, put one in mind or remind one of : c. acc rei, II Ti 2:14 , III Jo 10 ; c. dupl. acc (Thus., al.), Jo 14:26 ; c. acc pers., seq. περί , II Pe 1:12 ; id., seq. ὅτι , Ju 5 ; c. inf., Tit 3:1 ; pass., c. gen. rei, Lk 22:61 .†
G5279.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3403 (μιμνήσκω); to remind quietly, i.e. suggest to the (middle voice, one's own) memory
G5279.kjv: put in mind, remember, bring to (put in) remembrance

G2417.thayer: robbing temples, but possibly simply: sacrilegious.
G2417.mounce: one who despoils temples, commits sacrilege, Acts 19:37*
G2417.strongs: from G2411 (ἱερόν) and G4813 (συλάω); a temple-despoiler

G137.thayer: Aenon; Eusebius and Jerome place this site 8 (Roman) miles south of Scythopolis near the Jordan.
G137.mounce: Enon, pr. name, indecl., where Jn. was baptizing, Jn. 3:23*. See PhotoGuide.
G137.strongs: of Hebrew origin (a derivative of G5869, place of springs); Ænon, a place in Palestine

G2791.thayer: Cilicia, a Roman province between the Taurus range of mountains and the coast in the south-east corner of Asia Minor, linked up with the province of Syria.
G2791.mounce: Cilicia, a province of Asia Minor, Gal. 1:21
G2791.abbottSmith: Cilicia , a province of Asia Minor: Ac 6:9 15:23 , 41 21:39 22:3 23:34 27:5 , Ga 1:21 .†
G2791.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Cilicia, a region of Asia Minor

G1595.thayer: willing, with right good will, voluntary, spontaneous.
G1595.abbottSmith: usually of actions, voluntary : κατὰ ἑ. , of free will (Lft., in l), Phm 14 .†
G1595.strongs: neuter of a derivative from G1635 (ἑκών); voluntariness

G499.thayer: typical of, representing by type (or pattern), corresponding to, an image.
G499.mounce: of correspondent stamp or form; corresponding, in correspondent fashion, 1 Pet. 3:21; τὸ ἀντίτυπον, a copy, representation, Heb. 9:24*
G499.abbottSmith: act. striking back ; metaph., resisting , adverse .
G499.strongs: neuter of a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G5179 (τύπος); corresponding ("antitype"), i.e. a representative, counterpart

G5417.abbottSmith: to scourge : c. acc., Mt 27:26 , Mk 15:15 (eccl.).†
G5417.strongs: from a presumed equivalent of the Latin flagellum; to whip, i.e. lash as a public punishment

G4195.thayer: Pontus, a Roman province in the north of Asia Minor, bordering on the Black Sea, governed along with Bithynia.
G4195.abbottSmith: Pontus , a region of Asia Minor, bordering on the πόντος Εὔξεινος : Ac 2:9 , I Pe 1:1 .†
G4195.strongs: of Latin origin; a sea; Pontus, a region of Asia Minor

G413.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G1587 (ἐκλείπω); not left out, i.e. (by implication) inexhaustible

G918.thayer: Bartholomew, surname of Nathanael, one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.
G918.abbottSmith: Bartholomew , the Apostle (v.s. Ναθαναήλ ): Mt 10:3 , Mk 3:18 , Lk 6:14 , Ac 1:13 .†
G918.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and H08526); son of Tolmai; Bar-tholomæus, a Christian apostle

G1563.thayer: (a) there, yonder, in that place, (b) thither, there.
G1563.mounce: there, in that place, Mt. 2:13, 15; to that place, Mt. 2:22; 17:20
G1563.abbottSmith: properly, of place, there : Mt 2:13 5:24 , al.; οἱ ἐ. , Mt 26:71 ; οὗ . . . ἐ. , Mt 6:21 18:20 24:28 , Mk 6:10 , Lk 12:34 ; pleonastic, ὅπου . . . ἐ. (= שָׁם אֲשֶׁר , De 4:5 , al.), Re 12:6 , 14 (cf. Bl., § 50, 4).
G1563.strongs: of uncertain affinity; there; by extension, thither

G2711.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5594 (ψύχω); to cool down (off), i.e. refresh

G2897.mounce: also spelled κρεπάλη, drunken dissipation, Lk. 21:34*
G2897.strongs: probably from the same as G726 (ἁρπάζω); properly, a headache (as a seizure of pain) from drunkenness, i.e. (by implication) a debauch (by analogy, a glut)

G5143.thayer: I run, exercise myself, make progress.
G5143.mounce: an aperture, hole, eye of a needle, Lk. 18:25*
G5143.abbottSmith: to run : Mk 5:6 , Jo 20:2 , 4 , I Co 9:24 , 26 ; c. inf., Mt 28:8 ; seq. ἐπί , Lk 24:12 [T |[WH]| R, mg., om.]; εἰς , Re 9:9 ; ptcp., δραμών , c. indic., Mt 27:48 , Mk 15:36 , Lk 15:20 . Metaph., from runners in a race, of swiftness or of effort to attain an end: Ro 9:16 , Ga 2:2 5:7 , Phl 2:16 ; τ. ἀγῶνα (Hdt., Eur., al.), He 12:1 ; ὁ λόγος τ. κυρίου (cf. Ps 147:4 (146 15 ), ἕως τάχους δραμεῖται
G5143.strongs: apparently a primary verb (properly, ; compare G2359 (θρίξ)); which uses (the base of G1408 (δρόμος)) as alternate in certain tenses; to run or walk hastily (literally or figuratively)

G1263.thayer: I give solemn evidence, testify (declare) solemnly.
G1263.mounce: to make solemn affirmation, protest; to make a solemn and earnest charge, Lk. 16:28; Acts 2:40; to declare solemnly and earnestly, Acts 8:25; 18:5
G1263.abbottSmith: solemnly to protest : Lk 16:28 , Ac 2:40 8:25 10:42 18:5 20:21 , 23 , 24 23:11 28:23 , I Th 4:6 , He 2:6 ; in adjuration, seq. ἐνώπιον τ. θεοῦ , I Ti 5:21 , II Ti 2:14 4:1 (Cremer, 415).†
G1263.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3140 (μαρτυρέω); to attest or protest earnestly, or (by implication) hortatively

G1749.strongs: neuter of the same as G1747 (ἐνέδρα); an ambush, i.e. (figuratively) murderous design

G3485.thayer: a temple, a shrine, that part of the temple where God himself resides.
G3485.mounce: pr. a dwelling; the dwelling of a deity, a temple, Mt. 26:61; Acts 7:48; used figuratively of individuals, Jn. 2:19; 1 Cor. 3:16; spc. the cell of a temple; hence, the Holy Place of the Temple of Jerusalem, Mt. 23:35; Lk. 1:9; a model of a temple, a shrine, Acts 19:24
G3485.strongs: from a primary (to dwell); a fane, shrine, temple

G5364.strongs: adverb from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G444 (ἄνθρωπος); fondly to man ("philanthropically"), i.e. humanely

G5030.mounce: adverb of ταχύς, quickly, speedily; soon, shortly, 1 Cor. 4:19; Gal. 1:6; hastily, Lk. 14:21; 16:6; with inconsiderate haste, 1 Tim. 5:22
G5030.abbottSmith: quickly, hastily : Lk 14:21 16:6 , Jo 11:31 , I Co 4:19 , Phl 2:19 , 24 , II Ti 4:9 ; with suggestion of rashness, Ga 1:6 , II Th 2:2 , I Ti 5:22 .†
G5030.strongs: adverb from G5036 (ταχύς); briefly, i.e. (in time) speedily, or (in manner) rapidly

G2127.thayer: (lit: I speak well of) I bless; pass: I am blessed.
G2127.mounce: pr. to speak well of, in NT to bless, ascribe praise and glorification, Lk. 1:64; to bless, invoke a blessing upon, Mt. 5:44; to bless, confer a favor or blessing upon, Eph. 1:3; Heb. 6:14; pass. to be blessed, to be an object of favor or blessing, Lk. 1:28
G2127.abbottSmith: to speak well of, praise (cl.; LXX De 8:10 , al.): τ. θεόν , Lk 1:64 2:28 24:51 , 53 ( αἰνοῦντες , T, WH, mg.), Ja 3:9 ; absol., to give praise , Mt 14:19 26:26 (v. Swete on Mk 14:22 ), Mk 6:41 14:22 (v. Swete, in l.), Lk 24:30 , I Co 14:16 .
G2127.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3056 (λόγος); to speak well of, i.e. (religiously) to bless (thank or invoke a benediction upon, prosper)

G1776.thayer: I disturb, cause tumult, trouble, annoy.
G1776.mounce: to trouble, annoy; to be a trouble, Lk. 6:18; Heb. 12:15*
G1776.abbottSmith: to trouble : c. acc, He 12:15 . Pass., seq. ἀπό , Lk 6:18 .†
G1776.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3791 (ὀχλέω); to crowd in, i.e. (figuratively) to annoy

G5181.thayer: Tyrannus, an inhabitant of Ephesus, probably a rhetorician.
G5181.strongs: a provincial form of the derivative of the base of G2962 (κύριος); a "tyrant"; Tyrannus, an Ephesian

G5583.mounce: one who utters a falsehood, a liar, Jn. 8:44, 55; Rom. 3:4; 1 Tim. 1:10; Tit. 1:12; 1 Jn. 1:10; 2:4, 22; 4:20; 5:10*

G1219.mounce: public, belonging to the public, Acts 5:18; δημοσίᾳ, publicly, Acts 16:37; 18:28; 20:20*
G1219.abbottSmith: belonging to the people, public : Ac 5:18 ; dat. fem. used adverbially (cl.);
G1219.strongs: from G1218 (δῆμος); public; (feminine singular dative case as adverb) in public

G4293.mounce: to declare or announce beforehand, foretell, predict, Acts 3:18; 7:52*
G4293.abbottSmith: to announce beforehand : c. acc. et inf., Ac 3:18 ; seq. περί , Ac 7:52 .†
G4293.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2605 (καταγγέλλω); to anounce beforehand, i.e. predict, promise

G1703.mounce: a mocker, derider, scoffer, 2 Pet. 3:3; Jude 18*
G1703.strongs: from G1702 (ἐμπαίζω); a derider, i.e. (by implication) a false teacher

G588.thayer: I receive, welcome, entertain (with hospitality), embrace.
G588.mounce: to receive kindly or heartily, welcome, Lk. 8:40; 9:11; Acts 18:27; 28:30; to receive with hearty assent, embrace, Acts 2:41; to accept with satisfaction, Acts 24:3*
G588.abbottSmith: to accept gladly , welcome , receive : Lk 8:40 9:11 , Ac 18:27 21:17 28:30 ; metaph., c. acc. rei, Ac 2:41 24:3 (MM, s.v.; Cremer, 688).†
G588.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to take fully, i.e. welcome (persons), approve (things)

G1671.thayer: Hellas, the native name for Greece.
G1671.mounce: Hellas, Greece; in NT the southern portion of Greece as distinguished from Macedonia, Acts 20:2*
G1671.abbottSmith: with varying usage as to geographical limits; in NT = ' Αχαία (cf. Ac 18:12 ),
G1671.strongs: of uncertain affinity; Hellas (or Greece), a country of Europe

G1830.thayer: I search diligently, I examine carefully (minutely).
G1830.abbottSmith: to search out, search carefully : seq. περί , I Pe 1:10 .†
G1830.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2045 (ἐρευνάω); to explore (figuratively)

G2626.abbottSmith: to inundate , deluge : pass., II Pe 3:6 .†
G2626.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G2830 (κλύδων); to dash (wash) down, i.e. (by implication) to deluge

G2392.abbottSmith: a healing , cure : Ac 4:22 , 30 ; pl., Lk 13:32 .†

G4037.abbottSmith: to wait for : c. acc.., Ac 1:4 .†
G4037.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G3306 (μένω); to stay around, i.e. await

G2924.thayer: critical, able to judge or discern.
G2924.mounce: able or quick to discern or judge, Heb. 4:12*
G2924.abbottSmith: critical , able to discern or judge : c. gen. obj., He 4:12 .†

G3865.mounce: to look at things placed side by side, as in comparison, to compare in thus looking, to regard less in comparison, overlook, neglect, Acts 6:1*
G3865.abbottSmith: to examine side by side , compare .
G3865.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2334 (θεωρέω); to overlook or disregard

G970.strongs: probably akin to G979 (βίος) (through the idea of vital activity); force

G2465.thayer: equal to or like the angels.
G2465.mounce: equal or similar to angels, Lk. 20:36*
G2465.abbottSmith: like or equal to angels : Lk 20:36 .†
G2465.strongs: from G2470 (ἴσος) and G32 (ἄγγελος); like an angel, i.e. angelic

G4031.thayer: having full power over, mastering, gaining control over.
G4031.mounce: overpowering; περικρατὴς γενέσθαι, to become master of, to secure, Acts 27:16*
G4031.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2904 (κράτος); strong all around, i.e. a master (manager)

G3463.thayer: ten thousand; also used for a very large number, innumerable.
G3463.strongs: plural of an apparently primary word (properly, meaning very many); ten thousand; by extension, innumerably many

G537.mounce: to take leave of, say farwell to, Acts 21:6*
G537.abbottSmith: all , the whole , altogether : bef. subst. with art., as Lk 3:21 ; or after, as Mk 16:[15] ; absol., in masc., as Lk 5:26 ; in neut., as Ac 2:44 ; ἅ. οὗτοι , Ac 2:7 (LT); ἅ. ὑμεῖς , Ga 3:28 (TTr.). Most freq. in Lk, Ac (v. MM, VG , s.v.).
G537.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and G3956 (πᾶς); absolutely all or (singular) every one

G4369.thayer: I place (put) to, add; I do again.
G4369.mounce: to put to or near; to lay with or by the side of, Acts 13:36; to add, adjoin, Mt. 6:27, 33; Lk. 3:20; Acts 2:41; from the Hebrew, denote continuation, or repetition, Lk. 19:11; 20:11, 12; Acts 12:3
G4369.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place additionally, i.e. lay beside, annex, repeat
G4369.kjv: add, again, give more, increase, lay unto, proceed further, speak to any more

G3792.thayer: I gather a crowd, make a riot.
G3792.mounce: to collect a mob, create a tumult, Acts 17:5*
G3792.abbottSmith: to gather a crowd , make a riot : Ac 17:5 (not elsewhere).†
G3792.strongs: from G3793 (ὄχλος) and G4160 (ποιέω); to make a crowd, i.e. raise a public disturbance

G129.mounce: blood; of the color of blood; bloodshed; blood-guiltiness; natural descent
G129.abbottSmith: blood. In the ordinary sense: Mk 5:25 , Lk 8:43 , 44 22:44 , Jo 19:34 , Ac 15:20 , 29 21:25 , Re 8:7 , 8 11:6 16:3 , 4 , 6 19:13 . In special senses: of generation, origin, kinship (cl.): Jo 1:13 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.); as in OT (AR on Eph , 6:12 ), in the phrase σάρξ καὶ αἷ. ( αἷ. κ. σ. ), to indicate human nature as opp. to God and created spirits: Mt 16:17 , I Co 15:50 , Ga 1:16 , Eph 6:12 , He 2:
G129.strongs: of uncertain derivation; blood, literally (of men or animals), figuratively (the juice of grapes) or specially (the atoning blood of Christ); by implication, bloodshed, also kindred

G3560.mounce: pr. to put in mind; to admonish, warn, Acts 20:31; Rom. 15:14
G3560.abbottSmith: to admonish , exhort: c. acc. pers., Ac 20:31 , Ro 15:14 , I Co 4:14 , Col 1:28 3:16 , I Th 5:12 , 14 , II Th 3:15 .†
G3560.strongs: from the same as G3559 (νουθεσία); to put in mind, i.e. (by implication) to caution or reprove gently

G2215.thayer: spurious wheat, darnel; a plant that grows in Palestine which resembles wheat in many ways but is worthless.
G2215.mounce: zizanium, darnel, spurious wheat, a plant found in Palestine, which resembles wheat both in its stalk and grain, but is worthless, Mt. 13:25, 26, 27, 29, 30, 36, 38, 40*
G2215.abbottSmith: zizanium (EV, tares ), a kind of darnel, resembling wheat: Mt 13:25-27 , 29 , 30 , 36 , 38 , 40 (cf. DB , s.v. "Tares").†
G2215.strongs: of uncertain origin; darnel or false grain

G3914.mounce: to winter, spend the winter, Acts 27:12; 28:11; 1 Cor. 16:6; Tit. 3:12*
G3914.abbottSmith: to winter at a place: seq. εἰς , Ac 27:12 ; ἐν , Ac 28:11 ; πρὸς ὑμᾶς , I Co 16:6 ; ἐκεῖ , Tit 3:12 .†
G3914.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5492 (χειμάζω); to winter near, i.e. stay with over the rainy season

G3396.mounce: also spelled μείγνυμι, to mix, mingle, Mt. 27:34; Lk. 13:1; Rev. 8:7
G3396.abbottSmith: to mix , mingle : c. acc. et dat., Re 15:2 ; c. acc. seq. ἐν , Re 8:7 ; seq. μετά , Mt 27:34 , Lk 13:1 .†

G1819.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of the same as G160 (αἰφνίδιος); of a sudden, i.e. unexpectedly

G2973.thayer: Cosam, son of Elmadam and father of Addi.
G2973.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H07081); Cosam (i.e. Kosam) an Israelite

G1488.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G1488.strongs: second person singular present of G1510 (εἰμί); thou art

G4691.thayer: a babbler, gossiper, one who picks up seeds and trifles as does a bird.
G4691.mounce: pr. seed-picking; one who picks up and retails scraps of information; a gossip; a babbler, Acts 17:18*
G4691.strongs: from G4690 (σπέρμα) and G3004 (λέγω); a seed-picker (as the crow), i.e. (figuratively) a sponger, loafer (specially, a gossip or trifler in talk)

G3381.thayer: lest in any way, lest perhaps.
G3381.abbottSmith: as conjc., lest haply : in final sentences, I Co 9:27 , II Co 2:7 9:4 ; after verbs of fearing or taking heed, I Co 8:9 , II Co 11:3 12:20 , Ga 4:11 ; with an ellipse of ptcp. (sc. φοβούμενος ; cf. Bl., § 65, 3; Burton, § 225), I Th 3:5 (but v. infr.).
G3381.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G4458 (-πώς); lest somehow
G3381.kjv: lest (by any means, by some means, haply, perhaps)

G2989.mounce: to shine, give light, Mt. 5:15, 16; 17:2; to flash, shine, Lk. 17:24; Acts 12:7; 2 Cor. 4:6*
G2989.abbottSmith: to shine : Mt 5:15 , 16 17:2 , Lk 17:24 , Ac 12:7 , II Co 4:6 (cf. ἐκ- , περι-λάμπω ).†
G2989.strongs: a primary verb; to beam, i.e. radiate brilliancy (literally or figuratively)

G3154.thayer: I become vain or foolish, am perverted.
G3154.mounce: to make vain; from the Hebrew, pass. to fall into religious error, to be perverted, Rom. 1:21*
G3154.strongs: from G3152 (μάταιος); to render (passively, become) foolish, i.e. (morally) wicked or (specially), idolatrous

G923.thayer: Barsabbas, son of Sabbas, a surname of Joseph and Judas.
G923.mounce: Bar-sabas, pr. name (1) Joseph, surnamed Justus, Acts 1:23 (2) Judas, Acts 15:22*
G923.abbottSmith: Barsabbas : the surname of one Joseph: Ac 1:23 . The surname of one Judas: Ac 15:22 .†
G923.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and probably H06634); son of Sabas (or Tsaba); Bar-sabas, the name of two Israelites

G1851.mounce: pr. prominence, anything prominent; in NT eminence, distinction, Acts 25:23*
G1851.strongs: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning to stand out); prominence (figuratively)

G4356.thayer: a receiving, a taking to one's self, acceptance.

G5509.mounce: a tunic, vest, the inner garment which fitted close to the body, having armholes, and sometimes sleeves, and reaching below the knees, worn by both sexes, Mt. 5:40; 10:10; pl. χιτῶνες, clothes, garments in general, Mk. 14:63
G5509.abbottSmith: the garment worn next the skin (though two tunics were sometimes worn, v. Swete, Mk. , 117), a tunic : Mt 10:10 , Mk 6:9 14:63 , Lk 3:11 9:3 , Ju 23 ; disting. from ἱμάτιον (q.v.), Mt 5:40 , Lk 6:29 , Jo 19:23 , Ac 9:39 (v. DCG , i, 338 a , 340 a , 499 a )
G5509.strongs: of foreign origin (H03801); a tunic or shirt

G1470.mounce: to conceal in anything; to mix, intermix, Mt. 13:33; Lk. 13:21*
G1470.abbottSmith: to conceal in : c. acc., seq. εἰς , Mt 13:33 .†
G1470.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2928 (κρύπτω); to conceal in, i.e. incorporate with

G785.mounce: an asp, a species of serpent of the most deadly venom, Rom. 3:13*
G785.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a buckler (or round shield); used of a serpent (as coiling itself), probably the "asp"

G846.thayer: he, she, it, they, them, same.
G846.mounce: self, very; alone, Mk. 6:31; 2 Cor. 12:13; of one’s self, of one’s own motion, Jn. 16:27; used also in the oblique cases independently as a personal pron. of the third person; ὁ αὐτός, the same; unchangeable, Heb. 1:12; κατὰ τὸ αὐτό, at the same time, together, Acts 14:1; ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό, in one and the same place, Mt. 22:34; at the same time, together, Acts 3:1
G846.strongs: from the particle (perhaps akin to the base of G109 (ἀήρ) through the idea of a baffling wind) (backward); the reflexive pronoun self, used (alone or in the comparative G1438 (ἑαυτοῦ)) of the third person , and (with the proper personal pronoun) of the other persons
