G846.kjv: her, it(-self), one, the other, (mine) own, said, (self-), the) same, ((him-, my-, thy- )self, (your-)selves, she, that, their(-s), them(-selves), there(-at, - by, -in, -into, -of, -on, -with), they, (these) things, this (man), those, together, very, which

G5178.thayer: I torture, break on the wheel, beat to death.
G5178.mounce: pr. to beat a drum; to drum upon; in NT to torture, beat to death with rods and clubs, Heb. 11:35*
G5178.strongs: from a derivative of G5180 (τύπτω) (meaning a drum, "tympanum"); to stretch on an instrument of torture resembling a drum, and thus beat to death

G5487.mounce: to favor, visit with favor, to make an object of favor, to gift, Eph. 1:6; pass. to be visited with free favor, be an object of gracious visitation, Lk. 1:28*
G5487.strongs: from G5485 (χάρις); to grace, i.e. indue with special honor

G5040.mounce: a little child; τεκνία, an endearing appellation, my dear children, Jn. 13:33; 1 Jn. 2:1, 12, 28; 3:7, 18; 4:4; 5:21*
G5040.abbottSmith: a little child : as a term of endearment, in voc. pl., Jo 13:33 , Ga 4:19 , I Jo 2:1 , 12 , 28 3:7 , 18 4:4 5:21 .†
G5040.strongs: diminutive of G5043 (τέκνον); an infant, i.e. (plural figuratively) darlings (Christian converts)

G2112.abbottSmith: straightway, at once, directly : Ga 1:16 , Ja 1:24 , III Jo 14 (cf. Dalman, Words , 28 f.), Re 4:2 , and freq. in Mt, Lk, Jo, Ac (in Mk, εὐθύς ), q.v.)
G2112.strongs: adverb from G2117 (εὐθύς); directly, i.e. at once or soon
G2112.kjv: anon, as soon as, forthwith, immediately, shortly, straightway

G3455.mounce: to low, bellow, as a bull; also, to roar, as a lion, Rev. 10:3*
G3455.abbottSmith: prop., of oxen (onomatop.), to low , bellow ; of a lion, to roar : Re 10:3 .†
G3455.strongs: from a presumed derivative of ( to "moo"); to bellow (roar)

G1571.mounce: to cleanse thoroughly, purify, 2 Tim. 2:21; to purge out, eliminate, 1 Cor. 5:7*
G1571.abbottSmith: to cleanse thoroughly, cleanse out : c. acc, ἑαυτόν , II Ti 2:21 ; of the impurity removed, ζυμήν , I Co 5:7 .†
G1571.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2508 (καθαίρω); to cleanse thoroughly

G1291.thayer: I give a commission (instructions), order; I admonish, prohibit.
G1291.mounce: to determine, issue a decision; to state or explain distinctly and accurately; hence, to admonish, direct, charge, command, Acts 15:24; Heb. 12:20; when followed by a negative clause, to interdict, prohibit, Mt. 16:30; Mk. 5:43
G1291.strongs: middle voice from G1223 (διά) and G4724 (στέλλω); to set (oneself) apart (figuratively, distinguish), i.e. (by implication) to enjoin

G2819.thayer: (a) a lot, (b) a portion assigned; hence: a portion of the people of God assigned to one's care, a congregation.
G2819.mounce: a lot, die, a thing used in determining chances, Mt. 27:35; Mk. 15:24; Lk. 23:34; Jn. 19:24; Acts 1:26; assignment, investiture, Acts 1:17; allotment, destination, Col. 1:12; a part, portion, share, Acts 8:21; 26:18; a constituent portion of the Church, 1 Pet. 5:3*
G2819.abbottSmith: a lot ; that which is cast or drawn: Mt 27:35 , Mk 15:24 , Lk 23:34 , Jo 19:24 (LXX) , Ac 1:26 ; that which is obtained by casting: Ac 1:17 8:21 ; of a part in the Kingdom of God (cf. Wi 5:5 ), Ac 26:18 , Col 1:12 ; pl. of the "cure" of a presbyter, I Pe 5:3 (v. ICC , in l.).†
G2819.strongs: probably from G2806 (κλάω) (through the idea of using bits of wood, etc., for the purpose; a die (for drawing chances); by implication, a portion (as if so secured); by extension, an acquisition (especially a patrimony, figuratively)

G2583.thayer: (lit: a level, ruler), a rule, regulation, rule of conduct or doctrine, (b) a measured (defined) area, province.
G2583.mounce: a measure, rule; in NT prescribed range of action or duty, 2 Cor. 10:13, 15, 16; met. rule of conduct or doctrine, Gal. 6:16*
G2583.strongs: from (a straight reed, i.e. rod); a rule ("canon"), i.e. (figuratively) a standard (of faith and practice); by implication, a boundary, i.e. (figuratively) a sphere (of activity)

G3716.thayer: I walk in a straight course, walk uprightly.
G3716.mounce: to walk in a straight course; to be straightforward in moral conduct, Gal. 2:14*
G3716.abbottSmith: = cl. εὐθυπορέω , to walk straight : metaph., Ga 2:14 (not elsewhere).†
G3716.strongs: from a compound of G3717 (ὀρθός) and G4228 (πούς); to be straight-footed, i.e. (figuratively) to go directly forward

G3115.mounce: patience; patient enduring of evil, fortitude, Col. 1:11; Col. 3:12; 1 Tim. 1:16; 1 Pet. 3:20; slowness of avenging injuries, long-suffering, forbearance, clemency, Rom. 2:4; 9:22; 2 Cor. 6:6; Gal. 5:22; Eph. 4:2; 2 Tim. 4:2; Jas. 5:10; patient expectation, 2 Tim. 3:10; Heb. 6:12; 2 Pet. 3:15*
G3115.abbottSmith: patience , longsuffering : of men, esp. in experiencing troubles and difficulties, Col 1:11 , II Ti 3:10 , He 6:12 , Ja 5:10 ; of God's forbearance, Ro 2:4 9:22 , II Co 6:6 , Ga 5:22 , Eph 4:2 , Col 3:12 , I Ti 1:16 , II Ti 4:2 , I Pe 3:20 , II Pe 3:15 .†
G3115.strongs: from the same as G3116 (μακροθυμώς); longanimity, i.e. (objectively) forbearance or (subjectively) fortitude

G5470.mounce: contracted form of χάλκεος, made of copper, brass, or bronze, Rev. 9:20*

G2025.mounce: to smear upon, to anoint, Jn. 9:6, 11*
G2025.abbottSmith: to spread on, anoint : c. acc, Jo 9:11 ; id. seq. ἐπί , c. acc, Jo 9:6 (cf. MM, Exp. , xiv).†
G2025.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5548 (χρίω); to smear over

G2295.thayer: (a) concr: a marvel, wonder, (b) abstr: wonder, amazement.
G2295.mounce: a wonder; wonder, admiration, astonishment, 2 Cor. 11:14; Rev. 17:6*
G2295.strongs: apparently from a form of G2300 (θεάομαι); wonder (properly concrete; but by implication, abstract)

G4804.thayer: a disputer, as the Greek sophists.
G4804.mounce: a disputant, controversial reasoner, sophist, 1 Cor. 1:20*
G4804.strongs: from G4802 (συζητέω); a disputant, i.e. sophist

G4077.thayer: a fountain, spring, well, issue, flow.
G4077.mounce: a source, spring, fountain, Jas. 3:11, 12; a well, Jn. 4:6; an issue, flux, flow, Mk. 5:29; met. Jn. 4:14
G4077.abbottSmith: a spring , fountain : Ja 3:11 , II Pe 2:17 ; τ. ὑδάτων , Re 8:10 14:7 16:4 ; of a well fed by a spring, Jo 4:6 ; π. τοῦ αἵματος , of a flow of blood, Mk 5:29 ; metaph., Jo 4:14 , Re 7:17 21:6 .†
G4077.strongs: probably from G4078 (πήγνυμι) (through the idea of gushing plumply); a fount (literally or figuratively), i.e. source or supply (of water, blood, enjoyment) (not necessarily the original spring)

G1747.mounce: pr. a sitting in or on a spot; an ambush, or lying in wait, Acts 23:16; 25:3*
G1747.abbottSmith: a lying in wait, an ambush : Ac 23:16 (Rec. ἔνεδρον , a form freq. in LXX), Ac 25:3 .†
G1747.strongs: feminine from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); an ambuscade, i.e. (figuratively) murderous purpose

G2458.strongs: of Latin origin; Junias, a Christian

G4554.thayer: Sardis, an ancient city of Lydia in the province of Asia.
G4554.mounce: Sardis, the capital city of Lydia, in Asia Minor Rev. 1:11; 3:1, 4*. See PhotoGuide.
G4554.abbottSmith: Sardis , the chief city of Lydia: Re 1:11 3:1 , 4 .†
G4554.strongs: plural of uncertain derivation; Sardis, a place in Asia Minor

G1039.mounce: made of fine linen or fine cotton, Rev. 18:16; 18:8 (2x), 14*
G1039.abbottSmith: made of βύσσος , fine linen : Re 18:12 , 16 19:8 , 14 .†
G1039.strongs: from G1040 (βύσσος); made of linen (neuter a linen cloth)

G2379.mounce: an altar, Mt. 5:23, 24; Lk. 1:11; spc. the altar of burnt-offering, Mt. 23:35; Lk. 11:51; meton. a class of sacrifices, Heb. 13:10
G2379.abbottSmith: an altar : generally, Ja 2:21 ; pl., Ro 11:3 (LXX) ; metaph., He 13:10 (v. Westc., in l., and esp. his add. note on the history of the word, 455 ff.); of the altar of burnt-offering in the Temple, Mt 5:23 , 24 23:18-20 , 35 , Lk 11:51 , I Co 9:13 10:18 , He 7:13 , Re 11:1 ; of the altar of incense in the sanctuary ( Ex 30:1 , al.), Lk 1:11 ; symbolically in Heaven, Re 6:9 8:3 , 5 9:13 14:18 16:7 (
G2379.strongs: from a derivative of G2378 (θυσία); a place of sacrifice, i.e. an altar (special or genitive case, literal or figurative)

G344.thayer: I bend or turn back; I return.
G344.mounce: pr. to reflect, bend back; hence, to bend back one’s course, return, Mt. 2:12; Lk. 10:6; Acts 18:21; Heb. 11:15*
G344.abbottSmith: trans., to bend or turn back .
G344.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2578 (κάμπτω); to turn back

G4361.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and the same as G3983 (πεινάω); hungering further, i.e. intensely hungry

G3885.mounce: lame, palsied, used only as a noun in NT, paralytic, Mt. 4:24; 8:6; 9:2, 6
G3885.strongs: from a derivative of G3886 (παραλύω); as if dissolved, i.e. "paralytic"

G5180.thayer: I beat, strike, wound, inflict punishment.
G5180.mounce: to beat, strike, smite, Mt. 24:49; 27:30; to beat the breast, as expressive of grief or strong emotion, Lk. 18:13; 23:48; in NT met. to wound or shock the conscience of any one, 1 Cor. 8:12; from the Hebrew, to smite with evil, punish, Acts 23:3
G5180.abbottSmith: to strike, smite, beat : c. acc, Mt 24:49 , Mk 15:19 , Lk 12:45 , Ac 18:17 21:32 23:2 , 3 ; of mourners, τὰ στήθη , Lk 18:13 23:48 ; ἐπὶ τ. σιαγόνα , Lk 6:29 ; εἰς τ. κεφαλήν , Mt 27:30 . Metaph., of God inflicting evil: Ac 23:3 (cf. Ex 8:2 , Ez 7:9 , al.); of disquieting conscience, I Co 8:12 .†
G5180.strongs: a primary verb (in a strengthened form); to "thump", i.e. cudgel or pummel (properly, with a stick or bastinado), but in any case by repeated blows; thus differing from G3817 (παίω) and G3960 (πατάσσω), which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 (πλήσσω) with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 (ῥαπίζω) with the palm; as well as from G5177 (τυγχάνω), an accidental collision); by implication, to punish; figuratively, to offend (the conscience)

G1308.thayer: (a) trans: I carry through, hither and thither, (b) intrans: I am different, differ, and sometimes: I surpass, excel.
G1308.mounce: to convey through, across, Mk. 11:16; to carry different ways or into different parts, separate; pass. to be borne, driven, or tossed hither and thither, Acts 27:27; to be proclaimed, published, Acts 13:49; intrans. met. to differ, 1 Cor. 15:41; to excel, be better or of greater value, be superior, Mt. 6:26; 10:31; impers. διαφέρει, it makes a difference, it is of consequence; with οὐδέν, it makes no difference, it is nothing, Gal. 2:6
G1308.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear through, i.e. (literally) transport; usually to bear apart, i.e. (objectively) to toss about (figuratively, report); subjectively, to "differ", or (by implication) surpass
G1308.kjv: be better, carry, differ from, drive up and down, be (more) excellent, make matter, publish, be of more value

G876.strongs: apparently a primary word; froth, i.e. slaver

G3106.thayer: I bless, pronounce blessed or happy.
G3106.mounce: to pronounce happy, fortunate, Lk. 1:48; Jas. 5:11*
G3106.abbottSmith: to bless , pronounce blessed or happy : c. acc. pers., Lk 1:48 , Ja 5:11 .†
G3106.strongs: from G3107 (μακάριος); to beatify, i.e. pronounce (or esteem) fortunate

G3002.thayer: Lebbaeus, a pet-name for Thaddaeus, one of the twelve apostles of Jesus; the full form of the name is not known.
G3002.abbottSmith: Lebbæus : Mt 10:3 , Mk 3:18 (WH, mg.; Θαδδαῖος , q.v., Rec., WH, txt., RV, cf. WH, App. , 11, 24. In Lk 6:15 , Ac 1:13 , he is called Σίμων ὁ Ζηλωτής .)†
G3002.strongs: of uncertain origin; Lebbæus, a Christian

G4959.mounce: to groan or lament together, Rom. 8:22*
G4959.abbottSmith: to groan together or with : Ro 8:22 (c. dat. pers., Eur., Ion., 935).†
G4959.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4727 (στενάζω); to moan jointly, i.e. (figuratively) experience a common calamity

G1833.thayer: I examine, question, inquire at, search out.
G1833.mounce: to search out; to inquire by interrogation, examine strictly, Mt. 2:8; 10:11; to interrogate, Jn. 21:12*
G1833.abbottSmith: to examine closely, inquire carefully ( of ): seq. περί (c. ἀκριβῶς ), Mt 2:8 ; seq. τίς , Mt 10:11 ; c. acc pers., Jo 21:12 .†
G1833.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and (to examine); to test thoroughly (by questions), i.e. ascertain or interrogate

G1261.thayer: a calculation, reasoning, thought, movement of thought, deliberation, plotting.
G1261.mounce: reasoning, thought, cogitation, purpose, Mt. 15:19; Mk. 7:21; discourse, dispute, disputation, contention, Lk. 9:46; doubt, hesitation, scruple, Lk. 24:38
G1261.abbottSmith: a thought, reasoning, inward questioning : Mt 15:19 , Mk 7:21 , Lk 2:35 5:22 6:8 9:46, , 47 24:38 , Ro 1:21 14:1 , I Co 3:20 (LXX) , Phl 2:14 ; κριταὶ δ. πονηρῶν , gen. of qual. (cf. Pr 12:5 ), Ja 2:4 ; χωρὶς ὀργῆς καὶ δ. (where perh. δ. , like מְזִמָּה , in Ps 138(139):20 , al., implies evil intention), I Ti 2:8 (v. Hort, in l.; cf. Cremer, 400).†
G1261.strongs: from G1260 (διαλογίζομαι); discussion, i.e. (internal) consideration (by implication, purpose), or (external) debate

G2353.thayer: (lit: a nursling, hence probably) plur: cattle.
G2353.mounce: that which is reared (especially sheep and goats); pl. cattle, Jn. 4:12*
G2353.abbottSmith: a nurseling , esp. of animals (Eur., Plat., al.).
G2353.strongs: from G5142 (τρέφω); stock (as raised on a farm)

G4702.mounce: sown, fit to be sown; in NT τὰ σπόριμα, fields which are sown, fields of grain, cornfields, Mt. 12:1; Mk. 2:23; Lk. 6:1*
G4702.abbottSmith: fit for sowing, sown ; as subst., τὰ σ. , corn-fields : Mt 12:1 , Mk 2:23 , Lk 6:1 .†
G4702.strongs: from G4703 (σπόρος); sown, i.e. (neuter plural) a planted field

G2433.thayer: (a) I have mercy on, show favor to, (b) trans. with object of sins: I forgive, pardon.
G2433.mounce: to appease, render propitious; in NT to expiate, make an atonement or expiation for, Heb. 2:17; ἱλάσθητι, be gracious, show mercy, pardon, Lk. 18:13*
G2433.abbottSmith: in cl., c. acc. pers., to conciliate , appease , propitiate (= ἐξιλάσκομαι , Ge 32:20 , Pr 16:14 , Ma 1:9 , al.).
G2433.strongs: middle voice from the same as G2436 (ἵλεως); to conciliate, i.e. (transitively) to atone for (sin), or (intransitively) be propitious

G908.thayer: the rite or ceremony of baptism.
G908.mounce: pr. immersion; baptism, ordinance of baptism, Mt. 3:7; Rom. 6:4; met. baptism in the trial of suffering, Mt. 20:22, 23; Mk. 10:38, 39
G908.abbottSmith: immersion , baptism ; metaph., of affliction: Mk 10:38 , 39 , Lk 12:50 . Of the religious rite of baptism; of John's baptism: Mt 3:7 21:25 , Mk 11:30 , Lk 7:29 20:4 , Ac 1:22 10:37 18:25 19:3 ; β. μετανοίας , Mk 1:4 , Lk 3:3 , Ac 13:24 19:4 ; of Christian baptism; Ro 6:4 , Eph 4:5 , Col 2:12 (Tr., -μῷ , q.v.), I Pe 3:21 (cf. Cremer, 130; Tr., Syn. § xcix).†

G4286.thayer: a setting forth, the show-bread; predetermination, purpose.
G4286.mounce: a setting forth or before; οἱ ἄρτοι τῆς προθέσεως, and ἡ πρόθεσις τῶν ἄρτων, the shewbread, the twelve loaves of bread, corresponding to the twelve tribes, which were set out in two rows upon the golden table in the sanctuary, Mt. 12:4; Mk. 2:26; Lk. 6:4; Heb. 9:2; predetermination, purpose, Acts 11:23; 27:13; Rom. 8:28; 2 Tim. 3:10
G4286.abbottSmith: a setting forth (Plat., Plut., al.): οἱ ἄρτοι τῆς π. (cf. LXX, Ex 40:4 , 23 , I Ch 9:32 , II Ch 4:19 , I Mac 1:22 , II Mac 3:8 , elsewhere ἄρτοι ἐνώπιοι , Ex 25:29 ; οἱ ἄ. τοῦ προσώπου , Ne 10:33 ), Mt 12:4 , Mk 2:26 , Lk 6:4 ; ἡ π. τῶν ἄρτων , He 9:2 .
G4286.strongs: from G4388 (προτίθεμαι); a setting forth, i.e. (figuratively) proposal (intention); specially, the show-bread (in the Temple) as exposed before God

G255.mounce: pr. bringing in no revenue or profit; hence, unprofitable, useless; detrimental; ruinous, disastrous, Heb. 13:17*
G255.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G3081 (λυσιτελεῖ); gainless, i.e. (by implication) pernicious

G365.thayer: I renew; mid: I renew myself, am renewed.
G365.mounce: also spelled ἀνανεόομαι, to renew; pass. to be renewed, be renovated, by inward reformation, Eph. 4:23*
G365.abbottSmith: to renew : pass., Eph 4:23 (v. Cremer, 428; MM, VGT , s.v.)†
G365.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a derivative of G3501 (νέος); to renovate, i.e. reform

G361.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G264 (ἁμαρτάνω); sinless

G4631.strongs: from G4632 (σκεῦος); furniture, i.e. spare tackle

G4716.mounce: a stake; a cross, Mt. 27:32, 40, 42; Phil. 2:8; by impl. the punishment of the cross, crucifixion, Eph. 2:16; Heb. 12:2; meton. the crucifixion of Christ in respect of its import, the doctrine of the cross, 1 Cor. 17:18; Gal. 5:11; 6:12, 14; met. to take up, or bear one’s cross, to be ready to encounter any extremity, Mt. 10:38; 16:24
G4716.abbottSmith: an upright pale or stake (Hom., Hdt., Thuc., al.)
G4716.strongs: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a stake or post (as set upright), i.e. (specially), a pole or cross (as an instrument of capital punishment); figuratively, exposure to death, i.e. self-denial; by implication, the atonement of Christ

G5102.abbottSmith: a title, inscription : Jo 19:19 , 20 (in Christian Inscr., epitaph , v. MM. xxiv).†
G5102.strongs: of Latin origin; a titulus or "title" (placard)

G1045.thayer: Gad, one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
G1045.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01410); Gad, a tribe of Israelites

G35.thayer: of unrecorded genealogy, whose descent cannot be traced.
G35.mounce: not included in a genealogy; independent of genealogy, Heb. 7:3*
G35.abbottSmith: without genealogy , i.e. without recorded pedigree (cf. Ne 7:64 ): He 7:3 (Cremer, 152; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G35.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1075 (γενεαλογέω); unregistered as to birth

G336.thayer: taking away (of life), killing, slaying, murder.
G336.mounce: a taking up or away; a putting to death, murder, Acts 8:1*

G1062.thayer: a marriage, wedding, wedding-ceremony; plur: a wedding-feast.
G1062.mounce: a wedding; nuptial festivities, a marriage festival, Mt. 22:2; 25:10; Jn. 2:1, 2; Rev. 19:7, 9; any feast or banquet, Lk. 12:36; 14:8; the marriage state, Heb. 13:4
G1062.abbottSmith: a wedding , esp. a wedding-feast : Mt 22:8 , 10 , 11 , 12 , Jo 2:1-3 , Re 19:7 , 9 ; pl. (Field, Notes , 16), Mt 22:2-4 , 9 25:10 , Lk 12:36 14:8 . marriage : He 13:4 (Cremer, 666).†

G1992.mounce: word sent; an order, command; an epistle, letter, Acts 9:2; 15:30

G318.thayer: necessity, constraint, compulsion; there is need to; force, violence.
G318.mounce: necessity, Mt. 18:7; constraint, compulsion, 2 Cor. 9:7; obligation of duty, moral or spiritual necessity, Rom. 13:5; distress, trial, affliction, Lk. 21:23; 1 Cor. 7:26; 2 Cor. 6:4; 12:10; 1 Thess. 3:7
G318.abbottSmith: necessity : ἔχειν ἀ- , c. inf., to be compelled , Lk 14:18 23:17 (Rec., R, mg.), I Co 7:37 , Ju 3 , He 7:27 ; ἐξ ἀ., κατ ' ἀ. , of necessity , II Co 9:7 , He 7:12 , Phm 14 ; ἀ. μοι ἐπίκειται , n. is laid on me , I Co 9:16 ; c. inf. (= ἀναγκαῖον ἐστι ), Mt 18:7 , Ro 13:5 , He 9:16 , 23
G318.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the base of G43 (ἀγκάλη); constraint (literally or figuratively); by implication, distress
G318.kjv: distress, must needs, (of) necessity(-sary), needeth, needful

G170.thayer: I am without a suitable opportunity.
G170.mounce: to be without opportunity or occasion, Phil. 4:10*
G170.abbottSmith: to have no opportunity (opp. to εὐκαιρέω ): Phl 4:10 .†
G170.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2540 (καιρός) (meaning unseasonable); to be inopportune (for oneself), i.e. to fail of a proper occasion

G1756.thayer: effective, productive of due result, at work.
G1756.mounce: active, Phlm. 6; efficient, energetic, Heb. 4:12; adapted to accomplish a thing, effectual, 1 Cor. 16:9*
G1756.abbottSmith: at work, active, effective : I Co 16:9 , Phm 6 , He 4:12 (Cremer, 261).†
G1756.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2041 (ἔργον); active, operative

G5386.mounce: pr. a lover of science, a systematic philosopher, Acts 17:18*
G5386.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G4680 (σοφός); fond of wise things, i.e. a "philosopher"

G5615.thayer: in like manner, likewise, just so.
G5615.mounce: just so, in just the same way or manner, likewise, Mt. 20:5; 21:30
G5615.abbottSmith: strengthened for ὡς (in Hom, at the beginning of a clause, in the form ὣς δ' αὔτως , later in one word), in like manner, just so, likewise: Mt 20:5 21:30 , 36 25:17 , Mk 14:31 , Lk 13:5 20:31 , Ro 8:26 , I Ti 5:25 , Tit 2:6 ; with verb to be supplied from context, Mk 12:21 , Lk 22:20 , I Co 11:25 , I Ti 2:9 3:8 3:11 , Tit 2:3 .†
G5615.strongs: from G5613 (ὡς) and an adverb from G846 (αὐτός); as thus, i.e. in the same way
G5615.kjv: even so, likewise, after the same (in like) manner

G3831.mounce: pr. an assembly of an entire people; a solemn gathering at a festival; a festive convocation, Heb. 12:22*
G3831.abbottSmith: prop., a national festal assembly in honour of a god; hence, generally, any festal assembly : He 12:23 (for exx. in π. , v. MM, xviii).†
G3831.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and a derivative of G58 (ἀγορά); a mass-meeting, i.e. (figuratively) universal companionship

G1644.mounce: lightness in weight; hence, lightness of mind, levity, 2 Cor. 1:17*

G4175.mounce: the administration of a commonwealth; in NT equivalent to πολιτεία, a community, commonwealth, Phil. 3:20*
G4175.strongs: from G4176 (πολιτεύομαι); a community, i.e. (abstractly) citizenship (figuratively)

G2666.thayer: I drink down, swallow, devour, destroy, consume.
G2666.mounce: to drink, swallow, gulp down, Mt. 23:24; to swallow up, absorb, Rev. 12:16; 2 Cor. 5:4; to engulf, submerge, overwhelm, Heb. 11:29; to swallow greedily, devour, 1 Pet. 5:8; to destroy, annihilate, 1 Cor. 15:54; 2 Cor. 2:7*
G2666.abbottSmith: to drink down , swallow : Mt 23:24 , Re 12:16 .
G2666.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4095 (πίνω); to drink down, i.e. gulp entire (literally or figuratively)

G5308.mounce: high, lofty, elevated, Mt. 4:8; 17:1; τὰ ὑψηλά, the highest heaven, Heb. 1:3; upraised, Acts 13:17; met. highly esteemed, Lk. 16:15; φρονεῖν τὰ ὑψηλά, to have lofty thoughts, be proud, arrogant, Rom. 12:16
G5308.abbottSmith: high, lofty : ὄρος , Mt 4:8 17:1 , Mk 9:2 , Re 21:10 ; τεῖχος , ib. 12 ; μετὰ βραχίονος. , fig., Ac 13:17 (cf. Ex 6:6 , al); pl., ὑψηλά , of heaven ( Ps 92 (93):4 , Is 33:5 , al.), He 1:3 ; compar., ὑψηλότερος τ. οὐρανῶν , He 7:26 . Metaph: Lk 16:15 ; ὑψηλὰ φρονεῖν , Ro 11:20 12:16 , I Ti 6:17 (WH, txt., ὑψηλοφρονείν ).†
G5308.strongs: from G5311 (ὕψος); lofty (in place or character)

G1231.mounce: pr. to distinguish; to resolve determinately; to examine, inquire into, judicially, Acts 23:15; 24:22*
G1231.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1097 (γινώσκω); to know thoroughly, i.e. ascertain exactly

G1854.mounce: can function as an improper prep., without, out of doors; Mt. 12:46, 47; ὁ, ἡ, τὸ ἔξω, outer, external, foreign, Acts 26:11; 2 Cor. 4:16; met. not belonging to one’s community, Mk. 4:11; 1 Cor. 5:12, 13; out, away, from a place or person, Mt. 5:13; 13:48; as a prep., out of, Mk. 5:10
G1854.strongs: adverb from G1537 (ἐκ); out(-side, of doors), literally or figuratively

G3974.thayer: Paphos, a city at the western end of Cyprus.
G3974.mounce: Paphos, the chief city in the island of Cyprus. See PhotoGuide.
G3974.abbottSmith: Paphos , a city in Cyprus: Ac 13:6 , 13 .†
G3974.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Paphus, a place in Cyprus

G3754.thayer: that, since, because; may introduce direct discourse.
G3754.mounce: originally was the neuter of ὅστις, that, Mt. 2:16, 22, 23; 6:5, 16; often used pleonastically in reciting another’s words, Mt. 9:18; Lk. 19:42; Acts 5:23; as a causal particle, for that, for, because, Mt. 2:18; 5:3, 4, 5; 13:13; because, seeing that, since, Lk. 23:40; Acts 1:17
G3754.abbottSmith: As conjc, introducing an objective clause, that ; after verbs of seeing, knowing, thinking, saying, feeling: Mt 3:9 6:32 11:25 , Mk 3:28 , Lk 2:49 , Jo 2:22 , Ac 4:13 , Ro 1:13 8:38 10:9 , Phl 4:15 , Ja 2:24 , al.; elliptically, Jo 6:46 , Phl 3:12 , al. After εἶναι ( γίνεσθαι ): defining a demonstr. or pers. pron., Jo 3:19 16:19 , Ro 9:6 , I Jo 3:16 al.; c. pron. interrog., Mt 8:27 , Mk 4:41 , Lk
G3754.strongs: neuter of G3748 (ὅστις) as conjunction; demonstrative, that (sometimes redundant); causative, because
G3754.kjv: as concerning that, as though, because (that), for (that), how (that), (in) that, though, why

G4012.mounce: pr. of place, (1) gen., about, around; about, concerning, respecting, Mt. 2:8; 11:10; 22:31; Jn. 8:18; Rom. 8:3, et al. freq.; (2) acc., of place, about, around, round about, Mt. 3:4; Mk. 3:34; Lk. 13:8; οἱ περί τινα, the companions of a person, Lk. 22:49; a person and his companions, Acts 13:13; simply a person, Jn. 11:19; τὰ περί τινα, the condition, circumstances of any one, Phil. 2:23; of time, about, Mt. 20:3, 5, 6, 9; about, concerning, respecting, touching, Lk. 10:40; 1 Tim. 1:19; 6:21; Tit. 2:7
G4012.abbottSmith: with radical sense round about (as distinct from ἀμφί , on both sides ). C. gen., of place, about (poët.). Causal, about , on account of , concerning , in reference to : Mt 2:8 , Mk 1:44 , Lk 4:38 , Jo 16:26 , Ac 28:21 , al. mult.; τὰ περί , c. gen., the things concerning one, one's state or case : Mk 5:27 , Ac 1:3 28:15 , Eph 6:22 , al.; at the beginning of a sentence, περί , regarding , as to ,
G4012.strongs: from the base of G4008 (πέραν); properly, through (all over), i.e. around; figuratively with respect to; used in various applications, of place, cause or time (with the genitive case denoting the subject or occasion or superlative point; with the accusative case the locality, circuit, matter, circumstance or general period)
G4012.kjv: (there-)about, above, against, at, on behalf of, X and his company, which concern, (as) concerning, for, X how it will go with, ((there-, where-)) of, on, over, pertaining (to), for sake, X (e-)state, (as) touching, (where-)by (in), with

G2963.thayer: (a) abstr: lordship, (b) concr: divine or angelic lordship, domination, dignity, usually with reference to a celestial hierarchy.
G2963.mounce: lordship; constituted authority, Eph. 1:21; 2 Pet. 2:10; Jude 8; pl. authorities, potentates, Col. 1:16. The Ephesian and Colossian passage could also be speaking about angelic powers.*
G2963.abbottSmith: lordship , dominion : Eph 1:21 , II Pe 2:10 , Ju 8 ; pl., Col 1:16 (cf. Lft., Col 1:16 ; Mayor, Ju 8 ; DB , i, 616 f.).†
G2963.strongs: from G2962 (κύριος); mastery, i.e. (concretely and collectively) rulers

G4097.thayer: I sell; pass: I am a slave to, am devoted to.
G4097.mounce: to sell, Mt. 13:46; 18:25; met. with ὑπό, pass. to be sold under, to be a slave to, be devoted to, Rom. 7:14
G4097.abbottSmith: to sell : c. acc. rei, Mt 13:46 (on this pf., v. Bl., § 59, 5), Ac 2:45 4:34 5:4 ; c. gen. (of price), Mt 26:9 , Mk 14:5 , Jo 12:5 ; c. acc. pers. (of slavery), Mt 18:25 ; hence metaph., Ro 7:14 (cf. IV Ki 17:17 , I Mac 1:15 , al.).†
G4097.strongs: contracted from (to traverse; from the base of G4008 (πέραν)); to traffic (by travelling), i.e. dispose of as merchandise or into slavery (literally or figuratively)

G4874.thayer: I mingle together with, keep company with.
G4874.abbottSmith: to mix up together ; pass., reflex, and metaph., to associate with (Plut., Ath., al.): I Co 5:9 , 11 , II Th 3:14 (v. M, Th. , 117).†
G4874.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G303 (ἀνά) and G3396 (μίγνυμι); to mix up together, i.e. (figurative) associate with

G3569.strongs: from neuter plural of G3588 (ὁ) and G3568 (νῦν); the things now, i.e. (adverbially) at present

G2456.thayer: Julia, a Roman Christian, probably a slave or freed from the Imperial household.
G2456.strongs: feminine of the same as G2457 (Ἰούλιος); Julia, a Christian woman

G1901.mounce: pr. to stretch out farther; in NT mid. to reach out towards, strain for, Phil. 3:13*
G1901.abbottSmith: to extend : mid., to stretch forward : c. dat., Phl 3:13 .†
G1901.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1614 (ἐκτείνω); to stretch (oneself) forward upon

G4298.thayer: (originally of the pioneer cutting his way through brushwood), I advance, progress, make progress.
G4298.mounce: pr. to cut a passage forward; to advance, make progress; to advance, as time, to be far spent, Rom. 13:12; met. to advance in wisdom, age, or stature, Lk. 2:52; seq. ἐν, to make progress or proficiency in, Gal. 1:14; προκόπτω ἐπὶ πλεῖον, to proceed or advance further, 2 Tim. 2:16; 3:9; προκόπτω ἐπὶ τὸ χεῖρον, to grow worse and worse, 2 Tim. 3:13*
G4298.abbottSmith: to cut forward a way, forward , advance , in cl. trans. with neut. adj., as οὐδὲν π., τὰ πολλὰ π. In late writers (Polyb., al.), wholly intrans., to advance , progress : of time, Ro 13:12 . Metaph., Lk 2:52 , Ga 1:14 , II Ti 2:16 3:9 , 13 .†
G4298.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2875 (κόπτω); to drive forward (as if by beating), i.e. (figuratively and intransitively) to advance (in amount, to grow; in time, to be well along)
G4298.kjv: increase, proceed, profit, be far spent, wax

G3197.thayer: Melchi, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3197.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04428 with pronominal suffix, my king); Melchi (i.e. Malki), the name of two Israelites

G2770.thayer: I gain, acquire, win (over), avoid loss.
G2770.mounce: to gain as a matter of profit, Mt. 25:17; to win, acquire possession of, Mt. 16:26; to profit in the avoidance of, to avoid, Acts 27:21; in NT Χριστόν, to win Christ, to become possessed of the privileges of the Gospel, Phil. 3:8; to win over from estrangement, Mt. 18:15; to win over to embrace the Gospel, 1 Cor. 9:19, 20, 21, 22; 1 Pet. 3:1; absol. to make gain, Jas. 4:13
G2770.abbottSmith: to gain : c. acc., Mt 25:16 , 17 , 20 , 22 ; τ. κόσμον , Mt 16:26 , Mk 8:36 , Lk 9:25 ; absol., to make profit , get gain : Ja 4:13 . Metaph., c. acc. rei, to save oneself from , avoid : Ac 27:21 (Field, Notes , 145); c. acc. pers., to gain , win : Mt 18:15 , I Co 9:19-22 , Phl 3:8 ; pass., I Pe 3:1 .†
G2770.strongs: from G2771 (κέρδος); to gain (literally or figuratively)

G1510.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G1510.strongs: the first person singular present indicative; a prolonged form of a primary and defective verb; I exist (used only when emphatic)
G1510.kjv: am, have been, X it is I, was

G2508.mounce: to cleanse from filth; to clear by pruning, prune, Jn. 15:2; met. to cleanse from sin, make expiation
G2508.abbottSmith: to cleanse : of pruning, Jo 15:2 (cf. καθαρίζω ).†
G2508.strongs: from G2513 (καθαρός); to cleanse, i.e. (specially) to prune; figuratively, to expiate

G3700.thayer: I appear, am seen (by), let myself be seen (by).
G3700.abbottSmith: late present as from ὤφθην (= ὁράω ); mid., ὀπτάνομαι , to allow oneself to be seen , to appear . c. dat., Ac 1:3 . (For exx. from π. , v. Deiss., LAE , 79, 252 5 ; MM, ii, xviii.).†
G3700.strongs: a (middle voice) prolonged form of the primary (middle voice) ; which is used for it in certain tenses; and both as alternate of G3708 (ὁράω); to gaze (i.e. with wide-open eyes, as at something remarkable; and thus differing from G991 (βλέπω), which denotes simply voluntary observation; and from G1492 (εἴδω), which expresses merely mechanical, passive or casual vision; while G2300 (θεάομαι), and still more emphatically its intensive G2334 (θεωρέω), signifies an earnest but more continued inspection; and G4648 (σκοπέω) a watching from a distance)

G3576.thayer: blunt, dull, hence spiritually; sluggish, remiss, slack.
G3576.abbottSmith: sluggish , slothful : He 5:11 6:12 (for similar usage in π. , v. MM, xvii).
G3576.strongs: from a derivative of G3541 (νόθος); sluggish, i.e. (literally) lazy, or (figuratively) stupid

G1111.thayer: I whisper, murmur, grumble (generally of smoldering discontent).
G1111.mounce: to speak privately and in a low voice, mutter, Jn. 7:32; to utter secret and sullen discontent, express indignant complaint, murmur, grumble, Mt. 20:11; Lk. 5:30; Jn. 6:41, 43, 61; 1 Cor. 10:10*
G1111.abbottSmith: to mutter, murmur : I Co 10:10 ; seq. κατά , Mt 20:11 ; πρός , Lk 5:30 ; περί , Jo 6:41 , 61 ; μετ’ ἀλλήλων , Jo 6:43 ; c. acc., seq. περί , Jo 7:32 (cf. δια-γογγύζω ).†

G4606.mounce: Sidonian; an inhabitant of Σιδών, Sidon, Acts 12:20; Lk. 4:26*
G4606.strongs: from G4605 (Σιδών); a Sidonian, i.e. inhabitant of Sidon

G2146.thayer: I look well, make a fair show (a good outward appearance, and so win good opinion).
G2146.mounce: to carry or make a fair appearance, Gal. 6:12*
G2146.abbottSmith: to look well, make a fair show : metaph. (as in π. ; v. Deiss., LAE , 96), Ga 6:12 .†
G2146.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4383 (πρόσωπον); to be of good countenance, i.e. (figuratively) to make a display

G4088.thayer: bitterness, harshness, hence met: an embittered (resentful) spirit.
G4088.mounce: bitterness, Acts 8:23; Heb. 12:15; met. bitterness of spirit and language, harshness, Rom. 3:14; Eph. 4:31*
G4088.abbottSmith: bitterness ; of taste (Arist., Je 15:17 , al.); metaph., of temper, character, etc.: Ro 3:14 (LXX) , Eph 4:31 ; ῥίζα πικρίας , He 12:15 ; χολὴ π. , a malignant disposition , Ac 8:23 .†
G4088.strongs: from G4089 (πικρός); acridity (especially poison), literally or figuratively

G4079.strongs: neuter of a (presumed) derivative of (the blade of an oar; from the same as G3976 (πέδη)); a "pedal", i.e. helm

G5530.thayer: I use, make use of, deal with, take advantage of.
G5530.mounce: to use, make use of, employ, Acts 27:17; 1 Cor. 7:31; to take advantage of, 1 Cor. 7:21; 9:12, 15; to use, to treat, behave towards, Acts 27:3; 2 Cor. 13:10
G5530.abbottSmith: to use, make use of : c. dat. (cf. M, Pr. , 64, 158), Ac 27:17 , I Co 9:12 , 15 , I Ti 1:8 5:23 ; μᾶλλον χρῆσαι (i.e. the opportunity; v. M, Pr. , 247), I Co 7:21 ; as some­times in late writers (cf. M, Pr. , 64; Lft., Notes , 233), c. acc., I Co 7:31 ; of feelings, etc., to exercise, shew : II Co 1:17 3:12 ; c. adv., ἀποτόμως , to deal sharply, II Co 13:10 ; c. dat. pers. (cl.), to treat, deal wi
G5530.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb (perhaps rather from G5495 (χείρ), to handle); to furnish what is needed; (give an oracle, "graze" (touch slightly), light upon, etc.), i.e. (by implication) to employ or (by extension) to act towards one in a given manner

G4295.thayer: I am set (placed, put) before, am already there.
G4295.mounce: to lie or be placed before; met. to be proposed or set before, as a duty, example, reward, etc., Heb. 6:18; 12:1, 2; Jude 7; to be at hand, be present, 2 Cor. 8:12*
G4295.abbottSmith: to be set before one, to be set forth : He 6:18 12:1 , 2 , Ju 7 .
G4295.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to lie before the view, i.e. (figuratively) to be present (to the mind), to stand forth (as an example or reward)

G5003.thayer: I endure severe hardship, am harassed, complain.
G5003.mounce: to endure severe labor and hardship; to be harassed; complain, Jas. 4:9*
G5003.abbottSmith: to do hard labour, suffer hardship or distress : Ja 4:9 .
G5003.strongs: from G5005 (ταλαίπωρος); to be wretched, i.e. realize one's own misery

G3375.mounce: a month, Lk. 1:24, 26, 36, 56; in NT the new moon, the day of the new moon, Gal. 4:10
G3375.abbottSmith: verily , truly ; εἰ ( εἶ ) μ. (= cl., ἦ μ. in LXX and π. ), now verily , full surely : He 6:14 (LXX) .†
G3375.strongs: a stronger form of G3303 (μέν); a particle of affirmation (only with G2229 (ἦ)); assuredly

G5091.thayer: (a) I value at a price, estimate, (b) I honor, reverence.
G5091.mounce: to estimate in respect of worth; to hold in estimation, respect, honor, reverence, Mt. 15:4, 5, 8; 19:19; Mk. 7:10; to honor with reverent service, Jn. 5:23 (4x); 8:49; to treat with honor, manifest consideration towards, Acts 28:10; to treat graciously, visit with marks of favor, Jn. 12:26; mid. to price, Mt. 27:9
G5091.abbottSmith: to fix the value, price : c. acc pers., of Christ, Mt 27:9 (LXX) .
G5091.strongs: from G5093 (τίμιος); to prize, i.e. fix a valuation upon; by implication, to revere

G2619.mounce: to veil; mid. to veil one’s self, be veiled or covered, 1 Cor. 11:6, 7. In the pres act ind., 2nd sg, the personal ending σαι does not simplify as normal, κατακαυχᾶσαι*
G2619.abbottSmith: to cover up ; mid., to cover or veil oneself : I Co 11:6 ; τ. κεφαλήν , I Co 11:7 .†
G2619.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to cover wholly, i.e. veil

G459.mounce: lawless, without law, not subject to law, 1 Cor. 9:21; lawless, violating law, wicked, impious, Acts 2:23; a transgressor, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 22:37
G459.abbottSmith: lawless , wicked : Mk 15:28 , Lk 22:37 , Ac 2:23 , I Ti 1:9 , II Pe 2:8 ; ὁ ἄ. , II Th 2:8 (= ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας , II Th 2:3 ).
G459.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3551 (νόμος); lawless, i.e. (negatively) not subject to (the Jewish) law; (by implication, a Gentile), or (positively) wicked
G459.kjv: without law, lawless, transgressor, unlawful, wicked

G2411.thayer: a temple, either the whole building, or specifically the outer courts, open to worshippers.
G2411.strongs: neuter of G2413 (ἱερός); a sacred place, i.e. the entire precincts (whereas G3485 (ναός) denotes the central sanctuary itself) of the Temple (at Jerusalem or elsewhere)

G4396.thayer: a prophet, poet; a person gifted at expositing divine truth.
G4396.mounce: pr. a spokesman for another; spc. a spokesman or interpreter for a deity; a prophet, seer, Tit. 1:12; in NT a prophet, a divinely commissioned and inspired person, Mt. 14:5; Lk. 7:16, 39; Jn. 9:17; a prophet in the Christian church, a person gifted for the exposition of divine truth, 1 Cor. 12:28, 29; a prophet, a foreteller of the future, Mt. 1:22, et al. freq.; οἱ προφῆται, the prophetic scriptures of the Old Testament, Lk. 16:29
G4396.abbottSmith: one who acts as an interpreter or forth-teller of the Divine will (v. Lft., Notes , 83 f.; Tr., Syn. , § vi), a prophet ; in cl. (Æsch., Hdt., Plat., al.), of the interpreters of oracles. In NT, of the OT prophets: Mt 5:12 , Mk 6:15 , Lk 4:27 , Jo 8:52 , Ro 11:3 , al.; of prophets in general: Mt 10:41 13:57 21:46 , Mk 6:4 , Lk 13:33 , al.; of John the Baptist: Mt 21:26 , Mk 6:15 , Lk 1:76 ; of Chr
G4396.strongs: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί); a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet

G4811.mounce: to inform against; to accuse falsely; by impl. to wrong by false accusations; to extort money by false informations, Lk. 3:14; 19:8*
G4811.abbottSmith: to act the συκοφάντης (on conjectures as to the origin of the term, v. LS, s.v.), to accuse falsely (Aristoph., Xen., al.): Lk 3:14 (R, mg.) 19:8 (RV, exact wrongfully ; but cf. Hatch, Essays , 89 ff., v. also Field, Notes , 56 f.; MM, xxiv).†
G4811.strongs: from a compound of G4810 (σῦκον) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω); to be a fig-informer (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant", i.e. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort)
G4811.kjv: accuse falsely, take by false accusation

G4224.mounce: a drinking; a drinking together, drinking bout, 1 Pet. 4:3*
G4224.abbottSmith: a drinking bout , carousal : I Pe 4:3 .†
G4224.strongs: from the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); a drinking-bout or carousal

G5566.thayer: the north-west wind, and the quarter of the sky from which it comes.
G5566.mounce: Corus, or Caurus, the northwest wind; meton, the northwest quarter of the heavens, Acts 27:12*
G5566.strongs: of Latin origin; the north-west wind

G5168.mounce: a hole, perforation; eye of a needle, Mk. 10:25*
G5168.abbottSmith: = τρύμη , a hole : τ. ῥαφίδος , eye of a needle , Mk 10:25 .†
G5168.strongs: from a derivative of (to wear away; akin to the base of G5134 (τραῦμα), G5147 (τρίβος) and G5176 (τρώγω)); an orifice, i.e. needle's eye

G591.thayer: (a) I give back, return, restore, (b) I give, render, as due, (c) mid: I sell.
G591.mounce: to give in answer to a claim or expectation; to render a due, Mt. 12:36; 16:27; 21:41; 22:21; to recompense, Mt. 6:4, 6, 18; to discharge an obligation, Mt. 5:33; to pay a debt, Mt. 5:26; to render back, requite, Rom. 12:17; to give back, restore, Lk. 4:20; 9:42; to refund, Lk. 10:35; 19:8; mid., to sell, Acts 5:8; 7:9; Heb. 12:16; pass., to be sold, Mt. 18:25; to be given up at a request, Mt. 27:58
G591.abbottSmith: to give up or back , restore , return : Mt 27:58 , Lk 4:20 9:42 19:8 ; esp. of wages, debts, oaths, etc. (MM, s.v.), to render what is due, to pay (Deiss., LAE , 334 f.): absol., Mt 18:25 , 28 , Lk 7:42 . c. acc., Mt 5:26 18:29 , 30 , 34 20:8 21:41 22:21 , Mk 12:17 , Lk 10:35 12:59 20:25 , Ro 13:7 , He 12:11 , Re 22:2 ; ὅρκους , Mt 5:33 (cf. Nu 30:3 , De 23:21 al.); of conjugal duty, τ. ὀφειλήν ,
G591.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give away, i.e. up, over, back, etc. (in various applications)
G591.kjv: deliver (again), give (again), (re-)pay(-ment be made), perform, recompense, render, requite, restore, reward, sell, yield

G4595.thayer: I cause to rot, make corrupt.
G4595.mounce: to cause to putrify, rot, be corrupted or rotten, Jas. 5:2*
G4595.abbottSmith: to make corrupt : 2 pf. act, with mid, sense (v. M, Pr. , 154), σέσηπεν , has perished, become corrupted , Ja 5:2 .†
G4595.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to putrefy, i.e. (figuratively) perish

G535.strongs: from a derivative of G534 (ἀπάρτι); completion

G1614.thayer: I stretch out (forth), cast forth (as of an anchor), lay hands on.
G1614.mounce: to stretch out, Mt. 8:3; 12:13; to lay hands on any one, Lk. 22:53; to exert power and energy, Acts 4:30; to cast out, let down an anchor, Acts 27:30
G1614.abbottSmith: to stretch out or forth : τ. χεῖρα (as often in LXX), Mt 8:3 12:13 14:31 26:51 , Mk 1:41 3:5 , Lk 5:13 6:10 Jo 21:18 , Ac 26:1 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc pers., towards , Mt 12:49 ; against , Lk 22:53 ; εἰς ἴασιν , Ac 4:30 ; of anchors, to cast , Ac 27:30 .†
G1614.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and teino (to stretch); to extend

G4882.mounce: to send forth together with someone, 2 Cor. 12:18*
G4882.abbottSmith: to send along with : c. acc., II Co 12:18 (cf. MM, xxiv).†
G4882.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G649 (ἀποστέλλω); to despatch (on an errand) in company with

G3180.thayer: (a way of search after something, an inquiry; a method), scheming, craftiness, deceit.
G3180.abbottSmith: craft , deceit : Eph 4:14 6:11 (not found elsewhere; v. AR, in l.).†
G3180.strongs: from a compound of G3326 (μετά) and G3593 (ὁδεύω) (compare "method"); travelling over, i.e. travesty (trickery)

G5148.mounce: the space of three years, Acts 20:31*
G5148.abbottSmith: a period of three years : Ac 20:31 .†
G5148.strongs: from a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G2094 (ἔτος); a three years' period (triennium)

G3726.thayer: I adjure by, charge solemnly by.
G3726.mounce: to put to an oath; to obtest, adjure, conjure, Mk. 5:7; Acts 19:13*
G3726.abbottSmith: to make one swear (Xen., Polyb., a1.).
G3726.strongs: from G3727 (ὅρκος); to put on oath, i.e. make swear; by analogy, to solemnly enjoin

G1898.thayer: bringing in (besides or in addition), introduction, importation.
G1898.mounce: a superinduction, a further introduction, whether by way of addition or substitution, Heb. 7:19*
G1898.abbottSmith: a bringing in besides or in addition (Hipp., FlJ, al.): He 7:19 (cf. MM, Exp. , xiv).†
G1898.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G1521 (εἰσάγω); a superintroduction

G2249.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G2249.strongs: nominative plural of G1473 (ἐγώ); we (only used when emphatic)

G2197.thayer: Zechariah, (a) a priest referred to as a son of Jehoiada, (b) another priest, father of John the Baptist.
G2197.mounce: Zacharias, pr. name. (1) Son of Barachias, Mt. 23:35; Lk. 11:51. (2) Father of Jn. the Baptist, Lk. 1:5
G2197.abbottSmith: Zacharias , father of John the Baptist: Lk 1:5 , 12 , 13 , 18 , 21 , 40 , 59 , 67 3:2 .
G2197.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02148); Zacharias (i.e. Zechariah), the name of two Israelites

G5116.mounce: a topaz, a gem of a yellowish color, different from the modern topaz, Rev. 21:20*
G5116.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative (alternately) of (a "topaz"; of uncertain origin); a gem, probably the chrysolite

G1445.thayer: a Hebrew, particularly one who speaks Hebrew (Aramaic).
G1445.mounce: a Hebrew, one descended from Abraham the Hebrew, 2 Cor. 11:22; Phil. 3:5; in NT, a Jew of Palestine, one speaking Aramaic, opp. to Ἑλληνιστής, Acts 6:1*
G1445.abbottSmith: a Hebrew . In OT, of Israelites in contrast with those of another race ( Ge 14:13 , Ex 1:15 , De 15:12 , al.). In NT as the correlative of Ἑλληνιστής , a Jew who had adopted, in greater or less degree, Greek culture and Greek language. The distinction was not merely linguistic ( DB , ii, 325); as far as it was so, Συριστής would be a more correct Greek term for the Jew of Semitic speech (v.s. Ἑβρα
G1445.strongs: from G1443 (Ἐβέρ); a Hebræan (i.e. Hebrew) or Jew

G2661.mounce: to consider worthy of, Lk. 20:35; Acts 5:41; 2 Thess. 1:5*
G2661.abbottSmith: to deem worthy : c. acc. pers. et gen. rei, II Th 1:5 ; pass. seq. inf., Lk 20:35 , Ac 5:41 .†
G2661.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G515 (ἀξιόω); to deem entirely deserving

G4029.thayer: I lie about, surround; I am encompassed, surrounded, or clothed with, am in submission to.
G4029.mounce: to lie around, be surround, Heb. 12:1; to be hung around, Mk. 9:42; Lk. 17:2; to have around one’s self, to wear, Acts 28:20; to be in submission to, Heb. 5:2*
G4029.abbottSmith: to lie round about : μύλος , λίθος (RV, were hanged about ), Mk 9:42 , Lk 17:2 ; νεφος μαρτύρων , He 12:1 .
G4029.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to lie all around, i.e. inclose, encircle, hamper (literally or figuratively)

G4644.strongs: from G4642 (σκληρός) and G5137 (τράχηλος); hardnaped, i.e. (figuratively) obstinate

G2376.mounce: a small opening; a window, Acts 20:9; 2 Cor. 11:33*
G2376.strongs: from G2374 (θύρα); an aperture, i.e. window

G2866.mounce: in NT in better health, Jn. 4:52*
G2866.abbottSmith: well-dressed , elegant , fine ; compar. neut., κομψότερον ἔχειν (cf. κόμψως ἔχειν , Epict., and colloq. Eng., " to be doing finely "): Jo 4:52 .†
G2866.strongs: neuter comparative of a derivative of the base of 2865 (meaning, properly, well dressed, i.e. nice); figuratively, convalescent

G28.thayer: Hagar, the servant of Sarah, concubine of Abraham.
G28.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01904); Hagar, the concubine of Abraham

G1697.thayer: Hamor, a man whose sons sold a field at Shechem to Jacob.
G1697.mounce: also spelled Ἑμμόρ, Hamor, pr. name, indecl., Acts 7:16
G1697.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02544); Emmor (i.e. Chamor), a Canaanite

G165.thayer: an age, a cycle (of time), especially of the present age as contrasted with the future age, and of one of a series of ages stretching to infinity.
G165.mounce: pr. a period of time of significant character; life; an era; an age: hence, a state of things marking an age or era; the present order of nature; the natural condition of man, the world; ὁ αἰών, illimitable duration, eternity; as also, οἱ αἰῶνες, ὁ αἰῶν τῶν αἰώνων, οἱ αἰῶνες τῶν αἰώνων; by an Aramaism οἱ αἰῶνες, the material universe, Heb. 1:2
G165.abbottSmith: in cl., like Lat. aevum (LS, MM, VGT , s.v.), a space of time , as, a lifetime, generation, period of history, an indefinitely long period; in NT of an indefinitely long period, an age, eternity , usually c. prep. (MM, VGT ); of the past: ἀπ’ αἰ. (cf. Heb. מֵעוֹלָם ), Lk 1:70 ; of the future: εἰς τ. αἰ. (cf. לְעוֹלָם ), forever , Mt 21:19 ; id., c. neg., never , Jo 4:14 ; more strongly, εἰς τὸν αἰ
G165.strongs: from the same as G104 (ἀεί); properly, an age; by extension, perpetuity (also past); by implication, the world; specially (Jewish) a Messianic period (present or future)
G165.kjv: age, course, eternal, (for) ever(-more), (n-)ever, (beginning of the , while the) world (began, without end)

G2864.mounce: the hair; a head of long hair, 1 Cor. 11:15*
G2864.strongs: apparently from the same as G2865 (κομίζω); the hair of the head (locks, as ornamental, and thus differing from G2359 (θρίξ); which properly denotes merely the scalp)

G671.mounce: a using up, or, a discharge of an intended use, Col. 2:22*
G671.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G5530 (χράομαι); the act of using up, i.e. consumption

G5060.thayer: Tertius, who wrote the Epistle to the Romans at Paul's dictation.
G5060.mounce: Tertius, pr. name, a helper of Paul, Rom. 16:22*
G5060.strongs: of Latin origin; third; Tertius, a Christian

G273.thayer: blameless, free from fault or defect.
G273.mounce: blameless, irreprehensible, without defect, Lk. 1:6; Phil. 2:15; 3:6; 1 Thess. 3:13; Heb. 8:7*
G273.abbottSmith: blameless , free from fault (in π. of a marriage-contract; M, Th. , I, 3:13 ; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 1:6 , Phl 2:15 3:6 , I Th 3:13 (WH, mg., -ως ) He 8:7 .†
G273.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3201 (μέμφομαι); irreproachable

G962.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01004 and H05679); ferry-house; Bethabara (i.e. Bethabarah), a place on the Jordan

G1977.mounce: to throw upon, cast upon, Lk. 19:35; 1 Pet. 5:7*
G1977.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4496 (ῥίπτω); to throw upon (literally or figuratively)

G4590.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08035); Sem (i.e. Shem), a patriarch

G3037.thayer: a stone; met: of Jesus as the chief stone in a building.
G3037.mounce: a stone, Mt. 3:9; 4:3, 6; used figuratively, of Christ, Eph. 2:20; 1 Pet. 2:6; of believers, 1 Pet. 2:5; meton. a tablet of stone, 2 Cor. 3:7; a precious stone, Rev. 4:3
G3037.abbottSmith: a stone : Mt 4:6 , al.; pl., Mt 3:9 , al.; at the entrance of a tomb, Mt 27:60 , 66 28:2 , Mk 15:46 16:3 , 4 , Lk 24:2 , Jo 11:38 , 39 , 41 20:1 ; λ. μυλικός , Lk 17:2 , cf. Re 18:21 ; of building stones, Mt 21:42 , [ 44 ] 24:2 , Mk 12:10 13:1 , 2 , Lk 19:44 20:17 , 18 21:5 , 6 Ac 4:11 , I Pe 2:7 ; metaph., of Christ, λ. ἀκρογωναῖος , ἐκλεκτός , ἔντιμος , I Pe 2:6 (LXX) ; λ. ζῶν , I Pe 2:4 ; προσκ
G3037.strongs: apparently a primary word; a stone (literally or figuratively)

G2650.thayer: I wait, stay (with), remain, abide, dwell.
G2650.mounce: to remain; to abide, dwell, Acts 1:13*
G2650.abbottSmith: to remain permanently, abide : Ac 1:13 , I Co 16:6 , WH ( παραμ- , T, RV).†
G2650.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3306 (μένω); to stay fully, i.e. reside

G2140.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4160 (ποιέω); well-doing, i.e. beneficence

G1577.thayer: an assembly, congregation, church; the Church, the whole body of Christian believers.
G1577.mounce: a popular assembly, Acts 19:32, 39, 41; in NT the congregation of the children of Israel, Acts 7:38; transferred to the Christian body, of which the congregation of Israel was a figure, the Church, 1 Cor. 12:28; Col. 1:18; a local portion of the Church, a local church, Rom. 16:1; a Christian congregation, 1 Cor. 14:4
G1577.abbottSmith: prop., an assembly of citizens regularly convened (in Thuc., ii, 22, opp. to σύλλογος , a concourse ): Ac 19:32 , 39 , 41 . In LXX of the assembly, congregation, community of Israel (De 4:10 23:2, al.): Ac 7:38 , He 2:12 (LXX) . NT, esp. of an assembly or company of Christians, a (the) church ; of gatherings for worship: I Co 11:18 14:19 , 34 , 35 ; of local communities: Ac 8:3 , I Co 4:17 ; with
G1577.strongs: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω); a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)

G2399.thayer: (unofficial, hence) an amateur, an unprofessional man, a layman; an ungifted person.
G2399.mounce: pr. one in private life, one devoid of special learning or gifts, a plain person, Acts 4:13; 2 Cor. 11:6; ungifted, 1 Cor. 14:16, 23, 24*
G2399.abbottSmith: a private person , as opp. to the State or an official ( βασιλεῖς κ. ἰδιῶται , Pr 6:8 ; and cf. MM, Exp. , xv).
G2399.strongs: from G2398 (ἴδιος); a private person, i.e. (by implication) an ignoramus (compare "idiot")

G5306.mounce: posterior in place or time; subsequent, later, last, finally, Mt. 21:31; 1 Tim. 4:1
G5306.abbottSmith: latter, later : ἐν ὑ. καιροῖς , I Ti 4:1 (on the reading ὁ ὕ. , WH, for ὁ πρῶτος , v. WH, App. , in l.). Neut., τὸ ὕ. , used adverbially instead of ὑστέρως , afterwards, later : Mt 4:2 21:29 , 32 , 37 25:11 26:60 , Mk 16:14 , Lk 20:32 , Jo 13:36 , He 12:11 ; c. gen., Mt 22:27 .†
G5306.strongs: comparative from G5259 (ὑπό) (in the sense of behind); later

G1295.thayer: I save (rescue) through (some danger), bring safely to, escaped to.
G1295.mounce: to bring safely through; to convey in safety, Acts 23:24; pass. to reach a place or state of safety, Acts 27:43, 44; 28:1, 4; 1 Pet. 3:20; to heal, to restore to health, Mt. 14:36; Lk. 7:3*
G1295.abbottSmith: to bring safely through a danger: Lk 7:3 , Ac 27:43 ; seq. πρός , Ac 23:24 . Pass., to come safe through : Ac 28:1 ; ἐπὶ τ. γῆν , Ac 27:44 ; ἐκ τ. θαλάσσης , Ac 28:4 ; δι’ ὕδατος , I Pe 3:20 ; of sickness, to recover : Mt 14:36 .†
G1295.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4982 (σώζω); to save thoroughly, i.e. (by implication or analogy) to cure, preserve, rescue, etc.
G1295.kjv: bring safe, escape (safe), heal, make perfectly whole, save

G2970.mounce: pr. a festive procession, a merry-making; in NT a revel, lewd, immoral feasting, Rom. 13:13; Gal. 5:21; 1 Pet. 4:3*
G2970.abbottSmith: a revel , carousal : Ro 13:13 , Ga 5:21 , I Pe 4:3 .†
G2970.strongs: from G2749 (κεῖμαι); a carousal (as if letting loose)

G2863.thayer: I wear the hair long, allow the hair to grow out.
G2863.mounce: to have long hair, wear the hair long, 1 Cor. 11:14, 15*
G2863.abbottSmith: to wear long hair : I Co 11:14 , 15 .†
G2863.strongs: from G2864 (κόμη); to wear tresses of hair

G4929.thayer: I arrange with, direct, appoint, prescribe, ordain.
G4929.mounce: pr. to arrange or place in order together; in NT to order, charge, direct, Mt. 21:6, 26:19; 27:10*
G4929.abbottSmith: to prescribe, ordain, arrange : c. dat. pers, Mt 21:6 26:19 27:10 (LXX) .†
G4929.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to direct

G5078.mounce: art, skill, Acts 17:29; an art, trade, craft, Acts 18:3; Rev. 18:22*
G5078.abbottSmith: art, craft, trade : Ac 17:29 18:3 , Re 18:22 (WH, R, mg., om.).†
G5078.strongs: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); art (as productive), i.e. (specially), a trade, or (generally) skill

G1340.mounce: to feel or express reliance; to affirm confidently, insist, Lk. 22:59; Acts 12:15*
G1340.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G2478 (ἰσχυρός); to stout it through, i.e. asservate

G5485.thayer: (a) grace, as a gift or blessing brought to man by Jesus Christ, (b) favor, (c) gratitude, thanks, (d) a favor, kindness.
G5485.mounce: pleasing show, charm; beauty, gracefulness; a pleasing circumstance, matter of approval, 1 Pet. 2:19, 20; kindly bearing, graciousness, Lk. 4:22; a beneficial opportunity, benefit, 2 Cor. 1:15; Eph. 4:29; a charitable act, generous gift, 1 Cor. 16:3; 2 Cor. 8:4, 6; an act of favor, Acts 25:3; favor, acceptance, Lk. 1:30, 52; Acts 2:47; 7:10, 46; free favor, free gift, grace, Jn. 1:14, 16, 17; Rom. 4:4, 16; 11:5, 6; Eph. 2:5, 8; 1 Pet. 3:7; free favor specially manifested by God towards man in the Gospel scheme, grace, Acts 15:11; Rom. 3:24; 5:15, 17, 20, 21; 6:1; 2 Cor. 4:15; a gracious provision, gracious scheme, grace, Rom. 6:14, 15; Heb. 2:9; 12:28; 13:9; gracious dealing from God, grace, Acts 14:26; 15:40; Rom. 1:7; 1 Cor. 1:4; 15:10; Gal. 1:15; a commission graciously devolved by God upon a human agent, Rom. 1:5; 12:3; 15:15; 1 Cor. 3:10; 2 Cor. 1:12; Gal. 2:9; Eph. 3:8; grace, graciously bestowed divine endowment or influence, Lk. 2:40; Acts 4:33; 11:23; Rom. 12:6; 2 Cor. 12:9; grace, Acts 11:43; Rom. 5:2; Gal. 5:4; 2 Pet. 3:18; an emotion correspondent to what is pleasing or kindly; sense of obligation, Lk. 17:9; a grateful frame of mind, 1 Cor. 10:30; thanks, Lk. 6:32, 33, 34; Rom. 6:17; 1 Cor. 15:57; χάριν or χάριτας καταθέσθαι, to oblige, gratify, Acts 24:27; 25:9
G5485.abbottSmith: objectively, that which causes favourable regard, gracefulness, grace, loveliness of form, graciousness of speech (cl.; Ec 10:12 , Si 21:16 , al.): Col 4:6 ; λόγοι τ. χάριτος (gen. qual.), Lk 4:22 . Subjectively, on the part of the giver, grace, graciousness, kindness, goodwill, favour : Lk 2:52 , Ac 7:10 , al.; esp. in NT of the divine favour, grace , with emphasis on its freeness and universalit
G5485.strongs: from G5463 (χαίρω); graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
G5485.kjv: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

G4728.abbottSmith: narrow : fig (v MM, xxiii), Mt 7:13, 14 , Lk 13:24 .†
G4728.strongs: probably from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); narrow (from obstacles standing close about)

G2947.mounce: to roll; mid. to roll one’s self, to wallow, Mk. 9:20
G2947.abbottSmith: to roll , roll along . Pass., to be rolled ; of persons (in Hom, as sign of grief), to roll or wallow : Mk 9:20 .†
G2947.strongs: from the base of G2949 (κῦμα) (through the idea of circularity; compare G2945 (κύκλῳ), G1507 (εἱλίσσω)); to roll about

G7.thayer: Abijah, (a) a king, son of Rehoboam, (b) founder of the eighth class of priests.
G7.mounce: Abiajah, pr. name, indecl. Hebrew is Abijah. (1) the son of Rehoboam (1 Chr. 3:10) Mt. 1:7; (2) the division of priests to which Zechariah belonged (1 Chr. 24:10) Lk. 1:5*
G7.abbottSmith: Abia, Abijah . Son of Rehoboam ( III Ki 14:1 ): Mt 1:7 . A priest of the line of Eleazar ( I Ch 24:3 , 10 ): Lk 1:5 .†
G7.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H029); Abijah, the name of two Israelites

G2980.thayer: (I talk, chatter in classical Greek, but in NT a more dignified word) I speak, say.
G2980.mounce: to make vocal utterance; to babble, to talk; in NT absol. to exercise the faculty of speech, Mt. 9:33; to speak, Mt. 10:20; to hold converse with, to talk with, Mt. 12:46; Mk. 6:50; Rev. 1:12; to discourse, to make an address, Lk. 11:37; Acts 11:20; 21:39; to make announcement, to make a declaration, Lk. 1:55; to make mention, Jn. 12:41; Acts 2:31; Heb. 4:8; 2 Pet. 3:16; trans. to speak, address, preach, Mt. 9:18; Jn. 3:11; Tit. 2:1; to give utterance to, utter, Mk. 2:7; Jn. 3:34; to declare, announce, reveal, Lk. 24:25 et al.; to disclose, 2 Cor. 12:4
G2980.abbottSmith: to utter : of inanimate things, Re 4:1 10:4 ; metaph., He 11:4 12:24 . to talk , speak , say : absol., Mt 9:33 12:46 , Mk 5:35 , Lk 8:49 ; seq. ὡς , I Co 13:11 , Re 13:11 ; εἰς , I Co 14:9 ; ἐκ , Mt 12:34 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 10:19 , Mk 11:32 , Jo 8:30 , al.; c. dat. pers., Mt 12:46 , Lk 24:6 , Ro 7:1 , al.; c. acc. rei et dat. pers., Mt 9:18 , Jo 10:6 , al.; c. prep., πρός , μετά , περί , Mk 6:50 ,
G2980.strongs: a prolonged form of an otherwise obsolete verb; to talk, i.e. utter words
G2980.kjv: preach, say, speak (after), talk, tell, utter

G63.thayer: I spend the night in the open, bivouac.
G63.mounce: to remain in the open air, to live outside, especially by night, Lk. 2:8*
G63.abbottSmith: to live in the fields : Lk 2:8 .†
G63.strongs: from G68 (ἀγρός) and G832 (αὐλέω) (in the sense of G833 (αὐλή)); to camp out

G1425.mounce: pr. to use ill words; to reproach, revile, 1 Cor. 4:13*
G1425.strongs: from G1418 (δυσ-) and a derivative of G3539 (νοιέω); difficult of perception

G5117.thayer: a place, region, seat; an opportunity.
G5117.mounce: a place, locality, Mt. 12:43; Lk. 6:17; a limited spot or ground, Mt. 24:15; 27:33; Jn. 4:20; Acts 6:13; a precise spot or situation, Mt. 28:6; Mk. 16:6; Lk. 14:9; a dwelling place, abode, mansion, dwelling, seat, Jn. 14:2, 3; Acts 4:31; a place of ordinary deposit, Mt. 26:52; a place, passage in a book, Lk. 4:17; place occupied, room, space, Lk. 2:7; 14:9, 22; place, opportunity, Acts 25:16; Heb. 12:17; place, condition, position, 1 Cor. 14:16
G5117.abbottSmith: place : Lk 4:37 10:1 , 32 , Jo 5:13 6:10 , Ac 12:17 , I Co 1:2 , al.; τ. ἅγιος (cf. Is 60:13 ), Mt 24:15 ; ἔρημος , Mt 14:13 , al.; πεδινός , Lk 6:17 ; ἄνυδρος (pl.), Mt 12:43 , Lk 11:24 ; κατὰ τόπους (EV, in divers places ), Mt 24:7 , Mk 13:8 ; τραχεῖς τ. , Ac 27:29 ; τ. διθάλασσος (q.v.), ib. 41 ; ἑτοιμάζειν τ. , Jo 14:2 , 3 ; ἔχειν , Re 12:6 ; διδόναι , Lk 14:9 ; c. gen. defin., τ. βασάνου , Lk
G5117.strongs: apparently a primary word; a spot (general in space, but limited by occupancy; whereas G5561 (χώρα) is a large but participle locality), i.e. location (as a position, home, tract, etc.); figuratively, condition, opportunity; specially, a scabbard
G5117.kjv: coast, licence, place, X plain, quarter, + rock, room, where

G5056.thayer: (a) an end, (b) event or issue, (c) the principal end, aim, purpose, (d) a tax.
G5056.mounce: an end attained, consummation; an end, closing act, Mt. 24:6, 14; 1 Cor. 15:24; full performance, perfect discharge, Rom. 10:4; fulfillment, realization, Lk. 22:37; final dealing, developed issue, Jas. 5:11; issue, final stage, 1 Cor. 10:11; issue, result, Mt. 26:58; Rom. 6:21, 22; 1 Pet. 1:9; antitypical issue, 2 Cor. 3:13; practical issue, 1 Tim. 1:5; ultimate destiny, Phil. 3:19; Heb. 6:8; 1 Pet. 4:17; a tax or dues, Mt. 17:25; Rom. 13:7; εἰς τέλος, to the full, 1 Thess. 2:16; εἰς τέλος, continually, Lk. 18:5; εἰς τέλος, μέχρι, ἄχρι τέλους, throughout, Mt. 10:22; Mk. 13:13; Jn. 13:1; Heb. 3:6, 14; 6:11; Rev. 2:26
G5056.abbottSmith: end : most freq. of the termination or limit of an act or state (in NT also of the end of a period of time, cl. τελευτή ), Lk 1:33 , I Co 10:11 , II Co 3:13 , I Pe 4:7 ; by meton., of one who makes an end, Ro 10:4 ; ἕως ( ἄχρι, μέκρι ) τέλους , I Co 1:8 , II Co 1:13 , He 3:14 6:11 , Re 2:26 ; εἰς τ. , to or at the end , Mt 10:22 , Mk 13:13 , Lk 18:5 , Jo 13:1 (or here, to the uttermost , v. Westc.
G5056.strongs: from a primary (to set out for a definite point or goal); properly, the point aimed at as a limit, i.e. (by implication) the conclusion of an act or state (termination (literally, figuratively or indefinitely), result (immediate, ultimate or prophetic), purpose); specially, an impost or levy (as paid)

G2434.thayer: a propitiation (of an angry god), atoning sacrifice.
G2434.mounce: atoning sacrifice, sin offering, propitiation, expiation; one who makes propitiation/expiation, 1 Jn. 2:2; 4:10*

G1191.thayer: Derbe, a town in Lycaonia and in the southern part of the Roman province Galatia.
G1191.mounce: Derbe, a city of Lycaonia, Acts 14:6, 20; 16:0*. See PhotoGuide.
G1191.abbottSmith: Derbe , a city of Lycaonia: Ac 14:6 , 20 16:1 .†
G1191.strongs: of foreign origin; Derbe, a place in Asia Minor

G925.abbottSmith: to weigh down = βαρέω (q.v.): Lk 21:34 , Rec.†

G1765.mounce: to strengthen, impart strength and vigor, Lk. 22:43; intrans. to gain, acquire, or recover strength and vigor, be strengthened, Acts 9:19*
G1765.abbottSmith: to strengthen : in spiritual sense, Lk 22 [43] ; pass., Ac 9:19 (Rec. ἐνισχύσεν , became strong , as in LXX, Ge 12:10 48:2 , al.).†
G1765.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2480 (ἰσχύω); to invigorate (transitively or reflexively)

G4675.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G4675.strongs: genitive case of G4771 (σύ); of thee, thy
G4675.kjv: X home, thee, thine (own), thou, thy

G5119.mounce: then, at that time, Mt. 2:17; 3:5; 11:20; then, Mt. 12:29; 13:26; 25:31; ἀπὸ τότε, from that time, Mt. 4:17; 16:21; ὁ τότε, which then was, 2 Pet. 3:6
G5119.abbottSmith: then, at that time ; of concurrent events: Mt 2:17 3:5 and freq., Ro 6:21 ; seq. ptcp., Mt 2:16 , Ga 4:8 ; opp. to νῦν , Ga 4:29 , He 12:26 ; ὁ τ. κόσμος , II Pe 3:6 ; of consequent events, then, thereupon : Mt 2:7 3:5 4:1 and freq., Lk 11:26 ; τ. οὖν , Jo 11:14 19:1 , 16 20:8 ; εὐθέως τ. , Ac 17:14 ; ὅτε . . . τ. , Mt 13:26 21:1 , Jo 12:16 ; ἀπὸ τ. , Mt 4:17 16:21 26:16 , Lk 16:16 ; of things fut
G5119.strongs: from (the neuter of) G3588 (ὁ) and G3753 (ὅτε); the when, i.e. at the time that (of the past or future, also in consecution)

G4317.thayer: I bring to, bring near; I come to or towards, approach.
G4317.mounce: to lead or conduct to, bring, Lk. 9:41; Acts 16:20; to conduct to the presence of, to procure access for, 1 Pet. 3:18; to bring near; to near, in a nautical sense, Acts 27:27*
G4317.abbottSmith: trans., to bring or lead : c. acc. et dat., Ac 16:20 ; metaph., τ. θεῷ , I Pe 3:18 ; seq. ὧδε , Lk 9:41 ; pass., c. dat., Mt 18:24 ( προσηνέχθη , T); in forensic sense, to summon : Ac 12:6 ( προαγαγεῖν , T, WH, mg., R).
G4317.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead towards, i.e. (transitively) to conduct near (summon, present), or (intransitively) to approach

G3317.thayer: midnight, the middle of the period between sunset and sunrise.
G3317.abbottSmith: of or at midnight ; as subst., neut., τὸ μ. , midnight (Arist. and late writers): gen., Lk 11:5 ; μέχρι μ. , Ac 20:7 ; κατὰ τὸ μ. , Ac 16:25 ; acc. (Rec., gen.; v. Bl., § 34, 8), Mk 13:35 .†
G3317.strongs: neuter of compound of G3319 (μέσος) and G3571 (νύξ); midnight (especially as a watch)

G1093.thayer: the earth, soil, land, region, country, inhabitants of a region.
G1093.mounce: earth, soil, Mt. 13:5; Mk. 4:8, et al.; the ground, surface of the earth, Mt. 10:29; Lk. 6:49, et al.; the land, as opposed to the sea or a lake, Lk. 5:11; Jn. 21:8, 9, 11; the earth, world, Mt. 5:18, 35, et al.; by synec. the inhabitants of the earth, Mt. 5:13; 6:10; 10:34; a land, region, tract, country, territory, Mt. 2:20; 14:34;by way of eminence, the chosen land, Mt. 5:5; 24:30; 27:45; Eph. 6:3; the inhabitants of a region or country, Mt. 10:15; 11:24, et al.
G1093.abbottSmith: the earth , world : Lk 21:35 , Ac 1:8 , He 11:13 , Re 3:10 , al.; opp. to οὐρανός , Mt 5:18 11:25 , Mk 13:27 , al.
G1093.strongs: contracted from a primary word; soil; by extension a region, or the solid part or the whole of the terrene globe (including the occupants in each application)

G33.mounce: to lead. bring; lead away, drive off, as a booty of cattle; conduct, accompany; lead out, produce; conduct with force, drag, hurry away; guide, incite, entice; convey one’s self, go, go away; pass or spend as time; celebrate
G33.abbottSmith: come! used as adv. and addressed, like φέρε , to one or more persons: Ja 4:13 , 5:1 .†
G33.strongs: imperative of G71 (ἄγω); properly, lead, i.e. come on

G1018.thayer: (lit: to act as arbiter in the games), I rule, arbitrate.
G1018.mounce: pr. to be a director or arbiter in the public games; in NT to preside, direct, rule, govern, be predominate, Col. 3:15*
G1018.abbottSmith: prop., to act as umpire ; hence, generally, to arbitrate, decide (Isocr., Dem.; Lft., Col. , l.c.; MM, s.v.): Col 3:15 ; in some late writers, to direct, rule, control (so in Col., l.c., acc. to Thayer, s.v., Meyer, in l.), (cf. κατα-βραβεύω ).†
G1018.strongs: from the same as G1017 (βραβεῖον); to arbitrate, i.e. (genitive case) to govern (figuratively, prevail)

G5515.mounce: pale green; green, verdent, Mk. 6:39; Rev. 8:7; 9:4; pale, sallow, Rev. 6:8*
G5515.abbottSmith: pale green : χόρτος , Mk 6:39 , Re 8:7 ; πᾶν χ. , Re 9:4 ;
G5515.strongs: from the same as G5514 (Χλόη); greenish, i.e. verdant, dun-colored

G3643.thayer: I despise, hold in low esteem, make light of.
G3643.abbottSmith: to esteem lightly , think little of : c. gen., He 12:5 (LXX) .†
G3643.strongs: from a compound of G3641 (ὀλίγος) and ("care"); to have little regard for, i.e. to disesteem

G2383.thayer: Jairus, a Jewish ruler of the synagogue.
G2383.mounce: also spelled Ἰάειρος, Jairus, pr. name, Mk. 5:22; Lk. 8:41*
G2383.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02971); Jairus (i.e. Jair), an Israelite

G1103.thayer: (lit: born in wedlock), hence: real, true, genuine; with definite article: the true, genuine element.
G1103.mounce: lawful, legitimate, as children; genuine, in faith, etc.; 1 Tim. 1:2; Tit. 1:4; true, sincere, 2 Cor. 8:8; Phil. 4:3*
G1103.abbottSmith: prop., lawfully begotten, born in wedlock (in π. , γυνὴ γ. , a lawful wife ; MM, s.v.). true, genuine, sincere : Phl 4:3 , I Ti 1:2 , Tit 1:4 ; as subst., τὸ γ. = ἡ γνησιότης , sincerity , II Co 8:8 .†
G1103.strongs: from the same as G1077 (γενέσια); legitimate (of birth), i.e. genuine

G3959.thayer: Patara, a town on the coast of the Roman province Lycia.
G3959.mounce: Patara, a city on the seacoast of Lycia, in Asia Minor, Acts 21:1*
G3959.abbottSmith: Patara , a maritime city of Lycia: Ac 21:1 .†
G3959.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Patara, a place in Asia Minor

G2326.thayer: reaping, harvest; met: the harvest, crop.
G2326.mounce: a harvest, the act of gathering in the harvest, reaping, Jn. 4:35; met. the harvest of the Gospel, Mt. 9:37, 38; Lk. 10:2; a crop; met. the crop of vengeance, Rev. 14:15
G2326.abbottSmith: harvest ; the act: Jo 4:35 ; the time: fig., Mt 13:30 , 39 , Mk 4:29 the crop: fig., Mt 9:37 , 38 , Lk 10:2 , Re 14:15 .†
G2326.strongs: from G2325 (θερίζω); reaping, i.e. the crop

G3545.mounce: lawfully, agreeably to law or custom, rightfully, 1 Tim. 1:8; 2 Tim. 2:5*
G3545.abbottSmith: rightly , lawfully : I Ti 1:8 , II Ti 2:5 .†
G3545.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G3551 (νόμος); legitimately (specially, agreeably to the rules of the lists)

G5535.thayer: I need, have need of, want, desire.
G5535.mounce: to need, want, desire, Mt. 6:32; Lk. 11:8; 12:30; Rom. 16:2; 2 Cor. 3:1*
G5535.abbottSmith: to need, have need of : c. gen. rei, Mt 6:32 , Lk 11:8 12:30 , II Co 3:1 ; c. gen. pers. seq. ἐν , Ro 16:2 .†
G5535.strongs: from G5532 (χρεία); to make (i.e. have) necessity, i.e. be in want of

G2577.thayer: I work, am weary, am sick.
G2577.mounce: to tire with exertion, labor to weariness; to be wearied, tired out, exhausted, be discouraged, Heb. 12:3; to labor under disease, be sick, Jas. 5:15*
G2577.abbottSmith: to work ; hence, from the effect of continued work,
G2577.strongs: apparently a primary verb; properly, to toil, i.e. (by implication) to tire (figuratively, faint, sicken)

G1713.thayer: a merchant, trader; one on a journey.
G1713.mounce: pr. a passenger by sea; a traveller; one who travels about for traffic, a merchant, Mt. 13:45; Rev. 18:3, 11, 15, 23*
G1713.abbottSmith: a passenger on shipboard, one on a journey .
G1713.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G4198 (πορεύομαι); a (wholesale) tradesman

G4975.mounce: pr. near, of place; hence, nearly, almost, Acts 13:44; 19:26; Heb. 9:22*
G4975.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω) as adverb; nigh, i.e. nearly

G1112.mounce: a muttering, murmuring, low and suppressed discourse, Jn. 7:12; the expression of secret and sullen discontent, murmuring, complaint, Acts 6:1; Phil. 2:14; 1 Pet. 4:9*
G1112.abbottSmith: a murmuring, muttering : Jo 7:12 , Ac 6:1 ; ἄνευ γ. , I Pe 4:9 ; pl., χωρὶς γ. , Phl 2:14 .†

G1063.mounce: for; it is, however, frequently used with an ellipsis of the clause to which it has reference, and its force must then be variously expressed: Mt. 15:27; 27:23, et al.; it is also sometimes epexegetic, or introductory of an intimated detail of circumstances, now, then, Mt. 1:18
G1063.abbottSmith: verily then , hence, in truth, indeed, yea, then, why , and when giving a reason or explanation, for , the usage in NT being in general accord with that of cl.; explicative and epexegetic: Mt 4:18 19:12 , Mk 1:16 5:42 16:4 , Lk 11:30 , Ro 7:1 , I Co 16:5 , al. Conclusive, in questions, answers and exclamations: Mt 9:5 27:23 , Lk 9:25 22:27 , Jo 9:30 , Ac 8:31 16:37 19:35 , Ro 15:26 , I Co 9:10 , P
G1063.strongs: a primary particle; properly, assigning a reason (used in argument, explanation or intensification; often with other particles)
G1063.kjv: and, as, because (that), but, even, for, indeed, no doubt, seeing, then, therefore, verily, what, why, yet

G4274.mounce: a precursor, forerunner, one who advances to explore and prepare the way, Heb. 6:20*
G4274.abbottSmith: running forward , going in advance . As subst., ὁ π. , an advance guard , forerunner : He 6:20 .†
G4274.strongs: from the alternate of G4390 (προτρέχω); a runner ahead, i.e. scout (figuratively, precursor)

G4328.thayer: I expect, wait for, await, think, anticipate.
G4328.mounce: to look for, be expectant of, Mt. 11:3; Lk. 7:19, 20; Acts 3:5; 2 Pet. 3:12, 13, 14; to expect, Acts 28:6; to wait for, Lk. 1:21; 8:40; Acts 10:24; 27:33; absol. to think, anticipate, Mt. 24:50; Lk. 12:46
G4328.abbottSmith: to await , expect : Mt 24:50 , Lk 3:15 12:46 , Ac 27:33 28:6 ; c. acc. pers., Mt 11:3 , Lk 1:21 7:19 , 20 8:40 , Ac 10:24 ; c. acc. rei, II Pe 3:12-14 ; c. acc. et inf., Ac 28:6 ; c. inf., Ac 3:5 .†
G4328.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and dokeuo (to watch); to anticipate (in thought, hope or fear); by implication, to await
G4328.kjv: (be in) expect(-ation), look (for), when looked, tarry, wait for

G4787.thayer: I move together, stir up, excite.
G4787.mounce: to agitate, put in turmoil; to excite, Acts 6:12*
G4787.abbottSmith: to move together . Metaph., to excite, stir up : τ. λαόν , Ac 6:12 (Arist., Polyb., al.).†
G4787.strongs: from G4682 (σπαράσσω) and G2795 (κινέω); to move together, i.e. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition)

G909.mounce: pr. an act of dipping or immersion: a baptism, Col. 2:12; Heb. 6:2; an ablution, Mk. 7:4; Heb. 9:10*
G909.abbottSmith: a dipping , washing , lustration : Mk 7:4 ; of Jewish ceremonial, He 9:10 ; in He 6:2 , βαπτισμῶν διδαχήν ( -ῆς ), "the pl. and the peculiar form seem to be used to include Christian baptism with other lustral rites" (Westc, in l.).

G2969.thayer: a large village, a city which in constitution has only the status of a village; a country town.
G2969.mounce: a large village, market town, Mk. 1:38*
G2969.abbottSmith: a country town : Mk 1:38 (v. Swete, in l.).†
G2969.strongs: from G2968 (κώμη) and G4172 (πόλις); an unwalled city

G3609.mounce: the members, of a household, Mt. 24:45*
G3609.abbottSmith: in or of the house (opp. to ξένος, ἀλλότριος ); of things; τὰ οἰ. , household affairs or goods ; of persons, of the same family or kin ; as subst., οἱ οἰ. , kinsmen : I Ti 5:8 ; c. gen. pers., of the family of : metaph., τ. θεοῦ , Eph 2:19 ; τ. πίστεως (Lft., in l.), Ga 6:10 .†
G3609.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος); domestic, i.e. (as noun), a relative, adherent

G2283.thayer: Tamar, mother of Perez and Zerah by Judah, son of Jacob.
G2283.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08559); Thamar (i.e. Tamar), an Israelitess

G4014.thayer: (a) I strip off, strip from, take away, (b) I cast off, cut adrift, cast loose.
G4014.mounce: to light a fire, kindle, Lk. 22:55*
G4014.abbottSmith: to take away that which surrounds, take away , take off ( τείχη , Hdt., Thuc.; χιτῶνα , Plat.; δακτύλιον , Ge 41:42 , cf. σφρηγῖδα , Hdt., ii, 151): τ. κάλυμμα , pass., II Co 3:16 ; as nautical term (RV, cast off ), ἀγκύρας , Ac 27:40 ; absol., to cast loose : Ac 28:13 . Metaph., to take away entirely : ἐλπίς , pass., Ac 27:20 ; ἁμαρτίας , He 10:11 .†
G4014.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G138 (αἱρέομαι) (including its alternate); to remove all around, i.e. unveil, cast off (anchor); figuratively, to expiate

G3159.mounce: also spelled Ματθίας, BDAG 611, suggest it is a shortened form of Ματταθίας, Matthias, pr. name, Acts 1:23, 26*
G3159.strongs: apparently a shortened form of G3161 (Ματταθίας); Matthias (i.e. Mattithjah), an Israelite

G1159.thayer: I spend, bear expense, waste, squander.
G1159.mounce: to expend, be at expense, Mk. 5:26; Acts 21:24; 2 Cor. 12:15; to spend, waste, consume by extravagance, Lk. 15:14; Jas. 4:3*
G1159.abbottSmith: to spend, expend : c. acc., Mk 5:26 ; seq. ἐπί , c. dat. pers., Ac 21:24 ; ὑπέρ , II Co 12:15 .
G1159.strongs: from G1160 (δαπάνη); to expend, i.e. (in a good sense) to incur cost, or (in a bad one) to waste

G805.mounce: mid: to make fast, safe, or secure, Mt. 27:64, 65, 66; Acts 16:24*
G805.abbottSmith: to make firm , secure : mid., Mt 27:65 , 66 , Ac 16:24 ; pass., Mt 27:64 (MM, s.v.).†

G4201.thayer: Porcius, the middle (gentile) name of the procurator Festus.
G4201.abbottSmith: Porcius , prænomen of Porcius Festus (v.s. Φῆστος ): Ac 24:27 .†
G4201.strongs: of Latin origin; apparently swinish; Porcius, a Roman

G1182.mounce: tenth, Jn. 1:39; Rev. 11:13; 21:20; δεκάτη, i.e. μερίς, a tenth part, tithe, Heb. 7:2, 4, 8, 9*
G1182.abbottSmith: tenth : Jo 1:40 , Ac 19:9 (WH, txt., RV omit), Re 11:13 ; 21:20 .†

G948.mounce: to abominate, loathe, detest, abhor, Rom. 2:22; pass. to be abominable, detestable, Rev. 21:8*
G948.abbottSmith: to make foul ; pass., Re 21:8 ; mid., to turn away in disgust from , to detest : Ro 2:22 (Cremer, 137).†
G948.strongs: from a (presumed) derivative of (to stink); to be disgusted, i.e. (by implication) detest (especially of idolatry)

G1010.thayer: a member of a city council, in NT of the Sanhedrin at Jerusalem.
G1010.mounce: a counsellor, senator; member of the Sanhedrin, Mk. 15:43; Lk. 23:50*
G1010.abbottSmith: a councillor, a senator ; of a member of the Sanhedrin: Mk 15:34 , Lk 23:50 .†
G1010.strongs: from G1011 (βουλεύω); an adviser, i.e. (specially) a councillor or member of the Jewish Sanhedrin

G2390.thayer: I heal, generally of the physical, sometimes of spiritual, disease.
G2390.mounce: to heal, cure, Mt. 8:8; Lk. 9:2; met. to heal, spiritually, restore from a state of sin and condemnation, Mt. 13:15; Heb. 12:13
G2390.strongs: middle voice of apparently a primary verb; to cure (literally or figuratively)

G59.mounce: to buy; redeem, acquire by a ransom or price paid
G59.abbottSmith: to frequent the ἀγορά (Hdt., al.). to buy in the market , purchase (Xen., al.; LXX; in π. very common in deeds of sale, v. MM, VGT , s.v.): absol., Mt 21:12 , Mk 11:15 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 13:44 , 46 , al.; seq. παρά , c. gen. pers., Re 3:18 (LXX, Polyb.); ἐκ , Mt 27:7 ; c. gen. pret., Mk 6:37 , al.; metaph., I Co 6:20 7:23 , II Pe 2:1 , Re 5:9 14:3 , 4 .
G59.strongs: from G58 (ἀγορά); properly, to go to market, i.e. (by implication) to purchase; specially, to redeem

G4918.thayer: I press on all sides, crowd upon.
G4918.mounce: to press together; to press upon, crowd, Mk. 5:24, 31*
G4918.abbottSmith: to press together, press on all sides : Mk 5:24 , 31 .†
G4918.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2346 (θλίβω); to compress, i.e. crowd on all sides

G3950.mounce: provocation to anger; anger excited, indignation, wrath, Eph. 4:26*
G3950.abbottSmith: irritation ("distinguished from ὀργή as implying a less permanent state"; ICC , Eph. , 140; and v. Tr., Syn. , § xxxvii): Eph 4:26 .†

G3608.mounce: to know, Mt. 6:8; to know how, Mt. 7:11; from the Hebrew, to regard with favor, 1 Thess. 5:12. οἶδα is actually a perfect form functioning as a present, and ᾔδειν is actually a pluperfect form functioning as an aorist.
G3608.abbottSmith: a piece of fine linen , a linen cloth ; Lk 24:12 (WH, R, mg., om.), Jo 19:40 20:5 , 6 , 7 .†
G3608.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3607 (ὀθόνη); a linen bandage

G5133.mounce: a money changer, broker, banker, who exchanges or loans money for a premium, Mt. 25:27*
G5133.abbottSmith: a money­changer, banker : Mt 25:27 (Dem., Plut., al.).†
G5133.strongs: from G5132 (τράπεζα); a money-broker or banker

G4453.strongs: probably ultimately from (to be busy, to trade); to barter (as a pedlar), i.e. to sell

G4789.mounce: pr. a coheir, Rom. 8:17; a fellow participant, Eph. 3:6; Heb. 11:9; 1 Pet. 3:7*
G4789.abbottSmith: a co-inheritor, fellow-heir : Ro 8:17 , Eph 3:6 ; c. gen. rei, He 11:9 , I Pe 3:7 (for exx., v. Deiss., LAE , 88 f.).†
G4789.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2818 (κληρονόμος); a co-heir, i.e. (by analogy) participant in common

G4005.thayer: Pentecost, a feast of the Jews, the fiftieth day after Passover.
