G4005.mounce: Pentecost, or the Feast of Weeks; one of the three great Jewish festivals, so called because it was celebrated on the fiftieth day, reckoning from the second day of the feast of unleavened bread, i.e., from the 16th day of Nisan, Acts 2:1; 20:16; 1 Cor. 16:8*
G4005.abbottSmith: fiftieth . As subst., ἡ π. ; (sc. μερίς ), at Athens, a tax of two per cent.; (sc. ἡμέρα , i.e. the fiftieth day after the Passover), Pentecost , the second of the three great Jewish feasts ( To 2:1 , II Mac 12:32 ; ἑορτὴ ἑβδομάδων , De 16:10 , al.): Ac 2:1 20:16 , I Co 16:8 .†
G4005.strongs: feminine of the ordinal of G4004 (πεντήκοντα); fiftieth (G2250 (ἡμέρα) being implied) from Passover, i.e. the festival of "Pentecost"

G1362.abbottSmith: twofold, double : I Ti 5:17 , Re 18:6 ; διπλότιρον , in twofold measure : Mt 23:15 .†
G1362.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and (probably) the base of G4119 (πλείων); two-fold

G2624.thayer: I give as an inheritance, distribute by lot.
G2624.abbottSmith: to distribute by lot or as an inheritance ( Nu 34:18 , Jos 14:1 , al.): Ac 13:19 .
G2624.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of a compound of G2819 (κλῆρος) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to be a giver of lots to each, i.e. (by implication) to apportion an estate

G835.thayer: I lodge in the open, lodge, pass the night.
G835.mounce: pr. to pass the time in a court-yard; to lodge; hence, to pass the night in any place, to lodge at night, pass or remain through the night, Mt. 21:17; Lk. 21:37*
G835.abbottSmith: prop., to lodge in a courtyard .
G835.strongs: middle voice from G833 (αὐλή); to pass the night (properly, in the open air)

G3935.thayer: (a) I let pass, neglect, omit, disregard, (b) I slacken, loosen; pass: I am wearied.
G3935.mounce: to let pass beside, let fall beside; to relax, Lk. 11:42; perf. pass. part. παρειμένος, hanging down helplessly, unstrung, feeble, Heb. 12:12*
G3935.abbottSmith: to pass by or over , let alone , disregard : Lk 11:42 .
G3935.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and hiemi (to send); to let by, i.e. relax

G2687.thayer: I let down, lower; I keep down, restrain, pacify.
G2687.mounce: to arrange, dispose in regular order; to appease, quiet, pacify, Acts 19:35, 36*
G2687.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4724 (στέλλω); to put down, i.e. quell

G2121.thayer: opportune, timely, suitable; perhaps sometimes: holiday, festival.
G2121.mounce: timely, opportune, seasonable, convenient, Mk. 6:21; Heb. 4:16*
G2121.abbottSmith: timely, seasonable, suitable (Cremer, 740): ἡμέρα , Mk 6:21 (or empty , as in Byz. and MGr.; v. MM, Exp. , xiv); βοήθεια , He 4:16 .†
G2121.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G2540 (καιρός); well-timed, i.e. opportune

G4693.mounce: a cave, cavern, den, hideout, Mt. 21:13; Mk. 11:17; Lk. 19:46; Jn. 11:38; Heb. 11:38; Rev. 6:5*
G4693.abbottSmith: a cave, cavern : Jo 11:38 , He 11:38 , He 6:15 ; σ. (EV, den ) λῃστῶν ( Je 7:11 ), Mt 21:13 , Mk 11:17 , Lk 19:46 .†
G4693.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a grotto); a cavern; by implication, a hiding-place or resort

G2265.thayer: the Herodians, the partisans of Herod (Antipas).
G2265.mounce: Herodians, partisans of Ἡρῴδης, Herod Antipas, Mt. 22:16; Mk. 3:6; 12:13*
G2265.abbottSmith: Herodians , partisans of Herod (cf. τὸυς τὰ Ἡρῴδου φρονοῦντας , FlJ, Ant. , xiv, 15, 10): Mt 22:16 , Mk 3:6 12:13 .†
G2265.strongs: plural of a derivative of G2264 (Ἡρώδης); Herodians, i.e. partisans of Herod

G3871.mounce: to cover over, veil; met. pass. to be veiled from comprehension, Lk. 9:45*
G3871.abbottSmith: to cover by hanging something beside, to hide : metaph. (as Plat., al.), Lk 9:45 .†
G3871.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to cover alongside, i.e. veil (figuratively)

G1443.thayer: Eber, father of Peleg and son of Shelah.
G1443.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05677); Eber, a patriarch

G4511.mounce: a flowing; a morbid flux, Mk. 5:25; Lk. 8:43, 44*
G4511.abbottSmith: a flowing, issue : τ. αἵματος , Mk 5:25 , Lk 8:43, 44 .†
G4511.strongs: from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in the sense of its congener G4482 (ῥέω); a flux (of blood)

G4648.thayer: I look at, regard attentively, take heed, beware, consider.
G4648.mounce: to view attentively, watch; to see, observe, take care, beware, Lk. 11:35; Gal. 6:1; to regard, have respect to, 2 Cor. 4:18; Phil. 2:4; to mark, note, Rom. 16:17; Phil. 3:17*
G4648.abbottSmith: to look at, behold, watch, contemplate . Metaph., to look to, consider : c. acc. rei, II Co 4:13 , Phl 2:4 ; c. acc. pers., Ro 16:17 , Phl 3:17 ; seq. μή , Lk 11:35 ; σεαυτόν , seq. μή (v. M, Pr. , 192), Ga 6:1 (cf. ἐπι- , κατα-σκοπέω ).†
G4648.strongs: from G4649 (σκοπός); to take aim at (spy), i.e. (figuratively) regard
G4648.kjv: consider, take heed, look at (on), mark

G3834.abbottSmith: cleverness , in cl. nearly always in bad sense, craftiness , cunning , knavery : Lk 20:23 , I Co 3:19 (LXX, φρόνησις , for עׇרְמָה ) II Co 4:2 11:3 , Eph 4:14 .†
G3834.strongs: from G3835 (πανοῦργος); adroitness, i.e. (in a bad sense) trickery or sophistry

G4421.mounce: as adj., winged, with feathers; as noun, a bird, fowl, 1 Cor. 15:39*
G4421.abbottSmith: winged ; as subst., τὰ π. , birds : I Co 15:39 .†

G4719.abbottSmith: an ear of corn : Mt 12:1 , Mk 2:23 4:28 , Lk 6:1 .†
G4719.strongs: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a head of grain (as standing out from the stalk)

G210.thayer: unwilling, generally used where English would express by an adverb: unwillingly.
G210.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G1635 (ἑκών); unwilling

G2483.strongs: from G2482 (Ἰταλία); Italic, i.e. belonging to Italia

G2132.mounce: to have kind thoughts, be well affected or kindly disposed towards, make friends, Mt. 5:25*
G2132.abbottSmith: to be favourable, kindly disposed : c. dat., Mt 5:25 .†
G2132.strongs: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3563 (νοῦς); to be well- minded, i.e. reconcile

G557.mounce: pr. refutation; by impl. disrepute. contempt, Acts 19:27*
G557.abbottSmith: refutation , disrepute : ἐλθεῖν εἰς ἀ. , Ac 19:27 (not elsewhere; v. MM, s.v.).†
G557.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G1651 (ἐλέγχω); refutation, i.e. (by implication) contempt

G3477.thayer: Naggai, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3477.mounce: Naggai, Nagge, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:25*
G3477.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H05052); Nangæ (i.e. perhaps Nogach), an Israelite

G1721.thayer: inborn, ingrown, congenital, natural, rooted, implanted.
G1721.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω); implanted (figuratively)

G414.strongs: comparative of a derivative of G430 (ἀνέχομαι); more endurable

G1200.thayer: a keeper of a prison, a jailer.
G1200.mounce: a keeper of a prison, jailer, Acts 16:23, 27, 36*
G1200.abbottSmith: a prison-keeper, gaoler : Ac 16:23 , 27 , 36 (cf. ἀρχιδ- , Ge 39:21 ).†
G1200.strongs: from G1199 (δεσμόν) and G5441 (φύλαξ); a jailer (as guarding the prisoners)

G2645.thayer: left behind; the rest, the remainder.
G2645.mounce: remaining; οἱ κατάλοιποι, the rest, Acts 15:17*
G2645.abbottSmith: left remaining : ἱο κ. τ. ἀνθρώπων , Ac 15:17 (LXX) .†
G2645.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3062 (λοιποί); left down (behind), i.e remaining (plural the rest)

G3933.thayer: a maiden, virgin; extended to men who have not known women.
G3933.mounce: a virgin, maid, Mt. 1:23; 25:1, 7, 11; Acts 21:9; in NT also masc., chaste, Rev. 14:4
G3933.abbottSmith: a maiden , virgin : Mt 1:23 (LXX) 25:1 , 7 , 11 , Lk 1:27 , Ac 21:9 , I Co 7:25 ff., II Co 11:2 ; masc., of chaste persons ( CIG , 8784 b): Re 14:4 .†
G3933.strongs: of unknown origin; a maiden; by implication, an unmarried daughter

G3397.mounce: little, small in size quantity, etc. Mt. 13:32; Lk. 12:32; Rev. 3:8; small, little in age, young, not adult, Mk. 15:40; little, short in time, Jn. 7:33; μικρόν, i.e. χρόνον, a little while, a short time, Jn. 13:33; μετὰ μικρόν, after a little while, a little while afterwards, Mt. 26:73; little in number, Lk. 12:32; small, little in dignity, low, humble, Mt. 10:42; 11:11; μικρόν, as an adv., little, a little, Mt. 26:39
G3397.strongs: masculine or neuter singular of G3398 (μικρός) (as noun); a small space of time or degree

G5253.thayer: an upper room, the upper part of a house.
G5253.mounce: the upper part of a house, upper room, or chamber, Acts 1:13; 9:37, 39; 20:8*
G5253.abbottSmith: in cl., the upper story or upper rooms where the women resided (Hom., a1.).
G5253.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G5228 (ὑπέρ); a higher part of the house, i.e. apartment in the third story

G3523.mounce: can also be masc. with a gen. in ιδος (n-3c[2]), fasting, hungry, Mt. 15:32; Mk. 8:3*
G3523.strongs: from the inseparable negative particle (not) and G2068 (ἐσθίω); not eating, i.e. abstinent from food (religiously)

G3017.thayer: (Hebrew), Levi, (a) an ancestor of Jesus, (b) another ancestor of Jesus, (c) third son of Jacob, the patriarch, and founder of a tribe named after him, (d) son of Alphaeus, and called Matthew, a revenue officer and one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.
G3017.mounce: Levi, also spelled Λευίς (G3323), pr. name. When the NT refers to the Λευί of the OT, the word is indecl. (n-3g[2]); when it refers to a NT person, it is partially declined (n-3g[1]): Λευίς (nom); Λευίν (acc). (1) Levi, son of Jacob, Heb. 7:5, 9; Rev. 7:7. (2) Levi, son of Symeon, Lk. 3:29 (3) Levi, son of Melchi, Lk. 3:24
G3017.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03878); Levi, the name of three Israelites

G4307.mounce: forethought; providence, provident care, Acts 24:2; provision, Rom. 13:14*
G4307.abbottSmith: foresight , forethought : Ac 24:3 ; π. ποιεῖσθαι , c. gen. (Dem., 546, 6), make provision for , show care for : Ro 13:14 .†
G4307.strongs: from G4306 (προνοέω); forethought, i.e. provident care or supply

G2141.thayer: I have means, am prosperous, enjoy plenty.
G2141.mounce: to be in prosperous circumstances, enjoy plenty, Acts 11:29*
G2141.abbottSmith: to prosper, be well off : Ac 11:29 .†
G2141.strongs: from a compound of G2090 (ἑτοιμάζω) and the base of G4197 (πορεία); (intransitively) to be good for passing through, i.e. (figuratively) have pecuniary means

G3963.thayer: Patmos, a small rocky island in the Aegean sea, south-west of Ephesus.
G3963.mounce: Patmos, an island in the Aegean sea, Rev. 1:9*
G3963.abbottSmith: Patmos , an island in the Ægean Sea: Re 1:9 .†
G3963.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Patmus, an islet in the Mediterranean

G2237.thayer: pleasure, a pleasure, especially sensuous pleasure; a strong desire, passion.
G2237.mounce: pleasure, gratification; esp. sensual pleasure, Lk. 8:14; Tit. 3:3; Jas. 4:3; 2 Pet. 2:13; a passion, Jas. 4:1*
G2237.abbottSmith: pleasure : II Pe 2:12 ; pl., Lk 8:14 , Tit 3:3 , Ja 4:1 , 3 .†
G2237.strongs: from (to please); sensual delight; by implication, desire

G211.thayer: a phial or bottle of perfumed ointment, usually of alabaster.
G211.mounce: can be masculine, feminine (2x), or neuter (ἀλάβαστρον,   2x), an alabaster vase, Mk. 14:3 (2x); 26:7; Lk. 7:37*
G211.abbottSmith: a box of alabaster ( ἀλαβαστίτης ) for ointment: Mt 26:7 , Mk 14:3 , Lk 7:37 (v. DCG , i, 41b; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G211.strongs: neuter of (of uncertain derivation), the name of a stone; properly, an "alabaster" box, i.e. (by extension) a perfume vase (of any material)

G4238.thayer: I do, perform, accomplish; be in any condition, i.e. I fare; I exact, require.
G4238.mounce: meekness, gentleness of mind, kindness, 1 Tim. 6:11*
G4238.abbottSmith: to do , practise , be engaged in : Ac 19:19 , 36 , I Co 9:17 ; τ. ἴδια π. , to mind one's own business ( τὰ ἑαυτοῦ , Soph., Plat.), I Th 4:11 ; intrans., to act , Ac 17:7 .
G4238.strongs: a primary verb; to "practise", i.e. perform repeatedly or habitually (thus differing from G4160 (ποιέω), which properly refers to a single act); by implication, to execute, accomplish, etc.; specially, to collect (dues), fare (personally)
G4238.kjv: commit, deeds, do, exact, keep, require, use arts

G301.thayer: Amos, son of Manasseh and father of Josiah, an ancestor of Jesus.
G301.mounce: Amos, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:10; Lk. 3:25*
G301.abbottSmith: as in IV Ki 21:18 ff. B ( Α. Ἀμμών ; Jos., Ἀμμών , Ἄμωσσς ), Amon : Mt 1:10 .
G301.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0531); Amos, an Israelite

G5151.mounce: three times, thrice, Mt. 26:34, 75; ἐπὶ τρίς, to the extent of thrice, as many as three times, Acts 10:16; 11:10

G404.mounce: to recreate by fresh air; to refresh, cheer, 2 Tim. 1:16*
G404.abbottSmith: to refresh : c. acc. pers., II Ti 1:16 (MM, VGT , s.v.; Cremer, 588).†
G404.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5594 (ψύχω); properly, to cool off, i.e. (figuratively) relieve

G778.mounce: pr. to work materials; absol. to train or exert one’s self, make endeavor, Acts 24:16*
G778.strongs: probably from the same as G4632 (σκεῦος); to elaborate, i.e. (figuratively) train (by implication, strive)

G4575.thayer: (official Greek equivalent of Augustus), venerated, august, a title of the Roman emperors; hence secondarily: Augustan, imperial.
G4575.mounce: pr. venerable, august; ὁ Σεβαστός, i.q. Latin Augustus, Acts 25:21, 25; Augustan, or, Sebastan, named from the city Sebaste, Acts 27:1*
G4575.strongs: from G4573 (σεβάζομαι); venerable (august), i.e. (as noun) a title of the Roman Emperor, or (as adjective) imperial

G3977.strongs: from a derivative of G4228 (πούς) (meaning the ground); level (as easy for the feet)

G3431.thayer: I commit adultery (of a man with a married woman, but also of a married man).
G3431.mounce: trans. to commit adultery with, debauch, Mt. 5:28; absol. and mid. to commit adultery, Mt. 5:27; Jn. 8:4; to commit spiritual adultery, be guilty of idolatry, Rev. 2:22
G3431.abbottSmith: to commit adultery : absol., Mt 5:27 19:18 , Mk 10:19 , Lk 16:18 18:20 , Ro 2:22 13:9 , Ja 2:11 ; c. acc. fem., Mt 5:28 . Pass., of the woman, Mt 5:32 19:9 WH, mg.), Jo 8:[4] . Metaph., of idolatry (v.s. μιοιχαλίς , and cf. Je 3:9 , al.), seq. μετ’ αὐτῆς , Re 2:22 .†

G3888.mounce: to exercise a gentle influence by words; to soothe, comfort, console, Jn. 11:19, 31; 1 Thess. 5:14; to cheer, exhort, 1 Thess. 2:12*
G3888.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the middle voice of a derivative of G3454 (μῦθος); to relate near, i.e. (by implication) encourage, console

G4388.mounce: to place before; to set forth, propose publicly, Rom. 3:25; mid. προτίθεμαι, to purpose, determine, design beforehand, Rom. 1:13; Eph. 1:9*
G4388.abbottSmith: to set before , set forth publicly; so also in mid.: c. acc. pers., Ro 3:25 (for a suggested alt. rend., v. MM, xxii).
G4388.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place before, i.e. (for oneself) to exhibit; (to oneself) to propose (determine)

G4586.mounce: august, venerable; honorable, reputable, Phil. 4:8; grave, serious, dignified, 1 Tim. 3:8, 11; Tit. 2:2*
G4586.abbottSmith: reverend, august, venerable , in cl. of the gods and also of human beings.

G161.mounce: captivity, state of captivity; captive multitude, Eph. 4:8; Rev. 13:10*
G161.abbottSmith: captivity (Diod., al.): Re 13:10 ; pl., abstr. for concr., = αἰχμάλωτοι , Eph 4:8 (LXX) .†

G1789.thayer: I nourish (sustain) on, am educated in.
G1789.mounce: to nourish in, bring up or educate in, 1 Tim. 4:6*
G1789.abbottSmith: to train up, nurture ; pass., metaph., τοῖς λόγοις τ. πίστεως , I Ti 4:6 .†
G1789.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5142 (τρέφω); (figuratively) to educate

G2841.thayer: (a) I share, communicate, contribute, impart, (b) I share in, have a share of, have fellowship with.
G2841.mounce: to have in common, share, Heb. 2:14; to be associated in, to become a sharer in, Rom. 15:27; 1 Pet. 4:13; to become implicated in, be a party to, 1 Tim. 5:22; 2 Jn. 11; to associate one’s self with by sympathy and assistance, to communicate with in the way of aid and relief, Rom. 12:13; Gal. 6:6; Phil. 4:15*
G2841.abbottSmith: to have a share of , go shares in (something) with (some one), take part in : c. gen. rei (as usually in cl., so Pr 1:11 ), He 2:14 ; c. dat. rei, Ro 12:13 15:27 , I Ti 5:22 , I Pe 4:13 , II Jo 11 ; c. dat. pers., seq. εἰς , Phl 4:15 ; ἐν , Ga 6:6 (cf. συν-κοινωνέω ).†
G2841.strongs: from G2844 (κοινωνός); to share with others (objectively or subjectively)

G5474.thayer: orichalcum, a fine metal, or frankincense of a yellow color.
G5474.mounce: orichalcum, fine bronze, a factitious metal of which there were several varieties, the white being of the highest repute, or, deep-tinted frankincense, Rev. 1:15; 2:18*
G5474.abbottSmith: chalcolibanus , probably "a mixed metal of great brilliance" (Swete): Re 1:15 2:18 (RV, burnished brass ).†
G5474.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5475 (χαλκός) and G3030 (λίβανος) (in the implied mean of whiteness or brilliancy); burnished copper, an alloy of copper (or gold) and silver having a brilliant lustre

G5287.thayer: (lit: an underlying), (a) confidence, assurance, (b) a giving substance (or reality) to, or a guaranteeing, (c) substance, reality.
G5287.mounce: pr. a standing under; a taking of a thing upon one’s self; an assumed position, an assumption of a specific character, 2 Cor. 11:17; an engagement undertaken with regard to the conduct of others, a vouching, 2 Cor. 9:4; or of one’s self, a pledged profession, Heb. 3:14; an assured impression, a mental realizing, Heb. 11:1; a substructure, basis; subsistence, essence, Heb. 1:3*
G5287.abbottSmith: a support, base or foundation (in various senses).
G5287.strongs: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G2476 (ἵστημι); a setting under (support), i.e. (figuratively) concretely, essence, or abstractly, assurance (objectively or subjectively)

G1580.thayer: I carry out (of the city gate for burial).
G1580.mounce: to carry out, bring out; especially, to carry out a corpse for burial, Lk. 7:12*
G1580.abbottSmith: to carry out : as freq., a corpse for burial, Lk 7:12 .†
G1580.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2865 (κομίζω); to bear forth (to burial)

G2350.thayer: I disturb greatly, terrify, strike with panic; mid: I show agitation of mind.
G2350.mounce: to make a din, uproar; trans. to disturb, throw into commotion, Acts 17:5; in NT mid. to manifest agitation of mind, to raise a lament, Mt. 9:23; Mk. 5:39; Acts 20:10*
G2350.abbottSmith: to make a noise or uproar : mid., of loud and ostentatious lamentation, Mt 9:23 , Mk 5:39 , Ac 20:10 .
G2350.strongs: from G2351 (θόρυβος); to be in tumult, i.e. disturb, clamor
G2350.kjv: make ado (a noise), trouble self, set on an uproar

G5295.mounce: to run under; as a nautical term, to sail under the lee of, Acts 27:16*
G5295.abbottSmith: to run in under ; of navigators, to run in the lee of : c. acc. (v. M, Pr. , 65), Ac 27:16 .†
G5295.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to run under, i.e. (specially), to sail past

G5029.mounce: pr. quickly, soon; perhaps, possibly, Rom. 5:7; Phlm. 15*
G5029.strongs: as if neuter plural of G5036 (ταχύς) (adverbially); shortly, i.e. (figuratively) possibly

G1243.mounce: a division; a distinction, difference, diversity, 1 Cor. 12:4, 5, 6*
G1243.strongs: from G1244 (διαιρέω); a distinction or (concretely) variety

G1985.thayer: (used as an official title in civil life), overseer, supervisor, ruler, especially used with reference to the supervising function exercised by an elder or presbyter of a church or congregation.
G1985.mounce: pr. an inspector, overseer; a watcher, guardian, 1 Pet. 2:25; in NT an ecclesiastical overseer, Acts 20:28; Phil. 1:1; 1 Tim. 3:2; Tit. 1:7*
G1985.abbottSmith: a superintendent, guardian, overseer (cl.; for exx. v. LS, s.v.): Ac 20:28 , I Pe 2:25 ( ICC , in l.); as technical term for a religious office (Deiss., BS , 230 f.), in later Paul. epp. of the head of a church (Vg. episcopus ), a bishop : Phl 1:1 , I Ti 3:2 , Tit 1:7 (v. reff. s.v. ἐπισκοπή ).†
G1985.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4649 (σκοπός) (in the sense of G1983 (ἐπισκοπέω)); a superintendent, i.e. Christian officer in genitive case charge of a (or the) church (literally or figuratively)

G2835.thayer: a "quadrans," the smallest Roman copper coin, a quarter of an "as," the sixteenth part of a "sesterius".
G2835.mounce: a Roman brass coin, equivalent to the fourth part of an as, or ἀσσάριον, or to δύο λεπτά, Mt. 5:26; Mk. 12:42*
G2835.strongs: of Latin origin; a quadrans, i.e. the fourth part of an as

G4314.mounce: from; met. for the benefit of, Acts 27:34; with a dative, near, by, at, by the side of, in the vicinity of, Mk. 5:11; Lk. 19:37; with an accusative, used of the place to which anything tends, to, unto, towards, Mt. 2:12; 3:5, 13; at, close upon, Mt. 3:10; Mk. 5:22; near to, in the vicinity of, Mk. 6:45; after verbs of speaking, praying, answering to a charge, etc., to, Mt. 3:15; 27:14; of place where, with, in, among, by, at, etc., Mt. 26:55; Mk. 11:4; Lk. 1:80; of time, for, during, Lk. 8:13; 1 Cor. 7:5; near, towards, Lk. 24:29; of the end, object, purpose for which an action is exerted, or to which any quality, etc., has reference, to, Jn. 4:35; Acts 3:10; 27:12; before an infin. with τό, in order to, that, in order that, Mt. 6:1; 13:30; 26:12; so as to, so that, Mt. 5:28; of the relation which any action, state, quality, etc., bears to any person or thing, in relation to, of, concerning, in respect to, with reference to, Mt. 19:8; Lk. 12:41; 18:1; 20:19; as it respects, as it concerns, with relation to, Mt. 27:4; Jn. 21:22, 23; according to, in conformity with, Lk. 12:47; 2 Cor. 5:10; in comparison with, Rom. 8:18; in attention to, Eph. 3:4; of the actions, dispositions, etc., exhibited with respect to any one, whether friendly, towards, Gal. 6:10; Eph. 6:9; or unfriendly, with, against, Lk. 23:12; Acts 23:30; after verbs signifying to converse, dispute, make a covenant, etc., with, Lk. 24:14; Acts 2:7; 3:25
G4314.abbottSmith: C. gen., of motion from a place, from the side of , hence metaph., in the interests of , Ac 27:34 (cf. Page, in l.).
G4314.strongs: a strengthened form of G4253 (πρό); a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. toward (with the genitive case, the side of, i.e. pertaining to; with the dative case, by the side of, i.e. near to; usually with the accusative case, the place, time, occasion, or respect, which is the destination of the relation, i.e. whither or for which it is predicated)
G4314.kjv: about, according to , against, among, at, because of, before, between, (where-)by, for, X at thy house, in, for intent, nigh unto, of, which pertain to, that, to (the end that), X together, to (you) -ward, unto, with(-in)

G1597.abbottSmith: for a long time, from of old : II Pe 2:3 3:5 .†
G1597.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3819 (πάλαι); long ago, for a long while
G1597.kjv: of a long time, of old

G3111.mounce: meat market, marketplace, slaughter house, 1 Cor. 10:25*
G3111.abbottSmith: a meat-market : I Co 10:25 (v. Deiss., LAE , 274; MM, xvi).†
G3111.strongs: of Latin origin (macellum); a butcher's stall, meat market or provision-shop

G703.thayer: goodness, a gracious act, virtue, uprightness.
G703.mounce: goodness, good quality of any kind; a gracious act of God, 1 Pet. 2:9; 2 Pet. 1:3; virtue, uprightness, Phil. 4:8; 2 Pet. 1:5*
G703.abbottSmith: prop., whatever procures pre-eminent estimation for a person or thing, in Hom. any kind of conspicuous advantage. Later confined by philos. writers to intrinsic eminence— moral goodness , virtue ; of God: II Pe 1:3 ; of men: Phl 4:8 , II Pe 1:5 ; pl. ( Is 42:8 , 12 43:21 63:7 , Es 14:10 ), excellencies : I Pe 2:9 (the usage appears to be a survival of an early comprehensive sense in which the orig
G703.strongs: from the same as G730 (ἄῤῥην); properly, manliness (valor), i.e. excellence (intrinsic or attributed)

G2584.mounce: indecl. pr. name, Capernaum. See PhotoGuide.
G2584.strongs: of Hebrew origin (probably H03723 and H05151); Capernaum (i.e. Caphanachum), a place in Palestine

G4167.mounce: a flock of sheep, Lk. 2:8; 1 Cor. 9:7; meton. a flock of disciples, Mt. 26:31; Jn. 10:16*
G4167.abbottSmith: a flock , prop., of sheep: Mt 26:31 , Lk 2:8 , I Co 9:7 . Metaph., of Christ's followers, Jo 10:16 .†
G4167.strongs: contraction from G4165 (ποιμαίνω); a flock (literally or figuratively)

G1280.thayer: I am in trouble, doubt, difficulty; I am at a loss.
G1280.mounce: to be utterly at a loss; to be in doubt and perplexity, Lk. 9:7; Acts 2:12; 5:24; 10:17
G1280.abbottSmith: to be quite at a loss, be in great perplexity : absol., Ac 2:12 ; seq. διὰ τό , c. inf., Lk 9:7 ; περί , Ac 5:24 ; ἐν ἑαυτῷ , Ac 10:17 .†
G1280.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G639 (ἀπορέω); to be thoroughly nonplussed

G4599.abbottSmith: to strengthen : c. acc., I Pe 5:10 (Hesych.).†
G4599.strongs: from (bodily vigor; probably akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι)); to strengthen, i.e. (figuratively) confirm (in spiritual knowledge and power)

G2154.thayer: with clear meaning, intelligible, clear to the understanding.
G2154.mounce: pr. well marked, strongly marked; met. significant, intelligible, 1 Cor. 14:9*
G2154.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); well indicated, i.e. (figuratively) significant

G3844.thayer: gen: from; dat: beside, in the presence of; acc: alongside of.
G3844.mounce: (1) gen., from, indicating source or origin, Mt. 2:4, 7; Mk. 8:11; Lk. 2:1; οἱ παρ’ αὐτοῦ, his relatives or kinsmen, Mk. 3:21; τὰ παρ’ αὐτῆς πάντα, all her substance, property, etc., Mk. 5:26. (2) dat., with, in, among, etc., Mt. 6:1; 19:26; 21:25; 22:25; παρ’ ἑαυτῷ, at home, 1 Cor. 16:2; in the sight of, in the judgment or estimation of, 1 Cor. 3:19; 2 Pet. 2:11; 3:8. (3) acc., motion, by, near to, along, Mt. 4:18; motion, towards, to, at, Mt. 15:30; Mk. 2:13; motion terminating in rest, at, by, near, by the side of, Mk. 4:1, 4; Lk. 5:1; 8:5; in deviation from, in violation of, inconsistently with, Acts 18:13; Rom. 1:26; 11:24; above, more than, Lk. 13:2, 4; Rom. 1:25. (4) Misc., after comparatives, Lk. 3:13; 1 Cor. 3:11; except, save, 2 Cor. 11:24; beyond, past, Heb. 11:11; in respect of, on the score of, 1 Cor. 12:15, 16
G3844.abbottSmith: with radical sense, beside . C. gen. pers., from the side of , from beside , from , indicating source or origin, [in LXX for מֵאֵצֶל , מִיַד , מִלִּפְנֵי ;] after verbs of motion, Mk 14:43 , Lk 8:49 , Jo 15:26 , al.; after verbs of seeking, receiving, hearing, etc., Mk 8:11 12:2 , Jo 4:9 , 52 10:18 , Phl 4:18 , II Ti 1:18 , Ja 1:7 , al.; after passive verbs, of the agent (like ὑπό ), Mt 21:42 , Mk
G3844.strongs: a primary preposition; properly, near; i.e. (with genitive case) from beside (literally or figuratively), (with dative case) at (or in) the vicinity of (objectively or subjectively), (with accusative case) to the proximity with (local (especially beyond or opposed to) or causal (on account of)
G3844.kjv: above, against, among, at, before, by, contrary to, X friend, from, + give (such things as they), + that (she) had, X his, in, more than, nigh unto, (out) of, past, save, side…by, in the sight of, than, (there-)fore, with

G4943.thayer: I help together, join in serving.
G4943.mounce: to aid along with another, help together, 2 Cor. 1:11*
G4943.abbottSmith: to help together, join in serving : c. dat. instr., II Co 1:11 .†
G4943.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); to be a co-auxiliary, i.e. assist

G5051.mounce: a finisher, one who completes and perfects a thing; one who brings through to final attainment, perfecter, Heb. 12:2*
G5051.strongs: from G5048 (τελειόω); a completer, i.e. consummater

G748.thayer: high priestly, to which the chief priest belongs.
G748.mounce: belonging to or connected with the high-priest or his office, Acts 4:6*
G748.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and a derivative of G2413 (ἱερός); high-priestly

G454.mounce: want of understanding; folly, rashness, madness, Lk. 6:11; 2 Tim. 3:9*
G454.abbottSmith: folly , foolishness : II Ti 3:9 ; expressed in violent rage (cf. Plat., Tim. , 86B): Lk 6:11 .†
G454.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3563 (νοῦς); stupidity; by implication, rage

G2202.strongs: feminine of a derivative (at the second stage) from the same as G2218 (ζυγός); a fastening (tiller-rope)

G3054.mounce: to contend about words; by impl. to dispute about trivial things, 2 Tim. 2:14*
G3054.abbottSmith: to strive with words : II Ti 2:14 .†
G3054.strongs: from a compound of G3056 (λόγος) and G3164 (μάχομαι); to be disputatious (on trifles)

G4628.abbottSmith: the leg from the hip downwards: Jo 19:31-33 †
G4628.strongs: apparently from (to parch; through the idea of leanness); the leg (as lank)

G1161.thayer: a weak adversative particle, generally placed second in its clause; but, on the other hand, and.
G1161.mounce: a conjunctive particle, marking the superaddition of a clause, whether in opposition or in continuation, to what has preceded, and it may be variously rendered but, on the other hand, and, also, now, etc.; καὶ δέ, when there is a special superaddition in continuation, too, yea, etc. It sometimes is found at the commencement of the apodosis of a sentence, Acts 11:17. It serves also to mark the resumption of an interrupted discourse, 2 Cor. 2:10; Gal. 2:6
G1161.abbottSmith: copulative, but, in the next place, and, now (Abbott, JG , 104): Mt 1:2 ff., II Co 6:15 , 16 , II Pe 1:5-7 ; in repetition for emphasis, Ro 3:21 , 22 , 9:30 , I Co 2:6 , Ga 2:2 , Phl 2:8 ; in transition to something new, Mt 1:18 2:19 , Lk 13:1 , Jo 7:14 , Ac 6:1 , Ro 8:28 , I Co 7:1 8:1 , al.; in explanatory parenthesis or addition, Jo 3:19 , Ro 5:8 , I Co 1:12 , Eph 2:4 5:32 , al.; ὡς δέ , Jo 2:9
G1161.strongs: a primary particle (adversative or continuative); but, and, etc.
G1161.kjv: also, and, but, moreover, now (often unexpressed in English)

G1981.thayer: I raise a tent (over), dwell, spread a tabernacle over.
G1981.mounce: to quarter in or at; met. to abide upon, 2 Cor. 12:9*
G1981.abbottSmith: to tent upon, spread a tabernacle over : metaph., seq. ἐπ’ ἐμέ (RV, rest upon, cover ), II Co 12:9 .†
G1981.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4637 (σκηνόω); to tent upon, i.e. (figuratively) abide with

G940.thayer: I give the evil eye to, fascinate, bewitch, overpower.
G940.mounce: pr. to slander; thence, to bewitch by spells, or by any other means; to delude, Gal. 3:1*
G940.abbottSmith: to slander (Dem.). to blight by the evil eye , to fascinate , bewitch : Ga 3:1 .†
G940.strongs: akin to G5335 (φάσκω); to malign, i.e. (by extension) to fascinate (by false representations)

G2680.thayer: I build, construct, prepare, make ready.
G2680.mounce: to prepare, put in readiness, Mt. 11:10; Mk. 1:2; Lk. 1:17; 7:27; to construct, form, build, Heb. 3:3, 4; 9:2, 6; 11:7; 1 Pet. 3:20*
G2680.abbottSmith: to prepare , make ready : τ. ὁδόν , Mt 11:10 , Mk 1:2 , Lk 7:27 ; οἶκον , He 3:3 , 4 ; σκηνήν , He 9:2 , 6 ; κιβωτόν , He 11:7 , I Pe 3:20 ; pass. pf. ptcp., λαόν κ. , Lk 1:17 .†
G2680.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G4632 (σκεῦος); to prepare thoroughly (properly, by external equipment; whereas G2090 (ἑτοιμάζω) refers rather to internal fitness); by implication, to construct, create

G1826.thayer: (originally: I shall go out), I go out (away), depart.
G1826.mounce: to go out or forth, Acts 13:42; to depart, Acts 17:15; 20:7; ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν, to get to land, from the water, Acts 27:43
G1826.abbottSmith: to go forth : Ac 13:42 17:15 20:7 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc, Ac 27:43 .†
G1826.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and (to go); to issue, i.e. leave (a place), escape (to the shore)

G219.thayer: the cockcrow, as a period of time, between midnight and 3 a.m.
G219.mounce: the cock-crowing, the third watch of the night, intermediate to mid-night and daybreak, and termed cock-crow, Mk. 13:35*
G219.abbottSmith: cock-crowing , i.e. the third watch in the night: Mk 13:35 .†
G219.strongs: from G220 (ἀλέκτωρ) and G5456 (φωνή); cock-crow, i.e. the third night-watch

G406.thayer: Andrew, brother of Simon Peter, and one of the apostles of Jesus, belonging to Bethsaida.
G406.strongs: from G435 (ἀνήρ); manly; Andreas, an Israelite

G874.thayer: (a) a starting, a start, (b) cause, occasion, opportunity.
G874.mounce: pr. a starting point; means to accomplish an object; occasion, opportunity, Rom. 7:8, 11
G874.abbottSmith: prop., a starting point ; in war, a base of operations ; metaph., an occasion , incentive , opportunity (MM, s.v.): II Co 11:12 , Ga 5:13 ; ἀφ. λαμβάνειν , Lk 11:24 , WH, mg., Ro 7:8 , 11 ; ἀφ. διδόναι , II Co 5:12 , I Ti 5:14 .†
G874.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G3729 (ὁρμάω); a starting-point, i.e. (figuratively) an opportunity

G3333.thayer: mid: I summon to myself, send for.
G3333.mounce: to call from one place into another; mid. to call or send for, invite to come to oneself, Acts 7:14; 10:32; 20:17; 24:25*
G3333.abbottSmith: to call from one place to another . Mid., to send for : c. acc., Ac 7:14 10:32 20:17 24:25 .†
G3333.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G2564 (καλέω); to call elsewhere, i.e. summon

G4101.abbottSmith: having the gift of persuasion (Plat., Gorg. , 455 A). of persons, faithful , trusty (Plut.); of things, trustworthy , genuine : νάρδος π. , Mk 14:3 , Jo 12:3 .†

G368.thayer: indisputable, undeniable, not to be contradicted.
G368.mounce: not to be contradicted, indisputable, Acts 19:36*
G368.abbottSmith: not to be contradicted , undeniable : Ac 19:36 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G368.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G4483 (ῥέω); indisputable

G5501.mounce: worse, Mt. 9:16; more severe, Jn. 5:14; Heb. 10:29
G5501.abbottSmith: worse : Mt 9:16 12:45 27:64 , Mk 2:21 , Lk 11:26 , Jo 5:14 , I Ti 5:8 , He 10:29 , II Pe 2:20 ; εἰς ( ἐπὶ ) τὸ χεῖρον , Mk 5:26 , II Ti 3:13 .†
G5501.strongs: irregular comparative of G2556 (κακός); from an obsolete equivalent (of uncertain derivation); more evil or aggravated (physically, mentally or morally)

G5352.thayer: autumnal, in autumn, when fruit is expected.
G5352.abbottSmith: autumnal : δένδρα φ. , autumn trees (said to be without fruit therefore at a time when fruit might be expected; v. Mayor's elaborate note, Ep. Ju. , 55-59, and reff. there): Ju 12 .†
G5352.strongs: from derivative of (to wane; akin to the base of G5351 (φθείρω)) and G3703 (ὀπώρα) (meaning late autumn); autumnal (as stripped of leaves)

G2401.thayer: Idumea, Edom, a district of Arabia, immediately south of Judea.
G2401.mounce: Idumaea, a country south of Judea, Mk. 3:8*
G2401.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0123); Idumæa (i.e. Edom), a region East (and South) of Palestine

G3639.mounce: littleness or imperfectness of faith, Mt. 17:20*
G3639.abbottSmith: destructive , deadly : δίκην , II Th 1:9 , L, txt. (for ὄλεθρος , q.v.).†
G3639.strongs: from a primary (to destroy; a prolonged form); ruin, i.e. death, punishment

G660.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to jostle); to brush off

G4906.mounce: to eat with, Acts 10:41; 11:3; 1 Cor. 5:11; by impl. to associate with, live on familiar terms with, Lk. 15:2; Gal. 2:12*
G4906.abbottSmith: to eat with one: c. dat. pers., Lk 15:2 , Ac 10:41 11:3 , I Co 5:11 ; seq. μετά , c. gen. pers., Ga 2:12 .†
G4906.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2068 (ἐσθίω) (including its alternate); to take food in company with

G2341.thayer: I fight with wild beasts (i.e. wild beasts in human form); met: I am exposed to fierce hostility.
G2341.mounce: to fight with wild beasts; met. to be exposed to furious hostility, 1 Cor. 15:32*
G2341.abbottSmith: to fight with wild beasts (Diod., al.): I Co 15:32 .†
G2341.strongs: from a compound of G2342 (θηρίον) and G3164 (μάχομαι); to be a beast-fighter (in the gladiatorial show), i.e. (figuratively) to encounter (furious men)

G2599.mounce: to weigh down, depress, pass., be heavy, Mk. 14:40*
G2599.abbottSmith: to weigh down : metaph., c. acc. pers., II Co 12:16 .†
G2599.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G916 (βαρέω); to impose upon

G2232.thayer: a leader, guide; a commander; a governor (of a province); plur: leaders.
G2232.mounce: a guide; a leader; a chieftain, prince, Mt. 2:6; a Roman provincial governor, under whatever title, Mt. 10:18; 27:2; Lk. 20:20; Acts 23:24
G2232.strongs: from G2233 (ἡγέομαι); a leader, i.e. chief person (or figuratively, place) of a province

G5521.mounce: the bile, gall; in NT a bitter ingredient, as wormwood, Mt. 27:34; χολὴ πικρίας, intense bitterness, met. thorough disaffection to divine truth, utter estrangement, Acts 8:23*
G5521.abbottSmith: gall : Mt 27:34 (here prob. = myrrh , cf. Mk 15:23 , v. Swete, in l.; DCG , i, 634 a ); metaph., Ac 8:23 .†
G5521.strongs: feminine of an equivalent perhaps akin to the same as 5514 (from the greenish hue); "gall" or bile, i.e. (by analogy) poison or an anodyne (wormwood, poppy, etc.)

G232.strongs: from G231 (ἁλιεύς); to be a fisher, i.e. (by implication) to fish

G174.mounce: thorny, made of thorns, Mk. 15:17; Jn. 19:5*

G2505.mounce: can function as an adverb, just as, Mt. 27:10*
G2505.abbottSmith: just as , according as : Mt 27:10 (LXX) , Lk 1:2 , D (cf. Bl., § 78, 1; Phil. Gosp. , 8 f.).†
G2505.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the neuter plural of G3739 (ὅς); according to which things, i.e. just as

G1137.thayer: a corner; met: a secret place.
G1137.mounce: an exterior angle, projecting corner, Mt. 6:5; 21:42; an interior angle; by impl. a dark corner, obscure place, Acts 26:26; corner, extremity, or quarter of the earth, Rev. 7:1; 20:8
G1137.abbottSmith: an angle, a corner : Mt 6:5 , Ac 26:26 ; τ. τέσσαρας γ. τ. γῆς , Re 7:1 , 20:8 ; κεφαλὴ γνωνίας ( רֹאשׁ פִּנָּה , Ps 117(118):22 , LXX), Mt 21:42 , Mk 12:10 , Lk 20:17 , Ac 4:11 , I Pe 2:7 .†
G1137.strongs: probably akin to G1119 (γόνυ); an angle

G1465.mounce: pr. to cut or strike in; hence, to impede, interrupt, hinder, Rom. 15:22; 1 Thess. 2:18; 1 Pet. 3:7; Gal. 5:7; Acts 24:4*
G1465.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2875 (κόπτω); to cut into, i.e. (figuratively) impede, detain

G4791.mounce: one who partakes jointly; a coparticipant, Rom. 11:17; a copartner in service, fellow, 1 Cor. 9:23; Phil. 1:7; a sharer, 1 Cor. 9:23; Rev. 1:9*
G4791.abbottSmith: partaking jointly of : c. gen. rei, Ro 11:17 , I Co 9:23 ; id. c. gen. pers., Phl 1:7 ; seq. ἐν , Re 1:9 .†
G4791.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2844 (κοινωνός); a co-participant

G4283.thayer: I preach the gospel beforehand, foretell good tidings.
G4283.mounce: to announce joyful tidings beforehand, Gal. 3:8*
G4283.abbottSmith: to announce glad tidings beforehand : Ga 3:8 .†
G4283.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and G2097 (εὐαγγελίζω); to announce glad news in advance

G5000.thayer: antelope; Tabitha, a Christian woman at Joppa.
G5000.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H06646); the gazelle; Tabitha (i.e. Tabjetha), a Christian female

G2335.mounce: a beholding; a sight, spectacle, Lk. 23:48*
G2335.strongs: from the same as G2334 (θεωρέω); spectatorship, i.e. (concretely) a spectacle

G1796.thayer: I insult, outrage, treat contemptuously, mock at.
G1796.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5195 (ὑβρίζω); to insult

G1754.thayer: I work, am operative, am at work, am made to work, accomplish; mid: I work, display activity.
G1754.mounce: to effect, 1 Cor. 12:6, 11; Gal. 3:5; Eph. 1:11; Phil. 2:13; to put into operation, Eph. 1:20; absol. to be active, Mt. 14:2; Mk. 6:14; Eph. 2:2; in NT to communicate energy and efficiency, Gal. 2:8; pass. or mid. to come into activity, be actively developed; to be active, be in operation, towards a result, 2 Cor. 4:12; 2 Thess. 2:7; to be an active power or principle, Rom. 7:5; 1 Thess. 2:12; instinct with activity; in action, operative, 2 Cor. 1:6; Gal. 5:6; Eph. 3:20; Col. 1:29; earnest, Jas. 5:16
G1754.abbottSmith: intrans., to be at work or in action, to operate (opp. to ἀργέω ): seq. ἐν , c. dat. pers., Mt 14:2 , Mk 6:14 , Eph 2:2 ; c. dat. pers., seq. εἰς (Lft., in l), Ga 2:8 .
G1754.strongs: from G1756 (ἐνεργής); to be active, efficient
G1754.kjv: do, (be) effectual (fervent), be mighty in, shew forth self, work (effectually in)

G2667.mounce: to fall down, fall prostrate, Lk. 8:6; Acts 26:14; 28:6*
G2667.abbottSmith: to fall down : Ac 28:6 ; seq. εἰς , Ac 26:14 ; ἐπί c. acc., Lk 8:6 .†
G2667.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4098 (πίπτω); to fall down

G207.thayer: the Greek way of writing the Latin Aquila, a male proper name; the husband of Priscilla (Prisca), and a Jew, of a family belonging to (Sinope in ?) Pontus.
G207.mounce: Aquila, pr. name, Paul’s friend and Priscilla’s husband, Acts 18:2, 18, 26; Rom. 16:3; 1 Cor. 16:19; 2 Tim. 4:19*
G207.strongs: probably for Latin aquila (an eagle); Akulas, an Israelite

G4667.thayer: Smyrna, a great port of the Roman province Asia.
G4667.mounce: Smyrna, a maritime city of Ionia, in Asia Minor, Rev. 1:11; 2:8*. See PhotoGuide.
G4667.abbottSmith: Smyrna , an Ionian city on the Ægean: Re 1:11 2:8 .†
G4667.strongs: the same as G4666 (σμύρνα); Smyrna, a place in Asia Minor

G3703.mounce: autumn; the fruit season; meton. fruits, Rev. 18:14*
G3703.abbottSmith: late summer , early autumn (the time between the risings of Sirius and Arcturus, i.e. late July, all August and early September).
G3703.strongs: apparently from the base of G3796 (ὀψέ) and G5610 (ὥρα); properly, even-tide of the (summer) season (dog-days), i.e. (by implication) ripe fruit

G619.thayer: the faculty or experience of enjoyment.
G619.mounce: beneficial participation, 1 Tim. 6:17; enjoyment, pleasure, Heb. 11:25*
G619.abbottSmith: enjoyment : I Ti 6:17 , He 11:25 (for late exx., v. MM, s.v.).†
G619.strongs: from a comparative of G575 (ἀπό) and (to enjoy); full enjoyment

G4099.thayer: Pisidia, a country of Asia Minor, being the south-western part of the Roman province Galatia.
G4099.mounce: Pisidia, a country of Asia Minor, Acts 14:24*
G4099.abbottSmith: Pisidia , a region of Asia Minor: Ac 14:24 .†
G4099.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Pisidia, a region of Asia Minor

G3196.mounce: a member, limb, any part of the body, Mt. 5:29, 30; Rom. 12:4; 1 Cor. 6:15; 12:12
G3196.abbottSmith: a member , limb of the body: I Co 12:14 , 19 , 26 , Eph 4:16 (WH, mg.), Ja 3:5 ; pl. (as always in cl.), τὰ μ. : Mt 5:29 , 30 , Ro 6:13 , 19 7:5 , 23 12:4 , I Co 12:12 ff. Col 3:5 , Ja 3:6 4:1 . Metaph., πόρνης , I Co 6:15 ; of Christians, μ. ἀλλήλων , Ro 12:5 , Eph 4:25 ; Χριστοῦ , I Co 6:15 ; σώματος Χριστοῦ , I Co 12:27 , Eph 5:30 .†
G3196.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a limb or part of the body

G3931.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and a derivative of G58 (ἀγορά) (meaning to harangue an assembly); an address alongside, i.e. (specially), consolation

G491.thayer: an Antiochian, an inhabitant of Antioch.
G491.abbottSmith: a citizen of Antioch , an Antiochian : Ac 6:5 .†
G491.strongs: from G490 (Ἀντιόχεια); an Antiochian or inhabitant of Antiochia

G1410.thayer: (a) I am powerful, have (the) power, (b) I am able, I can.
G1410.mounce: to be able, either intrinsically and absolutely, which is the ordinary signification; or, for specific reasons, Mt. 9:15; Lk. 16:2 See ability; (be) able; can.
G1410.abbottSmith: to be able, have power , whether by personal ability, permission, or opportunity: c. inf. (M, Pr. , 205; WM, § 44, 3) pres., Mt 6:24 , Mk 2:7 , Jo 3:2 , I Co 10:21 , al.; c. inf. aor., Mt 3:9 , Mk 1:45 , Jo 3:3 , 4 Ro 8:39 , al.; c. acc., to be able to do something: Mk 9:22 , Lk 12:26 ; II Co 13:8 ; absol., to be able, capable, powerful : I Co 3:2 10:13 .
G1410.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to be able or possible
G1410.kjv: be able, can (do, + -not), could, may, might, be possible, be of power

G1714.thayer: I burn, set on fire, suffer inflammation.
G1714.mounce: also spelled ἐμπίπρημι and ἐμπρήθω, to set on fire, burn down, Mt. 22:7*
G1714.abbottSmith: to set on fire : πόλιν , Mt 22:7 ; pass., of the body, to become inflamed : Ac 28:6 (T; πίμπρημι WH, q.v.).†
G1714.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (to blow a flame); to enkindle, i.e. set on fire

G5023.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5023.strongs: nominative or accusative case neuter plural of G3778 (οὗτος); these things
G5023.kjv: + afterward, follow, + hereafter, X him, the same, so, such, that, then, these, they, this, those, thus

G1746.mounce: to enter, 2 Tim. 3:6; to put on, clothe, invest, array, Mt. 27:31; Mk. 15:17, 20; mid. clothe one’s self, be clothed, Mt. 22:11, 27, 31; trop. to be clothed with spiritual gifts, graces, or character, Lk. 24:49; Rom. 13:14
G1746.abbottSmith: c. acc pers., Mt 27:28 (WH, mg., R, mg.); c. dupl. acc, Mt 27:31 , Mk 15:20 , Lk 15:22 ; mid., to put on oneself, be clothed with : c. acc rei, Mt 6:25 , Mk 6:9 , Lk 8:27 12:22 , Ac 12:21 ; ptcp., Mt 22:11 , Mk 1:6 , II Co 5:3 , Re 1:13 15:6 19:14 ; of armour (fig.): Ro 13:12 , Eph 6:11 , 14 , I Th 5:8 ; metaph., δύναμιν , Lk 24:49 ; ἀφθαρσίαν, ἀθανασίαν , I Co 15:53-54 ; τ. καινὸν ἄνθρωπον , Eph
G1746.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1416 (δύνω) (in the sense of sinking into a garment); to invest with clothing (literally or figuratively)

G2139.abbottSmith: of sins, readily besetting : He 12:1 (on form and sense of the word, v. Westc., in l.).†
G2139.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of a presumed compound of G4012 (περί) and G2476 (ἵστημι); well standing around, i.e. (a competitor) thwarting (a racer) in every direction (figuratively, of sin in genitive case)

G2671.thayer: cursing; a curse; meton: a doomed one.
G2671.mounce: a cursing, execration, imprecation, Jas. 3:10; from the Hebrew, condemnation, doom, Gal. 3:10; Heb. 6:8; 2 Pet. 2:14; meton., a doomed one, one on whom condemnation falls, Gal. 3:13*
G2671.abbottSmith: a curse : Ga 3:10 , 13 , He 6:8 , II Pe 2:14 ; opp. to εὐλογία , Ja 3:10 ; concrete, of Christ, Ga 3:13 (v. Lft. in l.).†
G2671.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) (intensive) and G685 (ἀρά); imprecation, execration

G2418.mounce: to officiate as priest, perform sacred rites; in NT to minister in a divine commission, Rom. 15:16*
G2418.abbottSmith: to perform sacred rites ; c. acc., to minister in priestly service ( minister in sacrifice , R, mg.), τὸ εὐαγγέλιον , Ro 15:16 .†
G2418.strongs: from a compound of G2411 (ἱερόν) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); to be a temple-worker, i.e. officiate as a priest (figuratively)

G3497.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05283); Neeman (i.e. Naaman), a Syrian

G4872.mounce: to go up, ascend with someone, Mk. 15:41; Acts 13:31*
G4872.abbottSmith: to go up with : c. dat. pers., seq. εἰς , c. acc. loc., Mk 15:41 , Ac 13:31 .†
G4872.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G305 (ἀναβαίνω); to ascend in company with

G3011.thayer: a minister, servant, of an official character; of priests and Levites.
G3011.mounce: Aramaic for Why? Wherefore? Mt. 27:46; Mk. 15:34*
G3011.abbottSmith: in cl., one who discharges a public office at his own expense , then, generally,
G3011.strongs: from a derivative of G2992 (λαός) and G2041 (ἔργον); a public servant, i.e. a functionary in the Temple or Gospel, or (genitive case) a worshipper (of God) or benefactor (of man)

G3598.mounce: a way, road, Mt. 2:12; 7:13, 14; 8:28; 22:9, 10; means of access, approach, entrance, Jn. 14:6; Heb. 9:8; direction, quarter, region, Mt. 4:15; 10:5 the act of journeying, a journey, way, course, Mt. 10:10; Mk. 2:23; 1 Thess. 3:11; a journey, as regards extent, Acts 1:12; met. a way, systematic course of pursuit, Lk. 1:79; Acts 2:28; 16:17; a way, systematic course of action or conduct, Mt. 21:32; Rom. 11:33; 1 Cor. 4:17; a way, system of doctrine, Acts 18:26; ἡ ὁδός, the way of the Christian faith, Acts 19:9, 23, 24:22
G3598.abbottSmith: a way , path , road : Mt 2:12 , Mk 10:46 , Lk 3:5 , al.; κατὰ τὴν ὁ. , Lk 10:4 , al.; παρὰ τὴν ὁ. , Mt 13:4 , al.; c. gen. pers, (subj.), τὴν ὁ. ἑτοιμάζειν (fig.), Mt 3:3 , Mk 1:3 , al.; c. gen. term. (obj.), ἐθνῶν , Mt 10:5 ; τ. ἁγίων (fig.), He 9:8 ; acc., ὁδόν , with force of prep. (like Heb. דֶּרֶךְ ; Bl., § 34, 8; 35, 5), ὁ. θαλάσσης , Mt 4:15 (LXX) . A traveller's way , journey : ἐν τ. ὁ. ,
G3598.strongs: apparently a primary word; a road; by implication, a progress (the route, act or distance); figuratively, a mode or means

G451.thayer: Anna, a prophetess, who visited the infant Jesus.
G451.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02584); Anna, an Israelitess

G3998.abbottSmith: needy , poor : Lk 21:2 (for ex. in π. , v. MM, xix).†
G3998.strongs: prolongation from the base of G3993 (πένης); necessitous

G1836.thayer: next in order, the next day, the following day, at the period immediately following.
G1836.mounce: successively, in order; in NT with the article ὁ, ἡ, τό, ἑχῆς, next, Lk. 7:11; 9:37; Acts 21:1; 25:17; 27:18*
G1836.abbottSmith: in order, successively, next : τῇ ἑ. ἡμέρᾳ , Lk 9:37 ; ἐν τῷ ἑ. (sc. χρόνῳ ), soon after , Lk 7:11 ; τῇ ἑ. (sc. ἡμέρα ), Ac 21:1 25:17 27:18 .†
G1836.strongs: from G2192 (ἔχω) (in the sense of taking hold of, i.e. adjoining); successive

G3050.thayer: (a) reasonable, rational, (b) metaphorical, as contrasted with the literal.
G3050.mounce: pertaining to speech; pertaining to reason; in NT rational, spiritual, pertaining to the mind and soul, Rom. 12:1; 1 Pet. 2:2*
G3050.abbottSmith: reasonable , rational : λατρεία , Ro 12:1 ; τὸ λ. (i.e. spiritual ) γάλα (v. Hort, in l.; MM, xvi), I Pe 2:2 (in support of AV, milk of the word , v. ICC , in l.).†

G4983.thayer: body, flesh; the body of the Church.
G4983.mounce: the body of an animal; a living body, Mt. 5:29, 30; 6:22, 23, 25; Jas. 3:3; a person, individual, 1 Cor. 6:16; a dead body; corpse, carcass, Mt. 14:12; 27:52, 58; Heb. 13:11; the human body considered as the seat and occasion of moral imperfection, as inducing to sin through its appetites and passions, Rom. 7:24; 8:13; genr. a body, a material substance, 1 Cor. 15:37, 38, 40; the substance, reality, as opposed to ἡ σκιά, Col. 2:17; in NT met., the aggregate body of believers, the body of the Church, Rom. 12:5; Col. 1:18
G4983.abbottSmith: a body . Prop., of the human body, as always in Hom. (opp. to δέμας ), of the dead body: Mt 27:58 , 59 Mk 15:43 , al.; of the living body: Lk 11:34 , I Co 6:13 , al.; ἐν σ. εἶναι , He 13:3 ; as the instrument of the soul, τὰ διὰ τοῦ σ. , II Co 5:10 ; opp. to πνεῦμα , Ro 8:10 , I Co 5:3 7:4 , Ja 2:26 ; to ψυχή , Mt 6:25 10:28 , Lk 12:22 (cf. Wi 1:4 , al.); to τὸ π. καὶ ἡ ψ. , I Th 5:23 ; σ. ψυχικόν
G4983.strongs: from G4982 (σώζω); the body (as a sound whole), used in a very wide application, literally or figuratively

G5454.mounce: a den, lair, burrow, Mt. 8:20; Lk. 9:58*
G5454.abbottSmith: a hole, den, lair : Mt 8:20 , Lk 9:58 .†
G5454.strongs: of uncertain derivative; a burrow or lurking-place

G1150.thayer: I tame, subdue, involving obedience and restraint.
G1150.mounce: also spelled δανείζω, to subdue, tame, Mk. 5:4; Jas. 3:7; met. to restrain within proper limits, Jas. 3:8*
G1150.abbottSmith: to tame, subdue : Mk 5:4 , Ja 3:7 ; metaph., τ. γλῶσσαν , Ja 3:8 (Field, Notes , 237 f.).†
G1150.strongs: a variation of an obsolete primary of the same meaning; to tame

G973.thayer: a forceful, violent man; one who is eager in pursuit.
G973.mounce: one who uses violence, or is impetuous; one who is forceful in eager pursuit, Mt. 11:12*
G973.strongs: from G971 (βιάζω); a forcer, i.e. (figuratively) energetic

G2370.strongs: from a derivative of G2380 (θύω) (in the sense of smoking); to fumigate, i.e. offer aromatic fumes

G3731.mounce: violent or impetuous motion; violence, Rev. 18:21*
G3731.strongs: from G3730 (ὁρμή); an attack, i.e. (abstractly) precipitancy

G5346.mounce: to utter; to say, speak, Mt. 8:8; 14:8; 26:34, 61; to say, allege, affirm, Rom. 3:8
G5346.abbottSmith: to declare, say : freq. in quoting the words of another, Mt 13:29 26:61 , Lk 7:40 22:58 , Jo 1:23 , al.; interjected into the recorded words (cl.), Mt 14:8 , Ac 23:35 , al.; φησί , impersonal (Bl., § 30, 4), I Co 6:16 (Lft., Notes , 217; but cf. Bl., l.c.), II Co 10:10 (WH, mg., φασίν ), He 8:5 ; joined with synon. verb (cf. LS, s.v., II, 2), ἀποκριθεὶς αὐτῷ ἔφη , Lk 23:3 ; seq. πρός , Lk 22:70 ,
G5346.strongs: properly, the same as the base of G5457 (φῶς) and G5316 (φαίνω); to show or make known one's thoughts, i.e. speak or say

G1524.thayer: I go in, enter (originally: I shall go in).
G1524.mounce: to go in, enter, Acts 3:3; 21:18, 26; Heb. 9:6*
G1524.abbottSmith: to go in, enter : seq. εἰς , Ac 3:3 21:26 , He 9:6 ; πρὸς Ἰάκωβον , Ac 21:18 .†
G1524.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and (to go); to enter

G2678.thayer: I shake (the hand) up and down, wave; intrans: I beckon for silence.
G2678.mounce: to shake down or violently, Acts 19:33; τὴν χεῖρα, or τῇ χειρι, to wave the hand, beckon; to signal silence by waving the hand, Acts 12:17; 13:16; 21:40*
G2678.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G4579 (σείω); to sway downward, i.e. make a signal

G4385.thayer: I stretch out, tie up (for scourging), extend before.
G4385.mounce: to extend before; to stretch out, Acts 22:25*
G4385.abbottSmith: to stretch out , stretch forth : of preparations for scourging (v. Field, Notes , 136 f.), Ac 22:25 .†
G4385.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and (to stretch); to protend, i.e. tie prostrate (for scourging)

G2767.thayer: I mix, mingle, pour out for drinking.
G2767.mounce: to mix, mingle, drink; to prepare for drinking, Rev. 14:10; 18:6 (2x)*
G2767.abbottSmith: to mix , mingle , chiefly of the diluting of wine: Re 18:6 ; by oxymoron , κεκεράσμενος ἄκρατος , Re 14:10 .†
G2767.strongs: prolonged form of a more primary (which is used in certain tenses); to mingle, i.e. (by implication) to pour out (for drinking)

G4607.thayer: an assassin, murderer, bandit; one of the Sicarii.
G4607.abbottSmith: a bandit, assassin, one of the Sicarii (FlJ, B.J. , ii, 17, 6, al.): Ac 21:38 .†
G4607.strongs: of Latin origin; a dagger-man or assassin; a freebooter (Jewish fanatic outlawed by the Romans)

G4105.thayer: I lead astray, deceive, cause to wander.
G4105.mounce: to lead astray, cause to wander; pass. to go astray, wander about, stray, Mt. 18:12, 13; 1 Pet. 2:25; met. to mislead, deceive, Mt. 24:4, 5, 11, 24; pass. to be deceived, err, mistake, Mt. 22:29; to seduce, delude, Jn. 7:12; pass. to be seduced or wander from the path of virtue, to sin, transgress, Tit. 3:3; Heb. 5:2; Jas. 5:19
G4105.abbottSmith: to cause to wander , lead astray . Pass., to go astray , wander : Mt 18:12 , 13 , He 11:38 , I Pe 2:25 (cf. Is 53:6 ). Metaph., to lead astray , deceive : c. acc. pers., Mt 24:4 , 5 , 11 , 24 , Mk 13:5 , 6 , Jo 7:12 , II Ti 3:13 , I Jo 1:8 2:26 3:7 , Re 2:20 12:9 13:14 19:20 20:3 , 8 , 10 ; pass., to be led astray , to err : Mt 22:29 , Mk 12:24 , 27 , Lk 21:8 , Jo 7:47 , II Ti 3:13 , Tit 3:3 , He
G4105.strongs: from G4106 (πλάνη); to (properly, cause to) roam (from safety, truth, or virtue)
G4105.kjv: go astray, deceive, err, seduce, wander, be out of the way

G4910.mounce: to feast together with, 2 Pet. 2:13; Jude 12*
G4910.abbottSmith: to entertain together . Pass., to fare sumptuously or feast together or with : Ju 12 ; c. dat. pers., II Pe 2:13 (Arist., Luc., al.).†
G4910.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a presumed compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning to be in good condition, i.e. (by implication) to fare well, or feast); to entertain sumptuously in company with, i.e. (middle voice or passive) to revel together

G3882.thayer: adjacent to the sea, on the coast.
G3882.mounce: adjacent to the sea, maritime; ἡ παράλιος, i.e. χώρα, the sea coast, Lk. 6:17*
G3882.abbottSmith: by the sea ; ἡ π. (sc. χώρα , in LXX ἡ παραλία , De 1:7 , Jos 9:1 , Jth 1:7 , al.), the sea coast : Lk 6:17 .†
G3882.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G251 (ἅλς); beside the salt (sea), i.e. maritime

G2922.thayer: criterion; a law-court; a law-case before an arbiter; a cause, controversy.
G2922.mounce: pr. a standard or means by which to judge, criterion; a court of justice, tribunal, Jas. 2:6; a cause, controversy, 1 Cor. 6:2, 4*
G2922.abbottSmith: a means of judging , test , criterion .
G2922.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G2923 (κριτής); a rule of judging ("criterion"), i.e. (by implication) a tribunal

G4245.thayer: elder, usually used as subst.; an elder, a member of the Sanhedrin, an elder of a Christian assembly.
G4245.mounce: elder, senior; older, more advanced in years, Lk. 15:25; Jn. 8:9; Acts 2:17; an elder in respect of age, person advanced in years, 1 Tim. 5:1, 2; pl. spc. ancients, ancestors, fathers, Mt. 15:2; Heb. 11:2; as an appellation of dignity, an elder, local dignitary, Lk. 7:3; an elder, member of the Jewish Sanhedrin, Mt. 16:21; 21:23; 26:3, 47, 57, 59; an elder or presbyter of the Christian church, Acts 11:30; 14:23, et al. freq.
G4245.abbottSmith: an old man . Compar., πρεσβύτερος , -α , -ον , [in LXX chiefly for זָקֵן ;] of age, elder : ὁ νἱὸς ὁ π. , Lk 15:25 ; as subst., opp. to νεανίσκοι , Ac 2:17 ; to νεώτερος , I Ti 5:1 , 2 ; of the religious leaders of the past, Mt 15:2 , Mk 7:3 , 5 , He 11:2 (= οἱ πατέρες , He 1:1 ). Of dignity, rank or office (as found in π. and Inscr. of civil and religious offices, including priesthood, in Asia Mi
G4245.strongs: comparative of (elderly); older; as noun, a senior; specially, an Israelite Sanhedrist (also figuratively, member of the celestial council) or Christian "presbyter"

G1500.thayer: without a cause, purpose; purposelessly, in vain, for nothing.
G1500.mounce: without plan or system; without cause, rashly, Col. 2:18; to no purpose, in vain, Rom. 13:4; 1 Cor. 15:2; Gal. 3:4 (2x); 4:11*
G1500.abbottSmith: without cause or reason : Mt 5:22 (E, mg.), Col 2:18 ( ICC ). vainly, fruitlessly, to no purpose : Ro 13:4 , I Co 15:2 , Ga 3:4 4:11 , Col 2:18 .†
G1500.strongs: probably from G1502 (εἴκω) (through the idea of failure); idly, i.e. without reason (or effect)

G2196.thayer: Zerah, son of Judah and Tamar.
G2196.abbottSmith: Zerah , an ancestor of Jesus: Mt 1:3 .†
G2196.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02226); Zara, (i.e. Zerach), an Israelite

G1255.thayer: I converse together, talk of; of conversation passing from mouth to mouth.
G1255.mounce: to talk with; by impl. to consult, deliberate, Lk. 6:11; to divulge, publish, spread by rumor, Lk. 1:65*
G1255.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2980 (λαλέω); to talk throughout a company, i.e. converse or (genitive case) publish

G2665.thayer: (lit: that which is spread out downwards, that which hangs down), a curtain, veil, of that which separated the Holy of Holies from the outer parts of the temple at Jerusalem, also of an outer curtain at the entrance to the Holy Place in the same temple.
G2665.abbottSmith: in NT always the inner veil or curtain of the Temple (or Tabernacle): Mt 27:51 , Mk 15:38 , Lk 23:45 , He 6:19 9:3 ; fig., ἡ σὰρξ αὐτοῦ , He 10:20 .†
G2665.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and a congener of G4072 (πέτομαι); something spread thoroughly, i.e. (specially) the door screen (to the Most Holy Place) in the Jewish Temple

G4360.thayer: I am displeased or offended with.
G4360.mounce: to be vexed or angry at, Heb. 3:10, 17*
G4360.abbottSmith: to be angry with : c. dat., He 3:10 (LXX) , 17 .†
G4360.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and a form of (to be vexed with something irksome); to feel indignant at

G2719.thayer: I eat up, eat till it is finished, devour, squander, annoy, injure.
G2719.mounce: also spelled κατέσθω, to eat up, devour, Mt. 13:4; to consume, Rev. 11:5, to expend, squander, Lk. 15:30; met. to make a prey of, plunder, Mt. 23:13; Mk. 12:40; Lk. 20:47; 2 Cor. 11:20; to annoy, injure, Gal. 5:15
G2719.abbottSmith: to eat up , devour : c. acc., of seed, Mt 13:4 , Mk 4:4 , Lk 8:5 ; τέκνον , Re 12:4 ; βιβλαρίδιον , Re 10:9 , 10 ; metaph., οἰκίας , Mt 23:13 , Mk 12:40 , Lk 20:47 ; τ. βίον , Lk 15:30 ; c. acc. pers., Jo 2:17 (LXX) , II Co 11:20 , Ga 5:15 , Re 11:5 20:9 .†
G2719.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2068 (ἐσθίω) (including its alternate); to eat down, i.e. devour (literally or figuratively)

G4800.mounce: to live with; to continue in life with someone, 2 Cor. 7:3; to coexist in life with another, Rom. 6:8; 2 Tim. 2:11*
G4800.abbottSmith: to live with : opp. to συναποθανεῖν , II Co 7:3 ; of life in union with Christ, here and hereafter, Ro 6:8 , II Ti 2:11 .†
G4800.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2198 (ζάω); to continue to live in common with, i.e. co-survive (literally or figuratively)

G1210.thayer: I bind, tie, fasten; I impel, compel; I declare to be prohibited and unlawful.
G1210.mounce: to bind, tie, Mt. 13:30; 21:2; to bind, confine, Mt. 27:2; 14:3; to impede, hinder, 2 Tim. 2:9; to bind with infirmity, Lk. 13:16; to bind by a legal or moral tie, as marriage, Rom. 7:2; 1 Cor. 7:27, 39; by impl. to impel, compel, Acts 20:22; in NT to pronounce or declare to be binding or obligatory, or, to declare to be prohibited and unlawful, Mt. 16:19; 18:18
G1210.abbottSmith: to tie, bind, fasten ; c. acc. rei, seq. εἰς δεσμάς , Mt 13:30 ; of an ass, Mt 21:2 , Mk 11:2 , 4 Lk 19:30 . c. acc. pers., of swathing a dead body, ὀθονίοις , Jo 19:40 ; δεδομένος τ. πόδας . . . κειρίαις (Bl., §34, 6; Kühner 3 , iii, 125), Jo 11:44 ; of binding with chains, ἀγγέλους , Re 9:14 ; a demoniac, πέδαις κ. ἁλύσεσι , Mk 5:3 , 4 ; captives, Mt 12:29 14:3 22:13 27:2 , Mk 3:27 6:17 15:1 , 7
G1210.strongs: a primary verb; to bind (in various applications, literally or figuratively)
G1210.kjv: bind, be in bonds, knit, tie, wind

G348.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2875 (κόπτω); to beat back, i.e. check

G2596.thayer: gen: against, down from, throughout, by; acc: over against, among, daily, day-by-day, each day, according to, by way of.
G2596.mounce: down from, Mt. 8:32; down upon, upon, Mk. 14:3; Acts 27:14; down into; κατὰ βάθους, profound, deepest, 2 Cor. 8:2; down over, throughout a space, Lk. 4:14; 23:5; concerning, in cases of pointed allegation, 1 Cor. 15:15; against, Mt. 12:30; by, in oaths, Mt. 26:63; with an acc. of place, in the quarter of, about, near, at, Lk. 10;32; Acts 2:10; throughout, Lk. 8:39; in, Rom. 16:5; among, Acts 21:21; in the presence of, Lk. 2:31; in the direction of, towards, Acts 8:26; Phil. 3:14; of time, within the range of; during, in the course of, at, about, Acts 12:1; 27:27; distributively, κατ’ οἶκον, by houses, from house to house, Acts 2:46; kata; duvo, two and two, 1 Cor. 14:27; καθ’ ἡμέραν, daily, Mt. 26:55; trop., according to, conformable to, in proportion to, Mt. 9:29; 25:15; after the fashion or likeness of. Heb. 5:6; in virtue of, Mt. 19:3; as respects, Rom. 11:3; Acts 25;14; Heb. 9:9
G2596.abbottSmith: down , downwards . C. gen. (WM, § 47, k; Bl., § 42, 2). C. gen. rei, in local sense; down , down from : Mt 8:32 , Mk 5:13 , Lk 8:33 , I Co 11:4 ; throughout (late usage; Bl., § 42, 2): κ. ὅλης κ.τ.λ. , Lk 4:14 23:5 , Ac 9:31 10:37 ; in a peculiar adjectival phrase: ἡ κ. βάθους πτωχεία , deep or extreme poverty, II Co 8:2 . C. gen. pers., usually in hostile sense; against (in cl. only after verbs o
G2596.strongs: a primary particle; (prepositionally) down (in place or time), in varied relations (according to the case (genitive, dative or accusative) with which it is joined)
G2596.kjv: about, according as (to), after, against, (when they were) X alone, among, and, X apart, (even, like) as (concerning, pertaining to touching), X aside, at, before, beyond, by, to the charge of, (charita-)bly, concerning, + covered, (dai-)ly, down, every, (+ far more) exceeding, X more excellent, for, from … to, godly, in(-asmuch, divers, every, -to, respect of), … by, after the manner of, + by any means, beyond (out of) measure, X mightily, more, X natural, of (up-)on (X part), out (of every), over against, (+ your) X own, + particularly, so, through(-oughout, -oughout every), thus, (un-)to(-gether, -ward), X uttermost, where(-by), with

G5496.mounce: to lead by the hand, Acts 9:8; 22:11*
G5496.abbottSmith: to lead by the hand : Ac 9:8 22:11 .†
G5496.strongs: from G5497 (χειραγωγός); to be a hand-leader, i.e. to guide (a blind person)

G4452.mounce: enclitic particle, in any way, by any means, Acts 27:12; Rom. 1:10
G4452.abbottSmith: yet , v.s. μή-πω , μηδέ-πω , οὔ-πω , οὐδέ-πω , πώ-ποτε .
G4452.strongs: another form of the base of G4458 (-πώς); an enclitic particle of indefiniteness; yet, even; used only in the comparative. See G3369 (μηδέπω), G3380 (μήπω), G3764 (οὐδέπω), G3768 (οὔπω), G4455 (πώποτε).

G3520.strongs: probably from the base of G3491 (ναῦς); an island

G466.thayer: I fill up in place of someone else, complete, supply.
G466.mounce: to fill up, complete, supply, Col. 1:24*
G466.abbottSmith: to fill up in turn : Col 1:24 (v. Lft., in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G466.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G378 (ἀναπληρόω); to supplement

G3925.thayer: a camp, fort, castle, barracks, army in battle array.
G3925.mounce: an insertion besides; later, a marshalling of an army; an array of battle, army, Heb. 11:34; a camp, Heb. 13:11, 13; Rev. 20:9; a standing camp, fortress, citadel, castle, Acts 21:34, 37; 22:24; 23:10, 16, 32*
G3925.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G1685 (ἐμβάλλω); a throwing in beside (juxtaposition), i.e. (specially), battle-array, encampment or barracks (tower Antonia)

G274.abbottSmith: blamelessly (Lft., Notes , 28, 89; MM, VGT , s.v. -ος ): I Th 2:10 3:13 WH mg., 5:23.†

G4721.thayer: a flat roof of a house.
G4721.mounce: a roof, flat roof of a house, Mt. 8:8; Mk. 2:4; Lk. 7:6*
G4721.abbottSmith: a roof : Mk 2:4 ; of entering a house, εἰσέρχεσθαι ὑπὸ τὴν σ. , Mt 8:8 , Lk 7:6 (cf. MM, xxiii).†
G4721.strongs: strengthened from a primary (a "thatch" or "deck" of a building); a roof

G2828.thayer: properly: a dining couch; hence: a group of diners.
G2828.mounce: pr. a place for reclining; a tent, seat, couch; in NT a group of persons reclining at a meal. Lk. 9:14*
G2828.abbottSmith: a place for reclining ; hence, a hut ; an easy chair ; a couch .
G2828.strongs: from a derivative of G2827 (κλίνω); properly, reclination, i.e. (concretely and specially), a party at a meal

G3728.thayer: the taking of an oath, an oath.
G3728.mounce: the act of taking an oath; an oath, Heb. 7:20, 21, 28*
G3728.abbottSmith: affirmation on oath , an oath : He 7:20 , 21 , 28 .†
G3728.strongs: from a compound of G3727 (ὅρκος) and a derivative of G3660 (ὀμνύω); asseveration on oath

G746.thayer: (a) rule (kingly or magisterial), (b) plur: in a quasi-personal sense, almost: rulers, magistrates, (c) beginning.
G746.mounce: a beginning, Mt. 24:8; an extremity, corner, or, an attached cord, Acts 10:11; 11:5; first place, headship; high estate, eminence, Jude 6; authority, Lk. 20:20; an authority, magistrate, Lk. 12:11; a principality, prince, of spiritual existence, Eph. 3:10; 6:12; ἀπ’ ἀρχῆς, ἐξ ἀρχῆς, from the first, originally, Mt. 19:4, 8; Lk. 1:2; Jn. 6:64; 2 Thess. 2:13; 1 Jn. 1:1; 2:7; ἐν ἀρχῇ, κατ’ ἀρχάς, in the beginning of things, Jn. 1:1, 2; Heb. 1:10; ἐν ἀρχῇ, at the first, Acts 11:15; τὴν ἀρχήν, used adverbially, wholly, altogether, Jn. 8:25
G746.abbottSmith: beginning , origin ; absol., of the beginning of all things: of God as the Eternal, the First Cause, Re 21:6 (cf. 1:8 ); similarly, of Christ, Re 22:13 ; of Christ as the uncreated principle, the active cause of creation, Re 3:14 ; in his relation to the Church, Col 1:18 ; ἐν ἀ. , Jo 1:1 , 2 ; ἀπ᾽ ἀ. (and ἀπ᾽ ἀ. κτισεως ), Mt 19:4 , 8 24:21 , Mk 10:6 13:19 , Jo 8:44 , II Th 2:13 , II Pe 3:4 , I Jo
G746.strongs: from G756 (ἄρχομαι); (properly abstract) a commencement, or (concretely) chief (in various applications of order, time, place, or rank)
G746.kjv: beginning, corner, (at the, the) first (estate), magistrate, power, principality, principle, rule

G238.mounce: to say what is either designed or fitted to convey a meaning other than the literal one, to allegorize; ἀλληγορούμενος, adapted to another meaning, otherwise significant, Gal. 4:24*
G238.strongs: from G243 (ἄλλος) and (to harangue (compare G58 (ἀγορά))); to allegorize
G238.kjv: be an allegory (the Greek word itself)

G4324.mounce: to threaten in addition, utter additional threats, Acts 4:21*
G4324.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G546 (ἀπειλέω); to menace additionally

G643.thayer: I prepare for a journey, depart.
G643.abbottSmith: to pack and carry off ; mid., to pack and remove one's goods : Ac 21:15 , Rec. (v. ἐπισ- ).†
G643.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G4632 (σκεῦος); to pack up (one's) baggage

G1110.mounce: known, Jn. 18:15, 16, et al.; certain, incontrovertible, Acts 4:16; τὸ γνωστόν, that which is known or is cognizable, the unquestionable attributes, Rom. 1:19; subst. an acquaintance, Lk. 2:44; 23:49
G1110.abbottSmith: known : Ac 9:42 ; c. dat., Jo 18:15 , 16 (Rec.), Ac 1:19 2:14 4:10 13:38 15:18 19:17 28:22 , 28 ; γ. σημεῖον , a notable (EV) sign , one that is matter of knowledge, Ac 4:16 ; γνωστὸν , Ac 15:17 , 18 ; τὸ γνωστὸν θεοῦ , Ro 1:19 ; in pl., as subst., γνωστοί , acquaintances , Lk 2:44 23:49 ; so in sing., Jo 18:16 (WH).†

G2230.mounce: to be a guide, leader, chief; in NT to hold the office of a Roman provincial governor, Lk. 2:2; 3:1*
G2230.strongs: from G2232 (ἡγεμών); to act as ruler

G5546.mounce: a Christian, follower of Christ, Acts 11:26; 26:28; 1 Pet. 4:16*
G5546.abbottSmith: a Christian , the name first given to the disciples by pagan gentiles at Antioch: Ac 11:26 26:28 , I Pe 4:16 (v. reff. in Thayer, s.v.; also DB , i, 384).†
G5546.strongs: from G5547 (Χριστός); a Christian, i.e. follower of Christ

G5473.mounce: a vessel, copper, brazen utensil, Mk. 7:4*
G5473.strongs: diminutive from G5475 (χαλκός); a copper dish

G4111.thayer: I form, mould, as a potter his clay.
G4111.mounce: to form, fashion, mould, Rom. 9:20; 1 Tim. 2:13*
G4111.abbottSmith: to form , mould : Ro 9:20 (LXX) , I Ti 2:13 .†
G4111.strongs: a primary verb; to mould, i.e. shape or fabricate

G5125.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5125.strongs: dative case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); to (for, in, with or by) these (persons or things)
G5125.kjv: such, them, there(-in, -with), these, this, those

G249.thayer: without reason, irrational; contrary to reason, absurd.
G249.mounce: without speech or reason, irrational, brute, 2 Pet. 2:12; Jude 10; unreasonable, absurd, Acts 25:27*
G249.abbottSmith: without reason , irrational : ζῷα , II Pe 2:12 , Ju 10 .
G249.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3056 (λόγος); irrational

G4121.thayer: I have more than enough; I abound, increase.
G4121.mounce: to be more than enough; to have more than enough, to have in abundance, 2 Cor. 8:15; to abound, be abundant, 2 Thess. 1:3; 2 Pet. 1:8; to increase, be augmented, Rom. 5:20; to come into wider action, be more widely spread, Rom. 6:1; 2 Cor. 4:15; in NT trans. to cause to abound or increase, to augment, 1 Thess. 3:12
G4121.abbottSmith: Intrans. Of persons; to abound in (Arist.); to superabound : II Co 8:15 (LXX) . Of things, to abound , superabound : Ro 5:20 6:1 , II Co 4:15 , Phl 4:17 , II Th 1:3 , II Pe 1:8 .
G4121.strongs: from G4119 (πλείων); to do, make or be more, i.e. increase (transitively or intransitively); by extension, to superabound
G4121.kjv: abound, abundant, make to increase, have over

G3624.thayer: (a) a house, the material building, (b) a household, family, lineage, nation.
G3624.mounce: a house, dwelling, Mt. 9:6, 7; Mk. 2:1, 11; 3:20; place of abode, seat, site, Mt. 23:38; Lk. 13:35; met. a spiritual house or structure, 1 Pet. 2:5; meton. a household, family, Lk. 10:5; 11:17; a spiritual household, 1 Tim. 3:15; Heb. 3:6; family, lineage, Lk. 1:27, 69; 2:4; from the Hebrew, a people, nation, Mt. 10:6; 15:24
G3624.abbottSmith: prop., a house , dwelling : Ac 2:2 19:16 ; c. gen. poss., Mt 9:6 , 7 , Mk 2:11 , Lk 1:23 , al.; c. gen. attrib., ἐμπορίου , Jo 2:16 ; προσευχῆς , Mt 21:13 , al.; of a sanctuary (Hdt., Eur.): οἶ. τ. θεοῦ , of the tabernacle, Mt 12:4 , al.; the temple, Mt 21:13 , al.; metaph. of a city: Mt 23:38 , Lk 13:35 ; of the body, Mt 12:44 , Lk 11:24 ; of Christians, I Pe 2:5 ; ἐν οἴ (M, Pr. , 81 f.), at home
G3624.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a dwelling (more or less extensive, literal or figurative); by implication, a family (more or less related, literally or figuratively)

G2065.thayer: (a) I ask (a question), question, (b) I request, make a request to, pray.
G2065.mounce: to ask, interrogate, inquire of, Mt. 21:24; Lk. 20:3; in NT to ask, request, beg, beseech, Mt. 15:23; Lk. 4:38; Jn. 14:16
G2065.abbottSmith: to ask, question (cl.): absol., Lk 19:31 22:68 , Jo 8:7 c. acc pers., Jo 9:21 16:19 , 30 18:21 ; seq. λέγων , Mt 16:13 , Lk 23:3 , Jo 1:19 , 21 5:12 9:19 16:5 ; c. dupl. acc (WM, § 32, 4a), Mt 21:24 , Mk 4:10 , Lk 20:3 , Jo 16:23 (M, Pr. , 66 n ); c. acc pers., seq. περί , Lk 9:45 , Jo 18:19 .
G2065.strongs: apparently from G2046 (ἐρέω) (compare G2045 (ἐρευνάω)); to interrogate; by implication, to request

G5357.abbottSmith: a shallow bowl (= Lat. patera ), used for pouring libations, etc.: Re 5:8 15:7 16:1-17 17:1 21:9 .†
G5357.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a broad shallow cup ("phial")

G994.mounce: to cry out; to exclaim, proclaim, Mt. 3:3; 15:34; Acts 8:7; πρός τινα, to invoke, implore the aid of any one, Lk. 18:7
G994.abbottSmith: absol., to cry, call out : Mt 3:3 , 27:46 , Mk 1:3 15:34 , Lk 3:4 9:38 18:38 , Jo 1:23 , Ac 8:7 17:6 25:24 , Ga 4:27 . C. dat., to call on for help (Heb. זעק על , Ho 7:14 , al.), Lk 18:7 .†
G994.strongs: apparently a prolonged form of a primary verb; to halloo, i.e. shout (for help or in a tumultuous way)

G5032.mounce: adverb of ταχύς, quickly, speedily; soon, shortly, 1 Cor. 4:19; Gal. 1:6; hastily, Lk. 14:21; 16:6; with inconsiderate haste, 1 Tim. 5:22
G5032.strongs: neuter singular of the comparative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); more swiftly, i.e. (in manner) more rapidly, or (in time) more speedily

G4056.mounce: more, more abundantly, more earnestly, more vehemently, 2 Cor. 7:13; exceedingly, Gal. 1:14
G4056.kjv: more abundant(-ly), X the more earnest, (more) exceedingly, more frequent, much more, the rather

G4928.mounce: pr. a being held together; compression; in NT met. distress of mind, anxiety, Lk. 21:25; 2 Cor. 2:4
G4928.abbottSmith: a holding or being held together .

G3385.thayer: if not, unless, whether at all.
G3385.mounce: interrogative particle, used in questions expecting a negative answer; has the same use as μή in the form εἰ μήτε, Lk. 9:3; also when prefixed to an interrogative clause, Mt. 7:16; Jn. 4:29
G3385.abbottSmith: expecting a negative answer: Mt 7:16 26:22 , 25 , Mk 4:21 14:19 , Lk 6:39 , Jo 8:22 18:35 , Ac 10:47 , II Co 12:18 , Ja 3:11 ; in hesitant questions (v. M, Pr. , 170 n ), μ οὗτός ἐστιν , can this be , Mt 12:23 , Jo 4:29 ; μ. ἄρα , II Co 1:17 ; on εἰ μήτι ( Lk 9:13 , cf. Bl., § 65, 6), v.s. εἰ .†
G3385.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and the neuter of G5100 (τὶς); whether at all
G3385.kjv: not (the particle usually not expressed, except by the form of the question)

G1996.mounce: to gather to a place; to gather together, assemble, convene, Mt. 23:37; 24:31; Lk. 17:37
G1996.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4863 (συνάγω); to collect upon the same place

G3581.thayer: alien, new, novel; noun: a guest, stranger, foreigner.
G3581.mounce: strange, foreign, alien, Eph. 2:12, 19; strange, unexpected, surprising, 1 Pet. 4:12; novel, Heb. 13:9; subst. a stranger, Mt. 25:35, et al.; a host, Rom. 16:23
G3581.abbottSmith: foreign , alien : δαιμόνια , Ac 17:18 ; διδαχαί , He 13:9 ; c. gen. rei, strange to , estranged from , ignorant of : Eph 2:12 ; strange , unusual : I Pe 4:12 .
G3581.strongs: apparently a primary word; foreign (literally, alien, or figuratively, novel); by implication, a guest or (vice-versa) entertainer

G816.thayer: I direct my gaze, look steadily.
G816.mounce: to fix one’s eyes upon, look steadily, gaze intently, Lk. 4:20
G816.abbottSmith: to look fixedly , gaze (MM, s.v.): c. dat. pers., gaze upon : Lk 4:20 22:56 , Ac 3:12 10:4 14:9 23:1 ; seq. εἰς , c. acc. pers., Ac 3:4 6:15 13:9 ; metaph., Ac 1:10 7:55 11:6 , II Co 3:7 , 13 .†
G816.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and (to stretch); to gaze intently
G816.kjv: behold earnestly (stedfastly), fasten (eyes), look (earnestly, stedfastly, up stedfastly), set eyes

G509.thayer: (a) from above, from heaven, (b) from the beginning, from their origin (source), from of old, (c) again, anew.
G509.mounce: from above, from a higher place, Jn. 3:31; of time, from the first or beginning, Acts 26:5; from the source, Lk. 1:33; again, anew, Jn. 3:3, 7; Gal. 4:9; with a prep., the top or upper part, Mt. 27:51
G509.abbottSmith: from above : ἀπὸ ἄ. , Mt 27:51 , Mk 15:38 ; ἐκ τῶν ἄ. , Jo 19:28 ; meaning, from heaven: Jo 3:31 19:11 , Ja 1:17 3:15 , 17 .
G509.strongs: from G507 (ἄνω); from above; by analogy, from the first; by implication, anew
G509.kjv: from above, again, from the beginning (very first), the top

G4384.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5021 (τάσσω); to pre-arrange, i.e. prescribe

G2655.thayer: (properly a medical term: I stupefy, hence) I burden, encumber.
G2655.mounce: in NT to be burdensome to the disadvantage of any one, to be a dead weight upon; by impl. to be troublesome, burdensome to, in respect of maintenance, 2 Cor. 11:9; 12:13, 14*
G2655.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and (to be numb); to grow utterly torpid, i.e. (by implication) slothful (figuratively, expensive)

G2057.strongs: probably from G2060 (Ἑρμῆς); Hermas, a Christian

G2400.mounce: aorist middle imperative of εἶδον used as an interjection, Look! See! Lo! Mt. 1:23; Lk. 1:38; Acts 8:36
G2400.abbottSmith: lo , behold , see : Mt 10:16 11:8 13:3 , Mk 3:32 , Lk 2:48 , I Co 15:51 , Ja 5:9 , Ju 14 , Re 1:7 , al.; after gen. absol., Mt 1:20 2:1 , 13 12:46 , al.; καὶ ἰδού , Mt 2:9 (and freq.), Lk 1:20 10:25 , Ac 12:7 , al.; in elliptical sentences, taking the place of copula or predicate (like הִנֵּה in Heb.), Mt 3:17 , Lk 5:12 22:31 , 47 , Ac 8:27 , 36 al.
G2400.strongs: second person singular imperative middle voice of G1492 (εἴδω); used as imperative lo!;

G5235.mounce: pr. to cast or throw over or beyond, to overshoot; met. to surpass, excel; surpassing, 2 Cor. 3:10; 9:14; Eph. 1:19; 2:7; 3:19*
G5235.abbottSmith: trans., to throw over or beyond .
G5235.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw beyod the usual mark, i.e. (figuratively) to surpass (only active participle supereminent)

G3976.thayer: a shackle, fetter for the feet.
G3976.strongs: ultimately from G4228 (πούς); a shackle for the feet

G3055.thayer: contention about words, an unprofitable controversy.
G3055.mounce: contention or strife about words; by impl. a dispute about trivial things, unprofitable controversy, 1 Tim. 6:4*
G3055.abbottSmith: a strife of words : pl., I Ti 6:4 .†
G3055.strongs: from the same as G3054 (λογομαχέω); disputation about trifles ("logomachy")

G220.mounce: a cock, rooster, Mt. 26:34; Mk. 14:30; Lk. 22:34; Jn. 13:38
G220.strongs: from (to ward off); a cock or male fowl

G3059.mounce: reviling, railing, verbal abuse, 1 Tim. 5:14; 1 Pet. 3:9*
G3059.abbottSmith: abuse , railing : I Ti 5:14 , I Pe 3:9 .†

G3924.thayer: (a) adv. used as adj: besides, outside, without, left over, in addition, (b) prep: apart from, except.
G3924.mounce: to cast up, set up, throw up a palisade, Lk. 19:43*
G3924.abbottSmith: as adv., besides , in addition : τὰ π. (sc. γινόμενα ), II Co 11:28 .
G3924.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1622 (ἐκτός); near outside, i.e. besides
