G1343.thayer: (usually if not always in a Jewish atmosphere), justice, justness, righteousness, righteousness of which God is the source or author, but practically: a divine righteousness.
G1343.mounce: fair and equitable dealing, justice, Acts 17:31; Heb. 11:33; Rom. 9:28; integrity, virtue, Lk. 1:75; Eph. 5:9; in NT generosity, alms, 2 Cor. 9:10, v.r.; Mt. 6:1; piety, godliness, Rom. 6:13; investiture with the attribute of righteousness, acceptance as righteous, justification, Rom. 4:11; 10:4, et al. freq.; a provision or mean for justification, Rom. 1:17; 2 Cor. 3:9; an instance of justification, 2 Cor. 5:21
G1343.strongs: from G1342 (δίκαιος); equity (of character or act); specially (Christian) justification

G2559.thayer: I treat badly, afflict, embitter, make angry.
G2559.mounce: to harm, mistreat, cause evil to, oppress, Acts 7:6, 19; 12:1; 18:10; 1 Pet. 3:13; in NT to make angry, embitter, Acts 14:2*
G2559.abbottSmith: to ill-treat , afflict , distress : c. acc. pers., Ac 7:6 , 19 12:1 18:10 , I Pe 3:13 .
G2559.strongs: from G2556 (κακός); to injure; figuratively, to exasperate
G2559.kjv: make evil affected, entreat evil, harm, hurt, vex

G346.thayer: I sum up, summarize, recapitulate, gather up in one.
G346.mounce: to bring together several things under one, reduce under one head; to comprise, Rom. 13:9; Eph. 1:10*
G346.abbottSmith: to sum up , gather up , present as a whole: mid., Ro 13:9 , Eph 1:10 (on WH. v. Lft., Notes , 321 L; AR, in l.; Cremer, 354, 748).†
G346.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2775 (κεφαλαιόω) (in its original sense); to sum up
G346.kjv: briefly comprehend, gather together in one

G3387.mounce: interrogative particle, used in questions expecting a negative answer; has the same use as μή in the form εἰ μήτε, Lk. 9:3; also when prefixed to an interrogative clause, Mt. 7:16; Jn. 4:29
G3387.abbottSmith: (v.s. μή , I, III, and cf. Thayer, s.v. μήτις ).
G3387.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G5100 (τὶς); whether any
G3387.kjv: any (sometimes unexpressed except by the simple interrogative form of the sentence)

G1239.thayer: I offer here and there, distribute, divide, hand over.
G1239.mounce: to deliver from hand to hand; to distribute, divide, Lk. 11:22; 18:22; Jn. 6:11; Acts 4:35*
G1239.abbottSmith: to hand over, deliver : Re 17:13 , Rec.
G1239.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give throughout a crowd, i.e. deal out; also to deliver over (as to a successor)

G1706.thayer: I fall in, am cast in, am involved in.
G1706.mounce: to fall into, Mt. 12:11; Lk. 14:5; to encounter, Lk. 10:36; to be involved in, 1 Tim. 3:6, 7; 6:9; εἰς χεῖρας, to fall under the chastisement of, Heb. 10:31
G1706.abbottSmith: to fall into : seq. εἰς , Mt 12:11 , Lk 6:39 10:36 ; metaph., εἰς κρίμα , I Ti 3:6 ; ὀνειδισμόν I Ti 3:7 ; πειρασμόν , I Ti 6:9 ; εἰς χεῖρας θεοῦ (cf. II Ki 24:14 , I Ch 21:13 , Si 2:18 ), He 10:31 .†
G1706.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4098 (πίπτω); to fall on, i.e. (literally) to be entrapped by, or (figuratively) be overwhelmed with

G1197.mounce: a bundle, as of tares, Mt. 13:30*
G1197.abbottSmith: a bundle : δήσατε αὐτὰ εἰς δέσμας (D, Orig., omit εἰς , and Blass thinks original reading, δέσμας δέσμας ; v. Deiss., LAE , 125 4 ), Mt 13:30 .†

G1034.mounce: eatable, that may be eaten, Lk. 24:41*

G4517.thayer: I strengthen, render firm; imperative at the end of letters: farewell.
G4517.mounce: to strengthen, render firm; to be well, enjoy firm health; at the end of letters, like the Latin vale, farewell, Acts 15:29*
G4517.abbottSmith: to strengthen ; most freq. in pf., ἔρρωμαι , to put forth strength, be strong , hence, often in imperat., ἔρρωσο, ἔρρωσθε , farewell (Lat. vale ): Ac 15:29 23:30 Rec., R, mg.†
G4517.strongs: prolongation from (to dart; probably akin to G4506 (ῥύομαι)); to strengthen, i.e. (impersonal passive) have health (as a parting exclamation, good-bye)

G2610.thayer: I subdue (in warfare); I struggle against, conquer, overcome.
G2610.mounce: to subdue, vanquish, overcome, conquer, Heb. 11:33*
G2610.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G75 (ἀγωνίζομαι); to struggle against, i.e. (by implication) to overcome

G453.mounce: inconsiderate, unintelligent, unwise; Lk. 24:25; Rom. 1:14; Gal. 3:1, 3; Tit. 3:3; brutish, 1 Tim. 6:9
G453.abbottSmith: not thought on , not understood (Hom., Plat.).
G453.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3539 (νοιέω); unintelligent; by implication, sensual

G2728.thayer: I rust; pass: I am rusted, tarnished.
G2728.mounce: to cover with rust; pass. to rust, become rusty or tarnished, Jas. 5:3*
G2728.abbottSmith: to rust over ; pass., become rusted over : Ja 5:3 .†
G2728.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G2447 (ἰός); to rust down, i.e. corrode

G5046.thayer: perfect, (a) complete in all its parts, (b) full grown, of full age, (c) specially of the completeness of Christian character.
G5046.mounce: brought to completion; fully accomplished, fully developed, Jas. 1:4a; fully realized, thorough, 1 Jn. 4:18; complete, entire, as opposed to what is partial and limited, 1 Cor. 13:10; full grown of ripe age, 1 Cor. 14:20; Eph. 4:13; Heb. 5:14; fully accomplished in Christian enlightenment, 1 Cor. 2:6; Phil. 3:15; Col. 1:28; perfect in some point of character, without shortcoming in respect of a certain standard, Mt. 5:48; 19:21; Col. 4:12; Jas. 1:4b; 3:2; perfect, consummate, Rom. 12:2; Jas. 1:17, 25; compar. of higher excellence and efficiency, Heb. 9:11*
G5046.abbottSmith: having reached its end, finished, mature, complete, perfect ;
G5046.strongs: from G5056 (τέλος); complete (in various applications of labor, growth, mental and moral character, etc.); neuter (as noun, with G3588 (ὁ)) completeness

G993.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01123 and H07266); sons of commotion; Boanerges, an epithet of two of the apostles

G3412.thayer: Mitylene, the capital of the island of Lesbos in the northern Aegean sea.
G3412.mounce: Mitylene, the capital city of Lesbos, in the Aegean sea, Acts 20:14*
G3412.abbottSmith: Mytilene , Mitylene , chief city of Lesbos: Ac 20:14 .†
G3412.strongs: for (abounding in shellfish); Mitylene (or Mytilene), a town on the island of Lesbos

G5094.mounce: preciousness, costliness; meton. precious things, valuable merchandise, Rev. 18:19*
G5094.abbottSmith: preciousness, worth : Re 18:19 (cf. Hort on τιμή , I Pe 2:7 ).†
G5094.strongs: from G5093 (τίμιος); expensiveness, i.e. (by implication) magnificence

G3167.mounce: magnificent, splendid; τὰ μεγαλεῖα, great things, wonderful works, Acts 2:11*
G3167.strongs: from G3173 (μέγας); magnificent, i.e. (neuter, plural as noun) a conspicuous favor, or (subjectively) perfection

G886.mounce: not made with hands, Mk. 14:58; 2 Cor. 5:1; Col. 2:11*
G886.abbottSmith: not made by hands : Mk 14:58 , II Co 5:1 ; metaph., περιτομή ἀ. (i.e. spiritual ), Col 2:11 (MM, s.v.).†
G886.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5499 (χειροποίητος); unmanufactured, i.e. inartificial

G3411.abbottSmith: hired ; as subst., ὁ μ. , a hired servant , hireling : Mk 1:20 , Jo 10:12 , 13 .†
G3411.strongs: from G3409 (μισθόω); a wage-worker (good or bad)

G2459.thayer: Justus, (a) a surname of Joseph Barsabbas, one of the two nominated to fill Judas' place as apostle, (b) Titius Justus, a Corinthian Christian, (c) surname of Jesus, a Christian with Paul in Rome.
G2459.abbottSmith: Justus , the surname of, Joseph Barsabbas : Ac 1:23 . Titus , of Corinth: Ac 18:7 . Jesus , a Christian of Rome: Col 4:11 .†
G2459.strongs: of Latin origin ("just"); Justus, the name of three Christian

G5504.abbottSmith: yesterday : Jo 4:52 , Ac 7:28 , He 13:8 (on the form, v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 370 f.).†
G5504.strongs: of uncertain derivation; "yesterday"; by extension, in time past or hitherto

G564.mounce: pr. uncircumcised; met. uncircumcised in respect of untowardness and obduracy, Acts 7:51*
G564.abbottSmith: uncircumcised ; metaph. ( τ. ) καρδίαις (cf. Je 9:26 , al.): Ac 7:51 . ( ἄσημος , found in π. , appears to have been the word used by Greek-speaking Egyptians: v. Deiss., BS , 153; cf. also Cremer, 885; MM, s.v.)†
G564.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4059 (περιτέμνω); uncircumcised (figuratively)

G5284.mounce: to sail under; to sail under the lee, or, to the south of, an island, etc., Acts 27:4, 7*
G5284.abbottSmith: to sail under , i.e. under the lee of: c. acc, Ac 27:4 , 7 .†
G5284.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4126 (πλέω); to sail under the lee of

G4215.mounce: a river, stream, Mk. 1:5; Acts 16:13; met. and allegor. Jn. 7:38; Rev. 22:1, 2; a flood, winter torrent, for χείμαρρος ποταμός, Mt. 7:25, 27
G4215.strongs: probably from a derivative of the alternate of G4095 (πίνω) (compare G4224 (πότος)); a current, brook or freshet (as drinkable), i.e. running water

G5337.mounce: vile, refuse; evil, wicked; Jn. 3:20; 5:29; Rom. 9:11; 2 Cor. 5:10; Tit. 2:8; Jas. 3:16*
G5337.abbottSmith: slight, worthless, of no account , both of persons and things in various shades of meaning (v. LS, s.v.), in NT, as freq. in cl., always with distinct moral reference (v. Ellic., Past. Epp ., 203; Hort, Ja. , 85; Tr., Syn. , lxxxiv), worthless, bad : Jo 3:20 , Tit 2:8 , Ja 3:16 ; opp. to ἀγαθός (q.v.), Jo 5:29 , Ro 9:11 , II Co 5:10 .†
G5337.strongs: apparently a primary word; "foul" or "flawy", i.e. (figuratively) wicked

G595.thayer: a putting off, a laying down.
G595.mounce: a putting off or away, laying aside, a euphemism for death, 1 Pet. 3:21; 2 Pet. 1:14*
G595.abbottSmith: a putting away : I Pe 3:21 , II Pe 1:14 .†
G595.strongs: from G659 (ἀποτίθημι); a laying aside (literally or figuratively)

G5459.mounce: light-bringing; i.e. ἀστήρ, Lucifer, the morning star, met. 2 Pet. 1:19*
G5459.abbottSmith: light-bringing ; as subst., ὁ φ. , the morning star (Plat., al.; cf. ἐωσφόρος , Is 14:12 , Jb 3:9 , Ps 110:3 ): metaph., II Pe 1:19 (v. Mayor, in l.).†
G5459.strongs: from G5457 (φῶς) and G5342 (φέρω); light-bearing ("phosphorus"), i.e. (specially), the morning-star (figuratively)

G1600.mounce: to leap forth, rush out, Acts 14:14*
G1600.abbottSmith: to spread out (as a sail), stretch forth : Ro 10:21 (LXX) .†
G1600.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a form of G4072 (πέτομαι); to fly out, i.e. (by analogy) to extend

G3557.thayer: I rob; mid: I set apart for myself, appropriate for my own benefit, purloin.
G3557.mounce: to deprive, rob; mid. to misappropriate; to make secret reservation, Acts 5:2, 3; to purloin, Tit. 2:10*
G3557.abbottSmith: in Hom., as depon., to turn away ( from ), abandon .
G3557.strongs: middle voice from (apart or clandestinely); to sequestrate for oneself, i.e. embezzle

G952.mounce: pr. what is open and accessible to all; hence, profane, not religious, not connected with religion; unholy; a despiser, scorner, 1 Tim. 1:9; 4:7
G952.abbottSmith: permitted to be trodden , accessible (v. DCG , ii, 422 b ); hence, (opp. to ἱερός ) unhallowed , profane : of things, I Ti 4:7 6:20 , II Ti 2:16 ; of men, I Ti 1:9 , He 12:16 (cf. κοινός ; Cremer, 140).†
G952.strongs: from the base of G939 (βάσις) and (a threshold); accessible (as by crossing the door-way), i.e. (by implication, of Jewish notions) heathenish, wicked

G3324.mounce: full, full of, filled with, Jn. 19:29; 21:11; replete, Mt. 23:28; Rom. 1:29; 15:14; Jas. 3:8, 17; 2 Pet. 2:14*
G3324.abbottSmith: full : c. gen. rei, Jo 19:29 21:11 , Ja 3:8 ; metaph., of thoughts and feelings, Mt 23:28 , Ro 1:29 15:14 , II Pe 2:14 , Ja 3:17 (cf. Pr 6:34 ).†
G3324.strongs: of uncertain derivation; replete (literally or figuratively)

G5092.mounce: a pricing, estimate of worth; price, value, Mt. 27:9; price paid, Mt. 27:6; meton. a thing of price, and collectively, precious things, Rev. 21:24, 26; preciousness, 1 Pet. 2:7; substantial value, real worth, Col. 2:23; careful regard, honor, state of honor, dignity, Rom. 9:21; Heb. 5:4; honor conferred, observance, veneration, Rom. 2:7, 10; 12:10; mark of favor and consideration, Acts 28:10, honorarium, compensation, 1 Tim. 5:7
G5092.strongs: from G5099 (τίνω); a value, i.e. money paid, or (concretely and collectively) valuables; by analogy, esteem (especially of the highest degree), or the dignity itself

G4470.thayer: a piece of cloth, a remnant torn off.
G4470.mounce: a piece torn off; a bit of cloth, cloth, Mt. 9:16; Mk. 2:21*
G4470.strongs: from G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); a "rag," i.e. piece of cloth

G364.mounce: remembrance; a commemoration, memorial, Lk. 22:19; 1 Cor. 11:24, 25; Heb. 10:3*
G364.abbottSmith: remembrance : εἰς τ. ἐμὴν ἀ. , Lk 22:19 (WH om.), I Co 11:24 , 25 ; ἀ. ἁμαρτιῶν , He 10:3 (v. Abbott, Essays , 122 ff.; DCG , ii, 74 a ).†

G2713.mounce: can function as an improper prep., over against, opposite to, Mk. 11:2; 12:41; 13:3; ὁ, ἡ, τό, κατέναντι, opposite, Lk. 19:30; before, in the presence of, in the sight of, Rom. 4:17
G2713.abbottSmith: over against , opposite , before : ἡ, κ. κώμη , Lk 19:30 ; as prep., c. gen., Mt 21:2 27:24 ( ἀπ- , WH, mg.), Mk 11:2 12:41 ( ἀπ- , WH, mg.) 13:3 ; metaph., before , seq. θεοῦ , Ro 4:17 , II Co 2:17 12:19 .†
G2713.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1725 (ἔναντι); directly opposite

G1234.abbottSmith: of a number, to murmur or mutter among themselves : Lk 15:2 19:7 .†
G1234.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1111 (γογγύζω); to complain throughout a crowd

G2938.mounce: pr. a thing founded; in NT a created being, creature, 1 Tim. 4:4; Jas. 1:18; Rev. 5:13; 8:9*
G2938.strongs: from G2936 (κτίζω); an original formation (concretely), i.e. product (created thing)

G2660.thayer: met: I am pierced, stung, smitten.
G2660.mounce: to pierce through; to pierce with compunction and pain of heart, Acts 2:37*
G2660.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3572 (νύσσω); to pierce thoroughly, i.e. (figuratively) to agitate violently ("sting to the quick")

G954.thayer: Beelzebul, a name of Satan, the chief of evil spirits.
G954.mounce: variant spellings of Βεελζεβούβ and βεελζεβούλ, Beelzeboul, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 10:25
G954.abbottSmith: Beelzebul , Beelzebub , a name of Satan: Mt 10:25 12:24 , 27 , Mk 3:22 , Lk 11:15 , 18 , 19 .†
G954.strongs: of Chaldee origin (by parody on H01176); dung-god; Beelzebul, a name of Satan

G2363.thayer: Thyatira, a city of the old district Lydia, in the Roman province Asia.
G2363.mounce: Thyatira, a city of Lydia, Acts 16:14; Rev. 1:11; 2:18, 24*. See PhotoGuide.
G2363.abbottSmith: Thyatira , a city of Lydia: Ac 16:14 , Re 1:11 2:18 , 24 .†
G2363.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Thyatira, a place in Asia Minor

G2946.mounce: also spelled κύλισμα, a rolling, wallowing, 2 Pet. 2:22*
G2946.strongs: from G2947 (κυλιόω); a wallow (the effect of rolling), i.e. filth

G342.thayer: renewing; a renewal or change of heart and life.
G342.abbottSmith: renewal : Ro 12:2 , Tit 3:5 (Cremer, 324; MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G5097.mounce: to avenge, someone; in NT to punish, Acts 22:5; 26:11*
G5097.strongs: from a comparative of G5092 (τιμή) and ouros (a guard); properly, to protect one's honor, i.e. to avenge (inflict a penalty)

G1396.thayer: I bring into subjection, enslave, treat as a slave.
G1396.mounce: pr. to bring into slavery; to treat as a slave; to discipline into subjection, 1 Cor. 9:27*
G1396.abbottSmith: to make a slave, bring into bondage : I Co 9:27 (Cremer, 703).†
G1396.strongs: from a presumed compound of G1401 (δοῦλος) and G71 (ἄγω); to be a slave-driver, i.e. to enslave (figuratively, subdue)

G2995.abbottSmith: the larynx , throat : metaph., of speech (cf. Si 6:5 ), Ro 3:13 (LXX) .†

G3028.mounce: also spelled λῆψις, taking, receiving, Phil. 4:15*

G3198.thayer: Melchizedek, king and priest of Salem.
G3198.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04442); Melchisedek (i.e. Malkitsedek), a patriarch

G1051.mounce: milk, 1 Cor. 9:7; met. spiritual milk, consisting in the elements of Christian instruction, 1 Cor. 3:2; Heb. 5:12, 13; spiritual nutriment, 1 Pet. 2:2*
G1051.abbottSmith: milk : I Co 9:7 . Metaph., of elementary Christian teaching: I Co 3:2 , He 5:12 , 13 ; τὸ λογικὸν ἄδολον γ. , the rational (spiritual) genuine milk (v. Hort, in l.), I Pe 2:2 (in support of AV, milk of the word , v. ICC , in l.).†

G1616.mounce: pr. extension; in NT intenseness, intentness; ἐν ἐκτενείᾳ, intently, perseverance, earnestness, Acts 26:7*
G1616.abbottSmith: zeal, intentness, earnestness (cf. Deiss., BS, 262): Ac 26:7 .†

G1878.thayer: I remind, possibly: I remind again.
G1878.mounce: to remind, put in remembrance, Rom. 15:15*
G1878.abbottSmith: to remind again : c. acc pers., Ro 15:15 .†
G1878.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G363 (ἀναμιμνήσκω); to remind of

G4839.thayer: I remain or continue together with.
G4839.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3887 (παραμένω); to remain in company, i.e. still live

G5172.abbottSmith: softness, daintiness, luxuriousness : Lk 7:25 , II Pe 2:13 .†
G5172.strongs: from (to break up or (figuratively) enfeeble, especially the mind and body by indulgence); effeminacy, i.e. luxury or debauchery

G79.thayer: a sister, a woman (fellow-)member of a church, a Christian woman.
G79.mounce: a sister; near kinswoman, or female relative, a female member of the Christian community
G79.abbottSmith: a sister : Mt 19:29 , al.; metaph. (MM, VGT , s.v.), of a member of the Christian community: Ro 16:1 , I Co 7:15 Ja 21:15 , al.
G79.strongs: feminine of G80 (ἀδελφός); a sister (naturally or ecclesiastically)

G4171.mounce: war, Mt. 24:6; Mk. 13:7; battle, engagement, combat, 1 Cor. 14:8; Heb. 11:34; battling, strife, Jas. 4:1
G4171.abbottSmith: war : Mt 24:6 , Mk 13:7 , Lk 14:31 21:9 , He 11:34 ; π. ποιεῖν , seq. μετά , c. gen. (cf. πολεμέω ), Re 11:7 12:17 13:7 19:19 .
G4171.strongs: from (to bustle); warfare (literally or figuratively; a single encounter or a series)

G4580.strongs: of Latin origin; "second"; Secundus, a Christian

G3793.thayer: a crowd, mob, the common people.
G3793.mounce: a crowd, a confused multitude of people, Mt. 4:25; 5:1; 7:28; spc. the common people, Jn. 7:49; a multitude, great number, Lk. 5:29; 6:17; Acts 1:15; by impl. tumult, uproar, Lk. 22:6; Acts 24:18
G3793.abbottSmith: a moving crowd or multitude of persons, a throng : Mt 9:23 , Mk 2:4 , Lk 5:1 , Jo 5:13 , al.; pl., Mt 5:1 , Mk 10:1 , Lk 3:7 , and freq.; ὄ. ἱκανός , Mk 10:46 , al.; τοσοῦτος , Mt 15:33 ; οὐ μετ’ ὄχλου , Ac 24:18 ; ἄτερ ὄχλου , Lk 22:6 ; πᾶς ὁ ὄ. , Mt 13:2 , Mk 2:13 , al.; ὄ. πολύς ( π. ὄ ), Mt 20:29 , Mk 5:21 , al.; ὁ πολὺς ὄ. ( ὄ. π. ), the populace , the common people , Mk 12:37 (Swete, in l.;
G3793.strongs: from a derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning a vehicle); a throng (as borne along); by implication, the rabble; by extension, a class of people; figuratively, a riot
G3793.kjv: company, multitude, number (of people), people, press

G1523.mounce: to admit; to receive into favor, receive kindly, accept with favor, 2 Cor. 6:17*
G1523.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to take into one's favor

G3989.mounce: the deep, the open sea, Mt. 18:6; a sea, distinguished from the sea in general, and named from an adjacent country, Acts 27:5*
G3989.abbottSmith: the deep sea , the deep , the sea : Ac 27:5 ; τὸ π. τῆς θαλάσσης , Mt 18:6 .
G3989.strongs: of uncertain affinity; deep or open sea, i.e. the main

G3674.thayer: together, at the same place and time.
G3674.mounce: together; in the same place, Jn. 21:2; together at the same time, Jn. 4:36; 20:4; Acts 2:1*
G3674.abbottSmith: together ; prop., of place: Jo 21:2 , Ac 2:1 ; without idea of place: Jo 4:36 20:4 .†
G3674.strongs: genitive case of (the same; akin to G260 (ἅμα)) as adverb; at the same place or time

G3659.strongs: from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); a sight, i.e. (by implication) the eye

G316.mounce: necessary, indispensable, 1 Cor. 12:22; necessary, needful, right, proper, Acts 13:46; 2 Cor. 9:5; Phil. 1:24; 2:25; Heb. 8:3; near, intimate, closely connected, as friends, Acts 10:24
G316.abbottSmith: necessary : Ac 13:46 , I Co 12:22 , II Co 9:5 , Phl 2:25 , Tit 3:14 , He 8:3 ; comp. -αιότερον , Phl 1:24 .
G316.strongs: from G318 (ἀνάγκη); necessary; by implication, close (of kin)

G4246.mounce: an old man, aged person, Lk. 1:18; Tit. 2:2; Phlm. 9*
G4246.abbottSmith: an old man : Lk 1:18 , Tit 2:2 , Phm 9 (R, txt., but v. infr.).
G4246.strongs: from the same as G4245 (πρεσβύτερος); an old man

G1688.thayer: trans: I embark, put on board, step into.
G1688.mounce: to cause to step into or upon; to set in or upon; especially, to put on board, Acts 27:6*
G1688.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (to mount; causative of G1684 (ἐμβαίνω)); to place on, i.e. transfer (aboard a vessel)

G2075.strongs: second person plural present indicative of G1510 (εἰμί); ye are

G1927.thayer: I sojourn, am resident (temporarily, in a foreign city).
G1927.mounce: to dwell among a people; to be at home among one’s own people; and in NT to sojourn as a stranger among another people, Acts 2:10; 17:21*
G1927.abbottSmith: to be at home (Thuc., Plat., al.),
G1927.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G1218 (δῆμος); to make oneself at home, i.e. (by extension) to reside (in a foreign country)

G4818.thayer: I am moved to grief by sympathy.
G4818.mounce: to be grieved together with; to be grieved, Mk. 3:5*
G4818.abbottSmith: to make to grieve with (Arist.). Pass., to be moved to grief by sympathy: seq. ἐπί , c. dat., Mk 3:5 .†
G4818.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3076 (λυπέω); to afflict jointly, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone

G4431.mounce: a fall, crash, ruin, Mt. 7:27; met. downfall, ruin, Lk. 2:34*
G4431.abbottSmith: a falling , fall (Plat., Plut., al.): Mt 7:27 ; metaph., Lk 2:34 .†
G4431.strongs: from the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω); a crash, i.e. downfall (literally or figuratively)

G1976.mounce: also ἐπιρράπτω, to sew on, Mk. 2:21*
G1976.abbottSmith: to sew upon : seq. ἐπί , c. acc, Mk 2:21 .†
G1976.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G4476 (ῥαφίς); to stitch upon, i.e. fasten with the needle

G388.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4717 (σταυρόω); to recrucify (figuratively)

G5510.strongs: perhaps akin to the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (G5465 (χαλάω)) or G5494 (χειμών) (as descending or empty); snow

G4712.thayer: (a) a stadium, one eighth of a Roman mile, (b) a race-course for public games.
G4712.mounce: pr. a fixed standard of measure; a stadium, the eighth part of a Roman mile, and nearly equal to a furlong, containing 201.45 yards, about 192 meters, Lk. 24:13; Mt. 14:24; Jn. 6:19; 11:18; Rev. 14:20; 21:16; a race-course, a race, 1 Cor. 9:24*
G4712.strongs: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι) (as fixed); a stade or certain measure of distance; by implication, a stadium or race-course

G4409.thayer: I have preeminence, am chief, am first.
G4409.mounce: to be first, to hold the first rank or highest dignity, have the preeminence, be chief, Col. 1:18*
G4409.abbottSmith: to be first , pre-eminent , have the first place : Col 1:18 .†
G4409.strongs: from G4413 (πρῶτος); to be first (in rank or influence)

G4232.thayer: the palace at Jerusalem occupied by the Roman governor, or the quarters of the praetorian guard in Rome.
G4232.mounce: when used in reference to a camp, the tent of the general or commander-in-chief; hence, in reference to a province, the palace in which the governor of the province resided, Mt. 27:27; Mk. 15:16; Acts 23:35; the camp occupied by the praetorian cohorts at Rome, the praetorian camp, or, the Roman emperor’s palace, Phil. 1:13
G4232.strongs: of Latin origin; the prætorium or governor's courtroom (sometimes including the whole edifice and camp)

G2437.thayer: Illyricum, a Roman province, afterwards called Dalmatia, bounded by Pannonia on the north, Macedonia on the south, Moesia on the east, and the Adriatic Sea on the west.
G2437.mounce: Illyricum, a country between the Adriatic and the Danube, Rom. 15:19*
G2437.abbottSmith: Illyricum , a region bordering on the Adriatic Sea: Ro 15:19 .†
G2437.strongs: neuter of an adjective from a name of uncertain derivation; (the) Illyrican (shore), i.e. (as a name itself) Illyricum, a region of Europe

G443.abbottSmith: a murderer , manslayer (Eur.; v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Jo 8:44 , I Jo 3:15 .†
G443.strongs: from G444 (ἄνθρωπος) and (to kill); a manslayer

G37.thayer: I make holy, treat as holy, set apart as holy, sanctify, hallow, purify.
G37.mounce: to separate, consecrate; cleanse, purify, sanctify; regard or reverence as holy
G37.abbottSmith: to make holy , consecrate , sanctify ; [in LXX chiefly for קדשׁ pi., hi.;] to dedicate , separate , set apart for God ; of things: Mt 23:17 , 19 , II Ti 2:21 ; of persons: Christ, Jo 10:36 , 17:19 . to purify , make conformable in character to such dedication: forensically, to free from guilt, I Co 6:11 , Eph 5:26 , He 2:11 , 10:10 , 14 , 29 , 13:12 ; internally, by actual sanctification of life,
G37.strongs: from G40 (ἅγιος); to make holy, i.e. (ceremonially) purify or consecrate; (mentally) to venerate

G2378.thayer: abstr. and concr: sacrifice; a sacrifice, offering.
G2378.mounce: sacrifice, the act of sacrificing, Heb. 9:26; the thing sacrificed, a victim, Mt. 9:13; 12:7; the flesh of victims eaten by the sacrificers, 1 Cor. 10:18; in NT an offering or service to God, Phil. 4:18
G2378.strongs: from G2380 (θύω); sacrifice (the act or the victim, literally or figuratively)

G2644.mounce: to change, exchange; to reconcile; pass. to be reconciled, Rom. 5:10 (2x); 1 Cor. 7:11; 2 Cor. 5:18, 19, 20*
G2644.abbottSmith: prop., to change , exchange (esp. of money); hence, of persons, to change from emnity to friendship, to reconcile (for exx. in cl., v. Thayer, LS): of the reconciliation of man to God (Lft., Notes , 288; ICC on Ro 5:10 ; DCG , ii, 474, 797), II Co 5:18 , 19 ; pass., Ro 5:10 , II Co 5:20 ; of a woman returning to her husband, I Co 7:11 (cf. ἀπο-καταλλάσσω ).†
G2644.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); to change mutually, i.e. (figuratively) to compound a difference

G2428.thayer: (originally: the olive branch held in the hand of the suppliant), supplication, entreaty.
G2428.mounce: pr. an olive branch borne by suppliants in their hands; prayer, supplication, Heb. 5:7*
G2428.abbottSmith: of a suppliant ; as subst., ἡ ἱ. (sc. ῥάβδος ), in cl., an olive-branch , carried by a suppliant. In late Gk. = ἱκεσία , supplication : pl., δεήσεις κ. ἱ. , He 5:7 .
G2428.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G2425 (ἱκανός) (through the idea of approaching for a favor); intreaty

G2048.thayer: as an adj: deserted, desolate, waste; hence: the desert, to the east and south of Palestine; of a person: deserted, abandoned, desolate.
G2048.mounce: lone, desert, waste, uninhabited, Mt. 14:13, 15; Mk. 6:31, 32, 35; lone, abandoned to ruin, Mt. 23:38; Lk. 13:35; met. lone, unmarried, Gal. 4:27; as a subst. a desert, uninhabited region, waste, Mt. 3:1; 24:26; Acts 7:36
G2048.strongs: of uncertain affinity; lonesome, i.e. (by implication) waste (usually as a noun, G5561 (χώρα) being implied)

G149.mounce: strictly, deformed, opposed to καλός; met. shameful, indecent, dishonorable, vile, 1 Cor. 11:6; 14:35; Eph. 5:12; Tit. 1:11*
G149.abbottSmith: base , shameful : I Co 11:6 14:35 , Eph 5:12 , Tit 1:11 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G149.strongs: neuter of G150 (αἰσχρός); a shameful thing, i.e. indecorum

G869.strongs: adverb from G852 (ἀφανής) (contraction); unawares, i.e. unexpectedly

G3699.mounce: where, in which place, in what place, Mt. 6:19, 20, 21; Rev. 2:13; whither, to what place, Jn. 8:21; 14:4; ὅπου ἄν, or ἐάν, wherever, in whatever place, Mt. 24:28; whithersoever, Mt. 8:19; Jas. 3:4; met. where, in which thing, state, etc., Col. 3:11; whereas, 1 Cor. 3:3; 2 Pet. 2:11
G3699.abbottSmith: of place, correlat. of ποῦ (q.v.), where . Prop., of place, where ; in relative sentences, c. indic: Mt 25:24 , 26 , Mk 2:4 4:5 , 15 5:40 13:14 , Jo 3:8 6:62 7:34 , 36 11:32 14:3 17:24 20:19 , Ro 15:20 , Re 2:13 ; after nouns of place, for relat. prepositional phrase ( ἐν ᾧ. , etc.), Mt 6:19 , 20 13:5 26:57 28:6 , Mk 6:55 9:48 , Lk 12:33 , Jo 1:28 4:20 , 46 6:23 7:42 10:40 11:30 12:1 18:1 , 20 19:
G3699.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G4225 (πού); what(-ever) where, i.e. at whichever spot
G3699.kjv: in what place, where(-as, -soever), whither (+ soever)

G1918.thayer: I take to wife after, marry a deceased brother's wife.
G1918.mounce: to marry a wife by the law of affinity, Mt. 22:24*
G1918.abbottSmith: to enter into affinity with : c. dat. (LXX, ll. c., exc. Ge 38:8 ).
G1918.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G1062 (γάμος); to form affinity with, i.e. (specially) in a levirate way

G2568.abbottSmith: Fair Havens , a harbour in Crete: Ac 27:8 .†
G2568.strongs: plural of G2570 (καλός) and G3040 (λιμήν); Good Harbors, i.e. Fairhaven, a bay of Crete

G1405.thayer: I take hold of, grasp, catch.
G1405.mounce: pr. to grasp with the hand, clutch; to lay hold of, seize, take, catch, 1 Cor. 3:19*
G1405.abbottSmith: to grasp with the hand, to lay hold of : metaph., c. acc (M, Pr. , 65), I Co 3:19 (LXX) .†
G1405.strongs: perhaps akin to the base of G1404 (δράκων) (through the idea of capturing); to grasp, i.e. (figuratively) entrap

G369.mounce: pr. without contradiction or gainsaying; without hesitation, promptly, Acts 10:29*

G1733.mounce: eleven, indecl. numeral, Mt. 28:16; Mk. 16:14; Lk. 24:9, 33; Acts 1:26; 2:14*
G1733.strongs: from (the neuter of) G1520 (εἷς) and G1176 (δέκα); one and ten, i.e. eleven

G2204.thayer: (lit: I boil, am boiling), I burn (in spirit), am fervent.
G2204.mounce: to be zealous, earnest, eager, Rev. 3:19*
G2204.abbottSmith: to boil , be hot ; metaph., of anger, love, zeal: ptcp., fervent : τ. πνεύματι , Ac 18:25 , Ro 12:11 .†
G2204.strongs: a primary verb; to be hot (boil, of liquids; or glow, of solids), i.e. (figuratively) be fervid (earnest)

G473.thayer: (a) instead of, in return for, over against, opposite, in exchange for, as a substitute for, (b) on my behalf, (c) wherefore, because.
G473.mounce: over against; hence, in correspondence to, answering to, Jn. 1:16; in place of, Mt. 2:22; in retribution or return for, Mt. 5:38; in consideration of, Heb. 12:2, 16; on account of, Mt. 17:27; ἀνθ’ ὡ\u0001, because, Lk. 1:20
G473.abbottSmith: prop, in local sense, over against , opposite , hence;
G473.strongs: a primary particle; opposite, i.e. instead or because of (rarely in addition to)

G1835.strongs: the tenth multiple of G1803 (ἕξ); sixty

G2683.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G4639 (σκιά); to overshade, i.e. cover

G5397.abbottSmith: babbling, garrulous : I Ti 5:13 (EV, tattlers ; of things, φιλοσοφία , IV Mac, l.c. ).†
G5397.strongs: from (to bubble); a garrulous person, i.e. prater

G2800.mounce: a breaking, the act of breaking, Lk. 24:35; Acts 2:42

G3078.strongs: from G3080 (λύσις) and (trouble); grief-dispelling; Lysanias, a governor of Abilene

G3010.mounce: ministering; engaged in holy service, Heb. 1:14*
G3010.abbottSmith: of or for service , ministering : πνεύματα , He 1:14 .†
G3010.strongs: from the same as G3008 (λειτουργέω); functional publicly ("liturgic"); i.e. beneficient

G4566.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07854); Satan, i.e. the devil

G5033.thayer: most swiftly, most quickly, as quickly as possible.
G5033.mounce: adverb of ταχύς, quickly, speedily; soon, shortly, 1 Cor. 4:19; Gal. 1:6; hastily, Lk. 14:21; 16:6; with inconsiderate haste, 1 Tim. 5:22
G5033.strongs: neuter plural of the superlative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); most quickly, i.e. (with G5613 (ὡς) prefixed) as soon as possible

G5025.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5025.strongs: dative case and accusative case feminine plural respectively of G3778 (οὗτος); (to or with or by, etc.) these

G4169.mounce: of what kind, sort or species, Jn. 12:33; 21:19; what? which? Mt. 19:18; 21:23, 24, 27
G4169.abbottSmith: of what quality or sort : absol., n. pl., Lk 24:19 ; c. subst.; prop., in direct questions: Mt 19:18 21:23 22:36 24:42 , Mk 11:28 12:28 , Lk 6:32-34 , Jo 10:32 , Ac 4:7 7:49 , Ro 3:27 , I Co 15:35 , Ja 4:14 (but v.s. ποία ), I Pe 2:20 ; in indirect questions = ὁποῖος : Mt 21:24 , 27 24:43 , Mk 11:29 , 33 , Lk 12:39 20:2 , 8 , Jo 12:33 18:32 21:19 , Ac 23:34 , I Pe 1:11 , Re 3:3 ; ποίας (sc. ὁδοῦ )
G4169.strongs: from the base of G4226 (ποῦ) and G3634 (οἷος); individualizing interrogative (of character) what sort of, or (of number) which one

G2515.abbottSmith: a chair , seat : Mt 21:12 , Mk 11:15 ; of teachers, Mt 23:2 .†
G2515.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the same as G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a bench (literally or figuratively)

G602.mounce: a disclosure, revelation, Rom. 2:5; manifestation, appearance, Rom. 8:19; 1 Cor. 1:7; 2 Thess. 1:7; 1 Pet. 1:7, 13; 4:13; met. spiritual enlightenment, Lk. 2:32
G602.abbottSmith: an uncovering , laying bare (Plut.). Metaph., a revealing , revelation : a disclosure of divine truth, or a manifestion from God: Lk 2:32 , Ro 2:5 8:19 16:25 , I Co 1:7 14:6 , 26 , II Co 12:1 , 7 , Ga 1:12 2:2 , Eph 1:17 3:3 , II Th 1:7 , I Pe 1:7 , 13 4:13 , Re 1:1 .†
G602.kjv: appearing, coming, lighten, manifestation, be revealed, revelation

G2262.thayer: Er, son of Joshua and father of Elmadam.
G2262.abbottSmith: Er , an ancestor of Jesus: Lk 3:28 .†
G2262.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06147); Er, an Israelite

G1821.thayer: I send away, send forth (a person qualified for a task).
G1821.mounce: to send out or forth; to send away, dismiss, Lk. 1:53; to dispatch on a service or agency, Acts 7:12; to send forth as a pervading influence, Gal. 4:6
G1821.abbottSmith: to send forth : c. acc pers., Ac 7:12 12:11 , Ga 4:4 ; τ. ἐπαγγελίαν , Lk 24:49 ; τ. πνεῦμα , Ga 4:6 ; [ τ. κήρυγμα , Mk 16:1-20 , "shorter conclusion," WH ;] seq. εἰς , Ac 22:21 ; pass., ὁ λόγος , Ac 13:26 .
G1821.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G649 (ἀποστέλλω); to send away forth, i.e. (on a mission) to despatch, or (peremptorily) to dismiss

G3787.thayer: eye-service; service rendered only while the master watches.
G3787.mounce: also written ὀφθαλμοδουλεία, eye-service, service rendered only while under inspection, Eph. 6:6; Col. 3:22*
G3787.strongs: from G3788 (ὀφθαλμός) and G1397 (δουλεία); sight-labor, i.e. that needs watching (remissness)

G1155.abbottSmith: to lend money on interest: Lk 6:34, 35 ; mid., to borrow : Mt 5:42 .†
G1155.strongs: from G1156 (δάνειον); to loan on interest; reflexively, to borrow

G477.thayer: a proposition, tenet, opinion advanced by one party against another; opposition.
G477.mounce: pr. opposition; hence, a question proposed for dispute, disputation, 1 Tim. 6:20*
G477.strongs: from a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G5087 (τίθημι); opposition, i.e. a conflict (of theories)

G3747.mounce: contracted form, ὀστοῦν, οῦ, τό, a bone, Mt. 23:27; Lk. 24:39; Jn. 19:36; Heb. 11:22*
G3747.abbottSmith: a bone : contr., ὀστοῦν (v. supr.), Jo 19:36 (LXX) ; uncontr. (as in Hom., Hdt.), ὀστέα , Lk 24:39 ; ὀστέων , Mt 23:27 , He 11:22 .†

G359.thayer: a loosing, departing, departure (from this life); (Probably a metaphor from the yoking and unyoking of transport animals).
G359.mounce: pr. dissolution; met. departure, death, 2 Tim. 4:6*
G359.abbottSmith: a loosing , e.g. of a vessel from its moorings, hence, departure : from life, II Ti 4:6 .†

G2872.thayer: (a) I grow weary, (b) I toil, work with effort (of bodily and mental labor alike).
G2872.mounce: to be wearied or spent with labor, faint from weariness, Mt. 11:28; Jn. 4:6; in NT to labor hard, to toil, Lk. 5:5; Jn. 4:38
G2872.abbottSmith: (as in cl.) to grow weary : Mt 11:28 , Jo 4:6 , Re 2:3 (cf. Is 40:31 ).
G2872.strongs: from a derivative of G2873 (κόπος); to feel fatigue; by implication, to work hard

G3611.mounce: to dwell in, inhabit, 1 Tim. 6:16; intrans. to dwell, live; to cohabit, 1 Cor. 7:12, 13; to be indwelling, indwell, Rom. 7:17, 18, 20; 8:9, 11; 1 Cor. 3:16*
G3611.abbottSmith: trans., to inhabit : c. acc., I Ti 6:16 ;
G3611.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος); to occupy a house, i.e. reside (figuratively, inhabit, remain, inhere); by implication, to cohabit

G5277.strongs: a prolonged form for G5275 (ὑπολείπω); to leave behind, i.e. bequeath

G5088.thayer: I bear, bring forth, produce, beget, yield.
G5088.mounce: to bear, bring forth children, Mt. 1:21, 23; trop. to bear, produce, as the earth, yield, Heb. 6:7; met. to give birth to, Jas. 1:15
G5088.strongs: a strengthened form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses); to produce (from seed, as a mother, a plant, the earth, etc.), literally or figuratively
G5088.kjv: bear, be born, bring forth, be delivered, be in travail

G3529.mounce: victory; meton. a victorious principle, 1 Jn. 5:4*
G3529.strongs: apparently a primary word; conquest (abstractly), i.e. (figuratively) the means of success

G3032.thayer: a freedman, one of the class of manumitted slaves; a synagogue at Jerusalem appears to have been reserved for them.
G3032.mounce: a freedman, one who having been a slave has obtained his freedom, or whose father was a freed-man; in NT the λιβερτῖνοι probably denote Jews who had been carried captive to Rome, and subsequently manumitted, Acts 6:9*
G3032.abbottSmith: a freedman : ἡ συναγωγὴ ἡ λεγομένη Λιβερτίνων , Ac 6:9 (Bl. thinks the original reading was Λιβυστίνων , Phil. Gosp. , 69 f.).†
G3032.strongs: of Latin origin; a Roman freedman

G826.thayer: I flash, gleam, shine forth, appear white, bright; but perhaps: I see, see clearly, discern.
G826.mounce: to see distinctly, discern, or possibly to shine, give light at 2 Cor. 4:4*

G2815.thayer: Clement, a fellow-worker of Paul in Rome.
G2815.mounce: Clemens, Clement, pr. name, Latin, Phil. 4:3*
G2815.strongs: of Latin origin; merciful; Clemes (i.e. Clemens), a Christian

G4087.thayer: I make bitter, embitter; pass: I grow angry or harsh.
G4087.mounce: to embitter, render bitter, Rev. 10:9; pass. to be embittered, be made bitter, Rev. 8:11; 10:10; met. pass. to be embittered, to grow angry, harsh, Col. 3:19*
G4087.abbottSmith: to make bitter : Re 10:9 ; pass., Re 8:11 10:10 ; metaph., to embitter (LXX): pass., seq. πρός , Col 3:19 .†
G4087.strongs: from G4089 (πικρός); to embitter (literally or figuratively)

G3685.thayer: I profit, benefit, help; mid: I have profit, derive benefit.
G3685.mounce: optative, ὀναίμην, to receive profit, pleasure, etc.; with a gen., to have joy of, Phlm. 20*
G3685.abbottSmith: to profit , benefit , help ; mid., to have profit , derive benefit : optat. (M, Pr. , 195), c. gen., Phm 20 .†
G3685.strongs: a prolonged form of an apparently primary verb (, to slur); for which another prolonged form () is used as an alternate in some tenses (unless indeed it be identical with the base of G3686 (ὄνομα) through the idea of notoriety); to gratify, i.e. (middle voice) to derive pleasure or advantage from

G379.abbottSmith: without excuse , inexcusable (in Polyb., al., as a forensic term; v. Lft., Notes , 252): Ro 1:20 2:1 .†
G379.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G626 (ἀπολογέομαι); indefensible

G1648.thayer: Eleazar, son of Eliud, and father of Matthan.
G1648.mounce: reproof, 2 Tim. 3:16, a later equivalent to ἔλεγχος*
G1648.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0499); Eleazar, an Israelite

G3605.mounce: to smell, emit an odor; to have an offensive smell, stink, Jn. 11:39*
G3605.abbottSmith: to smell (i.e. emit a smell): Jo 11:39 .†
G3605.strongs: a primary verb (in a strengthened form); to scent (usually an ill "odor")

G674.thayer: I faint, breathe out life, die, am dismayed.
G674.mounce: pr. to breathe out, faint away, die; met. to faint at heart, be dismayed, Lk. 21:26*
G674.abbottSmith: to breathe out life , expire (Thuc.; LXX, IV Mac 15:18 ).
G674.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5594 (ψύχω); to breathe out, i.e. faint

G1003.thayer: Boaz, son of Salmon and Rahab, husband of Ruth, father of Obed.
G1003.mounce: also spelled Βόοζ, Boaz, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:32*
G1003.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01162); Booz, (i.e. Boaz), an Israelite

G3902.mounce: a distinguishing mark; an ensign of a ship, Acts 28:11*
G3902.abbottSmith: in cl., marked amiss , spurious , counterfeit . Later,
G3902.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); side-marked, i.e. labelled (with a badge (figure-head) of a ship)

G3541.abbottSmith: a bastard , base born , i.e. born of a slave or concubine: He 12:8 .†
G3541.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a spurious or illegitimate son

G803.mounce: pr. state of security from falling, firmness; safety, security, 1 Thess. 5:3; certainty, truth, Lk. 1:4; means of security, Acts 5:23*

G3733.mounce: bird, fowl; domestic hen, Mt. 23:37; Lk. 13:34*
G3733.abbottSmith: a bird ; specif., a cock , a hen : Mt 23:3 , Lk 13:34 (WH).†
G3733.strongs: probably from a prolonged form of the base of G3735 (ὄρος); a bird (as rising in the air), i.e. (specially), a hen (or female domestic fowl)

G586.thayer: I take off (deduct) a tenth part (of my property) (and give it away), pay tithe.
G586.mounce: to pay or give tithes of, Mt. 23:23; Lk. 11:42; 18:12; to tithe, levy tithes upon, Heb. 7:5*
G586.abbottSmith: to tithe , pay a tenth of : Lk 18:12 .†
G586.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G1183 (δεκατόω); to tithe (as debtor or creditor)

G2220.mounce: to leaven, cause to ferment, Mt. 13:33; Lk. 13:21; 1 Cor. 5:6; Gal. 5:9*
G2220.abbottSmith: to leaven : Mt 13:33 , Lk 13:21 , I Co 5:6 , Ga 5:9 .†
G2220.strongs: from G2219 (ζύμη); to cause to ferment

G4273.mounce: a betrayer, traitor, Lk. 6:16; Acts 7:52; 2 Tim. 3:4*
G4273.abbottSmith: a betrayer , traitor : Lk 6:16 , Ac 7:52 , II Ti 3:4 .†
G4273.strongs: from G4272 (προδίδωμι) (in the sense of giving forward into another's (the enemy's) hands); a surrender

G1585.thayer: I forget entirely, make to forget.
G1585.mounce: to make to forget; to forget entirely, Heb. 12:5*
G1585.abbottSmith: to escape notice utterly ; mid., to forget utterly : He 12:5 .†
G1585.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2990 (λανθάνω); to be utterly oblivious of

G2805.abbottSmith: crying , weeping , Ac 20:37 ; κ. καὶ ὀδυρμός , Mt 2:18 (LXX) ; ὁ κ. καὶ ὁ βρυγμός , Mt 8:12 13:42 , 50 22:13 24:51 25:30 , Lk 13:28 .†

G2789.thayer: intrans. and trans: I play on the harp, I harp, with acc. of the tune.
G2789.mounce: to play on a lyre or harp, 1 Cor. 14:7; Rev. 14:2*
G2789.abbottSmith: to play upon the lyre or harp : I Co 14:7 , Re 14:2 .†
G2789.strongs: from G2788 (κιθάρα); to play on a lyre

G4762.thayer: I turn, am converted, change, change my direction.
G4762.mounce: to twist; to turn, Mt. 5:39; to make a change of substance, to change, Rev. 11:6; absol. to change or turn one’s course of dealing, Acts 7:42; mid. to turn one’s self about, Mt. 16:23; Lk. 7:9; to turn back, Acts 7:39; to change one’s direction, to turn elsewhere, Acts 13:46; to change one’s course of principle and conduct, to be converted, Mt. 18:3
G4762.abbottSmith: to turn : c. acc. rei dat. pers., Mt 5:39 ; = ἀποσ- , to bring back , Mt 27:3 (cf. Is 38:8 ); reflexively (WM, § 38, 1), to turn oneself , Ac 7:42 ; c. acc. seq. εἰς , = μετασ- , to change , Re 11:6 . Pass., reflexive, to turn oneself : seq. εἰς , Jo 20:14 , Ac 7:39 13:46 ; ptcp., στραφείς , c. indic., Mt 7:6 9:22 16:23 , Lk 7:9 9:55 14:25 22:61 , Jo 1:38 20:16 ; id. seq. πρός , Lk 7:44 10:22 (WH,
G4762.strongs: strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή); to twist, i.e. turn quite around or reverse (literally or figuratively)
G4762.kjv: convert, turn (again, back again, self, self about)

G1782.thayer: hence, from this place, on this side and on that.
G1782.mounce: hence, from this place, Mt. 17:20; Lk. 4:9; ἐντεῦθεν καὶ ἐντεῦθεν, on each side, Rev. 22:2; hence, from this cause, Jas. 4:1
G1782.abbottSmith: of place, hence : Lk 4:9 13:31 , Jo 2:16 7:3 14:31 18:36 ; ἐ. καὶ ἐ. (for cl. ἔνθεν κ. ἔνθεν ), on this side and on that, on each side , Jo 19:18 ; similarly, ἐ. καὶ ἐκεῖθεν , Re 22:2 .
G1782.strongs: from the same as G1759 (ἐνθάδε); hence (literally or figuratively); (repeated) on both sides

G1163.thayer: it is necessary, inevitable; less frequently: it is a duty, what is proper.
G1163.mounce: it is binding, it is necessary, it is proper; it is inevitable, Acts 21:22
G1163.abbottSmith: one must, it is necessary : c. inf., Mt 26:54 , Mk 13:7 , Ac 5:29 , al.; c. acc. et inf., Mt 16:21 , Mk 8:31 , Jo 3:7 , Ac 25:10 , al.; with ellipse of acc., Mt 23:23 ; of acc., and inf., Mk 13:14 , Ro 1:27 8:26 ; οὐ (μὴ) δεῖ ( non licet ), ought not, must not : Ac 25:24 , II Ti 2:24 ; impf., ἔδει , of necessity or obligation in past time regarding a past event (Bl., § 63, 4), Mt 18:33 , Lk 15:32
G1163.strongs: 3rd person singular active present of G1210 (δέω); also deon deh-on'; neuter active participle of the same; both used impersonally; it is (was, etc.) necessary (as binding)
G1163.kjv: behoved, be meet, must (needs), (be) need(-ful), ought, should

G3872.abbottSmith: more usual than its variant in Gk. writers for a trust or deposit : I Ti 6:20 , II Ti 1:14 , Rec.†
G3872.strongs: from a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G2698 (κατατίθημι); something put down alongside, i.e. a deposit (sacred trust)

G1382.thayer: a trial, proof; tried, approved character.
G1382.mounce: trial, proof by trial, 2 Cor. 8:2; the state or disposition of that which has been tried and approved, approved character or temper, Rom. 5:4; 2 Cor. 2:9; Phil. 2:22; proof, document, evidence, 2 Cor. 8:2; 13:3*
G1382.abbottSmith: the process of trial, proving, test : II Co 8:2 9:13 .
G1382.strongs: from the same as G1384 (δόκιμος); test (abstractly or concretely); by implication, trustiness

G5382.mounce: kind to strangers, hospitable, 1 Tim. 3:2; Tit. 1:8; 1 Pet. 4:9*
G5382.abbottSmith: loving strangers, hospitable : I Ti 3:2 , Tit 1:8 , I Pe 4:9 .†
G5382.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G3581 (ξένος); fond of guests, i.e. hospitable

G570.abbottSmith: want of faith , unbelief : Mt 13:58 , Mk 6:6 9:24 16:14 , Ro 3:3 (but v.s. ἀπιστέω ) Ro 4:20 11:20 , 23 , I Ti 1:13 , He 3:12 , 19 ( DCG , ii, 775 a ; Cremer, 492).†
G570.strongs: from G571 (ἄπιστος); faithlessness, i.e. (negatively) disbelief (lack of Christian faith), or (positively) unfaithfulness (disobedience)

G2637.thayer: slanderous, back-biting; subst: a railer, defamer.
G2637.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G2980 (λαλέω); talkative against, i.e. a slanderer

G2080.thayer: within, inside, with verbs either of rest or of motion; prep: within, to within, inside.
G2080.mounce: can function as an improper prep., in, within, in the interior of, Mt. 26:58; Jn. 20:26; ὁ, ἡ, τὸ ἔσω, inner, interior, internal; met. within the pale of community, 1 Cor. 5:12; ὁ ἐσω ἄνθρωπος, the inner man, the mind, soul, Rom. 7:22
G2080.abbottSmith: prop., after verbs of motion ( to ) within, into : Mt 26:58 , Mk 14:54 ; c. gen., Mk 15:16 .
G2080.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς); inside (as preposition or adjective)

G354.mounce: a taking up, receiving up, Lk. 9:51*
G354.abbottSmith: a taking up : Lk 9:51 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G2076.strongs: third person singular present indicative of G1510 (εἰμί); he (she or it) is; also (with neuter plural) they are
G2076.kjv: are, be(-long), call, X can(-not), come, consisteth, X dure for a while, + follow, X have, (that) is (to say), make, meaneth, X must needs, + profit, + remaineth, + wrestle

G4608.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07941); an intoxicant, i.e. intensely fermented liquor

G1471.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G2949 (κῦμα); swelling inside, i.e. pregnant

G2199.thayer: Zebedee, father of the apostles James and John.
G2199.mounce: Zebedee, pr. name, the father of Jas. and John, Mt. 4:21; Mk. 10:35; Lk. 5:10; Jn. 21:1*
G2199.abbottSmith: Zebedee , father of James and John the Apostles: Mt 4:21 10:2 20:20 26:37 27:56 , Mk1:19 , 20 3:17 10:35 , Lk 5:10 , Jo 21:2 .†
G2199.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H02067); Zebedæus, an Israelite

G2712.thayer: full of images of idols, grossly idolatrous.
G2712.mounce: rife with idols, sunk in idolatry, grossly idolatrous, Acts 17:16*
G2712.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) (intensively) and G1497 (εἴδωλον); utterly idolatrous

G3015.strongs: from the same as G3014 (λέπρα); scaly, i.e. leprous (a leper)

G1041.thayer: an altar, platform; a slightly-elevated spot.
G1041.mounce: pr. a slightly-elevated spot, base, pedestal; hence, an altar, Acts 17:23*
G1041.abbottSmith: any raised place, a platform . an altar : Ac 17:23 (cf. θυσιαστήριον and v. DB , i, 75).†
G1041.strongs: from the base of G939 (βάσις); properly, a stand, i.e. (specially) an altar

G4837.thayer: I invite, encourage along with others; pass: I share in encouragement with.
G4837.mounce: to invite, exhort along with others; pass. to share in mutual encouragement, Rom. 1:12*
G4837.abbottSmith: to call upon or exhort together (Plat.). Pass., to be strengthened or comforted with and among : Ro 1:12 .†
G4837.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3870 (παρακαλέω); to console jointly

G919.thayer: Bar-Jesus, the name of the magician and false prophet at Paphos in Cyprus; he is also called Elymas.
G919.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and H03091); son of Jesus (or Joshua); Bar-jesus, an Israelite

G380.mounce: to roll back, unroll, unfold, Lk. 4:17*
G380.abbottSmith: to unroll : τ. βιβλίον , Lk 4:17 (WH, R, ἀνοίξας ).†
G380.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) (in the sense of reversal) and G4428 (πτύσσω); to unroll (a scroll or volume)

G4851.thayer: I bring together, collect; I am profitable to.
G4851.abbottSmith: trans. to bring together : c. acc. rei, Ac 19:19 .
G4851.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate); to bear together (contribute), i.e. (literally) to collect, or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as a noun) advantage
G4851.kjv: be better for, bring together, be expedient (for), be good, (be) profit(-able for)

G4137.mounce: pluperf., πεπληρώκει (3 sg), to fill, make full, fill up, Mt. 13:48; 23:32; Lk. 3:5; to fill up a deficiency, Phil. 4:18, 19; to pervade, Jn. 12:3; Acts 2:2; to pervade with an influence, to influence fully, possess fully, Jn. 16:6; Acts 2:28; 5:3; Rom. 1:29; Eph. 5:18; to complete, perfect, Jn. 3:29; Eph. 3:19; to bring to an end, Lk. 7:1; to perform fully, discharge, Mt. 3:15; Acts 12:25; 13:25; 14:26; Rom. 13:8; Col. 4:17; to consummate, Mt. 5:17; to realize, accomplish, fulfil, Lk. 1:20; 9:31; Acts 3:18; 13:27; from the Hebrew; to set forth fully, Rom. 15:19; Col. 1:25; pass. of time, to be fulfilled, come to an end, be fully arrived, Mk. 1:15; Lk. 21:24; Jn. 7:8; of prophecy, to receive fulfillment, Mt. 1:22, et al. freq.
G4137.abbottSmith: to fill , make full , fill to the full , c. acc.; of things: pass. ( σαψήνη , φάραγξ ), Mt 13:48 , Lk 3:5 (LXX) ; fig., Mt 23:32 ; but chiefly of immaterial things: πᾶσαν χρείαν , Phl 4:19 ; ἦχος ἐπλήρωσε τ. οἶκον , Ac 2:2 ; c. gen. rei (cl.), Ac 5:28 ; pass., seq. ἐκ , Jo 12:3 (cf. B1., § 36, 4); π. τ. καρδίαν , Jo 16:6 , Ac 5:3 ; metaph., of the all-pervading activity of Christ, Eph 4:10 ; mid.,
G4137.strongs: from G4134 (πλήρης); to make replete, i.e. (literally) to cram (a net), level up (a hollow), or (figuratively) to furnish (or imbue, diffuse, influence), satisfy, execute (an office), finish (a period or task), verify (or coincide with a prediction), etc.
G4137.kjv: accomplish, X after, (be) complete, end, expire, fill (up), fulfil, (be, make) full (come), fully preach, perfect, supply

G2416.thayer: I commit sacrilege, rob a temple.
G2416.mounce: to despoil temples, commit sacrilege, Rom. 2:22*
G2416.abbottSmith: to rob a temple ( commit sacrilege , R, mg.): Ro 2:22 .†
G2416.strongs: from G2417 (ἱερόσυλος); to be a temple-robber (figuratively)

G4727.thayer: I groan, expressing grief, anger, or desire.
G4727.mounce: to groan, sigh, Rom. 8:23; 2 Cor. 5:2, 4; Heb. 13:17; to sigh inwardly, Mk. 7:34; to give vent to querulous or censorious feelings, Jas. 5:9*
G4727.abbottSmith: to groan ("the word denotes feeling which is internal and unexpressed," Mayor, Ja. , 162): Mk 7:34 , II Co 5:2 , 4 , He 13:17 , Ja 5:9 (EV, murmur ); ἐν ἑαυτοῖς , Ro 8:23 (cf. ἀνα- , συ(ν)-στενάζω ).†
G4727.strongs: from G4728 (στενός); to make (intransitively, be) in straits, i.e. (by implication) to sigh, murmur, pray inaudibly

G3771.strongs: from G3772 (οὐρανός) and the enclitic of source; from the sky

G5460.mounce: radiant, lustrous, Mt. 17:5; enlightened, illuminated, Mt. 6:22; Lk. 11:34, 36 (2x)*
G5460.abbottSmith: bright, light : νεφέλη , Mt 17:5 ; opp. to σκοτεινός , Mt 6:22 , Lk 11:34 , 36 .†
G5460.strongs: from G5457 (φῶς); lustrous, i.e. transparent or well-illuminated (figuratively)

G335.mounce: pr. shamelessness; hence, persistence, without regard to time, place, or person, Lk. 11:8*
G335.abbottSmith: shamelessness , importunity : Lk 11:8 (for exx. from π. , v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G335.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle (compare G427 (ἄνευ))) and G127 (αἰδώς); impudence, i.e. (by implication) importunity

G306.thayer: I postpone, defer, especially: I postpone the trial of.
G306.mounce: to throw back; mid. to put off, defer, adjourn, Acts 24:22*
G306.abbottSmith: to defer , put off (MM, VGT , s.v.): mid., Ac 24:22 .†
G306.strongs: middle voice from G303 (ἀνά) and G906 (βάλλω); to put off (for oneself)

G1328.abbottSmith: an interpreter : I Co 14:28 (WH, mg.).† {Editor note:verse reference has διερμηνευτής , instead of this form}

G237.thayer: from another place, by another way.

G768.thayer: Asher, one of the sons of Jacob, and founder of one of the Twelve Tribes.
G768.mounce: Asher, pr. name, indecl. (Gen. 30:13; 49:20; 2 Chr. 30:11) Lk. 2:36; Rev. 7:6*
G768.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0836); Aser (i.e. Asher), an Israelite tribe

G1769.strongs: from G1770 (ἐννεύω); dumb (as making signs), i.e. silent from astonishment

G4602.strongs: apparently from (to hiss, i.e. hist or hush); silence

G427.thayer: without, without the cooperation (or knowledge) of.
G427.mounce: some classify as an improper preposition, without, Mt. 10:29; 1 Pet. 3:1; 4:9*

G3827.abbottSmith: very much , very great : Mk 8:1 , Rec. (RV, Edd., πάλιν πολλοῦ ).†
G3827.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and G4183 (πολύς); full many, i.e. immense

G2846.abbottSmith: a bed-chamber ; ὁ ἐπὶ τοῦ κ. , a chamberlain : Ac 12:20 .†

G1400.mounce: a male slave, or servant, of various degrees, Mt. 8:9, et al. freq.; a servitor, person of mean condition, Phil. 2:7; fem. δούλη, a female slave; a handmaiden, Lk. 1:38, 48; Acts 2:18; δοῦλος, used figuratively, in a bad sense, one involved in moral or spiritual thraldom, Jn. 8:34; Rom. 6:17, 20; 1 Cor. 7:23; 2 Pet. 2:19; in a good sense, a devoted servant or minister, Acts 16:17; Rom. 1:1; one pledged or bound to serve, 1 Cor. 7:22; 2 Cor. 4:5

G1732.thayer: a showing, proof, demonstration, sign, token.
G1732.mounce: a pointing out; met. manifestation, public declaration, Rom. 3:25, 26; a token, sign, proof, i.q. ἔνδειγμα, 2 Cor. 8:24; Phil. 1:28*
G1732.abbottSmith: a pointing out, showing forth, proof (v.s. ἔνδειγμα ): Ro 3:25 , 26 , II Co 8:24 , Phl 1:28 .†

G2727.mounce: pr. to sound in the ears, make the ears ring; to instruct orally, to instruct, inform, 1 Cor. 14:19; pass. to be taught, be instructed, Lk. 1:4; Rom. 2:18; Gal. 6:6; to be made acquainted, Acts 18:25; to receive information, hear report, Acts 21:21, 24*
G2727.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2279 (ἦχος); to sound down into the ears, i.e. (by implication) to indoctrinate ("catechize") or (genitive case) to apprise of

G111.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of (statute; from the base of G5087 (τίθημι)); illegal; by implication, flagitious

G269.thayer: peaceable, abstaining from fighting, not contentious.
G269.mounce: not disposed to fight; not quarrelsome or contentious, 1 Tim. 3:3; Tit. 3:2*
G269.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3163 (μάχη); peaceable

G2291.mounce: Terah, Abraham’s father, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:34*
G2291.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08646); Thara (i.e. Terach), the father of Abraham

G1889.thayer: Epaphras, Epaphroditus, a Colossian Christian, in captivity with Paul in Rome.
G1889.strongs: contracted from G1891 (Ἐπαφρόδιτος); Epaphras, a Christian

G1626.thayer: (strictly: a lifeless abortion) an untimely birth.
G1626.mounce: an abortion, baby prematurely born, 1 Cor. 15:8*
G1626.abbottSmith: an abortion, an untimely birth (v. Field, Notes , 179): I Co 15:8 .†
G1626.strongs: from a comparative of G1537 (ἐκ) and (to wound); a miscarriage (abortion), i.e. (by analogy) untimely birth

G397.thayer: I rear, bring up, nourish, educate.
G397.mounce: to nurse, as an infant, Acts 7:20; to bring up, educate, Acts 7:21; 22:3*
G397.abbottSmith: to nurse up , nourish , educate , bring up : Lk 4:16 , WΗ, mg., Ac 7:20 , 21 22:3 .†
G397.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G5142 (τρέφω); to rear (physically or mentally)

G1888.abbottSmith: prop. with ref. to theft, then generally, in the very act : as freq., neut. dat. after ἐπί , Jo 8:4 (Rec., ἐπαυτοφώρῳ ).†
G1888.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G846 (αὐτός) and (the dative case singular of) a derivative of (a thief); in theft itself, i.e. (by analogy) in actual crime

G3811.thayer: (a) I discipline, educate, train, (b) more severely: I chastise.
G3811.mounce: to educate, instruct children, Acts 7:22; 22:3; genr. παιδεύομαι, to be taught, learn, 1 Tim. 1:20; to admonish, instruct by admonition, 2 Tim. 2:25; Tit. 2:12; in NT to chastise, chasten, 1 Cor. 11:32; 2 Cor. 6:9; Heb. 12:6, 7, 10; Rev. 3:19; of criminals, to scourge, Lk. 23:16, 22*
G3811.abbottSmith: as in cl., to train children , hence, generally, to teach , instruct : Ac 7:22 22:3 , I Ti 1:20 , Tit 2:12 .
G3811.strongs: from G3816 (παῖς); to train up a child, i.e. educate, or (by implication), discipline (by punishment)

G4178.mounce: many times, often, frequently, Mt. 17:15; Mk. 5:4; 9:22
G4178.strongs: multiplicative adverb from G4183 (πολύς); many times, i.e. frequently

G3359.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and ops (the face); the forehead (as opposite the countenance)

G4138.thayer: (a) a fill, fullness; full complement; supply, patch, supplement, (b) fullness, filling, fulfillment, completion.
G4138.mounce: that which fills up; full measure, entire content, Mk. 8:20; 1 Cor. 10:26, 28; complement, full extent, full number, Gal. 4:4; Eph. 1:10; that which fills up a deficiency, a supplement, a patch, Mt. 9:16; fulness, abundance, Jn. 1:16; full measure, Rom. 15:29; a fulfilling, perfect performance, Rom. 13:10; complete attainment of entire belief, full acceptance, Rom. 11:12; full development, plenitude, Eph. 4:13; Col. 1:19; 2:9
G4138.abbottSmith: the result of the action involved in πληρόω (Lft., Col. , 255 ff.; AR, Eph. , 255 ff.), hence, in passive sense, that which has been completed , complement , plenitude , fullness (in Xen., Luc., Polyb., al., of a ship's crew or cargo, and by meton. of the ship itself): Jo 1:16 , Ro 11:12 , 25 15:29 , I Co 10:26 (LXX) , Eph 1:23 (AR, 42 ff.) 3:19 (AR. 87 ff.) 4:13 , Col 1:19 2:9 ; of time, Ga 4:4 ,
G4138.strongs: from G4137 (πληρόω); repletion or completion, i.e. (subjectively) what fills (as contents, supplement, copiousness, multitude), or (objectively) what is filled (as container, performance, period)
G4138.kjv: which is put in to fill up, piece that filled up, fulfilling, full, fulness

G5448.thayer: I inflate, puff up; pass: I am puffed up, arrogant, proud.
G5448.mounce: to inflate puff up; met. to inflate with pride and vanity, 1 Cor. 8:1; pass. to be inflated with pride, to be proud, vain, arrogant, 1 Cor. 4:6, 18, 19; 5:2; 13:4; Col. 2:18*
G5448.abbottSmith: to puff or blow up, inflate . Metaph., to puff up, make proud : I Co 8:1 . Pass., to be puffed up with pride: I Co 4:18 , 19 5:2 13:4 ; seq. ὑπό , Col 2:18 ; seq. ὑπὲρ . . . κατά , I Co 4:6 (on the form of the subjc., v. M, Pr. , 54; Bl., § 22, 3).†
G5448.strongs: from G5449 (φύσις) in the primary sense of blowing; to inflate, i.e. (figuratively) make proud (haughty)

G2192.mounce: pluperfect., ἐσχήκειν, to hold, Rev. 1:16; to seize, possess a person, Mk. 16:8; to have, possess, Mt. 7:29, et al. freq.; to have, have ready, be furnished with, Mt. 5:23; Jn. 5:36; 6:68; to have as a matter of crimination, Mt. 5:23; Mk. 11:25; to have at command, Mt. 27:65; to have the power, be able, Mt. 18:25; Lk. 14:14; Acts 4:14; to have in marriage, Mt. 14:4; to have, be affected by, subjected to, Mt. 3:14; 12:10; Mk. 3:10; Jn. 12:48; 15:22, 24; 16:21, 22; Acts 23:29; 1 Tim. 5:12; Heb. 7:28; 1 Jn. 1:8; 4:18; χάραν ἔχειν, to feel gratitude, be thankful, 1 Tim. 1:12; 2 Tim. 1:3; Phlm. 7; to hold, esteem, regard, Mt. 14:5; Lk. 14:18, 19; to have or hold as an object of knowledge, faith, or practice, Jn. 5:38, 42; 14:21; 1 Jn. 5:12; 2 Jn. 9; to hold on in entire possession, to retain, Rom. 15:4; 2 Tim. 1:13; Heb. 12:28; intrans. with adverbs or adverbial expression, to be, to fare, Mt. 9:12; Mk. 2:17; 5:23; Lk. 5:31; Jn. 4:52; Acts 7:1; 12:15; 15:36; 21:13; 2 Cor. 10:6; 12:14; 1 Tim. 5:25; 1 Pet. 4:5; τὸ νῦν ἔχον, for the present; in NT ἔχειν ἐν γαστρί, to be pregnant, Mt. 1:18; as also ἔχειν κοίτην, Rom. 9:10; ἔχειν δαιμόνιον, to be possessed, Mt. 11:18; of time, to have continued, to have lived, Jn. 5:5, 6; 8:57; of space, to embrace, be distant, Acts 1:12; mid. pr. to hold by, cling to; hence, to border upon, be next, Mk. 1:38; Lk. 13:33; Acts 20:15; 21:26; to tend immediately to, Heb. 6:9
G2192.abbottSmith: to have , as in cl., in various senses and constructions. Trans.; to have , hold , hold fast , etc.; to hold , as, in the hand: Re 5:8 14:6 , al.; ἐν τ. χειρί , Re 1:16 10:2 , al.; of arms and clothing, = φέρω , φορέω , to bear , wear : Mt 3:4 22:12 , al.; so freq. pres. ptcp. (LS, s.v., A, I, 6; Bl., § 74, 2), Mk 11:13 , Jo 18:10 , Re 9:17 , al.; of a woman, ἐν γαστρὶ ἔ ( κοίτην ἔ. ), to be with
G2192.strongs: a primary verb; to hold (used in very various applications, literally or figuratively, direct or remote; such as possession; ability, contiuity, relation, or condition)
G2192.kjv: be (able, X hold, possessed with), accompany, + begin to amend, can(+ -not), X conceive, count, diseased, do + eat, + enjoy, + fear, following, have, hold, keep, + lack, + go to law, lie, + must needs, + of necessity, + need, next, + recover, + reign, + rest, + return, X sick, take for, + tremble, + uncircumcised, use

G2368.mounce: incense, any odoriferous substance burnt in religious worship, Rev. 5:8; 8:3, 4; 18:13; or, the act of burning incense, Lk. 1:10, 11*
G2368.abbottSmith: fragrant stuff for burning, incense : ἡ ὥρα τοῦ θ. , Lk 1:10 ; θυσιαστήριον τοῦ θ. ( Ex 30:27 , al.), Lk 1:11 ; pl., Re 5:8 8:3 , 4 18:13 .†
G2368.strongs: from G2370 (θυμιάω); an aroma, i.e. fragrant powder burnt in religious service; by implication, the burning itself

G1612.thayer: I change for the worse, corrupt, pervert.
G1612.mounce: pr. to turn out of, to turn inside out; hence, to change entirely; in NT pass. to be perverted, Tit. 3:11*
G1612.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4762 (στρέφω); to pervert (figuratively)

G55.thayer: purely, sincerely, with pure motives, honestly.
G55.strongs: adverb from G53 (ἁγνός); purely, i.e. honestly

G30.abbottSmith: a vessel (v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 25:4 .†
G30.strongs: from (a pail, perhaps as bent; compare the base of G43 (ἀγκάλη)); a receptacle

G1582.thayer: I hang out, mid: I hang upon.
G1582.mounce: mid., to hang upon a speaker, fondly listen to, be earnestly attentive, Lk. 19:48*
G1582.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2910 (κρεμάννυμι); to hang upon the lips of a speaker, i.e. listen closely

G534.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G737 (ἄρτι); from now, i.e. henceforth (already)

G2825.thayer: a couch, bed, portable bed or mat, a couch for reclining at meals, possibly also a bier.
G2825.mounce: a couch, bed, Mt. 9:2, 6; Mk. 4:21; Rev. 2:22
G2825.strongs: from G2827 (κλίνω); a couch (for sleep, sickness, sitting or eating)

G2172.mounce: to pray, offer prayer, Acts 26:29; 2 Cor. 13:7, 9; Jas. 5:16; to wish, desire, Acts 27:29; Rom. 9:3; 3 Jn. 2*
G2172.abbottSmith: to pray : c. acc rei, II Co 13:9 ; c. dat. pers., τ. θεῷ , Ac 26:29 ; seq. πρὸς τ. θεόν , II Co 13:7 ; c. acc et inf., Ac 27:29 , III Jo 2 ; seq. ὑπέρ , Ja 5:16 ; ηὐχόμην εἶναι (on impf. here, v. ICC , in l., Lft., Phm 13 ), Ro 9:3 (Cremer, 718).†
G2172.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to wish; by implication, to pray to God

G4616.mounce: sindon; pr. fine Indian cloth; fine linen; in NT a linen garment, an upper garment or wrapper of fine linen, worn in summer by night, and used to envelope dead bodies, Mt. 27:59; Mk. 14:51, 52; 15:46; Lk. 23:53*
G4616.strongs: of uncertain (perhaps foreign) origin; byssos, i.e. bleached linen (the cloth or a garment of it)

G3338.thayer: (lit: I change one care or interest for another), I change my mind (generally for the better), repent, regret.
G3338.mounce: to change one’s judgment on past points of conduct; to change one’s mind and purpose, Heb. 7:21; to repent, regret, Mt. 21:29, 32; 27:3; 2 Cor. 7:8*
G3338.abbottSmith: depon., pass., to regret , repent one : Mt 21:30 , 32 27:3 , II Co 7:8 , He 7:21 (LXX) .†
G3338.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and the middle voice of G3199 (μέλω); to care afterwards, i.e. regret

G38.thayer: the process of making or becoming holy, set apart, sanctification, holiness, consecration.
G38.abbottSmith: as an active verbal noun in -μός , it signifies properly the process τὸ ἁγιάζειν , rather than the resultant state, ἁγιωσύνη , hence, consecration ; sanctification : so strictly in Ro 6:19 , 22 (but v. Meyer), I Co 1:30 , I Th 4:3 , 7 , II Th 2:13 , He 12:14 , I Pe 1:2 . Elsewhere it perhaps (Ellic.; but v. Milligan, Th. , 48) inclines to the resultant state: I Th 4:4 , I Ti 2:15 (Cremer, 55, 602)
G38.strongs: from G37 (ἁγιάζω); properly, purification, i.e. (the state) purity; concretely (by Hebraism) a purifier

G3738.strongs: middle voice from (a row or ring); to dance (from the ranklike or regular motion)

G2229.strongs: an adverb of confirmation; perhaps intensive of G2228 (ἤ); used only (in the New Testament) before G3303 (μέν); assuredly

G882.thayer: the Roman Province Achaia, governed by a proconsul, and practically conterminous with modern Greece before 1912.
G882.mounce: Achaia, the Roman province, comprehending all Greece to the south of Thessaly
G882.abbottSmith: Achaia , the Roman province: Ac 18:12 , 27 19:21 , Ro 15:26 , I Co 16:15 , II Co 1:1 9:2 11:10 , I Th 1:7 , 8 .†
G882.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Achaia (i.e. Greece), a country of Europe

G5549.thayer: I delay, tarry, linger, spend time.
G5549.mounce: to spend time; to linger, delay, be long, Mt. 24:48; 25:5; Lk. 1:21; 12:45; Heb. 10:37*
G5549.abbottSmith: to spend or take time, to tarry, linger, delay : Mt 24:48 25:5 , He 10:37 ; seq. ἐν , c. dat. loc., Lk 1:21 ; c. inf., Lk 12:45 .†
G5549.strongs: from G5550 (χρόνος); to take time, i.e. linger

G29.thayer: I impress (into my service), send (on an errand).
G29.mounce: to press, or compel another to go somewhere, or carry some burden, Mt. 5:41; 27:32; Mk. 15:21*
G29.abbottSmith: to impress into public service, employ a courier; hence, to compel to perform a service (prob. common in the vernac.; cf. Deiss., BS , 86f., MM, Exp. , iv; VGT , s.v.): Mt 5:41 , 27:32 , Mk 15:21 .†
G29.strongs: of foreign origin (compare H0104); properly, to be a courier, i.e. (by implication) to press into public service

G1186.abbottSmith: a tree : Mt 3:10 , al.; δ. ἀγαθόν , Mt 7:17, 18 ; δ. καλόν , Mt 12:33 , Lk 6:43 ; δ. σαπρόν , Mt 7:17 , 18 12:33 , Lk 6:43 ; γίνεσθαι δ. , Mt 13:32 ; γ. εἰς δ. , Lk 13:19 .

G89.mounce: unceasingly, by an unvarying practice, Rom. 1:9; 1 Thess. 1:2; 2:13; 5:17*
G89.strongs: adverb from G88 (ἀδιάλειπτος); uninterruptedly, i.e. without omission (on an appropriate occasion)

G5329.thayer: Perez, son of Judah and one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G5329.mounce: Perezs, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:3; Lk. 3:33*
G5329.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06557); Phares (i.e. Perets), an Israelite

G5112.thayer: more boldly, more freely, with more confidence.
G5112.strongs: neuter of the compound of a derivative of the base of 5111 (as adverb); more daringly, i.e. with greater confidence than otherwise

G2640.abbottSmith: a remnant : Ro 9:27 , Rec. (for ὑπόλειμμα , q.v.).†
G2640.strongs: from G2641 (καταλείπω); a remainder, i.e. (by implication) a few

G2533.mounce: Caiaphas, pr. name, the high priest from A.D. 18-36, Mt. 26:3, 57; Lk. 3:2; Jn. 11:49; 18:13f., 24, 28; Acts 4:6*
G2533.abbottSmith: Caiaphas (on the name, v. Exp. Times , x, 185): Mt 26:3 , 57 , Lk 3:2 , Jo 11:49 18:13 , 14 , 24 , 28 , Ac 4:6 .†
G2533.strongs: of Chaldee origin; the dell; Caiaphas (i.e. Cajepha), an Israelite

G4737.mounce: to crown; to crown as victor in the games,
G4737.strongs: from G4735 (στέφανος); to adorn with an honorary wreath (literally or figuratively)

G4984.mounce: bodily, of or belonging to the body, 1 Tim. 4:8; corporeal, material, Lk. 3:22*
G4984.abbottSmith: of or for the body, bodily : γυμνασία , I Ti 4:8 ;

G1610.thayer: I root out, pluck up by the roots.
G1610.mounce: to root up, eradicate, pull out by the roots, Mt. 13:29; 15:13; Lk. 17:6; Jude 12*
G1610.abbottSmith: to root out, pluck up by the roots : c. acc rei, Mt 13:29 15:13 Lk 17:6 , Ju 12 .†
G1610.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4492 (ῥιζόω); to uproot
G1610.kjv: pluck up by the root, root up

G792.mounce: a star, luminous body like a star, luminary, Mt. 2:2, 7, 9, 10; Rev. 1:16
G792.abbottSmith: a star : Mt 2:2 , 7 , 9 , 10 , Mk 13:25 , I Co 15:41 , Re 6:13 8:10 , 11 , 12 9:1 12:1 , 4 ; metaph., ὁ ἀ. ὁ πρωϊνός Re 2:28 22:16 ; ἀ. πλαῆται , Ju 13 ; ἀ. ἑπτά , symbolizing the angels of the seven churches, Re 1:16 , 20 2:1 3:1 (cf. ἄστρον , and v. DCG , ii, 674 f.; MM, s.v.).†
G792.strongs: probably from the base of G4766 (στρώννυμι); a star (as strown over the sky), literally or figuratively

G4042.thayer: a section or passage of Scripture.
G4042.mounce: lit., a compass, circumference, contents; fig., a section, a portion of Scripture, Acts 8:32*
G4042.strongs: from G4023 (περιέχω); a being held around, i.e. (concretely) a passage (of Scripture, as circumscribed)

G3880.thayer: I take from, receive from, or: I take to, receive (apparently not used of money), admit, acknowledge; I take with me.
G3880.mounce: pr. to take to one’s side; to take, receive to one’s self, Mt. 1:20; Jn. 14:3; to take with one’s self, Mt. 2:13, 14, 20, 21; 4:5, 8; to receive in charge or possession, Col. 4:17; Heb. 12:28; to receive as a matter of instruction, Mk. 7:4; 1 Cor. 11:23; 15:3; to receive, admit, acknowledge, Jn. 1:11; 1 Cor. 15:1; Col. 2:6; pass. to be carried off, Mt. 24:40, 41; Lk. 17:34, 35, 36
G3880.abbottSmith: c. acc. rei, like παραδέχομαι , correl. to παραδίδωμι , to receive from another: Col 4:17 , He 12:28 ; of the mind, I Co 11:23 15:1 , 3 , Ga 1:9 , Phl 4:9 ; c. inf., Mk 7:4 ; seq. παρά , c. gen., Ga 1:12 , I Th 2:13 4:1 , II Th 3:6 ; τ. Χριστόν (Lft., in l.), Col 2:6 .
G3880.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to receive near, i.e. associate with oneself (in any familiar or intimate act or relation); by analogy, to assume an office; figuratively, to learn

G2877.thayer: a little girl, a young girl; a girl, maiden.
G2877.abbottSmith: a colloquial word which survives in MGr. (Kennedy, Sourcs , 154), girl , maiden : Mt 9:24 , 25 14:11 , Mk 5:41 , 42 6:22 , 28 .†
G2877.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a maiden); a (little) girl

G1369.thayer: I cut asunder; met: I set at variance, make to be hostile.
G1369.mounce: to cut asunder, disunite; met. to cause to disagree, set at variance, Mt. 10:35*
G1369.abbottSmith: to cut apart, divide in two ; metaph., to set at variance : Mt 10:35 .†
G1369.strongs: from a derivative of G1364 (δίς); to make apart, i.e. sunder (figuratively, alienate)

G1114.thayer: a conjuror, juggler, sorcerer; a tricky (crafty) deceiver, imposter.
G1114.mounce: a juggler, diviner; hence, by impl. an impostor, cheat, 2 Tim. 3:13*
G1114.abbottSmith: a wailer . a wizard . an impostor (cf. γοητεία , trickery , II Mac 12:24 ): II Ti 3:13 .†
G1114.strongs: from (to wail); properly, a wizard (as muttering spells), i.e. (by implication) an imposter

G478.mounce: trans. to set in opposition; intrans. to withstand, resist, Heb. 12:4*
G478.abbottSmith: causal in pres. impf. fut. and 1 aor.; to replace , oppose .
G478.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G2525 (καθίστημι); to set down (troops) against, i.e. withstand

G2721.thayer: I rush, assault, rise up against.
G2721.mounce: to come upon suddenly, rush upon, assault, Acts 18:12*
G2721.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2186 (ἐφίστημι); to stand over against, i.e. rush upon (assault)

G1221.strongs: from G1211 (δή) and G4218 (ποτέ); a particle of generalization; indeed, at any time

G3818.abbottSmith: Pacatiana , the Western part of the Province of Phrygia, as constituted in iv/A.D.: 1Ti subscr. (Rec.).†
G3818.strongs: feminine of an adjective of uncertain derivation; Pacatianian, a section of Phrygia

G899.thayer: depth; deep water; met: fullness, immensity; an extreme degree; profundities, deep-laid plans.
G899.mounce: depth; τὸ βάθος, deep water, Lk. 5:4; Mt. 13:5; met. fullness, abundance, immensity, Rom. 11:33; an extreme degree, 2 Cor. 8:2; pl. profundities, deep-laid plans, 1 Cor. 2:10; Rev. 2:24
G899.abbottSmith: depth : Mt 13:5 , Mk 4:5 , Ro 8:39 , Eph 3:18 ; τὸ β. , the deep sea : Lk 5:4 ; metaph., β. πλούτου . . . Θεοῦ , Ro 11:33 ; τὰ β. τ. Θεοῦ (the Divine counsels), I Co 2:10 ; ἡ κατὰ βάθους πτωχεία , deep poverty , II Co 8:2 .†
G899.strongs: from the same as G901 (βαθύς); profundity, i.e. (by implication) extent; (figuratively) mystery

G3648.thayer: complete in every part, sound, perfect, entire.
G3648.mounce: whole, having all its parts, sound, perfect, complete in every part; in NT the whole, 1 Thess. 5:23; morally, perfect, faultless, blameless, Jas. 1:4*
G3648.abbottSmith: complete , entire ; in NT in ethical sense (as Wi 15:3 , IV Mac 15:17 ), I Th 5:23 ; ὁ. καὶ τέλειοι , Ja 1:4 .†
G3648.strongs: from G3650 (ὅλος) and G2819 (κλῆρος); complete in every part, i.e. perfectly sound (in body)

G5278.thayer: (a) I remain behind, (b) I stand my ground, show endurance, (c) I endure, bear up against, persevere.
G5278.mounce: intrans. to remain or stay behind, when others have departed, Lk. 2:43; trans. to bear up under, endure, suffer patiently, 1 Cor. 13:7; Heb. 10:32; absol. to continue firmly, hold out, remain constant, persevere, Mt. 10:22; 24:13
G5278.abbottSmith: in-trans., to stay behind : seq. ἐν , Lk 2:43 ; ἐκεῖ , Ac 17:14 .
G5278.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3306 (μένω); to stay under (behind), i.e. remain; figuratively, to undergo, i.e. bear (trials), have fortitude, persevere
G5278.kjv: abide, endure, (take) patient(-ly), suffer, tarry behind

G1032.thayer: I cause to gush forth, send forth.
G1032.mounce: pr. to be full, to swell with anything; to emit, send forth, Jas. 3:11*
G1032.abbottSmith: to be full to bursting ; of the earth producing vegetation. Of plants putting forth buds. Of springs, to gush with water: Ja 3:11 .†
G1032.strongs: a primary verb; to swell out, i.e. (by implication) to gush

G2021.mounce: to put hand to a thing; to undertake, attempt, Lk. 1:1; Acts 9:29; 19:13*
G2021.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5495 (χείρ); to put the hand upon, i.e. undertake

G2342.thayer: properly: a wild beast, hence: any animal; met: a brute.
G2342.mounce: a beast, wild animal, Mk. 1:13; Acts 10:12; met. a brute, brutish man, Tit. 1:12
G2342.abbottSmith: a wild beast , beast : Mk 1:13 , Ac 11:6 28:4 , 5 , Tit 1:12 , He 12:20 , Ja 3:7 , Re 6:8 ; of Antichrist, Re 11:7 , 13-20 .†
G2342.strongs: diminutive from the same as G2339 (θήρα); a dangerous animal

G1430.thayer: the roof (of a house), the top of the house.
G1430.mounce: pr. a house; synec. a roof, Mt. 10:27; 24:17
G1430.abbottSmith: chiefly in poets and late (not Attic) prose;
G1430.strongs: from (to build); properly, an edifice, i.e. (specially) a roof

G4047.mounce: a laying up, keeping; an acquiring or obtaining, acquisition, 1 Thess. 5:9; 2 Thess. 2:14; a saving, preservation, Heb. 10:39; a peculiar possession, specialty, Eph. 1:14; 1 Pet. 2:9*
G4047.strongs: from G4046 (περιποιέομαι); acquisition (the act or the thing); by extension, preservation

G5182.thayer: I agitate or disturb in mind, trouble.
G5182.abbottSmith: to disturb, trouble : pass., Lk 10:41 , Rec. (WH, R, θορυβάζω ).†
G5182.strongs: from (Latin turba, a crowd; akin to G2351 (θόρυβος)); to make "turbid", i.e. disturb

G5015.thayer: I disturb, agitate, stir up, trouble.
G5015.mounce: to agitate, trouble, as water, Jn. 5:7; met. to agitate, trouble the mind; with fear, to terrify, put in consternation, Mt. 2:3; 14:26; with grief, etc., affect with grief, anxiety, etc., Jn. 12:27; 13:21; with doubt, etc., to unsettle, perplex, Acts 15:24; Gal. 1:7
G5015.abbottSmith: to disturb, trouble, stir up : primarily in physical sense (Hom., Eur.): τ. ὕδωρ , Jo 1:5 [4], 7 . Metaph., of the mind (Æsch., Plat., al.), to trouble, disquiet, perplex : Ac 15:24 , Ga 1:7 5:10 ; ἑαυτόν ( troubled himself ; Westc., in l.), Jo 11:33 ; of a crowd, to stir up , Ac 17:8 , 13 ; pass., Mt 2:3 14:26 , Mk 6:50 , Lk 1:12 24:38 , Jo 12:27 ( Ps 5 (6):4 ) Ps 14:1 , 27 , I Pe 3:14 (LXX) ; τ.
G5015.strongs: of uncertain affinity; to stir or agitate (roil water)

G5020.thayer: I thrust down to Tartarus or Gehenna.
G5020.mounce: to cast or thrust down to Tartarus or Gehenna, 2 Pet. 2:4*
G5020.abbottSmith: to cast into hell : II Pe 2:4 (v. Mayor, in l.).†
G5020.strongs: from (the deepest abyss of Hades); to incarcerate in eternal torment

G2998.mounce: to hew stones; to cut out of stone, hew from stone, Mt. 27:60; Mk. 15:46; Lk. 23:53*
G2998.abbottSmith: to hew , hew out stones: Mt 27:60 , Mk 15:46 .†
G2998.strongs: from the same as the first part of G2991 (λαξευτός) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); to quarry

G114.thayer: I annul, make of no effect, set aside, ignore, slight; I break faith with.
G114.mounce: pr. to displace, set aside; to abrogate, annul, violate, swerve from; reject, condemn
G114.abbottSmith: to set aside, disregard (in Gramm., to reject as spurious ): διαθήκην , Ga 3:16 ; ἐντολήν , Mk 7:9 ; νόμον , He 10:28 ; πίστιν , I Ti 5:12 . to nullify, make void : Lk 7:30 (v. Field, Notes , 59), I Co 1:19 , Ga 2:21 . to reject : Mk 6:26 (Field, op. cit., 30), Lk 10:16 , Jo 12:48 , I Th 4:8 , Ju 8 (for exx. in π. , v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G114.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); to set aside, i.e. (by implication) to disesteem, neutralize or violate
G114.kjv: cast off, despise, disannul, frustrate, bring to nought, reject

G4106.thayer: a wandering; fig: deceit, delusion, error, sin.
G4106.mounce: a wandering; deceit, deception, delusion, imposture, fraud, Mt. 27:64; 1 Thess. 2:3; seduction, deceiving, Eph. 4:14; 2 Thess. 2:11; 1 Jn. 4:6; error, false opinion, 2 Pet. 3:17; wandering from the path of truth and virtue, perverseness, wickedness, sin, Rom. 1:27; Jas. 5:20; 2 Pet. 2:18; Jude 11*
G4106.abbottSmith: a wandering . Metaph., a going astray , an error (in NT always with respect to morals or religion): Mt 27:64 , Ro 1:27 , Eph 4:14 , I Th 2:3 , II Th 2:11 , Ja 5:20 , II Pe 2:18 3:17 , I Jo 4:6 , Ju 11 .†
G4106.strongs: feminine of G4108 (πλάνος) (as abstractly); objectively, fraudulence; subjectively, a straying from orthodoxy or piety

G4279.abbottSmith: to announce before . Mid., to promise before : c. acc. rei, Ro 1:2 , II Co 9:5 (Dio. Cass.).†
G4279.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and G1861 (ἐπαγγέλλω); to promise of old

G1839.thayer: (lit: I remove from a standing position), (a) in trans. tenses: I astonish, amaze, (b) in intrans. tenses: I am astonished, amazed; I am out of my mind, am mad.
