G1839.mounce: pr. to put out of its place; to astonish, amaze, Lk. 24:22; Acts 8:9, 11; intrans. to be astonished, Mt. 12:23; to be beside one’s self, Mk. 3:21; 2 Cor. 5:13
G1839.abbottSmith: causal in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to put out of its place ; metaph., ἐ. τινά φρενῶν (Eur.), to drive one out of his senses, hence , absol., to confound, amaze : c. acc pers., Lk 24:22 , Ac 8:9 , 11 .
G1839.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to put (stand) out of wits, i.e. astound, or (reflexively) become astounded, insane
G1839.kjv: amaze, be (make) astonished, be beside self (selves), bewitch, wonder

G978.thayer: Bithynia, a Roman province, north-west of Asia Minor and south-west of the Black Sea.
G978.mounce: Bithynia, a province of Asia Minor, Acts 16:7; 1 Pet. 1:1*
G978.abbottSmith: Bithynia , a province in Asia Minor: Ac 16:7 , I Pe 1:1 .†
G978.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Bithynia, a region of Asia

G4899.mounce: chosen along with others; elected to Gospel privileges along with, 1 Pet. 5:13*
G4899.abbottSmith: chosen together with, co-elect : ἡ ἐν Βαβυλῶνι συνεκλεκτή (on the meaning, v. ICC , in l.), I Pe 5:13 .†
G4899.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1586 (ἐκλέγομαι); chosen in company with, i.e. co-elect (fellow Christian)

G3537.thayer: a basin for washing hands or feet.
G3537.mounce: a basin for washing some part of the person, Jn. 13:5*

G4341.mounce: to call to one’s self, summon, Mt. 10:1; 15:10, 32; 18:2; to invite, Acts 2:39; to call to the performance of a thing, appoint, Acts 13:2; 16:10
G4341.abbottSmith: to call to . Mid., to call to oneself (v. M, Pr. , 157): c. acc. pers., Mt 10:1 , Mk 3:13 6:7 , Lk 7:19 , Ac 5:40 , Ja 5:14 , al. Metaph., of the Divine call: Ac 2:39 ; c. inf., Ac 16:10 (v. Bl., § 69, 4); seq. εἰς , Ac 13:2 .†
G4341.strongs: middle voice from G4314 (πρός) and G2564 (καλέω); to call toward oneself, i.e. summon, invite

G696.mounce: silver; meton. anything made of silver; money, Jas. 5:3
G696.abbottSmith: silver (on its relation to ἀργύριον , v. MM, s.v.): Mt 10:9 , Ac 17:29 , Ja 5:5 , Re 18:12 .†
G696.strongs: from (shining); silver (the metal, in the articles or coin)

G520.thayer: I lead, carry, take away; met: I am led astray, seduced.
G520.mounce: to lead away, Mt. 26:57; to conduct, Mt. 7:13, 14; pass. to be led off to execution, Acts 12:19; met. to be led astray, seduced, 1 Cor. 12:1
G520.abbottSmith: to lead away : Lk 13:15 , Ac 23:17 24:7 (R, mg.), I Co 12:2 ; esp. of leading to trial (so as law term in Attic), prison and death (MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 26:57 27:2 , 31 , Mk 14:44 , 53 15:16 , Lk 21:12 22:66 23:26 , Ac 12:19 ; of the direction of a way: Mt 7:13 , 14 (cf. συν-απ-άγω ).†
G520.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G71 (ἄγω); to take off (in various senses)
G520.kjv: bring, carry away, lead (away), put to death, take away

G2535.thayer: (Hebrew), Cain, son of Adam and Eve and brother of Abel.
G2535.mounce: Cain, pr. name, indecl., Heb. 11:4; 1 Jn. 3:12; Jude 11*
G2535.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07014); Cain, (i.e. Cajin), the son of Adam

G3021.mounce: to brighten, to make white, Mk. 9:3; Rev. 7:14*
G3021.abbottSmith: to whiten , make white : c. acc. rei, Mk 9:3 , Re 7:14 .†

G1535.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G5037 (τέ); if too

G3419.mounce: monument, memorial, Lk. 11:47; grave, tomb, Mt. 23:39; Mk. 5:2; Lk. 11:44; Jn. 11:17, 31, 38; Acts 13:29
G3419.strongs: from G3420 (μνήμη); a remembrance, i.e. cenotaph (place of interment)

G5491.thayer: a lip, mouth, shore, edge, brink; meton: language, dialect.
G5491.mounce: a lip, and pl. τὰ χείλη, the lips, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6; Rom. 3:13; Heb. 13:15; 1 Pet. 3:10; trop. χεῖλος τῆς θαλάσσης, the seashore, Heb. 11:12; meton. language, dialect, 1 Cor. 14:21*
G5491.abbottSmith: a lip (as in Heb., of the lip as the organ of speech): Mt 15:8 , Mk 7:6 , Ro 3:13 , I Co 14:21 , He 13:15 , I Pe 3:10 (LXX) . Metaph., of things, an edge, brink , etc. (Hom., Hdt., al.): of the sea­shore, He 11:12 .†
G5491.strongs: from a form of the same as G5490 (χάσμα); a lip (as a pouring place); figuratively, a margin (of water)

G5443.thayer: a tribe or race of people.
G5443.mounce: a tribe, Mt. 19:28; 24:30; Lk. 2:36; a people, nation, Rev. 1:7; 5:9
G5443.abbottSmith: a body of men united by kinship or habitation, a clan or tribe : of the tribes of Israel, Mt 19:28 , Lk 2:36 22:30 , Ac 13:21 , Ro 11:1 , Phl 3:5 , He 7:13 , 14 , Ja 1:1 , Re 5:5 7:4-8 21:12 ; of the tribes of the earth, the peoples and nations, Mt 24:30 , Re 1:7 5:9 7:9 11:9 13:7 14:6 .†
G5443.strongs: from G5453 (φύω) (compare G5444 (φύλλον)); an offshoot, i.e. race or clan

G4936.mounce: to run together, flock together, Mk. 6:33; Acts 3:11; to run in company with others, met. 1 Pet. 4:4*
G4936.abbottSmith: to run together or with : seq. ἐκεῖ , Mk 6:33 ; πρόσ , c. acc., Ac 3:11 ; metaph., I Pe 4:4 .†
G4936.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to rush together (hastily assemble) or headlong (figuratively)

G3462.abbottSmith: Ionic and poët. (comic), to anoint : Mk 14:8 .†
G3462.strongs: from G3464 (μύρον); to apply (perfumed) unguent to

G5146.mounce: pr. three-pronged; a thistle, thorn, Mt. 7:16; Heb. 6:8*
G5146.strongs: from G5140 (τρεῖς) and G956 (βέλος); properly, a crow-foot (three-pronged obstruction in war), i.e. (by analogy) a thorny plant (caltrop)

G2849.thayer: I chastise, curtail, punish; mid: I cause to be punished.
G2849.mounce: pr. to curtail, to coerce; to chastise, punish, Acts 4:21; 2 Pet. 2:9*
G2849.strongs: from (dwarf); properly, to curtail, i.e. (figuratively) to chastise (or reserve for infliction)

G1505.mounce: clearness, purity; met. sincerity, integrity, ingenuousness, 1 Cor. 5:8; 2 Cor. 1:12; 2:17*
G1505.abbottSmith: sincerity, purity : I Co 5:8 , II Co 1:12 2:17 .†
G1505.strongs: from G1506 (εἰλικρινής); clearness, i.e. (by implication) purity (figuratively)

G3468.thayer: a bruise, stripe, left on the body by scourging.
G3468.mounce: the mark of a blow; a stripe, a wound, 1 Pet. 2:24*
G3468.abbottSmith: a bruise , wound from a stripe : I Pe 2:24 (LXX) (Arist., Plut., al.).†
G3468.strongs: from ("moil"; probably akin to the base of G3433 (μόλις)) and probably (the face; from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι)); a mole ("black eye") or blow-mark

G5414.abbottSmith: a load : esp. of a ship's cargo (Hom., Hdt., and late prose writers), Ac 27:10 , Rec.†
G5414.strongs: from G5342 (φέρω); something carried, i.e. the cargo of a ship

G1570.thayer: cast out, exposed (to the elements), abandoned.
G1570.abbottSmith: cast out : ποιεῖν ἔ. = ἐκτιθέναι , Ac 7:19 .†
G1570.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); put out, i.e. exposed to perish

G4971.strongs: adverb from the same as G4970 (σφόδρα); very much

G2539.abbottSmith: although : c. ptcp., Phl 3:4 , He 5:8 7:5 12:17 , II Pe 1:12 .†
G2539.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G4007 (περ); and indeed, i.e. nevertheless or notwithstanding

G2035.strongs: from G2034 (ἑπτάκις) and G5507 (χίλιοι); seven times a thousand

G675.thayer: the township Appi Forum on the Appian Way, 43 Roman miles from Rome, was named.
G675.mounce: the forum or marketplace, of Appius; a village on the Appian road, near Rome, Acts 28:15*
G675.abbottSmith: Appius ; Ἀππίου Φόρον , Appii Forum (Market of Appius), a town in Italy: Ac 28:15 .†
G675.strongs: of Latin origin; (in the genitive, i.e. possessive case) of Appius, the name of a Roman

G2855.thayer: a money-changer, who changed heathen into Jewish money, for payment into the Temple treasury.
G2855.abbottSmith: a money-changer : Mt 21:12 , Mk 11:15 , Jo 2:15 (cf. κερματιοτής ).†
G2855.strongs: from a presumed derivative of (a small coin; probably akin to G2854 (κολλούριον)); a coin-dealer

G4901.thayer: I unite in bearing witness, sanction.
G4901.mounce: can also be spelled συνεπιτίθεμαι, mid., to set upon along with, join with others in an attack; to unite in impeaching, Acts 24:9*
G4901.abbottSmith: to join in attesting, bear witness together with : He 2:4 (Arist., Polyb., al.).†
G4901.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1957 (ἐπιμαρτυρέω); to testify further jointly, i.e. unite in adding evidence

G1087.thayer: the assembly or body of elders.
G1087.mounce: a senate, assembly of elders; the elders of Israel collectively, Acts 5:21*
G1087.abbottSmith: a council of elders , senate ; in NT, of the Sanhedrin ( Cl. Rev. , i, 43 f.; DB , ext. , 99): Ac 5:21 .†
G1087.strongs: from G1088 (γέρων); the eldership, i.e. (collectively) the Jewish Sanhedrin

G4271.thayer: manifest to all, evident, very clear.
G4271.mounce: previously manifest, before known; plainly manifest, very clear, prominently conspicuous, 1 Tim. 5:24, 25; Heb. 7:14*
G4271.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1212 (δῆλος); plain before all men, i.e. obvious

G2060.thayer: (a) Hermes, the messenger and herald of the Greek gods, or rather the corresponding Lycaonian deity, (b) Hermes, a Roman Christian.
G2060.mounce: Hermes or Mercury, son of Jupiter and Maia, the messenger and interpreter of the gods, and the patron of eloquence, learning, etc., Acts 14:12; Rom. 16:14*
G2060.strongs: perhaps from G2046 (ἐρέω); Hermes, the name of the messenger of the Greek deities; also of a Christian

G547.mounce: threat, commination, Acts 4:29; 9:1; harshness of language, Eph. 6:9*

G2600.mounce: the act of descending; a way down, descent, Lk. 19:37*

G1304.thayer: I tarry, continue, stay in a place.
G1304.mounce: pr. to rub, wear away by friction; met. to pass or spend time, to remain, stay, tarry, continue, Jn. 3:22; 11:54; Acts 12:19; 14:3, 28
G1304.abbottSmith: to rub hard, rub away, consume ; δ. χρόνον, ἡμέρας , to spend time : Ac 14:3 , 28 16:12 20:6 25:6 , 14 . Intransitively with ellipse of object (Bl., § 81, 1), to spend time, stay : Jo 3:22 11:54 15:35 , Ac 12:19 .†
G1304.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to wear through (time), i.e. remain

G4336.thayer: I pray, pray for, offer prayer.
G4336.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2172 (εὔχομαι); to pray to God, i.e. supplicate, worship

G4766.mounce: the thematic form of στρώννυμι, the μι form never being visible in the NT, to spread, to strew, Mt. 21:8; Mk. 11:8; to spread a couch, make your own bed, Acts 9:34; used of a supper-chamber, pass. to have the couches spread, to be prepared, furnished, Mk. 14:15; Lk. 22:12*
G4766.abbottSmith: to spread : ἱμάτια ἐν τ. ὁδῷ ( εἰς τ. ὁδόν ), Mt 21:8 , Mk 11:8 ; of making a bed, στρῶσον (sc. τ. κλίνην ) σεαυτῷ , Ac 9:34 ; of furnishing a room, ἐστρωμένον , spread with carpets or carpeted couches (cf. Ez 23:41 ), Mk 14:15 , Lk 22:12 .†
G4766.strongs: prolongation from a still simpler , (used only as an alternate in certain tenses) (probably akin to G4731 (στερεός) through the idea of positing); to "strew," i.e. spread (as a carpet or couch)

G3386.abbottSmith: let alone : i.e. according to context; much less ; much more : I Co 6:3 .†
G3386.strongs: from G3385 (μήτι) and G1065 (γέ); not at all then, i.e. not to say (the rather still)

G1838.thayer: condition, state, habit, use, especially: good condition of body or soul.
G1838.mounce: a condition of body or mind, strictly, as resulting from practice; habit, Heb. 5:14*
G1838.strongs: from G2192 (ἔχω); habit, i.e. (by implication) practice

G4334.thayer: I come up to, come to, come near (to), approach, consent (to).
G4334.mounce: to come or go to any one, approach, Mt. 4:3, 11; 5:1; 8:19, 25, et al. freq.; trop. to come or go to, approach, draw near, spiritually, Heb. 7:25; 11:6; 4:16; 1 Pet. 2:4; met. to assent to, accede to, concur in, 1 Tim. 6:3
G4334.abbottSmith: to approach , draw near : absol., Mt 4:11 , Lk 9:42 , al.; c. infin., Mt 24:1 , al.; c. dat. loc., He 12:18 , 22 ; dat. pers., Mt 5:1 , and freq., Jo 12:21 , al.; ptcp., προσελθών , c. indic., Mt 8:2 , and freq., Mk 1:31 , Lk 7:14 , al.; π. αὐτῷ , c. indic., Mt 4:3 , Mk 6:35 . Metaph., of approaching God: absol. ( Le 21:17 , De 21:5 , al.), He 10:1 , 22 ; τ. θεῷ , He 7:25 11:6 ; τ. θρόνῳ τ. χάριτο
G4334.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι) (including its alternate); to approach, i.e. (literally) come near, visit, or (figuratively) worship, assent to
G4334.kjv: (as soon as he) come (unto), come thereunto, consent, draw near, go (near, to, unto)

G2862.thayer: a colony, a city settlement of Roman (soldier) citizens; a garrison city.
G2862.abbottSmith: a colony : Ac 16:12 (v. Lft., Phi. , 50 f.).†
G2862.strongs: of Latin origin; a Roman "colony" for veterans

G1897.abbottSmith: conj., since indeed : Ro 3:30 (Rec.; εἴπερ , WH).†
G1897.strongs: from G1893 (ἐπεί) and G4007 (περ); since indeed (of cause)

G929.mounce: pr. examination by torture; torment, torture, Rev. 9:5; 14:11; 18:7, 10, 15*

G60.thayer: a lounger in the market-place, perhaps with the idea of agitator.
G60.mounce: one who visits the forum; a lounger, one who idles away his time in public places, a low fellow, Acts 17:5; pertaining to the forum, judicial; ἀγόραιοι, court days, Acts 19:38*
G60.abbottSmith: frequenting the ἀγορά , a lounger in the ἀγορά (Xen., al.): Ac 17:5 . In late writers (Strab., al.), proper to the ἀγορά : ἀγοραῖοι (sc. ἥμεραι ) ἀγονται (cf. Lat. conventus agere ), court-days are kept , Ac 19:38 (for exx. of both usages, v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G60.strongs: from G58 (ἀγορά); relating to the market-place, i.e. forensic (times); by implication, vulgar

G2354.thayer: intrans: I lament, wail; trans: I bewail.
G2354.mounce: to lament, bewail, Mt. 11:17; Lk. 7:32; 23:27; Jn. 16:20*
G2354.abbottSmith: intrans., to lament , wail : Mt 11:17 , Lk 7:32 , Jo 16:20 .

G3384.mounce: a mother, Mt. 1:18; 12:49, 50, et al. freq.; a parent city, Gal. 4:26; Rev. 17:5
G3384.abbottSmith: mother : Mt 1:18 2:11 , al.; fig., of one who takes the place of a mother, ἰδοὺ, ἡ μ. μου , Mt 12:49 (cf. Mt 12:50 , Mk 3:35 , Jo 19:27 , Ro 16:13 , I Ti 5:2 ); of a city, ἥτις ἐσὶν μ. ἡμῶν , Ga 4:26 ; symbolically of Babylon, ἡ μ. τ. πορνῶν , Re 17:5 .
G3384.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "mother" (literally or figuratively, immediate or remote)

G3587a.abbottSmith: to shave : pass. and mid., to shave oneself , have oneself shaved , aor., Ac 21:24 ; pf. (Att.), I Co 11:5 .†

G1474.thayer: I dash to the ground, level with the ground.
G1474.mounce: pr. to form a level and firm surface; to level with the ground, overthrow, raze, destroy, Lk. 19:44*
G1474.abbottSmith: to beat level like a threshing floor (Theophr.). to dash to the ground (Field, Notes , 74): Lk 19:44 (cf. Ps 136 (137):9 , Ho 14:1 ).†

G2698.thayer: (a) I lay down, deposit, (b) mid: I lay down or deposit a favor, with the view of receiving one in return, seek favor.
G2698.mounce: mid. to ay up for one’s self; χάριν, or χάριτας, to lay up a store of favor for one’s self, earn a title to favor at the hands of a person, to curry favor with, Acts 24:27; 25:9*
G2698.abbottSmith: to lay down , deposit , lay by : Mk 15:46 , T; mid., to lay up for oneself : χάριν (Hdt., al., v. LS, s.v.), c. dat. pers., Ac 24:27 25:9 .†
G2698.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place down, i.e. deposit (literally or figuratively)

G2827.thayer: trans: I rest, recline; I bend, incline; I cause to give ground, make to yield; intrans: I decline, approach my end.
G2827.mounce: pr. trans. to cause to slope or bend; to bow down, Lk. 24:5; Jn. 19:30; to lay down to rest, Mt. 8:20; Lk. 9:58; to put to flight troops, Heb. 11:34; intrans. of the day, to decline, Lk. 9:12; 24:29*
G2827.abbottSmith: to make to bend , to bow : τ. κεφαλήν (of one dying), Jo 19:30 ; τ. πρόσωτον (of terrified persons), Lk 24:5 .
G2827.strongs: a primary verb; to slant or slope, i.e. incline or recline (literally or figuratively)
G2827.kjv: bow (down), be far spent, lay, turn to flight, wear away

G856.abbottSmith: a privy , drain : Mt 15:17 , Mk 7:19 ( ὀχετόν , WH, mg.).†
G856.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a place of sitting apart, i.e. a privy

G4206.thayer: far, far off, at a distance.
G4206.mounce: in advance, far advanced; far, far off, at a distance, Mt. 15:8; Mk. 7:6; Lk. 14:32, can be an improper prep. with the gen. The comparative form of the adverb appears as πορρώτερον at Lk. 24:28.*
G4206.abbottSmith: far off : Mt 15:8 , Mk 7:6 (LXX) , Lk 14:32 ; comparat., πορρώτερον ( -ρω , T), Lk 24:28 .†
G4206.strongs: adverb from G4253 (πρό); forwards, i.e. at a distance

G5369.mounce: pleasure-loving; a lover of pleasure, 2 Tim. 3:4*
G5369.abbottSmith: loving pleasure : II Ti 3:4 (Polyb., Plut. al.).†
G5369.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G2237 (ἡδονή); fond of pleasure, i.e. voluptuous

G2902.thayer: I am strong, mighty, hence: I rule, am master, prevail; I obtain, take hold of; I hold, hold fast.
G2902.mounce: pr. to be strong; to be superior to any one, subdue, vanquish, Acts 2:24; to get into one’s power, lay hold of, seize, apprehend, Mt. 14:3; 18:28; 21:46; to gain, compass, attain, Acts 27:13; in NT to lay hold of, grasp, clasp, Mt. 9:25; Mk. 1:31; 5:41; to retain, keep under reserve, Mk. 9:10; met, to hold fast, observe, Mk. 7:3, 8; 2 Thess. 2:15; to hold to, adhere to, Acts 3:11; Col. 2:19; to restrain, hinder, repress, Lk. 24:16; Rev. 7:1; to retain, not to remit, sins, Jn. 20:23
G2902.abbottSmith: to be strong , mighty , hence, to rule , be master , prevail (so chiefly in cl.; in LXX: Es 1:1 , I Es 4:38 , Wi 14:19 , al.).
G2902.strongs: from G2904 (κράτος); to use strength, i.e. seize or retain (literally or figuratively)
G2902.kjv: hold (by, fast), keep, lay hand (hold) on, obtain, retain, take (by)

G3779.mounce: thus, in this way, Mt. 1:18; 2:5; 5:16; et al. freq.; ὁς μὲν οὕτως, ὁς δὲ οὕτως, one so, and another so, one in one way, and another in another, 1 Cor. 7:7; so, Mt. 7:12; 12:40; 24:27, 37, et al. freq.; thus, under such circumstances, Acts 20:11; in such a condition, viz., one previously mentioned, Acts 27:17; 1 Cor. 7:26, 40; and, perhaps, Jn. 4:6; in an ordinary way, at ease, like Latin sic, perhaps, Jn. 4:6
G3779.abbottSmith: in this way , so , thus ; referring to what precedes: Mt 5:16 6:30 , Mk 10:43 14:59 , Lk 1:25 2:48 15:7 , Jo 3:8 , Ro 1:15 , I Co 8:12 , al.; οὕτως καί , Mt 17:12 , Mk 13:29 , al.; pleonastically, resuming a ptcp. (cl.; v. Bl., § 74, 6), Ac 20:11 27:17 . Referring to what follows: Mt 1:18 6:9 , Lk 19:31 , Jo 21:1 , I Pe 2:15 ; bef. quotations from OT, Mt 2:5 , Ac 7:6 , I Co 15:45 , He 4:4 . C. adj
G3779.strongs: adverb from G3778 (οὗτος); in this way (referring to what precedes or follows)
G3779.kjv: after that, after (in) this manner, as, even (so), for all that, like(-wise), no more, on this fashion(-wise), so (in like manner), thus, what

G4502.thayer: Reuben, eldest son of the patriarch Jacob and founder of a tribe.
G4502.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07205); Ruben (i.e. Reuben), an Israelite

G4406.strongs: from G4404 (πρωΐ); dawning, i.e. (by analogy) autumnal (showering, the first of the rainy season)

G3791.mounce: pr. to mob; to disturb, trouble, Acts 5:16*
G3791.abbottSmith: to move , disturb ; hence, generally, to trouble , vex : pass., Ac 5:16 (act. absol., = pass., to be in a tumult , III Mac 5.41 ; cf. ἐν- , παρ-εν-οχλέω , and v. MM, xviii).†
G3791.strongs: from G3793 (ὄχλος); to mob, i.e. (by implication) to harass

G1482.thayer: pagan, heathen, gentile; subst: a Gentile, non-Jew.
G1482.mounce: national; in NT Gentile, heathen, not Israelites, Mt. 5:47; 6:7; 18:17; 3 Jn. 7*
G1482.abbottSmith: national (Polyb.). foreign (gramm.); in NT, as subst., ὁ ἐ. , the Gentile (the adj. "describes character rather than mere position"; cf. ἔθνος , and v. Cremer, 228): Mt 5:47 6:7 18:17 , III Jo 7 .†
G1482.strongs: from G1484 (ἔθνος); national ("ethnic"), i.e. (specially) a Gentile

G1502.mounce: (1) to yield, give place, submit, Gal. 2:5. (2) the perfect form ἔοικα (G2036) is from this same root and functions as a present, Jm 1:6, 23 (ἔοικεν, 3 sg). Some list it as a separate word, but see the discussion in Liddell and Scott*
G1502.strongs: apparently a primary verb; properly, to be weak, i.e. yield

G3626.abbottSmith: watching or keeping the house ; as subst., ἡ οἰ. , a housekeeper (Soph., Eur.; v. LS, s.v.).
G3626.strongs: from G3624 (οἶκος) and (a guard; be "ware"); a stayer at home, i.e. domestically inclined (a "good housekeeper")

G1268.thayer: I divide into portions, distribute; pass: I am spread about.
G1268.mounce: to distribute; to divulge, spread abroad, Acts 4:17*
G1268.abbottSmith: to distribute, divide : pass., to be spread about , Ac 4:17 .†
G1268.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G3551 (νόμος); to distribute, i.e. (of information) to disseminate

G3072.mounce: in the dialect of Lycaonia, Acts 14:11*
G3072.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G3071 (Λυκαονία); Lycaonistically, i.e. in the language of the Lycaonians

G3507.abbottSmith: a cloud (single and specific as opp. to νέφος , a great indefinite mass of vapour): Mt 17:5 24:30 26:64 , Mk 9:7 13:26 14:62 , Lk 9:34 , 35 12:54 21:37 , Ac 1:9 , I Th 4:17 , Ju 12 , Re 1:7 10:1 11:12 14:14-16 ; of the pillar of cloud in the wilderness ( Ex 14:19 , 20 , Ps 104:39 , al.): I Co 10:1 , 2 .†
G3507.strongs: from G3509 (νέφος); properly, cloudiness, i.e. (concretely) a cloud

G1129.mounce: pr. gymnastic exercise; hence, bodily discipline of any kind, 1 Tim. 4:8*

G3490.thayer: a captain (master) of a ship, ship-owner.
G3490.mounce: the master or owner of a ship, Acts 27:11*
G3490.strongs: from G3491 (ναῦς) and G2819 (κλῆρος) ("clerk"); a captain

G2055.strongs: from G2056 (ἔριφος); a kidling, i.e. (genitive case) goat (symbolically, wicked person)

G2659.mounce: in NT deep sleep, stupor, dullness, Rom. 11:8*
G2659.strongs: from G2660 (κατανύσσω); a prickling (sensation, as of the limbs asleep), i.e. (by implication, (perhaps by some confusion with G3506 (νεύω) or even with G3571 (νύξ))) stupor (lethargy)

G4639.thayer: a shadow, shade, thick darkness, an outline.
G4639.mounce: a shade, shadow, Mk. 4:32; Acts 5:15; met. a shadow, a foreshadowing, a vague outline, in distinction from ἡ εἰκών, the perfect image or delineation, and τὸ σῶμα, the reality, Col. 2:17; Heb. 8:5; 10:1; gloom; σκιὰ θανάτου, death shade, the thickest darkness, Mt. 4:16; Lk. 1:79*
G4639.abbottSmith: shadow, shade , caused by interception of light: Mk 4:32 , Ac 5:15 ; metaph., σ. θανάτου , of ignorance and error, Mt 4:16 , Lk 1:79 (LXX) .
G4639.strongs: apparently a primary word; "shade" or a shadow (literally or figuratively (darkness of error or an adumbration))

G5453.thayer: I grow, grow up, spring up.
G5453.mounce: to generate, produce; pass. to be generated, produced; of plants, to germinate, sprout, Lk. 8:6, 8; intrans. to germinate, spring or grow up, Heb. 12:15*
G5453.abbottSmith: trans., to bring forth, produce (of men, to beget ); pass., to spring up, grow : Lk 8:6 , 8 .
G5453.strongs: a primary verb; probably originally, to "puff" or blow, i.e. to swell up; but only used in the implied sense, to germinate or grow (sprout, produce), literally or figuratively

G917.mounce: heavily; met. with difficulty, dully, stupidly, Mt. 13:15; Acts 28:27*

G1496.thayer: a server (worshipper) of an image (an idol).
G1496.mounce: an idolater, worshipper of idols, 1 Cor. 5:10, 11; 6:9; 10:7; Eph. 5:5; Rev. 21:8; 22:15*
G1496.strongs: from G1497 (εἴδωλον) and the base of G3000 (λατρεύω); an image- (servant or) worshipper (literally or figuratively)

G3535.thayer: Nineveh, a city on the Tigris in Assyria.

G4673.mounce: a coffer; an urn for receiving the ashes of the dead; a coffin; in NT a bier, Lk. 7:14*

Mayor, in l.); τ. καρδιῶν , Ro 1:24 ; τ. κόσμου (ar

G5420.mounce: to fence in; by impl. to obstruct, stop, close up, Heb. 11:33; met. to silence, put to silence, Rom. 3:19; 2 Cor. 11:10*

G2261.strongs: probably from G2031 (ἔπος); properly, affable, i.e. mild or kind

G1446.thayer: the Hebrew language, or rather: Aramaic.

G2281.thayer: (a) the sea, in contrast to the land, (b) a particular sea or lake, e.g. the sea of Galilee (Tiberias), the Red Sea.
G2281.mounce: the sea, Mt. 23:15, Mk. 9:42; a sea, Acts 7:36; an inland sea, lake, Mt. 8:24
G2281.abbottSmith: the sea : Mt 23:15 , Mk 11:23 , Lk 21:25 , Ro 9:27 , He 11:12 , Ja 1:6 , Ju 13 , Re 7:1 , al.; τὸ πέλαγος τῆς θ. (v. Tr., ut infr.), Mt 18:6 ; ὁ οὐρανὸς κ. ἡ γῆ κ. ἡ θ. , of the whole world, Ac 4:24 , al. ( Hg 2:7 ); θ. ὑαλίνη , Re 4:6 15:2 ; of the Mediterranean, Ac 10:6 , 32 17:14 ; of the Red Sea, ἐρυθρὰ θ. , Ac 7:36 , I Co 10:1 , 2 , He 11:29 ; of an inland lake (as Heb. יָם ), θ. τ. Γαλιλαίας

G1025.mounce: a child; whether unborn, an embryo, fetus, Lk. 1:41, 44; or just born, an infant, Lk. 2:12, 16; Acts 7:19; or partly grown, Lk. 18:15; 2 Tim. 3:15; met. a babe in simplicity of faith, 1 Pet. 2:2*
G1025.abbottSmith: an unborn child : Lk 1:41 , 44 . a newborn child, a babe : Lk 2:12 , 16 18:15 , Ac 7:19 , I Pe 2:2 ; ἀπὸ βρέφους , from infancy , II Ti 3:15 .†

G3869.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2523 (καθίζω); to sit down near

G3335.thayer: (a) with gen: I take a share (part) of, share in, partake of, (b) with acc: I take after (later) or take instead.

G3335.abbottSmith: to have or get a share of , partake of: c. gen. rei, II Ti 2:6 , He 6:7 12:10 ; τροφῆς , Ac 2:46 27:33 , 34 ; c. acc. rei (of the whole), to get : καιρόν , Ac 24:25 (v. Bl., § 36, 1; MM, xvi).†

G5603.mounce: an ode, song, hymn, Eph. 5:19; Col. 3:16; Rev. 5:9; 14:3; 15:3*
G5603.abbottSmith: a song, ode , whether sad or joyful; in LXX and NT always in praise of God or Christ: Re 5:9 14:3 15:3 ; ᾠ πνευματικαί Eph 5:19 , Col 3:16 .†
G5603.strongs: from G103 (ᾄδω); a chant or "ode" (the general term for any words sung; while G5215 (ὕμνος) denotes especially a religious metrical composition, and G5568 (ψαλμός) still more specially, a Hebrew cantillation)

G2272.abbottSmith: quiet , tranquil : I Ti 2:2 , I Pe 3:4 .†

G5381.abbottSmith: love of strangers, hospitality : Ro 12:13 , He 13:2 .†

G3704.thayer: how, in order that, so that, that.

G62.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1121 (γράμμα); unlettered, i.e. illiterate

G3753.mounce: when, at the time that, at what time, Mt. 7:28; 9:25; Lk. 13:35, et al. freq.

(Hom.; of a def. fut. as opp. to the indef. fut. of ὅτα
G3753.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G5037 (τέ); at which (thing) too, i.e. when
G3753.kjv: after (that), as soon as, that, when, while

G1134.thayer: belonging to woman, of woman, female.

G1126.strongs: from (an old woman) and G1491 (εἶδος); crone-like, i.e. silly

G230.mounce: truly, really, Mt. 14:33; certainly, of a truth, Jn. 17:8; Acts 12:11: truly, actually, Jn. 4:18

G4006.strongs: from the perfect of the alternate of G3958 (πάσχω); reliance

G5532.mounce: use; need, necessity, requisiteness, Eph. 4:29; Heb. 7:11; personal need, an individual want, Acts 20:34; Rom. 12:13; Phil. 2:25; 4:16, 19; χρείαν ἔχω, to need, require, want, Mt. 6:8; 14:16; Mk. 2:25; Jn. 2:25; ἐστὶ χρεία, there is need, Lk. 10:42; τὰ πρὸς τὴν χρείαν, necessary things, Acts 28:10; a necessary business, affair, Acts 6:3
G5532.abbottSmith: need, necessity: Ac 28:10 , Tit 3:14 ; πρὸς οἰκοδομὴν τῆς χ. (RV, txt., for edifying as the need may be ; but v. Field, Notes , 192), Eph 4:29 ; ἕστι χ. , Lk 10:42 , He 7:11 ; χ. ἔχειν , c. gen., Mt 6:8 21:3 , Mk 11:3 , Lk 9:11 15:7 19:31 , 34 22:71 , Jo 13:29 , I Co 12:21 , 24 , I Th 4:12 , He 10:36 , Re 21:23 22:5 ; seq. τοῦ , c. inf. (Bl., § 71, 3), He 5:12 ; absol. (sc. gen.), Ac 2:45 4:35 ; c
G5532.strongs: from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) or G5534 (χρή); employment, i.e. an affair; also (by implication) occasion, demand, requirement or destitution

G5327.abbottSmith: a chasm, ravine : Lk 3:5 (LXX) (v. DB , iv, 845 f.).†

G3102.mounce: also spelled Ματθάτ, Mathat, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:24, 29*
G3102.strongs: feminine from G3101 (μαθητής); a female pupil

G825.thayer: Attalia, the port of Perga in Pamphylia.

G825.abbottSmith: Attalia , a city of Pamphylia: Ac 14:25 .†
G825.strongs: from (a king of Pergamus); Attaleia, a place in Pamphylia

G5429.mounce: considerate, thoughtful, prudent, discreet, Mt. 7:24; 10:16; 24:45; 25:2, 4, 8, 9; Lk. 12:42; sensible, wise, Rom. 11:25; 12:16; 1 Cor. 4:10; 10:15; 2 Cor. 11:19*
G5429.abbottSmith: practically wise, sensible, prudent : Mt 10:16 24:45 , Lk 12:42 , I Co 10:15 ; opp. to μωρός , Mt 7:24 25:2 , 4 , 8 , 9 , I Co 4:10 ; to ἄφρων , II Co 11:19 ; φ. παρ’ ἑαυτῷ (EV, wise in one's own conceit ), Ro 11:25 12:16 (cf. Pr 3:7 ); compar., -ώτερος , Lk 16:8 .†

G5113.mounce: one who is bold; in a bad sense, a presumptuous, audacious person, 2 Pet. 2:10*
G5113.abbottSmith: a bold, daring man : II Pe 2:10 (Thuc.).†

G1823.thayer: I flash forth like lightning, gleam, am radiant.

G1823.abbottSmith: to flash like lightning, gleam, be radiant : ἱματισμός , Lk 9:29 .†
G1823.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G797 (ἀστράπτω); to lighten forth, i.e. (figuratively) to be radiant (of very white garments)

G2362.thayer: a (king's) throne, seat; meton: power, dominion; a potentate.
G2362.mounce: a seat, a throne, Mt. 5:34; 19:28; Lk. 1:52; meton. power, dominion, Lk. 1:32; Heb. 1:8; a potentate, Col. 1:16
G2362.abbottSmith: in Hom., a seat , chair . Later, a throne , chair of state, seat of authority; of kings: metaph., of God, Mt 5:34 , Ac 7:49 (LXX) , Re 1:4 , al.; by meton., for kingly power , sovereignty , Lk 1:32 , 52 , Ac 2:30 ; for an angelic hierarchy, Col 1:16 ; of Christ, Mt 19:28 , Re 3:21 , al.; of Satan, Re 2:13 ; τ. θηρίου , Re 16:10 ; of the Apostles, Mt 19:28 , Lk 22:30 , cf. Re 20:4 ; of πρεσβύτεροι
G2362.strongs: from (to sit); a stately seat ("throne"); by implication, power or (concretely) a potentate

G105.mounce: an eagle, Rev. 12:14; or vulture, Lk. 17:37
G105.abbottSmith: an eagle : Re 4:7 8:13 (Rec. ἀγγέλου ) 12:14 . Where carrion is referred to, ἀ. is probably a vulture (cf. Jb 39:30 , Pr 30:17 ): Mt 24:28 , Lk 17:37 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G105.strongs: from the same as G109 (ἀήρ); an eagle (from its wind-like flight)

G2405.thayer: the duty (office) of a priest, priesthood.
G2405.abbottSmith: priesthood , office of priest : Lk 1:9 , He 7:5 .†
G2405.strongs: from G2407 (ἱερατεύω); priestliness, i.e. the sacerdotal function
G2405.kjv: office of the priesthood, priest's office

G1557.thayer: (a) a defense, avenging, vindication, vengeance, (b) full (complete) punishment.
G1557.mounce: vengeance, punishment, retributive justice, Lk. 21:22; Rom. 12:19; 2 Cor. 7:11; 1 Pet. 2:14; ἐκδίκησιν ποιεῖν, to vindicate, avenge, Lk. 18:7, 8; διδόναι ἐκδίκησιν, to inflict vengeance, Acts 7:24; 2 Thess. 1:8; Heb. 10:30*
G1557.abbottSmith: vengeance, vindication : Lk 21:22 , Ro 12:19 (v. MM, Exp. , xii), He 10:30 (LXX) , II Co 7:11 ; of the injured person, c. gen., Lk 18:7 , 8 ; c. dat., Ac 7:24 ; of the offender, c. gen. obj., I Pe 2:14 ; c. dat., II Th 1:8 (cf. Si 12:6 ).†

G4759.thayer: commander of the Roman emperor's body-guard.
G4759.strongs: from G4760 (στρατόπεδον) and G757 (ἄρχω); a ruler of an army, i.e. (specially), a Prætorian præfect

G3391.mounce: numeral one, Mt. 10:29, freq.; only, Mk. 12:6; one virtually by union, Mt. 19:5, 6; Jn. 10:30; one and the same, Lk. 12:52; Rom. 3:30; one in respect of office and standing, 1 Cor. 3:8; equivalent to τις, a certain one, Mt. 8:19; 16:14; a, an, Mt. 21:19; Jas. 4:13; εἱς ἕκαστος, each one, every one, Lk. 4:40; Acts 2:3; εἱς τὸν ἕνα, one another, 1 Thess. 5:11; εἱς καὶ εἱς, the one- and the other, Mt. 20:21; εἱς καθ’ εἱς and ὁδὲ καθ’ εἱς, one by one, one after another, in succession, Mk. 14:19; Jn. 8:9; as an ordinal, first, Mt. 28:1
G3391.strongs: irregular feminine of G1520 (εἷς); one or first
G3391.kjv: a (certain), + agree, first, one, X other

G2809.strongs: from G2813 (κλέπτω); stealing (properly, the thing stolen, but used of the act)

G5545.mounce: pr. anything which is applied by smearing; ointment; in NT an anointing, in the reception of spiritual privileges, 1 Jn. 2:20, 27*
G5545.strongs: from G5548 (χρίω); an unguent or smearing, i.e. (figuratively) the special endowment ("chrism") of the Holy Spirit

G1657.thayer: freedom, liberty, especially: a state of freedom from slavery.
G1657.abbottSmith: liberty : with reference to the religious life, I Co 10:29 , II Co 3:17 , Ga 2:4 5:1 , I Pe 2:16 , II Pe 2:19 ; ὁ νόμος τῆς ἐ. , Ja 1:25 2:12 ; ἡ ἐ. τῆς δόξης , Ro 8:21 ; ἐπ’ ἐ. , Ga 5:13 (on which formula, cf. Deiss., LAE , 327 ff.; Cremer, 251).†
G1657.strongs: from G1658 (ἐλεύθερος); freedom (legitimate or licentious, chiefly moral or ceremonial)

G4086.abbottSmith: the use of probable arguments , as opp. to demonstration ( ἀπόδειξις ); hence, persuasive speech : Col 2:4 (v. ICC , in l.).†
G4086.strongs: from a compound of a derivative of G3982 (πείθω) and G3056 (λόγος); persuasive language

G4825.mounce: a counsellor; advisor, one who shares one’s counsel, Rom. 11:34*
G4825.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1012 (βουλή); a consultor, i.e. adviser

G1128.thayer: I train by physical exercise; hence: train, in the widest sense.
G1128.mounce: pr. to train in gymnastic discipline; hence, to exercise in anything, train to use, discipline, 1 Tim. 4:7; Heb. 5:14; 12:11; 2 Pet. 2:14*
G1128.abbottSmith: properly, to exercise naked . Generally, to exercise, train the body or mind: I Ti 4:7 , He 5:14 12:11 , II Pe 2:14 .†
G1128.strongs: from G1131 (γυμνός); to practise naked (in the games), i.e. train (figuratively)

G5236.thayer: excess, surpassing excellence, preeminence; adv: exceedingly.
G5236.mounce: pr. a throwing beyond, an overshooting; extraordinary amount or character, transcendency, 2 Cor. 12:7; 4:7; καθ’ ὑπερβολήν, adverbially, exceedingly, extremely, Rom. 7:13; 2 Cor. 1:8; Gal. 1:13; a far better way, 1 Cor. 12:31; beyond all measure, 2 Cor. 4:17*
G5236.abbottSmith: a throwing beyond . Metaph., excess, superiority, excellence : II Co 4:7 12:7 ; καθ’ ὑπερβολήν , beyond measure, exceedingly , Ro 7:13 , I Co 12:31 , II Co 1:8 , Ga 1:13 ; κ. ὑ. εἰς ὑ. , beyond all measure , II Co 4:17 .†
G5236.strongs: from G5235 (ὑπερβάλλω); a throwing beyond others, i.e. (figuratively) supereminence; adverbially (with G1519 (εἰς) or G2596 (κατά)) pre- eminently
G5236.kjv: abundance, (far more) exceeding, excellency, more excellent, beyond (out of) measure

G222.thayer: Alexandrian, belonging to Alexandria in Egypt.
G222.strongs: from the same as G221 (Ἀλεξανδρεύς); Alexandrine, or belonging to Alexandria

G4775.mounce: to sit in company with, Mk. 14:54; Acts 26:30*
G4775.abbottSmith: to sit together or with : c. dat., Ac 26:30 ; seq. μετά , c. gen., Mk 14:54 .†
G4775.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2521 (κάθημαι); to seat oneself in company with

G2169.thayer: thankfulness, gratitude; giving of thanks, thanksgiving.
G2169.mounce: gratitude, thankfulness, Acts 24:3; thanks, the act of giving thanks, thanksgiving, 1 Cor. 14:16; conversation marked by the gentle cheerfulness of a grateful heart, as contrasted with the unseemly mirth of εὐτραπελία, Eph. 5:4
G2169.abbottSmith: thankfulness, gratitude (Polyb.; Es, Si, II Mac, ll. c.): Ac 24:3 .
G2169.strongs: from G2170 (εὐχάριστος); gratitude; actively, grateful language (to God, as an act of worship)

G287.thayer: a change, an exchange; hence plur: reciprocal good deeds (services), a fitting requital.
G287.abbottSmith: requital , recompense : I Ti 5:4 (for illustration from π. v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G1957.thayer: I testify earnestly, bear witness to.
G1957.mounce: to bear testimony to; to testify solemnly, 1 Pet. 5:12*
G1957.abbottSmith: to bear witness to : c. acc et. inf., I Pe 5:12 ,†
G1957.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3140 (μαρτυρέω); to attest further, i.e. corroborate

G3948.mounce: an inciting, incitement, Heb. 10:24; a sharp fit of anger, sharp contention, angry dispute, Acts 15:39*
G3948.strongs: from G3947 (παροξύνω) ("paroxysm"); incitement (to good), or dispute (in anger)

G2267.thayer: Herodion, a Christian in Rome, a relative of Paul.

G2700.abbottSmith: to strike down with an arrow , shoot dead : He 12:20 (LXX) (Rec.; WH, R, om.).†
G2700.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G5115 (τόξον); to shoot down with an arrow or other missile

G4048.thayer: I tear off, as of garments.
G4048.mounce: also spelled περιρρήγνυμι, to break or tear all around; to strip off, Acts 16:22*
G4048.abbottSmith: to break or tear off all around : freq. of garments, τ. ἱμάτια , Ac 16:22 .†
G4048.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); to tear all around, i.e. completely away

G2253.strongs: from a presumed compound of the base of G2255 (ἥμισυ) and G2348 (θνήσκω); half dead, i.e. entirely exhausted

G56.abbottSmith: ignorance (opp. to γνῶσις ): I Co 15:34 , I Pe 2:15 (v. Hort in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G56.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as negative particle) and G1108 (γνῶσις); ignorance (properly, the state)

G2079.thayer: extremely, utterly; to be at the extremity, to be "in extremis," to be at the last grasp.
G2079.mounce: to be in the last extremity, Mk. 5:23*
G2079.abbottSmith: extremely, utterly ; ἐ. ἔχειν (= Lat. in extremis esse ), only in late writers (cf. ἐν ἐσχάτοις εἶναι , FlJ, Ant. , ix, 8, 6), to be at the point of death : Mk 5:23 .†
G2079.strongs: adverb from G2078 (ἔσχατος); finally, i.e. (with G2192 (ἔχω)) at the extremity of life

G4841.mounce: to suffer with, sympathize, 1 Cor. 12:26; to suffer as another, endure corresponding sufferings, Rom. 8:17*
G4841.abbottSmith: to suffer together or with : Ro 8:17 , I Co 12:26 .
G4841.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3958 (πάσχω) (including its alternate); to experience pain jointly or of the same kind (specially, persecution; to "sympathize")

G4954.thayer: belonging to the same body; fig: of Jews and Gentiles belonging to the same Church.
G4954.mounce: united in the same body; met. pl. joint members in a spiritual body, Eph. 3:6*
G4954.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4983 (σῶμα); of a joint body, i.e. (figuratively) a fellow-member of the Christian community

G2349.abbottSmith: subject to death , mortal : Ro 6:12 8:11 , I Co 15:53 , 54 , II Co 4:11 5:4 .†

G3830.mounce: the keeper of a public inn or caravanserai, a host, Lk. 10:35*
G3830.strongs: from the same as G3829 (πανδοχεῖον); an innkeeper (warden of a caravanserai)

G4878.thayer: I lend a hand along with, take interest in (a thing) along with (others), assist jointly to perform some task, cooperate with, take my share in, help, aid.
G4878.mounce: pr. to take hold of with someone; to support, help, aid, Lk. 10:40; Rom. 8:26*
G4878.abbottSmith: to take hold with at the side for assistance ; hence, to take a share in, help in bearing , and generally, help (with various constructions, v. LXX, ll. c., Deiss., LAE , 83 f.): c. dat., Lk 10:40 , Ro 8:26 .†
G4878.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G482 (ἀντιλαμβάνομαι); to take hold of opposite together, i.e. co-operate (assist)

G788.mounce: nearer; very nigh, close; used as the compar. of ἄγχι, Acts 27:13*
G788.abbottSmith: nearer : Ac 27:13 (RV, close in shore ; v. Bl., § 11, 3; 44, 3; poets, Ion, and late prose).†
G788.strongs: neuter comparative of the base of G1451 (ἐγγύς); more nearly, i.e. very near

G868.thayer: I make to stand away, draw away, repel, take up a position away from, withdraw from, leave, abstain from.
G868.mounce: trans. to put away, separate; to draw off or away, withdraw, induce to revolt, Acts 5:37; intrans., and mid., to depart, go away from, Lk. 2:37; met. to desist or refrain from, let alone, Acts 5:38; 22:29; 2 Cor. 12:8; to make defection, fall away, apostatize, Lk. 8:13; 1 Tim. 4:1; Heb. 3:12; to withdraw from, have no intercourse with, 1 Tim. 6:5; to abstain from, 2 Tim. 2:19
G868.abbottSmith: trans. in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to put away , lead away ; metaph., to move to revolt : Ac 5:37 .
G868.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to remove, i.e. (actively) instigate to revolt; usually (reflexively) to desist, desert, etc.
G868.kjv: depart, draw (fall) away, refrain, withdraw self

G5189.mounce: stormy, tempestuous; with ἄνεμος it means hurricane, typhoon, whirlwind, Acts 27:14*
G5189.strongs: from a derivative of G5188 (τύφω); stormy (as if smoky)

G3737.mounce: bereaved of parents, orphan, Jas. 1:27; bereaved, desolate, Jn. 14:18
G3737.abbottSmith: prop., orphan , fatherless : Mk 12:40 , WH, mg, Ja 1:27 .
G3737.strongs: of uncertain affinity; bereaved ("orphan"), i.e. parentless

G3035.mounce: made of stone, Jn. 2:6; 2 Cor. 3:3; Rev. 9:20*
G3035.abbottSmith: of stone : Jo 2:6 , II Co 3:3 , Re 9:20 .†
G3035.strongs: from G3037 (λίθος); stony, i.e. made of stone

G5477.thayer: Canaan, the whole of Palestine or Palestine west of the river Jordan.
G5477.mounce: Canaan, the ancient name of Palestine, Acts 7:11; 13:19
G5477.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03667); Chanaan (i.e. Kenaan), the early name of Palestine

G1916.thayer: I cry out to or against.
G1916.abbottSmith: to cry out : Ac 25:24 (Rec., for βοάω , q.v.).†
G1916.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G994 (βοάω); to exclaim against

G2803.thayer: Claudia, a Christian woman in Rome; probably a freedwoman of the imperial household.
G2803.strongs: feminine of G2804 (Κλαύδιος); Claudia, a Christian woman

G4555.strongs: from the same as G4556 (σάρδιος); sardine (G3037 (λίθος) being implied), i.e. a gem, so called

G159.thayer: the cause, author; the culprit, the accused; the crime.
G159.mounce: causative; αἴτιος, an author or causer, Heb. 5:9; τὸ αἴτιον, equivalent to αἰτία
G159.abbottSmith: causative of , responsible for ; as subst., ὁ αἰ. , the cause , author : He 5:9 ; τὸ αἰ. , the cause , Ac 19:40 . blameworthy , culpable ; as subst., ὁ αἰ. , the culprit , the accused (Lat. reus ); τὸ αἰ. (= αἰτία , 3), the crime , Lk 23:4 , 14 , 22 .†
G159.strongs: from the same as G154 (αἰτέω); causative, i.e. (concretely) a causer

G736.thayer: a foresail, set on the bow.
G736.mounce: a topsail, foresail; or, according to others, the dolon of Pliny and Pollux, a small sail near the bow of the ship, which was hoisted when the wind was too strong to use the larger sails, Acts 27:40*
G736.abbottSmith: a fore-sail or top-sail : Ac 27:40 (v. DB , ext. , 366 b , 399 a ; MM, s.v.).†
G736.strongs: from a derivative of G737 (ἄρτι); properly, something ready (or else more remotely from G142 (αἴρω) (compare G740 (ἄρτος)); something hung up), i.e. (specially) the topsail (rather foresail or jib) of a vessel

G2092.strongs: from an old noun (fitness); adjusted, i.e. ready

G3645.mounce: also spelled ὀλεθρεύω, to destroy, cause to perish, Heb. 11:28*
G3645.strongs: from G3639 (ὄλεθρος); to spoil, i.e. slay

G382.thayer: uncountable, innumerable, that cannot be numbered.
G382.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G705 (ἀριθμέω); unnumbered, i.e. without number

G2881.mounce: Corinthian; an inhabitant of Κόρινθος, Corinth, Acts 18:8; 2 Cor. 6:11*
G2881.abbottSmith: Corinthian ; as subst., ὁ Κ. , a Corinthian : Ac 18:8 , 27 (WH, txt., R, omit), II Co 6:11 ; 1Co, 2Co, tit .†
G2881.strongs: from G2882 (Κόρινθος); a Corinthian, i.e. inhabitant of Corinth

G3631.mounce: wine, Mt. 9:17; Mk. 2:22; meton. the vine and its clusters, Rev. 6:6 met. οἶνος, a potion, οἶνος τοῦ θυμοῦ, a furious potion, Rev. 14:8, 10; 16:19; 17:2, 18:3
G3631.abbottSmith: wine : Mt 9:17 , Lk 1:15 , Jo 2:3 , al.; οἴνῳ προσέχειν , I Ti 3:8 ; δουλοῦσθαι , Tit 2:3 . Metaph., Re 14:8 , 10 16:19 17:2 18:3 19:15 . By meton. for ἄμπελος , Re 6:6 .
G3631.strongs: a primary word (or perhaps of Hebrew origin (H03196)); "wine" (literally or figuratively)

G5038.thayer: a wall, especially of a city.
G5038.mounce: a wall of a city, Acts 9:25; 2 Cor. 11:33; Heb. 11:30; Rev. 21:12, 14f., 17-19*
G5038.abbottSmith: a wall , esp. that about a town: Ac 9:25 , II Co 11:33 , He 11:30 , Re 21:12-19 †
G5038.strongs: akin to the base of G5088 (τίκτω); a wall (as formative of a house)

G4397.mounce: prophetic, uttered by prophets, Rom. 16:26; 2 Pet. 1:19*
G4397.abbottSmith: of prophecy , prophetic : Ro 16:26 , II Pe 1:19 .†
G4397.strongs: from G4396 (προφήτης); pertaining to a foreteller ("prophetic")

G1438.mounce: himself, herself, itself, Mt. 8:22; 12:26; 9:21; also used for the first and second persons, Rom. 8:23; Mt. 23:31; also equivalent to ἀλλήλων, Mk. 10:26; Jn. 12:19; ἀφ’ ἑαυτοῦ, ἀφ’ ἑαυτῶν, of himself, themselves, voluntarily, spontaneously, Lk. 12:57; 21:30; of one’s own will merely, Jn. 5:19; δι’ ἑαυτοῦ, of itself, in its own nature, Rom. 14:14; ἐξ ἑαυτῶν, of one’s own self, 2 Cor. 3:5; καθ’ ἑαυτόν, by one’s self, alone, Acts 28:16; Jas. 2:17; παρ’ ἑαυτῷ, with one’s self, at home, 1 Cor. 16:2; πρὸς ἑαυτόν, to one’s self, to one’s home, Lk. 24:12; Jn. 20:10; or, with one’s self, Lk. 18:11
G1438.abbottSmith: prop, of 3rd person (Lat. sui, sibi, se ), of himself, herself, itself , etc.: Mt 27:42 , Mk 15:31 , Lk 23:35 , al.; added to a middle verb, διεμερίσαντο ἑαυτοῖς , Jo 19:24 ; to an active verb, Ac 14:14 (M, Pr. , 157); ἀφ’ ἑαυτοῦ , Lk 12:57 21:30 , Jo 5:19 , al. (v.s. ἀπό ); δι’ ἑαυτοῦ , Ro 14:14 ; ἐν ἑ. , Mt 3:9 , Mk 5:30 , al.; εἰς ἑ. , Lk 15:17 ; καθ’ ἑαυτόν , Ac 28:16 , Ja 2:17 ; παρ’ ἑαυτῷ ,
G1438.strongs: from a reflexive pronoun otherwise obsolete and the genitive case (dative case or accusative case) of G846 (αὐτός); him- (her-, it-, them-, also (in conjunction with the personal pronoun of the other persons) my-, thy-, our-, your-) self (selves), etc.
G1438.kjv: alone, her (own, -self), (he) himself, his (own), itself, one (to) another, our (thine) own(-selves), + that she had, their (own, own selves), (of) them(-selves), they, thyself, you, your (own, own conceits, own selves, -selves)

G1800.thayer: Enosh (also Enos), son of Seth, and father of Cainan.
G1800.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0583); Enos (i.e. Enosh), a patriarch

G4422.thayer: I terrify, scare, strike with panic.
G4422.mounce: to terrify, affright; pass. to be terrified, Lk. 21:9; 24:37*
G4422.abbottSmith: to terrify . Pass., to be terrified : Lk 21:9 24:37 (WH, mg., θροηθέντες ).†
G4422.strongs: probably akin to the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω) (through the idea of causing to fall) or to G4072 (πέτομαι) (through that of causing to fly away); to scare

G3392.mounce: pr. to tinge, dye, stain; to pollute, defile, ceremonially, Jn. 18:28; to corrupt, deprave, Tit. 1:15 (2x); Heb. 12:15; Jude 8*
G3392.strongs: perhaps a primary verb; to sully or taint, i.e. contaminate (ceremonially or morally)

G2317.thayer: reverence for God, fear of God, godliness, piety.
G2317.mounce: worshipping of God, reverence towards God, piety, 1 Tim. 2:10*
G2317.abbottSmith: fear of God , godliness : I Ti 2:10 .†

G4277.thayer: I say previously, predict, proclaim, foretell.
G4277.mounce: to tell beforehand, to foretell, Mt. 24:25; Acts 1:16; Rom. 9:29; 2 Cor. 13:2; Gal. 5:21; 1 Thess. 3:4
G4277.abbottSmith: to say before : Ga 1:9 , I Th 4:6 (on the form -αμεν , v. WH, App. , 164), He 4:7 ; seq. ὅτι , II Co 7:3 13:2 , Ga 5:21 ; of prophecy, Mt 24:25 , Mk 13:23 , Ac 1:16 , Ro 9:29 , II Pe 3:2 , Ju 17 .
G4277.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2036 (ἔπω); to say already, to predict

G367.thayer: Ananias, (a) husband of Sapphira, a member of the early church at Jerusalem, (b) a member of the church at Damascus, (c) the high priest at Jerusalem.
G367.mounce: Ananias, pr. name I. A Christian of Jerusalem, Acts 5:1, etc. II. A Christian of Damascus, Acts 9:12, etc. III. High Priest, Acts 23:2; 24:1
G367.abbottSmith: Ananias ; of Jerusalem: Ac 5:1 , 3 5:5 . Of Damascus: Ac 9:10-13 , 17 22:12 . High Priest: Ac 23:2 24:1 .†
G367.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02608); Ananias, the name of three Israelites

G737.thayer: now, just now, at this moment.
G737.mounce: pr. at the present moment, close upon it either before of after; now, at the present juncture, Mt. 3:15; forthwith, presently; just now, recently, 1 Thess. 3:6; ἕως ἄρτι, until now, hitherto, Mt. 11:12; Jn. 2:10; ἀπ’ ἄρτι, or ἀπάρτι, from this time, henceforth, Mt. 23:39
G737.abbottSmith: just , just now , this moment : Mt 3:15 9:18 26:53 , Jo 13:7 , Ga 4:20 , I Th 3:6 (v. Lft., Notes , 44; Milligan, in l.), Re 12:10 ; opp. to past time, Jo 9:19 , 25 13:33 , I Co 16:7 , Ga 1:9 , 10 ; to future, Jo 13:37 16:12 , 31 , I Co 13:12 , II Th 2:7 , I Pe 1:6 , 8 ; ἄχρι τῆς ἄ. ὥρας , I Co 4:11 ; ἕως ἄ. , Mt 11:12 , Jo 2:10 5:17 16:24 , I Co 4:13 8:7 15:6 , I Jo 2:9 ; ἀπ᾽ ἄ. , v.s. ἀπάρτι (v.
G737.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G142 (αἴρω) (compare G740 (ἄρτος)) through the idea of suspension; just now
G737.kjv: this day (hour), hence(-forth), here(-after), hither(-to), (even) now, (this) present

G3023.mounce: a lion, Heb. 11:33; 1 Pet. 5:8; Rev. 4:7; 9:8, 17; 10:3; 13:2; met. a lion, cruel adversary, tyrant, 2 Tim. 4:17; a lion, a hero, deliverer, Rev. 5:5*

G1781.mounce: some list as ἐντέλλομαι, mid., to enjoin, charge, command, Mt. 4:6; 15:4; 17:9; to direct, Mt. 19:7; Mk. 10:3
G1781.abbottSmith: -ομαι , to command, enjoin, instruct : seq. περί He 11:22 ; c. inf., Mt 19:7 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 1:2 ; οὕτως , Ac 13:47 ; καθώς , Jo 14:31 ( ἐντολὴν ἔδωκεν , WH); seq. λέγων , Mt 17:9 ; c. inf., Jo 8:5 ; ἵνα , Mk 13:34 ; c. acc rei, Mt 28:20 , Mk 10:3 , Jo 15:14 , 17 ; seq. περί , c. gen. pers., Mt 4:6 and Lk 4:10 (LXX) ; διαθήκην ἐ. πρός , c. acc pers., He 9:20 (LXX) (cf. Si 45:3 ).†
G1781.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G5056 (τέλος); to enjoin

G5449.mounce: essence, Gal. 4:8; native condition, birth, Rom. 2:27; 11:21, 24; Gal. 2:15; Eph. 2:3; native species, kind, Jas. 3:7; nature, natural frame, 2 Pet. 1:4; nature, native instinct, Rom. 2:14; 1 Cor. 11:14; nature, prescribed course of nature, Rom. 1:26*
G5449.strongs: from G5453 (φύω); growth (by germination or expansion), i.e. (by implication) natural production (lineal descent); by extension, a genus or sort; figuratively, native disposition, constitution or usage

G1843.thayer: (a) I consent fully, agree out and out, (b) I confess, admit, acknowledge (cf. the early Hellenistic sense of the middle: I acknowledge a debt), (c) I give thanks, praise.
G1843.mounce: to agree, bind one’s self, promise, Lk. 22:6; mid. to confess, Mt. 3:6; to profess openly, Phil. 2:11; Rev. 3:5; to make open avowal of benefits; to praise, celebrate, Mt. 11:25; Lk. 10:21
G1843.abbottSmith: act. = cl. ὁμολογέω , to profess or agree to do (Field, Notes , 75): Lk 22:6 .
G1843.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3670 (ὁμολογέω); to acknowledge or (by implication, of assent) agree fully

G5325.mounce: pr. a rendering visible; a display; pomp, parade, Acts 25:23*
G5325.strongs: from a derivative of G5324 (φαντάζω); (properly abstract) a (vain) show ("fantasy")

G654.thayer: I turn away, pervert, remove; I restore, replace; mid: I desert, reject.
G654.mounce: to turn away; to remove, Acts 3:26; Rom. 11:26; 2 Tim. 4:4; to turn a people from their allegiance, to their sovereign, pervert, incite to revolt, Lk. 23:14; to replace, restore, Mt. 26:52; to turn away from any one, to slight, reject, repulse, Mt. 5:42; Tit. 1:14; Heb. 12:25; to desert, 2 Tim. 1:15
G654.abbottSmith: trans., c. acc., to turn away , remove : Ro 11:26 , II Ti 4:4 ; metaph., to turn away from allegiance, pervert : Lk 23:14 ; to turn back , return : μάχαιραν , Mt 26:52 . Pass., reflex., to turn oneself away from : c. acc., Mt 5:42 , II Ti 1:15 , Tit 1:14 , He 12:25 ; so act., absol., Ac 3:26 (cf. Si 8:5 ; Bl., § 53, 1; Cremer, 880).†
G654.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4762 (στρέφω); to turn away or back (literally or figuratively)

G2893.mounce: to lighten, make light or less heavy, Acts 27:38*

G3937.thayer: Parmenas, one of the original seven deacons at Jerusalem.
G3937.strongs: probably by contraction for (a derivative of a compound of G3844 (παρά) and G3306 (μένω)); constant; Parmenas, a Christian

G1663.thayer: Eliezer, son of Joram and father of Joshua.
G1663.abbottSmith: Eliezer , an ancestor of Jesus: Lk 3:29 .†
G1663.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0461); Eliezer, an Israelite

G3988.thayer: persuasion, conviction, a yielding to persuasion.
G3988.mounce: a yielding to persuasion, assent, Gal. 5:8*
G3988.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G3982 (πείθω); persuadableness, i.e. credulity

G2896.mounce: to utter a cry, Mt. 14:26; to exclaim, cry out, Mt. 9:27; Jn. 1:15; to cry for vengeance, Jas. 5:4; to cry in supplication, Rom. 8:15; Gal. 4:6
G2896.abbottSmith: prop. onomatop., of the raven, to croak , hence generally, of inartic. cries, to scream , cry out (Æsch., al.): Mk 5:5 9:26 15:39 (Rec., R, mg.), Lk 9:39 , al.; of crying for vengeance (cf. Ge 4:10 ), Ja 5:4 .
G2896.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to "croak" (as a raven) or scream, i.e. (genitive case) to call aloud (shriek, exclaim, intreat)

G2642.thayer: I stone down, stone to death, overwhelm with stones.
G2642.mounce: to stone, kill by stoning, Lk. 20:6*
G2642.abbottSmith: to cast stones at , to stone : Lk 20:6 .†
G2642.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3034 (λιθάζω); to stone down, i.e. to death

G2894.abbottSmith: a basket , probably of wicker-work, such as were carried by Jews for food: Mt 14:20 16:9 , Mk 6:43 8:19 Lk 9:17 13:8 , Jo 6:13 (cf. σφυρίς ).†

G3785.thayer: O that! I wish! Would that!, followed by indicative.
G3785.mounce: originally a ptcp (aor act ptcp nom sg neut) from ὀφείλω, used in NT as an interj. to introduce a wish that cannot be attained, O that! Would that! 1 Cor. 4:8; 2 Cor. 11:1; Gal. 5:12; Rev. 3:15*
G3785.abbottSmith: would that : with indic. aor., I Co 4:8 ; impf., II Co 11:1 , Re 3:15 ; fut., Ga 5:12 (a practicable wish, v. Bl., 206 f., 220. The construction with indic. is only found in late writers.)†
G3785.strongs: first person singular of a past tense of G3784 (ὀφείλω); I ought (wish), i.e. (interjection) oh that!

G3352.mounce: a sharing, partaking; communion, fellowship, 2 Cor. 6:14*

G1478.thayer: Hezekiah, son of Ahaz, father of Manasseh, and king of Judah (727-686 [?] B.C.).
G1478.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02396); Ezekias (i.e. Hezekeiah), an Israelite

G3423.thayer: I ask in marriage; pass: I am betrothed.
G3423.mounce: to ask in marriage; to betroth; pass. to be betrothed, engaged, Mt. 1:18; Lk. 1:27; 2:5*
G3423.strongs: from a derivative of G3415 (μνάομαι); to give a souvenir (engagement present), i.e. betroth

G2241.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0410 with pronominal suffix); my God

G3413.mounce: Michael, the archangel, indecl., Jude 9; Rev. 12:7*
G3413.abbottSmith: Michael , the Archangel (cf. Da 12:1 ): Ju 9 , Re 12:7 .†
G3413.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04317); Michael, an archangel

G4557.thayer: sardonyx, a precious stone, white streaked with red.
G4557.mounce: sardonyx, a gem exhibiting the color of the carnelian and the white of the calcedony, intermingled in alternate layers, Rev. 21:20*
G4557.abbottSmith: sardonyx , a stone marked by the red of the sard and the white of the onyx: Re 21:20 .†
G4557.strongs: from the base of G4556 (σάρδιος) and (the nail of a finger; hence the "onyx" stone); a "sardonyx", i.e. the gem so called

G3574.thayer: a night and day, twenty-four hours.
G3574.mounce: a day and night, twenty-four hours, 2 Cor. 11:25*
G3574.abbottSmith: lasting a night and a day ; as subst., ( τὸ ) ν. , a night and a day : II Co 11:25 (pl., Or. Sib. , 8, 203).†
G3574.strongs: from G3571 (νύξ) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); a day-and-night, i.e. full day of twenty-four hours

G1389.mounce: pr. to entrap, beguile; to adulterate, corrupt, falsify, 2 Cor. 4:2*
G1389.strongs: from G1388 (δόλος); to ensnare, i.e. (figuratively) adulterate

G786.thayer: implacable, "not to be bound by truce".
G786.mounce: pr. unwilling to make a treaty; hence, implacable, irreconcilable, 2 Tim. 3:3*
G786.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4689 (σπένδω); literally, without libation (which usually accompanied a treaty), i.e. (by implication) truceless

G2407.mounce: to officiate as a priest, perform sacred rites, Lk. 1:8*
G2407.abbottSmith: to be a priest , officiate as a priest : Lk 1:8 (the word is freq. in Inscr.; v. LS, s.v.; Deiss., BS , 215; LAE , 70; Cremer, 734).†
G2407.strongs: prolongation from G2409 (ἱερεύς); to be a priest, i.e. perform his functions

G2787.thayer: (properly: a wooden box, hence) the Ark, in which Noah sailed; the Ark of the Covenant.
G2787.mounce: a chest, coffer; the ark of the covenant, Heb. 9:4; Rev. 11:19; the ark of Noah, Mt. 24:38; Lk. 17:27; Heb. 11:7; 1 Pet. 3:20*
G2787.abbottSmith: a wooden box or chest : of Noah's ark, Mt 24:38 , Lk 17:27 , He 11:7 , I Pe 3:20 ; of the ark of the covenant, He 9:4 , Re 11:19 .†
G2787.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a box, i.e. the sacred ark and that of Noah

G4767.strongs: from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary (to hate); hated, i.e. odious

G3901.thayer: (lit: I flow past, glide past, hence) I am lost, perish, or merely: I drift away (fall away) from duty.
G3901.mounce: to flow beside; to glide aside from; to fall off from profession, decline from steadfastness, make forfeit of faith, Heb. 2:1*
G3901.abbottSmith: to flow by , drift away , slip away : He 2:1 .†
G3901.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and the alternate of G4482 (ῥέω); to flow by, i.e. (figuratively) carelessly pass (miss)

G4413.mounce: first in time, order, etc., Mt. 10:2; 26:17; first in dignity, importance, etc., chief, principal, most important, Mk. 6:21; Lk. 19:47; Acts 13:50; 16:12; as an equivalent to the compar. πρότερος (G4754), prior, Jn. 1:5, 30; 15:18; Mt. 27:64; adverbially, first, Jn. 1:42; 5:4; 8:7
G4413.abbottSmith: first , of Time or Place; absol., as subst., ὁ π. ., Lk 14:18 , Jo 19:32 , I Co 14:30 ; ὁ π. καὶ ὁ ἔσχατος , Re 1:17 2:8 22:13 ; neut., τὸ π. , opp. to τ. δεύτερον , He 10:9 ; τὰ π. , opp. to τ. ἔσχατα , Mt 12:45 , Lk 11:26 , II Pe 2:20 ; anarth., Mt 10:2 ; pl., Mt 19:30 , Mk 10:31 , Lk 13:30 ; ἐν πρώτοις (EV, first of all ), I Co 15:3 ; as adj.: πρώτῃ (sc. ἡμέρᾳ ) σαββάτου , Mk 16:[9] ; φυλακή ,
G4413.strongs: contracted superlative of G4253 (πρό); foremost (in time, place, order or importance)
G4413.kjv: before, beginning, best, chief(-est), first (of all), former

G81.thayer: brotherhood (in the collective sense), the members of the Christian Church, Christendom.
G81.mounce: brotherhood, the body of the Christian brotherhood, 1 Pet. 2:17; 5:9*
G81.abbottSmith: abstract, brotherhood, brotherly affection (LXX). Concrete, the brotherhood , the Christian community: I Pe 2:17 5:9 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G81.strongs: from G80 (ἀδελφός); brotherhood (properly, the feeling of brotherliness), i.e. the (Christian) fraternity

G576.thayer: I go or come out of, disembark, turn out, result, become, happen.
G576.mounce: to step off; to disembark from a ship, Lk. 5:2; Jn. 21:9; to become, result, happen, Lk. 21:13; Phil. 1:19*
G576.abbottSmith: to step off , disembark : Lk 5:2 , Jo 21:9 ; metaph., of events, to issue , turn out (Field, Notes , 74): Lk 21:13 , Phl 1:19 (MM, s.v.).†
G576.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G939 (βάσις); literally, to disembark; figuratively, to eventuate

G276.abbottSmith: immutable : He 6:18 ; as subst., τὸ ἀ. , immutability , He 6:17 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G276.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3346 (μετατίθημι); unchangeable, or (neuter as abstract) unchangeability

G2455.abbottSmith: Judah , son of Jacob: Mt 1:2 , 3 , Lk 3:33 ; φυλή Ἰ. , Re 5:5 7:5 ; by meton., of the tribe, He 7:14 ; of its confines, γῆ Ἰ. , Mt 2:6 ; πόλις Ἰ. , Lk 1:39 .
G2455.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03063); Judas (i.e. Jehudah), the name of ten Israelites; also of the posterity of one of them and its region

G5160.mounce: nourishment, food, Mt. 3:4; Lk. 12:23; Jn. 4:8; Acts 9:19; Jas. 2:15; provision, Mt. 24:45; sustenance, maintenance, Mt. 10:10; met. nourishment of the mind, of spiritual nourishment, Heb. 5:12, 14
G5160.strongs: from G5142 (τρέφω); nourishment (literally or figuratively); by implication, rations (wages)

G490.thayer: Antioch, (a) Antioch on the river Orontes, capital of the Province Syria, (b) "Pisidian" Antioch, not in Pisidia, but near Pisidia, in the Roman Province Galatia.
G490.mounce: Antioch, pr. name I. Antioch, the metropolis of Syria, where the disciples first received the name of Christians II. Antioch, a city of Pisidia, Acts 13:14; 14:19; 2 Tim. 3:11. See PhotoGuide.
G490.strongs: from (a Syrian king); Antiochia, a place in Syria

G3136.thayer: Martha, sister of Mary and Lazarus of Bethany.
G3136.strongs: probably of Chaldee origin (meaning mistress); Martha, a Christian woman

G1771.thayer: thinking, consideration; a thought, purpose, design, intention.
G1771.mounce: notion, idea; thought, purpose, intention, Heb. 4:12; 1 Pet. 4:1*
G1771.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G3563 (νοῦς); thoughtfulness, i.e. moral understanding

G4790.thayer: I am a partaker with, have fellowship with, am an accomplice in.
G4790.mounce: to be a joint partaker, participate with a person; in NT to mix one’s self up in a thing, to involve one’s self, be an accomplice in, Eph. 5:11; Rev. 18:4; to sympathize actively in, to relieve, Phil. 4:14*
G4790.abbottSmith: to have fellowship with or in (in cl., c. gen. rei, dat. pers.): c. dat. rei, Eph 5:11 , Re 18:4 ; id. c. gen. pers., Phl 4:14 .†
G4790.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2841 (κοινωνέω); to share in company with, i.e. co-participate in
G4790.kjv: communicate (have fellowship) with, be partaker of

G1956.thayer: I loose, release; I solve, settle, explain, interpret, decide.
G1956.mounce: to loose what has previously been fastened or entangled, as a knot; met. to solve, to explain, what is enigmatical, as a parable, Mk. 4:34; to settle, put an end to a matter of debate, Acts 19:39*
G1956.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3089 (λύω); to solve further, i.e. (figuratively) to explain, decide

G4134.thayer: full, abounding in, complete, completely occupied with.
G4134.mounce: full, filled, Mt. 14:20; 15:37; full of disease, Lk. 5:12; met. full of, abounding in, wholly occupied with, completely under the influence of, or affected by, Lk. 4:1; Jn. 1:14; Acts 9:36; full, complete, perfect, Mk. 4:28
G4134.abbottSmith: full , filled : Mt 14:20 15:37 ; c. gen. rei, Mk 8:19 ; λέπρας , Lk 5:12 ; metaph., of the soul: πνεύματος ἁγίου , Lk 4:1 , Ac 6:3 7:55 11:24 ; πίστεως , Ac 6:5 ; χάριτος , Ac 6:8 ; χ. καὶ ἀληθείας (where π. is indecl.; v. M, Pr. , 50; Milligan, NTD , 65, with reff. in each), Jo 1:14 ; δόλου , Ac 13:10 ; θυμοῦ , Ac 19:28 ; ἔργων ἀγαθῶν , Ac 9:36 .
G4134.strongs: from G4130 (πλήθω); replete, or covered over; by analogy, complete

G4612.strongs: of Latin origin; a semicinctium or half-girding, i.e. narrow covering (apron)

G5599.thayer: O, an exclamation, used in addressing someone.
G5599.strongs: a primary interjection; as a sign of the vocative case, O; as a note of exclamation, oh

G5318.thayer: apparent, clear, visible, manifest; adv: clearly.
G5318.mounce: apparent, manifest, clear, known, well-known, Mk. 4:22; 6:14; Gal. 5:19; in outward guise, externally, Rom. 2:28
G5318.abbottSmith: open to sight, visible, manifest : Ga 5:19 ; seq. ἐν , Ro 1:19 , I Jo 3:10 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 4:16 7:13 , I Ti 4:15 ; φ. γίνεσθαι (in LXX for בּחן ni., Ge 42:16 ), Mk 6:14 , Lk 8:17 , I Co 3:13 14:25 ; id. seq. ἐν , I Co 11:19 , Phl 1:13 ; φ. ποιεῖν , Mt 12:16 , Mk 3:12 ; εἰς φ. ἐλθεῖν (cf. Bl., § 47, 2), Mk 4:22 , Lk 8:17 ; ἐν τῷ φ. (opp. to ἐν τ. κρυπτῷ ), Ro 2:28 .†
G5318.strongs: from G5316 (φαίνω); shining, i.e. apparent (literally or figuratively); neuter (as adverb) publicly, externally
G5318.kjv: abroad, + appear, known, manifest, open (+ -ly), outward (+ -ly)

G2000.mounce: on the verge of falling, unsteady; met. insecure, hazardous, dangerous, Acts 27:9*
G2000.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and (to trip); figuratively, insecure

G3492.strongs: from G3491 (ναῦς); a boatman, i.e. seaman

G2166.thayer: the Euphrates, boundary river of the province Syria.
G2166.strongs: of foreign origin (compare H06578); Euphrates, a river of Asia

G4641.mounce: hardness of heart, obstinacy, perverseness, Mt. 19:8; Mk. 10:5; 16:14*
G4641.strongs: feminine of a compound of G4642 (σκληρός) and G2588 (καρδία); hard-heartedness, i.e. (specially), destitution of (spiritual) perception

G4758.thayer: I collect an army, enlist troops.
G4758.mounce: to collect or gather an army, enlist troops, 2 Tim. 2:4*
G4758.abbottSmith: to levy a troop, enlist soldiers : II Ti 2:4 (Diod., Plut., al.).†
G4758.strongs: from a compound of the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense); to gather (or select) as a warrior, i.e. enlist in the army

G123.thayer: sea-coast, (sandy) beach; shore (of sea or lake), land.
G123.abbottSmith: the sea-shore , beach (cf. Field, Notes , 146; DCG, i, 175 f.; MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 13:2 , 48 , Jo 21:4 , Ac 21:5 27:39 , 40 .†
G123.strongs: from (to rush) and G251 (ἅλς) (in the sense of the sea; a beach (on which the waves dash)

G3720.mounce: a later form of ὄρθριος, of or belonging to the morning, morning, Lk. 24:22*
G3720.strongs: from G3722 (ὄρθρος); relating to the dawn, i.e. matutinal (as an epithet of Venus, especially brilliant in the early day)

G489.abbottSmith: a reward , requital : in good sense, II Co 6:13 ; in bad sense, Ro 1:27 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G489.strongs: from a compound of G473 (ἀντί) and G3408 (μισθός); requital, correspondence

G2579.thayer: and if, even if, even, at least.
G2579.mounce: crasis, and if, Mk. 16:18; also if, Mt. 21:21; even if, if even, although, Jn. 10:38; if so much as, Heb. 12:20; also in NT simply equivalent to καί, as a particle of emphasis, by a pleonasm of ἄν, at least, at all events, Mk. 6:56; Acts 5:15; 2 Cor. 11:16
G2579.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1437 (ἐάν); and (or even) if
G2579.kjv: and (also) if (so much as), if but, at the least, though, yet

G865.thayer: not loving that which is good.
G865.mounce: not a lover of good and good men, 2 Tim. 3:3*
G865.abbottSmith: without love of good : II Ti 3:3 (not elsewhere in Gk. lit., but v. MM, s.v.).†
G865.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5358 (φιλάγαθος); hostile to virtue
G865.kjv: despiser of those that are good

G3923.thayer: I contribute besides, bring in besides, smuggle in.
G3923.mounce: to bring in beside; to bring into play, exhibit in addition, 2 Pet. 1:5*
G3923.abbottSmith: to bring in or supply besides : σπουδὴν π. (late Gk. for cl. σ. ποιεῖσθαι ; cf. Deiss., BS , 361), II Pe 1:5 .†
G3923.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1533 (εἰσφέρω); to bear in alongside, i.e. introduce simultaneously

G4231.mounce: to be occupied with or employed in any business, do business; to trade traffic, Lk. 19:13*
G4231.strongs: from G4229 (πρᾶγμα); to busy oneself with, i.e. to trade

G2876.strongs: perhaps from G2880 (κορέννυμι); a crow (from its voracity)

G16.thayer: the doing of that which is good, well-doing.

G3377.thayer: (a) I reveal, make known (in a law court), I lay information, inform, (b) I make known, point out.
G3377.mounce: to disclose what is secret, Jn. 11:57; Acts 23:30; 1 Cor. 10:28; to declare, indicate, Lk. 20:37*
G3377.abbottSmith: to disclose , declare , make known : Lk 20:37 , I Co 10:28 ; in forensic sense, to inform , report : Jo 11:57 ; pass., c. dat. pers., Ac 23:30 .†
G3377.strongs: probably from the same base as G3145 (μασσάομαι) and G3415 (μνάομαι) (i.e. , to strive); to disclose (through the idea of mental effort and thus calling to mind), i.e. report, declare, intimate

G2476.thayer: trans: (a) I make to stand, place, set up, establish, appoint; mid: I place myself, stand, (b) I set in balance, weigh; intrans: (c) I stand, stand by, stand still; met: I stand ready, stand firm, am steadfast.
G2476.mounce: pluperfect, ἑστάμην, also formed as στήκω   10x, trans. to make to stand, set, place, Mt. 4:5; to set forth, appoint, Acts 1:23; to fix, appoint, Acts 17:31; to establish, confirm, Rom. 10:3; Heb. 10:9; to set down, impute, Acts 7:60; to weigh out, pay, Mt. 26:15; intrans. to stand, Mt. 12:46; to stand fast, be firm, be permanent, endure, Mt. 12:25; Eph. 6:13; to be confirmed, proved, Mt. 18:16; 2 Cor. 13:1; to stop, Lk. 7:14; 8:44; Acts 8:38
G2476.abbottSmith: Trans, in pres., impf., fut. and 1 aor. act. and in the tenses of the pass. to make to stand , to place , set , set up , establish , appoint : c. acc. pers., Mk 7:9 , Ac 1:23 6:13 17:31 , He 10:9 ; id. seq. ἐπί , c. acc. loc., Mt 4:5 , Lk 4:9 ; ἐν μέσῳ , Mt 18:2 , Mk 9:36 , Jo 8:[3] ; ἐνώπιον , Ac 6:6 ; παρ’ ἑαυτῷ , Lk 9:47 ; ἐκ δεξιῶν , Mt 25:33 ; mid., to place oneself , to stand : Re 18:15 ; so
G2476.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary (of the same meaning, and used for it in certain tenses); to stand (transitively or intransitively), used in various applications (literally or figuratively)
G2476.kjv: abide, appoint, bring, continue, covenant, establish, hold up, lay, present, set (up), stanch, stand (by, forth, still, up)

G1954.thayer: remaining, that is left over, still left.
G1954.abbottSmith: still left, remaining : χρόνος , I Pe 4:2 .†
G1954.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3062 (λοιποί); left over, i.e. remaining

G3583.thayer: I dry up, parch, am ripened, wither, waste away.
G3583.mounce: to dry up, parch, Jas. 1:11; pass. to be parched, Mt. 13:6, et al.; to be ripened as corn, Rev. 14:15; to be withered, to wither, Mk. 11:20; of parts of the body, to be withered, Mk. 3:1, 3; to pine, Mk. 9:18
G3583.abbottSmith: to dry up , parch , wither . c. acc., τ. χόρτον , Ja 1:11 ; pass., to become or be dry or withered : of plants, Mt 13:6 21:19 , 20 , Mk 4:6 11:20 , 21 , Lk 8:6 , Jo 15:6 , I Pe 1:24 ; of ripened crops, Re 14:15 ; of liquids, Mk 5:29 , Re 16:12 ; of members of the body, to waste away , Mk 3:1 (cf. III Ki 13:4 ) 9:18 .†
G3583.strongs: from G3584 (ξηρός); to desiccate; by implication, to shrivel, to mature
G3583.kjv: dry up, pine away, be ripe, wither (away)

G545.thayer: unbelieving, disobedient, who will not be persuaded.
G545.mounce: who will not be persuaded, uncompliant; disobedient, Lk. 1:17; Acts 26:19; Rom. 1:30; 2 Tim. 3:2; Tit. 1:16; 3:3
G545.abbottSmith: disobedient : absol., Lk 1:17 , Tit 1:16 3:3 ; c. dat., Ac 26:19 , Ro 1:30 , II Ti 3:2 .†
G545.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3982 (πείθω); unpersuadable, i.e. contumacious

G3822.thayer: I make old, declare obsolete; pass: I grow old, become obsolete.
G3822.mounce: to make old; pass. to grow old, to become worn, Lk. 12:33; Heb. 1:11; met. to treat as antiquated, to abrogate, supersede, Heb. 8:13*
G3822.abbottSmith: to make or declare old : He 8:13 ; pass., to become old : of things worn out by time and use (cf. Jo 9:13 , Is 50:9 , al., and v.s. παλαιός ), Lk 12:33 , He 1:11 (LXX) ; τὸ παλαιούμενον , He 8:13 (where this and the act., v. supr., may have the sense of abrogate , v. LS, Zorell, s.v.).†
G3822.strongs: from G3820 (παλαιός); to make (passively, become) worn out, or declare obsolete

G5281.mounce: patient endurance, 2 Cor. 12:12; Col. 1:11; patient awaiting, Lk. 21:19; a patient frame of mind, patience, Rom. 5:3, 4; 15:4, 5; Jas. 1:3; perseverance, Rom. 2:7; endurance in adherence to an object, 1 Thess. 1:3; 2 Thess. 3:5; Rev. 1:9; ἐν ὑπομονῇ and δι’ ὑπομονῆς, constantly, perseveringly, Lk. 8:15; Rom. 8:25; Heb. 12:1; an enduring of affliction, etc., the act of suffering, undergoing, etc., 2 Cor. 1:6; 6:4
G5281.strongs: from G5278 (ὑπομένω); cheerful (or hopeful) endurance, constancy

G5542.thayer: a kind address; in a bad sense: plausible speaking.
G5542.abbottSmith: fair speaking : in bad sense, Ro 16:18 (in good sense also, eccl.).†
G5542.strongs: from a compound of G5543 (χρηστός) and G3004 (λέγω); fair speech, i.e. plausibility

G3313.mounce: a part, portion, division, of a whole, Lk. 11:36; 15:12; Acts 5:2; Eph. 4:16; a piece, fragment, Lk. 24:42; Jn. 19:23; a party, faction, Acts 23:9; allotted portion, lot, destiny, Mt. 24:51; Lk. 12:46; a calling, craft, Acts 19:27; a partner’s portion, partnership, fellowship, Jn. 13:8; pl. μέρη, a local quarter, district, region, Mt. 2:22; 16:13; Acts 19:1; Eph. 4:9; side of a ship, Jn. 21:6; ἐν μέρει, in respect, 2 Cor. 3:10; 9:3; Col. 2:16; 1 Pet. 4:16; μέρος τι, partly, in some part, 1 Cor. 11:18; ἀνὰ μέρος, alternately, one after another, 1 Cor. 14:27; ἀπὸ μέρους, partly, in some part or measure, 2 Cor. 1:14; ἐκ μέρους, individually, 1 Cor. 12:27; partly, imperfectly, 1 Cor. 13:9; κατὰ μέρος, particularly, in detail, Heb. 9:5
G3313.abbottSmith: a part , share , portion : Jo 13:8 , Ac 19:27 (Page, in l.), Re 20:6 22:19 ; hence (cl.), lot , destiny , Mt 24:51 , Lk 12:46 , Re 21:8 .
G3313.strongs: from an obsolete but more primary form of (to get as a section or allotment); a division or share (literally or figuratively, in a wide application)
G3313.kjv: behalf, course, coast, craft, particular (+ -ly), part (+ -ly), piece, portion, respect, side, some sort(-what)

G2488.thayer: Jotham, son of Uzziah and father of Ahaz.
G2488.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03147); Joatham (i.e. Jotham), an Israelite

G3478.thayer: Nazareth, a city of Galilee, where Jesus lived before His ministry.
G3478.mounce: see Ναζαρέθ, Mt. 2:23; Mk. 1:9; Jn. 1:45, 46*. See PhotoGuide.
G3478.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Nazareth or Nazaret, a place in Palestine

G1307.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5316 (φαίνω); appearing through, i.e. "diaphanous"

G5620.thayer: so that, therefore, so then, so as to.
G5620.strongs: from G5613 (ὡς) and G5037 (τέ); so too, i.e. thus therefore (in various relations of consecution, as follow)
G5620.kjv: (insomuch) as, so that (then), (insomuch) that, therefore, to, wherefore

G2288.mounce: death, the extinction of life, whether naturally, Lk. 2:26; Mk. 9:1; or violently, Mt. 10:21; 15:4; imminent danger of death, 2 Cor. 4:11, 12; 11:23; in NT spiritual death, as opposed to ζωή in its spiritual sense, in respect of a forfeiture of salvation, Jn. 8:51; Rom. 6:16
G2288.abbottSmith: death ; of the death of the body, whether natural or violent: Jo 11:13 , Phl 2:27 , He 7:23 , al; opp. to ζωή , Ro 8:38 , Phl 1:20 ; of the death of Christ, Ro 5:10 , Phl 3:10 , He 2:9 ; ῥυέσθαι (σώζειν) ἐκ θ. , II Co 1:10 , He 5:7 ; περίλυπος ἕως θανάτου , Mt 26:38 , Mk 14:34 ; μέχρι (ἄχρι) θ. , Phl 2:8 , Re 2:10 ; πληγὴ θανάτου , a deadly wound, Re 13:3 ; ἰδεῖν θάνατον , Lk 2:26 , He 11:5 ; γεύε
G2288.strongs: from G2348 (θνήσκω); (properly, an adjective used as a noun) death (literally or figuratively)

G109.thayer: air, the lower air we breathe.
G109.abbottSmith: in Hom., Hes., the lower air which surrounds the earth, as opp. to the purer αἰθήρ of the higher regions; generally, air (MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 22:23 , I Th 4:17 , Re 9:2 16:17 ; of the air as the realm of demons, Eph 2:2 ; ἀ. δέρειν , of striving to no purpose, I Co 9:26 ; εἰς ἀ. λαλεῖν , of speaking without effect, not being understood, I Co 14:9 .†
G109.strongs: from (to breathe unconsciously, i.e. respire; by analogy, to blow); "air" (as naturally circumambient)

G3778.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G3778.strongs: from the article G3588 (ὁ) and G846 (αὐτός); the he (she or it), i.e. this or that (often with article repeated)
G3778.kjv: he (it was that), hereof, it, she, such as, the same, these, they, this (man, same, woman), which, who

G4376.mounce: pr. a bringing to; in NT an offering, an act of offering up or sacrificing, Heb. 10:10, 14, 18; trop. Rom. 15:16; an offering, oblation, a thing offered, Eph. 5:2; Heb. 10:5, 8; a sacrifice, victim offered, Acts 21:26; 24:17*
G4376.strongs: from G4374 (προσφέρω); presentation; concretely, an oblation (bloodless) or sacrifice

G5331.abbottSmith: poët. and late prose form of φαρμακεία ,
G5331.strongs: from G5332 (φαρμακεύς); medication ("pharmacy"), i.e. (by extension) magic (literally or figuratively)

G4354.mounce: to speak to, converse with, Acts 13:43; 28:20*
G4354.abbottSmith: to speak to : Ac 28:20 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 13:43 .†
G4354.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2980 (λαλέω); to talk to, i.e. converse with

G5471.thayer: a worker in brass or copper; a smith.
G5471.mounce: pr. a coppersmith; hence, genr. a worker in metals, smith, 2 Tim. 4:14*
G5471.abbottSmith: a worker in metal , esp. a copper-smith : II Ti 4:14 .†
G5471.strongs: from G5475 (χαλκός); a copper-worker or brazier

G3510.thayer: a kidney (as a general emotional center), the reins.
G3510.mounce: a kidney; pl. νεφροί, the kidneys, reins; from the Hebrew the reins regarded as a seat of desire and affection, Rev. 2:23*
G3510.abbottSmith: a kidney ; pl., the kidneys , reins ; metaph., of the will and affections: ν. καί καρδίαι (thoughts), Re 2:23 .†
G3510.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a kidney (plural), i.e. (figuratively) the inmost mind

G3125.mounce: a mother; later, a grandmother, 2 Tim. 1:5*
G3125.abbottSmith: in cl., a child's name for mother .

G2744.mounce: to glory, boast, Rom. 2:17, 23; ὑπέρ τινος, to boast of a person or thing, to undertake a complimentary testimony to, 2 Cor. 12:5; to rejoice, exult, Rom. 5:2, 3, 11
G2744.abbottSmith: to boast or glory (in LXX, of joyous exultation, and so in the NT quotations, infr.; cf. DB , ii, 790 b ; Hort on Ja 1:9 ): absol., I Co 1:31 (LXX) I Co 4:7 13:3 , II Co 10:13 , 17 (LXX) 11:16 , 18 12:1 , 6 , Eph 2:9 , Ja 4:16 ; c. acc. rei, II Co 9:2 11:30 ; seq. ἐν (LXX), Ro 2:23 5:3 , I Co 3:21 , II Co 5:12 10:15 11:12 12:5 , 9 , Ga 6:13 , 14 , Ja 1:9 ; ἐν ( τ. ) θεῷ , Ro 2:17 5:11 ; ἐν κυρίῳ ,
G2744.strongs: from some (obsolete) base akin to that of (to boast) and G2172 (εὔχομαι); to vaunt (in a good or a bad sense)

G4735.mounce: that which forms an encirclement; a crown, Mt. 27:29; Rev. 4:4, 10; wreath, conferred on a victor in the public games, 1 Cor. 9:25; met. a crown, reward, prize, 2 Tim. 4:8; Jas. 1:12; a crown, ornament, honor, glory
G4735.abbottSmith: that which surrounds or encompasses (as a wall, a crowd: Hom., al.).
G4735.strongs: from an apparently primary (to twine or wreathe); a chaplet (as a badge of royalty, a prize in the public games or a symbol of honor generally; but more conspicuous and elaborate than the simple fillet, G1238 (διάδημα)), literally or figuratively

G4687.strongs: probably strengthened from G4685 (σπάω) (through the idea of extending); to scatter, i.e. sow (literally or figuratively)

G3971.mounce: received from one’s ancestors, paternal, ancestral, Acts 22:3; 24:14; 28:17*
G3971.abbottSmith: of one's fathers , received from one's fathers : Ac 22:3 24:14 28:17 .†

G4325.mounce: to spend besides, expend over and above, Lk. 10:35*
G4325.abbottSmith: to spend besides : c. acc., Lk 10:35 .†
G4325.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G1159 (δαπανάω); to expend additionally

G1115.thayer: Golgotha, a knoll outside the wall of Jerusalem.
G1115.mounce: Golgotha, pr. name, Mt. 27:33; Mk. 15:22; Jn. 19:17*
G1115.abbottSmith: Golgotha , the place of the crucifixion: Mt 27:33 , Mk 15:22 , Jo 19:17 .†
G1115.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01538); the skull; Golgotha, a knoll near Jerusalem

G3183.strongs: from G3184 (μεθύω); tipsy, i.e. (as noun) a sot

G1852.mounce: to awake, arouse from sleep, Jn. 11:11*
G1852.abbottSmith: to awaken out of sleep (= ἀφυπνίζω ): c. acc pers., Jo 11:11 .†

G2771.strongs: of uncertain affinity; gain (pecuniary or genitive case)

G4633.thayer: a tent, booth, tabernacle, abode, dwelling, mansion, habitation.
G4633.mounce: a tent, tabernacle; genr. any temporary dwelling; a tent, booth, Mt. 17:4; Heb. 11:9; the tabernacle of the covenant, Heb. 8:5; 9:1, 21; 13:10; allegor. the celestial or true tabernacle, Heb. 8:2; 9:11; a division or compartment of the tabernacle, Heb. 9:2, 3, 6; a small portable tent or shrine, Acts 7:43; an abode or seat of a lineage, Acts 15:16; a mansion, habitation, abode, dwelling, Lk. 16:9; Rev. 13:6
