G4633.abbottSmith: a tent, booth, tabernacle : Mt 17:4 , Mk 9:5 , Lk 9:33 , He 11:9 ; αἱ αἰώνιοι σ. , Lk 16:9 ; of the Mosaic tabernacle, He 8:5 9:2, 3 , 6 , 8 , 21 ; σ. τ. μαρτυρίου , Ac 7:44 ; of its heavenly prototype, He 8:2 9:11 , Re 13:6 15:5 21:3 ; of the temple, He 13:10 ; ἡ σ. τοῦ Μολόχ , Ac 7:43 (LXX) ; metaph., ἡ σ. Δαυείδ Ac 15:16 (LXX) .†
G4633.strongs: apparently akin to G4632 (σκεῦος) and G4639 (σκιά); a tent or cloth hut (literally or figuratively)

G3366.mounce: negative disjunctive particle, can function as an adverb and a conj, neither, and repeated, neither-nor, Mt. 6:25; 7:6; 10:9, 10; not even, not so much as, Mk. 2:2
G3366.abbottSmith: as conjc., continuing a negation or prohibition, but not , and not , nor : preceded by μή , Mt 6:25 22:29 , Mk 12:24 , Lk 14:12 , al.; ἵνα μή , Jo 4:15 ; ὅπως μή , Lk 16:26 ; μηδέ . . . μηδέ , neither . . . nor , Mt 10:10 , I Co 10:8 , 9 .
G3366.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G1161 (δέ); but not, not even; in a continued negation, nor
G3366.kjv: neither, nor (yet), (no) not (once, so much as)

G5170.thayer: Tryphaena, a Christian woman in Rome.
G5170.abbottSmith: Tryphæna : Ro 16:12 (v. Lft., Phl. , 175 f.; MM, xxv).†
G5170.strongs: from G5172 (τρυφή); luxurious; Tryphæna, a Christian woman

G3718.thayer: I cut straight; met: I handle correctly, teach rightly.
G3718.mounce: to cut straight; to set forth truthfully, without perversion or distortion, 2 Tim. 2:15*
G3718.abbottSmith: to cut straight , as a road ( τ. ὁδούς , fig., Pr 3:6 11:6 ). Metaph., τ. λόγον τ. ἀληθείας , II Ti 2:15 (V. Ellic. and CGT , in l.; not found elsewhere).†
G3718.strongs: from a compound of G3717 (ὀρθός) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος), to make a straight cut, i.e. (figuratively) to dissect (expound) correctly (the divine message)

G1270.strongs: from a compound of G1223 (διά) and G3539 (νοιέω); something thought through, i.e. a sentiment

G2096.thayer: Eve, the first woman; wife of first man Adam.
G2096.abbottSmith: Eve , wife of Adam: II Co 11:3 , I Ti 2:13 .†
G2096.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02332); Eua (or Eva, i.e. Chavvah), the first woman

G1953.abbottSmith: forgetful­ness : ἀκροατὴς ἐ. (gen. of qual.), Ja 1:25 .†
G1953.strongs: from a derivative of G1950 (ἐπιλανθάνομαι); negligence

G820.mounce: unhonored, without honor, Mt. 13:57; Mk. 6:4; despised, 1 Cor. 4:10; 12:23*
G820.abbottSmith: without honour , dishonoured , despised : Mt 13:57 , Mk 6:4 , I Co 4:10 ; comp., I Co 12:23 .†
G820.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5092 (τιμή); (negatively) unhonoured or (positively) dishonoured
G820.kjv: despised, without honour, less honourable (comparative degree)

G5186.thayer: I make blind, physically or mentally.
G5186.mounce: to blind, render blind; met. Jn. 12:40; 2 Cor. 4:4; 1 Jn. 2:11*
G5186.abbottSmith: to blind, make blind : metaph., Jo 12:40 (LXX, καμμύω ), II Co 4:4 , I Jo 2:11 .†
G5186.strongs: from G5185 (τυφλός); to make blind, i.e. (figuratively) to obscure

G3945.mounce: to be like, to resemble, Mt. 23:27*
G3945.abbottSmith: to be like : c. dat., Mt 23:27 .†

G2777.thayer: (lit: little head, then: the knob at the end of the wooden core of a roll of papyrus, then) a roll, volume, division (of a book).
G2777.mounce: in NT a roll, volume, division of a book, Heb. 10:7*
G2777.strongs: from G2776 (κεφαλή); properly, a knob, i.e. (by implication) a roll (by extension from the end of a stick on which the manuscript was rolled)

G2941.mounce: government, office of a governor or director; meton. a director, 1 Cor. 12:28*
G2941.strongs: from (of Latin origin, to steer); pilotage, i.e. (figuratively) directorship (in the church)

G5320.mounce: manifestly; clearly, plainly, distinctly, Acts 10:3; openly, publicly, Mk. 1:45; Jn. 7:10*
G5320.abbottSmith: manifestly, openly : Mk 1:45 ; opp. to ἐν κρυπτῷ „ Jo 7:10 ;
G5320.strongs: adverb from G5318 (φανερός); plainly, i.e. clearly or publicly

G3076.mounce: to occasion grief or sorrow to, to distress, 2 Cor. 2:2, 5; 7:8; pass. to be grieved, pained, distressed, sorrowful, Mt. 17:23; 19:22; to aggrieve, cross, vex, Eph. 4:30; pass. to feel pained, Rom. 14:15
G3076.abbottSmith: to distress , grieve , cause pain or grief : c. acc. pers., II Co 2:2 , 5 7:8 ; pass., Mt 14:9 17:23 18:31 19:22 26:22 , Mk 10:22 14:19 , Jo 16:20 21:17 , Ro 14:15 , II Co 2:4 , I Th 4:13 , I Pe 1:6 ; λ. καὶ ἀδημονεῖν , Mt 26:37 ; opp. to χαίρειν , II Co 6:10 ; κατὰ θεόν , II Co 7:9 , 11 ; τ. πνεῦμα τ. ἅγιον , Eph 4:30 (cf. συν-λνπέω ).†
G3076.strongs: from G3077 (λύπη); to distress; reflexively or passively, to be sad
G3076.kjv: cause grief, grieve, be in heaviness, (be) sorrow(-ful), be (make) sorry

G4390.thayer: I run before, outrun, run in advance.
G4390.mounce: to run before, or in advance, Lk. 19:4; Jn. 20:4*
G4390.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to run forward, i.e. outstrip, precede

G5343.mounce: absol. to flee, take to flight, Mt. 2:13; 8:33; to shrink, stand fearfully aloof, 1 Cor. 10:14; to make escape, Mt. 23:33; trans. to shun, 1 Cor. 6:18; 1 Tim. 6:11; 2 Tim. 2:22; to escape, Heb. 11:34
G5343.abbottSmith: to flee from or away, take flight : absol., Mt 8:33 26:56 , Mk 5:14 14:50 , Lk 8:34 , Jo 10:12 , Ac 7:29 ; seq. εἰς , Mt 2:13 10:23 24:16 (WH, txt.), Mk 13:14 , Lk 21:21 , Re 12:6 ; ἐπί , c. acc. loc., Mt 24:16 (WH, mg.); ἐκ , Ac 27:30 ; ἀπό , c. gen. loc. (cl.), Mk 16:8 ; id. c. gen. pers. (as in Heb.), Jo 10:5 , Ja 4:7 . Metaph.: absol., Re 16:20 ; c. acc. rei, I Co 6:18 , He 11:34 (v. M, Pr. ,
G5343.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to run away (literally or figuratively); by implication, to shun; by analogy, to vanish

G870.mounce: fearlessly, boldly, intrepidly, Phil. 1:14; securely, peacefully, tranquilly, Lk. 1:74; 1 Cor. 16:10; boldly, shamelessly, Jude 12*
G870.abbottSmith: without fear : Lk 1:74 , Phl 1:14 , I Co 16:10 , Ju 12 .†
G870.strongs: adverb from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G5401 (φόβος); fearlessly

G1285.mounce: to make known, declare, tell plainly, or fully, Mt. 13:36; 18:31*
G1285.abbottSmith: to make clear, explain fully : c. acc. rei, dat. pers., Mt 13:26 18:31 .†
G1285.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and (clear); to clear thoroughly, i.e. (figuratively) declare

G1788.thayer: (a) I turn to confusion, put to shame, (b) mid: I reverence, regard.
G1788.mounce: mid., to revere, reverence, regard, Mt. 21:37; Mk. 12:6; absol. to feel shame, be put to shame, 2 Thess. 3:14; Tit. 2:8; pass., be put to shame, 2 Thess. 3:14; Tit. 3:8
G1788.abbottSmith: to turn about ; metaph., put to shame : c. acc, I Co 4:14 ; pass., II Th 3:14 , Tit 2:8 ; mid., to reverence : c. acc pers. (cl. c. gen.), Mt 21:37 , Mk 12:6 , Lk 18:2 , 4 20:13 , He 12:9 (cf. MM, Exp. , iii, xiii).†
G1788.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to invert, i.e. (figuratively and reflexively) in a good sense, to respect; or in a bad one, to confound

G4250.mounce: can function as a temporal conj. and an improper prep., before, of time, Mt. 26:34, 75; Mk. 14:72; πρὶν ἤ, sooner than, before, Mt. 1:18; Lk. 2:26
G4250.abbottSmith: as adv. of time, before , formerly (cl.; III Mac 5:28 6:4 , 31 ).

G1844.thayer: I adjure, put to oath; I exorcise.
G1844.mounce: to put an oath to a person, to adjure, Mt. 26:63*
G1844.abbottSmith: to administer an oath to (Dem., Polyb., al.).
G1844.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3726 (ὁρκίζω); to exact an oath, i.e. conjure

G1761.mounce: the act of thought, reflection, Mt. 9:4; 12:25; Heb. 4:12; the result of thought, invention, device, Acts 17:29*
G1761.abbottSmith: consideration, pondering (EV, device ): Ac 17:29 ; pl., thoughts, feel­ings : Mt 9:4 12:25 , He 4:12 .†

G5231.mounce: can function as an improper prep., above, over, far above; of place, Eph. 4:10; Heb. 9:5; of rank, dignity, Eph. 1:21*
G5231.abbottSmith: above : as prep. c. gen., Eph 1:21 4:10 , He 9:5 .†
G5231.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G507 (ἄνω); above upward, i.e. greatly higher (in place or rank)

G1342.thayer: just; especially, just in the eyes of God; righteous; the elect (a Jewish idea).
G1342.mounce: just, equitable, fair, Mt. 20:4; Lk. 12:57; Jn. 5:30; Col. 4:1; of persons, just, righteous, absolutely, Jn. 17:25; Rom. 3:10, 26; 2 Tim. 4:8; 1 Pet. 3:18; 1 Jn. 1:9; 2:1, 29; Rev. 16:5; righteous by account and acceptance, Rom. 2:13; 5:19; in ordinary usage, just, upright, innocent, pious, Mt. 5:45; 9:13, et al. freq.; ὁ δίκαιος, the Just One, one of the distinctive titles of the Messiah, Acts 3:14; 7:52; 22:14
G1342.abbottSmith: in early Gk. writers of persons, observant of δίκη , custom, rule, right, righteous in performing duties to gods and men ; of things, righteous , in accordance with right.
G1342.strongs: from G1349 (δίκη); equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

G3188.mounce: black, Mt. 5:36; Rev. 6:5, 12, the form μέλαν means ink, 2 Cor. 3:3; 2 Jn. 12; 3 Jn. 13
G3188.strongs: neuter of G3189 (μέλας) as noun; ink

G3471.thayer: (a) I make foolish, turn to foolishness, (b) I taint, and thus: I am tasteless, make useless.
G3471.mounce: to be foolish, to play the fool; in NT trans. to make foolish, convict of folly, 1 Cor. 1:20; pass. to be convicted of folly, to incur the character of folly, Rom. 1:22; to be rendered insipid, Mt. 5:13; Lk. 14:34*
G3471.abbottSmith: cl., to be foolish , play the fool .
G3471.strongs: from G3474 (μωρός); to become insipid; figuratively, to make (passively, act) as a simpleton
G3471.kjv: become fool, make foolish, lose savour

G40.thayer: set apart by (or for) God, holy, sacred.
G40.mounce: separate from common condition and use; dedicated. Lk. 2:23; hallowed; used of things, τὰ ἅγια, the sanctuary; and of persons, saints, e.g., members of the first Christian communities; pure, righteous, ceremonially or morally; holy
G40.strongs: from (an awful thing) (compare G53 (ἁγνός), G2282 (θάλπω)); sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

G4955.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4714 (στάσις); a fellow-insurgent

G4090.abbottSmith: bitterly : metaph., (cf. πικρὸν δάκρυον , Hom.), ἔκλαυσε π. , Mt 26:75 , Lk 22:62 (v. MM, xviii, s.v. παρακολουθέω ).†
G4090.strongs: adverb from G4089 (πικρός); bitterly, i.e. (figuratively) violently

G5441.strongs: from G5442 (φυλάσσω); a watcher or sentry

G5258.mounce: sleep, Mt. 1:24; Lk. 9:32; Jn. 11:13; Acts 20:9; met. spiritual sleep, religious slumber, Rom. 13:11*
G5258.abbottSmith: sleep : Mt 1:24 , Lk 9:32 , Jo 11:13 , Ac 20:9 ; metaph., Ro 13:11 .†
G5258.strongs: from an obsolete primary (perhaps akin to G5259 (ὑπό) through the idea of subsilience); sleep, i.e. (figuratively) spiritual torpor

G2834.thayer: Cnidus, a town on the coast of Caria (south-west Asia Minor) near the island of Cos.
G2834.mounce: Cnidus, a city of Caria, in Asia Minor, Acts 27:7*
G2834.abbottSmith: Cnidus , a city (and peninsula) on S.W. coast of Asia Minor: Ac 27:7 .†
G2834.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Cnidus, a place in Asia Minor

G110.abbottSmith: immortality : I Co 15:53 , 54 I Ti 6:16 (cf. Cremer, 285 f.).†
G110.strongs: from a compound of G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2288 (θάνατος); deathlessness

G714.thayer: I keep off, assist; I suffice; pass: I am satisfied.
G714.mounce: pr. to ward off; thence; to be of service, avail; to suffice, be enough, Mt. 25:9; pass. to be contented, satisfied, Lk. 3:14; 1 Tim. 6:8; Heb. 13:5; 3 Jn. 10
G714.abbottSmith: to keep off ; c. dat., to assist .
G714.strongs: apparently a primary verb (but probably akin to G142 (αἴρω) through the idea of raising a barrier); properly, to ward off, i.e. (by implication) to avail (figuratively, be satisfactory)
G714.kjv: be content, be enough, suffice, be sufficient

G3762.abbottSmith: no , no one , none : with nouns, Lk 4:24 , Jo 10:41 , Ro 8:1 , al.; absol., Mt 6:24 , Mk 3:27 , Lk 1:61 , Jo 1:18 , Ac 18:10 , Ro 14:7 , al. mult.; c. gen. partit., Lk 4:26 , Jo 13:28 , al.; neut., οὐδέν , Mt 10:26 , al.; id. c. gen. partit., Lk 9:36 , Ac 18:17 , al.; οὐδὲν εἰ μή , Mt 5:13 , Mk 9:29 , al.; c. neg., strengthening the negation, Mk 15:4 , 5 , Lk 4:2 , Jo 3:27 , al.; adverbially, Ac 2
G3762.strongs: from G3761 (οὐδέ) and G1520 (εἷς); not even one (man, woman or thing), i.e. none, nobody, nothing
G3762.kjv: any (man), aught, man, neither any (thing), never (man), no (man), none (+ of these things), not (any, at all, -thing), nought

G782.thayer: I greet, salute, pay my respects to, welcome.
G782.mounce: to salute, greet, welcome, express good wishes, pay respects, Mt. 10:12; Mk. 9:15, et al. freq.; to bid farewell, Acts 20:1; 21:6; to treat with affection, Mt. 5:47; met. to embrace mentally, welcome to the heart of understanding, Heb. 11:13
G782.abbottSmith: to welcome , greet, salute : c. acc. pers., Mt 5:47 , Mk 9:15 , Ac 21:7 , al.; id. seq. ἐν φιλήματι , Ro 16:16 , I Co 16:20 , II Co 13:12 , I Th 5:26 , I Pe 5:14 ; τ. ἐκκλησίαν (Deiss., BS , 257), Ac 18:22 ; as term. tech. for conveying greetings at the end of a letter (MM, s.v.), used by an amanuensis (Milligan, NTD , 23), Ro 16:22 (on the aoristic pres., here and elsewhere, v. M, Pr. , 119; Bl.,
G782.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and a presumed form of G4685 (σπάω); to enfold in the arms, i.e. (by implication) to salute, (figuratively) to welcome

G4366.mounce: also spelled προσρήγνυμι, to break or burst upon, dash against, Lk. 6:48, 49*
G4366.abbottSmith: to break against , dash against ; trans. ( παιδία πέτραις , FlJ, Ant. , ix, 4, 6); intrans., c. dat.: Lk 6:48 , 49 (cf. προσπίπτω ).†
G4366.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); to tear towards, i.e. burst upon (as a tempest or flood)

G2520.thayer: I come down, come to; I am unfit, proper.
G2520.mounce: to reach, extend to; καθήκει, impers. it is fitting, meet, Acts 22:22; τὸ καθῆκον, what is fit, right, duty; τὰ μὴ καθήκοντα, by litotes for what is abominable or detestable, Rom. 1:28*
G2520.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2240 (ἥκω); to reach to, i.e. (neuter of present active participle, figuratively as adjective) becoming

G1479.mounce: also spelled ἐθελοθρησκεία, self-made religion, Col. 2:23*
G1479.abbottSmith: self-imposed worship : Col 2:23 (eccl.; cf. DB , iv, 923 a ; Cremer, 733).†
G1479.strongs: from G2309 (θέλω) and G2356 (θρησκεία); voluntary (arbitrary and unwarranted) piety, i.e. sanctimony

G1168.thayer: I shrink, am fearful, timid, cowardly.
G1168.mounce: to be timid, be in fear, Jn. 14:27*
G1168.abbottSmith: = the more freq. ἀποδ- , to be cowardly, timid, fearful : Jo 14:27 .†

G1881.thayer: intrans. tenses: I rise up against.
G1881.mounce: to raise up against; mid. to rise up against in rebellion, Mt. 10:21; Mk. 13:12*
G1881.abbottSmith: to raise up against ; mid., to rise up against : seq. ἐπί , c. acc pers., Mt 10:21 , Mk 13:12 .†
G1881.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G450 (ἀνίστημι); to stand up on, i.e. (figuratively) to attack

G2940.thayer: (lit: playing with dice, gaming, hence) trickery, sleight.
G2940.mounce: also spelled κυβία, pr. dice playing; met. craftiness, trickery, Eph. 4:14*
G2940.strongs: from (a "cube", i.e. die for playing); gambling, i.e. (figuratively) artifice or fraud

G867.abbottSmith: in cl. usually, arrival ; rarely, departure : Ac 20:29 (so in π. , cf. MM, s.v.; M, Pr. , 26, n.).†
G867.strongs: from G864 (ἀφικνέομαι); properly, arrival, i.e. (by implication) departure

G1950.mounce: to forget, Mt. 16:5; to be forgetful, neglectful of, to disregard, Phil. 3:13; Heb. 6:10; in NT in a passive sense, forgotten, Lk. 12:6
G1950.abbottSmith: to forget, neglect : c. inf., Mt 16:5 , Mk 8:14 ; c. gen., He 6:10 13:2 , 16 ; c. acc (as occasionally in cl.; MM, Exp. , xiv), Phl 3:13 ; ὁποῖος ἦν , Ja 1:24 ; pass. ptcp. (cf. Is 23:16 , Si 3:14 23:14 , Wi 2:4 ), Lk 12:6 .†
G1950.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2990 (λανθάνω); to lose out of mind; by implication, to neglect

G920.thayer: Bar-Jonas, son of Jonas, the surname of Simon Peter.
G920.mounce: also Βὰρ Ἰωνᾶ or Βαριωνᾶς, Bar-jona, pr. name, Mt. 16:17*
G920.abbottSmith: Bar-Jonah , a surname of Peter: Mt 16:17 .†
G920.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H01247 and H03124); son of Jonas (or Jonah); Bar-jonas, an Israelite

G5072.thayer: of four months, a period of four months.
G5072.mounce: of four months, four months in duration, Jn. 4:35*
G5072.abbottSmith: of four months, four months ; as subst., τ. (sc. ὥρα , but neut. in Rec.), Jo 4:35 (Thuc., al.).†
G5072.strongs: neuter of a compound of G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3376 (μήν); a four months' space

G3955.thayer: I speak freely, boldly; I am confident.
G3955.mounce: to speak plainly, freely, boldly, and confidently, Acts 13:46; 14:3
G3955.abbottSmith: to speak freely or boldly , be bold in speech : Ac 9:27 , 28 13:46 14:3 18:26 19:8 26:26 , Eph 6:20 , I Th 2:2 .†
G3955.strongs: middle voice from G3954 (παῤῥησία); to be frank in utterance, or confident in spirit and demeanor

G4881.mounce: to destroy together with others; mid. to perish or be destroyed with others, Heb. 11:31*
G4881.abbottSmith: to destroy with or together . Pass., to perish together ( with ): c. dat. pers., He 11:31 .†
G4881.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G622 (ἀπόλλυμι); to destroy (middle voice or passively, be slain) in company with

G3878.thayer: (a) I hear carelessly or incidentally, or I pretend not to hear, (b) I refuse to hear; I disobey, disregard.
G3878.mounce: to overhear, Mk. 5:36; to hear amiss, to fail to listen, neglect to obey, disregard, Mt. 18:17 (2x)*
G3878.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G191 (ἀκούω); to mishear, i.e. (by implication) to disobey

G386.mounce: a raising or rising up; resurrection, Mt. 22:23; meton. the author of resurrection, Jn. 11:25; met. an uprising into a state of higher advancement and blessedness, Lk. 2:34
G386.abbottSmith: a raising up , awakening , rising (in Inscr. of the erection of a monument, v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 2:34 .
G386.strongs: from G450 (ἀνίστημι); a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
G386.kjv: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

G1180.abbottSmith: = cl. τεσσαρεσκαίδεκα , more freq. in later Gk. than the older form and in MGr. (for thirteen and upwards) universal, fourteen : Mt 1:17 , II Co 12:2 , Ga 2:1 (cf. τεσσαρεσκαιδέκατος ).†
G1180.strongs: from G1176 (δέκα) and G5064 (τέσσαρες); ten and four, i.e. fourteen

G2747.thayer: Cenchreae, the port of Corinth on the Saronic Gulf.
G2747.mounce: Cenchreae, the port of Corinth on the Saronic Gulf; Acts 18:18; Rom. 16:1*
G2747.abbottSmith: Cenchreæ , a port of Corinth: Ac 18:18 , Ro 16:1 .†
G2747.strongs: probably from kegchros (millet); Cenchreæ, a port of Corinth

G1171.mounce: dreadfully, grievously, greatly, terribly, Mt. 8:6; Lk. 11:53*
G1171.strongs: adverb from a derivative of the same as G1169 (δειλός); terribly, i.e. excessively

G984.mounce: pr. to weaken, hinder, disable; to hurt, harm, injure, Mk. 16:18; Lk. 4:35*
G984.abbottSmith: to hurt, injure : c. acc., Mk 16:[18] , Lk 4:35 .†
G984.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to hinder, i.e. (by implication) to injure

G1873.mounce: gen., to listen or hearken to; to hear with favor, 2 Cor. 6:2*
G1873.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G191 (ἀκούω); to hearken (favorably) to

G1209.mounce: to take into one’s hands, etc., Lk. 2:28; 16:6, 7; to receive, Acts 22:5; 28:21; Phil. 4:18; to receive into and retain, contain, Acts 3:21; met. to receive by the hearing, learn, acquire a knowledge of, 2 Cor. 11:4; Jas. 1:21; to receive, admit, grant access to, receive kindly, welcome, Mt. 10:40, 41; 18:5; to receive in hospitality, entertain, Lk. 9:53; Heb. 11:31; to bear with, bear patiently, 2 Cor. 11:16; met. to receive, approve, assent to, Mt. 11:14; Lk. 8:13; Acts 8:14; 11:1; to admit, and by impl. to embrace, follow, 1 Cor. 2:14; 2 Cor. 8:17
G1209.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to receive (in various applications, literally or figuratively)

G1904.thayer: I come to, arrive, come on, come upon, attack.
G1904.mounce: to come to, Acts 14:19; to come upon, Lk. 1:35; 21:26; Acts 1:8; Jas. 5:1; to be coming on, to succeed, Eph. 2:7; to occur, happen to, Acts 8:24; 13:40; to come against, attack, Lk. 11:22*
G1904.abbottSmith: to come in upon : seq. ἐπί Lk 21:35 .†
G1904.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to supervene, i.e. arrive, occur, impend, attack, (figuratively) influence

G3647.strongs: from G3648 (ὁλόκληρος); integrity, i.e. physical wholeness

G104.thayer: always, unceasingly, perpetually; on every occasion.
G104.abbottSmith: ever ; of continuous time, unceasingly , perpetually : Ac 7:51 , 2 Co 4:11 6:10, Tit 1:12 , He 3:10 . Of successive occurrences, on every occasion (MM, VGT , s.v.): I Pe 3:15 , II Pe 1:12 .†
G104.strongs: from an obsolete primary noun (apparently meaning continued duration); "ever," by qualification regularly; by implication, earnestly

G4780.thayer: I conceal closely, cover up wholly.
G4780.mounce: to cover completely, to cover up; met. to conceal, Lk. 12:2*
G4780.abbottSmith: to veil or cover completely : c. acc., pass., Lk 12:2 .†
G4780.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to conceal altogether

G310.thayer: I shout upwards, cry out, raise my voice.
G310.mounce: to cry out or aloud, exclaim, Mt. 27:46*
G310.abbottSmith: to cry out : Mt 27:46 (WH, ἐβόησεν ; v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G310.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G994 (βοάω); to halloo

G4812.thayer: I plunder, lead captive; met: I make victim by fraud.
G4812.mounce: to carry off as a prey or booty; met. to make victims of fraud, Col. 2:8*
G4812.abbottSmith: to carry off as spoil, lead captive ( θυγατέρα , Heliod., Aeth. , 10, 35): metaph., Col 2:8 .†
G4812.strongs: from the base of G4813 (συλάω) and (the reduplicated form of) G71 (ἄγω); to lead away as booty, i.e. (figuratively) seduce

G131.thayer: I suffer from a continual flow (oozing) of blood.
G131.mounce: to have a flow of blood, Mt. 9:20*
G131.abbottSmith: to lose blood , suffer from a flow of blood (Hipp.): Mt 9:20 .†
G131.strongs: from G129 (αἷμα) and G4482 (ῥέω); to flow blood, i.e. have a hæmorrhage
G131.kjv: diseased with an issue of blood

G4054.strongs: neuter of G4055 (περισσότερος) (as adverb); in a more superabundant way
G4054.kjv: more abundantly, a great deal, far more

G2510.thayer: I lay hold of, fasten on to, seize, attack.
G2510.mounce: trans. to fasten or fit to; in NT equivalent to καθάπτομαι, to fix one’s self upon, fasten upon, take hold of, seize, Acts 28:3*
G2510.abbottSmith: to fasten on , put upon , c. acc.
G2510.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G680 (ἅπτομαι); to seize upon

G1797.thayer: I dream (see visions) in my sleep.
G1797.mounce: to dream, in NT to dream under supernatural impression, Acts 2:17; to dream delusion, have visions, Jude 8*
G1797.abbottSmith: to dream : ἐνυπνίοις ἐ. , Ac 2:17 (LXX) ; pres. ptcp., Ju 8 .†
G1797.strongs: middle voice from G1798 (ἐνύπνιον); to dream

G1790.abbottSmith: trembling with fear (Pint.): Ac 7:32 16:29 , He 12:21 ( ἔκτρ- , WH, mg.).†

G3651.strongs: from G3650 (ὅλος) and G5056 (τέλος); complete to the end, i.e. absolutely perfect

G2562.mounce: the stalk of grain, straw, stubble, 1 Cor. 3:12*
G2562.abbottSmith: a stalk of corn, stubble : I Co 3:12 .†
G2562.strongs: feminine of G2563 (κάλαμος); a stalk of grain, i.e. (collectively) stubble

G199.mounce: accurately, diligently, Mt. 2:8; Lk. 1:3; Acts 18:25; circumspectly, strictly, Eph. 5:15; precisely, distinctly, 1 Thess. 5:2
G199.strongs: adverb from the same as G196 (ἀκριβέστατος); exactly

G4358.mounce: to bring a ship to its station or to land; mid. to come to the land, Mk. 6:53*
G4358.abbottSmith: to bring a ship to anchor at ; usually in mid., to come to anchor near , and so pass. in late writers (Ael., Dio Cass.): Mk 6:53 .†
G4358.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and a derivative of the same as G3730 (ὁρμή) (meaning to tie (anchor) or lull); to moor to, i.e. (by implication) land at

G839.mounce: tomorrow, Mt. 6:30; ἡ αὔριον, i.e. ἡμέρα, the next day, Mt. 6:34
G839.abbottSmith: to-morrow : Mt 6:30 , Lk 12:28 , Ac 23:20 25:22 , I Co 15:32 , Lk 13:32 , 33 , Ja 4:13 ; ἡ αὔ. (sc. ἡμέρα , MM, s.v.), Mt 6:34 , Ac 4:3 ; ἐπὶ τὴν αὔ. , Lk 10:35 , Ac 4:5 ; τὸ (WH om.) τῆς αὔ. Ja 4:14 .†
G839.strongs: from a derivative of the same as G109 (ἀήρ) (meaning a breeze, i.e. the morning air); properly, fresh, i.e. (adverb with ellipsis of G2250 (ἡμέρα)) to-morrow

G4386.mounce: former, prior, Eph. 4:22; before, formerly, Jn. 6:62
G4386.strongs: neuter of G4387 (πρότερος) as adverb (with or without the article); previously

G5328.thayer: Pharaoh, the title of ancient Egyptian kings.
G5328.mounce: Pharaoh, pr. name, indecl., Acts 7:10, 13, 21; Rom. 9:17; Heb. 11:24*
G5328.abbottSmith: Pharaoh , the general title of the kings of Egypt: Ac 7:13 , 21 , Ro 9:17 , He 11:24 ; Φ. βασιλεὺς Αἰγύπτου , Ac 7:10 .†
G5328.strongs: of foreign origin (H06547); Pharao (i.e. Pharoh), an Egyptian king

G1102.strongs: by variation for a derivative from (to tease cloth); a cloth-dresser

G4957.mounce: to crucify with another, Mt. 27:44; Mk. 15:32; Jn. 19:32; pass. met. to be crucified with another in a spiritual resemblance, Rom. 6:6; Gal. 2:20*
G4957.abbottSmith: to crucify together with : pass., c. dat., Jo 19:32 ; id. seq. σύν , Mt 27:44 , Mk 15:32 . Metaph., of the mystical death of the Christian with Christ: Ro 6:6 , Ga 2:20 (Eccl.).†
G4957.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4717 (σταυρόω); to impale in company with (literally or figuratively)

G691.mounce: pr. to be unemployed; to be inoperative, to linger, 2 Pet. 2:3*
G691.abbottSmith: to be idle ; τὸ κρίμα . . . ἀ. , lingers (cf. MGr. ἀργά , late : MM, s.v.): II Pe 2:3 .†
G691.strongs: from G692 (ἀργός); to be idle, i.e. (figuratively) to delay

G5372.strongs: from G5368 (φιλέω); amiable; Philetus, an opposer of Christianity

G2231.mounce: leadership, sovereignty; in NT a reign, Lk. 3:1*
G2231.strongs: from G2232 (ἡγεμών); government, i.e. (in time) official term

G780.strongs: adverb from a derivative of the base of G2237 (ἡδονή); with pleasure

G1174.thayer: respectful of what is divine; religious, perhaps, rather than superstitious (the usual meaning).
G1174.mounce: reverencing the gods and divine things, religious; in a bad sense, superstitious; in NT careful and precise in the discharge of religious services, Acts 17:22*
G1174.abbottSmith: reverent to the deity, religious ; compar. -μονεστέρους (AV, too superstitious , R, txt., somewhat superstitious , a sense in wh. the word is sometimes used; cf. Field, Notes , 125), more religious, God fearing , than others, quite religious (Abbott, Essays , 105 ff.; Deiss., LAE , 285): Ac 17:22 .†
G1174.strongs: the compound of a derivative of the base of G1169 (δειλός) and G1142 (δαίμων); more religious than others

G3905.mounce: to extend, stretch out; to prolong, continue, Acts 20:7*
G3905.abbottSmith: to extend , prolong : τ. λόγον , Ac 20:7 .†
G3905.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and teino (to stretch); to extend along, i.e. prolong (in point of time)

G4703.mounce: a sowing; in NT seed, that which is sown, Mk. 4:26, 27; Lk. 8:5, 11; met. the seed sown in almsgiving, 2 Cor. 9:10*
G4703.strongs: from G4687 (σπείρω); a scattering (of seed), i.e. (concretely) seed (as sown)

G182.thayer: unsettled, unstable (though these are hardly strong enough equivalents), almost anarchic.
G182.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2525 (καθίστημι); inconstant

G2723.mounce: to speak against, accuse, Mt. 12:10; 27:12; Jn. 5:45
G2723.abbottSmith: to make accusation , accuse , in general: absol., Ro 2:15 ; c. gen. pers. (cl., WM, 254), Jo 5:45 ; irreg. c. acc. pers., Re 12:10 ; before a judge: absol., Ac 24:2 , 19 ; c. gen. pers., Mt 12:10 , Mk 3:2 , Lk 6:7 11:54 (WH, txt., R, om.), 23:2 , 10 , Jo 8:[6] , Ac 25:5 28:19 ; id. c. acc. rei (cl., but v. WM, 254), Mk 15:3 , 4 ; c. gen. rei (Dem.), Ac 24:8 25:11 ; seq. περί , c. gen. rei (Thuc.,
G2723.strongs: from G2725 (κατήγορος); to be a plaintiff, i.e. to charge with some offence

G376.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) (in the sense of intensity) and (maimed); crippled

G5211.strongs: from (the god of weddings); "hymenæal"; Hymenæus, an opponent of Christianity

G4904.mounce: a fellow laborer, associate, helper, Rom. 16:3, 9, 21; 2 Cor. 1:24
G4904.abbottSmith: a fellow-worker : c. gen. pers., Ro 16:3 , 9 , 21 , Phl 2:25 4:3 , I Th 3:2 , Rec., Phm 1 , 24 ; θεοῦ (cf. Lft., Notes , 41, 188), I Co 3:9 (pl.), I Th 3:2 [WH] R, mg. (R, txt., διάκονον τοῦ θ. ; on the original reading, v. ICC , in l.); τ. χαρᾶς ὑηῶν , II Co 1:24 ; τ. ἀληθείᾳ , III Jo 8 ; seq. εἰς , II Co 8:23 , Col 4:11 .†
G4904.strongs: from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); a co-laborer, i.e. coadjutor
G4904.kjv: companion in labour, (fellow-)helper(-labourer, -worker), labourer together with, workfellow

G5572.thayer: a false teacher, teacher of false things.
G5572.mounce: a false teacher, one who teaches false doctrines, 2 Pet. 2:1*
G5572.strongs: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G1320 (διδάσκαλος); a spurious teacher, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine

G3107.mounce: happy, blessed, as a noun it can depict someone who receives divine favor, Mt. 5:3, 4, 5, 7; Lk. 1: 45
G3107.abbottSmith: blessed , happy ( DCG , i, 177, 213): θεός ( δυνάστης ), I Ti 1:11 6:15 ; ἐλπίς , Tit 2:13 ; esp. in congratulations, usually with the omission of the copula (M, Pr. , 180; B1., § 30, 3), μ. ὁ , Mt 5:3 ff., Lk 6:20 ff., Jo 20:29 , Re 1:3 , al.; seq. ptcp., Lk 1:45 , al.; ὅς , Mt 11:6 , Lk 7:23 , Ro 4:7 , 8 ; ὅτι , Mt 13:16 , al.; ἐάν , Jo 13:17 , I Co 7:40 ; compar., μ. . . . μᾶλλον , Ac 20:35 ; -
G3107.strongs: a prolonged form of the poetical (meaning the same); supremely blest; by extension, fortunate, well off

G575.mounce: pr. forth, from, away from; hence, it variously signifies departure; distance of time or place; avoidance; riddance; derivation from a quarter, source, or material; origination from agency or instrumentality
G575.abbottSmith: from (i.e. from the exterior). Of separation and cessation; of motion from a place: Mt 5:29 , 30 7:23 , Lk 5:2 22:41 , al.; in partitive sense (M, Pr. , 72, 102, 245; MM, s.v.; Bl., § 40, 2), Mt 9:16 27:21 , Jo 21:10 , Ac 5:2 , al.; also after verbs of eating, etc.; of alienation (cl. gen. of separation), after such verbs as λούω (Deiss., BS , 227), λύω , σώζω , παύω , etc.; ἀνάθεμα ἀ. , Ro 9:3 ;
G575.strongs: a primary particle; "off," i.e. away (from something near), in various senses (of place, time, or relation; literal or figurative)
G575.kjv: (X here-)after, ago, at, because of, before, by (the space of), for(-th), from, in, (out) of, off, (up-)on(-ce), since, with

G2612.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) intensive and G1212 (δῆλος); manifest

G248.mounce: to thresh; to tread, 1 Cor. 9:9, 10; 1 Tim. 5:18*
G248.abbottSmith: to thresh : I Co 9:9 9:10 ; I Ti 5:18 .†
G248.strongs: from the same as G257 (ἅλων); to tread out grain

G587.thayer: worthy to be received (welcomed), acceptable, welcome, pleasant.

G3422.mounce: a record, memorial, Acts 10:4; honorable remembrance, Mt. 26:13; Mk. 14:9*
G3422.abbottSmith: a memorial : Mt 26:13 , Mk 14:9 , Ac 10:4 (where cf. Le 2:9 , 16 5:12 , Nu 5:28 , Si 45:16 , al.).†
G3422.strongs: from G3421 (μνημονεύω); a reminder (memorandum), i.e. record

G589.thayer: I am away from home, go into another country, am away, am abroad.
G589.mounce: to be absent from one’s home or country; to go on travel, Mt. 21:33; 25:14, 15; Mk. 12:1; Lk. 15:13; 20:9*
G589.strongs: from G590 (ἀπόδημος); to go abroad, i.e. visit a foreign land
G589.kjv: go (travel) into a far country, journey

G4432.strongs: from G4433 (πτωχεύω); beggary, i.e. indigence (literally or figuratively)

G3134.thayer: (Aramaic), either: Our Lord hath come, or: Our Lord cometh (will come, is at hand).
G3134.abbottSmith: Maran atha , i.e. the Lord cometh (but v. Dalman, Gr. , § 41, 1; 74, 3; Words , 328; Field, Notes , 180; ICC , in l.): I Co 16:22 .†
G3134.strongs: of Chaldee origin (meaning our Lord has come); maranatha, i.e. an exclamation of the approaching divine judgment

G118.thayer: I engage, compete, in an (athletic) contest.
G118.mounce: to strive, contend, be a champion in the public games, 2 Tim. 2:5*
G118.abbottSmith: to contend in games, wrestle , combat : II Ti 2:5 .†
G118.strongs: from (a contest in the public lists); to contend in the competitive games

G2010.thayer: I turn to, commit, entrust; I allow, yield, permit.
G2010.mounce: to give over, to leave to the entire trust or management of any one; hence, to permit, allow, suffer, Mt. 8:21; Mk. 5:13
G2010.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G5157 (τροπή); to turn over (transfer), i.e. allow
G2010.kjv: give leave (liberty, license), let, permit, suffer

G3907.strongs: from G3906 (παρατηρέω); inspection, i.e. ocular evidence

G3138.thayer: Mark, who also had the Hebrew name John, son of Mary, nephew of Barnabas, coadjutor of Barnabas, Paul, and Peter.
G3138.abbottSmith: Mark : Mk., tit. , Ac 12:12 , 25 15:37 , 39 , Col 4:10 , II Ti 4:11 , Phm 24 , I Pe 5:13 (v. Swete, Mk. , Intr. , xiii ff.; DB , iii, 245 ff).†
G3138.strongs: of Latin origin; Marcus, a Christian

G1164.thayer: an example, type; a thing shown.
G1164.mounce: pr. that which is shown, a specimen, sample; met. an example by way of warning, Jude 7*
G1164.abbottSmith: (cl.) a thing shown, a specimen . = cl. παράδ- (cf. ὑπόδ- , II Pe 2:6 ), an example (a warning): Ju 7 .†
G1164.strongs: from the base of G1166 (δεικνύω); a specimen (as shown)

G1645.abbottSmith: light in weight easy to bear : Mt 11:30 ; θλίψρις (EV, our light affliction ), II Co 4:17 .†
G1645.strongs: probably akin to G1643 (ἐλαύνω) and the base of G1640 (ἐλάσσων); light, i.e. easy

G682.thayer: Apphia, a Christian lady of Colossae, either wife or sister of Philemon.
G682.strongs: probably of foreign origin; Apphia, a woman of Collosæ

G289.strongs: from G288 (ἄμπελος) and G2041 (ἔργον); a vine-worker, i.e. pruner

G3781.thayer: (a) a debtor, one who owes, one who is indebted, (b) one who has sinned against another (an Aramaism), a sinner.
G3781.mounce: a debtor, one who owes, Mt. 18:24; met. one who is in any way bound, or under obligation to perform any duty, Rom. 1:14; 8:12; 15:27; Gal. 5:3; in NT one who fails in duty, a delinquent, offender, Mt. 6:12; a sinner, Lk. 13:4, cf. v. 2*
G3781.abbottSmith: a debtor : c. gen. (of the amount), Mt 18:24 . Metaph., of obligation or duty in general, with reference to favours received or injury done, etc.: Mt 6:12 , Ro 1:14 8:12 15:27 , Ga 5:3 ; of sinners, in relation to God (= Heb. חַיָּב ; cf. Si (He) 8:5 ( 6 )), Lk 13:4 .†
G3781.strongs: from G3784 (ὀφείλω); an ower, i.e. person indebted; figuratively, a delinquent; morally, a transgressor (against God)

G213.thayer: a boaster, one who gives one's self airs in a loud and flaunting way.
G213.abbottSmith: prop, a vagabond , hence, an impostor , a boaster : Ro 1:30 , II Ti 3:2 .†

G4553.thayer: a basket, generally of twisted cords.
G4553.mounce: twisted or plaited work; a netword of cords like a basket, basket of ropes, etc. 2 Cor. 11:33*
G4553.strongs: apparently of Hebrew origin (H08276); a basket (as interwoven or wicker-work

G1731.mounce: to manifest, display, Rom. 9:17, 22; Heb. 6:10; to give outward proof of, Rom. 2:15; to display a certain bearing towards a person; hence, to perpetrate openly, 2 Tim. 4:14*
G1731.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to indicate (by word or act)

G4861.mounce: united in mind, at unity, Phil. 2:2
G4861.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5590 (ψυχή); co-spirited, i.e. similar in sentiment

G1368.thayer: I strain, put through a sieve.
G1368.mounce: to strain, filter thoroughly; to strain out or off, Mt. 23:24*
G1368.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and (to filter); to strain out

G1172.mounce: to eat or dine, Lk. 17:8; 22:20; 1 Cor. 11:25; Rev. 3:20
G1172.abbottSmith: to take the chief meal of the day, to dine, to sup : Lk 17:8 22:20 (WH, br., R, mg. omits), I Co 11:25 ; metaph., Re 3:20 .†
G1172.strongs: from G1173 (δεῖπνον); to dine, i.e. take the principle (or evening) meal

G3441.abbottSmith: adj., alone , solitary , forsaken : c. verb., Mt 14:23 , Mk 6:47 , Lk 9:36 , al.; c. pron., Mt 18:15 , Mk 9:2 , al.; c. subst., Mk 9:8 , Lk 4:8 , al.; pleonast., οὐκ . . . εἰ μὴ μ. , Mt 12:4 , Lk 6:4 , al.; attrib., only , ( ὁ ) μ. θεός , Jo 5:44 17:3 , Ro 16:27 , I Ti 1:17 , Ju 25 . As adv., neut., μόνον , alone , only : referring to verb or predic., Mt 9:21 , Mk 5:36 , Ja 1:22 , al. (v. Bl., §44
G3441.strongs: probably from G3306 (μένω); remaining, i.e. sole or single; by implication, mere

G3323.mounce: the Messiah, the Anointed One, i.q. ὁ Χριστός, Jn. 1:42, 4:25*
G3323.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04899); the Messias (i.e. Mashiach), or Christ

G4180.strongs: from a compound of G4183 (πολύς) and G3056 (λόγος); loquacity, i.e. prolixity

G2807.mounce: a key, used in NT as the symbol of power, authority, etc. Mt. 16:19; Rev. 1:18; 3:7; 9:1; 20:1; met. the key of entrance into knowledge, Lk. 11:52*
G2807.abbottSmith: a key . Fig., τ. βασιλείας τ. οὐρανῶν , Mt 16:19 ; τ. γνώσεως , Lk 11:52 ; of David (cf. LXX, Is 22:22 ), Re 3:7 ; τ. θανάτου κ. τ. ᾅδου , Re 1:18 ; τ. ἀβύσσου , Re 20:1 ; τ. φρέατος τῆς ἀ. , Re 9:1 .†
G2807.strongs: from G2808 (κλείω); a key (as shutting a lock), literally or figuratively

G958.thayer: Benjamin, youngest son of Jacob, founder of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
G958.mounce: Benjamin, pr. name, indecl. Acts 13:21; Rom. 11:1; Phil. 3:5; Rev. 7:8*
G958.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01144); Benjamin, an Israelite

G3456.thayer: (properly: I turn up the nose as a sign of contempt), I sneer at, disdain.
G3456.mounce: to contract the nose in contempt and derision, toss up the nose; to mock, deride, Gal. 6:7*
G3456.abbottSmith: to turn up the nose or sneer at , mock : pass., Ga 6:7 (cf. ἐκ-μυκτηρίζω ).†
G3456.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G3455 (μυκάομαι) (meaning snout, as that whence lowing proceeds); to make mouths at, i.e. ridicule

G3426.thayer: a dry measure, nearly two English gallons.
G3426.mounce: a modius, a Roman measure for things dry, containing 16 sextarii, and equivalent to about a peck (8.75 liters); in NT a corn measure, Mt. 5:15; Mk. 4:21; Lk. 11:33*
G3426.abbottSmith: an adulteress : Ro 7:3 ; meton., for μοιχεία , II Pe 2:14 . Metaph., of infidelity to God (cf. Ez 16:15 ff. Ez 23:43 ff., al.), Ja 4:4 ; as an adj., Mt 12:39 16:4 , Mk 8:8 .†
G3426.strongs: of Latin origin; a modius, i.e. certain measure for things dry (the quantity or the utensil)

G4259.thayer: a porch, court before a building, vestibule.
G4259.mounce: the exterior court before an edifice, Mk. 14:68*
G4259.strongs: neuter of a presumed compound of G4253 (πρό) and G833 (αὐλή); a forecourt, i.e. vestibule (alley-way)

G4257.mounce: to hear beforehand or already, Col. 1:5*
G4257.abbottSmith: to hear beforehand : Col 1:5 (v. Lft., in l.).†
G4257.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G191 (ἀκούω); to hear already,i.e. anticipate

G4821.mounce: to reign with; met. to enjoy honor with, 1 Cor. 4:8; 2 Tim. 2:12*
G4821.abbottSmith: to advise, counsel : c. dat. pers., Jo 18:14 ; id. seq. inf., Re 3:18 .
G4821.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G936 (βασιλεύω); to be co-regent (figuratively)

G1678.thayer: Elmadam, father of Cosam, son of Er.
G1678.mounce: also spelled Ἐλμωδάμ, Elmadam, pr. name, indecl., Lk. 3:28*
G1678.abbottSmith: Elmadam , an ancestor of Jesus: Lk 3:28 .
G1678.strongs: of Hebrew origin (perhaps for H0486); Elmodam, an Israelite

G5140.mounce: three, Mt. 12:40. The frequency count does not include its occurrence in the name Τριῶν ταβερνῶν in Acts 28:15 (G5553)
G5140.abbottSmith: three : Mt 12:40 , al.; μετὰ τ. ἡμέρας = τῇ τρίτῃ ἡ. , Mk 10:34 , al. (cf. Field, Notes , 11 ff.)

G2899.mounce: a margin, border, edge, in NT a fringe, tuft, tassel, Mt. 9:20; 14:36; 23:5; Mk. 6:56; Lk. 8:44*
G2899.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a margin, i.e. (specially), a fringe or tassel

G1693.mounce: to be mad against, be furious toward, Acts 26:11*
G1693.abbottSmith: to rage against : c. dat., Ac 26:11 .†
G1693.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3105 (μαίνομαι); to rave on, i.e. rage at

G3570.thayer: adv. (a) of time: just now, even now; just at hand, immediately, (b) of logical connection: now then, (c) in commands and appeals: at this instant.
G3570.mounce: of time, now, at this very moment, an emphatic form of νῦν although it now carries the same meaning
G3570.abbottSmith: now ; of time: c. praes., Ac 24:13 , Ro 15:23 , 25 , I Co 13:13 , II Co 8:11 , 22 , Phm 9 , 11 ; c. pf., Ro 3:21 ; c. pret., Ro 6:22 11:30 (WH, mg.) 7:6 , Eph 2:13 , Col 1:21 3:8 ; ἡ ν. ἀπολογία , Ac 22:1 . Of logical sequence (not so in cl.): Ro 7:17 , I Co 5:11 ( νῦν , WH) 12:18 ( νῦν , WH, txt.) 15:20 , He 8:6 ( νῦν , WH, txt.), He 9:26 (cf. WM, 24, 579 n ).†
G3570.strongs: a prolonged form of G3568 (νῦν) for emphasis; just now

G2380.thayer: I sacrifice, generally an animal; hence: I kill.
G2380.mounce: to offer; to kill in sacrifice, sacrifice, immolate, Acts 14:13, 18; in NT to slaughter for food, Mt. 22:4
G2380.abbottSmith: to offer first fruits to a god.
G2380.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to rush (breathe hard, blow, smoke), i.e. (by implication) to sacrifice (properly, by fire, but genitive case); by extension to immolate (slaughter for any purpose)

G2788.abbottSmith: a lyre , harp : I Co 14:7 , Re 5:8 14:2 15:2 .†

G1517.strongs: from G1518 (εἰρηνοποιός); to be a peace-maker, i.e. (figuratively) to harmonize

G3970.thayer: handed down by (from) one's ancestors, inherited.
G3970.mounce: handed down or received by tradition from one’s fathers or ancestors, 1 Pet. 1:18*
G3970.abbottSmith: handed down from one's fathers , inherited : I Pe 1:18 (Diod., al.).†
G3970.strongs: from G3962 (πατήρ) and a derivative of G3860 (παραδίδωμι) (in the sense of handing over or down); traditionary

G2550.mounce: disposition for mischief, misfortune, malignity, Rom. 1:29*
G2550.strongs: from a compound of G2556 (κακός) and G2239 (ἦθος); bad character, i.e. (specially) mischievousness

G2517.thayer: in order, in succession, in the time immediately after, just after.
G2517.mounce: in a continual order or series, successively, consecutively, Lk. 1:3; Acts 11:4; 18:23; ὁ, ἡ, καθεξῆς, succeeding, subsequent, Lk. 8:1; Acts 3:24*
G2517.abbottSmith: successively , in order : Lk 1:3 , Ac 11:4 18:23 ; τῶν κ. ( those that succeeded him), Ac 3:24 .
G2517.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1836 (ἑξῆς); thereafter, i.e. consecutively; as a noun (by ellipsis of noun) a subsequent person or time

G926.thayer: heavy, weighty, burdensome, lit. and met; violent, oppressive.
G926.mounce: heavy; met. burdensome, oppressive or difficult of observance, as precepts, Mt. 23:4; 1 Jn. 5:3; weighty, important, momentous, Mt. 23:23; Acts 25:7; grievous, oppressive, afflictive, violent, Acts 20:29; authoritative, strict, stern, severe, 2 Cor. 10:10*
G926.abbottSmith: heavy : Mt 23:4 . Metaph., burdensome : ἐντολή , I Jo 5:3 ; severe (perh. impressive ): ἐπιστολή , II Co 10:10 ; weighty : τὰ βαρύτερα τ. νόμου , Mt 23:23 ; αἰτιώματα , Ac 25:7 ; violent , cruel (EV, grievous ): λύκος , Ac 20:29 .†
G926.strongs: from the same as G922 (βάρος); weighty, i.e. (fig) burdensome, grave

G2797.mounce: Kish, the father of Saul, pr. name, indecl., Acts 13:21*
G2797.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07027); Cis (i.e. Kish), an Israelite

G1434.mounce: a gift, free gift, Rom. 5:16; Jas. 1:17*

G3161.mounce: see also Μαθθίας, Mattathias, pr. name, Lk. 3:25, 26*
G3161.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04993); Mattathias (i.e. Mattithjah), an Israelite and a Christian

G615.thayer: I put to death, kill; fig: I abolish.
G615.mounce: also spelled ἀποκτέννω or ἀποκτένω, to kill, Mt. 14:5; to destroy, annihilate, Mt. 10:28; to destroy a hostile principle, Eph. 2:16; met. to kill by spiritual condemnation, Rom. 7:11; 2 Cor. 3:6
G615.abbottSmith: to kill : Mt 14:5 , al.; seq. instr. ἐν (q.v.), Eph 2:16 , Re 2:23 , al. Metaph.: Ro 7:11 ; τ. ἔχθραν , Eph 2:16 ; τὸ γράμμα ἀποκτείνει , II Co 3:6 (on the perfective force of this verb, v. M, Pr. , 114).
G615.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and (to slay); to kill outright; figuratively, to destroy

G5165.abbottSmith: a bowl, dish : Mt 26:23 , Mk 14:20 (Hippocr., Aristoph., Plut., al.).†
G5165.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of uncertain affinity; a bowl

G1043.thayer: the angel Gabriel, a messenger of God.
G1043.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01403); Gabriel, an archangel

G4419.mounce: a little wing; the extremity, the extreme point of a thing; a pinnacle, or apex of a building, Mt. 4:5; Lk. 4:9*
G4419.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4420 (πτέρυξ); a winglet, i.e. (figuratively) extremity (top corner)

G836.strongs: from the same as G109 (ἀήρ); a flute (as blown)

G3073.thayer: Lycia, a small Roman province on the south coast of Asia Minor.
G3073.mounce: Lycia, a province of Asia Minor, Acts 27:5*
G3073.abbottSmith: Lydia , a region of Asia Minor; Ac 27:5 .†
G3073.strongs: probably remotely from G3074 (λύκος); Lycia, a province of Asia Minor

G3566.strongs: from G3565 (νύμφη); a bride-groom (literally or figuratively)

G757.mounce: (1) pr. (act.) to be first; to rule, Mk. 10:42; Rom. 15:12 (2) mid. to begin, Mt. 4:17; to take commencement, Lk. 24:27; 1 Pet. 4:17
G757.strongs: a primary verb; to be first (in political rank or power)

G2845.thayer: (a) a bed, (b) a marriage bed; plur: repeated (immoral) sexual intercourse.
G2845.mounce: a bed, Lk. 11:7; the conjugal bed, Heb. 13:4; meton. sexual intercourse, concubitus; hence, lewdness, whoredom, chambering, Rom. 13:13; in NT conception, Rom. 9:10*
G2845.abbottSmith: a bed : Lk 11:7 ; esp. the marriage-bed : κ. ἀμίαντος , He 13:4 ; κ. ἔχειν , of a woman conceiving, Ro 9:10 (cf. peculiar phrases in LXX, Le 15:18 , al.); of illicit intercourse, pl., Ro 13:13 .†
G2845.strongs: from G2749 (κεῖμαι); a couch; by extension, cohabitation; by implication, the male sperm

G1240.abbottSmith: a successor : Ac 24:27 (for usage in LXX and π. in sense of court official, v. Deiss., BS , 115).†
G1240.strongs: from G1237 (διαδέχομαι); a successor in office

G1220.thayer: a denarius, a small Roman silver coin.
G1220.mounce: Latin denarius, a Roman silver coin; the name originally meant ten asses, Mt. 18:28; Mk. 6:37; Rev. 6:6
G1220.abbottSmith: the Lat. denarius , a Roman coin, nearly equal to the δραχμή , q.v.: Mt 18:28 20:2 , 9 , 13 22:19 , Mk 6:37 12:15 14:5 , Lk 7:41 10:35 20:24 , Jo 6:7 12:5 , Re 6:6 ; τὸ ἀνὰ δ. , Mt 20:10 .†
G1220.strongs: of Latin origin; a denarius (or ten asses)

G2038.thayer: I work, trade, perform, do, practice, commit, acquire by labor.
G2038.mounce: intrans. to work, labor, Mt. 21:28; Lk. 13:14; to trade, traffic, do business, Mt. 25:16; Rev. 18:17; to act, exert one’s power, be active, Jn. 5:17; trans. to do, perform, commit, Mt. 26:10; Jn. 6:28; to be engaged in, occupied upon, 1 Cor. 9:13; Rev. 18:17; to acquire, gain by one’s labor, Jn. 6:27
G2038.strongs: middle voice from G2041 (ἔργον); to toil (as a task, occupation, etc.), (by implication) effect, be engaged in or with, etc.
G2038.kjv: commit, do, labor for, minister about, trade (by), work

G3572.abbottSmith: to pierce : τ. πλευρὰν λόγχῃ , Jo 19:34 , [ Mt 27:49 ], WH.†
G3572.strongs: apparently a primary word; to prick ("nudge")

G3929.thayer: overlooking, suspension, remission of punishment for.
G3929.mounce: a letting pass; a passing over, Rom. 3:25*

G5590.thayer: (a) the vital breath, breath of life, (b) the human soul, (c) the soul as the seat of affections and will, (d) the self, (e) a human person, an individual.
G5590.mounce: breath; the principle of animal life; the life, Mt. 2:20; 6:25; Mk. 3:4; Lk. 21:19; Jn. 10:11; an inanimate being, 1 Cor. 15:45; a human individual, soul, Acts 2:41; 3:23; 7:14; 27:37; Rom. 13:1; 1 Pet. 3:20; the immaterial soul, Mt. 10:28; 1 Pet. 1:9; 2:11, 25; 4:19; the soul as the seat of religious and moral sentiment, Mt. 11:29; Acts 14:2, 22; 15:24; Eph. 6:6; the soul, as a seat of feeling, Mt. 12:18; 26:38; the soul, the inner self, Lk. 12:19
G5590.abbottSmith: breath (Lat. anima ), breath of life, life (Hom., al.; in Arist., of the vital principle ): Mt 6:25 , Mk 3:4 10:45 , Lk 12:22 , Jo 10:11 , Ac 20:10 , 24 , II Co 1:23 , Phl 2:30 , I Th 2:8 , al. the soul , as the seat of the will, desires and affections: Mt 26:38 , Mk 12:30 (LXX) 14:34 , Lk 1:46 , Jo 10:24 , Ac 14:2 , Phl 1:27 , al.; ἐκ ψυχῆς , from the heart , heartily : Eph 6:6 , Col 3:23 ; as a
G5590.strongs: from G5594 (ψύχω); breath, i.e. (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from G4151 (πνεῦμα), which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from G2222 (ζωή), which is mere vitality, even of plants: these terms thus exactly correspond respectively to the Hebrew H05315, H07307 and H02416)
G5590.kjv: heart (+ -ily), life, mind, soul, + us, + you

G106.thayer: unleavened, the paschal feast (a feast of the Hebrews); fig: uncorrupted, sincere.
G106.mounce: unleavened; τὰ ἄζυμα, the feast of unleavened bread; met. pure from foreign matter, unadulterated, genuine; τὸ ἄζυμον, genuineness, 1 Cor. 5:7, 8
G106.abbottSmith: unleavened : ἡ ἑορτὴ τῶν ἄ. ( הַג הַמָצָּה ), the paschal feast (also called τὰ ἄ. , Mk 14:1 ), Lk 22:1 ; ἡμέρα τῶν ἄ. , Mt 26:17 , Mk 14:12 , Lk 22:7 ; ἡμέραι , Ac 12:3 20:6 . Fig., of Christians, free from corruption : I Co 5:7 ; exhorted to keep festival, ἐν ἀζύμοις (sc. ἄρτοις , λάγανοις , or, indefinitely, "unleavened elements"), I Co 5:8 (Cremer, 724).†
G106.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2219 (ζύμη); unleavened, i.e. (figuratively) uncorrupted; (in the neutral plural) specially (by implication) the Passover week

G3101.abbottSmith: a disciple : opp. to διδάσκαλος , Mt 10:24 , Lk 6:40 ; Ἰωάννου , Mt 9:14 , Lk 7:18 , Jo 3:25 ; τ. Φαρισαίων , Mt 22:16 , Mk 2:18 , Lk 5:33 ; Μωυσέως , Jo 9:28 ; Ἰησοῦ , Lk 6:17 7:11 19:37 , Jo 6:66 7:3 19:38 ; esp. the twelve, Mt 10:1 11:1 , Mk 7:17 , Lk 8:9 , Jo 2:2 , al.; later, of Christians generally, Ac 6:1 , 2 , 7 9:19 , al.; τ. κυρίου , Ac 9:1 .
G3101.strongs: from G3129 (μανθάνω); a learner, i.e. pupil

G3886.mounce: to unloose from proper fixity or consistency of substance; to enervate or paralyze the body or limbs; pass. to be enervated or enfeebled, Heb. 12:12; pass. perf. part. παραλελυμένος, paralytic, Lk. 5:18, 24
G3886.abbottSmith: to loose from the side , set free .
G3886.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3089 (λύω); to loosen beside, i.e. relax (perfect passive participle, paralyzed or enfeebled)
G3886.kjv: feeble, sick of the (taken with) palsy

G3764.mounce: not yet, never yet, never, Jn. 7:39; 19:41; 20:9; Acts 8:16*
G3764.strongs: from G3761 (οὐδέ) and G4452 (-πω); not even yet
G3764.kjv: as yet not, never before (yet), (not) yet

G4912.thayer: (a) I press together, close, (b) I press on every side, confine, (c) I hold fast, (d) I urge, impel, (e) pass: I am afflicted with (sickness).
G4912.mounce: pr. to hold together; to confine, shut up, close; τὰ ὦτα, to stop the ears, Acts 7:57; to confine, as a besieged city, Lk. 19:43; to hold, hold fast, have the custody of any one, Lk. 22:63; to hem in, urge, press upon, Lk. 8:45; to exercise a constraining influence on, 2 Cor. 5:14; pass. to be seized with, be affected with, as fear, disease, etc., Mt. 4:24; Lk. 4:38; 8:37; Acts 28:8; to be in a state of mental constriction, to be hard pressed by urgency of circumstances, Lk. 12:50; Acts 18:5; Phil. 1:23*
G4912.abbottSmith: to hold together ( τ. συνέχον τ. πάντα , Wi 1:7 ): of closing the ears, Ac 7:57 ( τ. στόμα , Is 52:15 ); to hem in, press on every side : Lk 8:45 19:43 .
G4912.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold together, i.e. to compress (the ears, with a crowd or siege) or arrest (a prisoner); figuratively, to compel, perplex, afflict, preoccupy
G4912.kjv: constrain, hold, keep in, press, lie sick of, stop, be in a strait, straiten, be taken with, throng

G1348.strongs: from a derivative of G1349 (δίκη); a judger

G555.thayer: a putting off (as of a garment), a casting off.
G555.mounce: a putting or stripping off, renunciation, Col. 2:11*
G555.abbottSmith: a putting or stripping off : Col 2:11 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G3732.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of G3733 (ὄρνις); a birdling

G4268.mounce: foreknowledge; in NT previous determination, purpose, Acts 2:23; 1 Pet. 1:2*

G4292.thayer: I provoke, stimulate, challenge, call out.
G4292.mounce: to call out, challenge to fight; to provoke, irritate, with feelings of ungenerous rivalry, Gal. 5:26*
G4292.abbottSmith: to call forth . Most freq. in mid., to challenge ; hence, to provoke : Ga 5:26 ; to invite ( II Mac 8:11 ).†
G4292.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and G2564 (καλέω); to call forth to oneself (challenge), i.e. (by implication) to irritate

G5171.thayer: I live a luxurious life, live self-indulgently.
G5171.abbottSmith: to live daintily, luxuriously, to fare sumptuously : Ja 5:5 (cf. ἐν-τρυφάω ).†
G5171.strongs: from G5172 (τρυφή); to indulge in luxury

G5200.mounce: pr. wet, moist, humid; used of a tree, full of sap, fresh, green, Lk. 23:31*
G5200.abbottSmith: wet, moist , opp. to ξηρός : of wood, sappy, green : Lk 23:31 .†
G5200.strongs: from the base of G5205 (ὑετός); wet (as if with rain), i.e. (by implication) sappy (fresh)

G2608.thayer: I break down (in pieces), crush, break into.
G2608.mounce: to trace, draw in outline, write, Jn. 8:6*
G2608.abbottSmith: to break : c. acc. rei, Mt 12:20 (LXX) , Jo 19:31-33 .†
G2608.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and the base of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); to rend in pieces, i.e. crack apart

G378.thayer: I fill up, make up, complete the measure of, fulfill, carry out the commands (provisions, etc.) of.
G378.mounce: to fill up, complete, 1 Thess. 2:16; to fulfil, confirm, as a prophecy by the event, Mt. 13:14; to fill the place of any one, 1 Cor. 14:16; to supply, make good, 1 Cor. 16:17; Phil. 2:30; to observe fully, keep the law, Gal. 6:2*
G378.abbottSmith: to fill up , make full (in π. of completing contracts and making up rent; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.): τόπον , take one's place (cf. Heb. מָקוֹם מִלֵּא ), I Co 14:16 ; άμαρτίας , complete the number , I Th 2:16 ; τ. νόμον , observe perfectly , Ga 6:2 ; pass., προφητεία , fulfilled , Mt 13:14 .
G378.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G4137 (πληρόω); to complete; by implication, to occupy, supply; figuratively, to accomplish (by coincidence ot obedience)

G5593.abbottSmith: cold : sc. ὕδωρ (cf. Theogn., 263; Hdt., ii, 37), Mt 10:42 ; metaph., of indifferent persons, Re 3:15 , 16 †

G4669.mounce: to mingle or flavor with myrrh, Mk. 15:23*
G4669.abbottSmith: intrans., to be like myrrh (Diosc., i, 79).
G4669.strongs: from G4667 (Σμύρνα); to tincture with myrrh, i.e. embitter (as a narcotic)

G3582.thayer: a Roman measure, a pitcher or cup of any size.
G3582.mounce: a sextarius, a Roman measure, containing about one pint English; in NT used for a small vessel, cup, pot, Mk. 7:4*
G3582.strongs: as if from (properly, to smooth; by implication, (of friction) to boil or heat); a vessel (as fashioned or for cooking) (or perhaps by corruption from the Latin sextarius, the sixth of a modius, i.e. about a pint), i.e. (specially), a measure for liquids or solids, (by analogy, a pitcher)

G4128.thayer: a multitude, crowd, great number, assemblage.
G4128.mounce: fullness, amplitude, magnitude; a multitude, a great number, Lk. 1:10; 2:13; 5:6; a multitude, a crowd, throng, Mk. 3:7, 8; Lk. 6:17
G4128.abbottSmith: a great number , a multitude ; of things: ἰχθύων , Lk 5:6 , Jo 21:6 ; φρυγάνων , Ac 28:3 ; ἁμαρτιιῶν , Ja 5:20 , I Pe 4:8 ; τ. πλήθει , in multitude , He 11:12 ; of persons: Ac 21:22 (WH, R, om.); c. gen., Lk 2:13 , Jo 5:3 , Ac 5:14 ; π. πολύ ( πολὺ π. ), Mk 3:7 , 8 ; id. c. gen., Lk 6:17 23:27 , Ac 14:1 17:4 .
G4128.strongs: from G4130 (πλήθω); a fulness, i.e. a large number, throng, populace

G157.strongs: from a derivative of G156 (αἰτία); a thing charged

G4630.strongs: apparently of Latin origin; left-handed; Scevas (i.e. Scævus), an Israelite

G1215.thayer: I make a public speech, address a multitude.
G1215.mounce: to address a public assembly, to deliver a public oration, Acts 12:21*
G1215.strongs: from a compound of G1218 (δῆμος) and G58 (ἀγορά); to be a people-gatherer, i.e. to address a public assembly

G5576.thayer: I testify falsely, bear false witness.
G5576.mounce: to bear false witness, give false testimony, Mt. 19:18; Mk. 10:19; 14:56, 57; Lk. 18:20*
G5576.abbottSmith: to bear false witness : Mt 19:18 , Mk 10:19 , Lk 18:20 (LXX) ; seq. κατά , c. gen. pers., Mk 14:56 , 57 (Xen., Plat., al.).†
G5576.strongs: from G5575 (ψευδομάρτυρ); to be an untrue testifier, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence

G3760.thayer: by no means, not at all.
G3760.abbottSmith: in no wise , by no means : Mt 2:6 (OT).†
G3760.strongs: adverb from (the feminine) of G3762 (οὐδείς); by no means

G3049.thayer: I reckon, count, charge with; reason, decide, conclude; think, suppose.
G3049.mounce: (1) pr. to count, calculate; to count, enumerate, Mk. 15:28; Lk. 22:37; to set down as a matter of account, 1 Cor. 13:5; 2 Cor. 3:5; 12:6; to impute, Rom. 4:3; 2 Cor. 5:19; 2 Tim. 4:16; to account, Rom. 2:26; 8:36; εἰς οὐδὲν λογισθῆναι, to be set at nought, despised, Acts 19:27; to regard, deem, consider, Rom. 6:11; 14:14; 1 Cor. 4:1; 2 Cor. 10:2; Phil. 3:13; (2) to infer, conclude, presume, Rom. 2:3; 3:28; 8:18; 2 Cor. 10:2, 7, 11; Heb. 11:19; 1 Pet. 5:12; (3) to think upon, ponder, Phil. 4:8; absol. to reason, Mk. 11:31; 1 Cor. 13:11
G3049.abbottSmith: prop., of numerical calculation, to count , reckon : c. acc. seq. μετά , Mk 15:23 (LXX) (Rec., R, mg.), Lk 22:37 .
G3049.strongs: middle voice from G3056 (λόγος); to take an inventory, i.e. estimate (literally or figuratively)
G3049.kjv: conclude, (ac-)count (of), + despise, esteem, impute, lay, number, reason, reckon, suppose, think (on)

G202.thayer: a hearer of, a listener to.
G202.strongs: from (to listen; apparently an intensive of G191 (ἀκούω)); a hearer (merely)

G4946.mounce: Syracuse, a celebrated city of Sicily, Acts 28:12*
G4946.strongs: plural of uncertain derivation; Syracuse, the capital of Sicily

G2536.thayer: (Hebrew), Cainan, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G2536.abbottSmith: Cainam , Cainan ; son of Enos ( Ge 5:9 ): Lk 3:37 . Son of Arphaxad ( Ge 10:24 (LXX) : Lk 3:36 .†
G2536.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07018); Cainan (i.e. Kenan), the name of two patriarchs

G1685.abbottSmith: to cast into : seq. εἰς , Lk 12:5 (cf. MM, Exp. , vii, 93).†
G1685.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw on, i.e. (figuratively) subject to (eternal punishment)

G178.mounce: uncondemned in a public trial, Acts 16:37; 22:25*
G178.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2632 (κατακρίνω); without (legal) trial

G1350.abbottSmith: general term for a net : Mt 4:20 , 21 , Mk 1:18 , 19 , Lk 5:2 , 4-6 , Jo 21:6 , 8 , 11 .†
G1350.strongs: probably from a primary verb (to cast); a seine (for fishing)

G521.thayer: untrained, uneducated, showing a want of training or education, ignorant.
G521.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3811 (παιδεύω); uninstructed, i.e. (figuratively) stupid

G154.mounce: to ask, request; demand; desire, Acts 7:46
G154.abbottSmith: to ask , request : absol., Mt 7:7 , Ja 1:6 ; c. acc. pers., Mt 5:42 , Lk 6:30 ; c. acc. rei, seq. ἀπό , Mt 20:20 , I Jo 5:15 ; id. seq. παρά , Ac 3:2 , Ja 1:5 ; c. dupl. acc. Mt 7:9 , Mk 6:22 , Jo 16:23 . Mid. (on the distinction bet. mid. and act., v. M, Pr. , 160): absol., Mk 15:8 , Jo 16:26 , Ja 4:3 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 14:7 , Mk 6:24 , al.; c. acc. pers., Mt 27:20 , Lk 23:25 ; c. acc. rei, seq. π
G154.strongs: of uncertain derivation; to ask (in genitive case)
G154.kjv: ask, beg, call for, crave, desire, require

G3339.mounce: to change the external form, transfigure; mid. to change one’s form, be transfigured, Mt. 17:2; Mk. 9:2; to undergo a spiritual transformation Rom. 12:2; 2 Cor. 3:18*
G3339.abbottSmith: to transform , transfigure : pass., of Christ's transfiguration, Mt 17:2 , Mk 9:2 (cf. Lk 9:29 ); of Christians, Ro 12:2 , II Co 3:18 .†
G3339.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G3445 (μορφόω); to transform (literally or figuratively, "metamorphose")

G1630.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5401 (φόβος); frightened out of one's wits

G1656.mounce: pity, mercy, compassion, Mt. 9:13; 12:7; Lk. 1:50, 78; meton. benefit which results from compassion, kindness, mercies, blessing, Lk. 1:54, 58, 72; 10:37; Rom. 9:23
G1656.strongs: of uncertain affinity; compassion (human or divine, especially active)

G1509.strongs: from G1508 (εἰ μή) and the neuter of G5100 (τὶς); if not somewhat

G2762.thayer: a little hook, an apostrophe on letters of the alphabet, distinguishing them from other little letters, or a separation stroke between letters.
G2762.mounce: pr. a horn-like projection, a point, extremity; in NT an apex, or fine point; as of letters, used for the minutest part, a tittle, Mt. 5:18; Lk. 16:17*
G2762.abbottSmith: a little horn : of the point or extremity which distinguishes some Heb. letters from others (e.g. ד from ר ; DCG , ii, 733), Mt 5:18 , Lk 16:17 .†
G2762.strongs: feminine of a presumed derivative of the base of G2768 (κέρας); something horn-like, i.e. (specially) the apex of a Hebrew letter (figuratively, the least particle)

G764.mounce: to be impious, to act impiously or wickedly, live an impious life, 2 Pet. 2:6; Jude 15*
G764.abbottSmith: to be ungodly , act profanely : II Pe 2:6 ; c. cogn. acc. (MM, s.v.), Ju 15 .†
G764.strongs: from G765 (ἀσεβής); to be (by implied act) impious or wicked
G764.kjv: commit (live, that after should live) ungodly

G1548.abbottSmith: Rec. for γαμίσκω , q.v.: Lk 20:34 , 35 . Not elsewhere.†
G1548.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1061 (γαμίσκω); the same as 1547

G4136.mounce: full conviction, firm persuasion, assurance, 1 Thess. 1:5; Col. 2:2
G4136.abbottSmith: full assurance , confidence : I Th 1:5 ; τ. συνέσεως , Col 2:2 ; τ. ἐλπίδος , He 6:11 ; πίστεως , He 10:22 (cf. Lft. on Col 2:2 ; M, Th. , 9).†

G5617.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01954); Hosee (i.e. Hoshea), an Israelite

G467.mounce: to repay, give back, return, recompense, Lk. 14:14 (2x); Rom. 11:35; 12:19; 1 Thess. 3:9; 2 Thess. 1:6; Heb. 10:30
G467.abbottSmith: to give back as an equivalent , recompense , requital (the ἀντί expressing the idea of full, complete return; v. Lft., Notes , 46); in favourable sense: Lk 14:14 , Ro 11:35 , I Th 3:9 ; in unfavourable sense: Ro 12:19 , II Th 1:6 , He 10:30 .†
G467.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G591 (ἀποδίδωμι); to requite (good or evil)

G4147.thayer: I become rich, am rich, abound in.
G4147.mounce: to be or become rich, Lk. 1:25; 1 Tim. 6:9; trop. Lk. 12:21; met. to abound in, be abundantly furnished with, 1 Tim. 6:18; to be spiritually enriched, 2 Cor. 8:9
G4147.abbottSmith: to be rich , aor., to become rich : Lk 1:53 , I Ti 6:9 ; seq. ἀπό ( Si 11:18 ), Re 18:15 ; ἐκ , Re 18:3 , 19 ; ἐν (= cl., c. gen., dat., acc., cf. πλούσιος ), I Ti 6:18 . Metaph., π. εἰς θεόν , Lk 12:21 ; of God, εἰς πάντας , Ro 10:12 ; aor., I became rich , I Co 4:8 , II Co 8:9 , Re 3:18 ; pf., I have become rich , Re 3:17 .†
G4147.strongs: from G4148 (πλουτίζω); to be (or become) wealthy (literally or figuratively)
G4147.kjv: be increased with goods, (be made, wax) rich

G4227.thayer: Pudens, a Christian man in Rome.
G4227.strongs: of Latin origin; modest; Pudes (i.e. Pudens), a Christian

G1658.mounce: free, in a state of freedom as opposed to slavery, 1 Cor. 12:13; Gal. 3:28; free, exempt, Mt. 17:26; 1 Cor. 7:39; unrestricted, unfettered, 1 Cor. 9:1; free from the dominion of sin, etc., Jn. 8:36; Rom. 6:20; free in the possession of Gospel privileges, 1 Pet. 2:16
G1658.strongs: probably from the alternate of G2064 (ἔρχομαι); unrestrained (to go at pleasure), i.e. (as a citizen) not a slave (whether freeborn or manumitted), or (genitive case) exempt (from obligation or liability)

G3966.thayer: a patriarch, head or founder of a family.
G3966.mounce: a patriarch, head or founder of a family, Acts 2:29; 7:8, 9; Heb. 7:4*
G3966.strongs: from G3965 (πατριά) and G757 (ἄρχω); a progenitor ("patriarch")

G4098.thayer: I fall, fall under (as under condemnation), fall prostrate.
G4098.abbottSmith: to fall ; of descent, to fall , fall down or from : seq. ἐπί , c. acc. loc., Mt 10:29 , al.; εἰς , Mt 15:14 , al.; ἐς μέσῳ , c. gen., Lk 8:7 ; παρὰ τ. ὁδόν , Mt 13:4 , Mk 4:4 , Lk 8:5 ; seq. ἀπό , Mt 15:27 , al.; ἐκ , Mk 13:25 , Lk 10:18 , Re 8:10 9:1 . Metaph.: ὁ ἥλιος , seq. ἐπί , Re 7:16 ; ἀχλὺς κ. σκότος , Ac 13:11 ; ὁ κλῆρος , Ac 1:26 ; ὑπὸ κρίσιν , Ja 5:12 . Of prostration, of persons, to fa
G4098.strongs: probably akin to G4072 (πέτομαι) through the idea of alighting; to fall (literally or figuratively)

G4368.strongs: feminine of a derivative of G4291 (προΐστημι); a patroness, i.e. assistant

G1767.abbottSmith: nine : Lk 17:17 ; ἐνενήκοντα ἐ. , Mt 18:12 , 13 , Lk 15:4 , 7 .†

G1666.mounce: also spelled Ἐλισσαῖος, Elisha, pr. name, Lk. 4:27*
G1666.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0477); Elissæus, an Israelite

G5582.mounce: a falsehood, lie; in NT untruthfulness, Rom. 3:7*
G5582.abbottSmith: a lie, falsehood : Ro 3:7 (Plat., Plut. al.).†
G5582.strongs: from G5574 (ψεύδομαι); a fabrication, i.e. falsehood

G3403.thayer: I remember, call to mind, recall, mention.
G3403.mounce: to remember, recollect, call to mind, Mt. 26:75; Lk. 1:54, 72; 16:25; in NT, in a passive sense, to be called to mind, be borne in mind, Acts 10:31; Rev. 16:19
G3403.abbottSmith: to remind : mid. and pass.; reflexive , to remind oneself of , hence, to remember : c. gen. rei, Mt 26:75 , Lk 1:54 , 72 24:8 , Ac 11:16 , II Pe 3:2 , Ju 17 ; c. neg., of sins, = to forgive , He 8:12 10:17 (LXX) ; c. gen. pers., Lk 23:42 ; seq. ὅτι , Mt 5:23 27:63 , Lk 16:25 , Jo 2:17 , 22 12:16 ; ὡς , Lk 24:6 ; pf., μέμνημι , in pres. sense (cl.), c. gen. pers. (rei), I Co 11:2 , II Ti 1:4 ; pres
G3403.strongs: a prolonged form of G3415 (μνάομαι) (from which some of the tenses are borrowed); to remind, i.e. (middle voice) to recall to mind

G4537.abbottSmith: to sound a trumpet : Re 8:6 ff. 9:1 , 13 10:7 11:15 ; metaph., Mt 6:2 . Impers., the trumpet sounds : I Co 15:52 .†
G4537.strongs: from G4536 (σάλπιγξ); to trumpet, i.e. sound a blast (literally or figuratively)

G3587.thayer: I shave, shear, cut off the hair.
G3587.mounce: to cut off the hair, shear, shave, Acts 21:24; 1 Cor. 11:5, 6*
G3587.abbottSmith: late form of ξυρέω (q.v.) : I Co 11:6 ( ξυρᾶσθαι , Rec. and Edd., but v.s. ξύρω ).†
G3587.strongs: from a derivative of the same as G3586 (ξύλον) (meaning a razor); to shave or "shear" the hair

G582.mounce: a register, inventory; registration, enrollment, Lk. 2:2; Acts 5:37*
G582.strongs: from G583 (ἀπογράφω); an enrollment; by implication, an assessment

G1287.mounce: to disperse, scatter, Mt. 26:31; Mk. 14:27; to dissipate, waste, Lk. 15:13; 16:1; to winnow, or, to strew, Mt. 25:24, 26
G1287.abbottSmith: to scatter abroad, disperse : of sheep, Mt 26:31 = Mk 14:27 (LXX) ; of persons, Lk 1:51 , Ac 5:37 , opp. to συνάγω , Jo 11:52 ; of winnowing grain, Mt 25:24 , 26 ; metaph., of property, to squander, waste : Lk 15:13 16:1 .†
G1287.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4650 (σκορπίζω); to dissipate, i.e. (genitive case) to rout or separate; specially, to winnow; figuratively, to squander

G1882.abbottSmith: correction : of life, II Ti 3:16 (cf. MM, Exp. , xiv).†
G1882.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G461 (ἀνορθόω); a straightening up again, i.e. (figuratively) rectification (reformation)

G5079.mounce: an artisan; workman, mechanic, Acts 19:24, 38; Rev. 18:22; an architect, builder, Heb. 11:10*
G5079.abbottSmith: a craftsman, artificer : Ac 19:24 , 38 , Re 18:22 ; of God ( Wi 13:1 ), He 11:10 .†
G5079.strongs: from G5078 (τέχνη); an artisan; figuratively, a founder (Creator)

G52.thayer: ignorance, inadvertence, sometimes with the idea of willful blindness.
G52.abbottSmith: ignorance : Ac 3:17 17:30 , Eph 4:18 (with sense of wilful blindness; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.), I Pe 1:14 .†

G2818.mounce: an heir, Mt. 21:38; Gal. 4:1; a possessor, Rom. 4:13; Heb. 11:7; Jas. 2:5
G2818.abbottSmith: (as in cl.) an heir : Mt 21:38 , Mk 12:7 , Lk 20:14 , Ga 4:1 ; of those who as sons of God inherit the privileges of the Messianic Kingdom; of Christ himself, He 1:2 ; of Abraham, Ro 4:13 , 14 ; of Christians, Ro 8:17 , Ga 3:29 , Tit 3:7 , Ja 2:5 .
G2818.strongs: from G2819 (κλῆρος) and the base of G3551 (νόμος) (in its original sense of partitioning, i.e. (reflexively) getting by apportionment); a sharer by lot, i.e. inheritor (literally or figuratively); by implication, a possessor

G3909.thayer: I come by chance, chance to meet.
G3909.mounce: to happen, to chance upon, chance to meet, Acts 17:17*
G3909.abbottSmith: to happen to be near or present : Ac 17:17 .†
G3909.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5177 (τυγχάνω); to chance near, i.e. fall in with

G5251.abbottSmith: to exalt beyond measure, exalt to the highest place : Phl 2:9 .
G5251.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5312 (ὑψόω); to elevate above others, i.e. raise to the highest position

G689.thayer: Ram, son of Hezron and father of Amminadab.
G689.abbottSmith: Aram : Mt 1:3 , 4 , Lk 3:33 (R, txt., WH, Ἀρνεί ).†
G689.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07410); Aram (i.e. Ram), an Israelite

G2997.thayer: I burst asunder with a loud noise.
G2997.abbottSmith: (in cl., poët.) to clang , crash , crack ; in late prose, to crack or burst noisily : Ac 1:18 ( ἐλάκησεν , perh., however, from λακέω , q.v.; Bl., in l.).
G2997.strongs: a strengthened form of a primary verb, which only occurs in this and another prolonged form as alternate in certain tenses; to crack open (from a fall)

G1416.abbottSmith: to enter, sink into ; of the sun (sc. πόντον ), set : Mk 1:32 , Lk 4:40
G1416.strongs: prolonged forms of an obsolete primary (to sink); to go "down"

G1980.thayer: I look upon, visit, look out, select.
G1980.mounce: to prepare for a journey, Acts 21:15*
G1980.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G4649 (σκοπός); to inspect, i.e. (by implication) to select; by extension, to go to see, relieve

G1867.mounce: to praise, commend, applaud, Lk. 16:8; Rom. 15:11; 1 Cor. 11:2, 17, 22 (2x)*
G1867.abbottSmith: to praise : c. acc, Ro 15:11 , I Co 11:22 ; seq. ὅτι , Lk 16:8 , I Co 11:2 ; absol., seq. ὅτι , I Co 11:17 .†
G1867.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G134 (αἰνέω); to applaud

G1864.mounce: to contend strenuously in defence of, Jude 3*
G1864.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G75 (ἀγωνίζομαι); to struggle for

G688.thayer: Arabia, the district south of Palestine.
G688.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06152); Arabia, a region of Asia

G3389.thayer: a matricide, smiter of his mother.
G3389.mounce: one who murders or strikes his mother, matricide, 1 Tim. 1:9*
G3389.abbottSmith: a matricide : I Ti 1:9 (AV, R, txt., but v. infr.);
G3389.strongs: from G3384 (μήτηρ) and the base of G257 (ἅλων); a mother-thresher, i.e. matricide

G3603.strongs: from the neuter of G3739 (ὅς) and the third person singular present indicative of G1510 (εἰμί); which is
G3603.kjv: called, which is (make), that is (to say)

G4371.thayer: anything eaten with bread, especially fish or meat.
G4371.mounce: what is eaten besides; hence, genr. victuals, food, Jn. 21:5*
G4371.abbottSmith: Hellenistic for ὄψον (v.s. ὀψάριον ), a relish or dainty (esp. cooked fish ), to be eaten with bread: Jo 21:5 ( have ye taken any fish , Field, Notes , 109; Abbott, Essays , 105; cf. M, Pr. , 170 n ; MM, Exp. , xxi).†
G4371.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4314 (πρός) and G5315 (φάγω); something eaten in addition to bread, i.e. a relish (specially, fish; compare G3795 (ὀψάριον))

G5036.mounce: swift, fleet, quick; met. ready, prompt, Jas. 1:19; Mt. 28:7f.; Mk. 9:39; Lk. 15:22; Jn. 11:29
G5036.abbottSmith: quick, swift, speedy : opp. to βραδύς , Ja 1:19 .†
G5036.strongs: of uncertain affinity; fleet, i.e. (figuratively) prompt or ready

G956.mounce: a missile weapon, dart, arrow, Eph. 6:16*
G956.strongs: from G906 (βάλλω); a missile, i.e. spear or arrow

G1700.strongs: a prolonged form of G3449 (μόχθος); of me

G4779.mounce: to call together, Mk. 15:16; Lk. 15:6, 9; Acts 5:21; mid. to call around one’s self, Lk. 9:1; 23:13; Acts 10:24; 28:17*
G4779.abbottSmith: to call together : c. acc., Mk 15:16 , Lk 15:6 , 9 (v.l. -εῖται , v. B1., § 55, 1), Ac 5:21 . Mid., to call together to oneself : Lk 9:1 (and v.l. in 15:6 , 9 v. supr.) 23:13 , Ac 10:24 28:17 .†
G4779.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2564 (καλέω); to convoke

G3147.abbottSmith: to whip , scourge : c. acc., Ac 22:25 .†

G4262.mounce: belonging or pertaining to sheep; ἡ προβατικὴ (πύλη), the sheep gate, Jn. 5:2*
G4262.abbottSmith: of sheep : ἡ π. (sc. πύλη , v. supr.), the sheep-gate , Jo 5:2 .†
G4262.strongs: from G4263 (πρόβατον); relating to sheep, i.e. (a gate) through which they were led into Jerusalem

G112.thayer: without god, without (the only true) God, godless.
G112.mounce: an atheist; godless, estranged from the knowledge and worship of the true God, Eph. 2:12*
G112.abbottSmith: in cl. slighting or denying the gods (Plat.; cf. MM, VGT , s.v.); godless, ungodly (Pind.); abandoned by the gods (Soph.)
G112.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2316 (θεός); godless

G2355.strongs: from the base of G2360 (θροέω); wailing

G1951.thayer: I call; mid: I choose for myself; pass: I am named.
G1951.mounce: to call, Jn. 5:2; mid. to select for one’s self, choose, Acts 15:40*
G1951.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3004 (λέγω); to surname, select

G1064.thayer: the womb, stomach; of a woman: to be with child (lit: to have [a child] in the belly).
G1064.mounce: the belly, stomach; the womb, Lk. 1:31; ἐν γαστρὶ ἔχειν, to be with child, Mt. 1:18, 23; 24:19, et al.; γαστέρες, gluttons, Tit. 1:12
G1064.abbottSmith: the belly : metaph., a glutton , Tit 1:12 . the womb : ἐν γ. ἔχειν , to be with child , Mt 1:18 , 23 (LXX) 24:19 , Mk 13:17 , Lk 21:23 , I Th 5:3 , Re 12:2 ; εν γ. συλλαμβ. , to conceive , Lk 1:31 .†
G1064.strongs: of uncertain derivation; the stomach; by analogy, the matrix; figuratively, a gourmand

G3008.thayer: I act in the public service, render service, minister, in the widest sense, of some special public religious service, but also of the service of priests and Levites.
G3008.mounce: pr. to perform some public service at one’s own expense; in NT to officiate as a priest, Heb. 10:11; to minister in the Christian Church, Acts 13:2; to minister to, assist, succor, Rom. 15:27*
G3008.abbottSmith: in cl., at Athens, to supply public offices at one's own cost , render public service to the State , hence, generally,
G3008.strongs: from G3011 (λειτουργός); to be a public servant, i.e. (by analogy) to perform religious or charitable functions (worship, obey, relieve)

G2352.thayer: I crush, break, shatter; met: I break down.
G2352.mounce: to break, shiver; met., shattered, crushed by cruel oppression, Lk. 4:18*
G2352.abbottSmith: to break in pieces , shatter ; metaph., to break down : Lk 4:18 (LXX) .†

G676.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of a comparative of G4314 (πρός) and (to go); inaccessible

G2907.thayer: flesh; plur: pieces of flesh, kinds of flesh.
G2907.mounce: flesh, meat, a later form of κρέατος, Rom. 14:21; 1 Cor. 8:13*

G3803.thayer: a snare, trap (especially for catching birds) hence, met: stratagem, device, wile.
G3803.mounce: a snare, trap, Lk. 21:35; met. device, wile, 1 Tim. 3:7; 6:9; 2 Tim. 2:26; met. a trap of ruin, Rom. 11:9*
G3803.abbottSmith: poët. (Aristoph., al.) and late for πάγη , a trap , snare ; metaph. (as also in cl.): Lk 21:35 , Ro 11:9 (LXX) , I Ti 3:7 6:9 , II Ti 2:26 .†
G3803.strongs: from G4078 (πήγνυμι); a trap (as fastened by a noose or notch); figuratively, a trick or statagem (temptation)

G4986.strongs: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and G3962 (πατήρ); of a safe father; Sopatrus, a Christian

G340.thayer: I make fresh again, renew, restore.
G340.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and a derivative of G2537 (καινός); to restore

G2304.mounce: divine, pertaining to God, 2 Pet. 1:3, 4; τὸ θεῖον, the divine nature, divinity, Acts 17:29*
G2304.abbottSmith: divine : δύναμις , II Pe 1:3 ; φύσις (for parallel in π. , v. MM, Exp. , xv), II Pe 1:4 ; τὸ θ. , the Deity (so in cl.; of God, in Philo and FlJ), Ac 17:29 .†
G2304.strongs: from G2316 (θεός); godlike (neuter as noun, divinity)

G2033.mounce: seven, indecl. numeral, Mt. 15:34, 37; by Jewish usage for a round number, Mt. 12:45; Lk. 11:26

G1404.thayer: a dragon or huge serpent; met: Satan.
G1404.mounce: a dragon or large serpent; met. the devil or Satan, Rev. 12:3, 4, 7, 9, 13, 16, 17; 13:2, 4, 11; 16:13; 20:2
G1404.abbottSmith: a dragon , a mythical monster: fig., of Satan, Re 12:3-17 13:2 , 4 , 11 16:13 20:2 .†
G1404.strongs: probably from an alternate form of (to look); a fabulous kind of serpent (perhaps as supposed to fascinate)

G1140.thayer: an evil-spirit, demon; a heathen deity.
G1140.mounce: a heathen god, deity, Acts 17:18; 1 Cor. 10:20, 21; Rev. 9:20; in NT, a demon, evil spirit, Mt. 7:22; 9:33, 34; 10:8; 12:24
G1140.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G1142 (δαίμων); a dæmonic being; by extension a deity

G618.thayer: (a) I get back, receive back, (b) I get (receive) as due (deserved), (c) mid: I draw aside, separate.
G618.mounce: to receive what is due, sought, or needed, Lk. 23:41; Rom. 1:27; Gal. 4:5; Col. 3:24; 2 Jn. 8; to receive in full, Lk. 16:25; to receive back, recover, Lk. 6:34; 15:27; 18:30; mid. to take aside, lead away, Mk. 7:33
G618.abbottSmith: to receive from another; absol., to receive as one's due : Lk 16:25 18:30 (v.l. λάβῃ ) 23:41 , Ro 1:27 , Ga 4:5 , Col 3:24 , II Jo 8 .
G618.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to receive (specially, in full, or as a host); also to take aside

G1975.mounce: to travel to; to come to, Lk. 8:4*
G1975.abbottSmith: to travel, journey to : seq. πρός , c. acc pers., Lk 8:4 .†
G1975.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to journey further, i.e. travel on (reach)

G4847.abbottSmith: a fellow-citizen : pl., σ. τ. ἁγίων , opp. to ξένοι κ. πάροικοι , Eph 2:19 (Eur.; C.I. , 6446; condemned by Atticists; v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 255 f.).†
G4847.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4177 (πολίτης); a native of the same town, i.e. (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow-Christian)

G1310.mounce: to report, proclaim, publish, spread abroad, Mt. 9:31; 28:15; Mk. 1:45*
G1310.abbottSmith: in late writers only, to spread abroad : τ. λόγον (= הַדָּבָר , the matter ), Mk 1:45 , Mt 28:15 ; c. acc pers., to spread abroad one's fame : Mt 9:31 .†
G1310.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G5345 (φήμη); to report thoroughly, i.e. divulgate
G1310.kjv: blaze abroad, commonly report, spread abroad, fame

G3657.strongs: from G3658 (ὅμιλος); companionship ("homily"), i.e. (by implication) intercourse

G3407.thayer: a paid worker, hired servant, hireling (contrasted with a slave).
G3407.mounce: hired; as a subst., a hired servant, hireling, Lk. 15:17, 19*
G3407.abbottSmith: hired ; as subst., ὁ μ. , a hired servant : Lk 15:17 , 19 , 21 (Anth., Plut.).†

G2741.mounce: to be on fire, burn intensely, 2 Pet. 3:10, 12*
G2741.strongs: from G2740 (καῦσις); to set on fire

G4755.thayer: (a) a general or leader of the army, (b) a magistrate or governor, (c) captain of the temple.
G4755.mounce: a leader or commander of an army, general; a Roman praetor, provincial magistrate, Acts 16:20, 22, 35, 36, 38; στρατηγός τοῦ ἱεροῦ, the captain or prefect of the temple, the chief of the Levites who kept guard in and around the temple, Lk. 22:4, 52; Acts 4:1; 5:24, 26*
