G4755.abbottSmith: a military commander, a general (Hdt., al.).
G4755.strongs: from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G71 (ἄγω) or G2233 (ἡγέομαι); a general, i.e. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (prætor), the chief (præfect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens

G2843.thayer: willing to share, sociable, ready to communicate, beneficent.
G2843.mounce: social; in NT generous, liberal, beneficent, 1 Tim. 6:18*

G3057.mounce: pr. the head of a javelin; a spear, lance, Jn. 19:34*

G4464.thayer: a rod, staff, staff of authority, scepter.
G4464.mounce: a rod, wand, Heb. 9:4; Rev. 11:1; a rod of correction, 1 Cor. 4:21; a staff, Mt. 10:10; Heb. 11:21; a scepter, Heb. 1:8; Rev. 2:27
G4464.abbottSmith: a staff, rod : He 9:4 , Re 11:1 ; in particular,
G4464.strongs: from the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω); a stick or wand (as a cudgel, a cane or a baton of royalty)

G4444.mounce: a tower, Mt. 21:33; Mk. 12:1; Lk. 13:4; genr. a castle, palace, Lk. 14:28*
G4444.abbottSmith: a tower : Lk 13:4 ; of a watch-tower in a vineyard ( Is 5:2 ): Mt 21:33 , Mk 12:1 , and prob., Lk 14:28 .†
G4444.strongs: apparently a primary word ("burgh"); a tower or castle

G4200.mounce: a providing, procuring; meton. source of gain, 1 Tim. 6:5, 6*
G4200.strongs: from a derivative of (a way, i.e. means); furnishing (procuring), i.e. (by implication) money-getting (acquisition)

G2857.thayer: Colossae, a town of the Roman province Asia, in the Lycus valley, near Laodicea and Hierapolis.
G2857.mounce: also spelled Κολασσαεύς, Colossae, a city of Phrygia, Col. 1:2*. See PhotoGuide.
G2857.abbottSmith: Colossæ , a city on the Lycus in Phrygia: Col 1:2 .†
G2857.strongs: apparently feminine plural of kolossos ("colossal"); Colossæ, a place in Asia Minor

G2883.thayer: Cornelius, a centurion of the Roman army, stationed at Caesarea.
G2883.mounce: Cornelius, a Latin pr. name, Acts 10:1, 3, 17, 22, 24f., 30f.*
G2883.strongs: of Latin origin; Cornelius, a Roman

G4154.thayer: I blow, breathe, as the wind.
G4154.mounce: to breathe; to blow, as the wind, Mt. 7:25, 27
G4154.abbottSmith: to breathe , blow : of the wind, Mt 7:25 , 27 , Lk 12:55 , Jo 3:8 6:18 , Re 7:1 ; τῇ πνεούσῃ (sc. αὔρᾳ ), Ac 27:40 (cf. ἐκ- , ἐν- , ὑπο-πνέω ).†
G4154.strongs: a primary word; to breathe hard, i.e. breeze

G4350.thayer: I stumble, strike the foot against, beat upon, take offense at.
G4350.mounce: to dash against, to beat upon, Mt. 7:27; to strike the foot against, Mt. 4:6; Lk. 4:11; to stumble, Jn. 11:9, 10; met. to stumble at, to take offense at, Rom. 9:32; 14:21; 1 Pet. 2:8*
G4350.abbottSmith: trans., to strike (e.g. hand or foot) against : c. acc. seq. πρός , fig., Mt 4:6 , Lk 4:11 (LXX) .
G4350.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G2875 (κόπτω); to strike at, i.e. surge against (as water); specially, to stub on, i.e. trip up (literally or figuratively)

G5124.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5124.strongs: neuter singular nominative or accusative case of G3778 (οὗτος); that thing
G5124.kjv: here (-unto), it, partly, self(-same), so, that (intent), the same, there(-fore, -unto), this, thus, where(-fore)

G774.thayer: belonging to the Roman province Asia.
G774.mounce: belonging to the Roman province of Asia, Acts 20:4*
G774.abbottSmith: Asian , of Asia , Asiatic ; as subst., ὁ ( οἱ ) Ἀ. : Ac 20:4 .†
G774.strongs: from G773 (Ἀσία); an Asian (i.e. Asiatic) or an inhabitant of Asia

G4965.mounce: Sychar, indecl., a city of Samaria, Jn. 4:5*
G4965.abbottSmith: Sychar , a town of Samaria: Jo 4:5 .†
G4965.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07941); Sychar (i.e. Shekar), a place in Palestine

G525.thayer: I free (a person) from (anything), oftener in the middle voice: I am released from, am rid of (a person or thing), depart.
G525.mounce: to set free, deliver, set at liberty, Heb. 2:15; to rid judicially, Lk. 12:58; mid. to depart, remove, Acts 19:12*
G525.abbottSmith: to remove , release : He 2:15 ; pass., to depart : Ac 19:12 ; in legal sense (MM, VGT , s.v.), seq. ἀπό , c. gen. pers., to be quit of : Lk 12:58 (Cremer, 90, 632).†
G525.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G236 (ἀλλάσσω); to change away, i.e. release, (reflexively) remove

G1679.thayer: I hope, hope for, expect, trust.
G1679.mounce: to hope, expect, Lk. 23:8; 24:21; to repose hope and confidence in, trust, confide, Mt. 12:21; Jn. 5:45
G1679.abbottSmith: to look for, expect, hope ( for ): c. acc rei, Ro 8:24 , 25 , I Co 13:7 , He 11:1 ; c. dat. rei ( τ. τύχῃ , Thuc., iii, 97, 2), Mt 12:21 ; seq. καθώς , II Co 8:5 ; c. inf., Lk 6:34 23:8 , Ac 26:7 , Ro 15:24 , I Co 16:7 , II Co 5:11 , Phl 2:19 , 23 , I Ti 3:14 , II Jo 12 , III Jo 14 ; seq. ὅτι , c. pres., Lk 24:21 ; c. fut., Ac 24:26 , II Co 1:13 13:6 , Phm 22 . As in LXX (WM, § xxxiii, d; and esp.
G1679.strongs: from G1680 (ἐλπίς); to expect or confide

G10.thayer: Abiud, son of Zerubbabel and father of Eliakim.
G10.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H031); Abihud, an Israelite

G742.thayer: Arphaxad, son of Shem, and father of Cainan.
G742.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0775); Arphaxad, a post-diluvian patriarch

G2716.thayer: I effect by labor, achieve, work out, bring about.
G2716.mounce: to work out; to effect, produce, bring out as a result, Rom. 4:15; 5:3; 7:13; 2 Cor. 4:17; 7:10; Phil. 2:12; 1 Pet. 4:3; Jas. 1:3; to work, practice, realize in practice, Rom. 1:27; 2:9; to work or mould into fitness, 2 Cor. 5:5, despatch, Eph. 6:13
G2716.abbottSmith: to effect by labour , achieve , work out , bring about : c. acc., Ro 4:15 5:3 7:8 , 15 , 17 , 18 , 20 , II Co 7:10 , Eph 6:13 , Ja 1:3 ; pass., II Co 12:12 ; c. acc. rei et dat. pers., Ro 7:13 , II Co 4:17 7:11 9:11 ; id. seq. διά , c. gen. pers., Ro 15:18 ; τ. σωτηρίαν , Phl 2:12 ; c. acc. pers. seq. εἰς , II Co 5:5 (RV, wrought ); of evil deeds, Ro 1:27 2:9 , I Co 5:3 , I Pe 4:3 .†
G2716.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2038 (ἐργάζομαι); to work fully, i.e. accomplish; by implication, to finish, fashion

G409.abbottSmith: a man-slayer : I Ti 1:9 (cf. φονεύς , and v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G409.strongs: from G435 (ἀνήρ) and G5408 (φόνος); a murderer

G4860.thayer: I compute, reckon up, count together.
G4860.mounce: to calculate together, compute, reckon up, Acts 19:19*
G4860.abbottSmith: to reckon together, count up : Ac 19:19 .†
G4860.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5585 (ψηφίζω); to compute jointly

G1333.thayer: a period of two years, two years; according to ancient practice this means any period between one and two years.
G1333.mounce: the space of two years, Acts 24:27; 28:30*
G1333.abbottSmith: the space of two years : Ac 24:27 28:30 .†
G1333.strongs: from G1332 (διετής); a space of two years (biennium)

G0.mounce: employment of drugs for any purpose; sorcery, magic, enchantment, Rev. 18:23; Gal. 5:20*

G5410.mounce: a forum, marketplace; Φόρον Ἀππίου, Forum Appii, the name of a small town on the Appian way, according to Antoninus, forty-three Roman miles from Rome, or about forty English miles, Acts 28:15*
G5410.strongs: of Latin origin; a forum or market-place; only in comparative with G675 (Ἄππιος); a station on the Appian road

G1104.abbottSmith: sincerely, honourably : Phi 2:20 (for a parallel in π. , v. MM, s.v.).†
G1104.strongs: adverb from G1103 (γνήσιος); genuinely, i.e. really

G1431.mounce: a gift, free gift, benefit, Jn. 4:10; Acts 2:38
G1431.abbottSmith: a gift : Ac 11:17 , Ro 5:15 , He 6:4 ; δ. τ. θεοῦ , Jo 4:10 , Ac 8:20 ; τ. Χριστοῦ , Eph 4:7 ; τ. πνεύματος , Ac 2:38 10:45 ; τ. δικαιοσύνης , Ro 5:17 ; τ. χάριτος , Eph 3:7 ; ἀνεκδιηγήτὶῳ δ. , II Co 9:15 . Acc, δωρεάν , adverbially (as freq. in LXX),

G3809.thayer: discipline; training and education of children, hence: instruction; chastisement, correction.
G3809.mounce: education, training up, nurture of children, Eph. 6:4; instruction, discipline, 2 Tim. 3:16; in NT correction, chastisement, Heb. 12:5, 7, 8, 11*
G3809.strongs: from G3811 (παιδεύω); tutorage, i.e. education or training; by implication, disciplinary correction

G1834.thayer: I lead, show the way; met: I unfold, narrate, declare.
G1834.mounce: to be a leader; to detail, to set forth in language; to tell, narrate, recount, Lk. 24:35; Acts 10:8; to make known, reveal, Jn. 1:18; Acts 15:12, 14; 21:19*
G1834.abbottSmith: to lead, show the way ; metaph., to unfold, narrate, declare : c. acc rei, Lk 24:35 Ac 21:19 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 10:8 ; θεόν (understood), Jo 1:18 ; seq. ὅσα , Ac 15:12 ; καθώς , Ac 15:14 .†
G1834.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); to consider out (aloud), i.e. rehearse, unfold

G2833.thayer: I rub, tickle, scratch; pass: I itch.
G2833.mounce: to scratch; to tickle, cause titillation; in NT mid. met. to procure pleasurable excitement for, to indulge an itching, 2 Tim. 4:3*
G2833.strongs: from a primary (to scrape); to scratch, i.e. (by implication) to tickle

G1048.thayer: Gaza, an old town in the south of Palestine, on the sea-coast.
G1048.abbottSmith: Gaza , in OT, one of the five chief cities of the Philistines: Ac 8:26 .†
G1048.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05804); Gazah (i.e. Azzah), a place in Palestine

G941.thayer: (a) I carry, bear, (b) I carry (take) away.
G941.mounce: pr. to lift, raise, bear aloft; to bear, carry in the hands or about the person; carry as a message, Acts 9:15; to take away, remove, Mt. 8:17; Jn. 20:15; to take up, Jn. 10:31; Lk. 14:27; to bear as a burden endure, suffer; to sustain, Rom. 11:18; to bear with, tolerate; to sustain mentally, comprehend, Jn. 16:12
G941.abbottSmith: to take up with the hands, to lift : λίθους , Jo 10:31 .
G941.strongs: perhaps remotely derived from the base of G939 (βάσις) (through the idea of removal); to lift, literally or figuratively (endure, declare, sustain, receive, etc.)

G4627.mounce: pr. anything excavated or hollowed; a boat, skiff, Acts 27:16, 30, 32*
G4627.abbottSmith: anything scooped out , esp. a light boat, skiff : Ac 27:16 , 30 , 32 .†
G4627.strongs: a "skiff" (as if dug out), or yawl (carried aboard a large vessel for landing)

G4269.mounce: born earlier, elder; a progenitor, pl. progenitors; parents, 1 Tim. 5:4; forefathers, ancestors, 2 Tim. 1:3*

G5463.thayer: I rejoice, am glad; also a salutation: Hail.
G5463.mounce: to rejoice, be glad, be joyful, be full of joy, Mt. 2:10; 5:12; 18:13; Mk. 14:11; Rom. 12:12; 2 Cor. 2:3; a term of salutation, Hail! Mt. 26:49; λέγω χαίρειν, to greet, 2 Jn. 10:11; an epistolary forth, Health! Acts 15:23
G5463.abbottSmith: to rejoice, be glad : Mk 14:11 , Lk 15:5 , 32 19:6 , 37 22:5 23:8 , Jo 4:36 8:56 20:20 , Ac 5:41 8:39 11:23 13:48 , II Co 6:10 7:7 13:9 , Phl 2:17 , 28 , Col 2:5 , I Th 5:16 , I Pe 4:13 , III Jo 3 ; χ. καὶ ἀγαλλιᾶσθαι , Mt 5:12 ; κ. σκιρτᾶν , Lk 6:23 ; opp. to κλαίειν , Ro 12:15 , I Co 7:30 ; to κλαίειν κ. θρηνεῖν Jo 16:20 ; to λύπην ἔχειν , ib. 22 ; c. cogn. acc, χ. χαρὰν μεγάλην , Mt 2:10 ; χαρᾷ
G5463.strongs: a primary verb; to be "cheer"ful, i.e. calmly happy or well-off; impersonally, especially as salutation (on meeting or parting), be well
G5463.kjv: farewell, be glad, God speed, greeting, hall, joy(- fully), rejoice

G3012.mounce: a coarse cloth, with which servants were girded, a towel, napkin, apron, Jn. 13:4, 5*
G3012.strongs: of Latin origin; a "linen" cloth, i.e. apron

G1337.thayer: between two seas, which has sea on both sides.
G1337.mounce: surrounded on both sides by the sea; τόπος διθάλασσος, a shoal or sand-bank formed by the confluence of opposite currents, Acts 27:41*
G1337.abbottSmith: divided into two seas (as the Euxine, Strab., ii, 5 22 ).
G1337.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G2281 (θάλασσα); having two seas, i.e. a sound with a double outlet

G5080.thayer: I make liquid; pass: I melt (away).
G5080.mounce: to dissolve; pass. to melt, 2 Pet. 3:12*
G5080.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to liquefy

G5472.thayer: chalcedony, a small stone of various colors.
G5472.mounce: chalcedony, the name of a gem, generally of a whitish, bluish, or gray color, susceptible of a high and beautiful polish, and of which there are several varieties, as the onyx, modern carnelian, etc., Rev. 21:19*
G5472.abbottSmith: chalcedony , "supposed to denote a green silicate of copper found in the mines near Chalcedon " (Swete): Re 21:19 .†
G5472.strongs: from G5475 (χαλκός) and perhaps G1491 (εἶδος); copper-like, i.e. "chalcedony"

G2194.thayer: Zebulon, one of the sons of Jacob, and founder of one of the twelve tribes.
G2194.mounce: Zebulun, pr. name, indecl., an Israelite tribe, Mt. 4:13, 15; Rev. 7:8*
G2194.abbottSmith: Zebulun , Jacob's tenth son: the tribe of Z., Mt 4:13 , 15 , Re 7:8 .†
G2194.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02074); Zabulon (i.e. Zebulon), a region of Palestine

G5527.mounce: pasture, provender for cattle; food, provision, sustenance, for men, Acts 7:11*
G5527.abbottSmith: fodder (Polyb., Plut., al.): pl., Ac 7:11 (RV, sustenance ).†

G3123.mounce: more, to a greater extent, in a higher degree, Mt. 18:13; 27:24; Jn. 5:18; 1 Cor. 14:18; rather, in preference, Mt. 10:6; Eph. 4:28; used in a periphrasis for the comparative, Acts 20:35; as an intensive with a comparative term, Mt. 6:26; Mk. 7:36; 2 Cor. 7:13; Phil. 1:23; μᾶλλον δέ, yea rather, or, more properly speaking, Rom. 8:34; Gal. 4:9; Eph. 5:11
G3123.abbottSmith: Pos., very , very much , exceedingly (cl.; LXX ut supr.; in NT its place is taken by λίαν , σφόδρα , etc.).
G3123.strongs: neuter of the comparative of the same as G3122 (μάλιστα); (adverbially) more (in a greater degree)) or rather
G3123.kjv: + better, X far, (the) more (and more), (so) much (the more), rather

G4302.thayer: I tell (say) beforehand, forewarn, declare, tell plainly.
G4302.mounce: to tell beforehand, to foretell, Mt. 24:25; Acts 1:16; Rom. 9:29; 2 Cor. 13:2; Gal. 5:21; 1 Thess. 3:4
G4302.abbottSmith: to tell or say beforehand : II Co 13:2 , Ga 5:21 , I Th 3:4 (R, txt.; cf. προεῖπον , but v. infr.).
G4302.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3004 (λέγω); to say beforehand, i.e. predict, forewarn

G3132.thayer: I divine, practice soothsaying, suggesting the fraud involved in the practice.
G3132.mounce: to speak oracles, to divine, Acts 16:16*
G3132.strongs: from a derivative of G3105 (μαίνομαι) (meaning a prophet, as supposed to rave through inspiration); to divine, i.e. utter spells (under pretense of foretelling

G5006.mounce: of a talent weight, weighing a talent, Rev. 16:21*

G2764.thayer: of clay, made by a potter, earthen.
G2764.mounce: made by a potter, earthen, Rev. 2:27*
G2764.strongs: from G2766 (κέραμος); made of clay, i.e. earthen

G5028.mounce: a sepulchre, grave, tomb, Mt. 23:27, 29; 27:61, 64, 66; 28:1; met. Rom. 3:13*
G5028.strongs: masculine from G2290 (θάπτω); a grave (the place of interment)

G1344.thayer: I make righteous, defend the cause of, plead for the righteousness (innocence) of, acquit, justify; hence: I regard as righteous.
G1344.mounce: pr. to make or render right or just; mid. to act with justice, Rev. 22:11; to avouch to be good and true, to vindicate, Mt. 11:19; Lk. 7:29; to set forth as good and just, Lk. 10:29; 16:15; in NT to hold as guiltless, to accept as righteous, to justify, Rom. 3:26, 30; 4:5; 8:30, 33; pass. to be held acquitted, to be cleared, Acts 13:39; Rom. 3:24; 6:7; to be approved, to stand approved, to stand accepted, Rom. 2:13; 3:20, 28
G1344.strongs: from G1342 (δίκαιος); to render (i.e. show or regard as) just or innocent

G2500.thayer: (Hebrew), Joses (a) son of Eliezer, (b) son of Mary, half-brother of Jesus, (c) surnamed Barnabas (also called Joseph).
G2500.mounce: Joses, Joseph pr. name Mt. 27:56; Mk. 6:3; 15:40, 47*
G2500.abbottSmith: Joses ; brother of our Lord: Mk 6:3 , Mt 13:55 (Rec., v.s. Ἰωσήφ ). Son of Mary: Mt 27:56 ( -σήφ , WH, txt), Mk 15:40 , 47 . v.s. Βαρνάβας .†
G2500.strongs: perhaps for G2501 (Ἰωσήφ); Joses, the name of two Israelites

G1759.strongs: from a prolonged form of G1722 (ἐν); properly, within, i.e. (of place) here, hither

G3861.abbottSmith: contrary to received opinion , incredible , marvellous : pl., Lk 5:26 .†
G3861.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G1391 (δόξα) (in the sense of seeming); contrary to expectation, i.e. extraordinary ("paradox")

G2074.thayer: Hezron, son of Perez, father of Ram.
G2074.mounce: Hezron, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:3; Lk. 3:33*
G2074.abbottSmith: Esrom (AV), Hezron (RV), an ancestor of Jesus: Mt 1:3 , Lk 3:33 .†
G2074.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02696); Esrom (i.e. Chetsron), an Israelite

G916.thayer: I weight, load, burden, lit. and met.
G916.mounce: to be heavy upon, weigh down, burden, oppress, as sleep, Mt. 26:43; Mk. 14:40; Lk. 9:32; calamities, 2 Cor. 1:8; 5:4; or, trouble, care, expense, etc. 1 Tim. 5:16*
G916.abbottSmith: to depress , weigh down . In NT, in pass. only: Mt 26:43 , Lk 9:32 21:34 , II Co 1:8 5:4 , I Ti 5:16 .†

G5090.thayer: Timaeus, father of the blind beggar Bartimaeus.
G5090.strongs: probably of Chaldee origin (compare H02931); Timæus (i.e. Timay), an Israelite

G5333.abbottSmith: devoted to magical arts . As subst., ὁ φ. = φαρμακεύς , a magician, sorcerer : Re 21:8 22:15 .†

G2838.strongs: from G2837 (κοιμάω); sleeping, i.e. (by implication) repose

G4698.thayer: the inward parts; the heart, affections, seat of the feelings.
G4698.mounce: the chief intestines, viscera; the entrails, bowels, Acts 1:18; met. the heart, the affections of the heart, the tender affections, Lk. 1:78; 2 Cor. 6:12; 7:15; Phil. 1:8, 2:1; Col. 3:12; Phlm. 7, 20; 1 Jn. 3:17; meton. a cherished one, dear as one’s self, Phlm. 12*
G4698.abbottSmith: the inward parts (heart, liver, lungs, etc.; Lat. viscera ): Ac 1:18 . Metaph., of the seat of the feelings and of the feelings themselves (in Gk. poets, of anger, anxiety, etc.), the heart, affections (the characteristic LXX and NT reference of the word to feelings of kindness, benevolence and pity, is found in π. ; v. MM, xxiii; cf. Lft. on Phl 1:8 ): II Co 6:12 7:15 , Phm 7 , 12 , 20 , I Jo 3:1
G4698.strongs: probably strengthened from (the "spleen"); an intestine (plural); figuratively, pity or sympathy

G3715.abbottSmith: the most general ward for all kinds of desire , longing , appetite : of lust, Ro 1:27 .
G3715.strongs: from G3713 (ὀρέγομαι); excitement of the mind, i.e. longing after

G3680.abbottSmith: a reproach : Ro 15:3 , He 10:33 ; εἰς ὀ. ἐμπεσεῖν , I Ti 3:7 ; ὁ ὀ. τοῦ Χριστοῦ , He 11:26 13:13 .†

G2882.thayer: Corinth, in north-east Peloponnese, the capital of the Roman province Achaia.
G2882.mounce: Corinth, a celebrated city of Greece, Acts 18:1; 19:1; 1 Cor. 1:2; 2 Cor. 1:1, 23; 2 Tim. 4:20*
G2882.abbottSmith: Corinth : Ac 18:1 , 27 19:1 , I Co 1:2 , II Co 1:1 , 23 , II Ti 4:20 .†
G2882.strongs: of uncertain derivation; Corinthus, a city of Greece

G2402.strongs: a strengthened form of a primary idos (sweat); perspiration

G5401.thayer: (a) fear, terror, alarm, (b) the object or cause of fear, (c) reverence, respect.
G5401.mounce: fear, terror, affright, Mt. 14:26; Lk. 1:12; astonishment, amazement, Mt. 28:8; Mk. 4:41; trembling concern, 1 Cor. 2:3; 2 Cor. 7:15; meton. a terror, an object or cause of terror, Rom. 13:5; reverential fear, awe, Acts 9:31; Rom. 3:18; respect, deference, Rom. 13:7; 1 Pet. 2:18
G5401.strongs: from a primary (to be put in fear); alarm or fright

G1818.mounce: pr. to deceive thoroughly; to deceive, delude, Rom. 7:11; 16:18; 1 2 Cor. 3:18; 11:3; 2 Thess. 2:3; 1 Tim. 2:14*
G1818.abbottSmith: to deceive : c. acc, Ro 7:11 Ro 16:18 , I Co 3:18 , II Co 11:3 , II Th 2:3 ; pass., I Ti 2:14 .†
G1818.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G538 (ἀπατάω); to seduce wholly

G2257.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G2257.strongs: genitive case plural of G1473 (ἐγώ); of (or from) us

G4886.thayer: that which binds together; a band, bond.
G4886.mounce: that which binds together, Col. 2:19; a band of union, Eph. 4:3; Col. 3:14; a bundle, or, bond, Acts 8:23*
G4886.abbottSmith: that which binds together, a bond : of the ligaments of the body, Col 2:19 . Metaph., σ. ἀδικίας , Ac 8:23 ; τ. τελειότητος , Col 3:14 ; τ. εἰρήνης , Eph 4:3 (v. Lft., Col., ll. c.).†
G4886.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1199 (δεσμόν); a joint tie, i.e. ligament, (figuratively) uniting principle, control

G4914.mounce: use, custom; an established custom, practice, Jn. 18:39; 1 Cor. 8:7; 11:16*
G4914.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G2239 (ἦθος); mutual habituation, i.e. usage

G2311.thayer: I found, lay the foundation (lit. and met.).
G2311.mounce: to found, lay the foundation of, Mt. 7:25; Heb. 1:10; met. to ground, establish, render firm and unwavering, Eph. 3:17; Col. 1:23; 1 Pet. 5:10*
G2311.abbottSmith: to lay the foundation of , to found : c. acc., τ. γῆν , He 1:10 (LXX) ; pass., Mt 7:25 , Lk 6:48 ; metaph., I Pe 5:10 (R, mg., settle ); pass., Eph 3:18 , Col 1:23 .†
G2311.strongs: from G2310 (θεμέλιος); to lay a basis for, i.e. (literally) erect, or (figuratively) consolidate

G5.abbottSmith: used in the phrase Ἀ. ὁ πατήρ , Abba, Father (v. Swete on Mk 14:36 ): Mk 14:36 , Ro 8:15 , Ga 4:6 .†
G5.strongs: of Chaldee origin (H02); father as a vocative

G2646.mounce: lodging, inn, Lk. 2:7; a guest-chamber, Mk. 14:14; Lk. 22:11*
G2646.abbottSmith: = cl. καταγώγιον (cf. πανδοχεῖον ), an inn , lodgingplace : Lk 2:7 (so Ex 4:24 , and MGr.).
G2646.strongs: from G2647 (καταλύω); properly, a dissolution (breaking up of a journey), i.e. (by implication) a lodging-place

G2836.thayer: belly, abdomen, heart, a general term covering any organ in the abdomen, e.g. stomach, womb; met: the inner man.
G2836.mounce: a cavity; the belly, Mt. 15:17; Mk. 7:19; the stomach, Mt. 12:40; Lk. 15:16; the womb, Mt. 19:12; Lk. 1:15; from the Hebrew, the inner self, Jn. 7:38
G2836.abbottSmith: the belly (stomach or intestines or both): Mt 12:40 15:17 , Mk 7:19 , Lk 15:16 , Ro 16:18 , I Co 6:13 , Phl 3:19 , Re 10:9 , 10 .
G2836.strongs: from ("hollow"); a cavity, i.e. (especially) the abdomen; by implication, the matrix; figuratively, the heart

G1908.thayer: I insult, treat wrongfully, molest, revile.
G1908.mounce: to harass, insult, Lk. 6:28; to mistreat, abuse, 1 Pet. 3:16*
G1908.abbottSmith: to revile : c. acc pers., Lk 6:28 ; c. acc rei (but v. ICC , in l.), I Pe 3:16 .†
G1908.strongs: from a comparative of G1909 (ἐπί) and (probably) (threats); to insult, slander

G5568.thayer: a psalm, song of praise, the Hebrew book of Psalms.
G5568.mounce: impulse, touch of the chords of a stringed instrument; in NT a sacred song, psalm, Lk. 20:42; 24:44; Acts 1:20; 13:33; 1 Cor. 14:26; Eph. 5:19; Col. 3:16*
G5568.abbottSmith: a striking, twitching with the fingers (Eur., al.), hence, a striking of musical strings (Æsch., al.), and hence in later writers,
G5568.strongs: from G5567 (ψάλλω); a set piece of music, i.e. a sacred ode (accompanied with the voice, harp or other instrument; a "psalm"); collectively, the book of the Psalms

G2783.mounce: a herald, public messenger; in NT a proclaimer, publisher, preacher, 1 Tim. 2:7; 2 Tim. 1:11; 2 Pet. 2:5*
G2783.abbottSmith: a herald : I Ti 2:7 , II Ti 1:11 , II Pe 2:5 .†
G2783.strongs: from G2784 (κηρύσσω); a herald, i.e. of divine truth (especially of the gospel)

G1880.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G424 (ἀνέρχομαι); to come up on, i.e. return

G4188.strongs: from the alternate of G4095 (πίνω); a beverage

G2045.abbottSmith: to search, examine : Jo 7:52 ; c. acc rei, Jo 5:39 , Ro 8:27 , I Co 2:10 , Re 2:23 ; seq. orat. obliq., I Pe 1:11 .†
G2045.strongs: apparently from G2046 (ἐρέω) (through the idea of inquiry); to seek, i.e. (figuratively) to investigate

G4636.thayer: a tent, tabernacle; fig: of the human body.
G4636.mounce: a tent, tabernacle, lodging; met. the corporeal tabernacle, 2 Cor. 5:1, 4*
G4636.strongs: from G4633 (σκηνή); a hut or temporary residence, i.e. (figuratively) the human body (as the abode of the spirit)

G699.mounce: a pleasing, desire of pleasing, Col. 1:10*
G699.abbottSmith: pleasing , desire to please : Col 1:10 . In Gk. writers (Arist., Polyb.), most freq. in bad sense, but in π. , Inscr., and in Philo, as above (v. Deiss., BS , 224; MM, s.v.; Cremer, 642).†
G699.strongs: from a derivative of G700 (ἀρέσκω); complaisance

G2627.abbottSmith: a flood , deluge : Mt 24:38 , 39 , Lk 17:27 , II Pe 2:5 .†

G2673.thayer: (a) I make idle (inactive), make of no effect, annul, abolish, bring to naught, (b) I discharge, sever, separate from.
G2673.mounce: to render useless or unproductive, occupy unprofitable, Lk. 13:7; to render powerless, Rom. 6:6; to make empty and unmeaning, Rom. 4:14; to render null, to abrogate, cancel, Rom. 3:3, 31; Eph. 2:15; to bring to an end, 1 Cor. 2:6; 13:8; 15:24, 26; 2 Cor. 3:7; to destroy, annihilate, 2 Thess. 2:8; Heb. 2:14; to free from, dissever from, Rom. 7:2, 6; Gal. 5:4
G2673.abbottSmith: to make idle or inactive ( χέρας , Eur., Phœn. , 753): of soil occupied by an unfruitful tree, Lk 13:7 . Metaph. (Inscr.), to render inoperative or invalid , to abrogate, abolish : Ro 3:3 , 31 , I Co 1:28 6:13 13:11 15:24 , Ga 3:17 , Eph 2:15 , II Th 2:8 , II Ti 1:10 , He 2:14 ; pass., Ro 4:14 6:6 , I Co 2:6 13:8 , 10 15:26 , II Co 3:7 , 11 , 13 , 14 , Ga 5:11 ; seq. ἀπό (of persons), to be separa
G2673.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G691 (ἀργέω); to be (render) entirely idle (useless), literally or figuratively
G2673.kjv: abolish, cease, cumber, deliver, destroy, do away, become (make) of no (none, without) effect, fail, loose, bring (come) to nought, put away (down), vanish away, make void

G2576.mounce: to shut, close the eyes, Mt. 13:15; Acts 28:27*
G2576.abbottSmith: to shut the eyes : τ. ὀφθαλμούς , Mt 13:15 , Ac 28:27 ( Is 6:10 ).†
G2576.strongs: from a compound of G2596 (κατά) and the base of G3466 (μυστήριον); to shut down, i.e. close the eyes

G4420.abbottSmith: a wing : of birds, Mt 23:37 , Lk 13:34 , Re 12:14 ; of creatures seen in a vision, Re 4:8 9:9 .†
G4420.strongs: from a derivative of G4072 (πέτομαι) (meaning a feather); a wing

G2290.strongs: a primary verb; to celebrate funeral rites, i.e. inter

G772.thayer: (lit: not strong), (a) weak (physically, or morally), (b) infirm, sick.
G772.mounce: without strength, weak, infirm, Mt. 26:41; Mk. 14:38; 1 Pet. 3:7; helpless, Rom. 5:6; imperfect, inefficient, Gal. 4:9; feeble, without energy, 2 Cor. 10:10; infirm in body, sick, sickly, Mt. 25:39, 43, 44; weak, mentally or spiritually, dubious, hesitating, 1 Cor. 8:7, 10; 9:22; 1 Thess. 5:14; afflicted, distressed, oppressed with calamities, 1 Cor. 4:10
G772.abbottSmith: without strength , weak , feeble : I Co 1:27 4:10 12:22 , II Co 10:10 , Ga 4:9 , I Th 5:14 , He 7:18 , I Pe 3:7 . Rhetorically, τό ἀ. τ. θεοῦ , God's action of apparent weakness: I Co 1:25 ; of bodily debility, sick , sickly : Mt 25:39 (Rec.) Mt 25:43 , 44 , Lk 9:2 (Rec.) 10:9 , Ac 4:9 5:15 , 16 In moral and spiritual sense (MM, s.v.; Cremer, 526), Mt 26:41 , Mk 14:38 , Ro 5:6 , I Co 8:7 , 8:9 , 1
G772.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G4599 (σθενόω); strengthless (in various applications, literal, figurative and moral)
G772.kjv: more feeble, impotent, sick, without strength, weak(-er, -ness, thing)

G5210.mounce: you, gen., σοῦ, dat., σοί, acc., σέ, Mt. 1:20; 2:6
G5210.strongs: irregular plural of G4771 (σύ); you (as subjective of verb)

G3837.mounce: in all places, everywhere, Mk. 16:20; Lk. 9:6
G3837.strongs: genitive case (as adverb of place) of a presumed derivative of G3956 (πᾶς); universally

G1029.thayer: a noose or snare; a cord.
G1029.abbottSmith: a noose, a slip-knot, a halter : metaph., a restraint (not, as AV, R, txt., a snare ): I Co 7:35 .†

G1277.mounce: to sail through or over, Acts 27:5*
G1277.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4126 (πλέω); to sail through

G5302.thayer: I fall behind, am lacking, fall short, suffer need, am inferior to.
G5302.mounce: to be behind in place or time, to be in the rear; to fall short of, be inferior to, 2 Cor. 11:5; 12:11; to fail of, fail to attain, Heb. 4:1; to be in want of, lack, Lk. 22:35; to be wanting, Mk. 10:21; absol. to be defective, in default, Mt. 19:20; 1 Cor. 12:24; to run short, Jn. 2:3; mid. to come short of a privilege or standard, to miss, Rom. 3:23; absol. to come short, be below standard, 1 Cor. 1:7; to come short of sufficiency, to be in need, want, Lk. 15:14; 2 Cor. 11:9; Phil. 4:12; Heb. 11:37; to be a loser, suffer detriment, 1 Cor. 8:8; in NT ὑστερεῖν ἀπό, to be backwards with respect to, to slight, Heb. 12:15*
G5302.abbottSmith: to come late, be behind (opp. to προτερέω, φθάνω ; c. gen. rei, for ; c. gen. pers., later than ).
G5302.strongs: from G5306 (ὕστερος); to be later, i.e. (by implication) to be inferior; generally, to fall short (be deficient)
G5302.kjv: come behind (short), be destitute, fail, lack, suffer need, (be in) want, be the worse

G5408.mounce: a killing, slaughter, murder, Mt. 15:19; Mk. 7:21; 15:7
G5408.abbottSmith: murder, slaughter : Mk 15:7 , Lk 23:19 , 25 , Ac 9:1 , Ro 1:29 ; φ. μαχαίρης (cf. Ex 17:13 , al.), He 11:37 ; pl., Mt 15:19 , Mk 7:21 , Re 9:21 .†
G5408.strongs: from an obsolete primary (to slay); murder

G3746.strongs: from G3739 (ὅς) and G4007 (περ); who especially

G1414.thayer: I am powerful, have power, am able, am mighty.
G1414.mounce: to be powerful, mighty, to show one’s self powerful, 2 Cor. 9:8; 13:3; Rom. 14:4*
G1414.abbottSmith: to be able, be powerful, mighty : c. inf., Ro 14:4 , II Co 9:8 ; absol., opp. to ἀσθενῶ II Co 13:3 .†

G718.thayer: I fit, join; mid: (the middle indicating deep personal interest) I espouse, betroth; mid: I take a wife, give in marriage.
G718.mounce: to fit together; to join, unite, in marriage, espouse, betroth, 2 Cor. 11:2*
G718.strongs: from G719 (ἁρμός); to joint, i.e. (figuratively) to woo (reflexively, to betroth)

G3906.mounce: to watch narrowly, Acts 9:24; to observe or watch insidiously, Mk. 3:2; Lk. 6:7; 14:1; 20:20; to observe scrupulously, Gal. 4:10*
G3906.abbottSmith: to watch closely , observe narrowly : so mid., τ. πύλας , Ac 9:24 ; with evil intent, Mk 3:2 , Lk 20:20 (absol., v. Field, Notes , 74); so mid., Lk 6:7 14:1 .
G3906.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G5083 (τηρέω); to inspect alongside, i.e. note insidiously or scrupulously

G1910.thayer: (a) I set foot on, step on, (b) I mount (a horse), board (a vessel).
G1910.mounce: pr. to step upon; to mount, Mt. 21:5; to go on board, Acts 21:2; 27:2, to enter, Acts 20:18; to enter upon, Acts 21:4; 25:1*
G1910.abbottSmith: to get up on, mount : seq. ἐπί , c. acc, Mt 21:5 (LXX) ; to embark in (a boat), go aboard : c. dat., Ac 27:2 ; seq. εἰς , Ac 21:6 (Rec.); absol., Ac 21:2 .
G1910.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to walk upon, i.e. mount, ascend, embark, arrive
G1910.kjv: come (into), enter into, go abroad, sit upon, take ship

G2017.thayer: I shine upon, give light to.
G2017.mounce: to shine upon, give light to, enlighten, Eph. 5:14*
G2017.abbottSmith: to shine forth : fig., c. dat., Eph 5:14 (on v.l. -ψαύσει , v. ICC , Westc., AR, in l.).†
G2017.strongs: a form of G2014 (ἐπιφαίνω); to illuminate (figuratively)

G4591.thayer: I signify, indicate, give a sign, make known.
G4591.mounce: to indicate by a sign, to signal; to indicate, intimate, Jn. 12:33; 18:32; 21:19; to make known, communicate, Acts 11:28; Rev. 1:1; to specify, Acts 25:27*
G4591.abbottSmith: to give a sign, signify, indicate : c. acc. rei, Ac 25:27 , Re 1:1 (cf. MM, xxii); c. acc. et inf., Ac 11:28 ; seq. quæst. indir., Jo 12:33 18:32 21:19 .†
G4591.strongs: from (a mark; of uncertain derivation); to indicate

G4795.mounce: concurrence, coincidence, chance, accident; κατὰ συγκρυίαν, by chance, accidentally, Lk. 10:31*
G4795.abbottSmith: chance, coincidence : κατὰ σ. (v. MM, xxiii), Lk 10:31 (Hippocr., Eccl.).†
G4795.strongs: from a comparative of G4862 (σύν) and (to light or happen; from the base of G2962 (κύριος)); concurrence, i.e. accident

G1599.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3992 (πέμπω); to despatch

G1165.thayer: I hold up as an example, make a show of, expose.
G1165.mounce: to make a public show or spectacle of, Mt. 1:19; Col. 2:15*
G1165.abbottSmith: a rare word ( ICC , ll. c.), to make a show of, to expose : Mt 1:19 (Rec. παραδ- , q.v.), Col 2:15 .†

G4582.strongs: from (brilliancy; probably akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), through the idea of attractiveness); the moon

G2925.thayer: I knock, beat a door with a stick, to gain admittance.
G2925.abbottSmith: to strike , knock : at a door ( κόπτειν , in Att.), Mt 7:7 , 8 , Lk 11:9 , 10 12:36 , Ac 12:16 , Re 3:20 ; c. acc., τ. θύραν , Lk 13:25 , Ac 12:13 .†
G2925.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to rap

G145.mounce: an organ of perception; internal sense, Heb. 5:14*
G145.abbottSmith: sense , organ of perception : He 5:14 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G145.strongs: from a derivative of G143 (αἰσθάνομαι); properly, an organ of perception, i.e. (figuratively) judgment

G4123.thayer: a covetous or avaricious person; one desirous of having more.
G4123.mounce: one who has or claims to have more than his share; a covetous, avaricious person, one who defrauds for the sake of gain, 1 Cor. 5:10, 11; 6:10; Eph. 5:5*
G4123.abbottSmith: one desirous of having more , covetous : I Co 5:10 , 11 6:10 , Eph 5:5 .†
G4123.strongs: from G4119 (πλείων) and G2192 (ἔχω); holding (desiring) more, i.e. eager for gain (avaricious, hence a defrauder)

G1281.mounce: to despatch a matter thoroughly; to make profit in business, gain in trade, Lk. 19:15*
G1281.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4231 (πραγματεύομαι); to thoroughly occupy oneself, i.e. (transitively and by implication) to earn in business

G3797.mounce: late; latter, Jas. 5:7; poetic and later prose for ὄψιος*
G3797.abbottSmith: late : ὑετὸς ὄ. , the latter rain (v. DB , s.v. "rain"), with ellipsis of ὑετός (v. WM, 740), Ja 5:7 .†

G3798.mounce: evening, either before or after sundown
G3798.abbottSmith: late : ἡ ὥρα , Mk 11:11 (Rec., WH, mg.). In late writers, ἡ ὀψία (sc. ὥρα ), as subst., evening [in LXX: Jth 13:1 *]: Mt 8:16 14:15 , 23 16:2 20:8 26:20 27:57 , Mk 1:32 4:35 6:47 14:17 15:42 , Jo 6:16 , 20:19 .†
G3798.strongs: from G3796 (ὀψέ); late; feminine (as noun) afternoon (early eve) or nightfall (later eve)

G2463.abbottSmith: Iris , the messenger of the gods.
G2463.strongs: perhaps from G2046 (ἐρέω) (as a symbol of the female messenger of the pagan deities); a rainbow ("iris")

G93.strongs: from G94 (ἄδικος); (legal) injustice (properly, the quality, by implication, the act); morally, wrongfulness (of character, life or act)

G2406.thayer: the act or office of priesthood.
G2406.mounce: a priesthood; meton. a body of priests, 1 Pet. 2:5, 9*
G2406.abbottSmith: a priesthood , body of priests : ἱ ἅγιον , I Pe 2:5 ; βασ. ἱ , I Pe 2:9 (LXX, Ex 19:6 ).†
G2406.strongs: from G2407 (ἱερατεύω); the priestly fraternity, i.e. sacerdotal order (figuratively)

G5392.mounce: to muzzle, 1 Tim. 5:18; met. and by impl. to silence, put to silence; pass. to be silent, speechless, Mt. 22:12, 34; 1 Pet. 2:15; Mk. 1:25; trop. pass. to be hushed, as winds and waves, Mk. 4:39; Lk. 4:35*
G5392.abbottSmith: to muzzle : I Co 9:9 , I Ti 5:18 (LXX) ; metaph., to put to silence : c. acc. pers., Mt 22:34 , I Pe 2:15 ; pass., to be silenced, silent : Mt 22:12 , Mk 1:25 4:39 , Lk 4:35 .†

G4773.thayer: akin to, related; subst: fellow countryman, kinsman.
G4773.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1085 (γένος); a relative (by blood); by extension, a fellow countryman

G3741.thayer: holy, pious, godly, beloved of God.
G3741.mounce: pr. sanctioned by the supreme law of God, and nature; pious, devout, Tit. 1:8; pure, 1 Tim. 2:8; supremely holy, Acts 2:27; 13:35; Heb. 7:26; Rev. 15:4; 16:5; τὰ ὅσια, pledged bounties, mercies, Acts 13:34*
G3741.abbottSmith: religiously right , righteous , pious , Holy : of men, Tit 1:8 , He 7:26 ; by meton., ὁ. χεῖρας , I Ti 2:8 (cf. De 32:4 ); of God, Re 15:4 16:5 ; as subst., ὁ ὅ. , of the Messiah, Ac 2:27 13:35 (LXX); τὰ ὅ. Δανεὶδ τ. πιστά (Field, Notes , 121), Ac 13:34 (LXX) .†
G3741.strongs: of uncertain affinity; properly, right (by intrinsic or divine character; thus distinguished from 1342, which refers rather to human statutes and relations; from G2413 (ἱερός), which denotes formal consecration; and from G40 (ἅγιος), which relates to purity from defilement), i.e. hallowed (pious, sacred, sure)

G955.thayer: Belial, a demon, and in fact a name for Satan.
G955.mounce: pr. the point of a spear; a needle, Lk. 18:25*
G955.abbottSmith: Beliar , a name of Satan: II Co 6:15 (v. DB , i, 269).†
G955.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01100); worthlessness; Belial, as an epithet of Satan

G3493.thayer: Nahor, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G3493.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05152); Nachor, the grandfather of Abraham

G3018.thayer: (Hebrew), Levi, son of Alphaeus, the publican.
G3018.abbottSmith: Levi ; the son of Jacob: He 7:5 , 9 , Re 7:7 . Son of Melchi: Lk 3:24 . Son of Simeon: Lk 3:29 . Son of Alphæus (cf. Μαθθαῖος ): Mk 2:14 (WH, mg., Ἰάκωβον ), Lk 5:27 , 29 .†
G3018.strongs: a form of G3017 (Λευΐ); Lewis (i.e. Levi), a Christian

G4124.thayer: covetousness, avarice, aggression, desire for advantage.
G4124.mounce: some advantage which one possesses over another; an inordinate desire of riches, covetousness, Lk. 12:15; grasping, overreaching, extortion, Rom. 1:29; 1 Thess. 2:5; a gift exacted by importunity and conferred with grudging, a hard-wrung gift, 2 Cor. 9:5; a scheme of extortion, Mk. 7:22
G4124.abbottSmith: the character and conduct of a πλεονέκτης advantage. desire for advantage, grasping, aggression, cupidity, covetousness: Lk 12:15 , Ro 1:29 , II Co 9:5 , Eph 4:19 (v. ICC , in l) Eph 5:3 , Col 3:5 , I Th 2:5 , II Pe 2:3 2:14 ; pl. (v. Bl., § 32, 6), Mk 7:22 .†
G4124.strongs: from G4123 (πλεονέκτης); avarice, i.e. (by implication) fraudulency, extortion

G2904.thayer: dominion, strength, power; a mighty deed.
G2904.mounce: strength, power, might, force, Acts 19:20; Eph. 1:19; meton. a display of might, Lk. 1:51; power, sway, dominion, Heb. 2:14; 1 Pet. 4:11; 5:11
G2904.abbottSmith: strength , esp. as in Hom., of bodily strength.
G2904.strongs: perhaps a primary word; vigor ("great") (literally or figuratively)

G734.strongs: contracted from a compound of G735 (Ἄρτεμις) and G1435 (δῶρον); gift of Artemis; Artemas (or Artemidorus), a Christian

G241.mounce: of another race or nation, i.e., not a Jew; a stranger, foreigner, Lk. 17:18*
G241.abbottSmith: of another race , a foreigner ( = ἀλλόφυλος ; Cremer, 150; MM, VGT , s.v.): Lk 17:18 .†
G241.strongs: from G243 (ἄλλος) and G1085 (γένος); foreign, i.e. not a Jew

G2490.mounce: also spelled Ἰωάνα, Joanna, pr. name, Lk. 8:3; 24:10*
G2490.strongs: a form of G2491 (Ἰωάννης); Joannas, an Israelite

G487.abbottSmith: a ransom : I Ti 2:6 (v. CGT , in l.; and cf. λύτρον ).†
G487.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G3083 (λύτρον); a redemption-price

G913.thayer: Barak, one of the judges of Israel.
G913.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01301); Barak, an Israelite

G2043.thayer: I stick fast, prop, fix firmly.
G2043.mounce: to make to lean upon; to fix firmly; intrans. to become firmly fixed, stick fast, Acts 27:41*
G2043.abbottSmith: to prop, fix firmly : act., as mid., ἐρείσασα , of a ship driving ashore (RV, struck ), Ac 27:41 .†
G2043.strongs: of obscure affinity; to prop, i.e. (reflexively) get fast

G3506.thayer: I nod, make a sign, beckon.
G3506.mounce: to nod; to intimate by a nod or significant gesture, Jn. 13:24; Acts 24:10*
G3506.abbottSmith: to nod or beckon , as a sign : c. dat. pers. et inf., Jo 13:24 , Ac 24:10 (cf. δια- , ἐκ- , ἐν- , ἐπι- , κατα-νεύω ).†
G3506.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to "nod", i.e. (by analogy), signal

G2203.thayer: Zeus, the Greek god of the sky in all its manifestations, corresponding to the Roman Jupiter and to the leading god of the native Lycaonians.
G2203.mounce: the supreme god of the Greeks answering to the Jupiter of the Romans, Acts 14:12, 13*
G2203.abbottSmith: gen., Διός , dat., Διΐ , acc., Δία ( Δίαν , D, al.),
G2203.strongs: of uncertain affinity; in the oblique cases there is used instead of it a (probably cognate) name , which is otherwise obsolete; Zeus or Dis (among the Latins, Jupiter or Jove), the supreme deity of the Greeks

G1584.mounce: to shine out or forth, Mt. 13:43*
G1584.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2989 (λάμπω); to be resplendent

G2903.thayer: most excellent, an official epithet, used in addressing a Roman of high rank, and in the second century one of equestrian (as distinguished from senatorial) rank.
G2903.mounce: strongest; in NT κράτιστε, a term of respect, most excellent, noble, or illustrious, Lk. 1:3; Acts 23:26; 24:3; 26:25*
G2903.strongs: superlative of a derivative of G2904 (κράτος); strongest, i.e. (in dignity) very honorable

G298.mounce: amomum, an odoriferous shrub, from which a precious ointment was prepared, Rev. 18:13*
G298.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3469 (μωμάομαι); unblamable

G2059.thayer: (a) I translate, explain, (b) I interpret the meaning of.
G2059.mounce: to explain, interpret, translate, Jn. 1:42; 9:7; Heb. 7:2*
G2059.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G2060 (Ἑρμῆς) (as the god of language); to translate

G2364.thayer: a daughter; hence (Hebraistic?), of any female descendent, however far removed; even of one unrelated: my young lady.
G2364.mounce: a daughter, Mt. 9:18; 10:35, 37; in the vocative, an expression of affection and kindness, Mt. 9:22; from the Hebrew, one of the female posterity of any one, Lk. 1:5; met. a city, Mt. 21:5; Jn. 12:15; pl. female inhabitants, Lk. 23:28
G2364.abbottSmith: a daughter : Mt 9:18 10:35 , 37 14:6 15:22 , 28 , Mk 5:35 6:22 7:26 , 29 , Lk 2:36 8:42 , 49 12:53 , Ac 2:17 (LXX) 7:21 21:9 , He 11:24 . In NT, as in OT, not in cl.; as a form of friendly address (cf. Ru 2:2 , 22 al.): Mt 9:22 , Mk 5:34 , Lk 8:48 ; metaph.: sc. κυρίου , II Co 6:18 (cf. Is 43:6 ); of posterity: θ. Ἀαρών , Lk 1:5 ; Ἀβραάμ , Lk 13:16 (cf. Is 16:2 , IV Mac 15:28 , al); of habitation:
G2364.strongs: apparently a primary word (compare "daughter"); a female child, or (by Hebraism) descendant (or inhabitant)

G2443.mounce: that, in order that, Mt. 19:13; Mk. 1:38; Jn. 1:22; 3:15; 17:1; ἵνα μή, that not, lest, Mt. 7:1; in NT equivalent to ὥστε, so that, so as that, Jn. 9:2; also, marking a simple circumstance, the circumstance that, Mt. 10:25; Jn. 4:34; 6:29; 1 Jn. 4:17; 5:3
G2443.abbottSmith: adverb (poët., Hom., al.), of place, where , whither . of circumstance, when . Conjunction , prop., final, denoting purpose or end (cl.), that , in order that , usually the first word in the clause, but sometimes (cl. also) preceded by an emphatic word ( Ac 19:4 , Ro 11:31 (?), Ga 2:10 , al.); c. optat. (so in cl. after historic tenses): after a pres., Eph 1:17 (but WH, mg., subjc.; v. Burton, § 2
G2443.strongs: probably from the same as the former part of G1438 (ἑαυτοῦ) (through the demonstrative idea; compare G3588 (ὁ)); in order that (denoting the purpose or the result)
G2443.kjv: albeit, because, to the intent (that), lest, so as, (so) that, (for) to

G1525.thayer: I go in, come in, enter.
G1525.mounce: to go or come in, enter, Mt. 7:13; 8:5, 8; spc. to enter by force, break in, Mk. 3:27; Acts 20:29; met. with εἰς κόσμον, to begin to exist, come into existence, Rom. 5:12; 2 Jn. 7; or, to make one’s appearance on earth, Heb. 10:5; to enter into or take possession of, Lk. 22:3; Jn. 13:27; to enter into, enjoy, partake of, Mt. 19:23, 24; to enter into any one’s labor, be his successor, Jn. 4:38; to fall into, be placed in certain circumstances, Mt. 26:41; to be put into, Mt. 15:11; Acts 11:8; to present one’s self before, Acts 19:30; met. to arise, spring up, Lk. 9:46; from the Hebrew, εἰσέρχεσθαι καὶ ἐξέρχεσθαι, to go in and out, to live, discharge the ordinary functions of life, Acts 1:21
G1525.abbottSmith: to go in or into, enter : Mt 9:25 , Lk 7:45 , al.; seq. εἰς , Mt 10:12 , Mk 2:1 , al.; seq. διά ( πύλης, θύρας , etc.), Mt 7:13 , Jo 10:1 , al.; ὑπὸ τ. στέγην , Mt 8:8 ; c. adv.: ὅπου , Mk 14:14 , He 6:20 ; ὧδε , Mt 22:12 ; ἔσω , Mt 26:58 ; seq. πρός , c acc. pers., Mk 15:43 , Lk 1:28 , Ac 10:3 11:3 16:40 17:2 28:8 , Re 3:20 ; of demons taking possession, Mk 9:25 , Lk 8:30 22:3 , Jo 13:27 ; of foo
G1525.strongs: from G1519 (εἰς) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to enter (literally or figuratively)
G1525.kjv: X arise, come (in, into), enter in(-to), go in (through)

G455.mounce: trans. to open, Mt. 2:11; intrans. to be opened, to be open, Mt. 3:16; Jn. 1:52
G455.abbottSmith: to open ; trans., c. acc.; a door or gate, Ac 5:19 12:14 , Re 4:1 ; pass., Ac 12:10 16:26 , 27 ; metaph. of opportunity or welcome, Ac 14:27 , Col 4:3 , Re 3:20 ; pass., I Co 16:9 , II Co 2:12 , Re 3:8 ; absol. (sc. θύραν ), Ac 5:23 12:16 ; c. dat. pers., Lk 12:36 , Jo 10:3 ; metaph., Mt 7:7 , 8 25:11 , Lk 11:9 , 10 13:25 , Re 3:7 ; θησαυρούς ( Si 43:14 ), Mt 2:11 ; τ. μνημεῖα , Mt 27:52 ; τάφος ,
G455.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and (to open); to open up (literally or figuratively, in various applications)

G631.thayer: I wipe off, wipe clean; mid: I wipe myself off.
G631.mounce: to wipe off; mid. to wipe off one’s self, Lk. 10:11*
G631.abbottSmith: to wipe off , wipe clean : mid., Lk 10:11 .†
G631.strongs: middle voice from G575 (ἀπό) and (to squeeze, knead, smear); to scrape away

G3513.thayer: of affirmative swearing: by, with acc. of person or thing sworn by.
G3513.mounce: by, BDAG 690, calls it a “particle of strong affirmation,” and is followed by the person or thing (in the acc) by which the person swears, 1 Cor. 15:31*
G3513.strongs: probably an intensive form of G3483 (ναί); a particle of attestation (accompanied by the object invoked or appealed to in confirmation); as sure as

G4550.mounce: pr. rotten, putrid; hence, bad, of a bad quality, Mt. 7:17, 18; 12:33; Lk. 6:43; refuse, Mt. 13:48; met. corrupt, depraved, vicious, foul, impure, Eph. 4:29*
G4550.abbottSmith: rotten, corrupt, bad, worth­less : of trees and fruit (opp. to ἀγαθός, καλός ), Mt 7:17, 18 12:33 Lk 6:43 ; of fish, Mt 13:48 .
G4550.strongs: from G4595 (σήπω); rotten, i.e. worthless (literally or morally)

G4574.thayer: an object of worship or veneration.
G4574.mounce: an object of religious veneration and worship, Acts 17:23; 2 Thess. 2:4*
G4574.abbottSmith: an object of worship : Ac 17:23 , II Th 2:4 .†
G4574.strongs: from G4573 (σεβάζομαι); something adored, i.e. an object of worship (god, altar, etc)

G569.thayer: I am unfaithful, disbelieve, refuse belief, prove false.
G569.mounce: to refuse belief, be incredulous, disbelieve, Mk. 16:11, 16; Lk. 24:11, 41; Acts 28:24; to prove false, violate one’s faith, be unfaithful, Rom. 3:3; 2 Tim. 2:13
G569.abbottSmith: to disbelieve , be faithless : Mk 16:11 , 16 , Lk 24:11 , 41 , Ac 28:24 , I Pe 2:7 ; so prob. also Ro 3:3 , II Ti 2:13 ( ICC , CGT , in ll.; MM, s.v.).
G569.strongs: from G571 (ἄπιστος); to be unbelieving, i.e. (transitively) disbelieve, or (by implication) disobey

G3879.thayer: I stoop, peer in, look down, look intently.
G3879.mounce: to stoop beside; to stoop down in order to take a view, Lk. 24:12; Jn. 20:5, 11; to bestow a close and attentive look, to look intently, to penetrate, Jas. 1:25; 1 Pet. 1:12*
G3879.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2955 (κύπτω); to bend beside, i.e. lean over (so as to peer within)

G4826.mounce: Symeon, Simeon, pr. name. indecl. (1) Simeon, son of Juda, Lk. 3:30. (2) Simeon, son of Jacob, Rev. 7:7. (3) Simeon, a prophet of Jerusalem, Lk. 2:25, 34. (4) Simeon, or Simon Peter, Acts 15:14; 2 Pet. 1:1. (5) Simeon, called Niger, Acts 13:1*
G4826.abbottSmith: Simeon (so always AV; RV in Lk 2:25 , 34 only), Symeon , a Heb. name for which the Gk. Σίμων , q.v., was also used;
G4826.strongs: from the same as G4613 (Σίμων); Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites

G26.thayer: love, benevolence, good will, esteem; plur: love-feasts.
G26.mounce: love, generosity, kindly concern, devotedness; pl. love-feasts, Jude 12
G26.abbottSmith: love , goodwill , esteem . Outside of bibl. and eccl. books, there is no clear instance (with Deiss., LAE , 18:4, 70:2, cf. the same writer in Constr. Quar. , ii, 4; and with MM, VGT , s.v., cf. Dr. Moulton in Exp. Times , xxvi, 3, 139). In NT, like ἀγαπάω , -ῶ , Of men's love: to one another, Jo 13:35 ; to God, I Jo 2:5 . Of divine love; God's love: to men, Ro 5:8 ; to Christ, Jo 17:26 ; Christ's
G26.strongs: from G25 (ἀγαπάω); love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

G624.mounce: Apollonia, a city of Macedonia, Acts 17:1*. See PhotoGuide.
G624.abbottSmith: Apollonia , a city of Macedonia: Ac 17:1 .†
G624.strongs: from the pagan deity (i.e. the sun; from G622 (ἀπόλλυμι)); Apollonia, a place in Macedonia

G1001.thayer: I cast the line (for sounding), I sound.
G1001.abbottSmith: to heave the lead, take soundings : Ac 27:28 .†
G1001.strongs: from G1002 (βολίς); to heave the lead

G4916.mounce: to bury with; pass. in NT to be buried with Christ symbolically, Rom. 6:4; Col. 2:12*
G4916.abbottSmith: to bury together with : in symbolical sense, of baptism, αὐτῷ ( with Christ ), Ro 6:4 , Col 2:12 .†
G4916.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2290 (θάπτω); to inter in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate spiritually (to Christ by a sepulture as to sin)

G4024.thayer: I gird round; mid: I gird myself, generally for active work or travel.
G4024.mounce: also spelled περιζωννύω, to bind around with a girdle, gird; in NT mid. to gird one’s self in preparation for bodily motion and exertion, Lk. 12:37; 17:8; to wear a girdle, Rev. 1:13; 15:6
G4024.abbottSmith: to gird (c. dupl. acc., as ὁ περιζωννύων με δύναμιν , Ps 17 (18):32 ): pass., αἱ ὀσφύες περιεζωσμέναι , Lk 12:35 ; π. ζώνην χρυσῆν , Re 1:13 15:6 (but v. infr.). Mid., to gird oneself : Lk 12:37 17:8 (and so perh. Re 1:13 15:6 supr., cf. I Ki 2:4 ); c. acc. rei (fig.), τ. ὀσφύν , Eph 6:14 (seq. ἐν , cf. I Ch 15:27 ).†
G4024.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G2224 (ζώννυμι); to gird all around, i.e. (middle voice or passive) to fasten on one's belt (literally or figuratively)

G2912.thayer: a Cretan, an inhabitant of Crete.
G2912.mounce: a Cretan, an inhabitant of Κρήτη, Acts 2:11; Tit. 1:12*
G2912.strongs: from G2914 (Κρήτη); a Cretan, i.e. inhabitant of Crete

G2018.mounce: to bring upon or against, Jude 9; to inflict, Rom. 3:5*
G2018.abbottSmith: to bring upon or against : κρίσιν , Ju 9 .
G2018.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear upon (or further), i.e. adduce (personally or judicially (accuse, inflict)), superinduce

G472.thayer: trans: I hold against; intrans: I withstand; mid: I hold out against, hold firmly to, cleave to.
G472.mounce: to hold firmly, cling or adhere to; to be devoted to any one, Lk. 16:13; Tit. 1:9; to exercise a zealous care for any one, 1 Thess. 5:14; Mk. 6:24*
G472.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and the middle voice of G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself opposite to, i.e. (by implication) adhere to; by extension to care for

G140.mounce: to choose, choose with delight or love, Mt. 12:18*
G140.strongs: from a derivative of G138 (αἱρέομαι); to make a choice

G4807.thayer: a sycamore tree, black mulberry tree.
G4807.mounce: a sycamore tree, mulberry tree, i.q. συκομοραία, q.v., Lk. 17:6*
G4807.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08256) in imitation of G4809 (συκομωραία); a sycamore-fig tree

G2618.mounce: to burn up, consume with fire, Mt. 3:12; 13:30, 40
G2618.abbottSmith: to burn up , burn completely : c. acc. rei, Mt 13:30 , Ac 19:19 ; pass., I Co 3:15 , He 13:11 , II Pe 3:10 , Re 8:7 ; seq. πυρί , Mt 3:12 13:40 , Lk 3:17 (cf. Ex 29:14 , al.); ἐν πυρί ( De 9:21 , al.): Re 17:16 18:8 .†
G2618.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2545 (καίω); to burn down (to the ground), i.e. consume wholly

G5242.mounce: to hold above; intrans. to stand out above, to overtop; met. to surpass, excel, Phil. 2:3; 4:7; τὸ ὑπερέχον, excellence, preeminence, Phil. 3:8; to be higher, superior, Rom. 13:1; 1 Pet. 2:13*
G5242.abbottSmith: trans., to hold over or above .
G5242.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself above, i.e. (figuratively) to excel; participle (as adjective, or neuter as noun) superior, superiority

G4533.thayer: Salmon, son of Nahshon and father of Boaz.
G4533.abbottSmith: Salmon : Mt 1:4, 5 , Lk 3:32 ( Σαλά , WH, R, mg.).†
G4533.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08012); Salmon, an Israelite

G3995.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a wife's father

G5191.thayer: dark purple or blue, of the color of hyacinth.
G5191.mounce: hyacinthine, resembling the hyacinth in color, dark blue, Rev. 9:17*
G5191.abbottSmith: of hyacinth, hyacinthine (v.s. ὑάκινθος ), "doubtless meant to describe the blue smoke of a sulphurous flame" (Swete): Re 9:17 (Hom., Eur., al.).†
G5191.strongs: from G5192 (ὑάκινθος); "hyacinthine" or "jacinthine", i.e. deep blue

G3155.thayer: in vain, in an unreal way, to no purpose.
G3155.mounce: in vain, fruitlessly, without profit, Mt. 15:9; Mk. 7:7*
G3155.abbottSmith: in vain , to no purpose : Mt 15:9 , Mk 7:7 (LXX) .†
G3155.strongs: accusative case of a derivative of the base of G3145 (μασσάομαι) (through the idea of tentative manipulation, i.e. unsuccessful search, or else of punishment); folly, i.e. (adverbially) to no purpose

G3734.thayer: a setting of boundaries, definite limit; plur: bounds.
G3734.mounce: pr. the act of fixing boundaries; a bound set, certain bound, fixed limit, Acts 17:26*
G3734.abbottSmith: a setting of boundaries ; in pl., bounds ; Ac 17:26 .†
G3734.strongs: from a compound of the base of G3725 (ὅριον) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); a limit-placing, i.e. (concretely) boundary-line

G1650.thayer: a proof, possibly: a persuasion; reproof.
G1650.mounce: pr. a trial in order to proof, a proof; meton. a certain persuasion, Heb. 11:1*

G2889.thayer: the world, universe; worldly affairs; the inhabitants of the world; adornment.
G2889.mounce: (1) pr. order, regular disposition; ornament, decoration, embellishment, 1 Pet. 3:3; (2) the world, the material universe, Mt. 13:35; the world, the aggregate of sensitive existence, 1 Cor. 4:9; the lower world, the earth, Mk. 16:15; the world, the aggregate of mankind, Mt. 5:14; the world, the public, Jn. 7:4; in NT the present order of things, the secular world, Jn. 18:36; the human race external to the Jewish nation, the heathen world, Rom. 11:12, 15; the world external to the Christian body, 1 Jn. 3:1, 13; the world or material system of the Mosaic covenant, Gal. 4:3; Col. 2:8, 20
G2889.abbottSmith: order (Hom., Plat., al.). ornament , adornment , esp. of women (Hom., al.): I Pe 3:3 . Later, the world or universe , as an ordered system (Plat., al.): Ac 17:24 , Ro 4:13 , I Co 3:22 , Phl 2:15 , He 4:3 , al. In late writers only, the world , i.e. the earth (= ἡ οἰκουμένη , cf. Mt 4:8 with Lk 4:5 ): Mt 4:8 , Mk 16:[15] , Col 2:20 , I Ti 6:7 , al.; hence by meton., of the human inhabitants of the
G2889.strongs: probably from the base of G2865 (κομίζω); orderly arrangement, i.e. decoration; by implication, the world (in a wide or narrow sense, including its inhabitants, literally or figuratively (morally))

G5120.mounce: the prepositive article, answering, to a considerable extent, to the English definite article; but, for the principle and facts of its usage, consult a grammar; ὁ μὲν ... ὁ δέ, the one ... the other, Phil. 1:16, 17; Heb. 7:5, 6, 20, 21, 23, 24; pl. some ... others, Mt. 13:23; 22:5, 6; ὁ δέ, but he, Mt. 4:4; 12:48; οἱ δέ, but others, Mt. 28:17; used, in a poetic quotation, for a personal pronoun, Acts 17:28
G5120.strongs: properly, the genitive case of G3588 (ὁ); sometimes used for G5127 (τούτου); of this person

G4632.thayer: a vessel to contain liquid; a vessel of mercy or wrath; any instrument by which anything is done; a household utensil; of ships: tackle.
G4632.mounce: a vessel, utensil for containing anything, Mk. 11:16; Lk. 8:16; Rom. 9:21; any utensil, instrument; σκεύη, household stuff, furniture, goods, etc., Mt. 12:29; Mk. 3:27; the mast of a ship, or, the sail, Acts 27:17; met. an instrument, means, organ, minister, Acts 9:15; σκεύη ὀργῆς and σκεύη ἐλέους, vessels of wrath, or, of mercy, persons visited by punishment, or, the divine favor, Rom. 9:22, 23; the vessel or frame of the human individual, 1 Thess. 4:4; 1 Pet. 3:7
G4632.abbottSmith: a vessel, implement (for exx. in various senses, v. MM, xxii): Mk 11:16 , Lk 8:16 , Jo 19:29 , Ac 10:11 , 16 11:5 , Ro 9:21 , Re 18:12 ; pl., II Ti 2:20 , Re 2:27 ; τὰ σ. τῆς λειτουργίας , He 9:21 ; pl., τὰ σ. , utensils, goods , Mt 12:29 , Mk 3:27 , Lk 17:31 ; id. of the tackle or gear of a ship (Xen., Polyb., al.); so in sing., τὸ σ. , Ac 27:17 . Metaph., of persons: σ. ἐκλογῆς , Ac 9:15 ; ὀργῆς
G4632.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a vessel, implement, equipment or apparatus (literally or figuratively [specially, a wife as contributing to the usefulness of the husband])

G3532.strongs: from G3534 (νῖκος) and G2994 (Λαοδικεύς); victorious over the people; Nicolaus, a heretic

G5177.thayer: (a) gen: I obtain, (b) absol: I chance, happen; ordinary, everyday, it may chance, perhaps.
G5177.mounce: to hit an object; to attain to, to obtain, acquire, enjoy, Lk. 20:35; Acts 24:2; 26:22; 27:3; 2 Tim. 2:10; Heb. 8:6; 11:35; intrans. to happen, fall out, chance; common, ordinary, Acts 19:11; 28:2; as an adv., it may be, perchance, perhaps, 1 Cor. 16:6; εἰ τύχοι, as it so happens, as the case may be, 1 Cor. 14:10; 15:37*
G5177.strongs: probably for an obsolete (for which the middle voice of another alternate (to make ready or bring to pass) is used in certain tenses; akin to the base of G5088 (τίκτω) through the idea of effecting; properly, to affect; or (specially), to hit or light upon (as a mark to be reached), i.e. (transitively) to attain or secure an object or end, or (intransitively) to happen (as if meeting with); but in the latter application only impersonal (with G1487 (εἰ)), i.e. perchance; or (present participle) as adjective, usual (as if commonly met with, with G3756 (οὐ), extraordinary), neuter (as adverb) perhaps; or (with another verb) as adverb, by accident (as it were)
G5177.kjv: be, chance, enjoy, little, obtain, X refresh…self, + special

G1372.mounce: to thirst, be thirsty, Mt. 25:35, 37, 42, 44; met. to thirst after in spirit, to desire or long for ardently, Mt. 5:6; Jn. 4:14; 6:35
G1372.strongs: from a variation of G1373 (δίψος); to thirst for (literally or figuratively)

G3022.mounce: pr. light, bright; white, Mt. 5:36; 17:2; whitening, growing white, Jn. 4:35
G3022.abbottSmith: bright , brilliant : of clothing, Mt 17:2 , Mk 9:3 16:5 , Lk 9:29 , Ac 1:10 , Re 3:5 4:4 6:11 7:9 , 13 19:14 (cf. Ec 9:8 ); ὡς χιών , Mt 28:3 ; ἐν λ. (sc. ἱματίοις ), Jo 20:12 , Re 3:4 ; θρόνος , Re 20:11 .

G3502.thayer: a young bird, nestling; a young one.
G3502.mounce: also spelled νεοσσός, the young of birds, a young bird, chick, Lk. 2:24*

G373.thayer: I make to rest, give rest to; mid. and pass: I rest, take my ease.
G373.mounce: to cause to rest, to soothe, refresh, Mt. 11:28; mid. to take rest, repose, refreshment, Mt. 26:45; to have a fixed place of rest, abide, dwell, 1 Pet. 4:14
G373.abbottSmith: to give intermission from labour , to give rest , refresh : Mt 11:28 , I Co 16:18 , Phm 20 ; pass., Phm 7 , II Co 7:13 . Mid., to ta , rest, enjoy rest : Mt 26:45 , Mk 6:31 14:41 , Lk 12:19 , Re 6:11 14:13 ; as in Heb. of Is 11:2 ( נוּחַ עַל ), τὸ πνεῦμα ἐφ ' ὑμᾶς ἀ. , I Pe 4:14 . (In π. this word is used as a technical agricultural term; v. MM, VGT , s.v.; and cf. Le 26:34 f.; Cremer, 826.)†
G373.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3973 (παύω); (reflexively) to repose (literally or figuratively (be exempt), remain); by implication, to refresh

G3093.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H04026); the tower; Magdala (i.e. Migdala), a place in Palestine

G3349.mounce: to raise aloft; met. to unsettle in mind; pass. to be excited with anxiety, be in anxious suspense, Lk. 12:29*
G3349.abbottSmith: to raise on high (Thuc., Xen., al.; Ob 1:4 , Mi 4:1 , Ez 10:16 , 17 , 19 ). Metaph., to buoy up ; pass., to be elated , puffed up (Polyb., al., Ps 130 (131):1 , II Mac 5:17 7:34 , III Mac 6:5 ); to be anxious , in suspense (Polyb., v. 70, 10; FlJ, BJ , iv, 2, 5): Lk 12:29 .†
G3349.strongs: from a compound of G3326 (μετά) and a collateral form of G142 (αἴρω) or perhaps rather G109 (ἀήρ) (compare "meteor"); to raise in mid-air, i.e. (figuratively) suspend (passively, fluctuate or be anxious)

G2280.thayer: Thaddaeus, one of the twelve apostles.
G2280.abbottSmith: Thaddæus : Mt 10:3 , Mk 3:18 (WH, mg., Λεββαῖος , q.v.).†
G2280.strongs: of uncertain origin; Thaddæus, one of the Apostles

G1339.thayer: I put apart, separate, put some distance between.
G1339.mounce: to set at an interval, apart; to station at an interval from a former position, Acts 27:28; intrans. to stand apart; to depart, be parted, Lk. 24:51; of time, to intervene, be interposed, Lk. 22:59*
G1339.abbottSmith: to set apart, separate ; of time (or space), to make an interval, intervene : διαστάσης ὥρας μιᾶς , Lk 22:59 ; βραχὺ διαστήσαντες , Ac 27:28 . In pass., mid. and 2 aor., pf. and plpf. act., to part, withdraw : Lk 24:51 .†
G1339.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand apart, i.e. (reflexively) to remove, intervene
G1339.kjv: go further, be parted, after the space of

G1553.mounce: pr. to be absent from home, go abroad, travel; hence, to be absent from any place or person, 2 Cor. 5:6, 8, 9*
G1553.abbottSmith: to be from home, absent : II Co 5:6 ; seq. ἀπό , II Co 5:8 ; seq. ἐκ , II Co 5:9 (cf. ἀπο- , ἐνδημέω ).†
G1553.strongs: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and G1218 (δῆμος); to emigrate, i.e. (figuratively) vacate or quit

G4652.abbottSmith: dark : opp. to φωτεινός , Mt 6:23 , Lk 11:34 , 36 .†

G2933.thayer: a piece of landed property, a field; plur: possessions, property, possibly landed property, property in land.
G2933.mounce: a possession, property, field, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 10:22; Acts 2:45; 5:1*
G2933.abbottSmith: a possession , property : Mt 19:22 , Mk 10:22 , Ac 2:45 5:1 .†
G2933.strongs: from G2932 (κτάομαι); an acquirement, i.e. estate

G4016.thayer: I cast around, wrap a garment about, put on; hence mid: I put on to myself, clothe myself, dress; I draw (a line).
G4016.mounce: to cast around; to clothe, Mt. 25:36, 38, 43; mid. to clothe one’s self, to be clothed, Mt. 6:29, 31; Lk. 23:11; Jn. 19:2; Acts 12:8; Rev. 4:4
G4016.abbottSmith: to throw around or over , put on or over ; of siege or defensive works: χάρακά σοι , Lk 19:43 (WH, mg.; παρεμβ- , WH, txt.); of clothing, to put on , wrap about , clothe with : c. acc. rei, Lk 23:11 ; c. acc. pers., Mt 25:36 , 38 , 43 ; c. dupl. acc. (not cl.; Bl., § 34, 4), Jo 19:2 ; pf. pass., to have wrapped round one: c. acc. rei (cl.), Mk 14:51 16:5 , Re 7:9 , 13 10:1 11:3 12:1 17:4 18:16 19:
G4016.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw all around, i.e. invest (with a palisade or with clothing)
G4016.kjv: array, cast about, clothe(-d me), put on

G4854.mounce: pr. planted together, grown together; in NT met. grown together, closely entwined or united with, Rom. 6:5*
G4854.abbottSmith: congenital, innate (Plat., al.; III Mac, l.c. ).
G4854.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω); grown along with (connate), i.e. (figuratively) closely united to

G1188.thayer: on the right hand, right hand, right.
G1188.mounce: right, as opposed to left, Mt. 5:29, 30; Lk. 6:6; ἡ δεξιά, i.e. χείρ, the right hand, Mt. 6:3; 27:29; τὰ δεξιά, i.e. μέρη, the parts towards the right hand, the right hand side; καθίζειν, or, καθῆσθαι, or, ἑστάναι, ἐκ δεξιῶν [μερῶν] τινος, to sit or stand at the right hand of any one, as a mark of the highest honor and dignity which he can bestow, Mt. 20:20; 26:64; εἶνι ἐκ δεξιῶν [μερῶν] τινος, to be at one’s right hand, as a helper, Acts 2:25; δεξιὰς (χεῖρας) διδόναι, to give the right hand to any one, as a pledge of sincerity in one’s promises, Gal. 2:9
G1188.abbottSmith: the right : Mt 5:29 , Jo 18:10 , al.; ὅπλα δ. , weapons carried in the right hand, i.e. for offence, II Co 6:7 ; ἡ δ. χείρ , Mt 5:30 , Lk 6:6 , al.; ἡ δ. (sc. χείρ ), Mt 6:3 , al.; ἐπὶ τὴν δ. , in the right hand (R, txt., on R, mg.), Re 5:1 ; διδόναι τὴν δ. , in friend­ship (Deiss., BS , 251), Ga 2:9 ; metaph. of power, τῇ δ. αὐτοῦ , Ac 2:33 5:31 ; τὰ δ. , the right side , Mk 16:5 ; ἐκ δεξιῶν , on
G1188.strongs: from G1209 (δέχομαι); the right side or (feminine) hand (as that which usually takes)

G4935.abbottSmith: concisely, briefly: Ac 24:4 (for a similar ex., v. MM, xxiv).†
G4935.strongs: adverb from a derivative of G4932 (συντέμνω); concisely (briefly)

G3029.abbottSmith: very , exceedingly : Mt 2:16 4:8 8:28 27:14 , Mk 1:35 6:51 (Rec. λ. ἐκ περισσοῦ ) 9:3 16:2 , Lk 23:8 , II Ti 4:15 , II Jo 4 , III Jo 3 (cf. ὑτερλίαν ).†

G1020.strongs: from G1021 (βραδύς) and a prolonged form of G4126 (πλέω); to sail slowly

G4375.abbottSmith: of persons, in both act. and pass. sense (LXX, Es 5:1 , Si 4:7 20:13 );
G4375.strongs: from a presumed compound of G4314 (πρός) and G5368 (φιλέω); friendly towards, i.e. acceptable

G608.thayer: I shut fast, close, shut up.
G608.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2808 (κλείω); to close fully

G4952.thayer: I throw violently on the ground.
G4952.mounce: to tear to pieces; to convulse altogether, Mk. 9:20; Lk. 9:42*
G4952.abbottSmith: to convulse completely : c. acc. pers., Mk 9:20 , Lk 9:42 (cf. σπαράσσω ).†
G4952.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G4682 (σπαράσσω); to rend completely, i.e. (by analogy) to convulse violently

G4220.mounce: interrogative of πότερος, α, ον, which never occurs in NT other than in this form, whether?, Jn. 7:17*
G4220.abbottSmith: which of two . Neut., adverbially, πότερον , whether : Jo 7:17 .†
G4220.strongs: neuter of a comparative of the base of G4226 (ποῦ); interrogative as adverb, which (of two), i.e. is it this or that

G2100.thayer: I give pleasure to, please (perhaps with the added idea of: rendering good service to).
G2100.mounce: to please, Heb. 11:5, 6; pass. to take pleasure in, be well pleased with, Heb. 13:16*
G2100.abbottSmith: to be well pleasing : τ. θεῷ (LXX, ll. c.), He 11:5 , 6 . Pass., to be well pleased : c. dat., He 13:16 .†

G733.thayer: a male engaging in same-gender sexual activity; a sodomite, pederast.
G733.mounce: a male engaging in same-gender sexual activity, a sodomite, pedarest, 1 Cor. 6:9; 1 Tim. 1:10*
G733.abbottSmith: a sodomite : I Co 6:9 , I Ti 1:10 .†
G733.strongs: from G730 (ἄῤῥην) and G2845 (κοίτη); a sodomite
G733.kjv: abuser of (that defile) self with mankind

G2474.thayer: (Hebrew), Israel, surname of Jacob, then the Jewish people, the people of God.
G2474.abbottSmith: Israel : ὁ οἶκος Ἰ. , Ac 7:42 (LXX) , al.; λαός , Ac 4:10 ; υἱοί , Ac 5:21 , al.; αἱ φυλαὶ τοῦ Ἰ. , Mt 19:28 , al. By meton., for the Israelites, Mt 2:6 , Lk 1:54 , Ro 11:2 , al.; ὁ λαὸς Ἰ. , Ac 4:10 ; γῆ Ἰ. , Mt 2:20 , 21 ; βασιλεὺς Ἰ. , Mt 27:42 , Jo 1:50 ; ἡ ἐλπὶς τοῦ Ἰ. , Ac 28:20 ; ὁ Ἰ. τοῦ θεοῦ (of Christians), Ga 6:16 ; ὁ Ἰ. κατὰ σάρκα , I Co 10:18 .
G2474.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03478); Israel (i.e. Jisrael), the adopted name of Jacob, including his descendants (literally or figuratively)

G1116.thayer: Gomorrah, one of the destroyed cities on the Dead Sea.
G1116.mounce: Gomorrha (Gen. 19), pr. name, Mt. 10:15; Rom. 9:29; 2 Pet. 2:6; Jude 7*
G1116.abbottSmith: Gomorrah , one of the cities of the plain ( Ge 19 ): Mt 10:15 , Ro 9:29 (LXX) , II Pe 2:6 , Ju 7 .†
G1116.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06017); Gomorrha (i.e. Amorah), a place near the Dead Sea

G1288.mounce: to pull or tear asunder or in pieces, burst, Mk. 5:4; Acts 23:10*
G1288.abbottSmith: to break or tear asunder : pass., ἁλύσεις , Mk 5:4 ; Παῦλος , Ac 23:10 .†
G1288.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4685 (σπάω); to draw apart, i.e. sever or dismember

G1752.thayer: for the sake of, on account of, on account of which, wherefore, on account of what, why.
G1752.abbottSmith: on account of, because of : Mt 5:10 , 11 16:25 19:29 , Mk 8:35 , Lk 6:22 , Ac 28:20 , Ro 8:36 , II Co 3:10 ; ἕ. τούτου , Mt 19:5 ; τούτων , Ac 26:21 ; τίνος ἕ. , Ac 19:32 ; seq. τοῦ , c. inf., II Co 7:12 ; οὗ ἕν. , Lk 4:18
G1752.strongs: of uncertain affinity; on account of
G1752.kjv: because, for (cause, sake), (where-)fore, by reason of, that

G2259.abbottSmith: at which time , when ; seq. ἂν , c. subjc., whensoever : II Co 3:15 , 16 .†
G2259.strongs: of uncertain affinity; at which time

G5563.thayer: (a) I separate, put apart, (b) mid. or pass: I separate myself, depart, withdraw.
G5563.mounce: to divide, separate, Mt. 19:6; Mk. 10:9; Rom. 8:35, 39; to dissociate one’s self, to part, 1 Cor. 7:10, 11, 15; to withdraw, depart, Acts 1:4; 18:1, 2; Phlm. 15; to be aloof, Heb. 7:26*
G5563.abbottSmith: to separate, divide, put asunder : c. acc. rei, opp. to συζεύγνυμι Mt 19:6 , Mk 10:9 ; c. acc. pers., seq. ἀπό ( Wi 1:3 ), Ro 8:35 , 39 ; pf. pass ptcp., He 7:26 .
G5563.strongs: from G5561 (χώρα); to place room between, i.e. part; reflexively, to go away

G1158.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01840); Daniel, an Israelite

G3796.mounce: can function as an improper prep., late; put for the first watch, at evening, Mk. 11:19; 13:35; ὀψὲ σαββάτων, after the close of the Sabbath, Mt. 28:1
G3796.strongs: from the same as G3694 (ὀπίσω) (through the idea of backwardness); (adverbially) late in the day; by extension, after the close of the day

G173.thayer: a thorn-bush, prickly plant; a thorn.
G173.abbottSmith: a prickly plant , thorn , brier ; in NT always pl.: Mt 7:16 13:7 , 22 27:29 , Mk 4:7 , 18 , Lk 6:44 8:7 , 14 , Jo 19:2 , He 6:8 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G173.strongs: probably from the same as G188 (ἀκμήν); a thorn

G267.mounce: without testimony or witness, without evidence, Acts 14:17*
G267.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a form of G3144 (μάρτυς); unattested

G2369.thayer: (ordinarily: censer, but) either the altar of incense, or the shovel, on which the high-priest poured the coals, when he entered the Holy of Holies on the Day of Atonement.
G2369.mounce: an altar of burning incense, Heb. 9:4*
G2369.abbottSmith: as in cl. (Hdt., iv, 162; Thuc., vi, 46) and LXX, a censer : He 9:4 (but v. infr., and cf. Westc., in l.).
G2369.strongs: from a derivative of G2370 (θυμιάω); a place of fumigation, i.e. the alter of incense (in the Temple)

G5607.mounce: to be, to exist, Jn. 1:1; 17:5; Mt. 6:30; Lk. 4:25, freq.; ἐστί[ν], it is possible, proper, Heb. 9:5; a simple linking verb (“copula”) to the subject and predicate, and therefore in itself affecting the force of the sentence only by its tense, mood, etc., Jn. 1:1; 15:1, freq.; it also forms a frequent circumlocution with the participles of the present and perfect of other verbs, Mt. 19:22; Mk. 2:6
G5607.abbottSmith: with various uses and significations, like the English verb to be . As substantive verb. Of persons and things, to be, exist : Ac 17:28 , Jo 1:1 8:58 17:5 , al; ὁ ὢν καὶ ὁ ἦν (for past ptcp.), Re 1:4 , 8 4:8 11:17 16:5 (v. Swete, Ap. , 5; M, Pr. , 228); τὰ (μὴ) ὄντα , Ro 4:17 , I Co 1:28 . Of times, events, etc., to be, happen, take place : Mt 24:3 , Mk 14:2 15:42 , Lk 21:23 , Jo 4:6 , 23 5:10 , a

G4722.thayer: I cover, conceal, ward off, bear with, endure patiently.
G4722.mounce: to cover; to hold off, to hold in; hence, to hold out against, to endure patiently, 1 Cor. 9:12; 13:7; absol. to contain one’s self, 1 Thess. 3:1, 5*
G4722.abbottSmith: prop., to cover closely, to protect by covering , esp. to keep water in or out (Soph., Plat., al.).
G4722.strongs: from G4721 (στέγη); to roof over, i.e. (figuratively) to cover with silence (endure patiently)

G1895.mounce: since now, since indeed, considering that, Lk. 1:1*
G1895.strongs: from G1894 (ἐπειδή) and G4007 (περ); since indeed (of cause)

G221.thayer: an Alexandrian, a native (or resident) of Alexandria in Egypt.
G221.mounce: a native of Alexandria, an Alexandrine, Acts 6:9; 18:24
G221.strongs: from (the city so called); an Alexandreian or inhabitant of Alexandria

G3595.thayer: a leader, guide; met: an instructor, teacher.
G3595.mounce: a guide, leader, Acts 1:16; met. an instructor, teacher, Mt. 15:14; 23:16, 24; Rom. 2:19*
G3595.abbottSmith: a leader on the way, a guide : Ac 1:16 . Fig., ὁ. τυφλῶν , Ro 2:19 ; pl., Mt 15:14 23:16 , 24 .†
G3595.strongs: from G3598 (ὁδός) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); a conductor (literally or figuratively (teacher))

G5118.thayer: so great, so large, so long, so many.
G5118.mounce: so great, so much, Mt. 8:10; 15:33; so long, of time, Jn. 14:9; pl. so many, Mt. 15:33
G5118.abbottSmith: ( He 7:22 ) and (elsewhere, as usually in Attic) -οῦτον , correlat. of ὅσος , of quantity, size, number, so great, so much , pl., so many : Mt 8:10 , Lk 7:9 , He 12:1 , Re 18:7 , 16 ; of time, so long , χρόνος , Jo 14:9 , He 4:7 ; pl., Lk 15:29 ( ἔτη ), Jo 12:37 21:11 , I Co 14:10 ; seq. ὥστε , Mt 15:33 ; absol.: pl., Jo 6:9 , Ga 3:4 ; of price, τοσούτου , Ac 5:8 ; dat., τοσούτῳ κρείττων , He 1:4
G5118.strongs: from (so much; apparently from G3588 (ὁ) and G3739 (ὅς)) and G3778 (οὗτος) (including its variations); so vast as this, i.e. such (in quantity, amount, number of space)
G5118.kjv: as large, so great (long, many, much), these many

G2615.mounce: to reduce to absolute servitude, make a slave of, 2 Cor. 11:20; Gal. 2:4*
G2615.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1402 (δουλόω); to enslave utterly

G2439.thayer: I clothe, provide clothing for; pass: I am clothed.
G2439.mounce: to clothe; pass. to be clothed, Mk. 5:15; Lk. 8:35*
G2439.abbottSmith: to clothe : Mk 5:15 , Lk 8:35 (elsewhere only in π. ; cf. MM, Exp. , xv; Deiss., LAE , 78 f.).†

G4182.mounce: exceedingly various, multiform, manifold; by impl. immense, infinite, Eph. 3:10*
G4182.abbottSmith: much variegated , of greatly differing colours (Eur.).
G4182.strongs: from G4183 (πολύς) and G4164 (ποικίλος); much variegated, i.e. multifarious

G4484.thayer: Rhegium, a city in the south-west corner of Italy opposite Sicily (modern Reggio).
G4484.mounce: Rhegium, a city at the southwestern extremity of Italy, Acts 28:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G4484.abbottSmith: Rhegium , a town in Sicily: Ac 28:13 .†
G4484.strongs: of Latin origin; Rhegium, a place in Italy

G3873.thayer: I am at hand, am present with, rest with.
G3873.mounce: to lie near, be adjacent; met. to be at hand, be present, Rom. 7:18, 21*
G3873.abbottSmith: to lie beside , be near , be present : Ro 7:18 , 21 .†
G3873.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2749 (κεῖμαι); to lie near, i.e. be at hand (figuratively, be prompt or easy)

G4870.mounce: to follow in company with, accompany, Mk. 5:37; 14:51; Lk. 23:49*
G4870.abbottSmith: to follow along with, accompany : c. dat. pers., Mk 14:51 , Lk 23:49 ; seq. μετά , c. gen. pers., Mk 5:37 .†
G4870.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to accompany

G445.thayer: to act as proconsul, be a deputy.
G445.abbottSmith: to be proconsul : Ac 18:12 Rec. (v.s. ἀνθύπατος ).†
G445.strongs: from G446 (ἀνθύπατος); to act as proconsul

G2093.mounce: in readiness, preparedly, Acts 21:13; 2 Cor. 2:14; 1 Pet. 4:5*
G2093.abbottSmith: readily : I Pe 4:5 ; ἑ. ἔχω , to be ready (Deiss., BS , 252; MM, Exp. , xiv): c. inf., Ac 21:13 , II Co 12:14 .†

G1631.thayer: I put forth, cause to sprout.
G1631.mounce: lit. to cause to grow, to generate; to put forth, shoot, Mt. 24:32; Mk. 13:28*
G1631.abbottSmith: to cause to grow out, put forth (leaves): Mt 24:32 , Mk 13:28 .†
G1631.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5453 (φύω); to sprout up

G1081.mounce: what is born or produced, offspring, progeny, brood, Mt. 3:7; 12:34, et al.; fruit, produce, Mt. 26:29; Mk. 14:25, et al.; fruit, increase, Lk. 12:18; 2 Cor. 9:10
G1081.abbottSmith: offspring of men or animals: ἐχιδνῶν , Mt 3:7 12:34 23:33 , Lk 3:7 (and elsewhere in Rec. for γένημα , q.v.).†
G1081.strongs: from G1080 (γεννάω); offspring; by analogy, produce (literally or figuratively)

G5618.abbottSmith: just as, even as: Mt 6:2 20:28 , Ac 3:17 , I Co 8:5 , I Th 5:3 al.; in protasis, with οὕτως ( καί ) in apodosis: Mt 12:40 , Lk 17:24 , Jo 5:21 , Ro 5:19 , Ga 4:29 , Ja 2:26 , al
G5618.strongs: from G5613 (ὡς) and G4007 (περ); just as, i.e. exactly like

G1660.strongs: from the alternate of G2064 (ἔρχομαι); an advent

G4919.thayer: I break in pieces, crush, thoroughly weaken.
G4919.mounce: to crush to pieces; met. to break the heart of any one, to make to recoil in fear, Acts 21:13*
G4919.abbottSmith: to break in pieces, crush : metaph., τ. καρδίαν , Ac 21:13 .†
G4919.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to crumble); to crush together, i.e. (figuratively) to dispirit

G417.thayer: the wind; fig: applied to empty doctrines.
G417.mounce: the wind; met. a wind of shifting doctrine, Eph. 4:14
G417.abbottSmith: wind : Mt 11:7 14:24 , 30 , 32 , Mk 4:37 , 39 , 41 6:48 , 51 , Lk 7:24 8:23 , 24 , Jo 6:13 , Ac 27:7 , 14 , 15 , Ja 3:4 , Re 6:13 7:1 ; pl., Mt 7:25 , 27 8:26 , 27 , Lk 8:25 , Ac 27:4 , Ju 12 ; οἱ τέσσαρες ἄ. τῆς γῆς , Re 7:1 ; hence the four quarters of the heavens (v. Deiss., BS , 248; MM, VGT , s.v.), Mt 24:31 , Mk 13:27 ; metaph., of variable teaching, Eph 4:14 .†
G417.strongs: from the base of G109 (ἀήρ); wind; (plural) by implication, (the four) quarters (of the earth)

G1460.mounce: to boast in, or of, 2 Thess. 1:4*
G1460.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2730 (κατοικέω); to settle down in a place, i.e. reside

G3719.thayer: I rise early, come in the morning.
G3719.mounce: to rise early in the morning; to come with the dawn, Lk. 21:38*
G3719.abbottSmith: = cl. poët., ὀρθρεύω , to rise early : seq. πρός , c. acc. pers. (as Ps 62:1 (63) , al.), Lk 21:38 (v. Thumb, Hellen. , 123).†
G3719.strongs: from G3722 (ὄρθρος); to use the dawn, i.e. (by implication) to repair betimes

G4793.thayer: I join together, combine, compare, interpret, explain.
G4793.mounce: to combine, compound; to compare, to estimate by comparing with something else, or, to match, 2 Cor. 10:12 (2x); to explain, to illustrate, or, to suit, 1 Cor. 2:13*
G4793.abbottSmith: to compound, combine : I Co 2:13 , R, mg. 1 (Lft., Notes , 180 f.).
G4793.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2919 (κρίνω); to judge of one thing in connection with another, i.e. combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance)

G1702.mounce: to play upon, deride, mock, treat with scorn, ridicule, Mt. 20:19; 27:29; by impl. to delude, deceive, Mt. 2:16
G1702.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G3815 (παίζω); to jeer at, i.e. deride

G4270.thayer: (a) I write previously (aforetime); I write above (already), (b) I depict or portray openly, (c) I designate beforehand.
G4270.mounce: to write before, Rom. 15:4; Eph. 3:3; to make a subject of public notice; to set forth unreservedly and distinctly, Gal. 3:1; to designate clearly, Jude 4*
G4270.abbottSmith: to write before : Ro 15:4 , Eph 3:3 , Ju 4 .
G4270.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1125 (γράφω); to write previously; figuratively, to announce, prescribe
G4270.kjv: before ordain, evidently set forth, write (afore, aforetime)

G2810.thayer: Cleopas, one of the two companions of the risen Jesus from Jerusalem to Emmaus.
G2810.strongs: probably contracted from (compound of G2811 (κλέος) and G3962 (πατήρ)); Cleopas, a Christian

G779.mounce: a leather bag or bottle, bottle of skin, Mt. 9:17; Mk. 2:22; Lk. 5:37, 38
G779.abbottSmith: a leather bottle , wine-skin : Mt 9:17 , Mk 2:22 , Lk 5:37 , 38 .†
G779.strongs: from the same as G778 (ἀσκέω); a leathern (or skin) bag used as a bottle

G5239.thayer: I over-stretch, stretch out beyond the measure assigned to me.
G5239.abbottSmith: to stretch out overmuch : metaph., ἑαυτούς , II Co 10:14 .†
G5239.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1614 (ἐκτείνω); to extend inordinately

G616.thayer: I bring forth, give birth to (a child), a medical or physical word, marking the close of pregnancy.
