G616.mounce: pr. to bring forth, as women; met. to generate, produce, Jas. 1:15; to generate by spiritual birth, Jas. 1:18*
G616.abbottSmith: prop., "the medical word for birth as the close of pregnancy" (Hort, Ja. , 26 f.). In κοινή , "an ordinary syn. of τίκτω , but definitely 'perfectivised' (M, Pr. , 111 ff.; MM, s.v.) by the ἀπό , and so implying safe delivery," to bring forth , give birth to : Ja 1:15 , 18 .†
G616.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G2949 (κῦμα); to breed forth, i.e. (by transference) to generate (figuratively)

G495.thayer: on the opposite side or shore.
G495.mounce: can function as an improper preposition with the gen., opposite, Lk. 8:26*
G495.abbottSmith: on the opposite side : c. gen., Lk 8:26 .†
G495.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G4008 (πέραν); on the opposite side

G2875.thayer: (a) I cut, cut off, strike, smite, (b) mid: I beat my breast or head in lamentation, lament, mourn, sometimes with acc. of person whose loss is mourned.
G2875.mounce: to smite, cut; to cut off or down, Mt. 21:8; Mk. 11:8; mid. to beat one’s self in mourning, lament, bewail, Mt. 11:17; 24:30; Lk. 8:52; 23:27; Rev. 1:7; 18:9*
G2875.strongs: a primary verb; to "chop"; specially, to beat the breast in grief

G3770.thayer: in heaven, belonging to heaven, heavenly, from heaven.
G3770.strongs: from G3772 (οὐρανός); celestial, i.e. belonging to or coming from the sky

G2869.thayer: I abate, cease raging, am stilled.
G2869.mounce: pr. to grow weary, suffer exhaustion; to abate, be stilled, Mt. 14:32; Mk. 4:39; 6:51*
G2869.abbottSmith: to grow weary ; of the wind (Hdt., Jh 1:11 , 12 ), to abate , cease raging : Mt 14:32 , Mk 4:39 6:51 .†
G2869.strongs: from G2873 (κόπος); to tire, i.e. (figuratively) to relax

G5516.abbottSmith: six hundred and sixty-six (L, mg., R, mg., six hundred and sixteen ), the mystical number of the Beast: Re 13:18 (on the interpretation, v. Swete, Ap. , 172 f.).†
G5516.strongs: the 22nd, 14th and an obsolete letter (G4742 (στίγμα) as a cross) of the Greek alphabet (intermediate between the 5th and 6th), used as numbers; denoting respectively 600, 60 and 6; 666 as a numeral

G5159.thayer: I endure the ways of, put up with.
G5159.mounce: to bear with the disposition, manners, and conduct of any one, to put up with, Acts 13:18*
G5159.abbottSmith: to bear another's manners (R, txt., suffered he their manners ): c. acc pers., Ac 13:18 (Rec., WH, R, txt.; ἐτροφο- , T, R, mg.).
G5159.strongs: from G5158 (τρόπος) and G5409 (φορέω); to endure one's habits

G710.mounce: the left hand, Mt. 6:3; so ἐξ ἀριστερῶν, i.e. μερῶν, Lk. 23:33; 2 Cor. 6:7; Mk. 10:37*
G710.abbottSmith: left , on the left : ὅπλα , II Co 6:7 ; ἡ ἀ. (sc. χείρ ), Mt 6:3 ; ἐξ ἀριστερῶν , on the left (MM, s.v.): Mk 10:37 , Lk 23:33 .†
G710.strongs: apparently a comparative of the same as G712 (ἄριστον); the left hand (as second-best)

G4434.thayer: poor, destitute, spiritually poor, either in a good sense (humble devout persons) or bad.
G4434.mounce: reduced to beggary, mendicant; poor, indigent, Mt. 19:21; 26:9, 11; met. spiritually poor, Rev. 3:17; by impl. a person of low condition, Mt. 11:4; Lk. 4:18; 7:22; met. beggarly, sorry, Gal. 4:9; met. lowly, Mt. 5:3; Lk. 6:20
G4434.abbottSmith: of one who crouches and cowers, hence, as subst., a beggar : Lk 14:13 , 21 16:20 , 22 . As adj., prop., beggarly : metaph., στοιχεῖα , Ga 4:9 (v. Lft., in l.); in broader sense (opp. to πλούσιος ), poor : Mt 11:5 19:21 26:9 , 11 , Mk 10:21 12:42 , 43 14:5 , 7 , Lk 4:18 7:22 18:22 19:8 21:3 , Jo 12:5 , 6 , 8 13:29 , Ro 15:26 , II Co 6:10 , Ga 2:10 , Ja 2:2 , 3 , 6 , Re 13:16 ; π. τ. κόσμῳ , Ja 2:5
G4434.strongs: from (to crouch); akin to G4422 (πτοέω) and the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω)); a beggar (as cringing), i.e. pauper (strictly denoting absolute or public mendicancy, although also used in a qualified or relative sense; whereas G3993 (πένης) properly means only straitened circumstances in private), literally (often as noun) or figuratively (distressed)

G5255.mounce: giving ear; obedient, submissive, Acts 7:39; 2 Cor. 2:9; Phil. 2:8*
G5255.abbottSmith: giving ear, obedient, subject : Phl 2:8 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 7:39 ; εἰς πάντα , II Co 2:9 .†
G5255.strongs: from G5219 (ὑπακούω); attentively listening, i.e. (by implication) submissive

G3112.thayer: at a distance, far away, remote, alien.
G3112.mounce: far, far off, at a distance, far distant, Mt. 8:30; Mk. 12:34; met. οἱ μακράν, remote, alien, Eph. 2:13, 17; so οἱ εἰς μακράν, Acts 2:39
G3112.abbottSmith: a long way , far : Lk 15:20 , Ac 22:21 ; seq. ἀπό , Mt 8:30 , Lk 7:6 , Jo 21:8 , Ac 17:27 ; id. metaph., Mk 12:34 ; οἱ εἰς μ. , Ac 2:39 (cf. Is 2:2 ); metaph., οἱ ποτὲ ὄντες μ. (opp. to ἐγγύς ), Eph 2:13 ; οἱ μ. , Eph 2:17 .†
G3112.strongs: feminine accusative case singular of G3117 (μακρός) (G3598 (ὁδός) being implied); at a distance (literally or figuratively)

G4308.thayer: I see beforehand, foresee, see previously, am mindful of; mid: I pay regard to, set before me.
G4308.mounce: to foresee, Acts 2:31; Gal. 3:8; to see before, Acts 21:29; in NT to have vividly present to the mind, to be mindful of, Acts 2:25*
G4308.abbottSmith: to see before (as to place or time): c. acc., Ac 21:29 . Mid., c. acc., seq. ἐνώπιόν μου , Ac 2:25 (LXX) .†
G4308.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3708 (ὁράω); to behold in advance, i.e. (actively) to notice (another) previously, or (middle voice) to keep in (one's own) view

G1779.mounce: to prepare a body for burial, Mt. 26:12; absol. to make the ordinary preparations for burial, Jn. 19:40*
G1779.strongs: from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G5028 (τάφος); to inswathe with cerements for interment

G4999.thayer: taverns; Three Taverns, the name of a village or town on the Appian Way, about thirty-three miles from Rome.
G4999.mounce: taverns, used in the NT only in the transliterated Latin name of “Three Taverns” (Τριῶν ταβερνῶν), see G5553, Acts 28:15*
G4999.strongs: plural of Latin origin; huts or wooden-walled buildings; Tabernæ

G1543.thayer: a centurion of the Roman army.
G1543.mounce: to go forth, go out of, Heb. 11:15*
G1543.strongs: from G1540 (ἑκατόν) and G757 (ἄρχω); the captain of one hundred men

G3899.thayer: I go past, pass by, pass along by.
G3899.mounce: to pass by the side of; to pass along, Mt. 27:39; Mk. 2:23; 9:30; 11:20; 15:29*
G3899.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to travel near

G2182.abbottSmith: an inventor, contriver : κακῶν (cf. κακίας εὑρετής , II Mac 7:31 ), Ro 1:30 .†
G2182.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G2147 (εὑρίσκω); a discoverer, i.e. contriver

G4149.thayer: riches, wealth, abundance, materially or spiritually.
G4149.mounce: riches, wealth, opulence, Mt. 13:22; Lk. 8:14; in NT, πλοῦτος τοῦ Θεοῦ, or Χριστοῦ, those rich benefits, those abundant blessings which flow from God or Christ, Eph. 3:8; Phil. 4:19; meton. richness, abundance, Rom. 2:4; 11:33; 2 Cor. 8:2; meton. a spiritual enriching, Rom. 11:12
G4149.abbottSmith: riches , wealth : of external possessions, Mt 13:22 , Mk 4:19 , Lk 8:14 , I Ti 6:17 , Ja 5:2 , Re 18:16 ; of moral and spiritual conceptions, Ro 2:4 9:23 11:12 , 33 , II Co 8:2 , Eph 1:7 , 18 2:7 3:8 , 16 , Phl 4:19 , Col 1:27 2:2 , He 11:26 , Re 5:12 .†
G4149.strongs: from the base of G4130 (πλήθω); wealth (as fulness), i.e. (literally) money, possessions, or (figuratively) abundance, richness, (specially), valuable bestowment

G514.thayer: worthy, worthy of, deserving, comparable, suitable.
G514.mounce: pr. of equal value; worthy, estimable, Mt. 10:11, 13; worthy of, deserving, either good or evil, Mt. 10:10; correspondent to, Mt. 3:8; Lk. 3:8; Acts 26:20; comparable, countervailing, Rom. 8:18; suitable, due, Lk. 23:41
G514.abbottSmith: of weight , worth (often c. gen., cf. Pr 3:15 8:11 ), seq. πρός : Ro 8:18 (v. Field, Notes , 157);
G514.strongs: probably from G71 (ἄγω); deserving, comparable or suitable (as if drawing praise)

G2292.thayer: I am courageous, confident, of good cheer.
G2292.mounce: to be confident, courageous, 2 Cor. 5:6, 8; 7:16; 10:1, 2; Heb. 13:6*
G2292.abbottSmith: to be of good cheer or courage , to be confident : II Co 5:6 , 8 , He 13:6 ; τ. πεποιθήσει , II Co 10:2 ; seq. εἰς , II Co 10:1 ; ἐν , II Co 7:16 .†
G2292.strongs: another form for G2293 (θαρσέω); to exercise courage
G2292.kjv: be bold, X boldly, have confidence, be confident

G4900.mounce: to negotiate or bargain with someone; to reconcile, Acts 7:26*
G4900.abbottSmith: to drive together, force together : εἰς εἰρήνην , Ac 7:26 , Rec. (WH, RV, συνήλλασσεν ).†
G4900.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1643 (ἐλαύνω); to drive together, i.e. (figuratively) exhort (to reconciliation)

G4466.thayer: Ragau (or Reu), an ancestor of Jesus.
G4466.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07466); Ragau (i.e. Reu), a patriarch

G2142.strongs: from the same as G2141 (εὐπορέω); pecuniary resources

G1269.mounce: to signify by a nod, beckon, make signs, Lk. 1:22*
G1269.abbottSmith: to wink at, nod to, beckon to : Lk 1:22 .†
G1269.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3506 (νεύω); to nod (or express by signs) across an intervening space

G1212.mounce: pr. clearly visible; plain, manifest, evident, Mt. 26:73; 1 Cor. 15:27; Gal. 3:11*

G2773.thayer: properly: a changer of large into smaller coins, a money-changer.
G2773.strongs: from a derivative of G2772 (κέρμα); a handler of coins, i.e. money-broker

G5444.strongs: from the same as G5443 (φυλή); a sprout, i.e. leaf

G5394.thayer: I inflame, fire with passion, set on fire, burn up.
G5394.mounce: to set in a flame, kindle, inflame, Jas. 3:6 (2x)*
G5394.abbottSmith: to set on fire, burn, burn up : fig., Ja 3:6 (on the meaning of the sentence, v. Hort, in l.).†
G5394.strongs: from G5395 (φλόξ); to cause a blaze, i.e. ignite (figuratively, to inflame with passion)

G3900.thayer: a falling away, lapse, slip, false step, trespass, sin.
G3900.mounce: pr. a stumbling aside, a false step; in NT a trespass, fault, offence, transgression, Mt. 6:14, 15; Mk. 11:25, 26; Rom. 4:25; a fall in faith, Rom. 11:11, 12
G3900.strongs: from G3895 (παραπίπτω); a side-slip (lapse or deviation), i.e. (unintentional) error or (wilful) transgression

G2669.thayer: I exhaust by labor or suffering, wear out, overpower, oppress.
G2669.mounce: to exhaust by labor or suffering; to wear out, 2 Pet. 2:7; to overpower, oppress, Acts 7:24*
G2669.abbottSmith: to wear down , oppress , treat hardly : pass., Ac 7:24 , II Pe 2:7 .†
G2669.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and a derivative of G4192 (πόνος); to labor down, i.e. wear with toil (figuratively, harass)

G642.thayer: I separate from some one, am bereaved.
G642.mounce: lit., to make an orphan; fig., to deprive, bereave, 1 Thess. 2:17*
G642.abbottSmith: to be bereaved (prop., of a parent , Lft., Notes , 36); metaph., I Th 2:17 (where Field thinks it = χωρισθέντες , Notes , 199).†
G642.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G3737 (ὀρφανός); to bereave wholly, i.e. (figuratively) separate (from intercourse)

G2917.thayer: (a) a judgment, a verdict; sometimes implying an adverse verdict, a condemnation, (b) a case at law, a lawsuit.
G2917.mounce: judgment; a sentence, award, Mt. 7:2; a judicial sentence, Lk. 23:40; 24:20; Rom. 2:2; 5:16; an adverse sentence, Mt. 23:14; Rom. 13:2; 1 Tim. 5:12; Jas. 3:1; judgment, administration of justice, Jn. 9:39; Acts 24:25; execution of justice, 1 Pet. 4:17; a lawsuit; 1 Cor. 6:7; in NT judicial visitation, 1 Cor. 11:29; 2 Pet. 2:3; an administrative decree, Rom. 11:33
G2917.abbottSmith: the issue of a judicial process, hence, judgment , the decision passed on the faults of others: Mt 7:2 ; in forensic sense, c. gen. pun., Lk 24:20 ; esp. of the judgment of God, Ro 2:2 5:16 11:33 ( ICC , in l.), II Pe 2:3 , Ju 4 ; κ. λαμβάι εσθαι , Mt 23:13 (Rec., R, mg.), Mk 12:40 , Lk 20:47 , Ro 13:2 , Ja 3:1 ; ἔχειν , I Ti 5:12 ; βαστάζειν , Ga 5:10 ; ἐσθίειν ἑαυτῷ , I Co 11:29 ; εἰς κ. συνέρχε
G2917.strongs: from G2919 (κρίνω); a decision (the function or the effect, for or against ("crime"))
G2917.kjv: avenge, condemned, condemnation, damnation, + go to law, judgment

G1750.thayer: I wrap up, roll up in (something), wind in.
G1750.abbottSmith: to roll in, wind in : c. acc pers. et dat. rei, Mk 15:46 .†
G1750.strongs: from G1772 (ἔννομος) and the base of G1507 (εἱλίσσω); to enwrap

G3939.thayer: I sojourn, dwell in as a stranger.
G3939.mounce: to dwell beside; later, to reside in a place as a stranger, sojourn, be a stranger or sojourner, Lk. 24:18; Heb. 11:9*
G3939.abbottSmith: in cl., to dwell beside (c. acc.), among (c. dat.) or near by (absol.).
G3939.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to dwell near, i.e. reside as a foreigner

G5552.thayer: golden, made of gold, adorned with gold.
G5552.mounce: golden, made of or adorned with gold, 2 Tim. 2:20; Heb. 9:4; Rev. 1:12, 13, 20; 9:13, 20, 21:15

G3371.abbottSmith: no more , no longer : c. 2 aor. subjc., Mk 9:25 ; οὐ μ. , Mt 21:19 ; c. praes. subjc., Ro 14:13 ; c. praes. imperat., Lk 8:49 , Jo 5:14 8:[11] , Eph 4:28 , I Ti 5:23 ; c. optat., Mk 11:14 ; ἵνα μ. , II Co 5:15 , Eph 4:14 ; c. inf., Mk 1:45 2:2 , Ac 4:17 25:24 , Ro 6:6 , Eph 4:17 , I Pe 4:2 ; c. ptcp., Ac 13:34 , Ro 15:23 , I Th 3:1 , 5 .†
G3371.strongs: from G3361 (μή) and G2089 (ἔτι); no further
G3371.kjv: any longer, (not) henceforth, hereafter, no henceforward (longer, more, soon), not any more

G1579.mounce: to swim out to land, Acts 27:42*
G1579.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G2860 (κολυμβάω); to escape by swimming

G4828.mounce: to testify or bear witness together with another, confirm, add testimony, Rom. 2:15; 8:16; 9:1*
G4828.abbottSmith: to bear witness with : Ro 2:15 ; c. dat., Ro 8:16 ; seq. ὅτι , Ro 9:1 .†
G4828.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3140 (μαρτυρέω); to testify jointly, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence

G4579.thayer: I shake; fig: I agitate, stir up.
G4579.mounce: to shake, agitate, Heb. 12:26; pass. to quake, Mt. 27:51; 28:4; Rev. 6:13; met. to put in commotion, agitate, Mt. 21:10
G4579.abbottSmith: to shake, move to and fro : τ. γῆν , He 12:26 (LXX) ; pass., ἡ γῆ , Mt 27:51 (LXX) ; συκῆ , Re 6:13 . Metaph., to agitate, stir up , with fear or some other emotion: Mt 21:10 28:4 (cf. ἀνα- , δια- , κατα-σείω ).†
G4579.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to rock (vibrate, properly, sideways or to and fro), i.e. (generally) to agitate (in any direction; cause to tremble); figuratively, to throw into a tremor (of fear or concern)

G4284.thayer: trans: I hold before; mid: I excuse myself; intrans: I project, excel, surpass, have preeminence.
G4284.mounce: to have or hold before; intrans. and mid. to excel, surpass, have advantage or preeminence, Rom. 3:9*
G4284.abbottSmith: Trans., to hold before ; mid., to hold something before oneself (Hdt.), hence, metaph., to excuse oneself : Ro 3:9 , R, mg. (but v. Field, Notes , 152 f.; Lft., Notes , 266 f.; ICC and Vau., in l.).
G4284.strongs: middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself before others, i.e. (figuratively) to excel

G1341.abbottSmith: righteous judgment : Ro 2:5 (cf. τ. δικαίας κρίσεως , II Th 1:5 ; τὴν δ. κ. , Jo 7:24 . For use in π. , v. Deiss., LAE , 89 f.).†
G1341.strongs: from G1342 (δίκαιος) and G2920 (κρίσις); a just sentence

G1632.thayer: I pour out (liquid or solid); I shed, bestow liberally.
G1632.mounce: some list as the active ἐκχύννω; see ἐκχέω (G1772)
G1632.abbottSmith: to pour out : φιάλην , Re 16:1-4 , 8 , 10 , 12 , 17 ; κέρματα , Jo 2:15 ; αἷμα , Mt 23:35 (cf. MM, Exp. , xii), Lk 11:50 , Ac 22:20 , Ro 3:15 (LXX) Re 16:6 . Pass., αἷμα , Mt 26:28 , Mk 14:24 , Ac 22:20 ; οἶνος , Mt 9:17 , Lk 5:37 ; σπλάγχνα , Ac 1:18 . Metaph., τ. πνεῦμα , Ac 2:17 , 18 (LXX) , 33 10:45 , Tit 3:6 ; ἀγάπη , Ro 5:5 (cf. Si 36:8 , ὀργήν ); pass., of persons (like Lat. effundor ), to
G1632.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and (to pour); to pour forth; figuratively, to bestow
G1632.kjv: gush (pour) out, run greedily (out), shed (abroad, forth), spill

G3628.mounce: compassion; kindness, in relieving sorrow and want, Phil. 2:1; Col. 3:12; Heb. 10:28; favor, grace, mercy, Rom. 12:1; 2 Cor. 1:3*
G3628.abbottSmith: compassion , pity : σπλάγχνα οἰκτιρμοῦ , a heart of compassion , Col 3:12 ; in pl. (as LXX for Heb. רַחֲמִים ), οἰ. τ. θεοῦ , Ro 12:1 , He 10:28 ; ὁ πατὴρ τῶν οἰ , II Co 1:3 ; σπλάγχνα κ. οἰ. , Phl 2:1 .†

G3952.thayer: (a) presence, (b) a coming, an arrival, advent, especially of the second coming of Christ.
G3952.mounce: presence, 2 Cor. 10:10; Phil. 2:12; a coming, arrival, advent, Phil. 1:26; Mt. 24:3, 27, 37, 39; 1 Cor. 15:23
G3952.abbottSmith: usually in cl., a being present , presence : I Co 16:17 , II Co 10:10 ; opp. to ἀπουσία , Phl 2:12 (cf. II Mac 15:21 ).
G3952.strongs: from the present participle of G3918 (πάρειμι); a being near, i.e. advent (often, return; specially, of Christ to punish Jerusalem, or finally the wicked); (by implication) physically, aspect

G2662.thayer: I trample down, trample under foot (lit. and met.), spurn.
G2662.mounce: to trample upon, tread down or under feet, Mt. 5:13; 7:6; Lk. 8:5; 12:1; met. to trample on by indignity, spurn, Heb. 10:29*
G2662.abbottSmith: to tread down , trample under foot : Mt 7:6 , Lk 12:1 ; pass., Mt 5:13 , Lk 8:5 . Metaph., τ. υἱὸν τ. θεοῦ , He 10:29 .†
G2662.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3961 (πατέω); to trample down; figuratively, to reject with disdain

G1728.thayer: I begin (in), make a beginning, commence.
G1728.abbottSmith: to begin, make a beginning : Ga 3:3 , Phl 1:6 .†
G1728.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G756 (ἄρχομαι); to commence on

G3897.mounce: pr. near alongside; met. like, similar; neut. παραπλήσιον, adverbially, near to, nearly, with a near approach to, Phil. 2:27*
G3897.abbottSmith: coming near , nearly resembling . Neut. -ον , adverbially, = ίως , in a way nearly resembling : c. dat., Phl 2:27 .†
G3897.strongs: neuter of a compound of G3844 (παρά) and the base of G4139 (πλησίον) (as adverb); close by, i.e. (figuratively) almost

G2442.thayer: I desire earnestly, long for; I have a strong affection for, love fervently.
G2442.strongs: middle voice from (a yearning; of uncertain affinity); to long for

G4460.thayer: Rahab, a Canaanitess, who rescued the Hebrew spies at Jericho.
G4460.mounce: Rahab, pr. name, indecl. (Jsh. 2; 6:17, 25) Heb. 11:31; Jas. 2:25*
G4460.strongs: of Hebrew origin (G7343); Raab (i.e. Rachab), a Canaanitess

G871.mounce: to assimilate, cause to resemble, Heb. 7:3*
G871.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3666 (ὁμοιόω); to assimilate closely

G3104.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin; Mainan, an Israelite

G1825.mounce: to raise up from the dead, 1 Cor. 6:14; to raise up into existence, or into a certain condition, Rom. 9:17*
G1825.abbottSmith: to raise up : Ro 9:17 (cf. ICC , in l); from the dead, I Co 6:14 .†
G1825.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1453 (ἐγείρω); to rouse fully, i.e. (figuratively) to resuscitate (from death), release (from infliction)

G2138.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G3982 (πείθω); good for persuasion, i.e. (intransitively) complaint

G3579.thayer: (a) I entertain a stranger, (b) I startle, bewilder.
G3579.mounce: to receive as a guest, entertain, Acts 10:23; 28:7; Heb. 13:2; pass. to be entertained as a guest, to lodge or reside with, Acts 10:6, 18, 32; 21:16; to strike with a feeling of strangeness, to surprise; pass. or mid. to be struck with surprise, be staggered, be amazed, 1 Pet. 4:4, 12; intrans. to be strange; χενίζοντα, strange matters, novelties, Acts 17:20*
G3579.abbottSmith: to receive as a guest , entertain : c. acc. pers., Ac 10:23 28:7 , He 13:2 ; pass., Ac 10:6 , 18 , 32 21:16 .
G3579.strongs: from G3581 (ξένος); to be a host (passively, a guest); by implication, be (make, appear) strange

G1740.thayer: I glorify, acknowledge the glory belonging to, recognize as glorious; pass: I am glorified in.
G1740.mounce: to invest with glory; pass. to be glorified, to be made a subject of glorification, 2 Thess. 1:10, 12*

G1326.thayer: I wake out of sleep, arouse in general, stir up.
G1326.abbottSmith: to arouse completely, arouse as from rest or sleep: Lk 8:24 ; pass., Mk 4:39 , Lk 8:24 ; of the sea, Jo 6:18 . Metaph., of the mind: ἐν ὑπομνήσει , II Pe 1:13 3:1 .†
G1326.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G1453 (ἐγείρω); to wake fully; i.e. arouse (literally or figuratively)

G1537.thayer: from out, out from among, from, suggesting from the interior outwards.
G1537.mounce: ἐχ before vowels, with genitive, from, out of, a place, Mt. 2:15; 3:17; of, from, out of, denoting origin or source, Mt. 1:3; 21:19; of, from some material, Mt. 3:9; Rom. 9:21; of, from, among, partitively, Mt. 6:27; 21:31; Mk. 9:17; from, denoting cause, Rev. 8:11; 17:6; means or instrument, Mt. 12:33, 37; by, through, denoting the author or efficient cause, Mt. 1:18; Jn. 10:32; of, denoting the distinguishing mark of a class, Rom. 2:8; Gal. 3:7; of time, after, 2 Cor. 4:6; Rev. 17:11; from, after, since, Mt. 19:12; Lk. 8:27; for, with, denoting a rate of payment, price, Mt. 20:2; 27:7; at, denoting position, Mt. 20:21, 23; after passive verbs, by, of, from, marking the agent, Mt. 15:5; Mk. 7:11; forming with certain words a periphrasis for an adverb, Mt. 26:42, 44; Mk. 6:51; Lk. 23:8; put after words of freeing, Rom. 7:24; 2 Cor. 1:10; used partitively after verbs of eating, drinking, etc., Jn. 6:26; 1 Cor. 9:7
G1537.abbottSmith: from out of, from ( see Addendum, p. 492).
G1537.strongs: a primary preposition denoting origin (the point whence action or motion proceeds), from, out (of place, time, or cause; literal or figurative; direct or remote)
G1537.kjv: after, among, X are, at, betwixt(-yond), by (the means of), exceedingly, (+ abundantly above), for(- th), from (among, forth, up), + grudgingly, + heartily, X heavenly, X hereby, + very highly, in, …ly, (because, by reason) of, off (from), on, out among (from, of), over, since, X thenceforth, through, X unto, X vehemently, with(-out)

G5226.mounce: to yield, give way; absol. to be submissive, Heb. 13:17*
G5226.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and (to yield, be "weak"); to surrender

G728.thayer: an earnest, earnest-money, a large part of the payment, given in advance as a security that the whole will be paid afterwards.
G728.abbottSmith: an earnest , part payment in advance for security, a first instalment: II Co 1:22 5:5 , Eph 1:14 . (The word is found in cl. and was prob. brought to Greece by the Phœnicians (AR, Eph. , 1:14 ). It is found in π. with both spellings (v. Milligan, NTD , 73). In MGr. ἀρραβῶνα is an engagement ring ; v. MM, s.v.)†
G728.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06162); a pledge, i.e. part of the purchase-money or property given in advance as security for the rest

G4670.mounce: Sodom, (Gen. 19:24) one of the four cities of the vale of Siddim, now covered by the Dead sea, Mt. 11:23f.; Lk. 17:29; Rom. 9:29; 2 Pet. 2:6; Rev. 11:8
G4670.strongs: plural of Hebrew origin (H05467); Sodoma (i.e. Sedom), a place in Palestine

G3069.thayer: Lydda, Diospolis, Lod (modern Ludd), a city on the way to Joppa within a day's journey of Jerusalem.
G3069.mounce: Lydda, a town in Palestine, Acts 9:32, 35, 38*. See PhotoGuide.
G3069.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03850); Lydda (i.e. Lod), a place in Palestine

G2413.mounce: holy, divine, set apart, 2 Tim. 3:15; tὰ ἱερά, sacred rites, 1 Cor. 9:13*

G2868.abbottSmith: in cl., dust stirred up ( Ex 9:9 , Is 5:24 ); in NT, simply dust : Mt 10:14 , Lk 9:5 10:11 , Ac 13:51 22:23 .†
G2868.strongs: from the base of G2867 (κονιάω) and ornumi (to "rouse"); pulverulence (as blown about)

G5324.thayer: I cause to appear, make visible.
G5324.mounce: to render visible, cause to appear; pass. to appear, be seen; τὸ φανταζόμενον, the sight, spectacle, Heb. 12:21*
G5324.abbottSmith: to make visible . In cl. used in pass. only, = φαίνομαι , to become visible, appear : ptcp., He 12:21 .†
G5324.strongs: from a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω); to make apparent, i.e. (passively) to appear (neuter participle as noun, a spectacle)

G484.mounce: aid, assistance; meton, one who aids or assists, a help, 1 Cor. 12:28*
G484.abbottSmith: cl. a laying hold of , an exchange .

G1687.thayer: I enter, set foot on, intrude, pry into.
G1687.mounce: pr. to step into or upon; met. to search into, investigate; to pry into intrusively, Col. 2:18*
G1687.abbottSmith: to step in or on (Soph.), hence
G1687.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and a presumed derivative of the base of G939 (βάσις); equivalent to G1684 (ἐμβαίνω); to intrude on (figuratively)

G3559.abbottSmith: admonition : I Co 10:11 , Eph 6:4 , Tit 3:10 (Aristoph., Diod., al.).†
G3559.strongs: from G3563 (νοῦς) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); calling attention to, i.e. (by implication) mild rebuke or warning

G2263.strongs: perhaps by transposition from G2048 (ἔρημος) (through the idea of stillness); tranquil

G351.mounce: investigation, judicial examination, hearing of a cause, Acts 25:26*
G351.abbottSmith: an examination : spec. of legal preliminary investigation, Ac 25:26 (v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G2198.mounce: to live, to be possessed of vitality, to exercise the functions of life, Mt. 27:63; Acts 17:28; τὸ ζῆν, life, Heb. 2:15; to have means of subsistence, 1 Cor. 9:14; to live, to pass existence in a specific manner, Lk. 2:36; 15:13; to be instinct with life and vigor; hence, ζῶν, living, an epithet of God, in a sense peculiar to Himself; ἐλπὶς ζῶσα, a living hope vigor and constancy, 1 Pet. 1:3; ὕδωρ ζῶν, living water in respect of a full and unfailing flow, Jn. 4:10, 11; to be alive with cheered and hopeful feelings, 1 Thess. 3:8; to be alive in a state of salvation from spiritual death, 1 Jn. 4:9
G2198.abbottSmith: prop., to live , be alive (v. Syn., s.v. βίος ; in cl. usually of animal life, but sometimes of plants, as Arist., Eth. N , i, 7, 12): Ac 20:12 , Ro 7:1-3 , I Co 7:39 , Re 19:20 , al.; ἐν αὐτῷ ζῶμεν , Ac 17:28 ; ἐμοὶ τὸ ζῆν Χριστός , Phl 1:21 ; διὰ παντὸς τοῦ ζῆν (M, Pr. , 215, 249), He 2:15 ; ὃ δὲ νῦν ζῶ ἐν σαρκί , Ga 2:20 ; ζῇ ἐν ἐμοὶ Χριστός , Ga 2:20 ; (ὁ) ζῶν , of God ( אֵל חַי and cognate ph
G2198.strongs: a primary verb; to live (literally or figuratively)

G323.thayer: the proclamation of an appointment (to an office); perhaps rather admission to membership of a society.
G323.mounce: a showing forth, manifestation; public entrance upon the duty or office to which one is consecrated, Lk. 1:80*

G1911.thayer: (a) I throw upon, cast over, (b) I place upon, (c) I lay, (d) intrans: I strike upon, rush.
G1911.mounce: to cast or throw upon, Mk. 11:7; 1 Cor. 7:35; to lay on, apply to, Lk. 9:62; to put on, sew on, Mt. 9:16; Lk. 5:36; τὰς χεῖρας, to lay hands on, offer violence to, seize, Mt. 26:50; also, to lay hand to, undertake, commence, Acts 12:1; intrans. to rush, dash, beat into, Mk. 4:37; to ponder, reflect on, Mk. 14:72; to fall to one’s share, pertain to, Lk. 15:12
G1911.abbottSmith: trans., to cast, lay or put upon : c. acc et dat., Mk 11:7 , I Co 7:35 ; c. acc, seq. ἐπί c. acc, Re 18:19 , WH, mg.; τ. Χεῖρα ( -ας ) ἐπί (B1., § 37, 7), of seizing a prisoner, Mt 26:50 , Lk 20:19 21:12 , Jo 7:30 , Ac 5:18 21:27 ; c. dat. (Polyb.), Mk 14:46 Ac 4:3 ; c. inf., Ac 12:1 ; τὴν χ. ἐπ’ ἄροτρον , Lk 9:62 ; ἐπίβλημα ἐπὶ ἱμάτιον , Lk 5:36 ; ἐπὶ ἱματίῳ , Mt 9:16 .
G1911.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw upon (literal or figurative, transitive or reflexive; usually with more or less force); specially (with G1438 (ἑαυτοῦ) implied) to reflect; impersonally, to belong to
G1911.kjv: beat into, cast (up-)on, fall, lay (on), put (unto), stretch forth, think on

G4450.strongs: from G4442 (πῦρ); fire-like, i.e. (specially), flame- colored

G3444.abbottSmith: form , shape , appearance (Hom., Eur., Æsch., al.); in philos. lang. the specific character or essential form (Arist., v. Gifford, Inc. , 26 ff.): Mk 16:[12] , Phl 2:6 , 7 .†
G3444.strongs: perhaps from the base of G3313 (μέρος) (through the idea of adjustment of parts); shape; figuratively, nature

G2954.mounce: Cyprus, an island in the eastern part of the Mediterranean, Acts 11:19; 13:4; 15:39; 21:3; 27:4*
G2954.strongs: of uncertain origin; Cyprus, an island in the Mediterranean

G5115.strongs: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); a bow (apparently as the simplest fabric)

G1336.mounce: continuous, uninterrupted; εἰς τὸ διηνεκές, perpetually, Heb. 7:3; 10:1, 12, 14*
G1336.abbottSmith: unbroken, continuous : adverbially, εἰς τὸ δ. , continually (for exx., v. Deiss., BS , 251), He 7:3 10:1 , 12 , 14 .†
G1336.strongs: neuter of a compound of G1223 (διά) and a derivative of an alternate of G5342 (φέρω); carried through, i.e. (adverbially with G1519 (εἰς) and G3588 (ὁ) prefixed) perpetually

G216.thayer: unable to speak or articulate; speechless.
G216.mounce: unable to speak or artilculate, Mk. 7:37; 9:17, 25*
G216.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G2980 (λαλέω); mute

G5291.mounce: to stow under, spread underneath, Lk. 19:36*
G5291.abbottSmith: late form of ὑπο-στορέννυμι , to spread or strew under : c. acc rei, Lk 19:36 .†
G5291.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4766 (στρώννυμι); to strew underneath (the feet as a carpet)

G982.mounce: pertaining to this life or the things of this life, Lk. 21:34; 1 Cor. 6:3, 4*
G982.abbottSmith: pertaining to life , hence, worldly (Field, Notes , 171): Lk 21:34 , I Co 6:3 , 4 .†
G982.strongs: from a derivative of G980 (βιόω); relating to the present existence
G982.kjv: of (pertaining to, things that pertain to) this life

G819.mounce: dishonor, infamy, Rom. 1:26; shame, 1 Cor. 11:14; meanness, vileness, 1 Cor. 15:43; 2 Cor. 6:8; a dishonorable use, Rom. 9:21; 2 Tim. 2:20; κατὰ ἀτιμίαν, slightingly, disparagingly, 2 Cor. 11:21
G819.abbottSmith: dishonour , disgrace : I Co 11:14 , II Co 6:8 ; εἰς ἀ. , Ro 9:21 , II Ti 2:20 ; ἐν ἀ. , I Co 15:43 ; κατ᾽ ἀ. , II Co 11:21 ; πάθη ἀτιμίας , base passions , Ro 1:26 .†
G819.strongs: from G820 (ἄτιμος); infamy, i.e. (subjectively) comparative indignity, (objectively) disgrace

G3177.thayer: I translate (from one language into another), interpret.
G3177.mounce: to translate, interpret, Mt. 1:23; Mk. 5:41; 15:22, 34; Jn. 1:38, 41; Acts 4:36; 13:8*
G3177.abbottSmith: to translate , interpret : Mt 1:23 , Mk 5:41 15:22 , 34 , Jo 1:38 , 42 , Ac 4:36 13:8 (cf. ἑρμηνεύω ).†
G3177.strongs: from G3326 (μετά) and G2059 (ἑρμηνεύω); to explain over, i.e. translate

G3454.thayer: an idle tale, fable, fanciful story.
G3454.mounce: a word, speech, a tale; a fable, figment, 1 Tim. 1:4; 4:7; 2 Tim. 4:4; Tit. 1:14; 2 Pet. 1:16*
G3454.strongs: perhaps from the same as G3453 (μυέω) (through the idea of tuition); a tale, i.e. fiction ("myth")

G356.strongs: from a compound of G303 (ἀνά) and G3056 (λόγος); proportion

G78.thayer: Addi, son of Cosam, and father of Melchi, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G78.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H05716); Addi, an Israelite

G1241.thayer: I gird, tie around; mid: I gird round myself.
G1241.mounce: to gird firmly round, Jn. 13:4, 5; mid. to gird round one’s self, Jn. 21:7*
G1241.abbottSmith: to gird round : ἑαυτόν , Jo 13:4 ; pass., ib. 13:5 ; mid., to gird oneself with : c. acc, Jo 21:7 .†
G1241.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2224 (ζώννυμι); to gird tightly

G3898.mounce: like, in the same or like manner, Heb. 2:14*
G3898.strongs: adverb from the same as G3897 (παραπλήσιον); in a manner near by, i.e. (figuratively) similarly

G872.thayer: I look away from (something else) to, see distinctly.
G872.mounce: to view with undivided attention by looking away from every other object; to regard fixedly and earnestly, Heb. 12:2; to see distinctly, Phil. 2:23*
G872.abbottSmith: to look away from all else at , fix one's gaze upon : metaph. (MM, s.v.), He 12:2 ;
G872.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G3708 (ὁράω); to consider attentively

G1296.mounce: an injunction, institute, ordinance, Rom. 13:2; Acts 7:53*
G1296.abbottSmith: in late writers (Deiss., LAE , 86 ff.) for cl. διάταξις (wh., however, is found in LXX, Ps 118 (119):91 , al.);

G5421.mounce: a well, cistern, Lk. 14:5; Jn. 4:11, 12; a pit, Rev. 9:1, 2*
G5421.abbottSmith: a well : Lk 14:5 , Jo 4:11 , 12 ; φ. τῆς ἀβύσσου , Re 9:1 , 2 .†
G5421.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a hole in the ground (dug for obtaining or holding water or other purposes), i.e. a cistern or well; figuratively, an abyss (as a prison)

G2185.thayer: I come to, reach as far as.
G2185.mounce: to come or reach to, to reach a certain point or end; to reach, arrive at, 2 Cor. 10:13, 14*
G2185.abbottSmith: to come to, to reach : seq. ἄχρι , II Co 10:13 ; εἰς , ib. 14 .†
G2185.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a cognate of G2240 (ἥκω); to arrive upon, i.e. extend to

G2115.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G2372 (θυμός); in fine spirits, i.e. cheerful
G2115.kjv: of good cheer, the more cheerfully

G2928.mounce: to hide, conceal, Mt. 5:14; in NT to lay up in store, Col. 3:3; Rev. 2:17; κεκρυμμέμος, concealed, secret, Jn. 19:38
G2928.abbottSmith: to hide , conceal : c. acc., Mt 13:44 25:18 ; seq. ἐν , Mt 25:25 (pass., Mt 13:44 , Col 3:3 ); pass., Mt 5:14 , He 11:23 , I Ti 5:25 , Re 2:17 ; ἐκρύβη (on the tense and its formation, v. M, Pr. , 161; Bl., § 19, 3) κ. ἐξῆλθεν , Jo 8:59 ; seq. εἰς , Lk 13:21 , Re 6:15 ; ἀπό (in cl. more freq. dupl. acc.), Re 6:16 ; pass., Jo 12:36 , (Bl., § 34, 4). Metaph.: Mt 11:25 13:35 , Lk 11:52 , WH, mg., 18:
G2928.strongs: a primary verb; to conceal (properly, by covering)

G5288.thayer: I withdraw, draw back, keep back, shun, conceal.
G5288.mounce: pr. to let down, to stow away; to draw back, withdraw, Gal. 2:12; mid. to shrink back, recoil, Heb. 10:38; to keep back, suppress, conceal, Acts 20:20, 27*
G5288.abbottSmith: to draw in, let down ( ἱστίον, οὐράν , etc.).
G5288.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4724 (στέλλω); to withhold under (out of sight), i.e. (reflexively) to cower or shrink, (figuratively) to conceal (reserve)

G1806.thayer: I lead out, sometimes to death, execution.
G1806.mounce: to bring or lead out, conduct out of, Mk. 8:23; 15:20; Lk. 24:50
G1806.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead forth

G4568.thayer: a large measure equal to nearly three English gallons.
G4568.mounce: a satum or seah, a Hebrew measure for things dry, containing, as Josephus testifies, (Ant. 9.85) an Italian modius and one half, or 24 sextarii, and therefore equivalent to somewhat less than three gallons English, Mt. 13:33; Lk. 13:21*
G4568.abbottSmith: a Hebrew measure (= about a peck and a half): Mt 13:33 , Lk 13:21 .†
G4568.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05429); a certain measure for things dry

G5059.mounce: a prodigy, portent, Acts 2:19; a signal act, wonder, miracle, Mt. 13:22; Jn. 4:48; Acts 2:43
G5059.abbottSmith: a wonder, marvel : in NT always pl., τ. κ. σημεῖα , Mt 24:24 , al. (v.s. σημεῖον )
G5059.strongs: of uncertain affinity; a prodigy or omen

G4622.thayer: Zion, the hill; used for Jerusalem or heaven.
G4622.mounce: Zion, Mt. Zion, a hill within the city of Jerusalem, indecl., Heb. 12:22; Rev. 14:1; poetic use, Mt. 21:5; Jn. 12:15; people of Israel, Rom. 9:33; 11:26; new Jerusalem of Christianity, 1 Pet. 2:6*
G4622.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H06726); Sion (i.e. Tsijon), a hill of Jerusalem; figuratively, the Church (militant or triumphant)

G4000.mounce: five times one thousand, five thousand, Mt. 14:21; 16:9
G4000.strongs: from G3999 (πεντάκις) and G5507 (χίλιοι); five times a thousand

G4387.mounce: former, prior, Eph. 4:22; before, formerly, Jn. 6:62
G4387.abbottSmith: before , of time, place, rank, etc.; in NT always of Time, before , former : Eph 4:22 . Adverbially, πρότερον , before , aforetime , formerly : Jo 7:50 , II Co 1:15 , He 4:6 ; opp. to ἔπειτα , He 7:27 ; τὸ π. , Jo 6:62 9:8 , Ga 4:13 , I Ti 1:13 ; αἱ π. ἡμέραι , He 10:32 ; αἱ π. ἐπιθυμίαι , I Pe 1:14 .†
G4387.strongs: comparative of G4253 (πρό); prior or previous

G3129.thayer: I learn; with adj. or nouns: I learn to be so and so; with acc. of person who is the object of knowledge; aor. sometimes: to ascertain.
G3129.mounce: to learn, be taught, Mt. 9:13; 11:29; 24:32; to learn by practice or experience, acquire a custom or habit, Phil. 4:11; 1 Tim. 5:4, 13; to ascertain, be informed, Acts 23:27; to understand, comprehend, Rev. 14:3
G3129.abbottSmith: to learn , esp. by inquiry: absol., I Co 14:31 , I Ti 2:11 , II Ti 3:7 ; c. acc. rei, Jo 7:15 (sc. αὐτά ), Ro 16:17 , I Co 14:35 , Phl 4:9 , II Ti 3:14 , Re 14:3 ; seq. quaes. indir., Mt 9:13 ; Χριστόν , Eph 4:20 ( ICC , in l.); seq. ἀπό , c. gen. rei, Mt 24:32 , Mk 13:28 ; ἀπό , c. gen. pers., Mt 11:29 , Col 1:7 ; παρά , c. gen. pers., II Ti 3:14 ; ἐν , c. dat. pers., I Co 4:6 ; "point" aorist (M
G3129.strongs: prolongation from a primary verb, another form of which, , is used as an alternate in certain tenses; to learn (in any way)

G3745.thayer: how much, how great, how many, as great as, as much.
G3745.mounce: as great, as much, Mk. 7:36; Jn. 6:11; Heb. 1:4; 8:6; 10:25; ἐφ’ ὅσον χρόνον, for how long a time, while, as long as, Rom. 7:1; so, ἐφ’ ὅσον, i.e. χρόνον, Mt. 9:15; ὅσον χρόνον, how long, Mk. 2:19; neut. ὅσον repeated, ὅσον ὅσον, used to give intensity to other qualifying words, e.g., μικρόν, the very least, a very little while, Heb. 10:37; ἐφ’ ὅσον, in as much as, Mt. 25:40, 45; καθ’ ὅσον, by how much, so far as, Heb. 3:3; or, in as much as, as, so, Heb. 7:20; 9:27; pl. ὅσα, so far as, as much as, Rev. 1:2; 18:7; how great, how much, how many, what, Mk. 3:8; 5:19, 20; how many, as many as, all who, 2 Cor. 1:20; Phil. 3:15; 1 Tim. 6:1; ὅσος ἄν, or ἐάν, whoever, whatsoever, Mt. 7:12; 18:18
G3745.abbottSmith: how much , how many , how great , how far , how long , as much as , etc. (= Lat. quantus ); of number and quantity: m. pl., Mt 14:36 , Mk 3:10 , Ro 2:12 , al.; n. pl., Mt 17:12 , Mk 10:21 , Lk 11:8 , al.; πάτνες ( πάντα ) ὅ. , Mt 13:46 , Lk 4:40 , al.; seq. οὗτοι ( ταῦτα ), Ro 8:14 , Phl 4:8 ; c. indic., Mk 6:56 , Re 3:19 ; c. subjc. Mk 3:28 , al.; ὅ. ἄν , Mt 18:18 , Jo 11:22 , al.; of measure and
G3745.strongs: by reduplication from G3739 (ὅς); as (much, great, long, etc.) as
G3745.kjv: all (that), as (long, many, much) (as), how great (many, much), (in-)asmuch as, so many as, that (ever), the more, those things, what (great, -soever), wheresoever, wherewithsoever, which, X while, who(-soever)

G690.strongs: from G688 (Ἀραβία); an Arab or native of Arabia

G3039.thayer: I crush to powder, scatter like chaff.
G3039.mounce: pr. to winnow grain; in NT to scatter like chaff, crush, Mt. 21:44; Lk. 20:18*
G3039.strongs: from , the equivalent of (a winnowing fan or basket); to winnow, i.e. (by analogy), to triturate

G3126.mounce: wealth, riches, Lk. 16:9, 11; personified, like the Greek Πλοῦτος, Mammon, Mt. 6:24; Lk. 16:13*
G3126.strongs: of Chaldee origin (confidence, i.e. wealth, personified); mammonas, i.e. avarice (deified)

G4242.mounce: eldership, seniority; an embassy, legation; a body of ambassadors, legates, Lk. 14:32; 19:14*
G4242.strongs: from G4243 (πρεσβεύω); seniority (eldership), i.e. (by implication) an embassy (concretely, ambassadors)

G3067.thayer: a bath (of water, not the vessel), water for washing, washing.
G3067.mounce: a bath, water for bathing; a bathing, washing, ablution, Eph. 5:26; Tit. 3:5*
G3067.abbottSmith: a washing , bath : τ. ὕδατος , Eph 5:26 ; τ. παλιγγενεσίας , Tit 3:5 (v. AR, Eph. 5:26 ).†
G3067.strongs: from G3068 (λούω); a bath, i.e. (figuratively), baptism

G2547.thayer: and thence, and from there; and then afterwards.
G2547.mounce: crasis, and there, Mk. 10:1; Acts 7:4; 14:26; 20:15; 21:1; 27:4, 12; 28:15; and then, afterwards, Acts 13:21
G2547.abbottSmith: of place, and from thence , and thence : Mk 9:30 , Lk 11:53 , Ac 7:4 14:26 16:12 20:15 21:1 27:4 28:15 .†
G2547.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G1564 (ἐκεῖθεν); likewise from that place (or time)

G3864.mounce: by the sea-side, situated on the sea-coast, maritime, Mt. 4:13*
G3864.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G2281 (θάλασσα); along the sea, i.e. maritime (lacustrine)

G203.thayer: (a technical word of Jewish use) foreskin, prepuce: used sometimes as a slang term by Jews, of Gentiles.
G203.mounce: foreskin; uncircumcision, the state of being uncircumcised, Rom. 4:10; the abstract being put for the concrete, uncircumcised men, i.e., Gentiles, Rom. 4:9, et al
G203.abbottSmith: the prepuce , foreskin (LXX), hence abstr., uncircumcision : Ac 11:3 , Ro 2:25-27 3:30 4:10-12 , I Co 7:18-19 , Ga 5:6 6:15 , Col 2:13 3:11 . By meton., the uncircumcised : Ro 4:9 , Ga 2:7 , Eph 2:11 .†
G203.strongs: from G206 (ἄκρον) and probably a modified form of (the penis or male sexual organ); the prepuce; by implication, an uncircumcised (i.e. gentile, figuratively, unregenerate) state or person

G1068.thayer: Gethsemane, a small place between the brook Kidron and the Mount of Olives near Jerusalem.
G1068.mounce: Gethsemane, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 26:36; Mk. 14:32*
G1068.abbottSmith: Gethsemane : called in Jo 18:1 a κῆπος , but named only in Mt 26:36 , Mk 14:32 .†
G1068.strongs: of Chaldee origin (compare H01660 and H08081); oil-press; Gethsemane, a garden near Jerusalem

G1710.thayer: I travel as a merchant, engage in trade; I traffic in, make gain or business of.
G1710.mounce: to travel; to travel for business’ sake; to trade, traffic, Jas. 4:13; by impl., trans., to make a gain of, deceive for one’s own advantage, 2 Pet. 2:3*
G1710.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to travel in (a country as a pedlar), i.e. (by implication) to trade

G2926.thayer: a cellar, vault, hidden place, crypt.
G2926.mounce: a vault or closet, a cell for storage, dark secret place, Lk. 11:33*
G2926.strongs: feminine of G2927 (κρυπτός); a hidden place, i.e. cellar ("crypt")

G3644.strongs: from G3645 (ὀλοθρεύω); a ruiner, i.e. (specially), a venomous serpent

G1206.thayer: adj. where English requires an adv., on the second day, on the next day.
G1206.mounce: on the second day of a certain state or process, and used as an epithet of the subject or agent, Acts 28:13*
G1206.abbottSmith: on the second day : δευτεραῖοι ἤλθομεν , Ac 28:13 .†
G1206.strongs: from G1208 (δεύτερος); secondary, i.e. (specially) on the second day

G4498.thayer: Rhoda, a maidservant in the house of John Mark's mother at Jerusalem.
G4498.strongs: probably for rhode (a rose); Rode, a servant girl

G1628.thayer: I flee out, away, escape; with an acc: I escape something.
G1628.mounce: intrans. to flee out, to make an escape, Acts 16:27; 19:16; trans. to escape, avoid, Lk. 21:36; Rom. 2:3
G1628.abbottSmith: to flee away, escape : absol., Ac 16:27 , I Th 5:3 , He 2:3 ; seq. ἐκ , Ac 19:16 ; c. acc pers., He 12:25 ; c. rei, Lk 21:36 , Ro 2:3 ; τ. χεῖρας αὐτοῦ , II Co 11:33 .†
G1628.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G5343 (φεύγω); to flee out

G4494.thayer: I toss to and fro, fan, blow.
G4494.mounce: to fan, blow, ventilate; to toss, agitate, e.g. the ocean by the wind, Jas. 1:6*
G4494.abbottSmith: primarily, to fan a fire, hence generally, to make a breeze (Plut., al.). In pass. (cf. Philo, de incer. mund. , 24), to be tossed or blown by the wind : of waves, ἀνεμιζομένῳ καὶ ῥιπιζομένῳ , blown and raised with the wind (Hort, in l.), Ja 1:6 .†
G4494.strongs: from a derivative of G4496 (ῥίπτω) (meaning a fan or bellows); to breeze up, i.e. (by analogy) to agitate (into waves)

G4945.mounce: a banding by oath; a combination, conspiracy, Acts 23:13*
G4945.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3660 (ὀμνύω); a swearing together, i.e. (by implication) a plot

G497.mounce: to war against; to contravene, oppose, Rom. 7:23*
G497.abbottSmith: to make war against : c. dat., Ro 7:23 .†
G497.strongs: from G473 (ἀντί) and G4754 (στρατεύομαι); (figuratively) to attack, i.e. (by implication) destroy

G5301.thayer: hyssop, a stalk or stem of hyssop.
G5301.mounce: hyssop, in NT, however, not the plant usually so named, but probably the caper plant; a bunch of hyssop, Heb. 9:19; a hyssop stalk, Jn. 19:29*
G5301.abbottSmith: hyssop , of which a bunch was used in ritual sprinklings: He 9:19 ; of a branch or rod (?) of hyssop, Jo 19:29 (but v. Field, Notes , 106 ff., for suggestion to substitute ὑσσῷ here).†

G4570.thayer: (a) I extinguish, quench, (b) I suppress, thwart.
G4570.mounce: to extinguish, quench, Mt. 12:20; 25:8; Mk. 9:44, 46, 48; Eph. 6:16; Heb. 11:34; met. to quench, damp, hinder, thwart, 1 Thess. 5:19*
G4570.abbottSmith: of fire or things on fire, to quench : c. acc, Mt 12:20 , Eph 6:16 , He 11:34 ; pass., Mt 25:8 , Mk 9:[44, 46], 48 , Metaph. (cf. Ca 8:7 , IV Mac 16:4 ): τὸ πνεῦμα , I Th 5:19 .†
G4570.strongs: a prolonged form of an apparently primary verb; to extinguish (literally or figuratively)

G4196.strongs: of Latin origin; apparently "popular"; Poplius (i.e. Publius), a Roman

G2647.thayer: (lit: I loosen thoroughly), (a) trans: I break up, overthrow, destroy, both lit. and met., (b) I unyoke, unharness a carriage horse or pack animal; hence: I put up, lodge, find a lodging.
G2647.mounce: to dissolve; to destroy, demolish, overthrow, throw down, Mt. 24:2; 26:61; met. to nullify, abrogate, Mt. 5:17; Acts 5:38, 39; absol. to unloose harness, etc., to halt, to stop for the night, lodge, Lk. 9:12
G2647.abbottSmith: to destroy , cast down : Mt 24:2 , Mk 13:2 , Lk 21:6 ; τ. ναόν , Mt 26:61 27:40 , Mk 14:58 15:29 , Ac 6:14 ; οἰκίαν , II Co 5:1 ; opp. to οἰκοδομεῖν , Ga 2:18 . Metaph., to overthrow , annul , abrogate : Ac 5:38 , 39 ; τ. ἔργον τ. θεοῦ , Ro 14:20 ; τ. νόμον , Mt 5:17 ( II Mac 2:22 ).
G2647.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3089 (λύω); to loosen down (disintegrate), i.e. (by implication) to demolish (literally or figuratively); specially (compare G2646 (κατάλυμα)) to halt for the night
G2647.kjv: destroy, dissolve, be guest, lodge, come to nought, overthrow, throw down

G4697.thayer: I feel compassion, have pity on, am moved.
G4697.mounce: to be moved with pity or compassion, Mt. 9:36; 14:14; 20:34; Lk. 7:13; to be compassionate, Mt. 18:27
G4697.abbottSmith: to be moved as to the σπλάγχνα (q.v.), hence, to feel pity or compassion : absol., Lk 10:33 15:20 ; ptcp., σλαγχνισθείς , c. indic., Mt 18:27 20:34 , Mk 1:41 ; seq. ἐπίπ , c. dat. pers., Mt 14:14 , Lk 7:13 ; ἐπί , c. acc., Mt 15:32 , Mk 6:34 8:2 9:22 ; περί , Mt 9:36 . (The word is elsewhere found only in Sm: I Ki 23:21 , Ez 24:21 , Test. Zeb. , 4:6-7; v. Thayer, s.v.; MM, xxiii quote Thumb, Helle
G4697.strongs: middle voice from G4698 (σπλάγχνον); to have the bowels yearn, i.e. (figuratively) feel sympathy, to pity

G5187.thayer: I puff up, make haughty; pass: I am puffed up, am haughty.
G5187.mounce: to besmoke; met. to possess with the fumes of conceit; pass. to be demented with conceit, puffed up, 1 Tim. 3:6; 6:4; 2 Tim. 3:4; 1 Tim. 6:4*
G5187.abbottSmith: prop., to wrap in smoke ; used only metaph., to puff up, becloud with pride: pass., I Ti 3:6 6:4 , II Ti 3:4 (Dem., Arist., al.).†
G5187.strongs: from a derivative of G5188 (τύφω); to envelop with smoke, i.e. (figuratively) to inflate with self-conceit
G5187.kjv: high-minded, be lifted up with pride, be proud

G2992.thayer: (a) a people, characteristically of God's chosen people, first the Jews, then the Christians, (b) sometimes, but rarely, the people, the crowd.
G2992.mounce: a body of people; a concourse of people, a multitude, Mt. 27:25; Lk. 8:47; the common people, Mt. 26:5; a people, nation, Mt. 2:4; Lk. 2:32; Tit. 2:14; ὁ λαός, the people of Israel, Lk. 2:10
G2992.abbottSmith: the people at large (Hom., al.), esp. of people assembled: Mt 27:25 , Lk 1:21 3:15 al.; pl. (Hom., al., π. ; v. MM, xvi), Ac 4:27 .
G2992.strongs: apparently a primary word; a people (in general; thus differing from G1218 (δῆμος), which denotes one's own populace)

G350.thayer: I examine, inquire into, investigate, question.
G350.mounce: to sift; to examine closely, Acts 17:11; to scrutinize, scan, 1 Cor. 2:14, 15; 9:3; to try judicially, Lk. 23:14; to judge, give judgment upon, 1 Cor. 4:3, 4; to put questions, be inquisitive, 1 Cor. 10:25, 27; Acts 11:12 v.l
G350.abbottSmith: to examine , investigate , question (Lft., Notes , 181 f.): Ac 17:11 , I Co 2:14 , 15 4:3 , 4 9:3 10:25 10:27 14:24 ; in forensic sense (MM, VGT , s.v.; esp. of examination by torture; v. Field, Notes , 120 f.), Lk 23:14 , Ac 4:9 12:19 24:8 28:18 .†
G350.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2919 (κρίνω); properly, to scrutinize, i.e. (by implication) investigate, interrogate, determine
G350.kjv: ask, question, discern, examine, judge, search

G328.thayer: I gird up, brace up (with a view to active exertion); a metaphor from the girding of the flowing tunic, to prevent its hampering one in active work.
G328.mounce: to gird with a belt or girdle; to gird one’s self, 1 Pet. 1:13*
G328.abbottSmith: to gird up : fig., τ. ὀσφύας τ. διανοίας , I Pe 1:13 .†
G328.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2224 (ζώννυμι); to gird afresh

G3705.thayer: a spectacle, vision, that which is seen.
G3705.mounce: a thing seen, sight, appearance, Acts 7:31; a vision, Mt. 17:9; Acts 9:10, 12
G3705.abbottSmith: that which is seen ; a sight , spectacle : Mt 17:9 , Ac 7:31 ; an appearance , vision : Ac 9:10 , 12 (Rec.) 10:3 , 17 , 19 11:5 12:9 16:9 , 10 18:9 (cf. ὀπτασία ).†
G3705.strongs: from G3708 (ὁράω); something gazed at, i.e. a spectacle (especially supernatural)

G881.thayer: Ahaz, son of Jotham and father of Hezekiah.
G881.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0271); Achaz, an Israelite

G5602.thayer: here, the things here, what is here, what is going on here, the state of affairs here.
G5602.mounce: here, in this place, Mt. 12:6, 41; ὡδε ἠ ὡδε, here or there, Mt. 24:23; τὰ ὡδε, the state of things here, Col. 4:9; met. herein, in this thing, Rev. 13:10, 18; to this place, Mt. 8:29; 14:18
G5602.abbottSmith: prop., of manner, so (Hom., al.). In poets (rarely) and late writers, of place, hither (Bl, §25, 2): Mt 8:29 , Mk 11:3 , Lk 9:41 , Jo 6:25 , al.; ἕως ὧδε , Lk 23:5 ; here : Mt 12:6 , Mk 9:1 , Lk 9:33 , Jo 6:9 , al.; τὰ ὧδε , Col 4:9 ; opp. to ἐκεῖ , He 7:8 ; ὧδε . . . ἢ ὧδε (ἐκεῖ) , Mt 24:23 , Mk 13:21 ; metaph., here (i.e. in this circumstance or connection), I Co 4:2 , Re 13:10 , 18 14:12 17:9 .
G5602.strongs: from an adverb form of G3592 (ὅδε); in this same spot, i.e. here or hither

G1469.mounce: to judge or reckon among, consider as belonging to, adjudge to the number of; class with, place in the same rank, 2 Cor. 10:12*
G1469.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2919 (κρίνω); to judge in, i.e. count among

G4362.thayer: I fasten to, applied to Christ's being fastened to the cross.
G4362.mounce: to fix to, affix to, Acts 2:23*
G4362.abbottSmith: to fasten to : absol., to crucify , Ac 2:23 .†
G4362.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G4078 (πήγνυμι); to fasten to, i.e. (specially), to impale (on a cross)

G799.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G4793 (συγκρίνω); incomparable; Asyncritus, a Christian

G3756.mounce: negative adverb, originally the gen. of ὅς, spelled οὐκ if followed by a word beginning with a vowel and a smooth breathing, οὐχ if followed by a vowel and rough breathing, not, no, Mt. 5:37; 12:43; 23:37; for the peculiarities of its usage (especially as distinct from μή) consult a grammar
G3756.abbottSmith: not , no , used generally c. indic. and for a denial of fact (cf . μή ); absol. (accented), οὔ , no : Mt 13:29 , Jo 1:21 21:5 ; οὒ οὔ , Mt 5:37 , Ja 5:12 . Most freq. negativing a verb or other word, Mt 1:25 10:26 , 38 , Mk 3:25 9:37 , Jo 8:29 , Ac 7:5 , Ro 1:16 , Phl 3:3 , al.; in litotes, οὐκ ὀλίγοι (i.e. very many ), Ac 17:4 , al.; οὐκ ἄσημος , Ac 21:39 ; πᾶς . . . οὐ , c. verb, (like Heb. כֹּל
G3756.strongs: a primary word; the absolute negative (compare G3361 (μή)) adverb; no or not
G3756.kjv: + long, nay, neither, never, no (X man), none, (can-)not, + nothing, + special, un(-worthy), when, + without, + yet but

G2186.thayer: I stand by, am urgent, befall one (as of evil), am at hand, impend.
G2186.mounce: trans. to place upon, over, close by; intrans. to stand by or near, Lk. 2:38; 4:39; to come suddenly upon, Lk. 2:9; 24:4; to come upon, assault, Acts 6:12; 17:5; to come near, approach, Lk. 10:40; to impend, be instant, to be at hand, 1 Thess. 5:3; to be present, Acts 28:2; to be pressing, urgent, earnest, 2 Tim. 4:2
G2186.abbottSmith: causal in pres., impf., fut., 1 aor., to set upon or by, set up , etc. (Æsch., Hdt., Arist., al).
G2186.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to stand upon, i.e. be present (in various applications, friendly or otherwise, usually literal);
G2186.kjv: --assault, come (in, to, unto, upon), be at hand (instant), present, stand (before, by, over)

G4473.mounce: pr. a sprinkling; met. a cleansing, purification, Heb. 12:24; 1 Pet. 1:2*
G4473.abbottSmith: sprinkling : of the ceremonial sprinkling of blood for purification, He 12:24 , I Pe 1:2 (v. Hort, Pe. , 23 ff.).†

G2479.mounce: strength, might, power, Rev. 18:2; Eph. 1:19; faculty, ability, 1 Pet. 4:11; Mk. 12:30, 33; Lk. 10:27
G2479.abbottSmith: strength , might , power , force , ability : Re 5:12 7:12 ; ἰ. κ. δύναμις , II Pe 2:11 ; τ. κράτος τῆς ἰ. ( Is 40:26 ), Eph 1:19 6:10 ; ἡ δόξα τῆς ἰ. , II Th 1:9 ; ἐξ ἰ. , I Pe 4:11 ; ἐν ὅλῃ τ. ἰ. , Mk 12:30 , 33 (LXX) , Lk 10:27 (LXX) .†
G2479.strongs: from a derivative of (force; compare , a form of G2192 (ἔχω)); forcefulness (literally or figuratively)

G4637.thayer: I dwell as in a tent, encamp, have my tabernacle.
G4637.mounce: to pitch tent, encamp; to tabernacle, dwell in a tent; to dwell, have one’s abode, Jn. 1:14; Rev. 7:15; 12:12; 13:6; 21:3*
G4637.abbottSmith: to have one's tabernacle, to dwell (in π. of temporary dwelling, v. MM, Exp. , xxii): seq. ἐπί , c. acc. pers., Re 7:15 ; seq. ἐν , c. dat. pers., Jo 1:14 ; id. c. dat. loc., Re 12:12 13:6 ; seq. μετά , c. gen. pers., Re 21:3 (cf. ἐπι- , κατα-σκηνόω ).†
G4637.strongs: from G4636 (σκῆνος); to tent or encamp, i.e. (figuratively) to occupy (as a mansion) or (specially), to reside (as God did in the Tabernacle of old, a symbol of protection and communion)

G3314.thayer: (lit: midday, hence, the position of the sun at midday), the South.
G3314.mounce: mid-day, noon, Acts 22:6; meton. the south, Acts 8:26*
G3314.strongs: from G3319 (μέσος) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); midday; by implication, the south

G1315.thayer: I lay my hands upon, and so: I slay, kill.
G1315.abbottSmith: to have in hand, conduct, manage . Mid.
G1315.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and a derivative of G5495 (χείρ); to handle thoroughly, i.e. lay violent hands upon

G3556.thayer: a nestling, the young of birds.
G3556.mounce: the young of birds, a chick; pl. a brood of young birds, Mt. 23:37*

G1329.mounce: to explain, interpret, translate, Lk. 24:27; Acts 9:36; 1 Cor. 14:5, 13, 27; to be able to interpret, 1 Cor. 12:30*
G1329.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2059 (ἑρμηνεύω); to explain thoroughly, by implication, to translate

G4594.mounce: today, this day, Mt. 6:11, 30; 16:3; 21:28; now, at present, Heb. 13:8; 2 Cor. 3:15; ἡ σήμερον, i.e. ἡμέρα, sometimes expressed, this day, the present day, Acts 20:26; ἕως or ἄχρι τῆς σήμερον, until this day, until our times, Mt. 11:23; 27:8
G4594.abbottSmith: to-day : Mt 6:11 , Lk 4:21 , Ac 4:9 , al.; opp. to αὔριον , Mt 6:30 , Lk 12:28 , Ja 4:13 ; χθὲς καὶ σ. καὶ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας , He 13:8 ; ἡ σ. ἡμέρα , Ac 20:26 ; ἕως ( ἄχρι ) τῆς σ. ἡμέρας , Ro 11:8 , II Co 3:14 ; μέχρι ( ἕως ) τῆς σ. (sc. ἡμέρας ), Mt 11:23 27:8 ; as subst., τὸ σ. , He 3:13 ; id. in appos., ὁρίζει ἡμέραν, σ. , He 4:7 R, mg., (v. Westc., in l.).
G4594.strongs: neuter (as adverb) of a presumed compound of the article G3588 (ὁ) (t changed to s) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); on the (i.e. this) day (or night current or just passed); generally, now (i.e. at present, hitherto)

G4956.strongs: from a derivative of G4921 (συνιστάω); introductory, i.e. recommendatory

G2951.thayer: cumin, a plant used as a spice.
G2951.mounce: cumin, cuminum salivum of Linnaeus, a plant, a native of Egypt and Syria, whose seeds are of an aromatic, warm, bitterish taste, with a strong but not disagreeable smell, and used by the ancients as a condiment, Mt. 23:23*
G2951.strongs: of foreign origin (compare H03646); dill or fennel ("cummin")

G5335.abbottSmith: to affirm, assert : c. acc. et inf., Ac 24:9 25:19 ; c. inf. et nom., Ro 1:22 .†
G5335.strongs: prolongation from the same as G5346 (φημί); to assert

G903.thayer: Balaam, son of Beor of Pethor on the Euphrates, a soothsayer in the Old Testament.
G903.abbottSmith: Balaam ( Nu 22-24 ): II Pe 2:15 , Ju 11 , Re 2:14 .†
G903.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01109); Balaam, a Mesopotamian (symbolic of a false teacher)

G5227.thayer: opposite to, adverse; subst: an adversary.
G5227.mounce: over against; contrast, adverse, Col. 2:14; ὁ ὑπεναντίος, an opponent, adversary, Heb. 10:27*
G5227.abbottSmith: set over against, opposite . Metaph. (Plat., Arist., al.), opposed to, contrary to : c. dat. pers., Col 2:14 ; absol., as subst., ὁ ὑ. , He 10:27 (cf. Is 26:11 ).†
G5227.strongs: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1727 (ἐναντίος); under (covertly) contrary to, i.e. opposed or (as noun) an opponent

G1133.thayer: a woman, with all a woman's weakness, a poor weak woman, a silly woman.
G1133.mounce: a little woman; a trifling, weak, silly woman, 2 Tim. 3:6*
G1133.abbottSmith: a little woman ; contemptuously, a silly woman (EV): II Ti 3:6 .†
G1133.strongs: a diminutive from G1135 (γυνή); a little (i.e. foolish) woman

G4331.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G1448 (ἐγγίζω); to approach near

G325.thayer: I send up, deliver, hand over, yield.
G325.mounce: to give forth, up, or back; to deliver, present, Acts 23:33*
G325.abbottSmith: to give forth , send up , as of plants (Hdt., al.).
G325.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to hand over

G4600.mounce: the jawbone; in NT the cheek, Mt. 5:39; Lk. 6:29*
G4600.abbottSmith: the jawbone, jaw, cheek : Mt 5:39 , Lk 6:29 (cf. MM, xxii).†
G4600.strongs: of uncertain derivation; the jaw-bone, i.e. (by implication) the cheek or side of the face

G1683.abbottSmith: reflex pron. of first pers., used only in gen., dat. and acc sing.,
G1683.strongs: genitive case compound of G1700 (ἐμοῦ) and G846 (αὐτός); of myself so likewise the dative case , and accusative case

G3038.thayer: (adj: paved with stone), a mosaic pavement.
G3038.mounce: a pavement made of blocks of stone, Jn. 19:13*
G3038.abbottSmith: paved with stones , esp. of tessallated work ( Ca 3:10 ); as subst., τὸ λ. , a tessallated pavement : Jo 19:13 (cf. Γαββαθᾶ ).†
G3038.strongs: from G3037 (λίθος) and a derivative of G4766 (στρώννυμι); stone-strewed, i.e. a tessellated mosaic on which the Roman tribunal was placed

G2780.strongs: from G2779 (κῆπος) and (a warden); a garden-keeper, i.e. gardener

G2329.strongs: from the base of G2330 (θέρος); warmth

G3382.strongs: perhaps a primary word; a thigh

G2609.thayer: I lead down, bring down, either from a high place on land or to a lower (or actually to the sea-coast), or from the high seas to land.
G2609.mounce: to lead, bring, or conduct down, Acts 9:30; 22:30; 23:15, 20, 28; to bring a ship to land; pass. κατάγομαι, aor. κατήχθην, to come to land, land, touch, Lk. 5:11
G2609.abbottSmith: to bring down : c. acc. pers., Ac 22:30 , Ro 10:6 ; id. seq. εἰς , Ac 9:30 23:15 , 20 , 28 ; as nautical term, to bring to land : τ. πλοῖον ἐπὶ τ. γῆν , Lk 5:11 ; pass., seq. εἰς , Ac 27:3 28:12 .†
G2609.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead down; specially, to moor a vessel

G4528.mounce: Shealtiel, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:12; Lk. 3:27*
G4528.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07597); Salathiel (i.e. Shealtiel), an Israelite

G1363.mounce: to double; to render back double, Rev. 18:6*
G1363.abbottSmith: to double : δ. τὰ διπλᾶ , Re 18:6 .†

G928.thayer: I examine, as by torture; I torment; I buffet, as of waves.
G928.mounce: pr. to apply the lapis Lydius or touchstone; met. to examine, scrutinize, try, either by words or torture; in NT to afflict, torment; pass. to be afflicted, tormented, pained, by diseases, Mt. 8:6, 29, 35; to be tossed, agitated, as by the waves, Mt. 14:24
G928.abbottSmith: prop., to rub on the touchstone , put to the test . to examine by torture , hence, generally, to torture , torment , distress : Mt 8:6 , 29 14:24 , Mk 5:7 6:48 , Lk 8:28 , II Pe 2:8 , Re 9:5 11:10 12:2 14:10 20:10 .†

G934.thayer: courtiers, palaces, a body of kings, royal.
G934.mounce: royal, regal; met. possessed of high prerogatives and distinction, 1 Pet. 2:9; τὰ βασίλεια, i.e. δώματα, regal mansion, palaces, Lk. 7:25*
G934.abbottSmith: royal : I Pe 2:9 (LXX) (v. Hort, in l.).†

G3124.thayer: Malchus, a servant of the high-priest at Jerusalem.
G3124.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H04429); Malchus, an Israelite

G2228.mounce: can function as a conj (298x), either, or, Mt. 6:24; after comparatives, and ἄλλος, ἕτερος, expressed or implied, than, Mt. 10:15; 18:8; Acts 17:21; 24:21; intensive after ἀλλά and πρίν, Lk. 12:51; Mt. 1:18; it also serves to point an interrogation, Rom. 3:29
G2228.abbottSmith: disjunctive, or ; between single words: Mt 5:17 , Mk 6:56 , Lk 2:24 , Jo 6:19 , Ro 1:21 , al.; before a sentence expressing a variation, denial or refutation of a previous statement, freq. in interrog. form: Mt 7:4 , 9 , Mk 12:14 , Lk 13:4 , Ro 3:29 6:3 9:21 , I Co 6:9 , 16 9:6 , II Co 11:7 ; ἢ . . . ἤ , either . . . or , Mt 6:24 , Lk 16:13 , I Co 14:6 ; in a disjunctive question (as Lat. an after
G2228.strongs: a primary particle of distinction between two connected terms; disjunctive, or; comparative, than
G2228.kjv: and, but (either), (n-)either, except it be, (n-)or (else), rather, save, than, that, what, yea

G5169.thayer: a hole; the eye of a needle.
G5169.mounce: a hole; eye of a needle, Mt. 19:24*
G5169.abbottSmith: a hole : τ. ῥαφιδος , eye of a needle , Mt 19:24 (WH, txt., τρῆμα ).†
G5169.strongs: from a derivative of the base of G5168 (τρυμαλιά); an aperture, i.e. a needle's eye

G61.mounce: a catching, thing taken, draught of fishes, Lk. 5:4, 9*
G61.abbottSmith: the chase , a hunting or catching : Lk 5:4 . that which is taken , a catch : of fish, Lk 5:9 .†
G61.strongs: from G71 (ἄγω); (abstractly) a catching (of fish); also (concretely) a haul (of fish)

G817.mounce: improper prep with the gen., without, Lk. 22:6, 35*
G817.abbottSmith: in cl. most freq. in poets; without , apart from : c. gen., Lk 22:6 , 35 (for exx. from π. , v. MM, s.v.).†
G817.strongs: a particle probably akin to G427 (ἄνευ); aloof, i.e. apart from (literally or figuratively)

G5304.abbottSmith: need, want : opp. to τὸ περισσεῦον , Mk 12:44 ; καθ’ ὑ. , Phl 4:11 (eccl.).†
G5304.strongs: from G5302 (ὑστερέω); a falling short, i.e. (specially), penury

G950.thayer: I confirm, ratify, secure, establish; pass: I guarantee.
G950.mounce: to confirm, establish; to render constant and unwavering, 1 Cor. 1:8; to strengthen or establish by arguments or proofs, ratify, Mk. 16:20; to verify, as promises, Rom. 15:8
G950.abbottSmith: to confirm , establish , secure , of things (cl.): λόγον , Mk 16:[20] ; ἐπαγγελίας , Ro 15:8 ; of persons ( DCG , ii, 605): I Co 1:8 , II Co 1:21 . Pass., I Co 1:6 , Col 2:7 , He 2:3 13:9 (as an Attic legal term, to guarantee the validity of a purchase, establish or confirm a title; v. next word, Cremer, 139; cf. δια-β. ).†

G811.abbottSmith: wastefully : Lk 15:13 (EV, in riotous living ; but not necessarily dissolute; cf. MM, ut supr.; Milligan, NTD , 79).†
G811.strongs: adverb from the same as G810 (ἀσωτία); dissolutely

G3060.mounce: reviling, railing; as a subst. a reviler, railer, 1 Cor. 5:11; 6:10*
G3060.abbottSmith: railing , abusive ; as subst., ὁ λ. , a railer : I Co 5:11 6:10 .†

G3653.thayer: an unripe fig, one which, not ripening in due time, grows through the winter and falls off in the spring.
G3653.mounce: an unripe or unseasonable fig, such as, shaded by the foliage, does not ripen at the usual season, but hangs on the trees during winter, Rev. 6:13*
G3653.abbottSmith: an unripe fig , which grows in winter and usually falls off in the spring: Re 6:13 .†
G3653.strongs: of uncertain derivation; an unripe (because out of season) fig

G3593.abbottSmith: to travel , journey : Lk 10:33 (cf. δι- , συν-οδεύω ).†

G0937a.abbottSmith: prop., a petty king : Jo 4:46 , 49 WH, mg. (v.s. βασιλικός ).†

G4057.mounce: much, abundantly, vehemently, Acts 26:11; more, more abundantly, Mt. 27:23; Mk. 10:26; 15:14*
G4057.abbottSmith: beyond measure , exceedingly , abundantly : Mt 27:23 , Mk 10:26 15:14 , Ac 26:11 . Compar. περισσότερον (v.s. περισσός ); περισσοτέρως : II Co 1:12 2:4 7:15 11:23 , Ga 1:14 , Phl 1:14 , I Th 2:17 , He 2:1 13:19 ; π. μᾶλλον , II Co 7:13 ; opp. to ἧττον , II Co 12:15 .†
G4057.kjv: exceedingly, out of measure, the more

G426.thayer: I examine (a person on trial, a witness) judicially (frequently by the aid of torture).
G426.mounce: to examine thoroughly; to examine by torture, Acts 22:24, 29*
G426.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and (to test); to investigate (judicially)

G5336.abbottSmith: a manger : Lk 2:7 , 12 , 16 13:15 (v. DB , iii, 234 a , DCG , ii, 111 a ),†

G5390.mounce: with kindly feeling or manner, courteously, Acts 28:7*
G5390.strongs: adverb from G5391 (φιλόφρων); with friendliness of mind, i.e. kindly

G4509.strongs: of uncertain affinity; dirt, i.e. (morally) depravity

G1744.thayer: I clothe; mid: I enter, creep into.
G1744.strongs: from G1772 (ἔννομος) and G1416 (δύνω); to sink (by implication, wrap (compare G1746 (ἐνδύω)) on, i.e. (figuratively) sneak

G4489.abbottSmith: a public speaker, an orator : Ac 24:1 .†
G4489.strongs: from G4483 (ῥέω); a speaker, i.e. (by implication) a forensic advocate

G4788.thayer: I enclose, shut in, make subject to.
G4788.mounce: to shut up together, to hem in; to enclose, Lk. 5:6; met. to band under a sweeping sentence, Rom. 11:32; Gal. 3:22; pass. to be banded under a bar of disability, Gal. 3:23*
G4788.abbottSmith: to shut together, enclose, shut in on all sides : of a catch of fish, Lk 5:6 ; metaph. ( Ps 30 (31):9 , al.), Ro 11:32 , Ga 3:22, 23 .†
G4788.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2808 (κλείω); to shut together, i.e. include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to

G4130.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary (which appears only as an alternate in certain tenses and in the reduplicated form pimplemi); to "fill" (literally or figuratively (imbue, influence, supply)); specially, to fulfil (time)

G5353.strongs: from G5350 (φθέγγομαι); utterance, i.e. a musical note (vocal or instrumental)

G998.abbottSmith: (poët. -όος ), hasting to the war-cry (Hom.). helping, auxiliary ; as subst. (Hdt.), a helper : He 13:6 (LXX) .†
G998.strongs: from G995 (βοή) and theo (to run); a succorer

G1387.thayer: I act deceitfully, deceive, use fraud.
G1387.mounce: to deceive, use fraud or deceit, Rom. 3:13*
G1387.abbottSmith: to deceive : ἐδολιοῦσαν ( -σαν , freq. in κοινή Gk. for impf. 3rd pers. pl.), Ro 3:13 (LXX) .†

G526.thayer: I estrange, alienate; pass: I am alienated from.
G526.mounce: pass. to be alienated from, be a stranger to; alien, Eph. 2:12; 4:18; Col. 1:21*
G526.abbottSmith: to alienate , estrange ; pass.: Eph 2:12 4:18 , Col 1:21 (MM, VGT , s.v.; Cremer, 95, 633).†
G526.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and a derivative of G245 (ἀλλότριος); to estrange away, i.e. (passively and figuratively) to be non-participant

G2427.thayer: I make sufficient, render fit, qualify.
G2427.mounce: to make sufficient or competent, qualify, 2 Cor. 3:6; Col. 1:12*
G2427.abbottSmith: to make sufficient , render fit : c. dupl. acc., II Co 3:6 ; c. acc. pers. seq. εἰς , Col 1:12 .†
G2427.strongs: from G2425 (ἱκανός); to enable, i.e. qualify

G887.mounce: a mist; darkening, dimness, of the sight, Acts 13:11*
G887.abbottSmith: a mist , esp. a dimness of the eyes: Ac 13:11 (v. Tr., Syn. , § c).†
G887.strongs: of uncertain derivation; dimness of sight, i.e. (probably) a cataract

G4993.thayer: I am of sound mind, am sober-minded, exercise self-control.
G4993.mounce: to be of a sound mind, be in one’s right mind, be sane, Mk. 5:15; Lk. 8:35; to be calm, 2 Cor. 5:13; to be sober-minded, sedate, Tit. 2:6; 1 Pet. 4:7; to be of a modest, humble mind, Rom. 12:3*
G4993.abbottSmith: to be of sound mind or in one's right mind : Mk 5:15 , Lk 8:35 ; opp. to ἐκστῆναι , II Co 5:13 (Hdt.);
G4993.strongs: from G4998 (σώφρων); to be of sound mind, i.e. sane, (figuratively) moderate
G4993.kjv: be in right mind, be sober (minded), soberly

G669.mounce: to speak out, declare, particularly solemn, weighty, or pithy sayings, Acts 2:4, 14; 26:25*
G669.abbottSmith: to speak forth , give utterance : Ac 2:4 , 14 26:25 (MM, s.v.).†
G669.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5350 (φθέγγομαι); to enunciate plainly, i.e. declare

G2677.thayer: a bringing to a condition of fitness, perfecting.
G2677.mounce: a perfectly adjusted adaptation; complete qualification for a specific purpose, Eph. 4:12*

G3150.abbottSmith: idle or foolish talk : I Ti 1:6 .†
G3150.strongs: from G3151 (ματαιολόγος); random talk, i.e. babble

G1489.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G1065 (γέ); if indeed, seeing that, unless, (with negative) otherwise

G3846.mounce: to cast or throw by the side of; absol., a nautical term, to bring to, land, Acts 20:15*
G3846.abbottSmith: to throw to or beside , as fodder to horses (Hom., al.).
G3846.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw alongside, i.e. (reflexively) to reach a place, or (figuratively) to liken

G5307.strongs: from to weave; woven, i.e. (perhaps) knitted

G1316.thayer: I separate myself from, part from, go away.
G1316.abbottSmith: to separate entirely . Mid., to separate oneself, depart : seq. ἀπό Lk 9:33 .†
G1316.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the middle voice of G5563 (χωρίζω); to remove (oneself) wholly, i.e. retire

G4306.thayer: I take thought for beforehand, provide for, practice.
G4306.mounce: to perceive beforehand, foresee; to provide for, 1 Tim. 5:8; mid. to provide for one’s self; by impl. to apply one’s self to a thing, practice, strive to exhibit, Rom. 12:17; 2 Cor. 8:21*
G4306.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G3539 (νοιέω); to consider in advance, i.e. look out for beforehand (actively, by way of maintenance for others; middle voice by way of circumspection for oneself)

G2266.thayer: Herodias (died after A.D. 40), daughter of Aristobulus and granddaughter of Herod I, wife, first, of her uncle Herod, second, of his half-brother, her uncle Herod Antipas.
G2266.mounce: Herodias, pr. name, the wife of Herod Antipas, Mt. 14:3, 6; Mk. 6:17, 19, 22; Lk. 3:19*
G2266.abbottSmith: Herodias , granddaughter of Herod the Great: Mt 14:3 , 6 , Mk 6:17 , 19 , 22 , Lk 3:19 .†
G2266.strongs: from G2264 (Ἡρώδης); Herodias, a woman of the Heodian family

G4897.thayer: I enter together with, embark with.
G4897.mounce: to enter with someone, Jn. 18:15; to embark with, Jn. 6:22*
G4897.abbottSmith: to enter together : c. dat. pers., seq. εἰς , c. acc. loc., Jo 6:22 18:15 .†
G4897.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1525 (εἰσέρχομαι); to enter in company with
G4897.kjv: go in with, go with into

G47.abbottSmith: purity : I Ti 4:12 , 5:2 (Cremer, 58. For exx. of ceremonial use in π. , v. MM, Exp. , iv).†
G47.strongs: from G53 (ἁγνός); cleanliness (the quality), i.e. (specially) chastity

G5376.thayer: Philip, (a) one of the twelve apostles of Jesus, (b) tetrarch of the Ituraean and Trachonitic region, half-brother of Herod Antipas, tetrarch of Galilee, (c) one of the seven original deacons at Jerusalem and a missionary.
G5376.mounce: Philip, pr. name . (1) Philip, the Apostle, Mt. 10:3. (2) Philip, the Evangelist, Acts 6:5. (3) Philip, son of Herod the Great and Mariamne, Mt. 14:3 . (4) Philip, son of Herod the Great and Cleopatra, Mt. 16:13; Lk. 3:1
G5376.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and G2462 (ἵππος); fond of horses; Philippus, the name of four Israelites

G1764.thayer: I place in or upon; only in the intrans. tenses: I impend, am at hand, am present, threaten; as adj: present.
G1764.mounce: to place in or upon; intrans., to stand close upon; to be at hand, impend, to be present, Rom. 8:38; 2 Thess. 2:2; Heb. 9:9
G1764.abbottSmith: to place in ; in pf., plpf., 2 aor. and in mid., intrans.;
G1764.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G2476 (ἵστημι); to place on hand, i.e. (reflexively) impend, (participle) be instant

G4949.thayer: Syrophoenician, i.e. Phoenician (of Syria, in contrast to Carthage and its territory in North Africa).
G4949.mounce: a Syrophoenician woman, Phoenicia being included in Syria, Mk. 7:26*
G4949.abbottSmith: a Syropænician woman : Mk 7:26 , WH, txt., RV (cf. ἡ Φοινίκη Συρία , Diod., 19, 93).†
G4949.strongs: feminine of a compound of G4948 (Σύρος) and the same as G5403 (Φοινίκη); a Syro-phœnician woman, i.e. a female native of Phœnicia in Syria

G841.mounce: a competence of the necessaries of life, 2 Cor. 9:8; a frame of mind viewing one’s lot as sufficient, contentedness, 1 Tim. 6:6*
G841.abbottSmith: sufficiency (MM, s.v.): in subjective sense (v. Milligan, NTD , 57), II Co 9:8 ;
G841.strongs: from G842 (αὐτάρκης); self-satisfaction, i.e. (abstractly) contentedness, or (concretely) a competence

G3369.mounce: not yet, not as yet, Heb. 11:7*
G3369.strongs: from G3366 (μηδέ) and G4452 (-πω); not even yet

G3658.mounce: a mist, fog, a cloud, 2 Pet. 2:17*
G3658.strongs: from the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ) and a derivative of the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι) (meaning a crowd); association together, i.e. a multitude

G46.abbottSmith: uncarded , undressed , i.e. new (MM, VGT , s.v.): Mt 9:16 , Mk 2:21 .†
G46.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the same as G1102 (γναφεύς); properly, unfulled, i.e. (by implication) new (cloth)

G1592.thayer: I deride, scoff at, mock greatly.
G1592.mounce: to mock, deride, scoff at, Lk. 16:14; 23:35*
G1592.abbottSmith: to hold up the nose in derision at, scoff at : c. acc, Lk 16:14 23:35 .†
G1592.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3456 (μυκτηρίζω); to sneer outright at

G2733.strongs: residence (properly, the condition; but by implication, the abode itself)

G4753.mounce: an army, Mt. 22:7; Rev. 19:14, 19; an armed force, corps, Acts 23:10, 27; troops, guards, Lk. 23:11; Rev. 9:16*
G4753.strongs: from G4754 (στρατεύομαι); an armament, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic)

G5518.thayer: a Greek dry measure, equivalent to 1.92 pints.
G5518.mounce: a choenix, an Attic measure for things dry, being the 48th part of a medimnus, consequently equal to the 8th part of the Roman modius, and nearly equivalent to about one quart, being considered a sufficient daily allowance for the sustenance of one man, Rev. 6:6 (2x)*
G5518.abbottSmith: a chænix , a dry measure of rather less than a quart: Re 6:6 (EV, measure ).†
G5518.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a chœnix or certain dry measure

G5063.abbottSmith: of forty years, forty years old: Ac 7:23 13:18 .†
G5063.strongs: from G5062 (τεσσαράκοντα) and G2094 (ἔτος); of forty years of age

G1586.thayer: I pick out for myself, choose, elect, select.
G1586.mounce: to pick out; in NT to choose, select, Lk. 6:13; 10:42; in NT to choose out as the recipients of special favor and privilege, Acts 13:17; 1 Cor. 1:27
G1586.abbottSmith: to pick out, choose . In NT always mid. (exc. Lk 9:35 , ἐκλελεγμένος , WH, ἀγαπητός , R, mg.), to pick out for oneself, choose (cf. M, Pr. , 157 f.): c. acc rei, Lk 10:42 14:7 ; c. acc pers., Ac 6:5 15:22 , 25 ; of Christ (v. supr.), Lk 9:35 ; of Christ's choice of disciples, Lk 6:13 , Jo 6:70 13:18 15:16 , 19 Ac 1:2 ; of the Divine choice: of persons, Mk 13:20 , Ac 1:24 13:17 15:7 , Eph 1:4 , Ja
G1586.strongs: middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its primary sense); to select

G5480.mounce: an imprinted mark, Rev. 13:16, 17; 14:9, 11; 16:2; 19:20; 20:4; sculpture, Acts 17:29*
G5480.abbottSmith: a stamp, impress, mark : Re 13:16, 17 14:9 , 11 16:2 19:20 20:4 (v. Deiss., BS , 240 ff.);
G5480.strongs: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a scratch or etching, i.e. stamp (as a badge of servitude), or scupltured figure (statue)

G988.mounce: slander, railing, reproach, Mt. 15:19; Mk. 7:22; blasphemy, Mt. 12:31; 26:65
G988.abbottSmith: railing slander : Mt 12:31 15:19 , Mk 3:28 7:22 , Eph 4:31 , Col 3:8 , I Ti 6:4 , Ju 9 , Re 2:9 ;

G4902.mounce: to follow with, attend, accompany, Acts 20:4*
G4902.abbottSmith: to follow with, accompany : c. dat. pers., Ac 20:4 .†
G4902.strongs: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and a primary (to follow); to attend (travel) in company with

G2388.thayer: Jannai, an ancestor of Jesus; Jannai was the son of Joseph, and father of Melchi.
G2388.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H03238); Janna, an Israelite

G2580.strongs: of Hebrew origin (compare H07071); Cana, a place in Palestine

G5149.mounce: to creak, grating sound; to gnash, grind the teeth, Mk. 9:18*
G5149.abbottSmith: prop., of sounds by animals, to cry, chirp , etc.
G5149.strongs: apparently a primary verb; to creak (squeak), i.e. (by analogy) to grate the teeth (in frenzy)

G5543.mounce: useful, profitable; good, agreeable, Lk. 5:39; easy, as a yoke, Mt. 11:30; gentle, benign, kind, obliging, gracious, Lk. 6:35; Eph. 4:32; Rom. 2:4; 1 Pet. 2:3; good in character, disposition, etc., virtuous, 1 Cor. 15:33*
G5543.abbottSmith: serviceable, good ; of things, good, pleasant : of food (as often in cl.), οἶνος , Lk 5:39 ; ζυγός , Mt 11:30 (EV, easy ); in ethical sense, ἤθη , I Co 15:33 ; of persons, good, kind, gracious : Eph 4:32 ; of God, Lk 6:35 , I Pe 2:3 ; τ. χρηστὸν (= ἡ χρηστότης ) τοῦ θεοῦ , Ro 2:4 .†
G5543.strongs: from G5530 (χράομαι); employed, i.e. (by implication) useful (in manner or morals)

G2782.mounce: proclamation, proclaiming, public annunciation, Mt. 12:41; public inculcation, preaching, 1 Cor. 2:4; 15:14; meton. what is publicly inculcated, doctrine, Rom. 16:25; Tit. 1:3*
G2782.abbottSmith: in cl., that which is cried by a herald , a proclamation . In NT (v. Lft., Notes , 161), of God's heralds, proclamation , message , preaching (i.e. the substance as distinct from the act which would be expressed by † κήρυξις ): Mt 12:41 , Lk 11:32 , I Co 1:21 , II Ti 4:17 , Tit 1:3 ; c. gen. subjc., τὸ κ. μου , I Co 2:4 ; ἡμῶν , I Co 15:14 ; c. gen. obj., Ἰησοῦ Χρ. , Ro 16:25 .†
G2782.strongs: from G2784 (κηρύσσω); a proclamation (especially of the gospel; by implication, the gospel itself)

G4052.thayer: (a) intrans: I exceed the ordinary (the necessary), abound, overflow; am left over, (b) trans: I cause to abound.
G4052.mounce: to be over and above, to be superfluous, Mt. 14:20; Mk. 12:44; Lk. 21:4; to exist in full quantity, to abound, be abundant, Rom. 5:15; 2 Cor. 1:5; to increase, be augmented, Acts 16:5; to be advanced, be rendered more prominent, Rom. 3:7; of persons, to be abundantly gifted, richly furnished, abound, Lk. 15:17; Rom. 15:13; 1 Cor. 14:12; 2 Cor. 8:7; to be possessed of a full sufficiency, Phil. 4:12, 18; to abound in performance, 1 Cor. 15:58; to be a gainer, 1 Cor. 8:8; in NT trans., to cause to be abundant, 2 Cor. 4:15; 9:8; Eph. 1:8; to cause to be abundantly furnished, cause to abound, 1 Thess. 3:12; pass. to be gifted with abundance, Mt. 13:12; 25:29
G4052.abbottSmith: Prop., intrans., of things, to be over and above the number (Hes.), hence, to be or remain over : Mt 14:20 15:37 , Lk 9:17 , Jo 6:12 , 13 ; to abound , be in abundance : Mk 12:44 , Lk 12:15 21:4 , Ac 16:5 , Ro 3:7 5:15 , II Co 1:5 8:2 9:12 , Phl 1:26 . Of persons, to abound in , have in abundance : I Co 14:12 15:58 , Phl 4:12 , 18 ; c. gen., Lk 15:17 T; to be superior or better , to excel : absol.
G4052.strongs: from G4053 (περισσός); to superabound (in quantity or quality), be in excess, be superfluous; also (transitively) to cause to superabound or excel
G4052.kjv: (make, more) abound, (have, have more) abundance (be more) abundant, be the better, enough and to spare, exceed, excel, increase, be left, redound, remain (over and above)

G3866.thayer: a deposit, anything committed to one's charge or trust.
G3866.mounce: a deposit, a thing committed to one’s charge, a trust, 1 Tim. 6:20; 2 Tim. 1:12; 2 Tim. 1:14*
G3866.abbottSmith: a deposit or trust : I Ti 6:20 , II Ti 1:12 , 14 (for exx. of this form, v. MM, Exp. , iii, xviii).†
G3866.strongs: from G3908 (παρατίθημι); a deposit, i.e. (figuratively) trust
