G4938.mounce: a breaking, bruising; in NT destruction, ruin, Rom. 3:16*
G4938.abbottSmith: a fracture (Arist.; Le, l.c. , al.). Metaph., calamity, destruction ( Isa, l.c. , Wi 3:3 , al.): Ro 3:16 (LXX) .†
G4938.strongs: from G4937 (συντρίβω); concussion or utter fracture (properly, concretely), i.e. complete ruin

G1674.abbottSmith: a Greek (i.e. Gentile ) woman : Mk 7:26 , Ac 17:12 .†
G1674.strongs: feminine of G1672 (Ἕλλην); a Grecian (i.e. non-Jewish) woman

G2532.mounce: (1) and, Mt. 2:2, 3, 11; 4:22; (2) καί ... καί, both ... and; (3) as a cumulative particle, also, too, Mt. 5:39; Jn. 8:19; 1 Cor. 11:6; (4) emphatic, even, also, Mt. 10:30; 1 Cor. 2:10; in NT adversative, but, Mt. 11:19; also introductory of the apodosis of a sentence, Gal. 3:28; Jas. 2:4
G2532.abbottSmith: and . Copulative. Connecting single words; in general: Mt 2:18 16:1 , Mk 2:15 , Lk 8:15 , He 1:1 , al. mult.; repeated before each of the terms in a series, Mt 23:23 , Lk 14:21 , Ro 7:12 9:4 , al.; connecting numerals (WM, § 37, 4): Jo 2:20 , Ac 13:20 ; joining terms which are not mutually exclusive, as the part with the whole: Mt 8:33 26:59 , Mk 16:17 , Ac 5:29 , al. Connecting clauses and senten
G2532.strongs: apparently, a primary particle, having a copulative and sometimes also a cumulative force; and, also, even, so then, too, etc.; often used in connection (or composition) with other particles or small words
G2532.kjv: and, also, both, but, even, for, if, or, so, that, then, therefore, when, yet

G1743.thayer: I fill with power, strengthen, make strong.
G1743.mounce: to empower, invigorate, Phil. 4:13; 1 Tim. 1:12; 2 Tim. 4:17; mid. to summon up vigor, put forth energy, Eph. 6:10; 2 Tim. 2:1; pass. to acquire strength, be invigorated, be strong, Acts 9:22; Rom. 4:20*
G1743.abbottSmith: to make strong, strengthen : c. acc, pers., Phl 4:13 , I Ti 1:12 , II Ti 4:17 . Pass., Ac 9:22 ; c. dat., Ro 4:20 ; seq. ἐν , II Ti 2:1 ( ἐν Κυρίῳ ), Eph 6:10 (Cremer, 221).†
G1743.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G1412 (δυναμόω); to empower

G4261.thayer: I thrust forward, put forth (as of branches), produce.
G4261.mounce: to cast before, project; to put or urge forward, Acts 19:33; to put forth, as a tree its blossoms, etc., Lk. 21:30*
G4261.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw forward, i.e. push to the front, germinate

G1755.mounce: an effect, thing effected, activity, 1 Cor. 12:6; operation, working, 1 Cor. 12:10*

G2225.thayer: I preserve alive (lit: bring to birth), save.
G2225.mounce: pr. to bring forth living creatures; in NT to preserve alive, save, Lk. 17:33; Acts 7:19; 1 Tim. 6:13*
G2225.abbottSmith: in cl., to engender , produce alive , endue with life .
G2225.strongs: from the same as G2226 (ζῶον) and a derivative of G1096 (γίνομαι); to engender alive, i.e. (by analogy) to rescue (passively, be saved) from death

G717.abbottSmith: Har-Magedon (AV, Armageddon ): Re 16:16 (v. Swete, in l., but also Thayer, s.v.).†
G717.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02022 and H04023); Armageddon (or Har-Meggiddon), a symbolic name

G4587.mounce: pr. majesty; gravity, dignity, dignified seriousness, 1 Tim. 2:2; 3:4; Tit. 2:7*
G4587.abbottSmith: gravity : I Ti 2:2 3:4 (Vg. castitas , cf. Soph., Lex. , and Zorell, s.v.), Tit 2:7 .†

G3195.thayer: I intend, am about to; I delay, linger.
G3195.mounce: to be about to, be on the point of, Mt. 2:13; Jn. 4:47; it serves to express in general a settled futurity, Mt. 11:14; Lk. 9:31; Jn. 11:51; to intend, Lk. 10:1; participle μέλλων, μέλλουσα, μέλλον, future as distinguished from past and present, Mt. 12:32; Lk. 13:9; to be always, as it were, about to do, to delay, linger, Acts 22:16
G3195.abbottSmith: to be about to be or do; c. inf. (Bl., § 62, 4; 68, 2; M, Pr. , 114); of intending or being about to do of one's own free will: c. inf. praes., Mt 2:13 , Lk 10:1 , Ac 3:3 5:35 , He 8:5 , II Pe 1:12 (Field, Notes , 240), al.; c. inf. aor. (Bl., § 58, 3), Ac 12:6 , Re 3:16 ; of compulsion, necessity or certainty: c. inf. praes., Mt 16:27 , Lk 9:31 , Jo 6:71 , Ro 4:24 , al.; c. inf. aor., Ro 8:18 , G
G3195.strongs: a strengthened form of G3199 (μέλω) (through the idea of expectation); to intend, i.e. be about to be, do, or suffer something (of persons or things, especially events; in the sense of purpose, duty, necessity, probability, possibility, or hesitation)
G3195.kjv: about, after that, be (almost), (that which is, things, + which was for) to come, intend, was to (be), mean, mind, be at the point, (be) ready, + return, shall (begin), (which, that) should (after, afterwards, hereafter) tarry, which was for, will, would, be yet

G4592.thayer: a sign, miracle, indication, mark, token.
G4592.mounce: a sign, a mark, token, by which anything is known or distinguished, Mt. 16:3; 24:3; 2 Thess. 3:17; a token, pledge, assurance, Lk. 2:12; a proof, evidence, convincing token, Mt. 12:38; 16:1; Jn. 2:18; in NT a sign, wonder, remarkable event, wonderful appearance, extraordinary phenomenon, 1 Cor. 14:22; Rev. 12:1, 3; 15:1; a portent, prodigy, Mt. 24:30; Acts 2:19; a wonderful work, miraculous operation, miracle, Mt. 24:24; Mk. 16:17, 20; meton. a sign, a signal character, Lk. 2:34
G4592.strongs: neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); an indication, especially ceremonially or supernaturally

G5037.mounce: enclitic, can function as a conj., serving either as a lightly-appending link, Acts 1:15; and, Acts 2:3; or as an inclusive prefix, Lk. 12:45; both, Lk. 24:20; Acts 26:16
G5037.abbottSmith: τέ solitarium, and , denoting a closer affinity than καί between words and sentences which it connects (Bl., §77, 8): Mt 28:12 , Jo 4:42 , Ac 2:33 , 37 , 40 10:22 11:21 , al. Denoting a closer connection than simple καί, τὲ . . . καί, τὲ καί, τὲ . . . τέ ( Ac 26:16 Ro 14:8 ), as well . . . as also, both . . . and : Lk 12:45 , Ac 1:1 15:9 21:30 , al.; τὲ . . . δέ , and . . . and , Ac 19:2 ; τὲ γάρ
G5037.strongs: a primary particle (enclitic) of connection or addition; both or also (properly, as correlation of G2532 (καί))
G5037.kjv: also, and, both, even, then, whether

G284.thayer: Amminadab, son of Ram and father of Nahshon, one of the ancestors of Jesus.
G284.mounce: Aminadab, pr. name, indecl., Mt. 1:4; Lk. 3:33*
G284.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H05992); Aminadab, an Israelite

G2454.mounce: Judaism, the character and condition of a Jew; practice of the Jewish religion, Gal. 1:13, 14*
G2454.abbottSmith: Judaism , the observance of Jewish rites : Ga 1:13 , 14 .†
G2454.strongs: from G2450 (Ἰουδαΐζω); "Judaism", i.e. the Jewish faith and usages

G5380.mounce: fond of contention; contentious, disputatious, 1 Cor. 11:16*
G5380.abbottSmith: fond of strife, contentious : I Co 11:16 .†
G5380.strongs: from G5384 (φίλος) and (a quarrel; probably akin to G3534 (νῖκος)); fond of strife, i.e. disputatious

G3517.strongs: apparently from a derivative of the base of G3491 (ναῦς) (meaning wet); Nereus, a Christian

G3788.thayer: the eye; fig: the mind's eye.
G3788.mounce: an eye, Mt. 5:29, 38; 6:23; 7:3, 4, 5; ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός, an evil eye, an envious eye, envy, Mt. 20:15; Mk. 7:22; met. the intellectual eye, Mt. 13:15; Mk. 8:18; Jn. 12:40; Acts 26:18
G3788.abbottSmith: the eye (as in cl., chiefly pl.): Mt 5:38 , Mk 9:47 , Lk 6:41 , Jo 9:6 , al.; τοὺς ὀ. ἐξορύθσσειν (fig.), Ga 4:15 ; ἐπᾶραι , Lk 6:20 , Jo 6:5 ; ἀνοῖξα , Ac 9:40 ; id., of restoring sight, Mt 20:33 , Jo 9:10 , al.; ἐν ῥιπῇ ὀφθαλμοῦ , I Co 15:52 ; by anthropom., of God, He 4:13 , I Pe 3:12 ; pleonastically (cf. Thackeray, Gr. , 42 f.), εἶδον οἱ ὀ. μου , Lk 2:30 (similarly, Lk 4:20 10:23 , Jo 12:40 ,
G3788.strongs: from G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); the eye (literally or figuratively); by implication, vision; figuratively, envy (from the jealous side-glance)

G5248.thayer: (a) intrans: I abound exceedingly, (b) dep: I overflow.
G5248.mounce: to superabound; to abound still more, Rom. 5:20; mid. to be abundantly filled, overflow, 2 Cor. 7:4*
G5248.abbottSmith: to abound more exceedingly : Ro 5:21 . Mid., in same sense (RV, overflow ): c. dat. rei, II Co 7:4 .†
G5248.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4052 (περισσεύω); to super-abound

G4254.thayer: (a) trans: I lead forth; in the judicial sense, into court, (b) intrans. and trans: I precede, go before, (c) intrans: I go too far.
G4254.mounce: to lead, bring, or conduct forth, produce, Acts 12:6; 16:30; 25:26; intrans. to go before, to go first, Mt. 2:9; 21:9; Mk. 6:45; 1 Tim. 5:24; part. προάγων, ουσα, ον, preceding, previous, antecedent, 1 Tim. 1:18; Heb. 7:18; hence, in NT, trans. to precede, Mt. 14:22; to be in advance of, Mt. 21:31
G4254.abbottSmith: prop. trans., to lead on , lead forth or forward : c. acc. pers., Ac 16:30 17:5 ; of bringing forth to trial, Ac 12:6 (WH, txt., προσάγ- ); seq. ἐπί , c. gen. pers., Ac 25:26 .
G4254.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead forward (magisterially); intransitively, to precede (in place or time (participle, previous))

G744.mounce: old, ancient, of former age, Mt. 5:21, 33; of long standing, old, veteran, Acts 21:16; ἀφ’ ἡμερῶν ἀρχαίων, from early days, from an early period, of the Gospel, Acts 15:7

G1451.mounce: some view as an improper prep., followed by gen. or dat., near, as to place, Lk. 19:11; close at hand, Rom. 10:8; near, in respect of ready interposition, Phil. 4:5; near, as to time, Mt. 24:32, 33; near to God, as being in covenant with him, Eph. 2:13; οἱ ἐγγύς, the people near to God, the Jews, Eph. 2:17
G1451.abbottSmith: near ; of place: Jo 19:20 , 42 ; as prep. c. gen. (M, Pr. , 99), Lk 19:11 , Jo 3:23 6:19 , 23 11:18 , 54 , Ac 1:12 ; c. dat., Ac 9:38 27:8 ; superl., ἔγγιστα , Mk 6:36 , WH, mg.; metaph., ֹοἱ ἐ. , opp. to οἱ μακράν , Eph 2:17 ; ἐ. γίνεσθαι , Eph 2:13 ; ἐ. σου τὸ ῥῆμα , Ro 10:8 (LXX) . Of time: Mt 24:32 26:18 , Mk 13:28 , 29 , Lk 21:30 , 31 , Jo 2:13 6:4 7:2 11:55 , Re 1:3 22:10 ; ὁ κύριος ἐ. , Phl
G1451.strongs: from a primary verb (to squeeze or throttle; akin to the base of G43 (ἀγκάλη)); near (literally or figuratively, of place or time)
G1451.kjv: from , at hand, near, nigh (at hand, unto), ready

G1444.strongs: from G1443 (Ἐβέρ); Hebraic or the Jewish language

G3449.mounce: wearisome labor, toil, travail, 2 Cor. 11:27; 1 Thess. 2:9; 2 Thess. 3:8*
G3449.abbottSmith: toil , labour , hardship , distress : II Co 11:27 , I Th 2:9 , II Th 3:8 .†
G3449.strongs: from the base of G3425 (μόγις); toil, i.e. (by implication) sadness

G5564.thayer: a place, piece of land, field, property, estate.
G5564.mounce: a place, spot; Mt. 26:36; Mk. 14:32; a field, farm, estate, domain, Jn. 4:5; Acts 1:18, 19(2x); 4:34; 5:3, 8; 28:7*
G5564.strongs: diminutive of G5561 (χώρα); a spot or plot of ground
G5564.kjv: field, land, parcel of ground, place, possession

G4654.abbottSmith: to darken ; in NT always pass., -ομαι , to be darkened : of the heavenly bodies, Mt 24:29 , Mk 13:24 , Lk 23:45 (Rec., WH, mg.), Re 8:12 . Metaph., of the mind, Ro 1:21 11:10 (LXX) (cf. σκοτόω ).†
G4654.strongs: from G4655 (σκότος); to obscure (literally or figuratively)

G3173.thayer: large, great, in the widest sense.
G3173.mounce: great, large in size, Mt. 27:60; Mk. 4:32; great, much, numerous, Mk. 5:11; Heb. 11:26; great, grown up, adult, Heb. 11:24; great, vehement, intense, Mt. 2:10; 28:8; great, sumptuous, Lk. 5:29; great, important, weighty, of high importance, 1 Cor. 9:11; 13:13; great, splendid, magnificent, Rev. 15:3; extraordinary, wonderful, 2 Cor. 11:15; great, solemn, Jn. 7:37; 19:31; great in rank, noble, Rev. 11:18; 13:16; great in dignity, distinguished, eminent, illustrious, powerful, Mt. 5:19; 18:1, 4; great, arrogant, boastful, Rev. 13:5
G3173.abbottSmith: great ; of external form, bodily size, measure, extent: λίθος , Mt 2:60 ; δράκων , Re 12:3 ; ἰχθῦς , Jo 21:11 ; πόλις , Re 11:8 ; μάχαιρα , Re 6:4 , al. Of intensity and degree: δύναμις , Ac 4:33 ; φόβος , Mk 4:41 ; ἀγάπη , Jo 15:13 ; ἄνεμος , Jo 6:18 ; κραυγή , Ac 23:9 ; φῶς , Mt 4:16 ; πυρετός , Lk 4:38 ; θλίψις , Mt 24:21 . Of rank; of persons: θεός (MM, xvi), Tit 2:13 ; Ἄρτεμις , Ac 19:27 ; co
G3173.strongs: (including the prolonged forms, feminine , plural , etc.; compare also G3176 (μέγιστος), G3187 (μείζων)); big (literally or figuratively, in a very wide application)
G3173.kjv: (+ fear) exceedingly, great(-est), high, large, loud, mighty, + (be) sore (afraid), strong, X to years

G292.thayer: I ward off, defend myself against, resist, defend, assist.
G292.mounce: to ward off; to help, assist; to repel from oneself, resist, make a defence; to assume the office of protector and avenger, Acts 7:24*
G292.abbottSmith: to ward off , etc. Mid. to defend oneself against ; to requite ; = act., to defend , assist ( Is 59:16 ): c. acc. pers., Ac 7:24 (MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G292.strongs: middle voice of a primary verb; to ward off (for oneself), i.e. protect

G2447.mounce: a missile weapon, arrow, dart; venom, poison, Rom. 3:13; Jas. 3:8; rust, Jas. 5:3*
G2447.strongs: perhaps from (to go) or (to send); rust (as if emitted by metals); also venom (as emitted by serpents)

G4192.thayer: (a) labor, toil, (b) pain, anguish, distress, suffering.
G4192.mounce: labor, travail; pain, misery, anguish, Col. 4:13; Rev. 16:10, 11; 21:4*
G4192.strongs: from the base of G3993 (πένης); toil, i.e. (by implication) anguish

G2372.mounce: pr. the soul, mind; hence, a strong passion or emotion of the mind; anger, wrath, Lk. 4:28; Acts 19:28; pl. swellings of anger, 2 Cor. 12:20; Gal. 5:20
G2372.abbottSmith: passion , hot anger , wrath : He 11:27 ; ὀργὴ καὶ θ. , Ro 2:8 , cf. Col 3:8 ; πικρία κ. θ. , Eph 4:31 ; πλησθῆναι, πλήρης θυμοῦ , Lk 4:28 , Ac 19:28 ; θ. ἔχειν , Re 12:12 ; οἶνος τ. θυμοῦ τ. πορνείας (cf. Je 28 (51):7 ), Re 14:8 18:3 (cf. Re 17:2 ); οἶνος τ. θ. τ. θεοῦ , Re 14:10 ; id., τ. ὀργῆς τ. θεοῦ , Re 16:19 19:15 ; ὁ θ. τ. θεοῦ , Re 14:19 15:1 , 7 16:1 ; pl., impulses or outbursts of anger
G2372.strongs: from G2380 (θύω); passion (as if breathing hard)

G265.mounce: an error; sin, offence, Mk. 3:28; 4:12; Rom. 3:25; 1 Cor. 6:18
G265.abbottSmith: an act of disobedience to divine law (Lft., Notes , 273), a sinful deed , a sin : Mk 3:28 , 29 , Ro 3:25 , I Co 6:18 , II Pe 1:9 , WH, mg.; αἰώνιων ἁ. ( DCG , i, 788 a ), Mk 3:29 (for exx. from π. , v. MM, VGT , s.v.).†

G4540.thayer: Samaria, a small district of Palestine, bounded by Galilee on the north, and by Judaea on the south, and taking its name from the city of Samaria, the ancient capital of the kingdom of (northern) Israel.
G4540.mounce: Samaria, the city and region so called, Acts 8:14
G4540.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08111); Samaria (i.e. Shomeron), a city and region of Palestine

G5183.thayer: a Tyrian, an inhabitant of Tyre.
G5183.mounce: a Tyrian, an inhabitant of Tyre, Acts 12:20*
G5183.strongs: from G5184 (Τύρος); a Tyrian, i.e. inhabitant of Tyrus

G1106.mounce: the mind, as the means of knowing and judging; assent, Phlm. 14; purpose, resolution, Acts 20:3; opinion, judgment, 1 Cor. 1:10; 7:40; suggestion, suggested advice, as distinguished from positive injunction, Acts 20:3; 1 Cor. 7:25; 2 Cor. 8:10
G1106.abbottSmith: a means of knowing, a token (Theogn.). In Attic writers, the mind , its operations and results (v. Edwards, Lex. , App. , A); mind, understanding ; purpose, intention : Ac 20:3 ; judgment, opinion : I Co 1:10 , Re 17:13 ; counsel, advice : I Co 7:25 , 40 II Co 8:10 , Phm 14 ; royal purpose, decree (as in Da, I and II Es): Re 17:17 (Cremer, 671). †
G1106.strongs: from G1097 (γινώσκω); cognition, i.e. (subjectively) opinion, or (objectively) resolve (counsel, consent, etc.)
G1106.kjv: advice, + agree, judgment, mind, purpose, will

G299.abbottSmith: of sacrificial victims, without blemish : of Christ, He 9:14 , I Pe 1:19 ; ethically, unblemished , faultless : Eph 1:4 5:27 , Phl 2:15 , Col 1:22 , Ju 24 , Re 14:5 (Cremer, 425, 788; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G299.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and G3470 (μῶμος); unblemished (literally or figuratively)
G299.kjv: without blame (blemish, fault, spot), faultless, unblamable

G1608.mounce: to be given to fornication, indulge in immorality, Jude 7*
G1608.abbottSmith: strengthened form of πoρνεύω , implying excessive indulgence; mid, to give oneself up to fornication : Ju 7 .†
G1608.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4203 (πορνεύω); to be utterly unchaste

G5194.mounce: a transparent stone, crystal; also, glass, Rev. 21:18, 21*
G5194.strongs: perhaps from the same as G5205 (ὑετός) (as being transparent like rain); glass

G5478.thayer: Canaanite, a Biblical and archaic name for Phoenician.
G5478.strongs: from G5477 (Χαναάν); a Chanaanæan (i.e. Kenaanite), or native of gentile Palestine

G2879.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07141); Core (i.e. Korach), an Israelite

G5147.thayer: a worn path, beaten way, road, highway.
G5147.mounce: a beaten track; a road, highway, Mt. 3:3; Mk. 1:3; Lk. 3:4*
G5147.abbottSmith: a beaten track, a path : Mt 3:3 , Mk 1:3 , Lk 3:4 (LXX) .†
G5147.strongs: from (to "rub"; akin to , , and the base of G5131 (τράγος), G5134 (τραῦμα)); a rut or worn track

G3835.abbottSmith: ready to do anything ; in cl., chiefly in bad sense, knavish , crafty : II Co 12:16 ; in good sense, skilful , clever ( Pr 13:1 28:2 ).†
G3835.strongs: from G3956 (πᾶς) and G2041 (ἔργον); all-working, i.e. adroit (shrewd)

G1813.thayer: I plaster, wash over; I wipe off, wipe out, obliterate.
G1813.mounce: pr. to anoint or smear over; hence, to wipe off or away, Rev. 7:17; 21:4; to blot out, obliterate, Col. 2:14; Rev. 3:5; met. to wipe out guilt, Acts 3:19*
G1813.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G218 (ἀλείφω); to smear out, i.e. obliterate (erase tears, figuratively, pardon sin)

G3080.mounce: a loosing; in NT a release from the marriage bond, a divorce, 1 Cor. 7:27*
G3080.abbottSmith: a loosing : of divorce, I Co 7:27 .†
G3080.strongs: from G3089 (λύω); a loosening, i.e. (specially), divorce

G1515.thayer: peace, peace of mind; invocation of peace a common Jewish farewell, in the Hebraistic sense of the health (welfare) of an individual.
G1515.mounce: peace, Lk. 14:32; Acts 12:20; tranquillity, Lk. 11:21; Jn. 16:33; 1 Thess. 5:3; concord, unity, love of peace, Mt. 10:34; Lk. 12:51; meton. the author of peace, Eph. 2:14; from the Hebrew every kind of blessing and good, Lk. 1:79; 2:14, 29; meton. a salutation expressive of good wishes, a benediction, blessing, Mt. 10:13
G1515.abbottSmith: peace ; of public peace, freedom from war: Lk 14:32 , Ac 12:20 24:2 ; of the church, Ac 9:31 . Of peace between persons, concord, agreement : Mt 10:34 , Lk 12:51 , Ro 14:17 , I Co 7:15 , Ga 5:22 , Ja 3:18 ; ζητεῖν εἰ. , I Pe 3:11 ; διώκειν , II Ti 2:22 ; ib. seq. μετὰ πάντων , He 12:14 ; by meton., of him who brings peace, Eph 2:14 . As in LXX (= Heb. שָׁלוֹם , Aram. שְׁלָם ), of a state of security
G1515.strongs: probably from a primary verb (to join); peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
G1515.kjv: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again

G2878.thayer: a gift, offering, anything consecrated to God.
G2878.mounce: temple treasury, the sacred treasury, Mt. 27:6*
G2878.abbottSmith: an offering , a gift offered to God: Mk 7:11 (cf. κορβανᾶς ).†
G2878.strongs: of Hebrew and Chaldee origin respectively (H07133); a votive offering and the offering; a consecrated present (to the Temple fund); by extension (the latter term) the Treasury itself, i.e. the room where the contribution boxes stood

G1494.thayer: (of meat), sacrificed to an image (or an idol).
G1494.abbottSmith: sacrificed to idols : τὸ, τὰ εἰ , Ac 15:29 21:25 , I Co 8:1 , 4 , 7 , 10 10:19 , Re 2:14 , 20 .†
G1494.strongs: neuter of a compound of G1497 (εἴδωλον) and a presumed derivative of G2380 (θύω); an image-sacrifice, i.e. part of an idolatrous offering
G1494.kjv: (meat, thing that is) offered (in sacrifice, sacrificed) to (unto) idols

G1589.thayer: a choosing out, selecting, choice (by God).
G1589.mounce: the act of choosing out, election; in NT election to privilege by divine grace, Rom. 9:11; 11:5, 28; 1 Thess. 1:4; 2 Pet. 1:10; ἡ ἐκλογή, the elect, Rom. 11:7; ἐκλογῆς, equivalent to ἐκλεκτόν, by Hebraism, Acts 9:15*
G1589.abbottSmith: a choice, selection ; in NT, always of the Divine choice (EV, election): σκεῦος ἐκλογῆς , gen. qual., a chosen vessel; κατ ἐ. , Ro 9:11 11:5 , 28 ; c. gen. pers., I Th 1:4 , II Pe 1:10 ; by meton., ἡ ἐ. = οἱ ἐκλεκτοί , Ro 11:7 .†

G141.thayer: disposed to form sects, sectarian, heretical, factious.
G141.mounce: one who creates or fosters factions, Tit. 3:10*
G141.strongs: from the same as G140 (αἱρετίζω); a schismatic

G1588.thayer: chosen out, elect, choice, select, sometimes as subst: of those chosen out by God for the rendering of special service to Him (of the Hebrew race, particular Hebrews, the Messiah, and the Christians).
G1588.mounce: chosen out, selected; in NT chosen as a recipient of special privilege, elect, Col. 3:12; specially beloved, Lk. 23:35; possessed of prime excellence, exalted, 1 Tim. 5:21; choice, precious, 1 Pet. 2:4, 6
G1588.abbottSmith: choice, select (cl., rarely; Thuc., Plat., al.), hence, eminent : Ro 16:13 (cf. Ez 27:24 ).
G1588.strongs: from G1586 (ἐκλέγομαι); select; by implication, favorite

G5232.thayer: I increase exceedingly or beyond measure.
G5232.abbottSmith: to increase beyond measure : II Th 1:3 (v. Lft., Notes , 98).†
G5232.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G837 (αὐξάνω); to increase above ordinary degree

G4923.thayer: a company traveling together, a caravan.
G4923.mounce: pr. a journeying together; meton. a company of fellow travellers, caravan, Lk. 2:44*
G4923.strongs: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3598 (ὁδός) ("synod"); companionship on a journey, i.e. (by implication), a caravan

G4567.mounce: an adversary, opponent, enemy, perhaps, Mt. 16:23; Mk. 8:33; Lk. 4:8; elsewhere, Satan, the devil, Mt. 4:10; Mk. 1:13
G4567.abbottSmith: Satan (i.e. the adversary , as in LXX, ll. c.): Mt 4:10 12:26 , Mk 1:13 3:23 , 26 4:15 , Lk 10:18 11:18 13:16 22:3 , 31 , Jo 13:27 , Ac 5:3 26:18 , Ro 16:20 , I Co 5:5 7:5 , II Co 2:11 11:14 , I Th 2:18 , II Th 2:9 , I Ti 1:20 5:15 , Re 2:9 , 13 , 24 3:9 12:9 20:2 , 7 ; addressed in person of Peter, Mt 16:23 Mk 8:33 ; ἄγγελος Σατανᾶ , II Co 12:7 .†
G4567.strongs: of Chaldee origin corresponding to H04566 (with the definite affix); the accuser, i.e. the devil

G622.thayer: (a) I kill, destroy, (b) I lose, mid: I am perishing (the resultant death being viewed as certain).
G622.mounce: to destroy utterly; to kill, Mt. 2:13; to bring to nought, make void, 1 Cor. 1:19; to lose, be deprived of, Mt. 10:42; to be destroyed, perish, Mt. 9:17; to be put to death, to die, Mt. 26:52; to be lost, to stray, Mt. 10:6
G622.abbottSmith: Act., to destroy utterly , destroy , kill : Mk 1:24 , 9:22 , al.; τ. ψνχήν , Mt 10:28 , al.; to lose utterly : Mt 10:42 , al.; metaph., of failing to save, Jo 6:39 18:9 .
G622.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G3639 (ὄλεθρος); to destroy fully (reflexively, to perish, or lose), literally or figuratively

G4701.mounce: a sowing; seed sown; met. generative seed, generation, 1 Pet. 1:23*
G4701.strongs: from G4687 (σπείρω); a sowing, i.e. (by implication) parentage

G1832.mounce: 3rd person sing of the unused ἔχειμι (#1997) used impersonally, it is possible; it is permitted, it is lawful, Mt. 12:2, 4; Mk. 3:4; Lk. 6:9; Acts 22:25; 1 Cor. 6:12
G1832.strongs: third person singular present indicative of a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and G1510 (εἰμί); so also neuter present participle of the same (with or without some form of G1510 (εἰμί) expressed); impersonally, it is right (through the figurative idea of being out in public)

G4069.thayer: Persis, name of a Christian lady in Rome.
G4069.strongs: a Persian woman; Persis, a Christian female

G286.thayer: a lamb (as a type of innocence, and with sacrificial connotation).
G286.abbottSmith: a lamb : fig., of Christ ( DCG , ii, 620 b ), Jo 1:29 , 36 , Ac 8:32 (LXX) , I Pe 1:19 (cf. ἀρνίον ; Cremer, 102, 635).†
G286.strongs: apparently a primary word; a lamb

G2906.thayer: (a) a shout, cry, clamor, (b) outcry, clamoring against another.
G2906.mounce: a cry, outcry, clamor, shouting, Mt. 25:6; Lk. 1:42; Acts 23:9; Eph. 4:31; a cry of sorrow, wailing, lamentation, Rev. 21:4; a cry for help, earnest supplication, Heb. 5:7*
G2906.abbottSmith: crying , outcry , clamour : Mt 25:6 , Lk 1:42 , Ac 23:9 , Eph 4:31 , He 5:7 , Re 21:4 .†
G2906.strongs: from G2896 (κράζω); an outcry (in notification, tumult or grief)

G191.thayer: I hear, listen, comprehend by hearing; pass: is heard, reported.
G191.mounce: some list the future active as a middle deponent, ἀκούσομαι, to hear; to hearken, listen to, Mk. 4:3; Lk. 19:48; to heed, obey, Mt. 18:15; Acts 4:19; to understand, 1 Cor. 14:2; to take in or admit to mental acceptance, Mk. 4:33; Jn. 8:43, 47
G191.abbottSmith: to hear , listen , attend , perceive by hearing , comprehend by hearing . Intrans.: Mk 4:3 7:37 , Ja 2:5 , Re 2:7 , al.; τ. ὠσίν , Mt 13:15 (LXX) ; c. cogn. dat., ακοῇ ἀ. (v.s. ἀκοή ), Mt 13:14 , Ac 28:26 (LXX) ; ὁ ἔχων ὦτα ( οὖς ) ἀκούειν, ἀκουσάτω , Mt 11:15 , Mk 4:23 , Re 2:7 , al. Trans., prop. c. acc. rei, of thing heard, gen. pers., from whom heard (LS, s.v.): Ac 1:4 ; c. acc. rei, Mt 12:19
G191.strongs: a primary verb; to hear (in various senses)
G191.kjv: give (in the) audience (of), come (to the ears), (shall) hear(-er, -ken), be noised, be reported, understand

G1356.mounce: which fell from Jupiter, or heaven; τοῦ διοπετοῦς, i.e. ἀγάλματος, image or statue; for discussion of ellipsis see grammars, Acts 19:35*
G1356.abbottSmith: fallen from heaven (R, mg.): τὸ δ. (sc. ἄγαλμα , statue, image ), Ac 19:35 .†
G1356.strongs: from the alternate of G2203 (Ζεύς) and the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω); sky-fallen (i.e. an aerolite)

G2126.thayer: (lit: handling well, hence) cautious, circumspect; hence: God-fearing, pious.
G2126.mounce: pr. taking hold of well, i.e., warily; hence, cautious, circumspect; full of reverence towards God, devout, pious, religious, Lk. 2:25; Acts 2:5; 8:2; 22:12*
G2126.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); taking well (carefully), i.e. circumspect (religiously, pious)

G313.thayer: I beget again, beget into a new life.
G313.mounce: to beget or bring forth again; to regenerate, 1 Pet. 1:3, 23*
G313.abbottSmith: to beget again : metaph., of spiritual birth, I Pe 1:3 1:23 (cf. Cremer, 147; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G313.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G1080 (γεννάω); to beget or (by extension) bear (again)

G5156.mounce: pr. a trembling, quaking; trembling from fear, fear, terror, agitation of mind, Mk. 16:8; anxious, under solemn responsibility, 1 Cor. 2:3; reverence, veneration, awe, 2 Cor. 7:15; Eph. 6:5; Phil. 2:12*
G5156.abbottSmith: trembling, quaking , esp. from fear: Mk 16:8 ; φόβος κ. τ. (as in Ge 9:2 , Ex 15:16 , De 2:25 , Is 19:16 , al.), I Co 2:3 , II Co 7:15 , Eph 6:5 , Phl 2:12 .†
G5156.strongs: from G5141 (τρέμω); a "trembling", i.e. quaking with fear

G5127.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5127.strongs: genitive case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); of (from or concerning) this (person or thing)
G5127.kjv: here(-by), him, it, + such manner of, that, thence(-forth), thereabout, this, thus

G5224.thayer: things which one possesses, goods, property, belongings.
G5224.mounce: to begin; to come into existence; to exist; to be, subsist, Acts 19:40; 28:18; to be in possession, to belong, Acts 3:6; 4:37; goods, possessions, property, Mt. 19:21; Lk. 8:3; to be, Lk. 7:25; 8:41
G5224.strongs: neuter plural of present participle active of G5225 (ὑπάρχω) as noun; things extant or in hand, i.e. property or possessions
G5224.kjv: goods, that which one has, things which (one) possesseth, substance, that hast

G5531.strongs: probably the same as the base of G5530 (χράομαι); to loan

G339.mounce: to set up; intrans. to sit up, Lk. 7:15; Acts 9:40*
G339.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G2523 (καθίζω); properly, to set up, i.e. (reflexively) to sit up

G2634.thayer: I exercise authority over, overpower, master.
G2634.mounce: to get into one’s power; in NT to bring under, master, overcome, Acts 19:16; to domineer over, Mt. 20:25; Mk. 10:42; 1 Pet. 5:3*
G2634.abbottSmith: to gain or exercise dominion (over) ; absol. (Arist.); c. gen. (Arist., al.): Mt 20:25 , Mk 10:42 , Ac 19:16 , I Pe 5:3 .†
G2634.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2961 (κυριεύω); to lord against, i.e. control, subjugate
G2634.kjv: exercise dominion over (lordship), be lord over, overcome

G4210.mounce: contracted form is πορφύρεος, purple, Jn. 19:2, 5; purple clothing, Rev. 17:4; 18:16*
G4210.abbottSmith: purple : Jo 19:2 , 5 ; as subst., πορφυροῦν (sc. ἱμάτιον ), Re 17:4 18:6 .†
G4210.strongs: from G4209 (πορφύρα); purpureal, i.e. bluish red

G4480.mounce: a carriage with four wheels for travelling, a chariot, Rev. 18:13*
G4480.strongs: of Latin origin; a rheda, i.e. four-wheeled carriage (wagon for riding)

G1564.abbottSmith: of place, thence : Mt 4:21 , Mk 6:1 , al.

G494.thayer: Antipatris, a town, where was a Roman colony, on the road between Caesarea and Jerusalem.
G494.mounce: Antipatris, pr. name, Acts 23:31*. See PhotoGuide.
G494.abbottSmith: Antipatris , bet. Joppa and Caesarea: Ac 23:31 .†
G494.strongs: from the same as G493 (Ἀντίπας); Antipatris, a place in Palestine

G228.thayer: true (lit: made of truth), real, genuine.
G228.mounce: sterling, Lk. 16:11; real, Jn. 6:32; 1 Thess. 1:9; unfeigned, trustworthy, true, Jn. 19:35

G911.mounce: to dip, Jn. 13:26; Lk. 16:24; to dye, Rev. 19:13*
G911.abbottSmith: to dip : Lk 16:24 , Jo 13:26 ( ἐμβ- , L);
G911.strongs: a primary verb; to whelm, i.e. cover wholly with a fluid; in the New Testament only in a qualified or special sense, i.e. (literally) to moisten (a part of one's person), or (by implication) to stain (as with dye)

G3319.thayer: middle, in the middle, between, in the midst of.
G3319.mounce: mid, middle, Mt. 25:6; Acts 26:13; ἀνὰ μέσον, in the midst; from the Hebrew, in, among, Mt. 13:25; between, 1 Cor. 6:5; διὰ μέσου, through the midst of, Lk. 4:30; εἰς τὸ μέσον, into, or in the midst, Mk. 3:3; Lk. 6:8; ἐκ μέσου, from the midst, out of the way, Col. 2:14; 2 Thess. 2:7; from the Hebrew, from, from among, Mt. 13:49; ἐν τῷ μέσῳ, in the midst, Mt. 10:16; in the midst, in public, publicly, Mt. 14:6 ἐν μέσῳ, in the midst of; among, Mt. 18:20; κατὰ μέσον τῆς νυκτός, about midnight, Acts 27:27
G3319.abbottSmith: middle , in the middle or midst ; prop., as an adj.: Lk 23:45 , Jo 19:18 , Ac 1:18 ; c. gen. pl., Lk 22:55 , Jo 1:26 ; gen. temp. (Bl., § 36, 13), μέσης νυκτός , Mt 25:6 ; μ. ἡμέρας , Ac 26:13 . In adverbial phrases, neut., μέσον, τὸ μ. , as subst.: ἀνὰ μέσον , c. gen., between (cl.; in LXX: Ge 1:4 , al.), elliptically (but v. M, Pr. , 99), I Co 6:5 ; = ἐν μ. , among , in the midst of (Bl., § 39,
G3319.strongs: from G3326 (μετά); middle (as an adjective or (neuter) noun)
G3319.kjv: among, X before them, between, + forth, mid(-day, -night), midst, way

G4003.strongs: from G4002 (πέντε) and G2532 (καί) and G1182 (δέκατος); five and tenth

G1194.mounce: to skin, flay; hence, to eat, scourge, beat, Mt. 21:35; Mk. 12:3, 5; 13:9
G1194.strongs: a primary verb; properly, to flay, i.e. (by implication) to scourge, or (by analogy) to thrash

G4113.thayer: a street, public square, broad way.
G4113.mounce: a street, broad way, Mt. 6:5; 12:19; Lk. 10:10
G4113.strongs: feminine of G4116 (πλατύς); a wide "plat" or "place", i.e. open square

G1300.mounce: to complete, finish; intrans. to continue, persevere, in a certain state or course of action, Acts 27:33*
G1300.abbottSmith: prop. trans., to accomplish ; used with ellipse of obj. as intrans. (Bl., § 81, 1), and joined to participles (in Ac, l.c., to adj.; v. Bl., § 73, 4) with adverbial sense (= continuously ), to continue : Ac 27:33 .†
G1300.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5055 (τελέω); to accomplish thoroughly, i.e. (subjectively) to persist

G943.thayer: a "bath," a liquid measure among the Jews, containing 72 sextarii, that is, between eight and nine gallons.
G943.mounce: a bath, a measure for liquids, which is stated by Josephus (Ant. 8.57) to contain seventy-two sextarii, or about thirteen and one half gallons. Others estimate it to be nine gallons; and others, seven and one half gallons, Lk. 16:6*
G943.abbottSmith: bath , a Jewish liquid measure, = μετρητής (q.v.), or about 8 3/4 gals.: Lk 16:6 .†
G943.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01324); a bath, or measure for liquids

G4921.thayer: I place together, commend, prove, exhibit; instrans: I stand with; I am composed of, cohere.
G4921.mounce: also spelled συνιστάνω and συνιστάω, to place together; to recommend to favorable attention, Rom. 16:1; 2 Cor. 3:1; 10:18; to place in a striking point of view, Rom. 3:5; 5:8; Gal. 2:18; to stand beside, Lk. 9:32; to have been permanently framed, Col. 1:17; to possess consistence, 2 Pet. 3:5
G4921.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2476 (ἵστημι) (including its collateral forms); to set together, i.e. (by implication) to introduce (favorably), or (figuratively) to exhibit; intransitively, to stand near, or (figuratively) to constitute

G5050.mounce: a completing; a fulfillment, an accomplishment of predictions, promised, etc., Lk. 1:45; finality of function, completeness of operation and effect, Heb. 7:11*
G5050.strongs: from G5448 (φυσιόω); (the act) completion, i.e. (of prophecy) verification, or (of expiation) absolution

G5130.mounce: this, this person or thing, Mt. 3:3, 9, 17; 8:9; 10:2; 24:34, et al. freq.; used by way of contempt, this fellow, Mt. 13:55; 27:47; αὐτὸ τοῦτο, this very thing, this same thing, 2 Cor. 2:3; 7:11; εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο, and elliptically, αὐτὸ τοῦτο, for this same purpose, on this account, Eph. 6:18, 22; 2 Pet. 1:5; καὶ οὗτος, and moreover, Lk. 7:12; 16:1; 20:30; καὶ τοῦτο, and that too, 1 Cor. 6:6, 8; tοῦτο μὲν ... τοῦτο δέ, partly ... partly, Heb. 10:33
G5130.abbottSmith: this ; as subst., this one , he ; absol.: Mt 3:17 , Mk 9:7 , Lk 7:44 , 45 , Jo 1:15 , Ac 2:15 , al.; expressing contempt (cl.), Mt 13:55 , 56 , Mk 6:2 , 3 , Jo 6:42 , al.; εἰς τοῦτο , Mk 1:38 , Ro 14:9 ; μετὰ τοῦτο ( ταῦτα ; V. Westc. on Jo 5:1 ), Jo 2:12 11:7 , al.; epanaleptic (referring to what precedes): Mt 5:19 , Mk 3:35 , Lk 9:48 , Jo 6:46 , Ro 7:10 , al.; proleptic (referring to what follow
G5130.strongs: genitive case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); of (from or concerning) these (persons or things)
G5130.kjv: such, their, these (things), they, this sort, those

G4063.mounce: to run about, run up and down, Mk. 6:55*
G4063.abbottSmith: to run about : c. acc. loc., Mk 6:55 .†
G4063.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to run around, i.e. traverse

G1684.thayer: I step in; I go onboard a ship, embark.
G1684.mounce: to step in; to go on board a ship, embark, Mt. 8:23; 9:1; 13:2
G1684.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to walk on, i.e. embark (aboard a vessel), reach (a pool)
G1684.kjv: come (get) into, enter (into), go (up) into, step in, take ship

G1748.thayer: I lie in wait (ambush) for, seek to entrap (hence: I defraud, deceive).
G1748.mounce: to lie in wait or ambush for, Acts 23:21; to endeavor to entrap, Lk. 11:54*
G1748.abbottSmith: to lie in wait for : c. acc pers., Lk 11:54 , Ac 23:21 .†
G1748.strongs: from G1747 (ἐνέδρα); to lurk, i.e. (figuratively) plot assassination

G2729.thayer: I prevail against, overpower, get the upper hand.
G2729.mounce: to overpower, Mt. 16:18; absol. to predominate, get the upper hand, Lk. 21:36; 23:23*
G2729.abbottSmith: to overpower , prevail against , prevail : absol., Lk 23:23 ; c. inf., Lk 21:36 ; c. gen., Mt 16:18 .†
G2729.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2480 (ἰσχύω); to overpower

G1207.thayer: second-first, a word of uncertain meaning.
G1207.abbottSmith: second-first (in what sense, there is no satisfactory explanation. The reading is prob. not original, v. ICC , in l.; DCG , i, 411; ii, 541, 724): Lk 6:1 , WH, mg., R, mg.†
G1207.strongs: from G1208 (δεύτερος) and G4413 (πρῶτος); second-first, i.e. (specially) a designation of the Sabbath immediately after the Paschal week (being the second after Passover day, and the first of the seven Sabbaths intervening before Pentecost)

G195.thayer: strictness, accuracy, exactness, attention to detail, scrupulousness.
G195.mounce: accuracy, exactness; preciseness, or rigor, severe discipline, Acts 22:3*
G195.abbottSmith: exactness , precision (for exx., v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Ac 22:3 .†
G195.strongs: from the same as G196 (ἀκριβέστατος); exactness

G4272.thayer: I give before, give forth, betray.
G4272.mounce: to give before, precede in giving; Rom. 11:35*
G4272.abbottSmith: to give before , give first : Ro 11:35 ( Jb 41:2 ( 11 ) LXX, al.).
G4272.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G1325 (δίδωμι); to give before the other party has given

G518.thayer: I report (from one place to another), bring a report, announce, declare.
G518.mounce: to announce that with which a person is charged, or which is called for by circumstances; to carry back word, Mt. 2:8; to report, Mt. 8:33; to declare plainly, Heb. 2:12; to announce formally, 1 Jn. 1:2, 3
G518.abbottSmith: of a messenger, speaker, or writer, to report , announce , declare : c. acc. rei, Ac 4:23 , al.; c. dat. pers., Mt 2:8 , al.; seq. ὅτι , Lk 18:37 ; πῶς , Lk 8:36 ; περί , Lk 7:18 13:1 , Ac 28:21 , I Th 1:9 ; λέγων , Ac 22:26 ; c. acc. et inf., Ac 12:14 ; seq. εἰς , Mk 5:14 , Lk 8:34 (MM, VGT , s.v.; Cremer, 25).
G518.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to announce
G518.kjv: bring word (again), declare, report, shew (again), tell

G4065.mounce: to contemplate, reflect on; to despise, disregard, Tit. 2:15*
G4065.abbottSmith: to examine on all sides , consider carefully (Aristoph.).
G4065.strongs: from G4012 (περί) and G5426 (φρονέω); to think beyond, i.e. depreciate (contemn)

G1423.mounce: with difficulty, hardly, Mt. 19:23; Mk. 10:23; Lk. 18:24*

G2607.mounce: to determine against, condemn, blame, reprehend, Gal. 2:11; 1 Jn. 3:20, 21
G2607.abbottSmith: to blame , condemn : c. gen. pers., I Jo 3:20 , 21 ; pass., κατεγνωσμένος ἦν (RV, he stood condemned , v. Ellie., in l.; but cf. Field, Notes , 188): Ga 2:11 .†
G2607.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1097 (γινώσκω); to note against, i.e. find fault with

G1654.thayer: abstr: alms-giving, charity; concr: alms, charity.
G1654.mounce: pity, compassion; in NT an act of kindness, alms, almsgiving, Mt. 6:2, 3, 4; Lk. 11:41
G1654.strongs: from G1656 (ἔλεος); compassionateness, i.e. (as exercised towards the poor) beneficence, or (concretely) a benefaction

G2176.thayer: (lit: well-named, to avoid the evil omen attaching to the left), on the left-hand side, left.
G2176.mounce: of good name or omen; used also as an euphemism by the Greeks instead of ἀριστερός, which was a word of bad import, as all omens on the left denoted misfortune; the left, Mt. 20:21, 23; 25:33, 41
G2176.strongs: from G2095 (εὖ) and G3686 (ὄνομα); properly, well-named (good-omened), i.e. the left (which was the lucky side among the pagan Greeks); neuter as adverbial, at the left hand

G4156.thayer: strangled (i.e. killed without letting out the blood).
G4156.mounce: strangled, suffocated; in NT τὸ πνικτόν, the flesh of animals killed by strangulation or suffocation, Acts 15:20, 29; 21:25*
G4156.strongs: from G4155 (πνίγω); throttled, i.e. (neuter concretely) an animal choked to death (not bled)

G2884.mounce: a cor, the largest Jewish measure for things dry, equal to the homer, and about fifteen bushels English, according to Josephus, Lk. 16:7*
G2884.abbottSmith: a cor , a Hebrew measure (AV, RV, measure ): Lk 16:7 .†
G2884.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03734); a cor, i.e. a specific measure

G3402.mounce: an imitator, follower, 1 Cor. 4:16; 11:1; Eph. 5:1; 1 Thess. 1:6; 2:14; Heb. 6:12*
G3402.abbottSmith: in NT always (like the verb) in good sense, an imitator : I Co 4:16 11:1 , Eph 5:1 , I Th 1:6 2:14 , He 6:12 .†

G4289.mounce: ready in mind, prepared, prompt, willing, Mt. 26:41; Mk. 14:38; τὸ πρόθυμον, i.q. ἡ προθυμία, readiness, eagerness of mind, Rom. 1:15*
G4289.abbottSmith: willing , ready : Mt 26:41 , Mk 14:38 ; neut., τὸ π. = ἡ προθυμία (Thuc., al., III Mac 5:26 ), οὕτως τὸ κατ’ ἐμὲ (= cl. τὸ ἐμὸν ) π. , Ro 1:15 (but v. ICC , in l.).†
G4289.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2372 (θυμός); forward in spirit, i.e. predisposed; neuter (as noun) alacrity

G5049.strongs: adverb from G5046 (τέλειος); completely, i.e. (of hope) without wavering

G4723.mounce: barren, incapable of bearing children, Lk. 1:7, 36; 23:29; Gal. 4:27; Heb. 11:11*
G4723.abbottSmith: barren : of a woman, Lk 1:7 , 36 23:29 , Ga 4:27 (LXX) .†
G4723.strongs: a contraction from G4731 (στερεός) (as stiff and unnatural); "sterile"

G4939.thayer: one brought up with, a foster brother, an intimate friend.
G4939.mounce: nursed with another; one brought up (NIV) or educated with another, intimate friend, friend of the court (RSV) Acts 13:1*
G4939.abbottSmith: prop., one nourished or brought up with, a foster-brother : Ac 13:1 EV.
G4939.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G5162 (τροφός) (in a passive sense); a fellow-nursling, i.e. comrade

G1075.thayer: I put into a genealogy, reckon my descent.
G1075.mounce: to reckon one’s descent, derive one’s origin, Heb. 7:6*
G1075.abbottSmith: to trace ancestry, reckon genealogy ; pass., seq. ἐκ : He 7:6 .†
G1075.strongs: from G1074 (γενεά) and G3056 (λόγος); to reckon by generations, i.e. trace in genealogy

G936.thayer: (a) I rule, reign, (b) I reign over.
G936.mounce: to possess regal authority, be a king, reign; to rule, govern, Mt. 2:22; met. to be in force, predominate, prevail, Rom. 5:14, 17, 21; met. to be in kingly case, fare royally, 1 Cor. 4:8
G936.abbottSmith: to be king , to reign , rule : I Ti 6:15 ; c. gen. (cl.), Mt 2:22 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc. (= Heb. מלךְ על ; Bl., § 36, 8), Lk 1:33 19:14 , 27 , Ro 5:14 ; ἐπὶ τ. γῆς , on earth , Re 5:10 ; of God, Re 11:15 , 17 19:6 ; of Christ, Lk 1:33 , I Co 15:25 , Re 11:15 ; of Christians, Re 5:10 20:4 (constative aor., M, Pr. , 130), Re 20:6 22:5 . Metaph., Christians, Ro 5:17 , I Co 4:8 ; θάνατος , Ro 5:14 , 17
G936.strongs: from G935 (βασιλεύς); to rule (literally or figuratively)

G4601.thayer: intrans: I am silent, keep silence; trans: I keep secret; pass: I am kept secret.
G4601.mounce: to be silent, keep silence, Lk. 9:36; 20:26; Acts 15:12f.; 1 Cor. 14:28, 30, 34; Lk. 18:39; trans. to keep in silence, not to reveal, to conceal; pass. to be concealed, not to be revealed, Rom. 16:25*
G4601.abbottSmith: intrans., to be silent, keep silence : Lk 9:36 18:39 20:26 , Ac 12:17 15:12, 13 , I Co 14:28 , 30 , 34 .
G4601.strongs: from G4602 (σιγή); to keep silent (transitively or intransitively)

G1152.strongs: probably from the base of G1150 (δαμάζω); perhaps gentle; Damaris, an Athenian woman

G4817.thayer: I reason, reckon with myself, consider.
G4817.mounce: to reason together; to consider, deliberate, reason, Lk. 20:5*
G4817.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G3049 (λογίζομαι); to reckon together (with oneself), i.e. deliberate

G722.abbottSmith: later form of ἀρόω , to plough (MM, s.v.): Lk 17:7 , I Co 9:10 .†

G4803.strongs: from G4802 (συζητέω); mutual questioning, i.e. discussion

G3418.mounce: pr. a memorial, monument; a tomb, sepulchre, Mk. 5:3, 5; Lk. 8:27; 23:53; 24:1; Acts 2:29; 7:16; Rev. 11:9*
G3418.strongs: from G3415 (μνάομαι); a memorial, i.e. sepulchral monument (burial-place)

G1962.thayer: I nod to, assent to, consent.
G1962.mounce: to nod to; met. to assent to, consent, Acts 18:20*
G1962.abbottSmith: to nod in command or approval, to nod approval, consent : Ac 18:20 .†
G1962.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3506 (νεύω); to nod at, i.e. (by implication) to assent

G4705.abbottSmith: in haste ; hence, zealous, eager, diligent, earnest : seq. ἐν , II Co 8:22 ; compar., -οτεροσς ib. 17 (here in superl. sense, v. Bl., § 44, 3) 22 .†

G2625.thayer: I cause to recline at table; mid. and pass: I recline at table.
G2625.mounce: to cause to lie down, cause to recline at table, Lk. 9:14, 15; mid. to lie down, recline, Lk. 7:36; 14:8; 24:30*
G2625.abbottSmith: to lay down , make to lie down , esp. for meals: c. acc. pers., Lk 9:14 , 15 ; mid., to recline at meals: Lk 7:36 24:30 ; seq. εἰς , Lk 14:8 (cf. Jth 12:15 ).†
G2625.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2827 (κλίνω); to recline down, i.e. (specially) to take a place at table

G4907.thayer: a putting together in the mind, hence: understanding, practical discernment, intellect.
G4907.mounce: pr. a sending together, a junction, as of streams; met. understanding, intelligence, discernment, Lk. 2:47; 1 Cor. 1:19; meton. the understanding, intellect, mind, Mk. 12:33; Eph. 3:4; Col. 1:9; 2:2; 2 Tim. 2:7*
G4907.strongs: from G4920 (συνίημι); a mental putting together, i.e. intelligence or (concretely) the intellect

G3855.thayer: I pass by, depart, pass away.
G3855.mounce: to lead beside; intrans. to pass along or by, Mt. 20:30; Jn. 9:1; to pass on, Mt. 9:9, 27; intrans. and mid. to pass away, be in a state of transition, 1 Cor. 7:31; 1 Jn. 2:8, 17
G3855.abbottSmith: trans., to lead by , lead aside , lead into , lead forward , etc.
G3855.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead near, i.e. (reflexively or intransitively) to go along or away

G4613.mounce: Simon, pr. name. (1) Simon Peter, Mt. 4:18. (2) Simon (the Canaanite) the Zealot, Mt. 10:4; Acts 1:13 . (3) Simon, brother of Jesus, Mt. 13:55; Mk. 6:3. (4) Simon, the leper, Mt. 26:6; Mk. 14:3. (5) Simon, the Pharisee, Lk. 7:40. (6) Simon of Cyrene, Mt. 27:32. (7) Simon, father of Judas Iscariot, Jn. 6:71. (8) Simon, the sorcerer, Acts 8:9. (9) Simon, the tanner, of Joppa, Acts 9:43; 10:6
G4613.abbottSmith: a Greek name (transliterated סִימוֺן in Heb. ) used as a substitute for Συμεών (q.v.), Simon
G4613.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H08095); Simon (i.e. Shimon), the name of nine Israelites

G5489.abbottSmith: a sheet of paper , made of papyrus strips (v. Kennedy, Sources , 42; Milligan, NTD , 10-12; DB , iv, 945 f.): II Jo 12 (Plat., Inscr.).†
G5489.strongs: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a sheet ("chart") of writing-material (as to be scribbled over)

G1284.abbottSmith: to break asunder , burst , rend : δεσμά , Lk 8:29 ; pass., δίκτυα , Lk 5:6 ; ἱμάτια , χιτῶνας , in grief or anger (as Ge 37:29 , al.), Mt 26:65 , Ac 14:14 , Mk 14:63 .†
G1284.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); to tear asunder

G600.thayer: I set up again, restore to its original position or condition; hence: I restore, give back.
G600.mounce: also spelled ἀποκαθιστάνω, to restore a thing to its former place or state, Mt. 12:13; 17:11; Mk. 3:5; 8:25
G600.abbottSmith: to restore , i.e. to a former condition: of health, Mt 12:13 , Mk 3:5 8:25 , Lk 6:10 ; of social or political affairs, Mt 17:11 , Mk 9:12 , Ac 1:6 .
G600.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2525 (καθίστημι); to reconstitute (in health, home or organization)

G3652.thayer: Olympas, a Christian man in Rome.
G3652.strongs: probably a contraction from (Olympian-bestowed, i.e. heaven-descended); Olympas, a Christian

G4110.mounce: a thing formed or fashioned; spc. a potter’s vessel, Rom. 9:20*
G4110.abbottSmith: that which is moulded or formed : Ro 9:20 (LXX) .†

G3941.thayer: foreign, alien, subst: a foreigner, sojourner.
G3941.mounce: a neighbor; later, a sojourner, temporary resident, stranger, Acts 7:6, 29; Eph. 2:19; 1 Pet. 2:11*
G3941.abbottSmith: in cl. Attic, dwelling near , neighbouring ; as subst., a neighbour .
G3941.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and G3624 (οἶκος); having a home near, i.e. (as noun) a by-dweller (alien resident)

G2730.thayer: I dwell in, settle in, am established in (permanently), inhabit.
G2730.abbottSmith: trans., to inhabit , dwell in : c. acc., Lk 13:4 , Ac 1:19 2:9 , 14 4:16 9:32 , 35 19:10 , 17 , Re 17:2 ; of God, Mt 23:21 .
G2730.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to house permanently, i.e. reside (literally or figuratively)

G3432.thayer: an adulterer, that is, a man who is guilty with a married woman.
G3432.abbottSmith: an adulterer : Lk 18:11 , I Co 6:9 , He 13:4 .†
G3432.strongs: perhaps a primary word; a (male) paramour; figuratively, apostate

G806.mounce: securely, safely; without fail, safely, Mk. 14:44; Acts 16:23; certainly, assuredly, Acts 2:36*
G806.strongs: adverb from G804 (ἀσφαλής); securely (literally or figuratively)

G531.mounce: not transient; not to be superseded, unchangeable, Heb. 7:24*
G531.abbottSmith: inviolable , and so unchangeable: He 7:24 (v. Westc., in l.; Cremer, 653; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G531.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3845 (παραβαίνω); not passing away, i.e. untransferable (perpetual)

G1437.mounce: if it be that, if indeed, if at all events, Heb. 3:6, 14; 6:3
G1437.abbottSmith: representing something as "under certain circumstances actual or liable to happen," but not so definitely expected as in the case of εἰ c. ind. (Bl., §65, 4; cf. Jo 13:17 , I Co 7:36 ), if haply, if ; c. subjc. (cl.); pres.: Mt 6:22 , Lk 10:6 , Jo 7:17 , Ro 2:25 , 26 al.; aor. (= Lat. fut. pf.): Mt 4:9 16:26 (cf. ptcp. in Lk 9:25 ; M, Pr. , 230), Mk 3:24 , Lk 14:34 , Jo 5:43 , Ro 7:2 , al.; = cl.
G1437.strongs: from G1487 (εἰ) and G302 (ἄν); a conditional particle; in case that, provided, etc.; often used in connection with other particles to denote indefiniteness or uncertainty
G1437.kjv: before, but, except, (and) if, (if) so, (what-, whither-)soever, though, when (-soever), whether (or), to whom, (who-)so(-ever)

G3567.mounce: a bridal-chamber; in NT υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος, sons of the bridal-chamber, the bridegroom’s attendant friends, groomsmen, Mt. 9:15; Mk. 2:19; Lk. 5:34*
G3567.abbottSmith: the bride-chamber (Heb. חֻפָּה , LXX, παστός , Ps 18 (19):5 , Jl 2:16 ): Mt 22:10 (WH; γάμος , RV); οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ ν. (cf. cl. νυμφαγωγός, νυμφευτής ), the bridegroom's friends who have charge of the nuptial arrangements: Mt 9:15 , Mk 2:19 , Lk 5:34 .†

G4323.mounce: occurs in the NT only as a middle, to lay upon over and above; mid. to put one’s self in free communication with, to confer with, Gal. 1:16; to confer upon, to propound as a matter of consideration, Gal. 2:6*
G4323.abbottSmith: to lay on or offer besides ; mid., to lay on oneself in addition , undertake besides ; c. gen. pers., of giving or obtaining information, to consult , communicate : Ga 1:16 2:6 (Lft., in l.; cf. ἀνα-τίθημι ).
G4323.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G394 (ἀνατίθεμαι); to lay up in addition, i.e. (middle voice and figuratively) to impart or (by implication) to consult

G5018.mounce: of, or a native of Ταρσός, Tarsus, the metropolis of Cilicia, Acts 9:11; 21:39*
G5018.strongs: from G5019 (Ταρσός); a Tarsean, i.e. native of Tarsus

G1086.abbottSmith: Gergesene : Lk 8:26 , 37 TR, mg. 1 (LTr., WH, R, txt., Γερασηνῶν ; R, mg. 2 , Γαδαρηνῶν ).†
G1086.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01622); a Gergesene (i.e. Girgashite) or one of the aborigines of Palestine

G5024.strongs: neuter plural of G3588 (ὁ) and G846 (αὐτός) as adverb; in the same way

G1941.thayer: (a) I call (name) by a supplementary (additional, alternative) name, (b) mid: I call upon, appeal to, address.
G1941.mounce: pluperfect, ἐπεκέκλητο (3 sg), to call on; to attach or connect a name, Acts 15:17; Jas. 2:7; to attach an additional name, to surname, Mt. 10:3; pass. to receive an appellation or surname, Heb. 11:16; mid. to call upon, invoke, 2 Cor. 1:23; to appeal to, Acts 25:11, 12, 21
G1941.abbottSmith: to call, name, surname : c. acc (cl.), Mt 10:25 ; pass., Ac 1:23 4:36 10:5 , 18 , 32 11:13 12:12 , 25 , He 11:16 ; τ. ὄνομα , seq. ἐπί (denoting possession, as Heb. . עַל נִקְרָא שֵׁם . ), Ac 15:17 (LXX) , Ja 2:7 (see. CB on Am 9:12 ).
G1941.strongs: middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί) and G2564 (καλέω); to entitle; by implication, to invoke (for aid, worship, testimony, decision, etc.)

G302.thayer: an untranslatable word (under the circumstances, in that case, anyhow), the general effect of which is to make a statement contingent, which would otherwise be definite: it is thus regularly used with the subjunctive mood.
G302.mounce: For the various constructions of this particle, and their significance, consult a grammar. At the beginning of a clause, it is another form of ἐάν, if, Jn. 20:23
G302.abbottSmith: which cannot usually be separately translated in English, its force depending on the constructions which contain it (see further, LS, s.v.; WM, §xlii; M, Pr. , 165 ff.; MM, VGT , s.v.). In apodosis, c. indic, impf. or aor., expressing what would be or would have been if ( εἰ c. impf., aor. or plpf.) some condition were or had been fulfilled: Lk 7:39 17:6 , Jo 5:46 , Ga 1:10 , Mt 12:7 24:43 , I Co
G302.strongs: a primary particle, denoting a supposition, wish, possibility or uncertainty

G2219.thayer: leaven, ferment, both lit. and met.
G2219.mounce: leaven, yeast, Mt. 16:12; 13:33; met. leaven of the mind and conduct, by a system of doctrine or morals, used in a bad sense, Mt. 16:6, 11; 1 Cor. 5:6
G2219.abbottSmith: leaven : Mt 13:33 , Lk 13:21 ; τ. ἄρτου , Mt 16:12 . Metaph., of a moral influence or tendency, always, exc. in the Parable of the Leaven ( Mt 13:33 , Lk 13:21 ), for evil: I Co 5:6-8 , Ga 5:9 ; ζ. τ. Φαρισαίων , Mt 16:6 , 11 , Mk 8:15 , Lk 12:1 (Cremer, 723).†
G2219.strongs: probably from G2204 (ζέω); ferment (as if boiling up)

G3118.strongs: from G3117 (μακρός) and G5550 (χρόνος); long-timed, i.e. long-lived

G1719.mounce: terrible; in NT terrified, Lk. 24:5, 37; Acts 10:4; 24:25; Rev. 11:13*
G1719.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and G5401 (φόβος); in fear, i.e. alarmed

G2389.thayer: Jannes, a sorcerer at the court of the Pharaoh.
G2389.strongs: of Egyptian origin; Jannes, an Egyptian

G87.abbottSmith: not to be parted, mixed, undistinguishable (cf. Pr 25:1 , and v. Lft., Ignat. Eph. , §3). without uncertainty (Hort, in l., but v. MM, VGT , s.v.): Ja 3:17 .†
G87.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G1252 (διακρίνω); properly, undistinguished, i.e. (actively) impartial

G5528.mounce: an enclosure; pasture ground; fodder for beasts; in NT herbage, grass, Mt. 6:30; 14:19; a plant of corn, Mt. 13:26; Mk. 4:28
G5528.strongs: apparently a primary word; a "court" or "garden", i.e. (by implication, of pasture) herbage or vegetation

G1417.mounce: two, Mt. 6:24; 21:38, 31, et al. freq.; οἱ δύο, both, Jn. 20:4; δύο ἠ τρεῖς, two or three, some, a few, Mt. 18:20; from the Hebrew, δύο δύο, two and two, Mk. 6:7, i.q. ἀνὰ δύο, Lk. 10:1, and κατὰ δύο, 1 Cor. 14:27
G1417.abbottSmith: two : Mt 19:6 , Mk 10:8 , Jo 2:6 , al.; with pl. noun, Mt 9:27 10:10 , al.; οἱ, τῶν, τοὺς δ. , Mt 19:5 20:24 , Mk 10:8 , Eph 2:15 , al.; δ. ἐξ , Lk 24:13 ; distrib., ἀνὰ, κατὰ δ. , two and two, two apiece : Lk 10:1 (WH, ἀνὰ δ. [δύο] ), Jo 2:6 , I Co 14:27 ; δύο δύο (= ἀνὰ δ. , as LXX, Ge 6:19 for שְׁנַיִם שְׁנַיִם , but not merely "Hebraism," cf. μυρία μυρία , Æsch., Pers. , 981, and for usage in

G4814.mounce: to talk, converse, or discuss with, Mt. 17:3; Mk. 9:4; Lk. 4:36; 9:30; 22:4; Acts 25:12*
G4814.abbottSmith: to talk with or together : (c. dat. pers., Mk 9:4 , Lk 9:30 22:4 ; seq. μετά , c. gen., Mt 17:3 , Ac 25:12 ; . πρός , c. acc., Lk 4:36 .†
G4814.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G2980 (λαλέω); to talk together, i.e. converse

G3047.thayer: the south-west wind, and thus the quarter from which it comes.
G3047.mounce: pr. the south-west wind; meton. the south-west quarter of the heavens, Acts 27:12*
G3047.abbottSmith: the SW. wind : βλέποντα κατὰ λ. , Ac 27:12 (v. Page, in l.; Deiss., BS , 141).†
G3047.strongs: probably from (to pour a "libation"); the south(- west) wind (as bringing rain, i.e. (by extension) the south quarter)

G4415.thayer: the birthright, the rights of the first-born.
G4415.mounce: the rights of primogeniture, birthright, Heb. 12:16
G4415.abbottSmith: the rights of the first-born , birthright (= cl. ἡ πρεσβεία ): He 12:16 .†

G632.mounce: to portion off; to assign, bestow, 1 Pet. 3:7*
G632.abbottSmith: to assign , apportion : I Pe 3:7 . (In π. of a Prefect who renders to all their dues; v. MM, s.v.)†
G632.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and the base of G3551 (νόμος); to apportion, i.e. bestow

G73.thayer: an (athletic) contest; hence, a struggle (in the soul).
G73.mounce: place of contest, race-course, stadium; a contest, strife, contention; peril, toil
G73.abbottSmith: a gathering , esp. for games. a place of assembly . a contest, struggle, trial ; metaph. (MM, VGT , s.v.), of the Christian life as a contest and struggle: Phl 1:30 , I Th 2:2 , I Ti 6:12 , II Ti 4:7 He 12:1 ; solicitude, anxiety : Col 2:1 .†
G73.strongs: from G71 (ἄγω); properly, a place of assembly (as if led), i.e. (by implication) a contest (held there); figuratively, an effort or anxiety

G277.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G3334 (μετακινέω); immovable

G1252.thayer: I separate, distinguish, discern one thing from another; I doubt, hesitate, waver.
G1252.mounce: to separate, sever; to make a distinction or difference, Acts 15:9; 1 Cor. 11:29; to make to differ, distinguish, prefer, confer a superiority, 1 Cor. 4:7; to examine, scrutinize, estimate, 1 Cor. 11:31; 14:29; to discern, discriminate, Mt. 16:3; to judge, to decide a cause, 1 Cor. 6:5; to dispute, contend, Acts 11:2; Jude 9; to make a distinction mentally, Jas. 2:4; Jude 22; in NT to hesitate, be in doubt, doubt, Mt. 21:21; Mk. 11:23
G1252.abbottSmith: to separate , hence, to distinguish, discriminate, discern : μηδὲν δ. , Ac 11:12 ; οὐδὲν δ. μεταξύ , Ac 15:9 ; σε , I Co 4:7 ; τὸ σῶμα , I Co 11:29 .
G1252.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G2919 (κρίνω); to separate thoroughly, i.e. (literally and reflexively) to withdraw from, or (by implication) oppose; figuratively, to discriminate (by implication, decide), or (reflexively) hesitate
G1252.kjv: contend, make (to) differ(-ence), discern, doubt, judge, be partial, stagger, waver

G4428.thayer: I fold, roll up (as a scroll).
G4428.mounce: to fold; to roll up a scroll, Lk. 4:20*
G4428.abbottSmith: to fold ; of a scroll, to roll up : βιβλίον , Lk 4:20 (cf. ἀνα-πτύσσω ).†
G4428.strongs: probably akin to (to spread; and thus apparently allied to G4072 (πέτομαι) through the idea of expansion, and to 4429 through that of flattening; compare G3961 (πατέω)); to fold, i.e. furl a scroll

G4678.thayer: wisdom, insight, skill (human or divine), intelligence.
G4678.mounce: wisdom in general, knowledge, Mt. 12:42; Lk. 2:40, 52; 11:31; Acts 7:10; ability, Lk. 21:15; Acts 6:3, 10; practical wisdom, prudence, Col. 4:5; learning, science, Mt. 13:54; Mk. 6:2; Acts 7:22; scientific skill, 1 Cor. 1:17; 2:1; professed wisdom, human philosophy, 1 Cor. 1:19, 20, 22; 2:4, 5, 6; superior knowledge and enlightenment, Col. 2:23; in NT divine wisdom, Rom. 11:33; Eph. 3:10; Col. 2:3; revealed wisdom, Mt. 11:19; Lk. 11:49; 1 Cor. 1:24, 30; 2:7; Christian enlightenment, 1 Cor. 12:8; Eph. 1:8, 17; Col. 1:9, 28, 3:16; Jas. 1:5; 3:13
G4678.abbottSmith: skill, intelligence, wisdom , ranging from knowledge of the arts and matters of daily life to mental excellence in its highest and fullest sense;
G4678.strongs: from G4680 (σοφός); wisdom (higher or lower, worldly or spiritual)

G4258.mounce: to sin before; perf., to have already sinned, have sinned heretofore, 2 Cor. 12:21; 13:2*
G4258.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G264 (ἁμαρτάνω); to sin previously (to conversion)

G84.thayer: out of sight, obscurely, inconspicuously, uncertainly, without certain aim.
G84.abbottSmith: adv. uncertainly : of direction, I Co 9:26 .†

G4214.thayer: how much, how great, how many.
G4214.mounce: How great? How much? Mt. 6:23; Lk. 16:5, 7; 2 Cor. 7:11; πόσῳ, adverbially before a comparative, How much? By how much? Mt. 7:11; 10:25; Heb. 10:29; of time, How long? Mk. 9:21; of number, pl. How many? Mt. 15:34; 16:9, 10
G4214.abbottSmith: how much , how great , how many : Mt 6:23 , II Co 7:11 ; of time, Mk 9:21 ; neut., absol., Lk 16:5 , 7 ; dat., πόσῳ , adverbially, how much , Mt 12:12 ; id. seq. μᾶλλον , Mt 7:11 10:25 , Lk 11:13 12:24 , 28 , Ro 11:12 , 24 , Phm 16 , He 9:14 ; π. χείρονος τιμωρίας , He 10:29 ; pl., Mt 15:34 16:9 , 10 27:13 , Mk 6:38 8:5 , 19 , 20 15:4 , Lk 15:17 , Ac 21:20 .†
G4214.strongs: from an absolute (who, what) and G3739 (ὅς); interrogative pronoun (of amount) how much (large, long or (plural) many)

G597.mounce: pr. to lay up in store, hoard; met. to treasure up, secure, 1 Tim. 6:19*
G597.abbottSmith: to treasure up , store away : I Ti 6:19 .†
G597.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G2343 (θησαυρίζω); to treasure away

G1507.mounce: to roll, fold up, as garments, Heb. 1:12; Rev. 6:14*
G1507.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary but defective verb (of the same meaning); to coil or wrap

G4988.thayer: Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue at Corinth.
G4988.strongs: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G4599 (σθενόω); of safe strength; Sosthenes, a Christian

G5201.mounce: a water pot pitcher, Jn. 2:6, 7; a bucket, pail, Jn. 4:28*
G5201.abbottSmith: prop., a water-pot or jar : Jo 2:6 , 7 4:28 . More freq. in Attic = ἄγγος , a pot, urn or jar of any kind, as for holding wine, coins, etc. (v. Rutherford, NPhr. , 23; MM, xxv).†
G5201.strongs: from G5204 (ὕδωρ); a water-jar, i.e. receptacle for family supply

G3683.thayer: Onesiphorus, a Christian of the province of Asia.
G3683.strongs: from a derivative of G3685 (ὀνίνημι) and G5411 (φόρος); profit-bearer; Onesiphorus, a Christian

G1319.mounce: the act or occupation of teaching, Rom. 12:7; 1 Tim. 4:13; information, instruction, Rom. 15:4; 2 Tim. 3:16; matter taught, precept, doctrine, Mt. 15:9; 1 Tim. 1:10
G1319.abbottSmith: teaching, instruction , in both active and objective senses, most freq. the latter: Ro 12:7 15:4 , Eph 4:14 , I Ti 4:6 , 13 , 16 5:17 6:1 , 3 , II Ti 3:10 , 16 , Tit 2:7 , 10 ; ὑγιαινοῦσα δ. , I Ti 1:10 , II Ti 4:3 , Tit 1:9 2:1 ; pl., δ. τ. ἀνθρώπων , Col 2:22 ; δ. δαιμονίων , I Ti 4:1 , δ. διδάσκειν , Mt 15:9 , Mk 7:7 (LXX) (Cremer, 182).†
G1319.strongs: from G1320 (διδάσκαλος); instruction (the function or the information)

G1046.thayer: Gadarene, belonging to Gadara (an important Hellenized town, one of the Decapolis, and south-east of the Sea of Galilee).
G1046.mounce: an inhabitant of Gadara, the chief city of Perea, Mt. 8:28*
G1046.abbottSmith: Gadarene, of Gadara : Mt 8:28 (Rec. Γεργεσηνῶν ), Mk 5:1 , Rec. (Edd., Γερασηνῶν ), Lk 8:26 , 37 Rec. (Edd., Γερασηνῶν ).†
G1046.strongs: from (a town east of the Jordan); a Gadarene or inhabitant of Gadara

G148.mounce: vile or obscene language, foul talk, Col. 3:8*
G148.abbottSmith: abusive language , abuse (Lft., ICC , in l.; MM, VGT , s.v.): Col 3:8 .†
G148.strongs: from G150 (αἰσχρός) and G3056 (λόγος); vile conversation

G4007.abbottSmith: indeed , by far , etc. In the NT, it is always affixed to the word to which it relates, v.s. διόπερ , ἐάνπερ , εἴπερ , ἐπείπερ , ἐπειδήπερ , ἤπερ , καθάπερ , καίπερ , ὅσπερ , ὥσπερ .
G4007.strongs: from the base of G4008 (πέραν); an enclitic particle significant of abundance (thoroughness), i.e. emphasis; much, very or ever

G2502.mounce: Josiah, king of Judah (2 Ki. 22), Mt. 1:10, 11*
G2502.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02977); Josias (i.e. Joshiah), an Israelite

G1338.thayer: I pass through (to), come through (to), pierce.
G1338.mounce: to go or pass through; to penetrate, Heb. 4:12*
G1338.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G2425 (ἱκανός); to reach through, i.e. penetrate

G2254.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G2254.strongs: dative case plural of G1473 (ἐγώ); to (or for, with, by) us

G452.mounce: Annas, pr. name, short version of Ἅνανος, Lk. 3:2; Jn. 18:13, 24; Acts 4:6*
G452.abbottSmith: Annas , the high priest: Lk 3:2 , Jo 18:13 , 24 , Ac 4:6 .†
G452.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H02608); Annas (i.e. G367 (Ἀνανίας)), an Israelite

G1332.thayer: two years old, lasting two years.
G1332.mounce: of two years; of the age of two years, Mt. 2:16*
G1332.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G2094 (ἔτος); of two years (in age)

G4645.thayer: I harden, make hard, make stubborn.
G4645.mounce: to harden; met. to harden morally, to make stubborn, Heb. 3:8, 15; 4:7; as a negation of ἐλεεῖν, to leave to stubbornness and contumacy, Rom. 9:18; mid. and pass. to put on a stubborn frame, Acts 19:9; Heb. 3:13*
G4645.abbottSmith: to harden . Metaph., to harden, make stubborn : c. acc. pers., Ro 9:18 ; τ. καρδίας ( Ps 94 (95):8 ), He 3:8 , 15 4:7 . Pass., to become hardened : Ac 19:9 , He 3:13 .†
G4645.strongs: from G4642 (σκληρός); to indurate, i.e. (figuratively) render stubborn

G5229.thayer: lit: I raise beyond, uplift; mid: I lift myself up, exalt myself, am arrogant.
G5229.mounce: to raise or lift up above or over; mid. to lift up one’s self; met. to be over-elated, 2 Cor. 12:7 (2x); to bear one’s self arrogantly, to rear a haughty front, 2 Thess. 2:4*
G5229.abbottSmith: to lift or raise over . Mid., to uplift oneself : II Co 12:7 ; seq. ἐπί , c. acc, pers., II Th 2:4 (cf. II Mac 5:23 ).†
G5229.strongs: middle voice from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G142 (αἴρω); to raise oneself over, i.e. (figuratively) to become haughty
G5229.kjv: exalt self, be exalted above measure

G672.mounce: to go from or away, depart, Mt. 7:23; Lk. 9:39; Acts 13:13*
G672.abbottSmith: to go away , depart , withdraw : seq. ἀπό , Mt 7:23 , Lk 9:39 , Ac 13:13 (absol., Lk 20:20 , Tr., mg.).†
G672.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G5562 (χωρέω); to go away

G2622.thayer: I break in pieces, break up.
G2622.mounce: to break, break in pieces, Mk. 6:41; Lk. 9:16*
G2622.abbottSmith: to break up , break in pieces : Mk 6:41 , Lk 9:16 .†
G2622.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G2806 (κλάω); to break down, i.e. divide

G4966.mounce: Shechem, indecl., fem., a city of Samaria, Acts 7:16*. See PhotoGuide.
G4966.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07927); Sychem (i.e. Shekem), the name of a Canaanite and of a place in Palestine

G366.thayer: I become sober again, recover sound sense.
G366.mounce: to become sober; met. to recover sobriety of mind, 2 Tim. 2:26*
G366.abbottSmith: to return to soberness : metaph., II Ti 2:26 (cf. ἐκνήφω ).†
G366.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and G3525 (νήφω); to become sober again, i.e. (figuratively) regain (one's) senses

G2774.thayer: (a) the chief matter, the main point, (b) a sum of money.
G2774.mounce: a sum total; a sum of money, capital, Acts 22:28; the crowning or ultimate point to preliminary matters, Heb. 8:1*
G2774.abbottSmith: of the head ; metaph., principal , chief ; mostly as subst., κεφάλαιον, τό ; the chief point : He 8:1 . the sum total , amount : Ac 22:28 (other meaning, LS, s.v.).†
G2774.strongs: neuter of a derivative of G2776 (κεφαλή); a principal thing, i.e. main point; specially, an amount (of money)

G883.strongs: from G882 (Ἀχαΐα); an Achaian; Achaicus, a Christian

G1665.thayer: Elizabeth, mother of John the Baptizer.
G1665.mounce: Elizabeth, the wife of Zechariah and mother of John the Baptist, Lk. 1:5, 13, 24, 36, 57
G1665.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0472); Elisabet, an Israelitess

G2061.strongs: from G2060 (Ἑρμῆς) and G1096 (γίνομαι); born of Hermes; Hermogenes, an apostate Christian

G4339.thayer: (lit: that has come to), a proselyte, that is a non-Jew, who has been circumcised and has adopted the Jews' religion.
G4339.mounce: pr. a newcomer, a stranger; in NT a proselyte, convert from paganism to Judaism, Mt. 23:15; Acts 2:11; 6:5; 13:43*
G4339.abbottSmith: one who has arrived , a stranger .
G4339.strongs: from the alternate of G4334 (προσέρχομαι); an arriver from a foreign region, i.e. (specially), an acceder (convert) to Judaism ("proselyte")

G439.thayer: a coal-fire, a heap of burning coals.
G439.mounce: a mass or heap of live coals, Jn. 18:18; 21:9
G439.abbottSmith: a heap of burning coals : Jo 18:18 21:9 .†
G439.strongs: from G440 (ἄνθραξ); a bed of burning coals

G501.thayer: I draw (generally water from a deep well in the ground); perhaps: I draw out.
G501.mounce: to draw, e.g., wine, water, etc.; Jn. 2:8, 9; 4:7, 15*
G501.strongs: from (the hold of a ship); to bale up (properly, bilge water), i.e. dip water (with a bucket, pitcher, etc.)

G4380.abbottSmith: to have respect of persons : Ja 2:9 .†
G4380.strongs: from G4381 (προσωπολήπτης); to favor an individual, i.e. show partiality

G2374.mounce: a door, gate, Mt. 6:6; Mk. 1:33; an entrance, Mt. 27:60; in NT met. an opening, occasion, opportunity, Acts 14:27; 1 Cor. 16:9; meton. a medium or means of entrance, Jn. 10:7, 9
G2374.abbottSmith: a door : κλείειν ( ἀποκ- ) τὴν θ. , Mt 6:6 , Lk 13:25 ; pass., Mt 25:10 , Lk 11:7 , Jo 20:19 , 26 , Ac 21:30 ; ἀνοίγειν , Ac 5:19 ; pass., Ac 16:26 , 27 ; κρούειν , Ac 12:13 ; διὰ τῆς θ. , Jo 10:1 , 2 ; πρὸς τὴν θ. , Mk 1:33 11:4 (WH om. τήν ), Ac 3:2 ; τὰ πρὸς τὴν θ. , the space by the door, Mk 2:2 ; πρὸς τῇ θ. , Jo 18:16 ; ἐπὶ τῇ θ , Ac 5:9 ; πρὸ τῆς θ. , Ac 12:6 ; ἐπὶ τῶν θ. , Ac 5:23 ; ἡ θ. τ.
G2374.strongs: apparently a primary word (compare "door"); a portal or entrance (the opening or the closure, literally or figuratively)

G4326.thayer: I want more, need in addition.
G4326.mounce: to want besides or in addition, need, Acts 17:25*
G4326.abbottSmith: to want further , need in addition : Ac 17:25 .†
G4326.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G1189 (δέομαι); to require additionally, i.e. want further

G3875.thayer: (a) an advocate, intercessor, (b) a consoler, comforter, helper, (c) Paraclete.
G3875.mounce: one called or sent for to assist another; an advocate, one who pleads the cause of another, 1 Jn. 2:1; genr. one present to render various beneficial service, and thus the Paraclete, whose influence and operation were to compensate for the departure of Christ himself, Jn. 14:16, 26; 15:26; 16:7*
G3875.abbottSmith: called to one's aid in a judicial cause (Dion. Cass.); hence, most freq. as subst., ὁ π. , an advocate , pleader , intercessor (Dem., al.; so in Rabbinic lit., פְּרַקְלִיטָא ), "a friend of the accused person, called to speak to his character, or otherwise enlist sympathy in his favour" (Field, Notes , 102). In NT, specif., ὁ π. , of Christ, I Jo 2:1 (v. ICC , Jo. Epp. , 23 ff.); of the Holy Spiri

G5623.thayer: I help, benefit, do good, am useful (to), profit.
G5623.mounce: to help, profit, benefit, accomplish, Mt. 27:24; Mk. 7:11; Rom. 2:25; be of value, Jn. 6:63
G5623.abbottSmith: to help, benefit, do good, profit: absol., Ro 2:25 ; οὐδέν (do no good; v. Field, Notes , 21), Mt 27:24 , Jo 6:63 12:19 ; c. acc pers., He 4:2 ; c. dupl. acc, Mk 8:36 , I Co 14:6 , Ga 5:2 ; pass., He 13:9 ; c. acc, Mt 15:5 16:26 , Mk 5:26 7:11 , Lk 9:25 , I Co 13:3 .†
G5623.strongs: from the same as G5622 (ὠφέλεια); to be useful, i.e. to benefit

G5597.mounce: to rub in pieces, as the ears of grain, Lk. 6:1*
G5597.strongs: prolongation from the same base as G5567 (ψάλλω); to triturate, i.e. (by analogy) to rub out (kernels from husks with the fingers or hand)

G5340.strongs: adverb from participle of G5339 (φείδομαι); abstemiously, i.e. stingily

G4547.mounce: a sandal, a sole of wood or hide, covering the bottom of the foot, and bound on with leathern thongs, Mk. 6:9, Acts 12:8*
G4547.strongs: neuter of a derivative of (a "sandal"; of uncertain origin); a slipper or sole-pad

G4115.thayer: I enlarge, make broad; met: of the growth of tenderness and love.
G4115.mounce: to make broad, widen, enlarge, Mt. 23:5; pass. met. of the heart, from the Hebrew, to be expanded with kindly and genial feelings, 2 Cor. 6:11, 13*
G4115.abbottSmith: to make broad , enlarge , extend : c. acc. rei, Mt 23:5 . Metaph. (cf. Ps 118 (119):32 , al.), pass., ἡ καρδία , II Co 6:11 ; ὑμεῖς , II Co 6:13 .†
G4115.strongs: from G4116 (πλατύς); to widen (literally or figuratively)

G1299.thayer: I give orders to, prescribe, arrange.
G1299.mounce: pr. to arrange, make a precise arrangement; to prescribe, 1 Cor. 11:34; 16:1; Tit. 1:5; to direct, Lk. 8:55; Acts 20:13; to charge, Mt. 11:1; to command, Acts 18:2; to ordain, Gal. 3:19
G1299.abbottSmith: to charge, give orders to, appoint, arrange, ordain : c. dat., Mt 11:1 , I Co 9:14 16:1 ; seq. inf., Lk 8:55 , Ac 18:2 . Mid., I Co 7:17 , Ac 20:13 ; c. acc, I Co 11:34 ; c. dat, Tit 1:5 ; seq. inf., Ac 7:44 24:23 . Pass., τὸ διατεταγμένον , Lk 3:13 , Ac 23:31 ; τὰ διαταχθέντα , Lk 17:9 , 10 ; διαταγείς , Ga 3:19 .†
G1299.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange thoroughly, i.e. (specially) institute, prescribe, etc.
G1299.kjv: appoint, command, give, (set in) order, ordain

G1865.thayer: I gather together, pass: I crowd upon (some one), press around.
G1865.mounce: act., to gather together, to collect close upon, or beside; pass., to crowd upon, Lk. 11:29*
G1865.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and (to assemble); to accumulate

G441.thayer: desirous of pleasing men, a renderer of service to human beings (as opposed to God).
G441.mounce: desirous of pleasing men, Eph. 6:6; Col. 3:22*
G441.abbottSmith: studying to please men : Eph 6:6 , Col 3:22 (Cremer, 642; MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G441.strongs: from G444 (ἄνθρωπος) and G700 (ἀρέσκω); man-courting, i.e. fawning

G2738.mounce: heat, scorching or burning heat, Rev. 7:16; 16:9*
G2738.strongs: from G2545 (καίω); properly, a burn (concretely), but used (abstractly) of a glow

G1896.mounce: a proposed lexical form for the second aorist ἐπεῖδον
G1896.abbottSmith: with a view to bless: c. inf., Lk 1:25 (cf. DB , 136 b ).
G1896.strongs: and other moods and persons of the same tense; from G1909 (ἐπί) and G1492 (εἴδω); to regard (favorably or otherwise)

G2974.mounce: pr. blunt, dull, as a weapon; dull of hearing, deaf, Mt. 11:5; Mk. 7:32, 37; 9:25; Lk. 7:22; dumb, mute, Mt. 9:32, 33; 12:22; 15:30, 31; Lk. 1:22; meton. making dumb, causing dumbness, Lk. 11:14*
G2974.abbottSmith: blunt , dull . Metaph., of the senses, esp. of speech, dumb : Mt 9:32 , 33 12:22 15:30 , 31 , Lk 1:22 11:14 ; of hearing, deaf : Mt 11:5 , Mk 7:32 , 37 9:25 , Lk 7:22 .†
G2974.strongs: from G2875 (κόπτω); blunted, i.e. (figuratively) of hearing (deaf) or speech (dumb)

G2880.thayer: I fill, sate, glut, feed full, satisfy.
G2880.abbottSmith: to satisfy : pass., τροφῆς , Ac 27:38 ; metaph., of spiritual things, I Co 4:8 .†
G2880.strongs: a primary verb; to cram, i.e. glut or sate

G755.thayer: master of ceremonies at a dinner, master of the feast.
G755.abbottSmith: the superintendent of a banquet , whose duty it was to arrange the tables and food ( DB , ii, 253): Jo 2:8 , 9 .†
G755.strongs: from G746 (ἀρχή) and a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G2827 (κλίνω) (a dinner-bed, because composed of three couches); director of the entertainment

G4437.mounce: dense, thick; frequent, 1 Tim. 5:23; πυκνά, as an adverb, frequently, often, Lk. 5:33; so the compar. πυκνότερον, very frequently, Acts 24:26*
G4437.strongs: from the same as G4635 (σκηνοποιός); clasped (thick), i.e. (figuratively) frequent; neuter plural (as adverb) frequently

G2339.thayer: hunting, entrapping; prey, game; a net, means of capture.
G2339.mounce: hunting, the chase; met. means of capture, a cause of destruction, Rom. 11:9*
G2339.strongs: from (a wild animal, as game); hunting, i.e. (figuratively) destruction

G1374.thayer: (lit: of two souls, of two selves), double-minded, wavering.
G1374.abbottSmith: of two minds, wavering : Ja 1:8 4:8 (Cremer, 588; DB , iv, 528).†
G1374.strongs: from G1364 (δίς) and G5590 (ψυχή); two-spirited, i.e. vacillating (in opinion or purpose)

G5021.thayer: (a) I assign, arrange, (b) I determine; mid: I appoint.
G5021.mounce: to arrange; to set, appoint, in a certain station, Lk. 7:8; Rom. 13:1; to set, devote, to a pursuit, 1 Cor. 16:15; to dispose, frame, for an object, Acts 13:48; to arrange, appoint, place or time, Mt. 28:16; Acts 28:23; to allot, assign, Acts 22:10; to settle, decide, Acts 15:2*
G5021.abbottSmith: primarily, in military sense, then generally, to draw up in order, arrange in place, assign, appoint, order : c. dat. (acc) et inf. (Bl., § 72, 5), Ac 15:2 18:2 ( διατ- , WH) 22:10 ; ἑαυτούς , I Co 16:15 ; pass., Mt 8:9 (T [WH], R, txt., om.), Lk 7:8 , Ro 13:1 ; ὅσοι ἦσαν τεταγμένοι εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον (perh. in mid. sense; v. EGT and Page, in l.), Ac 13:48 ; mid., to appoint for oneself or by one's
G5021.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb (which latter appears only in certain tenses); to arrange in an orderly manner, i.e. assign or dispose (to a certain position or lot)

G4177.abbottSmith: a citizen : c. gen. loc., Lk 15:15 , Ac 21:39 .

G1695.thayer: Emmaus, a village not far from Jerusalem.
G1695.mounce: Emmaus, pr. name, indecl., of a village near Jerusalem, Lk. 24:13*. See PhotoGuide.
G1695.abbottSmith: Emmaus , a place 60 furlongs from Jerusalem: Lk 24:13 .†
G1695.strongs: probably of Hebrew origin (compare H03222); Emmaus, a place in Palestine

G1520.mounce: numeral one, Mt. 10:29, freq.; only, Mk. 12:6; one virtually by union, Mt. 19:5, 6; Jn. 10:30; one and the same, Lk. 12:52; Rom. 3:30; one in respect of office and standing, 1 Cor. 3:8; equivalent to τις, a certain one, Mt. 8:19; 16:14; a, an, Mt. 21:19; Jas. 4:13; εἱς ἕκαστος, each one, every one, Lk. 4:40; Acts 2:3; εἱς τὸν ἕνα, one another, 1 Thess. 5:11; εἱς καὶ εἱς, the one- and the other, Mt. 20:21; εἱς καθ’ εἱς and ὁδὲ καθ’ εἱς, one by one, one after another, in succession, Mk. 14:19; Jn. 8:9; as an ordinal, first, Mt. 28:1
G1520.kjv: a(-n, -ny, certain), + abundantly, man, one (another), only, other, some

G522.thayer: I take away, remove; pass: I am taken away, withdrawn.
G522.mounce: to take away; pass. to be taken away; to be withdrawn, Mt. 9:15; Mk. 2:20; Lk. 5:35*
G522.abbottSmith: to lift off , hence, to take away ; pass.: Mt 9:15 , Mk 2:20 , Lk 5:35 .†
G522.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G142 (αἴρω); to lift off, i.e. remove

G5114.strongs: comparative of a derivative of the primary (to cut; more comprehensive or decisive than G2875 (κόπτω), as if by a single stroke; whereas that implies repeated blows, like hacking); more keen

G5556.thayer: a chrysoprase, gem of a golden-greenish color.
G5556.mounce: a chrysoprase, a species of gem of a golden green color like that of a leek, Rev. 21:20*
G5556.strongs: from G5557 (χρυσός) and (a leek); a greenish-yellow gem ("chrysoprase")

G2527.thayer: one the whole, in general, altogether.
G2527.mounce: on the whole, entirely, in general, altogether, completely; with a negative, not at all, Acts 4:18*
G2527.abbottSmith: on the whole , in general : μή κ. , not at all , Ac 4:18 .†
G2527.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G3650 (ὅλος); on the whole, i.e. entirely

G343.mounce: to unveil, uncover; pass. to be unveiled, 2 Cor. 3:18; met. to be disclosed in true character and condition, 2 Cor. 3:14*
G343.abbottSmith: to unveil : metaph. of removing hindrance to perception of spiritual things, II Co 3:14 3:18 .†
G343.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) (in the sense of reversal) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to unveil

G215.abbottSmith: inexpressible , not to be uttered : Ro 8:26 .†
G215.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2980 (λαλέω); unspeakable

G4867.mounce: to gather; to bring together, Acts 19:25; pass. to come together, convene, Acts 12:12*
G4867.abbottSmith: to gather together, assemble : c. acc. pers., Ac 19:25 ; pass., Ac 12:12 .†
G4867.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to hoard); to convene

G2189.mounce: enmity, discord, feud, Lk. 23:12; Gal. 5:20; alienation, Eph. 2:14, 16; a principle or state of enmity, Rom. 8:7; Jas. 4:4*
G2189.strongs: feminine of G2190 (ἐχθρός); hostility; by implication, a reason for opposition

G4164.thayer: various, of different colors, diverse, various.
G4164.mounce: of various colors, variegated, checkered; various, diverse, manifold, Mt. 4:24
