G4164.abbottSmith: many-coloured , variegated . Metaph., various , manifold : Mt 4:24 , Mk 1:34 , Lk 4:40 , II Ti 3:6 , Tit 3:3 , He 2:4 13:9 , Ja 1:2 , I Pe 1:6 4:10 .†
G4164.strongs: of uncertain derivation; motley, i.e. various in character

G1052.thayer: a Galatian (meaning any inhabitant of the Roman province Galatia).
G1052.mounce: a Galatian, inhabitant of Galatia, Gal. 3:1*
G1052.strongs: from G1053 (Γαλατία); a Galatian or inhabitant of Galatia

G5016.abbottSmith: trouble, disturbance : τ. ὕδατος , Jo 5:[4]{editor note: v. 7?} . In pl. (as in cl.), tumults : Mk 13:8 , Rec.†
G5016.strongs: feminine from G5015 (ταράσσω); disturbance, i.e. (of water) roiling, or (of a mob) sedition

G4458.mounce: enclitic particle, in any way, by any means, Acts 27:12; Rom. 1:10
G4458.strongs: adverb from the base of G4225 (πού); an enclitic particle of indefiniteness of manner; somehow or anyhow; used only in composition

G5481.thayer: an impression, representation, exact reproduction; a graving-tool.
G5481.strongs: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a graver (the tool or the person), i.e. (by implication) engraving (("character"), the figure stamped, i.e. an exact copy or (figuratively) representation)

G5319.thayer: I make clear (visible, manifest), make known.
G5319.mounce: to bring to light, to set in a clear light; to manifest, display, Jn. 2:11; 7:4; 9:3; to show, Rom. 1:19; 2 Cor. 7:12; to declare, make known, Jn. 17:6; to disclose, Mk. 4:22; 1 Cor. 4:5; Col. 4:4; to reveal, Rom. 3:21; 16:26; Col. 1:26; to present to view, Jn. 21:1, 14; pass. to make an appearance, Mk. 16:12, 14; spc. of Christ, to be personally manifested, Jn. 1:31; Col. 3:4; 1 Pet. 1:20; 5:4; 1 Jn. 3:5; to be laid bare, appear in true character, 2 Cor. 5:10, 11
G5319.abbottSmith: to make visible, clear, manifest or known : c. acc rei, Jo 2:11 17:6 , Ro 1:19 , I Co 4:5 , II Co 2:14 11:6 , Col 4:4 , Tit 1:3 ; pass., Mk 4:22 , Jo 3:21 9:3 , Ro 3:21 16:26 , II Co 4:10 , 11 , Eph 5:13 , Col 1:26 , II Ti 1:10 , He 9:8 , I Jo 3:2 4:9 , Re 3:18 15:4 ; c. acc pers., of Christ, Jo 7:4 21:1 ; pass., II Co 3:3 5:10 , 11 , I Jo 2:19 ; of Christ, Mk 16:12 , 14 , Jo 1:31 21:14 , Col 3:4
G5319.strongs: from G5318 (φανερός); to render apparent (literally or figuratively)

G3066.thayer: Lucius, (a) of Cyrene, an early Christian, in the church of Antioch, by some identified with the evangelist Luke, (b) a Christian with Paul at Corinth, by some identified with (a).
G3066.mounce: Lucius, pr. name, (1) a person from Cyrene of Antioch, Acts 13:1. (2) a person who sends his greeting with Paul, Rom. 16:21*
G3066.strongs: of Latin origin; illuminative; Lucius, a Christian

G1858.thayer: I take part in a festival, keep a feast (allegorically).
G1858.mounce: to keep a feast, celebrate a festival, 1 Cor. 5:8*
G1858.strongs: from G1859 (ἑορτή); to observe a festival

G2990.thayer: I am hidden (concealed), lie hid, escape notice, sometimes with acc. of person from whom concealment takes place; I do so and so unconsciously, unknown to myself, I shut my eyes to so and so.
G2990.mounce: to be unnoticed; to escape the knowledge or observation of a person, Acts 26:26; 2 Pet. 3:5, 8; absol. to be concealed or hidden, escape detection, Mk. 7:24; Lk. 8:47; with a participle of another verb, to be unconscious of an action while being the subject or object of it, Heb. 13:2*
G2990.abbottSmith: to escape notice , be hidden ( from ): Mk 7:24 , Lk 8:47 ; c. acc. pers., Ac 26:26 , II Pe 3:5 , 8 ; as in common cl. idiom, seq. ptcp., ἔλαθον ξενίσαντες , entertained unawares , He 13:2 (cf. ἐκ- , ἐπι-λανθάνω ).†
G2990.strongs: a prolonged form of a primary verb, which is used only as an alternate in certain tenses; to lie hid (literally or figuratively); often used adverbially, unwittingly
G2990.kjv: be hid, be ignorant of, unawares

G2844.mounce: a fellow, partner, companion, Mt. 23:30; Lk. 5:10; 1 Cor. 10:18, 20; 2 Cor. 8:23; Phlm. 17; Heb. 10:33; a sharer, partaker, 2 Cor. 1:7; 1 Pet. 5:1; 2 Pet. 1:4*
G2844.strongs: from G2839 (κοινός); a sharer, i.e. associate

G1457.mounce: to initiate, consecrate, dedicate, renovate; to institute, Heb. 9:18; 10:20*
G1457.strongs: from G1456 (ἐγκαίνια); to renew, i.e. inaugurate

G1229.thayer: I announce throughout (the world), spread the news of, give notice of, teach.
G1229.mounce: to publish abroad, Lk. 9:60; Rom. 9:17; to certify to the public, Acts 21:26*
G1229.abbottSmith: to publish abroad, proclaim : c. acc, Lk 9:60 , Ac 21:26 , Ro 9:17 (LXX) .†
G1229.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to herald thoroughly

G2804.thayer: (a) Claudius, the fourth of the Roman Emperors, Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, who ruled A.D. 41-54, (b) Claudius Lysias, a tribune at Jerusalem.
G2804.mounce: Claudius, pr. name (1) The fourth Roman Emperor, Acts 11:28; 18:2. (2) Claudius Lysias, a Roman captain, Acts 23:26*
G2804.abbottSmith: Claudius ; the Emperor: Ac 11:28 18:2 . A military tribune, C. Lysias : Ac 23:26 .†
G2804.strongs: of Latin origin; Claudius, the name of two Romans

G4640.mounce: to leap, Lk. 1:41, 44; to leap, skip, bound for joy, Lk. 6:23*
G4640.strongs: akin to (to skip); to jump, i.e. sympathetically move (as the quickening of a fetus)

G3724.thayer: I separate, mark off by boundaries; I determine, appoint, designate.
G3724.mounce: to set bounds to, to bound; to restrict, Heb. 4:7; to settle, appoint definitively, Acts 17:26; to fix determinately, Acts 2:23; to decree, destine, Lk. 22:22; to constitute, appoint, Acts 10:42; 17:31; to characterize with precision, to set forth distinctively, Rom. 1:4; absol. to resolve, Acts 11:29*
G3724.abbottSmith: to separate , mark off by boundaries (so Nu 34:6 , Jo 13:27 ).
G3724.strongs: from G3725 (ὅριον); to mark out or bound ("horizon"), i.e. (figuratively) to appoint, decree, specify

G5505.thayer: a thousand, the number one thousand.
G5505.mounce: the number one thousand, a thousand, Lk. 14:31; Acts 4:4
G5505.abbottSmith: the number one thousand, a thousand : Lk 14:31 , Ac 4:4 , I Co 10:8 , Re 5:11 7:4-8 11:13 14:1 , 3 21:16 .†

G2028.thayer: I name, impose a name on; pass: am named.
G2028.mounce: to attach a name to; pass. to be named, Rom. 2:17*
G2028.abbottSmith: to name, call by a name, surname : pass., Ro 2:17 .†
G2028.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3687 (ὀνομάζω); to name further, i.e. denominate

G3857.mounce: a park, a forest where wild beasts were kept for hunting; a pleasure-park, a garden of trees of various kinds; used in the LXX for the Garden of Eden; in NT the celestial paradise, Lk. 23:43; 2 Cor. 12:4; Rev. 2:7*
G3857.abbottSmith: a park , pleasure-ground , garden (LXX), an orchard (in π. , v. MM, ii, xviii; Deiss., BS , 148).
G3857.strongs: of Oriental origin (compare H06508); a park, i.e. (specially), an Eden (place of future happiness, "paradise")

G2357.thayer: (refers probably to a careful observance of religious restrictions), religious (probably in a limited sense), devout.
G2357.abbottSmith: religious , careful of the outward forms of divine service (see previous word): Ja 1:26 .†
G2357.strongs: probably from the base of G2360 (θροέω); ceremonious in worship (as demonstrative), i.e. pious

G1828.thayer: (lit: I draw out of the right place, or I draw aside out of the right way), I entice.
G1828.mounce: to draw or drag out; met. to withdraw, allure, hurry away, Jas. 1:14*
G1828.abbottSmith: to draw out or away : metaph., ὑπὸ τ. ἐπιθυμίας , Ja 1:14 (v. Mayor, in l).†
G1828.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G1670 (ἑλκύω); to drag forth, i.e. (figuratively) to entice (to sin)

G2549.thayer: (a) evil (i.e. trouble, labor, misfortune), (b) wickedness, (c) vicious disposition, malice, spite.
G2549.mounce: malice, malignity, Rom. 1:29; Eph. 4:31; wickedness, depravity, Acts 8:22; 1 Cor. 5:8; in NT evil, trouble, calamity, misfortune, Mt. 6:34
G2549.abbottSmith: badness in quality (opp. to ἀρετή , excellence ).
G2549.strongs: from G2556 (κακός); badness, i.e. (subjectively) depravity, or (actively) malignity, or (passively) trouble

G3774.thayer: Uriah, husband of Bathsheba the mother of Solomon.
G3774.mounce: Urias, Uriah, pr. name (2 Sam. 11; 12:24), Mt. 1:6*
G3774.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H0223); Urias (i.e. Urijah), a Hittite

G4131.mounce: a striker, one apt to strike; a quarrelsome, violent person, 1 Tim. 3:3; Tit. 1:7*
G4131.abbottSmith: a striker , brawler : I Ti 3:3 , Tit 1:7 (Arist., Plut., al.).†
G4131.strongs: from G4141 (πλήσσω); a smiter, i.e. pugnacious (quarrelsome)

G2603.thayer: of the umpire in a contest: I decide against, take part against, condemn (perhaps with the idea of assumption, officialism).
G2603.mounce: pr. to give an unfavorable decision as respects a prize; hence, to decide against, Col. 2:18*
G2603.abbottSmith: to give judgment against , condemn (v. Field, Notes , 196; Abbott, Essays , 104 f.): Col 2:18 .†
G2603.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G1018 (βραβεύω) (in its original sense); to award the price against, i.e. (figuratively) to defraud (of salvation)

G4972.thayer: I seal, set a seal upon.
G4972.mounce: to seal, stamp with a seal, Mt. 27:66; Rev. 20:3; to seal up, to close up, conceal, Rev. 10:4; 22:10; to set a mark upon, distinguish by a mark, Eph. 1:13; 4:30; Rev. 7:3, 4, 5, 8; to seal, to mark distinctively as invested with a certain character, Jn. 6:27; mid. to set one’s own mark upon, seal as one’s own, to impress with a mark of acceptance, 2 Cor. 1:22; to deliver over safely to someone, Rom. 15:28; absol. to set to one’s seal, to make a solemn declaration, Jn. 3:33*
G4972.strongs: from G4973 (σφραγίς); to stamp (with a signet or private mark) for security or preservation (literally or figuratively); by implication, to keep secret, to attest
G4972.kjv: (set a, set to) seal up, stop

G2628.mounce: to follow closely or earnestly, Lk. 23:55; Acts 16:17*
G2628.abbottSmith: to follow after : Lk 23:55 ; c. dat. pers., Ac 16:17 .†
G2628.strongs: from G2596 (κατά) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to accompany closely

G3005.mounce: pr. a remnant; in NT a small residue, Rom. 11:5*

G2445.thayer: Joppa, a coast town of Judea, west-north-west of Jerusalem.
G2445.mounce: Joppa, a city of Palestine, Acts 9:36, 38, 42f.; 10:5, 8, 23, 32; 11:5, 13*. See PhotoGuide.
G2445.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03305); Joppe (i.e. Japho), a place in Palestine

G939.mounce: pr. a step; the foot, Acts 3:7*
G939.strongs: from (to walk); a pace ("base"), i.e. (by implication) the foot

G1690.thayer: I snort (with the notion of coercion springing out of displeasure, anger, indignation, antagonism), express indignant displeasure with some one; I charge sternly.
G1690.mounce: Attic spelling, ἐμβριμόομαι, to be greatly agitated, Jn. 11:33, 38; to charge or forbid sternly or vehemently, Mt. 9:30; Mk. 1:43; to express indignation, to censure, Mk. 14:5*
G1690.abbottSmith: to snort in (of horses, Æsch.), hence, to speak or act with deep feeling ( DCG , i, 62b);
G1690.strongs: from G1722 (ἐν) and (to snort with anger); to have indignation on, i.e. (transitively) to blame, (intransitively) to sigh with chagrin, (specially) to sternly enjoin

G3980.thayer: I obey one in authority, conform to advice, obey, follow.
G3980.mounce: to obey one in authority, Acts 5:29, 32; Tit. 3:1; genr. to obey, follow, or conform to advice, Acts 27:21*
G3980.abbottSmith: to obey one in authority, be obedient : Tit 3:1 ; c. dat., Ac 27:21 ; θεῷ , Ac 5:29 , 32 .†
G3980.strongs: from a compound of G3982 (πείθω) and G757 (ἄρχω); to be persuaded by a ruler, i.e. (genitive case) to submit to authority; by analogy, to conform to advice

G5507.strongs: plural of uncertain affinity; a thousand

G2543.mounce: and yet, though, although, Acts 14:17; Heb. 4:3*
G2543.abbottSmith: and yet , although : Ac 14:17 ; c. ptcp., He 4:3 .†
G2543.strongs: from G2532 (καί) and G5104 (τοί); and yet, i.e. nevertheless

G1999.abbottSmith: a gathering, a riotous throng : Rec. (for ἐπίστασις , q.v.), Ac 24:12 , II Co 11:28 .†
G1999.strongs: from the middle voice of a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G4921 (συνιστάω); a conspiracy, i.e. concourse (riotous or friendly)
G1999.kjv: that which cometh upon, + raising up

G4061.mounce: circumcision, the act or custom of circumcision, Jn. 7:22, 23; Acts 7:8; the state of being circumcised, the being circumcised, Rom. 2:25, 26, 27; 4:10; meton. the circumcision, those who are circumcised, Rom. 3:30; 4:9 met. spiritual circumcision of the heart and affection, Rom. 2:29; Col. 2:11; meton. persons spiritually circumcised, Phil. 3:3 See circumcision.
G4061.abbottSmith: circumcision ; of the rite itself: Jo 7:22 , 23 , Ac 7:8 , Ro 4:11 , Ga 5:11 , Phl 3:5 ; of the state of circumcision: Ro 2:25-28 3:1 , I Co 7:19 , Ga 5:6 6:15 , Col 3:11 ; ἐν π. ὤν , Ro 4:10 ; by meton., ἡ π. = οἱ περιτμηθέντες , the circumcised : Ro 3:30 4:9 , 12 15:8 , Ga 2:7-9 , Eph 2:11 ; οἱ ἐκ τῆς π. , of Jews, Ro 4:12 ; of Jewish Christians, Ac 11:2 , Ga 2:12 , Tit 1:10 ; οἱ ἐκ π. πιστοί ,
G4061.strongs: from G4059 (περιτέμνω); circumcision (the rite, the condition or the people, literally or figuratively)

G3158.strongs: probably a shortened form of G3161 (Ματταθίας); Matthat (i.e. Mattithjah), the name of two Israelites

G2321.thayer: Theophilus, a friend of Luke of equestrian rank, to whom the Gospel and Acts are dedicated.
G2321.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and G5384 (φίλος); friend of God; Theophilus, a Christian

G831.thayer: I domineer, govern, have mastery over.
G831.mounce: to have authority over, domineer, 1 Tim. 2:12*
G831.abbottSmith: to govern , exercise authority over : I Ti 2:12 .†
G831.strongs: from a compound of G846 (αὐτός) and an obsolete (a worker); to act of oneself, i.e. (figuratively) dominate

G3597.mounce: to journey, journeying, travel, Jn. 4:6; 2 Cor. 11:26*
G3597.strongs: from the same as G3596 (ὁδοιπορέω); travel

G3951.thayer: I stir up, incite, urge on.
G3951.mounce: to stir up, incite, instigate, Acts 13:50*
G3951.abbottSmith: to urge on , stir up : c. acc. pers., Ac 13:50 (Pind., Hipp., and late writers).†
G3951.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and (to spur); to urge along, i.e. stimulate (to hostility)

G2367.mounce: thyme, of θυία, thya, an aromatic evergreen tree, arbor vitae, resembling the cedar, and found in Libya, Rev. 18:12*
G2367.strongs: from a derivative of G2380 (θύω) (in the sense of blowing; denoting a certain fragrant tree); made of citron-wood

G1.thayer: alpha; the first letter of the Greek alphabet.
G1.mounce: first letter of Greek alphabet, Alpha, Rev. 1:8.; 21:6; 22:13*
G1.abbottSmith: alpha , the first letter of the Greek alphabet. As a numeral, αʹ = 1, α͵ = 1000. As a prefix, it appears to have at least two and perhaps three distinct senses: ἀ- (before a vowel, ἀν- ) negative , as in ἄ-γνωστος , ἄ-δικος . ἀ- , ἁ- copulative , indicating community and fellowship, as in ἁ-πλοῦς , ἀ-κολουθέω , ἀ-δελφός . An intensive force (LS, s. α ), as in ἀ-τενίζω is sometimes assumed (but v.
G1.strongs: of Hebrew origin; the first letter of the alphabet; figuratively, only (from its use as a numeral) the first:

G3495.thayer: a young man, youth, an attendant.
G3495.mounce: a young man, youth, Mk. 14:51; 16:5; used of one in the prime of life, Mt. 19:20, 22; νεανίσκοι, soldiers, Mk. 14:51
G3495.abbottSmith: a young man , youth : Mt 19:20 , 22 , Mk 14:51 16:5 , Lk 7:14 , Ac 2:17 (LXX) 23:18 , 22 , I Jo 2:13 , 14 ; of an attendant (cf. Ge 14:24 , al.): Ac 5:10 .†
G3495.strongs: from the same as G3494 (νεανίας); a youth (under forty)

G2822.thayer: called, invited, summoned by God to an office or to salvation.
G2822.mounce: called, invited, in NT called to privileges or function, Mt. 20:16; 22:14; Rom. 1:1, 6, 7; 8:28; 1 Cor. 1:1, 2, 24; Jude 1; Rev. 17:14
G2822.abbottSmith: called , invited (as to a banquet, Æsch., I Ki 1:41 , III Mac 5:14 ); in NT, always of the Divine call; to some office: κ. ἀπόστολος , Ro 1:1 , I Co 1:1 ; to salvation: Ro 8:28 , I Co 1:24 , Ju 1 ; κ. καὶ ἐκλεκτοὶ κ. πιστοί , Re 17:14 ; in gosp. (not in pl., v.s. καλέω ) disting. fr. ἐκλεκτός , Mt 20:16 (WH, txt., R, omit), 22:14 ; κ. Ἰησοῦ Χρ. , Ro 1:6 ; κ. ἅγιοι , saints by calling , Ro 1:7 , I
G2822.strongs: from the same as G2821 (κλῆσις); invited, i.e. appointed, or (specially), a saint

G1801.thayer: I take into my ear, give ear to, listen to.
G1801.mounce: to give ear, listen, pay attention to, Acts 2:14*
G1801.abbottSmith: to give ear to, hearken to : c. acc, Ac 2:14 .†
G1801.strongs: middle voice from a compound of G1722 (ἐν) and G3775 (οὖς); to take in one's ear, i.e. to listen

G2240.thayer: I have come, am present, have arrived.
G2240.mounce: to become, have arrived, Mt. 8:11; Mk. 8:3; Lk. 15:27; Rev. 15:4*
G2240.abbottSmith: pf. with pres. meaning (hence impf. = plpf.), to have come , be present : Mt 24:50 , Mk 8:3 (late pf., ἧκα , v. Swete, in l.; WH, App. , 169), Lk 12:46 15:27 , Jo 8:42 , He 10:7 , 9 , 37 , I Jn 5:20 , Re 2:25 3:3 , 9 15:4 ; seq. ἀπό , c. gen. loc., Mt 8:11 , Lk 13:29 ; ἐκ , Ro 11:26 ; id. seq. εἰς , Jo 4:47 ; μακρόθεν , Mk 8:3 ; ἐπί , c. acc., Re 3:3 . Metaph., of discipleship: Jo 6:37 ; of time a
G2240.strongs: a primary verb; to arrive, i.e. be present (literally or figuratively)

G822.mounce: an exhalation, vapor, smoke, Acts 2:19; Jas. 4:14*
G822.strongs: from the same as G109 (ἀήρ); mist

G4287.mounce: a time before appointed, set or appointed time, Gal. 4:2*
G4287.abbottSmith: appointed beforehand . In Attic law, as subst. (so always in cl.), ἡ π. (sc. ἡμέρα ), a day appointed beforehand , a previously appointed time : Ga 4:2 .†
G4287.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); fixed beforehand, i.e. (feminine with G2250 (ἡμέρα) implied) a designated day

G2811.thayer: glory, fame, praise; rumor, report, credit.
G2811.mounce: pr. rumor, report; good report, praise, credit, 1 Pet. 2:20*
G2811.strongs: from a shorter form of G2564 (καλέω); renown (as if being called)

G1885.strongs: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G757 (ἄρχω) (meaning a governor of a district, "eparch"); a special region of government, i.e. a Roman præfecture

G192.thayer: incontinence, intemperance (in wide sense), lack of restraint.
G192.mounce: intemperance, self-indulgence, Mt. 23:25; unruly appetite, lustfulness, 1 Cor. 7:5*
G192.abbottSmith: in Arist. and later writers = ἀκράτεια (Lft., Notes , 222 f.), want of power , hence want of self-control , incontinence : Mt 23:25 , I Co 7:5 .†

G1473.mounce: I, gen., ἐμοῦ [μου], dat., ἐμοί [μοι], acc., ἐμέ [με]
G1473.strongs: a primary pronoun of the first person I (only expressed when emphatic)

G5095.thayer: Timothy, a Christian of Lystra, helper of Paul.
G5095.mounce: Timotheus, Timothy, pr. name, son of Eunice, traveling companion of Paul, Acts 16:1; Rom. 16:21; 1 Cor. 4:17; 2 Cor. 1:1; Phil. 1:1; Col. 1:1; 1 Thess. 1:1; 1 Tim. 1:2, 18; 6:20; 2 Tim. 1:2
G5095.strongs: from G5092 (τιμή) and G2316 (θεός); dear to God; Timotheus, a Christian

G578.thayer: I look away from all else at one object, look steadfastly.
G578.mounce: pr. to look off from all other objects and at a single one; hence, to turn a steady gaze, to look with fixed and earnest attention, Heb. 11:26*
G578.abbottSmith: to look away from all else at one object ; hence, to look steadfastly : He 11:26 (cf. ἀφοράω ).†
G578.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G991 (βλέπω); to look away from everything else, i.e. (figuratively) intently regard

G1266.thayer: I divide up into parts, break up; I distribute.
G1266.mounce: to divide into parts and distribute, Mt. 27:35; Mk. 15:24; Acts 2:3; pass. in NT to be in a state of dissension, Lk. 11:17, 18; 12:52, 53
G1266.abbottSmith: to distribute : c. dat. pers., Ac 2:45 ; seq. εἰς , Lk 22:17 , pass., Ac 2:3 . Mid. to distribute among themselves : Mt 27:35 , Mk 15:24 , Lk 23:34 ; with redundant ἑαυτοῖς , Jo 19:24 (LXX) (v. M, Pr. , 157).
G1266.strongs: from G1223 (διά) and G3307 (μερίζω); to partition thoroughly (literally in distribution, figuratively in dissension)

G3673.thayer: of the same trade or craft.
G3673.mounce: to the same trade or occupation, Acts 18:3*
G3673.abbottSmith: practising the same craft , of the same trade : Ac 18:3 .†
G3673.strongs: from the base of G3674 (ὁμοῦ) and G5078 (τέχνη); a fellow-artificer

G2778.mounce: a census, assessment, enumeration of the people and a valuation of their property; in NT tribute, tax, Mt. 17:25; poll-tax, Mt. 22:17, 19; Mk. 12:14*
G2778.abbottSmith: a poll-tax : Mt 17:25 22:17 , Mk 12:14 (WH, mg., ἐπικεφάλαιον ); τ. νόμισμα τοῦ κ. , Mt 22:19 (v. MM, Exp. , xv).†
G2778.strongs: of Latin origin; properly, an enrollment ("census"), i.e. (by implication) a tax

G3176.strongs: superlative of G3173 (μέγας); greatest or very great

G4893.mounce: consciousness, Heb. 10:2; a present idea, persisting notion, impression of reality, 1 Pet. 2:19; conscience, as an inward moral impression of one’s actions and principles, Acts 23:1; 24:16; Rom. 9:1; 2 Cor. 1:12; conscience, as the inward faculty of moral judgment, Rom. 2:15; 13:5; 1 Cor. 8:7b, 10, 12; 10:25, 27, 28, 29; 2 Cor. 4:2; 5:11; 1 Tim. 1:5, 19; 3:9; 4:2; 2 Tim. 1:3; conscience, as the inward moral and spiritual frame, Tit. 1:15; Heb. 9:9, 14; 10:22; 13:18; 1 Pet. 3:16, 21*
G4893.abbottSmith: consciousness : c. gen. obj., He 10:2 , I Pe 2:19 .
G4893.strongs: from a prolonged form of G4894 (συνείδω); co-perception, i.e. moral consciousness

G312.thayer: I bring back word, report; I announce, declare.
G312.mounce: to bring back word, announce, report, Mk. 5:14; to declare, set forth, teach, Jn. 5:24
G312.abbottSmith: to bring back word , report (Æsch., Thuc., al.): Jo 5:15 (WH, εἶπεν ), Ac 14:27 15:4 , II Co 7:7
G312.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος); to announce (in detail)
G312.kjv: declare, rehearse, report, show, speak, tell

G64.mounce: to take in hunting, catch, Mk. 12:13*
G64.abbottSmith: to catch or take by hunting or fishing; metaph., Mk 12:13 .†
G64.strongs: from G61 (ἄγρα); to hunt, i.e. (figuratively) to entrap

G2256.mounce: also spelled ἡμίωρον, half an hour, Rev. 8:1*
G2256.strongs: from the base of G2255 (ἥμισυ) and G5610 (ὥρα); a half-hour

G1605.thayer: I strike with panic or shock; I amaze, astonish.
G1605.mounce: pr. to strike out of; hence, to strike out of one’s wits, to astound, amaze; pass., overwhelmed, Mt. 7:28; 13:54
G1605.abbottSmith: prop., to strike out, drive away .
G1605.strongs: from G1537 (ἐκ) and G4141 (πλήσσω); to strike with astonishment

G4285.thayer: I lead onward by example, go before, prefer.
G4285.mounce: to go before, precede, lead onward; met. to endeavor to take the lead of, vie with, or, to give precedence to, to prefer, Rom. 12:10*
G4285.abbottSmith: to go before as leader (in cl., c. gen., dat.): Ro 12:10 (Chrys., Vg., al.; v. ICC , in l.).
G4285.strongs: from G4253 (πρό) and G2233 (ἡγέομαι); to lead the way for others, i.e. show deference

G3691.mounce: sharp, keen, Rev. 1:16; 2:12; 14:14, 17, 18; 19:15; swift, nimble, Rom. 3:15*
G3691.strongs: probably akin to the base of G188 (ἀκμήν) ("acid"); keen; by analogy, rapid

G2570.thayer: beautiful, as an outward sign of the inward good, noble, honorable character; good, worthy, honorable, noble, and seen to be so.
G2570.mounce: pr. beautiful; good, of good quality or disposition; fertile, rich, Mt. 13:8, 23; useful, profitable, Lk. 14:34; καλόν ἐστι[ν], it is profitable, it is well, Mt. 18:8, 9; excellent, choice, select, goodly, Mt. 7:17, 19; καλόν ἐστι[ν], it is pleasant, delightful, Mt. 17:4; just, full measure, Lk. 6:38; honorable, distinguished, Jas. 2:7; good, possessing moral excellence, worthy, upright, virtuous, Jn. 10:11, 14; 1 Tim. 4:6; τὸ καλόν, and τὸ καλόν ἔργον, what is good and right, a good deed, rectitude, virtue, Mt. 5:16; Rom. 7:18, 21; right, duty, propriety, Mt. 15:26; benefit, favor, Jn. 10:32, 33
G2570.abbottSmith: primarily, of outward form ("related to . . . ἀγαθός as the appearance to the essence," Cremer, 339), fair , beautiful : λίθοι (EV, goodly ), Lk 21:5 . In reference to use, of that which is well adapted to its ends, good , excellent : of fish, τ. καλά (opp. to σαπρά ), Mt 13:48 ; σπέρμα , Mt 13:24 , 27 , 37 , 38 ; καρπός , Mt 3:10 7:17-19 12:33 , Lk 3:9 [WH] 6:43 ; δένδρον (opp. to σαρπόν ), Mt 12
G2570.strongs: of uncertain affinity; properly, beautiful, but chiefly (figuratively) good (literally or morally), i.e. valuable or virtuous (for appearance or use, and thus distinguished from G18 (ἀγαθός), which is properly intrinsic)
G2570.kjv: X better, fair, good(-ly), honest, meet, well, worthy

G4784.thayer: I give a vote with, assent to.
G4784.mounce: to set down together with; mid. to agree, accord, Lk. 23:51*
G4784.abbottSmith: to deposit together . Mid., to deposit one's vote with , hence, to agree with, assent to : c. dat., Lk 23:51 .†
G4784.strongs: middle from G4862 (σύν) and G2698 (κατατίθημι); to deposit (one's vote or opinion) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to accord with

G5073.mounce: contracted form of τετραπλόος, four times (as much), fourfold, quadruple, Lk. 19:8*
G5073.strongs: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and a derivative of the base of G4118 (πλεῖστος); quadruple

G4338.thayer: I fasten with nails, nail to.
G4338.mounce: to nail to, affix with nails, Col. 2:14*
G4338.abbottSmith: to nail to : c. acc. et dat., fig., Col 2:14 .†
G4338.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and a derivative of G2247 (ἧλος); to peg to, i.e. spike fast

G1009.mounce: a bunch or cluster of grapes, Rev. 14:18*
G1009.abbottSmith: a cluster of grapes : Re 14:18 (cf. στραφυλή ) .†
G1009.strongs: of uncertain derivation; a bunch (of grapes)

G1391.thayer: honor, renown; glory, an especially divine quality, the unspoken manifestation of God, splendor.
G1391.mounce: pr. a seeming; appearance; a notion, imagination, opinion; the opinion which obtains respecting one; reputation, credit, honor, glory; in NT honorable consideration, Lk. 14:10; praise, glorification, honor, Jn. 5:41, 44; Rom. 4:20; 15:7; dignity, majesty, Rom. 1:23; 2 Cor. 3:7; a glorious manifestation, glorious working, Jn. 11:40; 2 Pet. 1:3; pl. dignitaries, 2 Pet. 2:10; Jude 8; glorification in a future state of bliss, 2 Cor. 4:17; 2 Tim. 2:10; pride, ornament, 1 Cor. 11:15; 1 Thess. 2:20; splendid array, pomp, magnificence, Mt. 6:29; 19:28; radiance, dazzling lustre, Lk. 2:9; Acts 22:11
G1391.abbottSmith: in cl., expectation, judgment, opinion ( IV Mac 5:18 ). opinion, estimation in which one is held, repute ; in NT, always good opinion , hence reputation, praise, honour, glory : Lk 14:10 , Jo 12:43 , He 3:3 ; opp. to αἰσχύνη , Phl 3:19 ; to ἀτιμία , II Co 6:8 ; δ. κ. τιμή , Ro 2:7 , 10 , I Pe 1:7 , II Pe 1:17 ; ζητεῖν , Jo 5:44 7:18 8:50 , I Th 2:6 ; λαμβάνειν , Jo 5:41 , II Pe 1:17 , Re 5:12 ; δι
G1391.strongs: from the base of G1380 (δοκέω); glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

G5162.strongs: from G5142 (τρέφω); a nourisher, i.e. nurse

G2312.mounce: taught of God, divinely instructed, 1 Thess. 4:9*
G2312.abbottSmith: taught of God (cf. διδακτοὶ θεοῦ , Jo 6:45 ): I Th 4:9 .†
G2312.strongs: from G2316 (θεός) and G1321 (διδάσκω); divinely instructed

G4322.thayer: I fill up by adding to, supply.
G4322.mounce: to fill up by addition; to supply deficiencies, 2 Cor. 9:12; 11:9*
G4322.abbottSmith: to fill up by adding to , to supply fully : II Co 9:12 11:9 .†
G4322.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and G378 (ἀναπληρόω); to fill up further, i.e. furnish fully

G1412.thayer: I empower, fill with power, strengthen.
G1412.abbottSmith: to make strong, strengthen : Eph 6:10 (WH, mg.; ἐνδυν- , WH, txt., RV), Col 1:11 , He 11:34 .†

G3599.strongs: perhaps from the base of G2068 (ἐσθίω); a "tooth"

G4889.thayer: a fellow slave, fellow servant; of Christians: a fellow worker, colleague.
G4889.mounce: a fellow-slave, fellow-servant, Mt. 24:49; 18:28f., 31, 33; Col. 4:7; Rev. 6:11; 19:10; 22:9; a fellow-minister of Christ, Col. 1:7*
G4889.abbottSmith: a fellow-servant : Mt 18:28-33 24:49 ; of servants of the same divine Lord, Col 1:7 4:7 , Re 6:11 ; so of angels, Re 19:10 22:9 .†
G4889.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and G1401 (δοῦλος); a co-slave, i.e. servitor or ministrant of the same master (human or divine)

G2301.thayer: I make a public show of, expose to public shame.
G2301.mounce: to be exposed as in a theater, to be made a gazing-stock, object of scorn, Heb. 10:33*
G2301.abbottSmith: to make a spectacle of , expose to contempt (eccl.; ἐκθ- , Polyb.): pass., He 10:33 .†
G2301.strongs: from G2302 (θέατρον); to expose as a spectacle

G1982.mounce: to overshadow, Mt. 17:5; met. to shed influence upon, Lk. 1:35
G1982.abbottSmith: to throw a shadow upon, overshadow : c. dat., Ac 5:15 ; of a shining (Mt, l.c., and cf. Ex 40:29 , 35 , II Mac 2:8 ) cloud, c. dat., Mk 9:7 ; c. acc, Mt 17:5 , Lk 9:34 ; metaph. (cf. Pss, Pr, ll.c.), of the Holy Spirit, Lk 1:35 .†
G1982.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a derivative of G4639 (σκιά); to cast a shade upon, i.e. (by analogy) to envelop in a haze of brilliancy; figuratively, to invest with preternatural influence

G4677.thayer: Susannah, a woman of the retinue of Jesus.
G4677.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H07799 feminine); lily; Susannah (i.e. Shoshannah), an Israelitess

G201.thayer: auditorium, recitation hall, court room (for hearing cases).

G3688.mounce: donkey, ass, male or female, Mt. 21:2, 5, 7
G3688.strongs: apparently a primary word; a donkey

G2222.thayer: life, both of physical (present) and of spiritual (particularly future) existence.
G2222.mounce: life, living existence, Lk. 16:25; Acts 17:25; in NT spiritual life of deliverance from the proper penalty of sin, which is expressed by θάνατος, Jn. 6:51; Rom. 5:18; 6:4; the final life of the redeemed, Mt. 25:46; life, source of spiritual life, Jn. 5:39; 11:25; Col. 3:4
G2222.abbottSmith: life (in Hom., Hdt., = βίος , q.v.; later, existence , vita quâ vivimus , as distinct from βίος , vita quam vivimus ; opp. to θάνατος ); of natural life: Lk 16:25 , Ac 8:33 , I Co 15:19 , I Ti 4:8 , He 7:3 , Ja 4:14 ; πνεῦμα ζωῆς , Re 11:11 ; ψυχὴ ζωῆς ( Ge 1:30 ), Re 16:3 ; of the life of one risen from the dead, Ro 5:10 , He 7:16 . Of the life of the kingdom of God, the present life of grace and

G2446.thayer: the Jordan, a great river flowing due south and bounding Galilee, Samaria, and Judea on the east.
G2446.mounce: the river Jordan, Mt. 3:5; Mk. 10:1; Lk. 4:1; Jn. 3:26
G2446.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H03383); the Jordanes (i.e. Jarden), a river of Palestine

G2026.mounce: to build upon, 1 Cor. 3:10, 12, 14; pass. met. to be built upon as parts of a spiritual structure, Eph. 2:20; to build up, carry up a building; met. to build up in spiritual advancement,Col. 2:7; Jude 20*
G2026.abbottSmith: in NT, always metaph., of the spiritual life regarded as a building (Cremer, 449);
G2026.strongs: from G1909 (ἐπί) and G3618 (οἰκοδομέω); to build upon, i.e. (figuratively) to rear up

G330.thayer: I thrive or flourish again, revive.
G330.mounce: to grow or bloom again; to renew, Phil. 4:10*
G330.abbottSmith: to revive : Phl 4:10 (of MM, VGT , s.v.).†
G330.strongs: from G303 (ἀνά) and (to flourish); to revive

G2913.thayer: Crescens, a Christian, coadjutor of Paul.
G2913.mounce: Crescens, a Latin pr. name, 2 Tim. 4:10*
G2913.strongs: of Latin origin; growing; Cresces (i.e. Crescens), a Christian

G5185.mounce: blind, Mt. 9:27, 28; 11:5; 12:22; met. mentally blind, Mt. 15:14; 23:16
G5185.abbottSmith: blind : as subst., ὁ τ. , Mt 9:27 , Mk 8:22 , Lk 4:18 (LXX) , Jo 5:3 , al. Metaph.: Mt 15:14 23:16-26 , Jo 9:39-41 , Ro 2:19 , II Pe 1:9 , Re 3:17 .
G5185.strongs: from, G5187 (τυφόω); opaque (as if smoky), i.e. (by analogy) blind (physically or mentally)

G4435.mounce: together with the forearm, or, with care, carefully, Mk. 7:3*
G4435.abbottSmith: the fist : πυγμῇ νίψασθαι τ. χεῖρας (T, πυκνά ; Vg., Goth., Copt., crebro ), to wash the hands with the fist ( diligently , R, txt.; up to the elbow , R, mg.; the exact meaning is doubtful; v. Swete, in l.): Mk 7:3 .†
G4435.strongs: from a primary (the fist as a weapon); the clenched hand, i.e. (only in dative case as adverb) with the fist (hard scrubbing)

G1136.thayer: Gog, in Ezekiel a king of Magog, a land of the remote north; hence, in Revelation, of a people far remote from Palestine.
G1136.mounce: Gog, pr. name of a nation, indecl., Rev. 20:8*
G1136.strongs: of Hebrew origin (H01463); Gog, a symbolic name for some future Antichrist

G649.thayer: I send forth, send (as a messenger, commission, etc.), send away, dismiss.
G649.mounce: to send forth a messenger, agent, message, or command, Mt. 2:16; 10:5; to put forth into action, Mk. 4:29; to liberate, rid, Lk. 4:19; to dismiss, send away, Mk. 12:3
G649.abbottSmith: prop., to send away , to dispatch on service; to send with a commission, or on service; of persons: Christ, Mt 10:40 ; the apostles, 10:16 ; servants, Mk 12:2 ; angels, 13:27 ; of things: ὄνος , Mt 21:3 ; τὸ δρέπανον , Mk 4:29 ; τ. λόγον , Ac 10:36 ; τ. ἐπαγγελίαν (i.e. the promised Holy Spirit), Lk 24:49 , Rec.; seq. εἰς , Mt 20:2 , Lk 11:49 , Jo 3:17 ; ὀπίσω , Lk 19:14 ; ἔμπροσθεν , Jo 3:28 ; πρ
G649.strongs: from G575 (ἀπό) and G4724 (στέλλω); set apart, i.e. (by implication) to send out (properly, on a mission) literally or figuratively
G649.kjv: put in, send (away, forth, out), set (at liberty)

G741.thayer: prop: I arrange, make ready; I season, flavor.
G741.mounce: pr. to fit, prepare; to season, make savoury, Mk. 9:50; Lk. 14:34; Col. 4:6*
G741.strongs: from a presumed derivative of G142 (αἴρω); to prepare, i.e. spice (with stimulating condiments)

G2068.thayer: I eat, partake of food; met: I devour, consume (e.g. as rust does).
G2068.mounce: to eat, Mt. 12:1; 15:27; ἐσθίειν καὶ πίνειν, to eat and drink, to eat and drink in the usual manner, follow the common mode of living, Mt. 11:18; also with the associated notion of supposed security, Lk. 17:27; to feast, banquet, Mt. 24:49; met. to devour, consume, Heb. 10:27; Jas. 5:3; from the Hebrew, ἄρτον ἐσθίειν, to eat bread, to take food, take the usual meals, Mt. 15:2
G2068.abbottSmith: to eat ; absol.: Mt 14:20 , 21 Mk 6:31 , Jo 4:31 , al.; ἐν τ. φαγεῖν (on this aor. form, v. M, Pr. , 111), I Co 11:21 ; διδόναι φαγεῖν , c. dat. pers., Mk 5:43 , al.; ἐ. καὶ πίνειν , Mt 6:25 , 31 Lk 10:7 , al.; of ordinary use of food and drink, I Co 9:4 11:22 ; of partaking of food at table, Mk 2:16 , Lk 5:30 , al.; opp. to fasting, Mt 11:18 , Lk 5:33 , al.; of revelling, Mt 24:49 , Lk 12:45 . c.
G2068.strongs: strengthened for a primary (to eat); used only in certain tenses, the rest being supplied by G5315 (φάγω); to eat (usually literal)

G5565.mounce: can function as an improper prep., apart, Jn. 20:7; apart from, parted from, Jn. 15:5; Jas. 2:18, 20, 26; alien from, Eph. 2:12; apart from, on a distinct footing from, 1 Cor. 11:11; apart from, distinct from, without the intervention of, Rom. 3:21, 28; 4:6; apart from the company of, independently of, 1 Cor. 4:8; Heb. 11:40 without the presence of, Heb. 9:28; without the agency of, Jn. 1:3; Rom. 10:14; without the employment of, Mt. 13:34; Mk. 4:34; Heb. 7:20, 21; 9:7, 18, 22; without, Lk. 6:49; Phil. 2:14; 1 Tim. 2:8; 5:21; Phlm. 14; Heb. 10:28; 11:6; 12:8, 14; clear from, Heb. 7:7; irrespectively of, Rom. 7:8, 9; without reckoning, besides, Mt. 14:21; 15:38; 2 Cor. 11:28; with the exception of, Heb. 4:15
G5565.strongs: adverb from G5561 (χώρα); at a space, i.e. separately or apart from (often as preposition)

G3727.mounce: an oath, Mt. 14:7, 9; 26:72; meton. that which is solemnly promised, a vow, Mt. 5:33
G3727.strongs: from (a fence; perhaps akin to G3725 (ὅριον)); a limit, i.e. (sacred) restraint (specially, an oath)

G3920.mounce: secretly introduced, brought in stealthily, Gal. 2:4*
G3920.abbottSmith: brought in secretly (as spies or traitors): Ga 2:4 .†

G1848.thayer: I set at naught, ignore, despise.
G1848.mounce: also spelled ἐξουθενόω, to make light of, set at naught, despise, treat with contempt and scorn, Lk. 18:9; to neglect, disregard, 1 Thess. 5:20; ἐξουθενημένος, contemptible, 2 Cor. 10:10; of small account, 1 Cor. 1:28; 6:4; by impl. to reject with contempt, Acts 4:11
G1848.abbottSmith: to set at nought, despise utterly, treat with contempt : c. acc pers., Lk 18:9 23:11 , Ro 14:3 , 10 , I Co 16:11 ; c. acc rei, Ga 4:14 , I Th 5:20 ; pass., of persons: Mk 9:12 (T, -όω ), I Co 6:4 ; of things λίθος , Ac 4:11 (LXX ἀπεδοκίμασαν ); λόγος , II Co 10:10 ; τὰ ἐξουθενημένα , I Co 1:28 .†
G1848.strongs: a variation of G1847 (ἐξουδενόω) and meaning the same
G1848.kjv: contemptible, despise, least esteemed, set at nought

G2544.strongs: from G2543 (καίτοι) and G1065 (γέ); and yet indeed, i.e. although really

G411.thayer: indescribable, that cannot be thoroughly related, inexpressible.
G411.mounce: which cannot be related, inexpressible, unutterable, indescribable, 2 Cor. 9:15*
G411.strongs: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G1555 (ἐκδιηγέομαι); not expounded in full, i.e. indescribable

G541.thayer: a light flashing forth (from), radiation, gleam.
G541.abbottSmith: of light beaming from a luminous body, radiance , effulgence : He 1:3 .†
G541.strongs: from a compound of G575 (ἀπό) and G826 (αὐγάζω); an off-flash, i.e. effulgence

G957.mounce: better; βέλτιον, as an adv., very well, too well to need informing, 2 Tim. 1:18*
G957.abbottSmith: neut. as adv. , better , very well : II Ti 1:18 (also Ac 10:28 D). On elative use in these passages, v. M, Pr. , 78, 236.†
G957.strongs: neuter of a compound of a derivative of G906 (βάλλω) (used for the comparative of G18 (ἀγαθός)); better

G2592.mounce: to bear fruit, yield, Mk. 4:28; met, to bring forth the fruit of action or conduct, Mt. 13:23; Rom. 7:5; mid. to expand by fruitfulness, to develop itself by success, Col. 1:6, 10
G2592.abbottSmith: to bear fruit : χόρτον , Mk 4:28 (cf. Wi 10:7 ). Metaph., of conduct: Mt 13:23 , Mk 4:20 , Lk 8:15 , Ro 7:4 , 5 , Col 1:10 ; mid., Col 1:6 .†
G2592.strongs: from G2593 (καρποφόρος); to be fertile (literally or figuratively)

G1071.mounce: laughter; by impl. mirth, joy, rejoicing, Jas. 4:9*
G1071.strongs: from G1070 (γελάω); laughter (as a mark of gratification)

G3447.thayer: I make an image of a calf.
G3447.mounce: to form an image of a calf, Acts 7:41*
G3447.abbottSmith: to make a calf (as an image): Ac 7:41 (LXX, ἐποίησε μόσχον ).†
G3447.strongs: from G3448 (μόσχος) and G4160 (ποιέω); to fabricate the image of a bullock

G614.mounce: hidden away; concealed, Mk. 4:22; Lk. 8:17; stored up, Col. 2:3*
G614.abbottSmith: hidden : Mk 4:22 , Lk 8:17 , Col 2:3 (v. Lft., in l.; MM, s.v.).†
G614.strongs: from G613 (ἀποκρύπτω); secret; by implication, treasured

G4871.mounce: to cause to come together, collect, assemble, congregate; mid. to convene to one’s self, Acts 1:4*
G4871.abbottSmith: to assemble with : Ac 1:4 (EV, mg., eating with , but on this rendering and on the force of the pres. ptcp. here, v. Field, Notes , 110).†
G4871.strongs: from G4862 (σύν) and (to throng); to accumulate, i.e. convene

G1402.mounce: to reduce to servitude, enslave, oppress by retaining in servitude, Acts 7:6; 2 Pet. 2:19; met. to render subservient, 1 Cor. 9:19; pass. to be under restraint, 1 Cor. 7:15; to be in bondage, spiritually or morally, Gal. 4:3; Tit. 2:3; to become devoted to the service of, Rom. 6:18, 22*
G1402.abbottSmith: to enslave, bring into bondage : Ac 7:6 (LXX) , II Pe 2:19 ; metaph., I Co 9:19 ; pass., seq. ἐν , ib. 7:15 ; τ. θεῷ , Ro 6:22 ; τ. δικαιοσύνῃ , Ro 6:18 ; οἴνῳ , Tit 2:3 ; ὑπὸ τὰ στοιχεῖα τ. κόσμου , Ga 4:3 (Cremer, 217).†
G1402.strongs: from G1401 (δοῦλος); to enslave (literally or figuratively)
G1402.kjv: bring into (be under) bondage, X given, become (make) servant

G5246.mounce: pr. swollen, overgrown; of language, swelling, pompous, boastful, 2 Pet. 2:18; Jude 16*
G5246.abbottSmith: of excessive weight or size ; metaph., e xcessive, immoderate , in late writers, of arrogant speech (v. Mayor on Ju, l.c. ): n. pl., II Pe 2:18 , Ju 16 .†
G5246.strongs: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G3591 (ὄγκος); bulging over, i.e. (figuratively) insolent

G1366.thayer: (lit: twain-mouthed; hence: of a sword, as a drinker of blood), two-edged.
G1366.mounce: pr. having two mouths; two-edged, Heb. 4:12; Rev. 1:16; 2:12*
G1366.abbottSmith: of rivers and roads, double-mouthed, double branching .

G1673.strongs: from G1672 (Ἕλλην); Hellenic, i.e. Grecian (in language)

G4379.mounce: to touch upon, touch lightly, with the dative, Lk. 11:46*
G4379.abbottSmith: in poët. and late writers, to touch : c. dat., Lk 11:46 .†
G4379.strongs: from G4314 (πρός) and (to touch); to impinge, i.e. lay a finger on (in order to relieve)

G3903.thayer: I prepare; mid: I prepare, make preparations.
G3903.mounce: to prepare, make ready, 2 Cor. 9:2, 3; mid. to prepare one’s self, put one’s self in readiness, Acts 10:10; 1 Cor. 14:8*
G3903.abbottSmith: to prepare , make ready : absol. (sc. τ. δεῖπνον , etc.; of. Hdt., ix, 82, II Mac 2:27 ), Ac 10:10 ; mid., to prepare , make preparations : I Co 14:8 (cf. Je 50:42 ); pass., II Co 9:2 , 3 .†
G3903.strongs: from G3844 (παρά) and a derivative of G4632 (σκεῦος); to furnish aside, i.e. get ready
